skills.name: A2p Registration Inspector
skills.description: Diagnoses WHY a TCR A2P 10DLC brand or campaign submission was rejected (or is stuck pending). Pulls current state via GHL MCP + saved submission records, cross-checks against `company-profile.md` + `compliance-rules.md` + known TCR healthcare-rejection patterns. Outputs a structured diagnostic + fix plan. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Operationalize Step 1-2 of `a2p-resubmission.sop.md` (capture rejection precisely + categorize the failure). Healthcare/pharma-adjacent businesses get extra TCR scrutiny — this inspector knows that pattern. Hands a clean diagnostic to `a2p-resubmission-builder` so the resubmission package is right the first time.
best_for: Brand or campaign stuck >7 days pending
Pre-submission sanity check before pushing first-time
skills.estimated_time: 20-45 min per diagnostic
skills.body_html:
A2P Registration Inspector

The diagnostic gate for TCR A2P 10DLC failures. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Pulls the current TCR brand + campaign state (via GHL's A2P UI surface + saved submission records — GHL MCP does not expose TCR directly, so this composes documented inspection over the visible surface). Cross-checks brand info against company-profile.md, campaign use cases + sample messages against compliance-rules.md + TCR healthcare-rejection patterns, opt-in flow documentation completeness, HELP/STOP handling. Outputs a structured diagnostic naming the root cause + the specific fields to fix.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Authoring the corrected submission (a2p-resubmission-builder), final compliance review (compliance-officer), deployment to GHL (workflow-automation-builder), TCR human-review escalation (Chris + Twilio support).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (rejection-email | manual | weekly-cron | status-watcher-alert)
    required: true
  rejection_payload:
    type: object
    required: false  # required if trigger=rejection-email
    fields:
      reason_code: string  # TCR standardized code (capture verbatim)
      reason_text: string  # full rejection text from TCR
      scope: enum (brand | campaign | both)
      campaign_id: string  # if scope=campaign
      received_at: ISO
  location_id:
    type: string
    default: "JhHwwzVOQOZO2IFLSJAD"
  saved_submission_path:
    type: string
    default: "~/Projects/matrix-store/docs/ghl/a2p-compliance/blueprint.md"
validation:
  - For rejection-email trigger: full rejection text must be captured verbatim (no paraphrase)
  - Location ID must match Matrix's (`JhHwwzVOQOZO2IFLSJAD`)

RULES

  • Capture rejection verbatim — paraphrase = lost signal. Save full text + screenshot to _Logs/ghl/a2p/<YYYY-MM-DD>-rejection.md.
  • Healthcare/pharma = extra scrutiny — Matrix is FLAGGED in TCR. Compounding pharmacy + supplements pattern triggers manual review. Apply higher rigor.
  • Cross-check brand fields against company-profile.md exactly — legal name must match EIN of record (Matrix Advanced Solutions LLC pending Chris verification), HQ address (951 De Soto Road, Suite 427, Boca Raton, FL 33432), website (https://matrixadvancedsolutions.com), industry = HEALTHCARE.
  • Cross-check sample messages against compliance-phrase-detector — flag banned phrases ("FDA-approved", "doctor-recommended", "guaranteed", "risk-free"), missing opt-out, missing brand name, prohibited content (controlled-substance specifics, dosing advice).
  • Use-case mismatch is the #1 healthcare rejection driver — if "Mixed" is declared but samples include marketing language, TCR will reject. Force separation: transactional / marketing / customer-care.
  • Opt-in flow must be documentable — screenshot of web form + opt-in language + double-opt-in for marketing campaigns. If undocumented → flag as root cause.
  • HELP/STOP must appear in sample messages — at minimum first message + recurring messages.
  • Privacy + terms URLs must resolve — known blocker (see ghl-config.md § A2P prerequisite blockers: /privacy and /terms currently 404 on matrix-order-form.vercel.app).
  • Never invent EIN, DUNS, or registration numbers — flag [verify with Chris] if missing.
  • Confidence honest — H = direct evidence in rejection text; M = strong pattern match; L = inference only.

TOOLS

tools:
  mcps:
    - mcp__gohighlevel__locations_get-location (read Matrix location data)
    - mcp__gohighlevel__locations_get-custom-fields (any A2P data in custom fields)
    - WebFetch (TCR rejection-reason lookups, carrier policy docs)
    - WebSearch (recent healthcare-vertical TCR rejection patterns)
  internal_data:
    - _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (A2P section + prerequisite blockers)
    - _Shared Context/company-profile.md (legal entity, EIN, address)
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (banned phrases, TCPA discipline)
    - _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md (TCPA / A2P 10DLC pathway)
    - _Shared Context/sops/a2p-resubmission.sop.md (failure-category table)
    - _Logs/ghl/a2p/ (prior rejections + history)
    - ~/Projects/matrix-store/docs/ghl/a2p-compliance/blueprint.md (last saved submission)
  composed_skills:
    - compliance-phrase-detector (atomic — scans every sample message)
    - compliance-officer (consult for borderline content)
  llm:
    model: claude-sonnet-4 (judgment-heavy — pattern-matching healthcare rejections)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    diagnostic_id: string  # YYYY-MM-DD-<short-hash>
    trigger: enum
    scope: enum (brand | campaign | both)
    rejection_reason_verbatim: string
    rejection_reason_code: string | null
    root_cause_category:
      type: enum
      values:
        - brand_verification  # EIN/legal-name/address/DUNS mismatch
        - campaign_use_case   # use-case doesn't match opt-in source
        - sample_message      # banned phrase / missing opt-out / wrong tone
        - opt_in_docs         # consent capture not documented
        - industry_class      # healthcare classification missing extra docs
        - privacy_terms_404   # legal pages unreachable
        - throughput          # requested volume above vetting tier
        - other
      confidence: enum (H | M | L)
    specific_fields_failing:
      type: array
      items:
        field: string         # e.g., "brand.legal_business_name", "campaign[1].sample_messages[0]"
        current_value: string
        expected_value: string
        source_of_truth: string  # citation: company-profile.md / compliance-rules.md / etc.
    fix_required:
      type: array
      items:
        action: string        # imperative — "Change brand legal name to 'Matrix Advanced Solutions LLC'"
        owner: enum (assistant | chris | counsel)
        blocking: boolean
    escalation_needed: boolean
    escalation_reason: string | null  # if industry-class or counsel decision required
    estimated_resubmission_readiness: ISO  # when fix could be ready
  format: JSON (machine-readable) + Markdown diagnostic report
  quality_bar:
    - Rejection reason captured verbatim (not paraphrased)
    - root_cause_category populated with H/M/L confidence
    - Every fix_required action specific enough to execute
    - No invented values (every expected_value cites source-of-truth)
    - Stored at `_Logs/ghl/a2p/<YYYY-MM-DD>-diagnostic.md`

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream: a2p-resubmission-builder
  payload: full diagnostic schema above
  fallback_routing:
    - if escalation_needed = true (industry-class / counsel question) → Chris + counsel queue; chain pauses
    - if root_cause_category = privacy_terms_404 → `production-deploy.sop.md` to ship pages BEFORE resubmission-builder runs
    - if confidence = L → manual review by Chris before resubmission-builder fires
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/ghl/a2p/<YYYY-MM-DD>-diagnostic.{md,json}
    retention: indefinite (audit trail for TCR pattern analysis)

Eval cases

Stored at _Logs/eval-suites/a2p-registration-inspector/:

  1. Brand rejection — EIN mismatch → root_cause=brand_verification, confidence=H, specific field flagged
  2. Campaign rejection — "Mixed" use case with marketing samples → root_cause=campaign_use_case, fix=split campaigns
  3. Sample-message rejection — "FDA-approved" in copy → root_cause=sample_message, banned phrase named
  4. Opt-in undocumented → root_cause=opt_in_docs, fix=screenshot web form + add double-opt-in
  5. Healthcare extra-docs request → root_cause=industry_class, escalation_needed=true (Chris + counsel)
  6. /privacy or /terms 404 → root_cause=privacy_terms_404, blocking=true, ship pages first
  7. Stuck pending >14 days, no rejection received → manual trigger, output = status query + nudge plan
  8. Two-rejection pattern detected → escalation_needed=true (per SOP Step 8 — Twilio human review)

Anti-patterns

  • Paraphrasing the rejection text — kills the signal; TCR reason codes matter
  • Picking the most-likely cause when text is ambiguous — return confidence=L + Chris reviews
  • Skipping privacy/terms 404 check — known Matrix blocker; always verify before deeper diagnostic
  • Inventing EIN / DUNS / address — flag [verify with Chris]; never guess
  • Treating Matrix as a generic SMB — pharma-adjacent = healthcare scrutiny; apply rigor
  • Recommending resubmission without compliance-phrase-detector on samples

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read GHL location + custom fields
  • ✅ Read all _Shared Context/ + _Logs/
  • ✅ WebFetch TCR policy + reason-code docs
  • ✅ Write diagnostic to _Logs/ghl/a2p/
  • ⚠️ Cannot mutate GHL state (read-only)
  • ⚠️ Cannot submit to TCR (Chris approves via a2p-resubmission-builder)
  • 🚫 Cannot invent registration values

Pairing

Composed by: a2p-status-watcher, ghl-continuous-improvement, compliance-officer, Chris (manual) Composes: compliance-phrase-detector (atomic), compliance-officer (consult) Reads: ghl-config.md, company-profile.md, compliance-rules.md, regulatory-frameworks.md, sops/a2p-resubmission.sop.md

One-liner reminder

Capture rejection verbatim. Map to category. Cite source-of-truth for every fix. Healthcare = extra scrutiny. Confidence honest. Hand off clean.

skills.name: A2p Resubmission Builder
skills.description: Generates a corrected TCR A2P 10DLC brand + campaign resubmission package from an `a2p-registration-inspector` diagnostic. Produces corrected brand profile (JSON), use-case-correct campaign descriptions, 5-7 sample messages per campaign (every message passing `compliance-phrase-detector`), opt-in flow documentation, HELP/STOP specs. Composes `compliance-officer` final gate. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Operationalize Steps 3-6 of `a2p-resubmission.sop.md` (draft fix → compliance review → Chris approval → resubmit). Healthcare/pharma is high-scrutiny — this builder bakes in TCR's extra requirements (use-case split, opt-in proof, no claims language, brand-name + opt-out in every sample). Output is submission-ready; Chris approves; then `workflow-automation-builder` pushes to GHL.
best_for: Inspector diagnostic complete + fix is clear
First-time A2P submission after privacy/terms shipped
Splitting Mixed campaigns into separate use-cases
Refreshing sample messages after content changes
skills.estimated_time: 45-90 min per package
skills.body_html:
A2P Resubmission Builder

The package author. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Takes a diagnostic from a2p-registration-inspector and produces a complete, submission-ready A2P 10DLC resubmission package: corrected brand profile JSON, campaign-by-campaign use case + description + sample messages, opt-in flow documentation, HELP/STOP wiring spec. Every sample message passes compliance-phrase-detector and compliance-officer Gate 1 before the package leaves this skill. Healthcare scrutiny baked in — Matrix's pharma-adjacent profile means TCR requires extra documentation; this builder produces it.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Diagnosing failures (a2p-registration-inspector), pushing to GHL (workflow-automation-builder), shipping /privacy + /terms pages (production-deploy.sop.md), TCR human-review escalation.

INPUT

input:
  diagnostic:
    type: object (full output schema from a2p-registration-inspector)
    required: true
  scope:
    type: enum (brand-only | campaign-only | both)
    required: true
  campaigns_to_rebuild:
    type: array of campaign names
    required: false  # required if scope includes campaign
    default: ["MAS Order Status", "MAS Appointment Reminders", "MAS Affiliate Program", "MAS Cart Recovery", "MAS Account Notifications"]
  privacy_terms_live:
    type: boolean
    required: true  # MUST be true; otherwise block
validation:
  - privacy_terms_live must be true (blocker per `ghl-config.md`)
  - diagnostic.escalation_needed must be false (else route Chris + counsel first)
  - diagnostic.confidence ≥ M (else manual review before this skill runs)

RULES

  • Brand description MUST match company-profile.md exactly — legal name Matrix Advanced Solutions LLC (with [verify with Chris] flag on EIN until confirmed), HQ 951 De Soto Road, Suite 427, Boca Raton, FL 33432, website https://matrixadvancedsolutions.com, industry vertical Healthcare, sub-category Pharmaceuticals — Compounding.
  • Split "Mixed" use cases — TCR rejects healthcare Mixed pattern. Required split:
    • MAS Order Lifecycle (transactional only — order received/paid/shipped/delivered)
    • MAS Account Care (low-volume customer service — credentialing, missed-call text-back)
    • MAS Provider Marketing (marketing — clinic outreach + reorder reminders + affiliate; requires confirmed double-opt-in)
    • MAS Appointment Notifications (transactional reminders + post-call ask)
    • MAS Cart Recovery (marketing, 1-msg/30-day cap; confirmed opt-in only)
  • Every sample message MUST:
    • Lead with brand identifier ("Matrix" or "MAS")
    • Include opt-out in first message + recurring marketing messages ("Reply STOP to opt out")
    • Be under 160 chars where possible; never > 320
    • Pass compliance-phrase-detector (no banned phrases per compliance-rules.md)
    • Avoid pharma-claim language (no "FDA-approved", "doctor-recommended", "guaranteed", "treat / cure / prevent disease", dosing specifics)
    • Use no emoji, no URL shorteners, no all-caps shouting
  • Opt-in flow MUST be auditable per channel:
    • Web form (https://matrixadvancedsolutions.com + https://matrix-order-form.vercel.app) — screenshot + opt-in language captured
    • Voice consent (Mack inbound) — explicit "May we text you about your order?" + GHL conversation log
    • Email confirmation (double-opt-in for marketing) — confirm link → tag added
  • Industry = HEALTHCARE — sub-category Pharmaceuticals — Compounding triggers TCR extra-docs request. Attach: state pharmacy board licensure summary, 503-A/B classification disclosure, provider-only access language.
  • HELP / STOP / RESEND / UNSUBSCRIBE — every campaign defines explicit response messages.
  • Provider-only language — Matrix never sells D2C; sample messages reflect B2B (clinic → clinic) context.
  • No PHI in sample messages — sample names = "Dr. Sample" / "Sample Clinic"; no real patient or provider names.
  • All package artifacts versioned — _Logs/ghl/a2p/<YYYY-MM-DD>-package-v<N>/.

TOOLS

tools:
  mcps:
    - mcp__gohighlevel__locations_get-location (verify current brand state pre-write)
    - mcp__gohighlevel__locations_get-custom-fields
  internal_data:
    - _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (A2P section)
    - _Shared Context/company-profile.md (legal entity SOT)
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (banned phrases + TCPA)
    - _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md (A2P 10DLC pathway)
    - _Shared Context/sops/a2p-resubmission.sop.md
    - _Logs/outreach/email-templates/ (adapt to SMS length)
    - _Logs/ghl/a2p/<diagnostic-id> (input)
    - ~/Projects/matrix-advanced-solutions/PRIVACY POLICY - matrix.pdf
    - ~/Projects/matrix-advanced-solutions/TERMS OF SERVICE Matrix.pdf
  composed_skills:
    - compliance-phrase-detector (atomic — every sample message)
    - compliance-officer (Gate 1 — final review before Chris)
    - workflow-automation-builder (downstream — deploys after Chris approval)
  llm:
    model: claude-sonnet-4 (copy authoring with compliance constraints)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    package_id: string  # YYYY-MM-DD-v<N>
    package_path: string  # _Logs/ghl/a2p/<package_id>/
    brand_profile:
      legal_business_name: "Matrix Advanced Solutions LLC"
      ein: "[verify with Chris — current GHL config flags pending]"
      duns_number: "[verify with Chris — not currently in ghl-config]"
      hq_address: "951 De Soto Road, Suite 427, Boca Raton, FL 33432"
      website: "https://matrixadvancedsolutions.com"
      support_email: "support@matrixadvancedsolutions.com"
      industry_vertical: "Healthcare"
      industry_sub_category: "Pharmaceuticals — Compounding"
      business_type: "Private"
      brand_relationship: "Direct customer of CSP (Twilio via GHL)"
      stock_exchange: "Not listed"
      additional_docs:
        - description: "State pharmacy board licensure summary"
          path: "[Chris to provide]"
        - description: "503-A/B classification disclosure"
          path: "_Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md § 503-A/B"
        - description: "Provider-only access policy"
          path: "company-profile.md § Off-limits / constraints"
    campaigns:
      - name: string
        use_case: enum (transactional | marketing | mixed_low_volume | customer_care)
        description: string  # 100-500 char TCR-readable description
        message_flow: string  # how/when messages fire
        opt_in_source: string  # explicit channel + audit trail
        opt_in_keywords: ["START", "YES", "SUBSCRIBE"]
        opt_out_keywords: ["STOP", "END", "CANCEL", "UNSUBSCRIBE", "QUIT"]
        help_keywords: ["HELP", "INFO"]
        help_response: string  # exact text
        sample_messages:
          - text: string
            passes_compliance_phrase_detector: boolean (must be true)
            includes_brand: boolean (must be true)
            includes_opt_out: boolean (true for first + recurring marketing)
            char_count: number
        monthly_volume_estimate: number
        throughput_per_minute: number  # be conservative for healthcare
    opt_in_documentation:
      - channel: string
        screenshot_path: string
        capture_language_verbatim: string
        double_opt_in: boolean  # true for marketing campaigns
    chris_approval_ask:
      verify_required:
        - "EIN of record matches IRS records exactly"
        - "DUNS number (if applicable) on file"
        - "State pharmacy board license summary attached"
      review_required:
        - "Sample messages for each campaign"
        - "Opt-in flow documentation"
      sign_off_location: "_Logs/ghl/a2p/<package_id>/approval.md"
    submission_timing:
      recommended_submit_date: ISO
      reason: string  # e.g., "Submit Tuesday-Thursday to avoid weekend queue"
  format: JSON manifest + Markdown package + brand-profile.json + campaign-<n>.json
  quality_bar:
    - 100% of sample messages pass compliance-phrase-detector
    - 100% of sample messages include brand name
    - 100% of marketing-campaign first messages include opt-out
    - Every campaign has use_case explicitly named (no "Mixed" for marketing)
    - Brand fields cite company-profile.md or flag [verify with Chris]
    - compliance-officer Gate 1 signoff captured before Chris ask

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream: compliance-officer (Gate 1) → Chris approval → workflow-automation-builder
  payload: full package manifest + signed approval
  fallback_routing:
    - if compliance-officer Gate 1 fails → loop back, revise samples
    - if Chris rejects package → loop back with Chris's notes
    - if privacy_terms_live = false → block, route to `production-deploy.sop.md`
    - if EIN/DUNS unverified after 7 days → escalate to Chris with hard ask
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/ghl/a2p/<package_id>/
    retention: indefinite (TCR submission audit trail)

Eval cases

Stored at _Logs/eval-suites/a2p-resubmission-builder/:

  1. Healthcare brand registration first-time → full 5-campaign split + opt-in docs + extra-docs attached
  2. "Mixed → split" rebuild → 1 transactional, 1 customer-care, 1 marketing campaign produced
  3. Sample-message rebuild after compliance-phrase-detector flagged "guaranteed" → revised + passes
  4. Affiliate campaign requires double-opt-in → opt-in flow documented + confirm-link spec
  5. Cart recovery 1-msg/30-day cap → throughput + frequency disclosure in description
  6. Brand-only rebuild (EIN correction) → no campaign changes, just brand profile
  7. compliance-officer Gate 1 rejects opt-in language → builder revises until Gate 1 passes
  8. Chris rejects sample tone (too aggressive) → revised within 1 cycle

Anti-patterns

  • Submitting "Mixed" use cases for healthcare — auto-reject pattern
  • Sample messages without brand name — TCR rejects
  • Sample messages with banned phrases ("FDA-approved", "guaranteed") — auto-reject + compliance hit
  • Opt-in flow described in prose without screenshot — TCR wants proof, not description
  • Skipping compliance-officer Gate 1 — Chris's approval shouldn't be the first compliance check
  • Inventing EIN / DUNS / address — flag [verify with Chris]; never guess
  • Real provider / patient names in samples — PHI exposure
  • Submitting before /privacy + /terms are live — guaranteed rejection

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Author corrected brand profile + campaigns + samples
  • ✅ Compose compliance-phrase-detector + compliance-officer Gate 1
  • ✅ Write package to _Logs/ghl/a2p/<package_id>/
  • ⚠️ Cannot mutate GHL brand state (handoff to workflow-automation-builder)
  • ⚠️ Cannot submit to TCR (Chris approval required first)
  • 🚫 Cannot invent EIN / DUNS / registration numbers
  • 🚫 Cannot ship without /privacy + /terms live

Pairing

Composed by: a2p-registration-inspector (handoff), ghl-crm-architect, Chris (manual) Composes: compliance-phrase-detector, compliance-officer (Gate 1), workflow-automation-builder (downstream deploy) Reads: ghl-config.md, company-profile.md, compliance-rules.md, regulatory-frameworks.md, sops/a2p-resubmission.sop.md

One-liner reminder

Brand matches company-profile.md exactly. Split Mixed use cases. Every sample passes compliance-phrase-detector. Opt-in is audited not described. Healthcare = attach extra docs. Chris approves before submit.

skills.name: A2p Status Watcher
skills.description: Daily cron-poll of Matrix's TCR A2P 10DLC brand + campaign status via GHL. Detects state transitions (pending → approved / pending → rejected / approved → suspended). On rejection or suspension, auto-fires `a2p-registration-inspector`. Appends to `_Logs/ghl/a2p/status-history.csv`. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Close the silent-failure gap. Brands stuck pending or campaigns silently suspended kill SMS deliverability for weeks before anyone notices. This watcher catches the state change within 24 hours + auto-triggers diagnostic. Cheap to run; high leverage when something breaks.
best_for: Pre-launch verification before enabling SMS workflows
skills.estimated_time: 2-5 min per run
skills.body_html:
A2P Status Watcher

The daily detector. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Polls Matrix's TCR brand + campaign state visible via GHL (location JhHwwzVOQOZO2IFLSJAD). Compares against the previous day's snapshot. On state change, logs the transition + appends to history CSV. On adverse change (rejection / suspension / vetting-score drop), fires a2p-registration-inspector and notifies Chris. On approval, notifies Chris + queues post-approval validation (per a2p-resubmission.sop.md Step 9). Daily, Mon-Fri, 10am PT.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Diagnosing rejections (a2p-registration-inspector), authoring fixes (a2p-resubmission-builder), submitting (workflow-automation-builder), TCR direct access (GHL is the only Matrix surface).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (cron-daily | manual | post-submit-watch)
    required: true
  location_id:
    type: string
    default: "JhHwwzVOQOZO2IFLSJAD"
  previous_snapshot_path:
    type: string
    default: "_Logs/ghl/a2p/status-snapshot-latest.json"
validation:
  - location_id matches Matrix's
  - If previous_snapshot missing → treat as bootstrap; write fresh snapshot; no alert

RULES

  • Daily Mon-Fri 10am PT is the canonical cron — weekends skip (TCR doesn't process weekends).
  • Read-only against GHL — no mutations. Inspection only.
  • State transitions worth alerting on:
    • Brand: pending → approved (✅ celebrate + queue validation), pending → rejected (🚨 fire inspector), approved → suspended (🚨 fire inspector + Chris immediate ping)
    • Campaign: same enum per campaign
    • Vetting score drop > 10 points → 🟡 flag (precursor to issues)
    • Pending > 7 business days → 🟡 nudge (potential stuck)
    • Pending > 14 business days → 🟠 escalate (Twilio human-review per SOP Step 8)
  • Bootstrap behavior — first run writes fresh snapshot; no alerts (no prior state to compare).
  • Idempotency — same state two days in a row → no alert, log only. Don't spam.
  • Failure to fetch — GHL MCP error or rate limit → log + retry once; if still failing → write status=error + alert Chris.
  • Auto-fire chain — adverse transitions invoke a2p-registration-inspector with trigger=status-watcher-alert.
  • Privacy/terms check piggyback — every run also pings https://matrix-order-form.vercel.app/privacy + /terms (known blocker) and alerts if still 404.

TOOLS

tools:
  mcps:
    - mcp__gohighlevel__locations_get-location (read A2P state visible on location)
    - mcp__gohighlevel__locations_get-custom-fields (look for A2P fields if present)
    - WebFetch (ping /privacy + /terms URLs for 200/404)
  internal_data:
    - _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (A2P prerequisite blockers + 5 campaign names)
    - _Logs/ghl/a2p/status-snapshot-latest.json (prior state)
    - _Logs/ghl/a2p/status-history.csv (audit trail)
  composed_skills:
    - a2p-registration-inspector (fire on adverse state)
    - chain-monitor (digest delivery)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (state-comparison is deterministic; LLM only for human-friendly summary)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    run_id: string  # YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM
    run_at: ISO
    trigger: enum
    snapshot:
      brand:
        status: enum (not-submitted | pending | approved | rejected | suspended | error)
        vetting_score: number | null
        last_state_change: ISO | null
        days_in_current_state: number
      campaigns:
        - name: string
          status: enum
          vetting_score: number | null
          last_state_change: ISO | null
          days_in_current_state: number
      privacy_url_status: enum (200 | 404 | error)
      terms_url_status: enum (200 | 404 | error)
    transitions_detected:
      - scope: brand | campaign
        target: string
        from_state: string
        to_state: string
        severity: info | nudge | escalate | adverse
    actions_taken:
      - action: string  # e.g., "fired a2p-registration-inspector for campaign 'MAS Cart Recovery'"
        target_skill: string
        target_diagnostic_id: string | null
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2 | P3
        message: string
        recipients: array  # [Chris, chain-monitor]
    history_csv_appended: boolean
  format: JSON snapshot + CSV row appended + Markdown summary if transitions detected
  quality_bar:
    - Snapshot written to status-snapshot-latest.json (overwrites prior)
    - History CSV append-only (audit trail intact)
    - Every adverse transition invokes inspector + alerts Chris
    - No alert spam (idempotency on identical states)

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - On adverse: a2p-registration-inspector (auto-fire)
    - On approval: chain-monitor digest + Chris notification + SOP Step 9 validation queue
    - On stable: silent log only
    - On stuck > 14 business days: escalation queue (Chris + Twilio human-review per SOP Step 8)
  payload:
    - full snapshot
    - transitions_detected
    - run_id (for traceback)
  fallback_routing:
    - GHL MCP unavailable → log error + retry next cron run + Chris ping after 3 consecutive failures
    - privacy/terms 404 → block any in-flight resubmission package + alert Chris
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/ghl/a2p/status-snapshot-latest.json + _Logs/ghl/a2p/status-history.csv
    retention: indefinite

Eval cases

Stored at _Logs/eval-suites/a2p-status-watcher/:

  1. Bootstrap run (no prior snapshot) → fresh snapshot written, no alerts
  2. No state change → log only, no alert
  3. Brand pending → approved → P1 alert to Chris + queue SOP Step 9 validation
  4. Campaign pending → rejected → fires inspector, P0 alert
  5. Brand approved → suspended → fires inspector, P0 immediate Chris ping
  6. Pending > 7 days → P2 nudge
  7. Pending > 14 days → P1 escalate to Twilio human-review
  8. /privacy still 404 → P1 alert, block any in-flight resubmission package
  9. GHL MCP fails → retry; if 3rd failure → P1 Chris alert
  10. Vetting score drops 15 points → P2 flag for inspector preventive review

Anti-patterns

  • Alert spam on identical states — must be idempotent
  • Mutating GHL state — watcher is read-only; mutations live in resubmission-builder + workflow-automation-builder
  • Ignoring privacy/terms 404 — known Matrix blocker; check every run
  • Running on weekends — TCR doesn't process; just adds noise
  • Inferring TCR state from GHL UI alone when ambiguous — flag as error instead of guessing
  • Firing inspector for benign transitions (pending → approved) — that's a celebration, not a diagnostic

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read GHL location + custom fields (daily)
  • ✅ WebFetch /privacy + /terms (daily)
  • ✅ Write to _Logs/ghl/a2p/ (snapshot + history)
  • ✅ Fire a2p-registration-inspector on adverse transitions
  • ✅ Send alerts to Chris + chain-monitor
  • 🚫 Cannot mutate GHL state
  • 🚫 Cannot submit to TCR
  • 🚫 Cannot resolve rejections (handoff only)

Cron config

scheduled_task:
  id: a2p-status-watcher-daily
  cron: "0 17 * * 1-5"  # 10:00 AM PT = 17:00 UTC, Mon-Fri
  skill: a2p-status-watcher
  args: { trigger: cron-daily }
  alert_on_failure: chain-monitor + Chris

Note: cron registration handled by Chris via scheduled-tasks MCP after skill landed.

Pairing

Composed by: scheduled-tasks cron, chain-monitor, Chris (manual) Composes: a2p-registration-inspector (auto-fire on adverse), chain-monitor (digest) Reads: ghl-config.md, _Logs/ghl/a2p/

One-liner reminder

Daily Mon-Fri 10am PT. Read-only. Idempotent. Adverse = fire inspector. Approval = celebrate + validate. /privacy + /terms checked every run.

skills.name: Account Manager
skills.description: Owns the customer relationship AFTER customer-onboarding completes the 30-day milestone. Drives reorders, surfaces expansion, runs scheduled check-ins, watches for churn signals, escalates to humans when relationship matters. The agent that prevents the "customer activated then disappeared" failure mode.
skills.intent: Be the dedicated AM for every active customer. Track activity (orders, conversations, support tickets), spot churn risk early, drive reorder cadence, surface expansion opportunities, schedule + run periodic check-ins. Never auto-commits price/refund. Hands off to human AM for high-value relationship moments.
skills.estimated_time: 10-30 min per customer touch; ongoing per account
skills.body_html:
Account Manager

The post-onboarding owner. Closes the "customer activated then disappeared" gap.

When to use

  • Customer hits "active" status (post 30-day onboarding milestone)
  • Periodic check-in cadence (30 / 60 / 90 / quarterly)
  • Churn risk signal triggers (no orders in N days, support escalation, satisfaction drop)
  • Expansion / upsell opportunity surfaces
  • Renewal window approaches

When NOT to use

  • New customer in onboarding (use customer-onboarding)
  • Sales motion to a new prospect (use sales-strategy)
  • Single support ticket (use voice-ai-support-agent or email-triage)

Required inputs

  1. Customer / contact — GHL contact ID with status:active tag
  2. Account tier — standard / white-glove / enterprise (from onboarding)
  3. Last touch — most recent meaningful interaction
  4. Customer-defined success — captured during onboarding kickoff

If 4 missing, ask: "What did the customer define as 'successful' during their onboarding kickoff?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Pull account health

For the customer:

  • Order pattern: last N orders, cadence (weekly / monthly / sporadic), trend (up / flat / down)
  • Revenue this period: $X this 30/90 days vs prior
  • Engagement: last login to store, conversation responses, content opens
  • Support history: open / recent tickets, resolution outcomes
  • Custom field: customer_defined_success from kickoff

Step 2 — Compute health score

Signal Healthy At-risk Critical
Order cadence On expected pattern Slowing No order 60+ days
Revenue trend Stable or growing Flat 2+ months Declining 3+ months
Engagement Logging in, replying Sporadic Dark 30+ days
Support < 2 tickets/month, resolved More tickets, slow resolution Open critical or repeated escalation
Sentiment Positive in last touch Neutral Negative or complaint

Compute overall: 🟢 Healthy / 🟡 At-risk / 🔴 Churn-risk

Step 3 — Check-in cadence

Tier Cadence
Standard 30 / 60 / 90 days; then quarterly
White-glove 14 / 30 / 60 / 90 days; then monthly
Enterprise Weekly first month; biweekly second; monthly thereafter

Before each scheduled check-in:

  • Prep brief (use discovery-call-prep adapted)
  • Surface: what they ordered, what's working, expansion opportunity, any concerns
  • Schedule the call (or fire voice-ai-support-agent if a basic touch suffices)

Step 4 — Churn signals (auto-surface daily via ghl-daily-pulse)

If any:

  • No order in 30+ days (and pattern was monthly) → ⚠️ alert
  • No order in 60+ days → 🔴 alert
  • Open support ticket > 48h → 🔴 alert
  • Negative sentiment in last conversation → ⚠️ alert
  • Login activity dropped 80%+ → 🟡 alert

For each, generate a save play:

  • Outreach plan (channel + script)
  • Specific offer (training session, account audit, intro to AM)
  • Internal escalation (loop in human AM if high-value)

Step 5 — Expansion signals

If any:

  • Ordering same product 3+ times in a row → ready for bundle / volume tier
  • Asked about a product not in current tier → upgrade opportunity
  • Hiring NP or expanding clinic → cross-sell modality
  • Referred another clinic → introduce affiliate program (if not already)

For each, propose:

  • Specific offer
  • Channel (email + voice combo usually)
  • Owner (AI for low-touch; human AM for high-touch)

Step 6 — Quarterly Business Review (QBR) prep

For each customer, every 90 days:

  • Pull all metrics (orders, revenue, NPS, support tickets)
  • Compare to customer-defined success
  • Surface wins + gaps
  • Propose next-quarter expansion plan
  • Schedule QBR call

Step 7 — Output the account health report

Output structure

# Account Health: <Customer Name> — <Date>

## Customer
**Clinic**: <name>
**Tier**: <standard / white-glove / enterprise>
**AM (human)**: <name>
**Active since**: <date>
**Customer-defined success**: <captured>

## Health
**Overall**: 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴
**Reasoning**: <one paragraph>

| Signal | Value | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Order cadence | <pattern> | <status> |
| Revenue (90d) | $X vs $Y prior | <status> |
| Engagement | <metric> | <status> |
| Support | <metric> | <status> |
| Sentiment | <signal> | <status> |

## Recent activity (last 30 days)
- <activity summary>

## Customer-defined success — progress
- <goal 1>: <progress>
- <goal 2>: <progress>

## Next touch scheduled
- **Date**: <date>
- **Type**: <call / email / SMS>
- **Owner**: <AM name or skill>

## Expansion opportunities surfaced
| Opportunity | Signal | Proposed approach | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|
| <opp> | <signal> | <plan> | <AI / human AM> |

## Churn risks surfaced (if any)
| Risk | Signal | Save play | Urgency |
|---|---|---|---|
| <risk> | <signal> | <plan> | <immediate / soon> |

## ✅ I handled
- Pulled all metrics
- Computed health score
- Scheduled next touch
- Surfaced opportunities + risks
- Prepped brief for AM (if human-touch call)

## ⚠️ Need from you (or human AM)
- Approve expansion approach (if non-standard)
- Hold the scheduled call (if human AM owns this customer)
- Review save play for any 🔴 churn risk
- Decide on enterprise tier upgrade (if signaled)

## 🚫 You only
- Any pricing / contract changes
- Refund / credit decisions
- Escalation calls to your level

## Handoff
**To**: Human AM (for high-value touch), `discovery-call-prep` (for QBR call prep), `voice-ai-onboarding-agent` (for low-touch routine check-ins), `email-triage` (for email follow-ups)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-account-mgmt-<customer>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • AI handles every touch — high-value relationships need human moments
  • Reactive only — proactive cadence prevents most churn
  • No customer-defined success tracking — can't measure if account is actually winning
  • Expansion before retention — fix the churn risk first
  • Save plays without specifics — "reach out" isn't a plan; "send Loom on X with offer Y" is

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull metrics + compute health
  • ✅ Schedule routine check-ins (per tier cadence)
  • ✅ Send routine touch emails / SMS (templated)
  • ✅ Generate prep briefs for AM
  • ⚠️ Propose expansion offers (Chris / AM approves)
  • ⚠️ Schedule call on Chris's calendar (per approval-rules.md)
  • 🚫 Commit any pricing / refund / credit
  • 🚫 Modify customer's contract / tier without approval

Pairing

Inputs from: customer-onboarding (hands off active customers), ghl-daily-pulse (signals), GHL contact + order history Outputs to: Human AM (high-touch), voice-ai-onboarding-agent (low-touch routine), pmo-operator (customer portfolio tracked), weekly-review (account health summary) Chains with: sales-strategy (expansion is mini-sales motion), voice-ai-support-agent (escalations from support)

One-liner reminder

Proactive cadence by tier. Health computed daily. Save plays specific. Expansion only after retention. Human-touch high-value moments. Never auto-commit money.

skills.name: Aeo Specialist
skills.description: Answer Engine Optimization — surfaces Matrix as the direct-answer source for Google AI Overviews, Perplexity, ChatGPT search, Bing Copilot, Claude search. Deeper specialty than ai-search-optimizer; focused specifically on the direct-answer surfacing layer (structured data + extractability + citability at query-time). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Answer engines return ONE answer (or 3 max) per query — quoting and citing a small set of sources. AEO tunes Matrix content so it's the source that gets quoted + cited. Different from AI search optimizer's broader strategy: this skill is about per-page extractability at the moment of query.
best_for: Per-query AEO tuning of specific high-intent pages
Quarterly AEO audit across answer engines
skills.estimated_time: Per page: 1-3 hours. Quarterly audit: 1-2 days.
skills.body_html:
AEO Specialist (Answer Engine Optimization)

The direct-answer specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Optimizes Matrix content for direct-answer surfacing in answer engines (Google AI Overviews / Bing Copilot / Perplexity / ChatGPT search / Claude search). Different from broader AI search visibility: this focuses on per-page structure that makes content extractable + quotable + citable at the moment of query. Overlaps with voice-search-optimizer (featured snippets) but extends to LLM-driven engines.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Broader LLM authority strategy (geo-specialist), traditional Google rank (content-seo-specialist), voice-assistant snippets specifically (voice-search-optimizer — but coordinates), site-wide schema (technical-seo-specialist — but coordinates).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (page-aeo-tune / faq-build / quarterly-audit / new-query-target / answer-engine-shift)
    required: true
  target_query: string  # specific query Matrix wants to be answer for
  page_url: string  # which page should provide the answer
  answer_engines: array

RULES

rules:
  extractability_principles:
    - Direct answer in first 40-60 words after question/topic
    - Answer self-contained (doesn't require reading earlier context)
    - Quotable / citable sentence structure
    - Concrete + factual + specific (LLMs prefer specifics over generalities)
    - Author byline visible with credentials
    - Source citations inline (LLMs follow citation chains for veracity)
    - Update date visible
  schema_for_aeo:
    - FAQPage + Question + Answer
    - HowTo with explicit steps
    - DefinedTerm for glossary content
    - Article + Author (with sameAs links to LinkedIn / ORCID for credibility)
    - SpeakableSpecification when relevant
    - WebPage with mainEntity properly set
  content_structure:
    - Question as H2 / H3 (mirrors query intent)
    - Answer paragraph immediately follows (no preamble)
    - Bullet / numbered list for multi-step
    - Table for comparison
    - Definition for "what is" queries
  citability_signals:
    - First-party data / original research (LLMs preferentially cite)
    - Numeric specifics (LLMs love quotable stats)
    - Expert author bylines + credentials
    - Date + source for every claim
    - Internal consistency (LLMs distrust contradictory pages)
  prohibited:
    - Schema not matching visible content
    - Astroturfed citations
    - Made-up data
    - Misleading citation chains

TOOLS

  • ChatGPT search, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini AI Overviews, Bing Copilot (test queries)
  • Schema.org validator + Google Rich Results Test
  • Google Search Console (snippet capture data)
  • Read: seo-targets.md Tier 4-5 + alm-dt-platform.md (citation gold) + steve-kushner-profile.md (author credibility)
  • Compose: voice-search-optimizer, content-seo-specialist, technical-seo-specialist, prior-art-citation-formatter
  • Write: per-query test results, per-page AEO recommendations

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    audit_id: string
    target_query: string
    answer_engine_results:
      - engine: string
        matrix_cited: boolean
        cited_source: string
        position_in_answer: number
        snippet_used: string
    page_extractability:
      direct_answer_present: boolean
      direct_answer_position: word_index
      direct_answer_length: number
      schema_appropriate: boolean
      schema_validates: boolean
      citations_inline: boolean
      author_visible: boolean
      update_date_visible: boolean
    score: number  # 0-100 AEO readiness
    recommendations:
      - priority: P0 | P1 | P2
        action: string
        expected_lift: string
        executor: this skill | content-seo-specialist | technical-seo-specialist | copywriting
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

  • → content-seo-specialist (briefs for AEO-optimized content)
  • → technical-seo-specialist (schema deploy)
  • → copywriting (rewrite to extractability standards)
  • → geo-specialist (broader LLM strategy intersections)

Eval cases

  1. Tier 1 commercial query — direct-answer optimize the ranking page
  2. FAQ page audit — extractability + schema
  3. New AI Overview pattern (Google rolls out new format) → response strategy
  4. Lost AEO citation → diagnostic + recovery
  5. Per-page audit before publish — bake AEO in

Anti-patterns

  • Schema mismatching content
  • Burying direct answer below preamble
  • Vague generalities vs specific facts
  • Missing author byline + credentials
  • No update date (LLMs deprioritize stale)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + recommendations
  • ✅ AEO-optimization briefs
  • 🚫 Schema for content not visible

Pairing

Composed by: seo-strategy-lead Composes: voice-search-optimizer, content-seo-specialist, technical-seo-specialist, prior-art-citation-formatter Reads: seo-targets.md, alm-dt-platform.md, steve-kushner-profile.md

One-liner reminder

Direct answer in 40-60 words. Schema valid + matches content. Author byline + credentials. Inline citations. Update date visible. Test across 5 answer engines.

skills.name: Agent Build Plan
skills.description: End-to-end build plan for a new AI agent. Runs the full Master Planning Checklist from _Knowledge Base/15 across all 10 phases (gate, architecture, prompts, memory, conversation, domain, reliability, product, launch, post-launch). Outputs a ready-to-build spec.
skills.body_html:
Agent Build Plan

Run the Master Planning Checklist for a specific agent build. Output a complete spec.

This skill operationalizes _Knowledge Base/15 - Master Planning Checklist.md. Read it for the full checklist.

Purpose

A new agent build has a 10-phase planning surface. This skill walks every phase and produces a spec you can hand to engineering.

When to use

  • After should-i-build-an-agent returned "build a single agent" or "build multi-agent"
  • Before any code is written for the agent
  • When estimating effort or scoping for a sprint

When NOT to use

  • Decision hasn't been made (run should-i-build-an-agent first)
  • Refactor of an existing agent (use targeted skills + KB files instead)
  • Workflow (no agent involved)

Required inputs

  1. Problem statement (from should-i-build-an-agent)
  2. Decision verdict (single agent vs multi-agent)
  3. Domain (sales, marketing, SEO, tutor, ops, etc.) — drives which Phase 5 file to load
  4. Audience (internal / customer-facing / both)
  5. Stakes (low / medium / high — drives Phase 6 depth)

If 3 is missing, ask: "What domain is this agent in — sales, marketing, SEO, tutor, ops, or other?"

Workflow (the 10 phases from file 15)

Phase 0 — Gate (verify)

Confirm should-i-build-an-agent output. If skipped, run it first.

Phase 1 — Architecture

Reference: _Knowledge Base/02 - Agent Architecture & Components.md

Determine:

  • Loop pattern: ReAct / Plan-Execute / Reflection / Tool-use
  • Tools list with read/write classification (writes need dry-run mode)
  • Memory tiers (context window strategy, conversation memory, long-term)
  • Execution context (state passed between steps)
  • Single vs multi-agent
  • Failure modes
  • Deployment target (cloud / local / hybrid)

Phase 2 — Prompts

Reference: _Knowledge Base/03 - Prompt Engineering Playbook.md

Determine:

  • System prompt sections (role / purpose / context / instructions / constraints / examples / format)
  • Tool descriptions
  • Few-shot examples
  • Refusal / guardrail clauses
  • Iteration plan (how prompts will be tuned)

→ Optionally invoke prompt-engineer for the system prompt itself.

Phase 3 — Memory & RAG

Reference: _Knowledge Base/04 - Memory, RAG & Knowledge Design.md

Determine:

  • In-context vs RAG vs fine-tune per knowledge type
  • Chunking, embedding, retrieval mode
  • Citation tracking
  • Conversation summarization
  • Token budget

Phase 4 — Conversation design (if user-facing)

Reference: _Knowledge Base/05 - Conversation Design.md

If the agent is user-facing:

  • Brand voice as system-prompt block
  • Opening / active listening / question rhythm
  • Voice-specific (if voice agent)
  • Closing / next-step setting
  • Anti-patterns

Phase 5 — Domain layer

Pick the right file:

  • Sales/lead-gen → 06 - Sales & Lead-Gen Agent Patterns.md
  • Productized agent → 07 + 09
  • Marketing/content → 10
  • SEO → 11
  • Code/UI → 12
  • Autonomous decisions / analytics → 13
  • Tutor → 14

Apply that file's patterns to this spec.

Phase 6 — Reliability, evaluation, safety

Reference: _Knowledge Base/08 - Reliability, Evaluation & Safety.md

CRITICAL — design these BEFORE the agent:

  • Schema validation at every boundary
  • Audit middleware (every LLM call logged)
  • PII handling, idempotency, trust-but-verify
  • Golden eval set (20+ test cases)
  • Unit / integration / behavioral tests
  • Refusal taxonomy + escalation path
  • Prompt-injection guards
  • Kill switch (30-second halt)

Phase 7 — Product & GTM (if productized)

Reference: _Knowledge Base/09 - AI Product Management & Roadmap.md

Determine:

  • Minimum acceptable performance (metric + threshold)
  • Latency budget (p50 + p99)
  • Cost-per-task ceiling
  • Fallback behavior
  • ICP, pricing, trust signals
  • Onboarding flow
  • Retraining loop

Phase 8 — Launch readiness

  • Eval suite passes on held-out test set
  • Paper-trade / dry-run completed for side-effecting agent
  • Rollback plan, runbook, cost monitor, drift monitor wired up
  • Owner contactable, user communication plan

Phase 9 — Post-launch

  • Weekly review of eval metrics + drift
  • User-feedback ingestion path
  • Cost-per-task trend
  • Model-swap test (cheaper model viable?)
  • Retraining cadence

Output structure

# Agent Build Plan: <Agent Name>

## Phase 0 — Gate
**Verdict**: <single agent / multi-agent>
**Reasoning**: <from should-i-build-an-agent output>

## Phase 1 — Architecture
**Loop**: <ReAct / Plan-Execute / etc.>
**Tools**:
| Tool | Read/Write | Dry-run? |
|---|---|---|
| <tool> | <r/w> | <y/n> |

**Memory tiers**: <context strategy>
**Single vs multi-agent**: <choice + reasoning>
**Deployment**: <cloud / local / hybrid + reason>

## Phase 2 — Prompts
**System prompt skeleton**: <see prompt-engineer skill output>
**Few-shots needed**: <list>
**Guardrails**: <list>

## Phase 3 — Memory & RAG
**Knowledge sources**: <list with retrieval mode each>
**Chunking strategy**: <approach>
**Citation tracking**: <yes/no + how>

## Phase 4 — Conversation (if applicable)
**Brand voice block**: <link to brand-identity.md or inline>
**Opening / closing patterns**: <brief>

## Phase 5 — Domain layer
**Domain file applied**: <e.g., 06 - Sales & Lead-Gen Agent Patterns>
**Domain-specific patterns**: <list with citations>

## Phase 6 — Reliability, Eval, Safety
**Eval set**: <where it lives, how many cases>
**Audit middleware**: <approach>
**Refusal taxonomy**: <list of categories>
**Kill switch**: <how>

## Phase 7 — Product / GTM (if productized)
**Min acceptable performance**: <metric + threshold>
**Latency budget**: p50=<>, p99=<>
**Cost ceiling**: <per task>
**Pricing model**: <per-seat / per-task / per-outcome / tier>

## Phase 8 — Launch
**Rollback plan**: <one-liner>
**Runbook owner**: <name>
**Day 1 checks**: <list>

## Phase 9 — Post-launch
**Weekly review cadence**: <when>
**Drift monitor**: <metric>
**Retraining trigger**: <condition>

## Effort estimate
- Engineering: <weeks>
- ML / prompt work: <weeks>
- Eval data prep: <weeks>
- Total: <weeks>

## Open questions
- <question>
- <question>

## Next step
<invoke `prompt-engineer` for system prompt | open eng ticket | etc.>

Anti-patterns

  • Skipping Phase 6 — building the agent before the eval harness. The KB's #1 anti-pattern.
  • Phase 0 hand-wave — failing to verify the build decision.
  • Over-detailed Phase 1 when stakes are low — match depth to stakes.
  • No effort estimate — every spec ends with a time box.

Chain to next skill

→ Invoke prompt-engineer for the system prompt. → Run domain-specific KB skills (e.g., lead-magnet-generator for sales agents).

skills.name: Agent Cloner
skills.description: Clone an existing skill for a new use case. "Build me X like Y but for Z" becomes one command. Input: source skill + the variation. Output: a new SKILL.md that inherits the pattern + adapts the specifics. Saves 60-80% of authoring time vs from-scratch.
best_for: Building variants (e.g., voice-ai-support-agent cloned from voice-ai-sales-agent)
Adapting a Cowork / PM Skills skill to Matrix context
Cloning an inbound flow for a new department
skills.body_html:
Agent Cloner

When a successful pattern exists, clone + adapt is faster than from-scratch.

When to use

  • A new use case is structurally similar to an existing skill but differs in audience / domain / details
  • Adapting harness skills (Cowork / PM Skills / Anthropic skills) to Matrix-specific context
  • Building department variants of meta-skills
  • Versioning a skill (v1 → v2 with breaking changes — but use file 16's versioning pattern in frontmatter)

When NOT to use

  • The new use case is genuinely novel (use skill-builder from scratch)
  • The "clone" would only differ by trivial substitutions (just edit the original)
  • Tweaking an existing skill (don't clone — edit)

Required inputs

  1. Source skill — path to the existing SKILL.md to clone
  2. New skill name — kebab-case, doesn't conflict with existing
  3. What differs — explicit list of changes (audience / inputs / workflow / output / pre-auths)
  4. What's the same — confirm the inherited pattern (so we don't drift)

If 3 is missing, ask: "What's different in the new use case? List specifically — audience, inputs, output, pre-auths, anything else."

Workflow

Step 1 — Verify source + check for conflicts

# Source exists?
ls "<source-path>/SKILL.md"

# New name doesn't conflict?
find _Skills\ Library -name "<new-name>" | head

Step 2 — Identify what to keep vs change

For each section of the source SKILL.md:

  • Frontmatter: keep type / theme; replace name, triggers, best_for, scenarios
  • Purpose: rewrite for new use case (1 paragraph)
  • When to use + When NOT to use: usually 50-80% the same; adapt audience
  • Required inputs: review per new use case
  • Workflow: usually 70-90% the same; substitute domain-specific steps
  • Output structure: usually 90% the same template, swap variables
  • Anti-patterns: usually 80% the same
  • Pre-authorizations: review per new auth context
  • Pairing: update inputs from / outputs to per new chains

Step 3 — Generate the cloned SKILL.md

Apply the source pattern with the substitutions. Run through file 16's quality bar to validate.

Step 4 — Surface what changed (transparency)

Output a diff summary so the user can confirm the clone is what they wanted, not just a rename:

Source: voice-ai-sales-agent
Clone:  voice-ai-support-agent

Differences applied:
- Audience: outbound prospects → inbound customers (existing)
- Goal: book strategy call → resolve issue + log + escalate if needed
- Tools: removed cold-list ingest, added order-lookup
- Workflow: added "verify customer identity" step
- Pre-auths: no batch approval (no cold outbound); transfer-to-human more permissive

Sections kept verbatim: anti-patterns, pairing structure, brand voice constraints

Step 5 — Save + register

  1. Save to _Skills Library/<correct-folder>/<new-name>/SKILL.md
  2. Update agent-registry.md (add row + note "cloned from X")
  3. Update _Skills Library/README.md catalog
  4. Update orchestrator routing if new triggers
  5. Log to _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-clone-<name>.md

Output structure

# Agent Cloned: <new-name> (from <source-name>)

## Source
**Path**: `<source-path>/SKILL.md`
**Pattern**: <type / theme>

## New skill
**Path**: `_Skills Library/<folder>/<new-name>/SKILL.md`
**Frontmatter**:
- name: <new-name>
- triggers: <list>
- best_for: <list>
- estimated_time: <range>

## Diff summary
**What changed**:
- <change with reason>
- <change>
- <change>

**What's verbatim**:
- <section>
- <section>

## Quality bar
- [x] All 12 checks passed (or list deferred)

## Registry / routing updates
- ✅ Added to `agent-registry.md`
- ✅ Added to `_Skills Library/README.md`
- ✅ Routing updated in <orchestrator>

## Test plan
- Test 1: invoke with new triggers → verify output adapts to new use case
- Test 2: edge case specific to new domain → verify handling

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-clone-<new-name>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Cloning when trivial substitution would work — just edit the original
  • Cloning + then heavily diverging — defeats the purpose; consider from-scratch via skill-builder
  • Not surfacing the diff — user can't validate the clone
  • Cloning without registry update — new skill exists but isn't discoverable
  • Cloning routing changes silently — orchestrator routing changes need approval

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read source SKILL.md
  • ✅ Generate cloned SKILL.md
  • ✅ Save to _Skills Library/
  • ✅ Update registry + README (additive)
  • ⚠️ Update orchestrator routing (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Overwrite source skill (use versioning in frontmatter instead)

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • User clone request (source + variations)
  • Source SKILL.md
  • _Shared Context/agent-registry.md (conflict check)
  • _Knowledge Base/16 - Skill Authoring Conventions.md

Outputs to:

  • New SKILL.md
  • Updated registry + README
  • _Logs/agent-decisions/

Chains with:

  • reuse-scanner (before cloning — confirm clone is right approach vs reuse-as-is)
  • skill-builder (if scope diverges too far → switch to from-scratch)

Common Matrix clones (examples)

  • voice-ai-support-agent cloned from voice-ai-sales-agent (inbound customer support)
  • voice-ai-onboarding-agent cloned from voice-ai-inbound-qualifier (post-purchase calls)
  • cold-sms-sequence cloned from cold-email-sequence (SMS channel variant)
  • peptide-positioning cloned from offer-positioning (peptide-specific overrides)
  • clinic-discovery-call cloned from discovery-call-prep (Matrix-specific call sheet)

One-liner reminder

Source + diff → cloned skill. Surface what changed. Register. Test. 60-80% time saved vs from-scratch.

skills.name: Ai Search Optimizer
skills.description: SEO specialist for AI-search visibility — ChatGPT search, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini AI Overviews, Bing Copilot. Different signals than traditional SEO. Optimizes for being CITED by AI engines when prospects ask "what's the best peptide pharmacy" / "compare Matrix vs [competitor]" / "how do I find a 503-A pharmacy". Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: AI search is rapidly replacing traditional SEO for high-intent queries. Prospects ask ChatGPT, Perplexity, Gemini. Those engines cite sources differently than Google ranks pages. This specialist tunes Matrix's presence in AI knowledge graphs, cited sources, and authority signals that LLM-based search systems weight.
best_for: Boost citation rate when prospects query AI engines
skills.estimated_time: Audit: 4-8 hours. Quarterly cycle: 1-2 days work.
skills.body_html:
AI Search Optimizer

The LLM-citation specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns Matrix's visibility in AI-powered search engines — ChatGPT search, Perplexity, Claude search, Gemini AI Overviews, Bing Copilot. AI search engines pick a small number of "cited sources" per answer; getting cited drives traffic + authority. This specialist runs visibility audits ("what does ChatGPT say when asked about Matrix?"), identifies what sources AI engines cite for our target queries, and builds the authority + citation foundation that makes Matrix the cited source.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Traditional Google rank (content-seo-specialist, voice-search-optimizer), local SEO (local-seo-specialist), schema (technical-seo-specialist — but coordinates).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (visibility-audit / citation-target / wikipedia-eligibility / authority-build / quarterly-review)
    required: true
  target_queries:
    type: array
    required: false  # defaults to Tier 1 + Tier 5 from seo-targets.md
  ai_engines:
    type: array (chatgpt-search / perplexity / claude / gemini / bing-copilot)
    required: false  # defaults to all

RULES

rules:
  ai_search_signal_landscape:
    # AI engines weight different signals than Google
    - Wikipedia + Wikidata entries: very high weight (most-cited sources)
    - High-DR press citations: high weight
    - Industry directory rich profiles: medium-high
    - Original research / data: high weight (frequently cited verbatim)
    - LinkedIn company pages: medium
    - Reddit / Quora authoritative discussions: medium (organic only)
    - Author bylines + credentials: medium-high (E-E-A-T proxy)
    - Schema.org markup (esp. Organization + Person + Product): supports retrieval
    - Brand mentions co-occurring with topic: medium
    - Conference presentations + podcast transcripts: medium
    - Sci-research citations (PubMed) for clinical content: very high for medical
  audit_methodology:
    - Run 20-50 representative queries per quarter across each engine
    - For each: record current cited sources + Matrix's presence
    - Track citation rate over time (% of queries where Matrix is cited)
    - Identify gaps: queries where competitors cited, Matrix absent
  content_for_ai_citation:
    - Clear factual structure (LLMs prefer parseable structured info)
    - Author bylines + credentials visible
    - Citations to authoritative sources (LLMs follow citation chains)
    - Tables + lists (easier to extract)
    - Definition-style + statement-style content (easier to quote)
    - Update dates visible (recency matters)
    - Schema.org markup
  authority_building:
    - Wikipedia article when eligible (notable industry mentions are prerequisites)
    - Wikidata entry (machine-readable; AI engines parse)
    - LinkedIn company page rich + verified
    - PR outreach to high-DR industry outlets
    - Podcast interviews (transcripts become AI training + citation data)
    - Original research publication (data AI engines reference)
    - Conference speaking (talks indexed; Steve's publication portfolio is gold here)
  prohibited:
    - Astroturfing Wikipedia (single-purpose accounts banned + reputation damage)
    - Spam-citing in Reddit / Quora (community will detect + downvote)
    - Fake academic citations
    - Reciprocal-link schemes for citation farms

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - ChatGPT search (manual queries via browser)
    - Perplexity (API or browser; supports citations exposure)
    - Claude (manual queries — citation exposure)
    - Gemini AI Overviews (search-result view)
    - Bing Copilot
    - Wikipedia search + edit interface
    - Wikidata
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md (Tier 5 AI search targets)
    - _Shared Context/alm-dt-platform.md (Steve's publication portfolio — citation gold)
    - _Shared Context/steve-kushner-profile.md
    - _Shared Context/ip-portfolio.md (any granted IP = authority)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/seo/ai-search/<period>-audit.md
    - _Logs/seo/ai-search/citation-tracker.csv (per-query, per-engine, per-period)
    - _Logs/seo/ai-search/authority-build-plan.md
  composed_skills:
    - content-seo-specialist (content gaps for AI-citation candidates)
    - copywriting (long-form authority content)
    - prior-art-citation-formatter (Steve's papers properly cited)
    - partnership-agent (PR + press placement)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch + WebSearch
    - HuggingFace MCP (paper search; LLM knowledge graphs)
  llm:
    model: claude-sonnet-4 (audit requires reasoning about AI-engine outputs)
    typical_cost_per_audit: $1-5

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    audit_id: string
    period: ISO
    per_engine_visibility:
      - engine: chatgpt-search | perplexity | claude | gemini | bing-copilot
        queries_tested: number
        matrix_cited_count: number
        citation_rate_pct: number
        delta_vs_prior: number
        top_cited_competitors: array
        when_matrix_cited:
          - query: string
            cited_source: string  # which Matrix asset
            position: number
        when_matrix_absent:
          - query: string
            cited_alternative: string
            why_not_us: string
            suggested_action: string
    authority_gap_analysis:
      wikipedia_status: present | eligible-not-filed | not-eligible-yet
      wikidata_status: present | not-present
      press_density: count of high-DR mentions in last 12 mo
      original_research_count: integer
      podcast_appearance_count: integer
      conference_talks_count: integer (Steve's portfolio counts!)
    recommendations:
      - priority: P0 | P1 | P2
        action: string
        executor: this-skill | copywriting | partnership-agent | press / PR (external)
        expected_impact: string
        timeline: short-term | medium-term | long-term
    citation_targets_quarterly:
      - source: string  # publication, podcast, conference, directory
        why: string
        path: string
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - seo-strategy-lead (prioritization)
    - copywriting (authority content briefs)
    - partnership-agent (PR placements + podcast outreach)
    - ip-counsel-coordination (Wikipedia / Wikidata strategic decisions)
  trigger_method: quarterly cron + ad-hoc
  fallback_routing:
    - Citation rate dropping > 20% → diagnostic + recovery
    - Wikipedia article eligibility window passes (press density hit) → ip-counsel-coordination
    - Competitor citation spike → competitive-response brief

Eval cases

  1. Quarterly visibility audit across 5 AI engines, 50 queries
  2. New Wikipedia article eligibility test (press density check)
  3. ChatGPT cites competitor on Tier 1 query → analysis + counter-content
  4. Steve's publication portfolio not surfacing in AI cite → exposure boost
  5. Press density gap → partnership-agent outreach plan

Anti-patterns

  • Astroturfing Wikipedia — instant ban + reputation
  • Spam-citing Reddit / Quora
  • Fake authority signals
  • Generic "boost SEO for AI" — must be query-targeted

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + visibility tracking
  • ✅ Authority-build plan
  • ⚠️ Wikipedia article filing (counsel + Chris)
  • ⚠️ Press outreach budget (Chris)

Pairing

Composed by: seo-strategy-lead Composes: content-seo-specialist, copywriting, partnership-agent, prior-art-citation-formatter Reads: ChatGPT / Perplexity / Claude / Gemini / Bing-Copilot, alm-dt-platform.md, steve-kushner-profile.md, ip-portfolio.md

One-liner reminder

AI engines cite differently. Wikipedia + Wikidata + press density = highest signals. Steve's portfolio is gold. Authority compounds over 6-24 months. No black hat.

skills.name: Analytics Architect
skills.description: Designs + maintains the unified KPI dashboard across all departments. Defines what's measured, where data lives, how it rolls up. Coordinates inputs from ghl-daily-pulse + cfo-finance + voice-call-reviewer + sales-strategy + weekly-review. Produces the single source of truth for "how is Matrix doing".
skills.intent: Stop fragmented metrics. Define the canonical KPIs per department + the company-level dashboard. Each department-skill writes its KPIs to a known location; this skill aggregates + surfaces patterns + flags anomalies. Single dashboard the executive layer reads.
best_for: Designing the unified dashboard for the first time
Monthly KPI review (which moved, which stalled)
skills.estimated_time: 60-120 min initial design; 15-30 min per refresh
skills.body_html:
Analytics Architect

The KPI canonicalizer. One dashboard. Departments write to it; executives read it.

When to use

  • First-time dashboard design
  • Adding a new KPI (with definition, source, owner)
  • Monthly KPI review
  • Pre-board / pre-investor metrics pull
  • KPI sanity check (numbers diverging across reports)

When NOT to use

  • Single-metric lookup (just read from the source)
  • Department-specific reporting (use that department's skill)
  • Forecasting (use cfo-finance)

Required inputs

  1. Audience — executive / board / investor / department / cross-functional
  2. Period — week / month / quarter / YTD
  3. Comparison baseline — prior period / prior year / target

If unclear, ask: "Who reads this and how often?"

The KPI hierarchy

Company-level KPIs (top of dashboard)
  ↓
Department KPIs (one section per department)
  ↓
Agent / skill KPIs (which agents are producing the department KPIs)

Company-level (executive-only dashboard)

KPI Source Cadence Target
Revenue (period) cfo-finance Daily $/month
MRR / ARR cfo-finance Daily $
Cash runway cfo-finance Daily > 12 mo
New customers acquired ghl-daily-pulse Daily /month
Active customers account-manager (aggregated) Daily
Churn rate account-manager (aggregated) Monthly < %
Pipeline value ghl-daily-pulse Daily $
Pipeline velocity sales-strategy (aggregated) Weekly days
Health score project-overseer + voice-call-reviewer + account-manager Weekly composite

Per-department KPIs

Voice AI Department:

  • Meeting-set rate per agent
  • Connect rate per campaign
  • Resolution rate per inbound agent
  • Avg call length
  • Cost per call / cost per meeting
  • Customer satisfaction (post-call survey)
  • Eval-suite pass rate (continuous improvement signal)

Sales:

  • Connect → meeting rate
  • Meeting → proposal rate
  • Proposal → close rate
  • Avg deal size
  • Avg cycle days
  • Win rate
  • CAC

Marketing / Content:

  • Pieces published vs planned
  • Engagement rate
  • Lead conversion per channel
  • Cost per lead

Customer Success:

  • Onboarding completion rate
  • 30-day active rate
  • 90-day retention
  • NPS / CSAT
  • Account-health distribution (🟢🟡🔴 mix)

Engineering:

  • Tickets shipped per sprint
  • Build pass rate (lint/typecheck/tests)
  • Deploy frequency
  • Production incidents
  • Mean time to recovery (MTTR)

Finance:

  • Gross margin %
  • Net margin %
  • AR aging
  • Affiliate payout ratio
  • Burn rate

Operations / PMO:

  • Active projects count
  • Projects on track / at risk / off track
  • Open blockers
  • Weekly velocity

Per-agent KPIs (where applicable)

Each agent that produces measurable work logs its own KPI to _Logs/:

  • Skill X invoked N times this period
  • Avg time per invocation
  • Output quality (where measurable)
  • User satisfaction

Workflow

Step 1 — Define the dashboard scope

Per audience:

  • Executive (Chris): full company KPIs + department roll-ups
  • Department lead: that department's KPIs + relevant company context
  • Investor / board: company KPIs + select per-department highlights
  • Cross-functional: relevant subset

Step 2 — Pull data

For each KPI:

  • Source skill / system
  • Latest value
  • Prior period
  • Target / threshold
  • Trend (last 3-6 periods)

Step 3 — Compute roll-ups

For company-level KPIs that aggregate department metrics:

  • Sum / average / weighted-average per metric type
  • Health composite (department-level traffic-light → company-level signal)

Step 4 — Surface patterns

  • Movers: KPIs that changed > 10% vs prior period
  • Lagging indicators: KPIs trending wrong > 3 periods
  • Diverging metrics: two related KPIs moving in unexpected directions
  • Threshold breaches: KPIs that crossed alert thresholds

Step 5 — Generate the dashboard

Format options:

  • Markdown (default — works in any agent context)
  • HTML / static (for visual presentation)
  • Spreadsheet (for further analysis)
  • PDF (for board / investor)

Output structure

# Analytics Dashboard: <Period>

## Headline
<one sentence: the story this period>

## Company KPIs

| KPI | This period | Prior | Delta | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue | $X | $Y | <delta> | $T | 🟢/🟡/🔴 |
| MRR | $X | $Y | <delta> | $T | <status> |
| Cash runway | <N> mo | <M> mo | <delta> | > 12 mo | <status> |
| New customers | <N> | <M> | <delta> | <T> | <status> |
| Active customers | <N> | <M> | <delta> | <T> | <status> |
| Churn | <%> | <%> | <pp> | < <%> | <status> |
| Pipeline value | $X | $Y | <delta> | $T | <status> |

## Department snapshots

### Voice AI ⭐⭐
| KPI | This period | Prior | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Meeting-set rate (sales) | <%> | <%> | <status> |
| Resolution rate (support) | <%> | <%> | <status> |
| Avg call length | <sec> | <sec> | <status> |
| Customer satisfaction | <X>/5 | <Y>/5 | <status> |
| Eval-suite pass rate | <%> | <%> | <status> |

### Sales
[same format]

### Customer Success
[same format]

### Marketing / Content
[same format]

### Engineering
[same format]

### Finance
[same format]

### Operations
[same format]

## 🚨 Movers + flags

### Big movers (Δ > 10%)
- <KPI>: from <X> to <Y> (<reason hypothesis>)

### Lagging (3+ periods wrong direction)
- <KPI>: trending <up/down> for <N> periods → action needed

### Threshold breaches
- <KPI>: crossed <threshold> (severity: <H/M/L>)

### Diverging metrics
- <KPI A> ↑ while <KPI B> ↓ (typically correlated)

## Trend charts (linked or generated)
- <link to chart 1>
- <link to chart 2>

## ✅ I handled
- Pulled from all sources
- Computed roll-ups
- Surfaced movers + flags
- Generated dashboard

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Decide on flagged threshold breaches
- Approve new KPI definitions (if any)
- Approve target adjustments (if reality has shifted)

## 🚫 You only
- Investor / board version distribution
- Public-facing metrics decisions

## Handoff
**To**: `weekly-review` (consumes dashboard), `pmo-operator` (projects flagged via lagging KPIs), respective department leads (for action)

## Logged to
`_Logs/analytics/<period>-dashboard.md`
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-analytics-refresh.md`

KPI definition discipline

When adding a new KPI:

  • Name: clear, no jargon
  • Definition: formula in plain English
  • Source: which skill / system / file produces it
  • Cadence: how often updated
  • Owner: which agent or human ensures freshness
  • Target / threshold: what's good / bad
  • Why it matters: what decisions does it drive

Add to _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md (created by this skill on first run).

Anti-patterns

  • Vanity metrics (LLM calls / day, agents invoked) — track but don't put on the executive dashboard
  • No targets — KPIs without thresholds can't trigger action
  • Single-source numbers (no cross-check) — every important KPI verified from 2+ sources
  • Stale KPI definitions — quarterly audit
  • Too many KPIs on one dashboard — executives need 7-10 max; others go in drill-down
  • No "why it matters" documented — KPIs without decision context become noise

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull data from any connected source (read-only)
  • ✅ Compute roll-ups
  • ✅ Generate dashboards
  • ✅ Update _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md (additive)
  • ⚠️ Adjust KPI targets / thresholds (Chris approves)
  • ⚠️ Add a new company-level KPI (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Modify financial KPIs (only cfo-finance does this with CPA)

Pairing

Inputs from: every department's KPI-producing skill (cfo-finance, ghl-daily-pulse, voice-call-reviewer, account-manager, sales-strategy, etc.) Outputs to: Chris (executive dashboard), weekly-review, pmo-operator, department leads, investor / board materials Chains with: weekly-review (reads the dashboard), cfo-finance (financial section is theirs to own)

One-liner reminder

One canonical dashboard. KPI definitions registered. Sources cited. Targets explicit. Movers + flags surfaced. Executive view max 10 KPIs.

skills.name: Animated Website
skills.description: Turn any video into a luxury scroll-animated website with parallax effects
triggers: make a website from this video
skills.body_html:
Animated Website from Video

Turn any MP4 video into a stunning, scroll-driven animated website with parallax effects, glass morphism cards, and cinema-quality polish. One command, one HTML file, zero dependencies.

Requirements

  • FFmpeg installed and available in PATH
  • Python 3 (for local preview server)
  • An MP4 video file provided by the user

Workflow

Step 1: Analyze the Video

Run ffprobe on the provided MP4 to extract:

  • Total duration (seconds)
  • Frame rate (fps)
  • Resolution (width x height)
  • Total frame count

Report these stats to the user before proceeding.

Step 2: Extract Frames

Extract frames from the video at 2 fps (or adjust based on duration to stay under 120 frames total).

# Desktop frames (1920x1080 WebP, quality 80)
ffmpeg -i INPUT.mp4 -vf "fps=2,scale=1920:1080" -c:v libwebp -quality 80 frames/desktop_%04d.webp

# Mobile frames (960x540 WebP, quality 70)
ffmpeg -i INPUT.mp4 -vf "fps=2,scale=960:540" -c:v libwebp -quality 70 frames/mobile_%04d.webp

Store frames in a temporary frames/ directory next to the output file.

Step 3: Generate Section Content

Analyze the video context (filename, user description, or any provided brief) and generate content for 6 scroll sections:

Section Purpose Content Needed
Hero Full-screen opening Headline, subheadline, CTA button text
Features Key capabilities 3-4 feature cards with icon, title, description
Stats Social proof / numbers 3-4 animated stat counters with labels
How It Works Process breakdown 3-5 numbered steps with short descriptions
Gallery Visual showcase Parallax image grid with captions
CTA Final conversion Headline, body text, button text, button URL

Present the draft content to the user for approval before building.

Step 4: Build the HTML File

Generate a single self-contained HTML file with all assets base64-encoded inline:

Scroll Animation Engine:

  • Canvas element draws frames synced to scroll position
  • requestAnimationFrame loop for smooth playback
  • Frame preloading with progress indicator
  • Responsive breakpoint swaps desktop/mobile frame sets

Visual Effects:

  • Ambient floating particles (subtle, slow-moving, low opacity)
  • Film grain overlay (CSS noise animation)
  • Glass morphism cards (backdrop-filter: blur(16px), semi-transparent bg)
  • Letter-split text animations (each character staggers in on scroll)
  • Parallax gallery (layers move at different scroll speeds)
  • Navigation dots fixed on the right edge (highlight active section)

Design Tokens:

  • Background: #0a0a0b
  • Text primary: #ffffff
  • Text secondary: rgba(255,255,255,0.6)
  • Accent: user-chosen or default #D97757
  • Card bg: rgba(255,255,255,0.05)
  • Card border: rgba(255,255,255,0.1)
  • Font: Inter (loaded from Google Fonts) or system sans-serif fallback
  • Border radius: 16px on cards, 999px on buttons

Structure:

- Progress bar (top, fixed)
- Navigation dots (right, fixed)
- Section 1: Hero (full viewport, canvas background)
- Section 2: Features (glass cards grid)
- Section 3: Stats (animated counters)
- Section 4: How It Works (numbered steps)
- Section 5: Gallery (parallax grid)
- Section 6: CTA (centered, large)
- Footer (minimal)

Save the output as animated-site-[topic].html in the current working directory.

Step 5: Launch Preview

Start a local Python server and open in the default browser:

python3 -m http.server 8888 &
open http://localhost:8888/animated-site-[topic].html

Customization Options

After the initial build, offer these tweaks:

Option What it changes
Scroll speed Frames per scroll pixel (default: 0.5)
Accent color Primary highlight color (default: #D97757)
Section text Any headline, description, or CTA copy
Logo Add a logo image to the hero and nav
Font Swap Inter for another Google Font
Frame density More or fewer frames extracted (affects file size)
Sections Add, remove, or reorder the 6 sections

Rules

  • ALL assets must be inlined (base64 data URIs). The final HTML must work offline with zero external fetches (except optional Google Font).
  • Keep total file size under 50MB. If frames push it over, reduce frame count or quality.
  • Every section must be at least 100vh tall so scroll animations have room to breathe.
  • Test that navigation dots correctly highlight the active section.
  • Mobile responsive: stack cards vertically, use mobile frame set below 768px.
  • If FFmpeg is not installed, tell the user how to install it and stop.
  • Never hallucinate video content. If you cannot determine what the video is about, ask the user to describe it.

skills.name: Ar Ap Automation
skills.description: AR aging + auto-dunning + bad-debt flagging. AP intake + approval routing + payment scheduling per terms. Promotes the AR/AP 🟡 stub to 🟢. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
