skills.intent: AR uncollected = revenue we don't have. AP missed = vendor trust we don't have. This specialist owns both ledgers operationally — aging visibility, automated reminders in Matrix voice, AP intake with approval routing, payment scheduling per `vendors.md` terms.
best_for: AP bill intake (new vendor bill received)
skills.estimated_time: Weekly AR sweep: 30-60 min. Per-bill AP intake: 5-10 min.
skills.body_html:
AR / AP Automation

The receivables + payables operator. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns AR (accounts receivable) + AP (accounts payable) operations. AR: invoice aging buckets (current / 30 / 60 / 90+), automated dunning sequences in Matrix voice, bad-debt flagging at 90+. AP: vendor bill intake, approval routing per approval-rules.md, payment scheduling per vendors.md terms, ready-batch handoff to vendor-payment-orchestrator. Does NOT execute payments (vendor-payment-orchestrator does) and does NOT set credit policy (cfo-finance does).

Explicitly OUT of scope: Executing actual vendor payments, setting customer credit terms, refunds (order-lifecycle-manager + refund SOP), collections-agency escalation decisions (Chris + counsel).

INPUT

input:
  mode: ar-sweep | ap-intake | dunning-batch | bad-debt-review | term-renegotiation-prep
  trigger: weekly-cron | new-bill-received | new-invoice-issued | adhoc
  ar_context (if AR):
    invoice_data: from Stripe / GHL / accounting tool
    customer_tier: from GHL contact (per vendors / customer terms)
  ap_context (if AP):
    vendor_id: string
    bill_artifact: PDF / scan / email
    amount: number
    due_date: ISO
    payment_terms: from vendors.md

RULES

  • Dunning ladder — auto-friendly reminder at day 7 past due → firmer at 14 → personal AM call at 30 → finance escalation at 60 → bad-debt flag at 90.
  • All AR comms pass compliance-officer for tone (no scary collections language; Matrix voice).
  • Compose email-intent-classifier on customer replies to route disputes immediately.
  • PII: customer financial data masked in logs (last-4 only).
  • AP intake validates against vendors.md (known vendor, contracted rate, expected cadence).
  • Approval routing per approval-rules.md:
    • < $1,000: controller drafts, auto-batch for AP run
    • $1,000 - $10,000: Chris approves before batch
    • $10,000 - $50,000: Chris + cfo-finance review

    • $50,000: Chris + counsel

  • Duplicate-bill detection — flag any vendor + amount + ~date match within 30 days.
  • Bad-debt threshold — 90+ days with 3 failed contact attempts → flag + recommend write-off to cfo-finance.
  • Sub-brand voice respected on dunning (MuscleLock voice ≠ Matrix voice ≠ ClinIQ voice per sub-brands.md).

TOOLS

  • bookkeeping (sub-ledger source of truth)
  • payments-engine (Stripe — invoice state, payment status, refunds)
  • GHL (payments_list-transactions, contact + custom-field updates, conversation send)
  • Read: _Shared Context/vendors.md, approval-rules.md, sub-brands.md, ghl-config.md
  • Compose: compliance-officer (AR tone), email-intent-classifier (reply routing), sku-name-validator (AP bill SKU match)
  • Hand off to: vendor-payment-orchestrator (approved AP batch), account-manager (personal-touch dunning), cfo-finance (bad-debt + term renegotiation)
  • Write: _Logs/finance/ar/<YYYY-WW>-aging.csv, _Logs/finance/ap/<YYYY-WW>-pending.csv, _Logs/finance/dunning/<contact-id>-<seq>.md

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    mode: ar-sweep | ap-intake | dunning-batch | bad-debt-review
    period: ISO range
    ar_snapshot (if AR):
      total_outstanding: number
      aging:
        current: {count, amount}
        d30: {count, amount}
        d60: {count, amount}
        d90_plus: {count, amount}
      dso_days: number
      dunning_actions:
        - contact_id: string
          stage: reminder-1 | reminder-2 | am-call | finance-esc | bad-debt
          channel: email | sms | phone
          drafted: true
          approval_required: bool
      bad_debt_candidates:
        - invoice_id: string
          amount: number
          age_days: number
          recommendation: writeoff | continued-collection | dispute
    ap_snapshot (if AP):
      bills_intake_this_period: number
      pending_approval:
        - bill_id: string
          vendor: string
          amount: number
          due_date: ISO
          approval_tier: per approval-rules.md
      ready_for_payment_batch: array of bill_ids
      dpo_days: number
      duplicate_flags: array
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
  format: CSV + Markdown summary

HANDOFF

  • finance-controller-lead (weekly snapshot)
  • vendor-payment-orchestrator (approved AP batches)
  • account-manager (60-day dunning personal call)
  • cfo-finance (bad-debt write-off recommendations + term renegotiation)
  • forecasting-runway (AR collections expected → cash forecast)
  • Chris (approvals per tier)
  • compliance-officer (all customer AR comms review)

Eval cases

  1. Customer 14 days past due → auto-dunning reminder-2 drafted, queued
  2. Bill received from new vendor not in vendors.md → flag for intake-validation before approval
  3. Duplicate bill detected (same vendor + amount + ~date) → block + flag
  4. AR concentration > 25% in single customer → P2 alert
  5. DSO drifts from 28 → 45 days over 3 weeks → trend alert to CI engine
  6. AP > $50k pending → Chris + counsel review path
  7. Customer disputes invoice via reply → email-intent-classifier routes to AM + pauses dunning

Anti-patterns

  • Harsh dunning language — never; Matrix voice always (compliance-officer gate)
  • Auto-paying AP without approval gates
  • Missing duplicate-bill detection — fraud + error risk
  • Bad-debt held forever — write off at 90-120 days with documentation
  • AR dashboard without aging buckets — total-only hides risk
  • AP without DPO target — paying everything in 7 days kills working capital

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull AR / AP data
  • ✅ Draft dunning sequences (compliance-officer reviews tone)
  • ✅ Receive + categorize AP bills
  • ✅ Surface bad-debt + duplicate flags
  • ⚠️ Send dunning email (Chris approves first send of new template; thereafter auto with compliance-officer gate)
  • ⚠️ AP > $1,000 → Chris approval before payment batch
  • ⚠️ Bad-debt write-off (Chris + cfo-finance + CPA)
  • 🚫 Execute payment (handoff to vendor-payment-orchestrator)
  • 🚫 Set customer credit terms

Pairing

Composed by: finance-controller-lead, monthly-close-orchestrator Composes: compliance-officer, email-intent-classifier, bookkeeping, payments-engine Reads: vendors.md, approval-rules.md, sub-brands.md, ghl-config.md Hands off to: vendor-payment-orchestrator, account-manager, cfo-finance

One-liner reminder

AR dunning ladder in Matrix voice (compliance-gated). AP intake with approval tiers + duplicate detection. Hand approved batches to vendor-payment-orchestrator. Bad-debt at 90+. Never executes payments.

skills.name: Automation Scheduler
skills.description: Set up recurring workflows / automations. Wraps the scheduled-tasks MCP. Use to schedule daily prep, weekly review, daily GHL pulse, custom recurring skill invocations. Outputs a confirmation + cron-pattern reference.
skills.body_html:
Automation Scheduler

Set up recurring workflows so the assistant runs them without prompting.

Purpose

The strongest skills get more powerful when paired with a schedule:

  • prep-day at 7am daily → wake up to today's plan
  • ghl-daily-pulse at 8am weekdays → pipeline check before sales day
  • email-triage at noon → midday inbox sweep
  • weekly-review Friday 4pm → Friday close
  • project-overseer Friday 4pm → portfolio sweep with weekly review

This skill creates / lists / updates / removes those schedules.

When to use

  • User says "schedule X every day/week"
  • After authoring a new skill that benefits from recurrence
  • Audit / change existing schedules

When NOT to use

  • One-time future task (no recurrence needed — set a reminder)
  • Schedule already exists for this skill — modify instead

Required setup

  • mcp__scheduled-tasks__* MCP connected
  • The skill being scheduled must exist and be invokable

Workflow

Step 1 — Parse the request

Skill to invoke: <skill-name>
Frequency: daily | weekdays | weekly | monthly | custom-cron
Time: <HH:MM in user's timezone, default PT>
With arguments: <any context to pass to the skill>
Outputs to: <file path | inbox | TASKS.md | print>

Step 2 — Translate to cron

Frequency Cron
Daily 7am 0 7 * * *
Weekdays 7am 0 7 * * 1-5
Friday 4pm 0 16 * * 5
Monthly 1st 9am 0 9 1 * *
Every 4 hours 0 */4 * * *
Every 15 min during work */15 9-17 * * 1-5

Step 3 — Validate

Check before creating:

  • Skill exists in _Skills Library/
  • Output destination is writable
  • No duplicate schedule (same skill + same cron)
  • User has approved any side-effecting runs (e.g., auto-sending isn't scheduled — that always needs human-in-loop)

Step 4 — Create via scheduled-tasks MCP

mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task
  name: <descriptive name>
  cron: <expression>
  command: <skill invocation>
  output: <where>
  auth: <run-as-user>

Step 5 — Confirm + register

Write to automations.md in the project root for visibility:

# Automations

| Name | Skill | Schedule | Output | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Morning prep | prep-day | weekdays 7am | briefings/YYYY-MM-DD.md | active |
| ...

Recommended default schedules

These are the standard automations Chris should consider:

Name Skill Schedule Output Why
Morning prep prep-day Mon-Fri 7am briefings/YYYY-MM-DD.md Day starts with the plan
Midday inbox email-triage Mon-Fri 12:30pm direct response Catch the AM batch
End-of-day wrap email-triage (+ TASKS.md update) Mon-Fri 5:30pm direct response Don't leave inbox dirty
GHL daily pulse ghl-daily-pulse Mon-Fri 8am direct response Pipeline check pre-day
Weekly review weekly-review + project-overseer Fri 4pm reviews/YYYY-MM-DD.md Friday close
Monthly KB audit scan source-summaries staleness 1st of month 10am kb-audit/YYYY-MM.md Keep KB fresh

The user opts in to any combination via this skill.

Output structure

# Automation Scheduled

## ✅ Created
**Name**: <name>
**Skill**: <skill-name>
**Schedule**: <human-readable, e.g., "Weekdays at 7am PT">
**Cron**: `<cron expression>`
**Output destination**: <where results land>
**First run**: <next datetime it will fire>

## Registered in `automations.md` ✅

## Test run option
Want to test it now? Reply "test it" to fire once and verify.

## Currently active automations
<table of all current schedules>

## ⚠️ Heads up
- This automation runs even if you're offline. To pause: "/schedule pause <name>"
- Output goes to <path> — make sure that folder exists
- Sensitive actions (send / post / pay) are NEVER scheduled — they always require live approval

Anti-patterns

  • Scheduling side-effecting actions without human-in-loop — never. Send/post/pay always require live approval.
  • Cron with no end condition — make sure there's a way to pause / kill
  • Duplicate schedules — check before creating
  • Schedule that produces output nobody reads — if the output isn't reviewed, kill the schedule
  • Over-scheduling — start with 2-3 automations max; add more after they prove valuable

Modification operations

PAUSE

mcp__scheduled-tasks__update_scheduled_task — set status: paused

RESUME

update_scheduled_task — set status: active

DELETE

mcp__scheduled-tasks__... delete

→ Requires ⚠️ approval.

LIST

mcp__scheduled-tasks__list_scheduled_tasks

RUN NOW (test)

mcp__scheduled-tasks__... run (one-time fire)

What gets scheduled vs what stays manual

Type Schedule?
Read-only reports (briefings, pulses, reviews) ✅ Yes
Drafts that need approval ✅ Yes (output goes to user for review)
Sends / posts / commits ❌ Never schedule the commit step
Decision skills ❌ No — decisions need real-time context
Build / code generation ❌ No — needs live human collaboration

Pairing

  • Created by user explicitly OR proposed by other skills (e.g., prep-day suggests its own schedule)
  • Audited by weekly-review (which automations actually delivered value)
  • All schedules cataloged in automations.md for transparency

Failure handling

If a scheduled run fails:

  • Log to automations-failures.md with timestamp + error
  • After 3 consecutive failures, auto-pause + notify user
  • Common causes: MCP disconnected, file path invalid, skill renamed

One-liner reminder

Schedule the reports + drafts. Never schedule the sends. Audit weekly. Pause anything that produces unread output.

skills.name: Backend Dev
skills.description: Build API routes, server logic, webhooks, integrations on Next.js API routes (or FastAPI for heavy compute). Pydantic/Zod validation everywhere. Audit middleware on every LLM call. Idempotent writes. Never deploys to prod without approval.
skills.intent: Be the executor for backend tickets. Implement API surfaces, webhook receivers, integration glue, and server-side business logic. Apply trust-but-verify (validate every input, audit every LLM call, return structured errors). Match Matrix's stack (Next.js API routes default; FastAPI for ML/heavy compute).
best_for: Implementing API endpoint tickets from engineering-lead
Webhook receivers for GHL / Stripe / Retell / etc.
skills.estimated_time: 30-180 min per ticket
skills.body_html:
Backend Dev

The server-side executor. Schema-validated. Audit-logged. Idempotent.

When to use

  • Tickets from engineering-lead with backend owner
  • New API route, webhook, or server action
  • Integration glue (GHL ↔ Supabase ↔ Stripe ↔ Retell)
  • Background job / cron handler

When NOT to use

  • Frontend / UI work (use frontend-dev)
  • DB schema design (use database-schema)
  • Pure deploy / env config (use devops-deployment)

Required inputs

  1. Ticket / spec — from engineering-lead output
  2. Endpoint shape — method, path, request/response schema
  3. Integration target — which external system (if any)
  4. Auth requirements — public / authenticated / role-restricted

If 2 missing, ask: "What's the request/response schema? Include method + path + payload."

Workflow

Step 1 — Load context

  • Project CLAUDE.md (stack + conventions)
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (PHI / PCI / GDPR constraints)
  • _Shared Context/tool-access.md (which MCPs available)
  • Existing API routes for pattern matching

Step 2 — Define schemas (Zod or Pydantic)

Per _Knowledge Base/Source Summaries/kardell-financial-software-genai.md — schemas everywhere:

  • Request body
  • Query params
  • Response shape (success + each error class)
  • External integration payload

Never accept unvalidated input. Never return unvalidated output.

Step 3 — Implement the handler

Matrix Next.js pattern:

// app/api/<resource>/route.ts
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
import { z } from 'zod'

const RequestSchema = z.object({ ... })

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  // 1. Validate input
  const parsed = RequestSchema.safeParse(await req.json())
  if (!parsed.success) return NextResponse.json({error: 'invalid_input', details: parsed.error}, {status: 400})

  // 2. Auth
  // ... clerk / supabase auth check

  // 3. Business logic
  // ...

  // 4. Idempotency (for any mutating call)
  // ... idempotency key check

  // 5. Audit log
  // ... write to _Logs or Supabase audit table

  // 6. Return structured response
  return NextResponse.json({...}, {status: 200})
}

Step 4 — Apply trust-but-verify (every LLM call)

If the handler calls an LLM:

  • Schema-validate the output before using it
  • Log the prompt + response to audit
  • Fallback path if validation fails
  • Never feed LLM output directly to side-effecting code without validation

Step 5 — Tests

  • Unit test the handler (happy + 3-5 error paths)
  • Integration test for any external call (mocked)
  • Update / add to existing test suite

Step 6 — Run checks

npm run lint
npm run typecheck
npm test           # if test command exists

Step 7 — Output

Output structure

# Backend Built: <endpoint / handler>

## Ticket
**Description**: <one-line>
**Endpoint**: <METHOD path>

## Files changed
- `<path>` — created / modified

## Schema
**Request**: <Zod / Pydantic schema summary>
**Response (success)**: <schema>
**Response (errors)**: <list of error classes>

## Integrations called
| External | Method | Auth | Failure handling |
|---|---|---|---|
| <ext> | <method> | <auth> | <action on fail> |

## Audit
- Logged to: <audit destination>
- Idempotency: <key strategy or N/A>

## Checks
- [x] Lint
- [x] Typecheck
- [x] Tests pass
- [x] Schema validation on all I/O
- [x] Audit log writes on mutations
- [x] Error responses structured

## ✅ I handled
- Implementation + tests + checks

## ⚠️ Need from you
- Review PR before merge
- Approve any new env vars / secrets

## 🚫 You only
- Merge + deploy

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-backend-<endpoint>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • No schema validation — biggest sin in backend code
  • Unvalidated LLM output going to side-effecting code
  • No idempotency on mutating endpoints — retries cause duplicates
  • Bare try/except — never. Catch specific exceptions.
  • Secrets in code — environment variables only
  • Synchronous LLM calls in hot paths — queue + webhook back

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Create / modify route handlers
  • ✅ Add tests
  • ✅ Read env vars (no secrets leaked in logs)
  • ⚠️ Add new dependencies (Chris approves)
  • ⚠️ New env vars / secrets (Chris approves the var name + provisions the value)
  • 🚫 Deploy to prod
  • 🚫 Modify production DB schema (use database-schema)

Pairing

Inputs from: engineering-lead ticket, database-schema (data shape), _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md Outputs to: PR → review → devops-deployment Chains with: frontend-dev (API contract), database-schema, reuse-scanner (always first)

One-liner reminder

Schema-validate every I/O. Audit-log every LLM call. Idempotent writes. Tests with checks. Never auto-deploy.

skills.name: Bookkeeping
skills.description: Bookkeeping support — categorize transactions, reconcile bank/credit card vs GHL+Stripe records, prep monthly close packets for CPA. Never files taxes or moves money — drafts + organizes for human CPA review. For Matrix: Stripe + GHL payments + bank + affiliate payouts.
skills.estimated_time: 60-120 min monthly
skills.body_html:
Bookkeeping

Categorize. Reconcile. Prep the packet. Never file taxes; CPA does that.

When to use

  • Monthly close (mid-month for prior month)
  • Quarterly tax-prep (CPA needs the packet)
  • Pre-investor-update financial pull
  • Mid-month spot reconciliation if discrepancy noted

When NOT to use

  • Tax filings (CPA only)
  • Moving money (never auto)
  • Financial strategy / forecasting (use cfo-finance)
  • Pricing decisions (use pricing-margin)

Required inputs

  1. Period — month / quarter / YTD
  2. Data sources — Stripe + GHL + bank statements + credit card + accounting tool (QuickBooks / Xero / Wave / etc.)
  3. Chart of accounts — existing categorization scheme

If 2 missing, ask: "Which data sources to pull from — Stripe / GHL / bank / credit card / accounting tool?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Pull data

For the period:

  • Revenue: Stripe + GHL payments_list-transactions
  • Bank deposits + withdrawals: from bank export or Plaid
  • Credit card transactions: from card export
  • Refunds + chargebacks: from Stripe
  • Affiliate payouts: from GHL affiliate tracking
  • Outstanding invoices / AR: GHL invoices unpaid
  • Outstanding bills / AP: vendor invoices unpaid

Step 2 — Categorize each transaction

Apply the chart of accounts. Standard Matrix categories:

Income:

  • Product revenue — 503-A
  • Product revenue — 503-B
  • Product revenue — Hormone therapy
  • Product revenue — Bundles
  • Affiliate commission (income to Matrix when others refer)
  • Other income

COGS:

  • Compounding pharmacy costs
  • Lab costs (nugen / ram / florida / kaduceus)
  • Fulfillment / shipping
  • Payment processor fees (Stripe)
  • Affiliate payouts (Matrix's outflow to affiliates)

Operating Expenses:

  • Salaries / contractors
  • Software / SaaS subscriptions
  • Hosting (Vercel / Supabase)
  • Marketing — paid ads
  • Marketing — content / agency
  • Legal
  • Accounting (CPA fees)
  • Insurance
  • Office / rent
  • Travel
  • Bank / credit card fees
  • Other OpEx

Liabilities:

  • Sales tax payable
  • Affiliate commissions owed
  • Vendor payables
  • Loans / credit lines

For each transaction, apply category. Auto-categorize where confident (recurring vendor, clear keywords). Flag uncertain ones for CPA review.

Step 3 — Reconcile

Compare:

  • Stripe deposits → bank credits (should match within timing window)
  • GHL invoiced revenue → Stripe transactions (should match within fees)
  • Card statements → categorized expenses (should match)
  • Affiliate payouts in GHL → bank/Stripe outflows (should match)

For each discrepancy:

  • Document the gap
  • Hypothesis (timing? error? fee deduction?)
  • Resolution proposal (don't auto-fix)

Step 4 — Surface anomalies

  • Duplicate transactions (same amount + vendor on same day)
  • Missing categorization (uncategorized transactions)
  • Unusual amounts (transaction > 2× recent average for that category)
  • Vendor changes (new vendor not in COA)
  • Sales tax issues (collected but not separated)

Step 5 — Generate monthly close packet

For CPA review:

  • P&L draft (revenue - COGS - OpEx → net)
  • Balance sheet draft (assets / liabilities / equity snapshot)
  • Cash flow statement draft
  • AR aging report
  • AP aging report
  • Uncategorized transactions (list for CPA decision)
  • Anomalies + reconciliation discrepancies
  • Sales tax accrual (if applicable)
  • Affiliate commission liability

Format: spreadsheet (CSV / XLSX) + summary markdown.

Step 6 — Surface decisions

For Chris + CPA:

  • Uncategorized transactions needing decisions
  • Reconciliation gaps requiring investigation
  • Vendor relationship changes (new vendor / canceled)
  • Cash position warnings (if low)

Step 7 — Output

Output structure

# Bookkeeping: <Month YYYY>

## Period
**Range**: <YYYY-MM-DD to YYYY-MM-DD>
**Generated**: <date>
**For**: CPA review

## Headlines
- Revenue: $<X>
- COGS: $<Y>
- Gross margin: <%>
- OpEx: $<Z>
- Net: $<N>
- Cash position EOM: $<C>
- AR EOM: $<AR>
- AP EOM: $<AP>

## Transaction summary

### Income
| Category | Count | Total |
|---|---|---|
| Product revenue — 503-A | <N> | $<X> |
| Product revenue — 503-B | <N> | $<X> |
| ... | | |

### COGS
| Category | Count | Total |
|---|---|---|
| Compounding pharmacy | <N> | $<X> |
| ... | | |

### OpEx
| Category | Count | Total |
|---|---|---|
| Software | <N> | $<X> |
| ... | | |

## Reconciliation

### Stripe ↔ Bank
- Stripe gross: $<X>
- Stripe fees: $<Y>
- Stripe net deposit: $<Z>
- Bank credits: $<W>
- **Variance**: $<diff> — <explained / unexplained>

### GHL invoices ↔ Stripe
- GHL invoiced: $<X>
- Stripe collected: $<Y>
- **Variance**: $<diff> — <explained / unexplained>

### Card ↔ Categorized expenses
- Card statement: $<X>
- Categorized expenses: $<Y>
- **Variance**: $<diff> — <explained / unexplained>

## ⚠️ Uncategorized transactions (CPA decision)
| Date | Vendor | Amount | Suggested category | Note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ... | | | | |

## ⚠️ Anomalies
- <Duplicate? Unusual amount? New vendor?>
- ...

## ⚠️ Discrepancies needing investigation
- <Stripe deposit on date X doesn't match bank>
- ...

## Sales tax
- Collected: $<X>
- Remitted: $<Y>
- Owed: $<Z>

## Affiliate liability
- Earned by affiliates this period: $<X>
- Paid out: $<Y>
- Owed: $<Z>

## Files generated
- `_Logs/bookkeeping/<YYYY-MM>/transactions.csv`
- `_Logs/bookkeeping/<YYYY-MM>/p-and-l.csv`
- `_Logs/bookkeeping/<YYYY-MM>/balance-sheet.csv`
- `_Logs/bookkeeping/<YYYY-MM>/reconciliation.csv`
- `_Logs/bookkeeping/<YYYY-MM>/summary.md` (this file)

## ✅ I handled
- Pulled all data
- Categorized (high-confidence auto; uncertain flagged)
- Reconciled across sources
- Surfaced anomalies + discrepancies
- Generated CPA packet

## ⚠️ Need from CPA
- Review uncategorized transactions
- Resolve reconciliation discrepancies
- Confirm sales tax accrual
- Approve P&L / balance sheet as final
- File any required forms

## 🚫 I will never
- File taxes
- Issue refunds
- Move money
- Approve P&L as official without CPA
- Modify the chart of accounts without approval

## Handoff
**To**: Chris (review) + CPA (file)
**Updates**: `cfo-finance` dashboard for the period

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-bookkeeping-<period>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Auto-categorizing uncertain transactions — flag for CPA, don't guess
  • Closing without reconciliation — discrepancies compound
  • Hiding anomalies — every duplicate / unusual amount surfaces
  • Filing on behalf — never
  • Modifying chart of accounts without approval
  • Single-source pulls — every number cross-checked across sources

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull data from connected sources (read-only)
  • ✅ Categorize transactions (high-confidence auto)
  • ✅ Generate CPA packet
  • ✅ Save to _Logs/bookkeeping/
  • ⚠️ Approve auto-categorization rules (Chris + CPA approve new rules)
  • 🚫 File any tax forms
  • 🚫 Modify GHL / Stripe / bank records
  • 🚫 Approve P&L as final (CPA approves)
  • 🚫 Issue refunds / credits

Pairing

Inputs from: Stripe, GHL payments_*, bank exports, card exports, accounting tool, _Shared Context/products.md (COGS) Outputs to: Chris + CPA (review), cfo-finance (dashboard update), weekly-review (financial section) Chains with: cfo-finance (forecasts + dashboards consume this), vendor-management (vendor changes flow through here)

One-liner reminder

Pull. Categorize (high-confidence auto). Reconcile. Surface anomalies. Generate packet. Hand to CPA. Never file. Never move money.

skills.name: Bud Calculator
skills.description: Atomic micro-agent. Input formulation form + storage condition + USP class + sterility status. Output default Beyond-Use Date (BUD) per USP standards (USP <795> non-sterile / <797> sterile). Pure deterministic lookup. If stability data supplied → use extended BUD; otherwise USP defaults. Composed by `formulation-stability` and `formulation-batch-record`.
skills.intent: Apply USP BUD defaults consistently. BUD on a label without supporting data is a regulatory + safety hazard; this skill enforces the floor. When stability data justifies extension, captures that signal but never auto-extends past the USP default without explicit stability-data override input.
skills.body_html:
BUD Calculator

USP-anchored BUD defaults. No LLM. Pure lookup + math. Stability data optional override.

Why this exists

BUD on a label must be supported by:

  1. USP-default rule (when no stability data), OR
  2. Documented stability data per USP <1191>

Putting a longer BUD on a label without supporting data is:

  • USP violation (compounded products)
  • FDA exposure (drug class)
  • Patient safety risk (degradation past validated shelf life)
  • Audit finding

This skill enforces the floor.

USP BUD defaults (no stability data)

Per USP <795> (non-sterile) and USP <797> (sterile):

USP <797> sterile preparations

Sterility / form Storage Default BUD
Sterile aqueous, single-dose Room temp (controlled, 20-25°C) 24 hours
Sterile aqueous, single-dose Refrigerated (2-8°C) 14 days
Sterile aqueous, single-dose Frozen (-20°C) 45 days
Sterile non-aqueous (oil-based), single-dose Room temp 30 days
Sterile non-aqueous, single-dose Refrigerated 60 days
Sterile multi-dose with validated preservative Room temp 28 days from first puncture
Sterile multi-dose with validated preservative Refrigerated 28 days from first puncture (often used)
Sterile multi-dose WITHOUT validated preservative Any DO NOT USE — single-dose only

Note: These are USP <797> Category 1 defaults (for low-risk preparations). Category 2 + Category 3 have different defaults — implementer must verify category match. This skill assumes Category 1 unless specified otherwise via usp_category (future extension).

USP <795> non-sterile preparations

Form Aqueous? Storage Default BUD
Non-sterile aqueous Yes Refrigerated 14 days
Non-sterile aqueous Yes Room temp 14 days
Non-sterile non-aqueous (oil, alcohol, etc.) No Room temp 90 days
Solid (capsule, troche, tablet) N/A Room temp 180 days
Topical (cream, ointment, gel, lotion) Mixed Room temp 30 days

Supplement (DSHEA — different framework)

Supplements use manufacturer-stated expiration based on real-time stability data, typically 24-36 months. This skill returns usp_default_not_applicable for supplements with no stability data + flags requirement.

Cosmetic

Cosmetic shelf life is established by manufacturer based on stability data + microbial limits + preservative validation. Returns flag if no stability data.

Algorithm

1. Match form + storage + sterility to USP table
2. If multi-dose sterile WITHOUT validated preservative → BLOCK
3. Return default_bud_days from table
4. If stability_data provided:
   a. Verify all sample points pass acceptance criteria up to claimed extension
   b. For 503-B: verify cohort_batches ≥ 3
   c. Compute extended_bud_days = min(real_time_months, 2× accelerated_months) × 30
   d. Cap at 24 months unless additional cohort data
5. recommended_bud_days = stability-supported extended OR USP default
6. If stability_data partial (insufficient cohorts, missing timepoints) → use USP default + warning
7. Generate warnings for any edge cases

Output examples

Sterile aqueous single-dose, refrigerated, 503-A, no stability data

default_bud_days: 14
default_bud_basis: "USP <797> Category 1, sterile aqueous single-dose, refrigerated"
extended_bud_days: null
extended_bud_basis: null
recommended_bud_days: 14
recommendation_reasoning: "USP <797> default applies (no stability data provided)"
warning: null

Same product with 6-month real-time stability data (3 batches)

default_bud_days: 14
default_bud_basis: "USP <797> Category 1, sterile aqueous single-dose, refrigerated"
extended_bud_days: 180
extended_bud_basis: "Real-time stability data, 6 months, 3 cohort batches all pass acceptance"
recommended_bud_days: 180
recommendation_reasoning: "Extended BUD supported by stability data. Use 6-month real-time as basis."
warning: null

Multi-dose vial WITHOUT validated preservative

default_bud_days: null
default_bud_basis: null
extended_bud_days: null
extended_bud_basis: null
recommended_bud_days: null
recommendation_reasoning: "BLOCKED: sterile multi-dose WITHOUT validated antimicrobial preservative effectiveness per USP <51> is not permitted. Single-dose only."
warning: "Reformulate as single-dose OR add + validate preservative per USP <51>"

Topical cosmetic with no stability data

default_bud_days: null
default_bud_basis: null
extended_bud_days: null
extended_bud_basis: null
recommended_bud_days: null
recommendation_reasoning: "Cosmetic shelf life requires stability + microbial limits + preservative validation data. No USP default applies."
warning: "Run real-time stability program + preservative effectiveness per USP <51>; come back when data available."

Stability data insufficient (1 batch, no accelerated)

default_bud_days: 30
default_bud_basis: "USP <795>, non-aqueous topical, room temp"
extended_bud_days: null
extended_bud_basis: null
recommended_bud_days: 30
recommendation_reasoning: "Stability data insufficient (1 batch; ICH Q1A requires 3 batches for label-claim shelf life on regulated products). Defaulting to USP <795>."
warning: "Add 2 more cohort batches + accelerated stability to support extension."

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/micro-agents/bud-calculator/:

  1. Sterile aqueous single-dose refrigerated 503-A no data → 14 days ✅
  2. Sterile aqueous single-dose room temp 503-A no data → 24 hours ✅
  3. Sterile non-aqueous single-dose refrigerated → 60 days ✅
  4. Multi-dose without preservative → BLOCKED ❌
  5. Multi-dose with validated preservative refrigerated → 28 days from first puncture ✅
  6. Capsule supplement no data → flag (DSHEA path, not USP)
  7. Topical cosmetic no data → flag (cosmetic path)
  8. Sterile aqueous single-dose with 6-month real-time stability 3 batches → 180 days ✅
  9. Sterile aqueous with 1-batch only → USP default + warning
  10. Frozen sterile aqueous → 45 days ✅
  11. Non-sterile non-aqueous → 90 days ✅
  12. Pellet (subcutaneous implant) → requires stability data; no USP default applies
  13. Patch (transdermal) → requires stability data; no USP default
  14. Stability data 24-month with 3 batches → 24 months (cap at 24 mo without additional cohorts)
  15. Stability data 36-month with 5 batches → 36 months allowed

Composition

This micro-agent is called by:

  • formulation-stability — protocol design (what BUD do we target? what data do we need?)
  • formulation-batch-record — generating MBR + label artwork (what BUD goes on label?)
  • formulation-compliance — class-specific BUD validation
  • formulation-eval-harness — case 020 (BUD supported by data)
  • formulation-continuous-improvement — quarterly BUD extension review

Anti-patterns

  • Auto-extending BUD without stability data — regulatory + safety hazard
  • Hardcoding USP table here without source-of-truth reference — use regulatory-frameworks.md as canonical
  • Adding LLM judgment — pure lookup; no need
  • Allowing multi-dose without preservative validation — silent safety failure
  • Skipping per-class branching — supplements + cosmetics need different paths
  • Recommending without warning when data is borderline — warning is part of the output

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Compute BUD per USP lookup + supplied stability data
  • ✅ Return structured recommendation
  • ✅ Flag missing-data scenarios
  • ⚠️ Approve a BUD extension beyond table limits (requires compliance-officer + Steve)
  • 🚫 Skip USP source-of-truth check
  • 🚫 Auto-approve unsupported BUD

Pairing

Composed by: formulation-stability, formulation-batch-record, formulation-compliance, formulation-eval-harness, formulation-continuous-improvement Inputs from: USP table (encoded in regulatory-frameworks.md), stability program data, USP <51> preservative effectiveness data Outputs to: Calling orchestrator + flag to compliance-officer for any unusual case

One-liner reminder

USP table is the floor. Stability data extends. Multi-dose needs validated preservative. Supplements + cosmetics use stability data, not USP. Cap at 24 mo without additional cohorts. Always return reasoning + warnings.

skills.name: Budget Dashboard
skills.description: Generate an interactive financial dashboard as a self-contained HTML file with Apple Swiss design. Includes sliders to adjust income and expenses in real-time, donut chart breakdown, 12-month savings projection with investment scenarios, milestone tracker, and benchmark comparisons. Use this skill when the user says "budget dashboard", "financial dashboard", "interactive budget", "budget app", "visualize my budget", "budget planner", "where's my money going", "savings dashboard", or any variation of wanting to see their finances visually.
skills.body_html:
Budget Dashboard

Build an interactive, self-contained HTML financial dashboard with an Apple Swiss design aesthetic — clean white backgrounds, SF-style typography (use Instrument Sans + Newsreader from Google Fonts), generous whitespace, soft shadows, pill-shaped buttons, and Apple system colors (#34c759 green, #007aff blue, #ff2d55 pink, #ff9500 orange, #af52de purple).

What It Delivers

A single HTML file the user can open in any browser. Everything updates in real-time as they drag sliders. No backend, no dependencies beyond Chart.js from CDN.

Features Required

  1. Top stat cards (4 across) — Income, Expenses, Savings, Savings Rate — each with an icon, the value, and a subtitle
  2. Slider panel — Sliders for income and each expense category. Each slider shows current value and range. All sliders fire update() on input.
  3. Donut chart — Shows expense breakdown + savings as the largest slice. Center text shows savings rate %. Uses Chart.js doughnut.
  4. Legend — Color-coded list beside the donut with category names, dollar amounts, and percentages.
  5. 12-month projection chart — Line chart (Chart.js) with 3 scenario toggle buttons: "Just Saving" (0% return), "High-Yield Savings" (4.5% APY), "Invested" (7% annual). Chart color changes per scenario.
  6. Milestones — Progress bars toward: Emergency Fund 3mo, Emergency Fund 6mo, $100K, $250K. Each shows ETA in months/years.
  7. Benchmark comparisons — Housing, Food, Savings, Wants vs. standard guidelines (50/30/20 rule). Visual bar with guideline marker.
  8. Insight box — Dynamic text that changes based on savings rate (different messages for <20%, 20-50%, 50%+, and negative).

Instructions

Step 1: Gather the Numbers

Ask for (or help estimate):

For Personal:

  • Total monthly income (after tax)
  • Fixed expenses (rent, utilities, insurance, subscriptions, loan payments)
  • Variable expenses (food, gas, entertainment, shopping)
  • Current savings rate
  • Any debt payments
  • Financial goals

For Business:

  • Monthly revenue
  • Fixed costs (rent, software, salaries, insurance)
  • Variable costs (marketing, contractors, supplies, travel)
  • Growth investments
  • Tax set-aside percentage
  • Profit targets

Step 2: Build the Dashboard

Generate a self-contained HTML file with all CSS inline (in a <style> tag) and all JS inline (in a <script> tag). Import Chart.js from CDN:

<script src="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/Chart.js/4.4.1/chart.umd.min.js"></script>

Import fonts from Google Fonts:

<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Instrument+Sans:wght@400;500;600;700&family=Newsreader:ital,opsz,wght@0,6..72,400;0,6..72,500;0,6..72,600&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">

Step 3: Design Rules (Apple Swiss)

  • Background: #f5f5f7 (Apple light gray)
  • Cards: #ffffff with border-radius: 20px, soft shadow (0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.04), 0 4px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.04))
  • Typography: Instrument Sans for body/UI, Newsreader (serif) for large numbers and headings
  • Colors: Use Apple system colors — green #34c759, blue #007aff, pink #ff2d55, orange #ff9500, purple #af52de, teal #5ac8fa
  • Sliders: Clean white thumbs with blue border, thin 4px tracks
  • Buttons: Pill-shaped (border-radius: 100px), active state fills with blue
  • Spacing: Generous — 24-28px card padding, 16px grid gaps
  • Animations: Subtle fadeIn on load with staggered delays
  • Hover states: Cards lift slightly with enhanced shadow
  • No dark mode — keep it light and clean

Step 4: Populate with User's Numbers

Set the slider default values and state object to match the user's actual income and expense numbers. All sliders should have sensible min/max ranges (e.g., income 2k-30k, rent 0-5k).

Step 5: Save and Deliver

Save as: financial-dashboard.html

The file should be completely self-contained — opening it in any browser should display the full interactive dashboard with no other dependencies.

Step 6: Verify

Double-check:

  • All math adds up (income - expenses = savings)
  • Donut chart percentages total 100%
  • Projection chart uses correct compound interest formulas
  • Milestone ETAs are calculated correctly
  • All sliders update everything in real-time

Rules

  • Never give specific investment advice. Budget visualization is fine. "Put your money in X stock" is not.
  • Include a small footer disclaimer that this is a planning tool, not financial advice.
  • Always double-check the math. Budget totals must add up correctly.
  • If the user shares sensitive financial details, handle them with care and don't store them in memory.
  • Round to whole dollars.

skills.name: Business Listing Manager
skills.description: SEO specialist for free business directory presence. Claims + maintains Matrix profiles on Google Business, Apple Maps, Bing Places, Yelp, NPPES, FDA registered facility list, BBB, LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, X, Crunchbase, Wikidata, Glassdoor, Healthgrades, industry directories, AI business profiles (Perplexity, ChatGPT Business when live). 30+ directories. NAP consistency enforced. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Every free directory listing is a free citation, free authority signal, free distribution surface. Matrix wants presence on every relevant directory + perfect NAP consistency. This specialist owns the inventory + maintains it quarterly + adds new directories as they emerge.
skills.estimated_time: Initial 30+ sweep: 3-5 days. Quarterly audit: 1 day. Per-listing setup: 15-30 min.
skills.body_html:
Business Listing Manager

The directory-presence specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns Matrix's presence across 30+ free business directories. Claims listings, maintains NAP consistency, populates rich content (descriptions, photos, hours, services), monitors for unauthorized listings (duplicates / scrapers), and tracks emerging directories. Quarterly audit ensures every listing remains accurate + optimized. Closely coordinates with local-seo-specialist for the Tier 1-2 critical listings.

Explicitly OUT of scope: GBP optimization beyond basic claim (local-seo-specialist owns deep GBP), review acquisition (review-acquisition-strategist), content writing (just descriptions; long-form is copywriting).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (foundation-sweep / quarterly-audit / new-listing / nap-correction / sub-brand-rollout / duplicate-cleanup / emerging-directory)
    required: true
  scope:
    type: enum (all-listings / specific-tier / specific-directory)
    required: false
    default: all-listings
  sub_brand:
    type: enum (Matrix / MuscleLock / Peptide Map / ClinIQ / all)
    required: false

RULES

rules:
  nap_canonical:
    - Use canonical NAP from `_Shared Context/company-profile.md`
    - EVERY listing must match exactly (no abbreviation drift, no formatting variance)
    - Phone: (XXX) XXX-XXXX consistently
    - Website: canonical URL (no www inconsistency)
  listing_priorities:
    - Tier 1 (per seo-targets.md): Google + Apple + Bing + Yelp first, before anything else
    - Tier 2-3 industry: NPPES (always verified for Matrix B2B), state pharmacy boards
    - Tier 4 general business: LinkedIn / Facebook / Instagram / YouTube / X
    - Tier 5 AI-search-relevant: ChatGPT Business / Perplexity / Wikidata
    - Per-sub-brand listings exist where the sub-brand has independent presence
  listing_optimization_per_directory:
    - Long description: rich, on-message, includes key terms naturally
    - Photos: minimum 5 per listing (logo, exterior, interior, team, product)
    - Hours: accurate + holiday hours
    - Services / categories: thorough
    - Attributes: all applicable
    - Verification: must verify (phone or postcard) for full features
  duplicate_management:
    - Quarterly: search each Tier 1-3 directory for unauthorized duplicate listings
    - Submit duplicate-removal requests
    - Track until resolved
  emerging_directories:
    - Quarterly scan for new AI-search business directories (ChatGPT business / Perplexity / Claude / Gemini business profiles)
    - Quarterly scan for new industry-specific directories
    - Test + register if relevant
  prohibited:
    - Listings on irrelevant directories (spammy citations hurt)
    - Keyword-stuffed descriptions (Yelp / Google penalty)
    - Fake or stretched categories
    - Reciprocal-link schemes

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - Google Business Profile API
    - Apple Business Connect
    - Bing Places for Business API
    - Yelp for Business
    - Facebook Business
    - LinkedIn (manual)
    - Crunchbase (manual)
    - Wikidata (machine-readable)
    - Industry directories (per `seo-targets.md`)
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md (target directory list)
    - _Shared Context/company-profile.md (canonical NAP)
    - _Shared Context/sub-brands.md
    - _Logs/seo/local/nap-canonical.md
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/seo/listings/directory-tracker.csv (master list with status per directory)
    - _Logs/seo/listings/quarterly-audit-<period>.md
  composed_skills:
    - local-seo-specialist (GBP + Apple Maps + Bing Places coordination)
    - compliance-phrase-detector (listing descriptions reviewed)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch + WebSearch
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (mostly form-filling + status tracking)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    audit_id: string
    period: ISO
    tier_summary:
      tier_1_live_count: number  # target: 4/4
      tier_2_live_count: number
      tier_3_live_count: number
      tier_4_live_count: number
      tier_5_live_count: number
      tier_6_live_count: number
      total_live: number  # target: 30+
      nap_consistent_count: number
      nap_inconsistent_count: number
    directory_status:
      - directory: string
        tier: 1-6
        listing_url: string
        status: live | pending | claimed-not-verified | not-claimed | nap-drift | duplicate-exists | suspended
        nap_consistency: pass | fail
        last_updated: ISO
        notes: string
    duplicates_detected:
      - directory: string
        duplicate_url: string
        action: removal-requested | manual-cleanup | resolved
    emerging_directory_candidates:
      - directory: string
        why: string
        priority: P0 | P1 | P2
    recommendations:
      - action: string
        priority: string
        owner: this skill | local-seo-specialist | external (counsel for IP-rich profiles)
  format: JSON + Markdown
  quality_bar:
    - All Tier 1-3 directories checked
    - NAP consistency verified per directory
    - Duplicates tracked through resolution

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - seo-strategy-lead (status reporting)
    - local-seo-specialist (Tier 1-3 deep coordination)
    - review-acquisition-strategist (Yelp / Google reviews tie-in)
  trigger_method: quarterly cron + new-sub-brand trigger + emerging-directory ad-hoc
  fallback_routing:
    - Tier 1 directory listing suspended → P0 emergency recovery
    - Mass NAP drift detected (10+ directories) → urgent audit + cleanup
    - Duplicate listing won't resolve via standard request → counsel-loop
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/seo/listings/

Eval cases

  1. Foundation sweep — claim all Tier 1-3 directories (initial rollout)
  2. Quarterly NAP audit — find + fix drift
  3. Duplicate listing found on Google Maps → resolve
  4. New AI directory emerges (Perplexity Business launches publicly) → register
  5. Sub-brand launch — separate listing rollout
  6. Listing suspended on Yelp → recovery

Anti-patterns

  • Spammy directory submissions — citations from low-quality directories hurt
  • NAP drift left — silent killer
  • Set-and-forget on Tier 1 listings — quarterly audit mandatory
  • Skipping verification step (loses rich features)
  • Keyword-stuffed business name in any listing

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Claim + maintain listings
  • ✅ NAP audit + correction
  • ✅ Submit duplicate-removal requests
  • ⚠️ Counsel-loop for IP / trademark angle (when relevant)
  • 🚫 Misrepresent business

Pairing

Composed by: seo-strategy-lead Composes: local-seo-specialist, review-acquisition-strategist, compliance-phrase-detector Reads: seo-targets.md, company-profile.md, sub-brands.md

One-liner reminder

30+ directories Tier 1-6. NAP canonical everywhere. Verify always. Quarterly audit non-negotiable. Watch for emerging AI business directories. No spammy directories.

skills.name: Call Handoff Orchestrator
skills.description: Smooth handoff between channels — voice → SMS → email → human (or any combo). The "context never gets re-asked" enforcer. Captures conversation state at handoff, passes to next channel with summary, ensures the receiving party / agent / human has full context. Cuts customer friction across multi-touch journeys.
skills.intent: Stop making customers re-state their problem when switching channels. Capture: who I'm talking to, what they want, what we've discussed, what's the next step. Pass that across modality boundaries (voice → SMS, voice → human, web chat → voice, etc.). Every handoff goes through this orchestrator.
best_for: Voice → human transfer with full context
Voice → SMS for catalog link / appointment link
Voice → email for long-form info / catalog
Web chat → voice for sensitive conversation
skills.estimated_time: Mid-call: seconds. Multi-channel sequence design: 30 min
skills.body_html:
Call Handoff Orchestrator

The skill that makes context travel with the customer. No more "tell me again from the top."

When to use

  • Voice agent transfers to a human (warm transfer with summary)
  • Voice agent ends call but caller wants follow-up info via text/email
  • Web chat escalates to a voice call
  • SMS conversation reaches a step that needs voice
  • Multi-step customer journey crosses channels
  • Onboarding hands off from kickoff voice call → SMS reminders → 14-day human check-in

When NOT to use

  • Single-channel conversation (no handoff needed)
  • Handoff to a system that already has the context (e.g., booking confirmation that auto-references)
  • Internal team-to-team handoff (use task-list-manager / standard handoff template)

Required inputs

  1. Source — which channel + agent is handing off
  2. Target — which channel + agent receives
  3. Context to pass — what the receiver needs to know
  4. Customer / contact — the GHL contact this is about
  5. Reason for handoff — why this is happening (informs receiver)
  6. Urgency — immediate / soon / async

If 1-2 missing, ask: "Source channel + target channel?"

The handoff record (structured payload)

Every handoff produces this object (logged to GHL contact + _Logs/agent-decisions/):

handoff:
  id: <uuid>
  source:
    channel: voice | chat | sms | email | in-person
    agent: <agent name or "human:[name]">
    started_at: <timestamp>
  target:
    channel: voice | chat | sms | email | in-person | human
    agent: <agent name or "human:[name]">
    initiated_at: <timestamp>
  contact:
    ghl_id: <id>
    name: <name>
    clinic: <clinic>
    role: <role>
  context:
    intent: <what they want — one sentence>
    captured_info: <list of facts captured>
    open_questions: <list>
    objections_raised: <list>
    decisions_pending: <list>
    next_step_promised: <what was promised in source channel>
  reason: <why handoff happened>
  urgency: immediate | soon | async
  state_to_resume: <if conversation should pick up at a specific stage>
  notes: <free text>

Channel-to-channel patterns

Voice → Human (mid-call)

Mechanism: Warm transfer (Retell / Twilio function call) Payload to human: Brief — 1-2 sentence context spoken in introduction + full record dropped into Slack/SMS to receiver Example:

[Voice agent]: "Let me get [human name] on the line — one moment." [Voice agent → human via Twilio whisper]: "Got Dr. Patel from [clinic] on the line. Calling about [reason]. Captured: [3 facts]. They want: [intent]." [Human picks up]: "Hi Dr. Patel — I just got the summary from Alex. Let's pick up where you left off — you were saying..."

Voice → SMS (mid-call)

Mechanism: send_followup_sms function Payload: Templated SMS with specific link/info Example:

[Voice agent]: "I'll text you the calendar link right now — should land in about 10 seconds." [SMS sent]: "Hey Dr. Patel — here's the link to book your strategy call with Matrix: [link]. — Mia"

Voice → Email (post-call)

Mechanism: send_followup_email function Payload: Templated email with attachments / links + custom note based on call Example:

[Voice agent]: "I'll send the catalog to dr.patel@clinic.com along with a quick summary of what we talked about. Should be in your inbox in about a minute." [Email sent]: subject="Matrix catalog + recap of our conversation"; body includes call summary + CTA

Web Chat → Voice (caller-initiated)

Mechanism: Chat surfaces "want to talk via voice?" button → schedules immediate callback or live voice Payload: Full chat transcript + summary handed to voice agent at call start Example:

[Chat → voice agent]: "Pulling up our chat — I see you were asking about [topic]. Let me pick that up where we left off."

SMS → Voice (escalation)

Mechanism: SMS sequence detects an SMS that exceeds bot capability → offers callback Payload: SMS thread summary Example:

[SMS]: "Looks like this needs more detail than text can handle — want a quick callback in the next 10 minutes?" [Voice callback]: "Hey Dr. Patel — Alex here, picking up our text thread. You were asking about [topic]."

Voice → Human Calendar Booking (async)

Mechanism: book_strategy_call function + confirmation Payload: Calendar event with full context in the invite description + pre-call email Example:

[Voice agent]: "Booked you for Thursday at 2pm with [AM name]. They'll have the full context from our chat — you won't have to re-explain. Confirmation just hit your inbox."

Workflow

Step 1 — Capture state from source

Before initiating handoff, snapshot:

  • Intent (one sentence)
  • Facts captured (numbered)
  • Open questions
  • Objections raised
  • Decisions pending
  • What was promised in source channel

Step 2 — Format payload for target channel

Adapt the record for the receiving format:

  • Human: brief spoken intro (10-15 sec) + full payload dropped via Slack/SMS
  • SMS: < 160 char message with clear next step
  • Email: full payload + structured next steps
  • Voice (new call): spoken intro that references prior context

Step 3 — Execute the handoff

Per channel-specific function:

  • Voice transfer: Twilio warm transfer + whisper
  • SMS: send_followup_sms with payload-derived template
  • Email: send_followup_email with attachments
  • Calendar: book_* function with full context in invite

Step 4 — Log to GHL

Update the GHL contact with:

  • Handoff record (custom field or note)
  • Tag: handoff:<source>-to-<target>
  • Most recent channel touched
  • Next-step promised

Step 5 — Confirm receipt

For human handoffs: confirm the receiving human acknowledges (Slack reaction, SMS reply, calendar accept). If no ack within window → escalate.

For automated handoffs: confirm send success via webhook response.

Step 6 — Output (mid-call)

For mid-call handoffs, the orchestrator runs synchronously and outputs:

✅ Handoff executed
   <source-agent> → <target>
   Reason: <reason>
   Context delivered: <yes / no>
   Receiver acknowledged: <yes / no / pending>
   Logged: <GHL note ID + _Logs path>

For async handoffs, output is a project log entry + the next-touch task in TASKS.md.

Anti-patterns

  • Customer re-asked to state their problem — the orchestrator failed
  • Lost context at handoff (handoff happened but receiver doesn't have the record)
  • No next-step promised documented — receiver doesn't know what was committed
  • Untracked handoffs — every one logs to GHL + _Logs/
  • Handoff to unavailable human without fallback (orchestrator should check availability before transferring)
  • Channel mismatch (SMS to a non-SMS-consented contact)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Log handoff records to GHL contact (note + tag)
  • ✅ Execute voice-to-voice transfer
  • ✅ Execute voice-to-SMS / voice-to-email via templates
  • ✅ Generate handoff payload
  • ⚠️ Book on human's calendar (per approval-rules.md parameters)
  • ⚠️ Transfer to specific human at non-business hours
  • 🚫 Send SMS to non-consented contacts
  • 🚫 Auto-escalate to executives without explicit "escalate" intent

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • Source agent (voice / chat / SMS / etc.)
  • GHL contact data
  • _Shared Context/approval-rules.md (consent + handoff rules)
  • Human availability schedule (if escalating to human)

Outputs to:

  • Target agent / human / channel
  • GHL contact (note + tag + custom fields)
  • _Logs/agent-decisions/

Chains with:

  • All voice-ai-* agents (every one calls this for handoffs)
  • customer-onboarding (multi-touch journey handoffs)
  • email-triage (when voice hands to email follow-up)
  • task-list-manager (when handoff creates a follow-up task)

One-liner reminder

Capture state. Format per target channel. Execute. Log to GHL. Confirm receipt. Never make the customer re-explain.

skills.name: Cash Flow Monitor
skills.description: Daily cash position across all accounts (operating / payroll / reserve). Surfaces anomalies (large outflows, missed receipts, balance below floor). Composes Stripe + bank-feed reads. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Cash is oxygen. You should know the position daily, not weekly. This specialist runs a daily check across all accounts, surfaces anomalies, and feeds upstream into forecasting + vendor-payment-orchestrator (pre-batch check).
skills.estimated_time: Daily run: 5-10 min. Anomaly investigation: 15-30 min.
skills.body_html:
Cash Flow Monitor

The daily cash sentinel. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Runs daily (default 7:30am PT, ahead of chain-monitor 7am Friday digest) reading cash positions across all Matrix accounts: operating, payroll, reserve, plus Stripe + corporate-card balances. Computes total available cash, day-over-day delta, surfaces anomalies (outflows > $10k unexpected, missed expected receipts, balance below per-account floor). Feeds forecasting-runway, gates vendor-payment-orchestrator pre-batch. Does NOT move cash between accounts, recommend investments, or set safety floors (Chris + cfo-finance do).

Explicitly OUT of scope: Cash sweeps / investments (Chris + treasury planned), payment execution (vendor-payment-orchestrator), revenue recognition (bookkeeping), forecast modeling (forecasting-runway).

INPUT

input:
  trigger: daily-cron-7:30am-PT | pre-batch-check | adhoc
  accounts_to_check:
    - account_id: operating
    - account_id: payroll
    - account_id: reserve
    - account_id: stripe-balance
    - account_id: corporate-card (Brex / Ramp)
  expected_movements (optional):
    expected_receipts: from ar-ap-automation
    expected_payments: from vendor-payment-orchestrator scheduled

RULES

  • Read-only across all accounts. Never initiates a transfer.
  • Per-account safety floor (configured in _Shared Context/integration-map.md or vendors.md finance section):
    • Operating: $25,000 floor → alert P1 if < floor
    • Payroll: 2 weeks of payroll committed → alert P0 if insufficient
    • Reserve: $100,000 floor → alert P1
  • Anomaly thresholds:
    • Single unexpected outflow > $10k → P1 alert
    • Single unexpected outflow > $25k → P0 alert + immediate Chris notification
    • Expected receipt missed by > 3 days → P2 alert
    • Day-over-day total delta > 15% drop → P1 alert
  • Stripe payout timing factored — outflow vs payout-in-transit are different.
  • PII / banking data masked in all logs (last-4 only; never full account #).
  • Source-of-truth tie-out — bank-feed balance vs accounting tool reconciled (variance > $50 = flag).
  • Weekend / holiday — no alert spam; queue alerts for next business day except P0.
  • Cross-CI awareness — read latest chain-monitor for context (e.g., big sales day → expect cash up).

TOOLS

  • Bank feed API or daily statement (whichever integration is live per integration-map.md)
  • Stripe API (payments-engine composed for balance + payouts-in-transit)
  • Corporate-card API (Brex / Ramp balance + recent transactions)
  • bookkeeping (cross-reference)
  • ar-ap-automation (expected receipts / payments)
  • vendor-payment-orchestrator (scheduled outflows)
  • Read: _Shared Context/integration-map.md, vendors.md, kpi-registry.md
  • Compose: analytics-architect (visualization)
  • Write: _Logs/finance/cash/<YYYY-MM-DD>-snapshot.json + _Logs/finance/cash/anomalies/<id>.md

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    snapshot_id: string
    snapshot_at: ISO
    cash_positions:
      - account_id: string
        balance: number
        last_updated: ISO
        floor: number
        floor_status: above | at | below
    totals:
      total_available_cash: number
      d_over_d_delta: number
      d_over_d_delta_pct: number
      stripe_in_transit: number
      total_including_in_transit: number
    anomalies:
      - type: large-unexpected-outflow | missed-expected-receipt | below-floor | source-of-truth-variance
        severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        amount: number
        recommendation: string
    expected_movements_24h:
      - direction: in | out
        amount: number
        source_or_destination: string
        confidence: H | M | L
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        notified: array (chris | cfo-finance | controller-lead)
  format: JSON + Markdown summary

HANDOFF

  • finance-controller-lead (daily snapshot)
  • forecasting-runway (starting-cash actuals for forecast)
  • vendor-payment-orchestrator (pre-batch check response)
  • Chris (P0 + P1 alerts immediate)
  • cfo-finance (P1+ alerts + runway implications)
  • chain-monitor (cross-CI feed)

Eval cases

  1. Standard day, all accounts above floor, normal delta → green snapshot
  2. $30k unexpected outflow detected → P0 alert + Chris immediate
  3. Operating drops below $25k floor → P1 alert + cfo-finance notified
  4. Stripe payout delayed 2 days → in-transit calc adjusts; alert if persistent
  5. Bank-feed shows variance vs accounting > $200 → reconciliation flag to bookkeeping
  6. Pre-batch check, batch $40k vs operating $35k → P0 block to vendor-payment-orchestrator
  7. Weekend P2 (above floor, all fine) → queued for Monday digest, not alerted

Anti-patterns

  • Initiating transfers — never; read-only
  • Alerting weekly — should be daily
  • No safety floors — risk of overdraft / payroll fail
  • PII in logs — never full account numbers
  • Ignoring Stripe in-transit — over/under-states position
  • Source-of-truth drift unflagged — close discipline failure
  • Alert spam on weekends for non-P0 — controller fatigue

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read all account balances
  • ✅ Compute deltas + anomalies
  • ✅ Surface alerts per severity
  • ✅ Feed forecasting + vendor-batch checks
  • ⚠️ Recommend cash sweep (Chris + cfo-finance execute)
  • 🚫 Initiate any transfer
  • 🚫 Modify safety floor (config change requires Chris)
  • 🚫 Reconcile sub-ledger (handoff to bookkeeping)

Pairing

Composed by: finance-controller-lead, vendor-payment-orchestrator (pre-batch), forecasting-runway Composes: payments-engine (Stripe), bookkeeping, ar-ap-automation, analytics-architect Reads: integration-map.md, vendors.md, kpi-registry.md Hands off to: Chris, cfo-finance, chain-monitor

One-liner reminder

Daily cash across all accounts. Read-only. Per-account floors. Anomaly thresholds tiered. Gates vendor batches. Feeds forecast. Never moves money.

skills.name: Cfo Finance
skills.description: Financial strategy + cash planning + monthly close support. Owns the financial dashboard (revenue, MRR, runway, burn, margin, CAC, LTV, payment terms exposure). Coordinates pricing-margin + bookkeeping (planned). Never approves money movements — drafts + escalates to Chris + CPA.
skills.intent: Be the CFO function for Matrix. Track revenue + cash + margin + runway. Surface trends. Forecast. Surface risks. Prepare board / investor materials. Never moves money or files taxes — drafts and hands to CPA / counsel.
best_for: Pricing decisions (financial input — handoff to pricing-margin)
skills.estimated_time: 60-120 min monthly close; 30 min weekly snapshot
skills.body_html:
CFO Finance

The financial function. Dashboards, forecasts, board prep. Never moves money.

When to use

  • Monthly close review
  • Pre-board / pre-investor financial section
  • Cash runway concern
  • Margin / unit economics question
  • Pricing decision (financial input → handoff to pricing-margin for the actual recommendation)

When NOT to use

  • Daily bookkeeping (use bookkeeping planned)
  • Tax filings (use CPA — tax-prep planned, but always defer to human CPA)
  • Pricing recommendation itself (use pricing-margin)
  • Approving any payment / refund / charge

Required inputs

  1. Data sources — Stripe / GHL payments / bank / accounting software / spreadsheet
  2. Period — month / quarter / YTD
  3. Question — close / forecast / board / runway / trend

If 1 missing, ask: "Where's the financial data — Stripe, GHL, accounting tool, or a spreadsheet path?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Pull data

  • Revenue (Stripe / GHL payments_list-transactions)
  • Subscription / recurring (if applicable)
  • Refunds + chargebacks
  • Bank cash position (manual or bank API)
  • Outstanding invoices (GHL invoices)
  • Cost-to-deliver (per products.md + bookkeeping)
  • Operating expenses (accounting tool)
  • Headcount cost (HR)

Step 2 — Calculate core metrics

Metric Formula Target / threshold
Revenue sum of completed transactions —
MRR / ARR recurring revenue × 12 (if subs) —
Gross margin (Revenue - COGS) / Revenue 50%+ target
Net margin (Revenue - COGS - OpEx) / Revenue 15%+ target (early stage)
CAC Sales+marketing spend / new customers < 1/3 of LTV
LTV Avg revenue per customer × avg retention months 3× CAC minimum
LTV : CAC LTV / CAC 3:1 minimum
Payback period CAC / monthly margin per customer < 12 months
Burn rate OpEx + COGS - Revenue per month —
Runway Cash / burn rate > 12 months ideal
AR / AP outstanding invoices in / out track aging
Affiliate liability accrued commissions unpaid accrue monthly

Step 3 — Trend analysis

For each metric, vs prior:

  • Last month vs this month
  • Last 3 months trend
  • vs target

Flag any:

  • ⚠️ Trending wrong direction 3+ months
  • 🔴 At or near runway threshold
  • 🔴 Negative gross margin on any SKU
  • 🟡 LTV:CAC below 3:1
  • 🟡 Payback > 12 months

Step 4 — Forecast (if requested)

Build a 3-6 month rolling forecast:

  • Revenue: based on pipeline (ghl-daily-pulse + close-rate assumption) + recurring base
  • COGS: scaled with revenue
  • OpEx: known fixed + variable assumptions
  • Cash: starting + revenue - burn each month
  • Runway: when does cash hit safety threshold?

State the assumptions clearly. Use ranges (low/mid/high) not single numbers.

Step 5 — Surface decisions for Chris

What needs a CFO-style decision:

  • Pricing changes (handoff to pricing-margin)
  • Customer-level credit / payment-term changes
  • Vendor cost negotiations
  • Hiring / firing financial impact
  • Investment / spending decisions > $1k

Step 6 — Board / investor section (if applicable)

Per _Templates/weekly-report-template.md investor variant:

  • Revenue / MRR / runway headline
  • Pipeline value
  • Unit economics
  • Cash status + runway
  • Major asks for board

Step 7 — Output

Output structure

# CFO Report: <Period>

## Headline
<one sentence: what's the financial story>

## Core metrics

| Metric | This period | Prior | Delta | Target | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue | $X | $Y | <delta> | $T | 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 |
| MRR | $X | $Y | <delta> | $T | <status> |
| Gross margin | <%> | <%> | <pp> | 50% | <status> |
| Net margin | <%> | <%> | <pp> | 15% | <status> |
| CAC | $X | $Y | <delta> | < $T | <status> |
| LTV | $X | $Y | <delta> | > $T | <status> |
| LTV:CAC | <ratio> | <ratio> | <delta> | 3:1 | <status> |
| Burn | $X/mo | $Y/mo | <delta> | — | — |
| Cash | $X | $Y | <delta> | — | — |
| Runway | <N> months | <M> | <delta> | > 12 | <status> |

## Trend flags
- 🔴 <flag with detail>
- 🟡 <flag with detail>

## Forecast (if requested)

### Revenue (3-month rolling)
| Month | Low | Mid | High |
|---|---|---|---|
| Next | $X | $Y | $Z |
| +1 | $A | $B | $C |
| +2 | $D | $E | $F |

### Cash position
| Month | Starting | Revenue (mid) | Burn | Ending |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Next | $X | $Y | -$Z | $W |
| +1 | ... | | | |
| +2 | ... | | | |

**Runway at mid forecast**: <N months>

## Decisions needed

### ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- <Pricing decision: handoff to pricing-margin recommended>
- <Customer credit decision: ...>
- <Vendor cost decision: ...>
- <Hiring impact: ...>

### 🚫 You + CPA only
- Tax filings
- Year-end financial statements
- Equity / stock decisions
- Refunds / write-offs

## Board section (if applicable)
<formatted for board / investor consumption>

## ✅ I handled
- Data pull from sources
- Metric calculation
- Trend analysis
- Forecast (with assumptions)
- Surfaced decisions

## 🚫 I will never
- Move money
- Issue refunds
- Charge cards
- File taxes
- Sign financial documents

## Handoff
**To**:
- `pricing-margin` (for any pricing-related decisions)
- Bookkeeper / CPA (for actual close + filings)
- `weekly-review` (financial snapshot)
- Investor relations agents (planned — for board / investor materials)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-cfo-<period>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Moving money — never. Drafts only.
  • Single-number forecasts — always use low/mid/high
  • Hidden assumptions — state every assumption explicitly
  • Ignoring AR aging — uncollected revenue isn't revenue
  • No runway warning when runway < 12 months — surface immediately
  • Pricing recommendation directly — hand off to pricing-margin
  • CPA work — defer all tax / compliance filings to actual CPA

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull financial data from connected sources (read-only)
  • ✅ Calculate metrics + forecasts
  • ✅ Draft board / investor sections
  • ✅ Surface decisions + risks
  • ⚠️ Update financial KPIs in dashboards
  • 🚫 Move money (any direction)
  • 🚫 Charge / refund / chargeback
  • 🚫 File taxes
  • 🚫 Approve material spending decisions (Chris + advisors)

Pairing

Inputs from: GHL payments_*, Stripe (if direct), bookkeeping software, _Shared Context/products.md (COGS), _Shared Context/offers.md (pricing) Outputs to: Chris, CPA, board, pricing-margin, weekly-review Chains with: pricing-margin, bookkeeping (planned), weekly-review, pmo-operator

One-liner reminder

Pull data. Calculate. Trend. Forecast with assumptions. Surface decisions. Hand to CPA for filings. Never move money.

skills.name: Chain Monitor
skills.description: Cross-cutting observability layer for every specialist chain at Matrix. Tracks per-chain state (running / paused / escalated / completed / failed), per-step latency + cost, conversion rates per step, stalled chains, over-budget chains, and SLA breaches. Daily digest to Christo + ad-hoc alerts. Built per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md` for specialist chain agents.
skills.intent: Chains without monitoring become black boxes — debugging impossible, improvement guesswork. This skill watches every chain in flight, surfaces patterns + anomalies, and feeds the Friday CI engines with chain-quality data. Without this, the specialist-chain model can't be scaled safely.
best_for: Pre-Friday CI engine prep (each dept CI needs chain-quality data)
Detecting cost / latency regressions in chains
skills.estimated_time: Daily digest: 10-15 min auto-run. Specific chain debug: 5-15 min.
skills.body_html:
Chain Monitor

A specialist digital employee for chain observability. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

The chain-monitor tracks every specialist chain in flight at Matrix — lead-to-booked-demo, customer-refund-request, new-customer-onboarding, inbound-call-to-handoff, etc. Maintains real-time chain status; computes per-step metrics; detects anomalies; produces daily digests; feeds chain-quality data into Friday CI engines.

Explicitly OUT of scope: building chains (micro-agent-team-builder), executing chain steps (specialist agents), approving chain decisions (chain-step owners), strategic chain redesign (pmo-operator consulting micro-agent-team-builder).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (daily-cron / chain-event / ad-hoc-query)
    required: true
  chain_id:
    type: string
    required: false  # required for ad-hoc query mode; optional for daily / event triggers
  query_scope:
    type: enum (all-active / single-chain / specific-dept / specific-time-window / stalled-only / failed-only / over-budget-only)
    required: false
    default: all-active
  time_window:
    type: object
    fields:
      from: ISO timestamp
      to: ISO timestamp
    required: false  # defaults: last 7 days for digest, all-time for specific debug
validation:
  - For chain-event trigger: chain_id required
  - For ad-hoc query: query_scope required

RULES

rules:
  data_handling:
    - Read-only on chain state (never modifies chain in-flight)
    - PHI never appears in digest output (chains may touch PHI; monitor sanitizes)
    - Aggregate metrics first; specific examples sanitized
  thresholds:
    stalled_chain: > 24 hours at same step without progress
    over_budget_chain: cost > 2x chain target ($1 default for lead-to-demo)
    over_latency_chain: total duration > 3x chain target (24h default)
    high_human_intervention: > 30% of chains in window need human review
    high_failure_rate: > 5% chains failed in window
    high_compliance_block_rate: > 10% chains blocked at compliance step
  alerting:
    - Stalled chain > 48h → ping AM responsible for that contact + Christo
    - Failed chain in regulated step → ping compliance-officer + Christo
    - Cost-overrun > $10 per chain (any) → ping Christo immediately
    - Daily digest at 7am PT → Christo + Chris
  observability_discipline:
    - Every metric has a source log entry
    - Every alert has the underlying chain_id linked
    - No alerting noise — only thresholds that crossed
    - Trend > snapshot (3 data points minimum to call a regression)
  failure_handling:
    - If chain state files unreadable: alert engineering; serve last-known-good digest
    - If alerting fails: write to _Logs/chain-monitor/alerts-fallback.md
    - Never fail silently on critical alerts (compliance + cost + safety)

TOOLS

tools:
  internal_data_read:
    - _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/ — chain state per run
    - _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/<step>/<contact_id>.json — per-step output
    - GHL custom fields (chain status tags + per-contact chain history)
    - All specialist agent logs (per their SKILL.md observability section)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/chain-monitor/digests/<YYYY-MM-DD>.md — daily digest
    - _Logs/chain-monitor/alerts/<YYYY-MM-DD>.md — alerts fired
    - _Logs/chain-monitor/metrics-history.csv — append-only metric log
    - _Logs/chain-monitor/anomalies/<chain_id>-<date>.md — specific anomaly drill-down
  composed_skills:
    - analytics-architect (KPI computation patterns)
    - pmo-operator (chain tickets that stall → project-board entry)
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (read contact chain-status tags)
    - scheduled-tasks (daily cron trigger; alerts schedule)
    - WebFetch (if external chain-state needed — rare)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (default — most analysis is aggregation + threshold checks)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (anomaly root-cause hypothesis generation)
    typical_cost_per_run:
      daily-digest: $0.05-0.15
      ad-hoc-debug: $0.02-0.10

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    digest_id: string  # uuid
    generated_at: ISO timestamp
    scope: string  # what was queried
    chain_inventory:
      total_chains_active: number
      by_type:
        - chain_type: string  # e.g., "lead-to-booked-demo"
          count: number
          breakdown:
            running: number
            paused: number
            escalated: number
            completed_success: number
            completed_other: number
            failed: number
    per_chain_metrics:
      - chain_type: string
        runs_in_window: number
        avg_duration_hours: number
        avg_cost_dollars: number
        success_rate: number (0-1)
        step_conversion_rates:
          - step_name: string
            entered: number
            exited_to_next: number
            dropped: number
            conversion_pct: number
        bottleneck_step: string
        bottleneck_reason: string
    stalled_chains:
      - chain_id: string
        chain_type: string
        contact_id: string  # anonymized in output (last 4 chars)
        stuck_at_step: string
        stuck_for_hours: number
        last_activity: ISO timestamp
        suggested_action: string
    cost_anomalies:
      - chain_id: string
        chain_type: string
        actual_cost: number
        expected_cost: number
        delta_pct: number
        root_cause_hypothesis: string
    failure_patterns:
      - failure_signature: string
        count: number
        first_seen: date
        suggested_fix: string
    sla_breaches:
      - chain_type: string
        breach_type: enum (latency / cost / failure-rate / compliance-block-rate / human-intervention-rate)
        target: string
        actual: string
        trend: enum (worsening / stable / improving)
    asks:
      - urgency: enum (immediate / today / this-week)
        ask: string
        owner: string
  format:
    - JSON (machine-readable; feeds CI engines)
    - Markdown digest (human-readable; for Christo + Chris)
  quality_bar:
    - All active chains accounted for (no orphans)
    - Every alert has source chain_id linked
    - Trends use ≥ 3 data points
    - No PHI in digest text
    - Anomalies have hypothesis + suggested action, not just "weird thing happened"

Example digest (abbreviated)

# Chain Monitor Digest — 2026-05-12

## Snapshot
- Active chains: 47 (lead-to-demo: 32, refund: 8, onboarding: 5, inbound-handoff: 2)
- Completed in last 24h: 18 (15 success, 3 routed-nurture)
- Failed in last 24h: 1 (refund chain, Stripe webhook timeout)

## Alerts (action required)
- 🔴 1 stalled chain: lead-to-demo, contact ending ...a4b2, stuck at clinic-research-agent for 28h. **Suggested action**: re-run research with fallback web sources OR move to manual research.
- 🟡 Cost anomaly: 1 lead-to-demo chain ran $1.85 (vs $0.60 target) — clinic-research-agent used Sonnet 4 times due to ambiguous data. Root cause: prospect had no website + NPI mismatch.

## Patterns (last 7 days)
- ✅ Lead-to-demo success rate: 14% (up from 11% — outreach-writer self-check improvements landed)
- ⚠️ Refund chain compliance-block rate: 8% (target < 5%) — pattern: CS-drafted refund comms missing CAN-SPAM footer in 4 of 6 blocked cases. **Action**: update CS template.
- ✅ Onboarding chain completion within 14 days: 100% (target 90%)

## Asks for Christo
- IMMEDIATE: re-route or manually unstick the 28h stalled chain (link)
- TODAY: approve CS template update for refund comms (compliance-officer prepared draft)
- THIS WEEK: chain-monitor proposes adding `clinic-research-agent` timeout escalation at 1h (currently 5min budget but no escalation if hit)

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - Christo (daily digest delivery via EA `ea-christo-mac-coo`)
    - Friday CI engines (chain-quality metrics feed into respective dept CI):
      - lead-to-demo chain metrics → ghl-continuous-improvement
      - refund chain metrics → ecommerce-continuous-improvement
      - onboarding chain metrics → ghl-continuous-improvement
      - inbound-handoff chain metrics → voice-call-reviewer
  payload:
    - Per-CI engine: subset of digest relevant to that department
    - Per-Christo: full digest in markdown
  trigger_method:
    - Daily cron at 7am PT via `scheduled-tasks` MCP
    - Event-triggered alerts (stalled / failed / cost-anomaly thresholds crossed)
    - Ad-hoc invocation by Christo or any specialist agent
  fallback_routing:
    - If alert delivery fails: write to `_Logs/chain-monitor/alerts-fallback.md` + retry every 5 min for 1 hour
    - If chain state unreadable: alert engineering immediately; serve last-known-good
    - If high-confidence anomaly with no clear owner: escalate to Christo
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/chain-monitor/digests/ + metrics-history.csv (append-only)
    retention: indefinite (trend analysis needs full history)
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/chain-monitor/runs/<digest_id>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - digest_generation_duration
      - chains_analyzed_count
      - alerts_fired_count
      - cost_of_this_digest
