          our_answer: string
          anchor_reference: string (approved-claim ID or publication)
      disqualifiers:  # when NOT to compete
        - scenario: string
          recommended_action: enum (no-bid / nurture / partner)
      wedge_plays:  # where we win
        - scenario: string
          play: string
          evidence: string
      pricing_signal:
        public_list_observed: string | null
        anchor_strategy: enum (value / volume / tier / not-applicable)
    flags:
      - flag: enum (low-confidence / counsel-review-needed / new-claim-attempted / disparagement-risk)
        detail: string
    distribution:
      ghl_kb_url: string
      slack_post_ts: string
  format: Markdown card (AM-readable) + JSON manifest
  quality_bar:
    - All sections populated OR explicitly "insufficient data" + research-next-step
    - ≥ 2 public sources cited per fact claim
    - No disparagement
    - No trade-secret leaks
    - No new claims (every claim in approved-claims library)

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream: AMs (via GHL knowledge base + Slack post)
  conditional_downstreams:
    - first-publish or new-claim flag → compliance-officer (review before publish)
    - counsel-review-needed flag → ip-counsel-coordination
    - low-confidence flag → market-research (deeper research pass)
    - per-deal request → closer-support (card embedded in deal coaching)
  trigger_method: GHL knowledge base update + Slack #sales-competition post
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/battlecards/<competitor>/v<n>.md + ghl-kb
    retention: indefinite (versioned)
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/competitive-battlecard-builder/<card_id>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - card_version
      - confidence
      - sources_cited_count
      - cost
      - flags

Eval cases

  1. Quarterly refresh, existing card, signal stable → minor update + republish
  2. New competitor flagged in deal → full card built within 4 business hours
  3. Insufficient public data → "low-confidence card" published + research plan
  4. Competitor on legal-restricted list → STOP + Chris + counsel
  5. Disparagement language attempted in draft → self-check fails + re-draft
  6. New claim attempted (not in approved list) → compliance-officer review
  7. SERP signal > 25% change → off-cycle refresh triggered
  8. Won 5 deals vs this competitor in quarter → wedge plays updated with patterns
  9. Lost 5 deals vs this competitor → disqualifiers updated; route to win-loss-analyst
  10. Trade-secret detail attempted in card → self-check fails + re-draft

Anti-patterns

  • Disparagement framing ("they're worse because...") instead of factual contrast
  • New claims invented for the card (every claim must already be approved)
  • Trade-secret leak in differentiation section
  • Card stale > 90 days with no refresh trigger
  • Single-source claims (require ≥ 2 public sources per fact)
  • Skipping disqualifiers section (knowing when NOT to compete is half the value)
  • Counsel-restricted competitor mention without counsel review

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build + refresh battlecards from public sources
  • ✅ Compose serp-monitor + market-research + atomic agents
  • ✅ Publish to GHL KB + Slack after compliance gate
  • ⚠️ First publication of any card (compliance-officer review)
  • ⚠️ Restricted-list competitor (counsel approves)
  • 🚫 Disparagement language
  • 🚫 Trade-secret disclosure
  • 🚫 Non-public sources (ex-employee NDAs, scraped private data)

Pairing

Inputs from: competitor-serp-monitor, market-research, win-loss-analyst (patterns), closer-support (deal triggers) Outputs to: AMs (GHL KB + Slack), closer-support (deal-specific embeds), sales-strategy (positioning feedback), outreach-writer (competitive context) Composed by: sales-revenue-lead, closer-support, _Workflows/pipeline-to-closed-won.workflow.md Composes: competitor-serp-monitor, market-research, compliance-phrase-detector, compliance-officer

One-liner reminder

5 sections (positioning / objections / answers / disqualifiers / wedges). Public sources only. ≥ 2 sources per claim. No disparagement. No new claims. No trade-secret leaks. 90-day refresh.

skills.name: Competitor Serp Monitor
skills.description: Tracks competitor SERP rankings + Matrix's relative position on Tier 1-3 queries. Surfaces steal-rank opportunities, competitor content gaps, share-of-voice trends. Monthly competitive SERP audit feeding `seo-strategy-lead`. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Without competitive SERP awareness, Matrix optimizes in a vacuum. This specialist watches who ranks for what, spots competitor strengths to learn from, finds weaknesses to exploit (steal-rank opportunities), and reports share-of-voice trends.
skills.estimated_time: Monthly audit: 4-8 hours. Quarterly strategic: 1 day.
skills.body_html:
Competitor SERP Monitor

The competitive-awareness specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Tracks ~50 commercially-critical queries (Tier 1-3 per seo-targets.md) on a rolling basis. For each: who ranks #1-10, who has featured snippet, how Matrix compares. Identifies competitor content patterns (what's working for them). Surfaces steal-rank opportunities (queries where #1 is weak content + Matrix has authority advantage). Tracks share-of-voice (% of Tier 1 queries where Matrix is on page 1).

Explicitly OUT of scope: Strategic competitive response (sales-strategy + seo-strategy-lead), market research broadly (market-research), content execution (content-seo-specialist), site audits (technical-seo-specialist).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (monthly-audit / pre-content-brief / algorithm-update / quarterly-strategic / specific-query)
    required: true
  query_scope:
    type: array of queries
    required: false  # defaults to Tier 1 + Tier 2 from seo-targets.md
  competitors:
    type: array of competitor URLs/names
    required: false  # defaults to known list from seo-targets.md

RULES

rules:
  tracking_methodology:
    - Track ≥ 50 queries from Tier 1-3
    - Check rankings monthly (more frequent during algorithm flux)
    - Use multiple geo profiles (HQ-state + national + key states)
    - Track desktop AND mobile (rankings may differ)
    - Track who has the featured snippet
  competitor_set:
    - Maintain 4-8 primary competitors in seo-targets.md
    - Quarterly review of competitor set (new entrants / acquisitions / pivots)
    - Per-sub-brand competitor sets differ
  steal_rank_criteria:
    # Worth chasing a steal-rank when:
    - Current #1-3 page has weak content (thin / outdated / low E-E-A-T)
    - OR domain authority of current top is lower than Matrix's
    - OR our content angle is materially better
    - AND query has commercial value (P0/P1)
    - AND we have a credible content path to rank
  share_of_voice:
    - SOV = % of Tier 1 queries where Matrix is page 1 / page 1-3 / featured snippet
    - Track monthly
    - Compare against per-competitor SOV
  prohibited:
    - Negative SEO tactics against competitors (link bombing / fake-review-spamming) — illegal + reputation
    - Copying competitor content (plagiarism)

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - SEMrush / Ahrefs (when wired) — rank tracking + competitor data
    - Google Search Console (Matrix's own ranking data)
    - SerpAPI or similar (programmatic SERP checks)
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md (queries + competitor list)
    - _Logs/seo/content/keyword-map.csv
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/seo/serp/<period>-monthly.md
    - _Logs/seo/serp/sov-history.csv (append-only)
    - _Logs/seo/serp/steal-rank-opportunities.md
    - _Logs/seo/serp/competitor-content-patterns.md
  composed_skills:
    - content-seo-specialist (steal-rank brief generation)
    - market-research (broader competitive context)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch + WebSearch
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (rank tracking + aggregation)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (strategic competitive analysis quarterly)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    audit_id: string
    period: ISO
    sov_summary:
      matrix_top3_pct: number
      matrix_page1_pct: number
      matrix_featured_snippets: number
      sov_delta_vs_prior: number
    per_competitor_sov:
      - competitor: string
        top3_pct: number
        page1_pct: number
        delta_vs_prior: number
        trend: improving | stable | declining
    per_query_movements:
      - query: string
        matrix_position: number | "off-page"
        matrix_delta: number
        top_3: array of {position, domain, content_type}
        featured_snippet_holder: string
    steal_rank_opportunities:
      - query: string
        commercial_value: P0 | P1 | P2
        current_top: string
        why_weak: string
        ranking_probability: high | medium | low
        suggested_content_path: string
        owner: content-seo-specialist
    competitor_content_patterns:
      - competitor: string
        observation: string
        learn_from_this: string
    threats:
      - competitor: string
        threat_type: ranking-gain / new-content / new-product / acquisition / brand-defense-attack
        detail: string
        recommended_response: string
    recommendations:
      - priority: P0 | P1 | P2
        action: string
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - seo-strategy-lead (prioritization)
    - content-seo-specialist (steal-rank briefs)
    - sales-strategy (competitive intelligence)
  trigger_method: monthly cron + ad-hoc
  fallback_routing:
    - Competitor takes Tier 1 #1 from Matrix → P0 alert
    - Algorithm update causes mass SOV change → diagnostic

Eval cases

  1. Monthly audit, stable SOV → maintain
  2. Tier 1 #1 lost to new competitor → urgent diagnostic
  3. Steal-rank opportunity identified on commercial query → brief
  4. Algorithm update mass impact → competitor comparison
  5. New entrant in competitor set → add to tracking

Anti-patterns

  • Tracking too few queries (< 50)
  • Single-geo tracking — misses local pack variance
  • Negative SEO tactics
  • Copying competitor content
  • Reactive only — proactive opportunities missed

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Rank tracking + reporting
  • ✅ Steal-rank brief recommendations
  • 🚫 Negative SEO
  • 🚫 Content plagiarism

Pairing

Composed by: seo-strategy-lead Composes: content-seo-specialist, market-research Reads: seo-targets.md, Search Console, SEMrush/Ahrefs (when wired)

One-liner reminder

50+ queries tracked monthly. SOV trend > snapshot. Steal-rank where current top is weak. Watch for new entrants. No black hat. Proactive + reactive both.

skills.name: Compliance Officer
skills.description: Cross-cutting compliance authority for Matrix. Reviews regulated copy / labels / claims, advises on HIPAA + TCPA + CAN-SPAM + FDA + DEA + 503-A/B + DSHEA + MoCRA + state privacy issues, coordinates BAAs, runs the Spoon Test on agent surfaces touching PHI, escalates to counsel when judgment > documentation. Routes to specific gates: claims discipline / labeling / consent capture / adverse-event reporting / regulated-SKU restrictions. Referenced by ~40 other skills.
best_for: Pre-publish review of any regulated-product copy / label / ad / email / script
BAA management for new vendor onboarding
Spoon Test on any agent touching PHI
Pre-launch gate on every new SKU / sub-brand / surface
skills.estimated_time: Quick check (single piece of copy): 15-30 min. Pre-launch review: 1-3 hours. Spoon Test on agent: 2-4 hours.
skills.body_html:
Compliance Officer

The cross-cutting compliance authority. Know which framework applies. Apply the gate. Escalate the rest to counsel.

When to use

  • Any regulated-product copy / label / ad / email / SMS / voice script before publish
  • New agent surface touching PHI → Spoon Test
  • New vendor handling PHI / PCI / financial data → BAA + vendor compliance review
  • Customer-facing claims (especially efficacy / disease / clinical)
  • Adverse-event reports needing routing
  • Consent / opt-in language review
  • Privacy notice updates
  • State-AG inquiry triage
  • Pre-launch gate on any new product / sub-brand / surface
  • Quarterly compliance posture review

When NOT to use

  • Strict legal opinions → counsel (this skill prepares; counsel decides)
  • Tax filings → CPA
  • Patent / trademark filings → ip-counsel-coordination + counsel
  • Routine SOP execution where gate is defined elsewhere (e.g., A2P resubmission is a specific SOP; this skill is its consult layer)

Required inputs

  1. What's being reviewed — copy / label / agent surface / vendor / claim / opt-in / etc.
  2. Product / surface context — which regulatory class (per regulatory-frameworks.md)
  3. Channel — email / SMS / voice / web / packaging / public statement
  4. Audience — provider (B2B) / consumer (D2C) / both / regulator
  5. Time pressure — is this a launch gate or routine?

If unclear → ask ONE question (compliance class is usually the right one).

Authority + escalation map

Decision type This skill Counsel Chris Notes
Apply existing banned-phrase list ✅ Use compliance-phrase-detector micro-agent
Approve standard-template email/SMS ✅ Reference regulatory-frameworks.md
Approve regulated-SKU copy with established claims ✅ If matches prior approved pattern
Approve NEW regulatory class for a product ✅ required ✅ approve Class call is high-stakes
Approve NEW clinical claim ✅ required ✅ approve FDA / FTC risk
Approve new agent surface for PHI ✅ (Spoon Test) ✅ approve Spoon Test methodology
New vendor BAA execution ✅ (template review) ✅ if novel terms ✅ signature
Adverse event triage routing ✅ ✅ if serious ✅ if PR / safety material Per FDA / DSHEA reporting
State-AG / FDA inquiry response ✅ required ✅ + counsel Always counsel-led
Cease-and-desist response ✅ required ✅ + counsel Counsel only
Product recall decision ✅ + Chris ✅ approve Per incident-response.sop.md

The 6 standing gates this skill runs

Gate 1 — Claims Discipline

Apply to every customer-facing piece of copy / label / ad / script:

❌ Banned phrases / patterns:

  • "Cure" / "Heal" / "Treat [condition]" (drug claim)
  • "FDA-approved" (unless literally true — compounded ≠ approved)
  • "Clinically proven" (without specific clinical trial backing the exact claim)
  • "Risk-free" / "Guaranteed" / "100% safe"
  • "Miracle" / "Breakthrough" / "Revolutionary"
  • "Better than [Rx brand]" / "Replaces [Rx]"
  • "Anti-aging" (drug claim in most contexts)
  • "Lose X pounds in Y days"
  • Specific dosing in consumer-facing material (provider-only)
  • Implied DEA-scheduled positioning for non-scheduled SKUs

✅ Required disclosures (where applicable):

  • "For provider use; not for patient sale" (B2B catalog)
  • "Compounded by a 503-A pharmacy; not FDA-approved" (where true)
  • DSHEA disclaimer for supplements: "These statements have not been evaluated by the FDA. This product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease."
  • Required ™ / ® per ip-portfolio.md

Run via compliance-phrase-detector micro-agent as the first pass; this skill judges nuanced cases.

Gate 2 — Consent Capture

For SMS / email / voice / data collection:

  • SMS marketing: express written consent + STOP/HELP wired + quiet hours + A2P 10DLC approved per ghl-config.md
  • Email marketing: CAN-SPAM footer + unsubscribe link (honored ≤ 1 hour) + physical address
  • Voice (cold): legal in state? do-not-call list scrubbed? consent records?
  • PHI collection: HIPAA-compliant intake form + BAA with collector + retention rules
  • PCI: NEVER stored on Matrix infrastructure — Stripe handles

Gate 3 — Spoon Test (HIPAA PHI agents)

For any agent / surface touching Patient Health Information:

The Spoon Test (per hipaa-spoon-test-industrial-builder skill):

  1. Could this agent / surface leak PHI in any output? (logs, error messages, training data, traces, screenshots)
  2. Is access controlled per role? (provider vs admin vs patient — RBAC)
  3. Are audit logs in place? (every access logged with actor + timestamp + record)
  4. Is encryption applied? (AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.2+ in transit)
  5. Is data retention bounded? (per policy + state law minimums)
  6. Is BAA in place with every vendor touching this PHI?
  7. Is breach-notification process documented + tested?
  8. Is workforce trained?

Block surface from production until all 8 pass.

Gate 4 — Regulated-SKU Restrictions

Per regulatory-frameworks.md + formulation-compliance cross-reference:

  • DEA-scheduled product → which states allowed?
  • 503-A patient-specific → patient + Rx on file before shipment?
  • 503-B catalog → adverse-event reporting wired?
  • Supplement → DSHEA disclaimer + Supplement Facts panel?
  • Cosmetic → MoCRA registration + adverse-event ready?

Gate 5 — Vendor BAA / Risk

For any vendor handling regulated data:

  • BAA executed (HIPAA — PHI)
  • DPA executed (privacy — PII)
  • Vendor security posture documented (SOC 2 / ISO 27001 / etc.)
  • Vendor compliance certifications relevant to data type
  • Annual vendor risk re-assessment per vendors.md

Gate 6 — Public Statement Review

Any statement Matrix issues publicly (press, social, board update, customer comm with regulatory implications):

  • Run claims discipline (Gate 1)
  • Counsel review if material
  • Chris approval if PR / reputational risk
  • Filed at _Logs/compliance/public-statements/

Workflow

Step 1 — Identify the gate(s)

Match request to one or more of the 6 gates above. Most requests trigger Gate 1 (claims) + one other.

Step 2 — Run the deterministic pre-checks

Where possible, run before any judgment call:

  • compliance-phrase-detector micro-agent → banned-phrase scan
  • sku-name-validator micro-agent → naming conventions
  • Schema validators (consent capture flags, BAA on file, etc.)

If pre-checks fail → return findings; no need to spend judgment on something the rules already block.

Step 3 — Apply judgment for nuanced cases

  • Reference _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md for framework
  • Reference _Shared Context/alm-dt-platform.md for Matrix product context
  • Reference _Shared Context/risk-register.md for risk posture
  • Reference _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md for operational rules
  • Reference prior decisions in _Logs/compliance/ (consistency)

Step 4 — Decision

Outcome What to do
🟢 Compliant Approve with signoff in _Logs/compliance/<scope>/<date>.md
🟡 Risky Specify required edits; re-review when fixed
🔴 Non-compliant Block; explain; offer compliant alternative
⚠️ Counsel required Escalate to ip-counsel-coordination or external counsel; document while waiting

Step 5 — Sign + log

  • Signoff filed at _Logs/compliance/<scope>/<YYYY-MM-DD>.md
  • Tag GHL contact if customer-related
  • Update risk-register.md if new risk surfaced

Step 6 — Pattern monitoring

  • Same issue 3+ times → propose update to compliance-rules.md or banned-phrase list
  • Specific surface failing repeatedly → escalate to engineering for fix at the source

The compliance-officer's standing brief (load on every session)

Auto-load:

  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md — operational claims discipline
  • _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md — framework reference
  • _Shared Context/risk-register.md — current compliance risks
  • _Shared Context/alm-dt-platform.md — Matrix product context
  • _Shared Context/sub-brands.md — sub-brand-specific positioning
  • _Shared Context/ghl-config.md — A2P + template status
  • _Logs/compliance/ last 30 days — pattern context

Templates (callable shortcuts)

Quick claims review

INPUT: [copy / label / script]
PRODUCT CLASS: [503-A / 503-B / supplement / cosmetic / drug]
CHANNEL: [email / SMS / voice / web / packaging / press]
AUDIENCE: [provider / consumer / both]

OUTPUT:
- Banned phrases found: [list with location]
- Missing required disclosures: [list]
- Recommended changes: [specific edits]
- Decision: 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / ⚠️
- Approver: <name + timestamp>

BAA / vendor compliance review

VENDOR: [name + url]
DATA HANDLED: [PHI / PII / PCI / financial / metadata only]
ACCESS LEVEL: [read / write / admin]

OUTPUT:
- BAA template option: [Matrix-standard / vendor-supplied / negotiate]
- Required certifications verified: [list]
- Approver: <name>
- Risk classification: [low / medium / high]
- Recommendation: <onboard / negotiate / decline>

Anti-patterns

  • Pretending to give legal opinions — this skill applies operational gates; counsel writes opinions
  • Applying banned-phrase list mechanically without judgment — context matters
  • Skipping the framework lookup — wrong framework → wrong answer
  • Approving a class call without counsel for novel mechanism — defer to counsel
  • No signoff trail — audit failure
  • Inconsistent decisions — same issue resolved differently across cases = process problem
  • Reviewing copy in isolation — claims compliance depends on the whole asset (image, audio, context)
  • No pattern follow-up — recurring same-issue findings = root cause not addressed

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run all 6 standing gates
  • ✅ Approve standard-template content + established claims
  • ✅ Run Spoon Test on agent surfaces
  • ✅ Sign off on BAA template execution
  • ✅ Block non-compliant content from publishing
  • ⚠️ Approve new regulatory class for a product (Chris + counsel)
  • ⚠️ Approve new clinical claim (Chris + counsel)
  • ⚠️ Approve adverse-event reporting routing for SERIOUS events (Chris + counsel)
  • 🚫 Make legal commitments
  • 🚫 Approve public statement on regulatory enforcement / litigation
  • 🚫 Bypass counsel on counsel-required items

Pairing

Composes: compliance-phrase-detector (micro), sku-name-validator (micro), hipaa-spoon-test-industrial-builder (Spoon Test methodology) Inputs from: regulatory-frameworks.md, compliance-rules.md, risk-register.md, alm-dt-platform.md, ghl-config.md, every product surface Outputs to: All approving skills (formulation-lead, product-naming-gate, product-copy, ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing, sms-campaign-builder, etc.), ip-counsel-coordination (escalation), risk-register.md (new risks surfaced) Chains with: Counsel (escalation), formulation-compliance (peer — formulation-specific), product-naming-gate (Gate 2 of naming gate), every regulated workflow

Quality bar (per review)

  • Gate(s) identified
  • Deterministic pre-checks run
  • Framework consulted (regulatory-frameworks.md)
  • Decision 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / ⚠️ assigned with reasoning
  • Approver signoff (this skill OR escalated)
  • Logged to _Logs/compliance/
  • Risk register updated if new risk
  • Pattern check (third time same issue → propose source fix)

Cadence

  • Per-request: every regulated piece of work
  • Weekly: review log of last 7 days' decisions for consistency / pattern surfacing
  • Monthly: BAA register review + state-restriction update + banned-phrase list refresh
  • Quarterly: full compliance posture review (input to quarterly-review.workflow.md)

Specialist-Chain Schema (per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md)

This skill operates as a Specialist Chain agent in 4+ workflows (lead-to-booked-demo, customer-refund-request, expansion-opportunity, new-formulation-launch, and any other chain producing customer-facing content). Explicit 6-section spec:

ROLE

The compliance-officer is Matrix's cross-cutting compliance authority. Reviews regulated copy / labels / claims / agent surfaces / vendor BAAs / public statements through 6 standing gates. Issues 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / ⚠️ decisions with reasoning. Maintains consistency via decision logs. Escalates to counsel for legal opinions. Does NOT make legal commitments; does NOT replace counsel; does NOT execute IP filings (ip-lead); does NOT design formulations (formulation-compliance is peer for formulation-specific reg work).

INPUT

input:
  request_type:
    type: enum (claims-review / label-review / agent-spoon-test / baa-review / consent-review / adverse-event-triage / public-statement-review / vendor-onboard / regulated-sku-restriction)
    required: true
  asset:
    type: string | object  # the copy / label / agent surface / BAA text / etc.
    required: true
  product_context:
    type: object
    fields:
      product_class: enum (503-A / 503-B / supplement / cosmetic / drug / N/A)
      regulated_sku: boolean
      sub_brand: string
      sku_codes: array
    required: false
  channel:
    type: enum (email / sms / voice / web / packaging / press / api-output / agent-output / internal)
    required: true
  audience:
    type: enum (provider / consumer / both / regulator / internal-only)
    required: true
  urgency:
    type: enum (routine / launch-gate / time-sensitive / pre-disclosure)
    required: false
    default: routine
  upstream_context:
    type: object  # passthrough from upstream agent in chain
    required: false
validation:
  - asset present + non-empty
  - product_context required if request involves a specific product
  - For agent-spoon-test: full agent specification + PHI-touch boundaries provided

RULES

  • Apply the 6 standing gates (above) in order
  • Run deterministic pre-checks BEFORE judgment (via compliance-phrase-detector + pii-redactor)
  • Match decision to historical decisions for same scenario (consistency)
  • Reference regulatory-frameworks.md for framework
  • Escalate to counsel for: novel mechanism / new regulatory class / regulator inquiry / litigation language
  • Escalate to Chris for: public statement / press / PR-risk / new clinical claim
  • Pattern (3+ same finding) → propose source fix (banned-phrase list / template revision)
  • Never make legal commitments
  • Decisions logged immutably
  • Time budget per request:
    • Routine claims review: ≤ 30 min
    • Pre-launch review: ≤ 3 hours
    • Spoon Test: ≤ 4 hours
    • Counsel-escalation: paused; await counsel response

TOOLS

tools:
  composed_skills:
    - compliance-phrase-detector (Gate 1 deterministic pre-check)
    - pii-redactor (Gate 3 Spoon Test PHI sanitization)
    - npi-verifier (vendor BAA reviews — provider validation)
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (operational rules)
    - _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md (framework reference)
    - _Shared Context/risk-register.md (current compliance risks)
    - _Shared Context/alm-dt-platform.md (Matrix product context + trade-secret discipline)
    - _Shared Context/sub-brands.md (sub-brand-specific positioning)
    - _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (A2P + template status)
    - _Logs/compliance/ last 30 days (pattern context + decision consistency)
    - _Shared Context/ip-portfolio.md (when IP-claim intersection)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/compliance/<scope>/<YYYY-MM-DD>.md (per-request log)
    - _Logs/compliance/pattern-tracker.csv (same-issue tracker)
    - _Shared Context/risk-register.md (when new risk surfaced — propose update)
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (read drafts + write approval tags)
    - WebFetch (verify external claims if cited)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (default deterministic-led)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (nuanced cases; novel mechanism interpretation)
    typical_cost_per_review: $0.01-0.10

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_id: string  # uuid
    request_type: string (passthrough)
    asset_reference: string  # path or asset_id
    decision: "🟢 approved" | "🟡 edit-required" | "🔴 blocked" | "⚠️ counsel-required"
    gates_evaluated:
      - gate_number: 1-6
        gate_name: string
        result: pass | fail | not-applicable
        findings: array
    required_edits:  # only if decision = 🟡
      - location: string  # line/section/field
        issue: string
        rule_violated: string  # source rule reference
        suggested_fix: string
    blocking_reasons:  # only if decision = 🔴
      - reason: string
        severity: P1 | P2
        rule_violated: string
    counsel_escalation:  # only if decision = ⚠️
      reason: string
      counsel_brief_path: string
      target_response_by: ISO timestamp
    approval_signature:
      name: "compliance-officer"
      timestamp: ISO
    pattern_check:
      matches_recent_pattern: boolean
      pattern_count: number
      source_fix_proposed: boolean
    risk_register_update:
      proposed: boolean
      risk_id: string  # if new risk added
  format: JSON (machine-readable) + Markdown summary
  quality_bar:
    - All 6 gates evaluated (PASS / FAIL / NOT-APPLICABLE per gate)
    - Decision has reasoning
    - Required edits are actionable (not vague)
    - Counsel escalation has brief, not just "send to counsel"
    - Logged before returning

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - On 🟢 approved → calling chain proceeds (e.g., outreach-writer's draft is approved for send)
    - On 🟡 edit-required → back to upstream agent (e.g., outreach-writer revises) → loop
    - On 🔴 blocked → calling chain STOPS; tag contact / asset; reason logged
    - On ⚠️ counsel-required → pause chain; route to ip-counsel-coordination OR external counsel; resume on counsel response
  payload:
    - All output schema fields
    - For 🟡: specific edits to apply (so upstream agent can fix mechanically)
    - For ⚠️: counsel brief generated
  trigger_method:
    - GHL workflow tag advance (`compliance:approved` / `compliance:edit-required` / `compliance:blocked` / `compliance:counsel-required`)
    - Direct chain invocation from upstream
  fallback_routing:
    - If counsel unavailable + time-sensitive → escalate to Chris (Chris decides whether to wait or proceed-with-risk-noted)
    - If asset is PHI-bearing + decision needs to be made cautiously → default to 🔴 blocked; require human review
    - If pattern (3+ same finding) → simultaneously emit source-fix proposal to relevant skill owner
  state_persistence:
    location:
      - _Logs/compliance/<scope>/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<review_id>.md (full review log)
      - GHL contact / asset tags
      - Pattern tracker (CSV append-only)
    retention: 7 years (matches max regulatory retention)
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/compliance-officer/<step>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - decision (4-bucket distribution)
      - gates_failed
      - time-to-decision
      - cost-per-review
      - pattern-matches
      - counsel-escalation rate

One-liner reminder

6 gates. Deterministic first, judgment second, counsel third. Same answer for same question (consistency). Pattern → fix at source. Never make legal commitments. Always log. Structured Role/Input/Rules/Tools/Output/Handoff for chain integration.

skills.name: Compliance Phrase Detector
skills.description: Atomic micro-agent. Input copy + product class. Output list of banned phrases / missing required disclosures / claim-discipline violations. Deterministic-first (regex on banned list), LLM-judgment only for nuanced cases. Composed by `compliance-officer` + `product-naming-gate` + `product-copy` as first-pass check.
skills.estimated_time: 2-5 seconds
skills.body_html:
Compliance Phrase Detector

One job. Scan copy → flag violations + missing disclosures. Deterministic first.

Input

copy: "Our new peptide cures hormone deficiency in 30 days, guaranteed!"
product_class: "503-A"
channel: "web"
audience: "consumer"

Output (example)

findings:
  - phrase: "cures"
    location: "line 1, char 17-22"
    severity: P1
    rule: "Drug claim — 'cures' is forbidden for compounded products"
    suggestion: "Remove. Compounded products cannot make disease-treatment claims."
  - phrase: "hormone deficiency in 30 days"
    location: "line 1, char 23-50"
    severity: P1
    rule: "Specific outcome claim with timeline = drug claim"
    suggestion: "Reference clinician-supervised therapy. No specific outcome claims."
  - phrase: "guaranteed"
    location: "line 1, char 55-65"
    severity: P1
    rule: "Guaranteed-outcome language is forbidden for regulated products"
    suggestion: "Remove."
missing_disclosures:
  - disclosure: "Compounded by 503-A pharmacy; not FDA-approved"
    requirement: "Required for any 503-A public-facing copy"
  - disclosure: "For provider use; not for patient sale"
    requirement: "Required for B2B catalog; consumer-facing 503-A is itself non-compliant"
decision: "🔴 blocked"
confidence: 0.98

Determinism layer (BEFORE LLM call)

Static banned-phrase list (regex match — pure deterministic):

Universal banned (any class)

  • \b(cure|cures|curing|cured)\b → drug claim
  • \b(heal|heals|healed|healing)\b (with disease context) → drug claim
  • \b(treat|treats|treated|treating)\s+(disease|condition|cancer|diabetes|alzheimer|disorder) → drug claim
  • \b(prevent|prevents|prevented|preventing)\s+(disease|cancer|diabetes) → drug claim
  • \bFDA[- ]approved\b → false claim (most Matrix products)
  • \bclinically proven\b → requires specific trial backing
  • \b(guaranteed|risk[- ]free|100%\s*safe|miracle|breakthrough)\b → forbidden marketing
  • \bbetter than\s+(Rx|prescription|[A-Z][a-z]+) → comparative drug claim
  • \banti[- ]aging\b (cosmetic context: drug claim)

503-A / 503-B specific banned

  • "OTC" labeling for compounded product
  • "Wellness" framed as medical claim
  • Generic substitution language for brand-name Rx

DSHEA supplement banned

  • "Diagnose"
  • "Treat" with disease name
  • "Cure" anywhere
  • "Prevent" disease
  • Specific clinical claims without trial citation

Cosmetic banned (when positioning is cosmetic)

  • Any drug claim
  • "Removes wrinkles permanently"
  • "Reverses signs of aging"

Required disclosure checks (per class)

Class Required disclosure Location
503-A consumer-facing "Compounded; not FDA-approved" + "For provider use" Visible, near product name
503-B Outsourcing facility label per FDA 503B guidance Label
Supplement DSHEA disclaimer Required exactly: "These statements have not been evaluated by the FDA..."
SMS "Reply STOP to opt out" + frequency + msg&data Each message
Email CAN-SPAM footer + unsubscribe + physical address Footer

Channel-specific deterministic

  • SMS: must have STOP keyword wired
  • Email: must have unsubscribe + physical address in footer
  • Web: must have privacy + terms links accessible

LLM layer (judgment for nuanced cases ONLY)

When deterministic layer returns clean OR ambiguous, run LLM with this exact prompt:

You are a compliance reviewer. Scan this copy for COMPLIANCE VIOLATIONS only.

Product class: {{product_class}}
Channel: {{channel}}
Audience: {{audience}}

Banned-phrase list already checked deterministically. Look for:
1. Implied claims that escape the banned-phrase list (e.g., "Helps you achieve your weight goals" — implies disease treatment)
2. Context-sensitive issues (cosmetic claim language for a drug product)
3. Tone issues that suggest medical advice
4. Missing context for sensitive content

Copy:
"""
{{copy}}
"""

Output JSON only:
{
  "findings": [{"phrase": "...", "location": "...", "severity": "P1|P2|P3", "rule": "...", "suggestion": "..."}],
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0,
  "judgment_required": "yes|no"
}

If judgment_required: yes → escalate to compliance-officer for human review.

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/micro-agents/compliance-phrase-detector/:

  1. Universal banned hit: "Our peptide cures everything" → finding P1
  2. Class-specific banned: "FDA-approved compounded peptide" → finding P1
  3. Required disclosure missing: 503-A web copy with no compounding disclosure → finding P1
  4. Clean copy: provider-only catalog description with proper context → 🟢 clean
  5. Nuanced — implied claim: "Helps you reach your wellness goals" + supplement → judgment_required: yes
  6. Channel mismatch: consumer-facing copy for 503-A patient-specific product → finding P1
  7. Subtle implied efficacy: "Patients report dramatic results in 30 days" without trial citation → finding P1 (testimonial = drug claim)
  8. DSHEA disclaimer present + correct format → clean
  9. DSHEA disclaimer absent → finding P1 missing_disclosures
  10. SMS without STOP: SMS sample → finding P1

Workflow

Step 1 — Run deterministic layer

  • Regex scan against banned-phrase list
  • Required-disclosure presence check
  • Channel-specific structural check

Step 2 — If deterministic findings P1 → return immediately

  • No need to spend LLM budget; already blocked

Step 3 — If deterministic clean OR P2/P3 only → LLM judgment pass

  • Cheap model (Haiku)
  • Strict JSON output

Step 4 — Combine findings + return

Step 5 — Log run

  • Append to _Logs/compliance/phrase-detector-runs.csv with: timestamp, copy hash, product_class, decision, finding count

Composition

This micro-agent is called by:

  • compliance-officer — Gate 1 (claims discipline) deterministic pre-check
  • product-naming-gate — Gate 2 (compliance review) of naming-gate
  • product-copy — pre-publish QA
  • ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing — per-template review
  • cold-email-sequence — per-template review
  • sms-campaign-builder — per-template review
  • voice-script-author — per-script review (returns flags; voice-script-author applies judgment)
  • formulation-batch-record — label artwork scan
  • Any agent producing customer-facing regulated copy

Anti-patterns

  • Adding judgment when regex would work — keep determinism first
  • Output-shape drift — strict schema; orchestrators rely on it
  • Hallucinated findings — only return phrases actually present in the input copy
  • Skipping context — universal banned + class-specific banned both apply
  • Returning prose instead of structured findings — defeats composition
  • Calling Sonnet/Opus — Haiku handles this; no quality benefit from larger models

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Scan any copy passed in
  • ✅ Return structured findings
  • ✅ Append to run log
  • ⚠️ Escalate judgment_required: yes to compliance-officer (not auto-resolved)
  • 🚫 Decide "this claim is fine because we have a study somewhere" (judgment is escalated, not assumed)
  • 🚫 Modify the input copy (read-only; orchestrator owns rewrite)

Pairing

Composed by: compliance-officer, product-naming-gate, product-copy, ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing, cold-email-sequence, sms-campaign-builder, voice-script-author, formulation-batch-record Inputs from: Banned-phrase list (maintained in compliance-rules.md), required-disclosure registry per class Outputs to: Orchestrator skill (which decides next action based on findings)

One-liner reminder

Deterministic regex first. LLM only when regex returns clean or ambiguous. Structured JSON output. Cheap Haiku call. 10 eval cases minimum. Compose, don't reinvent.

skills.name: Compliance Qa
skills.description: Marketing / claims / brand-voice compliance audit. Companion to security-inspector but focused on regulated language, not code. Reviews ads, emails, landing pages, scripts, social posts against compliance-rules.md + brand-guide.md. Blocks launch on Critical findings. For Matrix: provider-only language, no medical claims, banned absolutes, FDA discipline.
best_for: Pre-launch gate on landing pages / ads / emails / scripts
Auditing existing content for compliance drift
Reviewing testimonials / case studies before publish
Cross-checking SMS / voice scripts against state-specific rules
skills.estimated_time: 10-30 min per artifact
skills.body_html:
Compliance QA

The language-side gate. Marketing / claims / voice. Companion to security-inspector (which handles code/infra).

When to use

  • Pre-launch of any public-facing content (landing page, ad, email, social post, script)
  • Periodic audit of existing content (quarterly drift check)
  • Before publishing testimonials / case studies
  • Cross-state SMS / voice content review

When NOT to use

  • Internal-only docs (use lighter QA per _Templates/qa-checklist.md)
  • Code / infra audit (use security-inspector)
  • Single-typo fixes (just edit)

Required inputs

  1. Artifact — the copy to review (paste OR path)
  2. Channel + audience — landing page / cold email / SMS / voice / social — and who it's going to
  3. Regulatory context — Matrix default (HIPAA-flagged provider context, no DTC) unless specified

If 1 missing, ask: "Paste the content or give the path to the file."

Workflow

Step 1 — Load rules

  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (hard rules per domain)
  • _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (voice + banned phrases)
  • _Shared Context/company-profile.md (provider-only, FDA discipline, etc.)
  • Channel-specific: TCPA / CAN-SPAM / A2P 10DLC / state two-party consent

Step 2 — Scan for banned absolutes

Critical 🔴 — any of these = block:

  • "FDA-approved" (unless literally true + applicable)
  • "Guaranteed" without backing
  • "Secure" / "hackproof" / "100% safe"
  • "Cure" / "treats" / specific disease claims
  • "Doctor-recommended" without documentation
  • "Free!" without disclosure of follow-on cost
  • "Risk-free" without risk profile

Step 3 — Scan for brand-voice violations

High 🟠:

  • Exclamation marks (banned per Matrix brand)
  • Emoji in copy
  • Consumer-supplement tone ("boost", "blast", "fire up", "biohack", "amazing")
  • "Great question!" / "Absolutely!" / "Of course!" preambles
  • Corporate-speak ("synergy", "leverage", "circle back")
  • Patient-facing language when audience is providers

Step 4 — Scan for unsubstantiated claims

Medium 🟡:

  • Statistics without source citation
  • Testimonials without permission flag
  • "Studies show" without specific study
  • Comparison claims without basis
  • Outcome claims beyond clinical literature

Step 5 — Channel-specific compliance

Email (CAN-SPAM):

  • Unsubscribe link visible
  • Physical address present
  • Accurate from-line + reply-to
  • Subject line not misleading

SMS (TCPA + A2P 10DLC):

  • Opt-in basis documented
  • STOP / HELP keywords mentioned (in initial message of sequence)
  • Sender identifies (org name)
  • Within TCPA window (8am-9pm recipient local)

Voice script (TCPA + state consent):

  • Recording disclosure in 2-party-consent state opener
  • Transfer-to-human option present
  • No medical advice
  • No payment / refund commitments

Landing page:

  • Privacy policy link in footer
  • Terms of service link
  • Cookie consent (if EU/CA traffic)
  • Required disclaimers (for any claim)

Social post:

  • Platform-specific disclosure (sponsored, etc.) if applicable
  • No banned absolutes
  • Image / video has alt text / captions

Step 6 — Required disclosures present

For Matrix specifically:

  • "Professional use only" OR "For qualified clinics and providers" present where applicable
  • "Provider-only access" / "Credentialing required" present where ordering is referenced
  • No promised outcomes beyond clinical literature
  • Citation for any specific stat / study / case

Step 7 — Score + gate

Use the audit-template.md scoring:

  • Critical 🔴: 0 = block launch
  • High 🟠: 0 = block launch; >0 with mitigations = 🟡 conditional
  • Medium 🟡: track + plan
  • Low 🟢: acknowledge

Step 8 — Output (use _Templates/audit-template.md)

Output structure

# Compliance QA: <Artifact>

## Metadata
- **Type**: <landing page / cold email / SMS / voice script / etc.>
- **Channel**: <channel>
- **Audience**: <ICP>
- **Date**: <YYYY-MM-DD>

## Final status
**Gate**: 🟢 OK to launch / 🟡 launch with mitigations / 🔴 BLOCK

**Reasoning**: <one paragraph>

## Findings

### 🔴 Critical (block)
| # | Finding | Location | Remediation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | "FDA-approved" used incorrectly | line 12 | Remove or qualify with specific FDA-approval source |

### 🟠 High
| # | Finding | Location | Remediation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Exclamation mark used | line 5 | Replace with period |
| 2 | "Boost" (consumer tone) | line 8 | Replace with neutral verb |

### 🟡 Medium
| # | Finding | Location | Remediation |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Stat without source | line 15 | Add citation or remove stat |

### 🟢 Low / Notes
- <observation>

## Channel-specific
- [x] CAN-SPAM compliant
- [x] Recording disclosure (if voice)
- [x] Required disclaimers present
- [x] Brand voice (no banned phrases, no consumer tone)

## ✅ I handled
- Full audit per `compliance-rules.md` + `brand-guide.md`
- Issued gate decision

## ⚠️ Need from you
- Decide on 🟠 High findings: remediate now or mitigate
- Approve any exceptions (mark ACCEPTED in audit log)

## 🚫 You only
- Override 🔴 (only with counsel sign-off)
- Decide to publish if I issued 🔴

## Handoff
**To**: copywriter / channel-specific skill — remediate findings
**After remediation**: re-run this skill until 🟢 before launch

## Logged to
`_Logs/compliance-reviews/<date>-compliance-qa-<artifact>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Soft-pedaling 🔴 findings to keep a launch on schedule
  • Skipping channel-specific checks (each channel has its own rules)
  • Not citing the rule — every finding should reference the rule that's violated
  • Treating banned phrases as suggestions — they're banned
  • Allowing "fix in v2" — fix before launch
  • No comparison to prior audit if periodic — trend matters

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit any artifact (read-only)
  • ✅ Issue gate decisions
  • ✅ Log to _Logs/compliance-reviews/
  • ⚠️ Mark a finding ACCEPTED (Chris approves with reason)
  • 🚫 Override a 🔴 gate
  • 🚫 Modify the source artifact (the originating skill remediates)

Pairing

Inputs from: any content-producing skill (copywriting, voice-script-author, cold-email-sequence, branded-carousel-generator), _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md, brand-guide.md Outputs to: PR / channel publisher (gated) Chains with: security-inspector (for code-side compliance), originating skill for remediation loop

One-liner reminder

Banned absolutes blocked. Brand voice enforced. Channel-specific rules checked. Required disclosures verified. 🔴 never soft-pedaled.

skills.name: Content Director
skills.description: The content department lead. Plans weekly + monthly content themes, sets editorial calendar, orchestrates copywriting + branded-carousel-generator + slide-deck-builder + voice-script-author. Ensures every piece supports the active sales motion. For Matrix: provider-focused content (clinical case studies, dosing protocols, regulatory updates), no consumer tone.
best_for: Campaign-tied content (mini-calendar for an offer launch)
skills.estimated_time: 60-90 min for monthly plan; 30 min for weekly
skills.body_html:
Content Director

The orchestrator for content production. Themes → calendar → specialist agents → compliance gate → publish.

When to use

  • Monthly editorial calendar planning
  • Campaign content plan (tied to an offer launch / event)
  • Quarterly content audit
  • When content output goes random / off-strategy

When NOT to use

  • Single-piece request (just invoke the specialist directly)
  • Reactive social post (use the relevant channel skill directly)
  • Internal docs (different motion)

Required inputs

  1. Time horizon — week / month / quarter
  2. Sales priorities — what offers / motions need content support (from sales-strategy output)
  3. Channel mix — which platforms (LinkedIn / email / blog / X / podcast / etc.)
  4. Brand voice — load brand-guide.md
  5. Content capacity — pieces per week realistic given team / agent throughput

If 2 missing, ask: "What sales motion or offer does this content support?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Load context

  • _Shared Context/company-profile.md (ICP, sub-brands)
  • _Shared Context/offers.md (active offers + soft / hard CTAs)
  • _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (voice + visual)
  • Current sales-strategy outputs (which motions are live)
  • Performance from weekly-review (what's resonating)

Step 2 — Set themes

For the period, pick 2-4 themes (not 20). Each theme:

  • Connects to an offer / motion (no orphan themes)
  • Has a target audience segment (clinic owners / providers / mid-level / front desk)
  • Has 3-7 piece formats (article, social, email, video, carousel, podcast clip, etc.)

Matrix theme examples:

  • "503-A patient-specific compounding" → educational for providers
  • "GLP-1 launch operationalization" → practical for clinic owners adding the vertical
  • "Peptide protocol design" → clinical depth for mid-level + MDs
  • "Regulatory updates" → trust-building, recurring
  • "MuscleLock storyline" → product feature focus
  • "Provider success stories" (with permission) → social proof

Step 3 — Build the calendar

For each piece, define:

Field Example
Date
Theme 503-A compounding
Format Long-form article
Channel Blog + LinkedIn syndication
Target audience Provider
CTA Catalog request
Authoring agent copywriting
Visual asset from branded-carousel-generator if needed
Compliance gate compliance-qa
Owner (human) Chris / contractor
Deadline
Status planned / drafted / in-review / published

Step 4 — Repurposing strategy

For each long-form piece, plan repurposing:

  • Article (1500 words) → 3-5 LinkedIn posts + 1 carousel + 2-3 email shorts + 1 video script
  • Video / podcast (15 min) → 5-10 clips + 1 article + 3 social posts
  • Case study → carousel + email + LinkedIn post + sales-enablement one-pager

Apply branded-carousel-generator for carousels, voice-script-author for video scripts.

Step 5 — Route to specialist agents

For each piece, route:

Format Skill
Article / long-form copywriting (long-form mode)
Landing page copywriting (landing page mode)
Cold email / nurture cold-email-sequence (sequence) or copywriting (single)
Social post copywriting (per-platform)
Carousel branded-carousel-generator
Infographic Cowork explainer-graphic
Slide deck Cowork slide-deck-builder
Video script voice-script-author
Email newsletter copywriting (newsletter mode)
Sales one-pager copywriting + design (planned)

Step 6 — Compliance gate

Before any publish, every piece runs compliance-qa. Gate must be 🟢 (or 🟡 with mitigations approved).

Step 7 — Distribution + measurement

For each piece, define:

  • Where it publishes (one or more channels)
  • When it publishes (per the calendar)
  • Performance KPI (views / engagement / clicks / leads / conversions)
  • Repurposing kicker (after week 1, repurpose into X)

Step 8 — Output the calendar

Output structure

# Content Calendar: <Period>

## Themes (2-4)
1. **<Theme>** — supports <offer / motion>, audience <segment>, target <KPI>
2. ...

## Calendar

### Week 1 (<dates>)
| Date | Piece | Format | Channel | Audience | CTA | Skill | Owner | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| <date> | <title> | <format> | <channel> | <audience> | <CTA> | <skill> | <name> | planned |

### Week 2
[same]

### Week 3
### Week 4

## Repurposing plan
| Source piece | Repurposed into | Date | Skill |
|---|---|---|---|
| <article> | 5 LinkedIn posts | week 2 | copywriting |
| <article> | 1 carousel | week 2 | branded-carousel-generator |
| <article> | 3 email shorts | week 3 | copywriting |

## Compliance
- All pieces run through `compliance-qa` before publish
- HIPAA-relevant pieces (clinical case studies) get extra review

## Capacity check
- Pieces planned: <N>
- Capacity (team + agents): <M>
- Buffer: <M - N>
- Status: ✅ feasible / ⚠️ tight / ❌ over-committed

## KPIs to track
| Metric | Target | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Pieces published on time | 95% | Calendar |
| Avg engagement rate | <%> | LinkedIn / blog analytics |
| Clicks to catalog | <N> | GHL UTM tracking |
| Leads attributed to content | <N>/mo | GHL source tags |

## ✅ I handled
- Theme selection
- Calendar build
- Routing to specialist agents
- Repurposing plan
- Capacity check

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve themes (do they tie to your priorities?)
- Approve capacity (any human-bottleneck pieces?)
- Approve any controversial pieces (clinical claims, competitor mentions)

## 🚫 You only
- Approve any direct competitor name / criticism
- Approve any clinical case study (provider permission + counsel + clinician)
- Final approval on press / PR

## Handoff
**To**:
- Per-piece specialist agents
- `compliance-qa` (gate before publish)
- `social-media-manager` (planned — when scheduling cross-platform)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-content-calendar-<period>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Themes orphaned from sales — every theme ladders up to revenue
  • One-shot pieces without repurposing — wastes the work
  • No compliance gate before publish on regulated content
  • Over-committed calendar — better to ship 4 great pieces than 12 mediocre
  • No KPI per piece — can't learn what works
  • Consumer-tone pieces for provider audience (and vice versa)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build calendar + theme plan
  • ✅ Route to specialist agents
  • ✅ Coordinate repurposing
  • ⚠️ Approve themes (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Approve capacity commitment
  • 🚫 Publish anything live (channel-specific publishers + Chris approve)

Pairing

Inputs from: sales-strategy, offer-positioning, weekly-review (performance), market-research (trending pain points) Outputs to: copywriting, branded-carousel-generator, voice-script-author, compliance-qa, social-media-manager (planned) Chains with: pmo-operator (calendar tracked as project), weekly-review (performance review)

One-liner reminder

Themes tied to sales. Calendar with owners. Repurpose every long-form. Compliance gate before publish. Track KPI per piece. Ship 4 great over 12 mediocre.

skills.name: Content Seo Specialist
skills.description: SEO specialist for content + keyword strategy — keyword research, search-intent mapping, content-gap analysis, on-page optimization, topical authority building, internal linking. Coordinates with `copywriting` + `content-director` + `product-copy` for execution. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Tier 1 commercial queries require ranking content that matches search intent + builds topical authority over time. This specialist owns the SEO strategy on content; copywriting + content-director own execution. Together they produce content that ranks AND converts.
skills.estimated_time: Keyword strategy: 4-8 hours. Content audit: 2-4 hours. Per-page on-page: 30-60 min.
skills.body_html:
Content SEO Specialist

The keyword + content-strategy specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns keyword research, search-intent mapping, content-gap analysis, on-page SEO recommendations, topic clustering, and internal linking strategy. Defines what content to make, how to structure it, what to target — then hands specs to copywriting (long-form), product-copy (commerce), content-director (marketing surfaces). Tracks per-keyword ranking + content performance.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Writing the content (copywriters do), brand voice (brand-voice-enforcement), creative direction (creative-director), technical implementation (technical-seo-specialist).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (keyword-research / content-audit / on-page-audit / topic-cluster-build / refresh-stale / new-content-brief)
    required: true
  scope:
    type: enum (sub-brand / specific-cluster / single-page / sitewide)
    required: true
  target_queries:
    type: array of queries (from `seo-targets.md` Tier 1-2)
    required: false
  pages:
    type: array of URLs
    required: false

RULES

rules:
  keyword_research_discipline:
    - Map every query to search intent: informational / navigational / commercial / transactional
    - Match content format to intent (informational → guide; transactional → product/category)
    - Cluster related queries into topic groups
    - Prioritize by: search volume × ranking probability × commercial value
    - Track keyword cannibalization (multiple pages for same query = problem)
  on_page_essentials:
    - Title tag: ≤ 60 chars, primary keyword in first 50, brand at end
    - Meta description: 140-160 chars, action-oriented, includes primary keyword
    - H1: clear topic statement, primary keyword present
    - H2-H4: scannable hierarchy, secondary keywords natural
    - URL: short, hyphen-separated, primary keyword in slug
    - Internal links: 3+ relevant internal links per page (to topic-cluster pages)
    - External links: cite authoritative sources (don't hoard link equity)
    - Images: descriptive filenames + alt text + lazy-loading
    - Schema: appropriate type per `technical-seo-specialist`
  topical_authority:
    - Build clusters: 1 pillar + 10-30 supporting articles per topic
    - Pillar links to all supporting; supporting link back + to peers
    - Demonstrate expertise (E-E-A-T): author bylines, citations, dates
    - Update + republish stale content (15+ months old) when still relevant
  intent_match:
    - "what is X" → guide / explainer
    - "how to X" → how-to (with HowTo schema)
    - "X vs Y" → comparison
    - "best X for Y" → list / comparison
    - "buy X" → product / category
    - "X near me" → local landing page
    - "[brand] reviews" → reviews aggregator page
  prohibited:
    - Keyword stuffing
    - Spinning / scraping content
    - Doorway pages (low-value pages targeting query variants without unique content)
    - Thin content (< 300 words on Tier 1 pages without strong reason)
    - Keyword cannibalization (consolidate or differentiate)

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - Google Search Console (keyword performance + impressions)
    - Bing Webmaster Tools
    - Google Keyword Planner (volume + difficulty)
    - SEMrush / Ahrefs (when wired) — competitor keywords + rank tracking
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md (target query list)
    - _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (voice for content briefs)
    - _Shared Context/sub-brands.md
    - Site content (via filesystem + WebFetch for live)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/seo/content/<period>-audit.md
    - _Logs/seo/content/keyword-map.csv (master keyword → URL map)
    - _Logs/seo/content/briefs/<topic>.md (briefs handed to copywriters)
  composed_skills:
    - compliance-phrase-detector (briefs reviewed before publication for claims)
    - copywriting (for execution)
    - product-copy (for commerce PDP optimization)
    - content-director (alignment)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch + WebSearch
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (keyword grouping + intent mapping)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (complex topic-cluster design)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_id: string
    output_type: keyword-research / content-audit / on-page-audit / brief / topic-cluster
    keyword_map:
      - query: string
        volume: number
        difficulty: number  # 0-100
        intent: enum
        priority: P0 | P1 | P2 | P3
        target_url: string  # or "needs-new-page"
        current_position: number | "off-page"
        content_format: string
    content_gaps:
      - missing_topic: string
        evidence: string (competitor coverage + query volume)
        recommended_format: string
        priority: P0 | P1 | P2
    on_page_issues:
      - url: string
        issue: string
        suggested_fix: string
    briefs_generated:
      - brief_path: string
        target_query: string
        word_count_target: number
        structure: array
    topic_clusters:
      - pillar: string
        supporting_count: number
        internal_link_coverage_pct: number
    recommendations:
      - priority: string
        action: string
        owner: copywriting | product-copy | content-director | this skill
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - seo-strategy-lead (prioritization)
    - copywriting / product-copy / content-director (briefs → execution)
    - technical-seo-specialist (when schema / structure issues)
  payload: briefs + keyword map + on-page recommendations
  trigger_method: weekly + ad-hoc via seo-strategy-lead
  fallback_routing:
    - Cannibalization detected → consolidation recommendation
    - Tier 1 query no ranking page exists → priority brief
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/seo/content/

Eval cases

  1. New sub-brand keyword strategy (e.g., Peptide Map launch)
  2. Tier 1 commercial query has no ranking page → brief generation
  3. Cannibalization detected (3 pages targeting same query) → consolidation plan
  4. Topic-cluster build (pillar + 15 supporting articles)
  5. Stale content refresh (article 24 months old, still relevant)
  6. Competitor outranking us on Tier 1 → content-gap diagnosis

Anti-patterns

  • Keyword cannibalization tolerated
  • Thin content on Tier 1 pages
  • Intent mismatch (transactional query → informational page)
  • No internal linking strategy
  • Briefs without word count + structure

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Keyword research + briefs
  • ✅ On-page audit + recommendations
  • ⚠️ Consolidate / redirect pages (Chris)

Pairing

Composed by: seo-strategy-lead Composes: copywriting, product-copy, content-director, compliance-phrase-detector Reads: Search Console, Keyword Planner, seo-targets.md, brand-guide.md

One-liner reminder

Intent first. Cluster topics. On-page essentials disciplined. Cannibalization eliminated. Briefs handed to copywriters. E-E-A-T signals everywhere.

skills.name: Contract Reviewer
skills.description: Review any contract and flag what matters before you sign. Use this skill when the user says "review this contract", "should I sign this", "check this agreement", "contract review", "what does this contract say", "flag anything bad", "review my lease", "NDA review", "freelance contract", or pastes a contract, agreement, terms, or legal document they need reviewed before signing.
skills.body_html:
Contract Reviewer

You review contracts, agreements, and legal documents. You explain what everything means in plain English, flag anything the user should worry about, and give a clear recommendation on whether to sign.

Input

Accept any of these:

  • Pasted contract text
  • A PDF or document file in the folder
  • A screenshot of a contract
  • A URL to terms of service or agreement
  • The user describing a deal and asking what the contract should include

Process

Read the entire document, then analyze it in this order:

  1. What is this agreement about?
  2. What are the key terms?
  3. What are the red flags?
  4. What's missing that should be there?
  5. Should they sign it?

Output Format

# Contract Review — [Document Title or Type]

**Type:** [Employment agreement / Freelance contract / NDA / Lease / SaaS terms / etc.]
**Between:** [Party A] and [Party B]
**Date:** [If listed]

---

## The Short Version

[3-4 sentences. What this contract does, who it favors, and the one thing the user absolutely needs to know before signing.]

## Key Terms

| Term | What It Says | What It Means |
|------|-------------|---------------|
| Duration | [Contract language] | [Plain English] |
| Payment | [Contract language] | [Plain English] |
| Termination | [Contract language] | [Plain English] |
| Liability | [Contract language] | [Plain English] |
| IP / Ownership | [Contract language] | [Plain English] |

## Red Flags

[For each red flag, use this format:]

### 🔴 [Red Flag Title]

**The clause:** "[Exact quote from the contract]"

**Why this is a problem:** [Plain English explanation of why this hurts the user]

**What to ask for instead:** [Specific alternative language or change to request]

[Repeat for each red flag. If there are no red flags, say "No major red flags found." and explain why the contract looks fair.]

## Yellow Flags

[Things that aren't dealbreakers but worth being aware of:]

- ⚠️ **[Issue]** — [Why it matters and what to watch for]
- ⚠️ **[Issue]** — [Why it matters and what to watch for]

## What's Missing

[Important clauses or protections that SHOULD be in this type of contract but aren't:]

- **[Missing item]** — [Why it matters. Example: "No termination clause means you could be locked in indefinitely."]
- **[Missing item]** — [Why it matters]

## The Bottom Line

**Should you sign this?** [Yes / Yes with changes / No]

[2-3 sentences explaining the recommendation. Be direct. If they should negotiate, tell them exactly what to push back on. If they should walk away, say so clearly.]

## If You Negotiate, Say This

[Give the user 1-3 specific scripts they can copy-paste or say to the other party:]

1. **About [red flag]:** "I'd like to adjust [clause]. Can we change it to [specific alternative]? That way we're both protected."

2. **About [red flag]:** "[Script]"

Special Contract Types

Freelance / Contractor Agreements

Always check for:

  • Payment terms and timeline (net 15, 30, 60?)
  • Kill fee (what happens if they cancel the project?)
  • IP ownership (do you keep your portfolio rights?)
  • Scope creep protection (how are additional requests handled?)
  • Non-compete clauses (are they reasonable in scope and duration?)

NDAs

Always check for:

  • Duration (forever is a red flag)
  • Definition of "confidential information" (too broad?)
  • Mutual vs. one-way (is only one party protected?)
  • Carve-outs (can you still discuss publicly available info?)

Employment Contracts

Always check for:

  • Non-compete (duration, geography, scope)
  • IP assignment (do they own everything you create, even on your own time?)
  • At-will vs. fixed term
  • Benefits and equity vesting schedule
  • Severance terms

Leases

Always check for:

  • Early termination penalty
  • Rent increase caps
  • Maintenance responsibility
  • Subletting rights
  • Security deposit return terms

SaaS / Terms of Service

Always check for:

  • Data ownership and portability
  • Price increase notice requirements
  • Auto-renewal and cancellation process
  • Service level guarantees
  • Limitation of liability

Rules

  • Always explain legal terms in plain English. If you use a legal term, immediately define it.
  • Be direct about red flags. Don't soften bad clauses to be polite. If something is one-sided, say so.
  • Always note which party the contract favors. Most contracts favor whoever drafted them.
  • Give specific, actionable negotiation scripts. Not "you should negotiate this" but "say this exact thing."
  • Include the disclaimer: "This is not legal advice. For high-stakes contracts (employment, large deals, partnerships), consult a lawyer. This review helps you understand what you're signing and know what questions to ask."
  • If a contract is genuinely fair and balanced, say so. Don't manufacture problems that aren't there.
  • Flag auto-renewal clauses every single time. They are the #1 thing people miss and regret.

skills.name: Conversation Channel Router
skills.description: For any outreach or reply, pick the right channel — voice, email, SMS, web chat, in-person, asynchronous video, social DM. Considers urgency, relationship stage, modality preferences, compliance, and reply-velocity needs. Assistant-level skill the orchestrator invokes before calling a channel-specific skill.
skills.intent: Stop defaulting to email. Match the channel to the situation. For Matrix specifically: clinic owners answer phones, ignore inboxes. Voice is often the right answer even when email feels easier. This skill is the channel-selector that makes the rest of the agent work hit the right channel.
best_for: Escalation channel choice (when email is failing)
skills.estimated_time: 2-5 min
skills.body_html:
Conversation Channel Router

The skill the assistant invokes before any outreach to pick the right channel. Voice is underused; this skill keeps it on the table.

When to use

  • Any outreach where channel is unspecified
  • Reply decision when multiple channels are available
  • Re-attempting contact after a missed earlier touch
  • Designing a multi-touch sequence (channel mix matters)
  • Customer issue escalation (when text isn't working)

When NOT to use

  • Channel already specified by user / context (don't second-guess)
  • Channel dictated by system (e.g., LinkedIn reply on LinkedIn)
  • Internal communication with team (use whatever's normal)

The channel matrix

For each channel, what it's good for + what it costs + when to use:

Channel Speed Effort (user) Compliance Best for
Voice (live) Instant High (must answer) TCPA (outbound), call recording disclosure Cold qualification with hot signal, sensitive conversations, deal closing, complex Q&A
Voice (AI agent) Instant when picked up Same as live TCPA + state-specific consent Outbound cold qualify at scale, inbound front desk, after-hours coverage
Voicemail Async (recipient checks) Low (passive) TCPA window Followup after no-answer, drip touches
Email Async (minutes-hours-days) Very low CAN-SPAM Detail-heavy info, catalog send, formal follow-up, async work
SMS Near-instant Very low TCPA + A2P 10DLC Confirmations, reminders, quick Qs, mobile-first audience
Web chat (live) Instant in-session Low (already on site) recording disclosure recommended Site-visitor qualification, support escalation
Web chat (AI agent) Instant 24/7 Low recording disclosure After-hours web, scale qualification
In-app chat / customer portal Async Very low varies Existing-customer ops
Social DM (LinkedIn / IG / X) Mixed Low platform-specific Warm intros, content engagement followup
In-person Real-time Very high n/a Major deals, complex relationship moments, conferences
Async video (Loom / Tavus) Async Low to consume n/a (depending on use) Personalized cold + warm pitches, demos at scale
Conference call / video (Zoom) Scheduled High both sides recording disclosure Discovery calls, demos, multi-stakeholder meetings
Mail (physical) Slow days High to produce n/a Premium-positioning rare touches (signing-bonus packets, contract delivery)

Decision factors (apply in order)

1. What did the recipient prefer last?

If the recipient previously responded on channel X, default to channel X. Don't switch them without reason. Exception: if X has clearly failed (no response in 14 days), try a different channel.

2. Recipient archetype / role

Archetype Strong channels Weak channels
Clinic owner / physician Voice (esp. in clinic hours), text after intro Email (often ignored or staff-filtered)
NP / PA / mid-level provider Voice mid-day, text, email Social DM (rare)
Practice administrator Email, phone DM (rare)
Front desk / MA Phone (live or AI), text LinkedIn (rare)
C-suite enterprise buyer Email + LinkedIn warm intro Cold phone (gatekeeper-heavy)
Founder / startup CEO Twitter/X, email, warm intro Cold phone (cold), LinkedIn cold (mixed)
Customer (existing) Whatever they used last + SMS for ops Cold email feels weird
Investor Warm intro → email → in-person Cold phone (almost always)
Vendor / partner Email, scheduled call Cold phone (rude unless pre-cleared)

For Matrix's ICP (clinic owners + providers): voice is undervalued + email is over-relied-on.

3. Urgency

  • Sub-hour (real urgency, dollars on the line) → Voice (live or AI), SMS
  • Same-day → Voice, SMS, email with "today" in subject
  • This week → Email, SMS
  • No specific deadline → Whatever the recipient prefers

4. Information density

  • Single fact / answer → SMS or voice
  • Decision support / data → Email with attached doc
  • Complex multi-stakeholder → Email + scheduled call
  • Pitch / story → Voice (live or async video)
  • Confirmation / reminder → SMS

5. Relationship stage

Stage Right channel
First touch (cold) Email + voicemail combo, or warm intro
First touch (warm) Voice (asks for permission via SMS first if mobile)
Discovery scheduled SMS reminder, email confirmation
Active deal Voice for negotiation, email for proposal docs
Customer onboarding Voice for kickoff, email for docs, SMS for status
Customer ops SMS for quick, email for involved
Renewal / expansion Voice (account manager call)
Win-back (churned) Voice or warm intro — email almost never works

6. Compliance

  • TCPA restricts voice + SMS to consented contacts (or B2B exemptions where applicable)
  • CAN-SPAM governs email
  • State two-party consent affects voice recording
  • A2P 10DLC required for US business SMS
  • HIPAA — never include PHI in unencrypted channels (no SMS or email with patient identifiers)
  • GDPR / CCPA — opt-in + deletion rights for EU/CA contacts

7. Scale needs

  • 1-to-1, high effort → Live voice, in-person, custom video
  • 1-to-10 high quality → Voice AI agent, personalized email batch
  • 1-to-100+ → Email automation, voice AI, SMS sequences (where consented)

8. Failure mode acceptable

  • "Must reach today" → Voice (live or agent) + SMS backup
  • "Must reach this week" → Voice + email
  • "OK if it takes 2 weeks" → Email sequence

Workflow

Step 1 — Restate the situation

In one sentence:

"You want to [action] with [recipient archetype] who [context]. Urgency: [level]. Relationship: [stage]."

Step 2 — Walk the decision factors

Check each of the 8 factors. Eliminate channels that don't fit.

Step 3 — Output recommendation

Format:

## Recommended channel

**Primary**: <channel> — <one-line why>

**Backup** (if primary fails or no response): <channel> — <when to switch>

**Sequence** (if multi-touch): <ordered>
- Touch 1: <channel> on day <X>
- Touch 2: <channel> on day <Y>
- Touch 3: <channel> on day <Z>
- (after N touches without response: stop or kill)

**Compliance check**: <any required pre-action — consent, opt-in, disclosure>

**Next skill to invoke**:
- For voice → `voice-ai-sales-agent` (if outbound) or `voice-ai-inbound-qualifier` (if inbound design)
- For email → `cold-email-sequence` or `copywriting`
- For SMS → SMS campaign skill (planned) or manual draft
- For chat → web chat agent
- For in-person → calendar booking
- For warm intro → identify the mutual connection + ask

Step 4 — Hand off

Once channel is picked, hand off the rest of the work to the channel-specific skill.

Output structure (example)

# Channel Recommendation — <Use Case>

## Situation
You want to reach 30 clinic owners in TX who just hired their first NP (job-posting signal) about adding 503-A patient-specific compounding to their offering.

## Factors
| Factor | Value |
|---|---|
| Recipient archetype | Clinic owner / physician (TX) |
| Urgency | This week (signal is fresh) |
| Info density | Pitch — voice or async video > email |
| Relationship | Cold — but with intent signal |
| Compliance | TCPA — B2B + verified-list reduces risk; SMS requires A2P |
| Scale | 30 contacts — high enough for voice AI |
| Failure acceptable | OK if 25-50% don't connect (voicemail OK) |
| Last response | None — first touch |

## Recommendation

**Primary**: Outbound voice AI agent (`voice-ai-sales-agent`)
- Signal-driven cold call
- TCPA window enforced
- Voicemail script ready (~50% will hit voicemail)
- 30 dials over 3 days @ 10/day per agent

**Backup**: Email with catalog (`cold-email-sequence` 3-touch)
- Triggered automatically for voicemail / no-answer
- Touch 1: send catalog same-day-as-voicemail
- Touch 2: 3-day reframe email
- Touch 3: 7-day soft break-up

**Compliance check**:
- [ ] List verified (NPI active, not in DNC)
- [ ] TX is one-party consent — recording disclosure not strictly required but recommended
- [ ] A2P 10DLC active for the SMS callback option

**Next skill to invoke**: `voice-ai-sales-agent` with this list as input.

Anti-patterns

  • Defaulting to email — for Matrix's ICP (clinic owners), voice often outperforms
  • Cold-phoning C-suite enterprise without warm intro — gatekeeper-heavy
  • Spamming all channels at once — feels desperate; sequence them
  • Switching channels without reason — if the recipient responded on X, default to X
  • Ignoring recipient archetype — a startup founder isn't a clinic admin isn't a hospital VP
  • Channel without compliance check — every channel has rules (TCPA / CAN-SPAM / A2P / HIPAA)
  • One-channel campaigns in 2026 — multi-touch multi-channel is the norm

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Analyze + recommend
  • ✅ Save recommendation to _Logs/
  • ⚠️ Approval to execute (per the next skill's approval rules)

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • Outreach context (from user or workflow)
  • _Shared Context/company-profile.md (ICP)
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md
  • GHL contact history (for "what did they respond to last")

Outputs to:

  • The channel-specific skill (voice / email / SMS / chat / in-person)
  • _Logs/agent-decisions/

Companion to:

  • prep-day (which uses this when surfacing today's outreach options)
  • weekly-review (review channel performance trends)

One-liner reminder

Match channel to recipient + urgency + info density + relationship + compliance. For Matrix: don't default to email. Voice is underused.

skills.name: Conversation Flow Designer
skills.description: Design a conversation flow for any modality — voice AI, web chat, SMS, in-app chat, FB Messenger DMs. Outputs a state machine, branching decisions, info capture per state, handoff conditions, and edge-case handling. Modality-agnostic — applied by voice-ai-* and chat-* skills.
skills.intent: Be the canonical conversation flow designer. One skill produces flows that downstream skills (voice / chat / SMS) implement in their modality. Apply the principles from _Knowledge Base/05 - Conversation Design + the 5-pillar framework + the Decision Helper / Council patterns for branching.
best_for: Before building a voice / chat / SMS agent
Refactoring a flow that's losing conversions
Adding a new branch to existing flow
Cross-modality flow (same flow on voice + chat)
skills.estimated_time: 30-60 min for new flow; 15 min for branch addition
skills.body_html:
Conversation Flow Designer

The skill that designs HOW a conversation moves. Modality-agnostic. Downstream skills (voice-ai-*, chat agents, SMS sequences) implement the flow in their channel.

Source: _Knowledge Base/05 - Conversation Design (Voice & Chat).md + the harness conversation-design patterns.

When to use

  • Before authoring any conversation agent (voice, chat, SMS)
  • When refactoring a flow that's underperforming
  • Adding a new branch / use case to an existing flow
  • Cross-channel flow (e.g., start on chat → escalate to voice)

When NOT to use

  • Single-message reply (use email-drafter or copywriting)
  • Free-form open-ended chat without states (use general LLM)
  • One-shot transactional confirmation (just send it)

Required inputs

  1. Use case — outbound qualify / inbound front-desk / discovery / support triage / booking / etc.
  2. Modality — voice / chat / SMS / mixed
  3. ICP — who's on the other end
  4. Success outcome — what defines a "successful" conversation
  5. Failure modes accepted — what's an acceptable bad outcome (e.g., voicemail OK; full refusal OK; gatekeeper OK)
  6. Available actions — what functions the agent can call (lookup, book, transfer, log)

If 1 or 4 is missing, ask: "What's the use case and what counts as a successful conversation?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Apply the 5 pillars (from KB 05)

Every flow respects:

  1. Presence — agent stays focused on the user's message, not the script
  2. Rapport — opens warm + named; uses common ground signals
  3. Active listening — restates the user in one sentence before responding (the load-bearing move)
  4. Mirroring — tone + pace + vocabulary
  5. Graceful close — summary + next step

Step 2 — Map the canonical 5-stage arc

Every conversation has these stages (adapt naming per use case):

1. OPEN       (greeting + frame + permission)
2. DISCOVER   (capture info via prescribed-narrow questions)
3. PROPOSE    (offer the relevant next step / solution)
4. HANDLE     (handle objections / clarifications / refinements)
5. CLOSE      (lock the next step + warm exit)

For each stage, define:

  • Goal of the stage (what must be captured / decided to advance)
  • Entry signal (how does the conversation get here)
  • Exit signal (what triggers the next stage)
  • Time budget (rough — voice agents need this)
  • Failure path (what happens if this stage can't complete)

Step 3 — Branch at each stage

For each stage, design 3-5 branches:

OPEN branches:

  • Receptive ("hi how can I help") → DISCOVER
  • Curt / gatekeeper ("who's this") → identification + re-attempt
  • Wrong person ("not interested") → soft pivot or polite close
  • Voicemail (outbound only) → voicemail script + end
  • Out-of-window (after-hours, outbound) → reschedule

DISCOVER branches:

  • ICP-match → PROPOSE
