  • ICP-mismatch but related → soft pivot + alternative resource → CLOSE
  • ICP-mismatch unrelated → polite close
  • Refuses to share info → trust-build attempt then close if still refused

PROPOSE branches:

  • Interest → HANDLE
  • Soft interest ("send me info") → email-followup CLOSE
  • Hard no → polite CLOSE
  • Question → ANSWER then re-PROPOSE

HANDLE branches (per common objection):

  • Price → reframe to ROI / value equation
  • Trust → proof / social proof
  • Timing → urgency or future-pace
  • Authority ("I need to ask my partner") → equip + book follow-up with both
  • Existing vendor → switch-cost analysis + wedge

CLOSE branches:

  • Booked → confirmation + reminder cadence
  • Email follow-up → templated send + 24h reminder
  • Hard no → tag do-not-contact if requested + polite exit

Step 4 — Specify info to capture per state

For each state where info matters, list the minimum capture + nice-to-have fields.

Example (Matrix outbound qualification):

  • Minimum: name, role, clinic name, primary pain (1 of 5 categorized choices)
  • Nice-to-have: timeline, current vendor, monthly volume, decision-maker if not on call

Step 5 — Handoff / escalation rules

Define when the agent should hand off:

  • To human — request for human, complex medical Q, billing dispute, legal threat, emotional escalation
  • To SMS — after voicemail or to send a calendar link
  • To email — for catalog request, document send, long-form info
  • To another agent — flow ends in different department's scope (e.g., qualified → routes to a Voice AI Sales Agent → routes to human discovery prep)

Handoff includes context transfer: don't make the receiving party re-ask.

Step 6 — Edge cases checklist

  • What if the caller talks over the agent? (interruption handling)
  • What if speech recognition fails / mis-hears? (clarification request, max 2 attempts)
  • What if the function call fails? (filler script + fallback action)
  • What if the network drops? (resume / restart logic)
  • What if the call goes silent for >10s? (check-in then end)
  • What if the user asks "are you a bot"? (honesty by default — confirm AI but reaffirm capability)
  • What if user asks for callback? (book it + confirm + send link)
  • What if user wants to "speak with a manager"? (transfer-to-human)

Step 7 — Output the flow doc

Output structure

# Conversation Flow: <Use Case>

## Quick reference
- **Modality**: <voice / chat / SMS / mixed>
- **Channel**: <Retell / Vapi / web chat / GHL SMS / etc.>
- **Use case**: <outbound qualify / inbound front-desk / etc.>
- **ICP**: <one-liner>
- **Success**: <one-line definition>
- **Estimated avg conversation length**: <sec / messages>

## State machine


                 ┌────────────┐
                 │   OPEN     │
                 └─────┬──────┘
                       │
          ┌────────────┼───────────────┐
          ▼            ▼               ▼
    ┌─────────┐  ┌──────────┐   ┌───────────┐
    │ Voicemail│  │Gatekeeper│   │  Direct   │
    └────┬────┘  └────┬─────┘   └─────┬─────┘
         │            │               │
         ▼            ▼               ▼
     END (vm)    re-attempt       ┌─────────┐
                                  │DISCOVER │
                                  └────┬────┘
                      [branches per Step 3]
                                  │
                                  ▼
                              [PROPOSE]
                                  │
                                  ▼
                              [HANDLE]
                                  │
                                  ▼
                              [CLOSE]


## States (detailed)

### OPEN
- **Goal**: get acknowledgment + permission to continue
- **Entry**: dialer initiates / caller picks up
- **Exit**: confirmed continue OR voicemail OR refused
- **Time budget**: 10-20 sec
- **Branches**:
  | Branch | Trigger | Next state |
  |---|---|---|
  | Receptive | normal greeting back | DISCOVER |
  | Voicemail | beep detection | VOICEMAIL → END |
  | Gatekeeper | "Dr. X's office, can I help" | IDENTIFY (then re-OPEN with provider) |
  | Refused | "not interested" | CLOSE-cold |

[Repeat per state]

## Information capture map

| State | Required to capture | Nice-to-have |
|---|---|---|
| OPEN | confirmation of recipient | role |
| DISCOVER | clinic name, primary pain, role | timeline, monthly volume, current vendor |
| PROPOSE | reception to proposal | objection (if any) |

## Functions / actions available

| Function | Used in state | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| <function> | <state> | <purpose> |

## Handoff rules

| Trigger | Target | Context to pass |
|---|---|---|
| "want a human" | human (transfer or callback) | full transcript + key facts |
| medical question | human + log to follow-up | question + caller info |
| catalog request | email automation | name + email + interest tag |

## Edge cases handled
- [ ] Interruptions
- [ ] Speech recognition failures
- [ ] Function call failures
- [ ] Silence > 10 sec
- [ ] "Are you a bot?"
- [ ] Callback request
- [ ] Escalation request

## Failure-end states
- **VOICEMAIL_LEFT** — voicemail script, then end
- **GATEKEEPER_BLOCKED** — capture gatekeeper name + best time to call back, end
- **REFUSED** — polite close, tag `do-not-contact` if explicit, end
- **TRANSFER_HUMAN** — transfer + log
- **TECHNICAL_FAIL** — apology + offer callback + end
- **SUCCESS_BOOKED** — confirmation + send reminders + log
- **SUCCESS_FOLLOWUP** — email/SMS follow-up + log

## Variables tracked across states
- caller_id (phone number)
- caller_name (captured during DISCOVER if not in DB)
- clinic_name
- primary_pain
- agent_should_transfer (boolean)
- meeting_booked_at (if SUCCESS_BOOKED)

## Brand voice constraints (from `_Shared Context/brand-guide.md`)
For Matrix:
- Authoritative, clinical, direct
- No exclamation marks (in TTS too — replace `!` with `.`)
- No "great question" / "absolutely" / "of course"
- Peer-level talk to providers (don't explain medicine to them)
- Always compliance-aware framing

## Handoff to downstream skills
- `voice-script-author` — write the actual spoken language for each state
- `voice-ai-agent-builder` — implement on the chosen platform
- `ghl-crm-architect` — wire the function backends (lookup, book, tag, etc.)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-conversation-flow-<use-case>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Linear scripts — production calls always branch; no linear flow survives real conversations
  • No voicemail path for outbound — half of calls
  • No "are you a bot" handler — users always ask; have an honest answer ready
  • Branching only at success/fail — branch within DISCOVER, within PROPOSE, etc.
  • No edge-case checklist — interruptions / failures kill agents in production
  • Designing flow + writing scripts in one pass — separate concerns; design here, write scripts in voice-script-author
  • Same flow for voice + chat without channel-specific tuning — voice has time pressure + interruption; chat has scrollback + multitasking

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design any flow
  • ✅ Save flow specs to _Logs/
  • ⚠️ Production deployment of the flow (via voice-ai-agent-builder)

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • Use case statement (from user or voice-ai-agent-builder)
  • _Shared Context/company-profile.md
  • _Shared Context/brand-guide.md
  • _Shared Context/offers.md
  • _Knowledge Base/05 - Conversation Design.md

Outputs to:

  • voice-script-author (writes the spoken language)
  • voice-ai-agent-builder (implements on platform)
  • Chat / SMS agents (when applicable)

One-liner reminder

5 pillars + 5-stage arc + branch at every state + capture per state + handoff rules + edge cases. Modality-agnostic. Pass to script author.

skills.name: Copywriting
skills.description: The master copywriter. Produces ads, landing-page copy, emails, social posts, scripts, sales pages — any sales/marketing copy in the brand voice. Inputs an Offer Brief (from offer-positioning) and target channel/format; outputs publication-ready copy + variations + compliance-cleared claims.
skills.intent: Produce copy that's true to brand voice, applies proven structure (hook → retain → reward / problem → agitate → solve / etc.), passes claims-compliance, and gives the user 2-3 variations to pick from. One copywriter agent — not a separate one per channel.
best_for: Cold email copy (single — for sequences use `cold-email-sequence`)
Ad copy (Meta, Google, LinkedIn — same brand voice)
skills.estimated_time: 15-45 min depending on length
skills.body_html:
Copywriting

One copywriter agent. Works across ads, pages, emails, posts, scripts. Voice stays consistent because it always reads brand-guide.md first.

When to use

  • After offer-positioning produces an Offer Brief
  • For any new sales / marketing copy
  • For copy variations / A/B tests
  • For rewriting underperforming copy

When NOT to use

  • Long-form thought-leadership content (use longform-content — planned)
  • Carousel slides (use branded-carousel-generator)
  • Multi-email sequences (use cold-email-sequence — orchestrates copywriting under the hood)
  • Technical documentation (use a doc-focused skill)

Required inputs

  1. Channel + format — landing page hero / cold email / Meta ad / Google ad / LinkedIn post / etc.
  2. Offer Brief — from offer-positioning OR pasted by user
  3. Audience temperature — cold (never heard of you) / warm (knows you) / hot (ready to buy)
  4. Goal — book demo / opt-in / direct purchase / reply / click-through
  5. Constraints — character limits / word counts / specific CTAs / banned phrases
  6. Variations requested — default 3

If 1 or 2 is missing, ask: "What channel + format, and is there an offer brief I should read?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Load context (auto)

Read in this order:

  1. _Shared Context/brand-guide.md — voice rules
  2. _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md — banned claims
  3. The Offer Brief — positioning, wedge, value stack, objections, hooks
  4. _Shared Context/offers.md — for context consistency
  5. Individual Skills/brand-identity.md — for visual / typographic reference if it's a page/ad

Step 2 — Pick the structural template

Match channel to proven structure:

Channel Structure
Landing page hero Headline (clear benefit) + subhead (specific proof) + CTA
Landing page section Problem → Agitate → Solve OR Feature → Benefit → Proof
Cold email Subject (4-7 words lowercase) + first-line signal-reference + body (3-5 sentences) + one CTA
Meta ad (feed) Hook (3-5 words) + Body (1-3 sentences) + CTA
Meta ad (story) Visual + 1 sentence + swipe-up
Google search ad 3 headlines + 2 descriptions per RSA
LinkedIn post Hook + setup + meat (often a story) + lesson + CTA
YouTube short script Hook (3 sec) + Promise (5 sec) + Payoff (30-50 sec) + CTA
Sales page Header → Promise → Proof → Mechanism → Bonuses → Price → Guarantee → CTA → FAQ
Nurture email Subject + Single idea (1 lesson, 1 story, 1 CTA)
Webinar invite Curiosity headline + scarcity + RSVP CTA

Step 3 — Apply Hormozi's hook patterns

Steal from these patterns based on audience temperature:

  • Curiosity hook: counterintuitive claim ("Why most cold emails fail before they're opened")
  • Insight hook: specific data point ("3% of buyers are in-window. Outreach to the rest costs you brand equity.")
  • Pain hook: name the exact frustration ("Spent 6 hours building a landing page — got 0 leads. Here's what's broken.")
  • Outcome hook: vivid result ("Booked 10 demos in 14 days with this 4-line cold email")
  • Question hook: hard not to answer ("What's your current cost per qualified meeting?")
  • Permission hook: low-friction ask ("Worth me sending a 90-second Loom on this?")

Step 4 — Write variation 1 (apply structure + hook)

Apply the structure. Use 1 hook style. Hit the FOMU wedge in the body. End with one clear CTA.

Word count discipline:

  • Cold email: < 100 words
  • LinkedIn post: 80-200 words
  • Landing page hero: < 25 words
  • Sales page: 800-3000 words (depends on offer complexity)
  • Meta ad body: < 125 chars (above the "see more" fold)

Step 5 — Write 2-3 variations

Same Offer Brief, different hooks / angles:

  • Variation A: Curiosity hook + outcome-led body
  • Variation B: Pain hook + mechanism-led body
  • Variation C: Question hook + permission-led body

Or whatever 3 angles fit the channel.

Step 6 — Compliance pass (auto)

Run output through compliance-rules.md:

  • No banned absolute phrases ("secure", "compliant", "guaranteed")
  • No medical / financial / legal claims that need substantiation
  • No testimonials without permission flag
  • No competitor disparagement
  • Required disclaimers present (if applicable to channel)

If violations → revise + flag what was changed and why.

Step 7 — QA pass (auto)

Run output through qa-checklist.md Copy QA section:

  • Voice match ✓
  • Audience-adaptive ✓
  • Clear next step ✓
  • Length appropriate ✓
  • Banned phrases absent ✓

Step 8 — Output with variations + reasoning

Output structure

# Copy: <Channel / Format> — <Offer Name>

## Quick spec
- **Channel**: <landing page hero / cold email / Meta ad / etc.>
- **Audience temp**: <cold / warm / hot>
- **Goal**: <book demo / opt-in / purchase / etc.>
- **Word count**: <target>
- **Variations**: 3

## Variation A — <Hook type>
**Hook**: <hook line>
**Body**:

Why this works: <one-line — what proven pattern this uses>

Variation B —

Hook: Body:

<copy>

CTA:

Why this works:

Variation C —

Hook: Body:

<copy>

CTA:

Why this works:

Compliance pass

  • No banned absolute phrases
  • No unsubstantiated medical / financial / legal claims
  • No competitor disparagement
  • Required disclaimers present (if applicable)
  • Banned brand phrases absent

Recommendation

My pick: Variation <A/B/C> because <one-line reasoning tied to audience temperature + goal>.

A/B test idea (if running paid / cold): test vs with hypothesis .

Assets needed (if any)

  • Visual concept:
  • Proof to surface: <case study X, testimonial Y>

Handoff (use _Templates/handoff-template.md)

To (depending on channel):

  • Landing page → Front-End Dev / Web Designer
  • Cold email → SDR / cold-email-sequence
  • Paid ad → Meta Ads / Google Ads / Creative Director
  • Social → Social Media Manager / branded-carousel-generator
  • Sales script → Sales team / discovery-call-prep

Approval required: yes — Chris approves pick + variation selected for production

Risks surfaced

  • <risk: e.g., specific claim needs case study citation before live>

Logged to

_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-copy-<channel>-<slug>.md


## Anti-patterns

- **Generic copy** ("Unlock your potential!" — no specific dream outcome)
- **Multi-CTA** in any single piece — one ask per piece
- **Long preamble before the hook** — lead with the strongest line
- **Insider jargon for cold audiences** — match audience temperature
- **Claims without proof citation** — every claim either generic-enough-to-be-fact OR cited
- **Skipping the variations** — single-shot copy is worse than 3 angles to pick from
- **No "why this works" reasoning** — Chris should know what pattern each variation is leveraging
- **Banned brand phrases** ("Hope you're well!", "Just checking in", "Synergy") — see brand-guide.md

## Pre-authorizations

- ✅ Draft any copy
- ✅ Generate variations
- ⚠️ Use specific testimonials / case studies — verify permission first
- ⚠️ Claim specific stats / data — cite the source inline
- 🚫 Submit / publish anywhere — that's downstream agent + Chris approval

## Pairing

**Inputs from**:
- `offer-positioning` Offer Brief (primary)
- `market-research` (for hook ideas, language to steal)
- `_Shared Context/brand-guide.md` (voice)
- `_Shared Context/compliance-rules.md` (claims discipline)

**Outputs to**:
- Channel-specific agents (Landing Page Builder, Cold Email Sequence, Paid Ads, Social Manager)
- `_Logs/agent-decisions/`

**Chains commonly with**:
- `offer-positioning` → `copywriting` → (Landing Page OR Cold Email OR Paid Ad)
- For sequences: `copywriting` → `cold-email-sequence` (which uses copywriting under the hood)
- For carousels: `copywriting` → `branded-carousel-generator`

## One-liner reminder

**Read brand-guide first. Match structure to channel. 3 variations with different hooks. Compliance pass before output. One CTA per piece.**

skills.name: Creative Director
skills.description: The visual brand lead. Sister to content-director on the visual side. Sets creative direction across the brand — defines campaign visual systems, briefs designers / image-prompt agents, ensures consistency across landing pages / ads / decks / social. For Matrix: maintains the cinematic + brand palette discipline; protects MuscleLock and Peptide Map flagship visual storytelling.
best_for: Briefing designers (human or AI) for any visual asset
Designing a new sub-brand visual treatment (e.g., MuscleLock, Peptide Map)
skills.estimated_time: 30-60 min per creative brief
skills.body_html:
Creative Director

The visual brand lead. Every visual asset has a brief from here.

When to use

  • New campaign visual system needed
  • Briefing designers (human or AI image agents)
  • Auditing existing visuals for consistency
  • Sub-brand visual treatment (e.g., MuscleLock flagship product storyline)
  • Pre-launch visual review

When NOT to use

  • Single-asset typo fix (just edit)
  • Brand-identity baseline definition (use _Shared Context/brand-guide.md + Individual Skills/brand-identity.md directly)
  • Content writing (use copywriting / content-director)

Required inputs

  1. Campaign / asset purpose — what's being created
  2. Channel(s) — where it lives (landing page / social / ad / print / deck)
  3. Audience — same ICP framework
  4. Mood / energy — premium cinematic vs vibrant store vs editorial
  5. Existing assets — what's already in flight that this must match / extend

If 1-2 missing, ask: "What's the asset for + where does it ship?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Load brand context

  • _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (voice + claims discipline)
  • Individual Skills/brand-identity.md (visual spec template)
  • Matrix's two palettes:
    • Cinematic / Enterprise: navy-950 / steel grays / restrained blue/cyan + gold — for marketing site, investor decks, premium positioning, sub-brand storytelling (MuscleLock, Peptide Map)
    • Brand / Store: pink #ff4db8 + purple #c46bff + cyan #3ad7ff + gold #ffab00 on #0a0815 — for storefront, order forms, admin, vibrant communication

Step 2 — Pick the palette

Decision matrix:

Asset type Default palette
Marketing site sections (matrixadvancedsolutions.com) Cinematic
Investor / board / press Cinematic
MuscleLock / Peptide Map storytelling Cinematic
Storefront / order form Brand
Email marketing Brand (lighter touches of cinematic)
Social posts Mix — content-dependent
Ad creative Mix — A/B both palettes; cinematic for premium, brand for energy
Slide decks Cinematic (premium)
Educational content Cinematic

Step 3 — Define the system

For the campaign / asset, document:

Color application:

  • Primary color (60-70% of usage)
  • Accent color (20-30%)
  • Highlight color (5-10%)
  • Background

Typography:

  • Headline: Inter Bold/SemiBold (cinematic) — sizing scale
  • Body: Inter Regular
  • Letter spacing rules

Photography / imagery direction:

  • Provider-grade (clinical, not stock-y)
  • Real clinical environments (or curated stock that doesn't scream stock)
  • No memes / no obviously-AI-generated
  • Subject framing rules

Motion language (if applicable):

  • Framer Motion reveal patterns
  • Cinematic, directional, restrained
  • Reduced-motion support always
  • Duration: 0.4-0.8s typical for reveals

Layout grid:

  • max-w-7xl for content sections
  • max-w-4xl for narrative
  • py-24 to py-32 desktop
  • py-12 to py-16 mobile

Icons / illustration:

  • Lucide for utility icons
  • No emoji-as-illustration
  • 3D / R3F for hero scenes (flagship pages only)

Step 4 — Write the creative brief

For each asset:

# Creative Brief: <Asset Name>

## Purpose
<one-line>

## Channel
<where it lives>

## Audience
<ICP from market-research / company-profile>

## Palette
**Mode**: <cinematic / brand>
**Primary**: <hex>
**Accent**: <hex>
**Background**: <hex>

## Mood
<premium-cinematic / energetic / editorial / functional>

## Typography
- Headline: <font + size + weight + letter-spacing>
- Body: <font + size + weight>

## Imagery
- Style: <description>
- Specific shots needed: <list>
- Avoid: <list — stock-y / overly-clinical / etc.>

## Layout
- Grid: <max-width + padding>
- Hierarchy: <H1 → H2 → body → CTA placement>
- Whitespace: <generous / tight>

## Motion (if applicable)
- Reveal: <pattern>
- Duration: <ms>
- Reduced-motion: <required behavior>

## CTAs
- Primary: <copy + visual treatment>
- Secondary: <copy + treatment>

## Specifications per channel

### If web (landing page / site section)
- Hero: <ratio + treatment>
- Sections: <list>
- Mobile breakpoint: <treatment>

### If social
- Aspect ratio: <1:1 / 4:5 / 9:16>
- Carousel: <Y/N + slide count>
- Caption first line: <hook>

### If ad
- Format: <Meta feed / story / reel / Google display / etc.>
- Dimensions: <spec>
- Variation count: <A/B/C>

### If deck (slides)
- Slide count: <range>
- Master template: <link>

### If print (label / packaging)
- Dimensions: <spec>
- Print specs: <CMYK / spot / etc.>

## Compliance constraints (per compliance-rules.md)
- No banned absolutes
- Required disclaimers
- Photo permission documented (if real customers)
- Logo usage: per brand-identity.md

## Deliverables
- <list of files / formats needed>
- <due date>

## Reference assets
- <link to existing assets that set the bar>

Step 5 — Route to executors

Asset type Executor
HTML / landing page frontend-dev (with this brief as input)
Carousel branded-carousel-generator
Infographic Cowork explainer-graphic
Slide deck Cowork slide-deck-builder
Generated images AI image-prompt agent (banana / FLUX / etc. via harness skills)
Video script voice-script-author (script) + video production agent (planned)
Custom design Human designer (with this brief)
Label / packaging Human designer + print spec

Step 6 — Visual QA (post-execution)

Once the asset is built, run through:

  • Palette match
  • Typography hierarchy correct
  • Logo usage correct (clearspace, contrast)
  • Accessibility (color contrast WCAG AA, alt text)
  • Mobile responsive (if web)
  • Compliance phrases present
  • No banned imagery / claims

Output structure

# Creative Brief Generated: <Asset Name>

## Brief
(See full creative brief above — saved as `_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-creative-brief-<asset>.md`)

## Palette
**Mode**: <cinematic / brand>
**Hex codes**: <list>

## Routing
**Executor**: <human designer / frontend-dev / branded-carousel-generator / etc.>
**Estimated deliverable**: <date>

## Compliance pre-flight
- [x] No banned absolutes
- [x] Photo permission flag (if real customers used)
- [x] Logo usage rules attached

## ✅ I handled
- Loaded brand context
- Picked palette + system
- Wrote creative brief
- Routed to executor

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve mood / palette choice (if non-default)
- Approve real-customer photo usage (per documented permissions)
- Final visual QA before publish

## 🚫 You only
- Sign off on celebrity / influencer image rights
- Approve trademark / IP-sensitive design
- Approve any design that uses generated AI images of real people

## Handoff
**To**: executor skill + visual QA loop + `compliance-qa` (claims in copy)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-creative-brief-<asset>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Random palette per asset — pick the right one for the context, stick with it
  • Multiple personas / brands in one creative — keep MuscleLock distinct from main; storyline matters
  • Generic stock photography — kills brand
  • Logo distortion / wrong clearspace — strict rules
  • Animation just for animation — must serve content, must support reduced-motion
  • No accessibility check — color contrast / alt text mandatory
  • AI-generated humans without disclosure when used in advertising — disclosure required in some jurisdictions

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Generate creative briefs
  • ✅ Choose between cinematic and brand palettes per the matrix
  • ✅ Route to executor skills
  • ⚠️ Add a NEW palette / mode (Chris approves — brand evolution)
  • ⚠️ Add new visual system to brand-identity.md (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Use unlicensed imagery
  • 🚫 Use customer photos without documented permission
  • 🚫 Modify logo / wordmark

Pairing

Inputs from: content-director (campaign theme drives visual system), offer-positioning (the offer's visual needs), _Shared Context/brand-guide.md, Individual Skills/brand-identity.md Outputs to: frontend-dev, branded-carousel-generator, slide-deck-builder, explainer-graphic, AI image agents (banana / FLUX), human designers Chains with: compliance-qa (claims in any copy on the visual), marketing-analytics (creative performance)

One-liner reminder

Pick palette (cinematic vs brand). Write brief per asset. Route to executor. Visual QA before publish. Two palettes, never mix. Logo / clearspace / contrast strict.

skills.name: Customer Health Scoring
skills.description: Daily-refreshed per-account customer health score (0-100) composed from product usage, order cadence, support tickets, NPS, and AM signals. Tiers accounts as healthy / at-risk / critical and feeds downstream specialists. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: CS without health scoring is firefighting. This specialist gives the team a single 0-100 number per account, refreshed daily, that pre-routes work to the right intervention before symptoms become churn.
skills.estimated_time: Daily refresh: 10-20 min auto. Single account: 1-2 min.
skills.body_html:
Customer Health Scoring

Per-account health scorer. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Computes a deterministic 0-100 health score per activated account, refreshed daily. Inputs are weighted signals: order cadence (recency + frequency vs cohort baseline), product usage / catalog logins, support ticket volume + sentiment, NPS score + recency, AM-flagged qualitative signals, payment health (Stripe), and tenure-adjusted baseline. Outputs a tier (healthy ≥ 70 / at-risk 40-69 / critical < 40) and signal breakdown. Feeds churn-risk-predictor, expansion-playbook-runner, AM cohort dashboards.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Forward-looking churn prediction (churn-risk-predictor), rescue execution (retention-rescue-playbook), expansion outreach (expansion-playbook-runner), individual relationship work (account-manager).

INPUT

  • Trigger: daily-cron / per-account-query / cohort-query
  • Scope: single account_id / cohort_id / all-active
  • Time window: trailing 90 days (default)

RULES

  • Score formula is deterministic + auditable (no LLM scoring; signals weighted explicitly)
  • Weights configurable in _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md (default: order_cadence 30 / usage 20 / support 15 / nps 15 / am_signal 10 / payment 10)
  • Tier thresholds: healthy ≥ 70 / at-risk 40-69 / critical < 40
  • Score deltas ≥ 15 in 7 days flagged as volatility-alert
  • Run pii-redactor on any cross-account aggregate output
  • Tenure-normalize: < 30 days uses onboarding-cohort baseline (not active-customer)
  • Sub-brand respected (sub-brands.md) — clinical vs supplements have different cadence expectations
  • Cache score for 24h; invalidate on payment failure / NPS submission / critical support ticket

TOOLS

  • GHL MCP (read contacts + custom fields + conversations + opportunities)
  • Stripe (payment health + invoice status)
  • Supabase (ecommerce orders + usage data via ecommerce-schema)
  • Compose: pii-redactor, analytics-architect
  • Read: kpi-registry.md, sub-brands.md
  • Write: _Logs/cs/health-scoring/<date>/scores.json + per-account history append
  • LLM: Haiku only for AM-signal natural-language summarization (optional)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    refresh_run_id: string
    refresh_timestamp: ISO
    per_account_scores:
      - account_id: string
        tenure_days: number
        tier: enterprise | white-glove | high-touch | standard
        sub_brand: matrix | musclelock | peptide-map | cliniq
        score: number  # 0-100
        prior_score: number
        delta_7d: number
        status_tier: healthy | at-risk | critical
        volatility_flag: boolean
        signals:
          order_cadence: {score: number, weight: number, drivers: array}
          product_usage: {score: number, weight: number, drivers: array}
          support_volume: {score: number, weight: number, drivers: array}
          nps_recent: {score: number, weight: number, last_submitted: ISO}
          am_qualitative: {score: number, weight: number, last_note: string}
          payment_health: {score: number, weight: number, status: string}
        recommended_next_action: string
    cohort_aggregate:
      total_active: number
      healthy_count: number
      at_risk_count: number
      critical_count: number
      median_score: number
      score_distribution: object
    alerts:
      - severity: P1 | P2
        account_id: string  # redacted in external view
        issue: string
  format: JSON + Markdown digest

HANDOFF

  • churn-risk-predictor (consumes daily snapshot)
  • account-manager (per-AM cohort dashboard)
  • expansion-playbook-runner (healthy + tenure ≥ 90d candidates)
  • customer-success-lead (rolled into weekly KPI snapshot)
  • chain-monitor (volatility alerts + critical-tier P1)

Eval cases

  1. New account day 7 → tenure-normalized to onboarding baseline (not critical)
  2. Order cadence drops 50% → at-risk flag
  3. NPS detractor + ticket spike same week → critical + volatility-alert
  4. Healthy account 18-month tenure → expansion candidate
  5. Score volatility ≥ 15 in 7 days → flagged for AM attention
  6. Sub-brand cadence differs (MuscleLock vs Matrix Rx) → weights respect
  7. PII handling: external dashboard shows tier counts only; AM view shows account-level

Anti-patterns

  • LLM-scored health (non-deterministic; cannot audit)
  • Single signal dominance (order cadence alone misses NPS + support)
  • Same weights all tiers (enterprise needs AM signal weighted heavier)
  • Score without action (recommended_next_action mandatory)
  • PII leak in cohort outputs

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read GHL + Stripe + Supabase data
  • ✅ Compute + persist scores daily
  • ✅ Emit alerts to chain-monitor
  • ⚠️ Reweight signals (Chris approves config change)
  • 🚫 PII leak in any output
  • 🚫 Auto-trigger customer-facing action (only flags + recommends)

Pairing

Composed by: customer-success-lead, churn-risk-predictor, expansion-playbook-runner, account-manager Composes: pii-redactor, analytics-architect Reads: GHL, Stripe, Supabase, kpi-registry.md, sub-brands.md Outputs to: churn-risk-predictor, AMs, customer-success-lead, chain-monitor

One-liner reminder

Deterministic 0-100 daily. Signals weighted + auditable. Tiers route work. Volatility ≥ 15 flagged. PII discipline always. Score → action, never score alone.

skills.name: Customer Onboarding
skills.description: Designs + executes the onboarding journey for a new clinic / customer. Builds the per-customer onboarding checklist, automation sequence, and personal touchpoints. Triggered when a deal closes; tracks completion through to first-order + first-success. For Matrix: NPI verification → catalog access → first order → 30-day check-in.
skills.estimated_time: 15-30 min setup per customer; ongoing 30-90 days execution
skills.body_html:
Customer Onboarding

The handoff from sales-won to customer-success. Closes the gap where "deal closed but customer didn't activate."

When to use

  • Closed-won deal needs onboarding flow triggered
  • Reactivating a churned customer
  • Enterprise / high-touch account kickoff
  • Building / updating the standard onboarding template

When NOT to use

  • Pre-sale (use sales-strategy / discovery-call-prep)
  • Day-to-day customer ops (use account-management planned)
  • Single support ticket (use support-triage)

Required inputs

  1. Customer record — GHL contact ID
  2. Closed-won deal — opportunity ID + offer + price
  3. Stakeholders — names + roles on customer side
  4. Compliance status — credentialing complete? BAA signed if applicable?
  5. Tier of touch — self-serve / standard / white-glove / enterprise

If 1 missing, ask: "Which customer / contact in GHL?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Verify pre-conditions

  • NPI verified in NPPES (Matrix-specific)
  • Credentialing intake complete
  • BAA signed if customer will touch PHI
  • Payment method on file
  • First order placed OR clear next-step for ordering

If any missing → resolve before starting onboarding flow (don't fake it).

Step 2 — Pick the onboarding tier

Tier What it includes Time investment
Self-serve Automated welcome sequence + KB access 0 human hours
Standard Welcome + kickoff call (30 min) + 30-day check-in 1 hour
White-glove Welcome + onboarding session (60 min) + 14-day + 30-day + 60-day check-ins + dedicated account manager 3-4 hours
Enterprise Multi-stakeholder kickoff + custom integration support + weekly first month + monthly thereafter 8+ hours

For Matrix: most clinics → Standard. Multi-location → White-glove. Enterprise rare.

Step 3 — Generate the per-customer checklist

# Onboarding: <Customer Name> (<Tier>)

## Day 0 — Deal closed
- [ ] Welcome email sent (auto via GHL)
- [ ] Account manager assigned: <name>
- [ ] Calendar link for kickoff sent
- [ ] CRM stage moved to "Onboarding"
- [ ] Tag `status:onboarding` applied

## Day 1-3 — Account provisioning
- [ ] NPI re-verified (NPPES)
- [ ] Credentialing packet processed
- [ ] BAA signed (if applicable)
- [ ] Storefront access granted (matrix-order-form)
- [ ] Account manager intro email sent

## Day 3-7 — Kickoff call
- [ ] Kickoff call scheduled
- [ ] Call held: <date>
- [ ] Topics covered: catalog walk-through, ordering flow, account manager intro, dosing guide, education resources
- [ ] Call notes logged to GHL contact

## Day 7-14 — First order
- [ ] First catalog browse activity logged
- [ ] First order placed: <date> / <amount>
- [ ] Order shipped + delivered
- [ ] Confirmation that order received correctly

## Day 14-30 — Adoption check
- [ ] 14-day check-in call/email
- [ ] Re-order pattern emerging? Y/N
- [ ] Any blockers reported
- [ ] Success criteria defined for the customer

## Day 30 — First milestone
- [ ] 30-day review call
- [ ] Outcomes vs expectations discussed
- [ ] Expansion opportunities identified
- [ ] Tag `status:active` applied
- [ ] CRM stage moved to "Active Customer"
- [ ] Onboarding flow closed

Step 4 — Wire the automation in GHL

Per ghl-crm-architect + workflow-automation-builder:

  • Day 0 welcome email + SMS (auto)
  • Day 3 reminder if no first order activity
  • Day 7 reminder if no kickoff call booked
  • Day 14 check-in template (account-manager sends)
  • Day 30 review reminder (account-manager schedules call)
  • Tag transitions tracked on each milestone

Step 5 — Schedule the human touchpoints

For Standard tier:

  • Kickoff call (Day 3-7) — 30 min
  • 30-day review (Day 28-32) — 30 min

For White-glove tier (add):

  • Onboarding session (Day 3-7) — 60 min
  • 14-day check-in (Day 12-16) — 15 min
  • 60-day expansion review (Day 58-62) — 30 min

Step 6 — Define success criteria for the customer

In the kickoff call, work with customer to define:

  • What does "successful onboarding" look like for THEM (first order? first re-order? first dollar of revenue from these products? )
  • Their KPI (patients on new modality, revenue from new vertical, etc.)

Track this in the contact's custom fields for 30-day review.

Step 7 — Output the onboarding plan

Output structure

# Onboarding Plan: <Customer Name>

## Customer
**Name**: <name>
**Clinic**: <clinic>
**Contact ID (GHL)**: <id>
**Deal**: <opportunity ID + offer>
**Tier**: <self-serve / standard / white-glove / enterprise>
**Account manager**: <name>

## Pre-conditions
- [x] NPI verified
- [x] Credentialing complete
- [ ] BAA signed (if applicable)
- [x] Payment method on file

## Onboarding checklist
(See full template above)

## Automation wired
- Day 0: welcome email ✅
- Day 3: first-order reminder (conditional) ✅
- Day 7: kickoff reminder (conditional) ✅
- Day 14: check-in (account-manager-sent) ✅
- Day 30: review reminder ✅

## Human touchpoints scheduled
| Touch | Type | Date | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|
| Kickoff | call | <date> | <AM name> |
| 14-day | email / call | <date> | <AM name> |
| 30-day | call | <date> | <AM name> |

## Customer-defined success
<paragraph: what success looks like to this customer>

## ✅ I handled
- Onboarding plan + automation wiring + checklist
- Scheduled all human touchpoints
- Tagged + staged in GHL

## ⚠️ Need from you (or AM)
- Hold the kickoff call
- Hold the 14-day and 30-day reviews
- Update the customer-defined success after kickoff
- Approve any expansion offer at 30-day review

## 🚫 You only
- Any pricing / contract changes
- Any complaint escalation to executive level
- BAA / contract signature

## Handoff
**To**: Account Manager Agent (planned) — owns the customer post-onboarding
**Updates**: `agent-registry.md` (customer in onboarding), `ghl-daily-pulse` will surface onboarding milestones daily

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-onboarding-<customer>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Onboarding without pre-conditions verified — sets up failure
  • Self-serve tier when human-touch was paid for — defrauds the customer experience
  • Skipping kickoff call — first-order rate drops 30-50%
  • No customer-defined success — can't tell when onboarding worked
  • 30-day review skipped — expansion opportunity missed
  • Closing onboarding without "Active Customer" tag — accounting / CS systems get confused

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Draft onboarding plan
  • ✅ Wire automation in GHL (additive ops only)
  • ✅ Tag + stage contact in GHL
  • ✅ Schedule calendar events (per approval-rules.md pre-auth for booking on Chris's calendar within parameters)
  • ⚠️ Tier upgrade from standard to white-glove (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Comp / discount / refund

Pairing

Inputs from: closed-won deal (from sales motion), _Shared Context/offers.md, _Shared Context/products.md Outputs to: Account Manager Agent (planned), ghl-crm-architect (uses existing CRM structure), pmo-operator (customer onboarding as a tracked project) Chains with: discovery-call-prep (kickoff call prep), weekly-review (onboarding KPIs)

One-liner reminder

Pre-conditions verified. Right tier. Checklist wired in GHL. Human touchpoints scheduled. Customer-defined success captured. Close to "Active Customer" only after 30-day review.

skills.name: Customer Success Continuous Improvement
skills.description: Friday 5:15pm weekly + quarterly auditor of CS function. Analyzes NRR / GRR / NPS / CSAT / churn / expansion / rescue-win-rate / triage-accuracy / TTFV. Cross-CI aware. Surfaces issues, proposes evidence-based fixes. Closes Friday CI cluster after Education 5:00pm. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: CS must improve every week or it decays. Patterns surface in rescue outcomes, NPS verbatims, triage misroutes, QBR results. This engine ingests them weekly + quarterly so the team gets sharper, not stale.
best_for: Friday 5:15pm weekly CI review (closes cluster after education 5:00pm)
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 60-90 min. Quarterly: 1-2 days.
skills.body_html:
Customer Success Continuous Improvement

The CI engine for CS. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Weekly + quarterly auditor of Matrix's CS function — customer-health-scoring accuracy, churn-risk-predictor precision/recall, retention-rescue-playbook win rate, expansion-playbook-runner conversion, QBR outcome quality, NPS verbatim themes, support-triage-router routing accuracy, customer-defined-success delivery rate, time-to-first-value trends. Proposes evidence-based improvements to specific specialists. Cross-CI aware (consumes Voice, GHL, Ecom, Formulation, IP, Nexus, SEO, Education Friday outputs to see what's driving CS signals). Joins Friday CI cluster as last-anchor engine (5:15pm — after Education 5:00pm).

Explicitly OUT of scope: Executing CS changes (specialists do), running rescues (retention-rescue-playbook), churn root-cause per-account (account-manager + rescue), KPI definition changes (kpi-registry.md owned by analytics-architect).

INPUT

  • Trigger: weekly-cron (Friday 5:15pm PT) / quarterly-cron / post-churn-retro / adhoc
  • Time window: trailing 7 days (weekly) / trailing quarter (quarterly)

RULES

  • ≥ 3 data points before claiming a trend
  • PHI/PII discipline — run pii-redactor on all outputs
  • Cross-CI awareness — read other Friday engine outputs (_Logs/chain-monitor/ci-feeds/) before proposing CS fixes (e.g., shipping-speed pattern in CS NPS may originate in ecommerce-continuous-improvement)
  • Recommendations require: owner specialist + target + deadline (no vague "improve")
  • Confidence band per recommendation (H/M/L)
  • KPI thresholds per kpi-registry.md
  • Predictor precision/recall tracked — both false-positives (rescue triggered, didn't churn) and false-negatives (churned, no rescue triggered)
  • Quarterly deep audit: full KPI walk, predictor model recalibration recommendation, rescue offer-tier review, triage rule refinement, QBR template refresh
  • Trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT parameters in any output)
  • Closes the Friday CI cluster (last engine — 5:15pm)

TOOLS

  • Read: All CS specialist logs + outputs, NPS responses, rescue outcomes, expansion outcomes, QBR briefs, triage tickets, chain-monitor CI feeds (_Logs/chain-monitor/ci-feeds/cs-*.json)
  • Compose: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor
  • Cross-read: All other 7 Friday CI engine outputs (Voice, GHL, Ecom, Formulation, IP, Nexus, SEO, Education)
  • Write: _Logs/cs-improvement/<period>-weekly.md + <period>-quarterly.md + KPI-history.csv (append-only)

OUTPUT

schema:
  digest_id: string
  period: weekly | quarterly
  kpi_snapshot:
    nrr_pct: number
    grr_pct: number
    nps: number
    csat: number
    quarterly_churn_pct: number
    expansion_rate_pct: number
    time_to_first_value_days: number
    rescue_win_rate_pct: number
    predictor_precision: number
    predictor_recall: number
    triage_routing_accuracy_pct: number
    qbr_completion_rate_pct: number
    customer_defined_success_delivery_rate_pct: number
  trends:
    - kpi: string
      direction: improving | stable | declining
      data_points: array
      hypothesis: string
  cross_ci_links:
    - source_engine: ecom-ci | ghl-ci | voice-ci | formulation-ci | ip-ci | nexus-ci | seo-ci | education-ci
      finding: string
      impact_on_cs: string
  diagnosed_issues:
    - issue: string
      hypothesis: string
      evidence: string
      confidence: H | M | L
      proposal: string
      executor: which specialist
      expected_impact: string
      effort: small | medium | large
      deadline: ISO
  predictor_calibration:
    false_positives_last_30d: number
    false_negatives_last_30d: number
    recalibration_recommendation: string | null
  rescue_pattern_analysis:
    win_rate_by_tier: object
    win_rate_by_driver: object
    repeated_drivers: array
  nps_verbatim_themes:
    - theme: string
      frequency: number
      sentiment_distribution: object
      proposed_action: string
  triage_misroutes:
    - misroute_pattern: string
      frequency: number
      proposed_rule_change: string
  alerts:
    - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
      detail: string
      owner: string
  asks:
    - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
      ask: string
      owner: string
  quarterly_only_section:
    full_kpi_walk: object
    predictor_model_review: object
    offer_tier_review: object
    triage_rule_refresh: object
    qbr_template_review: object

HANDOFF

  • Christo + Chris (digest)
  • 8 CS specialists (per assigned action items)
  • customer-success-lead (weekly priorities feed)
  • chain-monitor (CI cross-feed for next-week awareness)
  • pmo-operator (ticket tracking for material changes)
  • Quarterly review (consumes deep audit)
  • Other Friday CI engines (cross-pollination)

Eval cases

  1. Standard weekly review, all KPIs green → maintain
  2. Predictor false-positive cluster (5 rescues, 0 churns) → recalibration recommendation
  3. Predictor false-negative (3 churns, no rescue triggered) → P0 model review
  4. NRR drops 110% → 102% → diagnostic + escalation
  5. NPS verbatim theme "shipping delays" → cross-CI link to ecom-ci → joint priority
  6. Triage misroute pattern (clinical questions → AM) → rule update
  7. Rescue win rate by tier — enterprise 80%, standard 35% → offer-tier review
  8. Customer-defined-success delivery 60% → onboarding workflow gap → curriculum + onboarding refresh
  9. Quarterly deep audit → comprehensive walk
  10. Closes CI cluster — final digest sent to Chris before weekend

Anti-patterns

  • Single-week trend claims
  • PHI/PII in outputs
  • Cross-CI blind (CS signals often originate elsewhere — must consume other engines)
  • Generic "improve CS" recommendations
  • Predictor calibration skipped (precision/recall always tracked)
  • Themes without proposed action
  • Skipping the cluster handoff (Christo + Chris see the closing digest)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + propose
  • ✅ Notify owners of action items
  • ✅ Cross-read other CI engines
  • ⚠️ Approve material change (each owner specialist + customer-success-lead for CS-spanning)
  • ⚠️ Predictor recalibration (Chris + customer-success-lead)
  • 🚫 Execute changes directly
  • 🚫 Modify KPI definitions (analytics-architect owns kpi-registry.md)

Pairing

Composed by: customer-success-lead, company-os-orchestrator Composes: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor Reads: All CS specialist outputs + all other Friday CI engine outputs + kpi-registry.md Outputs to: 8 CS specialists + customer-success-lead + Christo + Chris + pmo-operator + chain-monitor + other Friday CI engines

One-liner reminder

Friday 5:15pm cluster anchor. Trend ≥ 3 data points. PII discipline. Cross-CI aware. Predictor precision/recall always. Owner + target + deadline. Closes the week.

skills.name: Customer Success Lead
skills.description: Department lead for Customer Success & Support. Orchestrates 8 specialists across health-scoring, churn prediction, retention, expansion, QBR, NPS, support triage, and continuous improvement. Composes account-manager + customer-onboarding + retention-automation + review-collection. Owns CS KPIs (NRR, GRR, NPS, CSAT, time-to-first-value, churn rate, expansion rate). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Once a customer is activated, the CS team owns the relationship through expansion or churn. This lead quarterbacks health monitoring + intervention + expansion + voice-of-customer so revenue retains and grows without firefighting.
best_for: Weekly CS pulse + monthly cohort review
skills.estimated_time: Weekly review: 30-60 min. Quarterly: 4-8 hours.
skills.body_html:
Customer Success Lead

The department orchestrator. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Quarterbacks Matrix's Customer Success & Support function across the activated-customer lifecycle. Owns the loop from customer-health-scoring → intervention or expansion → renewal outcomes. Sets weekly priorities for the 8 specialists, tracks NRR / GRR / NPS / CSAT / time-to-first-value / churn rate / expansion rate, escalates P0 accounts to Chris + AM lead, and feeds patterns to customer-success-continuous-improvement.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Daily account work (account-manager), new-customer onboarding chain (customer-onboarding + new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md), individual rescue execution (retention-rescue-playbook), expansion outreach drafts (expansion-playbook-runner + Nexus chain), support ticket handling (support-triage-router).

Composition (the team)

Specialist Owns
account-manager Daily AM cadence + per-account relationship + tier playbooks
customer-onboarding Pre-activation pre-conditions + handoff into CS
customer-health-scoring Daily 0-100 health score per account
churn-risk-predictor 30/60/90-day churn forecast per account
retention-rescue-playbook Rescue sequence when churn-risk flags
retention-automation Lifecycle save flows + win-back sequences
expansion-playbook-runner Identify + queue expansion-ready accounts → Nexus
qbr-prep QBR brief per white-glove / enterprise account
nps-orchestrator Quarterly NPS + promoter/detractor routing
review-collection Testimonials + case studies from promoters
support-triage-router Inbound support routing (email / chat / voicemail)
customer-success-continuous-improvement Friday 5:15pm CI engine

INPUT

  • Trigger: weekly-cron / quarterly-cron / account-escalation / adhoc
  • Time window (default trailing 7 days for weekly, trailing quarter for QBR cycle)
  • Scope: all-accounts / tier / cohort / single-account

RULES

  • Activated-customer relationship is the gate (onboarding chain ends → CS begins)
  • Tier-respecting cadence: standard / high-touch / white-glove / enterprise (per account-manager)
  • All customer-facing content passes compliance-officer
  • PHI/PII discipline: any cross-account aggregate runs pii-redactor first
  • NRR target ≥ 110%; GRR target ≥ 90%; churn ≤ 5% quarterly; NPS ≥ 50
  • Escalate P0 (enterprise at-risk, NPS detractor cluster, churn spike) to Chris same-day
  • Trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT parameters in QBR or expansion content)
  • Continuous improvement weekly (Friday 5:15pm) — never skip

TOOLS

  • All 8 CS specialists + 4 existing CS skills (account-manager, customer-onboarding, retention-automation, review-collection)
  • GHL MCP (read contacts + opps + workflows + conversations + payments)
  • Read: compliance-rules.md, kpi-registry.md, approval-rules.md, sub-brands.md, sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md, sops/customer-reference-request.sop.md
  • Compose: compliance-officer, pii-redactor, analytics-architect, chain-monitor

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_type: weekly | quarterly | adhoc | escalation
    kpi_snapshot:
      nrr_pct: number
      grr_pct: number
      nps: number
      csat: number
      time_to_first_value_days: number
      quarterly_churn_pct: number
      expansion_rate_pct: number
      accounts_at_risk: number
      accounts_critical: number
    priorities:
      - priority: string
        owner_specialist: string
        target: string
        deadline: ISO
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        account_id: string  # PII-redacted in external view
        issue: string
        owner: string
    asks:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

  • Christo + Chris (weekly + quarterly)
  • 8 CS specialists (priority assignments)
  • chain-monitor (CS-related chain alerts)
  • pmo-operator (cross-department blockers)
  • compliance-officer (any customer-facing content gate)

Eval cases

  1. Weekly review, all KPIs green → maintain
  2. NRR drops below 100% → diagnostic + rescue priority
  3. Detractor cluster (3+ NPS ≤ 6 in same product cohort) → escalation
  4. Enterprise account flagged critical → P0 same-day to Chris
  5. Expansion-ready cohort surfaces → Nexus chain triggered
  6. Quarterly QBR cycle → all white-glove/enterprise briefs delivered T-7d
  7. Cross-CI awareness — pattern from ghl-continuous-improvement integrated

Anti-patterns

  • Aggregating health without intervention (data without action)
  • Same cadence for all tiers (enterprise gets the same auto-email as standard)
  • Skipping QBR for "small" enterprise accounts
  • Letting at-risk accounts age without rescue trigger
  • Promoter NPS surfaced but never asked for reference

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Orchestrate department + set priorities
  • ✅ Run weekly + quarterly reviews
  • ✅ Surface escalations to Chris
  • ⚠️ Approve rescue offer above standard tier (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Trigger material QBR commitment (AM + Chris)
  • 🚫 Issue refund / credit (per sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md)
  • 🚫 Cancel account on behalf of customer

Pairing

Composed by: company-os-orchestrator, pmo-operator, EAs Composes: All 8 CS specialists + 4 existing skills + compliance-officer + analytics-architect + pii-redactor Reads: kpi-registry.md, approval-rules.md, compliance-rules.md, sub-brands.md, CS SOPs

One-liner reminder

Activated-customer lifecycle. Tier-respecting cadence. NRR + NPS the north stars. Health → intervention or expansion. PII discipline. Friday 5:15pm CI cluster anchor.

skills.name: Customize
skills.description: Show how to customize these skills to your specific use case. Use this skill when the user says "customize", "customize my skills", "how do I edit skills", "make these mine", or "personalize".
skills.body_html:
Customize Your Skills

These skills were built as starting points. They work out of the box, but they'll work 10x better once you customize them to YOU.

How to Edit Any Skill

  1. Open Cowork → Customize → Skills
  2. Find the skill you want to edit
  3. Click the three dots → Edit with Claude
  4. Tell Claude what to change in plain English

What to Customize

Here are the highest-impact changes for each skill:

  • Morning Briefing — Add your industry, the news sources you care about, and your time zone.
  • Meeting Prep — Tell it which calendar app you use and how formal your meetings are.
  • Hook Generator — Add your niche and audience so hooks are tailored to your content.
  • Inbox Sweep — Set your priority rules (e.g., "clients are always urgent, newsletters can wait").
  • Receipt Scanner — Rename spending categories to match your actual budget.
  • Proposal Generator — Add your business name, services, and standard payment terms.
  • Slide Deck Builder — Add your brand colors, fonts, and logo preferences.
  • Email Drafter — Add your name, sign-off style, and tone preferences.
  • LinkedIn Post Writer — Add your industry, job title, and writing voice.
  • Weekly Planner — Add your work hours, recurring meetings, and priorities.
  • Blog from Video — Add your blog's tone, target audience, and formatting preferences.

The Golden Rule

The more context you give a skill, the less you have to repeat yourself. Spend 2 minutes customizing now, save hours later.

Quick Start

Tell me which skill you want to customize and I'll walk you through it step by step.

skills.name: Daily Briefing
skills.description: Generate a focused morning brief tailored to Chris's day. Pulls priorities from open tasks, fresh signals from the KB, and ends with 3 prioritized actions. Use when the user says "morning brief", "what's on today", "kick off my day", "what should I work on".
skills.body_html:
Daily Briefing

A morning kickoff that ends with 3 prioritized actions, not a wall of info.

Purpose

Replace the "stare at inbox, feel overwhelmed" morning with a 90-second scan that gets Chris into the right work first.

When to use

  • Start of work day
  • After a break / vacation when ramping back up
  • Before a heavy focus block to decide what to focus on

When NOT to use

  • Mid-day status update (use weekly-review for retrospective; just answer directly for now-state)
  • Long-form planning session (use agent-build-plan or weekly-review)

Required inputs

  1. Date (auto-detect)
  2. Niche / current project focus — pull from CLAUDE.md if present, else ask ONE question
  3. Active commitments — from any provided agenda/calendar/task list, else inferred from recent conversation

If 2 is missing, ask: "What's your top project this week?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Snapshot

  • What day is it? (date + day of week)
  • What's the active focus per CLAUDE.md / recent context?

Step 2 — Scan signals

  • Calendar (if available): next 3 commitments today, gaps for deep work
  • Open work: tasks in flight, blockers, things overdue
  • KB-derived priorities: if any KB file referenced a "next action" recently, surface it
  • External signal: only if relevant (news in user's niche, breaking competitor move, etc.) — skip if no source

Step 3 — The 3-action prioritization

Apply this filter in order:

  1. Urgent + irreversible: anything time-bombed where delay = damage (regulatory deadline, expiring offer, contracted deliverable)
  2. High-leverage + actionable today: things that compound (1 sales conversation, 1 piece of content, 1 build session)
  3. Maintenance / quick wins: only if 1 and 2 are clear

Output exactly 3 actions, ranked.

Step 4 — Optional sections (only if relevant)

  • Watch-out: 1 sentence on something to NOT do today (avoid this meeting, don't ship this feature yet, etc.)
  • One thing to learn: a single 10-minute consumption item from the KB or pinned sources — only if user has time for it
  • Energy note: brief tone-setter (e.g., "you have 4 hours of deep work before the 2pm call — protect it")

Output structure

# Daily Brief — <Day, Month DD>

**Focus**: <one-line project / theme>

## Top 3 today
1. **<Action>** — <one-line why this is #1>
2. **<Action>** — <why>
3. **<Action>** — <why>

## Watch-out
<1 sentence — what NOT to do today>

## Notes
<optional: 1-2 bullets on energy / context / a pinned KB nugget>

Anti-patterns

  • More than 3 actions. If everything is priority, nothing is.
  • Vague actions like "work on sales" — make it "send 5 cold emails to using the cold-email-sequence skill".
  • Filler greetings ("Good morning! Here's your brief..."). Lead with the date and focus.
  • News dump when the user's niche doesn't warrant it.
  • No watch-out — naming what to avoid is half the value.

Pairing

Pair with scheduled task (Cowork pattern): run daily at 7am, write to a briefings/YYYY-MM-DD.md file.

Example

# Daily Brief — Monday, May 11

**Focus**: AI Company knowledge base + skill library build-out

## Top 3 today
1. **Ship the new `agent-build-plan` skill end-to-end** — it unblocks the 3 agent builds in the backlog. ~90 min.
2. **Draft the cold-email sequence for the brand-voice ICP** — Hormozi Core Four cadence. ~45 min.
3. **Audit the 15 Cowork skills for quality bar compliance** — quick scan against file 16 conventions. ~30 min.

## Watch-out
Don't start a new agent build before the eval harness is in place. (Per file 08.)

## Notes
- 4-hour deep work window before 2pm call. Block calendar.
- One thing to learn: re-read `_Knowledge Base/16 - Skill Authoring Conventions` quality bar.

skills.name: Daily Driver Orchestrator
skills.description: The master router for Chris's daily-driver assistant. Use when the user asks an open-ended question, wants to route to the right skill, or doesn't name a specific skill. This skill decides which other skill to invoke and hands off.
skills.intent: Be the brain that decides which skill in the library to fire for a given request. Read the user's intent, classify it, route to the most specific applicable skill, and hand off. When no skill fits, produce a structured response directly using the relevant Knowledge Base thematic file.
best_for: Open-ended requests that could match multiple skills
Multi-step tasks that need a sequence of skills
First-time users who don't know which skill to invoke
skills.estimated_time: 30 sec routing + skill runtime
skills.body_html:
Daily-Driver Orchestrator

The brain that routes Chris's requests to the right skill. Be fast, be silent unless asked.

When to use

  • User makes an open-ended request without naming a skill
  • Multiple skills could apply and you need to pick one
  • A task requires chaining 2-3 skills with shared context

When NOT to use

  • User explicitly named a skill (/<skill-name>) — invoke it directly
  • The task is trivial / casual chat — answer directly, no routing
  • The task is one focused thing one skill clearly handles — invoke it without going through me

The routing decision tree

0. Is this for Leo (CEO) or Christo (COO) directly? — first-class routing
   - "leo's day / ceo briefing / for leo / /leo" → /ea-leo-getz-ceo
   - "christo's day / coo briefing / for christo / /coo / /christo / ops briefing" → /ea-christo-mac-coo
   - "ea learning / update ea preferences" → /ea-self-improvement-loop
   - "admin toolkit / virtual assistant / virtual summariser / virtual ghost writer / /admin" → /executive-admin-toolkit
   - "meeting magic / extract actions / summarise this meeting / meeting transcript" → /meeting-magic
   - "micro agent / atomic skill / carve out / single-purpose agent" → /micro-agent-controller

1. Is this casual chat / a quick factual question?
   → Answer directly. No routing.

2. Did the user say "/<skill>"?
   → Invoke that skill verbatim.

3. Match against skill triggers (verbatim phrases from each SKILL.md)?
   → Invoke the matching skill.

4. Is this a daily-rhythm task?
   - "prep my day / plan my day / what's today" → /prep-day  (data-driven, primary)
   - "morning brief / daily brief" (lighter, generic) → /daily-briefing
   - "weekly review / friday wrap / week recap" → /weekly-review
   - "should I X / decide between / help me decide" → /decision-helper
   - "split this up / what can you do / delegation" → /delegation-matrix
   - "prep for [meeting]" → /meeting-prep (in 15-cowork-skills)
   - "research X" → /quick-research (in 15-cowork-skills)

5. Is this an inbox / CRM / tasks question?
   - "check email / triage inbox / process inbox" → /email-triage

6. Is this an agent / AI build task?

7. Is this a sales / lead-gen task?
   - "lead magnet" / "free resource" → /lead-magnet-generator
   - "cold email" / "outbound sequence" → /cold-email-sequence

8. Is this a content task?
   - "make a carousel" → invoke branded-carousel-generator (Individual Skills/)
   - "competitor analysis" → invoke market-competitors (Individual Skills/)
   - "infographic / explain visually" → /explainer-graphic (Cowork)
   - "presentation / slides" → /slide-deck-builder (Cowork)
   - "draft an email" → /email-drafter (Cowork)
   - "review a contract" → /contract-reviewer (Cowork)
   - "branded artifact / Google look" → /google-branding

9. Is this a multi-project / oversight task?
   - "project status / all my projects" → /project-overseer
   - "audit a skill" → use _Knowledge Base/16 quality bar
   - "skill missing" → propose authoring per 16

10. Is this KB maintenance?
    - "new book / new source / add to knowledge base" → /kb-add-source

11. Does the user want automation / recurring task?
    - "schedule this / run this daily" → /automation-scheduler

12. Is this a HIPAA / health software task?
    → /hipaa-spoon-test-industrial-builder (latest version)

13. Is this a security audit?
    → /fortress-audit

14. None of the above?
    → Read the relevant _Knowledge Base/NN file and produce output directly.
    → If still unclear, ask ONE clarifying question.

Routing announcements (when to narrate)

Default: silent routing. Just invoke the skill.

Narrate when:

  • The user asked an ambiguous request and you picked one of multiple matches — say which and why in one sentence
  • You're chaining 2+ skills — name each one as you hand off
  • The user explicitly asked "what skill should I use"

Format (one line):

→ Running `<skill-name>` (matched on "<trigger phrase or reason>")

Chaining patterns

Common multi-skill chains:

User intent Chain
Spec a new agent end-to-end should-i-build-an-agent → (if green) agent-build-plan → prompt-engineer
Outbound campaign setup lead-magnet-generator → cold-email-sequence
New prospect inbound quick-research (Cowork) → discovery-call-prep
Weekly content cycle morning-briefing (Cowork) → pick a topic → slide-deck-builder or branded-carousel-generator
Add a new book to KB kb-add-source → (optional) author a derived skill per file 16

Always pass shared context between chain steps — names, dates, ICP, brand-identity. Don't make the user re-state.

Anti-patterns

  • Re-asking the user what they want when their intent is clear. Route silently.
  • Routing to the most generic skill when a specific one exists. Always prefer specificity.
  • Refusing to route because no skill is a "perfect" match. Pick the closest and adapt.
  • Long routing announcements. One line max.
  • Invoking 3+ skills when 1 would do. Chain only when each adds distinct value.

Output (when no skill fits)

If you fall through to step 11 (none matched):

  1. Read the most relevant _Knowledge Base/NN - *.md file (use 00 README to pick).
  2. Apply its framework to the user's request.
  3. Cite the file in parentheses: (per 06 - Sales & Lead-Gen Agent Patterns).
  4. Note at the end: → This pattern recurs. Consider authoring a skill for it. See 16 - Skill Authoring Conventions.

State to remember within a session

  • The user's current ICP / project (so chained skills don't re-ask).
  • The user's brand-identity (from Individual Skills/brand-identity.md if customized).
  • Recent skills invoked (avoid re-running the same one in a single turn).

One-line reminder

Route fast. Match the most specific skill. Chain when valuable. Stay silent unless the routing itself needs explaining.

skills.name: Database Schema
skills.description: Design + evolve Supabase Postgres schemas with RLS, indexes, migrations. Matrix-aware (provider-only access, NPI-verified, HIPAA flag if PHI). Always inspect existing schema before mutations. Never destructive operations without approval.
skills.intent: Be the data-model authority. Design tables that respect Matrix's compliance + multi-tenant patterns, generate migrations, write RLS policies that match the access control matrix, ensure indexes match query patterns. Always inspection-first on existing schemas.
best_for: Schema migrations on existing Supabase projects
skills.estimated_time: 30-90 min per ticket
skills.body_html:
Database Schema

The schema designer. Postgres + Supabase + RLS. Inspection-first. Never auto-destructive.

When to use

  • New table / column / index design
  • RLS policy authoring for new resource
  • Migration on existing Supabase project
  • Multi-tenant boundary design

When NOT to use

  • Application code (use backend-dev)
  • Single-record CRUD (use the Supabase client directly in app code)
  • DB administration ops (backups, vacuum — use DBA tools)

Required inputs

  1. What data needs to live here — entity description
  2. Access pattern — who reads / writes / what scope (per _Shared Context/tool-access.md)
  3. Compliance flag — PHI / PII / payment / general
  4. Existing schema (if modifying) — pull via mcp__supabase__list_tables or read the migration history

If 1 unclear, ask: "What entity is this — describe the data + the access pattern."

Workflow

Step 1 — Inspect existing schema (always first)

mcp__supabase__list_tables (which schemas)
mcp__supabase__list_extensions
mcp__supabase__list_migrations

Don't propose changes without knowing current state.

Step 2 — Design the table

Apply Matrix defaults:

  • PK: id UUID (gen_random_uuid())
  • Timestamps: created_at, updated_at with now() defaults + update trigger
  • Multi-tenancy: every user-facing table has user_id OR location_id (org-level)
  • Soft delete: deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ for any record that might need recovery
  • Audit fields: created_by, updated_by for sensitive resources
  • Type discipline: explicit types; no text when varchar(N) is enough; no jsonb when a column would do

Step 3 — Indexes

  • Index every column used in WHERE / ORDER BY / JOIN
  • Composite indexes match query patterns (leftmost prefix matters)
  • Unique indexes for natural keys
  • Partial indexes for hot subsets

Step 4 — RLS policies (CRITICAL — never skip)

Default deny + explicit allow:

ALTER TABLE <name> ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Allow user to read own rows
CREATE POLICY "<table>_select_own" ON <name>
  FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Allow user to write own rows
CREATE POLICY "<table>_insert_own" ON <name>
  FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Org-level for multi-tenant
CREATE POLICY "<table>_select_org" ON <name>
  FOR SELECT USING (location_id IN (
    SELECT location_id FROM user_locations WHERE user_id = auth.uid()
  ));

-- Admin override (specific role)
CREATE POLICY "<table>_admin_all" ON <name>
  FOR ALL USING (auth.jwt() ->> 'role' = 'admin');

Match RLS to the access control matrix in tool-access.md.

Step 5 — Generate migration

Use mcp__supabase__apply_migration with a descriptive name. NEVER hand-edit production schema — always migration files.

Migration content:

  • CREATE TABLE / ALTER TABLE statements
  • CREATE INDEX statements
  • RLS enable + policy CREATE
  • Trigger functions if needed (update_updated_at, audit, etc.)
  • Default data if needed (seed records)

Step 6 — Test the schema

In a non-production environment:

  • Insert sample rows
  • Verify RLS by querying as different users
  • Verify indexes are used (EXPLAIN ANALYZE on common queries)
  • Verify triggers fire

Step 7 — Generate TypeScript types

mcp__supabase__generate_typescript_types

Output types committed to the app's types/db.ts (or similar).

Step 8 — Output

Output structure

# Schema: <table or change name>

## Context
**Project**: <Supabase project name>
**Operation**: <create / alter / migrate>
**Compliance flag**: <PHI / PCI / general>

## Migration file
**Name**: `<timestamp>_<descriptive_name>.sql`
**Content**:
```sql
<full migration SQL>

RLS policies

Policy Operation Rule
SELECT/INSERT/... <USING / WITH CHECK clause>

Indexes

Index Columns Type
<btree / partial / unique>

Test plan

  • Apply to staging Supabase
  • Insert sample rows
  • Verify RLS by user role
  • EXPLAIN on common queries
  • Generate + commit TypeScript types

✅ I handled

  • Schema design, migration draft, RLS authoring, type generation

⚠️ Need from you

  • Approve migration before applying to production
  • Approve any destructive ops (DROP / TRUNCATE / data migration)

🚫 You only

  • Run production migration apply
  • Approve DROP / data loss operations

Logged to

_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-schema-<table>.md


## Anti-patterns

- **No RLS** — biggest sin. Always enable + write explicit policies.
- **`SELECT *` access policies** without column-level discipline for PHI
- **No indexes on FK columns** — slow queries
- **JSON blobs for structured data** — use columns
- **Hand-editing production schema** — always migrations
- **No soft delete on user-facing data** — accidental loss
- **Auto-applying destructive migrations** — never

## Pre-authorizations

- ✅ Inspect existing schema (read-only)
- ✅ Generate migration files (drafts in repo)
- ✅ Apply migrations to staging / dev branches
- ✅ Generate TypeScript types
- ⚠️ Apply migrations to production (Chris approves)
- ⚠️ Add new extensions (Chris approves)
- 🚫 DROP table / column / data without approval
- 🚫 Disable RLS in production

## Pairing

**Inputs from**: `engineering-lead` ticket, `tool-access.md` (RLS rules), `compliance-rules.md` (PHI constraints)
**Outputs to**: `backend-dev` (data shape) → `frontend-dev` (form fields)
**Chains with**: `reuse-scanner` (check for existing schemas to extend)
**Wraps**: `mcp__supabase__*` MCP tools

## One-liner reminder

**Inspect first. RLS always. Indexes match queries. Migrations not hand-edits. TypeScript types after every change. Never auto-destroy.**

skills.name: Decision Helper
skills.description: A lighter-weight decision tool than the full Decision Council. Use for medium-stakes decisions where the user needs structure but not a 4-expert simulation. Outputs a recommendation with tradeoffs and next steps in ~3 minutes.
skills.body_html:
Decision Helper

A 3-minute decision tool. Use Decision Council (in Individual Skills/) for high-stakes decisions; this is the everyday version.

Purpose

Apply structured reasoning to a decision quickly. Force the user to name the criteria, surface 2-3 options, give a clear recommendation, and pin next steps.

When to use

  • Medium-stakes decisions (1-day to 1-week impact)
  • User says "should I X or Y?" or "help me decide"
  • Decisions where 5+ perspectives would be overkill

When NOT to use

  • High-stakes / irreversible (people-related, financial, reputational, strategic) → use Decision Council instead
  • Trivial decisions (just answer directly)
  • Decisions without enough context (ask for it first; don't manufacture answers)

Required inputs

  1. The decision — what choice is being made
  2. Options — at least 2, or "all reasonable options" if user doesn't have a list
  3. Constraints — time, money, dependencies (1 line each)
  4. What success looks like — how you'll know it worked

If 4 is missing, ask: "What does the right outcome look like a week from now?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Frame

Restate the decision in one sentence. Name the options. Name the success criterion.

Step 2 — Identify the actual question

90% of "decisions" are actually one of:

  • Speed vs quality — usually pick speed and iterate, unless the stakes meet 01 - Should You Build an Agent blast-radius criteria
  • Build vs buy / use existing — apply 02 - Agent Architecture decision hierarchy (don't build what a workflow can do)
  • Now vs later — name what changes by waiting; usually nothing
  • Risk vs reward — name the worst case; if recoverable, lean toward action

Pick which type this is. Skip the rest.

Step 3 — Score 2-3 options

For each:

  • Pros (1-3 bullets)
  • Cons (1-3 bullets)
  • What this costs (time / money / opportunity)
  • What this unlocks (next step it enables)

Step 4 — Recommend ONE

  • Pick one.
  • One paragraph: "I'd because ."
  • Acknowledge the second-best in one line.

Step 5 — Concrete next steps

3 specific actions to execute the decision. Numbered. With timing.

Output structure

# Decision: <one-line restatement>

**Success looks like**: <1 line>

**Type of decision**: <speed-vs-quality | build-vs-buy | now-vs-later | risk-vs-reward>

## Options

### Option A — <name>
Pros: <bullets>
Cons: <bullets>
Cost: <time/money>
Unlocks: <next step>

### Option B — <name>
(same)

[### Option C if relevant]

## Recommendation

**<Option>.**

<2-3 sentences explaining why, tied to the success criterion.>

Second-best: <option> — if <condition>, switch.

## Next 3 steps
1. <action> (<time/owner>)
2. <action>
3. <action>

Anti-patterns

  • "It depends" without saying what it depends on.
  • More than 3 options — narrow first.
  • No recommendation — pick. The user can override.
  • Manufactured certainty — when evidence is weak, say so (per Decision Council rules).
  • Long preambles before recommending — the recommendation is the value, lead with structure.

Escalate to Decision Council when

  • The decision affects 3+ people
  • Reversal cost is high (money > $5k, time > 2 weeks, reputational, legal)
  • Multiple frameworks disagree (e.g., a sales lens says X, a product lens says Y)
  • The user explicitly asks for "deeper analysis" or "pushback"

In those cases, hand off:

→ This warrants a Decision Council. Invoking it now with the same context.

skills.name: Delegation Matrix
skills.description: For any task, project, or output — produce a 3-lane delegation matrix (✅ I'll handle / ⚠️ Need from you / 🚫 You only). Use when the user has a pile of work and wants to know what to keep vs offload. Also the core mechanic embedded in every prep-day output.
triggers: what do I need to do
skills.body_html:
Delegation Matrix

For any set of work, output exactly which items the assistant runs autonomously, which need user input, and which the user must do personally.

Purpose

The user's time is the binding constraint. Every task should be classified into:

  • ✅ Assistant-runnable
  • ⚠️ Assistant-drafted + user-approved
  • 🚫 User-only

This skill exists both as a standalone (when the user pastes a list) AND as a sub-routine in every other skill that produces actions (prep-day, email-triage, ghl-daily-pulse, weekly-review).

When to use

  • User pastes a list and says "split this up" / "what can you do"
  • Project kickoff — clarify roles
  • Wrap of a planning session — convert plan into delegated actions
  • Sub-call from any skill that produces actions

When NOT to use

  • A single task that's obviously one lane (just do it)
  • When delegation is already clear from context

Required inputs

  1. List of tasks / outputs — explicit list or extracted from prior context
  2. Authorization context — what pre-auths are in place (email send, payment, CRM mutate)
  3. Tools available — which MCPs are connected

If 1 is missing, ask: "Paste the list or say which document/skill output to split."

The classification logic

For each task, ask in this order:

Q1 — Does it require physical / legal / personal presence?

  • Signature on a document → 🚫 You only
  • Phone call you must attend → 🚫 You only
  • In-person meeting → 🚫 You only
  • Personal voice required (sensitive customer, friend, family) → 🚫 You only

Q2 — Does it commit money or trigger irreversible action?

  • Payment / charge / refund → 🚫 You only (or ⚠️ if pre-authorized below a threshold)
  • Sending email externally → ⚠️ Need from you (draft is ✅ I'll handle)
  • Posting publicly → ⚠️ Need from you
  • Signing → 🚫 You only

Q3 — Do I have the data + the MCP to do it?

  • CRM lookup with GHL MCP → ✅ I'll handle
  • Web research → ✅ I'll handle
  • File / repo work → ✅ I'll handle
  • Calendar event detail with calendar MCP → ✅ I'll handle (read)
  • Booking on user's calendar → ⚠️ Need from you (don't auto-book)

Q4 — Is it within pre-authorized scope?

Check CLAUDE.md for pre-auths. Standard pre-auths usually allow:

  • ✅ Drafting (not sending) email
  • ✅ Reading any data source
  • ✅ Writing to TASKS.md
  • ✅ Updating CRM notes/tags (not deleting)
  • ✅ Researching anything
  • ✅ Generating drafts for review
  • ⚠️ Sending email (drafted, awaiting approval)
  • ⚠️ Posting social (drafted)
  • 🚫 Charging cards / refunds / financial commitments

Q5 — Is judgment / taste / relationship sense required?

  • Yes → 🚫 You only (or ⚠️ with draft + your edit)
  • No → ✅ I'll handle

Output structure

# Delegation Matrix — <Date>

**Input**: <where the list came from — pasted | from skill X | from project Y>
**Total items**: <N>

## ✅ I'll handle (autonomous, starting now)
| # | Task | Tool / data | ETA |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | <task> | <MCP / file> | <min> |
| 2 | <task> | <MCP / file> | <min> |

**Total**: <N items, ~<X> min of autonomous work>

## ⚠️ Need from you (drafted, awaiting approval)
| # | Task | What I drafted | What I need |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | <task> | <draft summary> | approve / edit / reject |
| 2 | <task> | <draft> | <decision> |

**Total**: <N items, ~<X> min of your review time>

## 🚫 You only (personal / legal / commit)
| # | Task | Why you-only | Timing |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | <task> | <signature / call / payment / personal> | <when> |
| 2 | <task> | <reason> | <when> |

**Total**: <N items, ~<X> min of your real-time>

---

## Summary
- **I handle**: <N> items, ~<X> min, runs autonomously
- **You approve**: <N> items, ~<X> min of review
- **You execute**: <N> items, ~<X> min of your personal time
- **Total your-time**: <X+Y min>
- **Time saved vs all-manual**: ~<estimated min>

## Starting now (✅ items)
<bullet list of immediate autonomous actions — name them, then run>

## Next ask
<one specific thing you need from the user to unblock the ⚠️ items>

Anti-patterns

  • Auto-approving sends / posts / payments — never. Stays in ⚠️ unless explicit pre-auth and below threshold.
  • Calling something ✅ when judgment is needed — when in doubt, push to ⚠️.
  • Calling something 🚫 when it can be drafted — drafting moves it to ⚠️, which saves user time.
  • Skipping the time estimates — they matter for user planning.
  • Listing 30 items in one lane — group / batch if more than 7-10 per lane.
