## ⚠️ Blocked / decisions needed (Chris)
- <blocker> — <what's needed>

## 🚫 You only
- Approve launch gate
- Decide regulated-SKU policy

## Logged to
`_Logs/ecommerce-builds/<store>/<date>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Skipping the launch-gate checklist to "move fast" — half the post-launch bugs you'll see are in those boxes
  • Building the storefront before the schema is stable — UI re-work is the most expensive kind
  • Letting marketing lifecycle wait until "after launch" — abandoned-cart pays for itself in week 1
  • Single Stripe + Shippo + Supabase fan-out without idempotency — see integration-map.md; every webhook needs dedupe
  • No eval baseline before catalog work — you won't see regressions
  • Building from scratch when matrix-store patterns exist — clone, don't redo

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Decompose + sequence the build
  • ✅ Invoke any of the 12 specialists
  • ✅ Generate full plan docs + handoffs
  • ⚠️ Approve launch (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Approve regulated-SKU policy (Chris + compliance-officer)
  • 🚫 Charge customers / push live without approval

Pairing

Composes: All 13 e-commerce specialists below Inputs from: reuse-scanner, engineering-lead, creative-director, compliance-officer, analytics-architect, ghl-config.md, integration-map.md, brand-guide.md, sub-brands.md, products.md, pricing-margin Outputs to: devops-deployment (launch), account-manager (post-launch CS handoff), analytics-architect (KPI dashboard) Chains with: voice-ai-onboarding-agent (B2B portal kickoff), ghl-crm-architect (customer data flow)

One-line reminder

Foundations → catalog → plumbing → surface → ops → growth → launch gate. Every box checked. No shortcuts at the checkout flow.

skills.name: Ecommerce Lifecycle Marketing
skills.description: Build and operate the e-commerce lifecycle automations — abandoned cart, browse abandonment, post-purchase, replenishment, win-back, VIP. Email + SMS + voice fallback. Compliance-aware (CAN-SPAM, TCPA, A2P 10DLC). Clone of `cold-email-sequence` adapted for transactional/lifecycle ecom shape.
skills.intent: Capture the revenue sitting in abandoned carts. Drive repeat orders with timed replenishment. Win back churning customers. Each lifecycle is a versioned, evaluable, A/B-testable sequence — not a one-shot blast.
best_for: Adding new lifecycle (replenishment for a recurring SKU)
skills.estimated_time: Full 6-sequence lifecycle build: 2-4 weeks. Single sequence: 2-3 days.
skills.body_html:
E-commerce Lifecycle Marketing

The post-cart-pre-cart revenue motion. Versioned. Compliance-aware. Evaluable.

Lineage

Clones: cold-email-sequence Differs: transactional + behavioral triggers (cart events, order events) instead of cold-list seeding; ecom-shape templates; replenishment timing math; tighter compliance gates on regulated SKUs

When to use

  • Fresh store launching lifecycle motions
  • Existing flows underperforming
  • Adding a new lifecycle (replenishment for a new recurring SKU)
  • Sub-brand launch — needs lifecycle scaffolding

When NOT to use

  • Cold outreach to non-customers → use cold-email-sequence
  • Single broadcast campaign → use copywriting or cold-email-sequence (campaign mode)
  • Voice script → use voice-script-author

The standard 6-sequence lifecycle

# Sequence Trigger Channel mix Goal
1 Abandoned cart Cart with items, no checkout, 1h+ Email × 3 + SMS × 1 Recover the cart
2 Browse abandonment PDP viewed 3x, no add-to-cart Email × 2 Soft re-engage
3 Post-purchase Order delivered + 3 days Email × 3 Onboard + cross-sell
4 Replenishment Order delivered + (90% of typical reorder cycle) Email + SMS reminder Trigger repeat order
5 Win-back No order in 90 days (was active) Email × 3 Reactivate
6 VIP LTV > $X OR top 10% by order count Email + SMS + voice (high-value) Loyalty + retention

Required inputs

  1. Sub-brand voice — per sub-brands.md
  2. Compliance posture — regulated SKUs need different copy
  3. GHL workflow access — sequences live in GHL workflows, not custom code
  4. Existing baseline — open rate / CTR / conversion to measure against
  5. A2P status — SMS portions require approved campaigns

Sequence specifications

1. Abandoned cart

Trigger: cart_items exists for customer/anon, no checkout.session.completed, 1h since last activity

Step Timing Channel Tone CTA
1 T+1h Email "Forgot something?" — soft Return to cart
2 T+24h SMS (if opt-in) "Your cart's still here — Y/N?" Return + 1-tap reply
3 T+48h Email "Anything we can help with?" + show items Return + offer support
4 T+72h Email "Last chance — saving your spot for X more hours" Return + optional 5% incentive (NOT first send; flag-controlled)

After 72h: drop from sequence; cart eventually expires per inventory-manager.

2. Browse abandonment

Trigger: Customer (logged in or known via email) viewed same PDP 3+ times in 7 days without add-to-cart

Step Timing Channel CTA
1 +24h after 3rd view Email "Have a question about [product]?" + FAQ + chat link
2 +5 days Email Reviews + alternative SKUs in same category

3. Post-purchase (onboarding)

Trigger: order state → delivered + 3 days

Tailored to product type:

Peptide / Hormone (B2B provider):

  • T+3d: "Quick check-in — first administration go smoothly?" + dosing reference link + support escalation path
  • T+14d: "Patient outcomes so far?" + reorder reminder if applicable
  • T+30d: Reorder reminder + provider community invite

Supplement (MuscleLock D2C):

  • T+3d: "Day 3 — here's what to expect" + how-to + measurement protocol
  • T+14d: "Halfway through — track progress" + UGC ask
  • T+25d: "Running low — reorder + save with subscribe"

4. Replenishment

Trigger: delivered + (90% of typical cycle for that SKU)

Cycle by category:

  • Daily-use supplement (30-day supply) → ping at T+25d
  • Peptide vial (10-day course) → ping at T+8d
  • Subscription replenishment → handled by subscription system, not this sequence
  • Bulk-only SKUs → ping at T+(min cycle × 0.9)

Single email + SMS (if opt-in). Direct: "Time to reorder?" + 1-click reorder link.

5. Win-back

Trigger: customer active in last 12 months, no order in 90 days

Step Timing Tone Offer
1 T+90d "We miss you — anything we could improve?" No offer; relationship-first
2 T+105d "Here's what's new" — show 3 new arrivals or category updates No offer
3 T+120d "Come back" — small incentive (10% / free shipping / etc.) Limited-time

After 3 sends: drop to quarterly newsletter cadence; tag winback:exhausted.

6. VIP

Trigger: customer in top 10% by LTV OR by order count over 12 months

  • Monthly: VIP-only product previews / early access
  • Quarterly: handwritten note from AM (account-manager skill triggers)
  • Birthday / anniversary: SMS + gift
  • Reorder: white-glove ("Your usual ready to ship?")

Channel rules (TCPA / CAN-SPAM / A2P)

  • Email: must have CAN-SPAM footer + unsubscribe + physical address; unsubscribe honored ≤ 10 days (we honor ≤ 1h)
  • SMS: requires explicit opt-in at signup or checkout; STOP/HELP wired; quiet hours enforced (8am-9pm local); A2P 10DLC campaign approved per _Shared Context/ghl-config.md
  • Voice (VIP only): respect quiet hours; AM-initiated, not automated cold

Workflow

Step 1 — Audit existing flows

  • Read current GHL workflows
  • Identify gaps vs the 6-sequence canon
  • Measure baselines (open / CTR / conversion / revenue per send)

Step 2 — Design (per sequence)

  • Use sequence spec table above as starting point
  • Customize timing to product category
  • Compliance-officer reviews any regulated-SKU copy

Step 3 — Write copy

  • Per send: subject + preview + body + CTA
  • Brand voice from sub-brands.md
  • Compliance-checked for regulated SKUs
  • Mobile-formatted (most opens are mobile)

Step 4 — Build in GHL

  • Workflow per sequence
  • Triggers: cart events, order events, custom-field timers
  • Branches by SKU type / customer tier
  • Hand-off via workflow-automation-builder to deploy

Step 5 — A/B framework

  • Every send has variant A (control) + B (test)
  • Hypothesis logged in _Logs/lifecycle/<sequence>/experiments.md
  • Sample size adequate before declaring winner
  • Winning variant becomes control for next round

Step 6 — Eval harness baseline

  • Invoke ghl-eval-harness baseline before launching
  • Establish: deliverability rate, unsubscribe rate, conversion rate

Step 7 — Launch + monitor

  • Soft launch: 10% of eligible customers
  • Monitor 48h for deliverability / complaints / unsubscribes
  • Ramp to 100% if clean

Step 8 — Weekly review

  • Part of ghl-continuous-improvement (Fridays)
  • Per-sequence performance vs baseline
  • Identify drop-offs / fix opportunities

Anti-patterns

  • Abandoned-cart spam — > 4 sends in 72h destroys list
  • Generic post-purchase ("Thanks for ordering!") — wastes the highest-engagement moment
  • Replenishment timing wrong — sending replenishment 1 week after delivery for a 90-day supply
  • Win-back with no relationship — leads with discount; should lead with "we miss you"
  • Regulated-SKU copy with efficacy claims — fast track to FTC attention
  • SMS without A2P approval — carrier-blocked
  • Skipping A/B framework — flying blind
  • Identical sequences across sub-brands — voice mismatch

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design + draft sequences
  • ✅ Build in GHL staging
  • ✅ A/B variants
  • ⚠️ Launch live (Chris reviews first deploy of each sequence)
  • ⚠️ Add SMS portion (requires A2P approved per ghl-config.md)
  • 🚫 Send to unverified list / non-consented contacts

Pairing

Inputs from: ecommerce-backend (cart + order events), order-lifecycle-manager (state triggers), product-catalog-builder (category attributes for replenishment cycles), sub-brands.md, compliance-rules.md, ghl-config.md Outputs to: ghl-continuous-improvement (weekly review), analytics-architect (lifecycle revenue reports), account-manager (VIP escalations) Chains with: workflow-automation-builder (deploys to GHL), cold-email-sequence (peer for non-ecom contexts), voice-ai-support-agent (replenishment voice variant), sms-campaign-builder (SMS portions)

One-line reminder

6 sequences. Versioned + A/B. Compliance-aware. Regulated copy passes compliance-officer. Soft-launch then ramp. Weekly review in CI.

skills.name: Ecommerce Scaffolder
skills.description: Bootstrap a new e-commerce repo with Matrix's canonical stack — Next.js 16 App Router + Tailwind v4 + Supabase + Clerk + Stripe + Shippo + Vercel. Outputs a runnable repo with auth wired, schema migrations, Stripe webhooks, and CI. Clone of `project-scaffolder` adapted for e-commerce.
skills.intent: Skip 30-60 minutes of repo setup. New e-com project starts the same way every time — same conventions, same env vars, same webhook wiring, same eslint/prettier/tsconfig. Stamp it out; customize only the brand layer.
best_for: Side-by-side B2B portal next to main D2C store
skills.estimated_time: 20-30 min (interactive); 5 min (one-shot with all inputs)
skills.body_html:
E-commerce Scaffolder

Stamp out a runnable e-com repo in 20 min instead of half a day.

Lineage

Clones: project-scaffolder Differs: pre-wires Stripe webhook routes, Shippo client, Supabase schema migrations for catalog, Clerk for provider gating

When to use

  • New e-commerce repo from zero
  • New sub-brand needs its own store (won't share matrix-store codebase)
  • Spike / prototype before committing to full build

When NOT to use

  • Adding to an existing matrix-store repo → use frontend-dev + backend-dev directly
  • Non-commerce site → use project-scaffolder
  • Static marketing site → much lighter scaffold; use a Next.js minimal template

Required inputs

  1. Store name / brand (e.g., "MuscleLock store")
  2. Sub-brand reference (which sub-brands.md entry — drives colors, voice, layout)
  3. Catalog type (B2C / B2B / hybrid — drives auth & gating)
  4. Regulated SKUs? (yes/no — drives compliance gates + age verification)
  5. Repo location (GitHub org / local path)

What gets scaffolded

<store>-repo/
├── README.md                          (auto-generated with run instructions)
├── CLAUDE.md                          (project-specific context — links to sub-brand)
├── package.json                       (Next.js 16, React 19, TS 5.6, Tailwind v4)
├── next.config.ts
├── tailwind.config.ts                 (extends sub-brand palette from sub-brands.md)
├── tsconfig.json                      (strict, paths aliased)
├── .env.example                       (every needed var documented)
├── .gitignore + .gitattributes
├── .github/workflows/
│   ├── ci.yml                         (lint + typecheck + test on PR)
│   └── deploy.yml                     (Vercel preview + prod)
├── supabase/
│   ├── migrations/                    (catalog schema seeded — see ecommerce-schema)
│   ├── seed.sql                       (sample products for dev)
│   └── config.toml
├── src/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── (storefront)/
│   │   │   ├── page.tsx               (home)
│   │   │   ├── catalog/page.tsx       (collection)
│   │   │   ├── product/[slug]/page.tsx (PDP)
│   │   │   ├── cart/page.tsx
│   │   │   └── checkout/page.tsx
│   │   ├── (account)/
│   │   │   ├── orders/page.tsx
│   │   │   └── settings/page.tsx
│   │   └── api/
│   │       ├── stripe/webhook/route.ts     (idempotent — see integration-map.md)
│   │       ├── shippo/webhook/route.ts
│   │       ├── checkout/route.ts
│   │       └── inventory/[sku]/route.ts
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── product-card.tsx
│   │   ├── cart-drawer.tsx
│   │   ├── checkout-form.tsx
│   │   └── brand/                     (sub-brand-specific bits)
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── supabase.ts                (server + client variants)
│   │   ├── stripe.ts
│   │   ├── shippo.ts
│   │   ├── clerk.ts
│   │   └── analytics.ts
│   └── middleware.ts                  (Clerk auth + regulated-SKU gating if applicable)
├── tests/
│   ├── unit/
│   ├── integration/
│   │   ├── checkout.test.ts           (placeholder — fills in during build)
│   │   └── webhook-dedupe.test.ts
│   └── e2e/                           (Playwright — smoke test scaffold)
└── docs/
    ├── architecture.md
    ├── env-vars.md
    └── webhook-receivers.md

Workflow

Step 1 — Reuse check

Invoke reuse-scanner for the brand. If an existing repo covers 80%+, abort + redirect.

Step 2 — Confirm inputs

One-question fallback. If unclear: ask which sub-brand + B2B/B2C + regulated y/n.

Step 3 — Generate

  • Create repo (local + GitHub via gh repo create)
  • Stamp from template (see structure above)
  • Run pnpm install + initial typecheck
  • Apply Supabase migrations to dev project
  • Wire Vercel project + env vars

Step 4 — Brand pass

  • Tailwind theme from sub-brands.md palette
  • Logo + favicons from brand asset library
  • Voice tokens loaded from brand-guide.md

Step 5 — Verify runnable

  • pnpm dev starts cleanly
  • Home page renders sample product
  • Sign-up / sign-in works (Clerk)
  • Add-to-cart → checkout works in test mode
  • Stripe webhook receives test event + persists

Step 6 — Handoff

Output handoff to ecommerce-lead with:

  • Repo URL
  • Dev URL (local + Vercel preview)
  • What's wired (✅) vs what's still placeholder (⚠️)

Conventions baked in

  • Server Components by default — Client only where state demands it
  • Webhook idempotency — every receiver uses event_id dedupe table per integration-map.md
  • RLS on every Supabase table — see ecommerce-schema
  • No secrets client-side — lib/ server-only utilities flagged
  • Eslint config: matrix-canonical (no any, no console, no unused)
  • Conventional commits + Husky pre-commit lint
  • Vercel preview = PR deploy — every PR gets a real URL
  • OpenTelemetry traces for checkout + webhook paths

Anti-patterns

  • Hand-rolling each new store from scratch — defeats the scaffolder
  • Skipping the env-var checklist — broken Stripe in prod is no fun
  • Branding before the plumbing works — fix order: runnable first, beautiful second
  • Pasting matrix-store code instead of importing shared packages — drift accumulates fast
  • No RLS on Supabase tables — every table gets a policy on day 1

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Generate the repo + commit initial scaffold
  • ✅ Create Vercel + Supabase dev projects (test tier)
  • ✅ Apply schema migrations to dev
  • ⚠️ Provision prod Vercel / Supabase projects (Chris — billing)
  • ⚠️ Create GitHub repo in main org (Chris — access mgmt)
  • 🚫 Connect Stripe live keys (Chris)

Pairing

Inputs from: reuse-scanner, sub-brands.md, brand-guide.md, integration-map.md, ecommerce-schema Outputs to: ecommerce-schema (apply real catalog schema), ecommerce-backend (build on top), ecommerce-storefront (style + flesh out UI) Chains with: engineering-lead (architecture decisions), compliance-officer (regulated-SKU gates)

One-line reminder

Stamp it, don't hand-roll it. The 5th store should be 5x cheaper to start than the 1st.

skills.name: Ecommerce Schema
skills.description: Design and migrate the database schema for an e-commerce store — products, variants, prices, inventory, carts, orders, refunds, customers, addresses. Supabase / Postgres. Clone of `database-schema` adapted for e-commerce canonical shape.
skills.intent: Get the foundational tables right on day 1. Schema mistakes are the most expensive to fix later — every UI, every API, every report depends on this layer. Apply the canonical e-com shape, with RLS, indexes, audit columns, and webhook-idempotency tables.
best_for: Adding catalog to an existing Supabase project
Reviewing an existing schema before scale
skills.estimated_time: 60-90 min for full canonical schema; 15-30 min to add a single table
skills.body_html:
E-commerce Schema

The canonical Supabase / Postgres shape for an e-commerce store.

Lineage

Clones: database-schema Differs: opinionated tables for the e-com shape, pre-built RLS policies for B2C and B2B-gated patterns, webhook-idempotency tables wired

When to use

  • Fresh repo after ecommerce-scaffolder
  • Adding e-commerce to an existing app
  • Schema audit before traffic spike / big campaign

When NOT to use

  • Single-table change → use database-schema directly
  • Non-e-com app → use database-schema

The canonical tables

-- Catalog
products                  (id, slug, title, brand_id, category_id, status, created_at, ...)
product_variants          (id, product_id, sku, name, status, attributes jsonb, ...)
product_images            (id, product_id, variant_id?, url, alt, sort_order, kind)
categories                (id, slug, parent_id?, name, sort_order)
brands                    (id, slug, name, sub_brand_ref)
attributes                (id, name, kind, allowed_values jsonb)

-- Pricing
prices                    (id, variant_id, currency, list_cents, sale_cents?, starts_at, ends_at)
price_tiers               (id, variant_id, tier_name, min_qty, price_cents)
promo_codes               (id, code, kind, value_cents_or_pct, starts_at, ends_at, max_uses, used)

-- Inventory
inventory                 (variant_id PK, on_hand, reserved, reorder_point, reorder_qty, location)
inventory_movements       (id, variant_id, delta, kind, ref_table, ref_id, created_at)

-- Customers
customers                 (id, clerk_user_id, email, kind, npi?, verified_at?, created_at)
addresses                 (id, customer_id, kind, line1, line2?, city, region, postal, country, default)

-- Carts + checkout
carts                     (id, customer_id?, anon_id?, status, currency, created_at, expires_at)
cart_items                (id, cart_id, variant_id, qty, unit_price_cents, created_at)

-- Orders
orders                    (id, customer_id, status, currency, subtotal_cents, tax_cents,
                           shipping_cents, discount_cents, total_cents, created_at, paid_at?, ...)
order_items               (id, order_id, variant_id, sku_snapshot, title_snapshot, qty,
                           unit_price_cents, total_cents)
order_addresses           (order_id PK, ship_address jsonb, bill_address jsonb)
order_events              (id, order_id, event, payload jsonb, created_at)  -- state machine log

-- Payments + refunds
payments                  (id, order_id, processor, processor_ref, amount_cents, status, created_at)
refunds                   (id, order_id, payment_id, amount_cents, reason, status, created_at)

-- Shipments
shipments                 (id, order_id, carrier, service, tracking, label_url, status, created_at)
shipment_items            (id, shipment_id, order_item_id, qty)

-- Webhooks (idempotency)
webhook_events            (id PK = event_id, source, kind, payload jsonb, received_at, processed_at?)
webhook_dlq               (id, source, payload, error, retried_at, status)

-- Audit
audit_log                 (id, actor_kind, actor_id, table_name, row_id, action, before jsonb, after jsonb, at)

Required inputs

  1. B2C / B2B / hybrid — drives Clerk gating + customer.kind enum
  2. Multi-currency? — usually no (USD); set early
  3. Regulated? — adds NPI verification + provider gating columns
  4. Estimated SKU count — affects index strategy
  5. Multi-warehouse? — usually no; if yes, inventory becomes multi-row per variant

RLS policies (canonical)

Every table gets RLS on; default deny. Standard policies:

  • public: read products + variants where status='active'; read prices currently valid
  • customer-owned: read/write own carts, orders, addresses (via clerk_user_id)
  • service-role: webhook receivers + admin operations
  • provider-gated (regulated): catalog reads require customers.verified_at IS NOT NULL

Workflow

Step 1 — Confirm shape

Walk through inputs; show the table list scoped to choices.

Step 2 — Generate migrations

  • Use Supabase migration format (timestamped SQL files)
  • One migration per logical group (catalog / pricing / inventory / orders / payments / etc.)
  • Each migration includes: CREATE, indexes, RLS policies, helper functions
  • Always include rollback in comments

Step 3 — Generate types

  • Invoke generate_typescript_types after applying migrations
  • Save to src/lib/db.types.ts
  • Re-export curated types from src/lib/db.ts

Step 4 — Seed dev data

  • Sample brand + 5 categories + 10 products + 30 variants + prices + inventory
  • Sample customer (provider + consumer)
  • Sample completed order for testing reports

Step 5 — Verify

  • All migrations run cleanly on a fresh DB
  • RLS smoke test: anon can read public products; cannot read another customer's orders
  • Index strategy validated against expected query patterns

Indexes baked in

  • products(slug), products(brand_id, status), products(category_id, status)
  • product_variants(sku), product_variants(product_id)
  • prices(variant_id, starts_at, ends_at)
  • orders(customer_id, created_at DESC), orders(status, created_at)
  • order_items(order_id)
  • inventory(reorder_point) partial WHERE on_hand <= reorder_point (low-stock alerts)
  • webhook_events(source, kind, received_at) for dedupe lookups

Audit columns

Every table gets created_at, updated_at (trigger), deleted_at? (soft delete where appropriate). audit_log captures before/after on financially material tables (orders, payments, refunds, prices).

Anti-patterns

  • No RLS — every table on day 1, default deny
  • Storing money as float — always integer cents
  • Mutating prices in place — append + invalidate; auditability matters for legal/tax
  • No idempotency table for webhooks — guaranteed double-charges otherwise
  • Skipping order_events log — debugging refund disputes without state history is misery
  • One giant products.metadata jsonb with everything in it — promote frequently-queried attrs to columns

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Generate migration SQL
  • ✅ Apply to dev / staging
  • ✅ Generate types
  • ⚠️ Apply to prod (Chris + devops-deployment per production-deploy.sop.md)
  • 🚫 Drop / rename live columns without explicit Chris approval

Pairing

Inputs from: ecommerce-scaffolder (repo target), ecommerce-lead (catalog scope) Outputs to: ecommerce-backend (types + tables), product-catalog-builder (uses tables), inventory-manager, order-lifecycle-manager Chains with: database-schema (peer for non-e-com tables in same project), code-qa (RLS verification)

One-line reminder

RLS on every table. Money in cents. Audit log for anything financially material. Idempotency for every webhook.

skills.name: Ecommerce Storefront
skills.description: Build the customer-facing UI for an e-commerce store — home, collection, PDP, cart, checkout, account. Next.js App Router + Tailwind v4. Responsive, accessible, fast (LCP < 3s mobile). Clone of `frontend-dev` adapted for e-commerce shape.
skills.intent: Ship a customer-facing storefront that converts. Server Components by default, mobile-first responsive, brand-aligned per `sub-brands.md`, accessibility AA-compliant. Pairs with `ecommerce-backend` for data + `product-imagery` for assets.
best_for: Redesign of existing PDP / cart / checkout
Adding new surface (collection page, lookbook, etc.)
skills.estimated_time: PDP: 2-4h. Cart + checkout: 6-10h. Full store skin: 1-2 weeks.
skills.body_html:
E-commerce Storefront

The customer-facing UI for the store. Fast. Mobile-first. Brand-aligned. Accessible.

Lineage

Clones: frontend-dev Differs: pre-built component patterns for product card / cart drawer / checkout form, accessibility patterns baked in, conversion-tuned UX defaults

When to use

  • Fresh storefront after ecommerce-scaffolder + ecommerce-backend
  • Redesign of any e-com surface
  • Adding new surface (lookbook, gift guide, collection landing)

When NOT to use

  • Non-commerce UI → use frontend-dev
  • One-off marketing page → use ghl-funnel-builder or frontend-dev
  • Admin UI (orders / inventory management) → use frontend-dev (admin is internal-tool flavor)

The canonical surfaces

Home /

  • Hero (sub-brand voice + CTA)
  • Featured collections
  • Bestsellers strip
  • Social proof / reviews
  • Newsletter / signup
  • Trust signals (HIPAA / 503-A / etc. as applicable)

Collection /catalog/[category]

  • Filter rail (left on desktop, drawer on mobile)
  • Sort dropdown
  • Product grid (4-col desktop, 2-col mobile)
  • Pagination (cursor-based)
  • Empty state for filter combos returning 0
  • Infinite scroll OR pagination — pick one + commit

Product Detail Page /product/[slug]

  • Image gallery (zoom on hover desktop, swipe mobile)
  • Title + brand + sub-brand
  • Price (with sale strikethrough if applicable)
  • Variant selectors (size / form / strength / etc.)
  • Quantity + Add-to-Cart (sticky on mobile)
  • Description (rendered from product-copy output)
  • Specs table
  • Reviews (if surfaced)
  • Related products
  • Trust badges + return policy
  • Sticky add-to-cart bar (mobile only, appears after scroll)

Cart /cart (page + drawer)

  • Line items with thumbnail + variant + qty controls
  • Subtotal / discount / shipping estimate / tax estimate
  • Promo code input
  • Checkout CTA (primary)
  • Continue shopping link
  • Estimated delivery date
  • Empty state with featured items

Checkout /checkout

  • Single page (Shopify-style) OR 3-step — pick one
  • Email first (so we can fire abandoned-cart workflow even if they bail)
  • Shipping address (Google Places autocomplete)
  • Shipping method (rates from Shippo)
  • Payment (Stripe Elements — saves PCI scope)
  • Order summary persistent on right (top on mobile)
  • Trust signals near payment
  • Loading + error states for every async transition
  • Server-side validation matches client-side

Account /account/*

  • Orders list + detail
  • Saved addresses
  • Saved payment methods
  • Subscription management (if applicable)
  • Profile / preferences

Required inputs

  1. Sub-brand reference — drives colors, typography, voice
  2. Surfaces in scope — home / catalog / PDP / cart / checkout / account / specific subset
  3. Catalog size — affects filter strategy
  4. B2B / B2C / hybrid — affects price display (Net 30 selector? quote button?)
  5. Existing component library? — reuse where possible

Conversion-tuned defaults (deviate only with data)

  • Add-to-cart: large, single-color, top of fold on PDP
  • Cart drawer opens on add (don't navigate away)
  • Free shipping bar at top of cart when threshold close
  • Express checkout (Apple Pay / Google Pay / Shop Pay) above email field
  • One-click reorder on past orders
  • Persistent cart for logged-in users
  • Anonymous cart cookie for non-logged-in (drives abandoned-cart even pre-auth)
  • Stock urgency ("Only 3 left") — only when real + accurate
  • Estimated delivery date on PDP and cart — concrete dates beat "5-7 business days"

Workflow

Step 1 — Confirm surfaces + brand

  • One-question fallback if scope unclear
  • Pull sub-brand palette + voice from sub-brands.md

Step 2 — Build components first

  • Atom: button, input, badge, price
  • Molecule: product card, variant selector, qty stepper, address form
  • Organism: hero, filter rail, cart drawer, checkout step

Step 3 — Build surfaces in this order

  1. PDP (highest-stakes; most-built component)
  2. Cart drawer + cart page
  3. Checkout
  4. Collection
  5. Home
  6. Account

Step 4 — Accessibility pass

  • Keyboard navigation works for every flow
  • Focus rings visible
  • ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
  • Color contrast WCAG AA
  • Screen-reader test on PDP + checkout

Step 5 — Performance pass

  • Image optimization (next/image with proper sizes)
  • Server Components for static / data-fetching
  • Client Components only for interactive bits
  • Code-split heavy components (zoom viewer, etc.)
  • LCP < 3s p75 mobile

Step 6 — Smoke tests

  • Add to cart → checkout → order placed (Stripe test mode)
  • Refund → customer sees updated state in account
  • Mobile + tablet + desktop pass on real devices

Component conventions

  • Server Component default; 'use client' only when needed
  • Props typed strictly; no any
  • Composable variants via cva or similar
  • Test IDs for Playwright (data-testid="add-to-cart")
  • Loading + error + empty states for every async surface

Anti-patterns

  • All Client Components — kills LCP + bundles bloat
  • Skipping mobile-first — most traffic is mobile
  • Stock urgency lies — "only 3 left!" when there are 300 = brand damage
  • Slow PDP because of full hi-res images in initial paint — use next/image with srcset
  • Checkout > 1 page when single-page works — extra step = drop-off
  • Form errors only on submit — validate as user types where reasonable
  • Hiding shipping cost until last step — biggest cart-abandonment driver

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build any UI surface
  • ✅ Apply brand theme from sub-brands.md
  • ✅ Test against staging backend
  • ⚠️ Replace primary brand identity (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Change checkout flow structure (1-page vs 3-step) (Chris — conversion stakes)
  • 🚫 Ship to prod without launch-gate review

Pairing

Inputs from: ecommerce-scaffolder, ecommerce-schema (types), ecommerce-backend (APIs), sub-brands.md, brand-guide.md, product-imagery, product-copy Outputs to: code-qa (review), devops-deployment (ship), analytics-architect (instrumentation hooks), ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (abandoned-cart hooks) Chains with: frontend-dev (non-commerce surfaces in same repo), creative-director (brand review)

One-line reminder

Mobile first. Server Component default. Express checkout above the fold. PDP loads in 3s. Every state has a state.

skills.name: Education Continuous Improvement
skills.description: Friday 5:00pm weekly + quarterly auditor of Matrix's education program. Surfaces content drift, learner outcomes, UI friction, cert pass-rate trends, resource center engagement, weak-area patterns. Proposes improvements. Joins CI cluster as last engine. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Education must improve every week or it decays. Content gets stale, UIs accumulate friction, weak-areas reveal curriculum gaps. This specialist auditing weekly catches drift before it compounds.
best_for: Friday 5:00pm weekly CI review (joins cluster after SEO 4:45pm)
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 60-90 min. Quarterly: 1-2 days.
skills.body_html:
Education Continuous Improvement

The CI engine for education. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Weekly + quarterly auditor of the entire education program — Matrix Academy LMS + Resource Center + sales rep certification pipeline. Tracks per-cohort outcomes, content freshness, quiz item analysis, roleplay scorecard patterns, UI funnel drop-off, resource-center engagement, support-ticket-traced-to-education-gap patterns. Proposes evidence-based improvements to specific specialists. Joins Friday CI cluster as last engine (5:00pm — after SEO 4:45pm).

Explicitly OUT of scope: Executing curriculum changes (specialists do), pass/fail decisions (sales-rep-certification-manager), UI implementation (lms-ui-flow-optimizer + frontend-dev).

INPUT

  • Trigger: weekly-cron / quarterly-cron / post-cohort-retro / adhoc
  • Time window: last 7 days (weekly) / last quarter (quarterly)

RULES

  • ≥ 3 data points before claiming trend
  • PHI/PII discipline (run pii-redactor on outputs)
  • Cross-CI awareness (read voice/GHL/ecom/formulation/IP/SEO outputs to see what's affecting education — e.g., new SKU from formulation requires curriculum update; new objection pattern in voice calls feeds rep certification)
  • KPI thresholds per education-program.md
  • Recommendations require: owner + target + deadline (no vague "improve")
  • Quarterly deep audit covers: content freshness, item analysis, certification thresholds, UI flow, resource center engagement

TOOLS

  • Read: Matrix Academy LMS Supabase data, learner-progress-tracker outputs, quiz-and-assessment-builder item-analysis data, chain-monitor CI feed (_Logs/chain-monitor/ci-feeds/education-*.json if exists)
  • Compose: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor
  • Read: All other Friday CI engine outputs for cross-pollination
  • Write: _Logs/education-improvement/<period>-weekly.md + <period>-quarterly.md + KPI-history.csv (append-only)

OUTPUT

schema:
  digest_id: string
  period: weekly | quarterly
  kpi_snapshot:
    new_rep_time_to_cert_avg_days: number
    first_attempt_pass_rate_pct: number
    annual_recert_completion_pct: number
    content_freshness_pct: number
    quiz_first_attempt_pass_rate: number
    ui_funnel_dropoff_pct: number
    resource_center_mau: number
    support_tickets_education_traced: number
  trends:
    - kpi: string
      direction: improving | stable | declining
      data_points: array
      hypothesis: string
  diagnosed_issues:
    - issue: string
      hypothesis: string
      evidence: string
      confidence: H | M | L
      proposal: string
      executor: which specialist
      expected_impact: string
      effort: small | medium | large
  alerts:
    - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
      detail: string
      owner: string
  asks:
    - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
      ask: string
      owner: string
  quarterly_only_section:
    content_freshness_report: ...
    item_analysis_report: ...
    cert_threshold_validation: ...
    ui_flow_dropoff_audit: ...

HANDOFF

  • Christo + Chris (digest)
  • 8 other education specialists (per assigned action items)
  • chain-monitor (CI cross-feed)
  • pmo-operator (ticket tracking)
  • Quarterly review (consumes deep audit)

Eval cases

  1. Standard weekly review, all green → maintain
  2. Cert pass rate drops 70% → 50% → diagnostic (content issue vs cohort issue)
  3. Module 5 (dosing) item analysis bad → question quality OR content gap
  4. Resource center MAU declining → engagement-issue diagnosis
  5. UI funnel drop-off spike at "module 3 → 4" → ux-flow investigation
  6. Support tickets surge on a topic → resource-center content gap
  7. Quarterly deep audit → comprehensive

Anti-patterns

  • Single-week trend claims
  • PHI/PII in cross-cohort outputs
  • Generic "improve education" recommendations
  • Cross-CI blind (must consume other engines' Friday outputs)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + propose
  • ✅ Notify owners of action items
  • ⚠️ Approve material change (each owner specialist + cert-manager for cert-relevant)
  • 🚫 Execute changes directly

Pairing

Composed by: education-training-lead Composes: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor Reads: All education-specialist outputs + other Friday CI engine outputs Outputs to: 8 specialists + Christo + Chris + pmo-operator + chain-monitor

One-liner reminder

Friday 5pm + quarterly. Trend ≥ 3 data points. PII discipline. Cross-CI awareness. Recommendations with owner + target + deadline. Closes the Friday CI cluster.

skills.name: Education Training Lead
skills.description: Department lead for Education & Training. Orchestrates 9 specialists across Sales Rep Certification (highest priority) + Customer/Provider Resource Center. Operates Matrix Academy LMS as canonical surface (`~/Projects/Matrix Academy/`). Sets quarterly priorities + continuous content + UI flow improvement. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: New products roll out constantly; sales reps need to ramp before working; providers need education to use products correctly. This lead orchestrates the team toward two outcomes: (1) every new rep certifies in < 14 days, (2) the resource center stays current with every new SKU + protocol.
skills.estimated_time: Quarterly: 4-8 hours. Weekly review: 30-60 min.
skills.body_html:
Education & Training Lead

The department orchestrator. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Quarterbacks Matrix's education infrastructure. Two tracks: (1) Sales Rep Certification — every new rep must graduate before working; (2) Customer/Provider Resource Center — continuously updated dosing guides + pairing tools + product education. Operates Matrix Academy LMS at ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/ as the canonical production surface. Sequences specialist work, tracks KPIs, reports up to Christo + Chris weekly.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Hands-on content writing (clinical-curriculum-author + sales-curriculum-author), specific cert decisions (sales-rep-certification-manager), individual roleplay sessions (roleplay-coach-agent), UI engineering (frontend-dev + lms-ui-flow-optimizer).

Composition (the team)

Specialist Owns
sales-rep-certification-manager Graduation gate — pass/fail decisions, cohort management, activation
clinical-curriculum-author 10 clinical modules (governance/intake/labs/sterile/dosing/peptide-classes/evidence/protocols/longitudinal/operations)

quiz-and-assessment-builder 250 MC questions + 250 flashcards; refreshes per content change
roleplay-coach-agent 7-mode AI roleplay (Warm Lead / Objection Gauntlet / etc.)
learner-progress-tracker Per-learner state; cert status; nudges; reminders
resource-center-curator Provider/customer-facing education hub + dosing pairing tools
education-continuous-improvement Friday 5pm weekly + quarterly deep audit
lms-ui-flow-optimizer UI flow tightening; drop-off reduction; A/B tests

INPUT

  • New rep batch (HR triggers)
  • New SKU launch (formulation-lead Phase 5 → curriculum update)

  • Quarterly strategy cycle

RULES

  • Sales rep certification is the HIGHEST priority (revenue + compliance gate)
  • Critical-fail domains (per education-program.md) are non-negotiable
  • All curriculum changes pass compliance-officer Gate 1 before publish
  • Trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT parameters in educational content)
  • Matrix Academy LMS is canonical; don't duplicate elsewhere
  • Continuous improvement (Friday weekly + quarterly deep)
  • Sub-brand voice respected (sub-brands.md)

TOOLS

  • All 9 education specialists
  • Matrix Academy LMS (~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/)
  • Content sources: ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/content/, ~/Projects/dosing guide/Knowledge Base/
  • Read: education-program.md, dosing-reference.md, compliance-rules.md, regulatory-frameworks.md
  • Compose: compliance-officer, creative-director, formulation-compliance (for clinical content review)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_type: weekly | quarterly | adhoc | cohort-kickoff
    kpi_snapshot:
      avg_time_to_cert_days: number
      first_attempt_pass_rate_pct: number
      annual_recert_completion_pct: number
      content_freshness_pct: number
      ui_funnel_dropoff_pct: number
      resource_center_mau: number
    priorities:
      - priority: string
        owner_specialist: string
        target: string
        deadline: ISO
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        owner: string
    asks:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

  • Christo + Chris (weekly + quarterly)
  • 9 specialists (per priority assignments)
  • chain-monitor (education-related alerts)
  • HR / recruiting (new rep cohort coordination)
  • compliance-officer (content compliance gate)

Eval cases

  1. Weekly review, all KPIs green → maintain
  2. Rep cert pass rate drops < 60% → diagnostic
  3. New SKU launch → curriculum-update workflow triggered
  4. UI funnel drop-off > 25% → lms-ui-flow-optimizer priority
  5. Content freshness < 80% → curriculum refresh sprint

Anti-patterns

  • Letting reps work uncertified — never; the gate is the gate
  • Curriculum drift (months of stale content)
  • Manual rep-cert tracking (use the LMS + tracker)
  • Resource center as afterthought — drives provider product confidence + reorder behavior
  • UI friction tolerated because "the content is what matters"

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Orchestrate department
  • ✅ Set quarterly priorities
  • ⚠️ Activate rep (certification-manager confirms; lead approves)
  • ⚠️ Major curriculum overhaul (Chris + compliance-officer)
  • 🚫 Override cert thresholds

Pairing

Composed by: company-os-orchestrator, pmo-operator Composes: All 9 education specialists + compliance-officer + creative-director Reads: education-program.md, dosing-reference.md, sub-brands.md

One-liner reminder

Two tracks: rep certification (priority) + provider resource center. Matrix Academy LMS canonical. Continuous improvement. Critical-fail domains non-negotiable. Activation gated.

skills.name: Email Drafter
skills.description: Write professional emails for any situation — outreach, follow-ups, negotiations, updates
skills.body_html:
Email Drafter

Write a professional, ready-to-send email for any situation. Give it the context — who you're writing to, what about, and what tone — and get a polished email you can copy-paste or tweak.

Instructions

Step 1: Understand the Situation

From the user's request, identify:

  • Recipient — Who is this going to? (client, boss, prospect, vendor, team, stranger)
  • Purpose — What's the goal? (introduce yourself, follow up, negotiate, update, ask for something, say no)
  • Tone — Formal, professional-casual, friendly, firm, apologetic, enthusiastic?
  • Key points — What MUST be communicated?
  • Relationship context — First contact? Ongoing relationship? Responding to something?

If the user's request is clear enough, write the email immediately. Only ask clarifying questions if critical information is missing (like who it's going to or what it's about).

Check CLAUDE.md for the user's name, role, and communication style if available.

Step 2: Write the Email

Structure:

Subject: [Clear, specific subject line — not generic]

[Greeting — match the relationship: "Hi Sarah," / "Dear Mr. Chen," / "Hey team,"]

[Opening line — context or connection. Never start with "I hope this email finds you well." Instead, reference something specific: their recent work, a shared connection, or get straight to the point.]

[Body — 2-4 short paragraphs max. One idea per paragraph. Lead with the most important thing.]

[Clear ask or next step — what do you want them to DO after reading this?]

[Sign-off — match the tone: "Best," / "Thanks," / "Looking forward to hearing from you,"]
[User's name]

Step 3: Email Type Templates

Use the right structure based on the email type:

Type Key Elements
Cold outreach Lead with value/relevance, not yourself. Why should they care? One clear CTA.
Follow-up Reference the previous interaction. Add new value, don't just "check in."
Negotiation State your position clearly. Use "I'd like to propose" not "I was wondering if maybe."
Update/status Lead with the headline. Details below. End with next steps.
Saying no Be direct and kind. Give a brief reason. Offer an alternative if possible.
Introduction Who you are (1 sentence), why you're reaching out (1 sentence), what you're asking (1 sentence).
Thank you Be specific about what you're thanking them for. Don't be generic.
Apology Acknowledge what happened, take responsibility, state what you're doing about it. No excuses.
Request State the ask upfront. Explain why briefly. Make it easy for them to say yes.

Step 4: Quality Rules

Rule Why
Subject line must be specific "Q1 Budget Proposal — Review by Friday" not "Following up"
First sentence must earn the second If the opening is boring, they stop reading
Max 5 sentences per paragraph Walls of text don't get read
One clear call to action Don't make them guess what you want
No filler phrases Cut "I just wanted to," "I was wondering if," "I hope this finds you well"
Match their energy If they wrote 3 sentences, don't write 3 paragraphs
Proofread tone Read it as the recipient. Does it sound pushy? Passive? Unclear?

Step 5: Present Options

Show the email and offer:

  • "Want me to make it shorter / longer / more formal / more casual?"
  • "Want a second version with a different angle?"
  • "Should I draft a follow-up to send if they don't respond in [X] days?"

Output

A complete, ready-to-send email with subject line. Clean text the user can copy directly into their email client.

skills.name: Email Intent Classifier
skills.description: Atomic micro-agent. Input email body + subject. Output classified intent (question / complaint / sale / order-status / refund / partnership / spam / FYI / urgent / other) + confidence + suggested routing. Determinism layer for obvious patterns, LLM (Haiku) for ambiguous. Composed by `email-triage`, EAs, CS workflows.
skills.intent: 90% of inbound email triage is pattern-based — clear sales pitches, obvious complaints, transactional confirmations. Burn cheap deterministic + Haiku cycles on the 90%, surface the 10% nuanced to humans / EAs. Saves Leo's and Christo's time + makes inbox triage routing reliable.
triggers: what kind of email is this
skills.estimated_time: 2-5 seconds
skills.body_html:
Email Intent Classifier

90% deterministic + cheap-LLM. Saves Leo / Christo time.

Determinism layer (BEFORE LLM)

Spam patterns (auto-classify spam)

  • Sender domain on known-spam list
  • Body contains lottery / inheritance scam language
  • "Click here" + suspicious URLs
  • Crypto pump language
  • "Dear Sir/Madam" + body length < 100 chars + clear template
  • Unknown sender + attachment with suspicious extension

Transactional FYI (auto-classify FYI)

  • Sender = no-reply / receipts@ / notifications@
  • Subject contains: "receipt", "confirmation", "order #", "shipment", "your order"
  • Body contains: order number + total + standard transactional structure
  • Suggested routing: auto-archive after recording in PMO if material

Obvious sales pitches (auto-classify sale-pitch)

  • Subject contains: "demo", "schedule a call", "interested in your business", "AI solution for..."
  • Body mentions our company by name + offers their service
  • Unknown sender + sender is in marketing-tool domain
  • Suggested routing: cold-pitch funnel OR auto-archive based on Chris's preferences

Customer complaint indicators (high-confidence complaint)

  • Subject contains: "complaint", "refund", "unhappy", "disappointed", "terrible"
  • Sentiment markers (would still verify with LLM): "demand", "unacceptable", "cancelling"
  • Sender is active customer
  • Suggested routing: CS (escalate to AM if VIP customer)

Legal / regulatory threat (priority flag)

  • Words: "subpoena", "cease and desist", "FDA", "FTC", "state attorney general", "complaint to regulator", "litigation", "attorney", "represent"
  • Suggested routing: Chris + counsel IMMEDIATELY; never auto-respond

Press / media inquiry

  • Sender domain matches known press / media
  • Subject: "interview request", "story", "comment for article", "press inquiry"
  • Suggested routing: Chris ONLY (never auto-handle); response coordinated through brand

LLM layer (for ambiguous cases)

If deterministic layer returns ambiguous OR low-confidence, run Haiku with:

You are an email intent classifier for Matrix Advanced Solutions.

Classify this email into ONE of:
- question (customer / prospect asking about products / process)
- complaint (negative — issue with order / service / product)
- sale (us selling) — note: this is rare in inbox; this is "warm reply to outbound"
- order-status (where's my order)
- refund (explicit refund request)
- partnership (proposal / inquiry — could be vendor, co-marketing, distribution)
- spam (commercial pitch / unsolicited)
- FYI (informational only)
- urgent (time-sensitive — flight, emergency, deadline)
- other (doesn't fit cleanly)

Sender: {{sender_email}} (known customer: {{sender_known}}, status: {{customer_status}})
Subject: {{subject}}
Body:
{{body}}

Output JSON:
{
  "intent": "...",
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0,
  "reasoning": "<one sentence>",
  "suggested_routing": "leo | christo | am | cs | sales | compliance | sales-strategy | auto-archive | human-review",
  "detected_urgency": "routine | time-sensitive | urgent | emergency",
  "flags": ["regulated-claim" | "legal-threat" | "press-inquiry" | ...]
}

Routing logic

Intent Default routing Override conditions
question (prospect) sales If existing customer → AM
question (customer) AM If technical → CS
complaint CS If VIP → AM; if regulatory → compliance
order-status CS (auto-respond with order link) —
refund CS (per refund SOP) If > $500 → escalate
partnership sales-strategy If strategic (CEO-tier) → Leo
sale (warm reply) sales —
spam auto-archive —
FYI auto-archive (after PMO note if material) —
urgent Chris OR Leo OR Christo (per content) —
other human-review —
ANY with legal-threat flag Chris + counsel NEVER auto-respond
ANY with press-inquiry flag Leo NEVER auto-respond

Output examples

Clear customer complaint

intent: complaint
confidence: 0.94
reasoning: "Active customer using language 'terrible experience' + requesting refund."
suggested_routing: cs
detected_urgency: time-sensitive
flags: []

Legal threat

intent: complaint
confidence: 0.98
reasoning: "Sender's attorney; cease-and-desist subject; legal language throughout."
suggested_routing: human-review
detected_urgency: urgent
flags: [legal-threat]

Obvious spam

intent: spam
confidence: 0.99
reasoning: "Crypto pump language + unknown sender + template body."
suggested_routing: auto-archive
detected_urgency: routine
flags: []

Press inquiry to Leo

intent: other
confidence: 0.91
reasoning: "Journalist requesting comment on industry trends; sender from known press domain."
suggested_routing: leo
detected_urgency: time-sensitive
flags: [press-inquiry]

Eval cases

1-3. Universal spam patterns → spam / auto-archive 4-6. Transactional confirmations → FYI / auto-archive 7-9. Customer complaints → complaint / CS 10. Refund explicit ask → refund / CS 11. Order status check → order-status / CS auto-respond 12. Partnership pitch from vendor → partnership / sales-strategy 13. Cease-and-desist letter → flag legal-threat → human-review 14. Press inquiry → flag press-inquiry → Leo 15. Active-customer technical question → question / AM 16. Prospect asking about pricing → question / sales 17. Urgent customer escalation → urgent / Christo 18. Internal team email forwarded → FYI / auto-archive 19. Compliance / regulatory inquiry from FDA / state AG → flag legal-threat → human-review 20. Ambiguous email mixing 3 topics → other / human-review

Composition

  • email-triage — primary parent skill (this is its workhorse)
  • ea-leo-getz-ceo — Leo's inbox triage
  • ea-christo-mac-coo — Christo's inbox triage
  • account-manager — customer-routed emails
  • compliance-officer — flag-detected emails
  • sales-strategy — partnership-routed
  • prep-day / daily-briefing — daily inbox summary

Anti-patterns

  • Auto-responding to anything flagged legal-threat / press-inquiry — never
  • Auto-archiving FYI without recording material — losing information
  • Calling Sonnet/Opus — Haiku handles this; no quality benefit
  • Ignoring sender-known flag — known customer's "complaint" is different from unknown sender's
  • Confidence < 0.7 → still auto-routing — surface to human-review instead
  • Same email classified differently across days — consistency matters

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Classify any inbound email
  • ✅ Return structured intent + routing
  • ✅ Flag legal / press / regulatory items
  • 🚫 Auto-respond on any legal-threat / press-inquiry flag
  • 🚫 Auto-archive flagged items without recording
  • 🚫 Modify the email itself

One-liner reminder

Deterministic patterns first → Haiku for ambiguous. Confidence < 0.7 → human-review. NEVER auto-respond to legal / press flags. 20 eval cases.

skills.name: Email Triage
skills.description: Read Gmail inbox, classify each message (Urgent / Action / FYI / Junk), draft replies for top-N urgent items, archive obvious junk, surface anything that requires the user's personal touch. Outputs a triage report with the 3-lane delegation matrix.
skills.body_html:
Email Triage

Process the inbox so the user only sees what needs them.

Purpose

Inbox-zero is overrated; inbox-prioritized is the goal. Classify, draft, archive — surface only what needs human judgment.

When to use

  • After prep-day flags urgent emails
  • Mid-day inbox processing (after lunch)
  • End-of-day final pass before logging off

When NOT to use

  • A single specific email the user already named (just open / respond to it)
  • Personal email account (this skill assumes work inbox)

Required setup

  • Gmail MCP connected (mcp__gmail__*) OR Claude in Chrome with Gmail logged in
  • Permission to label / archive (read-only is the safe default; ask before mutating)

Workflow

Step 1 — Confirm connection and scope

mcp check: Gmail
inbox scope: <Inbox / Important / Today's unread / last N>
default: unread received in last 24h

Step 2 — Classify each email

Apply this taxonomy:

Class Criteria Action
🚨 Urgent Time-sensitive + irreversible if missed (contract deadline, expiring offer, escalated customer) ⚠️ Surface immediately, draft reply
🎯 Action Requires a reply or task, but not urgent ✅ Draft reply for user approval
👀 FYI Worth knowing, no action ✅ Summarize, archive
🤝 Relationship Personal touch needed (warm intro, recruiter, customer praise) 🚫 Flag for user — don't draft, just summarize and queue
🚮 Junk Newsletter, promo, automated notice with no action ✅ Auto-archive (only if pre-authorized)
❓ Unclear Hard to classify ⚠️ Surface with note, don't act

Step 3 — Draft replies for Urgent + Action

For each draftable email:

  • Recipient context: who they are, prior thread context
  • Tone match: their writing style + Chris's default (direct, warm, no fluff)
  • 3-7 sentences max unless explicitly a long-form reply
  • One clear ask or next step

Pull tone from Individual Skills/brand-identity.md if customized. Otherwise use Chris's default voice from CLAUDE.md.

Step 4 — Junk handling (with caution)

Auto-archive ONLY if:

  • Sender domain is in pre-authorized "junk OK to archive" list (newsletters, transactional)
  • Subject matches obvious junk patterns
  • No links to attachments / payments

When uncertain → leave it. Don't risk archiving something important.

Step 5 — Output

Output structure

# Email Triage — <Day, Month DD HH:MM>

**Scope**: <inbox / unread / last 24h>
**Total scanned**: <X>
**Status**: <Mode: read-only | Mode: with mutations (archived: Y, labeled: Z)>

---

## 🚨 Urgent (needs you NOW)

### From: <Sender Name <email>> — <Subject>
**Why urgent**: <one-line>
**Drafted reply**:

[Repeat per urgent item]

🎯 Action (drafts ready)

From: —

Why: Drafted reply:

<draft>

⚠️ Approve to send.

[Repeat]

🤝 Relationship (you-only)

From: —

Summary: Suggested move: <book a call / personal reply / forward> 🚫 No draft — your voice needed.

[Repeat]

👀 FYI (summarized, archived)

  • — — ✅ archived
  • — — ✅ archived

🚮 Junk (auto-archived)

  • newsletters, promos, transactional notices
  • Auto-archived per pre-auth rules
  • (or: "kept in inbox — no auto-archive rule matched")

❓ Unclear (your call)

  • From : —

Delegation summary

✅ I handled

  • Drafted replies (Urgent + Action)
  • Archived junk
  • Summarized FYI
  • Total time saved: ~ minutes

⚠️ Need from you

  • drafts to approve
  • unclear to classify

🚫 You only

  • relationship emails to personally write
  • minutes estimated for personal replies

## Anti-patterns

- **Auto-replying without approval** — never. Drafts go to ⚠️ Need from you.
- **Bulk-archiving anything that LOOKS like junk** — if uncertain, leave it.
- **Drafting in robot voice** — use Chris's tone (direct, warm, no fluff).
- **Long-summarizing FYI emails** — one line is enough.
- **Reading the full inbox every time** — scope to unread + last 24h by default.
- **Treating recruiter / personal-network emails as Action** — they're Relationship.

## Pre-authorizations user can grant

- [ ] Auto-archive from these sender domains: <list>
- [ ] Auto-archive these subject patterns: <list>
- [ ] Auto-label by class (Urgent/Action/FYI)
- [ ] Auto-send approved drafts after Chris hits ⚠️ Approve
- [ ] **NEVER** auto-send without approval — keep this rule absolute

## Pairing

- Pair with `task-list-manager` — Action emails become TASKS.md items
- Pair with `prep-day` — runs at morning prep
- Pair with `weekly-review` — surfaces email patterns (e.g., "you got 5 cold pitches this week — want a filter?")

## Connection-failure fallback

If Gmail MCP isn't connected:

→ Gmail MCP isn't connected. Run /mcp to add it, or open Gmail in Chrome and I'll work via Claude in Chrome.


## One-liner reminder

**Classify, draft, surface what's human. Never auto-send. Relationship emails get summary, not draft.**

skills.name: Employee Engagement Pulse
skills.description: Monthly eNPS pulse + quarterly engagement deep survey + action-plan generation per low-score area + sentiment tracking across cohorts (tenure / role / location). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Engagement is a leading indicator of retention + productivity. Run a tight monthly pulse (1-2 questions, eNPS-anchored) and a deep quarterly survey. Convert results into action plans — never let a low score sit. Surface cohort differentials (new vs tenured, remote vs HQ, by role) so interventions are targeted.
skills.estimated_time: Monthly pulse: 20-30 min admin + 1-2 days collection. Quarterly deep: 2-4 hours design + 1 week collection.
skills.body_html:
Employee Engagement Pulse

The engagement signal layer. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Runs Matrix's engagement instrumentation. Monthly: 1-2 question eNPS-anchored pulse ("How likely are you to recommend Matrix as a place to work?" + 1 rotating dimension). Quarterly: deep survey across canonical dimensions (manager / role / growth / comp-perception / culture / wellbeing). Cohort-aware (tenure bucket, role family, location). Generates action plans per low-score area. Never executes interventions directly — drafts plans + hands to hr-lead.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Individual coaching (performance-management), comp decisions (comp-band-analyst + Chris), termination decisions, public retention claims (any external comm passes compliance-officer).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (monthly-pulse / quarterly-deep / cohort-investigation / post-event-pulse / adhoc)
    required: true
  scope:
    cohort_filter: optional (e.g., "new-hires-last-6-months" / "remote-only" / "sales-team")
    period: ISO range
  prior_results: reference to last result for trend
validation:
  - Minimum response rate threshold: 60% for valid signal; flag if below
  - Cohort size ≥ 5 for cohort-specific output (else aggregate to protect anonymity)

RULES

rules:
  anonymity_first:
    - Individual responses NEVER surfaced
    - Cohort size < 5 → aggregate up; do not report
    - Free-text comments anonymized + paraphrased before any cross-team distribution
    - `pii-redactor` composed on every output
  survey_design:
    monthly_pulse:
      - eNPS anchor (0-10 likelihood to recommend) — always
      - 1 rotating dimension question (12-month rotation covering all canonical dimensions)
      - 1 free-text "What's one thing we should change?"
      - Total: 3 questions max — respect time
    quarterly_deep:
      - eNPS anchor
      - Canonical dimensions (rate 1-5):
        * Manager effectiveness
        * Role clarity + autonomy
        * Growth opportunity
        * Comp + benefits perception (signal-only — `comp-band-analyst` does actual analysis)
        * Culture + values alignment
        * Wellbeing + balance
        * Tooling + enablement
        * Cross-team collaboration
      - 3 free-text questions (start / stop / continue)
  scoring:
    enps:
      promoters: 9-10
      passives: 7-8
      detractors: 0-6
      score: %promoters - %detractors (range -100 to +100)
      benchmarks:
        excellent: > 50
        strong: 30-50
        average: 10-30
        concerning: 0-10
        alarm: < 0
    dimension_scores:
      green: avg ≥ 4.0
      yellow: 3.0-3.9
      red: < 3.0 → action plan required
  action_plan_required_for:
    - Any dimension < 3.0
    - eNPS drop > 15 points in single period
    - Cohort divergence > 20 points from company average
  trend_discipline:
    - ≥ 3 data points before claiming trend
    - Avoid over-interpreting single survey
  pii_discipline:
    - No individual identifiers in cross-team output
    - Free-text comments quoted only with no metadata attached
    - Cohort-specific findings only when cohort ≥ 5

TOOLS

  • Survey tool: simple form (e.g., Google Form / Tally / internal) — pick what's running
  • Read: kpi-registry.md, _Logs/personnel/ (tenure data — read-only, redacted)
  • Compose: pii-redactor, analytics-architect (trend math), compliance-officer (any external-facing summary)
  • Output: _Logs/hr/engagement/<period>-<type>.md + eNPS-history.csv (append-only)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    survey_id: string
    survey_type: monthly-pulse | quarterly-deep | cohort | post-event
    period: ISO range
    response_metrics:
      total_eligible: number
      total_responded: number
      response_rate_pct: number
      valid_signal: boolean (true if ≥ 60%)
    enps:
      score: number (-100 to +100)
      promoters_pct: number
      passives_pct: number
      detractors_pct: number
      trend_vs_last: number (delta)
      benchmark: excellent | strong | average | concerning | alarm
    dimension_scores: (quarterly only)
      - dimension: string
        avg_score: number (1-5)
        status: green | yellow | red
        trend: improving | stable | declining
    cohort_breakdowns: (when cohort ≥ 5)
      - cohort: string
        enps: number
        dimension_avg: number
        note: string
    themes_from_freetext:
      - theme: string
        sentiment: positive | neutral | negative
        frequency: number
        sample_quote: string (anonymized)
    action_plans:
      - low_score_area: string
        hypothesis: string
        proposal: string
        owner: string
        deadline: ISO
        expected_impact: string
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown summary (anonymized) + PII-redacted distribution version
  quality_bar:
    - Response rate stated
    - All action plans have owner + deadline + expected impact
    - PII discipline visibly applied
    - Cohort minimums respected

HANDOFF

  • hr-lead (digest + action plans)
  • Manager(s) of affected cohort (if action plan involves their team)
  • Chris (if eNPS alarm)
  • pmo-operator (action plans tracked as projects)
  • compliance-officer (any external-facing summary)
  • hr-continuous-improvement (consumes for CI engine)

Eval cases

  1. Standard monthly pulse, eNPS stable → maintain; no action plans
  2. eNPS drops 25 points → P1 alert + free-text theme analysis + diagnostic
  3. Quarterly deep shows "Manager effectiveness" at 2.8 for one cohort → manager-specific action plan
  4. Remote cohort 25 points below HQ cohort → location-specific investigation
  5. New-hire cohort (0-6 months) at concerning eNPS → onboarding-coordinator + training-builder review
  6. Response rate 45% → flag invalid signal; investigate participation
  7. Free-text theme: "comp lagging" → trigger comp-band-analyst interaction
  8. Post-event pulse after major change (re-org / new tool / leadership shift) → focused dimension check

Anti-patterns

  • Single-question "rate us 1-10" with no follow-up — no diagnostic value
  • Naming individuals from free-text — kills trust immediately
  • Cohort breakdowns with < 5 respondents — anonymity broken
  • Action plans without owner/deadline — no follow-through
  • Surveying without acting — kills future response rates
  • Quarterly without monthly — too lagging
  • Monthly with too many questions — kills response rate

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design + run pulses + deep surveys
  • ✅ Anonymize + synthesize results
  • ✅ Draft action plans
  • ✅ Notify hr-lead + relevant managers
  • ⚠️ Distribute survey internally (Chris approves design before first send)
  • ⚠️ External-facing summary (Chris + compliance-officer)
  • ⚠️ Action plan execution (manager + hr-lead approve)
  • 🚫 Disclose individual responses
  • 🚫 Make personnel decisions from survey data
  • 🚫 Promise specific changes to employees

Pairing

Composed by: hr-lead Composes: pii-redactor, analytics-architect, compliance-officer Reads: kpi-registry.md, _Logs/personnel/ (tenure, redacted) Outputs to: hr-lead, managers (cohort-specific), Chris (alerts), hr-continuous-improvement, pmo-operator

One-liner reminder

Monthly pulse (3 questions max). Quarterly deep (8 dimensions). eNPS anchored. Anonymity first. Cohort ≥ 5 minimum. Action plans with owner + deadline. Survey without action = trust dead.

skills.name: Engineering Lead
skills.description: The technical architect for any software build. Takes a problem statement and produces architecture brief, stack decision, breakdown into tickets, integration plan, and approval gates. Reuses the Matrix Next.js 16 + Tailwind v4 + GHL stack pattern as default. Front-loads the decisions that otherwise get re-debated each project.
skills.intent: Be the single source of architectural truth before any code is written. Pick the stack. Map the system. Break into tickets. Identify integrations. Flag compliance gates. Output is what a frontend/backend/database/devops agent (or human dev) builds against.
best_for: Any software project beyond a single file
skills.body_html:
Engineering Lead

The architect before any code. Pick the stack once, not per-debate.

When to use

  • New software project requires technical scoping
  • Mid-project refactor of architecture
  • Replatform / integration redesign
  • Pre-build ticket breakdown

When NOT to use

  • Single-file script (just write it)
  • Bug fix (use direct dev work)
  • Content / docs project (use project-scaffolder instead)

Required inputs

  1. Problem statement — what software needs to exist
  2. User of the software — internal team / customer-facing / public
  3. Volume / scale estimate — single-user / 100 users / 100k users / undefined
  4. Compliance posture — none / HIPAA / PCI / GDPR / SOX / multiple
  5. Time + budget — when needed, what's the budget
  6. Integration requirements — what external systems must connect

If 1 or 3 is missing, ask: "What's the software for, and roughly how many users / requests per day at year 1?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Run reuse-scanner

Always first. Don't reinvent. If something close exists in ~/Projects/, base the decision on extension.

Step 2 — Apply the decision hierarchy (KB 01)

Is this even a software project, or:

  • A workflow (use n8n / Zapier / Make)?
  • A GHL automation (use workflow-automation-builder)?
  • An AI agent (use voice-ai-agent-builder or chat agent path)?
  • A no-code SaaS configuration (use existing tools)?

Only proceed if it's genuinely custom software.

Step 3 — Pick the stack

Matrix default stack (use unless reason to deviate):

  • Frontend: Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4
  • UI library: shadcn/ui (Radix primitives), Lucide icons, Framer Motion for motion
  • Forms: React Hook Form + Zod for validation
  • Backend: Next.js API routes (or separate FastAPI for heavy compute)
  • Database: Supabase (Postgres) — RLS for multi-tenant
  • Auth: Clerk (or Supabase Auth)
  • Payments: Stripe
  • CRM integration: GoHighLevel (via REST API)
  • File storage: Supabase Storage (or S3 if scaling)
  • Email: GHL transactional + (if needed) Resend
  • Hosting: Vercel
  • Observability: Vercel Analytics + Sentry

When to deviate:

  • Heavy compute → add a separate Python/FastAPI service (Vercel Functions for light, AWS Lambda or Fly.io for heavy)
  • Real-time → Supabase Realtime or socket.io on a Node service
  • ML inference → Modal / Replicate / Hugging Face Inference Endpoints
  • 3D / graphics-heavy → R3F + Drei (already in matrix-advanced-solutions)
  • Mobile → React Native (if cross-platform needed) or Native iOS/Android (rare)

Step 4 — Map the system

Produce a system map:

  • Pages / routes (frontend)
  • API surface (endpoints, methods, request/response shapes)
  • Data model (tables, relations, indexes, RLS rules)
  • Auth model (roles, permissions, RBAC matrix)
  • Integrations (which external API/MCP for each)
  • Background jobs (cron, webhooks, queues)
  • Edge cases (offline, slow network, auth expired, partial data)

Step 5 — Break into tickets

Each ticket:

  • Title (verb + outcome)
  • Description (1-2 sentences)
  • Acceptance criteria (specific, testable)
  • Estimated time (hours)
  • Dependencies (other tickets)
  • Owner (which agent: frontend / backend / database / devops / integration)

Group tickets into milestones:

  • M0: Project scaffold + auth + DB schema
  • M1: Core read paths (display existing data)
  • M2: Core write paths (create/update flows)
  • M3: Integration + automation
  • M4: Polish + accessibility + performance
  • M5: Production deploy + monitoring

Step 6 — Identify compliance gates

Per compliance-rules.md:

  • HIPAA → run security-inspector mid-build + before launch (Spoon Test)
  • Payment data → PCI scope minimization (Stripe-hosted forms; never your servers touch raw cards)
  • Customer data → privacy policy + deletion flow + audit logs
  • A2P 10DLC if SMS features
  • BAAs needed for any vendor touching PHI

Mark gates as approval points in the milestone plan.

Step 7 — Define KPIs + monitoring

Before launch:

  • p50 / p99 latency targets
  • Error rate ceiling
  • Cost-per-request ceiling
  • User-facing SLO (uptime / time-to-first-action)
  • Business KPIs (per project — conversions, completions, etc.)

Wire dashboards before users hit production.

Step 8 — Risk register

Top 3-5 risks with severity + mitigation:

  • Technical risks (new tool / pattern / integration)
  • Compliance risks (regulatory exposure)
  • Scope risks (creep beyond plan)
  • Dependency risks (waiting on vendor / decision)
  • Cost risks (per-request cost unbounded)

Step 9 — Output the build plan

Output structure

# Engineering Plan: <Project Name>

## Problem
<one-line>

## Decision: build vs alternative
**Verdict**: <build custom / use SaaS / GHL automation / etc.>
**Reasoning**: <one paragraph>

## Stack
**Frontend**: <stack>
**Backend**: <stack>
**Database**: <stack>
**Auth**: <stack>
**Payments**: <stack>
**Integrations**: <list>
**Hosting**: <stack>
**Observability**: <stack>
**Deviations from Matrix default**: <list with reason>

## System map
**Pages / routes**: <list>
**API endpoints**: <list>
**Data model**:
| Table | Fields | Relations | RLS rule |
|---|---|---|---|
| ... | ... | ... | ... |

**Auth / RBAC**:
| Role | Read | Write | Admin |
|---|---|---|---|
| <role> | <scope> | <scope> | <scope> |

**Integrations**:
| External | Direction | Method | Compliance flag |
|---|---|---|---|
| GHL | bidir | REST | — |
| Stripe | out | webhook | PCI |
| Supabase | bidir | client SDK | — |

## Milestones + tickets

### M0 — Scaffold (est: <hrs>)
| Ticket | Description | AC | Est | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| T001 | <title> | <desc> | <criteria> | <hrs> | <agent> |

### M1 — Core read (est: <hrs>)
[same format]

### M2 — Core write
### M3 — Integration
### M4 — Polish
### M5 — Production launch

## Compliance gates
- [ ] HIPAA: `security-inspector` mid-M3 + before M5
- [ ] PCI: Stripe-hosted only — never our servers
- [ ] Privacy policy live before M5
- [ ] Audit logs in place from M1

## KPIs / monitoring
- Latency: p50 <X> ms, p99 <Y> ms
- Error rate: < <Z>%
- Cost: < $<W> per request
- Business KPI: <metric + target>

## Risk register

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| <risk> | H/M/L | <action> |

## Total estimate
- M0-M5: <hrs> developer time
- Wall-clock (with reviews/gates): <weeks>

## 3-lane delegation

### ✅ I'll handle
- Architecture brief (this doc)
- Milestone plan
- Ticket breakdown
- Risk register
- KPI definition

### ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve stack choice (especially deviations)
- Approve milestone plan
- Approve each compliance gate
- Approve production launch

### 🚫 You only
- Sign vendor contracts (Vercel / Supabase / etc.)
- Approve any HIPAA-bearing build (counsel + clinician)
- Hire any contractor for engineering work

## Handoff
**To**:
- `frontend-dev` (planned), `backend-dev` (planned), `database-schema` (planned), `devops-deployment` (planned), `api-integrations` (planned)
- `security-inspector` (compliance gates)
- `pmo-operator` (project tracking — register milestones)

**Approval required**: yes — Chris approves architecture before any ticket is opened

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-eng-plan-<project>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Picking the stack before understanding the problem — problem first, then tool
  • Reinventing Matrix's default stack without reason — deviation costs consistency
  • No ticket breakdown — leaves devs guessing
  • No compliance gates in regulated builds — exposure
  • No risk register — every project has 3+ risks; surface them
  • No KPIs before launch — can't measure success
  • Skipping reuse-scanner — wastes the leverage of existing patterns

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Generate architecture + plan
  • ✅ Break into tickets (planning only — no execution)
  • ✅ Update risk register
  • ⚠️ Approve stack deviations
  • ⚠️ Open tickets in tracking system (Linear / GitHub Issues)
  • 🚫 Run any provisioning (vercel deploy, supabase create) without approval
  • 🚫 Sign contracts / commit money

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • Problem statement + business context
  • reuse-scanner output
  • _Shared Context/company-profile.md
  • _Shared Context/products.md (if building product)
  • Existing Matrix projects (matrix-store, matrix-advanced-solutions) — pattern source

Outputs to:

  • pmo-operator (registers project + milestones)
  • Frontend / backend / database / devops agents (planned)
  • security-inspector (compliance gates)
  • agent-registry.md (this project entered)

Chains with:

  • reuse-scanner (always first)
  • should-i-build-an-agent (when the project might be an agent, not software)
  • project-scaffolder (after approval — scaffold the project)
  • workflow-automation-builder (if any GHL automations are part of the system)

One-liner reminder

Reuse-scan first. Default to Matrix stack unless reason to deviate. System map → milestones → tickets. Compliance gates pre-launch. Risks named. KPIs defined.

skills.name: Executive Admin Toolkit
skills.description: A callable utility skill for any agent needing to draft executive / administrative content — emails, briefs, agendas, summaries, action items, decision frames, interview questions, job descriptions, performance reviews. Codifies the McGeorge 3 Ps prompt formula + 6 prompt types + 9 virtual-role patterns into a single composable toolkit. EAs (Leo + Christo) lean on this heavily.
skills.intent: Stop reinventing the same admin patterns across agents. Every email draft, every meeting brief, every action-item extraction goes through this toolkit so output is consistent, voice-aligned, and not generic AI-slop. Other skills compose this rather than rolling their own pattern.
best_for: EAs (Leo + Christo) drafting any admin content
Cross-department agents that draft emails / briefs / agendas / interview questions / job descriptions
Replacing ad-hoc 'write me a thing' prompts with a structured framework
skills.estimated_time: Per draft: 5-15 min. Composable into larger workflows.
skills.body_html:
Executive Admin Toolkit

The utility layer for admin / executive drafting. 3 Ps + 6 prompt types + 9 virtual roles. Composable. Voice-consistent.

Source

Adapted from McGeorge, D. The ChatGPT Revolution: How to Simplify Your Work and Admin with AI (2023). See _Knowledge Base/Source Summaries/McGeorge - The ChatGPT Revolution Admin with AI.md for full extraction.

When to use

  • Any time an agent needs to produce admin / executive-facing content
  • Composable inside larger skills (EAs, sales, CS, marketing)
  • When the request maps cleanly to one of the 9 virtual roles below

When NOT to use

  • Highly-specialized output (use the dedicated skill — e.g., cold-email-sequence for outbound)
  • Long-form content (use copywriting long-form)
  • Voice scripts (use voice-script-author)
  • Code (use eng skills)

The 3 Ps prompt formula (always apply)

Every output is shaped by:

Dimension Question to answer
Perspective Who is the speaker / writer? What's the context?
Purpose What outcome do they want? What action should the reader take?
Personality What tone / style? (professional, warm, direct, persuasive, calm, urgent, etc.)

When invoking this skill, capture the 3 Ps before generating. If the caller hasn't specified, ASK (one question — McGeorge's discipline).

The 6 prompt types

Match the request to a type:

# Type Use when
1 Exploratory Open ideation, gathering options
2 Customised Fill-in-the-blank for specific context
3 Context Complex situation needs guidance
4 Step-by-step Procedural / task instructions
5 Evaluation Critique existing work
6 Follow-up Drill / refine prior output

Use multiple types in conversation chain: exploratory → customised → step-by-step → evaluation → follow-up.

The 9 work virtual roles

Each role has a defined pattern. When caller invokes this skill, identify which role applies.

1. Virtual HR Administrator

Use for: job descriptions, interview questions, employee manual updates, briefs to recruitment agencies, performance review structures, onboarding checklists

Template prompts:

  • "Update this job description for [role] incorporating [changes since baseline]. Apply our brand voice from brand-guide.md."
  • "Write a brief for a recruitment agency using the above job description."
  • "Provide 5 interview questions for [role] that assess [specific competency]."
  • "Update our employee handbook section on [topic], highlighting areas needing Chris's input."

2. Virtual Personal Assistant

Use for: drafting emails, scheduling reasoning, meeting agendas, calendar logic, follow-up notes

Template prompts:

  • "Write a professional email to [recipient] requesting [outcome]. Tone: [tone]. Length: [N words]."
  • "Draft an agenda for a 30-min meeting with [participants] to cover [topics]."
