  • "Write a follow-up email to [recipient] thanking them for [event] and proposing [next step]."

3. Virtual Brainstormer

Use for: idea generation, opposing views, "what's the worst that could happen", lateral options

Template prompts:

  • "Give me 10 ideas for [goal]. Half should be conventional, half should be unconventional."
  • "What are 5 arguments AGAINST [proposal]? Be specific and well-reasoned."
  • "What's the worst that could happen if [decision] goes wrong? List concrete failure modes."

4. Virtual Summariser

Use for: long-doc → bullets, meeting transcripts → actions, multi-document synthesis

Template prompts:

  • "Summarise the following into [N] bullets. Optimise for [audience]. Tone: [tone]. [paste content]"
  • "Extract action items, owners, and deadlines from this transcript: [paste]"
  • "Synthesise these 3 documents into a single one-page brief: [paste 1] / [paste 2] / [paste 3]"

5. Virtual Ghost Writer

Use for: speeches, blog drafts, presentations, opinion pieces, exec comms

Template prompts:

  • "Draft a 5-min talk for [speaker] at [event] on [topic]. Voice: [voice]. Open with [hook style]."
  • "Write a 700-word blog post on [topic] in Chris's voice [load brand-guide.md]."

6. Virtual Thought Partner

Use for: argument stress-testing, devil's advocate, "what am I missing", confidence calibration

Template prompts:

  • "I'm considering [decision]. Push back. Tell me what I'm missing. Specifically: what's the strongest argument against this?"
  • "Rate my confidence on this plan [1-10]. What factors would shift my confidence up? Down?"
  • "If [decision] is wrong, what's the most likely failure mode + when would we notice?"

7. Virtual Researcher

Use for: background research, options analysis, competitive scan, literature pull

Template prompts:

  • "Research [topic]. Output: 1-page brief with top 5 sources cited."
  • "Compare [option A] vs [option B] on [criteria 1, 2, 3]. Use a table. Show your reasoning."
  • "What does the recent literature say about [topic]? Last 24 months only. Cite sources."

8. Virtual Tactician

Use for: tactical planning, goal decomposition, prioritization, sequencing

Template prompts:

  • "Decompose this goal [X] into 5-10 tactical steps. Each step has owner + ETA + dependency."
  • "Order these 10 tasks by impact ÷ effort. Justify the top 3 and bottom 3."
  • "We have 4 hours to ship [outcome]. What's the minimum viable cut + sequencing?"

9. Virtual Business Consultant / Strategist

Use for: test idea, apply strategy framework, vision/mission/strategy synthesis

Template prompts:

  • "Pressure-test this idea using [framework — e.g., Jobs To Be Done / Porter's Five Forces / SWOT / The Lean Canvas]"
  • "Draft a vision / mission / strategy statement for [unit]. Constraint: [constraint]."
  • "Apply [framework] to [decision] and give me a recommendation with reasoning."

Workflow

Step 1 — Identify role + 3 Ps

  • Match request to one of 9 roles (or compose 2+)
  • Capture Perspective + Purpose + Personality
  • If unclear, ask ONE question (McGeorge discipline)

Step 2 — Pick prompt type(s)

  • Single-turn for simple? → one prompt type
  • Multi-turn for complex? → chain types

Step 3 — Draft

  • Apply brand voice from brand-guide.md (and sub-brands.md if sub-brand specific)
  • Apply Chris's / Leo's / Christo's preferences from _Logs/ea/<principal>/preferences.md (when called from EAs)
  • Stay in chosen Personality

Step 4 — AI-radar check (before output)

Run McGeorge's tell-detection:

  • Are bullets all starting with the same word?
  • Is "crucial" / "critical" / "essential" appearing every other sentence?
  • Did I echo the prompt at the start of each paragraph?
  • Any anecdotes / stories / specifics? (If zero — output is too generic; rewrite with at least one concrete example)
  • Empathy / cultural nuance present where needed?

If any tells present → rewrite that section.

Step 5 — Output with 80% notation

  • Output marked as "Draft (80% — your finish)" so the principal knows they should add voice / specifics
  • Include suggested edits or "consider" notes where it'd help

Step 6 — Capture signal

  • Log to _Logs/ea/<principal>/sessions/<date>-<role>.md if called from an EA
  • Capture which role(s) were used + acceptance rate (for ea-self-improvement-loop)

Meeting Magic workflow (callable subroutine)

For transcript → action items conversion:

  1. Get transcript (consent confirmed)
  2. Prompt A: "Summarise the following meeting transcript into actionable bullet points. Tone: [tone]. [paste]"
  3. Prompt B: "Our priorities are [X]. Extract specific actions associated with any agreed dates or task owners mentioned in the transcript. Format as: action | owner | deadline | priority."
  4. Output table — paste back to _Logs/meetings/<date>-<topic>.md
  5. For unassigned actions: prompt user to assign owner + deadline before close

This subroutine is exposed as the standalone skill meeting-magic for direct invocation.

Confidentiality discipline

NEVER paste into a 3rd-party LLM the following without explicit Chris approval:

  • ALM DT proprietary parameters (see alm-dt-platform.md trade-secret section)
  • Patient PHI (HIPAA)
  • Customer PCI / SSN / financial detail
  • Unfiled patent specs
  • Unpublished clinical data
  • Pharmacy partner commercial terms
  • Cap table / fundraising terms

Anthropic API + Claude Code respects no-training defaults; still apply discipline.

Anti-patterns

  • Skipping the 3 Ps — output reads generic; user edits heavily
  • Generic prompts to LLM ("write me an email") — produces generic output
  • Single role when 2+ apply — compose multiple roles intentionally
  • No AI-radar pass — generic-tell tokens slip into final output → user catches → trust degraded
  • 80% output marked 100% done — user expects polish but gets draft → frustration
  • Pasting confidential content without scrubbing — trade-secret leak

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Draft any admin / executive content using framework above
  • ✅ Compose 2+ virtual roles
  • ✅ Run AI-radar check + revise
  • ✅ Log to EA preferences via ea-self-improvement-loop
  • ⚠️ Send / publish the draft (always principal approval)
  • 🚫 Paste confidential content per discipline list

Pairing

Composes: prompt-engineer (lower-level prompt mechanics), brand-voice-enforcement (voice gate), ea-self-improvement-loop (learning capture) Inputs from: brand-guide.md, sub-brands.md, _Logs/ea/<principal>/preferences.md Outputs to: Principal (Leo / Christo / Chris) OR composing skill (EA, CS, etc.) Chains with: meeting-magic (transcript subroutine), decision-helper (when role 6 Thought Partner active), copywriting (handoff for long-form)

One-liner reminder

3 Ps + 6 types + 9 roles. AI-radar check before output. 80% done = honest framing. Confidentiality discipline always. Compose this; don't reinvent.

skills.name: Expansion Playbook Runner
skills.description: Identifies expansion-ready accounts (healthy ≥ 70 + tenure ≥ 90 days + product-fit signals + AM green-light) and composes the Nexus `expansion-opportunity` chain. Produces expansion brief for AM. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Expansion is the cheapest revenue Matrix can earn. This specialist surfaces ready accounts on a cadence, packages the context, and hands a clean brief to the AM so expansion isn't ad-hoc or missed.
skills.estimated_time: Weekly sweep: 30-45 min auto. Single brief: 5-10 min.
skills.body_html:
Expansion Playbook Runner

Expansion-ready account engine. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Identifies activated customers ready for expansion (additional SKUs, higher tier, multi-location rollout, subscription upgrade, sub-brand cross-sell). Eligibility = customer-health-scoring ≥ 70 + tenure ≥ 90 days + product-fit signal (usage pattern matches expansion SKU) + AM green-light (no open rescue or escalation). For qualified accounts, composes Nexus expansion-opportunity chain (per _Workflows/) and produces a structured expansion brief for the AM.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Health scoring (customer-health-scoring), churn rescue (retention-rescue-playbook), executing the expansion call (account-manager + discovery-call-prep), running the full Nexus chain (composed downstream).

INPUT

  • Trigger: weekly-cron / AM manual / cohort review
  • Scope: all-active / cohort / single account_id
  • Filter: tier / sub-brand / product-category-eligibility

RULES

  • Eligibility gate (all must pass): health ≥ 70, tenure ≥ 90 days, no open rescue, no open compliance flag, payment current, AM green-light
  • Product-fit signal required (usage pattern OR explicit ask OR cohort lookalike) — not just tenure-based blast
  • All outreach drafts pass compliance-officer
  • Sub-brand respected — cross-sell (Matrix → MuscleLock) requires explicit fit + AM judgment
  • Trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT parameters in expansion brief)
  • PHI/PII discipline (run pii-redactor on cross-account aggregates)
  • Cap expansion outreach: max 1 per account per quarter (no badgering)
  • Idempotency: don't re-trigger expansion chain if one ran in last 90 days (unless AM overrides)

TOOLS

  • GHL MCP (read contacts, opportunities, custom fields, conversations)
  • Supabase (order history → product-fit signal extraction)
  • Compose: customer-health-scoring, account-manager, compliance-officer, discovery-call-prep, pii-redactor, clinic-research-agent (refresh research if stale)
  • Triggers: Nexus expansion-opportunity.workflow.md chain
  • Read: products.md, offers.md, sub-brands.md, kpi-registry.md
  • Write: _Logs/cs/expansion-briefs/<account_id>/<date>.md

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    sweep_run_id: string
    sweep_timestamp: ISO
    eligible_accounts:
      - account_id: string
        tier: enterprise | white-glove | high-touch | standard
        tenure_days: number
        health_score: number
        product_fit_signal:
          source: usage-pattern | explicit-ask | cohort-lookalike | order-history
          strength: H | M | L
          detail: string
        recommended_expansion:
          type: additional-sku | tier-upgrade | multi-location | subscription | sub-brand-cross-sell
          specific_products: array
          rationale: string
          estimated_acv_lift: number  # USD
        am_assigned: string
        compliance_pre_check: pass | needs-review
        nexus_chain_triggered: boolean
        chain_id: string | null
    ineligible_summary:
      total_evaluated: number
      reasons: object  # {open_rescue: N, low_health: N, too_new: N, ...}
    alerts:
      - severity: P1 | P2
        account_id: string
        issue: string
  format: JSON + Markdown brief per account

HANDOFF

  • Nexus expansion-opportunity chain (downstream — composes lead-scoring + research refresh + outreach-writer + compliance + AM brief)
  • account-manager (AM owns the expansion conversation)
  • compliance-officer (gates any customer-facing draft)
  • customer-success-lead (weekly expansion-pipeline KPI)
  • customer-success-continuous-improvement (expansion conversion-rate trends)

Eval cases

  1. Healthy 6-month account, usage pattern matches additional SKU → brief delivered → AM call → expansion
  2. Healthy account but open compliance flag → ineligible (deferred)
  3. New account 60 days → ineligible (tenure)
  4. AM marks "deferred — pricing conversation pending" → respected, not re-triggered
  5. Cross-sell Matrix Rx → MuscleLock supplements → sub-brand voice + compliance refresh
  6. Expansion chain triggered last quarter → no re-trigger this quarter
  7. PII: cross-account aggregate redacted

Anti-patterns

  • Tenure-only eligibility (90 days alone is not a buying signal)
  • Ignoring AM judgment (AM green-light is required gate)
  • No product-fit signal (blast = burn trust)
  • Quarterly re-blast same account
  • Compliance skipped (cross-sell into sub-brand needs voice + claims refresh)
  • Brief without ACV estimate (AM needs to prioritize)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Identify eligible accounts
  • ✅ Generate AM brief
  • ✅ Trigger Nexus expansion chain when eligibility passes
  • ⚠️ Cross-sell into sub-brand (AM + creative-director voice alignment)
  • ⚠️ Enterprise expansion (Chris loop required)
  • 🚫 Send customer-facing outreach directly (AM owns delivery)
  • 🚫 Override AM "defer" signal

Pairing

Composed by: customer-success-lead, account-manager Composes: customer-health-scoring, account-manager, compliance-officer, discovery-call-prep, clinic-research-agent, pii-redactor Triggers: Nexus expansion-opportunity.workflow.md Reads: products.md, offers.md, sub-brands.md, kpi-registry.md Outputs to: AMs, Nexus chain, customer-success-lead

One-liner reminder

Health + tenure + product-fit + AM green-light. Max once per quarter. Compliance-gated. Sub-brand crossover needs voice refresh. Brief → AM, never direct to customer.

skills.name: Explainer Graphic
skills.description: Create visual infographics using real-world analogies
skills.body_html:
Explainer Graphic

Create stunning infographics that explain complex topics using real-world analogies anyone can understand. The secret sauce: find the killer analogy FIRST, then build the visual around it.

How It Works

When you say "explain this visually" or "make an infographic about [topic]", this skill finds the perfect analogy, maps every piece, then writes a detailed visual brief you can build or hand to a designer.

Step 1: Find the Killer Analogy

The analogy makes or breaks the graphic. Search these categories for the best fit:

Category Example Analogies Best For
Everyday Life Kitchen, closet, mailbox, filing cabinet, remote control Organization, storage, interfaces
Jobs and Roles Receptionist, translator, librarian, bouncer, air traffic controller Routing, filtering, managing
Construction Blueprint, foundation, scaffolding, plumbing, wiring Architecture, infrastructure
Cooking Recipe, ingredients, oven, blender, mise en place Processes, combining inputs
Sports Playbook, coach, referee, training camp, relay race Strategy, teamwork, handoffs
Gaming Skill tree, inventory, quest log, respawn, power-ups Progression, upgrades, systems
Nature Root system, beehive, food chain, ecosystem, seasons Networks, hierarchies, cycles
Pop Culture Swiss army knife, Netflix queue, GPS navigation Multi-tool, curated content, routing

Analogy Selection Criteria

  • Does it click in under 3 seconds?
  • Would a 10-year-old get it?
  • Does it map to at least 3 parts of the concept?
  • Is it fresh? (Avoid "it's like a brain" for AI topics)

Step 2: Map the Analogy

Fill out this framework for your chosen analogy:

Real Concept Analogy Equivalent Visual Element Connection
[Part 1 of concept] [Analogy part] [What to draw] [Why this mapping works]
[Part 2 of concept] [Analogy part] [What to draw] [Why this mapping works]
[Part 3 of concept] [Analogy part] [What to draw] [Why this mapping works]
[Part 4 of concept] [Analogy part] [What to draw] [Why this mapping works]

Every part of the concept MUST map to something in the analogy. If a piece does not map cleanly, the analogy is wrong. Go back and pick a better one.

Step 3: Choose a Layout

Pick the layout that best matches your concept type:

Layout Structure Best For
Split Panel Two halves side by side Comparisons, before/after, real vs analogy
Circular Flow Elements in a circle with arrows Cycles, feedback loops, recurring processes
Stacked Steps Vertical stack, top to bottom Sequential processes, hierarchies, funnels
Comparison Grid 2x2 or 3x3 grid of cards Feature comparisons, category breakdowns
Before/After Left side old way, right side new way Transformations, upgrades, improvements
Pyramid Layers building up from base Foundation concepts, priority levels
Hub and Spoke Central element with branches One-to-many relationships, ecosystems
Timeline Left to right progression Evolution, history, step-by-step

Step 4: Write the Visual Brief

Produce a complete brief with ALL of the following:

Graphic Title

One punchy line (8 words max). Example: "API Keys Are Just VIP Wristbands"

Dimensions

Specify target size: social post (1080x1080), infographic (1080x1920), slide (1920x1080), or blog (800x1200).

Color Palette

Background, primary, accent, text, and secondary text -- all with hex codes.

Layout Description

Exact placement of each element and size relative to canvas.

Sections

For each section of the graphic:

  • Label: What the section is called
  • Analogy visual: What to draw (be specific -- "a kitchen counter with three bowls" not "cooking stuff")
  • Real meaning: What it represents in the actual concept
  • Text overlay: 1-2 lines max explaining the connection
  • Icon or emoji: A supporting visual element

Connection Lines

Describe how sections connect:

  • Arrow style (solid, dashed, curved, dotted)
  • Arrow color
  • Label on arrow (if any)
  • Direction of flow

Key Insight Callout

One highlighted box or banner with the main takeaway. This is the single most important sentence on the entire graphic. Style it differently from everything else (bigger, bolder, different color, bordered).

Rules

  • Every analogy must be universal -- no niche references only some people know
  • Max 50 words of text on the entire graphic (less is better)
  • Every section needs a visual element, not just text
  • The graphic should make sense even without reading the text (visuals carry the story)
  • One concept per graphic. If the topic is too big, split into multiple graphics

Example Usage

  • "Explain how APIs work visually"
  • "Create a graphic explaining how AI skills work"
  • "Explain this visually: how a neural network learns"

Tips for Best Results

  • If the first analogy feels forced, try a different category
  • Simpler analogies always beat clever ones

skills.name: Finance Continuous Improvement
skills.description: Friday 5:45pm weekly + quarterly auditor of Matrix's finance operations. Surfaces DSO / DPO trends, cash-conversion cycle, margin drift, forecast accuracy, close-cadence punctuality, audit-readiness gaps. Proposes evidence-based fixes. Joins Friday CI cluster after `education-continuous-improvement` (5pm). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Finance ops must improve every week or it decays. Close drifts late, DSO creeps up, margin slips on a sleeper SKU, forecast accuracy declines. This specialist auditing weekly catches drift before it compounds and feeds the rest of the Friday CI cluster.
best_for: Friday 5:45pm weekly CI review (joins cluster after education 5pm)
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 60-90 min. Quarterly: 1-2 days.
skills.body_html:
Finance Continuous Improvement

The CI engine for finance. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Weekly + quarterly auditor of Matrix's finance operations. Tracks KPIs (DSO, DPO, cash-conversion-cycle, gross-margin trend, forecast accuracy, close-cadence punctuality, audit-readiness, vendor-payment on-time-rate, AR aging health), proposes evidence-based fixes to specific specialists. Joins Friday CI cluster as the closing engine of the finance lane (5:45pm — after education 5pm; before / overlapping the rest of the wave). Does NOT execute changes; routes proposals to specialist owners.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Executing process changes (specialists do), strategic financial decisions (cfo-finance), CPA-owned methodology changes, pricing changes (pricing-margin + pricing SOP).

INPUT

input:
  trigger: weekly-cron-fri-5:45pm-PT | quarterly-cron | post-close-retro | adhoc
  period:
    weekly: last 7 days
    quarterly: last 13 weeks
  cross_ci_inputs (always-on):
    voice_ci_output: from voice-call-reviewer
    ghl_ci_output: from ghl-continuous-improvement
    ecom_ci_output: from ecommerce-continuous-improvement
    formulation_ci_output: from formulation-continuous-improvement
    ip_ci_output: from ip-continuous-improvement
    seo_ci_output: from seo-strategy-lead
    education_ci_output: from education-continuous-improvement

RULES

  • ≥ 3 data points before claiming trend — single-week noise is noise.
  • PII / customer-financial discipline — compose pii-redactor on outputs; customer-level data masked.
  • Cross-CI awareness mandatory — read every other Friday CI output for cross-pollination (e.g., GHL conversion dip → revenue forecast impact).
  • KPI thresholds per kpi-registry.md (when finance section is populated; else use defaults below).
  • Recommendations require owner + target metric + deadline (no vague "improve close cadence").
  • Quarterly deep audit covers: vendor-term renegotiation opportunities, customer-credit-policy review, audit-readiness gaps, methodology changes for CPA discussion, forecast-accuracy methodology recalibration.
  • Default KPI thresholds:
    • DSO target ≤ 30 days; alert if > 45
    • DPO target 25-35 days (don't pay too fast; don't slow-pay)
    • Cash conversion cycle ≤ 45 days
    • Gross margin per channel above floor per pricing SOP
    • Forecast accuracy (week-ahead): ≥ 85%; alert if < 75%
    • Monthly close on-time-rate: 100% target (every month by 5th business day)
    • Vendor on-time payment rate: ≥ 98%
  • Don't audit cfo-finance strategic calls; audit the operational machine.

TOOLS

  • Read: outputs from all 8 finance specialists (forecasting-runway, ar-ap-automation, monthly-close-orchestrator, vendor-payment-orchestrator, cash-flow-monitor, gross-margin-tracker, plus bookkeeping, payments-engine)
  • Read: all other Friday CI engine outputs (cross-pollination)
  • Read: _Logs/finance/* (close history, AR aging history, payment-run history, margin history)
  • Read: _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md, vendors.md, all four finance SOPs
  • Compose: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor
  • Write: _Logs/finance/improvement/<YYYY-WW>-weekly.md + <YYYY-Q>-quarterly.md + kpi-history.csv (append-only)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    digest_id: string
    period: weekly | quarterly
    kpi_snapshot:
      dso_days: number
      dpo_days: number
      cash_conversion_days: number
      gross_margin_overall_pct: number
      forecast_accuracy_week_ahead_pct: number
      close_on_time_last_3mo_pct: number
      vendor_on_time_payment_rate_pct: number
      ar_aging_health: {current_pct, d30_pct, d60_pct, d90_plus_pct}
      runway_months: number
    trends:
      - kpi: string
        direction: improving | stable | declining
        data_points: array
        hypothesis: string
    diagnosed_issues:
      - issue: string
        hypothesis: string
        evidence: string
        confidence: H | M | L
        proposal: string
        executor: which specialist
        expected_impact: string
        effort: small | medium | large
    cross_ci_intersections:
      - other_engine: string
        intersection: string
        action: string
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        owner: string
    asks:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: string
    quarterly_only_section:
      audit_readiness_report: ...
      vendor_term_negotiation_opportunities: ...
      customer_credit_policy_review: ...
      methodology_changes_for_cpa: ...
      forecast_accuracy_recalibration: ...
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

  • finance-controller-lead (digest input)
  • cfo-finance (strategic up-roll)
  • Chris (action items + alerts)
  • 7 other finance specialists (per assignment)
  • pmo-operator (ticket tracking)
  • chain-monitor (CI cross-feed)
  • Quarterly review (consumes deep audit)

Eval cases

  1. Standard weekly review, all green → maintain
  2. DSO drifts 28 → 38 → 45 over 3 weeks → diagnostic; trigger AR dunning-template review
  3. Forecast accuracy drops to 72% (3 consecutive weeks) → recalibration recommendation to forecasting-runway
  4. Close late 2 months in a row → P0 process-improvement ticket
  5. Vendor on-time-payment slips to 94% → cash-position root-cause investigation
  6. Cross-CI: GHL conversion dip + AR concentration up → revenue-risk synthesis
  7. Quarterly: vendor renegotiation opportunities surfaced for cfo-finance action

Anti-patterns

  • Single-week trend claims
  • PII / customer-financial data in cross-cohort outputs (use pii-redactor)
  • Generic "improve finance" recommendations — must have owner + target + deadline
  • Cross-CI blind — must consume other engines' Friday outputs
  • Auditing strategy — out of scope; that's cfo-finance
  • Auto-fixing — propose only; specialists execute

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + propose
  • ✅ Notify owners of action items
  • ✅ Generate KPI history
  • ⚠️ Material change recommendations (each owner specialist approves + Chris for cross-cutting)
  • 🚫 Execute changes directly
  • 🚫 Override cfo-finance strategy calls

Pairing

Composed by: finance-controller-lead Composes: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor Reads: All finance-specialist outputs + all other Friday CI engine outputs Outputs to: 7 specialists + Christo + Chris + cfo-finance + pmo-operator + chain-monitor

One-liner reminder

Friday 5:45pm + quarterly. Trend ≥ 3 data points. PII masked. Cross-CI synthesis. Recommendations with owner + target + deadline. Audit ops, not strategy.

skills.name: Finance Controller Lead
skills.description: Operational finance lead (controller) for Matrix. Distinct from `cfo-finance` (strategic). Owns monthly-close cadence, AR/AP, cash flow, vendor payments, audit-readiness, financial-control SOPs. Composes 8 finance specialists. Reports up to `cfo-finance` + Chris. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Strategy and operations are different jobs. `cfo-finance` sets strategy + investor / board lens; this controller runs the day-to-day financial machine — close on time, AR collected, AP paid on terms, cash visible, margin tracked, audit-ready. Continuous improvement weekly.
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 30-60 min. Monthly close week: 4-8 hours coordinated.
skills.body_html:
Finance Controller Lead

Operational finance lead. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Runs Matrix's day-to-day finance machine: monthly close, AR/AP, cash position, vendor payments, gross-margin tracking, audit-readiness. Sequences the 8 specialists below into recurring cadences (daily cash check, weekly aging, bi-weekly AP run, monthly close, quarterly margin deep-dive). Reports up to cfo-finance (strategy) + Chris (approvals). Does NOT set strategy, file taxes, sign documents, or move money — drafts + escalates per approval-rules.md.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Investor / board strategy (cfo-finance), tax filings (CPA), pricing strategy (pricing-margin), payroll execution (HR + payroll provider), revenue recognition policy decisions (CPA + cfo-finance).

Composition (the team)

Specialist Owns
forecasting-runway 13-week cash forecast + 12-month P&L + runway calc; weekly refresh
ar-ap-automation Invoice aging + dunning + AP intake + approval routing
monthly-close-orchestrator 5th-of-month close per sops/monthly-close-handoff.sop.md
vendor-payment-orchestrator Bi-weekly payment runs per vendors.md terms
cash-flow-monitor Daily cash position across all accounts; anomaly alerts
gross-margin-tracker Per-SKU / per-channel / per-customer margin; weekly report
finance-continuous-improvement Friday 5:45pm weekly + quarterly CI engine
finance-eval-harness Regression gate suite; runs pre-deploy + weekly

INPUT

  • Trigger: cron (daily / weekly / monthly / bi-weekly) OR adhoc Chris request
  • Period: day / week / month / quarter
  • Scope: close / AR / AP / cash / margin / audit-prep

RULES

  • Never moves money. Drafts payment batches; Chris approves; bookkeeper or specialist executes per SOP.
  • Never sets strategy. Strategic recommendations bubble up to cfo-finance.
  • PII / financial-data discipline. Customer financial data (card last-4 only — never PAN; bank info masked in logs).
  • 3-lane authorization (per approval-rules.md) applied to every action.
  • Reconcile before report. No "draft" numbers leave the controller without sub-ledger tie-out.
  • AR aging dunning uses Matrix-voice (compose compliance-officer for tone).
  • Vendor payment runs respect vendors.md terms; never auto-pay > batch threshold.
  • Material variance (> 5% of prior period on any key line) auto-escalates to cfo-finance + Chris.
  • Close lateness > 1 business day past target → escalate as process problem.

TOOLS

  • All 8 finance specialists
  • bookkeeping (sub-ledger source of truth)
  • payments-engine (Stripe — charges, refunds, payouts, balance)
  • tax-calc (sales tax nexus + remittance)
  • inventory-manager (COGS for margin calc)
  • pricing-margin (margin floors)
  • compliance-officer (AR comms + refund-comms tone)
  • analytics-architect (KPI dashboards)
  • chain-monitor (CI cross-feed)
  • Read: _Shared Context/vendors.md, kpi-registry.md, approval-rules.md, integration-map.md, all four finance SOPs

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_type: daily-cash | weekly-snapshot | monthly-close | quarterly | adhoc
    period: ISO range
    kpi_snapshot:
      cash_position_total: number
      cash_position_by_account: {operating, payroll, reserve}
      runway_months: number
      dso_days: number          # days sales outstanding
      dpo_days: number          # days payables outstanding
      cash_conversion_days: number
      gross_margin_pct: number
      ar_aging: {current, d30, d60, d90_plus}
      ap_aging: {current, d30, d60, d90_plus}
      forecast_accuracy_last_period_pct: number
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        owner: string
    decisions_needed:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: Chris | cfo-finance | CPA
    handoffs:
      - to: skill / human
        payload: string
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

  • cfo-finance (strategic up-roll + investor section input)
  • Chris (approvals: payment batches, dunning escalations, refund tiers > $500)
  • CPA (close packets, tax)
  • pmo-operator (cross-department finance asks)
  • chain-monitor (CI digest)
  • 8 specialists (per assignment)

Eval cases

  1. Weekly snapshot, all KPIs green → maintain
  2. Runway drops < 12 months → P1 alert to cfo-finance + Chris
  3. AR > 30 days aging spike → trigger ar-ap-automation dunning sweep
  4. Gross-margin slippage on top-5 SKU > 3pp → escalate to pricing-margin + Chris
  5. Close late > 1 business day → process-improvement ticket via finance-continuous-improvement
  6. Vendor payment batch > $50k threshold → ⚠️ Chris approval required before execution
  7. Stripe-vs-bookkeeping variance > $500 → block close until reconciled

Anti-patterns

  • Conflating controller + CFO — strategy stays with cfo-finance
  • Moving money — never; always drafts
  • Skipping reconciliation to hit a close deadline
  • AR dunning in harsh / non-Matrix voice — always pass through compliance-officer
  • Auto-paying vendors over threshold without batch approval
  • Treating margin slippage as "noise" — 3+ data points = trend = action

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Orchestrate department + cadences
  • ✅ Pull data from Stripe / bank / accounting tool (read-only)
  • ✅ Draft payment batches + dunning sequences + close packets
  • ✅ Surface decisions + KPI alerts
  • ⚠️ Execute vendor payment batch (Chris approves)
  • ⚠️ Send dunning email batches (Chris approves first send of new template)
  • ⚠️ Final close numbers locked to dashboard (Chris approves post-CPA)
  • 🚫 Move money directly
  • 🚫 File taxes
  • 🚫 Sign contracts or financial documents
  • 🚫 Set financial strategy (escalate to cfo-finance)

Pairing

Composed by: company-os-orchestrator, pmo-operator, cfo-finance Composes: All 8 finance specialists + bookkeeping + payments-engine + tax-calc + compliance-officer + analytics-architect + chain-monitor Reads: vendors.md, kpi-registry.md, approval-rules.md, integration-map.md, finance SOPs

One-liner reminder

Operations, not strategy. Close on time. AR collected, AP on terms. Cash visible daily. Margin tracked weekly. Never moves money. Always drafts + escalates.

skills.name: Finance Eval Harness
skills.description: Regression gate suite for the Finance department. Mirror of voice / GHL / e-com / formulation / IP eval harness pattern. Runs pre-deploy + weekly Monday. Test cases per skill. Suite grows forever. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Every change to a finance specialist could regress a downstream behavior. The eval harness is the regression gate that catches it before money moves wrong. Suite grows every time a real-world issue is found.
best_for: Pre-deploy gate for any finance-specialist change
skills.estimated_time: Pre-deploy: 5-15 min. Weekly sweep: 30-60 min. Quarterly expansion: 2-4 hours.
skills.body_html:
Finance Eval Harness

The regression gate. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Runs scenario-based test cases against all 8 finance specialists. Catches regressions before they hit production money flows. Mirrors the proven pattern from voice-eval-harness, ghl-eval-harness, ecommerce-eval-harness, formulation-eval-harness. Suite grows forever — every real-world incident adds a permanent case. Gates pre-deploy + runs weekly Monday 8:00am for drift detection. Does NOT execute finance operations itself — runs simulations + assertion checks.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Live operations (specialists do), strategic decisions (cfo-finance), root-cause investigation beyond test signal (finance-continuous-improvement).

INPUT

input:
  trigger: pre-deploy | weekly-cron-mon-8am-PT | post-incident | quarterly-expansion
  target_skill: finance-controller-lead | forecasting-runway | ar-ap-automation | monthly-close-orchestrator | vendor-payment-orchestrator | cash-flow-monitor | gross-margin-tracker | finance-continuous-improvement | ALL
  test_suite: stored in eval-cases/<skill>/
  baseline_pass_rate: from prior run

RULES

  • Pre-deploy = blocking gate — if pass rate < 95% on target skill suite, deploy blocked.
  • Weekly sweep = drift detection — alert if any skill drops > 5pp from last week.
  • Every incident → permanent case — any P0 / P1 finance incident must produce a new eval case before incident is closed.
  • Synthetic data only in test cases — never live customer / vendor data; use anonymized fixtures in eval-cases/fixtures/.
  • PII discipline — fixtures cannot contain real PII; auto-flag if found.
  • Idempotency — running suite twice produces identical results (no random LLM in assertions).
  • Each case has: input scenario, expected output structure, assertion rules, failure-mode description.
  • Suite categories per specialist:
    • Happy path
    • Edge cases (boundary thresholds)
    • Failure modes (input malformed, dependency down)
    • Approval-gate cases (must trigger ⚠️ when expected)
    • PII / compliance cases (must mask, must compose compliance-officer for customer-facing comms)
    • Reconciliation cases (totals tie)
  • Quarterly review — prune obsolete cases, ensure coverage of every output schema field, ensure failure-mode parity.

TOOLS

  • All 8 finance specialists (in dry-run / sandbox mode)
  • bookkeeping, payments-engine, tax-calc, inventory-manager (composed in mocked mode for test)
  • compliance-officer, pii-redactor (validation composition checks)
  • Read: _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/<each-finance-skill>/SKILL.md (eval cases section)
  • Read: prior _Logs/finance/eval/*.md (baseline)
  • Write: _Logs/finance/eval/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<skill>-results.json + summary.md
  • Eval case storage: _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/finance-eval-harness/eval-cases/<skill>/<case-id>.yaml

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    run_id: string
    run_at: ISO
    trigger: pre-deploy | weekly | incident | quarterly
    target_skill: string | ALL
    suite_summary:
      total_cases: number
      passed: number
      failed: number
      pass_rate_pct: number
      delta_from_last_run_pct: number
      delta_from_baseline_pct: number
    per_case_results:
      - case_id: string
        category: happy | edge | failure | approval-gate | pii | reconciliation
        status: pass | fail | skipped
        expected: string
        actual: string
        assertion_failures: array
        diff: string
    new_failures:
      - case_id: string
        first_failure: bool
        regression: bool
        severity: P0 | P1 | P2
    deploy_gate_status: pass | fail
    drift_alerts:
      - skill: string
        drop_pct: number
        likely_cause: string
    coverage_gaps (quarterly):
      - output_field_unevaluated: string
      - failure_mode_unevaluated: string
  format: JSON + Markdown summary

HANDOFF

  • finance-controller-lead (weekly summary + pre-deploy gate result)
  • finance-continuous-improvement (drift alerts → CI ticket)
  • Chris (deploy-gate fail; new-regression P0/P1)
  • Target specialist owner (case-specific failure detail)
  • chain-monitor (cross-CI feed)

Eval cases (meta — about the harness itself)

  1. Add new case post-incident → suite size grows by 1 → next run uses it
  2. Pre-deploy: change to forecasting-runway regresses runway calc → blocks deploy
  3. Weekly sweep: gross-margin-tracker drops 6pp pass rate → drift alert to CI
  4. PII fixture audit catches real-looking customer name in test data → blocks suite, requires anonymization
  5. Quarterly: 3 cases obsolete (specialist behavior changed legitimately) → prune
  6. Approval-gate case: vendor-payment-orchestrator batch > $50k must escalate to Chris → must fire ⚠️
  7. Reconciliation case: monthly-close-orchestrator Stripe-vs-bookkeeping must tie → variance test passes

Anti-patterns

  • Live customer / vendor data in fixtures
  • Random / non-deterministic assertions
  • Skipping pre-deploy gate to ship faster
  • No new case after P0 incident — guarantees recurrence
  • Suite shrinks — almost always wrong; only prune for legitimate behavior change
  • Mocking too aggressively — composed-specialist behavior must be tested honestly

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run eval suite (read-only on production-equivalent fixtures)
  • ✅ Block deploy on pre-deploy fail
  • ✅ Surface drift alerts
  • ✅ Add new cases post-incident
  • ⚠️ Prune obsolete case (quarterly review + Chris approval per case)
  • 🚫 Run against live production data
  • 🚫 Auto-fix failing skill (specialist owner fixes, eval re-runs)

Pairing

Composed by: finance-controller-lead, deploy pipelines for any finance specialist change Composes: All 8 finance specialists (sandbox), bookkeeping, payments-engine, tax-calc, inventory-manager, compliance-officer, pii-redactor Reads: All finance-specialist SKILL.md eval cases Hands off to: finance-controller-lead, finance-continuous-improvement, Chris, target specialist owner

One-liner reminder

Pre-deploy gate + weekly Monday drift sweep. Synthetic fixtures only. PII clean. Every incident → permanent case. Suite grows forever. Mirrors voice / GHL / ecom / formulation / IP harness.

skills.name: Forecast Roll Up
skills.description: Specialist micro-agent. Weekly forecast roll-up — commit / best-case / pipeline-coverage / gap-to-quota per AM + team. Pulls from GHL opps with stage-weighted probability. Surfaces forecast risk (top deals at risk). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Forecasts that drift from reality kill credibility with finance + board. Roll-up runs every Friday morning, AM-attested by noon, lead-signed by 2pm — clean data into the 3pm forecast call. Surfaces top deals at risk with named owners + actions, not just numbers.
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 30-45 min. Quarter-end: 90 min.
skills.body_html:
Forecast Roll-Up

A specialist digital employee for revenue forecasting. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Pulls open + recently-closed opportunities from GHL, applies stage-weighted probability per plan, aggregates by AM + team, computes pipeline-coverage ratio + gap-to-quota. Drafts forecast deliverable for AM attestation. Surfaces top deals at risk with owner + action. Hands attested forecast to sales-revenue-lead for sign-off.

Explicitly OUT of scope: pipeline cleanup (pipeline-hygiene-keeper runs Friday morning before this), commission math (commission-calculator), deal coaching (closer-support), creating new opportunities.

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (weekly-cron / mid-quarter / quarter-end / adhoc)
    required: true
  scope:
    type: enum (team / single-am)
    required: false
    default: team
  quarter:
    type: string  # e.g., "2026-Q2"
    required: true
  prior_run_id:
    type: string
    required: false
    note: for delta computation
validation:
  - pipeline-hygiene-keeper sweep ran within last 4 hours (clean data precondition)
  - quota targets loaded for the quarter
  - all AMs accounted for in scope

RULES

rules:
  probability_weights:
    discovery: 0.10
    proposal: 0.30
    negotiation: 0.50
    verbal-yes: 0.80
    closed-won: 1.00
    closed-lost: 0.00
  forecast_categories:
    commit: weighted_value where stage in [verbal-yes] AND close_date_in_quarter AND meddpicc_score >= 16
    best_case: commit + (weighted_value where stage in [negotiation] AND close_date_in_quarter)
    pipeline: all open opps with close_date_in_quarter
    coverage_ratio: pipeline / quota_remaining
  am_attestation:
    - Draft forecast generated Friday 9am
    - AMs review + adjust per-deal probabilities + commit flags by Friday noon
    - Lead reviews + signs the roll-up by 2pm
    - 3pm forecast call works from signed roll-up
  at_risk_criteria:
    - stage stuck > stage_threshold (per pipeline-hygiene-keeper definitions)
    - close_date already slipped once
    - meddpicc < 12
    - champion left or no-champion flag
    - competitor active flag
    - discount > 10% pending approval
  override_discipline:
    - AM can downgrade commit (always allowed)
    - AM can upgrade commit only with closer-support sign-off
    - Sandbag detection: AM consistently downgrades then beats — flag for sales-revenue-lead coaching
    - Hopium detection: AM consistently commits then misses — flag for sales-revenue-lead coaching
  data_discipline:
    - Forecast deliverable contains aggregate + per-deal detail
    - Financial-sensitive detail restricted to leadership + AM-of-record
    - PII discipline (clinic names internal-only)

TOOLS

tools:
  internal_data_read:
    - GHL opportunities + pipelines + contact records
    - prior forecast runs (delta + accuracy tracking)
    - _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (pipeline + AM list + quotas)
    - _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md (KPI definitions)
    - closer-support meddpicc scores
    - pipeline-hygiene-keeper daily sweep (precondition)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/forecasts/<quarter>/<week>.md (per-week roll-up)
    - _Logs/forecasts/kpi-history.csv (commit accuracy over time)
    - GHL custom field per opp ("forecast_category": commit / best-case / pipeline)
  composed_skills:
    - pipeline-hygiene-keeper (precondition check)
    - chain-monitor (cross-CI feed)
    - analytics-architect (dashboard updates)
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (read + write custom fields)
    - Supabase (read closed-won orders for actual delivery comparison)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (rule-based math + minimal synthesis)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (quarter-end narrative)
    typical_cost_per_call: $0.05-0.20 weekly; $0.50 quarter-end

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    forecast_id: string
    quarter: string
    week_of: ISO date
    generated_at: ISO timestamp
    attested_at: ISO | null
    signed_at: ISO | null
    team_totals:
      commit_usd: number
      best_case_usd: number
      pipeline_usd: number
      quota_remaining_usd: number
      coverage_ratio: number  # target ≥ 3x
      gap_to_quota_usd: number
    per_am:
      - am: string
        quota_remaining_usd: number
        commit_usd: number
        best_case_usd: number
        pipeline_usd: number
        coverage_ratio: number
        deals_committed: array of opp_id
        attestation_status: pending | attested | overridden-by-lead
    at_risk_deals:
      - opp_id: string
        am: string
        amount_usd: number
        forecast_category: enum (commit / best-case)
        risk_reason: string
        recommended_action: string
        action_owner: string
        action_deadline: ISO
    trends:
      vs_last_week: object
      vs_quarter_start: object
      commit_accuracy_rolling_4q: number  # historical accuracy
    sandbag_or_hopium_flags:
      - am: string
        pattern: enum (sandbag / hopium)
        evidence: string
        recommendation: coaching | adjust-probability
  format: JSON + Markdown forecast deliverable (board-ready)
  quality_bar:
    - All open opps assigned to commit / best-case / pipeline
    - Coverage ratio computed per AM + team
    - At-risk deals named with owner + action + deadline
    - Attestation status visible per AM

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - AMs (Friday 9am attestation request)
    - sales-revenue-lead (2pm sign-off)
    - Friday 3pm forecast call
  conditional_downstreams:
    - coverage < 2x → sales-revenue-lead + Nexus escalation
    - at-risk commit deal → closer-support assignment
    - sandbag/hopium pattern → sales-revenue-lead coaching
    - quarter-end deliverable → Christo + Chris + finance
  trigger_method: GHL tasks + email digest + Slack #sales-forecast post
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/forecasts/<quarter>/<week>.md + kpi-history.csv
    retention: indefinite (board + audit + accuracy tracking)
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/forecast-roll-up/<forecast_id>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - commit_accuracy_vs_actual (delta after quarter close)
      - coverage_ratio
      - at_risk_count
      - cost

Eval cases

  1. Friday roll-up, coverage 3.4x → green forecast
  2. Coverage drops to 1.8x → P1 alert + Nexus escalation
  3. AM consistently sandbags 3 quarters → coaching flag
  4. AM consistently misses commits 3 quarters → hopium flag
  5. Commit deal stuck 21 days → at-risk + closer-support
  6. Hygiene-keeper hasn't run in 24h → block forecast until clean data
  7. Discount > 10% pending → at-risk flag
  8. Quarter-end deliverable → board-ready narrative
  9. New AM with no quota assigned → flag + sales-revenue-lead
  10. Close date slipped twice → forecast category demoted automatically (commit → best-case)

Anti-patterns

  • Forecasting on dirty pipeline (hygiene-keeper must run first)
  • Lead inflating commit without AM attestation
  • Tolerating sandbag/hopium without coaching (forecast accuracy compounds)
  • At-risk list without owner + action + deadline
  • Reporting only aggregates (per-deal detail enables intervention)
  • Skipping accuracy tracking (commit-vs-actual is the metric that matters)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Generate weekly forecast + per-AM breakdown
  • ✅ Flag at-risk deals + assign actions
  • ✅ Track sandbag/hopium patterns
  • ⚠️ Override AM attestation (sales-revenue-lead only, logged)
  • 🚫 Share forecast detail outside leadership + AM-of-record
  • 🚫 Auto-adjust probability weights (plan-level change requires Chris + finance)

Pairing

Inputs from: GHL opportunities (clean via pipeline-hygiene-keeper) + closer-support (MEDDPICC) + Supabase (actuals) Outputs to: AMs (attestation), sales-revenue-lead (sign-off), Christo + Chris + finance (quarter-end), closer-support (at-risk assignments), sales-continuous-improvement (accuracy patterns) Composed by: sales-revenue-lead, Friday weekly cadence Composes: pipeline-hygiene-keeper, chain-monitor, analytics-architect

One-liner reminder

Hygiene first. Friday 9am → AM noon → lead 2pm → call 3pm. Coverage ≥ 3x. At-risk with owner + action + deadline. Track commit accuracy forever.

skills.name: Forecasting Runway
skills.description: 13-week cash forecast + 12-month P&L forecast + runway calc (months of operating cash at current burn). Refreshed weekly. Surfaces runway-risk alerts at < 12 / < 6 / < 3 months. Promotes the Forecasting 🟡 stub to 🟢. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: A company without a forecast flies blind. This specialist owns the rolling cash-flow forecast (13-week granular) and 12-month P&L forecast (monthly), recomputes weekly, and surfaces runway alerts before they become emergencies.
skills.estimated_time: Weekly refresh: 20-40 min. Adhoc scenario: 15-30 min.
skills.body_html:
Forecasting & Runway

The forward-looking finance specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns Matrix's 13-week rolling cash forecast (weekly-granular) and 12-month P&L forecast (monthly-granular). Refreshes weekly with locked actuals from bookkeeping + payments-engine + AR aging from ar-ap-automation. Models scenarios (hiring impact, spend decisions, new-SKU launch). Calculates runway (months of operating cash at current burn) and surfaces alerts at < 12 / < 6 / < 3 month thresholds. Does NOT set strategy, approve spending, or move money.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Strategic forecast assumptions (cfo-finance owns), pricing model decisions (pricing-margin), revenue commit (sales-revenue-lead, planned), spending approval (Chris).

INPUT

input:
  trigger: weekly-cron | adhoc-scenario | runway-alert-recheck
  source_data:
    actuals_thru_date: ISO date
    bookkeeping_close_state: closed | in-progress | not-closed
    cash_positions: from cash-flow-monitor
    ar_aging: from ar-ap-automation
    ap_aging: from ar-ap-automation
    pipeline_value: from ghl-daily-pulse OR sales lead (planned)
    recurring_revenue_base: from subscription-manager
  scenario_inputs (optional):
    new_hire: {role, salary, start_date}
    one_time_spend: {amount, date, category}
    new_sku_launch: {projected_volume, price, COGS}

RULES

  • Three forecasts always — low / mid / high (range, not point estimate).
  • State every assumption explicitly in output (no hidden conversion rates, no hidden retention rates).
  • Actuals lock weekly — once a week is in actuals, it's not re-forecasted.
  • Don't inflate to make runway look better. Honest > comforting.
  • Pipeline weighted by stage probability per ghl-config.md Order Lifecycle stages (don't take pipeline at face value).
  • Recurring revenue discounted for churn estimate.
  • COGS scales with revenue (per-unit COGS from inventory-manager or pricing-margin).
  • Fixed OpEx held flat unless explicit known change (lease, hire, etc.).
  • Runway thresholds: < 12 months = P2; < 6 months = P1; < 3 months = P0.
  • PII discipline — no customer-level data in forecasts (aggregated only).
  • Confidence interval stated for every range.

TOOLS

  • bookkeeping (actuals — P&L, balance sheet, cash flow)
  • payments-engine (Stripe — revenue actuals, refunds, fees)
  • cash-flow-monitor (daily cash positions)
  • ar-ap-automation (aging → expected collections, expected payments)
  • subscription-manager (recurring revenue base + churn)
  • pricing-margin (margin assumptions)
  • inventory-manager (per-unit COGS)
  • ghl-daily-pulse (pipeline)
  • Read: _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md, vendors.md (known committed AP)
  • Compose: analytics-architect (visualization)
  • Write: _Logs/finance/forecast/<YYYY-WW>-13wk.csv + <YYYY-MM>-12mo.csv + summary.md

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    forecast_id: string
    refresh_date: ISO
    actuals_thru: ISO
    cash_forecast_13wk:
      - week: ISO
        starting_cash: number
        revenue_low: number
        revenue_mid: number
        revenue_high: number
        cogs: number
        opex: number
        ar_collections_expected: number
        ap_payments_committed: number
        ending_cash_mid: number
    pnl_forecast_12mo:
      - month: ISO
        revenue: {low, mid, high}
        cogs: number
        gross_margin_pct: number
        opex: number
        ebitda: number
    runway:
      cash_now: number
      burn_rate_monthly: number
      runway_months_at_current_burn: number
      runway_alert: P0 | P1 | P2 | green
    assumptions:
      - assumption: string
        value: string
        confidence: H | M | L
        source: string
    sensitivity_analysis:
      - lever: revenue | COGS | OpEx | hire | churn
        delta_low: string
        delta_high: string
        runway_impact_months: number
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
  format: CSV + Markdown summary

HANDOFF

  • finance-controller-lead (parent — weekly snapshot input)
  • cfo-finance (strategic up-roll + board section)
  • Chris (P0 / P1 alerts immediate)
  • pmo-operator (capacity planning input)
  • finance-continuous-improvement (forecast-accuracy tracking)
  • finance-eval-harness (regression cases)

Eval cases

  1. Weekly refresh, runway stable > 18 mo → green
  2. Pipeline drops 30% → runway recompute → alert if crossing threshold
  3. Hire scenario (+$120k/yr) → runway delta surfaced
  4. New SKU launch → revenue + COGS scenario modeled
  5. Last 4 weeks of forecast accuracy < 80% → flag to CI engine (assumptions need recalibration)
  6. Stripe payout delay → 13-week cash impact modeled
  7. Major customer churn → recurring base reduction modeled

Anti-patterns

  • Single-number forecasts — always range
  • Hidden assumptions — every lever stated
  • Inflated pipeline weights — use historic close-rate, not "what we hope"
  • Re-forecasting locked weeks — actuals are actuals
  • Ignoring AP commitments — known outflows must be in forecast
  • Burying P0 runway alerts in a long report — surface at top

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull actuals + build forecasts
  • ✅ Model scenarios on demand
  • ✅ Surface runway alerts
  • ⚠️ Locked forecast goes to investor deck (Chris + cfo-finance approve)
  • 🚫 Move money / approve hire / commit spend (always Chris)
  • 🚫 Override actuals

Pairing

Composed by: finance-controller-lead, cfo-finance, pmo-operator Composes: bookkeeping, payments-engine, cash-flow-monitor, ar-ap-automation, subscription-manager, analytics-architect Reads: kpi-registry.md, vendors.md

One-liner reminder

13-week cash + 12-month P&L. Weekly refresh. Low / mid / high always. Every assumption stated. Runway < 12 / < 6 / < 3 → P2 / P1 / P0. Honest beats comforting.

skills.name: Formulation Batch Record
skills.description: Generate and maintain master batch records, COA (Certificate of Analysis) templates, SDS (Safety Data Sheet), label artwork specs, and release-testing protocols. Master batch record is the authoritative manufacturing procedure; every executed batch is documented against it. Required gate before manufacturing.
skills.intent: Manufacturing without a master batch record is gambling. This skill produces the canonical document set — master batch record, COA template, SDS, label artwork — so the manufacturing partner (or in-house pharmacy) follows a validated procedure every time, with documentation that satisfies regulators.
skills.estimated_time: Full document set: 8-16 hours. Update only: 1-3 hours.
skills.body_html:
Formulation Batch Record

The canonical manufacturing document set. Master batch record is law; every executed batch is its child.

When to use

  • Phase 4 of any new formulation (after stability protocol locked)
  • Reformulation requires batch record revision
  • Manufacturing partner change (new partner gets new validated batch record + procedure)
  • Audit prep (records must be ready to produce in a regulatory inspection)

When NOT to use

  • Routine batch execution (manufacturing partner runs the procedure; this skill maintains the master)
  • Stability testing (already covered by formulation-stability)
  • Product launch comms → marketing skills

Required inputs

  1. Formulation spec (from formulation-designer)
  2. Compliance class + labeling spec (from formulation-compliance)
  3. Stability protocol + BUD (from formulation-stability)
  4. Manufacturing partner — facility name + capability profile
  5. Target batch size — typical batch (drives equipment / scaling)

Document set produced

1. Master Batch Record (MBR)

The authoritative recipe + procedure for the formulation. Per ICH / cGMP expectations:

# Master Batch Record — <Product Name> — <Strength / Form>
MBR-ID: <unique id>
Version: <semver>
Effective date:
Supersedes: <prior MBR>

## 1. Product identification
- Name (brand + generic)
- Strength / dose per unit
- Form (vial / patch / etc.)
- Lot number convention
- Pack size

## 2. Materials
| # | Material | Supplier | Grade | Required qty per batch | Storage | Specs (USP/etc.) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## 3. Equipment
- Required equipment (calibrated)
- Required environment (cleanroom class, temperature, RH)
- Required PPE

## 4. In-process controls (IPC)
- IPC point 1: <step> — test — acceptance criteria
- IPC point 2: ...

## 5. Manufacturing procedure (step-by-step)
- Step 1: ...
- Step 2: ...
- ... each step has:
  - Action
  - Expected outcome
  - Verification (sign-off boxes)
  - Time required
  - Failure response

## 6. Sampling for release testing
- Sample size + sampling points
- Retention sample reserve

## 7. Filling / packaging
- Container spec
- Closure spec
- Labeling procedure (label artwork reference)
- Tamper evidence

## 8. Release-test panel
- Reference: COA template
- Required tests + acceptance criteria

## 9. Storage + shipping requirements
- Bulk product
- Finished product
- Cold-chain requirements (if applicable)

## 10. Documentation requirements
- Executed batch record retention: 7 years (typical)
- All signoffs required
- Deviation handling

## 11. Revision history
- v1.0 — initial — 2026-05-11 — Steve

## 12. Signoffs
- Formulator: Steve — date
- Quality: <name> — date
- Compliance: <name> — date

2. COA Template (Certificate of Analysis)

One per batch — released only when COA passes.

# Certificate of Analysis
Product: <name>
Lot: <number>
Manufacturing date:
Release date:
Expiration / BUD:
Manufacturer: <facility>

## Specifications + results
| Test | Specification | Result | Pass/Fail |
|---|---|---|---|
| Appearance | <spec> | <result> | ✅/❌ |
| Assay | 90.0-110.0% | <result> | ✅/❌ |
| pH | <spec> | | |
| Sterility | Pass | | |
| Endotoxin | < limit | | |
| ... | | | |

## Conclusion
- Meets specification: yes / no
- Released for distribution: yes / no

## Signoffs
- QC: <name> — date
- QA release: <name> — date

3. SDS (Safety Data Sheet)

Per OSHA HCS / GHS standard. 16 sections. Required for any chemical product:

  1. Identification
  2. Hazard identification
  3. Composition / ingredients
  4. First-aid measures
  5. Fire-fighting measures
  6. Accidental release measures
  7. Handling + storage
  8. Exposure controls / PPE
  9. Physical + chemical properties
  10. Stability + reactivity
  11. Toxicological information
  12. Ecological information
  13. Disposal considerations
  14. Transport information
  15. Regulatory information
  16. Other information

4. Label artwork spec

Physical label spec — what gets printed on the container + secondary packaging. Per compliance class label requirements (from formulation-compliance). Includes:

  • Dieline / dimensions
  • Required text + placement
  • Required iconography (Rx legend, Schedule II indicator, biohazard, etc.)
  • Lot + expiration print location (variable-data field)
  • Color spec
  • Material spec

Hand off to design + print partner; PDF proofs reviewed by Steve + compliance.

5. Release-test protocol

The standard operating procedure for testing each batch before release. Tighter version of stability protocol — runs at T=0 on every batch.

Workflow

Step 1 — Intake all upstream artifacts

  • Spec from formulation-designer
  • Compliance class + labeling from formulation-compliance
  • BUD + stability protocol from formulation-stability

Step 2 — Draft MBR

Use template above. Translate spec into step-by-step procedure with manufacturing partner's equipment + environment in mind.

Step 3 — Procedure walkthrough with Steve + manufacturing partner

  • Walk MBR with partner; they identify operational gaps
  • Iterate until partner confirms executable
  • Capture deviations from generic template that this product requires

Step 4 — Draft COA template

Define release-test panel + spec per test. Match stability acceptance criteria where overlapping; release tests can be stricter.

Step 5 — Draft SDS

Pull toxicology from active monographs + supplier SDS for excipients. Synthesize per OSHA HCS / GHS.

Step 6 — Label artwork spec

Per compliance class + product specifics. Send to design partner.

Step 7 — Release-test SOP

Procedure for running release tests + signoff before product moves to distribution.

Step 8 — Validation batches

  • Run 3 pilot batches using MBR
  • All 3 must pass release testing
  • Inter-batch variability evaluated
  • Final MBR validated → frozen (only revised via formal change control)

Step 9 — Document archive

Master files at _Logs/formulation/<id>/mbr/:

  • mbr-v<X>.md
  • coa-template.md
  • sds.pdf (or md)
  • label-artwork.pdf
  • release-test-sop.md
  • validation-batches/<lot>-release-results.csv

Step 10 — Change control

Future revisions require formal change control:

  • Proposed change
  • Impact assessment (regulatory, stability, supply)
  • Approval per approval-rules.md
  • Revalidation if change is significant (3 batches with new MBR)
  • Document trail

Manufacturing partner expectations

The MBR is what the partner runs. They commit to:

  • Following the MBR exactly OR raising a deviation request
  • Documenting every executed batch as an "Executed Batch Record" (EBR) — file with our copy retained 7 years
  • Running release tests per the protocol
  • Reporting OOS results immediately
  • Maintaining cGMP compliance per class (USP <795/797/800> for 503-A; cGMP for 503-B)

Anti-patterns

  • MBR written by marketing or engineering — must be chemistry-led + compliance-reviewed
  • No validation batches — running MBR on customer-bound product without first 3 successful pilots
  • Informal changes to MBR via Slack — every change goes through change control
  • MBR + EBR mismatch tolerated — undetected deviations compound into product issues
  • SDS missing — first OSHA inspection finding
  • Label artwork drafted without compliance review — every print run gets compliance signoff
  • COA template doesn't match stability acceptance criteria — internal inconsistency = audit finding
  • No retention samples — when a customer reports an issue, no way to investigate

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Draft MBR + COA + SDS + label artwork spec + release-test SOP
  • ✅ Maintain document archive
  • ✅ Run validation batch tracking
  • ⚠️ Lock MBR v1.0 for production (Steve + quality + compliance triad)
  • ⚠️ Approve change control to active MBR (per approval-rules.md)
  • ⚠️ Change manufacturing partner (Chris + Steve + new MBR validation)
  • 🚫 Release product without COA signoff
  • 🚫 Modify executed batch records after the fact

Pairing

Inputs from: formulation-designer, formulation-compliance, formulation-stability, manufacturing partner, vendor-management (supplier specs) Outputs to: Manufacturing partner (executes MBR), formulation-lead (launch gate), compliance-officer (regulatory file), account-manager (customer-facing artifacts like SDS), product-catalog-builder (label / packaging attributes) Chains with: formulation-lead (orchestrator), Steve, manufacturing partner, design partner, lab partner

One-line reminder

MBR is law. COA gates release. SDS published. Label artwork compliance-approved. 3-batch validation before production. Change control for every revision. Records retained 7 years.

skills.name: Formulation Compliance
skills.description: Regulatory + quality compliance review for every formulation. Classifies regulatory pathway (503-A patient-specific / 503-B catalog / supplement / cosmetic / drug). USP monograph alignment. cGMP applicability. DEA scheduling. State restrictions. Labeling requirements. Required gate before any formulation proceeds to stability + manufacturing.
skills.intent: Get the regulatory class right at design phase, not at launch. A peptide misfiled as a supplement, a 503-B SKU with patient-specific labeling, a controlled substance without DEA registration — these are existential errors. This skill is the discipline layer. Pairs with `compliance-officer` (broader org compliance) and `formulation-lead`.
best_for: Regulatory class question on a new chemistry
skills.estimated_time: Standard review: 1-3 hours. Complex (controlled substance / novel mechanism): 4-12 hours + counsel.
skills.body_html:
Formulation Compliance

The regulatory discipline gate. No formulation proceeds past design without class + USP + cGMP + DEA + labeling cleared.

When to use

  • Phase 2 of any new formulation (after design lands)
  • Regulatory class question on a candidate
  • DEA / state restriction check
  • Labeling review pre-launch
  • Quarterly portfolio audit against current regs
  • When a regulation changes (FDA guidance update, state law change)

When NOT to use

  • Broader org compliance (HIPAA, PCI, TCPA) → compliance-officer
  • Marketing claims compliance → compliance-officer + product-copy
  • Patent / trademark → ip-lead department
  • Vendor compliance (FDA registration of supplier) → vendor-management

Required inputs

  1. Formulation spec — from formulation-designer output
  2. Use case — provider-prescribed patient-specific (503-A) vs catalog (503-B) vs supplement
  3. Active ingredient(s) — for DEA + USP lookup
  4. Volume / scale — affects cGMP applicability
  5. Distribution — direct to provider clinic / pharmacy partner / D2C

The regulatory class decision (most important)

503-A — Patient-specific compounding

  • What: Pharmacy compounds for an individual patient with valid prescription
  • Reg path: State board of pharmacy + FDA Section 503A
  • Constraints:
    • No bulk manufacturing
    • Patient + prescriber identified per unit
    • Limited to substances on FDA bulk drug list (or USP/NF compendial)
    • BUD per USP <797> (sterile) / <795> (non-sterile)
  • Matrix products: most provider-specific peptide therapy
  • Labeling: patient-specific Rx label

503-B — Outsourcing facility (catalog compounding)

  • What: Larger-scale; can sell without patient-specific Rx
  • Reg path: Voluntary FDA registration; subject to cGMP + FDA inspection
  • Constraints:
    • Substances must be on FDA's published list of bulk drug substances OR appear on FDA's drug shortage list
    • Reports of adverse events required
    • Quarterly product reporting to FDA
  • Matrix products: catalog SKUs sold without per-patient Rx
  • Labeling: per FDA 503B requirements

Supplement (DSHEA)

  • What: Dietary supplement under DSHEA
  • Reg path: FDA — premarket notification for new ingredients; cGMP per 21 CFR 111
  • Constraints:
    • Cannot claim to diagnose / treat / cure / prevent disease
    • Structure-function claims OK with disclaimer
    • GRAS or pre-DSHEA-1994 ingredient
  • Matrix products: MuscleLock supplements
  • Labeling: Supplement Facts panel

Cosmetic

  • What: Skin / hair / body care without drug claims
  • Reg path: FDA cosmetics regs (lighter than drug)
  • Constraints: cannot make drug claims; ingredient safety
  • Matrix products: some topical serums positioned as cosmeceutical

Drug (NDA / ANDA)

  • What: FDA-approved drug
  • Reg path: full FDA approval (NDA / ANDA / OTC monograph)
  • Constraints: clinical trials; manufacturing per cGMP
  • Matrix products: not currently — but reformulations might escalate to drug class

Misclassification is the #1 regulatory risk. When uncertain → escalate to counsel.

USP monograph alignment

USP-NF publishes compendial standards. For each active:

  • Compendial active (has USP monograph): formulation must meet identity, assay, impurity limits per the monograph
  • Non-compendial active: must justify identity + purity + assay independently; often via supplier COA + independent testing

Decision tree:

  1. Is the active in USP-NF? → Apply monograph specs.
  2. Is it on FDA bulk drug list? → Acceptable for 503-A; need 503-B-specific status check.
  3. Is it neither? → Likely cannot be compounded under 503-A or 503-B; supplement / cosmetic / drug only.

cGMP applicability

  • 503-A pharmacies: USP <795> / <797> / <800> for compounding (not full cGMP)
  • 503-B outsourcing facilities: cGMP applies (21 CFR Part 210/211)
  • Supplements: cGMP per 21 CFR Part 111
  • Cosmetics: GMP guidance (not mandatory; coming)
  • Drugs: full cGMP

Each level dictates: documentation rigor, facility, training, batch records, stability validation, COA expectations.

DEA scheduling

Some Matrix-relevant substances:

Substance Schedule
Testosterone III (anabolic steroid)
Most peptides (BPC-157, TB-500, etc.) Generally unscheduled — but FDA enforcement discretion varies
Ketamine III
GHB I (illicit) / III (Xyrem prescription)
GLP-1 agonists Unscheduled
Hormones (non-androgen) Unscheduled

DEA-scheduled requires:

  • DEA registration of facility
  • Controlled-substance handling procedures
  • Inventory + ordering / 222 forms (for II)
  • State-level corollary registration
  • Specific labeling
  • Shipping restrictions

Always re-check current schedule status before launching anything that touched the controlled space historically.

State restrictions

Some states restrict specific compounded products or have additional licensing:

  • California: stricter compounding rules; pharmacy must be CA-licensed
  • New York: separate compounding registration
  • Texas: separate 503-B-equivalent state regs
  • Several states: peptide-specific restrictions (changing year to year)

Maintain _Shared Context/state-restrictions.md (create if absent) with current state status per substance.

Labeling requirements

503-A label (per USP <797> / <795>)

  • Patient name + Rx number
  • Prescriber name
  • Drug name + strength + form
  • Quantity
  • Compound date + BUD
  • Storage instructions
  • Pharmacy name + address
  • "For [name] only — not for resale"

503-B label

  • Established name + strength
  • Lot number
  • BUD
  • Storage instructions
  • Outsourcing facility name + address
  • Statement: "This is a compounded drug"
  • Cautionary text per FDA 503B guidance

Supplement label

  • Statement of identity (e.g., "Creatine Monohydrate")
  • Net quantity
  • Supplement Facts panel
  • Manufacturer / distributor name + address
  • Allergen statements
  • "These statements have not been evaluated by the FDA. This product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease."
  • Lot + expiration

Workflow

Step 1 — Intake spec

Pull formulation spec from formulation-designer. Confirm active(s) + form + intended distribution.

Step 2 — Classify

Walk decision tree above. Classify regulatory class with explicit reasoning. If ambiguous → counsel.

Step 3 — USP / monograph check

For each active: look up compendial status. Pull monograph if applicable. Add specs to formulation requirements.

Step 4 — cGMP applicability

Match class → cGMP level. Confirm Matrix's pharmacy partner has appropriate registration + capability.

Step 5 — DEA + state check

  • DEA schedule lookup (current)
  • State restrictions (check against state-restrictions.md)
  • Output: states this can ship to (drives fulfillment-shipping blocklist)

Step 6 — Labeling spec

Generate labeling spec per class. Include all required elements. Feed to formulation-batch-record for label artwork.

Step 7 — Adverse event reporting setup

  • 503-B + drug class: FDA MedWatch reporting wired
  • Supplement: FDA serious adverse event reporting (SAER)
  • Track in _Logs/compliance/ae-reports/

Step 8 — Compliance report

# Compliance Review — <formulation id>

## Classification
- Class: 503-A / 503-B / Supplement / Cosmetic / Drug
- Reasoning: <one paragraph>
- Confidence: high / medium / low (low → counsel)

## USP / monograph
- Active <name>: compendial status + applicable monograph
- Required specs to meet: <list>

## cGMP level
- Required: USP <797> / cGMP-supplement / etc.
- Pharmacy partner capability confirmed: yes / no

## DEA
- Schedule: unscheduled / II / III / IV / V
- Handling requirements: <if scheduled>

## State restrictions
- Can ship to: <states list>
- Cannot ship to: <states list> + reasons

## Labeling spec
<full label content for batch-record>

## Adverse event reporting
- Path: <FDA MedWatch / SAER / N/A>
- Wired: yes / no

## Risk assessment
- Reg risk: low / medium / high
- Counsel needed: yes / no
- Recommendation: proceed / hold / decline / escalate

Step 9 — Signoff or escalate

  • Low/medium risk → compliance-officer signoff = clear
  • High risk → counsel review required
  • Capture signoff in _Logs/formulation/<id>/compliance-signoff.md

Anti-patterns

  • Assuming class from prior similar product — every formulation gets fresh classification
  • Skipping USP monograph — non-compendial proceedings without justification = audit finding
  • DEA status check stale — schedules change; re-check at launch + annually
  • State restriction list out of date — peptide rules change frequently
  • Labeling text drafted by marketing — must be compliance-led; marketing reviews for voice consistency only
  • Adverse event path unwired — first AE will be a fire drill instead of a process
  • "Low risk" without counsel on novel mechanism — first-of-kind always gets counsel

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run classification + monograph + DEA + state reviews
  • ✅ Generate compliance report + labeling spec
  • ✅ Signoff for low/medium risk
  • ⚠️ Signoff for high risk (counsel required first)
  • ⚠️ Approve novel mechanism / first-of-kind (counsel + Chris)
  • 🚫 Approve a formulation class without resolving ambiguity
  • 🚫 Bypass DEA registration for scheduled substance

Pairing

Inputs from: formulation-designer (spec), compliance-officer (broader rules), vendors.md (supplier reg status), state-restrictions.md Outputs to: formulation-batch-record (labeling spec), formulation-stability (BUD requirements), fulfillment-shipping (state blocklist), product-copy (claims discipline reference), formulation-lead (gate signoff) Chains with: compliance-officer (peer; cross-cutting compliance), counsel (escalation), ip-lead (some patents intersect with reg path)

One-line reminder

Class first. USP monograph. cGMP level. DEA + state check. Labeling spec. AE path wired. Counsel on ambiguity. No formulation past Phase 2 without this signoff.

skills.name: Formulation Continuous Improvement
skills.description: Weekly auditor of the formulation department — Steve's queue health, stability data on schedule, adverse-event patterns, customer-complaint clusters, supplier reliability, catalog hygiene (active vs stale SKUs), regulatory landscape changes, IP filing follow-through. Produces evidence-based change proposals weekly + quarterly portfolio review. Mirror of voice / GHL / e-commerce CI engines.
skills.intent: Keep the formulation surface improving instead of decaying. Catalog touches money + safety + reputation + compliance — silent drift is the most expensive kind. Every Friday: scan all formulation surfaces, identify what's working, what's drifting, what's at risk. Propose specific evidence-based changes.
best_for: Weekly Friday review (4:00pm PT — after voice + GHL + e-com CI)
Post-launch performance analysis (did the new SKU land as expected?)
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 60-90 min. Quarterly deep audit: 4-8 hours.
skills.body_html:
Formulation Continuous Improvement

The auditor + proposer for everything formulation. Friday weekly + quarterly deep. Trust the data. Propose with evidence.

Lineage

Mirror of ghl-continuous-improvement + voice-call-reviewer + ecommerce-continuous-improvement. Same shape; formulation-specific surfaces.

When to use

  • Weekly Friday review (default cadence; runs 4pm PT after voice 3pm + GHL 3:30pm + e-com 3:45pm)
  • Quarterly deep audit
  • After a major formulation launch / supplier change / regulatory update
  • Pre-quarterly-portfolio review (feeds into quarterly-review.workflow.md)
  • When an adverse event / OOS / compliance finding triggers re-evaluation

When NOT to use

  • Single-formulation diagnosis → use formulation-lead direct
  • Real-time incident → use incident-response.sop.md
  • Initial design → use formulation-development.workflow.md

Surfaces audited

1. Steve's queue + bandwidth

  • Active formulations (target 1-3)
  • Stability monitoring (passive load)
  • Intake backlog (target < 15)
  • Parked candidates (review quarterly)
  • Steve's bench-time utilization vs capacity

2. Catalog hygiene

  • Active SKU count vs target
  • SKUs with no sales in 90 days (retire / refresh / discontinue?)
  • SKUs missing assets (image / copy / price / inventory row)
  • SKU naming convention compliance
  • Cold-chain flag accuracy

3. Stability program

  • Timepoints on schedule (none past due)
  • OOS rate (target < 1%)
  • Open OOS investigations
  • BUD extensions in flight
  • Lab partner performance (turnaround time + accuracy)

4. Compliance posture

  • Regulatory class accuracy (audit per quarter)
  • USP monograph compliance
  • DEA schedule status (changes since last review)
  • State restriction updates
  • Labeling compliance (per formulation-eval-harness runs)
  • Adverse event reports filed on schedule

5. Batch records + manufacturing

  • MBR change-control hygiene (no informal changes)
  • Executed batch records (EBR) match MBR
  • COA pass rate per SKU
  • Manufacturing-partner reliability
  • Validation-batch documentation current

6. IP follow-through

  • Patent strategy candidates in Tier 1 — filed?
  • Trademark filings in flight — office actions responded?
  • Disclosure status — anything publicly disclosed without filing?
  • Trade-secret discipline holding (no leaks in copy / training material)
  • Joint-IP collaborations clarified with counsel?

7. Supplier / vendor reliability

  • Primary supplier on-time delivery rate
  • Quality complaints per supplier
  • Cost trend per ingredient
  • Single-source risks (per vendors.md + R-V-01)

8. Pharmacy partner performance

  • Order fulfillment turnaround
  • Quality control pass rate
  • Cold-chain compliance
  • Compounding-error rate
  • Regulatory event status (any FDA inspection findings?)

9. Customer / provider signal

  • Complaint patterns per SKU
  • Reorder rate (engagement)
  • Provider feedback collected by AM
  • Adverse-event reports (DSHEA / FDA MedWatch)
  • Catalog requests / unmet asks

10. Market fit (commercial)

  • Revenue per SKU vs expectations
  • Margin trend per SKU
  • Refund rate per SKU (signal of product issue)
  • Competitive moves (new competitor formulations in same category)
  • Provider preference shifts

Workflow (weekly)

Step 1 — Pull data

  • Last 7 days of catalog + sales + complaints
  • Stability dashboard (current vs scheduled)
  • Eval-harness last-run (formulation + adjacent)
  • Steve's queue file
  • IP portfolio status
  • Vendor + pharmacy-partner metrics

Step 2 — Compute KPIs

  • Per surface above (use kpi-registry.md for definitions)
  • Highlight top 5 movers vs baseline

Step 3 — Spot patterns

  • Cluster complaints by SKU / category / state / supplier
  • Identify outliers
  • Flag drift > 1 stddev from baseline
  • Cross-reference with risk-register.md for early warnings

Step 4 — Diagnose top 3-5 issues

  • Hypothesis (not just symptom)
  • Evidence (data + customer quotes + lab results)
  • Confidence level

Step 5 — Propose changes (evidence-based)

  • Per issue: specific change + executor skill + expected impact + effort + risk
  • Order by impact ÷ effort

Step 6 — Approval gate

  • Output proposal markdown
  • Steve reviews chemistry-relevant items
  • Chris approves business-relevant items
  • Compliance-officer approves regulatory items

Step 7 — Hand off
