  • Per approved proposal: invoke target skill (formulation-designer, formulation-compliance, formulation-stability, formulation-batch-record, product-catalog-builder, vendor-management, ip-counsel-coordination, etc.)
  • Run formulation-eval-harness before any change ships

Step 8 — Measure (1-4 weeks later)

  • Per shipped change: actual vs expected
  • Update impact-history.csv
  • Playbook update if change underperformed

Step 9 — Log

  • Weekly: _Logs/formulation-improvement/<YYYY-WW>-weekly.md
  • Quarterly: _Logs/formulation-improvement/<YYYY-Q>-deep-audit.md

Step 10 — Quarterly deep audit

Adds to weekly:

  • Full catalog retirement review (dead SKUs)
  • IP portfolio review (filings + maintenance + competitive scan)
  • Manufacturing partner audit (regulatory + quality)
  • Supplier diversification review
  • Reformulation candidate list (any SKU 2+ years without reformulation)
  • Steve's career-relevant publication / conference pipeline (his publication portfolio is Matrix's defensive prior art)

Output template (weekly)

# Formulation CI: Week <YYYY-WW>

## Snapshot
- Active SKUs: <N>
- Revenue from catalog: $<X> (Δ vs prior week)
- Refund rate: <%>
- Stability program: <N timepoints due; N OOS open>
- Steve's queue: <N active; N intake; N parked>
- IP portfolio: <N pending filings; N maintenance due>

## Top 5 movers
| Surface | Metric | Δ | Significance |

## Issues diagnosed
### 1. <Issue> — <surface>
- Symptom
- Hypothesis
- Evidence
- Confidence: H / M / L
- Proposal: <change> via `<skill>`
- Expected impact + effort + risk

### 2. ...

## Wins this week
- ...

## Asks for Steve + Chris + compliance
- Approve proposals X, Y
- Decisions needed: <specific>

## Eval-harness status
- formulation-eval: <green / yellow / red>
- New failures vs baseline

## Logged to
`_Logs/formulation-improvement/<YYYY-WW>-weekly.md`

Cross-pollination with other CI engines

Formulation has unique inputs other CI engines don't see:

  • Lab partner stability data
  • Pharmacy partner quality data
  • Adverse-event reports
  • Steve's bench observations
  • Regulatory landscape

But many findings cross over:

  • Pricing / margin → e-commerce CI
  • Customer-complaint patterns → e-commerce CI + voice-call-reviewer
  • Provider preference shifts → sales strategy
  • IP risk → quarterly review

The weekly CI engines (voice 3pm → GHL 3:30 → e-com 3:45 → formulation 4pm) sync via shared _Logs/ and risk-register.md. Findings cross-reference automatically.

Anti-patterns

  • Reporting without proposing — surfacing problems without solutions = noise
  • Proposing without evidence — gut-feel changes in formulation domain are dangerous
  • Skipping the measure-after step — never learn whether changes work
  • Treating chemistry-curiosity as market-fit — Steve's interests are signal but not approval
  • Letting eval-harness drift — quarterly refresh mandatory
  • Quarterly audit skipped — drift compounds; regulatory exposure grows silently

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read all surfaces + pull data
  • ✅ Compute KPIs + cluster complaints
  • ✅ Draft proposals
  • ✅ Run eval-harness reports
  • ⚠️ Approve proposals (Steve for chemistry; Chris for business; compliance-officer for regulatory)
  • ⚠️ Ship proposed changes (per executing skill's own pre-auth rules)
  • 🚫 Make changes directly without going through executing skill + approval
  • 🚫 Bypass stability data for BUD extension

Pairing

Inputs from: All 6 formulation skills + analytics-architect (catalog KPIs) + vendor-management (supplier data) + compliance-officer (compliance findings) + formulation-eval-harness (regression status) + risk-register.md Outputs to: Steve (chemistry items), Chris (business items), compliance-officer (regulatory items), formulation-lead (orchestrates approved changes), pmo-operator (ticket tracking), quarterly-review.workflow.md (consumes deep audit) Chains with: voice-call-reviewer + ghl-continuous-improvement + ecommerce-continuous-improvement (Friday CI cluster), quarterly-review.workflow.md

One-liner reminder

Friday weekly + quarterly deep. Diagnose → evidence → propose → measure. Steve approves chemistry; Chris approves business; compliance-officer approves regulatory. The catalog improves or it decays.

skills.name: Formulation Designer
skills.description: Design the actual chemistry + delivery-form spec for a new formulation. Active ingredient(s), concentration, excipients, penetration enhancers, base, pH, osmolality. Form-specific specs for peptides (vial), topical serum, transdermal patch, troche, pressed pellet, capsule. Steve drives the chemistry; this skill structures + documents.
skills.intent: Capture the formulation spec in a single canonical document so Steve's chemistry decisions, the manufacturing partner's procedure, and the COA template all reference one source of truth. Form-specific design templates ensure nothing critical is missed (e.g., penetration enhancer for topical, adhesion for patch, dissolution time for troche).
best_for: New formulation entering design phase (Phase 1 of formulation-lead)
Variant generation (different strength / size of existing)
skills.estimated_time: Per formulation: 2-6 hours of Steve's time + ~30 min documentation. Variant: 30-60 min.
skills.body_html:
Formulation Designer

The chemistry-spec capture layer. Steve decides; this skill structures + makes it the single source of truth.

When to use

  • Phase 1 of any new formulation (after market-fit + IP gates pass)
  • Reformulation needing spec update
  • New variant (same chemistry, different strength / volume / size)
  • Audit existing spec against current best-practice

When NOT to use

  • Market fit decision → formulation-market-fit
  • Regulatory class decision → formulation-compliance
  • Stability protocol → formulation-stability
  • Manufacturing procedure (downstream) → formulation-batch-record

Required inputs

  1. Use case — what providers will reach for this
  2. Delivery form — vial / topical / patch / troche / pellet / capsule
  3. Active ingredient(s) — name + grade + source
  4. Target concentration / strength
  5. Volume / size per unit

Form-specific design templates

Peptide injectable (vial / multi-dose)

form: peptide_injectable
active:
  - name: <e.g., Semaglutide>
    cas: <CAS number>
    grade: USP / research / cGMP
    source: <supplier per vendors.md>
    concentration_mg_ml: <X>
volume_ml: <e.g., 5>
vial_size: 5mL / 10mL / 30mL
multi_dose: true / false
preservatives:
  - <e.g., benzyl alcohol 0.9%> — required for multi-dose
diluent_base: <e.g., bacteriostatic water for injection>
ph_target: 6.5-7.5
osmolality_target: 280-310 mOsm/kg
stability_factors:
  - temperature_sensitivity: refrigerated 2-8°C
  - light_sensitive: yes / no
  - reconstitution: powder + diluent OR pre-dissolved
sterility: sterile filtration 0.22 micron
cold_chain_required: true / false
notes: <Steve's chemistry notes>

Topical serum

form: topical_serum
active:
  - name:
    concentration_pct: <X>
base:
  - aqueous / oil-in-water / water-in-oil / hydroalcoholic
penetration_enhancers:
  - <e.g., transferosome / liposome / niosome>
  - <e.g., DMSO 5%>
viscosity_cP: <range>
ph_target: <skin-compatible 4.5-6.5 typical>
preservatives: <e.g., phenoxyethanol 0.8%>
fragrance: none (clinical product)
packaging: <pump bottle / dropper / airless>
shelf_appearance: <clear / milky / colored>
notes:

Transdermal patch

form: transdermal_patch
active:
  - name:
    dose_per_patch_mg: <X>
    release_duration_hours: <e.g., 24 / 72 / 168>
patch_type: matrix / reservoir / drug-in-adhesive
backing_material: <e.g., polyester>
adhesive: <e.g., acrylic / silicone>
release_liner: <removable per use>
permeation_enhancer: <if applicable>
size_cm2: <e.g., 25>
target_skin_site: <upper arm / abdomen / etc.>
wear_time_hours: <X>
notes:

Troche (buccal/sublingual lozenge)

form: troche
active:
  - name:
    dose_per_troche_mg: <X>
base: polyethylene_glycol / sucrose / acacia
flavoring: <e.g., mint / vanilla — sugar-free>
size_g: <e.g., 1g>
dissolution_time_minutes: <e.g., 15-30 sublingual>
absorption_route: buccal / sublingual
preservatives: <minimal>
storage: room temperature, dry
notes:

Pressed pellet (subcutaneous implant)

form: pressed_pellet
active:
  - name: <e.g., testosterone>
    dose_per_pellet_mg: <e.g., 100>
pellet_diameter_mm: <e.g., 3.2>
pellet_length_mm: <e.g., 9>
release_duration_months: <e.g., 4-6>
sterility: sterile (gamma irradiation typical)
insertion_kit: trocar size + suture kit
patient_label_caution: <site care, restrictions>
notes:

Capsule (oral)

form: capsule
active:
  - name:
    dose_per_capsule_mg: <X>
capsule_shell: gelatin / vegetarian / DRcaps (delayed-release)
fill: powder / liquid / pellet
fill_excipients:
  - <bulking agent, anti-caking, flow aid>
disintegration_time_minutes: <X>
gastric_protection: enteric coating yes / no
size: 00 / 0 / 1 / 2 / 3
color: <e.g., clear / opaque>
batch_size_typical: <e.g., 1000 capsules>
notes:

Workflow

Step 1 — Confirm form + intake

Pull from intake record (market-fit + IP outcomes). Confirm delivery form.

Step 2 — Open form-specific template

Use template above. Steve fills in chemistry decisions; this skill structures.

Step 3 — Ingredient sourcing check

  • Each ingredient: confirm supplier per vendors.md
  • Confirm grade (cGMP / USP / research)
  • Confirm cost-per-unit (feeds margin floor for product-pricing-engine)
  • Flag any single-source risk → escalate to vendor-management

Step 4 — Compatibility check

  • Active + excipient compatibility (precipitation, degradation, pH shift)
  • Container compatibility (glass / plastic leaching, especially for peptides)
  • For topical: skin sensitization profile of base
  • For patch: skin irritation profile of adhesive
  • For troche: taste + mouthfeel sanity

Step 5 — pilot batch decision

  • How many units in first pilot? (typically 5-20)
  • Lab time required
  • Cost of pilot batch
  • Capture in queue

Step 6 — Document the spec

File the completed template at _Logs/formulation/<id>/spec.yaml + a human-readable spec.md with Steve's narrative notes.

Step 7 — Handoff

  • To formulation-compliance for regulatory class + USP monograph review
  • To formulation-stability for stability protocol design
  • To formulation-batch-record for master batch record drafting
  • To vendor-management if any new vendor needed

Variant generation (same chemistry, different scale)

When generating variants of an approved formulation (e.g., 5mg vial + 10mg vial of same peptide):

  • Linear scaling rarely works; always sanity-check pH / osmolality / preservative ratio
  • New SKU + spec yaml per variant
  • Stability protocol may be abbreviated if scaling within validated range
  • Compliance review re-run (different dose ≠ same regulatory)

Anti-patterns

  • Spec drift — bench notes ≠ documented spec; always reconcile to single source
  • Unverified ingredient grade — research-grade in a 503-A product is non-compliant
  • Skipping compatibility check — pH shift discovered at stability month 3 = restart
  • No cold-chain attribute on a heat-sensitive peptide — fulfillment will ship it on ground
  • Pellet without sterilization plan — non-starter for implant
  • Patch without permeation data — drug doesn't get through skin = inert
  • Linear scaling for variants without re-checking — preservatives + pH need re-validation

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Structure spec + capture Steve's decisions
  • ✅ Run ingredient sourcing check
  • ✅ Flag compatibility risks
  • ⚠️ Lock spec for handoff to compliance / stability (Steve approves)
  • ⚠️ Substitute ingredients (Steve — chemistry call)
  • 🚫 Decide regulatory class (that's formulation-compliance)
  • 🚫 Approve for launch (that's formulation-lead gate)

Pairing

Inputs from: formulation-market-fit (use case), ip-lead (any patent angle to design around), vendors.md (supplier list) Outputs to: formulation-compliance (regulatory class), formulation-stability (testable spec), formulation-batch-record (recipe + procedure), product-pricing-engine (cost basis), vendor-management (procurement) Chains with: formulation-lead (orchestrator), Steve directly (chemistry decisions)

One-line reminder

Form-specific template. Source of truth for the chemistry. Compatibility check before bench. Spec at _Logs/formulation/<id>/spec.yaml. Steve decides; skill structures.

skills.name: Formulation Eval Harness
skills.description: Regression gate for any formulation department change — new SKU launches, stability data updates, batch-record revisions, compliance reclassifications, label changes, supplier changes. Test corpus grows with every production issue. Required before any formulation moves through phase gates or any active SKU gets modified. Mirror of voice / GHL / e-commerce eval harness patterns.
skills.intent: Never let a formulation-department change ship without regression evidence. The catalog touches regulatory compliance, patient safety, financial liability, and Matrix's reputation — invisible drift here is the most expensive kind. Build the corpus once, grow forever, gate every change.
best_for: Pre-launch gate for any new formulation
Weekly regression run on active catalog
Adding a case after a stability OOS / adverse event / customer complaint
skills.estimated_time: 30-90 min depending on suite size
skills.body_html:
Formulation Eval Harness

The regression gate for the catalog. No formulation change ships without a green run.

Lineage

Mirror of voice-eval-harness + ghl-eval-harness + ecommerce-eval-harness. Same shape; formulation-specific cases.

When to use

  • Before any new formulation moves Phase 5 → Phase 6 launch gate
  • Before any active SKU gets a label change / supplier change / reformulation
  • Weekly automated regression run on entire active catalog (catches drift)
  • Adding a new case after stability OOS / adverse event / customer complaint
  • A/B comparing two formulation variants

When NOT to use

  • Initial design / Phase 1-4 work (run after surfaces exist; eval is for regression)
  • Single sample QC during stability testing (use lab partner's panel)
  • Customer-specific adverse-event investigation (use compliance-officer + clinical lead path)

Required inputs

  1. Scope — full catalog / specific SKU(s) / specific change type
  2. Environment — staging catalog / live catalog read-only
  3. Comparison baseline — last green run / pre-change snapshot

Eval suite structure

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/formulation/<scope>/:

_Logs/eval-suites/formulation/matrix-catalog/
├── suite.yaml                          # metadata + thresholds
├── fixtures/
│   ├── test-formulation-503a.yaml
│   ├── test-formulation-503b.yaml
│   ├── test-formulation-supplement.yaml
│   ├── test-formulation-cosmetic.yaml
│   ├── test-formulation-alm-dt-family.yaml
│   ├── test-state-blocklist.yaml
│   └── test-pharmacy-partner.yaml
├── cases/
│   ├── classification/
│   │   ├── 001-503a-class-correct.yaml
│   │   ├── 002-503b-class-correct.yaml
│   │   ├── 003-supplement-vs-drug-claim.yaml
│   │   ├── 004-dea-schedule-check.yaml
│   │   └── 005-state-restriction-applied.yaml
│   ├── labeling/
│   │   ├── 010-503a-label-required-fields.yaml
│   │   ├── 011-503b-label-required-fields.yaml
│   │   ├── 012-supplement-facts-panel.yaml
│   │   ├── 013-dshea-disclaimer-present.yaml
│   │   ├── 014-bud-on-label.yaml
│   │   └── 015-storage-instructions-correct.yaml
│   ├── stability/
│   │   ├── 020-bud-supported-by-data.yaml
│   │   ├── 021-stability-timepoints-on-schedule.yaml
│   │   ├── 022-503b-3-batch-validation.yaml
│   │   ├── 023-accelerated-stability-run.yaml
│   │   └── 024-oos-investigation-completed.yaml
│   ├── batch-record/
│   │   ├── 030-mbr-frozen-vs-executed.yaml
│   │   ├── 031-coa-template-matches-mbr.yaml
│   │   ├── 032-sds-current.yaml
│   │   ├── 033-label-artwork-compliance-signoff.yaml
│   │   └── 034-validation-3-batches-pass.yaml
│   ├── ip/
│   │   ├── 040-trademark-cleared.yaml
│   │   ├── 041-product-naming-gate-passed.yaml
│   │   ├── 042-patent-disclosure-status.yaml
│   │   └── 043-trade-secret-not-leaked-in-copy.yaml
│   ├── pricing-margin/
│   │   ├── 050-margin-above-floor.yaml
│   │   ├── 051-stripe-price-synced.yaml
│   │   └── 052-b2b-tier-pricing-correct.yaml
│   ├── catalog-integrity/
│   │   ├── 060-sku-naming-convention.yaml
│   │   ├── 061-required-attributes-set.yaml
│   │   ├── 062-cold-chain-flag-correct.yaml
│   │   ├── 063-active-sku-has-image.yaml
│   │   ├── 064-active-sku-has-copy.yaml
│   │   ├── 065-active-sku-has-price.yaml
│   │   └── 066-active-sku-has-inventory-row.yaml
│   └── alm-dt-family/
│       ├── 070-alm-dt-products-cite-platform.yaml
│       ├── 071-no-trade-secret-disclosure.yaml
│       └── 072-license-posture-respected.yaml
├── runs/
│   ├── 2026-05-11-pre-launch.json
│   └── 2026-05-11-weekly.json
└── README.md

Case shape (example)

# 010-503a-label-required-fields.yaml
id: 503a-label-required-fields
category: labeling
description: 503-A patient-specific compounded product label has all USP-required fields.
preconditions:
  fixture: test-formulation-503a
  product_class: 503-A
expectations:
  - label_field_present: patient_name
  - label_field_present: rx_number
  - label_field_present: prescriber_name
  - label_field_present: drug_name_and_strength
  - label_field_present: quantity
  - label_field_present: compound_date
  - label_field_present: bud
  - label_field_present: storage_instructions
  - label_field_present: pharmacy_name_and_address
  - label_text_contains: "For [name] only — not for resale"
fail_modes:
  - missing_patient_name: "USP <797> violation — label rejected"
  - missing_bud: "Cannot ship without BUD on label"
severity: P1

Suite metadata (suite.yaml)

name: formulation-evals
version: 2026.05.1
threshold:
  block_change_on:
    p1_failures: 0           # any P1 fail blocks formulation phase transition
    p2_failures: 1           # > 1 P2 fail blocks
    p3_failures: 3           # > 3 P3 fail warns
  weekly_drift_alert:
    new_failures: 1
severity_levels:
  P1: customer-safety OR compliance-violation OR financial-material
  P2: data-quality OR labeling-completeness OR margin-floor
  P3: cosmetic OR documentation-gap

Workflow

Step 1 — Pick scope

  • Pre-launch: run full suite on the target formulation
  • Weekly: run full suite against active catalog
  • Targeted: run subset matching change area (e.g., supplier change → run stability + batch-record subsets)

Step 2 — Load fixtures

  • Test formulations per class
  • State blocklist data
  • Pharmacy partner capability data

Step 3 — Execute cases

  • For each case: run assertions against actual data
  • Capture: actual vs expected
  • Snapshot artifacts for failure cases

Step 4 — Score

  • Per case: pass / fail / skip (with reason)
  • Per category: pass rate
  • Per severity: pass rate
  • Overall: green / yellow / red per suite.yaml

Step 5 — Report

  • Output to _Logs/eval-suites/formulation/<scope>/runs/<date>-<scope>.json
  • Markdown summary: top failures, regression vs last run, action items
  • Weekly: trend chart vs last 4 weeks

Step 6 — Gate decision

  • Pre-launch / phase-transition: green → proceed; red → block + alert
  • Weekly: red → escalate to formulation-continuous-improvement for proposal

Step 7 — Grow the suite (post-incident discipline)

  • Every stability OOS → new case
  • Every customer complaint cluster → new case
  • Every compliance finding → new case
  • Every adverse event → new case

What this catches (and what it doesn't)

Catches:

  • Missing required label fields by class
  • BUD without supporting stability data
  • Margin floor violations
  • SKU naming convention violations
  • Stale stability data (timepoints not run on schedule)
  • Active SKU missing image / copy / price / inventory
  • Trademark not cleared before SKU activation
  • Cold-chain flag incorrect for cold-chain product
  • 503-A vs 503-B classification mismatched to label
  • DEA schedule check missed
  • State restriction not enforced
  • ALM DT trade-secret disclosure in public copy
  • COA template drift from MBR

Does NOT catch (needs other tools):

  • Actual product safety (chemistry — Steve / lab partners)
  • Real-world efficacy (clinical studies)
  • Patient-specific adverse events (compliance-officer + clinical lead)
  • Counterfeit detection (separate process)
  • Theft / shrinkage (operations)

Anti-patterns

  • Eval suite grows once, never — staleness = silent failure
  • Mocking stability data — must reflect real lab results
  • No P1 case for label-required fields — most expensive class of violation
  • Skipping post-incident case growth — defeats the harness purpose
  • Bypassing the gate for "this one launch" — every bypass is a future incident

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build + maintain suite
  • ✅ Run against staging catalog
  • ✅ Run against active catalog read-only
  • ✅ Block phase transitions on red runs
  • ⚠️ Bypass gate (Chris + reason logged + remediation deadline)
  • 🚫 Run state-changing cases against active customer data

Pairing

Inputs from: All formulation skills (formulation-lead, formulation-designer, formulation-compliance, formulation-stability, formulation-batch-record, formulation-market-fit), product-catalog-builder, compliance-officer, ip-portfolio.md Outputs to: formulation-lead (phase-gate signal), formulation-continuous-improvement (weekly synthesis), compliance-officer (compliance findings) Chains with: voice-eval-harness + ghl-eval-harness + ecommerce-eval-harness (sibling patterns; cross-pollinate techniques)

One-liner reminder

Catalog touches money + safety + reputation + compliance. P1 fails block phase transitions. Suite grows from every incident. State-changing tests never on live customer data.

skills.name: Formulation Lead
skills.description: Department lead for Formulation. Owner persona — Steve (biochemist, lead formulator). Orchestrates new-formulation intake, design, compliance, stability testing, batch records, market fit. Covers all delivery forms — peptides, topical serums, transdermal patches, troches, pressed pellets, capsules. Every new product passes through this department before launch.
skills.intent: Make sure every formulation Matrix puts in front of a provider is technically sound, regulatory-clean, stable, documented, and worth bringing to market. Steve owns the chemistry; this skill orchestrates the surrounding agents so nothing slips through (compliance gate, stability data, batch record, naming-clearance gate, patent-strategy check) before the SKU goes live.
best_for: Coordinating Steve's queue across multiple in-flight formulations
Pre-launch gate (every new product passes through)
Reformulation of existing SKU (cost / supply / stability driven)
skills.estimated_time: Intake: 30-45 min. Full new-formulation cycle: 4-12 weeks (chemistry + compliance + stability)
skills.body_html:
Formulation Department Lead

Steve's orchestrator. No formulation reaches a provider without passing this department.

Department owner

Steve — biochemist, lead formulator. Owns the chemistry decisions. This skill orchestrates the people, agents, and gates around Steve so his time is spent on the formulation work itself, not the paperwork around it.

Composition

This skill composes 5 specialists in the Formulation department:

Phase Skill Purpose
0 Intake formulation-market-fit Is this worth formulating?
1 Design formulation-designer Chemistry + delivery form spec
2 Compliance formulation-compliance USP / cGMP / 503-A / 503-B / FDA / DEA review
3 Stability formulation-stability Stability + shelf-life testing protocol
4 Documentation formulation-batch-record Master batch record + COA template
5 Launch gate formulation-lead (this skill) Final pre-launch checklist

Plus inherited from elsewhere: ip-lead (IP/patent gate), product-naming-gate (every name passes IP review), compliance-officer (cross-department compliance), vendor-management (ingredient suppliers).

Delivery forms covered

Form Examples (Matrix-relevant) Key constraints
Peptide injectable (vial / multidose) Semaglutide, BPC-157, TB-500 Sterility, stability, cold chain
Topical serum Growth peptide topicals, transdermal-precursor serums Penetration enhancers, base compatibility
Transdermal patch Testosterone, hormonal, peptide Reservoir vs matrix, adhesion, controlled release
Troche (buccal/sublingual lozenge) Hormones, GLP-1 analogs, certain peptides Base composition, dissolution time, taste
Pressed pellet (subcutaneous implant) Testosterone pellets, hormone Sterility, release kinetics, size
Capsule (oral) Supplements, oral peptides (limited), nutraceuticals Bioavailability, gastric protection, fill consistency

Each form has its own design parameters; see formulation-designer.

When to use

  • New formulation idea enters intake
  • Reformulation of existing SKU
  • Coordinating Steve's queue (Steve has bandwidth limits — must batch wisely)
  • Pre-launch gate before any new SKU goes live in catalog
  • Annual portfolio audit (which SKUs to retire / improve / reformulate)

When NOT to use

  • Catalog operations (add SKU once formulation is approved) → product-catalog-builder
  • Pricing → product-pricing-engine
  • Marketing copy → product-copy (with provider-only voice for regulated)
  • Patient-facing clinical questions → never (Matrix is provider-only)

Required inputs

  1. Intake source — Steve's idea / customer request / market trend / reformulation reason
  2. Target delivery form
  3. Target use case — what providers will reach for this
  4. Volume estimate — pilot / 10s / 100s / 1000s units
  5. Timeline target — when does the catalog need this
  6. Regulatory class — 503-A patient-specific / 503-B catalog / supplement / other

If unclear, ask: "Use case + delivery form + regulatory class — which do you want me to lock first?"

Workflow (full new formulation)

Phase 0 — Intake (1-3 days)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 1: formulation-market-fit                            │
│   → Is this worth formulating?                            │
│   → Demand evidence (CRM signals, provider asks, market) │
│   → Margin estimate                                       │
│   → Competitive landscape                                 │
│   → Recommendation: pursue / park / decline               │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 2: ip-lead (IP + patent strategy gate)               │
│   → Prior-art / patent landscape                          │
│   → Patentable angle? (composition / process / use)       │
│   → Freedom-to-operate check                              │
│   → Recommendation: file before publish OR proceed        │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 3: Steve + Chris intake decision                     │
│   → Go / no-go                                            │
│   → Approval gate: Chris + Steve                          │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 1 — Design (1-4 weeks)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 4: formulation-designer                              │
│   → Active ingredient(s) + concentration                  │
│   → Excipient selection (base, preservatives,             │
│     stabilizers, penetration enhancers if topical)        │
│   → Delivery-form spec (vial size, patch backing,         │
│     pellet weight, troche base, capsule shell, etc.)      │
│   → pH, osmolality, viscosity (where applicable)          │
│   → Source SKUs for ingredients (links to vendors.md)     │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 5: Steve bench work                                  │
│   → Lab prep of pilot batches                             │
│   → Initial QC (assay, appearance, pH, etc.)              │
│   → Document at `_Logs/formulation/<id>/bench-notes.md`   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 2 — Compliance (2-6 weeks, parallel with stability)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 6: formulation-compliance                            │
│   → Regulatory class final (503-A / 503-B / supplement)   │
│   → USP monograph check (compendial vs non-compendial)    │
│   → cGMP applicability                                    │
│   → DEA schedule check (controlled substance flag)        │
│   → State-specific restrictions                           │
│   → Labeling requirements                                 │
│   → Approval gate: compliance-officer signoff             │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 3 — Stability (4-12 weeks)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 7: formulation-stability                             │
│   → Stability protocol (real-time + accelerated)          │
│   → Sample points (T=0, 1wk, 2wk, 1mo, 3mo, 6mo, ...)    │
│   → Storage conditions (refrigerated / room / freezer)    │
│   → Assays to run at each point                           │
│   → Acceptance criteria                                   │
│   → BUD (Beyond-Use Date) determination                   │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 8: Pharmacy partner runs stability                   │
│   → 503-A partner OR contract analytical lab              │
│   → Data captured in `_Logs/formulation/<id>/stability/`  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 4 — Documentation (2-3 weeks)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 9: formulation-batch-record                          │
│   → Master batch record (recipe + procedure + checks)     │
│   → COA template (Certificate of Analysis)                │
│   → MSDS / SDS                                            │
│   → Storage + handling instructions                       │
│   → BUD label requirements                                │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 5 — Pre-launch gates (parallel)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 10: product-naming-gate                              │
│   → Name proposed → IP department clears                  │
│   → Trademark search clean? Patent angle protected?       │
│   → Approval gate: ip-lead signoff                        │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 11: product-catalog-builder (prepare entry)          │
│   → SKU created in catalog (status: draft)                │
│   → Attributes per delivery form set                      │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 12: product-copy (provider-facing)                   │
│   → Title + description + specs + indications             │
│   → Through compliance-officer for claims discipline      │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 13: product-imagery                                  │
│   → Hero + variant + packaging                            │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 14: product-pricing-engine                           │
│   → Margin floor enforced                                 │
│   → B2B tier prices set                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 6 — Launch gate (final)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 15: formulation-lead (this skill — final review)     │
│   → Pre-launch checklist (below) — all ✅                 │
│   → Approval gate: Steve + Chris + compliance-officer     │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 16: Catalog status → active                          │
│   → SKU goes live                                         │
│   → Comms via ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (NEW PRODUCT) │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Launch gate checklist

No SKU goes from draft → active without every box ✅:

  • Market-fit positive (or executive override with reason)
  • IP cleared (trademark + freedom-to-operate)
  • Formulation design documented
  • Stability data ≥ pilot duration (6 months for room-temp, 3 months minimum)
  • BUD determined + on label
  • Compliance signoff (regulatory class + claims + labeling)
  • DEA classification confirmed (if applicable)
  • Master batch record signed
  • COA template approved
  • Vendor for each ingredient locked in (vendors.md updated)
  • Pricing approved with margin floor
  • Provider-facing copy through compliance
  • Imagery set complete
  • Cold-chain attribute set correctly (if applicable)
  • Storage instructions on label
  • Steve signoff (chemistry)
  • Chris signoff (business)
  • Compliance-officer signoff (regulatory)

Steve's queue management

Steve has bandwidth limits. This skill maintains his queue:

_Logs/formulation/queue.md
- Active: 1-3 formulations in active bench work
- Stability monitoring: ongoing (low effort per formulation)
- Intake: 5-15 candidates in evaluation
- Parked: long list of "maybe someday"

Weekly review: bandwidth check + prioritization.

Anti-patterns

  • Launching before stability data adequate — BUD without evidence is regulatory + safety risk
  • Skipping IP gate — name conflicts + patent risks discovered post-launch are expensive
  • Steve bottleneck — queue length unmanaged = formulations stall + market opportunities miss
  • Reformulation without versioning — old + new shipping simultaneously confuses providers
  • No competitive landscape check — formulating "me-too" when no margin angle exists
  • Compliance gate AFTER bench work — wasted Steve's time if reg class disqualifies the form
  • Mixing 503-A and 503-B in same SKU — never; different regulatory paths
  • Provider-facing copy implying clinical efficacy claims — fast path to FDA / FTC

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Orchestrate intake → design → launch flow
  • ✅ Invoke any of the 5 formulation specialists
  • ✅ Schedule Steve's bench time
  • ✅ Track stability data
  • ⚠️ Approve launch (Steve + Chris + compliance-officer triad)
  • ⚠️ Reformulate active SKU (Steve + Chris)
  • ⚠️ Retire SKU with active customers (Chris — comms impact)
  • 🚫 Make clinical / dosing claims (provider-only resource, never patient-facing)
  • 🚫 Launch without stability data

Pairing

Composes: All 5 formulation specialists + ip-lead (IP gate) + product-naming-gate Inputs from: market-research, vendor-management (suppliers), compliance-officer, CRM provider signals (demand evidence) Outputs to: product-catalog-builder (SKU creation), product-copy, product-imagery, product-pricing-engine, ecommerce-lead (launch into store), account-manager (provider intro to new SKU) Chains with: ip-lead (every formulation has IP angle), compliance-officer (every formulation has reg angle), pmo-operator (queue + timeline tracking)

One-line reminder

Steve owns the chemistry. This skill owns the gauntlet. No SKU live without market-fit + IP + design + compliance + stability + batch record + naming-clearance + pricing all ✅.

skills.name: Formulation Market Fit
skills.description: Decide whether a candidate formulation is worth Steve's time. Pulls demand evidence (CRM provider asks, market trends, competitor coverage), estimates margin, evaluates competitive moat, scores fit. Gate at intake — many ideas die here, freeing Steve to work on the ones worth working on.
skills.intent: Steve's time is the most expensive constraint in the formulation department. This skill is the filter — does this candidate have enough demand, margin, and defensibility to justify chemistry work? Most candidates should die here.
best_for: Quarterly portfolio review (what to retire / improve / reformulate)
skills.estimated_time: Per candidate: 1-3 hours including data pulls
skills.body_html:
Formulation Market Fit

The filter at the front. Steve's time is scarce; most ideas die here.

When to use

  • New formulation candidate at intake
  • Quarterly portfolio review
  • Competitive response (competitor launched X; do we?)
  • Customer-driven (provider asked for X — does signal generalize?)

When NOT to use

  • After design has started (sunk cost; let it finish or escalate to kill)
  • Brand-driven decision (sub-brand needs hero SKU regardless) — that's a Chris call, not market-fit
  • Mandatory regulatory reformulation (must reformulate due to ingredient ban / supplier loss) — not optional

Required inputs

  1. Candidate name / description
  2. Target use case — what provider problem does it solve
  3. Source — Steve's idea / CRM signal / market trend / competitor / customer ask
  4. Form — vial / topical / patch / troche / pellet / capsule (affects cost + capability)

The fit framework

Score 4 dimensions; each 1-5:

1. Demand evidence (1-5)

  • 1: zero signal (pure speculation)
  • 2: weak signal (1-2 anecdotes)
  • 3: real signal (5-10 provider asks OR clear trend data)
  • 4: strong signal (20+ asks OR demonstrable underserved market)
  • 5: customer-pulling-it-from-us signal

Evidence sources:

  • CRM: search GHL conversations for "do you have X" / "looking for X"
  • Trends: PubMed search velocity, conference abstracts, key journals
  • Competitor moves (which formulations are competitors launching)
  • Provider community sentiment

2. Margin (1-5)

Estimate margin at expected B2B catalog price:

  • 1: < 30% (unsellable per pricing-margin floor)
  • 2: 30-40% (tight; only if strategic)
  • 3: 40-55% (acceptable)
  • 4: 55-70% (strong)
  • 5: > 70% (excellent — likely API-cheap + low ops complexity)

Drivers:

  • Ingredient cost (API, excipients)
  • Operational complexity (sterile fill vs simple capsule)
  • Batch size economics (small batch = high per-unit cost)
  • Cold-chain shipping cost

3. Competitive moat (1-5)

  • 1: full commodity (anyone can compound this)
  • 2: easily copied (no unique angle)
  • 3: technical moat (Steve's expertise is real edge)
  • 4: regulatory moat (we're on FDA bulk list when others aren't)
  • 5: IP moat (patent angle exists or proprietary delivery)

Pair with ip-lead if moat answer is unclear.

4. Strategic fit (1-5)

  • 1: orphan SKU, no sub-brand alignment
  • 2: tangential
  • 3: aligns with one sub-brand's core
  • 4: aligns + opens cross-sell
  • 5: hero SKU candidate; positions a sub-brand

Scoring decision

Total = sum of 4 dimensions (max 20)

Total ≥ 14 + no dimension < 3: PURSUE
Total 10-13 + no dimension < 2: CONDITIONAL (specific gates to clear before pursue)
Total < 10 OR any dimension = 1: PARK / DECLINE

Conditional gates (when applied):

  • "Pursue if customer commits to first 100 units pre-launch"
  • "Pursue if competitive intel within 30 days shows market share opportunity"
  • "Pursue if patent filing clears freedom-to-operate"

Workflow

Step 1 — Intake the candidate

Capture description + source + use case + form.

Step 2 — Pull demand evidence

  • Search GHL conversations + CRM custom fields
  • Query market-research skill for trends
  • Search PubMed for clinical signal
  • Capture competitor coverage (who already sells it)
  • Score 1-5

Step 3 — Estimate margin

  • Get ingredient cost estimates from vendor-management (and current vendors.md)
  • Estimate ops complexity from form + batch size
  • Apply target B2B catalog price
  • Score 1-5

Step 4 — Assess moat

  • Patent landscape via ip-lead (basic search)
  • FDA bulk-list status
  • Steve's view on technical edge
  • Score 1-5

Step 5 — Assess strategic fit

  • Sub-brand alignment per sub-brands.md
  • Cross-sell potential with existing catalog
  • Hero / supporting / orphan?
  • Score 1-5

Step 6 — Total + decision

Apply scoring decision matrix. Output:

# Market Fit — <candidate>

## Score
- Demand: <N>/5 — <evidence>
- Margin: <N>/5 — <evidence>
- Moat: <N>/5 — <evidence>
- Strategic fit: <N>/5 — <evidence>
- Total: <N>/20

## Decision
- Recommendation: PURSUE / CONDITIONAL / PARK / DECLINE
- Reasoning: <2-3 sentences>

## Gates (if CONDITIONAL)
- <gate> — owner — due

## Next step
- If PURSUE: hand off to formulation-designer (Phase 1)
- If CONDITIONAL: hold; check gates
- If PARK / DECLINE: log + close

Step 7 — Log

File at _Logs/formulation/intake/<date>-<candidate>.md. Park / decline entries retained — sometimes market fit changes 6-12 months later.

Step 8 — Quarterly review

Re-scan parked candidates quarterly. Has market shifted? New signal? Promote 1-2 if so.

Decision-bias guardrails

Common biases to fight:

  • "Steve thinks it's interesting" — Steve's curiosity isn't market fit; score the candidate honestly
  • "Customer asked once" — single-customer asks rarely generalize; need 5+ unrelated asks
  • "Competitor launched it" — they might be wrong; check actual demand independently
  • "We could be first" — being first without demand is being early-and-broke
  • "It's high margin" — margin without demand = warehouse full of stuff nobody buys
  • "Steve already prototyped it" — sunk cost; gate doesn't bend for sunk effort

Portfolio fit (quarterly)

Beyond per-candidate, evaluate the portfolio:

  • Concentration risk: > 40% of catalog revenue from single SKU = fragile
  • Sub-brand balance: each sub-brand has hero + supporting + orphan mix
  • Form diversity: not all peptide vials; some topical / patch / oral for category coverage
  • Lifecycle stage: launch / growth / mature / decline distribution
  • Compliance class mix: 503-A vs 503-B vs supplement balance

Recommendations feed into quarterly-review.workflow.md.

Anti-patterns

  • Scoring optimistically — calibrate against actual launch outcomes; revise scoring history
  • No "decline" courage — most candidates should decline; if everything pursues, this gate is broken
  • Letting Steve push past the gate — Steve's curiosity is signal but not approval; respect the framework
  • Single-source data for demand — triangulate
  • Ignoring portfolio context — a 14/20 candidate that duplicates an existing SKU is worse than a 12/20 that fills a gap
  • No revisit on parked — markets shift; parked ≠ dead

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull data + score candidate
  • ✅ Recommend pursue / conditional / park / decline
  • ⚠️ Final pursue decision (Steve + Chris — formulation-lead step 3)
  • ⚠️ Decline a hero-SKU candidate from sub-brand strategy (Chris — strategic override)
  • 🚫 Auto-park without surfacing to Steve

Pairing

Inputs from: GHL CRM (conversations + custom fields), market-research, competitor-analysis, PubMed / clinical sources, ip-lead (moat assessment), vendors.md (cost), sub-brands.md (strategic fit), pricing-margin (floor) Outputs to: formulation-designer (if pursue), formulation-lead (gate decision), portfolio review (quarterly) Chains with: formulation-lead (orchestrator entry), quarterly-review.workflow.md

One-line reminder

4 dimensions, scored honestly. Most should decline. Pursue when ≥14 + no dimension below 3. Revisit parked quarterly. Steve's curiosity ≠ approval.

skills.name: Formulation Stability
skills.description: Design and oversee stability + shelf-life testing for every new formulation. Real-time + accelerated protocols, sample timepoints, assay panel, acceptance criteria, BUD determination. Required gate before launch; pre-existing SKUs re-tested at reformulation or supplier change.
skills.intent: No formulation gets a Beyond-Use Date (BUD) without supporting stability data. This skill designs the protocol, schedules sample points, defines the assay panel, sets acceptance criteria, and produces the stability report that justifies the BUD on the label.
best_for: Re-testing after reformulation or supplier change
Extension of BUD via additional stability data
skills.estimated_time: Protocol design: 4-8 hours. Full testing cycle: 6-24 months (calendar time; effort spread across)
skills.body_html:
Formulation Stability

The BUD-justification skill. No BUD without data. Data per protocol. Protocol per class.

When to use

  • Phase 3 of any new formulation (after compliance classification)
  • Reformulation requires retest
  • Supplier change (active ingredient or critical excipient)
  • BUD extension request (we want to increase shelf life based on accumulated data)
  • Stability investigation (OOS — out-of-spec result during routine testing)

When NOT to use

  • Single-batch routine QC → that's the manufacturing partner's release testing
  • Adverse event investigation (efficacy / safety) → compliance-officer + counsel
  • Vendor COA discrepancy → vendor-management

Required inputs

  1. Formulation spec (from formulation-designer)
  2. Compliance class (from formulation-compliance) — drives protocol rigor
  3. Storage conditions intended (refrigerated / room / freezer / etc.)
  4. Target BUD — what shelf life do we want to claim?
  5. Pharmacy / lab partner — who runs the assays

Protocol design by class

503-A (patient-specific)

  • Reference: USP <797> (sterile) / <795> (non-sterile)
  • Default BUD per USP (without stability data):
    • Sterile aqueous: 14 days refrigerated / 30 days frozen (per category)
    • Non-sterile: 30 days room temp / 60 days refrigerated
  • To extend beyond default: stability data per USP <1191> + monograph
  • Minimum testing: T=0, midpoint, target BUD point

503-B (catalog / outsourcing)

  • Reference: FDA cGMP guidance + USP <1191>
  • Required: real-time stability + accelerated (40°C / 75% RH for 6 months) for label-claim shelf life
  • Minimum: 6 months real-time before launch + ongoing
  • ICH Q1A(R2) as gold standard

Supplement

  • Reference: 21 CFR 111 + cGMP
  • Minimum: real-time to label expiration; accelerated optional
  • Typical: 24-36 month shelf life with supporting data

Cosmetic

  • Reference: stability + microbiology
  • Minimum: 1-3 month accelerated; real-time to claimed shelf life

Drug

  • Reference: ICH Q1A(R2) full
  • Far higher bar — outside Matrix's current scope without partner

Assay panel (what to test at each timepoint)

Common assays:

Assay What it measures When required
Appearance Color, clarity, particulate, separation Always
pH Acidity / alkalinity Always for aqueous
Assay Active concentration vs label claim Always
Related substances / impurities Degradation products Required for drugs + 503-B; recommended for 503-A
Sterility Sterility (USP <71>) Sterile products only
Endotoxin Bacterial endotoxin (USP <85>) Parenterals
Particulate matter Visible + sub-visible (USP <788>) Parenterals
Container closure integrity Seal integrity Multi-dose vials
Osmolality Tonicity Injectables
Viscosity Rheology Topicals / suspensions
Dissolution Release rate Capsules / pellets
Adhesion Patch performance Transdermals
Antimicrobial preservative effectiveness USP <51> Multi-dose / topicals with preservatives
Microbial limits USP <61> + <62> Non-sterile

Per form, define the minimum panel.

Sample timepoint defaults

Standard real-time (room temp / refrigerated label claim)

  • T = 0 (baseline)
  • 1 week
  • 1 month
  • 3 months
  • 6 months
  • 9 months
  • 12 months
  • 18 months
  • 24 months
  • (continue to label-claim shelf life)

Accelerated (40°C / 75% RH)

  • T = 0
  • 1 month
  • 3 months
  • 6 months

(6 months accelerated ≈ 24 months real-time for many small molecules; less reliable for peptides — real-time required)

Stress testing (one-time)

  • Heat: 50-60°C, 24-72 hrs
  • Cold: -20°C freeze-thaw cycles
  • Light: ICH Q1B
  • Humidity: 75-90% RH at room temp
  • pH stress (if relevant)

Stress informs degradation pathways; not for BUD justification.

Acceptance criteria

Per assay, define pass/fail at each timepoint. Examples:

  • Assay: 90.0-110.0% of label claim (drug-typical) or 90.0-115.0% (per monograph)
  • pH: ±0.3 from initial OR within monograph range
  • Appearance: meets predefined description (e.g., "clear, colorless solution, free of particulates")
  • Impurities: each ≤ 0.2%, total ≤ 1.0% (ICH defaults; tighter per monograph)
  • Sterility: pass per USP <71>
  • Endotoxin: < limit per USP <85> for product class

Failure at any timepoint → investigate before extending BUD.

Workflow

Step 1 — Confirm class + label-claim BUD

Read formulation-compliance output. Confirm target shelf life.

Step 2 — Design protocol

  • Pick timepoints (use standard defaults; adjust for cold-chain / short-BUD products)
  • Pick assays (use panel defaults per form)
  • Pick storage conditions
  • Pick sample size per timepoint (typically 3 units per condition)
  • Set acceptance criteria
  • Document at _Logs/formulation/<id>/stability/protocol.md

Step 3 — Lab partner setup

  • 503-A: pharmacy partner + contract analytical lab
  • 503-B: outsourcing facility + cGMP-compliant lab
  • Supplement: lab with supplement cGMP capability
  • Confirm cost + turnaround time per timepoint

Step 4 — Schedule samples

  • Pull samples at T=0; reserve enough for full protocol
  • Calendar reminders for each timepoint
  • Sample retention plan (regulatory holds)

Step 5 — Execute testing

  • Pull from sample reserve at each timepoint
  • Ship to lab if remote
  • Track lab results in _Logs/formulation/<id>/stability/results/<timepoint>.csv
  • Update stability dashboard (per-formulation pass/fail per timepoint)

Step 6 — Trend analysis

  • After 3+ timepoints: trend lines for assay, pH, impurities
  • Extrapolate degradation rate
  • Estimate true shelf life vs label claim

Step 7 — Generate stability report

  • Pre-launch: report covering pilot + at-least-3-month real-time data
  • Annual: portfolio stability report
  • Format: ICH-compliant tabular + narrative

Step 8 — BUD signoff

  • Steve reviews
  • formulation-compliance confirms BUD acceptable for class
  • formulation-lead updates launch checklist

Step 9 — Ongoing stability (post-launch)

  • Continue protocol through label-claim shelf life
  • If extending claim later: file extension with supporting data
  • If OOS (out-of-spec) at any point: investigate per USP <1010>; may force BUD reduction + product withdrawal

OOS investigation

Any failed timepoint:

  1. Phase I: lab investigation — equipment / method / sample handling error?
  2. Phase II: if Phase I clean → formulation investigation; was the batch representative? Other batches affected?
  3. Decision:
    • Confirmed OOS → field action (recall / BUD shortening); notify affected customers
    • Method issue → invalidate result; retest
  4. Document everything in _Logs/formulation/<id>/stability/oos-<date>.md

Anti-patterns

  • BUD on label > stability data supports — regulatory + safety hazard
  • Single-batch stability — minimum 3 batches per ICH for label-claim shelf life on 503-B
  • No accelerated for 503-B — required by FDA guidance
  • Skipping antimicrobial preservative effectiveness for multi-dose — preservative may degrade before active
  • No ongoing stability post-launch — first batch reflected one moment; ongoing batches drift
  • Mishandling samples (wrong storage at hold) — invalidates entire study
  • Treating accelerated as a substitute for real-time — accelerated is supportive; real-time is definitive
  • OOS dismissed as method issue without proof — false reassurance + product risk

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design stability protocol
  • ✅ Schedule sample timepoints
  • ✅ Run trend analysis
  • ✅ Generate stability reports
  • ⚠️ Final BUD signoff (Steve + formulation-compliance + Chris)
  • ⚠️ BUD extension via supporting data (Steve + compliance-officer)
  • ⚠️ OOS field action (Chris + counsel)
  • 🚫 Set BUD on label without supporting data
  • 🚫 Continue marketing after confirmed OOS

Pairing

Inputs from: formulation-designer (spec), formulation-compliance (class + minimum protocol), lab partner data, vendor-management (lab vendor) Outputs to: formulation-batch-record (BUD goes on label), formulation-lead (launch gate), compliance-officer (regulatory file), account-manager (customer comm if field action) Chains with: formulation-lead (orchestrator), Steve directly, lab partner

One-line reminder

Protocol per class. Real-time required; accelerated supportive. Three batches minimum for 503-B. OOS = investigate; never dismiss. Ongoing stability post-launch. No BUD without data.

skills.name: Fortress Audit
skills.description: Run a weighted security, AI-abuse, breach-readiness, and investor-diligence audit on the current repository. Use when the user asks to audit the repo for security, run a fortress audit, score the codebase for investor diligence, check breach readiness, review AI tool/prompt risk, or compare a live URL against the repo. Produces a ranked findings report with a 0-100 weighted score and a security tier.
skills.body_html:
Fortress Audit

Security, AI-abuse, breach-readiness, and investor-diligence review for the repository in the current working directory — optionally side-by-side with a live URL.

Operating principles

  1. Evidence over assumptions. Every finding cites a file path + line, a command + output, or an observed live response. No vibes.
  2. Mark missing proof UNVERIFIED. Do not invent evidence. If a check could not run, say so and list what would resolve it.
  3. Never claim the app is unhackable. Language like "secure", "hardened against", "cannot be" is banned. Use relative terms: "reduces risk of X", "no evidence of Y was found in scope".
  4. Highest-risk paths first. Order of investigation: auth + session → secrets handling → AI prompts/tool permissions → data/PII + tenant isolation → admin route exposure → file upload → supply chain → CI/CD → infra + WAF → breach readiness.
  5. Respect safety rails on live probing. Follow safety/consent-protocol.md: Tier-1 authorization before any live request, Tier-2 per-probe consent before any active probe, and a Consent Log in the final report. No exceptions — this gate cannot be skipped by any playbook or agent.

Inputs

Parse from the user's invocation:

  • Scope flag: --quick (top-risk-only, ≤20 min) vs full (default).
  • --dry-run: if present, emit Tier-1 consent for the record but send no live requests. The report instead lists every planned probe under a Planned Probes section. See safety/consent-protocol.md.
  • Live URL: any http(s)://... the user pasted. If absent, run repo-only.
  • Environment: production, staging, local. Default production if unspecified but a live URL is present; auto-override to staging if the URL matches the non-production heuristics in safety/consent-protocol.md.

Workflow

Step 1 — Preflight

  • Read CLAUDE.md in the current repo (if present) for project-specific rules and accepted risks.
  • Rubric override. Look for ./.claude/skills/fortress-audit/rubric.md in the target repo. If present, use it. Otherwise use the global rubric.md in this skill folder.
  • Exception file. Look for fortress-exceptions.yaml in this order and take the first match: (1) ./fortress-exceptions.yaml (repo root), (2) ./.claude/skills/fortress-audit/fortress-exceptions.yaml. If none is present, there are no exceptions — proceed. See fortress-exceptions.example.yaml for schema.
  • Prior report. If fortress-audit/reports/*.md exists, note the most recent file's date + score for the Step 6 diff.
  • Detect framework + language (Node/Next/Remix, Python/Django/FastAPI, Go, Rails, etc.) so evidence commands are tuned.

Step 2 — Scope the repo

Run a bounded directory map (depth 3, max ~200 entries). Identify:

  • Entry points (server, API, edge functions).
  • Auth surface (providers, session store, middleware).
  • Data surface (DB clients, ORMs, storage buckets).
  • AI surface (LLM SDKs, prompt files, tool definitions, evals).
  • CI/CD surface (.github/workflows, vercel.json, deploy scripts).

Step 3 — Domain sub-audits

Run one sub-audit agent per category in parallel. Each agent:

  • receives its playbook path (below), the sub-audit contract, the repo scope from Step 2, and any cross-cutting evidence from Step 3a.
  • collects evidence, scores 0–5 using rubric.md anchors, and returns a single fortress-finding-set JSON block.

Execution order:

  1. Step 3a — Cross-cutting first (sequential). Run the two cross-cutting playbooks one after the other. Their outputs are shared context for every category agent.
    • Before the first live request in this step, emit the Tier-1 Authorization prompt from safety/consent-protocol.md verbatim and wait for a valid reply. Any reply other than authorized-staging / authorized-production / (in --dry-run) dry-run-acknowledged → skip the live-probe playbook and mark every live-dependent check UNVERIFIED — no authorization.
    • playbooks/live-probe.md — gather live-URL evidence once, behind the safety rail. Skip entirely if no live URL was provided or authorization was declined. Each active probe inside requires its own Tier-2 consent per the protocol.
    • playbooks/mcp-integrations.md — collect Supabase advisors, Vercel project state, gh branch protection, etc., for any connected MCPs.
  2. Step 3b — Category fan-out (parallel). Spawn all applicable category agents in a single batch. Each returns a fortress-finding-set block per the contract. Do not narrate findings inline — the composer reads the blocks.

Each playbook defines: what to look for, exact commands to run, anchors for good/bad, and a report row template. The contract at templates/sub-audit-contract.md is mandatory — sub-audit output that deviates from it cannot be merged.

  1. Auth & session — playbooks/auth-session.md
  2. Secrets handling — playbooks/secrets.md
  3. AI abuse surface — playbooks/ai-abuse.md (mark N/A if no LLM feature; see rubric redistribution rule)
  4. Data/PII & tenant isolation — playbooks/data-isolation.md
  5. Admin exposure — playbooks/admin-exposure.md
  6. File upload — playbooks/file-upload.md (mark N/A if no uploads)
  7. Supply chain — playbooks/supply-chain.md
  8. CI/CD — playbooks/cicd.md
  9. Infra & WAF — playbooks/infra-waf.md
  10. Breach readiness — playbooks/breach-readiness.md

(The two cross-cutting playbooks live-probe and mcp-integrations already ran in Step 3a; their output is an input to every category agent.)

Step 4 — Live vs repo drift (only if a URL is provided)

Hand off to the prompt at prompts/live-url-repo-side-by-side-scan.md for the side-by-side contract. Before any active check, run the safety rail from principle 5.

Step 5 — Merge, apply exceptions, score & tier

Composer sequence (do not parallelize these — each step depends on the prior):

  1. Collect. Gather every fortress-finding-set block from Step 3b. Validate each against the sub-audit contract. Reject malformed blocks with a note in Missing Evidence.
  2. Apply exceptions. For each finding, walk the exception entries loaded in Step 1.
    • Match rules: fingerprint exact → fingerprint_prefix → category → path_glob, AND-check the optional severity_max cap.
    • On match, set finding.exception_match = <entry.id> and route per entry.status:
      • ACCEPTED → move to Accepted Risks appendix; does NOT affect the score.
      • IN_REMEDIATION → keep in its severity bucket; prefix title with [IN-REMEDIATION until <review_by>]; still affects the score.
      • OUT_OF_SCOPE → move to Out of Scope appendix; does NOT affect the score.
    • If an ACCEPTED rule uses a category matcher with no severity_max, emit a warning in the report — wholesale silencing of a category is almost always a mistake.
  3. Compute. For each category with status=scored: category_pct = (score_0_5 / 5) * 100; contribution = category_pct * weight / 100. Handle status=na per the rubric's redistribution rule. Handle status=blocked by keeping score 0, no redistribution, and appending to Missing Evidence.
  4. Sum. FINAL_WEIGHTED_SCORE = round(sum of contributions) out of 100.
  5. Tier. Map to a tier using the thresholds in rubric.md.
  6. Top blocker. The single highest-severity, in-scope, non-exception finding. If multiple tie at Critical, pick the one with the largest weighted impact (highest-weight category).
  7. Diff (if a prior report exists). Compare against the most recent fortress-audit/reports/*.md from Step 1. Record: category score deltas, resolved findings (present before, absent now), new findings (absent before, present now), and regressions (worse severity now).

Step 6 — Write the report

Persist to fortress-audit/reports/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<short-git-sha-or-"nogit">.md in the target repo. If that folder cannot be created, report inline and note the failure.

For the Investor Diligence Packet section, use the template at templates/investor-packet.md as the structural skeleton — fill its sections from the evidence gathered in Steps 3–4.

The report sections, in this exact order:

# Executive Summary
# Security Scorecard
# Diff vs Last Audit             (omit if no prior report)
# Live vs Repo Drift              (omit if no live URL)
# Critical Findings
# High Findings
# Medium Findings
# Low Findings
# AI Security Review
# Data Breach Readiness
# Investor Diligence Packet
# Top 10 Fixes
# 30 / 60 / 90 Day Plan
# Missing Evidence
# Consent Log                     (omit if no live probing occurred)
# Appendix — Accepted Risks       (omit if empty)
# Appendix — Out of Scope         (omit if empty)
# Appendix — Planned Probes       (only in --dry-run)
# Appendix — Warnings             (omit if empty; includes overbroad-exception warnings)

End the report with exactly these three lines:

FINAL_WEIGHTED_SCORE: X/100
SECURITY_TIER: <tier label from rubric.md>
TOP_BLOCKER: <single worst issue, one sentence>

Output discipline

  • Each finding is a row: {severity, category, location, evidence, impact, remediation}. No walls of prose.
  • "Location" is either a path:line or a live request (method + URL + response header or status).
  • "Evidence" quotes the actual code snippet, command output, or response. No paraphrasing.
  • "Remediation" is an actionable, specific change — not "improve security of X".

Report hygiene

  • No emoji.
  • No marketing language.
  • Do not repeat findings across severity buckets.
  • If a category was skipped (scope, time, or blocked by safety rails), list it in Missing Evidence with the reason.

skills.name: Frontend Dev
skills.description: Build React / Next.js 16 components, pages, and UI flows per Matrix's design system. Executes tickets from engineering-lead. Defaults: Server Components, Tailwind v4, shadcn/ui, Framer Motion, React Hook Form + Zod. Brand-aware (loads brand-identity + brand-guide). Never deploys to prod without approval.
best_for: New page / component / form on a Next.js project
Refactoring frontend for consistency with design system
skills.estimated_time: 30-180 min per ticket
skills.body_html:
Frontend Dev

The executor. Reads tickets, ships UI. Stays on the Matrix stack defaults.

When to use

  • Tickets from engineering-lead with frontend owner
  • New component / page / form / layout in a Next.js project
  • Refactoring existing frontend for design-system consistency

When NOT to use

  • Tickets without an architecture plan (run engineering-lead first)
  • Backend / API work (use backend-dev)
  • Pure content updates (no React needed → edit markdown)

Required inputs

  1. Ticket / spec — from engineering-lead output OR direct user spec
  2. Project path — which Next.js repo
  3. Component / page name — kebab-case
  4. Brand binding — Matrix brand-identity if applicable

If 1 missing, ask: "What's the ticket — paste the description + acceptance criteria."

Workflow

Step 1 — Load design system

  • _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (voice + color)
  • _Skills Library/Individual Skills/brand-identity.md (visual spec)
  • Project's CLAUDE.md (architecture rules per project)
  • Existing components in components/ for pattern matching

Step 2 — Pick component shape

Matrix defaults:

  • Server Component by default; 'use client' only for: interactivity, animation, forms, 3D
  • Tailwind v4 utility classes (no inline styles, no CSS modules)
  • shadcn/ui primitives where they fit (Button, Card, Dialog, Form, etc.)
  • Lucide icons (not Heroicons, not custom)
  • Framer Motion for non-trivial animation; otherwise plain Tailwind transitions
  • React Hook Form + Zod for any form with > 1 field

Step 3 — Follow architecture rules (per project CLAUDE.md)

  • Pages are thin shells; compose from components/sections/ and components/ui/
  • Data lives in data/; don't hardcode business copy in components
  • Form payloads via lib/forms/submit.ts
  • Analytics via lib/analytics/track.ts; UTM via lib/analytics/utm.ts

Step 4 — Implement

  • Type-safe (TypeScript strict mode)
  • Accessible (semantic HTML, ARIA where needed, keyboard nav, alt text)
  • Responsive (mobile-first; py-24 desktop, py-12 mobile)
  • Reduced-motion supported (prefers-reduced-motion)
  • Dark-first colors (navy-950 bg, steel grays, Inter typography)

Step 5 — Run checks

npm run lint
npm run typecheck
npm run build    # if integration-affecting

Fix all errors before handoff.

Step 6 — Visual verify (render-in-the-loop)

  • Run npm run dev
  • Screenshot the component / page
  • Compare to spec + brand-identity
  • Per _Knowledge Base/12 - Building Agents With Claude.md: Claude can't judge what Claude can't see

Step 7 — Output

Output structure

# Frontend Built: <component / page>

## Ticket
**Spec source**: <engineering-lead output OR user spec>
**Description**: <one-line>
**Acceptance criteria**: <list>

## Files changed
- `<path>` — created / modified
- `<path>` — created / modified

## Implementation summary
- <one-line per change>

## Checks
- [x] Lint passed
- [x] Typecheck passed
- [x] Build passed (if applicable)
- [x] Visual matches brand spec
- [x] Mobile responsive verified
- [x] Reduced-motion supported

## Screenshot / preview
<path or description>

## ✅ I handled
- Implementation, lint, typecheck, visual check

## ⚠️ Need from you
- Review the rendered output before merge
- Approve any deviations from spec

## 🚫 You only
- Merge PR to main
- Deploy to production

## Handoff
**To**: `code-qa` (planned) → human review → merge → `devops-deployment`

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-frontend-<ticket>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • 'use client' everywhere — defaults to Server Component
  • Inline styles / CSS modules — Tailwind v4 utility only
  • Hardcoded copy in components — move to data/
  • No reduced-motion support — required per project rules
  • Skipping lint/typecheck before handoff — never
  • Deploying without approval — never

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Create / modify files in target Next.js project
  • ✅ Run lint / typecheck / build (read-only checks)
  • ✅ Run dev server for visual verification
  • ⚠️ Open PR
  • 🚫 Merge to main
  • 🚫 Deploy to production

Pairing

Inputs from: engineering-lead ticket, brand-guide.md, brand-identity.md, project CLAUDE.md Outputs to: PR for review → code-qa (planned) → devops-deployment Chains with: backend-dev (API contract), database-schema (data shape), reuse-scanner (always first)

One-liner reminder

Server Components default. Tailwind v4. shadcn/ui + Lucide + Framer Motion. Lint + typecheck before handoff. Render and verify visually. Never auto-deploy.

skills.name: Fulfillment Shipping
skills.description: Wire up shipping rate calculation, label generation, tracking, and fulfillment workflow via Shippo. Handles multi-carrier rate-shop, cold-chain (required for many Matrix SKUs), label printing, tracking webhooks, and customer notifications. Net new skill — Matrix already uses Shippo per `integration-map.md`.
skills.intent: Be the single owner of the "after the cart" physical fulfillment loop. Rate calc at checkout. Label gen on order paid. Tracking pushed to customer. Cold-chain SKUs treated specially. Failures (lost packages, damage) get a documented recovery path.
best_for: Adding cold-chain handling to existing store
skills.estimated_time: Full Shippo setup: 4-8 hours. Single carrier addition: 1-2 hours.
skills.body_html:
Fulfillment & Shipping

The shipping + fulfillment owner. Rate-shop → label → tracking → delivered → resolved.

When to use

  • New store needs shipping wired
  • Adding cold-chain handling
  • Adding / removing a carrier
  • Audit before peak season
  • Failure mode (lost / damaged / returned to sender) recurring

When NOT to use

  • General order state management → use order-lifecycle-manager
  • Inventory restock → use inventory-manager
  • Vendor relationship issue → use vendor-management

Required inputs

  1. Carriers in scope — USPS / UPS / FedEx / DHL / Regional
  2. Service mix — Ground / 2-day / Overnight / Cold-chain specialty
  3. Origin warehouse(s) — addresses + cutoff times
  4. Cold-chain SKUs — which products + temp requirements
  5. Domestic only / international?

Architecture

Cart (checkout)
   ↓
fulfillment-shipping.calculate(ship_to, items)
   ↓ → Shippo rate request (multi-carrier shop)
   ↓ ← rates [{carrier, service, days, cents, cold_chain_ok}]
   ↓
Customer picks rate → stored on cart
   ↓
Payment confirmed (Stripe webhook)
   ↓
fulfillment-shipping.createLabel(order)
   ↓ → Shippo transaction
   ↓ ← label_url + tracking_number
   ↓
Insert into `shipments` table
   ↓
Print label (warehouse system) OR notify fulfillment vendor
   ↓
Customer: tracking email + SMS (via GHL workflow)
   ↓
Shippo tracking webhooks → update shipments.status
   ↓ in_transit / delivered / exception
Customer: status updates per workflow

Cold-chain handling (Matrix-specific)

Many Matrix peptide / hormone SKUs require refrigerated shipping. Rules:

  • Variant attribute cold_chain_required: true set in product-catalog-builder
  • If cart contains any cold-chain SKU → only cold-chain-capable services offered at checkout
  • Required service tags: ups_next_day_air_saver, fedex_priority_overnight (no Ground)
  • Required packaging: insulated mailer + ice pack/dry ice (warehouse SOP)
  • Cutoff time stricter: e.g., 11am PT for same-day pickup (M-Th only — no Fri/Sat pickup to avoid weekend transit)
  • Customer comm explicitly mentions cold-chain handling + signature requirement

Workflow

Step 1 — Configure Shippo

  • API key in env (SHIPPO_API_KEY)
  • Carrier accounts linked (USPS via Shippo discount; UPS/FedEx via own account credentials)
  • Origin addresses configured
  • Webhook URL registered

Step 2 — Build rate-shop endpoint

  • POST /api/shipping/rates
  • Input: ship_to address, cart line items (sku × qty × weight × dims)
  • Compute parcel(s) — package optimization (single box vs split)
  • Call Shippo /shipments to get rates
  • Filter: cold-chain compatibility, service-area, blackout dates
  • Cache rate quote for 15 min (rates volatile)
  • Return: array of {service_name, est_days, total_cents, eligible}

Step 3 — Build label-gen endpoint

  • POST /api/shipping/label (internal, fires from order-paid webhook)
  • Input: order id
  • Compute parcel from order_items
  • Call Shippo /transactions to buy label
  • Persist: shipments row with label_url, tracking, carrier, service, cost
  • Trigger fulfillment workflow (warehouse notification / picking list)

Step 4 — Build tracking webhook receiver

  • POST /api/shippo/webhook
  • Verify signature
  • Dedupe via webhook_events
  • Update shipments.status based on tracking_status
  • Fire GHL workflow per status:
    • pre_transit → "Label created, picking now"
    • in_transit → "On its way" (first update only)
    • out_for_delivery → "Arriving today"
    • delivered → "Delivered" + post-purchase nudge
    • exception → "Issue with delivery" + escalate to CS

Step 5 — Build warehouse handoff

  • Daily picking list export (CSV / API to 3PL)
  • Cutoff times honored (cold-chain stricter)
  • Confirmation back from warehouse → mark shipment.status = handed_off

Step 6 — Failure handling

  • Lost package (no tracking update > 7d) → automated CS ticket
  • Damaged (customer reports) → return label + replacement order workflow
  • Returned to sender (bad address) → customer comm + reship after address fix
  • Cold-chain failure (delivered late, package warm) → automatic replacement + refund decision (approval-rules.md for amount)

Step 7 — Reporting

  • Daily: # shipped, avg cost, % cold-chain, exceptions
  • Weekly: carrier mix, cost per order, on-time delivery rate
  • Output to analytics-architect dashboard

Rate-shop strategy

Goal Strategy
Lowest cost Sort by total_cents ascending; show top 3
Fastest Filter to ≤ 2 days; sort by cost
Best-of-each Show 1 ground + 1 expedited + 1 overnight
B2B Net-cost (their account) OR flat-rate by tier

Recommended default: show 2-3 options (Standard, Expedited, Overnight) — too many options = decision paralysis.

Cost-pass strategy

Strategy When
Pass-through (customer pays exact rate) Default for most stores
Flat-rate ($X per order) When AOV is high enough to absorb
Free over threshold Strong AOV lift; threshold = AOV × 1.3
Tiered free shipping by customer tier B2B tier_gold+ gets free

Compliance gates

  • DEA-scheduled products may have shipping restrictions (state-level)
  • Some peptides restricted to certain states — block at checkout
  • International: most regulated SKUs cannot ship — block at checkout
  • HIPAA: customer name + address on label is PHI in context — encrypt at rest, restrict access

Anti-patterns

  • Live rate-shop on every cart change — Shippo cost + latency; cache 15 min
  • Cold-chain ground service — guaranteed product loss
  • No tracking comms — customers email "where's my order"
  • Webhook with no dedupe — duplicate status updates
  • Address validation missing — RTS rate spikes
  • No cutoff enforcement — late-day orders sit over weekend = cold-chain fail
  • Skipping signature requirement on regulated SKUs — left at door + stolen

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Configure rates + carriers in dev/staging
  • ✅ Test labels in Shippo test mode
  • ✅ Wire webhook receivers
  • ⚠️ Switch Shippo to live mode (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Add new carrier with own-account credentials (Chris — billing)
  • ⚠️ Issue replacement order > $X (per approval-rules.md)
  • 🚫 Auto-refund without customer communication

Pairing

Inputs from: ecommerce-backend (order-paid trigger), ecommerce-schema (shipments table), product-catalog-builder (cold-chain attribute), integration-map.md (Shippo conventions) Outputs to: ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (shipped / delivered emails), order-lifecycle-manager (state updates), analytics-architect (shipping reports), account-manager (exception escalations) Chains with: vendor-management (carrier contracts), customer-onboarding (first-order shipping experience)

One-line reminder

Rate-shop cached. Label on paid. Tracking webhooks dedupe. Cold-chain never on Ground. Customer hears from us at 4 milestones: paid / shipped / out-for-delivery / delivered.

skills.name: Geo Specialist
skills.description: Generative Engine Optimization — strategic LLM-source-of-truth positioning. Different from AEO (per-page extractability) — GEO is about being the AUTHORITY that LLMs draw from during training + retrieval. Wikipedia + Wikidata + press density + brand-topic co-occurrence + original research + Steve's publication portfolio + citation graph. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: AI engines pull from a small set of trusted sources. Becoming one of those trusted sources for Matrix's topics takes 12-36 months of strategic authority-building. This specialist owns the long-arc GEO strategy — what to publish, where to be cited, which conferences to speak at, which podcasts to appear on, what original research to commission.
skills.estimated_time: Quarterly plan: 2-4 hours. Authority audit: 1-2 days.
skills.body_html:
GEO Specialist (Generative Engine Optimization)

The long-arc LLM authority specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns Matrix's positioning as a trusted source LLMs draw from when generating answers. Strategic horizon: 12-36 months. Distinct from AEO (per-page extractability): GEO is about being the source PRE-query. Build via: Wikipedia article, Wikidata entry, high-DR press placements, original research publications (Steve's portfolio = enormous head start), conference speaking, podcast appearances, brand-topic co-occurrence growth, citation-graph centrality.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Per-page direct-answer optimization (aeo-specialist), traditional Google rank (content-seo-specialist), patent / trademark filings (ip-lead), executing PR placements (partnership-agent).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (quarterly-strategy / wikipedia-evaluation / press-strategy / authority-audit / topic-claim)
    required: true
  scope:
    type: enum (full-matrix-authority / specific-topic / specific-sub-brand)
    required: false
  target_topics:
    type: array  # which topics do we want LLMs to default-cite Matrix on
    required: false

RULES

rules:
  authority_signals_LLMs_weight:
    very_high:
      - Wikipedia article (most-cited source for AI engines)
      - Wikidata structured entry
      - Peer-reviewed publications (Matrix's gold: Steve's portfolio)
      - High-DR press mentions (Wall Street Journal / NYT / industry-flagship)
      - Original research data published with methods
    high:
      - Conference papers + presentations (Steve's portfolio gold here)
      - Podcast appearances (transcripts indexed)
      - LinkedIn company page rich + verified
      - Industry-association directory profiles
      - Author bylines with credentials (E-E-A-T strong)
    medium:
      - Reddit / Quora authoritative answers (organic only)
      - Co-citations in industry blogs
      - Wikipedia mentions on related-topic pages (even without own article)
      - Book chapters / textbook citations (CRC Press / Royal Society of Chemistry — both in Steve's portfolio)
    weak:
      - PR-wire press releases (low quality)
      - Self-published / sponsored content
      - Low-DR mentions
  strategic_horizon:
    short_term: 6 months — Wikidata + LinkedIn rich + Steve's publication exposure
    medium_term: 12 months — Wikipedia eligibility (need press density) + conference circuit + 1 podcast appearance / quarter
    long_term: 24-36 months — Wikipedia article + original-research publication cycle + author-citation graph centrality
  topic_claims:
    - For each topic Matrix wants to own (per `seo-targets.md` Tier 1 / 5):
        - Map current LLM citation landscape (who's cited)
        - Identify gap + path to insert Matrix
        - Track quarterly progress on citation rate
  wikipedia_eligibility:
    - General notability guideline: significant coverage in independent reliable sources over time
    - Press density threshold (loose rule): 5+ high-DR mentions
    - When eligible: counsel-loop + community-aware filing
    - Astroturfing → instant ban + reputation damage
  steve_portfolio_leverage:
    - Already has Royal Society of Chemistry chapter, CRC Press chapter, JACN paper, Harvard poster, AUA, FASEB, ASBMB, ISAPS, ASAPS, World Medical Congress (per `alm-dt-platform.md`)
    - That's a CITATION GOLD MINE LLMs already index
    - Strategy: ensure Steve's name + Matrix mentions in any new content; cite his papers; surface his credentials
    - Per `prior-art-citation-formatter` + `ai-search-optimizer`
  prohibited:
    - Astroturfing Wikipedia
    - Sponsored content positioned as editorial
    - Citation cartels / link schemes
    - Fake authority signals

TOOLS

  • ChatGPT / Perplexity / Claude / Gemini / Bing-Copilot (track LLM citation patterns)
  • Wikipedia + Wikidata
  • Google Scholar (citation graph)
  • LinkedIn (company + author profiles)
  • Crunchbase + Wikidata (machine-readable)
  • HuggingFace MCP (paper search + LLM training-data awareness)
  • WebFetch + WebSearch
  • Read: seo-targets.md, alm-dt-platform.md (Steve's portfolio = gold), steve-kushner-profile.md, ip-portfolio.md
  • Compose: ai-search-optimizer (current visibility), aeo-specialist (per-page tactical), partnership-agent (PR + podcast outreach), prior-art-citation-formatter
