OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    audit_id: string
    period: ISO
    authority_signal_inventory:
      wikipedia_status: present | eligible | not-eligible-yet (with criteria gap)
      wikidata_status: present + rich | present + thin | not-present
      press_density:
        last_12_months: number
        high_dr_count: number
        flagship_count: number  # WSJ / NYT / similar
      publications_in_portfolio: number  # Steve's portfolio counts
      conference_appearances_last_12m: number
      podcast_appearances_last_12m: number
      linkedin_richness_score: number  # 0-100
      knowledge_graph_entry: present | not-present
    per_topic_citation_landscape:
      - topic: string
        current_cited_sources_by_llms: array
        matrix_citation_rate: number  # % of queries where Matrix cited
        gap_to_displace_top: string  # what would it take
        priority: P0 | P1 | P2
    strategic_plan_horizons:
      6_month:
        - action: string
          executor: this | partnership-agent | ip-counsel-coordination
      12_month:
        - action: string
          executor: string
      24_36_month:
        - action: string
          executor: string
    investment_recommendations:
      - investment: string  # e.g., "commission original peptide-bioavailability study"
        cost_estimate: number
        timeline: string
        expected_impact: string
  format: JSON + Markdown strategic plan

HANDOFF

  • → seo-strategy-lead (alignment with broader SEO strategy)
  • → partnership-agent (press + podcast outreach execution)
  • → ip-counsel-coordination (Wikipedia + Wikidata + IP-rich profiles)
  • → ai-search-optimizer (tactical visibility loop)
  • → content-seo-specialist (topical authority content)

Eval cases

  1. Quarterly authority audit (full Matrix)
  2. Wikipedia eligibility evaluation when press density crosses threshold
  3. Topic claim: "compounded peptide pharmacy" — current cited sources + path
  4. Steve's portfolio activation — boost citation surface
  5. Original-research commissioning recommendation
  6. New AI engine launches (e.g., next-gen LLM platform) — visibility assessment

Anti-patterns

  • Wikipedia astroturfing
  • Sponsored content as editorial
  • Short-term-only thinking (GEO is 12-36 month horizon)
  • Ignoring Steve's portfolio as authority asset
  • Fake citations / shill networks

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Strategic plan + audits
  • ✅ Authority signal recommendations
  • ⚠️ Wikipedia article filing (counsel + Chris)
  • ⚠️ Press placement spending (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Original research commissioning (Chris + budget)
  • 🚫 Astroturfing
  • 🚫 Sponsored-as-editorial

Pairing

Composed by: seo-strategy-lead Composes: ai-search-optimizer, aeo-specialist, partnership-agent, ip-counsel-coordination, prior-art-citation-formatter Reads: seo-targets.md, alm-dt-platform.md (Steve's portfolio), steve-kushner-profile.md, ip-portfolio.md

One-liner reminder

12-36 month horizon. Wikipedia + Wikidata + press density + peer-reviewed pubs = gold. Steve's portfolio is already gold; activate it. Authority compounds; shortcuts destroy. No astroturfing.

skills.name: Ghl Affiliate Manager
skills.description: Manage Matrix's affiliate program in GHL (10% → 12% → 15% tiered commission). Affiliate onboarding, code generation (MAS-{FNAME}-{4RAND}), tier tracking, monthly payouts, performance reports, payout disputes. Drafts; never auto-pays.
skills.intent: Run the affiliate program operationally. Sign up new affiliates, assign codes, track performance per affiliate, calculate monthly commission accruals, prep payout packets for finance, surface high-performers + slow movers. Chris approves payouts.
skills.estimated_time: 30-60 min monthly; 5-10 min per signup
skills.body_html:
GHL Affiliate Manager

The affiliate program operator. Matrix has tiered 10/12/15% commission. This skill runs it.

When to use

  • New affiliate signing up
  • Monthly commission accrual + payout prep
  • Affiliate performance review
  • Tier upgrade decision
  • Payout dispute investigation

When NOT to use

  • Building affiliate program from scratch (use offer-positioning + Chris decisions)
  • Paying affiliates (manual via Stripe / banking — Chris approves)
  • Recruiting affiliates (use recruiting if scaling that)

Required inputs

  1. Operation — signup / monthly review / payout prep / tier upgrade / dispute
  2. Affiliate / period — specific affiliate or month/quarter

If signup, ask: "Name, email, clinic/business, recommended tier (default tier 1: 10%)"

Matrix affiliate program structure

  • Tier 1: 10% commission — default for new affiliates
  • Tier 2: 12% commission — upgrade at $5,000 lifetime referred revenue
  • Tier 3: 15% commission — upgrade at $25,000 lifetime referred revenue
  • Code format: MAS-{FNAME}-{4RAND} (auto-generated)
  • Attribution: 90-day cookie + code-based fallback
  • Payout cadence: monthly, on the 15th, for prior month's accrued commissions
  • Minimum payout: $50 (rolls over if under)
  • Payment method: Stripe transfers or check
  • 1099 reporting: required for US affiliates earning $600+/year

Workflow per operation

A. Affiliate signup

  1. Verify applicant: real clinic / valid business
  2. Generate unique code: MAS-{FNAME-upper}-{4-rand-alphanumeric}
  3. Add to GHL contact with tags: affiliate, affiliate:tier1, source:affiliate-program
  4. Custom fields: affiliate_code, affiliate_tier, affiliate_start_date, lifetime_referred_revenue: 0
  5. Send welcome email with: their code, how to use it, dashboard link (if any), commission structure, payout cadence
  6. Add to monthly affiliate update sequence (newsletter)
  7. Update _Logs/affiliate/<code>.md registry

B. Monthly commission accrual

For each affiliate, for the period:

  • Sum referred revenue (from contacts tagged with affiliate code)
  • Apply tier % to net revenue (after Stripe fees, after refunds)
  • Calculate this-month commission
  • Calculate lifetime referred revenue (cumulative)
  • Check tier-upgrade thresholds — propose upgrade if crossed
  • Subtract minimum payout if not reached (rolls over)

Output: a monthly-affiliate-commissions-<YYYY-MM>.csv for finance.

C. Payout prep

For each affiliate ≥ $50 commission this month:

  • Affiliate name + email
  • Code
  • Tier
  • Referred revenue this month
  • Commission $
  • Payment method on file
  • Tax form on file? (W-9 for US, W-8BEN for non-US)
  • Notes (if any disputes / hold)

Hand to bookkeeping + Chris for review + execution.

D. Tier upgrade decision

When affiliate crosses threshold:

  • Generate upgrade notification email
  • Update tier in GHL
  • Update commission going forward (NOT retroactive — current month uses new tier)
  • Welcome to new tier with benefits clarification

E. Affiliate performance review (monthly)

Surface:

  • Top 10 affiliates by referred revenue
  • Affiliates with 0 referrals 90+ days (stale)
  • Affiliates trending up / down
  • Avg referred deal size per affiliate
  • Conversion rate (referred contact → closed deal) per affiliate

For stale: propose re-engage email (or remove if inactive 12+ months). For top performers: propose tier upgrade if not yet, or special recognition / case study.

F. Dispute investigation

If affiliate disputes a commission:

  1. Pull all contacts attributed to their code in the disputed period
  2. Verify attribution (cookie + code consistent)
  3. Verify revenue ($ amount net of fees + refunds)
  4. Verify tier % applied correctly
  5. Surface findings; defer to Chris for resolution

Output structure

# Affiliate Manager: <Operation> — <Period or Affiliate>

## Operation: <signup / monthly review / payout prep / upgrade / dispute>

## Results

### If Signup
**New affiliate**: <name>
**Code generated**: `<code>`
**Tier**: 1 (10%)
**Welcomed**: ✅ email sent
**Logged**: `_Logs/affiliate/<code>.md`

### If Monthly Review
**Period**: <YYYY-MM>
**Active affiliates**: <N>
**Total referred revenue this month**: $<X>
**Total commission accrued**: $<Y>

**Payouts ≥ $50 threshold**:
| Affiliate | Code | Tier | Revenue | Commission | Payment method | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

**Tier upgrades proposed**:
| Affiliate | From tier | To tier | Threshold crossed |
|---|---|---|---|

**Stale (90+d no referral)**:
| Affiliate | Last referral | Action |
|---|---|---|

### If Payout Prep
**File**: `_Logs/affiliate/<YYYY-MM>-payout-packet.csv`
**Total to pay**: $<X>
**Affiliate count**: <N>
**Awaiting tax form**: <N>

### If Dispute
**Affiliate**: <name>
**Disputed period**: <period>
**Their claim**: <claim>
**My finding**: <finding with evidence>
**Recommendation**: <action>

## ✅ I handled
- (per operation)

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve tier upgrades
- Approve payout list before any disbursement
- Authorize re-engage of stale affiliates
- Resolve disputes

## 🚫 You only
- Execute payouts (Stripe transfer / check)
- Approve any change to commission structure
- Approve removal of an affiliate from program
- Sign 1099 forms (year-end)

## Handoff
**To**: `bookkeeping` (commission as liability + payouts as expense), `cfo-finance` (affiliate cost in margin), Chris (payout authorization)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-affiliate-<operation>.md`
`_Logs/affiliate/<YYYY-MM>-summary.md`

Continuous improvement

Quarterly: review program health via ghl-continuous-improvement rollup.

Pattern Action
Top 10% of affiliates drive > 80% of referrals Focus retention + tier upgrade on them
Many sign-ups, low activation rate (< 20% referring within 30d) Improve affiliate onboarding
Tier 1 stalls — no movement to Tier 2 Lower Tier 2 threshold OR boost Tier 1 incentive
High commission cost relative to LTV Renegotiate or restructure

Anti-patterns

  • Auto-paying without Chris approval — never
  • Retroactive tier changes — current month uses new tier, not back-applied
  • Ignoring stale affiliates — they're costing nothing but creating noise
  • No 1099 tracking — IRS exposure for high-earning affiliates
  • No fraud detection — affiliates self-referring own purchases

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Affiliate signup + code generation
  • ✅ Add to GHL tags / custom fields
  • ✅ Send welcome + monthly newsletters
  • ✅ Calculate accruals + draft payout packets
  • ✅ Propose tier upgrades
  • ⚠️ Apply tier upgrade in GHL (Chris approves)
  • ⚠️ Send dispute response (Chris reviews)
  • 🚫 Execute payouts
  • 🚫 Issue 1099s
  • 🚫 Remove affiliate from program without approval

Pairing

Inputs from: GHL contacts_* (tagged affiliates) + payments_list-transactions (referred revenue), bookkeeping (financial reconciliation) Outputs to: bookkeeping, cfo-finance, Chris (payout authorization) Chains with: ghl-continuous-improvement (quarterly program review), weekly-review (affiliate KPIs roll up)

One-liner reminder

Tier 1/2/3 at 10/12/15%. Monthly accrual + payout prep. Never auto-pay. 1099 tracking. Stale affiliate re-engage. Quarterly health review.

skills.name: Ghl Continuous Improvement
skills.description: Weekly review of all GHL surfaces — pipelines, workflows, conversations, forms, contact data, automations. Surfaces patterns, proposes specific changes, ships through ghl-eval-harness. The continuous-improvement engine for the GHL Department (mirror of voice-call-reviewer for the rest of GHL).
skills.intent: Stop letting GHL config drift. Each week, pull pipeline data + workflow performance + conversation outcomes + form conversion + contact-health signals. Find what's working, what's broken, what's stale. Propose specific, evidence-based changes. Block changes that fail eval.
best_for: After any major workflow change — measure impact
skills.estimated_time: 45-90 min weekly; 2-3 hours quarterly
skills.body_html:
GHL Continuous Improvement

The improvement engine for the GHL Department. Weekly. Specific. Evidence-driven.

When to use

  • Weekly review (auto-scheduled Friday 3:30pm — right after voice-call-reviewer)
  • Post-campaign GHL analysis
  • Quarterly deep audit
  • Anomaly investigation when KPI drops

Matrix config reference (load first)

Always load _Shared Context/ghl-config.md first. Baseline state of Matrix's GHL:

  • 6-stage MAS Order Lifecycle pipeline with SLAs
  • 7 production workflows (with triggers, email/SMS templates, expected outcomes)
  • Tag taxonomy with strict prefixes
  • 5 A2P 10DLC campaigns (currently PENDING — SMS workflows silently fail)
  • Mack voice agent + calendar wiring
  • Website connector gaps (privacy/terms 404, chat widget not installed)

Improvement proposals reference what's documented vs reinventing.

When NOT to use

  • Voice agent review (use voice-call-reviewer)
  • Single-record debug (call the MCP directly)
  • One-off workflow build (use ghl-crm-architect + workflow-automation-builder)

Required inputs

  1. Location ID — which Matrix GHL sub-account
  2. Period — last 7 days / 30 days
  3. Focus (optional) — pipelines / workflows / conversations / forms / all

If 1 missing for multi-tenant agencies, ask: "Which location?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Pull surfaces in parallel

Pipelines (via opportunities_*):

  • All active pipelines + stages
  • Opportunities in each stage
  • Days-in-stage distribution
  • Stage transitions this period
  • Win/lose ratios per pipeline

Workflows (via list):

  • All active automations
  • Trigger fire counts
  • Step completion rates per workflow
  • Step failure rates
  • Workflows that haven't fired in 30+ days (stale)

Conversations (via conversations_*):

  • Volume by channel (email / SMS / chat / FB / IG / voice)
  • Response time distributions
  • Unanswered conversations (> 4h, > 24h, > 7d)
  • Sentiment signals (from recent inbound)

Forms / Surveys:

  • Form fill rate (impressions → submits)
  • Drop-off field per form
  • Conversion to next-stage rate

Contact data:

  • New contacts (24h, 7d)
  • Tag distribution
  • Stale tags (not used in 90 days)
  • Custom field completeness per contact
  • Duplicate contacts

Calendar:

  • Bookings + no-shows per calendar
  • Reschedule rate
  • Time-to-first-booking from first touch

Step 2 — Pattern detection (the evidence layer)

For each surface, classify what's good / broken / stale:

Surface Healthy At-risk Critical
Pipeline stage avg days-in-stage Within SLA 1.5× SLA 2× SLA + opportunity stuck
Workflow trigger rate Stable Dropped 30% Hasn't fired in 30+ days
Conversation response time < 4h business hours 4-24h > 24h to inbound
Form conversion rate Stable / improving -10% vs prior -25%+ vs prior
Contact tag noise < 20 tags per contact avg 20-40 > 40 tag bloat

Step 3 — Generate improvement proposals

For each pattern, propose specific change:

Issue Evidence Proposal Owner skill Priority
Opportunity stuck at "Discovery Scheduled" 14d avg (SLA = 5d) 23 deals affected Add T+5d auto-nudge SMS via workflow workflow-automation-builder High
Workflow "Welcome Sequence v1" hasn't fired in 45 days 0 fires; old trigger Either re-wire trigger or archive workflow-automation-builder + ghl-workflow-auditor Medium
Catalog Request form: 67% drop on field 4 (NPI) 200 form views, 134 dropped at field 4 Move NPI to post-submission credentialing flow ghl-form-builder High
Tag bloat: 47 contacts with urgent-2024-old-campaign (unused) 47 contacts, no workflow uses tag Bulk untag (with approval) ghl-crm-architect Low
Conversation response time: SMS avg 18h (target 4h) 89 SMS inbound, 12 responded < 4h Wire auto-reply with "we received you, AM follows up in business hours" ghl-conversation-analytics + workflow-automation-builder High

Step 4 — Validate via ghl-eval-harness

For each proposed workflow change, run through eval before deploy. Catches regressions early.

Step 5 — Approval gate (Chris)

Surface the report. Chris approves which proposals ship this cycle (typically top 3-5).

Step 6 — Execute

Hand approved proposals to workflow-automation-builder (additive ops auto, refactors approved).

Step 7 — Monitor first week post-change

If any KPI drops sharply post-change → auto-pause + rollback.

Output structure

# GHL Improvement Review: <Period>

## Summary
- **Location**: <id>
- **Period**: <range>
- **Status**: <🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 overall>

## Pipeline health
| Pipeline | Active opps | Avg cycle | Win rate | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## Stage dropoff (worst-performing stages)
| Pipeline → Stage | Avg days | SLA | Deals stuck | Pattern |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## Workflow performance
- Active workflows: <N>
- Fired this period: <N>
- Stale workflows (30d+ no fire): <N>
- Failed steps: <N>

| Workflow | Fires | Success rate | Avg duration | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## Conversations
| Channel | Volume | Avg response | Unanswered > 24h | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## Forms
| Form | Views | Submissions | Conversion | Drop field |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## Contact data hygiene
- New contacts (7d): <N>
- Duplicate contacts identified: <N>
- Stale tags (no use 90d): <N>
- Custom field completeness avg: <%>

## 🛠 Improvement proposals
| # | Issue | Evidence | Proposal | Owner skill | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | <issue> | <evidence> | <change> | <skill> | <High/Med/Low> |

## ✅ I handled
- Pulled all GHL surfaces
- Pattern detection + classification
- Specific change proposals with evidence

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve which proposals ship this cycle (top 3-5)
- Approve any bulk operations (tag cleanup, duplicate merge)
- Defer / kill any proposals not worth pursuing

## 🚫 You only
- Approve destructive cleanups (delete workflows, merge contacts)
- Approve any pricing / billing config changes

## Handoff
**To** (per approved proposals):
- `workflow-automation-builder` (for workflow changes)
- `ghl-crm-architect` (for structural changes)
- `ghl-conversation-analytics` (for conversation-specific patterns)
- `ghl-pipeline-optimizer` (for pipeline-specific patterns)
- `ghl-form-builder` (for form-specific changes)
- `ghl-eval-harness` (regression check before ship)

## Logged to
`_Logs/campaign-results/ghl/<location>/<date>-review.md`
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-ghl-improvement.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Vibes-based "the CRM feels slow" — must cite specific evidence
  • Auto-deploying changes without eval pass
  • Cleaning up stale workflows without checking dependencies first
  • Tag deletion without bulk-untag step — orphans contacts
  • Single weekly proposal of "everything" — focus on 3-5 highest-leverage

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull GHL data (read-only)
  • ✅ Classify patterns
  • ✅ Generate proposals
  • ✅ Run ghl-eval-harness on proposed changes
  • ⚠️ Approve proposals to ship (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Bulk operations (tag, duplicate merge) — Chris approves
  • 🚫 Auto-deploy changes without approval
  • 🚫 Delete workflows / contacts / data

Pairing

Inputs from: GHL MCP (all data sources), ghl-daily-pulse (daily signals), voice-call-reviewer (voice patterns overlap) Outputs to: workflow-automation-builder, ghl-crm-architect, ghl-conversation-analytics, ghl-pipeline-optimizer, ghl-form-builder, ghl-eval-harness Chains with: ghl-continuous-improvement.workflow.md (the weekly cycle), weekly-review (rolls up)

Scheduled automation

Recommended cron via automation-scheduler:

  • Weekly Friday 3:30pm: run for primary Matrix location → output to _Logs/campaign-results/ghl/

One-liner reminder

Pull surfaces. Classify. Propose with evidence. Eval before deploy. Approve top 3-5 per cycle. Monitor first week. Mirror of voice-call-reviewer for GHL.

skills.name: Ghl Conversation Analytics
skills.description: Analyzes ALL GHL conversations (email, SMS, web chat, FB DM, IG DM, voice transcripts) — response times, sentiment, intent classification, dropoff stages, agent vs human performance. Surfaces patterns that no single-channel review would catch. Companion to voice-call-reviewer for non-voice channels.
skills.intent: Stop treating each channel's conversations in isolation. GHL holds all channels in one place — analyze them together. Surface cross-channel patterns: when does a customer touch turn into a deal, which channel resolves fastest, where do conversations stall, which AI agents perform best.
best_for: Weekly cross-channel conversation review (part of ghl-continuous-improvement)
skills.body_html:
GHL Conversation Analytics

The cross-channel conversation analyzer. Goes beyond voice-call-reviewer to cover SMS / email / chat / DMs.

When to use

  • Weekly cross-channel review (slot in ghl-continuous-improvement)
  • Specific channel deep-dive
  • Agent performance audit (AI vs human; which AI agent wins which conversation types)
  • Sentiment trend tracking (any decline → 🔴)
  • Post-campaign — did the conversations work?

When NOT to use

  • Voice-only review (use voice-call-reviewer)
  • Single-conversation debug (just read it directly)
  • Outbound campaign performance (use the campaign skill's review)

Required inputs

  1. Location ID
  2. Period — 7d / 30d / specific window
  3. Channels — all / specific list (SMS / email / web chat / DM)
  4. Filters (optional) — by tag / pipeline / agent / etc.

If 1 missing, ask which location.

Workflow

Step 1 — Pull conversations via conversations_*

For each conversation in scope:

  • Channel
  • Direction (inbound / outbound)
  • Initial message timestamp
  • Reply timestamps (each side)
  • Resolution (booked / catalog sent / transferred / closed / abandoned)
  • Outcome tag
  • Agent / human handler

Step 2 — Compute response time metrics

Metric Definition
First-response time (FRT) Time from inbound → first outbound reply
Time-to-resolution (TTR) Time from inbound → outcome tag applied
Inbound-to-action ratio % of inbound that resulted in any agent action
Idle abandonment % of conversations agent abandoned > 24h

Compute distributions: median, p75, p90, p99.

Step 3 — Classify intent + outcome

Sample 30-50 conversations per channel. Classify intent:

Common intents (Matrix):

  • Catalog request
  • Credentialing question
  • Order status
  • Demo / strategy call request
  • Product question
  • Pricing question
  • Complaint
  • Affiliate inquiry
  • Hostile / spam

Outcomes:

  • Resolved (autonomously by agent)
  • Transferred to human (with outcome of human follow-up)
  • Booked meeting / call
  • Catalog sent
  • Closed without action
  • Abandoned (no outcome reached)
  • Escalated (complaint / hostile)

Build the intent × outcome matrix per channel.

Step 4 — Sentiment signal (lightweight)

Classify outbound + inbound for sentiment:

  • Positive (engaged, satisfied)
  • Neutral (transactional)
  • Negative (frustrated, confused, hostile)

Track trends. Sudden sentiment shift = alert.

Step 5 — Agent performance comparison

For each AI agent (Mia / Alex / Sam / human-AM):

  • Volume handled
  • Avg FRT + TTR
  • Resolution rate (autonomous)
  • Transfer rate
  • Customer satisfaction (post-conv survey)
  • Cost per conversation

Surface: which agent wins which intent?

Step 6 — Cross-channel patterns

Look for:

  • Channel hopping — customer started on chat → switched to email → escalated to voice (handoff orchestrator working?)
  • Channel preference by ICP — clinic owners → voice; admins → email; younger providers → SMS
  • Response-time-by-channel — which is fastest / slowest?
  • Time-of-day patterns — when do most inbounds hit, when do we miss SLA?

Step 7 — Generate proposals

For each pattern, specific change:

Issue Evidence Proposal Owner skill
SMS FRT avg 18h (target 4h) 89 inbound, 12 responded < 4h Wire auto-acknowledgment SMS via workflow workflow-automation-builder
23% of chat conversations end on "we'll have someone call you" — but no callback fires 47 chats this period Auto-fire callback task assigned to AM after chat ends ghl-crm-architect + workflow
Mia voice agent: 67% resolution on catalog requests / Alex: 81% Same intent type, Alex performs better Re-evaluate Mia's catalog-request flow; clone Alex's pattern voice-script-author
Negative sentiment up 15% on SMS since [date] 23 negative inbound (vs baseline 9) Investigate; likely tied to [recent campaign / event] qualitative deep-dive

Step 8 — Output

Output structure

# GHL Conversation Analytics: <Period>

## Volume
| Channel | Inbound | Outbound | Total |
|---|---|---|---|
| SMS | <N> | <N> | <N> |
| Email | <N> | <N> | <N> |
| Web chat | <N> | <N> | <N> |
| FB DM | <N> | <N> | <N> |
| IG DM | <N> | <N> | <N> |
| Voice transcript | <N> | <N> | <N> |

## Response time (median / p75 / p90 / p99)
| Channel | FRT median | FRT p90 | TTR median | TTR p90 |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## Intent × Outcome matrix (per channel)

### SMS
| Intent | Resolved | Transferred | Booked | Abandoned | Escalated |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

(Repeat per channel)

## Sentiment trend
- Positive: <%> (prior <%>)
- Neutral: <%>
- Negative: <%>

⚠️ Alerts: <any sharp shifts>

## Agent performance
| Agent | Channels | Volume | FRT med | Resolution % | Transfer % | CSAT |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mia (voice) | voice | <N> | — | <%> | <%> | <X>/5 |
| Alex (voice) | voice | <N> | — | <%> | <%> | <X>/5 |
| AI chat | web | <N> | <sec> | <%> | <%> | <X>/5 |
| Human AM | all | <N> | <hr> | <%> | n/a | <X>/5 |

## Cross-channel patterns
- <pattern with evidence>
- <pattern with evidence>

## 🛠 Improvement proposals
(Per Step 7 table)

## ✅ I handled
- Pulled all conversations
- Computed metrics
- Classified intent + outcome
- Sentiment signal
- Agent comparison

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve proposals to ship via ghl-continuous-improvement
- Approve any agent-specific changes (script tweaks per voice-call-reviewer or here)

## 🚫 You only
- Decide to retire / replace an underperforming agent
- Investigate any conversation flagged hostile / litigious

## Handoff
**To**: `ghl-continuous-improvement` (rolls up findings), `voice-call-reviewer` (for voice-channel deep dive), `voice-script-author` (for script changes), `workflow-automation-builder` (for response-time wiring)

## Logged to
`_Logs/campaign-results/ghl-conversations/<date>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Aggregating across channels without separation — each has different cadence
  • Sentiment classification without sampling for accuracy — use a sample-validate loop quarterly
  • Comparing AI agents to humans on volume — humans handle harder cases; compare on resolution rate of comparable intent
  • Ignoring time-of-day patterns — SLA misses cluster around shift transitions
  • No specific proposal per pattern — surfacing without action is noise

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull all GHL conversations (read-only)
  • ✅ Classify + analyze
  • ✅ Generate reports
  • ⚠️ Approve specific change proposals (handoff)
  • 🚫 Modify any conversation / contact directly

Pairing

Inputs from: GHL conversations_*, conversations_get-messages, conversations_search-conversation Outputs to: ghl-continuous-improvement (rolls up), voice-call-reviewer (voice-specific deep dive), specific script/flow agents for improvements Chains with: ghl-continuous-improvement.workflow.md, weekly-review

One-liner reminder

All channels. Response time + intent + outcome + sentiment + agent. Cross-channel patterns. Specific evidence-based proposals. Hand to ghl-continuous-improvement for ship decisions.

skills.name: Ghl Crm Architect
skills.description: Design or refactor a GoHighLevel sub-account architecture — pipelines, opportunity stages, contact fields, tags, calendars, conversations, automations. Outputs a CRM build map + the workflow steps to execute. Wraps the gohighlevel MCP.
skills.intent: Be the architect for GHL CRM structure — not the operator. Designs pipelines, stages, fields, tags, calendars, and the automation graph. Outputs a build map a workflow-automation agent (or Chris) can execute. Always inspection-first on existing accounts; never overwrites without approval.
best_for: Pipeline / stage refactor on existing account
Adding a new offer's funnel + automation to existing CRM
skills.estimated_time: 30-90 min for full design; 15 min for audit
skills.body_html:
GHL CRM Architect

The structural designer for GoHighLevel sub-accounts. Designs the system. Doesn't execute mutations without approval.

When to use

  • Setting up a new GHL sub-account
  • Refactoring a messy existing account
  • Adding a new offer's funnel to existing CRM
  • Pre-launch: building the pipeline + automations for a campaign
  • Audit: spotting structural issues in an existing account

When NOT to use

  • Daily CRM operations (use ghl-daily-pulse)
  • Single-contact lookup or update (call the MCP directly)
  • Conversation-level reply drafting (use email-triage for inbox-style work)

Required setup

  • mcp__gohighlevel__* MCP connected
  • Location / sub-account ID identified
  • Goals stated: what offers + funnels live here?

Required inputs

  1. Account context: location ID, agency-owned or single-location, current state (new / existing)
  2. Offers it serves — pull from _Shared Context/offers.md or user-provided list
  3. ICP / segments — from market-research / offer-positioning if available
  4. Channels in use — email, SMS, voice, chat, social, ads
  5. Existing assets (for refactor) — current pipelines, automations, contact count
  6. Compliance constraints — HIPAA flag, A2P 10DLC status, consent flow status

If 1-2 missing, ask: "Is this a new account or existing? And which offers / funnels need to live in it?"

Matrix config reference

Always load _Shared Context/ghl-config.md first. It contains the authoritative current state:

  • Location ID, contact emails, hours
  • Pipeline (MAS Order Lifecycle) + stage IDs + colors
  • Custom fields (Order ID, Stripe Payment Intent, Lab Slug, Payment Terms, Affiliate Code, Total, Order Items)
  • Tag taxonomy with strict prefixes
  • Calendar IDs (Mack-linked discovery call)
  • Lab partner slugs (nugen / ram / florida / kaduceus)
  • 7 production workflows + email/SMS template IDs
  • A2P 10DLC campaign registrations
  • Website connector readiness

Don't re-derive what's already documented. Reference + extend.

Workflow

Step 1 — Inspection (always first)

If existing account:

Read via gohighlevel MCP:
- opportunities_get-pipelines (list all pipelines + stages)
- locations_get-custom-fields (all contact custom fields)
- locations_get-location (account meta + features enabled)
- contacts_get-contacts (sample to understand tag patterns)
- list calendars
- list conversations (sample for channel mix)
- list workflows/automations (if accessible)

Produce a current-state map: pipelines, stages, fields, tags, calendars, conversation channels, automations, custom fields. Flag inconsistencies.

If new account: skip to Step 2.

Step 2 — Pipeline design (per offer)

For each offer in scope, design a pipeline:

Standard B2B sales pipeline (default stages):

  1. MQL — captured, no contact yet
  2. Contacted — first reach made
  3. Engaged — replied / showed interest
  4. Discovery scheduled — call booked
  5. Discovery completed — call held, qualified
  6. Proposal sent
  7. Negotiation
  8. Closed Won
  9. Closed Lost (with sub-tags for reason)

Adjust per offer:

  • Self-serve / freemium: shorter (Trial Started → Active → Paid → Churned)
  • High-ticket: longer (more discovery / multi-stakeholder stages)
  • E-commerce: different (Cart → Checkout → Customer → Repeat)
  • Service / coaching: different (Application → Discovery → Enrolled → In-program → Graduated)

For each stage, define:

  • Entry criteria (when does a contact enter this stage?)
  • Exit criteria (when do they advance?)
  • SLA (max days in stage before flag)
  • Owner (who's responsible)
  • Required actions per stage

Step 3 — Contact fields & tags

Custom fields (per ICP):

  • Demographic: industry, company size, role, geography
  • Behavioral: source, first-touch, last-touch, lead score
  • Operational: account manager, renewal date, MRR
  • Compliance: opted in (SMS), opted in (email), consent dates

Tag hierarchy (use prefixes for clarity):

  • source:<value> (e.g., source:linkedin, source:ad-meta, source:referral)
  • stage:<value> (mirrors pipeline stage for cross-pipeline reporting)
  • segment:<value> (ICP segment)
  • interest:<value> (offer or topic)
  • status:<value> (active, churned, paused)
  • do-not-contact (for unsubscribes / TCPA)

Avoid: free-form tags ("hot lead", "important"), redundant tags, tag bloat.

Step 4 — Calendars

For each call type:

  • Discovery call (30 min, fits Chris's calendar, includes prep prompt)
  • Demo (45 min)
  • Onboarding (60 min, post-purchase)
  • Customer check-in (15-30 min, recurring)

Each calendar:

  • Availability window
  • Buffer time
  • Required form fields before booking
  • Confirmation + reminder sequence
  • No-show recovery automation

Step 5 — Conversation channels

Map each channel:

  • Email: domain, deliverability setup (SPF/DKIM/DMARC), sender identity
  • SMS: A2P 10DLC registered? consent flow in place? quiet hours configured?
  • Web chat: hours, fallback to email after-hours
  • FB / IG DM: who owns? response SLA?
  • Voice: AI handler? routing? voicemail strategy?

Compliance check per channel:

  • SMS: opt-in proof + STOP/HELP keywords + sender ID required
  • Email: unsubscribe link + physical address + accurate from-line
  • Voice: TCPA-compliant for outbound? consent for recording?

Step 6 — Automation graph

Sketch the workflow logic (not the GHL workflow builder syntax — that's a downstream operator's job).

For each automation:

  • Trigger: what event starts it
  • Conditions: filters before action
  • Actions: tasks created, fields updated, messages sent, stage moved
  • Wait steps: delays, business-hours gates
  • Exit conditions: when to stop / branch
  • Failure path: what happens if a step fails

Common automations to design (per offer):

  • New contact welcome
  • Lead-magnet delivery
  • Cold sequence (handed off to cold-email-sequence skill for copy)
  • Discovery-call booking confirmation + reminders
  • Discovery-call no-show recovery
  • Post-purchase onboarding
  • Renewal reminder
  • Churn-risk save flow

Step 7 — Output the Build Map

Produce a CRM Build Map (the deliverable) — this is the spec a workflow-automation agent or human admin can execute.

Step 8 — Approval gate before any mutations

Never execute pipeline/stage/field deletions or restructures without Chris approval. Always show the proposed changes + impact before committing.

For ✅ safe operations (per approval-rules.md):

  • Read all data
  • Add new fields, tags, pipelines, stages (additive)
  • Update tag / field VALUES on contacts (not field DEFINITIONS)

For ⚠️ approval-required:

  • Restructure existing pipelines (move/rename stages)
  • Delete fields or tags
  • Merge contacts
  • Modify access controls / users

For 🚫 never:

  • Delete contact data
  • Modify payment / billing config without explicit Chris + approval

Output structure

# GHL CRM Build Map: <Account / Project Name>

## Account context
- **Location ID**: <id>
- **State**: <new / existing-refactor / existing-extension>
- **Offers in scope**: <list>
- **Compliance flags**: <HIPAA / A2P / GDPR / etc.>

## Current state (if refactor)
- **Pipelines existing**: <list>
- **Custom fields existing**: <count + categories>
- **Tags existing**: <count + patterns>
- **Issues spotted**: <bullets>

## Proposed pipelines

### Pipeline 1: <Offer Name> Sales
| Stage # | Name | Entry criteria | Exit criteria | SLA | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | MQL | <criteria> | <criteria> | 3 days | <owner> |
| 2 | ... | | | | |

[Repeat per pipeline]

## Custom fields

### Demographic
- `industry` (dropdown: <values>)
- `company_size` (dropdown)
- `role` (text)
- ...

### Behavioral
- `lead_source` (dropdown)
- `lead_score` (number)
- `first_touch_at` (date)
- ...

### Compliance
- `email_consent` (boolean)
- `sms_consent` (boolean)
- `consent_date` (date)
- ...

## Tag hierarchy
- `source:linkedin` / `source:ad-meta` / `source:referral` / ...
- `stage:mql` / `stage:contacted` / ...
- `segment:<ICP-segment>` / ...
- `interest:<offer-slug>` / ...
- `status:active` / `status:churned` / ...
- `do-not-contact`

## Calendars

| Calendar | Length | Buffer | Required form fields | Reminders |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Discovery | 30 min | 15 min | Name, company, role, pain | T-24h email + T-1h SMS |
| Demo | 45 min | 15 min | + tech stack, team size | T-24h + T-1h |
| Onboarding | 60 min | 0 | + access to systems | T-24h + day-of |

## Conversation channels

| Channel | Status | Compliance | Owner / handler |
|---|---|---|---|
| Email | active | SPF/DKIM/DMARC ✓ | Chris / SDR |
| SMS | active | A2P 10DLC ✓ + opt-in flow ✓ | SDR |
| Web chat | active | hours: 9-5 PT | AI handler + Chris escalation |
| Voice | planned | TCPA check needed | Retell AI agent + Chris closer |

## Automations (logic — not GHL syntax)

### A1: New contact welcome
- **Trigger**: contact created with `source:` tag
- **Conditions**: `email_consent = true`
- **Actions**:
  1. Send welcome email (Cold Email Sequence skill drafts the copy)
  2. Wait 1 day
  3. Add to `interest:<inferred>` based on source page
  4. Move to pipeline stage `MQL`
- **Exit**: when stage moves to `Contacted`
- **Failure path**: log to admin

[Repeat per automation]

## Compliance checklist
- [ ] A2P 10DLC registration complete (if SMS)
- [ ] SPF/DKIM/DMARC verified on sending domain
- [ ] Unsubscribe link in every email template
- [ ] STOP/HELP keywords working on all SMS
- [ ] Consent capture in every form
- [ ] Privacy policy link on every form
- [ ] HIPAA-specific: no PHI in tags / fields / conversations (if applicable)

## Migration plan (if refactor)
1. Snapshot current state (export contacts + pipelines)
2. Build new structure in parallel (don't delete first)
3. Tag-based migration of contacts
4. Validate sample of 20 contacts post-migration
5. Cut over conversations & automations
6. Decommission old structures after 14-day stability window

## ✅ I'll handle (autonomous, after Chris approves the Build Map)
- Add new fields, tags, pipelines, stages
- Update existing contact tag/field VALUES (per migration plan)
- Read all data
- Generate workflow logic specs

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve the Build Map (this document)
- Approve pricing of any tiers if reflected in CRM
- Approve restructure / deletion of any existing pipelines
- Approve A2P 10DLC registration submission (separate brand approval)

## 🚫 You only
- Sign the A2P 10DLC brand registration
- Approve any HIPAA-bearing accounts before going live
- Approve charging customer cards (if Stripe is wired here)

## Handoff (use _Templates/handoff-template.md)
**To**:
- Workflow Automation Agent (planned) — to build the automations in GHL
- Cold Email Sequence / SMS Campaign — to populate copy in templates
- Compliance QA — to verify pre-launch

## Risks surfaced
- <risk: e.g., existing contact tags are inconsistent — migration will surface duplicates>
- <risk: e.g., A2P 10DLC not yet registered — SMS automations can't go live yet>

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-ghl-crm-architect-<account>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Building before inspecting existing accounts → wipes / collides with current data
  • Tag bloat — free-form tags accumulate; use prefixed hierarchy from day 1
  • No SLA per stage → contacts rot in stage
  • Missing exit criteria → unclear when contact advances
  • No compliance checklist before SMS/email goes live → TCPA / CAN-SPAM exposure
  • Auto-executing structural changes — always approval first
  • Single-pipeline-for-everything — one pipeline per offer (most accounts do this wrong)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ All MCP reads (pipelines, fields, contacts, conversations, calendars)
  • ✅ Add new fields / tags / pipelines / stages (additive only)
  • ✅ Update tag/field VALUES on contacts per documented migration plan
  • ⚠️ Restructure / delete existing pipelines / fields / stages → Chris approves
  • ⚠️ Merge contacts → Chris approves
  • 🚫 Delete contacts → never auto

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • offer-positioning (which offers + their funnels)
  • market-research (ICP — drives field design)
  • _Shared Context/offers.md
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md

Outputs to:

  • Workflow Automation Agent (planned) — executes the build in GHL
  • cold-email-sequence / SMS / Voice agents — populate copy
  • ghl-daily-pulse — operates over the resulting structure
  • Compliance QA — pre-launch verification

Related GHL MCP tools used:

  • opportunities_get-pipelines
  • locations_get-custom-fields
  • locations_get-location
  • contacts_*
  • calendars_get-calendar-events
  • conversations_*
  • social-media-posting_*

One-liner reminder

Inspect existing first. Tag hierarchy with prefixes. SLA per stage. Compliance checklist before live. Additive ops auto. Restructure ops always ⚠️.

skills.name: Ghl Daily Pulse
skills.description: Pull a daily GoHighLevel snapshot — new contacts (24h), hot opportunities, today's calendar events, unanswered conversations, recent payments. Outputs actionable items with the 3-lane delegation matrix. Use as part of prep-day or on-demand for CRM check-ins.
skills.body_html:
GoHighLevel Daily Pulse

A 30-second CRM scan that surfaces only what needs attention today.

Purpose

GHL has a lot of surface area (contacts, conversations, opportunities, calendars, social, payments). This skill filters to the 5-10% that matters today.

When to use

  • As a component of prep-day (default)
  • Before a sales day to surface hot deals
  • After a campaign to see signal
  • Weekly review to spot stale opportunities

When NOT to use

  • Deep pipeline analysis (use opportunities_search-opportunity directly or a dedicated CRM review skill)
  • Single-contact lookup (just call contacts_get-contact directly)

Required setup

  • GoHighLevel MCP connected (mcp__gohighlevel__*)
  • Location ID known (single-location user) OR list to pick from (agency user)
  • Pipeline IDs identified (sales / lead-gen / customer-success / etc.)

If MCP not connected: stop, output the connection message, exit.

Workflow

Step 1 — Identify scope

  • Location:
  • Pipelines:
  • Time window: last 24h for "new", last 7d for "hot opportunities aging"

Step 2 — Parallel queries

Run these in parallel (when MCP supports parallel calls):

  1. New contacts (last 24h):

    gohighlevel.contacts_get-contacts (filter by createdAt >= 24h ago)

    Pull name, source, tags, any custom field signals.

  2. Hot opportunities (aging in current stage OR stage-changed today):

    gohighlevel.opportunities_search-opportunity
- filter by lastStageChangeAt >= today OR (currentStageDaysIn > X stage-specific threshold)

  3. Today's calendar events:

    gohighlevel.calendars_get-calendar-events (date range = today)

  4. Unanswered conversations:

    gohighlevel.conversations_search-conversation (unread=true OR lastInboundAt without reply > 4h)

  5. Recent payments / orders (last 24h):

    gohighlevel.payments_list-transactions

    New revenue or refunds — both signals.

  6. Social posting status:

    gohighlevel.social-media-posting_get-posts (scheduled today + posted today)

Step 3 — Apply prioritization

For each item, classify:

  • Hot: requires action today (stalled opportunity, unanswered customer message, missed payment)
  • Warm: requires action this week
  • FYI: trend signal only (new contact volume, social engagement)

Step 4 — Output with delegation

Output structure

# GHL Pulse — <Day, Month DD HH:MM>

**Location**: <name>
**Pipelines scanned**: <names>
**Status**: <connected, X calls run>

---

## 🔥 Hot today

### Opportunities aging
| Name | Stage | Days in stage | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| <opportunity> | <stage> | <N> | <action> |

### Unanswered conversations
| Contact | Last inbound | Hours overdue | Channel |
|---|---|---|---|
| <name> | <preview> | <hrs> | <SMS/Email/FB/etc> |

### Today's calendar events
| Time | Contact | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| <HH:MM> | <name> | <call/demo/intake> | <link or context> |

---

## 📈 New (last 24h)

### New contacts
- Total: <N>
- Top sources: <list>
- Notable: <names with reason — high-fit, repeat domain, signal>

### New revenue
- Total: $<X>
- Notable transactions: <list>

### Recent refunds / failed
- <list, with red flag if pattern>

---

## 📊 This week trend (lightweight)
- Inbound volume: <N this week vs N last week>
- Conversion rate: <opportunities created / contacts created>
- Avg time to first response: <hours>

---

## Delegation

### ✅ I'll handle (autonomous)
- Update CRM notes / tags for <N> contacts based on signals
- Draft replies for <N> unanswered conversations (push to `email-triage` or `conversations_send-a-new-message` queue)
- Reschedule / send reminders for <N> calendar events
- Flag <N> opportunities as "needs review" (move tag)

### ⚠️ Need from you (approve)
- <N> drafted replies waiting for approval
- <N> opportunity stage-changes proposed
- <N> contacts proposed for follow-up sequence

### 🚫 You only
- <N> calls today on the calendar (your attendance required)
- <N> customer escalations needing your personal voice
- Any payment issues (refunds, charges) — your decision

---

## Maintenance flags
- <N> contacts have stale data (no activity 30+ days) — consider cleanup
- <N> opportunities have no next-step set — risk of stalling
- <social posting issue>: <X scheduled posts failed to send>

Anti-patterns

  • Dumping the full pipeline — defeats the purpose. Filter aggressively.
  • Auto-replying to conversations — drafts only. Conversations need approval.
  • Auto-moving opportunities — propose stage changes, don't execute without approval.
  • Counting trends without context — "5 new contacts" only matters if it's high/low vs baseline.
  • Skipping the 3-lane plan — that's the point of the output.

Pre-authorizations user can grant

  • Auto-update CRM notes/tags on contacts (never delete)
  • Auto-add follow-up tasks to TASKS.md
  • Auto-send scheduled reminders for calendar events
  • NEVER auto-send conversation messages
  • NEVER auto-charge / refund

Pairing

  • Called by prep-day
  • Pairs with email-triage (unanswered conversations may overlap with email)
  • Pairs with task-list-manager (hot items become tasks)
  • Pairs with weekly-review (trend lines surface)

Specific GHL tool mapping

Signal MCP tool
New contacts gohighlevel.contacts_get-contacts
Opportunities aging gohighlevel.opportunities_search-opportunity
Calendar gohighlevel.calendars_get-calendar-events
Conversations gohighlevel.conversations_search-conversation + _get-messages
Payments gohighlevel.payments_list-transactions
Social gohighlevel.social-media-posting_get-posts
Custom fields gohighlevel.locations_get-custom-fields
Pipelines gohighlevel.opportunities_get-pipelines

One-liner reminder

Filter to today's 5-10%. Drafts not sends. Stage-change proposals not commits. Calls and approvals always 🚫 You only.

skills.name: Ghl Eval Harness
skills.description: Regression gate for GHL workflow changes. Test suite grows forever — every production failure becomes a new eval case. Catches: broken triggers, untracked field references, idempotency violations, infinite loops, missing fallbacks. Required before any workflow change ships to production.
best_for: Pre-deploy gate for GHL workflow / automation changes
Weekly regression run on active workflows
Adding a test case after a production incident
skills.estimated_time: 15-45 min depending on suite size
skills.body_html:
GHL Eval Harness

The regression gate. Mirror of voice-eval-harness for GHL workflows.

When to use

  • Before any GHL workflow / automation change ships to production
  • Weekly automated run on active workflows (catches drift)
  • Adding a new eval case after a production failure
  • A/B comparing two workflow versions

When NOT to use

  • Initial workflow authoring (use ghl-crm-architect + workflow-automation-builder first; eval comes after)
  • Single-record debug (call MCP directly)
  • One-time spot check on a small workflow

Required inputs

  1. Workflow under test — workflow ID or path
  2. Eval suite — test cases for this workflow
  3. Comparison baseline — vs last run / vs target

If 2 missing, ask: "Which eval suite — or build a fresh one?"

Eval suite structure

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/ghl/<workflow-name>/:

_Logs/eval-suites/ghl/welcome-sequence/
├── suite.yaml                    # metadata + thresholds
├── cases/
│   ├── 001-happy-path.yaml
│   ├── 002-missing-email-field.yaml
│   ├── 003-duplicate-trigger.yaml
│   ├── 004-tag-already-applied.yaml
│   ├── 005-stop-keyword-received.yaml
│   ├── 006-rate-limit-hit.yaml
│   ├── 007-no-sms-consent.yaml
│   ├── 008-contact-deleted-mid-workflow.yaml
│   └── (grows from production failures)
└── runs/
    └── YYYY-MM-DD-HHmm.md

Case file shape

id: 001-happy-path
description: New contact opts in, full welcome sequence runs
trigger:
  type: contact_created
  payload:
    email: test+001@example.com
    phone: +15555550001
    custom_fields:
      sms_consent: true
      consent_date: 2026-05-10
expected_outcome: workflow_completed
pass_criteria:
  - welcome_email_sent: true
  - welcome_sms_sent: true
  - sequence_completed_in: < 96 hours
  - no_duplicate_messages: true
  - exit_tag_applied: status:onboarded
  - no_steps_failed: true
soft_assertions:
  - first_email_open_attempted: true (best-effort)
  - sms_within_tcpa_window: true

Workflow

Step 1 — Bootstrap the suite (if first time)

Start with baseline cases per workflow type:

For a contact-trigger workflow (welcome sequence, lead-magnet delivery, etc.):

  • 001 — Happy path: full payload, full sequence runs
  • 002 — Missing email → no email sent, SMS only
  • 003 — Missing SMS consent → email only
  • 004 — Duplicate trigger (same contact fires twice) — idempotency
  • 005 — STOP keyword mid-sequence → sequence halts + DNC tag
  • 006 — Rate limit hit (carrier throttle for SMS) — retry / queue
  • 007 — Contact deleted mid-workflow → graceful exit
  • 008 — Tag already applied (workflow logic dependent) — idempotent

For a pipeline-stage workflow (move-to-next-stage automation):

  • 001 — Happy path: condition met, stage moves
  • 002 — Condition not met — stage doesn't move
  • 003 — Concurrent updates — race condition handling
  • 004 — Opportunity reverted — workflow re-fire behavior

For a conversation-reply workflow (auto-reply, routing):

  • 001 — Inbound from known contact → routed correctly
  • 002 — Inbound from unknown contact → contact created + routed
  • 003 — Within TCPA window — sends
  • 004 — Outside TCPA window — queued
  • 005 — STOP received — DNC + halt
  • 006 — Customer asks for "human" — transfer triggered

Step 2 — Run each case

For each case file:

  1. Create test contact / opportunity / event matching the case payload
  2. Fire the trigger (via MCP or simulated webhook)
  3. Wait for workflow execution (with timeout)
  4. Capture actual outcomes
  5. Check against pass_criteria and soft_assertions
  6. Mark PASS / FAIL / SOFT
  7. Clean up test entity (so eval doesn't pollute prod data)

Step 3 — Aggregate the run

Metric Pass Fail Soft fail
Total cases
Hard pass rate <%>
Soft pass rate <%>
Critical pass rate (compliance) <%>

Step 4 — Regression check vs baseline

  • NEW failures: previously passed → now fails → 🔴 regression
  • NEW passes: previously failed → now passes → ✅ improvement
  • Persistent failures: failed N+ runs → escalate

Step 5 — Apply gate decision

Result Decision
100% hard pass + 100% critical pass 🟢 GREEN — OK to deploy
100% hard pass + some soft fails 🟡 YELLOW — deploy with ticket
Hard fail in non-critical case 🟡 YELLOW with explicit list — Chris decides
Hard fail in CRITICAL case (compliance: TCPA / consent / STOP / etc.) 🔴 RED — BLOCK
Any NEW regression vs baseline 🔴 RED — BLOCK; fix or document

Step 6 — Output

Output structure

# GHL Eval Run: <Workflow Name>

## Run metadata
- **Workflow**: <name + version>
- **Suite**: `_Logs/eval-suites/ghl/<workflow>/`
- **Cases**: <N>
- **Started**: <timestamp>
- **Duration**: <minutes>

## Aggregate
- Hard pass: <N>/<M> (<%>)
- Soft pass: <N>/<M> (<%>)
- Critical (compliance) pass: <N>/<M> (<%>)

## Gate decision
**Status**: 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴

**Reasoning**: <one paragraph>

## Per-case results

### 🟢 Passes (<N>)
- ✅ 001 happy-path
- ✅ 002 missing-email
- ...

### 🟡 Soft fails (<N>)
| Case | Soft criterion failed |
|---|---|

### 🔴 Hard fails (<N>)
| Case | Critical? | What failed | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|

## Regression check vs <prior date>
- **New failures**: <list> ← 🔴 critical
- **New passes**: <list> ← improvement
- **Persistent failures (3+ runs)**: <list> ← escalate

## Recommendations
| # | Failure | Proposed fix | Owner skill |
|---|---|---|---|

## ✅ I handled
- Ran suite
- Aggregated + diffed
- Applied gate decision
- Cleaned up test entities

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- If 🟡 YELLOW: decide deploy + ticket vs fix-first
- Approve any "acceptance" of soft-fail case

## 🚫 You only
- Override 🔴 RED gate
- Delete eval cases (suite only grows; deletion is Chris-only)

## Handoff
**If 🟢**: → `workflow-automation-builder` for production push
**If 🟡**: → addressing skill (workflow-automation-builder / ghl-crm-architect) + open ticket
**If 🔴**: → fix-first loop

## Logged to
`_Logs/eval-suites/ghl/<workflow>/runs/<timestamp>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Deploying without eval — never
  • Adding cases without auto-failing them first — regression-test-driven
  • Eval that pollutes prod — clean up test entities every run
  • Critical cases not marked critical — TCPA / consent / STOP must hard-block
  • Manual-only runs — schedule weekly for drift detection

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run suite against staging
  • ✅ Create/cleanup test entities (in test sub-account or with _eval_ tag)
  • ✅ Diff runs
  • ✅ Add new cases (additive)
  • ⚠️ Apply gate (informs deploy; doesn't auto-deploy)
  • 🚫 Run against production with real contacts
  • 🚫 Delete eval cases

Pairing

Inputs from: ghl-continuous-improvement (failure patterns → new cases), workflow-automation-builder (workflow under test), _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md Outputs to: workflow-automation-builder (deploy gate), ghl-crm-architect (fix loop) Chains with: ghl-continuous-improvement.workflow.md

Scheduled automation

  • Weekly Monday 7am: full eval run on every production workflow
  • On-demand: before any workflow deploy

One-liner reminder

Suite grows forever. Compliance cases are critical. Clean up test entities. Diff vs baseline. New regression = 🔴. Mirror of voice-eval-harness for GHL.

skills.name: Ghl Form Builder
skills.description: Build + optimize GHL forms (contact forms, catalog requests, credentialing intake, survey forms). Field design, validation, conversion tracking, A/B testing. Integrates with pipelines + workflows. Continuous improvement: monthly conversion review surfaces drop-fields.
skills.estimated_time: 30-60 min per form
skills.body_html:
GHL Form Builder

The form designer + optimizer. Minimum-fields-for-purpose. Conversion-tracked.

When to use

  • New form needed (catalog request, intake, survey)
  • Existing form underperforming (low conversion)
  • A/B test variation
  • Conversion-rate optimization pass

When NOT to use

  • Long-form data entry (use customer-portal forms inside GHL membership)
  • Internal data ops (use back-office tooling)
  • Off-platform forms (these route through GHL via API or different tool)

Required inputs

  1. Form purpose — what action does the form trigger
  2. Audience — cold prospect / warm lead / customer / provider
  3. Required fields — minimum data needed
  4. Downstream wiring — which pipeline + workflow + tag fires on submit
  5. Compliance — consent required (TCPA / GDPR / etc.)

If 1-2 missing, ask: "What's the form for + who fills it out?"

Form design principles (Matrix)

  • Minimum viable fields: every field has a justification or gets cut
  • Provider-only friction: NPI / credentialing required upfront only for ordering — soft for catalog request
  • No multi-page for short forms (< 5 fields = single page)
  • Multi-step for longer (5+ fields = 2-3 steps with progress indicator)
  • Mobile-first: most provider leads come from mobile (LinkedIn / SMS / email)
  • Brand voice in field labels (no "First Name *" — use "What's your name?" or similar warm phrasing)
  • No exclamation marks in field labels / button text
  • Single clear CTA ("Get the catalog" not "Submit")

Standard form types for Matrix

Catalog Request Form (cold prospect)

Goal: capture email + light qualification → send catalog → book strategy call Fields (4 max):

  1. Name
  2. Email
  3. Clinic name
  4. NPI (optional at this stage; verify post-submit)

Wiring:

  • Triggers: send catalog email, tag interest:catalog, add to nurture sequence
  • Pipeline: New Lead → Catalog Requested stage
  • Workflow: catalog-followup-sequence (4-touch over 14 days)

Credentialing Intake (qualified — wants to order)

Goal: get provider verified + account-ready Fields (~10):

  1. Name
  2. Email
  3. Phone
  4. Clinic name + address
  5. NPI (required + verified via NPPES)
  6. Specialty
  7. Role (MD / DO / NP / PA / DNP / Admin)
  8. Tax ID / Business name
  9. Shipping address
  10. Initial product interest (multi-select)

Wiring:

  • Triggers: NPPES verification flow, BAA email if applicable, onboarding kickoff scheduling
  • Pipeline: New Lead → Credentialing
  • Workflow: credentialing-process (multi-step with manual touchpoints)

Strategy Call Booking

Goal: get a call on calendar Fields (3 max):

  1. Name
  2. Email
  3. Calendar selection (uses GHL native calendar)

Wiring:

  • Triggers: calendar confirmation email + SMS reminder T-24h + T-1h, discovery-call-prep fires for Chris/AE
  • Pipeline: New Lead → Discovery Scheduled

NPS Survey (post-onboarding 30-day)

Goal: capture satisfaction + identify save / expansion opportunities Fields (3 max):

  1. NPS score (0-10)
  2. Reason / context (open text)
  3. (Conditional) What would make this 10? (if NPS < 9)

Wiring:

  • Triggers: response tagging (nps:promoter / nps:passive / nps:detractor), detractor → retention-automation, promoter → ask for review / referral

Workflow

Step 1 — Determine form type + audience

Match the use case to standard types above. If novel, design from scratch.

Step 2 — Field list (minimum viable)

Each field passes the test: "If I cut this, does the downstream workflow break?"

  • If no → cut it
  • If yes → keep + justify in form spec

Mobile-first: ≤ 5 fields above the fold on a 375px-wide screen.

Step 3 — Compliance + consent

For SMS consent: required checkbox if collecting phone For GDPR/CCPA: required if any EU/CA traffic For HIPAA: never collect PHI in marketing forms (catalog request, etc.)

Step 4 — Wire downstream

  • On submit: which workflow fires
  • Pipeline stage: contact enters which stage
  • Tags: what's tagged
  • Custom field updates: what's filled
  • Notifications: who's notified (account manager, sales, etc.)

Step 5 — Build the form in GHL

Via workflow-automation-builder or directly:

  • Form name + slug
  • Field structure
  • Validation rules
  • Submit redirect (thank-you page or email-confirmation)
  • Trigger workflow ID

Step 6 — Visual QA + mobile test

  • Desktop test: render, submit, downstream fires
  • Mobile test (real device or DevTools mobile emulation)
  • Auto-fill test (browsers shouldn't block)
  • Tab-order test (keyboard navigation)
  • Accessibility: labels associated, ARIA where needed, contrast

Step 7 — Conversion tracking setup

Track per form:

  • Impressions (page views with form visible)
  • Submission rate (submits / impressions)
  • Field-level dropoff (which field is the last filled before abandonment)
  • Conversion to next-stage (submit → opportunity → meeting / sale)

Set up via:

  • GHL's native form analytics
  • UTM tracking on form URL
  • Conversion pixels (Meta / Google) if running paid

Step 8 — Output

Output structure

# Form Built: <Name>

## Spec
- **Type**: <catalog request / credentialing / booking / survey / etc.>
- **Audience**: <cold / warm / customer / provider>
- **URL slug**: `/forms/<slug>`

## Fields
| # | Field | Required | Type | Justification |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## Compliance
- [x] No PHI in non-credentialing forms
- [x] SMS consent checkbox (if collecting phone)
- [x] Privacy policy link
- [x] Terms link

## Wiring
- **On submit triggers workflow**: <workflow name>
- **Pipeline stage**: <pipeline → stage>
- **Tags applied**: <list>
- **Custom fields updated**: <list>
- **Notifications**: <who>

## QA passed
- [x] Desktop render + submit
- [x] Mobile test
- [x] Auto-fill compatible
- [x] Keyboard nav (accessibility)
- [x] Color contrast WCAG AA
- [x] Downstream wiring verified

## Conversion tracking
- Form analytics in GHL: enabled
- UTM on form URL: <param scheme>
- Conversion pixels: <Meta / Google / none>
- Baseline conversion target: <%>

## ✅ I handled
- Form design
- Built in GHL
- Wired downstream
- Tested all surfaces

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve final form before publish
- Authorize any UTM scheme / pixel integration

## 🚫 You only
- Approve any form that collects PHI (HIPAA review)
- Approve any form on a regulated landing page (FDA / state)

## Handoff
**To**: `workflow-automation-builder` (for the trigger workflow), `compliance-qa` (claims in form copy), `frontend-dev` (if embedded outside GHL)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-form-<slug>.md`

Continuous improvement

Monthly: pull form analytics + propose optimizations.

Pattern Action
Drop-off > 50% on a specific field Move to post-submit OR make optional OR remove
Submission rate < 20% on impression Reduce field count OR simplify copy OR test new headline
Mobile submission rate < desktop by > 30% Mobile UX issue — fix layout / field types
Submitted but no next-stage progression Downstream wiring issue — investigate

Anti-patterns

  • 15-field intake forms for cold catalog request — kills conversion
  • Required NPI on first touch — NPI verification is a credentialing-stage thing, not a catalog-request thing
  • No mobile test — most providers click from mobile
  • Auto-fill blocked — fix field types / autocomplete attrs
  • "Submit" button — use specific CTA copy
  • No conversion tracking — can't optimize without data

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design form + field structure
  • ✅ Build in GHL (staging)
  • ✅ Wire downstream workflows (additive)
  • ✅ Conversion tracking setup
  • ⚠️ Publish form on production site (Chris approves)
  • ⚠️ Embed pixels (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Collect PHI in marketing forms

Pairing

Inputs from: offer-positioning (what CTA leads here), marketing-research (audience for the form), _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md Outputs to: workflow-automation-builder (trigger workflow), ghl-crm-architect (pipeline staging), compliance-qa (form copy claims) Chains with: ghl-continuous-improvement (monthly form perf review), ghl-eval-harness (regression test for form-triggered workflows)

One-liner reminder

Minimum viable fields. Mobile-first. Brand voice in labels. No PHI in marketing forms. Downstream wired before publish. Conversion-tracked. Monthly review.

skills.name: Ghl Funnel Builder
skills.description: Build landing pages + funnels via GHL's native funnel builder. For simple conversion pages where GHL's drag-drop is faster than custom Next.js. Wires forms + payment + tracking. For Matrix: most use case is custom Next.js (matrix-store), but GHL funnels work for one-off campaigns / events / quick A/B tests.
triggers: build a landing page in ghl
best_for: Campaign-specific landing pages (event registration, lead magnet)
skills.estimated_time: 60-120 min per funnel
skills.body_html:
GHL Funnel Builder

The GHL-native landing-page builder. For quick + simple. Hand off to frontend-dev when complexity warrants.

When to use

  • Campaign-specific landing page (event, lead magnet, one-off promo)
  • A/B test variants (faster than spinning up Next.js variants)
  • Webinar / event registration pages
  • Simple opt-in / catalog request flow

When NOT to use

  • Matrix's main marketing site (use Next.js — already built at matrix-advanced-solutions)
  • Storefront / e-commerce (use matrix-store custom)
  • Complex interactive UI (use frontend-dev)
  • Sub-brand flagship pages (Peptide Map / MuscleLock — custom Next.js)

Required inputs

  1. Page purpose — what action does this page drive
  2. Audience temp — cold / warm / hot
  3. Offer / CTA — what the user gets / does
  4. Brand mode — cinematic vs brand palette (per creative-director)
  5. Form / conversion wiring — which form fires, which workflow, which pipeline stage

If 1-2 missing, ask: "What's the page for + audience temperature?"

When GHL native vs custom Next.js

Need GHL native Custom Next.js
Quick campaign page ✅ overkill
A/B testing simple variants ✅ possible but slower
Storefront / catalog overkill (use matrix-store) ✅
Flagship marketing site underpowered ✅
3D / R3F / heavy motion not supported ✅
Multi-step funnel with branching possible ✅ better
Event / webinar page ✅ overkill
Lead-magnet opt-in ✅ overkill

Default for Matrix: GHL native for quick / campaign-tied pages; Next.js for evergreen / flagship.

Workflow

Step 1 — Pick funnel shape

Standard shapes:

Opt-in page (single offer):

  • Hero (headline + subhead + CTA)
  • Value proof (3-5 bullets)
  • Form (3 fields max)
  • Trust signals (logos, testimonial)
  • Footer

Webinar registration:

  • Hero (event title + date)
  • What you'll learn (3 bullets)
  • Host bios
  • Form (3 fields)
  • Reminder of date
  • Footer

Lead magnet (catalog / guide download):

  • Hero (the offer + visual)
  • What's inside (4 bullets)
  • Form (3-4 fields)
  • Social proof
  • Footer

Event landing:

  • Hero (date + location + theme)
  • Agenda (collapsible)
  • Speakers (with photos)
  • Form / RSVP CTA
  • Sponsors / partners
  • Footer

Step 2 — Apply creative direction

Invoke creative-director for the visual brief. Get palette (cinematic vs brand), typography, imagery direction.

For Matrix:

  • Cinematic palette for premium / enterprise audience
  • Brand palette for storefront / store-adjacent
  • Inter typography
  • Dark-first (cinematic) or vibrant (brand)

Step 3 — Write the copy

Invoke copywriting with:

  • Channel: landing page
  • Section: per funnel-shape sections
  • Brand voice (per brand-guide.md)

Step 4 — Compliance check

Run output through compliance-qa:

  • No banned absolutes
  • Required disclaimers (especially if any medical / financial framing)
  • Privacy policy + terms links in footer
  • Cookie consent banner (if EU/CA traffic)

Step 5 — Build in GHL

In GHL's funnel builder:

  • Create funnel + page
  • Apply brand styling (custom CSS for palette match if needed)
  • Drag in sections per the funnel shape
  • Add the form (from ghl-form-builder)
  • Configure CTAs
  • Add tracking (GA / Meta Pixel / GHL native)

Step 6 — Mobile test

GHL funnels are responsive by default but verify:

  • Hero readable on mobile
  • Form usable on mobile (autofill, keyboard types per field)
  • CTAs above the fold on small screens
  • Load time < 3 seconds

Step 7 — Conversion tracking

Set up:

  • GHL native page analytics
  • UTM scheme for traffic source attribution
  • Conversion pixels (Meta / Google / TikTok if paid running)
  • A/B variant tracking (if testing)

Step 8 — Output

Output structure

# GHL Funnel Built: <Name>

## Spec
- **Type**: <opt-in / webinar / lead magnet / event / etc.>
- **Audience**: <ICP + temp>
- **Offer**: <one-line>
- **Brand mode**: <cinematic / brand>

## Pages
| Step | URL slug | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | /<slug> | Landing |
| 2 | /<slug>/thanks | Thank you (post-submit) |

## Sections (per page)
- <list per Step 1's shape>

## Copy
**Source**: `copywriting` output: `_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-copy-landing-<funnel>.md`

## Form wiring
**Form**: <form name from `ghl-form-builder`>
**On submit**: <workflow name>
**Pipeline**: <pipeline → stage>
**Tags**: <list>

## Compliance check
- [x] `compliance-qa` passed
- [x] Privacy + terms links in footer
- [x] No banned absolutes
- [x] Required disclaimers

## QA
- [x] Desktop render
- [x] Mobile test
- [x] Form submit works
- [x] Downstream workflow fires
- [x] Tracking pixel fires
- [x] Load time < 3s

## Conversion tracking
- GHL page analytics: enabled
- UTM scheme: <details>
- Pixels: <Meta / Google / etc.>
- A/B variants (if any): <list with traffic split>

## Baseline conversion target
<% > based on industry / past campaigns

## ✅ I handled
- Funnel shape design
- Built in GHL (staging)
- Wired form + workflow
- Mobile + tracking tested

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve copy + visual before publish
- Approve any pixel / tracking integration
- Authorize publish

## 🚫 You only
- Approve any clinical claim on the page (handoff to compliance-qa + counsel)
- Approve customer-logo / testimonial usage (permission docs)

## Handoff
**To**: `compliance-qa` (claims), `ghl-form-builder` (form), `creative-director` (visual brief), Chris (publish approval)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-funnel-<name>.md`

Continuous improvement

Monthly: review funnel analytics + propose optimizations via ghl-continuous-improvement.

Pattern Action
Conversion rate < target A/B test new hero / CTA / form
Mobile conversion < desktop by 30%+ Mobile UX issue
High traffic, low form-fill Page issue (above-fold, scroll behavior, trust signals)
Form-fill but no next-stage Downstream workflow issue
Bounced traffic from specific source Source quality + landing match issue

Anti-patterns

  • Long-form sales pages in GHL for cold traffic — wrong audience temp
  • Custom CSS hacks in GHL — fight the tool; if you're hacking, use Next.js
  • No mobile test — half the traffic
  • No tracking before launch — can't measure
  • Same landing page for all sources — match landing to source

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design funnel shape
  • ✅ Build in GHL (staging)
  • ✅ Wire form + workflow
  • ✅ Mobile + tracking test
  • ⚠️ Publish on production
  • ⚠️ Embed paid-ad pixels
  • 🚫 Anything that touches PHI

Pairing

Inputs from: creative-director (visual brief), copywriting (copy), ghl-form-builder (form), offer-positioning (the offer) Outputs to: workflow-automation-builder (downstream automation), compliance-qa (final check) Chains with: ghl-continuous-improvement (monthly funnel review), analytics-architect (conversion KPIs)

One-liner reminder

GHL native for quick / campaign / one-off. Next.js for evergreen / flagship. Mobile-tested. Tracking before launch. Monthly conversion review.

skills.name: Ghl Pipeline Optimizer
skills.description: Pipeline-specific deep-dive. Analyzes stage velocity, dropoff points, win/lose ratios, deal aging, automation coverage per stage. Recommends stage restructure, SLA tightening, auto-nudge wiring, dead-deal cleanup. Triggered weekly via ghl-continuous-improvement.
skills.intent: Stop deals dying in stages. Identify which pipelines / stages are killing velocity. Recommend specific changes (new stage / merge stages / SLA / auto-nudge). Apply Hawkins discipline (prescriptive narrowing, 3% buying-window) to the pipeline structure itself.
skills.body_html:
GHL Pipeline Optimizer

The pipeline doctor. Stage-by-stage. Evidence-driven.

When to use

  • Weekly pipeline health (auto-pulled into ghl-continuous-improvement)
  • Quarterly pipeline restructure decision
  • After a sales-strategy change — does the pipeline still match?
  • Stuck-deals cleanup
  • New offer needs new pipeline → start from existing analysis

When NOT to use

  • Designing pipeline from scratch (use ghl-crm-architect)
  • Single-deal management (use sales / account-manager)
  • Reporting only (use analytics-architect)

Required inputs

  1. Location ID
  2. Pipeline(s) — specific or all
  3. Period — last 30d / 90d / YTD

Workflow

Step 1 — Pull pipeline data via opportunities_*

For each pipeline:

  • All stages + opportunity counts per stage
  • Stage transitions (in / out per stage per period)
  • Days-in-stage distribution per stage
  • Deal value distribution
  • Win rate (Closed Won / total exited stages)
  • Lose rate + loss reasons
  • Avg cycle (first stage → close)

Step 2 — Compute health per stage

For each stage:

Metric Definition Healthy At-risk Critical
Throughput Deals advancing / period ≥ expected -25% -50%
Avg days-in-stage Median days < SLA 1.5× SLA 2× SLA
Conversion rate Stage → next-stage ≥ baseline -10pp -25pp+
Loss rate Stage → closed-lost < target +10pp +25pp
Stage skipping % skip to next-next-stage < 10% 10-25% > 25% (process issue)

Step 3 — Find the bottleneck

The stage with worst throughput × longest days-in-stage = pipeline bottleneck.

Investigate qualitatively (sample 5-10 stuck deals):

  • Are deals genuinely working through? (slow but progressing)
  • Or are they dying? (no activity, no exit)
  • What's the common pattern of dying deals (same ICP segment? competing vendor? pricing objection?)

Step 4 — Apply Hawkins discipline

Per _Knowledge Base/06 - Sales & Lead-Gen Agent Patterns.md:

  • 3% rule: only 3% in-window — most slow deals are 97% not in window. Discipline: disqualify faster, not nurture longer.
  • Prescriptive narrowing: each stage should have ONE clear next action, not menu of possibilities.
  • FOMU: stuck deals often lost the unique-only-us framing.

Step 5 — Recommend changes

Issue Proposal
Stage with 2× SLA + low conversion Tighten SLA; add T+SLA auto-nudge SMS/email; auto-disqualify at 3× SLA
Stage with high skip rate Reconsider stage existence; merge with adjacent
Stage that everyone passes through quickly + low exit Probably redundant; consider removal
Loss rate clustered on one stage Investigate root cause (pricing? competition? objection?) — feed to sales-strategy
New deals slow at first stage Could be slow source quality OR slow qualification — drill in
Win rate < target on specific ICP Re-target or sharpen qualification

Step 6 — Surface dead deals

Deals stuck in stage > 2× SLA AND no activity 30+ days:

  • Propose: auto-tag at-risk, fire retention-automation if active customer; otherwise disqualify with reason
  • Don't auto-delete — Chris approves the cleanup

Step 7 — Stage-level automation coverage

For each stage, check:

  • Is there an SLA?
  • Is there an auto-nudge if SLA exceeded?
  • Is there a disqualify condition?
  • Is there a stage-exit task creation?

Gaps in coverage = improvement opportunity.

Step 8 — Output

Output structure

# Pipeline Optimizer: <Pipeline Name>

## Snapshot
- **Pipeline**: <name>
- **Active opportunities**: <N>
- **Total value**: $<X>
- **Avg cycle**: <days>
- **Period analyzed**: <range>

## Stage health
| Stage | Count | Avg days | SLA | Throughput | Conv → next | Loss rate | Skip rate | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## 🐌 Bottleneck stage
**Stage**: <name>
**Issue**: <one paragraph>
**Sample evidence**:
- <stuck deal 1 with context>
- <stuck deal 2 with context>
- <stuck deal 3 with context>

## 🪦 Dead deals (stuck > 2× SLA, no activity 30+d)
| # | Opportunity | Value | Stage | Days stuck | Last activity | Proposed action |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## Loss-reason patterns
| Reason | Count | % of losses | Trend |
|---|---|---|---|

## Win/lose by ICP segment
| Segment | Win rate | Avg deal size | Avg cycle |
|---|---|---|---|

## Automation coverage gaps
| Stage | Missing | Proposal |
|---|---|---|
| <stage> | No SLA / auto-nudge / disqualify rule | <wire what> |

## 🛠 Improvement proposals
| # | Issue | Evidence | Proposal | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | <issue> | <evidence> | <change> | <skill> |

## ✅ I handled
- Pulled pipeline data
- Computed stage health
- Identified bottleneck + dead deals
- Loss-reason patterns
- Generated proposals

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve restructure changes (merge/split stages)
- Approve dead-deal cleanup actions
- Approve new automation coverage wiring

## 🚫 You only
- Approve deletion of stages with active deals
- Approve mass-disqualification beyond N deals

## Handoff
**To**:
- `ghl-crm-architect` (for structural pipeline changes)
- `workflow-automation-builder` (for auto-nudge / disqualify wiring)
- `ghl-eval-harness` (validate changes)
- `sales-strategy` (if loss patterns suggest strategy issue)
- `retention-automation` (for active-customer dead deals)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-pipeline-opt-<pipeline>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Renaming stages without measuring — change-for-change-sake
  • Adding stages without removing — pipeline bloat
  • Auto-deleting dead deals — Chris approves cleanup
  • Ignoring loss-reason patterns — they're free strategy signal
  • No automation coverage check — every stage needs SLA + nudge + exit

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull pipeline data
  • ✅ Compute health
  • ✅ Generate proposals
  • ⚠️ Approve structural changes (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Approve dead-deal cleanup
  • 🚫 Auto-delete pipelines / stages / deals

Pairing

Inputs from: GHL opportunities_*, sales-strategy KPIs, ghl-daily-pulse Outputs to: ghl-crm-architect, workflow-automation-builder, sales-strategy, retention-automation Chains with: ghl-continuous-improvement (rolls findings up), ghl-eval-harness (validates changes)

One-liner reminder

Health per stage. Find the bottleneck. Sample stuck deals. Dead deals → action. Loss patterns → strategy. Automation coverage gaps. Evidence-driven proposals.

skills.name: Ghl Workflow Auditor
skills.description: Quarterly deep audit of all GHL workflows. Identifies dead workflows, redundant logic, broken triggers, untracked field references, idempotency issues, infinite-loop risks, missing failure paths. Proposes cleanup. Companion to ghl-continuous-improvement (which does weekly review).
skills.intent: Prevent workflow sprawl. Quarterly audit catches: workflows nobody uses, broken/orphaned triggers, redundant logic across workflows, missing fallbacks, contacts that loop between workflows, security issues. Proposes consolidation + cleanup. Never auto-deletes.
best_for: Pre-migration audit (before snapshot deploy / location move)
skills.estimated_time: 60-180 min depending on workflow count
skills.body_html:
GHL Workflow Auditor

The quarterly deep cleaner. Prevents workflow sprawl. Companion to weekly ghl-continuous-improvement.

When to use

  • Quarterly schedule (March / June / Sept / Dec)
  • Pre-migration audit (before snapshot deploy)
  • Post-incident investigation
  • Post-rebuild cleanup
  • When workflow count grows past ~30 (sprawl signal)

When NOT to use

  • Weekly review (use ghl-continuous-improvement)
  • Single-workflow debug (just read it)
  • Building new workflow (use ghl-crm-architect + workflow-automation-builder)

Required inputs

  1. Location ID
  2. Scope — all workflows / specific category / triggered-by-X
  3. Last audit date — for delta-tracking

Workflow

Step 1 — Inventory all workflows

Pull every workflow:

  • Name + description
  • Trigger type + event
  • Status (active / paused / archived)
  • Created date / last modified
  • Last fired date
  • Total fires (lifetime + this period)
  • Step count

Step 2 — Classify each workflow

Class Criteria Action
🟢 Active + healthy Fires regularly, predictable outcomes Keep
🟡 Active + thin signal Fires occasionally, value unclear Investigate
🟡 Active + slow Long avg execution, may have efficiency issues Optimize
🔴 Active + broken Trigger fires but steps fail Fix immediately
⚫ Stale (no fire 60+ days) Inactive but enabled Archive or kill (with approval)
⚫ Orphaned trigger Trigger doesn't exist anymore (deleted form / tag / etc.) Archive
🔁 Redundant Multiple workflows doing similar thing Consolidate
♾️ Loop risk Workflow A triggers B which triggers A URGENT — investigate

Step 3 — Cross-reference dependencies

For each workflow, map:

  • Trigger source: form / tag / stage / event / webhook
  • Modifies: which tags / fields / stages / contacts
