  • Sends: SMS / email / webhook out
  • Reads from: which custom fields / tags

This dependency graph reveals:

  • Workflows whose trigger no longer exists (orphans)
  • Workflows whose target tag/field doesn't exist (broken downstream)
  • Redundant chains (workflow A sets tag X, workflow B triggers on tag X — could be one workflow)
  • Potential loops (mutual triggering)

Step 4 — Identify cleanup candidates

For each candidate, document:

  • What it is: workflow name + purpose
  • Why cleanup: stale / orphaned / redundant / broken / loop risk
  • Recommendation: archive / merge into [other] / fix / delete
  • Risk if cleaned up: any downstream that breaks
  • Required approval: chris for any active workflow with contacts touched recently

Step 5 — Security + compliance review

Per workflow, check:

  • No PHI/PII in workflow names / step descriptions
  • Sends respect TCPA window (SMS workflows)
  • STOP keyword handling present (SMS sequences)
  • Idempotency on mutating workflows
  • No infinite-loop potential (mutual triggers / no exit conditions)
  • Sensitive data not in webhook payloads to third parties

Step 6 — Performance + efficiency review

For each active workflow:

  • Avg execution time
  • Steps that fail most often
  • Steps that take longest
  • Duplicate logic across workflows

Surface efficiency proposals.

Step 7 — Quarterly cleanup plan

Output is a punch list:

  • Workflows to archive (Chris approves)
  • Workflows to consolidate (with merge plan)
  • Workflows to fix (with specific fixes)
  • Workflows to delete after cooling-off period (typically 30 days archived → delete)
  • Workflows to add (gaps identified)

Step 8 — Output

Output structure

# GHL Workflow Audit: <Location> — Q<N> <YYYY>

## Inventory
- **Total workflows**: <N>
- **Active**: <N>
- **Paused / archived**: <N>

## Classification
| Class | Count | % |
|---|---|---|
| 🟢 Active + healthy | <N> | <%> |
| 🟡 Active + thin signal | <N> | <%> |
| 🟡 Active + slow | <N> | <%> |
| 🔴 Active + broken | <N> | <%> |
| ⚫ Stale (60+d no fire) | <N> | <%> |
| ⚫ Orphaned trigger | <N> | <%> |
| 🔁 Redundant | <N> | <%> |
| ♾️ Loop risk | <N> | <%> |

## 🔴 Critical (fix immediately)
| Workflow | Issue | Recommendation |
|---|---|---|

## 🔁 Consolidation opportunities
| Workflows | Pattern | Merge into | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| A + B | Both fire on tag X, do overlapping work | Merge into A; archive B | low |

## ⚫ Archive candidates
| Workflow | Last fired | Reason | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|

## ♾️ Loop / risk
| Workflows | Pattern | Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|

## Performance issues
| Workflow | Avg execution | Failing step | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|

## Security + compliance findings
| Finding | Severity | Workflow | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| PHI in workflow name | High | <name> | Rename |
| STOP keyword not handled | Critical | <SMS sequence> | Add handler |
| No idempotency on mutating workflow | High | <workflow> | Add idempotency key |
| Mutual trigger between A & B | Critical | A + B | Break loop |

## Quarterly cleanup plan
| # | Action | Target | Approval | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Archive | <workflow> | ⚠️ Chris | self | <date> |
| 2 | Merge | <A> into <B> | ⚠️ Chris | self + ghl-crm-architect | <date> |
| 3 | Fix loop | <A> ↔ <B> | 🔴 immediate | self | today |

## ✅ I handled
- Inventoried + classified all workflows
- Mapped dependencies
- Found cleanup candidates + risks
- Security + compliance pass

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve archive list
- Approve consolidation plan
- Approve fix priority for 🔴
- Approve deletion list (after archive cooling period)

## 🚫 You only
- Delete workflows with active contacts touched in last 7 days
- Approve any change that touches PHI / financial workflows
- Migrate workflows across locations / sub-accounts

## Handoff
**To**:
- `workflow-automation-builder` (for actual changes after approval)
- `ghl-crm-architect` (for consolidations + restructures)
- `ghl-eval-harness` (regression check for any kept-but-modified workflows)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-workflow-audit-Q<N>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Mass-archiving stale workflows without dependency check — orphans downstream
  • Consolidating without re-eval — merged workflow may have regressions
  • Ignoring loop risk because "it's been fine" — loops fail at scale
  • No security pass — PHI / TCPA / consent issues hide
  • Quarterly cadence skipped — sprawl compounds

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read all workflows (read-only audit)
  • ✅ Map dependencies
  • ✅ Classify + propose
  • ⚠️ Archive workflows (Chris approves each)
  • ⚠️ Consolidate / merge (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Delete (only after archive cooling-off + explicit Chris approval)
  • 🚫 Modify production workflows without workflow-automation-builder and ghl-eval-harness pass

Pairing

Inputs from: GHL workflows MCP, ghl-continuous-improvement (weekly patterns roll up here) Outputs to: workflow-automation-builder, ghl-crm-architect, ghl-eval-harness, security-inspector (for security findings) Chains with: ghl-continuous-improvement.workflow.md, pmo-operator (cleanup tracked as quarterly project)

Scheduled automation

  • Quarterly (1st of March / June / Sept / Dec) 9am: full audit via automation-scheduler

One-liner reminder

Quarterly cadence. Classify all workflows. Map dependencies. Find loops + orphans + redundancy. Security + compliance pass. Cleanup plan with approvals. Never auto-delete.

skills.name: Google Branding
skills.description: Apply Google's official brand colors and typography to artifacts. Use when creating documents, presentations, websites, or any visual content that should follow Google's brand guidelines, design standards, or look-and-feel. Includes official color hex codes, typography recommendations, and implementation patterns.
skills.body_html:
Google Branding

Apply Google's official brand colors (#4285F4 blue, #EA4335 red, #FBBC05 yellow, #34A853 green) and typography guidelines to create artifacts with Google's distinctive look-and-feel.

When to Use This Skill

Use this skill when:

  • Creating artifacts that should match Google's brand identity
  • User requests Google colors, brand colors, or visual style guidelines
  • Building presentations, documents, or websites with Google's aesthetic
  • Implementing Google's design language or Material Design
  • User mentions wanting a "Google look" or references Google's branding

Quick Reference

Colors:

  • Blue: #4285F4 (primary)
  • Red: #EA4335 (accent)
  • Yellow: #FBBC05 (highlight)
  • Green: #34A853 (success)

Typography:

  • Use Roboto for body text (open-source Google font)
  • Use Poppins or Roboto for headings
  • Avoid proprietary Google Sans (restricted to official Google use)

Instructions

1. Review Guidelines First

Before creating any artifact with Google branding, read the comprehensive reference:

  • colors-and-typography.md - Complete color codes, typography guidelines, and implementation examples

2. Apply Colors Consistently

Use Google's four official brand colors:

:root {
  --google-blue: #4285F4;
  --google-red: #EA4335;
  --google-yellow: #FBBC05;
  --google-green: #34A853;
}
  • Use Blue as the primary color (60-70% of color usage)
  • Use Red, Yellow, and Green as accents (30-40% combined)
  • Never modify these exact hex values

3. Typography Implementation

For HTML/React artifacts:

<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Roboto:wght@300;400;500;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">

For documents (DOCX/PPTX):

  • Use Roboto if available, otherwise Arial or another clean sans-serif
  • Maintain consistent font weights (Regular 400, Medium 500, Bold 700)

4. Design Principles

Follow Google's design philosophy:

  • Simple and clean - Avoid clutter, embrace whitespace
  • Geometric and modern - Use clean lines and shapes
  • Accessible - Ensure proper color contrast for readability
  • Playful - The four-color palette conveys approachability

5. Common Patterns

For React/HTML artifacts:

  • Use CSS variables for colors
  • Import Roboto from Google Fonts
  • Apply consistent spacing and clean layouts

For DOCX/PPTX artifacts:

  • Set theme colors to the four Google colors
  • Use Roboto or clean sans-serif fonts
  • Create visual hierarchy with color accents

Examples

HTML/React Color Application

.header {
  background-color: var(--google-blue);
  color: white;
}

.cta-button {
  background-color: var(--google-red);
  color: white;
}

.success-message {
  border-left: 4px solid var(--google-green);
}

.highlight {
  background-color: var(--google-yellow);
}

Multi-Color Logo/Header Style

Use all four colors in sequence for a distinctive Google feel:

  • Blue for main elements
  • Red, Yellow, Green as accent bars, borders, or icons

Resources

  • references/colors-and-typography.md - Comprehensive guide with all color codes, typography details, implementation patterns, and design principles. Read this for detailed information about specific use cases.

Notes

  • Google Sans and Product Sans are proprietary - use Roboto or Poppins as alternatives
  • Official colors are verified from Google's brand guidelines
  • These guidelines help create artifacts that feel cohesive with Google's design language while respecting trademark and licensing restrictions

skills.name: Gross Margin Tracker
skills.description: Per-SKU + per-channel + per-customer gross-margin tracking. Composes `pricing-margin` + COGS from `inventory-manager`. Flags margin slippage. Weekly report to `cfo-finance` + sales-revenue-lead. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Average margin hides the SKUs that are killing you. This specialist tracks margin per-SKU, per-channel (D2C / B2B tiers / promo), per-customer, surfaces slippage trends, and recommends action (reprice / drop / negotiate).
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 30-45 min. Quarterly deep-dive: 2-4 hours.
skills.body_html:
Gross Margin Tracker

The unit-economics auditor. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Tracks gross margin (Revenue - COGS) / Revenue at three resolutions: per-SKU, per-channel (D2C list / D2C sale / B2B tier_standard / B2B tier_gold / B2B tier_platinum / promo per sops/pricing-change-deployment.sop.md), and per-customer. Composes pricing-margin for floors + inventory-manager for per-unit COGS + payments-engine for revenue actuals. Surfaces slippage trends (3+ data-point declines), margin-floor breaches, and high-customer-concentration risk. Weekly report to cfo-finance + sales-revenue-lead (when present). Does NOT change prices, drop SKUs, or renegotiate customer contracts.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Price changes (product-pricing-engine + sops/pricing-change-deployment.sop.md), SKU lifecycle decisions (product-catalog-builder + Chris), customer-contract renegotiation (AM + Chris), COGS allocation methodology (CPA + cfo-finance).

INPUT

input:
  trigger: weekly-cron | pre-pricing-change-baseline | quarterly-deep-dive | adhoc
  period: ISO range
  resolution: sku | channel | customer | all
  cohort_filter (optional):
    sku_category: string
    customer_segment: string
    channel: string

RULES

  • Margin = (Revenue - COGS) / Revenue. Use revenue net of refunds + chargebacks.
  • COGS from inventory-manager per-unit landed cost — never estimate when data exists.
  • Margin floors per sops/pricing-change-deployment.sop.md:
    • D2C list 60% / D2C sale 50% / B2B standard 45% / B2B gold 40% / B2B platinum 35% / promo 30% absolute
  • Slippage trend = 3+ consecutive weekly data points trending down OR > 3pp drop in a single week.
  • Concentration risk: any single customer > 25% of margin → P1 alert.
  • PII discipline — customer-level outputs masked (use account_id, not customer name in logs); only aggregate visible in non-secure surfaces.
  • Per-customer requires account-manager context before action — never recommend "drop" without AM relationship review.
  • Bundle margin computed as weighted; flag any constituent SKU below floor inside bundle.
  • Promo deep-dive — flag promo margin < absolute floor (30%) immediately as fix-or-end.
  • Reconcile to bookkeeping monthly P&L gross margin total — variance > 1pp = methodology issue.

TOOLS

  • pricing-margin (margin floors + repricing recommendations)
  • inventory-manager (per-unit COGS, landed cost)
  • payments-engine (revenue actuals net of refunds)
  • bookkeeping (P&L reconciliation)
  • ghl-daily-pulse (channel attribution)
  • Read: _Shared Context/products.md, offers.md, kpi-registry.md, sops/pricing-change-deployment.sop.md
  • Compose: analytics-architect (visualization + dashboards), pii-redactor (customer-level output sanitization)
  • Hand off to: cfo-finance, pricing-margin, account-manager, product-catalog-builder
  • Write: _Logs/finance/margin/<YYYY-WW>-by-sku.csv + by-channel.csv + by-customer.csv + summary.md

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    report_id: string
    period: ISO range
    overall_gross_margin_pct: number
    overall_revenue_net: number
    overall_cogs: number
    by_sku:
      - sku: string
        revenue_net: number
        cogs: number
        margin_pct: number
        margin_delta_vs_prior_pct: number
        floor_applicable: number
        floor_status: pass | warning | breach
        slippage_trend: improving | stable | declining
    by_channel:
      - channel: D2C-list | D2C-sale | B2B-standard | B2B-gold | B2B-platinum | promo
        revenue_net: number
        cogs: number
        margin_pct: number
    by_customer:
      - account_id: string  # masked for PII
        revenue_net: number
        cogs: number
        margin_pct: number
        margin_share_of_total_pct: number
        concentration_flag: bool
    flags:
      - flag: floor-breach | slippage-trend | concentration | bundle-constituent-below-floor | promo-below-absolute-floor
        sku_or_customer: string
        severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        recommendation: string
        owner: pricing-margin | account-manager | product-catalog-builder | cfo-finance
    quarterly_deep_dive (optional):
      bottom_10_skus_by_margin: array
      top_10_skus_by_margin_dollars: array
      customer_lifetime_margin: array
  format: CSV + Markdown summary

HANDOFF

  • finance-controller-lead (weekly snapshot)
  • cfo-finance (strategic up-roll + monthly close packet)
  • pricing-margin (slippage → reprice review)
  • account-manager (per-customer margin context before any action)
  • product-catalog-builder (sustained-low-margin SKU lifecycle review)
  • monthly-close-orchestrator (monthly margin packet line)
  • forecasting-runway (margin assumption input)
  • sales-revenue-lead (when present — channel-mix awareness)

Eval cases

  1. Weekly sweep, all SKUs above floor → green
  2. Top-3 SKU drops 4pp in single week → P1 alert + recommend pricing-margin review
  3. Promo running at 22% margin (below absolute 30% floor) → P0 flag, pause-promo recommendation
  4. Single customer = 32% of margin → concentration P1 alert
  5. Bundle "GLP-1 Launch" with one constituent SKU at 25% margin → bundle-level review trigger
  6. Variance to bookkeeping P&L gross margin = 2pp → methodology investigation
  7. Quarterly deep-dive → bottom-10 SKUs identified + lifecycle recommendation per

Anti-patterns

  • Reporting average margin only — hides SKU killers
  • Estimating COGS when inventory-manager has actuals
  • Recommending customer drop without AM context
  • Ignoring bundle constituents below floor
  • Per-customer PII in non-secure logs
  • Single-week slippage panic (need 3+ data points)
  • Not reconciling to P&L — methodology drift

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull revenue + COGS + compute margins
  • ✅ Surface flags + recommendations
  • ✅ Generate per-customer aggregates (masked)
  • ⚠️ Per-customer named report (Chris + AM approve audience)
  • ⚠️ Recommendation to product-pricing-engine (Chris approves before pricing change deploys via SOP)
  • 🚫 Change prices / drop SKUs / end customer
  • 🚫 Override margin floors

Pairing

Composed by: finance-controller-lead, monthly-close-orchestrator, forecasting-runway Composes: pricing-margin, inventory-manager, payments-engine, bookkeeping, analytics-architect, pii-redactor Reads: products.md, offers.md, kpi-registry.md, sops/pricing-change-deployment.sop.md Hands off to: cfo-finance, pricing-margin, account-manager, product-catalog-builder

One-liner reminder

Per-SKU + per-channel + per-customer. Floors per pricing SOP. 3+ data points before "trend". PII masked. Concentration > 25% = P1. Reconcile to P&L monthly.

skills.name: Hipaa Health App Builder
skills.description: Inspect, harden, and build HIPAA-oriented websites, ecommerce stores, patient portals, health apps, emergency medical ID workflows, EMR/FHIR integrations, and clinician-reviewed prescription workflows. Use when building or reviewing healthcare software that creates, receives, stores, displays, or transmits PHI/ePHI. Always run inspection first before adding features.
skills.body_html:
HIPAA-Oriented Health App Builder Skill

Use this skill for healthcare websites, ecommerce stores, portals, mobile apps, intake flows, EMR integrations, emergency medical ID systems, medication request workflows, and protected backend repositories.

This skill is inspection-first. Do not build until you identify the privacy, security, medical-safety, and operational gaps.

Correct term: HIPAA, not HIPPA.

Core rule

Never claim a product is “HIPAA compliant” as a final legal conclusion. Say “HIPAA-oriented,” “designed to support HIPAA safeguards,” or “requires legal/security review, BAAs, policies, training, and operational controls.”

Mandatory sequence

  1. Scope the system
  2. Classify data
  3. Run repo/site inspection
  4. Produce gap report
  5. Create remediation plan
  6. Build or patch only after risks are mapped
  7. Add tests and audit evidence
  8. Produce deployment checklist

Safety boundaries

Never build autonomous prescribing, diagnosis, or medical decision authority.

Allowed:

  • medication request intake
  • medication history capture
  • prescription draft queue for licensed clinician review
  • e-prescribing integration stubs
  • pharmacy routing placeholders
  • clinician approval workflow
  • audit trail for clinician decision

Not allowed:

  • AI signs or submits prescriptions
  • AI creates final prescriptions without a licensed prescriber
  • controlled-substance EPCS without certified EPCS integration, identity proofing, access control, and MFA
  • bypass of DEA, state board, pharmacy, or eRx network rules
  • hardcoded prescriber DEA/NPI credentials
  • patient-facing diagnosis or dosing claims without clinician review

Required inspection inputs

Ask for or inspect:

  • stack: frontend, backend, database, auth, hosting, payments, messaging, storage
  • whether PHI/ePHI is collected
  • user types: patient, provider, admin, pharmacy, support, developer
  • current repo tree
  • environment variable pattern
  • database schema or ORM models
  • auth middleware
  • API routes
  • logging and analytics code
  • uploads and storage buckets
  • integrations: EHR/EMR, FHIR, Surescripts/eRx, Stripe, Twilio, GHL, email, pharmacy, lab, AI model APIs
  • deployment target
  • BAA status for vendors

Inspection-first commands

When repo access exists, inspect before editing:

pwd
find . -maxdepth 3 -type f | sed 's#^./##' | sort | head -300
git status --short
rg -n "(password|secret|token|api[_-]?key|private[_-]?key|BEGIN RSA|DEA|NPI|ssn|dob|dateOfBirth|diagnosis|medication|prescription|allerg|lab|patient|phi|ephi|hipaa|stripe|twilio|surescripts|fhir|hl7)" . --hidden -g '!node_modules' -g '!dist' -g '!build' -g '!coverage' -g '!*.lock'
rg -n "(console\.log|debugger|localStorage|sessionStorage|document\.cookie|analytics|pixel|gtag|fbq|posthog|mixpanel|sentry|logflare|logger|winston|pino)" . --hidden -g '!node_modules' -g '!dist' -g '!build' -g '!coverage' -g '!*.lock'

If available, run scripts/hipaa_repo_inspection.sh from this skill.

Build standards

Architecture

Use a protected backend by default:

  • server-side API for all PHI/ePHI operations
  • no PHI in client logs, browser storage, query strings, analytics, pixels, or unauthenticated pages
  • database access only through server routes or tightly scoped RLS
  • private storage buckets for patient uploads
  • signed URLs with short TTL for files
  • encrypted secrets in platform secret manager
  • separate dev, staging, production
  • no production PHI in local dev or previews

Authentication

Require:

  • MFA for providers, admins, pharmacists, and developers
  • strong session management
  • short-lived sessions for privileged users
  • role-based access control
  • tenant isolation for clinic/provider accounts
  • least-privilege service roles
  • break-glass emergency access with extra logging and required reason

Authorization

Implement explicit checks on every protected route:

  • patient sees own records only
  • provider sees assigned patients only
  • clinic admin sees clinic scope only
  • support sees minimum necessary fields only
  • developer cannot see PHI unless specifically authorized
  • pharmacy/lab roles see only needed order fields

Audit trail

Log every access, export, creation, update, delete, upload, download, clinical review, prescribing action, and break-glass access.

Audit events must include:

  • actor ID
  • actor role
  • patient ID or record ID
  • tenant/clinic ID
  • action
  • object type
  • object ID
  • timestamp
  • IP/user agent where appropriate
  • reason for privileged or emergency access
  • success/failure

Do not log PHI values in event text.

Encryption

Require:

  • TLS in transit
  • encryption at rest
  • encrypted backups
  • key rotation plan
  • no secrets in repo
  • no PHI in unencrypted exports
  • PHI-safe log redaction

Vendor/BAA gate

Any vendor that creates, receives, maintains, or transmits PHI/ePHI must be marked BAA required before production.

Common BAA-sensitive vendors:

  • cloud host
  • database
  • object storage
  • email/SMS provider when PHI appears in messages
  • logging/observability tool
  • AI model/API provider if PHI is sent
  • analytics tool if PHI or identifiable behavior is captured
  • payment/order system if health information ties to identity
  • eRx, pharmacy, lab, EMR/EHR/FHIR vendors

Block production if BAA status is unknown for a PHI-touching vendor.

Website/ecommerce constraints

For healthcare ecommerce:

  • separate public catalog from provider/patient protected areas
  • no PHI in public product browsing
  • prescription/clinical items behind credentialed access
  • NPI/license verification for provider-only workflows
  • no medical claims beyond approved copy
  • no patient-specific recommendations without clinician review
  • no PHI in abandoned cart, ad retargeting, Meta/Google pixels, or open email flows
  • patient order statuses must avoid sensitive details in notifications

Emergency Medical ID / Emergency Record ID

Build as patient-controlled minimum necessary emergency access.

Default emergency record fields:

  • emergency ID
  • full name or preferred display name
  • date of birth optional by configuration
  • emergency contacts
  • allergies
  • current medications
  • major conditions
  • implanted devices
  • blood type if known
  • clinician/clinic contact
  • insurance optional
  • patient directives optional

Security rules:

  • use short public emergency profile, not full medical chart
  • use QR/token lookup with rate limiting
  • show emergency view only
  • require patient consent setup
  • log every emergency access
  • allow patient to deactivate/regenerate QR/token
  • do not expose SSN, payment data, full address, full chart, labs, or clinical notes by default

EMR/EHR/FHIR integration

Use standards-first design:

  • Patient.identifier for EMR IDs
  • FHIR Patient, AllergyIntolerance, MedicationStatement, Condition, Observation, DocumentReference where applicable
  • OAuth2/OIDC for EHR authorization
  • SMART on FHIR where applicable
  • external_id mapping table for each EMR/EHR vendor
  • idempotent sync jobs
  • webhook signature verification
  • patient access/export path where required

Prescription workflow

Build only clinician-reviewed prescribing support.

Required modules:

  • patient medication request or clinician order intent
  • medication/allergy/condition checks as warnings only
  • clinician review screen
  • prescriber identity and authorization check
  • eRx network integration boundary
  • EPCS flag for controlled substances
  • MFA/identity proofing placeholders where EPCS applies
  • final sign action only by licensed prescriber in certified workflow
  • audit event for draft, review, changes, approval, rejection, submit

Never store final controlled-substance signing credentials in app code.

AI safety rules

If AI is used:

  • do not send PHI to vendors without BAA and approved data-flow record
  • de-identify unless PHI is required
  • AI output must be draft/support only
  • require clinician review for medical content
  • display confidence and source fields only when grounded
  • store prompts/outputs only when retention policy allows
  • redact PHI from debug logs

Gap report format

Use templates/security-findings-report.md.

Every finding needs:

  • severity: Critical, High, Medium, Low
  • area: Privacy, Security, Medical Safety, Architecture, Vendor/BAA, Data, UX, Deployment
  • evidence: file/path/route/config
  • risk
  • fix
  • test
  • production blocker: yes/no

Build output format

When building or patching, produce:

  • files changed
  • exact changes made
  • safeguards added
  • remaining risks
  • tests run
  • deployment blockers
  • next steps

Production blocker checklist

Block production if any are missing:

  • BAA map for PHI-touching vendors
  • MFA for privileged users
  • RBAC/tenant isolation
  • audit logging
  • encryption in transit and at rest
  • secure secrets management
  • breach/incident workflow
  • backups and restore test plan
  • PHI-safe logging
  • no PHI in analytics/pixels
  • private file storage
  • clinician-only prescription finalization
  • eRx/EPCS certification path where prescribing is live
  • legal/privacy notice review
  • role-based training and access policy

Related references

Read as needed:

  • references/compliance-baseline.md
  • references/security-architecture.md
  • references/inspection-rubric.md
  • references/prescribing-boundaries.md
  • references/emergency-medical-id.md
  • templates/security-findings-report.md

skills.name: Hipaa Industrial Health App Builder
skills.description: Inspection-first builder for HIPAA-oriented healthcare websites, ecommerce stores, patient portals, apps, EMR ID flows, prescription-draft workflows, and protected backend repositories. Use when asked to audit, harden, build, or patch healthcare software handling PHI, ePHI, patient intake, prescriptions, lab results, orders, medical IDs, telehealth, payments, or provider portals.
skills.body_html:
HIPAA Industrial Health App Builder

Use this skill to inspect, harden, and build healthcare software that touches PHI or ePHI. The workflow is mandatory:

  1. Inspect first.
  2. Produce a gap report.
  3. Produce a changes-needed list.
  4. Build or patch only after the findings are mapped.
  5. Re-run inspection.
  6. Produce final residual-risk notes.

Do not claim legal certification. Build HIPAA-oriented technical safeguards, compliance evidence, and audit-ready documentation. The covered entity or business associate still needs legal review, signed BAAs, policies, training, and operational controls.

Immediate safety rules

  • Correct spelling is HIPAA, not HIPPA.
  • Treat all patient identifiers, symptoms, diagnoses, prescriptions, labs, payments tied to care, provider notes, medical IDs, and order metadata as PHI/ePHI when linked to a person.
  • Never place PHI in public pages, URLs, query strings, browser localStorage, analytics tools, ad pixels, logs, error trackers, console output, emails, screenshots, or test fixtures.
  • Never create autonomous prescribing.
  • AI may create clinician-reviewed medication draft text only.
  • Controlled-substance workflows must be routed through DEA/EPCS-ready systems, identity proofing, MFA, certified eRx workflows, and licensed prescriber review.
  • Emergency Medical ID views must reveal minimum necessary emergency data, with break-glass audit logging and rate limiting.
  • Payments must be separated from PHI where possible. Do not put diagnosis, medication details, or visit notes in payment processor metadata.
  • Do not ship a production health app until the inspection report has no Critical findings and all High findings have owner-approved remediation or documented exception.

Required artifact sequence

Every project response using this skill should create or update these outputs:

  1. SECURITY_FINDINGS_REPORT.md
  2. CHANGES_NEEDED.md
  3. BUILD_PLAN.md
  4. DATA_MAP.md
  5. THREAT_MODEL.md
  6. ACCESS_CONTROL_MATRIX.md
  7. VENDOR_BAA_REGISTER.csv
  8. MIGRATION_PLAN.md or schema files
  9. TEST_AND_VERIFICATION_REPORT.md
  10. RESIDUAL_RISK_REGISTER.md

Use templates/ for starting points.

Phase 0. Scope detection

Before editing code, identify:

  • App type: marketing site, ecommerce store, provider portal, patient portal, telehealth app, EMR-lite, EMR integration, prescription draft system, emergency medical ID, backend API, mobile app.
  • PHI touchpoints: intake, labs, orders, medication, messages, records, images, payments, analytics, support tickets.
  • System role: covered entity, business associate, subcontractor, non-HIPAA wellness store, hybrid.
  • Deployment: Vercel, AWS, Azure, GCP, Supabase, Firebase, Render, GHL, Shopify, Stripe, Twilio, SendGrid, Sentry, PostHog, Meta Pixel, Google Analytics.
  • Auth model: patient, provider, admin, pharmacy, support, emergency viewer, service account.
  • Backend: SQL, Supabase, Prisma, Postgres, MongoDB, S3/object storage, queues, webhooks.

If scope is unknown, assume the strictest healthcare posture.

Phase 1. Industrial inspection

Run or adapt:

bash scripts/hipaa_repo_inspection.sh .

For Python-only environments:

python3 scripts/hipaa_industrial_inspector.py --root . --out security-inspection
python3 scripts/generate_findings_report.py --json security-inspection/findings.json --out SECURITY_FINDINGS_REPORT.md

Inspect these layers:

  • Data map and PHI inventory
  • Authentication and MFA
  • Authorization and RBAC/ABAC
  • Database schema and row-level security
  • Backend API validation
  • Session and cookie controls
  • Encryption at rest and in transit
  • Secrets and environment handling
  • Logging and audit trails
  • Error handling and telemetry
  • File upload and object storage
  • Email, SMS, webhook, and notification flows
  • Ecommerce/payment metadata
  • Admin dashboards and support access
  • Emergency break-glass access
  • Prescription draft boundaries
  • CI/CD gates
  • Dependency vulnerabilities
  • Security headers
  • CSP
  • CORS
  • Rate limiting
  • Backups and disaster recovery
  • Incident response and breach workflow
  • Vendor BAA coverage
  • Test data and seed fixtures
  • AI prompt and output safety

Use references/inspection-engine.md for the full checklist.

Phase 2. Findings and changes-needed list

Create a report with this order:

  • Executive summary
  • Critical blockers
  • High-risk gaps
  • Medium hardening items
  • Low cleanup items
  • False positives
  • Unknowns
  • Files affected
  • Exact remediation steps
  • Owner and priority
  • Build impact
  • Verification command

Severity rules:

  • Critical: PHI public exposure, missing auth on PHI endpoint, secrets committed, no RLS on multi-tenant PHI tables, PHI in analytics/pixels/logs, insecure prescription signing, unaudited emergency access, default admin credentials, exposed object bucket.
  • High: no MFA for providers/admins, weak RBAC, missing audit logs, broad service-role key use, missing encryption evidence, missing BAA for PHI vendor, unsafe webhook handling, missing rate limits.
  • Medium: weak security headers, incomplete validation, missing test coverage, weak session expiry, missing data retention logic.
  • Low: docs, comments, naming cleanup, non-blocking policy gaps.

Do not make code changes until this list exists.

Phase 3. Builder takeover

When the inspection is complete, build or patch in this order:

  1. Data model and schema
  2. RLS and database permissions
  3. Auth and MFA gates
  4. API authorization middleware
  5. Audit log service
  6. PHI-safe logging wrapper
  7. Emergency access workflow
  8. Prescription draft workflow
  9. File storage and signed URLs
  10. Notification redaction layer
  11. Admin and provider dashboards
  12. Ecommerce checkout separation
  13. Security headers and CSP
  14. CI/CD gates
  15. Tests and verification docs

Use references/secure-architecture.md, references/database-schema-guide.md, and templates/protected_backend_schema.sql.

Phase 4. Healthcare build patterns

Healthcare website

  • Public marketing pages must contain no PHI.
  • Contact forms must warn users not to submit sensitive medical data unless the form is protected.
  • Intake links must route to authenticated protected forms.
  • Disable tracking pixels on protected routes.
  • Add clear privacy notice and consent capture where relevant.

Ecommerce store

  • Separate product browsing from patient medical data.
  • Keep cart metadata non-clinical.
  • Use provider approval gates for prescription-only or credentialed products.
  • Do not store diagnosis or prescription details in payment metadata.
  • Use order-to-care linkage only inside protected backend tables.
  • Use vendor BAA checks for any service receiving ePHI.

Patient portal

  • Unique user ID per patient.
  • MFA or risk-based reauth for sensitive records.
  • Device/session management.
  • PHI-safe messaging.
  • Download access logs where appropriate.

Provider portal

  • Provider identity verification.
  • Role-based access by clinic, location, provider, support role.
  • MFA required.
  • Prescription drafts require human review and attestation.
  • Audit every record view, export, change, prescription draft, emergency access, and admin override.

Emergency Medical ID

  • QR or ID lookup shows minimum necessary emergency profile.
  • No full chart access from public QR.
  • Break-glass access needs reason capture where possible.
  • Audit access source, timestamp, IP hash, user agent hash, viewed fields, and emergency reason.
  • Rate limit lookups.
  • Allow patient/provider to revoke or rotate public token.

Prescription draft workflow

  • AI may generate structured draft recommendations for clinician review.
  • Draft status must be draft, pending_review, approved_by_clinician, sent_to_erx, voided, or rejected.
  • Only licensed prescribers may approve.
  • Controlled substances require EPCS-ready external flow.
  • Every prescription draft must include source data, reviewer, timestamp, attestation, and audit trail.

Phase 5. Verification gates

Before marking complete, run:

  • Unit tests
  • Integration tests
  • Auth bypass tests
  • RLS tests
  • API negative tests
  • Upload tests
  • Logging redaction tests
  • Dependency scan
  • Secret scan
  • Build
  • Lint
  • Typecheck
  • Manual PHI leakage review

Block release if:

  • Critical findings remain.
  • High findings lack remediation or written exception.
  • RLS tests fail.
  • Any protected route is reachable unauthenticated.
  • Any service-role key reaches frontend code.
  • Any PHI appears in logs, URLs, analytics, seed files, or client storage.

File map

Use these references as needed:

  • references/compliance-operating-system.md
  • references/inspection-engine.md
  • references/secure-architecture.md
  • references/database-schema-guide.md
  • references/prescription-emr-boundaries.md
  • references/emergency-medical-id-spec.md
  • references/vendor-baa-register.md
  • references/deployment-and-ci-gates.md
  • references/ai-medical-safety.md
  • references/testing-rubric.md

Use these templates:

  • templates/SECURITY_FINDINGS_REPORT.md
  • templates/CHANGES_NEEDED.md
  • templates/BUILD_PLAN.md
  • templates/DATA_MAP.md
  • templates/THREAT_MODEL.md
  • templates/ACCESS_CONTROL_MATRIX.md
  • templates/VENDOR_BAA_REGISTER.csv

skills.name: Hr Continuous Improvement
skills.description: Friday 6:00pm weekly + quarterly auditor of HR & Recruiting. Tracks time-to-hire, hire-to-productive, retention by cohort, eNPS trend, review-cadence health, comp-band drift. Proposes evidence-based fixes. Mirror of `education-continuous-improvement` pattern. Joins Friday CI cluster after Education 5pm. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: HR drift is invisible until it costs you — a missed paperwork SLA, a hire stuck at 60 days non-productive, an eNPS decline ignored for two quarters. This specialist audits weekly, proposes specific fixes to specific owner specialists, hands off to `hr-lead` for approval. Closes the Friday CI cluster behind Education.
best_for: Friday 6:00pm weekly CI review (joins cluster after Education 5pm)
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 60-90 min. Quarterly: 1-2 days.
skills.body_html:
HR Continuous Improvement

The CI engine for HR. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Weekly + quarterly auditor of the HR program — recruiting funnel, interview calibration, onboarding completion, training ramp, performance-review cadence, comp-band health, engagement trend, retention by cohort. Proposes evidence-based improvements to specific HR specialists. Joins Friday CI cluster at 6:00pm (after education-continuous-improvement at 5pm). Cross-CI aware (reads Voice/GHL/Ecom/Formulation/IP/SEO/Education Friday outputs to spot people-impact of other-dept changes).

Explicitly OUT of scope: Executing HR changes (specialists do), hire/termination decisions (Chris), comp decisions (Chris + finance), individual coaching (performance-management).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (weekly-cron / quarterly-cron / post-cohort-retro / retention-alarm / adhoc)
    required: true
  time_window: last 7 days (weekly) / last quarter (quarterly)

RULES

rules:
  trend_discipline:
    - ≥ 3 data points before claiming trend
  pii_discipline:
    - All outputs run through `pii-redactor`
    - Cohort minimums respected (≥ 5 for cohort-specific findings)
    - No individual-employee callouts in cross-team distribution
  cross_ci_awareness:
    - Read Friday outputs from prior CI engines (chain-monitor, voice, ghl, ecom, formulation, ip, seo, education)
    - Spot people-impact: "ecom ship-time issue → ops team overload" → engagement risk
  thresholds_per_kpi_registry:
    - Time-to-offer avg: target ≤ 30 days
    - Offer acceptance rate: target ≥ 80%
    - Hire-to-productive: role-specific (sales rep: ≤ 21 days post-Day-90; engineer: 60-90 days)
    - 12-month retention: target ≥ 85%
    - eNPS: target ≥ 30
    - Comp-band-in-range: target ≥ 90%
    - Review-cadence completion: target ≥ 95%
  recommendations_require:
    - Owner specialist + target + deadline (no vague "improve hiring")
    - Hypothesis + evidence + confidence (H/M/L)
    - Expected impact + effort estimate
  quarterly_deep_covers:
    - Recruiting funnel stage-by-stage drop-off + benchmark
    - Time-to-productive by role family
    - Retention cohort analysis (tenure / role / location)
    - Comp-band drift + market refresh recommendation
    - Engagement deep-dive
    - Review-cadence compliance
    - Onboarding checklist effectiveness
    - Manager-effectiveness signals (from engagement + perf data)

TOOLS

  • Read: _Logs/personnel/ (PII-redacted aggregates), _Logs/hr/, _Logs/agent-decisions/ (recruiting + scorecard outcomes), KPI registry
  • Read all Friday CI outputs: _Logs/chain-monitor/ci-feeds/*.json if exists, plus prior CI engines' weekly digests
  • Compose: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor, pmo-operator
  • Write: _Logs/hr-improvement/<period>-weekly.md + <period>-quarterly.md + KPI-history.csv (append-only)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    digest_id: string
    period: weekly | quarterly
    kpi_snapshot:
      open_reqs: number
      time_to_offer_avg_days: number
      offer_acceptance_rate_pct: number
      hire_to_productive_avg_days: number
      retention_12mo_pct: number
      enps_score: number
      comp_band_in_range_pct: number
      review_cadence_completion_pct: number
      onboarding_paperwork_sla_pct: number
    trends:
      - kpi: string
        direction: improving | stable | declining
        data_points: array
        hypothesis: string
    cross_ci_observations:
      - source_dept: string
        observation: string
        people_impact: string
    diagnosed_issues:
      - issue: string
        hypothesis: string
        evidence: string
        confidence: H | M | L
        proposal: string
        executor: which specialist
        expected_impact: string
        effort: small | medium | large
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        owner: string
    asks:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: string
    quarterly_only_section:
      recruiting_funnel_audit: object
      retention_cohort_analysis: object
      comp_band_drift_summary: object
      engagement_deep_dive: object
      manager_effectiveness_signals: object
  format: JSON + Markdown (PII-redacted)

HANDOFF

  • hr-lead (digest + action items)
  • 7 HR specialists (per assigned actions)
  • Christo + Chris (weekly digest summary)
  • chain-monitor (CI cross-feed)
  • pmo-operator (action items tracked)
  • Quarterly review (consumes deep audit)

Eval cases

  1. Standard weekly, all KPIs green → maintain
  2. Time-to-offer creeps from 25 → 38 days → diagnostic (sourcing channel? scorecard delay?)
  3. Hire-to-productive for engineers 80 → 110 days → training-builder engineering path review
  4. eNPS drops 20 points → trigger employee-engagement-pulse cohort investigation
  5. Cross-CI: ecom CI shows fulfillment-team strain → engagement risk flag for ops cohort
  6. Comp-band drift > 15% out-of-range → comp-band-analyst refresh
  7. Quarterly deep audit → full diagnostic across all dimensions
  8. Sales rep cert pass rate dropping (from Education CI) → recruiting screening tightness review

Anti-patterns

  • Single-week trend claims
  • PII leakage in cross-team output
  • Generic "improve hiring" without owner / target / deadline
  • Cross-CI blind (must consume other engines' outputs)
  • No action follow-through tracking (pmo-operator ticketing)
  • Surveying without action (drives engagement worse)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + propose
  • ✅ Notify owners of action items
  • ✅ Track action-item follow-through
  • ⚠️ Approve material change (hr-lead + each owner specialist)
  • ⚠️ Comp-band refresh trigger (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Execute changes directly
  • 🚫 Surface individual employee names in cross-team output

Pairing

Composed by: hr-lead Composes: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor, pmo-operator Reads: All 7 HR specialist outputs + all other Friday CI engine outputs + _Logs/personnel/ (aggregated, redacted) Outputs to: 7 HR specialists + Christo + Chris + pmo-operator + chain-monitor

One-liner reminder

Friday 6pm + quarterly. Trend ≥ 3 data points. PII discipline strict. Cross-CI awareness. Recommendations with owner + target + deadline. Closes the Friday CI cluster after Education.

skills.name: Hr Eval Harness
skills.description: Regression gate for any HR change — comp-band math, offer-letter compliance (state disclosure + ADA + required clauses), onboarding checklist coverage, IP assignment routing, scorecard rubric integrity, PII redaction. Suite grows forever — every production HR issue becomes a new eval case. Mirror of `ecommerce-eval-harness` + `ghl-eval-harness` patterns. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: HR change blind = legal exposure + retention damage. Build the eval corpus once; grow forever. Block deploys on regression. Weekly run catches drift (e.g., state added a new disclosure law; checklist drift; counsel template stale).
best_for: Pre-deploy gate for offer template / comp logic / onboarding checklist change
New eval case after production HR issue
skills.estimated_time: 30-60 min depending on suite size
skills.body_html:
HR Eval Harness

The regression gate for HR. No HR change ships without a green run.

Lineage

Mirror of ecommerce-eval-harness + ghl-eval-harness + voice-eval-harness. Same pattern; HR-specific cases.

When to use

  • Before any HR change ships (offer template / comp band math / onboarding checklist / scorecard rubric / IP-assignment template / state-disclosure language)
  • Weekly automated regression run (catches state-law drift)
  • Adding new case after production HR issue (e.g., missed CA disclosure → new case)
  • A/B comparing two offer-letter variants
  • Annual employment-law sweep (state updates rolled into corpus)

When NOT to use

  • Initial build (eval is for regression, not bootstrap)
  • Individual employee performance disputes (use performance-management)
  • Counsel-review substitute (always use counsel for legal review)

Required inputs

  1. Scope — full suite / subset (e.g., "offer-letter cases only")
  2. Environment — staging (default for pre-deploy) / prod (weekly read-only)
  3. Comparison baseline — last green run

INPUT

input:
  trigger: pre-deploy | weekly-cron | adhoc | annual-employment-law-sweep
  scope: full | subset (specify)
  baseline: ref to last green run

RULES

rules:
  suite_grows_forever:
    - Every production HR issue → new eval case before fix ships
    - Every state-law change → new disclosure case
    - Every counsel-template update → corresponding case
  green_threshold:
    - All P0 (compliance, legal exposure) cases must pass — zero failures
    - P1 cases ≥ 95% pass
    - P2 cases ≥ 90% pass
  pii_discipline_in_cases:
    - All fixture data synthetic — no real employee data
    - Test SSNs / addresses use canonical test values (e.g., 000-00-0000)
  weekly_run_artifacts:
    - Pass/fail breakdown
    - Regression delta vs prior week
    - New failure root-cause analysis
    - Alert on any P0 fail

Eval suite structure

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/hr/:

_Logs/eval-suites/hr/
├── suite.yaml                          # metadata + thresholds
├── fixtures/
│   ├── test-candidate-ca.json
│   ├── test-candidate-co.json
│   ├── test-candidate-ny.json
│   ├── test-candidate-wa.json
│   ├── test-candidate-fl.json
│   ├── test-candidate-exec.json
│   ├── test-candidate-1099-sales-rep.json
│   ├── test-candidate-engineer-above-band.json
│   ├── test-candidate-internal-promotion.json
│   ├── test-employee-day-30.json
│   ├── test-employee-day-90.json
│   ├── test-employee-pip-case.json
│   └── test-employee-promotion-case.json
├── cases/
│   ├── offer-letter/
│   │   ├── 001-ca-salary-disclosure-present.yaml
│   │   ├── 002-co-salary-disclosure-present.yaml
│   │   ├── 003-ny-salary-disclosure-present.yaml
│   │   ├── 004-wa-salary-disclosure-present.yaml
│   │   ├── 005-at-will-language-state-customized.yaml
│   │   ├── 006-ip-assignment-referenced-as-condition.yaml
│   │   ├── 007-nda-condition-present.yaml
│   │   ├── 008-expiration-date-set.yaml
│   │   ├── 009-signing-bonus-clawback-present.yaml
│   │   ├── 010-exec-routed-to-counsel.yaml
│   │   └── 011-non-compete-state-blocked.yaml
│   ├── comp-band/
│   │   ├── 020-band-min-mid-max-calculated.yaml
│   │   ├── 021-location-adjustment-applied.yaml
│   │   ├── 022-above-band-blocks-without-approval.yaml
│   │   ├── 023-below-min-flags-p1.yaml
│   │   └── 024-tenure-adjustment-respected.yaml
│   ├── onboarding/
│   │   ├── 030-i9-section1-deadline-tracked.yaml
│   │   ├── 031-i9-section2-3day-deadline.yaml
│   │   ├── 032-system-access-blocked-without-paperwork.yaml
│   │   ├── 033-sales-rep-tag-training-only-not-active.yaml
│   │   ├── 034-sales-rep-handoff-to-education-cert.yaml
│   │   ├── 035-non-sales-handoff-to-training-builder.yaml
│   │   ├── 036-day-90-handoff-to-performance-management.yaml
│   │   └── 037-ip-assignment-blocks-code-access.yaml
│   ├── scorecard/
│   │   ├── 040-rubric-1-5-anchors-present.yaml
│   │   ├── 041-tenure-adjustment-applied.yaml
│   │   ├── 042-critical-fail-blocks-pass.yaml
│   │   └── 043-calibration-debrief-required.yaml
│   ├── engagement/
│   │   ├── 050-cohort-minimum-5-respected.yaml
│   │   ├── 051-individual-anonymized.yaml
│   │   ├── 052-enps-math-correct.yaml
│   │   └── 053-action-plan-required-on-red-score.yaml
│   ├── performance-review/
│   │   ├── 060-pip-requires-counsel.yaml
│   │   ├── 061-promotion-requires-chris-approval.yaml
│   │   ├── 062-comp-recommendation-via-band-analyst.yaml
│   │   ├── 063-tenure-adjustment-applied.yaml
│   │   └── 064-360-anonymized.yaml
│   ├── compliance/
│   │   ├── 070-no-discrimination-protected-class.yaml
│   │   ├── 071-ada-language-in-job-post.yaml
│   │   ├── 072-equal-opportunity-language.yaml
│   │   ├── 073-pii-redacted-in-cross-team-output.yaml
│   │   └── 074-personnel-file-restricted-access.yaml
│   └── handoff-integrity/
│       ├── 080-hr-does-not-set-rep-active-tag.yaml
│       ├── 081-cert-decision-stays-with-education.yaml
│       ├── 082-onboarding-day-90-handoff-firing.yaml
│       └── 083-offer-acceptance-triggers-onboarding-coordinator.yaml
└── reports/
    └── <date>-run-report.md

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    run_id: string
    timestamp: ISO
    scope: string
    total_cases: number
    passed: number
    failed: number
    pass_rate_pct: number
    by_priority:
      p0_pass_pct: number  # must be 100
      p1_pass_pct: number  # must be >= 95
      p2_pass_pct: number  # must be >= 90
    new_failures:
      - case_id: string
        category: offer-letter | comp-band | onboarding | scorecard | engagement | performance-review | compliance | handoff
        priority: P0 | P1 | P2
        failure_reason: string
        affected_skill: string
        suggested_fix: string
    regression_delta_vs_baseline:
      newly_failing: array
      newly_passing: array
      net_change: number
    green: boolean (true if all priority thresholds met)
  format: JSON + Markdown report → `_Logs/eval-suites/hr/reports/<date>-run.md`

HANDOFF

  • Deploy gate: block on red
  • hr-lead (alerts on red runs)
  • Affected specialist (per failed-case category)
  • compliance-officer (any P0 compliance failure)
  • hr-continuous-improvement (consumes for weekly trend)

Eval cases (of the eval harness itself)

  1. Pre-deploy run green → allow ship
  2. P0 compliance case fails (CA disclosure missing) → block deploy + alert
  3. New state law (e.g., MA salary disclosure 2024) → new case added → suite refreshed
  4. Production issue (offer letter missed IP assignment reference) → new case → corpus grows
  5. Weekly drift run shows offer-template change broke clawback case → root-cause + fix
  6. Annual employment-law sweep → all state disclosure cases re-validated
  7. Counsel template update (NDA refresh) → re-run NDA reference cases
  8. False-positive failure → suite tuning ticket (don't silence — investigate)

Anti-patterns

  • Skipping eval before HR change ships — legal exposure compounds
  • Silencing failing cases — fix root cause; don't disable
  • Real employee data in fixtures — synthetic only
  • No P0/P1/P2 priority — can't gate properly
  • Manual-only validation — drift over weeks
  • Stale fixtures (state laws change)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run regression suite
  • ✅ Add new eval cases
  • ✅ Block deploys on red
  • ⚠️ Tune case priorities (hr-lead approves)
  • ⚠️ Retire cases (rare; never retire P0 compliance)
  • 🚫 Override red run to ship (compliance failures are absolute block)
  • 🚫 Use real employee data in fixtures

Pairing

Composed by: hr-lead (deploy gate), hr-continuous-improvement (consumes outcomes) Composes: synthetic fixture generator, compliance-officer (P0 fail review), analytics-architect (trend math) Reads: All HR specialist outputs (latest versions), _Logs/eval-suites/hr/ Outputs to: Deploy gates, hr-lead, specialists per failure, pmo-operator

One-liner reminder

Suite grows forever. P0 (compliance/legal) must be 100% pass. Synthetic fixtures only. Block deploys on red. Weekly drift catches state-law + template drift. Mirror of voice/GHL/ecom harnesses.

skills.name: Hr Lead
skills.description: Department lead for HR & Recruiting. Orchestrates 7 specialists + composes recruiting + interview-scorecard + training-builder + performance-management. Owns hire-to-productive cycle time, retention, eNPS, comp-band health, review cadence. Hands new sales reps to `sales-rep-certification-manager` for cert (Education dept). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Recruiting is a funnel; HR is the operating system. The lead sequences specialists across the full employee lifecycle: open req → offer → onboarding → productive → reviewed → retained. Holds the line on comp bands, review cadence, and engagement. Hands the rep-cert gate to Education — coordinates the hire, doesn't certify.
skills.estimated_time: Quarterly: 4-6 hours. Weekly review: 30-45 min.
skills.body_html:
HR & Recruiting Lead

The department orchestrator. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Quarterbacks Matrix's people operations. Sequences specialist work across the lifecycle: open req → screened candidates → scored interviews → offer → signed → onboarded → trained → productive → reviewed → retained. Owns department KPIs: hire-to-productive cycle days, 12-month retention rate, eNPS, comp-band-in-range %, review-cadence completion %. Reports weekly to Christo; quarterly to Chris.

Scope boundary — explicit: HR coordinates the hiring + provisioning of new sales reps (paperwork, IP assignment, GHL seat, system access, manager intro). Education's sales-rep-certification-manager owns the certification decision — pass/fail, activation, live-lead eligibility. HR hands off after Day 1 provisioning; resumes ownership after cert pass for non-cert career work (performance reviews, comp, engagement).

Also out of scope: customer onboarding (new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md owns), specific termination decisions (Chris + counsel), legal employment matters (counsel), payroll execution (finance).

Composition (the team)

Specialist Owns
recruiting Job post + sourcing + applicant screening
interview-scorecard Structured assessment design + calibration
offer-package-builder Offer letter draft + comp package + equity grant prep
new-hire-onboarding-coordinator First 30/60/90 — paperwork + provisioning + role-branch handoff
training-builder Role-specific ramp (non-sales roles)
performance-management Quarterly tracking + coaching + PIP support
performance-review-builder Structured quarterly + annual review packets
comp-band-analyst Per-role bands + semi-annual benchmarking + drift flagging
employee-engagement-pulse Monthly eNPS + quarterly engagement deep survey
hr-continuous-improvement Friday 6pm weekly + quarterly deep audit
hr-eval-harness Regression gate — comp logic + offer-letter compliance + onboarding checklist coverage

INPUT

  • Headcount-plan approval (cfo-finance / Chris)
  • New role open (department head request)
  • Quarterly review cycle trigger
  • Retention or eNPS alarm from hr-continuous-improvement
  • Termination event (post-decision — never initiates)

RULES

  • Comp bands respected unless Chris + finance override
  • All employee comms pass compliance-officer (employment-law claims, ADA language, anti-discrimination)
  • All employee data PII-redacted in cross-team logs (pii-redactor composed)
  • Sales rep hires → handoff to sales-rep-certification-manager after Day 1 provisioning
  • Non-sales hires → training-builder role-specific path
  • IP assignment signed before any code/spec access (_Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md — counsel-reviewed)
  • Zero discrimination — protected-class filtering is absolute block
  • Never communicate comp directly to employees (Chris does)
  • Performance review cadence is quarterly; annual is the consolidation

TOOLS

  • All 8 HR specialists + composes recruiting, interview-scorecard, training-builder, performance-management
  • Hands to: sales-rep-certification-manager (Education dept) for sales rep cert
  • Read: _Shared Context/company-profile.md, brand-guide.md, compliance-rules.md, approval-rules.md, kpi-registry.md
  • Templates: _Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md, _Templates/qa-checklist.md
  • Compose: compliance-officer, pii-redactor, pmo-operator
  • GHL MCP (seat provisioning), Google Workspace (account creation — coordinated via IT)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_type: weekly | quarterly | adhoc | cohort-kickoff | req-open
    kpi_snapshot:
      open_reqs: number
      time_to_offer_avg_days: number
      offer_acceptance_rate_pct: number
      hire_to_productive_avg_days: number
      retention_12mo_pct: number
      enps_score: number (-100 to 100)
      comp_band_in_range_pct: number
      review_cadence_completion_pct: number
    priorities:
      - priority: string
        owner_specialist: string
        target: string
        deadline: ISO
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        owner: string
    asks:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

  • Christo (weekly) + Chris (quarterly)
  • 8 HR specialists (per priority assignments)
  • sales-rep-certification-manager (Education) — new sales reps after Day 1 provisioning
  • chain-monitor (HR-related alerts)
  • pmo-operator (open-req as project)
  • compliance-officer (employment-law gate)

Eval cases

  1. Standard weekly review, all KPIs green → maintain
  2. eNPS drops 30+ → trigger employee-engagement-pulse deep survey + diagnostic
  3. New sales rep hire → new-hire-onboarding-coordinator then hand to sales-rep-certification-manager
  4. Non-sales hire → new-hire-onboarding-coordinator then training-builder role-specific
  5. Comp band drift > 15% of employees out-of-range → comp-band-analyst refresh + Chris recs
  6. Retention drops below 80% (12mo) → cohort diagnostic
  7. Quarterly review cycle → performance-review-builder batch generation

Anti-patterns

  • Certifying reps inside HR — that's Education's gate; never override
  • Skipping IP assignment before access grants
  • Manual paperwork tracking — use onboarding-coordinator checklist
  • Communicating comp directly — Chris owns the conversation
  • Generic engagement surveys — must drive action plans
  • Hire decisions made by skill — drafts only; humans decide

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Orchestrate department
  • ✅ Set quarterly priorities
  • ✅ Schedule reviews + cohort kickoffs
  • ⚠️ Open new req (Chris approves headcount)
  • ⚠️ Extend offer (drafted by offer-package-builder; Chris signs)
  • ⚠️ Comp band change (Chris + finance approve)
  • ⚠️ Coaching plan / PIP (manager + Chris approve)
  • 🚫 Final hire decision
  • 🚫 Termination signature / execution
  • 🚫 Direct comp communication to employee
  • 🚫 Override Education's cert decision

Pairing

Composed by: company-os-orchestrator, pmo-operator, cfo-finance (headcount-plan handoff) Composes: 8 HR specialists + recruiting + interview-scorecard + training-builder + performance-management + compliance-officer + pii-redactor Reads: company-profile.md, kpi-registry.md, approval-rules.md, compliance-rules.md Hands to: sales-rep-certification-manager (Education dept) for rep cert

One-liner reminder

Hire → onboard → productive → review → retain. Sales reps hand off to Education for cert; HR resumes for performance + comp. Comp bands held. Reviews quarterly. Bands semi-annual. Engagement monthly. Continuous improvement Friday 6pm.

skills.name: Integration Agent
skills.description: Wires together MCPs, APIs, webhooks, and external services. Specialist between backend-dev and devops — focused on the integration glue (GHL ↔ Stripe ↔ Supabase ↔ Retell ↔ Shippo). Tests integration paths, handles edge cases (retries, deduplication, idempotency), maintains the integration map.
skills.intent: Be the integration specialist. Implement webhook receivers, scheduled API pulls, two-way sync logic, idempotent commit patterns, retry + backoff strategies, and dead-letter handling. Tests every integration path end-to-end. Maintains the canonical integration map.
best_for: Wiring a new MCP / API into Matrix systems
Webhook receiver implementation (Stripe / GHL / Retell / etc.)
Integration debugging when things go wrong
skills.estimated_time: 30-180 min per integration
skills.body_html:
Integration Agent

The glue specialist. APIs + webhooks + MCPs talking to each other reliably.

When to use

  • New external service needs to talk to Matrix systems
  • New webhook receiver (Stripe events, GHL contact updates, Retell call ends)
  • Two-way sync logic (Stripe ↔ GHL, Supabase ↔ GHL)
  • Integration debugging
  • Maintaining the integration map

When NOT to use

  • General backend logic (use backend-dev)
  • Deploy / env config (use devops-deployment)
  • DB schema (use database-schema)
  • API design / business logic (use backend-dev, this skill implements the glue)

Required inputs

  1. Systems involved — A and B (e.g., GHL + Stripe)
  2. Direction — one-way / two-way / event-driven
  3. Trigger — what initiates the sync (webhook / cron / user action)
  4. What syncs — specific fields / objects
  5. Failure handling — retry / dead-letter / alert / fallback

If 4 unclear, ask: "What fields / objects sync between A and B?"

Integration patterns

Pattern When
Webhook receiver External service pushes events to us (Stripe, GHL, Retell)
Scheduled pull We poll an API on a cron (less ideal but sometimes needed)
Two-way sync Both systems update each other (use idempotency keys carefully)
Fan-out One event triggers actions in N systems
Aggregator Many sources → one normalized store
Dead-letter Failed integrations land here for review

Workflow

Step 1 — Map the integration

Document:

  • Source system: who sends / has the data
  • Target system(s): who receives
  • Event type: what triggers it
  • Payload shape: what's in the message (with Zod schema)
  • Auth: how systems authenticate (API key / OAuth / signed webhook)
  • Direction: one-way / two-way
  • Idempotency strategy: how we avoid double-processing
  • Failure handling: retry policy / dead-letter destination
  • Latency budget: how fast must this be

Step 2 — Implement webhook receivers (the common case)

Matrix Next.js pattern:

// app/api/webhooks/<source>/route.ts
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
import { z } from 'zod'

// 1. Schema for the webhook payload
const PayloadSchema = z.object({
  event_type: z.string(),
  data: z.object({ /* ... */ }),
  // ...
})

export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
  // 2. Verify signature (signed webhook)
  const signature = req.headers.get('x-signature')
  const body = await req.text()
  if (!verifySignature(body, signature, WEBHOOK_SECRET)) {
    return NextResponse.json({error: 'invalid_signature'}, {status: 401})
  }

  // 3. Parse + validate payload
  const parsed = PayloadSchema.safeParse(JSON.parse(body))
  if (!parsed.success) return NextResponse.json({error: 'invalid_payload'}, {status: 400})

  // 4. Idempotency check
  const eventId = parsed.data.id || `${parsed.data.event_type}-${parsed.data.timestamp}`
  if (await alreadyProcessed(eventId)) {
    return NextResponse.json({status: 'duplicate'}, {status: 200})
  }

  // 5. Process the event (queue for async if heavy)
  try {
    await processEvent(parsed.data)
    await markProcessed(eventId)
    return NextResponse.json({status: 'processed'}, {status: 200})
  } catch (err) {
    // 6. Failure handling
    await logToDeadLetter(eventId, parsed.data, err)
    return NextResponse.json({error: 'processing_failed'}, {status: 500})
  }
}

Step 3 — Implement two-way sync (more complex)

For two-way syncs (e.g., Stripe customer ↔ GHL contact), the rules:

  • One source of truth per field — never both systems can write the same field independently
  • Last-write-wins logic uses timestamps from a synchronized clock
  • Use idempotency keys on all writes
  • Loop detection: if Matrix → A → webhook → Matrix, prevent infinite loop
  • Backoff on conflicts: if both updated same field in same window, surface for human decision

Step 4 — Failure handling

For every integration, decide:

  • Retry policy: exponential backoff (e.g., 1s, 5s, 30s, 5m, 1h) up to N attempts
  • Dead letter: failed-after-retries items go to a queue/table for human review
  • Alerting: when dead letter receives X items in Y window → alert Chris
  • Manual replay: easy CLI / dashboard to replay a dead-letter item after fix

Step 5 — Test paths end-to-end

For every integration, test:

  • Happy path — normal payload, normal result
  • Bad payload — schema validation fails → 400, logged
  • Auth failure — bad signature / expired token → 401, logged
  • Duplicate event — idempotency check catches it → 200 noop
  • Processing failure — DB down, etc. → 500, dead-letter, retry scheduled
  • Retry then success — transient failure recovers within retry budget
  • Retry exhausted — permanent failure → dead letter + alert

Step 6 — Maintain the integration map

_Shared Context/integration-map.md (create on first run):

# Integration Map

## Active integrations

### Stripe webhook → Matrix
- **Event types**: payment_intent.succeeded, charge.refunded, etc.
- **Endpoint**: `/api/webhooks/stripe`
- **Signature verification**: yes (STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET)
- **Idempotency**: event.id stored in `processed_events` table
- **Failure**: retry 3x w/ backoff; dead-letter to `webhook_failures` table; alert on > 5/hr
- **Owner**: Chris

### GHL contact.created webhook → Matrix
- ...

### Matrix → Retell agent end → GHL contact note
- ...

### Matrix → Shippo shipment.created → GHL contact update
- ...

### (per integration)

Step 7 — Output

Output structure

# Integration Built: <Source> → <Target>

## Spec
- **Systems**: <A> ↔ <B>
- **Direction**: one-way / two-way / event-driven
- **Trigger**: <webhook / cron / user action>
- **Auth**: <method>
- **Idempotency**: <key strategy>
- **Latency budget**: <ms>

## Files changed
- `<path>` — created / modified

## Schema
**Inbound payload**: <Zod schema summary>
**Outbound side-effects**: <list>

## Failure handling
- **Retry policy**: <details>
- **Dead-letter destination**: <path / table>
- **Alert threshold**: <X failures in Y window>
- **Manual replay**: <how>

## Test results
- [x] Happy path
- [x] Bad payload → 400
- [x] Auth failure → 401
- [x] Duplicate → 200 noop
- [x] Processing failure → 500 + dead letter
- [x] Retry then success
- [x] Retry exhausted → dead letter + alert

## Integration map updated
- `_Shared Context/integration-map.md` — new entry added

## ✅ I handled
- Implementation
- Schema validation
- Idempotency
- Failure handling + dead-letter
- Full path testing
- Integration map update

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Review PR
- Approve any new env vars / secrets
- Decide alert routing (Slack / SMS / email)

## 🚫 You only
- Merge + deploy to production
- Approve any retry / dead-letter policy that affects customer-facing flows
- Approve any new vendor relationship (handoff to `vendor-management`)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-integration-<source>-<target>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • No idempotency on writes — duplicate events double-charge / double-create
  • Bare try/catch swallowing errors — silent failures
  • No dead letter — failed events lost forever
  • No signature verification on webhooks — security risk
  • Synchronous processing of heavy work — webhook timeouts
  • Two-way sync without conflict rules — data drift
  • Stale integration map — debugging next incident is brutal

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Create / modify webhook receivers
  • ✅ Implement retry + dead-letter logic
  • ✅ Update _Shared Context/integration-map.md
  • ✅ Run end-to-end tests in staging
  • ⚠️ Add new env vars / secrets (Chris approves)
  • ⚠️ Deploy to production (per devops-deployment)
  • 🚫 Manually replay dead-letter items affecting customers without approval
  • 🚫 Disable failure-handling for "easier debugging" — never

Pairing

Inputs from: engineering-lead (integration architecture), backend-dev (business logic the integration triggers), database-schema (data layer) Outputs to: devops-deployment (deploy), code-qa (review), security-inspector (signature verification check) Chains with: vendor-management (vendor APIs catalogued there), workflow-automation-builder (GHL-side glue)

One-liner reminder

Schema everything. Idempotency always. Signature verify webhooks. Dead-letter failures. Map all active integrations. Test all 7 paths.

skills.name: Interview Scorecard
skills.description: Design structured interview scorecards per role. Defines competencies, behavioral questions, work-sample exercises, scoring rubric. Standardizes evaluation across interviewers. Companion to recruiting — recruiting screens applicants; this skill makes interviews fair and comparable.
skills.intent: Replace gut-feel interviews with structured assessment. For each role, define 4-6 competencies, 2-3 behavioral questions per competency, a work-sample task, and a 1-5 scoring rubric. Same scorecard across all interviewers per role — calibration baked in.
best_for: Designing scorecards for a new role
Updating scorecards based on past hire outcomes
skills.estimated_time: 60-90 min per role
skills.body_html:
Interview Scorecard

The structured-interview designer. Same questions, same rubric, comparable scores.

When to use

  • New role needs interview design (companion to recruiting)
  • Existing role's interview process feels random / inconsistent
  • Past hires aren't working out → revisit competencies
  • Multiple interviewers need calibration

When NOT to use

  • Single-interview screen (use ad-hoc questions if needed; no need to formalize)
  • Reference checks (different process)
  • Performance reviews of existing employees (use performance-management planned)

Required inputs

  1. Role title + level — what we're hiring for
  2. Critical competencies — top 4-6 capabilities this role needs
  3. Failure modes — what causes hires in this role to fail (informs interview focus)
  4. Stakeholders — who's interviewing (each interviewer can own 1-2 competencies)

If 2 missing, ask: "What are the top 4-6 capabilities this person MUST have?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Pick competencies (4-6 max)

For each role, identify the load-bearing capabilities. Examples:

SDR / Sales:

  • Discovery / question-asking skill
  • Resilience / follow-through
  • Brand-voice alignment (clinical + peer-level)
  • CRM hygiene + process
  • Coachability

Designer:

  • Brand alignment (cinematic + clinical)
  • Visual hierarchy + typography
  • Cross-functional collaboration
  • Speed + iteration
  • Portfolio quality + breadth

Engineer:

  • Stack fit (Next.js + Tailwind + TS for Matrix's stack)
  • Code quality + testing discipline
  • Problem decomposition
  • Communication (async + sync)
  • Ownership / autonomy

Account Manager:

  • Provider empathy + clinical literacy
  • Retention orchestration
  • Cross-functional escalation
  • Data literacy (reading account health)
  • Communication

Marketer:

  • Brand voice mastery
  • Channel-specific expertise
  • Analytics + measurement
  • Creative brief authoring
  • Stakeholder management

Step 2 — Behavioral questions per competency

For each competency, 2-3 behavioral questions (STAR format: Situation → Task → Action → Result):

Competency: <name>

Q1: "Tell me about a time you [behavior demonstrating competency]."
   Probe: What was the context? What did you actually do? What happened?
   Looking for: <specific signals + anti-signals>

Q2: "Walk me through a [scenario] you handled that didn't go well."
   Probe: What would you do differently? What did you learn?
   Looking for: <self-awareness + growth signals>

Q3: "How would you approach [hypothetical scenario specific to Matrix]?"
   Looking for: <approach signals — process + judgment>

Step 3 — Work-sample / take-home (if applicable)

For roles where sample work is critical:

Role Sample task
Designer Brief: "Design a landing page hero for a new offer (provided brief). 4 hours max. Submit Figma."
Engineer Take-home: "Build a small feature with our actual stack. 2-4 hours. Submit GitHub repo."
Copywriter Brief: "Write a cold email to for . Brand voice from this guide. 30 min."
SDR Role-play: "Cold call this scenario, here's the context, persona will be played by ."
Account Manager Scenario: "Review this account-health report. What's your save play?"

Keep work-samples ≤ 4 hours unless paid. Disrespectful otherwise.

Step 4 — Scoring rubric (1-5)

For each question / sample, define anchors:

Score Anchor
5 — Exemplary Beyond expectations. Demonstrated mastery + nuance + adaptability.
4 — Strong Solid demonstration of competency. Would hire on this signal.
3 — Acceptable Adequate but not impressive. Hire only if other signals strong.
2 — Concerning Notable gap. Hire only if growth signals + role allows ramp.
1 — Block Cannot do this role. Hard decline.

Step 5 — Build the scorecard doc

# Interview Scorecard: <Role Title>

## Role
**Title**: <title>
**Level**: <level>
**Reports to**: <name>
**Comp range**: $<X> – $<Y>

## Competencies (4-6)
1. <Competency 1>
2. <Competency 2>
...

## Interview structure
| Stage | Length | Owner | Competencies covered |
|---|---|---|---|
| Phone screen | 30 min | Recruiter / Chris | Brand alignment, basic fit |
| Hiring manager interview | 60 min | Hiring manager | Top 2 competencies |
| Peer interview | 45 min | Future peer | 1-2 competencies + culture |
| Work sample | 2-4 hr (async) | Candidate | Domain skill |
| Founder interview | 30-45 min | Chris | Ownership / autonomy / Matrix fit |
| Reference check | 30 min | Recruiter | Confirmation of all |

## Questions + rubrics

### Competency 1: <Name>
- **Q1**: <question>
  - Looking for: <signals>
  - Anti-signals: <red flags>
  - Score 1-5
- **Q2**: <question>
  - ...

### Competency 2: <Name>
[same format]

### (... repeat per competency)

## Work-sample task
**Description**: <brief>
**Time budget**: <≤ 4 hours>
**Submission**: <format>
**Evaluation criteria**:
- <criterion 1> (1-5)
- <criterion 2> (1-5)
- ...

## Scoring + decision

### Score sheet (per interviewer)
| Question / sample | Score (1-5) | Notes |
|---|---|---|

### Decision rule
- Total score ≥ <X> → advance / offer
- Mixed signals → debrief with all interviewers, calibrate
- Any 1-score (block) on a load-bearing competency → decline

### Calibration
- All interviewers must use same rubric
- Post-interview debrief required (everyone shares score + reason BEFORE discussing)
- Disagreements → senior interviewer mediates

Step 6 — Output

Output structure

# Interview Scorecard Generated: <Role>

## Scorecard
**Path**: `_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-scorecard-<role>.md`

## Competencies (4-6)
<list>

## Interview structure
<table of stages>

## Work-sample (if any)
<brief description>

## Scoring + decision rule
<summary>

## Calibration plan
- All interviewers reviewed scorecard before interviews
- Post-interview debrief required
- Senior interviewer mediates disagreements

## ✅ I handled
- Designed competencies + behavioral questions
- Built rubric anchors
- Specified work-sample (if applicable)
- Defined decision rule

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris / hiring team)
- Review + approve competencies
- Approve work-sample task design
- Approve comp range disclosure
- Train interviewers on the scorecard before any interview

## 🚫 You only
- Conduct the interviews
- Make hiring decisions
- Disregard / override scorecard (please don't — it's there for a reason)

## Handoff
**To**: `recruiting` (uses scorecard to screen), hiring team (uses for interviews), HR onboarding (post-hire)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-scorecard-<role>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Generic "tell me about yourself" interviews — no signal
  • Single interviewer for all signals — biased
  • Work sample > 4 hours without compensation — disrespectful
  • No rubric anchors — scores become opinion not signal
  • Skipping calibration debrief — first-impression bias dominates
  • Hiring on culture-fit only — leads to monoculture; use behavioral evidence
  • "Vibe check" replacing structured assessment — bad outcomes correlate

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design scorecards
  • ✅ Author questions + rubrics
  • ✅ Update past scorecards based on hire outcomes
  • ⚠️ Approve work-sample task (Chris + hiring manager)
  • ⚠️ Approve interview structure changes
  • 🚫 Conduct interviews
  • 🚫 Make hiring decisions
  • 🚫 Disregard rubric in actual hiring

Pairing

Inputs from: recruiting (role context), past hire outcomes (what worked / didn't), Matrix's brand-guide (voice alignment competency) Outputs to: recruiting (screens applicants), hiring team (interviews), pmo-operator (hiring project tracking) Chains with: recruiting (always — companion), compliance-qa (interview questions reviewed for prohibited topics)

One-liner reminder

4-6 competencies. 2-3 behavioral questions per. Work sample ≤ 4 hr. Rubric with anchors. Same scorecard, all interviewers. Calibration debrief required.

skills.name: Inventory Manager
skills.description: Own stock levels per variant — on-hand, reserved, available — plus reorder points, low-stock alerts, restock workflows, and audit. Hooks into cart (reservation), payment (commit), refund (restock), return (inspect + restock or write-off). Net new skill.
skills.intent: Keep the inventory table accurate or the entire ordering motion lies to customers. Every movement (sale, refund, return, manual adjust) writes an `inventory_movements` row. Reorder points trigger procurement workflows. Low stock triggers UI signals. Out-of-stock blocks add-to-cart at the source.
skills.estimated_time: Full setup: 4-6 hours. Single variant adjustment: 2 min.
skills.body_html:
Inventory Manager

The truth source for stock. Reserve on cart. Commit on paid. Restock on refund/return.

When to use

  • New store needs inventory tracking
  • Adding reorder-point automation
  • Low-stock alert system
  • Inventory audit / reconciliation
  • Multi-warehouse split

When NOT to use

  • Catalog setup → use product-catalog-builder
  • Procurement / vendor PO → use vendor-management
  • Demand forecasting → use analytics-architect

Required inputs

  1. Per-variant initial stock — count + location
  2. Reorder points — at what on-hand count do we trigger a restock?
  3. Reorder quantities — how many to order each time
  4. Single warehouse or multi? — affects table shape
  5. Sell-through-stock policy — when to allow back-order vs block?

Data shape

inventory (variant_id PK, on_hand int, reserved int, reorder_point int, reorder_qty int, location varchar)
  available = on_hand - reserved

inventory_movements (
  id, variant_id, delta (signed int), kind, ref_table, ref_id, actor, note, created_at
)
  -- kind: sale | refund | return | manual_adjust | restock_receipt | reserve | reserve_release | write_off | transfer

Movement matrix

Event Movement Notes
Add to cart reserve (delta=−1 to reserved, +0 to on_hand) Reservation expires with cart
Cart abandoned / expired reserve_release (delta=+1 to reserved as decrement) Release inventory
Order paid sale (delta=−1 to on_hand) + reserve_release Commit
Order cancelled (pre-fulfillment, post-payment) refund (delta=+1 to on_hand) Returns to sellable
