Refund issued (no return) refund (delta=+1) OR write-off Per CS judgment + condition
Return received (good condition) return (delta=+1) Sellable
Return received (damaged) write_off (delta=0, kind logged) Not sellable
Manual adjustment manual_adjust (signed) Always logged with actor + reason
Restock receipt (PO arrived) restock_receipt (delta=+N) Tied to PO ref
Transfer between locations transfer (paired entries) Multi-warehouse

Workflow surfaces

Reservation on cart-add

POST /api/cart/items
  ↓
1. Check available = on_hand - reserved
2. If available < requested qty → 409 with current available + suggestion
3. If available >= requested:
   - Insert inventory_movement (kind='reserve', delta_to_reserved=+qty)
   - Set cart_items reservation expires_at = now + 15 min (or cart expiration)
   - Return ok
4. Background job: release expired reservations every 1 min

Sale commit on payment

Stripe webhook: payment confirmed
  ↓
1. For each order_item:
   - inventory_movement (kind='sale', delta=−qty to on_hand)
   - Release the matching reserve
2. If on_hand <= reorder_point → fire low-stock alert
3. If on_hand = 0 → set product_variants.status = 'out_of_stock' (auto-revert when restocked)

Restock on refund

Refund issued (via order-lifecycle-manager)
  ↓
1. Decision: is item restockable?
   - Yes (no shipment) → inventory_movement (kind='refund', delta=+qty)
   - Yes (returned good condition) → inventory_movement (kind='return', delta=+qty)
   - No (damage / regulated / hygiene) → inventory_movement (kind='write_off', delta=0, note=reason)
2. If variant was out_of_stock → flip back to active

Reorder-point automation

Daily job:
  For each variant where on_hand <= reorder_point AND no open PO:
    1. Insert into procurement_queue
    2. Notify vendor-management
    3. Email Chris + ops lead
    4. Add to weekly procurement review

Low-stock UI signals

  • on_hand <= 5 (or 1.5× avg daily sales) → show "Only N left" on PDP
  • on_hand = 0 → hide add-to-cart; show "Notify me when back in stock" CTA
  • "Notify me" subscriptions stored in restock_notifications; fire on restock

Audit / reconciliation

  • Weekly: total on_hand per variant should equal sum of all movements
  • Variance > 5 units OR > 5% of on_hand → escalate to ops lead
  • Audit log captures every manual_adjust with actor + reason

Multi-warehouse (optional, future)

If multi-warehouse:

  • inventory keyed on (variant_id, location) instead of just variant_id
  • Cart reservation picks closest warehouse with stock
  • Order may split across warehouses → multiple shipments
  • Transfer movements when rebalancing

For now: single warehouse default; document multi as future expansion.

Anti-patterns

  • Direct UPDATE on inventory.on_hand — must always go through a movement
  • No reservation at cart-add — overselling on race conditions
  • Reservation never expires — abandoned carts hoard inventory forever
  • Restocking damaged returns — quality complaints + bad reviews
  • No reorder-point automation — stockouts surprise no one but us
  • "Notify me" subscriptions never firing — customer trust killed
  • Negative inventory allowed silently — symptom of oversell + must be loud
  • Manual adjust without reason — audit log useless

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build movement tables + automation
  • ✅ Apply schema in staging
  • ✅ Run reconciliation reports
  • ⚠️ Adjust inventory in prod > 10 units (Chris + reason)
  • ⚠️ Write-off > $X (per approval-rules.md)
  • 🚫 Override negative inventory silently
  • 🚫 Modify inventory_movements rows after-the-fact (append corrections; never edit history)

Pairing

Inputs from: ecommerce-schema (tables), product-catalog-builder (variants), ecommerce-backend (cart + checkout triggers), order-lifecycle-manager (refund/return triggers) Outputs to: vendor-management (reorder triggers), ecommerce-storefront (stock signals), analytics-architect (inventory turn, stockout rates), account-manager (key-customer stockout alerts) Chains with: bookkeeping (inventory valuation for monthly close), cfo-finance (working-capital tied up in inventory)

One-line reminder

Every movement logged. Reserve at cart. Commit at paid. Restock at refund/return (or write-off). Reorder point automated. Negative inventory loud.

skills.name: Investor Relations
skills.description: Keep investor-facing materials current — pitch deck (v1, v2), monthly investor update, data room, financial section, KPI snapshots. Drafts; never sends without Chris approval. For Matrix: maintains matrix-investor-deck v1 + v2, ready for outreach cycles.
skills.estimated_time: 60-120 min monthly; 30 min per ad-hoc request
skills.body_html:
Investor Relations

Keeps investor materials current. Drafts only. Chris signs/sends.

When to use

  • Monthly investor update (cadence)
  • Pre-investor-outreach cycle (deck refresh)
  • Due-diligence document request from a prospective investor
  • Board meeting prep
  • Annual report

When NOT to use

  • Internal stakeholder updates (use weekly-review or pmo-operator)
  • Customer-facing updates (different motion)
  • Tax filings (CPA)
  • Press / PR (PR agent planned)

Required inputs

  1. Audience — existing investors / prospective investors / board / advisors
  2. Format — deck / one-pager / email update / full report / data-room set
  3. Period — month / quarter / annual
  4. Specific asks / focus — if responding to a specific request

If 2 missing, ask: "What format — deck, email update, data room, or specific document?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Pull current state

For Matrix investor materials, pull from:

  • Existing decks: ~/Projects/Enterprise Synergy/matrix-investor-deck (v1) + matrix-investor-deck-v2 (v2)
  • KPIs: from analytics-architect dashboard
  • Financials: from cfo-finance (revenue, MRR, runway, margins)
  • Customer count: from account-manager aggregates
  • Pipeline: from ghl-daily-pulse
  • Operating updates: from weekly-review summaries
  • Compliance / certifications: from security-inspector posture
  • Product roadmap: from pmo-operator master board

Step 2 — Refresh the deck (if applicable)

Standard investor deck structure (Matrix v2 baseline):

1. Cover (logo, tagline, audience-specific date)

2. The problem (provider supply pain — specific, sourced)

3. The solution (clinic growth infrastructure — what we are)

4. Why now (regulatory / market / tech tailwinds)

5. Product (catalog + service + systems — high-level)

6. Market size (TAM / SAM / SOM — sourced)

7. Traction (revenue, customers, growth — last 6-12 months)

8. Business model (per-product + service + affiliate)

9. Unit economics (CAC / LTV / payback / margin)

10. Go-to-market (channels, conversion, expansion)

11. Competition (the 3-tier mix from market-research + our FOMU wedge)

12. Team (Chris + key advisors / partners)

13. Roadmap (next 12-24 months)

14. The ask (round size, use of funds, valuation parameters)

15. Appendix (financials, case studies, regulatory posture)

For each section, identify what needs refresh from current state.

Step 3 — Apply brand voice + compliance

Per _Shared Context/brand-guide.md:

  • Authoritative + clinical (investor-grade authority)
  • No exclamation marks
  • No hype
  • Sourced claims only (every stat cited)

Per compliance-rules.md:

  • No FDA-approval claims unless literally true
  • No financial projections labeled as guarantees
  • Forward-looking statements properly disclaimed
  • Trademark + IP claims accurate

Step 4 — Generate the monthly investor update (email)

Format (per _Templates/weekly-report-template.md investor variant):

# Matrix Advanced Solutions — Investor Update <Month YYYY>

## Headline
<one sentence: the story this month>

## Numbers
- Revenue: $<X> (vs $<Y> prior month, <delta>%)
- MRR: $<X>
- New customers: <N>
- Active customers: <N>
- Cash runway: <N> months
- Pipeline value: $<X>

## Big wins
- <Concrete shipped item with impact>
- <Concrete shipped item with impact>

## Operating highlights
- <Department milestone>
- <Department milestone>

## Asks for investors / advisors
- <Specific intro request>
- <Specific advice request>
- (Or: "no asks this month")

## Next 30 days
1. <Specific priority>
2. <Specific priority>
3. <Specific priority>

## Risks + flags
- <Material risk with mitigation>

## Open questions
- <If any — invites engagement>

— Chris

Step 5 — Data room organization

Standard sections (each in its folder):

_Logs/investor/data-room/
├── 01-company-overview/
│   ├── deck-v2.pdf
│   ├── one-pager.pdf
│   └── executive-summary.pdf
├── 02-financials/
│   ├── p-and-l-trailing-12-months.xlsx
│   ├── balance-sheet.xlsx
│   ├── cash-flow.xlsx
│   ├── revenue-by-product.xlsx
│   └── unit-economics-model.xlsx
├── 03-customers/
│   ├── customer-list-anonymized.xlsx
│   ├── case-studies/
│   └── testimonials/
├── 04-product/
│   ├── product-catalog.pdf
│   ├── service-tiers.pdf
│   └── roadmap.pdf
├── 05-team/
│   ├── leadership-bios.pdf
│   └── advisors.pdf
├── 06-legal-compliance/
│   ├── corporate-structure.pdf
│   ├── ip-trademarks.pdf
│   ├── compliance-posture-summary.pdf
│   └── (BAAs, vendor contracts — high-sensitivity, separate access)
├── 07-market/
│   ├── market-sizing.pdf
│   ├── competitive-landscape.pdf
│   └── regulatory-context.pdf
└── 08-fundraising/
    ├── round-terms.pdf
    └── use-of-funds.pdf

Each document version-controlled. Access logged.

Step 6 — Track investor engagement

Per investor / prospective investor:

  • Stage (cold / met / interested / DD / committed / passed)
  • Last touch date
  • Materials shared (which docs from data room)
  • Asks made / fulfilled
  • Next step + date

Surface for Chris weekly: who's overdue for an update, who's at risk of cooling.

Step 7 — Output

Output structure

# Investor Materials Refresh: <Period>

## Headline
<one sentence>

## Materials refreshed
- [x] Monthly investor update (email): `_Logs/investor/updates/<month>.md`
- [x] Deck v2 KPIs refreshed: `_Logs/investor/deck-v2-<month>.pdf`
- [x] Data room: financials section updated
- [ ] Data room: customer section (awaiting case study approval)

## KPIs snapshot
| KPI | Value | Trend (3-mo) | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| Revenue | $<X> | <up/flat/down> | cfo-finance |
| MRR | $<X> | <trend> | cfo-finance |
| Active customers | <N> | <trend> | account-manager |
| Cash runway | <N> mo | <trend> | cfo-finance |
| ... | | | |

## Forward-looking statements (disclaimed in materials)
- <statement with disclaimer>

## Engagement tracker

### Active conversations
| Investor | Stage | Last touch | Next step | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <name> | <stage> | <date> | <action> | Chris |

### Overdue follow-ups (> 30 days)
| Investor | Last touch | Suggested re-engage approach |
|---|---|---|

## ✅ I handled
- Pulled current state from cfo-finance / analytics / account-manager
- Refreshed deck KPIs (data layer only)
- Drafted monthly investor email
- Organized data room
- Surfaced engagement gaps

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Review draft investor email before send
- Approve deck changes before re-export
- Approve any case study / testimonial inclusion (real customer)
- Sign off on data room sharing with new prospect

## 🚫 You only
- Send investor communications (you, not me)
- Sign term sheets / SAFEs / docs
- Share data room access (you control access logs)
- Negotiate terms

## Handoff
**To**: Chris (review + send), counsel (for any contract / term review), `cfo-finance` (financial accuracy double-check)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-investor-relations-<period>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Stale KPIs in the deck — every share-cycle refreshes
  • Hype language — investors detect immediately; brand-voice discipline applies
  • Forward statements without disclaimer — securities-law exposure
  • Auto-sending investor emails — never. Chris reviews each.
  • Sharing data room broadly — access logged, controlled
  • No engagement tracker — investors cool when not updated; need rhythm

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull current KPIs from connected sources
  • ✅ Refresh deck data layer (numbers, charts)
  • ✅ Draft monthly investor email
  • ✅ Organize data room
  • ✅ Track engagement state
  • ⚠️ Send investor email (Chris signs/sends)
  • ⚠️ Share data room access (Chris approves access)
  • ⚠️ Add new case study / testimonial (with documented permission)
  • 🚫 Sign / negotiate / commit
  • 🚫 Make forward projections without "forward-looking" disclaimer
  • 🚫 Add advisor / board claims without their approval

Pairing

Inputs from: cfo-finance (financials), analytics-architect (KPIs), account-manager (customer aggregates), pmo-operator (roadmap), market-research (competitive context) Outputs to: Chris (final review + send), counsel (term review), prospective investors (via Chris) Chains with: weekly-review (engagement reminders), creative-director (deck visual refresh)

One-liner reminder

KPIs always current. Brand voice (no hype). Disclaimers on forward statements. Drafts only. Chris signs / sends / negotiates. Data room access logged.

skills.name: Invoice Generator
skills.description: Generate a professional PDF invoice from a few details. Use this skill when the user says "create an invoice", "invoice this client", "bill them", "send an invoice", "generate invoice", "make an invoice for", or any variation of wanting to bill someone for work done.
skills.body_html:
Invoice Generator

Create a clean, professional invoice as a PDF from a few simple inputs. No templates needed, no software to install. Just tell it who to bill, what for, and how much.

Instructions

Step 1: Gather Invoice Details

From the user's request, identify:

  • Client name — Who is being billed?
  • Client email or address — Optional but include if provided
  • Your business name — Pull from CLAUDE.md if available
  • Invoice number — Auto-generate if not provided (format: INV-YYYY-001)
  • Date — Today's date unless specified
  • Due date — Default to Net 30 unless specified
  • Line items — What services or products are being billed?
  • Amounts — Price per item or flat rate
  • Tax rate — Only include if the user mentions tax
  • Payment instructions — Bank details, PayPal, Venmo, etc. Pull from CLAUDE.md or ask.

If you have enough to build the invoice, build it. Only ask if you're missing the client name or the amounts.

Step 2: Calculate Totals

For each line item:

  • Quantity x Rate = Line Total

Then:

  • Subtotal = Sum of all line totals
  • Tax = Subtotal x Tax Rate (if applicable)
  • Total Due = Subtotal + Tax

Double-check your math. Invoices with wrong totals destroy credibility.

Step 3: Build the Invoice

Use clean, professional formatting:

[Your Business Name]
[Your Address / Contact — if available]

INVOICE

Invoice #: [Number]
Date: [Issue Date]
Due: [Due Date]
Bill To: [Client Name / Company]

---

| Description              | Qty | Rate     | Amount   |
|--------------------------|-----|----------|----------|
| [Service/Product]        | [X] | $[X.XX]  | $[X.XX]  |
| [Service/Product]        | [X] | $[X.XX]  | $[X.XX]  |

---

                                    Subtotal: $[X.XX]
                                    Tax (X%): $[X.XX]
                                    TOTAL DUE: $[X.XX]

Payment Terms: [Net 30 / Due on Receipt / etc.]
Payment Method: [Bank transfer / PayPal / Venmo / etc.]

[Payment details if provided]

Thank you for your business.

Step 4: Generate the PDF

Use Python with reportlab or fpdf to generate a properly formatted PDF invoice. Save it to the user's workspace folder.

Name the file: Invoice-[ClientName]-[Number].pdf

Step 5: Deliver

Provide the file link and a one-line summary:

  • Invoice total
  • Due date
  • Client name

Offer: "Want me to adjust anything, add notes, or create a recurring template for this client?"

Rules

  • Always double-check math. Subtotals and totals must be correct.
  • Keep it clean and professional. No decorative clutter.
  • Use the user's business name and details from CLAUDE.md when available.
  • Default to USD unless the user specifies another currency.
  • Never fabricate payment details. If you don't have them, leave a placeholder.

skills.name: Ip Continuous Improvement
skills.description: Weekly + quarterly auditor of the IP department — patent prosecution pipeline, trademark filings + renewals, copyright registration backlog, trade-secret hygiene, competitor IP scan, counsel-spend hygiene, defensive registrations, IP-risk register intersections. Completes the CI pattern across all 5 priority departments (Voice + GHL + E-com + Formulation + IP).
skills.intent: IP assets degrade if you stop paying attention — maintenance fees miss, specimens of use lapse, competitor filings silently encroach, defensive registrations expire. This CI engine sweeps the IP surface weekly + quarterly, surfaces drift, and proposes evidence-based changes. Counsel decides the legal; this skill drives the operational rhythm.
best_for: Weekly Friday review (4:15pm PT — last of the CI cluster)
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 60-90 min. Quarterly deep audit: 4-8 hours.
skills.body_html:
IP Continuous Improvement

The auditor + proposer for everything IP. Patents + trademarks + copyrights + trade secrets + domains + handles. Maintenance never misses. Drift never lasts.

Lineage

Mirror of ghl-continuous-improvement + voice-call-reviewer + ecommerce-continuous-improvement + formulation-continuous-improvement. Same shape; IP-specific surfaces.

When to use

  • Weekly Friday review (4:15pm PT — last of the Friday CI cluster)
  • Quarterly deep audit
  • Pre-investor diligence (IP posture is a major question)
  • Pre-acquisition diligence (IP cleanliness can make or break a deal)
  • Pre-major-disclosure event (conference, press, paper) — IP gate before any public step
  • When a competitor IP signal surfaces

When NOT to use

  • Specific IP matter / filing → use ip-lead direct
  • Naming clearance → use trademark-clearance / product-naming-gate
  • Patent strategy → use patent-strategy
  • Real-time legal threat → counsel immediately

Surfaces audited

1. Patent pipeline

  • Provisional applications: 12-month clock per filing — convert / let lapse decision approaching?
  • Non-provisional applications: office action status per matter
  • Granted patents: maintenance fees due (3.5 / 7.5 / 11.5 year)
  • PCT applications: 30-month national-phase decisions pending
  • Patent landscape — new competitor filings affecting our space

2. Trademark pipeline

  • Pending applications: office action status
  • Recently-allowed (ITU): statement-of-use deadline
  • Registered marks: Section 8 (5-6 year), Section 9 (10-year) renewals
  • Specimens of use current per registered mark
  • Defensive marks — still in use? (non-use → cancellation risk)
  • Foreign filings — Madrid Protocol vs national renewals

3. Copyright registrations

  • Software code: rolling annual registrations (matrix-store, MAS, ai-command-center, etc.)
  • Brand assets registered (logos, packaging design)
  • Educational content (Peptide Map curriculum)

4. Trade secret hygiene

  • Access controls current per _Shared Context/ip-portfolio.md
  • NDAs on file for every party with access
  • No leaks in public copy (per compliance-phrase-detector ALM DT trade-secret rule)
  • Quarterly access audit

5. Domains + handles

  • Renewal dates per domain (target buffer: renew 60 days before expiration)
  • Squatting / cybersquatting issues
  • Missing defensive registrations for active products
  • Social handles — still owned / not lapsed

6. Competitive scan

  • New competitor filings (USPTO + WIPO)
  • Industry trademark patterns shifting
  • Potentially conflicting marks
  • Patent grants in our active space

7. Counsel relationship

  • Matter list reconciled with our records
  • Hourly spend vs budget per matter
  • Invoice review (scope alignment per ip-counsel-coordination)
  • Counsel-firm performance — turnaround, quality, value

8. Cross-references

  • risk-register.md IP risks current
  • ALM DT family disclosure-status reconciliation (per alm-dt-platform.md)
  • Steve's publication-as-prior-art map current
  • Joint-IP ownership-chain questions resolved

Workflow (weekly)

Step 1 — Pull current state

  • Active patent matters per _Logs/ip/matters/
  • Trademark application status from USPTO TSDR API or counsel report
  • Recent office actions (next 30 days due)
  • Maintenance fees due (next 90 days)
  • Defensive domain / handle renewal calendar
  • Counsel invoice ledger
  • Last quarter's quarterly audit deltas

Step 2 — Compute KPIs

  • active matters

  • matters with deadlines in next 30 / 90 days

  • $ IP spend YTD vs budget

  • competitor filings of concern (this period)

  • defensive gaps (active products without trademark filed)

  • Trade-secret access audit pass/fail

Step 3 — Spot patterns

  • Same matter type repeatedly delayed → counsel-firm capacity issue
  • Same office-action reason → recurring claim-drafting issue
  • Same competitor filing same categories → strategic threat

Step 4 — Diagnose top 3-5 issues

  • Hypothesis (not just symptom)
  • Evidence (matter logs + counsel comms)
  • Confidence level

Step 5 — Propose changes

  • Per issue: specific action + executor + expected impact + effort + risk
  • Examples:
    • "File Section 8 use declaration for MARK-X by [date] to avoid cancellation"
    • "Counsel-call to revise claim strategy on patent app MAS-04 after 2 office-action rejections"
    • "Register MAS-PEP-RETA-* trademark family before launch (currently blocking)"
    • "Acquire @musclelock TikTok handle (currently held by inactive squatter — UDRP feasible)"

Step 6 — Approval gate

  • Chris approves cost / strategic decisions
  • Counsel approves legal items
  • Compliance-officer approves any cross-cutting items

Step 7 — Hand off

  • Per approved proposal: invoke ip-counsel-coordination (counsel work) OR trademark-clearance (clearance) OR patent-strategy (strategy refinement)

Step 8 — Measure (1-4 weeks later)

  • Per shipped action: outcome captured

Step 9 — Log

  • Weekly: _Logs/ip-improvement/<YYYY-WW>-weekly.md
  • Quarterly: _Logs/ip-improvement/<YYYY-Q>-deep-audit.md

Quarterly deep audit

Adds to weekly:

  • Full portfolio inventory (every patent / trademark / copyright / domain / handle / trade secret)
  • Maintenance calendar refresh (next 12 months)
  • Competitive landscape report (new filings + grants)
  • Trade-secret access audit (who has access to what; still warranted?)
  • Foreign filing strategy review (where do we need to expand?)
  • Licensing inbound/outbound review
  • Counsel-firm performance review (annual: do we keep this firm?)
  • Strategic IP recommendations (what to file next based on roadmap?)
  • Spend vs budget reconciliation

Output template (weekly)

# IP CI: Week <YYYY-WW>

## Snapshot
- Active patents (pending): <N>
- Active patents (granted): <N>
- Active trademarks (pending): <N>
- Active trademarks (registered): <N>
- Maintenance fees due next 30d: <N>
- Maintenance fees due next 90d: <N>
- Counsel spend YTD: $<X>
- Defensive gaps: <N> (active products without filed mark)

## Deadlines next 30 days
| Mark / patent | Action | Due | Owner | Notes |

## Diagnosed issues
### 1. <Issue>
- Symptom
- Hypothesis
- Evidence
- Confidence: H/M/L
- Proposal: <change> via `<skill>`
- Expected impact / effort / risk

## Competitive scan
- New filings of concern: <list>
- New grants in our space: <list>
- Action recommended: <yes/no — what>

## Trade-secret hygiene
- Access audit: pass / fail
- Leaks detected this period: <none / list>

## Wins this week
- ...

## Asks for Chris / counsel
- Approve proposals X, Y
- Decisions needed: <specific>

## Logged to
`_Logs/ip-improvement/<YYYY-WW>-weekly.md`

Cross-pollination with other CI engines

  • Formulation CI → IP CI: new formulation → potential patent / trademark filings
  • E-commerce CI → IP CI: new product → mandatory product-naming-gate
  • Compliance officer → IP CI: regulatory class changes can affect mark distinctiveness
  • Voice / GHL CI → IP CI: brand mark use in customer-facing scripts

Anti-patterns

  • Skipping maintenance deadlines — abandonment is irreversible
  • No defensive search — competitors file too-similar marks unchallenged
  • Counsel as researcher — counsel time is for opinion; this CI prepares the work
  • Annual instead of quarterly — annual is too slow for IP portfolio drift
  • Skipping trade-secret hygiene audit — courts won't protect un-protected secrets
  • Letting joint-IP ownership ambiguity persist — every quarter without resolution makes worse
  • No competitive scan — silent encroachment compounds

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read all IP portfolio data
  • ✅ Compute KPIs + maintenance calendar
  • ✅ Run defensive + competitive searches
  • ✅ Draft proposals
  • ⚠️ Approve filings / counsel work (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Approve abandonment (Chris — irreversible)
  • ⚠️ Approve enforcement action (Chris + counsel)
  • 🚫 Make legal commitments
  • 🚫 Disclose trade-secret content for analysis purposes

Pairing

Inputs from: ip-portfolio.md, ip-lead, patent-strategy, trademark-clearance, ip-counsel-coordination, compliance-officer (regulatory intersections), counsel matter list, alm-dt-platform.md, risk-register.md Outputs to: Chris (approval ask), counsel (briefs via ip-counsel-coordination), pmo-operator (ticket tracking), quarterly-review.workflow.md (consumes deep audit) Chains with: voice-call-reviewer + ghl-continuous-improvement + ecommerce-continuous-improvement + formulation-continuous-improvement (Friday CI cluster)

One-liner reminder

Friday weekly + quarterly deep. Maintenance never misses. Defensive + competitive scans every quarter. Counsel decides legal; we drive operational rhythm. The portfolio compounds or erodes.

skills.name: Ip Counsel Coordination
skills.description: Brief, queue, and track external IP counsel work. Prepares counsel briefs (so counsel time is spent on judgment, not gathering), maintains the matter list, tracks deadlines (office actions, maintenance fees, renewals), reviews counsel invoices for scope alignment, and keeps `ip-portfolio.md` current.
skills.intent: External IP counsel is expensive (~$400-700/hr). Every hour they spend collecting information we could have given them is wasted spend. This skill is the operational interface — briefs are tight, deadlines never miss, invoices match scope, and Matrix's IP portfolio stays clean.
best_for: Briefing counsel on new IP matter
Tracking ongoing IP matters (filings, office actions, deadlines)
skills.estimated_time: Per brief: 1-2 hours. Quarterly matter list maintenance: 2-4 hours.
skills.body_html:
IP Counsel Coordination

The counsel interface. Tight briefs. No missed deadlines. Invoices match scope.

When to use

  • New IP matter requires counsel (patent filing, trademark filing, opposition, enforcement, opinion)
  • Office action received (USPTO or foreign)
  • Maintenance fee / renewal approaching
  • Counsel invoice received — review for scope alignment
  • Quarterly matter list refresh
  • Counsel-firm change consideration

When NOT to use

  • IP strategy work (do that first via patent-strategy / trademark-clearance)
  • Non-IP legal matters (corporate counsel; different firm typically)
  • Internal-only IP work (no counsel needed)

Required inputs

  1. Matter type — patent / trademark / copyright / opposition / enforcement / opinion / portfolio review
  2. Strategic brief — from patent-strategy or trademark-clearance or ip-lead
  3. Budget — fee cap if any
  4. Timeline — deadline (especially priority dates, office action responses)
  5. Counsel relationship — primary firm OR specific attorney name

Counsel relationship

Matrix has primary IP counsel relationship (firm + lead attorney name + secondary contact). Maintain in _Shared Context/vendors.md under IP counsel:

  • Firm name + address
  • Lead attorney (relationship owner)
  • Secondary attorney (for capacity / specialty)
  • Hourly rates
  • Engagement letter / billing terms
  • Conflict-check status
  • Specialties (pharma + biotech > general IP; some firms strong in trademark, others in patent prosecution)

Specialty firms used as needed:

  • International filings → may use foreign correspondent counsel via primary firm
  • Litigation → litigation-specialist firm (if it comes to that — escalates beyond this skill)
  • High-stakes patent prosecution → specialist firm for complex cases

Brief structure (the document we send counsel)

Every counsel ask uses this structure:

# Counsel Brief — <Matter Title>

## Matter type
<patent app / trademark app / opposition / enforcement / opinion / etc.>

## Strategic context
<1-2 paragraphs — why this matters, business value, deadline pressure>

## What we need from counsel
- <specific deliverable 1>
- <specific deliverable 2>
- <specific deliverable 3>

## What's already done (no need to redo)
- Prior-art search complete; see `_Logs/ip/inventions/<id>/prior-art.md`
- Strategy brief at `_Logs/ip/inventions/<id>/strategy.md`
- Inventor disclosure form completed
- Trademark clearance search complete; see <link>

## Background facts
- Inventor(s): <names + contributions>
- Disclosure status: <yes/no, details>
- Geographic scope: <US / PCT / specific countries>
- Key dates: <priority date, disclosure deadline, etc.>

## Decisions counsel needs to make / advise on
- <decision 1>
- <decision 2>

## Budget + timeline
- Fee cap: <if any>
- Target completion: <date>
- Hard deadline: <if any>

## Approver
- Chris must approve filing decisions + invoices
- Steve must review chemistry / technical content
- This skill tracks + coordinates

## Attached / referenced
- <list of documents counsel needs>

Matter tracking

Maintain _Logs/ip/matters/ with one file per matter:

# Matter: <Name / Mark / Patent Title>

## Identifiers
- Matter ID: <internal>
- Counsel file ref:
- USPTO serial / app no:
- Reg / patent no (if granted):

## Status
- Phase: pre-filing / filed / examination / office action / allowed / registered / abandoned / litigated
- Last update: <date>
- Next action: <what, by when, owner>

## Deadlines (next 90 days + critical)
- <date> — <action> — <consequence of miss>

## Timeline (history)
- <date> — <event> — <counsel time + cost>

## Communications
- <date> — counsel sent / received: <summary>

## Costs
- Counsel time billed: <hours × rate>
- USPTO / WIPO fees: <amount>
- Other: <amount>
- Running total: <amount>

Deadline calendar

Maintain _Logs/ip/deadlines.md (or in calendar tool) — critical IP deadlines + alerts:

Date Matter Action Lead time alert Owner

Standard lead times:

  • Office action response: 30 days before due (USPTO 3-month response window typical)
  • Maintenance fee: 90 days before due (grace period exists but pay early)
  • PPA conversion: 90 days before 12-month deadline
  • Foreign filing (PCT national phase): 6 months before 30-month deadline
  • Trademark renewal: 6 months before 10-year renewal
  • Trademark Section 8 (use declaration): 9-12 months before 6th anniversary

Office action handling

When USPTO sends an office action:

Step 1: Triage

  • Patent: substantive (claim rejection) vs procedural (formal objection)
  • Trademark: substantive (likelihood of confusion, descriptiveness) vs procedural (specimen, classification)

Step 2: Brief counsel within 1 week

  • Forward office action
  • Request: response strategy + cost estimate + timeline
  • Decision needed by:

Step 3: Strategy decision

  • Respond + argue (typical)
  • Amend claims (patent)
  • Abandon (if matter no longer valuable)
  • Appeal (rare; counsel advises)

Step 4: Approve cost + timeline

  • Chris approves response budget

Step 5: Counsel drafts response

  • Inventor / Matrix reviews
  • File before deadline

Step 6: Update matter file

  • Capture response + outcome

Invoice review

When counsel invoice arrives:

Per-line check

  • Activity matches a known matter (or flag)
  • Time entry reasonable for the work
  • Rate matches engagement letter
  • No surprise charges (international fees, foreign counsel, etc.)

Scope check

  • Did counsel stay within approved scope?
  • Out-of-scope work without preapproval → flag
  • Approve OR query OR push back

Tracking

  • Per-matter running cost
  • Quarterly: total IP spend
  • Annual: IP spend as % of revenue (benchmark)

Payment

  • Approve for finance to pay
  • Hold if any line in dispute

Quarterly matter list maintenance

Every quarter:

  1. Pull active matter list from counsel
  2. Reconcile against our _Logs/ip/matters/ list
  3. Pull _Shared Context/ip-portfolio.md for cross-reference
  4. Identify:
    • Stale matters (no action > 6 months — chase counsel OR consider abandoning)
    • Approaching deadlines (next 6 months — surface to Chris)
    • Renewal decisions (maintain or let lapse?)
  5. Generate quarterly IP report for Chris + counsel call

Anti-patterns

  • Briefs that lack the strategic context — counsel reasonably charges for understanding work
  • No deadline calendar — missed deadlines = abandoned IP
  • Invoice approved without review — over-spend compounds quickly
  • Out-of-scope work approved silently — sets expectation for next time
  • No quarterly review — drift; missed renewals; stale matters
  • Counsel doing prior-art search work — should be done by us in patent-strategy
  • Forwarding raw office actions without triage — wastes counsel's first hours
  • Single-firm reliance for all IP types — some firms weak outside their specialty
  • Approving filings without budget commitment — first invoice surprises

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Brief counsel on approved matters
  • ✅ Track matters + deadlines
  • ✅ Review invoices for scope
  • ✅ Maintain matter files
  • ✅ Quarterly portfolio + counsel relationship review
  • ⚠️ Approve new matter / filing (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Approve invoice payment (Chris if > $X; finance otherwise per approval-rules.md)
  • ⚠️ Abandon matter (Chris — irreversible)
  • ⚠️ Counsel-firm change (Chris)
  • 🚫 Commit Matrix to legal positions without counsel review
  • 🚫 Approve out-of-scope work after the fact silently

Pairing

Inputs from: ip-lead, patent-strategy, trademark-clearance (strategic briefs), Chris (approvals), external counsel (deliverables + invoices) Outputs to: External counsel (briefs + approvals), ip-portfolio.md (registry update), cfo-finance (IP spend), _Logs/ip/matters/ (matter files), Chris (quarterly portfolio report) Chains with: ip-lead (orchestrator), cfo-finance (budget + invoices), vendor-management (counsel firm = vendor)

One-line reminder

Tight briefs save counsel hours. Matter file per matter. Deadlines never missed. Invoices reviewed line-by-line. Quarterly portfolio sweep. Counsel for judgment; we do operations.

skills.name: Ip Lead
skills.description: Department lead for IP (Patents + Trademarks + Copyrights + Trade Secrets). Orchestrates trademark clearance, patent strategy, prior-art search, counsel coordination, and the product-naming-gate that every new product / label / brand passes through. Protects Matrix before anything goes out.
skills.intent: Make sure nothing leaves Matrix unprotected. Every name → trademark cleared. Every novel formulation → patent evaluated. Every label / packaging → IP-cleared. Counsel handles the legal work; this department gathers, prepares, sequences, and tracks so counsel's time is spent on judgment, not paperwork.
best_for: Naming clearance for any public-facing name
Patent strategy on novel formulations / processes / claims
skills.estimated_time: Per intake: 30-60 min orchestration. Counsel work: variable (days to weeks).
skills.body_html:
IP Department Lead

The protection gauntlet. Nothing public-facing leaves Matrix without passing through here.

Department owner

External counsel handles the legal work (drafting patent applications, filing trademarks, prosecuting). This department is the operational layer that prepares, sequences, and tracks — so counsel's time is high-leverage and Matrix is consistently protected.

Chris owns the IP strategy. This skill is the operator.

Composition

This skill composes 4 specialists in the IP department:

Skill Purpose
trademark-clearance Search before commit; clear or pivot on names
patent-strategy What to patent, when; prior-art landscape
ip-counsel-coordination Brief + queue + track counsel work
product-naming-gate Mandatory gate — every new product name passes

Plus inherited: compliance-officer (regulatory + IP intersection), brand-voice-enforcement (some IP touches brand expression).

Coverage

  • Trademarks: brand names, product names, slogans, logos, sub-brands (MuscleLock, Peptide Map, ClinIQ, Matrix Advanced Solutions, plus per-product names)
  • Patents: novel formulations, delivery systems, manufacturing processes, methods of use
  • Copyrights: original written content (educational materials like Peptide Map), software, marketing creative
  • Trade secrets: proprietary formulation specs, customer lists, internal processes — protected via NDA + access control
  • Domain + handles: register early; defensive registrations
  • Industrial design / trade dress: packaging design, container shapes (where distinctive)

When to use

  • New product / formulation entering pre-launch (mandatory IP gate)
  • New name / sub-brand under consideration
  • Steve identifies novel chemistry / process — patent question
  • Competitor IP signal (filing, grant, litigation)
  • Annual / quarterly portfolio review
  • IP licensing inbound or outbound
  • IP enforcement (someone infringing) or defense (we're accused)

When NOT to use

  • Routine product launch with established name (still passes product-naming-gate but lighter)
  • Generic chemistry (no novelty) — note + skip
  • Sub-licensed third-party IP (managed by counsel directly with vendor)

Required inputs

  1. Intake type — formulation IP / naming / brand / copyrighted asset / licensing / enforcement
  2. Description — what we're protecting (or evaluating)
  3. Timeline pressure — is there a launch date that gates this
  4. Budget — affects strategy (filing internationally vs US-only)
  5. Confidentiality status — has this been disclosed publicly yet? (Prior art clock)

The IP intake decision tree

IF formulation / process / novel use:
   → patent-strategy first
   → if patentable + valuable: ip-counsel-coordination (provisional or non-provisional)
   → if not: document defensive publication option
IF product / brand name:
   → trademark-clearance
   → product-naming-gate
   → if clear: counsel files via ip-counsel-coordination
IF educational content / software:
   → copyright is automatic on creation; document
   → register with USPTO for litigation leverage (cheap, worth it)
IF process / spec / customer data:
   → trade-secret route — NDA + access control + classification
IF licensing inbound (vendor offering IP):
   → freedom-to-operate analysis + counsel review
IF enforcement (3rd party infringing OR Matrix accused):
   → immediate counsel; this department supports

Workflow (new formulation IP gate)

Step 1: Intake from formulation-lead
   → New formulation candidate; description + chemistry + use case
   ↓
Step 2: patent-strategy
   → Prior art landscape search
   → Patentability assessment (novelty, non-obviousness, utility)
   → Claim type (composition / process / use)
   → Freedom-to-operate (does this infringe known patents?)
   → Recommendation: file / defensive publish / pass
   ↓
Step 3: Approval gate — Chris
   → Patent filing budget approval
   → Strategy approval
   ↓
Step 4: ip-counsel-coordination
   → Brief counsel with patent-strategy output
   → Provisional patent application (cheap; 12-month priority)
   → Or non-provisional if confidence + budget high
   → Track filing status
   ↓
Step 5: Disclosure controls
   → Until filed: NDA required for any external disclosure
   → Conference papers / public presentations gated
   ↓
Step 6: Post-filing
   → 12-month clock to convert provisional → non-provisional
   → International (PCT) decision at 12 months
   → Continuing apps strategy
   ↓
Step 7: Hand back to formulation-lead
   → IP cleared (or strategy documented) → formulation can proceed to launch

Workflow (new name clearance — product-naming-gate)

Step 1: Intake — proposed name(s)
   → Capture full list of candidates (more = better; many will fail)
   ↓
Step 2: trademark-clearance
   → USPTO TESS search
   → Common-law search (Google, social handles, domains)
   → International (if planned)
   → Phonetic + similar-mark search
   → Per-class evaluation (drug / supplement / cosmetic / etc.)
   → Score: clear / risky / blocked per name
   ↓
Step 3: Product-naming-gate
   → Combine: trademark clearance + brand-voice fit + compliance suitability
     (e.g., no implied medical claims in name)
   → Final recommendation: this name OK / use this alternative / start over
   ↓
Step 4: ip-counsel-coordination
   → File intent-to-use application (ITU) on chosen name
   → Or file actual-use if launching soon
   → Track to registration
   ↓
Step 5: Defensive registrations
   → Domain (matrix-product-X.com)
   → Social handles (@matrixproductX on X / Insta / etc.)
   → International domains if relevant
   ↓
Step 6: Hand back
   → Name cleared + filed → product can launch with name

Workflow (quarterly portfolio review)

Step 1: Inventory current IP
   → Active trademark registrations + applications
   → Active patent applications + grants
   → Copyrights filed
   → Trade-secret documentation status
   → Domains + handles
   ↓
Step 2: Health check per asset
   → Trademark: maintenance fees + actual use (specimens required)
   → Patent: maintenance fees + competitive coverage
   → Copyright: registration kept current
   → Domain: renewals
   ↓
Step 3: Competitive scan
   → New competitor filings (USPTO + foreign)
   → Industry trademark patterns
   → Potentially conflicting marks
   ↓
Step 4: Recommendations
   → File new
   → Abandon stale (don't waste on assets with no business value)
   → Enforce (if infringement detected)
   → Defend (if challenged)
   ↓
Step 5: Counsel session
   → ip-counsel-coordination schedules quarterly counsel call
   → Recommendations reviewed + executed
   ↓
Step 6: Update _Shared Context/ip-portfolio.md
   → Single source of truth for what Matrix owns

IP portfolio registry

Maintain _Shared Context/ip-portfolio.md (create if absent) with:

# IP Portfolio — Matrix Advanced Solutions

## Trademarks
| Mark | Class(es) | Status | Filing date | Reg # | Renewal due |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## Patents
| Title | Type (utility/design/provisional) | Status | Filing date | App / Reg # | Inventor(s) | Maintenance due |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## Copyrights
| Title | Type | Filed | Reg # |
|---|---|---|---|

## Domains
| Domain | Renewal date | Owner contact |
|---|---|---|

## Social handles
| Handle | Platform | Status |
|---|---|---|

## Trade secrets
| Asset | Classification | Access control | Last review |
|---|---|---|---|

## Licensing
| Type | Party | Terms | Renewal |
|---|---|---|---|

Anti-patterns

  • Filing patents on things with no commercial value — maintenance fees compound; only file if defending real revenue or strategic moat
  • Skipping trademark clearance — discovering conflict after marketing spend = forced rename + sunk cost
  • Public disclosure before filing — destroys novelty; 1-year grace exists but don't rely on it
  • Trademark filed but not actually used — gets cancelled; choose only names you'll deploy
  • Trade secrets with no NDA — not protected
  • Counsel doing search work — expensive use of counsel time; we prep, counsel decides
  • Quarterly review skipped — portfolio drifts; renewals miss; competitive position erodes
  • Domain registered without trademark first — confuses the trademark application

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run IP intake + clearance + strategy work
  • ✅ Brief counsel + track counsel work
  • ✅ Maintain IP portfolio registry
  • ✅ File defensive domain / handle registrations
  • ⚠️ Approve patent filing (Chris — budget + strategy)
  • ⚠️ Approve trademark application (Chris — name commitment)
  • ⚠️ Approve abandonment of existing IP (Chris — usually irreversible)
  • ⚠️ Approve enforcement action (Chris + counsel — cost + reputation)
  • 🚫 Make legal commitments without counsel review
  • 🚫 Disclose protectable material publicly before filing

Pairing

Composes: 4 IP specialists Inputs from: formulation-lead (new formulations), product-catalog-builder (new SKUs), creative-director + brand-voice-enforcement (brand work), sub-brands.md, external counsel Outputs to: formulation-lead (IP gate result), product-catalog-builder (cleared names + claims), compliance-officer (regulatory + IP overlap), cfo-finance (IP spend tracking), risk-register.md (IP risks) Chains with: External counsel (every filing), quarterly-review.workflow.md, pmo-operator (IP project tracking)

One-line reminder

Nothing public-facing without IP clearance. Counsel for judgment; this department for prep. Patent before disclosure. Trademark before commit. Portfolio quarterly. Defensive registrations cheap.

skills.name: Kb Add Source
skills.description: Extract a new book, course, PDF, video, or repo into the AI Company knowledge base. Produces a source summary in _Knowledge Base/Source Summaries/ and updates relevant thematic skill files. Use when oversight catches a new source the user wants extracted.
skills.body_html:
KB Add Source

Extract a new source into the knowledge base. This is the oversight skill that keeps the KB current.

Purpose

The KB only stays useful if new sources get extracted in a consistent format. This skill enforces the format.

When to use

  • A new book / course / video / repo arrives in the project folder
  • User says "add this to the knowledge base"
  • Daily-driver oversight catches a pattern repeating across requests (3+ times) and proposes a new source

When NOT to use

  • Just reading a source for personal interest (no extraction needed)
  • The source is already in the KB (check _Knowledge Base/Source Summaries/ first)

Required inputs

  1. Source path — file location (PDF, EPUB, MD, ZIP, folder)
  2. Source type — book / course / video / paper / repo / skill pack / web article
  3. Domain — which thematic KB file(s) it relates to (per 00 - README.md)
  4. Extraction depth — full / sampled / catalog-only

If 1 is missing, ask: "Where's the source — file path or URL?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Inventory the source

For each source type:

  • PDF: open via Read with pages parameter; sample TOC + 3-5 chapter ranges
  • EPUB: unzip to /tmp; find HTML; sample TOC + key chapters
  • Video course: find subtitle files (.vtt, .srt); strip timestamps; sample 5-10 lessons
  • Repo / skill pack: read README.md, SKILL.md, sample 5-10 files
  • Audio (mp3): cannot extract directly; note as "audio source, requires transcription"

Step 2 — Apply the source-summary template

Every source summary lives at _Knowledge Base/Source Summaries/<slug>.md with this shape:

---
source: <Full title — Author / Org (Year)>
domain: <one-line domain>
extracted_for: agent build planning
---

# <Short Title> — Distilled Knowledge

## What this source covers
2-3 sentences. Scope. Note if extraction is partial.

## Core concepts (for planning)
- 8-15 bullets, each one-line + why it matters during planning

## Frameworks / patterns
For each named framework: name, what it solves, key components.

## Quotes worth keeping
3-6 short verbatim quotes.

## Anti-patterns
What the source warns against.

## Implications for agent building
2-5 specific takeaways for agents — what to design differently because of this source.

## Cross-references
Which `_Knowledge Base/NN - *.md` files this source feeds into.

## Planning checklist (if applicable)
6-12 items a planner runs through when applying this source.

Slug naming: <author-or-org>-<short-title>.md, lowercase, kebab.

Step 3 — Decide cross-references

Read _Knowledge Base/00 - README.md. For each thematic file (01-16), ask: "Does this source change anything in this file?"

  • No → no cross-ref needed
  • Yes, adds a pattern → note in source summary's Cross-references section; consider updating the thematic file
  • Yes, contradicts current guidance → flag explicitly; bring to user before changing thematic files

Step 4 — Update INDEX

Add the new source to _Knowledge Base/00 - README.md's "Source summaries" section, under the right domain.

Step 5 — (Optional) Author a derived skill

If the source contains a specific, repeatable framework that doesn't yet have a SKILL.md, propose authoring one:

  • Use _Knowledge Base/16 - Skill Authoring Conventions.md as the authoring guide
  • Place in _Skills Library/KB-Skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md
  • Update INDEX

Don't auto-author — propose first, get user approval (this is a creative decision).

Step 6 — Move the source file

If the source isn't already in the right category folder:

  • Books → AI Agents/, AI Business/, Sales & Marketing/, etc. (per existing taxonomy)
  • Skill packs → _Skills Library/
  • Repos → AI Development/

Step 7 — Decide retention

After successful extraction:

  • Books / PDFs: confirm with user before deleting (irreversible)
  • Skill packs / repos: keep (they're working tools, not raw sources)
  • Videos: keep if storage allows; otherwise note the location externally

Output structure

# KB Add Source: <Source Title>

## Inventory
- **Type**: <book / course / video / repo / pack>
- **Path**: <file path>
- **Extraction depth**: <full / sampled / catalog>
- **Pages / lessons / files read**: <list>

## Output

### Source summary file
- **Path**: `_Knowledge Base/Source Summaries/<slug>.md`
- **Word count**: ~<X>
- **Sections covered**: <list>

### Cross-references added
- File NN — <thematic file>: <how this source updates it>
- (or: "no thematic file updates needed")

### INDEX update
- Added to `_Knowledge Base/00 - README.md` under <domain> section

### Derived skill (optional)
- **Proposed**: <skill-name> at `_Skills Library/KB-Skills/<name>/SKILL.md`
- **Rationale**: <why this should be a skill>
- **Status**: <proposed — awaiting user approval / authored>

### File location
- **Source file moved to**: <path>
- **Retention recommendation**: <keep / delete after user confirmation>

## Open questions
- <any extraction issues or ambiguities>

Anti-patterns

  • Skipping the template — inconsistent summaries break searchability
  • Padding with generic AI knowledge — only extract what's in the source
  • Auto-authoring derived skills without asking — that's a creative decision
  • Deleting source files without explicit user confirmation — irreversible
  • Cross-referencing every thematic file — only mention if it actually changes something
  • Reading the whole source for large works (>200 pages) — sample TOC + key chapters

Quality bar (before marking done)

  • Source summary written with all template sections
  • Frontmatter has source, domain, extracted_for
  • Quotes verbatim from source (not paraphrased)
  • Cross-references identified or explicitly none
  • INDEX updated
  • Source file in correct folder
  • Retention decision logged (kept vs proposed delete)

Pairing

→ After extraction, may pair with agent-build-plan (if the source enables a new agent class) or skill-authoring per file 16. → During weekly-review, oversight surfaces sources awaiting extraction.

One-liner reminder

One source = one summary file in template format + INDEX update + optional derived skill proposal. Confirm before deletion.

skills.name: Knowledge Base For Agents
skills.description: General RAG layer for non-voice agents. Indexes Matrix's offers, products, brand-guide, compliance rules, FAQs, SOPs, dosing guide, KB summaries. Text agents (email, chat, content, research) query mid-task. Companion to voice-knowledge-base (which is voice-formatted).
skills.intent: Stop every agent re-loading the same shared context. Provide a queryable RAG layer the non-voice agents call via lookup_kb(question). Returns 1-3 relevant chunks + citations. Grows from gaps surfaced by ghl-continuous-improvement + content-director + customer interactions.
best_for: Standing up RAG for the first time
Onboarding a new agent that needs lookup capability
skills.estimated_time: 60-120 min initial build; 5 min per new entry
skills.body_html:
Knowledge Base for Agents (general)

The RAG layer for text agents. Sister to voice-knowledge-base (which is voice-formatted).

When to use

  • Standing up an agent's lookup layer
  • Adding new entries from production gaps
  • Quarterly audit of accuracy / freshness
  • Adapting a voice-kb entry for text context (different format)

When NOT to use

  • Voice agents (use voice-knowledge-base — different output format)
  • Real-time external data (use a function call to live system)
  • Decision-making (KB serves info; agent decides)

What's different from voice-kb

Voice KB General KB
≤ 30 words per answer Up to 200 words + structured (lists, tables)
Spoken-friendly text only Markdown formatting OK
Numbers spelled when < 20 Numbers as digits
URLs simplified URLs verbatim with [text](url)
No markdown Markdown lists, tables, code blocks
Single answer 1-3 chunks + citations

Index structure

Stored in Supabase or vector DB. Each entry:

id: kb-001
title: "Catalog request process"
question_aliases:
  - "How does someone request the Matrix catalog"
  - "What's the catalog request flow"
  - "Catalog access"
content: |
  ## Catalog Request Process

  Providers request the catalog via the form at `/forms/catalog-request` (3 fields: name, email, clinic name).

  On submit:
  1. Email automation triggers: catalog PDF delivered via [MAS · catalog_delivery] email template
  2. Contact tagged `interest:catalog` + `source:<form-source>`
  3. Contact added to nurture sequence (4-touch over 14 days)
  4. Pipeline: New Lead → "Catalog Requested" stage
  5. NPI verification offered in nurture (not required upfront)

  Bridge to next step: 7-day soft ask for strategy call booking.
source: "ghl-config.md + ghl/workflows/blueprint.md"
last_verified: "2026-05-10"
tags: ["catalog", "onboarding", "lead-flow"]
agent_safe: true
compliance_flag: "no-clinical"
related_kb_ids: ["kb-007 credentialing", "kb-023 nurture sequences"]

Categories to cover (Matrix initial KB ~100-200 entries)

Company + offers

  • What Matrix sells (catalog overview)
  • Provider-only access model
  • Sub-brands (MuscleLock, Peptide Map, ClinIQ)
  • Compounding partner relationship
  • Lab partners (nugen / ram / florida / kaduceus)

Catalog / products

  • 503-A patient-specific compounding
  • 503-B catalog products
  • Hormone therapy (pellets, creams, troches — pellets backordered)
  • Peptides (categories + common products)
  • Protocol bundles (6 named)
  • IV drips coming soon
  • SARMs preorder-intent only (not for sale)

Pricing + payment

  • Payment terms (Immediate / EOD / EOW / Net 30)
  • Tier upgrade rules
  • Affiliate commission (10/12/15%)
  • Min payout ($50, rolls over)

Credentialing + access

  • NPPES NPI verification process
  • Documents required
  • Typical timing (24-48h)
  • BAA when needed

Ordering + fulfillment

  • How to place a first order
  • Shipping carriers + ETA
  • Temperature-controlled shipping
  • Returns / replacement policy

Compliance + claims

  • Never claim FDA-approval beyond what's actually approved
  • Never give medical advice (provider audience knows literature)
  • Provider-only language always
  • HIPAA: never patient data in marketing channels
  • A2P 10DLC current status (PENDING — affects SMS)

Brand + voice

  • Voice rules (from brand-guide.md)
  • Banned phrases
  • Palette (cinematic vs brand modes)
  • Sub-brand voice nuances

Operations

  • Hours (M-F 9-5 CT)
  • Contact emails segmented (hello / credentialing / support / leo)
  • Key contacts (Chris as founder + decision-maker; Leo as sales lead)
  • Calendar IDs for booking links

Sub-brand specifics

  • MuscleLock — performance / recovery, strength caps, SNP-X2 delivery
  • Peptide Map — flagship education, provider trust-builder
  • ClinIQ — sibling brand, HIPAA-aware healthcare builds

Common provider questions

  • "Lead time on Semaglutide?" → 2-3 business days post-confirmation
  • "What's your refund policy?" → returns/replacement on damaged/wrong shipments
  • "Can I use this for [specific patient case]?" → defer to clinical judgment
  • (50+ FAQ entries)

Workflow

Step 1 — Curate from sources

Pull from existing _Shared Context/ files + Matrix project docs:

  • company-profile.md, brand-guide.md, offers.md, products.md
  • compliance-rules.md, tool-access.md, approval-rules.md
  • ghl-config.md, sub-brands.md, integration-map.md
  • matrix-store/docs/ (GHL config + integration docs)
  • dosing guide/matrix-dosing-guide/ (clinical reference — provider-only)
  • Cross-project CLAUDE.md files

Step 2 — Convert to KB entry format

Each entry:

  • Title + question aliases (3-5 ways someone might ask)
  • Content (markdown OK, ≤ 200 words)
  • Source citation (where it came from)
  • last_verified date
  • Tags
  • Compliance flag (clinical / financial / general / sensitive)
  • Related KB IDs

Step 3 — Index for retrieval

  • Embed each entry (vector embeddings)
  • Store in Supabase pgvector or dedicated vector DB
  • Index question_aliases separately for keyword match boost
  • Cache common queries

Step 4 — Wire as a function

Text agents call lookup_kb(question, max_chunks=3):

  1. Embed the question
  2. Vector-search top 5 candidates
  3. Re-rank by recency + agent_safe + similarity
  4. Return top 1-3 chunks with citations
  5. If no match above threshold → return "I don't have that — escalate or note as gap"

Step 5 — Log gaps for continuous improvement

Every "I don't have that" + the actual question gets logged. ghl-continuous-improvement + content-director surface these weekly → new entries proposed.

Step 6 — Quarterly audit

Every quarter:

  • Verify last_verified dates (entries > 6 months unverified flagged)
  • Re-check compliance flags
  • Compare entry accuracy vs current source files
  • Add new entries from gap log

Output structure (when querying)

# KB Result: <question>

## Top match
**Entry**: <id> — <title>
**Confidence**: <score>
**Content**:
<content>

**Source**: <citation>
**Last verified**: <date>

## Related
- <related entry 1>
- <related entry 2>

Output structure (when adding entries)

# KB Entries Added: <N>

## New entries
| ID | Title | Tags | Compliance | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## Coverage update
- Total entries: <N> (was <M>)
- Categories newly covered: <list>

## Gaps still open
- <gap question> (surfaced <N> times in production)

## ✅ I handled
- Curated + formatted
- Indexed in vector DB
- Wired callable function

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve entries with clinical / regulatory / financial language
- Approve new categories (require strategic decision)

## 🚫 You only
- Approve entries that make medical claims
- Approve entries referencing specific customer cases
- Approve any entry with patient identifiers

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-kb-update.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Duplicating shared-context content — entries should ADD context or SYNTHESIZE, not just copy
  • Long entries — > 200 words means it should be a doc, not a KB chunk
  • No agent_safe review — entries with potential PHI / clinical advice flagged
  • No source citation — entries can't be audited
  • No recency tracking — stale entries deceive agents
  • Auto-adding without compliance check — every entry runs through compliance-qa pass

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Curate from existing source files
  • ✅ Format + index
  • ✅ Wire as lookup_kb function
  • ✅ Log production gaps
  • ⚠️ Add new entries with clinical / regulatory language (Chris approves)
  • ⚠️ Recalibrate ranking weights (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Add entries with patient identifiers
  • 🚫 Add entries that imply medical advice

Pairing

Inputs from: All _Shared Context/*.md files, Matrix project docs, voice-knowledge-base (voice-formatted variants) Outputs to: All non-voice agents (email, chat, content, research) via lookup_kb Chains with: ghl-continuous-improvement (gap surfacing), kb-add-source (when new docs need KB entries), compliance-qa (entry review)

One-liner reminder

Short entries with citations. Markdown OK. agent_safe checked. Vector + keyword indexed. Log gaps from production. Quarterly accuracy audit.

skills.name: Lab Partner Qualifier
skills.description: Specialist micro-agent. cGMP peptide lab specific verification. FDA registered drug establishment lookup, cGMP audit verification (third-party / NSF / ISO), API vs finished-dosage capability mapping against Matrix product gaps, international (China / India / EU) FDA Form 483 history + import-alert search. Receives supplier-research-agent dossier, applies lab-specific deep checks, hands off to compliance-officer. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Matrix's peptide catalog depends on cGMP-grade synthesis. "We make peptides" on a website is not qualification. This specialist verifies FDA registration, audit history, capability per Matrix's peptide gap list, and surfaces international red flags (Form 483, import alerts, recent enforcement) before any partnership conversation.
best_for: After supplier-research-agent identifies cGMP peptide lab candidate
International peptide lab verification (China / India / EU)
Capability matching against Matrix peptide product gap
skills.estimated_time: Per lab: 10-20 min (longer for international). Batch of 10: ~3 hours.
skills.body_html:
Lab Partner Qualifier

A specialist digital employee for cGMP peptide lab verification. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md. Loads _Shared Context/supplier-discovery-policy.md first.

ROLE

Runs cGMP-peptide-lab-specific deep verification on candidates handed off by supplier-research-agent. Verifies FDA registered drug establishment status (US labs), cGMP certification + third-party audit history (NSF / ISO / equivalent), API vs finished-dosage capability per Matrix's peptide gap list (per products.md), and for international labs (China / India / EU) runs FDA Form 483 inspection history + import-alert search + recent enforcement-action check. Produces a lab-verification dossier supplement handed to compliance-officer for final gate before any partnership-outreach.

Explicitly OUT of scope: initial supplier discovery + research dossier (supplier-research-agent), compounding pharmacy verification (pharmacy-partner-qualifier), banned-claim review (compliance-officer + compliance-phrase-detector), outreach drafting (outreach-writer), licensing / contract terms (ip-counsel-coordination), formulation specification (formulation-designer).

INPUT

input:
  supplier_slug: { type: string, required: true, source: supplier-research-agent }
  research_id: { type: string, required: true, source: supplier-research-agent }
  csv_row: { type: object, required: true, source: supplier-research-agent dossier }
  category: { type: enum, required: true, values: [cgmp_peptide_lab] }
  initial_fda_registration_number: { type: string, required: false }
  country: { type: string, required: true }  # drives US-only vs international path
  initial_peptide_capabilities_claimed: { type: array, required: false }  # what supplier says they make
validation_before_proceeding:
  - category == cgmp_peptide_lab
  - country present (determines verification path)
  - csv_row from upstream

RULES

  • US lab path:
    • must verify FDA registered drug establishment (accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cder/daf/) — registration number active
    • cGMP cert verified via third-party (NSF / ISO 9001 / ISO 13485 if applicable / FDA inspection record)
    • Last cGMP audit date within 24 months preferred
  • International lab path (China / India / EU / other):
    • must run FDA Form 483 inspection history search for the entity
    • must check FDA import-alert list (entity on alert → P0 flag)
    • Check recent FDA enforcement actions in last 36 months
    • Higher confidence bar for cGMP claim (require document / third-party audit URL)
  • Capability mapping: confirm peptide list against Matrix gaps per products.md (semaglutide / tirzepatide / GHK-Cu / BPC-157 / sermorelin / CJC-1295 / ipamorelin / etc.)
  • API vs finished-dosage: confirm which (lab might supply API only; Matrix may need finished-dosage capability)
  • "Research-only" labs: if lab markets to research-only buyers exclusively → flag (research_only_lab); Matrix needs cGMP-grade for clinical compounding
  • Never assume from website language — verify against FDA / public-record databases
  • Never fabricate FDA registration numbers, audit dates, or Form 483 records
  • If FDA registration absent + intl lab + import-alert hit → P0 flag → escalate
  • PII discipline (lab QA contact name + title OK; emails/phones marked [verify before contact] if non-public)
  • Trade-secret discipline (do not disclose Matrix ALM DT detail in dossier)

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - FDA registered drug establishment lookup (accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cder/daf/)
    - FDA Form 483 / Establishment Inspection Reports
    - FDA Import Alert search
    - FDA Warning Letter search
    - FDA Enforcement Actions database
    - NSF International registry
    - ISO certification lookups (where public)
    - WebFetch + WebSearch (supplier sites, lab certs, trade press)
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/supplier-discovery-policy.md
    - _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md (FDA + import + cGMP)
    - _Shared Context/products.md (Matrix peptide gap list)
    - _Shared Context/alm-dt-platform.md (READ-ONLY — context for capability mapping; never publish)
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/supplier-discovery/qualification/<supplier_slug>-lab.json
    - _Logs/supplier-discovery/extracted/cgmp-peptide-labs-{us|international}.csv (update verify_queue + scores)
  composed_skills:
    - compliance-phrase-detector (pre-handoff)
    - pii-redactor (before log write)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch + WebSearch
  llm:
    model: claude-sonnet-4 (regulatory + international verification requires careful reading)
    typical_cost_per_call: $0.15-0.40

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    supplier_slug: string
    qualification_id: string
    completed_at: ISO timestamp
    country: string
    verification_path: enum (us | international)
    fda_drug_establishment:
      registered: boolean | unverified | not-applicable
      registration_number: string
      status: enum (active | inactive | unverified)
      verified_via_url: string
    cgmp_certification:
      cgmp_claimed: boolean
      third_party_audit_source: enum (NSF | ISO_9001 | ISO_13485 | FDA_inspection | self_declared | unverified)
      last_audit_date: ISO date | unknown
      audit_doc_url: string | unverified
    international_checks:  # populated if verification_path == international
      fda_form_483_history:
        any_hits: boolean
        details: array of { date, finding_summary, url }
      fda_import_alert:
        on_alert_list: boolean
        alert_number: string
        verified_via_url: string
      recent_enforcement_actions_36mo:
        any_hits: boolean
        details: array of { date, type, url }
    peptide_capability:
      api_capability: boolean | unverified
      finished_dosage_capability: boolean | unverified
      sterile_fill_finish: boolean | unverified
      cold_chain_capable: boolean | unverified
      catalog_or_custom_synth: enum (catalog | custom | both | unknown)
      matrix_gap_match:
        - peptide_name: string  # e.g., semaglutide, tirzepatide, BPC-157
          confirmed_capability: boolean | unverified
          matrix_relevance_score: number  # 0-100
    capacity_signal:
      lot_size_kg_or_g: string | unknown
      moq: string | unknown
      lead_time_weeks: number | unknown
    lab_qualification_score: number  # 0-100, supplements partnership_readiness_score
    flags:
      - flag: enum (fda_unregistered | import_alert_hit | form_483_recent | enforcement_action_recent | research_only_lab | capability_overclaim | audit_doc_unverified | intl_no_third_party_audit)
        evidence: string
        severity: P0 | P1 | P2
    handoff_recommendation: enum (compliance-officer | pause-escalate | route-back-to-research)
    quality:
      data_points_verified: number
      data_points_unverified: number
      sources_cited: array of URLs
  format: JSON + Markdown summary appended to dossier
  quality_bar:
    - US labs: FDA registered drug establishment verification mandatory
    - International labs: Form 483 + import-alert checks mandatory
    - Capability claims matched against Matrix product gap list with confidence
    - All factual claims cite a public-record URL
    - compliance-phrase-detector run on markdown before handoff

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream: compliance-officer
  payload:
    - supplier-research-agent dossier
    - lab-partner-qualifier verification supplement
    - combined partnership_readiness_score + lab_qualification_score
  fallback_routing:
    - import_alert_hit (P0): pause + escalate to Chris (do NOT proceed)
    - fda_unregistered + international: pause + escalate
    - form_483_recent (within 24 mo): downscore + escalate to compliance-officer for risk assessment
    - enforcement_action_recent: pause + escalate
    - research_only_lab: route back to supplier-sourcing-lead (capability mismatch)
    - capability_overclaim: downscore + flag in dossier
    - data_points_verified < 6 (or < 8 for intl): route back to research
  state_persistence:
    location:
      - _Logs/supplier-discovery/qualification/<supplier_slug>-lab.json
      - CSV row updated (extracted/cgmp-peptide-labs-{us|international}.csv)
    retention: 24 months
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/chains/supplier-discovery/<supplier_slug>/lab-qualification.json
    metrics_emitted: [duration_seconds, fda_registered, on_import_alert, lab_qualification_score, handoff_recommendation]

Eval cases

  1. US lab, FDA registered + NSF cGMP audit current → high score, route to compliance-officer
  2. US lab claims cGMP but no third-party audit findable → audit_doc_unverified flag, downscore
  3. Chinese lab, no Form 483 hits, no import-alert, third-party ISO 9001 audit → proceed cautiously, route to compliance-officer with intl flag
  4. Indian lab on FDA import-alert list → import_alert_hit P0 + pause + escalate
  5. EU lab with FDA Form 483 finding 8 months ago → form_483_recent flag + compliance-officer risk review
  6. US lab markets exclusively "research only — not for human use" → research_only_lab flag, capability mismatch, route back
  7. Lab claims semaglutide + tirzepatide capability but website only shows BPC-157 → capability_overclaim flag
  8. US lab with FDA enforcement action 12 mo ago → enforcement_action_recent flag + escalate
  9. Intl lab, sparse public data → partial dossier + route back to research for additional pass
  10. Lab confirmed API-only but Matrix gap requires finished-dosage sterile fill → downscore, note in handoff

Anti-patterns

  • Trusting supplier-website cGMP claim without third-party audit verification
  • Skipping Form 483 + import-alert checks for international labs
  • Fabricating FDA registration / audit dates to fill CSV
  • Treating "research-only" labs as Matrix-fit (they're not for clinical compounding)
  • Capability overclaim accepted at face value
  • Trade-secret leak (ALM DT detail in capability-fit reasoning)
  • PII leak of QA contact private info

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Query FDA + NSF + ISO + public regulatory databases
  • ✅ Web scrape supplier websites + trade press (robots.txt-respecting)
  • ✅ Persist qualification dossier + CSV update
  • ✅ Hand off to compliance-officer
  • ⚠️ Escalate import_alert_hit / fda_unregistered (intl) / enforcement_action_recent to Chris
  • 🚫 Send any communication to lab
  • 🚫 Fabricate registration / audit / Form 483 / import-alert data
  • 🚫 Disclose ALM DT trade-secret detail
  • 🚫 Persist PII without pii-redactor

Pairing

Inputs from: supplier-research-agent Outputs to: compliance-officer (compliance gate before any outreach) Composed by: supplier-sourcing-lead Composes: compliance-phrase-detector, pii-redactor Reads: supplier-discovery-policy.md, regulatory-frameworks.md, products.md, compliance-rules.md, alm-dt-platform.md

One-liner reminder

FDA drug-establishment lookup is canonical. International = Form 483 + import-alert mandatory. Capability matched against Matrix gap with confidence. "Research-only" = capability mismatch. [verify before contact] beats fabricated.

skills.name: Label Compliance Reviewer
skills.description: Regulatory + brand + IP gate on every label BEFORE print partner handoff. Verifies required fields per regulatory class (503-A USP <797> / 503-B FDA / DSHEA supplement / MoCRA cosmetic), trademark symbols correct (™ / ®), trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT parameters), brand voice. Composes `compliance-phrase-detector` + `product-naming-gate` + `compliance-officer`. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: A non-compliant label costs $5K-50K in pulled print runs + delayed launches + worst case FDA enforcement. This gate catches issues BEFORE production. Compliance-officer + product-naming-gate already check pieces; this specialist applies the label-specific checklist.
skills.estimated_time: Per label: 5-15 min. Per batch of 50: 1-3 hours.
skills.body_html:
Label Compliance Reviewer

The pre-print gate. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Applies regulatory + brand + IP gate to every label before print-vendor handoff. Per regulatory class, validates required-field presence + correct text + correct positioning. Runs trademark + trade-secret + claims-discipline checks. Composes existing compliance + IP skills. Issues 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / ⚠️ decision per label.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Label design (label-template-designer), variable-data generation (label-variable-data-engine), print-vendor specs (print-vendor-coordination), strategic compliance (compliance-officer — but composes).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (batch-review / single-sku / template-pre-launch / quarterly-audit)
    required: true
  labels:
    - sku: string
      packaging_form: string
      regulatory_class: enum (503-A / 503-B / supplement / cosmetic)
      sub_brand: string
      label_files:
        ai: string
        pdf_print: string
        png: string
      template_path: string
      csv_row: object  # variable-data values that filled the label
validation:
  - All labels have at least PDF for inspection
  - Regulatory class declared

RULES

rules:
  required_fields_by_class:
    503-A:
      - patient_name (variable; verify token present)
      - rx_number (variable)
      - prescriber_name (variable)
      - drug_name_with_strength
      - form
      - quantity
      - compound_date (variable; verify token + format)
      - bud (variable; verify token + format)
      - storage_instructions
      - pharmacy_name_and_address
      - "For [patient] only — not for resale" statement
    503-B:
      - established_name_with_strength
      - lot_number (variable)
      - outsourcing_facility_name_and_address
      - "This is a compounded drug" statement
      - cautionary_text_per_fda_guidance
      - bud
    supplement:
      - statement_of_identity
      - net_quantity
      - supplement_facts_panel
      - manufacturer_or_distributor_address
      - allergen_statement (when applicable)
      - dshea_disclaimer (verbatim: "These statements have not been evaluated by the FDA. This product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease.")
      - lot_number
      - expiration
    cosmetic:
      - identity
      - net_quantity
      - manufacturer_or_distributor
      - ingredient_declaration (INCI compliant per FDA cosmetic guidance)
      - mocra_facility_registration (when applicable)
  trademark_checks:
    - ® or ™ on every Matrix mark per `ip-portfolio.md`
    - Correct symbol (® only on USPTO-registered; ™ on pending or unregistered)
    - Sub-brand marks symbol-aware
  trade_secret_discipline:
    - NO ALM DT proprietary parameters (per `alm-dt-platform.md`)
    - NO SK713 SLP specific composition
    - NO process-specific manufacturing parameters
  claims_discipline:
    - Run `compliance-phrase-detector` on every text element
    - Banned-phrase scan per `compliance-rules.md`
    - No implied medical claims on supplement / cosmetic
    - No "FDA-approved" claim unless literally true
  brand_voice:
    - `brand-voice-enforcement` runs on non-variable text
    - Sub-brand visual treatment confirmed per `sub-brands.md`
  spec_compliance:
    - CMYK color mode
    - 300 DPI minimum
    - Bleed + crop marks correct
    - PDF/X-1a validated for print version
    - Total ink coverage < 300%
  failure_modes:
    - Missing required field → 🔴 block
    - Banned phrase detected → 🔴 block
    - Trade-secret leak → 🔴 block + escalate to Chris
    - Wrong trademark symbol → 🟡 fix-required
    - Brand voice violation → 🟡 fix-required
    - Spec deviation (CMYK / DPI) → 🟡 fix-required
    - Novel claim attempted → ⚠️ counsel + compliance-officer

TOOLS

  • compliance-phrase-detector (atomic — banned phrases)
  • product-naming-gate (name cleared)
  • compliance-officer (escalation)
  • brand-voice-enforcement (text-content gate)
  • PDF/X-1a validator
  • Read: label-specs.md, regulatory-frameworks.md, compliance-rules.md, ip-portfolio.md, alm-dt-platform.md
  • Write: review log per label

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_id: string
    labels_reviewed: number
    per_label:
      - sku: string
        decision: 🟢 approved | 🟡 fix-required | 🔴 blocked | ⚠️ counsel-required
        regulatory_class: string
        required_fields_check:
          - field: string
            present: boolean
            correct: boolean
            issue: string
        trademark_check: pass | fail
        trade_secret_check: pass | fail
        claims_check: pass | fail
        brand_voice_check: pass | fail
        spec_check: pass | fail
        required_edits:
          - location: string
            issue: string
            fix: string
        approval_signature: string + timestamp
    summary:
      approved_count: number
      fix_required_count: number
      blocked_count: number
      counsel_required_count: number
    pattern_alerts:
      - pattern: string  # e.g., "5 of 8 labels missing DSHEA disclaimer"
        root_cause_hypothesis: string
        suggested_fix_at_template: string
  format: JSON + Markdown report

HANDOFF

  • 🟢 → print-vendor-coordination
  • 🟡 → loop back to label-variable-data-engine (if CSV issue) OR label-template-designer (if template issue)
  • 🔴 → block; root cause; fix at source
  • ⚠️ → compliance-officer + counsel

Eval cases

  1. Batch of 20 peptide vials, all compliant → 🟢 approve
  2. Missing DSHEA disclaimer on supplement → 🔴 block + template fix
  3. Banned phrase ("cures") on label → 🔴 block
  4. Trade-secret leak (ALM DT param visible) → 🔴 block + Chris
  5. ® on unregistered mark → 🟡 fix (change to ™)
  6. Wrong sub-brand color → 🟡 fix
  7. PDF/X-1a validation fail → 🟡 fix
  8. Novel clinical claim attempted → ⚠️ counsel
  9. Pattern: 5 labels same issue → template fix recommendation

Anti-patterns

  • Approving with fields missing
  • Per-label fixes without template-fix when pattern
  • Skipping trademark-symbol check
  • Letting trade-secret leak slide "just one label"

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Apply all gates
  • ✅ Sign off on 🟢
  • ⚠️ 🟡 fix-required (re-review after fix)
  • 🚫 Override 🔴 block without root-cause fix
  • 🚫 Approve novel claim without counsel

Pairing
