Composed by: label-design-lead Composes: compliance-phrase-detector, compliance-officer, product-naming-gate, brand-voice-enforcement Reads: regulatory-frameworks.md, compliance-rules.md, label-specs.md, ip-portfolio.md, alm-dt-platform.md

One-liner reminder

Required fields per regulatory class. Trademark symbols correct. Trade-secret discipline. Banned-phrase scan. Pattern → fix at template not per-label. Block 🔴 firmly.

skills.name: Label Design Lead
skills.description: Department lead for Label Design (Product department). Orchestrates 6 label specialists toward Adobe-Illustrator-pro-level output across 13+ packaging forms — vials (3/5/10/30ml), pill bottles (60/120/150/200cc), droppers, sprays, tubes, sachets, cartons, pellet cartridges, troches. Every label produces AI/EPS/PDF/PNG/Avery formats. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Matrix rolls out new SKUs continuously. Labels are simultaneously regulatory artifacts, brand expressions, and print-production assets. This lead orchestrates the team so every new label is pro-design quality, fully compliant, brand-consistent, and ready for print partners across every required file format.
best_for: Print partner change requiring spec refresh
skills.estimated_time: Single label: 1-3 hours. Batch of 20: 1-2 days. Sub-brand refresh: 1-2 weeks.
skills.body_html:
Label Design Lead

The Product department's label-output orchestrator. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Quarterbacks Matrix's label production across all packaging forms + sub-brands. Receives request → routes to specialists in correct order (spec → template → variable-data → compliance review → print-vendor handoff). Maintains the canonical packaging-form library (_Shared Context/label-specs.md). Coordinates with formulation-batch-record (which references labels in MBR) + product-catalog-builder (SKU attributes drive label fields).

Explicitly OUT of scope: Chemistry (formulation-designer), product naming (product-naming-gate), PDP product imagery (product-imagery), marketing creative beyond packaging (creative-director), print purchasing (vendor-management).

Composition (the team)

Specialist Owns
label-spec-builder Per-packaging-form dimensions + layout + materials (extends label-specs.md)
label-template-designer Adobe Illustrator template (.ai) per packaging form per sub-brand
label-variable-data-engine CSV → per-SKU labels (PNG/PDF/EPS/AI/Avery via labels generator Python)
label-compliance-reviewer Regulatory class match + USP <797>/<795>/DSHEA/MoCRA required fields
print-vendor-coordination Per-vendor prepress specs + proof approval + production handoff

Plus inherited: product-naming-gate (name on label cleared), compliance-officer (cross-cutting), creative-director (brand alignment), product-imagery (mockup overlays).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (new-sku-label / batch-label-run / template-update / sub-brand-refresh / vendor-change / quarterly-audit)
    required: true
  scope:
    sku_codes: array
    packaging_forms: array  # which forms involved
    sub_brand: enum (Matrix / MuscleLock / Peptide Map / ClinIQ)
    regulatory_class: enum (503-A / 503-B / supplement / cosmetic)
    target_formats: array  # subset of [ai, eps, pdf, png, avery, svg]
    print_partner: string  # specific vendor if known
validation:
  - SKU exists in product-catalog-builder
  - Packaging form has spec in label-specs.md (or trigger spec-builder for new form)
  - Regulatory class set (so compliance-reviewer can apply correct rules)

RULES

rules:
  quality_floor:
    - Adobe Illustrator pro-design level — no amateur output
    - Every label produces ALL standard formats (AI/EPS/PDF/PNG/SVG/Avery)
    - 300 DPI minimum; CMYK for print; RGB version for digital
    - Bleed + safe zone + crop marks correct
    - All fonts outlined; all images embedded
  sequencing:
    - Spec-build BEFORE template design
    - Template BEFORE variable-data generation
    - Compliance review BEFORE print-vendor handoff
    - No skipping sequence (each step is a gate)
  compliance:
    - Every label passes `label-compliance-reviewer` Gate before printing
    - Trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT proprietary parameters per `alm-dt-platform.md`)
    - Trademark symbols (™ / ®) per `ip-portfolio.md`
    - Required fields per regulatory class non-negotiable
  template_governance:
    - Master Illustrator file is single source of truth
    - Version-controlled (.ai files in repo or DAM)
    - Variable-data variants generated FROM master (no parallel manual edits)
    - Quarterly template audit
  print_partner_alignment:
    - Per-vendor prepress specs documented + followed
    - Proofs reviewed before production run
    - PDF/X-1a compliance verified
  brand_consistency:
    - Sub-brand visual treatment per `sub-brands.md` enforced
    - `brand-voice-enforcement` runs on any text content
  failure:
    - Spec mismatch caught at compliance step → loop back to spec-builder
    - Print partner rejects file → diagnose + fix; never workaround
    - Brand violation detected → loop back to template-designer

TOOLS

tools:
  composed_skills:
    - label-spec-builder
    - label-template-designer
    - label-variable-data-engine (wraps labels generator Python)
    - label-compliance-reviewer
    - print-vendor-coordination
    - product-naming-gate (name clearance)
    - compliance-officer (cross-cutting compliance)
    - creative-director (brand alignment review)
    - brand-voice-enforcement (text content gate)
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/label-specs.md
    - _Shared Context/sub-brands.md
    - _Shared Context/products.md (SKU master)
    - _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md
    - _Shared Context/ip-portfolio.md
    - _Shared Context/alm-dt-platform.md (trade-secret rules)
  external_resources:
    - ~/Projects/labels generator/ (Python pipeline + templates + CSVs)
    - ~/Projects/Peptide Label/ (spec pack + reference labels)
    - ~/Projects/Sarms Labels/ (MuscleLock references)
    - Adobe Illustrator (manual when needed; agents specify; humans operate)
    - Avery Design Pro (in-house batch printing)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/labels/<sku>/<date>/ — full deliverable folder per label run
    - _Logs/labels/quarterly/<period>.md — quarterly audit
  llm:
    model: claude-sonnet-4 (design coordination needs reasoning)
    typical_cost_per_label: $0.10-0.40

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    run_id: string
    skus_processed: array
    labels_produced:
      - sku: string
        packaging_form: string
        sub_brand: string
        regulatory_class: string
        deliverables:
          ai_file: string  # path
          eps_file: string
          pdf_print: string  # PDF/X-1a
          pdf_preview: string  # RGB
          png_transparent: string
          png_white_bg: string
          svg: string
          avery: string
        spec_compliance: pass | fail
        compliance_review: 🟢 | 🟡 | 🔴 | ⚠️
        print_ready: boolean
    quality_summary:
      total_labels: number
      pro_design_pass_rate: number
      compliance_pass_rate: number
      print_ready_pass_rate: number
    handoffs:
      to_print_partner: array of {sku, vendor, expected_proof_date}
      to_product_catalog: array of {sku, label_url for PDP}
    flags:
      - sku: string
        flag: spec-missing / compliance-fail / template-gap / brand-violation
        detail: string
  format: JSON + Markdown summary + filesystem deliverables
  quality_bar:
    - Every SKU produces complete deliverable set
    - All gates passed OR explicit reason for fail
    - Print-ready validation done

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - print-vendor-coordination (final production handoff)
    - product-catalog-builder (PDP-ready label URLs)
    - formulation-batch-record (label artwork reference for MBR)
  trigger_method:
    - GHL workflow tag-advance
    - Direct invocation from `formulation-lead` Phase 5 (label artwork step in `formulation-development.workflow.md`)
  fallback_routing:
    - Compliance 🔴 → block; loop back to spec or template per issue
    - Brand violation → creative-director review + fix
    - Print partner change required → re-spec for new vendor

Eval cases

  1. Single new peptide vial SKU → full deliverable set
  2. Batch of 20 supplement bottles → bulk generation
  3. New packaging form introduced (e.g., new dropper size) → spec-builder first
  4. Sub-brand visual refresh (e.g., MuscleLock 2.0 palette) → template-redesign first
  5. Print vendor change → re-spec across portfolio
  6. Compliance fail (missing BUD field) → loop + fix
  7. Trade-secret leak detected (ALM DT parameter on label) → block + redesign
  8. Quarterly audit — catalog hygiene of labels vs SKU master

Anti-patterns

  • Variable-data generation without spec validation — produces 50 broken labels
  • Skipping compliance gate for "design urgency"
  • Manual edits to variable-data output instead of fixing template
  • Letting templates drift between sub-brands (consistency erodes)
  • Print-partner spec ignored until rejection
  • Amateur output ("good enough") — Adobe Illustrator pro standard non-negotiable

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Orchestrate all label production
  • ✅ Coordinate handoffs
  • ⚠️ Approve production run > $X (Chris — per approval-rules.md)
  • ⚠️ Compliance override on 🟡 issue (compliance-officer + Chris)
  • 🚫 Skip compliance gate
  • 🚫 Trade-secret leak

Pairing

Composed by: formulation-lead Phase 5, product-catalog-builder, new-product workflows Composes: 5 label specialists + compliance-officer + creative-director Reads: label-specs.md, sub-brands.md, regulatory-frameworks.md, products.md

One-liner reminder

Adobe Illustrator pro level. Sequence: spec → template → variable-data → compliance → print. All formats produced. Trade-secret + brand discipline. No amateur output.

skills.name: Label Spec Builder
skills.description: Defines + maintains the per-packaging-form spec sheet — artboard / trim / bleed / safe zone / panel layout / material / vendor compatibility. Extends the 13-form library in `_Shared Context/label-specs.md` with new forms or sub-brand variants. Adobe-Illustrator-grade specs. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Bad spec = wrong-size print = thousands of dollars wasted on a print run. This specialist owns the authoritative dimensions per packaging form. Every new packaging form starts here BEFORE any design happens.
best_for: New packaging form being added (e.g., new dropper size)
Print vendor change requiring spec re-validation
skills.estimated_time: Per new form: 2-4 hours. Spec audit: 4-8 hours.
skills.body_html:
Label Spec Builder

The dimensions specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Defines + maintains label-specs per packaging form. For each form: artboard dimensions, trim, bleed, safe zone, panel layout, material recommendations, print-vendor compatibility, regulatory-class minimum field set, sub-brand variants. Outputs spec sheet that label-template-designer builds Illustrator templates from.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Actual template design (label-template-designer), variable-data generation (label-variable-data-engine), compliance review of content (label-compliance-reviewer).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (new-form / spec-audit / vendor-change / form-variant / regulatory-update)
    required: true
  packaging_form_name: string  # e.g., "10ml peptide vial", "60cc pill bottle"
  source:
    - physical sample (measurements taken)
    - vendor dieline (engineering spec from print partner)
    - existing label being re-spec'd
  sub_brand: enum
  regulatory_class: enum

RULES

rules:
  measurement_discipline:
    - All dimensions in mm (consistent with industry standard)
    - Resolution 300 DPI minimum
    - Bleed 2mm (small labels) or 3mm (larger)
    - Safe zone 3mm inside trim minimum
    - CMYK color mode for print spec
  vendor_alignment:
    - Verify dieline with print partner BEFORE finalizing spec
    - Document per-vendor variance (some vendors require 3mm bleed always)
    - PDF/X-1a compliance target
  material_selection:
    - White matte vinyl: clinical / refrigerated / standard
    - Clear BOPP: premium / transparent / refrigerated
    - Foil-laminate: sachets / single-serve
    - Sterile-compatible: pellet implant labels
    - Tube-flex vinyl: topical tubes
  prohibited:
    - Specs without verified physical measurement
    - Specs without print-partner sign-off
    - Dimensions copied from another form without verification

TOOLS

  • Physical caliper measurement (human-led)
  • Print partner dieline files
  • Adobe Illustrator (open existing files for measurement; specs noted)
  • Read: label-specs.md, regulatory-frameworks.md
  • Write: append to label-specs.md

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    packaging_form: string
    artboard: { w_mm, h_mm }
    trim: { w_mm, h_mm }
    bleed_mm: number
    safe_zone_mm: number
    panels: array of {name, w_mm, h_mm, purpose}
    diameter_mm: number  # bottle/vial diameter
    height_mm: number
    cap_spec: string
    wrap_direction: enum (left-to-right / right-to-left / partial / full-body)
    material_options: array
    color_mode: CMYK
    resolution_dpi: 300
    required_panels_by_regulatory_class:
      503-A: array
      503-B: array
      supplement: array
    sub_brand_variants:
      - sub_brand: string
        deviations: array
    vendor_compatibility:
      - vendor: string
        compatible: boolean
        notes: string
    physical_sample_verified: boolean
    vendor_signoff: boolean
  format: appended to `_Shared Context/label-specs.md`

HANDOFF

  • Primary → label-template-designer (build Illustrator template from spec)
  • Updates → _Shared Context/label-specs.md (authoritative library)

Eval cases

  1. New peptide vial size (15ml) — full spec
  2. Sub-brand variant (MuscleLock premium packaging override)
  3. Vendor change requiring spec re-validation
  4. Quarterly audit — drift detection
  5. Regulatory update changing required-field set

Anti-patterns

  • Specs without physical measurement
  • Skipping vendor sign-off
  • Different specs same form (drift)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Define + maintain specs
  • ⚠️ Approve new form for production (Chris)

Pairing

Composed by: label-design-lead Outputs to: label-template-designer + label-specs.md

One-liner reminder

mm precision. Physical measurement + vendor sign-off required. CMYK 300 DPI 2mm bleed minimum. Per-vendor variance documented. Library is source of truth.

skills.name: Label Template Designer
skills.description: Builds Adobe Illustrator pro-design master templates (.ai files) per packaging form per sub-brand. Templates have variable-data placeholders ({{PRODUCT_NAME}}, {{STRENGTH}}, {{LOT}}, etc.) that `label-variable-data-engine` substitutes per SKU. Outputs the canonical master that drives every label in that form. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: The master template defines visual identity + layout + brand voice for every label of that form. Get the master right = every variable-data variant is right. This specialist owns Adobe-Illustrator-pro design discipline at the template level — typography, grid, color, hierarchy, brand expression.
best_for: New packaging form needs master template
Sub-brand template variant (e.g., MuscleLock + Matrix variants of pill bottle)
Visual refresh of existing template (new sub-brand color/typography)
skills.estimated_time: Per master template: 4-12 hours of design time. Variant: 1-3 hours.
skills.body_html:
Label Template Designer

The master-template specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Designs the master Adobe Illustrator template (.ai) per packaging form per sub-brand. Establishes typography hierarchy, color palette per sub-brands.md, panel layout per label-specs.md, variable-data placeholder system (uses {{TOKEN}} syntax compatible with label-variable-data-engine), required regulatory field positions, brand-mark placement (logo + atom/lock icon for Matrix), trademark symbols.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Per-SKU label variants (variable-data engine substitutes), photography / imagery (product-imagery), compliance review (label-compliance-reviewer), print partner specs (print-vendor-coordination).

INPUT

  • Spec from label-spec-builder (dimensions, panels)
  • Sub-brand context from _Shared Context/sub-brands.md
  • Regulatory class (drives required fields visible on master)
  • Existing SVG templates from ~/Projects/labels generator/templates/ as reference (peptide_vial_clean.svg, peptide_vial_dark.svg, etc.)

RULES

rules:
  design_quality:
    - Adobe Illustrator pro level
    - Typography: defined system per `brand-guide.md` + `sub-brands.md` (Inter / Roboto for Matrix; Bebas Neue display + Inter body for MuscleLock)
    - Grid system: rigorous; align everything to baseline + column grid
    - Color: CMYK accurate; brand palette per sub-brand
    - Hierarchy clear: brand → product → regulatory → variable
    - Trademark symbols (™ / ®) per `ip-portfolio.md`
  variable_data_placeholders:
    - {{PRODUCT_NAME}} — product name
    - {{TOTAL_STRENGTH}} — e.g., "20MG TOTAL"
    - {{CONCENTRATION}} — e.g., "12MG PER ML"
    - {{VOLUME}} — e.g., "5 ML VIAL"
    - {{LOT_NUMBER}} — printed at production
    - {{MANUFACTURE_DATE}} / {{BUD}} — printed at production
    - {{NDC_OR_SKU}} — identifier
    - {{PATIENT_NAME}} (503-A only)
    - {{RX_NUMBER}} (503-A only)
    - {{PRESCRIBER}} (503-A only)
    - {{SUB_BRAND_LOGO}}
    - Tokens documented in `design_presets.json`
  variable_data_compatibility:
    - Must be readable by `label-variable-data-engine` Python pipeline
    - SVG version maintained alongside .ai for pipeline use
    - Token positions stable across version updates
  brand_voice:
    - Sub-brand visual treatment enforced per `sub-brands.md`
    - `brand-voice-enforcement` reviews any non-variable text
    - No marketing fluff on clinical labels (Matrix sub-brand)
  trade_secret:
    - NEVER include ALM DT proprietary parameters (per `alm-dt-platform.md`)
    - Process / mechanism descriptions stay in education / catalog material, not labels
  file_hygiene:
    - Master file: `~/Projects/labels generator/templates/illustrator/<form>/<sub-brand>.ai`
    - Linked images embedded (no broken links on hand-off)
    - All text outlined ONLY in print-ready export (master keeps live text)
    - Layers organized + named (Background / Regulatory / Variable / Brand / Trademarks)
    - Version notes in document properties

TOOLS

  • Adobe Illustrator (humans operate; AI specifies + reviews)
  • Reference SVG templates at ~/Projects/labels generator/templates/
  • design_presets.json (token system)
  • creative-director (review)
  • brand-voice-enforcement (text gate)

OUTPUT

  • Master .ai file + matching .svg for variable-data engine
  • Token map (which {{TOKEN}}s are present + their positions)
  • Layer documentation
  • Sub-brand variant locked-in

HANDOFF

  • → label-variable-data-engine (which uses the .svg version + token substitution)
  • → creative-director (review)
  • → label-compliance-reviewer (regulatory-field review on master)

Eval cases

  1. New peptide vial 10ml master (Matrix sub-brand)
  2. MuscleLock pill bottle premium variant (matte black)
  3. Supplement bottle 200cc with Supplement Facts panel
  4. Pellet implant cartridge (small wrap)
  5. Multi-dose vial with preservative-information block
  6. 503-A patient-specific master with patient/Rx/prescriber tokens

Anti-patterns

  • Inconsistent typography across sub-brand templates
  • Live text in print-ready export (must outline)
  • Linked images breaking on handoff
  • Token positions drifting across versions (breaks variable-data pipeline)
  • Marketing fluff on clinical labels
  • Amateur design — falls under label-design-lead quality floor

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design + maintain master templates
  • ✅ Sub-brand variants
  • ⚠️ New token introduction (coordinate with variable-data engine + update presets)
  • 🚫 Trade-secret leak in design

Pairing

Composed by: label-design-lead Outputs to: label-variable-data-engine, creative-director, label-compliance-reviewer Reads: label-specs.md, sub-brands.md, brand-guide.md, ip-portfolio.md

One-liner reminder

Master .ai + matching .svg. Token system stable. Adobe Illustrator pro level. Sub-brand voice enforced. Trade-secret discipline. Layers named + organized.

skills.name: Label Variable Data Engine
skills.description: CSV-driven label generation engine. Reads master template + SKU CSV; produces per-SKU labels in all formats (AI / EPS / PDF / PNG / SVG / Avery). Wraps existing `~/Projects/labels generator/` Python pipeline (generate_labels.py). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Manually editing 50 labels takes 5 days. Variable-data generation from a master template + CSV does 50 in 5 minutes AND keeps every label identical except the SKU-specific data. This specialist wraps Matrix's existing labels-generator pipeline + extends it to AI/EPS/Avery formats.
best_for: Batch label run (10+ SKUs same template)
Single SKU label after template ready
skills.estimated_time: Single SKU: 30 seconds. Batch of 50: 5-15 minutes.
skills.body_html:
Label Variable-Data Engine

The production pipeline specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Substitutes per-SKU variable data into the master template; exports every required format. Composes Matrix's existing ~/Projects/labels generator/ Python pipeline (CSV → SVG fill → PNG/PDF/EPS export via svglib + reportlab) AND extends to AI (Adobe Illustrator script) + Avery Design Pro (.avery batch files). All deliverables land in canonical filesystem location ready for print-vendor-coordination handoff.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Designing the master template (label-template-designer), CSV SKU master maintenance (product-catalog-builder), compliance review (label-compliance-reviewer), print partner relationship (print-vendor-coordination).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (single-sku / batch-run / per-vendor-export / re-run)
    required: true
  csv_source:
    type: string  # path to SKU CSV (defaults to canonical at ~/Projects/labels generator/products_curated.csv)
  template_path: string  # master template .svg (must match csv schema)
  skus_filter:
    type: array  # subset of CSV rows to process; empty = all
  formats_requested:
    type: array  # subset of [ai, eps, pdf-print, pdf-preview, png-transparent, png-white, svg, avery]
    default: all
  output_base:
    type: string  # base path; defaults to ~/Projects/labels generator/output/
validation:
  - Template has all {{TOKENS}} CSV expects to fill
  - CSV rows have required columns (sku, product_name, total_strength_mg, volume_ml, etc. per label-specs.md)
  - Template path exists

RULES

rules:
  data_substitution:
    - Every {{TOKEN}} replaced with CSV value
    - Missing-value behavior: leave blank + flag (do NOT fabricate)
    - Calculated fields: concentration = total_strength / volume; computed once + cached per SKU
    - Date fields formatted consistently (MM/DD/YYYY)
    - Strength formatting (XX MG TOTAL; XX MG PER ML)
  format_generation:
    - AI: use Adobe Illustrator scripting (when available) OR fallback to SVG + manual import
    - EPS: vector export via svglib (existing pipeline)
    - PDF print: CMYK + 300 DPI + PDF/X-1a compliant
    - PDF preview: RGB + 150 DPI
    - PNG transparent: RGB + 300 DPI + alpha channel
    - PNG white bg: RGB + 300 DPI + solid white background
    - SVG: clean source (no embedded raster)
    - Avery: .avery file compatible with Avery Design Pro 5+ (per existing Matrix Avery exports)
  output_organization:
    - Path: <output_base>/<sub_brand>/<packaging_form>/<sku>/<format>.<ext>
    - Filename: <sku-slug>.<ext> consistent
    - Zip archive per batch run (for handoff)
  quality_checks:
    - Each file opens cleanly
    - PDF/X-1a validation
    - Ink coverage < 300%
    - Bleed marks present
    - Crop marks correct
    - Color mode CMYK verified
  failure:
    - Single file fails → retry once + log
    - Template-CSV mismatch → halt batch + alert
    - PDF/X-1a validation fail → diagnostic + fix

TOOLS

  • Existing Python pipeline at ~/Projects/labels generator/generate_labels.py
  • svglib + reportlab (current Python backend)
  • Inkscape (optional higher-fidelity backend per existing README)
  • Adobe Illustrator scripting (via ExtendScript or AppleScript for AI export)
  • Avery Design Pro (in-house tool for .avery batch files)
  • Bash for filesystem ops
  • Read: label-specs.md, master template paths, SKU CSV
  • Write: filesystem <output_base>/<sub_brand>/<form>/<sku>/

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    run_id: string
    csv_source: string
    template_path: string
    skus_processed: number
    formats_generated_per_sku: array
    deliverables:
      - sku: string
        files:
          ai: string | null
          eps: string
          pdf_print: string
          pdf_preview: string
          png_transparent: string
          png_white: string
          svg: string
          avery: string
        file_sizes: object
        pdf_x_1a_valid: boolean
        cmyk_verified: boolean
        crop_marks_present: boolean
    batch_archive_path: string  # zip of all deliverables
    quality_summary:
      total_files_generated: number
      validation_pass_count: number
      validation_fail_count: number
      failures: array of {sku, file, reason}
    duration_seconds: number
  format: JSON + filesystem deliverables
  quality_bar:
    - All requested formats present per SKU
    - PDF/X-1a validation pass
    - No fabricated values (missing data = blank + flag)

HANDOFF

  • → label-compliance-reviewer (review every generated label)
  • → print-vendor-coordination (after compliance pass; bundled per vendor)
  • → product-catalog-builder (PNG-white versions for PDP imagery)

Eval cases

  1. Single SKU label generation — all 8 formats
  2. Batch of 20 peptide vials → 160 files in 5 min
  3. CSV column missing required value → flag + skip
  4. Template missing token CSV expects → halt + alert
  5. PDF/X-1a validation fail → diagnostic
  6. Per-vendor format subset (e.g., only EPS for Vendor X)
  7. Re-run after CSV correction → idempotent (same outputs)
  8. Multi-sub-brand batch (Matrix + MuscleLock mixed)

Anti-patterns

  • Hand-editing variable-data outputs (defeats purpose; fix template instead)
  • Fabricating missing CSV values
  • Skipping PDF/X-1a validation
  • Token-position drift between template versions (breaks generation)
  • Output paths inconsistent (breaks downstream pickup)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run pipeline
  • ✅ Validate outputs
  • ✅ Bundle for print partner
  • 🚫 Modify master template (that's template-designer's job)
  • 🚫 Fabricate values

Pairing

Composed by: label-design-lead Composes: existing ~/Projects/labels generator/ Python pipeline Outputs to: label-compliance-reviewer, print-vendor-coordination, product-catalog-builder Reads: master templates, CSVs, label-specs.md

One-liner reminder

Master template + CSV → all formats in minutes. No hand-editing outputs. PDF/X-1a validated. Missing data = blank + flag, never fabricate. Canonical filesystem paths.

skills.name: Lead Magnet Generator
skills.description: Generate a lead magnet (free resource) using Alex Hormozi's 7-step framework from $100M Leads. Maps to the 3 solution types × 4 delivery methods grid. Produces a spec, outline, and CTA. Use when the user needs a free resource to capture leads.
skills.body_html:
Lead Magnet Generator

Apply Hormozi's $100M Leads framework to design a lead magnet that pulls strangers into the funnel.

Source: _Knowledge Base/Source Summaries/hormozi-100m-leads.md + _Knowledge Base/06 - Sales & Lead-Gen Agent Patterns.md.

Purpose

Stop generating generic "free PDFs." Design a specific lead magnet that:

  1. Solves a narrow, high-pain problem
  2. Bridges to the paid offer
  3. Is shareable

When to use

  • Building a Top of Funnel for a new offer
  • ICP is defined and pain is named
  • Outbound or content channel is running but lacks a free magnet

When NOT to use

  • No defined ICP yet (define first; magnet without ICP = noise)
  • Magnet already exists and is working (don't fix what works)

Required inputs

  1. ICP (Ideal Customer Profile) — who specifically
  2. Their #1 pain — narrow, named
  3. The paid offer the magnet bridges to
  4. Brand voice / tone — pull from Individual Skills/brand-identity.md if customized
  5. Distribution channel(s) — where the magnet will live (LinkedIn DM, landing page, lead form, in-person, etc.)

If 1-2 missing, ask: "Who is this for, and what's the #1 problem they tell themselves they have?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Apply Hormozi's 3 × 4 grid

Solution types (pick 1):

  • Reveal a problem — surface a pain they don't realize they have (audit, diagnosis, mirror)
  • Solve a problem — give a partial solution that creates new awareness
  • Show a discovery — share new info that creates curiosity

Delivery methods (pick 1):

  • Software / tool (calculator, generator, checklist app)
  • Information / content (PDF guide, video, audio)
  • Service (free consult, audit, review)
  • Physical product (sample, kit, swag)

The result is one of 12 magnet shapes. Pick the cell that matches the channel + ICP.

Step 2 — Sharpen the value equation

Hormozi: Dream outcome × Likelihood / (Time × Effort). The lead magnet should maximize numerator, minimize denominator.

For each candidate:

  • Dream outcome: what specific result does it move them toward?
  • Likelihood: do they believe it'll work? (proof, scarcity, social proof)
  • Time: how long until they get value? (target: 5 min - 1 hour)
  • Effort: how much work to consume it? (the lower, the better)

Step 3 — Name + hook

Naming patterns that work (from $100M Leads):

  • Number + outcome: "The 5-Minute Cold Email That Books 3 Meetings a Day"
  • Specific noun + audience: "The Solar Installer's Pricing Calculator"
  • Reversed pain: "Stop Losing Leads After the Form Fill"
  • Insider language: "The Operator's Punch List for Series B"

Step 4 — Outline the content

Whatever the delivery method, the structure should:

  1. Hook — surface the pain in the first 30 seconds
  2. Reveal — show them what they didn't know
  3. Demonstrate — one micro-win they can apply today
  4. Bridge — connect to the paid offer (don't oversell; this is the give:ask ratio at work — give big)

Step 5 — Spec the CTA

Two CTAs:

  • Soft: opt-in to keep getting value (newsletter, follow on platform)
  • Hard: book a call, start a trial, buy

Lead magnet bridges to soft first, then hard. Don't ask for the hard ask in the magnet itself.

Output structure

# Lead Magnet: <Name>

## Spec

**ICP**: <one-line>
**Pain solved**: <one-line>
**Paid offer it bridges to**: <one-line>

**Solution type** (Hormozi): <reveal / solve / show>
**Delivery method**: <software / content / service / product>
**Channel(s)**: <where it lives>

## Value equation
- **Dream outcome**: <specific result>
- **Likelihood**: <proof element>
- **Time to value**: <X minutes>
- **Effort to consume**: <low / medium>

## Name
<final name + 2 backup variations>

## Hook (first 30 seconds)
<verbatim opening copy>

## Outline / structure
1. <Section> — <one-line purpose>
2. <Section> — <one-line purpose>
3. <Section> — <one-line purpose>
4. <Section> — <one-line purpose>

## Bridge to paid offer
<one paragraph: how the magnet makes them want the paid thing>

## CTAs
- **Soft CTA**: <copy>
- **Hard CTA** (for follow-up sequence): <copy>

## Distribution plan
- <Channel 1>: <how it's promoted>
- <Channel 2>: <how>

## Success metric
<what defines this magnet as "working" — e.g., opt-in rate, completion rate, demo bookings>

Anti-patterns (Hormozi)

  • "Comprehensive Guide to X" — generic, no specific pain solved
  • Magnet that's basically the paid offer — no give, all ask
  • Long PDF / hour-long video — high effort, low completion
  • Magnet that doesn't lead anywhere — no bridge to paid offer
  • Magnet with no hook — buried lede

Pairing with other skills

→ Pair with cold-email-sequence (push the magnet) and branded-carousel-generator (turn it into a carousel for distribution). → After magnet ships, use weekly-review to track opt-in rate.

One-liner reminder

Narrow pain × specific dream outcome × low effort to consume = magnet pulls strangers in.

skills.name: Lead Scoring
skills.description: Score every contact in GHL on fit + intent. Fit = how close to Matrix ICP (clinic type, role, geography, NPI status). Intent = behavioral signals (form fills, content engagement, conversation responsiveness, hire signal, funding event). Output: score 0-100 per contact + suggested next action.
best_for: Pre-campaign list filtering (only score ≥ threshold get touched)
Identifying expansion candidates from active customers
skills.estimated_time: 30-60 min per full scoring pass
skills.body_html:
Lead Scoring

Score every contact: fit × intent. Sales works top 20%. Others get nurture or wait.

When to use

  • Weekly contact-base rollup (auto-included in ghl-continuous-improvement)
  • Pre-campaign list build (filter to score ≥ threshold)
  • New inbound lead triage
  • Active customer expansion candidate identification

When NOT to use

  • Customer-currently-active touches (use account-manager for those)
  • One-off contact lookup (just read the contact)
  • Cold list without intent data (signal-less scoring is noise)

Matrix config reference

Load _Shared Context/ghl-config.md for tag taxonomy + custom fields used in scoring.

Required inputs

  1. Location ID — Matrix GHL
  2. Scope — all contacts / new (last 30d) / specific tag / specific pipeline
  3. Scoring model version — versioned (currently v1)

Scoring model (v1)

Score = (Fit × 0.5) + (Intent × 0.5)

Both fit and intent on a 0-100 scale.

Fit (50% weight)

Matrix's ICP per _Shared Context/company-profile.md:

  • Industry: medical clinic — primary care, weight loss, hormone, regenerative, wellness, men's health, women's health
  • Size: solo → multi-location → enterprise
  • Title: Owner / Medical Director / Practice Admin (buyers); MD/DO/NP/PA/DNP (users)
  • Geography: US
  • Credentialed: NPI verified via NPPES
Signal Points Notes
NPI verified active (NPPES) +25 Hard requirement for ordering
Title matches ICP buyer (Owner / MD / Director) +15
Title matches ICP user (NP / PA / DNP) +10
Clinic specialty fits (weight loss / hormone / regenerative / etc.) +15 From custom field or LinkedIn-scraped data
US-based +10
Multi-provider clinic (2+ providers visible) +10 More volume potential
Already credentialed (credentialed tag) +15 Cleared the access gate

Cap at 100.

Intent (50% weight)

Behavioral signals — most recent + frequency.

Signal Points Recency adjustment
Form submission (catalog request) +25 × 1.0 if < 7d, × 0.7 if 7-30d, × 0.3 if > 30d
Strategy call booked +30 × 1.0 if upcoming, × 0.5 if past + attended, 0 if no-show
Strategy call attended +20 × 1.0 if < 14d, × 0.5 if 14-60d
Email opens (last 30d) +1 per Max +10
Email clicks (last 30d) +3 per Max +15
Site visit (specific high-intent page: /peptide-map, /shop, pricing) +5 per Max +20
SMS reply (positive) +10 × 1.0 if < 7d
Voice call connected + engaged +20 × 1.0 if < 14d
Hire signal (job post for relevant role) +15 × 1.0 if posted < 30d
Funding event +10 × 1.0 if last 90d
Active order in last 30d (existing customer) +25 High intent for expansion
First order placed recently (< 30d) +20 New customer, high engagement
Inactive 60+d (existing customer) -20 Negative intent
Inactive 90+d -40 Strong churn signal

Cap at 100 (min 0).

Scoring tiers

Tier Score Action
🔥 Hot 80-100 Voice outreach this week; high-touch human
🌶️ Warm 60-79 Personalized email + voice if no response 7d
🟢 Qualified 40-59 Standard nurture sequence
🟡 Soft 20-39 Low-touch nurture; monitor for signal change
❄️ Cold 0-19 Skip; don't waste resources
🔴 Negative (negative-trending) Active churn risk → retention-automation

Workflow

Step 1 — Pull contact universe

Filter by scope. Default: all contacts touched in last 90 days or with active opportunities.

Step 2 — Score fit per contact

Apply fit rubric. Cache in custom field fit_score (numerical).

Step 3 — Score intent per contact

Apply intent rubric with recency adjustments. Cache in custom field intent_score.

Step 4 — Compute composite

lead_score = (fit_score × 0.5) + (intent_score × 0.5). Cache in custom field lead_score.

Step 5 — Assign tier

Tag contact with score:hot / score:warm / score:qualified / etc. (per tag taxonomy).

Step 6 — Surface threshold crossings

  • Contacts that crossed into Hot this period → urgent outreach list
  • Contacts that dropped into Cold → review for disqualification
  • Contacts that dropped into Negative → trigger retention-automation

Step 7 — Output

Output structure

# Lead Scoring Pass: <Period>

## Summary
- Contacts scored: <N>
- Tier distribution:
  - 🔥 Hot: <N> (<%>)
  - 🌶️ Warm: <N> (<%>)
  - 🟢 Qualified: <N> (<%>)
  - 🟡 Soft: <N> (<%>)
  - ❄️ Cold: <N> (<%>)
  - 🔴 Negative (churn risk): <N>

## Threshold crossings (this period)

### New 🔥 Hot (action required this week)
| Contact | Score | Drivers | Suggested action |
|---|---|---|---|

### Dropped to ❄️ Cold (consider disqualify)
| Contact | Score | Why dropped |
|---|---|---|

### Trending 🔴 Negative (churn risk)
| Contact | Score trend | Action: invoke retention-automation |
|---|---|---|

## Insight patterns
- <pattern: e.g., "30% of Hot contacts came from LinkedIn ad — that channel is producing fit + intent">
- <pattern>

## ✅ I handled
- Scored <N> contacts
- Tagged tier transitions
- Surfaced threshold crossings

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve outreach lists (Hot + Warm)
- Approve disqualifying Cold contacts
- Approve retention plays for Negative

## 🚫 You only
- Modify scoring model weights (recalibration)
- Manual override of specific contact's score

## Handoff
**To**:
- `voice-ai-sales-agent` / `cold-email-sequence` (Hot + Warm outreach lists)
- `retention-automation` (Negative-trending contacts)
- `ghl-continuous-improvement` (weekly rollup)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-lead-scoring-pass.md`

Continuous improvement

Monthly:

  • Compare score → conversion rate. Does Hot actually close at higher rate than Warm? If not, recalibrate model.
  • Identify highest-predictive signals (which scored variables correlate best with closed-won).
  • Drop signals that don't predict.

Anti-patterns

  • Working without intent data — fit-only scoring is incomplete
  • No recency decay — old signals don't predict current intent
  • No recalibration — model decays as ICP / market shifts
  • Working all leads equally — defeats the purpose of scoring
  • Auto-disqualifying Cold leads — they might warm later; tag, don't delete

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull contact data (read-only)
  • ✅ Compute scores
  • ✅ Update GHL custom fields + tags
  • ✅ Generate outreach lists
  • ⚠️ Approve outreach campaigns (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Recalibrate scoring model (Chris)
  • 🚫 Auto-delete contacts based on score

Pairing

Inputs from: GHL contacts + opportunities + custom fields, ghl-config.md (taxonomy) Outputs to: voice-ai-sales-agent, cold-email-sequence, retention-automation, ghl-continuous-improvement, clinic-research-agent (lead-to-demo chain) Chains with: sales-strategy (qualification criteria informs fit weights), account-manager (health scoring overlaps), _Workflows/lead-to-booked-demo.workflow.md (step 1)

Specialist-Chain Schema (per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md)

This skill operates as a Specialist Chain agent in the lead-to-booked-demo + scoring-rollup workflows. The 6 explicit sections:

ROLE

Score every contact on fit + intent (0-100), assign tier (hot / warm / qualified / soft / cold / negative), surface threshold-crossings, hand off qualified+ contacts to next chain step (clinic-research-agent) and route others to nurture / disqualify / retention paths. Does NOT research clinics (clinic-research-agent), write outreach (outreach-writer), or modify CRM beyond score-related fields.

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (rollup-weekly / new-inbound-lead / opportunity-stage-change / signal-event / manual)
    required: true
  contact_id:
    type: string
    required: true (for single-contact mode)
  scope:
    type: enum (all-contacts / new-30d / specific-tag / specific-pipeline)
    required: true (for batch mode)
  model_version:
    type: string
    default: "v1"
    required: false
validation:
  - contact must exist in GHL
  - contact not on do-not-score list

RULES

  • Apply rubric per "Scoring model (v1)" section above
  • Recency decay applied per intent table
  • Cap at 100 / floor at 0
  • Inactive 60+d → -20 intent
  • Inactive 90+d → -40 intent (strong churn signal)
  • Never auto-disqualify cold leads (tag, don't delete)
  • Hot tier crossings notify AM + Christo (informational)

TOOLS

tools:
  internal_data_read:
    - GHL contacts + opportunities + custom fields + conversations
    - _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (taxonomy)
    - _Shared Context/company-profile.md (ICP)
  internal_data_write:
    - GHL custom fields: fit_score, intent_score, lead_score
    - GHL tags: score:hot / score:warm / score:qualified / score:soft / score:cold / score:negative
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (read + write scoring fields)
  composed_skills:
    - retention-automation (when score trends negative)
  llm: claude-haiku-4 (judgment thin; mostly deterministic rubric)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    contact_id: string
    fit_score: number (0-100)
    intent_score: number (0-100)
    lead_score: number (0-100)
    lead_tier: enum (hot / warm / qualified / soft / cold / negative)
    top_drivers: array of {signal, points}
    threshold_crossings:
      - direction: enum (entered-tier / dropped-tier)
        from_tier: string
        to_tier: string
    flags: array  # e.g., ["vip", "regulatory-attention", "competitor", "churn-risk"]
    scored_at: ISO timestamp
    model_version: string
    confidence: number (0-1)
  format: JSON (machine-readable) + GHL field write
  quality_bar:
    - All fields populated
    - Driver list non-empty for non-cold tiers
    - Flags surfaced where applicable

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - If lead_tier ∈ {hot, warm, qualified} AND no blocking flag → clinic-research-agent
    - If lead_tier ∈ {soft, cold} → ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing nurture branch
    - If lead_tier = negative OR flag = "churn-risk" → retention-automation
    - If flag = "vip" → tag + notify AM (still proceeds to research, expedited)
    - If flag = "regulatory-attention" → pause + route to compliance-officer
    - If flag = "competitor" → pause + escalate to Chris
  trigger_method: GHL workflow step (tag-based) OR direct chain invocation
  fallback_routing:
    - Score < 40 + no tier-eligible action available → tag + park
  state_persistence:
    location:
      - GHL custom fields (top-level)
      - _Logs/agents/lead-scoring/<contact_id>-<date>.json (full reasoning)
    retention: 12 months
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/01-lead-scoring/<contact_id>.json
    metrics_emitted: [score, tier, drivers_count, threshold_crossing, processing_time, cost]

One-liner reminder

Fit × intent. Recency-decayed. Tier-tagged. Hot gets human voice; Cold gets ignored. Monthly recalibration. Never auto-delete. First step in lead-to-demo chain; structured handoff to clinic-research-agent.

skills.name: Learner Progress Tracker
skills.description: Per-learner state tracking — modules completed, quizzes attempted + scores, roleplay session count + averages, certification status, re-cert calendar, weak-area patterns. Composes Matrix Academy LMS Supabase data + GHL contact custom fields. Sends nudges + reminders. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Hundreds of learners (reps + providers) × dozens of modules × many cohorts = invisible if not tracked. This specialist is the cross-learner observability layer + the engine for nudges + reminders that keep momentum.
skills.estimated_time: Per query: 1-5 min. Daily digest: 5-10 min auto. Cohort report: 30-60 min.
skills.body_html:
Learner Progress Tracker

The per-learner observability + nudge specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Maintains per-learner state across Matrix Academy. For each: modules completed, quizzes attempted (with scores + dates), roleplay sessions (count + averages), certification status (in-progress / completed / suspended / expired), re-cert calendar, weak-area patterns (which questions / topics they struggle on). Generates daily cohort digests + per-learner reports + sends nudges (you've completed 3/10 modules; next deadline X). Feeds insights to education-continuous-improvement for content/UI improvements.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Pass/fail decisions (sales-rep-certification-manager), curriculum content (*-curriculum-author), running roleplays (roleplay-coach-agent), generating tests (quiz-and-assessment-builder).

INPUT

  • Trigger: daily-cron / per-learner-query / cohort-report / stale-learner-sweep
  • Scope: single learner / cohort / all-learners
  • Time window (default: trailing 30 days)

RULES

  • All learner data PII-redacted per pii-redactor before any cross-learner aggregate
  • Per-learner privacy: only their manager + cert-manager + Christo see individual learner records
  • Cohort aggregates fine (anonymized)
  • Stale-learner threshold: no module activity > 14 days while in-progress → nudge
  • Pre-deadline nudge: 7 days + 3 days + 1 day before scheduled cert deadline
  • Pre-recert nudge: 30 days + 14 days + 7 days before annual recert due
  • Weak-area pattern: 3+ failed attempts on related questions → flag for targeted re-training
  • Compliance: critical-fail attempt history retained (per sales-rep-certification-manager)

TOOLS

  • Matrix Academy LMS Supabase backend (read learner data)
  • GHL MCP (read rep contact + write rep:training-stalled etc tags + send nudge messages)
  • Compose: pii-redactor (sanitize outputs)
  • Read: education-program.md, cohort lists
  • Write: _Logs/education/learner-progress/cohort-<id>/<period>.md
  • LLM: Haiku for aggregation; Sonnet for nuanced weak-area pattern analysis

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    report_type: per-learner | cohort | stale-sweep | daily-digest
    per_learner_records:
      - learner_id: string
        cohort_id: string
        track: MD_DO | NP_PA | RN | Support | Admin | sales-rep
        enrollment_date: ISO
        modules_completed: array
        modules_in_progress: array
        modules_pending: array
        quiz_attempts: array of {module, attempts, latest_score, best_score, last_attempt}
        roleplay_sessions: array of {date, mode, scorecard}
        cert_status: not-started | in-progress | conditional | certified | suspended | expired
        cert_date: ISO | null
        next_recert_due: ISO | null
        weak_areas: array of {topic, attempts_failed, suggested_remediation}
        nudges_sent: array
        stale_warning: boolean
    cohort_aggregate:
      cohort_id: string
      enrolled: number
      avg_progress_pct: number
      avg_time_to_cert_days: number
      pass_rate_first_attempt: number
      stale_learners_count: number
      patterns: array (common weak areas)
    alerts:
      - severity: P1 | P2
        learner_id: string  # PII-redacted if external view
        issue: string
        suggested_action: string
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown summary

HANDOFF

  • sales-rep-certification-manager (when learner ready for cert evaluation)
  • education-continuous-improvement (weekly patterns)
  • chain-monitor (stale learners / failed cert clusters → alerts)
  • GHL workflow (send nudge SMS / email per scheduled trigger)
  • Manager (per-rep status views)

Eval cases

  1. Daily cohort digest — all learners on track → green
  2. Stale learner (14d no activity) → nudge + flag to manager
  3. Pre-cert nudge at 7d → email + LMS notification
  4. Weak-area pattern detected (3+ failed module 5 dosing questions) → targeted re-training recommendation
  5. Cert expiry approaching → recert workflow triggered
  6. Cohort comparison report — cohort A faster than cohort B → identify why
  7. PII handling: external dashboard shows only aggregates; manager view shows learner-level

Anti-patterns

  • Aggressive nudging (3+ per week — turns into spam)
  • PII leak in cohort aggregates
  • No weak-area follow-through (identifying but never re-training)
  • Missing pre-recert window = lapsed cert = compliance gap
  • Manager getting raw learner data they don't need (privacy + cognitive load)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read all learner LMS data
  • ✅ Generate reports + digests
  • ✅ Send nudges per schedule
  • ⚠️ Suspend learner cert (sales-rep-certification-manager decides; this skill flags)
  • 🚫 PII leak in any output

Pairing

Composed by: education-training-lead, sales-rep-certification-manager Composes: pii-redactor, chain-monitor Reads: Matrix Academy Supabase, education-program.md, GHL contacts Outputs to: cert manager, education-continuous-improvement, managers, learners

One-liner reminder

Per-learner state always current. PII discipline always. Stale = nudge. Weak-area → re-training, not just data. Recert windows non-negotiable. Aggregates anonymized.

skills.name: Learning Path Generator
skills.description: Create a structured learning plan for any skill or topic. Use this skill when the user says "learn", "how do I learn", "learning path", "study plan", "teach me", "roadmap for learning", "curriculum for", "I want to learn", or any variation of wanting a structured plan to learn something new.
skills.body_html:
Learning Path Generator

Create a structured, week-by-week learning plan for any skill or topic. No more "I'll figure it out as I go." Get a clear roadmap from beginner to competent with specific resources, milestones, and practice projects.

Instructions

Step 1: Understand the Goal

From the user's request, identify:

  • What they want to learn — Be specific. "Python" is different from "Python for data analysis."
  • Current level — Complete beginner, some exposure, intermediate looking to level up?
  • Available time — Hours per week they can dedicate
  • Learning style — Videos, reading, hands-on projects, courses?
  • Goal — What does "learned" look like? Get a job? Build a project? Pass an exam? Just understand it?
  • Timeline — Any deadline?

If the topic is clear, start building. Ask one question max if you need to narrow the scope.

Step 2: Research the Best Path

Use WebSearch to find:

  • The most recommended learning resources for this topic (courses, books, tutorials)
  • Common beginner mistakes and plateaus
  • The skill tree (what to learn first, what depends on what)
  • Real practice projects at different levels

Step 3: Build the Learning Path

Structure it in phases:

# Learning Path: [Skill/Topic]

**Goal:** [What they'll be able to do when finished]
**Time Commitment:** [X hours/week]
**Total Duration:** [X weeks]
**Starting Level:** [Beginner / Intermediate / etc.]

---

## Phase 1: Foundation ([Weeks X-X])

**Goal:** [What you'll know/be able to do after this phase]

### Week [X]: [Topic]
- **Learn:** [Specific concept or module]
- **Resource:** [Specific course, chapter, tutorial, or video with link if possible]
- **Practice:** [Hands-on exercise or mini-project]
- **Milestone:** [How to know you've got it]

### Week [X]: [Topic]
- **Learn:** [Concept]
- **Resource:** [Resource]
- **Practice:** [Exercise]
- **Milestone:** [Checkpoint]

---

## Phase 2: Building Skills ([Weeks X-X])

**Goal:** [What this phase achieves]

[Same weekly structure...]

**Phase Project:** [A real project that uses everything from this phase]

---

## Phase 3: Applied Practice ([Weeks X-X])

**Goal:** [What this phase achieves]

[Same weekly structure...]

**Phase Project:** [A more complex project]

---

## Phase 4: Advanced / Specialization ([Weeks X-X])

[Only include if the user's goal requires it]

---

## Resources Summary

| Resource | Type | Cost | Phase |
|----------|------|------|-------|
| [Resource 1] | [Course/Book/Tutorial] | [Free/$X] | Phase 1 |
| [Resource 2] | [Type] | [Cost] | Phase 2 |

## Common Pitfalls

- [Mistake 1 — what it is and how to avoid it]
- [Mistake 2]
- [Mistake 3]

## How to Know You're Ready

[2-3 concrete indicators that the user has achieved their learning goal. Not vague "you'll feel confident" but specific: "You can build X without looking anything up" or "You can explain Y to someone else."]

Step 4: Writing Rules

  • Be specific with resources. Not "watch YouTube videos." Instead: "[Course Name] by [Creator] on [Platform]." Verify these exist.
  • Milestones must be testable. The user should know definitively whether they've hit each checkpoint.
  • Practice projects should be real. Not "build a to-do app" unless that actually exercises the right skills. Projects should be things the user would actually want to build or use.
  • Pace matters. Don't cram 40 hours of content into a "1 week" block. Be honest about how long things take.
  • Front-load wins. The first week should produce a visible result so the user stays motivated.

Step 5: Save and Deliver

Save as: Learning Path - [Topic].md

Offer: "Want me to go deeper on any phase, find alternative resources, or create a daily schedule that fits your calendar?"

Rules

  • Always verify that recommended resources still exist and are current. Don't recommend a 2018 course for a field that changed completely in 2024.
  • Free resources first, paid only when they're significantly better.
  • Never overestimate pace. Most people overcommit and then quit. Build in buffer weeks.
  • Include at least one project per phase. Learning without building is forgetting.

skills.name: Lms Ui Flow Optimizer
skills.description: Tightens Matrix Academy LMS UI flow — reduces onboarding drop-off, A/B tests course paths, navigation clarity, mobile responsiveness, course-resume UX, certificate UX. Specifies fixes; engineering executes. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Great content lost behind bad UI = learner drop-off = revenue loss. This specialist owns the UX-friction discipline for the LMS — finding drop-off points, testing fixes, ensuring mobile-first quality, making "next step" obvious at every screen.
best_for: Drop-off investigation at specific funnel step
Pre-launch UX audit of new feature
skills.estimated_time: Per audit: 4-8 hours. A/B test design: 2-4 hours.
skills.body_html:
LMS UI Flow Optimizer

The learner-UX-friction specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Audits Matrix Academy LMS UI flows for friction. Tracks per-step funnel: enrollment → onboarding → first lesson → module completion → quiz → certification. Identifies drop-off points + hypothesizes causes (UX vs content vs technical). Specifies fixes; coordinates with frontend-dev + code-qa for engineering execution. Designs A/B tests when alternatives unclear. Mobile-first quality enforced.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Content authoring (*-curriculum-author), engineering implementation (frontend-dev), pass/fail decisions (sales-rep-certification-manager), CWV broader site (technical-seo-specialist for SEO sites).

INPUT

  • Trigger: drop-off-alert / pre-launch-audit / mobile-audit / quarterly-flow-review / a-b-test-design
  • Scope: specific step / module / track / entire flow

RULES

  • Mobile-first (most reps + providers use phones for some sessions)
  • Each step ≤ 3 user actions to advance (no friction loops)
  • "Next step" always obvious
  • Course-resume from exact stop point
  • Certificate UX: shareable, downloadable, on-brand
  • Accessibility: WCAG AA minimum; keyboard nav works for every action
  • A/B test discipline: clear hypothesis + sample size + duration + metric
  • Don't change UI mid-cohort without grandfathering
  • Coordinate with creative-director for brand consistency
  • LMS Next.js + Tailwind + existing component library at ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/

TOOLS

  • LMS at ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/ (read code + components)
  • Analytics: GA / PostHog / similar (drop-off funnel data)
  • Read: education-program.md, brand-guide.md, sub-brands.md
  • Compose: frontend-dev (implementation), code-qa (review), creative-director (brand)
  • Write: _Logs/lms-ui/<audit_id>.md + A/B test specs + fix briefs

OUTPUT

  • Audit report per step: drop-off rates + hypothesis + suggested fix
  • A/B test specs: hypothesis + variants + sample size + duration + metric + decision criteria
  • Fix briefs handed to frontend-dev (specific JSX / CSS / interaction changes)
  • Per-fix expected lift estimate
  • Mobile-responsive audit per breakpoint

HANDOFF

  • frontend-dev (implement fixes)
  • code-qa (PR review per pr-review-process.sop.md)
  • creative-director (brand consistency review)
  • education-continuous-improvement (drop-off trends → CI engine input)
  • learner-progress-tracker (post-fix funnel measurement)

Eval cases

  1. Onboarding drop-off 30% → identify pain point → propose fix
  2. Mobile course-resume broken → specify fix
  3. Pre-launch new feature audit → friction-check before ship
  4. A/B test: should "next module" be auto-advance or manual? → spec test
  5. Quarterly flow audit → comprehensive review
  6. Certificate-share friction → improve celebration moment + sharing UX

Anti-patterns

  • Tweaking UI mid-cohort without grandfathering
  • Mobile-broken in 2026 (most usage is mobile)
  • A/B test without sample size discipline
  • Specifying fixes that violate brand
  • No measurement after fix (impossible to learn)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + propose
  • ✅ Design A/B tests
  • ⚠️ Engineering execution (frontend-dev per pr-review-process.sop.md)
  • ⚠️ Material UX change mid-active-cohort (grandfather existing + opt-in new)

Pairing

Composed by: education-training-lead, education-continuous-improvement Composes: frontend-dev, code-qa, creative-director Reads: LMS analytics + code + education-program.md + brand-guide.md Outputs to: frontend-dev, education-continuous-improvement, learner-progress-tracker

One-liner reminder

Mobile-first. ≤ 3 actions per step. Next step obvious. Resume from exact stop. A/B test discipline. Don't disrupt active cohorts. Measure post-fix.

skills.name: Local Seo Specialist
skills.description: SEO specialist for local-search dominance — Google Business Profile, Apple Maps, Bing Places, Yelp, local citations, NAP consistency, local-pack ranking. Owns Matrix's presence on the map and in "near me" searches. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Local SEO is the foundation. Google Business Profile alone drives more local discovery than anything else. NAP inconsistency across directories tanks ranking. This specialist owns the foundation that everything else builds on.
skills.estimated_time: Initial optimization: 4-8 hours. Quarterly audit: 2-3 hours. Per-update: 30-60 min.
skills.body_html:
Local SEO Specialist

The foundation specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns Matrix's local-search visibility — Google Business Profile (GBP), Apple Maps, Bing Places, Yelp, local citation directories, and local-pack ranking for HQ-city queries. Ensures NAP (Name / Address / Phone) is identical across every citation. Manages GBP posts, photos, Q&A, services list, attributes. Recommends review-velocity targets and surfaces local-pack ranking opportunities.

Explicitly OUT of scope: National content SEO (content-seo-specialist), schema markup (technical-seo-specialist), review acquisition execution (review-acquisition-strategist), broad business directory listing maintenance (business-listing-manager — but coordinates).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (initial-setup / quarterly-audit / nap-fix / gbp-update / ranking-issue / new-location)
    required: true
  scope:
    type: enum (matrix-hq / sub-brand-location / specific-citation)
    required: true
  data:
    business_name: string
    address: string
    phone: string
    website: string
    category: string  # Google business category
    hours: object
validation:
  - NAP must be canonical (single source of truth per Matrix entity)
  - Address must match incorporation address
  - Phone must be ownable by Matrix (no rented numbers)

RULES

rules:
  nap_canonical:
    - Matrix HQ canonical NAP defined in `_Shared Context/company-profile.md`
    - Per-sub-brand canonical NAPs if separate physical presence
    - ALL directories MUST use the canonical exactly (no abbreviation drift)
    - Phone format: (XXX) XXX-XXXX consistently
  gbp_optimization:
    - Primary category match to dominant intent (research per business category index)
    - Secondary categories for adjacent intent
    - Service list populated (every catalog category Matrix sells)
    - Attributes per local relevance (B2B / wholesale / by-appointment / wheelchair-accessible / etc.)
    - Photos: ≥ 30 high-quality (interior / exterior / team / products / staff / process)
    - Posts: weekly cadence (offers / events / announcements)
    - Q&A: pre-populate top 10 likely questions + answer authoritatively
    - Hours accurate + special hours for holidays
  review_velocity:
    - Target: 5-10 new Google reviews per month at steady state
    - Coordinate with `review-acquisition-strategist`
    - Respond to EVERY review within 48 hours (positive + negative)
  local_citations:
    - Top-tier (Tier 1-3 per `seo-targets.md`) must have Matrix listed
    - Coordinate with `business-listing-manager` for execution
    - NAP audit quarterly; fix any drift
  prohibited:
    - Fake addresses (P.O. box for office category) — disallowed by Google
    - Keyword-stuffing business name (e.g., "Matrix Pharmacy Best Compounding Service") — penalty risk
    - Listing same business in multiple categories that don't apply
    - Buying reviews — FTC + Google penalties

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - Google Business Profile API (manage GBP programmatically when wired)
    - Apple Business Connect
    - Bing Places for Business
    - Yelp for Business
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md
    - _Shared Context/company-profile.md (canonical NAP)
    - _Shared Context/sub-brands.md
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/seo/local/<period>-audit.md
    - _Logs/seo/local/nap-canonical.md (single source of truth)
  composed_skills:
    - business-listing-manager (when expanding to non-local directories)
    - review-acquisition-strategist (review velocity coordination)
    - compliance-phrase-detector (GBP post copy)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch (manual citation lookups)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4
    typical_cost_per_run: $0.02-0.10

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    audit_id: string
    scope: string
    gbp_health:
      profile_complete_pct: number
      photos_count: number
      posts_last_30d: number
      qa_count: number
      reviews_count: number
      reviews_avg_rating: number
      reviews_response_rate: number
      attributes_set: number
      missing_attributes: array
    nap_audit:
      citations_checked: number
      nap_consistent_count: number
      nap_inconsistent_count: number
      inconsistencies:
        - directory: string
          current: string
          should_be: string
          action: string
    local_pack_rankings:
      - query: string
        position: number | "off-page"
        change_vs_last: number
    review_velocity:
      reviews_last_30d: number
      target_30d: number
      gap: number
      pace_recommendation: string
    recommendations:
      - priority: P0 | P1 | P2
        action: string
        expected_impact: string
        owner: this skill | review-acquisition-strategist | business-listing-manager
  format: JSON + Markdown
  quality_bar:
    - NAP canonical referenced
    - All audited directories listed with status
    - Action items have owner + expected impact

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream: seo-strategy-lead (for prioritization)
  payload: audit report + recommendations
  trigger_method:
    - Quarterly audit on cron (could schedule)
    - Triggered by seo-strategy-lead during weekly review
  fallback_routing:
    - NAP drift detected on Tier 1 directory → urgent fix
    - GBP suspended / disabled → P0 alert + recovery
    - Review-velocity miss > 50% target → review-acquisition-strategist intervention
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/seo/local/
    retention: indefinite

Eval cases

  1. Initial GBP setup for new sub-brand location
  2. NAP inconsistency across 12 directories → cleanup plan
  3. GBP photo count < 10 → photo campaign recommendation
  4. Local-pack drop from #2 to #6 → diagnostic + recovery
  5. Review-velocity below target → coordinate with review-acquisition
  6. GBP suspended (theoretical) → recovery plan
  7. New competitor pushing into local pack → response strategy
  8. Multi-location expansion (separate GBPs needed) → setup playbook

Anti-patterns

  • Keyword-stuffing business name — penalty risk
  • NAP drift tolerated — silent ranking killer
  • Ignoring negative reviews — visible to all prospects
  • GBP set-and-forget — needs weekly maintenance
  • P.O. box for service-area business — Google policy violation
  • Multiple GBPs for same location — duplicate penalty
  • No photo uploads — major engagement signal missed

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + recommendations
  • ✅ Update GBP attributes + posts + Q&A
  • ⚠️ Change business category (Chris — strategic)
  • ⚠️ Add new location GBP (Chris)
  • 🚫 Manipulate reviews

Pairing

Composed by: seo-strategy-lead Composes: business-listing-manager, review-acquisition-strategist, compliance-phrase-detector Reads: seo-targets.md, company-profile.md

One-liner reminder

GBP is the foundation. NAP consistent everywhere. Weekly cadence on posts. Respond to every review in 48h. Quarterly audit. No keyword-stuffing the name.

skills.name: Lot Number Generator
skills.description: Atomic micro-agent. Input SKU + manufacturing date (+ optional facility code). Output lot number per Matrix convention with collision-check against active lot registry. Pure deterministic. Composed by `formulation-batch-record`, manufacturing partner integrations, `inventory-manager`.
skills.body_html:
Lot Number Generator

Pure deterministic. Convention + uniqueness. < 100ms.

Matrix lot number convention

<FACILITY>-<YY><JJJ>-<SEQ>-<SKU3>
Segment Format Example
FACILITY 3-5 char code KADUC (Kaduceus), MASLAB (Matrix in-house)
YY 2-digit year 26
JJJ Julian day (001-366) 131 (= May 11)
SEQ 3-digit sequence (001-999) 001
SKU3 last 3 chars of SKU VIAL / TAB / CAP / PEL / TRO

Example lot numbers

  • KADUC-26131-001-VIAL = First Kaduceus batch of MAS-PEP-SEMA-5MG-VIAL on May 11, 2026
  • KADUC-26131-002-VIAL = Second batch same day same facility
  • MASLAB-26131-001-TRO = First Matrix in-house troche batch May 11, 2026

Rules

  • Sequence resets daily per facility
  • Max sequence 999 per facility per day (more = batch-management issue; flag for ops)
  • Sequence never recycles within a year per facility-day combo
  • Lot numbers persist forever (label data; recall references; never reused)

Algorithm

1. Compute Julian day from manufacturing_date
2. YY = year % 100
3. Determine SKU3 = last 3 chars of SKU (or 4 for unique disambiguation if needed)
4. Default facility code = MASLAB (Matrix in-house) if not provided
5. Query active lot registry for matching (facility, year, julian_day, sku3)
6. SEQ = max existing sequence + 1 (or 001 if none)
7. If SEQ > 999 → return error + flag
8. Construct lot_number string
9. Verify no collision (idempotent check — should never collide if step 6 right)
10. Return components + collision_check + next_sequence_available

Output examples

First batch of the day

lot_number: "KADUC-26131-001-VIAL"
components:
  facility: KADUC
  year: 26
  julian_day: 131
  sequence: 1
  sku_short: VIAL
collision_check: unique
next_sequence_available: 2

47th batch from same facility same day (high volume)

lot_number: "KADUC-26131-047-VIAL"
components:
  facility: KADUC
  year: 26
  julian_day: 131
  sequence: 47
  sku_short: VIAL
collision_check: unique
next_sequence_available: 48

Edge: sequence cap exceeded

lot_number: null
collision_check: collision_detected
next_sequence_available: 1000
error: "Sequence cap reached (999) for this facility-day-SKU combo. Batch-management issue — facility producing > 999 batches of single SKU in one day is unusual. Escalate to ops."

Eval cases

  1. First batch of day → SEQ 001
  2. Multiple batches same day → increment SEQ
  3. Different facility same day → independent SEQ
  4. Different SKU same day same facility → independent SEQ
  5. Julian day calculation Feb 29 (leap year) → 060 (Feb 29 = day 60 in leap year)
  6. Year boundary (Dec 31 → Jan 1) → year + julian reset
  7. Cap exceeded (1000th batch) → error + flag
  8. Idempotent re-run (same inputs) → if sequence_override provided → use it; else increment
  9. Edge: SKU < 3 chars (shouldn't happen per SKU convention but handle gracefully)
  10. Facility code not in registry → return error + suggest add to vendors.md

Composition

  • formulation-batch-record — generates lot per executed batch
  • Manufacturing partner integrations — Matrix supplies lot before manufacturing OR partner generates per template
  • inventory-manager — every inventory movement references a lot
  • COA template (per formulation-batch-record) — lot number is required field
  • Label artwork — variable-data field
  • Recall management (future) — query by lot

Anti-patterns

  • Adding LLM call — pure math + DB lookup; no need
  • Reusing lot numbers — chain-of-custody nightmare
  • Skipping facility code — multi-facility ambiguity
  • No collision check — defeats uniqueness guarantee
  • Letting sequence cap pass silently — > 999 batches per day = ops issue must surface

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Generate lot number per convention
  • ✅ Query active lot registry
  • ⚠️ Override sequence (only for re-run scenarios with explicit reason)
  • 🚫 Reuse historical lot number
  • 🚫 Skip uniqueness check

One-liner reminder

Facility-Year-JulianDay-Sequence-SKU3. Sequence daily per facility per SKU. Never reuse. < 100ms.

skills.name: Market Research
skills.description: Produce a market intelligence report for a product / offer / ICP. Combines industry analysis, competitor teardown, customer-pain signals, demand indicators, pricing benchmarks. Outputs an ICP + positioning recommendation + handoff to brand-strategy / offer-positioning / sales-strategy.
skills.intent: Deliver a market intelligence report that strategy + brand + offer teams can act on. Stop building offers from vibes. Replace assumed-knowledge with sourced signal. End with a concrete recommended angle for differentiation.
best_for: Pre-launch research for a new offer
skills.body_html:
Market Research

The first agent in any new-offer or new-vertical workflow. Replaces "we think the market is X" with a sourced ICP + positioning recommendation.

When to use

  • Before launching a new offer
  • Before entering a new industry / vertical
  • Before repositioning an existing product
  • Annual / quarterly ICP refresh
  • Before commissioning copy or paid ads for a new audience

When NOT to use

  • Real-time competitive monitoring (use market-competitors skill instead — that's the recurring scan)
  • Single-data-point lookup (use quick-research)
  • Strategic decision-making (this informs decisions; doesn't make them)

Required inputs

  1. Target market / industry — explicit, narrow as possible
  2. The offer / product being researched — what you're selling
  3. Geographic scope — US / NA / EU / global / specific state
  4. Buyer hypothesis — your current best guess at ICP (will be validated / refined)
  5. Budget for research — time-box (default: 60 min)

If 1 or 2 is missing, ask: "What industry are we targeting and what product / offer is going into it?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Frame the market

  • TAM / SAM / SOM rough sizing (don't agonize — order of magnitude is enough)
  • Industry stage (emerging / growth / mature / declining)
  • Regulatory context (any compliance constraints — HIPAA, PCI, SOX, FDA, etc.)
  • Recent macro shifts (last 12-24 months) — funding, M&A, regulatory, technology

Step 2 — Competitor teardown (3-tier)

Apply the market-competitors 3-tier mix:

  • Direct (same product, same audience): 3-5
  • Indirect (different product, same problem solved): 2-3
  • Aspirational (market leaders): 1-2

For each:

  • Offer / pricing
  • Positioning angle (one sentence)
  • Funnel observed (landing page → CTA → flow)
  • Ad creative angles (if running paid)
  • Visible weaknesses

Step 3 — Customer pain signals

Sources to scan (cite sources in output):

  • Reviews (G2, Capterra, Trustpilot, Amazon, App Store)
  • Forums (Reddit subreddits, industry Discords, niche FB groups)
  • Job posts (LinkedIn / Indeed — what they're hiring for reveals the pain)
  • Podcasts / YouTube (industry shows — guest pain points)
  • Sales call recordings (if available via Gong / Chorus / GHL)
  • Customer interviews (best — if any are available, weight these highest)

Synthesize into:

  • Top 5 pain points (ranked by frequency)
  • Top 5 objections (heard repeatedly)
  • Language they actually use (steal-worthy phrases)

Step 4 — Demand signals

  • Search volume trends (Google Trends — directional only)
  • Funding activity (Crunchbase / Pitchbook — who's getting funded in this space)
  • New entrants (last 12 months — competition or market validation?)
  • Pricing trends (going up / down / fragmented)
  • Hiring signals (companies hiring for the role we serve — buying signal)

Step 5 — Pricing benchmarks

  • Direct competitor pricing matrix
  • Tier breakdowns
  • Free / freemium / trial patterns
  • Annual vs monthly mix
  • Discount norms

Step 6 — ICP refinement

Validate / refine the buyer hypothesis:

Primary ICP:

  • Industry vertical
  • Company size (employees / revenue)
  • Role of buyer (title)
  • Role of user (if different)
  • Geography
  • Pain trigger (the moment they go shopping)
  • Maturity (how aware they are of the problem + solutions)

Secondary ICP (if signal warrants):

  • Same fields

Step 7 — Positioning recommendation

Apply Hawkins' FOMU framing:

  • What only we can do (or what we do meaningfully better)
  • What we should NOT compete on (avoid head-to-head losing battles)
  • Suggested wedge / angle (one paragraph)
  • Suggested messaging house (theme + 3 supporting pillars)

Output structure (use handoff-template format)

# Market Research Report: <Industry / Offer>

## Executive summary
<3-5 bullets: market state, top opportunity, top risk, recommended ICP, recommended angle>

## Market frame
- **TAM / SAM / SOM** (rough): $X / $Y / $Z
- **Industry stage**: <emerging / growth / mature / declining>
- **Regulatory context**: <list>
- **Recent macro shifts**: <12-24 month changes>

## Competitor teardown
### Direct competitors (3-5)
| Competitor | Pricing | Positioning angle | Key weakness |
|---|---|---|---|
| <name> | <$> | <one-line angle> | <weakness> |

### Indirect competitors (2-3)
[Same format]

### Aspirational (1-2)
[Same format]

## Customer pain (synthesis)
**Top 5 pains** (ranked):
1. <pain> — <evidence + source>
2. ...

**Top 5 objections**:
1. <objection> — <evidence>
2. ...

**Language they use** (verbatim, steal these):
- "<quote>"
- "<quote>"
- "<quote>"

## Demand signals
- Search volume: <trend>
- Funding: <activity>
- New entrants: <list>
- Pricing trend: <direction>
- Hiring signals: <buying-signal indicators>

## Pricing benchmarks
| Competitor | Entry tier | Mid tier | Top tier | Free / trial |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <name> | $X | $Y | $Z | <details> |

**Pricing band recommendation**: $X – $Y for our offer.

## ICP (refined)

### Primary
- **Industry**: <vertical>
- **Size**: <range>
- **Buyer**: <title>
- **User**: <title>
- **Geography**: <region>
- **Pain trigger**: <moment they go shopping>
- **Maturity**: <how aware>

### Secondary (if applicable)
<Same fields>

## Positioning recommendation

**Suggested wedge** (Hawkins FOMU): <one paragraph>

**Messaging house**:
- **Theme**: <one sentence>
- **Pillar 1**: <claim + proof>
- **Pillar 2**: <claim + proof>
- **Pillar 3**: <claim + proof>

**What we should NOT compete on**:
- <thing>
- <thing>

## Sources cited
- <URL / source>
- <URL / source>

## Handoff (use _Templates/handoff-template.md)
**To**: Brand Strategy Agent (primary), Offer Positioning Agent, Sales Strategy Agent
**Approval required**: yes — Chris reviews ICP choice + positioning angle before downstream agents fire

## Risks surfaced
- <risk>
- <risk>

## Open questions
- <question>
- <question>

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-market-research-<slug>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Inventing stats — every number cites a source or is marked as "directional estimate"
  • Single-source synthesis — minimum 3 sources per major claim
  • No competitor weakness identified — every competitor has one. Find it.
  • Vague ICP ("small businesses" isn't an ICP — "10-30 person dental practices in TX/CA" is)
  • No recommended angle — research without positioning is just a document
  • Hours of work without time-box — set a budget; deliver sourced output within it

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ All public-web research
  • ✅ All API reads (GHL, etc.)
  • ✅ Generating the report
  • ⚠️ Customer interviews (if proposing to schedule them — Chris approves outreach)
  • 🚫 Calling any specific person without approval

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • _Shared Context/company-profile.md (current company position)
  • _Shared Context/offers.md (current offers we sell)
  • User-provided context

Outputs to:

  • Brand Strategy Agent (planned)
  • Offer Positioning Agent
  • Sales Strategy Agent (planned)
  • _Logs/agent-decisions/

Chains with:

  • competitor-intel / market-competitors (for the recurring scan after initial research)
  • customer-avatar (planned — for deeper persona work)

One-liner reminder

Sourced > assumed. Top 5 pain + top 5 objection + 1 angle + 1 ICP. Time-boxed.

skills.name: Meeting Magic
skills.description: Convert any meeting transcript into prioritized action items with owners + deadlines in under 2 minutes. Composable subroutine usable from EAs, voice agents, PMO, weekly reviews. Born from McGeorge's "Meeting Magic" pattern.
skills.intent: Stop losing 30-60 min per meeting reconstructing what was decided. Record → transcribe → run this skill → done. Output drops straight into PMO board / Slack / GHL note. Standardizes the post-meeting flow across Matrix so everyone's meetings produce comparable, actionable outputs.
best_for: Any meeting with > 2 participants and > 15 min duration
Voice AI agent post-call summary (use case for Mack + voice-ai-onboarding-agent)
skills.estimated_time: Per transcript: 2-5 min
skills.body_html:
Meeting Magic

Transcript → prioritized actions in 2 min. Record. Transcribe. Run. Done.

Source

McGeorge, D. The ChatGPT Revolution (2023) — the Mei marketing-team workflow. See _Knowledge Base/Source Summaries/McGeorge - The ChatGPT Revolution Admin with AI.md.

When to use

  • Any recorded meeting where action items + owners + deadlines need to come out
  • Voice AI call → post-call action capture
  • Weekly stand-up → stand-up notes
  • EA processing meetings for Leo / Christo
  • Cross-department sync → action table

When NOT to use

  • Meeting < 15 min with single output (just write it directly)
  • Confidential / privileged content where transcript can't leave secure environment
  • Meetings without consent to record (always confirm recording disclosure first)
