Required inputs

  1. Transcript — paste or path to file
  2. Priority lens — what frame to prioritize against ("customer experience improvements" / "revenue impact" / "compliance risk" / etc.)
  3. Owner roster (optional) — list of likely owners so EA can resolve "John" → "John Smith, AM"
  4. Existing project board context (optional) — so actions can be tagged to projects

Workflow

Step 1 — Confirm consent

  • Was meeting recorded with all participants' consent?
  • If unclear → flag + ask before processing
  • For voice AI calls: confirm recording disclosure was played

Step 2 — Clean the transcript (silent pre-process)

  • Strip filler ("um", "uh", "you know")
  • Identify speakers if available (or note "Speaker A / B / C")
  • Note any non-action items separately (jokes, side conversations)

Step 3 — Prompt A: Synthesis

Summarise the following meeting transcript into actionable bullet points.

Audience: [internal team / executive / customer]
Tone: [neutral / urgent / formal / casual — match the meeting]
Format: bulleted, scannable

[paste transcript]

Step 4 — Prompt B: Action extraction

Our priorities are [priority lens — e.g., "improving customer experience" / "reducing refund rate" / "shipping the Q3 launch"].

Pull out specific actions associated with any agreed dates or task owners mentioned in the original transcript.

For each action, output:
- Action (specific, verb-led)
- Owner (resolve name from roster if provided)
- Deadline (date OR "TBD")
- Priority (P0 / P1 / P2)
- Dependencies (if any other action blocks this)
- Project tag (if any matches active project board)

Step 5 — Format output as table

# Meeting: <Topic> — <Date>

## Participants
<name list>

## Summary (3-5 bullets)
- ...
- ...

## Decisions made
- ...

## Actions
| # | Action | Owner | Deadline | Priority | Project |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |
| 2 | ... | TBD | TBD | P1 | ... |

## Open questions (no decision made)
- ...

## Side notes
- <topics raised but not actioned>

## Next meeting
- Date: ...
- Pre-work needed: ...

Step 6 — Resolve unknowns

For any action with TBD owner or TBD deadline:

  • EA pings the relevant person to claim ownership
  • For voice AI calls: handoff to AM via call-handoff-orchestrator
  • Don't leave TBDs lingering > 48h

Step 7 — Distribute

  • Email to participants (EA-drafted, approval-to-send required)
  • Drop into project board (if pmo-operator integration active)
  • Tag relevant GHL contacts (for customer-facing meetings)
  • Save to _Logs/meetings/<date>-<topic>.md

Step 8 — Action follow-through

For each action with owner + deadline:

  • Calendar reminder T-3 days
  • Status check on deadline day
  • Escalation if missed > 2 days (to assigned escalator)

Meeting hygiene to teach participants

For Meeting Magic to work well, train participants to speak in "extractable" language:

  • Say "Action!" before assigning → "Action! John will get [X] done by [date]"
  • State decisions explicitly → "Decision: we're going with option B"
  • State commitments out loud → "I'll have it done by Friday" not "I'll try"
  • Avoid pronoun ambiguity → "Mary will" not "she'll"

McGeorge documents teams adopting this rhythm and getting near-100% extraction quality.

Integration with existing skills

  • Voice AI calls (Mack inbound + Mia outbound + Sam kickoff): every call's transcript runs through this skill for action capture
  • Voice-call-reviewer: Friday weekly review uses this as the per-call subroutine
  • PMO-operator: receives action tables, files into project board
  • GHL daily pulse: surfaces overdue actions from recent meetings
  • EAs (Leo + Christo): process all principal meetings through this

Anti-patterns

  • Skipping consent check — recording without disclosure can be illegal (state-dependent + customer-trust)
  • Running on noisy transcript — clean first; ambiguous transcripts produce ambiguous actions
  • Leaving TBDs unresolved — defeats the purpose; assign owner + deadline within 48h
  • No project / context tagging — actions disconnected from goals drift
  • One-time-only — no follow-through — extraction without enforcement = no improvement
  • Running on confidential content in a 3rd-party LLM without scrubbing first

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Process transcripts with consent verified
  • ✅ Generate action table + summary
  • ✅ Log to _Logs/meetings/
  • ✅ Calendar follow-up reminders
  • ⚠️ Distribute summary externally (sender approval)
  • ⚠️ Assign owner without that person's confirmation (capture as "proposed owner" first)
  • 🚫 Process recording without consent disclosure

Pairing

Composes: executive-admin-toolkit (Virtual Summariser pattern), prompt-engineer (lower-level) Inputs from: voice AI agents (transcripts), Zoom/Teams (transcripts), iOS Notes / Otter.ai Outputs to: PMO board, GHL notes, principal inbox, _Logs/meetings/ Chains with: pmo-operator, voice-call-reviewer, ea-leo-getz-ceo, ea-christo-mac-coo, call-handoff-orchestrator

One-liner reminder

Record → transcribe → 2 prompts → table → distribute → follow through. Train participants to say "Action! [name] will [task] by [date]". 95% extraction quality.

skills.name: Micro Agent Controller
skills.description: Meta-skill for designing ATOMIC single-purpose micro-agents — regex / math / lookup functions packaged as deterministic agents. One of two micro-agent flavors at Matrix. For SPECIALIST CHAIN agents (digital-employee-style with Role/Input/Rules/Tools/Output/Handoff), use `micro-agent-team-builder` instead.
skills.intent: Atomic agents are the lowest-level building block — pure deterministic where possible, cheap LLM where necessary, composable into specialist chains. Most agent failures come from skills doing too many things at once; atomic agents fix this by being radically narrow. Use this pattern when the function is well-defined and no real judgment is needed. Use `micro-agent-team-builder` when domain expertise + handoff matters.
best_for: Carving a too-broad skill into focused atoms
Designing a new specialist that should be composable, not orchestrating
Cost-optimizing repetitive LLM calls (small focused prompts = small models = cheap)
Building eval suites — atomic skills are easier to test
skills.estimated_time: Per micro-agent: 30-60 min design + 1-2 hours implementation + eval cases
skills.body_html:
Micro-Agent Controller — Design Pattern

The "build it small, do one thing" pattern for agents. Atomic. Deterministic where possible. Composable. Cheap.

Two micro-agent flavors at Matrix

Pattern This skill (micro-agent-controller) micro-agent-team-builder
Flavor Atomic / deterministic Specialist chain (digital employee)
Examples sku-name-validator, bud-calculator, price-margin-check, lot-number-generator, compliance-phrase-detector clinic-research-agent, outreach-writer, lead-scoring, compliance-officer
Scope ONE function (regex / math / lookup) ONE domain (research, copy, compliance review)
LLM usage None or cheap-Haiku for ambiguous edge cases Haiku (default) / Sonnet (judgment)
Has personality / identity No (utility function) Yes (digital employee with voice)
Output Single result + flags Rich structured dossier
Cost per call $0.00-0.003 $0.01-0.20
Best when Function well-defined, no judgment Domain expertise + chain handoff matters
Template (inline in this SKILL.md) _Templates/micro-agent.template.md

Both patterns coexist. Specialist chain agents typically compose atomic agents internally (e.g., outreach-writer composes compliance-phrase-detector for its self-check).

Background — why atomic micro-controllers

In embedded systems, a microcontroller is a small dedicated chip that does ONE function reliably (read a sensor, control a motor, time a process). You don't put a full operating system on a microcontroller — that would be overkill and unreliable. Instead, you compose dozens of microcontrollers into a larger system.

The same principle applies to AI agents. Common failure modes of monolithic skills:

  • Inconsistent output — too many decision points = output drifts
  • Hard to eval — what does "good" look like when the skill does 12 things?
  • High LLM cost — large context per call = expensive
  • Tight coupling — change one thing, break another
  • Hard to debug — failure modes blur together

Micro-controller agents fix these by:

  • Narrow scope — ONE input shape, ONE output shape, ONE responsibility
  • Deterministic where possible — use rules / regex / functions for anything that doesn't need an LLM
  • Composable — orchestrators chain them
  • Eval-friendly — input → expected output is testable
  • Cheap — small context, small model where appropriate (Haiku / GPT-4o-mini)

When to use (this meta-skill)

  • An existing skill keeps producing inconsistent output → time to carve it into atoms
  • New use case is well-defined + repetitive → build as micro-controller from the start
  • Cost analysis shows a skill is expensive due to large context → split into smaller pieces
  • Eval coverage is poor because skill has too many surfaces → break apart for testability

When NOT to use

  • Strategic / open-ended decision support (these need broad context + reasoning — keep them as full skills)
  • One-off tasks that won't repeat (don't over-engineer)
  • Work where context loss between micro-agents breaks the output (orchestration overhead > savings)

The micro-controller design checklist

Every micro-controller agent must answer YES to all of:

  1. Single responsibility — Can I state what it does in one sentence with no "and"s?
  2. Defined input shape — What's the exact shape of the input? (schema or YAML or specific format)
  3. Defined output shape — What's the exact shape of the output?
  4. Deterministic checks — What deterministic validation runs before/after the LLM call?
  5. Evaluable — Can I write 5 test cases with clear pass/fail criteria?
  6. Cheap LLM call — Can this run on a cheap model (Haiku / mini)? If not, why not?
  7. No side-effects in the agent itself — Side-effects happen in the orchestrator, not the atom
  8. Compose-target identified — Which orchestrator(s) will call this?

If any of those is NO, either redesign or build as a full skill instead.

The micro-controller template

Every micro-controller SKILL.md follows this minimal shape:

---
name: <verb-noun-style — e.g., extract-phone, classify-intent, score-sentiment>
description: <single sentence — input X, output Y>
type: micro
theme: <single theme>
triggers:
  - "<exact trigger>"
input_schema:
  field_a: string
  field_b: string (optional)
output_schema:
  result: string
  confidence: number (0-1)
  reason: string
model: claude-haiku-4 (default) | claude-sonnet-4 (if reasoning needed)
estimated_cost_per_call: $<X>
estimated_time: <seconds>
---

# <Name>

ONE-LINE PURPOSE.

## Input
<schema definition>

## Output
<schema definition>

## Determinism layer
<rule checks BEFORE the LLM call — early-return where possible>
- IF [condition] → return [deterministic result]; skip LLM
- IF [edge case] → return [error result]; flag

## LLM prompt template

<exact prompt, no fluff>

Input: {{input}} Output (JSON only):


## Eval cases
5+ input → expected output pairs, stored in `_Logs/eval-suites/micro-agents/<name>/`

## Anti-patterns
- Multi-purpose creep
- Output-shape drift
- LLM call when determinism would have worked

Examples of good micro-controller candidates

For Matrix's agent stack, these are natural micro-controllers:

Already-implicit micro-controllers (could be formalized)

  • ghl-tag-classifier — input: contact + recent activity → output: tier tag (hot/warm/cold)
  • email-intent-classifier — input: email body → output: intent label (question/complaint/sale/spam)
  • compliance-phrase-detector — input: copy → output: list of banned phrases found
  • price-margin-check — input: variant + proposed price → output: margin % + pass/fail vs floor
  • sku-name-validator — input: SKU string → output: pass/fail against Matrix convention regex
  • appointment-time-parser — input: natural language time → output: ISO timestamp
  • pdf-page-extractor — input: PDF path + page range → output: text content
  • state-tax-status-lookup — input: ship state + SKU category → output: taxability flag

Newly-warranted micro-controllers (to design)

  • inventor-attribution-extractor — input: invention disclosure draft → output: list of named inventors + their specific contribution
  • prior-art-citation-formatter — input: URL/title/authors → output: properly formatted citation
  • trademark-strength-scorer — input: candidate name → output: distinctiveness ladder rating (fanciful/arbitrary/suggestive/descriptive/generic)
  • lot-number-generator — input: SKU + date → output: lot number per Matrix convention
  • bud-calculator — input: form + storage temp + USP class → output: default BUD per USP
  • formulation-component-validator — input: ingredient + grade → output: pass/fail against per-class requirements
  • meeting-action-resolver — input: action with "TBD" owner → output: probable owner from roster

Workflow (designing a new micro-controller)

Step 1 — Identify the carve-out

What broad-skill behavior is showing inconsistency / cost / eval problems? Pinpoint the function.

Step 2 — Write the one-sentence purpose

If you can't state it in one sentence without "and"s, the scope is wrong.

Step 3 — Define input + output schemas

Strict shapes. Use TypeScript-style or YAML.

Step 4 — Find the determinism layer

What can be done WITHOUT the LLM? Regex, lookup tables, simple math, schema validation. Put these BEFORE the LLM call as early-returns.

Step 5 — Write the minimal LLM prompt

Single sentence + input + output format spec. No personality, no role-play, no instructions about how to think.

Step 6 — Pick the cheapest model that works

Default Haiku. Upgrade only if eval shows quality gap.

Step 7 — Write 5+ eval cases

Each case has input + expected output. Run before promoting to production.

Step 8 — Set cost + latency targets

What's the budget per call? P95 latency target?

Step 9 — Integrate into orchestrator

Identify which existing skill will compose this. Update that orchestrator to call this atom.

Step 10 — Monitor

Per-call: success/fail, latency, cost. Per-week: eval suite still green? Pattern of failures?

Anti-patterns

  • Building micro-controllers for one-off tasks — overhead doesn't pay back
  • Skipping determinism layer — LLM doing what regex could have done = wasted cost
  • Allowing output-shape drift — defeats composability; orchestrators break
  • Calling Sonnet/Opus when Haiku works — burn rate without quality benefit
  • No eval suite — micro-controller invisibly degrades
  • Adding "intelligence" — keep them stupid; intelligence lives in orchestrators
  • Side-effects in the atom — atoms should be pure; orchestrators do side-effects
  • Coupling atoms together — atoms should only know their own input/output, not each other

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design + implement new micro-controllers
  • ✅ Refactor a broad skill into micro-controllers + a thinner orchestrator (with code review)
  • ✅ Choose cheaper model for an atom
  • ✅ Add eval cases
  • ⚠️ Replace an existing production skill with a micro-controller chain (run both in parallel first; switchover on eval parity)
  • 🚫 Delete an existing skill being replaced before parity confirmed in production

Pairing

Composes / informs: every other skill in the library — this is meta-architecture Inputs from: pattern observation in the broader stack, cost reports, eval failure reports Outputs to: new micro-controller skills + refactored orchestrators Chains with: skill-builder (this defines a flavor of skill to build), engineering-lead (architecture review), eval harnesses (validation)

One-liner reminder

One sentence purpose. Defined input/output. Determinism first, LLM last. Cheap model. 5 eval cases. Compose, don't conflate. Atoms stay atomic.

skills.name: Micro Agent Team Builder
skills.description: Meta-skill for designing CHAINS of specialist micro-agents that work as a team. Each agent in the chain has Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff — output flows cleanly into next agent's input. Pair with `micro-agent-controller` (atomic regex agents) for the lower-level layer. This skill builds digital-employee teams.
skills.intent: One big AI = general assistant. Micro agents = specialized team. This meta-skill is how new department workflows get built — by chaining specialists, not by adding scope to one mega-skill. Every chain is inspectable, measurable, and replaceable agent-by-agent.
best_for: Designing a new department workflow (e.g., lead-to-booked-demo, refund-handling, onboarding-flow)
Refactoring a monolithic skill into a clean chain of specialists
Audit existing workflow for chain-design quality
Cost / latency optimization on a multi-step process
skills.estimated_time: Per chain design: 1-3 hours. Per specialist build: 2-4 hours.
skills.body_html:
Micro-Agent Team Builder

The meta-pattern for digital-employee teams. Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff. Specialists chain. Output flows.

Source

Matches Christo's definition (2026-05-11):

Micro AI agents are small, specialized AI workers. They do one narrow job well instead of trying to run the whole company. Think of them like digital employees with defined roles, rules, tools, and handoff points.

A strong system uses: Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff.

Two micro-agent flavors at Matrix:

Flavor Pattern Examples
Specialist Chain (this skill — most common) Digital-employee-style agents with domain expertise + structured handoff clinic-research-agent, outreach-writer, lead-scoring, compliance-officer, discovery-call-prep
Atomic (micro-agent-controller skill) Single deterministic functions (regex / math / lookup) sku-name-validator, bud-calculator, price-margin-check

When to use this skill

  • New department workflow needs building (lead-to-booked-demo, refund-handling, etc.)
  • Existing skill is too broad → carve into specialist chain
  • Audit an existing chain for inspectability / cost / handoff cleanliness
  • Cost / latency optimization on a multi-step process

When NOT to use

  • Need a single atomic function → use micro-agent-controller
  • The "chain" is actually just one specialist + tool calls → just build the one skill
  • The work doesn't repeat — one-off custom code is fine

The chain design checklist

A well-designed specialist chain answers YES to all of:

  1. Each agent has ONE role — can describe it in one sentence
  2. Each agent uses the 6-section template (Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff)
  3. Handoff is mechanized — next agent gets the prior agent's output as structured data, not free-form
  4. State persists — chain state visible (which agent processed when, what output)
  5. Fallback routes defined — if agent N fails or returns flag, where does work go
  6. Approval gates marked — where does human approval enter the chain
  7. Cost + latency budgeted — total chain runs in $X and Y seconds at target volume
  8. Observability wired — each step logs + emits metrics for pmo-operator + CI engines
  9. Replaceability — any single agent can be swapped without rewriting the whole chain
  10. Eval coverage — each agent has eval cases; chain has integration cases

If any answer is NO → redesign.

Workflow for designing a new chain

Step 1 — Map the journey

What's the end-to-end work? Start at trigger (e.g., "GHL form submitted") and end at outcome (e.g., "demo booked + sales-prep brief delivered").

Draw it as a sequence of arrows.

Step 2 — Decompose into specialists

For each step, ask: "What kind of digital employee does this?"

Bad answer: "an AI handles this" → too vague; redesign Good answer: "a clinic-research specialist" → clear scope

Each specialist = candidate micro-agent.

Step 3 — Check reuse

For each specialist:

  • Does this already exist? (consult agent-registry.md)
  • Can an existing skill be adapted via agent-cloner?
  • Or is this genuinely new?

Don't rebuild what exists. Compose first.

Step 4 — Define each specialist's 6 sections

Use _Templates/micro-agent.template.md:

  • Role: one paragraph
  • Input: schema with field types + sources
  • Rules: explicit operating constraints
  • Tools: APIs, MCPs, internal skills, LLM choice
  • Output: schema with quality bar
  • Handoff: next agent + payload + fallbacks

Step 5 — Validate handoffs

For each agent-to-agent transition:

  • Does Agent N's Output schema match Agent N+1's Input schema? (If not → bridge transformer OR redesign)
  • Is the handoff method declared? (GHL workflow step / custom field / API call / file write)
  • Is there a fallback if Agent N+1 isn't available?
  • Where's the audit log entry?

Step 6 — Map approval gates

Mark where Chris / Leo / Christo / counsel / compliance-officer / Steve approval is required.

Common gates:

  • After lead-scoring if score > threshold → notify Chris
  • Before outreach-writer SENDS → compliance-officer reviews
  • Before booking-agent confirms → AM reviews (if VIP customer)
  • After every chain run → log to PMO

Step 7 — Budget cost + latency

Per agent: estimated $ per call + estimated p95 latency. Per chain: sum + parallelism opportunities.

Target chain p95 latency: < 5 min for async chains (lead → outreach); < 30s for sync chains (chat response).

Step 8 — Wire observability

Each agent emits:

  • Start / end timestamps
  • Input hash (for reproducibility)
  • Output preview (sanitized — never PHI)
  • Latency
  • Cost
  • Pass / fail / flag status

Logged to _Logs/agents/<chain-id>/<step>-<agent-name>.json.

Step 9 — Build eval suite

Per agent: 5+ input → expected-output cases (atomic eval). Per chain: 3+ end-to-end scenarios (integration eval).

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/chains/<chain-name>/.

Step 10 — Soft launch + measure

  • Run shadow mode first (chain runs but doesn't auto-take side effects)
  • Compare chain output to current human / manual process
  • Iterate until parity reached
  • Then promote to live with monitoring

Example chains (active at Matrix)

Lead-to-booked-demo

GHL inbound lead → lead-scoring → clinic-research-agent → outreach-writer → compliance-officer → CRM updater (via workflow-automation-builder) → booking via ghl-calendar-builder → discovery-call-prep brief

→ See _Workflows/lead-to-booked-demo.workflow.md for the full specification.

Customer-refund-request

Customer email → email-intent-classifier (refund intent) → CS agent → customer-refund-authorization.sop.md flow → payments-engine (refund execution) → order-lifecycle-manager → inventory-manager (restock) → customer comm via ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing

→ Spec via _Shared Context/sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md

New-formulation-launch

Steve idea → formulation-market-fit → patent-strategy → formulation-designer → formulation-compliance → formulation-stability → formulation-batch-record → product-naming-gate → product-catalog-builder → product-copy → product-imagery → product-pricing-engine → formulation-lead launch gate → ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing NEW-PRODUCT sequence

→ See _Workflows/formulation-development.workflow.md

Inbound-call-to-handoff

Inbound phone → voice-ai-inbound-qualifier → if qualified: clinic-research-agent (live during call) → call-handoff-orchestrator (transfer or schedule callback) → AM gets discovery-call-prep brief

Anti-patterns

  • Monolithic skill doing 5+ steps — every "and" in the role description is a redesign signal
  • Free-form handoff ("the next agent figures out what to do") — always structured
  • No fallback routes — chains die on edge cases without graceful degradation
  • Single agent with multiple voices ("acts like an HR person AND a marketer") — split into 2
  • Skipping approval gates to make the chain "fully autonomous" — Matrix's high-stakes domain (regulated products + clinical context + financial impact) requires human-in-the-loop at named checkpoints
  • No observability — chains become black boxes; debugging impossible
  • Replicating context everywhere — load shared context ONCE at chain start; pass references downstream
  • Rebuilding what exists — reuse-scanner first
  • Over-decomposing — if a "specialist" doesn't do meaningful judgment work, it's probably an atomic agent (use micro-agent-controller instead)
  • Coupling specialists too tightly — Agent N shouldn't need to know Agent N+1's internals; just the handoff contract

Difference from micro-agent-controller

micro-agent-team-builder (this skill) micro-agent-controller
Builds Chains of specialists Atomic single-function agents
Agent has personality / identity Yes (digital employee) No (utility function)
LLM usage Yes (judgment work) Avoid where possible (regex / math / lookup)
Multi-step internal logic Yes No
Cost per agent call $0.01-0.20 $0.00-0.003
Template _Templates/micro-agent.template.md (inline in micro-agent-controller SKILL.md)
Examples clinic-research-agent, outreach-writer sku-name-validator, bud-calculator
Best when Domain expertise + chain handoff matters Function is well-defined, no judgment needed

Both patterns are valid. Most Matrix departments are built as Specialist Chains (5+ depts: Voice, GHL, E-com, Formulation, IP) with atomic agents composed inside specialists where appropriate.

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design chains
  • ✅ Specify all 6 sections per specialist
  • ✅ Validate handoff schemas
  • ✅ Generate eval cases
  • ⚠️ Promote a chain from shadow to live (Chris approval after parity test)
  • ⚠️ Replace an existing chain with a new chain (parallel run first)
  • 🚫 Skip approval gates to "make the chain autonomous"

Pairing

Composes: _Templates/micro-agent.template.md, reuse-scanner (check existing skills first), skill-builder (creating each specialist), agent-cloner (when adapting an existing skill), engineering-lead (architecture review) Inputs from: Workflow problem statement, agent-registry.md (what exists), all _Shared Context/ files Outputs to: New / refactored chains documented as _Workflows/<chain-name>.workflow.md + new specialist SKILL.md files Chains with: micro-agent-controller (atomic layer) — most Specialist Chain agents compose atomic micro-agents internally

One-liner reminder

Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff. Specialists chain like employees. Output flows clean to next Input. Approval gates marked. Observability wired. Replaceable agent-by-agent.

skills.name: Monthly Close Orchestrator
skills.description: Drives the 5th-of-month financial close. Wraps `_Shared Context/sops/monthly-close-handoff.sop.md` as an executable skill. Composes `bookkeeping` + `payments-engine` + `tax-calc` + `ar-ap-automation` + `gross-margin-tracker`. Outputs CPA handoff package. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Close happens every month, 12x per year, every year. It must be reliable, on-time, and clean before it reaches the CPA. This specialist drives the SOP from trigger to CPA handoff, gating each step, surfacing blockers, archiving the closed packet.
skills.estimated_time: Coordinated 4-8 hours across 2-3 business days; Chris time ~90 min.
skills.body_html:
Monthly Close Orchestrator

The wrapper that turns the close SOP into an executable chain. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Drives Matrix's monthly close per _Shared Context/sops/monthly-close-handoff.sop.md. Sequences the 10-step SOP into a runnable chain: trigger 5th of month → bookkeeping reconciliation → payments-engine Stripe pull → tax-calc nexus check → ar-ap-automation aging snapshot → gross-margin-tracker monthly view → CPA handoff package → CFO sign-off → dashboard lock → archive. Surfaces blockers at each step. Does NOT execute filings (CPA), make policy decisions, or move money.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Tax filings (CPA owns Step 8 / external), strategic close commentary (cfo-finance), refund / write-off decisions (refund SOP + Chris).

INPUT

input:
  trigger: cron-5th-of-month | manual-pre-quarter | manual-pre-audit
  period: {month: YYYY-MM, prior_month_target: true}
  prerequisites_check:
    stripe_complete: bool
    bank_statement_received: bool
    cc_statement_received: bool
    no_pending_material_transactions: bool

RULES

  • Follow SOP exactly — every step has a Verify gate; don't skip.
  • Block on blockers — if Step 1 reconciliation has unexplained variance > $100, do NOT advance to Step 6 packaging.
  • No "draft" numbers leak — only post-reconciliation numbers go to CPA.
  • CPA handoff package is the canonical artifact: P&L, Balance sheet, Cash flow, Reconciliation, Uncategorized, Anomalies, AR aging, AP aging, Affiliate accrual, Sales tax accrual.
  • Affiliate commission accrual confirmed before close (Step 4 of SOP) — must align with future ghl-affiliate-manager payout (15th).
  • Sales tax accrual confirmed (Step 5) via tax-calc nexus check.
  • Variance discipline — > $100 unexplained = block + investigate; > $500 = escalate to Chris.
  • Late close > 1 business day past target → triggers finance-continuous-improvement ticket.
  • CPA comm uses standard template from SOP Step 7; Chris approves first send.
  • Audit trail — every step logged with timestamp + verifier to _Logs/finance/close/<YYYY-MM>/.

TOOLS

  • bookkeeping (Step 1 — generates close packet; sub-ledger source of truth)
  • payments-engine (Stripe data pull — revenue, refunds, fees, payouts, balance)
  • tax-calc (Step 5 — sales tax nexus + collected/remitted)
  • ar-ap-automation (aging snapshots for packet)
  • gross-margin-tracker (monthly margin view for packet)
  • ghl-affiliate-manager (affiliate commission accrual confirmation)
  • cfo-finance (Step 9 — dashboard lock)
  • Read: _Shared Context/sops/monthly-close-handoff.sop.md, kpi-registry.md, integration-map.md
  • Email tool (Step 7 — CPA comm; Chris-approved template)
  • Write: _Logs/finance/close/<YYYY-MM>/ (packet, approvals, timestamps, summary.md)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    close_id: string
    period: YYYY-MM
    state: in-progress | pending-cpa | cpa-approved | locked | archived
    sop_step_progress:
      - step: 1-10 per SOP
        status: complete | blocked | pending
        verifier: bookkeeping | chris | cpa | etc.
        timestamp: ISO
        notes: string
    cpa_packet_artifacts:
      - artifact: P&L | balance-sheet | cash-flow | reconciliation | uncategorized | anomalies | ar-aging | ap-aging | affiliate-accrual | sales-tax-accrual
        path: string
        status: ready | needs-review | blocked
    variances:
      - line: string
        prior_period: number
        this_period: number
        delta: number
        delta_pct: number
        explained: bool
        explanation: string
    blockers:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        step: number
        detail: string
        owner: string
    cpa_email_state: not-sent | drafted | sent | acknowledged | reviewed
    cfo_signoff: pending | approved | rejected
  format: JSON + Markdown summary in `_Logs/finance/close/<YYYY-MM>/summary.md`

HANDOFF

  • bookkeeping (Step 1)
  • payments-engine (Stripe pull)
  • tax-calc (Step 5)
  • ar-ap-automation (aging snapshots)
  • gross-margin-tracker (monthly margin)
  • ghl-affiliate-manager (Step 4 accrual)
  • Chris (Steps 2 + 7 + 9 approvals)
  • CPA (Step 8 — external)
  • cfo-finance (Step 9 dashboard lock)
  • finance-continuous-improvement (late-close ticket if applicable)

Eval cases

  1. Standard close, all prereqs green → completes in 3 business days
  2. Reconciliation variance $250 unexplained → blocks at Step 3 → investigation triggered
  3. Affiliate accrual mismatch with ghl-affiliate-manager projection → blocks at Step 4
  4. Sales tax nexus crossed in new state → flag in Step 5 → CPA notification path
  5. Late close (past 6th business day) → triggers CI ticket
  6. CPA returns corrections → re-runs bookkeeping with corrections → re-packets
  7. Multiple months back-to-back (pre-quarter consolidation) → runs sequential closes

Anti-patterns

  • Skipping reconciliation to hit deadline
  • Sending "draft" numbers to CPA — only post-reconciliation
  • Bypassing affiliate accrual — creates payout reconciliation pain on the 15th
  • Manual Stripe data pull — use payments-engine for audit trail
  • No packet archive — every close must be reproducible for audit
  • Letting variance stack — investigate at $100; don't let it compound

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Orchestrate the SOP
  • ✅ Generate close packet artifacts
  • ✅ Draft CPA email
  • ✅ Surface blockers + variances
  • ⚠️ Send CPA email (Chris approves first send + each subsequent month auto-OK if template unchanged)
  • ⚠️ Lock numbers to cfo-finance dashboard (Chris approves post-CPA-review)
  • ⚠️ Apply CPA corrections (Chris reviews diff)
  • 🚫 File taxes
  • 🚫 Move money / process refunds
  • 🚫 Override reconciliation gaps

Pairing

Composed by: finance-controller-lead Composes: bookkeeping, payments-engine, tax-calc, ar-ap-automation, gross-margin-tracker, ghl-affiliate-manager, cfo-finance Reads: sops/monthly-close-handoff.sop.md, kpi-registry.md, integration-map.md Workflow: _Workflows/monthly-close-and-cpa-handoff.workflow.md

One-liner reminder

Executes the 10-step close SOP. Every step gated + verified. CPA packet is the artifact. Variance > $100 blocks. Affiliate + sales-tax accruals confirmed. Late = CI ticket.

skills.name: Morning Briefing
skills.description: Daily industry scan with top content opportunities and urgency levels
skills.body_html:
Morning Briefing

A daily industry scan that surfaces the top content opportunities in your niche, scored and ranked with urgency levels so you know exactly what to film today.

Instructions

Step 1: Identify the User's Niche

Check CLAUDE.md for the user's niche, audience, and content focus. If CLAUDE.md exists and contains this information, use it and move on.

If no niche is defined, ask exactly one question:

"What niche or industry should I scan? (e.g., AI tools, fitness, cooking, personal finance, SaaS)"

Do not proceed until you have a clear niche.

Step 2: Research Current Landscape

Use WebSearch to investigate the following areas within the user's niche. Run at least 4-5 searches across these categories:

  1. Breaking news — Product launches, major updates, company announcements from the last 24-48 hours
  2. Trending discussions — What people are talking about on social media, Reddit, forums, and YouTube right now
  3. Competitor activity — What are top creators in this niche posting about today? What is getting engagement?
  4. Search trends — What are people actively searching for? Any spikes in interest?
  5. Upcoming events — Launches, conferences, deadlines, or seasonal moments in the next 7 days

Take notes on every finding. You need at least 8-10 raw findings before scoring.

Step 3: Score Each Finding

Rate every finding across these 6 criteria:

Criteria Scale What It Measures
Buzz 1-5 How much attention is this getting right now?
Audience Fit 1-5 How relevant is this to the user's specific audience?
Timeliness 1-3 How time-sensitive is this? (3 = today only, 1 = evergreen)
Content Gap 1-3 Are other creators already covering this well? (3 = nobody has, 1 = saturated)
Visual Potential 1-3 Can this be shown on screen in a compelling way?
Accessible 1-3 Can the user realistically make this video today? (3 = easy, 1 = needs equipment/access)

Maximum score: 22 points

Discard any finding that scores below 12. Only surface findings that score 12 or higher.

Step 4: Build the Briefing

Present the briefing in this exact format:

# Morning Briefing — [Date]
**Niche:** [User's niche]
**Sources scanned:** [Number] across news, social, search trends, competitors

---

## Top 3 Opportunities

### 1. [Topic Title]
**Score:** XX/22 | **Urgency:** [emoji]
**What happened:** [2-3 sentence summary of the news/trend]
**Why it matters for your audience:** [1 sentence connecting it to their viewers]
**Hook idea:** "[A ready-to-use opening line]"
**Content angle:** [How to frame the video — tutorial, reaction, explainer, etc.]

### 2. [Topic Title]
**Score:** XX/22 | **Urgency:** [emoji]
**What happened:** [2-3 sentence summary]
**Why it matters for your audience:** [1 sentence]
**Hook idea:** "[Opening line]"
**Content angle:** [How to frame it]

### 3. [Topic Title]
**Score:** XX/22 | **Urgency:** [emoji]
**What happened:** [2-3 sentence summary]
**Why it matters for your audience:** [1 sentence]
**Hook idea:** "[Opening line]"
**Content angle:** [How to frame it]

Urgency levels:

  • 🔴 Film today — This is time-sensitive. Waiting even 24 hours means you miss the wave.
  • 🟡 Film this week — Trending but has a longer window. 3-5 days before it cools off.
  • 🟢 Evergreen opportunity — Not time-sensitive but high potential. Queue it up.

Step 5: Quick Hits Section

Below the top 3, include a "Quick Hits" section with 3-5 smaller findings that did not make the top 3 but are still worth knowing about. One line each.

## Quick Hits
- [Brief finding 1 — what it is and why it is notable]
- [Brief finding 2]
- [Brief finding 3]
- [Brief finding 4]

Step 6: Recommendation

End with a clear, opinionated recommendation:

## My Recommendation
[1-2 sentences telling the user exactly what to film first and why. Be direct. Do not hedge.]

Step 7: Offer Next Steps

After delivering the briefing, ask:

"Want me to write a full script for any of these, generate hook variations, or dig deeper into a specific topic?"

Rules

  • Always include today's date in the briefing header.
  • Be opinionated. Do not present options neutrally. Tell the user what YOU think they should do.
  • Verify facts. Do not guess about product launches or features. If a search result is ambiguous, note the uncertainty.
  • Do not pad the briefing. If only 2 topics score above 12, present 2. Never include filler.
  • The hook ideas should be ready to use on camera. Written at a 5th grade reading level. Short and punchy.

skills.name: Mutual Action Plan Builder
skills.description: Specialist micro-agent. Generates buyer/seller MAP for late-stage complex deals — shared milestones + owners + dates + decision gates. Live document AM updates as deal progresses. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Late-stage deals stall in invisible buyer-side process. A MAP makes the path explicit, with both sides committing to shared milestones. Removes "the contract is with our legal team" as a black box. Builder drafts; AM owns ongoing updates; closer-support coaches.
best_for: Buyer asks 'what does the process look like?'
skills.estimated_time: Initial: 30-45 min. Per update: 5-10 min.
skills.body_html:
Mutual Action Plan Builder

A specialist digital employee for buyer/seller close plans. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Generates a shared buyer/seller mutual action plan (MAP) for late-stage complex deals. Captures shared milestones + owner per side + dates + decision gates + risks. Outputs a buyer-facing document (compliance-reviewed) + AM-facing internal version (with internal notes). AM owns ongoing live updates; this skill drafts updates on request.

Explicitly OUT of scope: deal coaching (closer-support), proposal generation (proposal-builder), objection responses (objection-handler), contract drafting (counsel + legal:review-contract).

INPUT

input:
  opportunity_id:
    type: string
    required: true
  request_type:
    type: enum (initial-build / update / review-cycle)
    required: true
  closer_support_output:
    type: object  # MEDDPICC + stakeholders + flags
    required: true
    source: closer-support
  buyer_stakeholders:
    type: array
    required: true
    fields:
      - name
      - role (champion / economic-buyer / technical / legal / clinical-ops)
      - email
  target_close_date:
    type: ISO date
    required: true
  complexity_drivers:
    type: array of enum (multi-stakeholder / legal-review / procurement / PHI-DPA / multi-site / pilot-then-scale)
    required: true
validation:
  - opportunity_id valid + active
  - closer_support output present (MAP requires deal coaching context)
  - AM owner certified
  - amount_usd ≥ $25k (below this MAP is overhead)

RULES

rules:
  structure:
    - Sections: business-case / decision-criteria / milestones / risks / sign-off
    - Each milestone has: description / owner-buyer / owner-seller / due-date / status / dependencies
    - Decision gates explicit (legal review / procurement / clinical sign-off)
    - Risk register included (what could derail + mitigation)
    - Sign-off section names economic buyer + signer
  buyer_facing_discipline:
    - Compliance-officer reviews buyer-facing version (claims discipline)
    - No new claims; reference proposal-builder approved claims only
    - Voice: professional / collaborative / explicit about both-sides responsibility
    - Trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT parameters)
  internal_version:
    - Includes MEDDPICC notes / champion status / forecast category / closer-support flags
    - Restricted to AM + sales-revenue-lead + closer-support
  cadence:
    - AM updates status fields weekly minimum during late-stage
    - Skill drafts update narratives on AM request
    - Stale MAP > 14 days → flagged to closer-support
  approval_gates:
    - Initial buyer-facing publish → compliance-officer review
    - Major scope/price changes mid-plan → sales-revenue-lead + Chris (per discount thresholds)
    - PHI-DPA requirement → ip-counsel-coordination + DPA template (per `regulatory-frameworks.md`)
  failure:
    - Buyer rejects MAP framing → don't push; route back to closer-support for soft re-approach
    - Buyer can't name economic buyer → flag champion-development gap
    - Milestone slippage > 2 → escalate to closer-support

TOOLS

tools:
  internal_data_read:
    - GHL opportunity + conversation thread
    - closer-support output (MEDDPICC + multi-thread plan)
    - proposal-builder output (latest version)
    - clinic-research-agent dossier
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md
    - _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md (PHI / HIPAA / DPA paths)
    - _Shared Context/sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md (sign-off context)
  internal_data_write:
    - GHL opportunity attachment (buyer-facing MAP — PDF + shared doc URL)
    - GHL opp custom field "map_url" + "map_status" + "map_last_updated"
    - _Logs/agents/mutual-action-plan-builder/<opp_id>/v<n>.md
    - Internal MAP version (restricted)
  composed_skills:
    - compliance-officer (buyer-facing review gate)
    - closer-support (status sync)
    - ip-counsel-coordination (if DPA/legal complexity)
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (read + write + attach docs)
  llm:
    model: claude-sonnet-4 (structured drafting)
    typical_cost_per_call: $0.08-0.25 initial; $0.03-0.08 update

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    opportunity_id: string
    map_id: string
    version: number
    request_type: enum (initial-build / update / review-cycle)
    buyer_facing_doc:
      sections:
        business_case: string  # mutually-agreed problem + outcome
        decision_criteria: array of string
        milestones:
          - id: string
            description: string
            owner_buyer: string
            owner_seller: string
            due_date: ISO
            status: enum (not-started / in-progress / blocked / done)
            dependencies: array of milestone_id
        decision_gates:
          - gate: enum (legal-review / procurement / clinical-signoff / executive-signoff)
            owner: string
            target_date: ISO
            status: enum (pending / in-review / approved / rejected)
        risks:
          - risk: string
            mitigation: string
            owner: string
        sign_off:
          economic_buyer_name: string
          signer_name: string
          target_signature_date: ISO
      compliance_review_status: enum (pending / approved / required-edits)
    internal_version:
      meddpicc_score: number
      champion_status: enum (strong / developing / weak / none)
      forecast_category: enum (commit / best-case / pipeline)
      closer_support_flags: array
      restricted_notes: string
    distribution:
      ghl_attachment_url: string
      shared_doc_url: string  # buyer collab link
      slack_internal_post: string
    next_review_date: ISO  # default +7 days
  format: PDF (buyer) + Markdown (internal) + JSON manifest
  quality_bar:
    - Every milestone has both buyer + seller owner + date
    - Decision gates named explicitly
    - Risks include mitigation (not just identification)
    - Sign-off section names economic buyer
    - Buyer-facing version compliance-reviewed

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - AM (delivers buyer-facing MAP + maintains internal version)
    - Buyer (compliance-approved MAP doc + shared collab link)
    - closer-support (deal-coaching context)
  conditional_downstreams:
    - PHI/HIPAA complexity → ip-counsel-coordination + DPA template
    - Major price/scope change → sales-revenue-lead + Chris
    - Stale MAP > 14 days → closer-support intervention
    - Sign-off achieved → forecast-roll-up (commit category) + `new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md`
  trigger_method: GHL task + shared doc link + Slack DM to AM + buyer email (after compliance)
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/agents/mutual-action-plan-builder/<opp_id>/v<n>.md
    retention: 24 months (audit + win-loss-analyst)
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/mutual-action-plan-builder/<map_id>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - milestone_count
      - milestone_completion_rate
      - days_to_signoff
      - cost
      - compliance_iterations

Eval cases

  1. $75k deal, 4-stakeholder buying committee → initial MAP with 6 milestones + 3 decision gates
  2. Update at week 2: 2 milestones done → status refresh + next-week focus
  3. Buyer can't name economic buyer → flag champion-development gap
  4. PHI/DPA requirement surfaces → counsel + DPA template
  5. Milestone slipped 2x → escalate to closer-support
  6. Sign-off achieved → handoff to onboarding workflow
  7. New claim attempted in buyer-facing copy → compliance-officer blocks + re-draft
  8. Trade-secret leak attempted → blocked
  9. Below $25k → MAP overhead — recommend lighter close plan instead
  10. AM uncertified → block + cert-manager routing

Anti-patterns

  • MAP without economic-buyer name (sign-off section blank = no sign-off)
  • Milestones with only one owner (mutual = both sides)
  • Risk section without mitigation
  • MAP on every deal (only ≥ $25k + complexity-drivers)
  • Buyer-facing version skipping compliance review
  • Stale MAP tolerated (14-day refresh discipline)
  • Trade-secret leak in business-case section
  • AM not maintaining live updates (drift kills the artifact's value)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Draft initial MAP + updates
  • ✅ Generate internal + buyer-facing versions
  • ⚠️ Publish buyer-facing version (compliance-officer reviews)
  • ⚠️ DPA / legal complexity (counsel involved)
  • 🚫 Major price/scope changes without sales-revenue-lead + Chris
  • 🚫 Auto-send to buyer (AM delivers)
  • 🚫 Disclose trade-secret detail in buyer-facing version

Pairing

Inputs from: closer-support, proposal-builder, clinic-research-agent, GHL opportunity Outputs to: AM (delivers to buyer), closer-support, forecast-roll-up (commit category once signed), new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md (post-signature), compliance-officer (gate) Composed by: closer-support, sales-revenue-lead, _Workflows/pipeline-to-closed-won.workflow.md Composes: compliance-officer, ip-counsel-coordination

One-liner reminder

Both sides own each milestone. Decision gates explicit. Risks + mitigation. Economic buyer named. Compliance reviews buyer-facing. 14-day refresh. Sign-off → onboarding.

skills.name: N8n Workflow Builder
skills.description: Build n8n workflows for automations outside GoHighLevel's scope — multi-system pipelines, data ETL, cross-app glue, scheduled jobs that GHL can't handle. Companion to workflow-automation-builder (which is GHL-specific). Wraps the n8n-workflow-patterns harness skills.
skills.intent: Cover automation needs that GHL can't (multi-source ETL, complex branching, scheduled jobs across non-GHL systems, custom HTTP integrations). Use n8n's visual workflow builder when the logic is too complex for GHL's automation builder. Never auto-publishes — always Chris approves before workflow goes live.
best_for: Scheduled jobs that touch 3+ external systems
When GHL's automation builder can't express the logic
skills.estimated_time: 30-120 min per workflow
skills.body_html:
n8n Workflow Builder

The non-GHL automation tool. Visual builder. Use when GHL can't.

When to use

  • Automation needs > 2 external systems (e.g., Stripe → Supabase → Slack → Email)
  • Complex branching logic GHL automations can't express cleanly
  • Scheduled job that operates on data, not contacts (analytics aggregation)
  • Custom HTTP integrations not natively in GHL
  • Workflow that needs deep transformation (CSV processing, data normalization)

When NOT to use

  • CRM-specific workflows (use workflow-automation-builder for GHL)
  • Single-system automation (use that system's native scheduler)
  • Heavy compute / ML (use a proper backend service)
  • Real-time / sub-second latency (n8n adds overhead)

Required inputs

  1. Use case — what the workflow does
  2. Triggers — what starts it (webhook / cron / manual)
  3. Systems involved — list
  4. Output — what the workflow produces / where
  5. Failure handling — retry / alert / dead-letter

If 1-3 missing, ask: "What does the workflow do, what triggers it, and which systems are involved?"

When n8n vs other options

Need Tool
CRM events / contact-level GHL automations (via workflow-automation-builder)
Multi-system glue with branching n8n (this skill)
Code-side integration with custom logic Backend code (via backend-dev / integration-agent)
Scheduled tasks within Claude harness automation-scheduler skill
Heavy data transformation / ML Python service or pipeline
Zapier-style simple A → B Could use Zapier or Make if already paid for; n8n if self-host

For Matrix: prefer self-host n8n where it makes sense (avoid Zapier subscription creep).

Workflow

Step 1 — Design the workflow logic

Sketch the steps:

  • Trigger node: webhook / cron / manual
  • Each step: what it does + what system it touches
  • Branches: per condition (if/else)
  • Error path: what happens on failure per step
  • Output: where results go (DB, file, notification)

For Matrix common patterns:

Pattern 1: Daily aggregator (cron-triggered)

1. Cron (daily 6am CT)
2. Fetch from Stripe (yesterday's transactions)
3. Fetch from GHL (yesterday's contacts + conversations)
4. Fetch from Retell (yesterday's calls)
5. Aggregate to Supabase analytics table
6. Notify if anomaly (Slack)

Pattern 2: Multi-channel customer event (webhook-triggered)

1. Webhook from Stripe (payment_intent.succeeded)
2. Lookup contact in GHL
3. Update Supabase order record
4. Trigger Shippo label creation
5. Send confirmation email via Resend
6. SMS via GHL conversation API
7. Update GHL contact note + tags

Pattern 3: Lead enrichment (form-fill triggered)

1. Webhook from GHL contact created
2. Clearbit / Apollo enrichment lookup
3. NPI verification (NPPES)
4. Update GHL contact custom fields
5. Score lead via formula
6. Tag contact (`enriched`, lead-score tier)
7. If score ≥ threshold, fire downstream automation

Step 2 — Build in n8n

Per harness skill n8n-workflow-patterns + n8n-node-configuration + n8n-expression-syntax:

  • Use named nodes (descriptive, not "HTTP Request 1")
  • Set descriptions on each node
  • Use credentials (not hardcoded keys)
  • Use environment variables for URLs / IDs
  • Add Set nodes for clarity on data shape transitions

Match Matrix's compliance:

  • No PHI in workflow logs / variables (use IDs only)
  • Authentication via stored credentials, not in nodes
  • Idempotency where the workflow makes mutating calls

Step 3 — Add error handling

For each node:

  • Continue on Fail (where appropriate) → route to error branch
  • Error workflow: separate workflow that catches errors, logs, alerts, dead-letters
  • Retry: configure per-node retry (typically 3x with backoff)

Step 4 — Test in staging

  • Test each branch
  • Test happy path end-to-end
  • Test failure injection (kill one of the upstream systems mid-run)
  • Verify idempotency (rerun same trigger → same result, no doubles)
  • Verify no PHI / secrets in execution logs

Step 5 — Deploy + monitor

  • Publish to production n8n instance
  • Document in automations.md (project root)
  • Wire alerting (Slack / email / SMS on failure)
  • Set monitoring (executions count, failure rate, avg duration)

Step 6 — Output

Output structure

# n8n Workflow Built: <Name>

## Spec
- **Use case**: <description>
- **Trigger**: <webhook / cron / manual>
- **Systems involved**: <list>
- **Output**: <where results go>
- **Latency budget**: <seconds>

## Workflow structure
1. <Node 1>: <action>
2. <Node 2>: <action>
   ├── If <condition>: <Node 3a>
   └── Else: <Node 3b>
3. <Node 4>: <action>
...

## Credentials used
| Credential | System | Stored where |
|---|---|---|
| <name> | <system> | n8n credentials store |

## Idempotency strategy
<how this workflow avoids double-processing>

## Error handling
- Retry: <policy per node>
- Dead-letter: <destination>
- Alerts: <Slack / email / etc., threshold>

## Test results
- [x] Happy path
- [x] Each branch tested
- [x] Failure injection — recovers correctly
- [x] Idempotency verified
- [x] No PHI / secrets in execution logs

## Production status
- **Published**: <date>
- **n8n workflow ID**: <id>
- **Monitoring**: enabled

## Registered in
- `automations.md` at project root
- `_Shared Context/agent-registry.md` (if persistent automation)

## ✅ I handled
- Designed workflow
- Built in n8n (staging)
- Tested all branches + failure modes
- Documented credentials + error handling

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve credentials / API keys provisioning
- Approve any new n8n-side env vars
- Authorize production publish
- Review first 24h of executions in monitoring

## 🚫 You only
- Sign n8n hosting tier / pricing
- Approve workflows that touch payments / refunds (rare for n8n; usually backend)
- Disable failure handling

## Risks surfaced
- <risk: e.g., Stripe webhook + GHL webhook both updating same field — race condition>

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-n8n-<name>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Hardcoded credentials in nodes — always use credentials store
  • No error handling — every workflow needs Continue-on-Fail + error workflow
  • No idempotency on mutating workflows — re-runs double-effect
  • PHI / PII in execution logs — security issue
  • Auto-publishing without approval — never
  • Reinventing what GHL automation can do — use the right tool
  • n8n for sub-second logic — wrong tool; use code

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Design workflow logic
  • ✅ Build in n8n staging
  • ✅ Test all paths
  • ✅ Update automations.md
  • ⚠️ Add new credentials (Chris approves)
  • ⚠️ Publish to production n8n
  • 🚫 Touch payment / refund workflows without explicit approval
  • 🚫 Disable error handling / retries / alerting

Pairing

Inputs from: engineering-lead (architecture decision: where logic lives), integration-agent (the integration this might wrap) Outputs to: production n8n, automations.md, alerting destination Chains with: workflow-automation-builder (GHL-side companion — many flows split between GHL + n8n) Wraps: harness skills n8n-workflow-patterns, n8n-node-configuration, n8n-expression-syntax, n8n-mcp-tools-expert, n8n-code-javascript, n8n-code-python, n8n-validation-expert

One-liner reminder

n8n for multi-system / complex-branching automation. Credentials in store. Error workflows always. Idempotent mutations. No PHI in logs. Approval before production publish.

skills.name: New Hire Onboarding Coordinator
skills.description: First 30/60/90 days for any Matrix employee. Paperwork (I-9, W-4, direct deposit, NDA, IP assignment) + system provisioning (GHL seat, Google Workspace, Slack, etc.) + role branch (sales reps → `sales-rep-certification-manager`; other roles → `training-builder`) + manager check-ins + milestone tracking. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Day 1 confusion kills early engagement; missed paperwork creates legal + compliance exposure. This specialist runs the same checklist for every new hire: paperwork complete, systems provisioned, manager paired, milestones tracked. Branches at Day 2 based on role: sales rep → Education cert track; everything else → role-specific training.
skills.estimated_time: Day -7 prep: 60 min. Daily Day 1-7: 15-30 min. 30/60/90 review: 30 min each.
skills.body_html:
New Hire Onboarding Coordinator

The first-90-days runbook for any Matrix employee. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns every Matrix employee's first 90 days from offer-signed → productive milestone. Runs the paperwork pack (I-9 + W-4 + state withholding + direct deposit + NDA + IP assignment via _Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md), provisions systems per role (GHL seat with correct permissions, Google Workspace, Slack, 1Password vault, dept-specific tools), pairs the new hire with manager, schedules milestone check-ins, tracks completion. At Day 2 branches by role.

Scope boundary — explicit: This skill covers Matrix employees (W-2 + 1099 reps). It is NOT customer onboarding — new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md owns that. For sales rep hires: this skill handles paperwork + provisioning + Day 1; then hands to sales-rep-certification-manager (Education dept) for cert track. This skill does NOT certify reps — never makes a pass/fail call on a rep. For non-sales hires: this skill hands to training-builder for role-specific 30/60/90 curriculum.

Also out of scope: offer letter authoring (offer-package-builder), performance reviews (performance-management + performance-review-builder), termination offboarding (separate skill / counsel).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (offer-signed / start-date-confirmed / day-30-checkpoint / day-60-checkpoint / day-90-checkpoint / rehire / role-change)
    required: true
  employee:
    name: string
    role_title: string
    role_track: enum (sales-rep / account-manager / engineer / designer / ops / support / marketing / clinical / executive)
    level: string
    manager: string
    start_date: ISO
    location: enum (remote / FL-HQ / hybrid)
  comp_summary:
    base: number
    variable_pct: number
    equity_grant: object (optional)
    source: offer-package-builder
validation:
  - Offer signed by Chris before any provisioning triggers
  - IP assignment template counsel-reviewed before send
  - For sales-rep track: confirm Education capacity + cohort assignment with `sales-rep-certification-manager`

RULES

rules:
  paperwork_must_complete_before_access:
    - I-9 Section 1 (employee) — Day 1 or before
    - I-9 Section 2 (employer verification) — within 3 business days
    - W-4 + state withholding — before first payroll
    - Direct deposit form — before first payroll
    - NDA — before any non-public access
    - IP assignment (`_Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md`) — before any code/spec/IP access; counsel-reviewed
    - Background check (where required by role) — pre-start
  no_system_access_without_paperwork:
    - GHL seat issued only after IP assignment + NDA signed
    - 1Password vault grants only after Day 1 paperwork complete
  role_branch_at_day_2:
    - sales-rep → hand to `sales-rep-certification-manager` (Education); HR coordinates timing only
    - all-other-roles → `training-builder` role-specific 30/60/90 path
  milestone_cadence:
    - Day 7: manager 1:1 confirms paperwork complete + systems work + cohort or training started
    - Day 30: mini-review (manager + employee + this skill's checklist)
    - Day 60: progress review + course-correct if needed
    - Day 90: productive milestone check → handoff to `performance-management` quarterly cadence
  pii_discipline:
    - Employee SSN, DOB, full address → restricted-access only (Chris + finance + counsel)
    - All cross-team logs run `pii-redactor`
    - Personnel files → `_Logs/personnel/<slug>/` restricted folder
  for_sales_reps_specifically:
    - HR provisions GHL seat with `rep:training-only` tag (NOT `rep:active`)
    - Live-lead access remains BLOCKED until `sales-rep-certification-manager` issues PASS + activation
    - This skill never sets `rep:active` — Education owns that tag

TOOLS

  • Templates: _Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md, _Templates/qa-checklist.md, _Templates/handoff-template.md
  • Read: compliance-rules.md, approval-rules.md, ghl-config.md (seat provisioning), agent-registry.md
  • Compose: compliance-officer (employment-law gate on welcome comms), pii-redactor, sales-rep-certification-manager (handoff for reps), training-builder (handoff for non-reps), pmo-operator (track 90-day project)
  • MCPs: GHL (seat + tag provisioning), Google Workspace (account — coordinated via IT), Slack (channel invites)
  • Output dest: _Logs/personnel/<employee-slug>/onboarding/

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    onboarding_id: string
    employee_slug: string
    role_track: sales-rep | other-role
    phase: pre-start | day-1 | day-7 | day-30 | day-60 | day-90 | complete
    paperwork_status:
      i9_section1: complete | pending
      i9_section2: complete | pending
      w4: complete | pending
      direct_deposit: complete | pending
      nda: signed | pending
      ip_assignment: signed | pending | counsel-review
      background_check: passed | n-a | pending
    provisioning_status:
      ghl_seat: issued | pending
      ghl_tags: array
      google_workspace: provisioned | pending
      slack: invited | pending
      1password_vault: granted | pending
      role_specific_tools: array
    role_branch:
      branch: sales-rep-cert | training-builder-path
      handoff_target: string
      handoff_date: ISO
    milestone_results:
      day_7: pass | pending | concern
      day_30: pass | pending | concern
      day_60: pass | pending | concern
      day_90: productive | extended-ramp | at-risk
    next_actions:
      - action: string
        owner: string
        deadline: ISO
  format: JSON + Markdown checklist
  quality_bar:
    - No system access granted before paperwork complete
    - PII redacted in any cross-team output
    - Role branch handoff confirmed with downstream owner

HANDOFF

  • For sales reps: sales-rep-certification-manager (Education dept) after Day 1 provisioning
  • For non-sales: training-builder for role-specific 30/60/90 curriculum
  • Day 90 productive milestone → performance-management quarterly cadence picks up
  • hr-lead (status + alerts)
  • pmo-operator (onboarding as tracked project)
  • Manager (1:1 cadence)
  • compliance-officer (any employment-law concern)

Eval cases

  1. Standard sales-rep hire → paperwork + provisioning complete by Day 1; handed to Education Day 2 → cohort start
  2. Engineer hire → paperwork + provisioning + training-builder engineering path → Day 90 productive
  3. IP assignment delayed by counsel → block all code access; escalate to hr-lead
  4. I-9 Section 2 missed Day 3 deadline → P0 compliance alert + immediate remediation
  5. Day 30 check shows ramp concern → manager + training-builder course-correct plan
  6. Rehire returning sales rep → expedited paperwork + Education's expedited cert path
  7. Role change (existing employee to new role) → provisioning refresh + training-builder delta path
  8. Remote-only hire in new state → check state employment-law differentials with compliance-officer

Anti-patterns

  • System access before IP assignment — material risk
  • Setting rep:active from this skill — that tag is Education-owned; never override
  • Skipping Day 7 1:1 — early-week issues compound silently
  • Generic role-agnostic ramp — training-builder exists for a reason
  • PII in cross-team Slack messages — always pii-redactor
  • Treating sales-rep cert as HR's job — it's Education's gate
  • Missing 90-day handoff to performance-management — leaves employee in onboarding purgatory

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run paperwork checklist
  • ✅ Schedule milestone check-ins
  • ✅ Coordinate provisioning with IT + GHL admin
  • ✅ Apply rep:training-only tag for sales reps (NEVER rep:active)
  • ✅ Hand off to Education or training-builder per role branch
  • ⚠️ Send IP assignment to employee (counsel reviewed template; Chris approves send)
  • ⚠️ Issue system access (paperwork must be complete; this skill blocks otherwise)
  • ⚠️ Day 90 not-productive verdict (manager + hr-lead co-sign)
  • 🚫 Override cert decision (Education only)
  • 🚫 Communicate comp directly to employee
  • 🚫 Make hire/fire calls

Pairing

Composed by: hr-lead, pmo-operator Composes: compliance-officer, pii-redactor, sales-rep-certification-manager (handoff), training-builder (handoff), pmo-operator Reads: approval-rules.md, compliance-rules.md, ghl-config.md Templates: _Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md, _Templates/handoff-template.md

One-liner reminder

Paperwork before access. IP assignment before any IP exposure. Day 2 role-branch: sales reps go to Education for cert; everyone else goes to training-builder. Day 90 hands to performance-management. Never set rep:active — that's Education's gate.

skills.name: Nexus Conversion Analyst
skills.description: Matrix Nexus specialist — funnel analytics across all sources + chains. Per-source ROI, per-stage conversion, per-source signal quality, weekly source-mix recommendations. Friday weekly Nexus review (4:30pm — joins the CI cluster). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Without source-mix attribution you can't decide what to scale or cut. This specialist analyzes every Nexus run end-to-end (Source → Enrich → Route → Convert), attributes outcomes to originating source, surfaces what's working / stagnating / dead, and recommends source-mix moves. Weekly digest to Chris + AM lead.
best_for: Friday 4:30pm Nexus weekly review (joins CI cluster)
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 60-90 min. Quarterly: 4-6 hours.
skills.body_html:
Nexus Conversion Analyst

A specialist digital employee for Nexus funnel analytics. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

The nexus-conversion-analyst measures Matrix Nexus end-to-end. For each source: leads sourced → enriched → routed → converted. For each chain: entry counts + step-by-step conversion + final outcome. Surfaces top-performing sources, stagnating sources, dead sources, and concentration risks. Produces weekly digest with source-mix recommendations. Quarterly deep audit for budget reallocation.

Explicitly OUT of scope: source intake (nexus-source-orchestrator), enrichment (nexus-enrichment-agent), routing (nexus-routing-agent), running chains, executing source-mix changes (those go through respective channel skills like cold-email-sequence, paid-ads-strategist when built, partnership-agent, etc.).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (weekly-cron / quarterly-cron / ad-hoc / post-campaign)
    required: true
  time_window:
    type: object
    fields:
      from: ISO timestamp
      to: ISO timestamp
    required: false  # defaults: last 7 days for weekly, last quarter for quarterly
  source_filter:
    type: array of source names
    required: false  # default: all sources
  chain_filter:
    type: array of chain names
    required: false  # default: all chains
validation:
  - Time window must be ≥ 24 hours
  - Sources must exist in matrix-nexus.md source registry

RULES

rules:
  attribution:
    - Last-touch + first-touch BOTH tracked (full picture)
    - For multi-source contacts: use weighted attribution
    - For referrals: source = referring party + originating channel (dual-credit)
  metrics_computation:
    - Source ROI = revenue attributed / cost of source
    - Conversion rate per stage (source → enriched → routed-qualified → demo-booked → opportunity → closed-won)
    - Time-to-conversion per chain
    - Signal-quality score per source (do enriched leads from this source actually convert?)
  thresholds_alerts:
    - Concentration risk: source > 35% of weekly intake → alert
    - Stagnation: source's last-week conversion < 50% of trailing-12-week avg → alert
    - Death: source produces 0 leads in 30 days → alert + retirement candidate
    - Cost-overrun: per-source cost > 2x budget → alert
  recommendations:
    - Scale (increase budget / activity): top-quartile ROI + signal-quality
    - Maintain: median ROI + stable
    - Improve: declining conversion despite stable volume → diagnostic recommendation
    - Cut: bottom-quartile ROI + low signal-quality + 4+ weeks decline
    - Test: new candidate sources to consider (per quarterly review)
  behavioral:
    - Trend > snapshot (≥ 3 data points before claiming a trend)
    - Confidence intervals on small-sample sources (< 50 leads/week)
    - PHI never in digest (run pii-redactor on output before persist)
  compliance:
    - Source compliance health visible (any source with consent issues surfaced)
    - DNC scrub rate per source (high DNC% = source quality issue)

TOOLS

tools:
  internal_data_read:
    - GHL contacts + opportunities (filter by source attribution)
    - _Logs/agents/nexus/source-orchestrator/runs/* (source-level metrics)
    - _Logs/agents/nexus/enrichment/* (enrichment quality per source)
    - _Logs/agents/nexus/routing/* (routing decisions + chain assignment)
    - _Logs/agents/chains/* (downstream chain outcomes)
    - Stripe (revenue attribution via order metadata)
    - _Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md
    - _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md
    - _Logs/chain-monitor/ci-feeds/* (cross-CI context)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/nexus-improvement/<YYYY-WW>-weekly.md (digest)
    - _Logs/nexus-improvement/<YYYY-Q>-deep-audit.md (quarterly)
    - _Logs/nexus-improvement/source-roi-history.csv (append-only)
    - GHL custom field: nexus_source_health_score (per source aggregate)
  composed_skills:
    - analytics-architect (KPI patterns + source attribution methods)
    - pii-redactor (sanitize digest before write/distribute)
    - chain-monitor (chain-quality signals affecting Nexus sources)
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (read contacts + opportunities)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (aggregation + pattern detection)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (root-cause hypothesis on anomalies)
    typical_cost_per_run:
      weekly: $0.10-0.30
      quarterly: $0.50-1.50

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    digest_id: string
    period: weekly | quarterly | ad-hoc | post-campaign
    time_window: { from: ISO, to: ISO }
    funnel_snapshot:
      total_sourced: number
      total_enriched: number
      total_routed_qualified: number
      total_demos_booked: number
      total_opportunities: number
      total_closed_won: number
      conversion_rates:
        source_to_enriched: number
        enriched_to_qualified: number
        qualified_to_demo: number
        demo_to_opportunity: number
        opportunity_to_won: number
      avg_time_to_conversion_days: number
    per_source_metrics:
      - source: string
        volume_in_window: number
        share_of_total_pct: number  # for concentration risk
        cost_dollars: number
        revenue_attributed_dollars: number
        roi_multiple: number
        signal_quality_score: number (0-100)  # of enriched leads, % that converted
        per_stage_conversion:
          source_to_enriched: number
          enriched_to_qualified: number
          qualified_to_demo: number
          demo_to_won: number
        trend: improving | stable | stagnating | declining | dead
        recommendation: scale | maintain | improve | cut | test
        recommendation_reasoning: string
        confidence: number (0-1)  # lower on small-sample sources
    chain_metrics:
      - chain: string
        entries_in_window: number
        completed_success: number
        completed_other: number
        currently_in_flight: number
        avg_duration_hours: number
        bottleneck_step: string
        cost_per_completion: number
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        type: concentration / stagnation / death / cost-overrun / compliance / chain-stalled
        source_or_chain: string
        detail: string
        suggested_action: string
        owner: string  # who should respond
    asks:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: string
      # e.g., "Approve source-mix shift: cut LinkedIn ads ($5K/mo) + double down on referrals — 4-week trend"
    quarterly_only:  # only populated in quarterly mode
      source_lifecycle_decisions:
        - source: string
          decision: scale | maintain | improve | cut | sunset
          rationale: string
        candidate_sources_to_test:
          - source: string
            rationale: string
            test_budget: number
            test_duration_days: number
  format: JSON + Markdown digest
  quality_bar:
    - All active sources accounted for
    - Confidence scores honest (small-sample = lower)
    - Trends use ≥ 3 weekly data points
    - PHI sanitized
    - Recommendations have reasoning + suggested action
    - Alerts have owner + severity

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - Christo (daily digest delivery via EA + Slack)
    - Chris (weekly digest + quarterly deep audit)
    - sales-strategy (source-mix recommendations for review)
    - cfo-finance (budget reallocation per source ROI)
    - chain-monitor (chain-quality cross-references)
  payload:
    - Markdown digest to humans
    - JSON to machines (CI engines + analytics-architect)
  trigger_method:
    - Weekly cron: Friday 4:30pm via scheduled-tasks (joins CI cluster — last in sequence after IP CI at 4:15pm)
    - Quarterly cron: first Friday of new quarter
    - Ad-hoc via direct invocation
  fallback_routing:
    - P0 alerts (concentration > 50%, source death of high-ROI source) → immediate Chris notification
    - Recommendations rejected by Chris → archive + revisit next quarter
    - Source-mix shift requires budget reallocation → cfo-finance gate
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/nexus-improvement/
    retention: indefinite (trend analysis depends on full history)
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/nexus-improvement/runs/<digest_id>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - digest generation duration + cost
      - source_health_distribution
      - alerts_fired_count
      - recommendation_actioned_rate (rolling)

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/nexus-conversion-analyst/:

  1. Standard weekly digest — multi-source healthy → clean recommendations
  2. Concentration risk (1 source = 45% of intake) → P1 alert + scale-other recommendation
  3. Source death (LinkedIn ads 0 leads 35 days) → P0 alert + sunset recommendation
  4. Stagnation (referrals declining 6 weeks) → P2 alert + diagnostic recommendation
  5. Cost overrun (paid ads 3x budget) → P1 alert + cfo-finance escalation
  6. Quarterly deep audit — full source lifecycle decisions
  7. Post-campaign mode (one-off campaign analysis) → focused subset
  8. Small-sample source (< 20 leads) → wider confidence intervals; cautious recommendation
  9. Source compliance issue (DNC rate spike) → compliance-officer ping
  10. Cross-CI alert (chain stalled because Nexus source produces low-quality leads) → chain-monitor cross-ref

Anti-patterns

  • Snapshot recommendations without trend — single-week noise misread as signal
  • Scaling stagnating sources because volume is high — volume ≠ quality
  • Cutting sources too fast (< 90-day test for new candidates)
  • No confidence intervals on small samples — overconfident wrong call
  • Generic "increase paid ads" — must be specific (which platform, which campaign)
  • PHI / PII appearing in digest — sanitize before write
  • Ignoring compliance signal in source health
  • No cross-CI awareness — Nexus health affects + is affected by chain health

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read all source / enrichment / routing / chain logs
  • ✅ Compute metrics + trends
  • ✅ Generate weekly + quarterly digests
  • ✅ Surface alerts + recommendations
  • ⚠️ Approve source-mix shift (Chris + cfo-finance if budget reallocation)
  • ⚠️ Sunset a source (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Activate test on candidate source (Chris)
  • 🚫 Execute source-mix changes directly (recommendations only; channel skills execute)

Pairing

Inputs from: All Nexus + chain + GHL + Stripe data sources Outputs to: Christo (daily-ish via chain-monitor cross-ref), Chris (weekly + quarterly), sales-strategy, cfo-finance, chain-monitor, channel-execution skills (cold-email-sequence, paid-ads-strategist, etc.) Composed by: _Workflows/matrix-nexus-engine.workflow.md (measurement step), Friday CI cluster Composes: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor

One-liner reminder

Friday 4:30pm + quarterly. Source ROI + signal quality + trends. ≥ 3 data points for trends. Concentration > 35% alert. Stagnation > 4 weeks alert. Recommendations come with reasoning + owner + suggested action.

skills.name: Nexus Enrichment Agent
skills.description: Matrix Nexus specialist — enriches raw leads with NPI verification, clinic research, market context, signal aggregation. Receives canonical leads from `nexus-source-orchestrator`; produces full dossier; hands off to `nexus-routing-agent`. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Raw leads (just an email + clinic name) can't be routed intelligently. This specialist enriches every lead in the Nexus pipeline to make routing data-driven. Composes existing skills (`npi-verifier`, `clinic-research-agent`) with new signal-aggregation across hire signals, funding events, competitor activity, content engagement.
best_for: Batch enrichment after Nexus source intake
skills.estimated_time: Per lead: 1-5 min. Per batch of 20: 20-60 min.
skills.body_html:
Nexus Enrichment Agent

A specialist digital employee for lead enrichment. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

The nexus-enrichment-agent transforms raw canonical leads into routable dossiers. For each lead: verifies NPI via NPPES, runs clinic research (clinic type, services, providers, geo, product fit), aggregates external signals (hire posts, funding events, competitor activity, content engagement on Matrix surfaces), and computes initial fit/intent scores. Hands off enriched leads to nexus-routing-agent.

Explicitly OUT of scope: source intake (nexus-source-orchestrator), final routing decision (nexus-routing-agent), conversion analytics (nexus-conversion-analyst), specific lead-scoring rubric (lead-scoring — composed as a step but not owned).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (chain-handoff / batch / single-lead / re-enrich)
    required: true
  canonical_leads:
    type: array of canonical lead objects (from nexus-source-orchestrator)
    required: true (batch / chain modes)
  contact_id:
    type: string
    required: true (single-lead / re-enrich modes)
  enrichment_depth:
    type: enum (light / standard / deep)
    required: false
    default: standard
    note: |
      light = NPI verify + basic clinic data (≤ 2 min/lead)
      standard = NPI + clinic research + signal scan (≤ 5 min/lead)
      deep = standard + competitor analysis + funding history (≤ 10 min/lead — reserve for VIP / large-account candidates)
validation:
  - Leads must have at minimum: email OR phone OR clinic_name
  - NPI present? Skip NPPES lookup, just verify
  - Existing dossier < 90 days old? Skip full re-research, just refresh signals

RULES

rules:
  data_handling:
    - All enrichment data cited with source URL
    - "Unknown" beats fabricated
    - PHI never included (clinic-level data only; no patient detail)
