    - Trade-secret ALM DT detail never disclosed in any output
  discovery_tier_order:
    - "Tier 1 (FREE, PRIMARY): Google Places API — Text Search + Place Details. ALWAYS called first for name/address/phone/website/hours/rating."
    - "Tier 2 (FREE, FALLBACK): WebFetch /contact /about /team /privacy for mailto + regex emails; WebSearch for news + job posts + funding signals. Only after Tier 1 logged."
    - "Tier 3 (PAID, LAST RESORT): Hunter.io Domain Search + Email Verifier — invoked ONLY for rows still missing email after Tiers 1+2. Skip Hunter call if email already populated."
    - Every dossier records *_source per data point (email_source, phone_source, website_source) with values like 'google-places', 'web-scrape', 'hunter'.
    - Free-quota guardrails — alert if projected Google Places spend > $180/mo (90% of free credit) OR Hunter usage > 200 credits per batch; halt Tier 3 calls if cap breached.
    - Tier-skip allowed only if Tier-(N-1) tool is explicitly unavailable (logged with reason); never skip to save time.
  enrichment_quality:
    - For B2B provider leads: NPI verification mandatory
    - For B2C / consumer leads (MuscleLock): skip NPI; rely on social + email-domain signals
    - Confidence per data point honest; no inflation
    - Sources cited: ≥ 3 distinct sources for any "verified" status
  signal_aggregation:
    - Hire signals (job posts < 30 days old) — strong intent indicator
    - Funding events (last 12 months) — capacity-to-pay indicator
    - Adjacent-service signals (clinic newly offering GLP-1 / hormone / etc.) — fit indicator
    - Competitor-customer signals — fit + objection-anticipation
    - Content engagement (Matrix Peptide Map / dosing guide visits) — warm-lead indicator
  behavioral:
    - Per-lead time budget per depth (light 2min / standard 5min / deep 10min)
    - Batch concurrency: max 10 parallel leads at any time
    - If lead is competitor → flag + halt enrichment (don't waste budget; route to Chris)
    - If lead is on FDA-attention list → flag + route to compliance-officer
  compliance:
    - Scraping respects ToS (already validated upstream)
    - DNC scrub at enrichment (refresh) in addition to source-orchestrator scrub
    - No public disclosure of trade-secret content (per alm-dt-platform.md)
  failure:
    - Single source unreachable → continue with remaining; mark dossier partial
    - All sources unreachable → quarantine + retry later
    - Compliance flag → route + halt

TOOLS

tools:
  composed_skills:
    - npi-verifier (atomic — NPPES API)
    - clinic-research-agent (specialist — clinic type / services / providers / fit)
    - lead-scoring (specialist — preliminary score during enrichment)
    - compliance-phrase-detector (atomic — sanitize before logging)
    - pii-redactor (atomic — output sanitization)
  internal_data_read:
    - GHL contact + conversations + opportunity history
    - _Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md
    - _Shared Context/ghl-config.md
    - _Shared Context/products.md (for product-fit reasoning)
    - _Shared Context/sub-brands.md
    - _Shared Context/alm-dt-platform.md (READ-ONLY for product context; trade-secret discipline)
    - Existing enrichment dossier (if re-enrich mode)
  internal_data_write:
    - GHL custom fields: enrichment_dossier_id, npi_verified, clinic_type, services_offered, matrix_product_fit_top3, hire_signal_active, funding_signal, content_engagement_score
    - GHL tags: enrichment:done, fit:high|medium|low, signal:hire-active, signal:funded-recent, signal:competitor-customer, etc.
    - _Logs/agents/nexus/enrichment/<contact_id>-<date>.json (full dossier)
  external_apis:
    # Tier 1 — FREE, PRIMARY (always first)
    - Google Places API (Text Search + Place Details) — env GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY; covers name/address/phone/website/hours/rating/business_status
    - Google Business Profile data (returned inline by Places when claimed)
    - NPPES (free, via npi-verifier)
    # Tier 2 — FREE, FALLBACK (only after Tier 1)
    - WebFetch (clinic websites: /contact /about /team /privacy → mailto + regex emails)
    - WebSearch (clinic news + LinkedIn job posts + funding events)
    - PitchBook / Crunchbase (when wired — funding signal)
    - Matrix's own content analytics (web visits to peptide-map / dosing-guide / catalog)
    # Tier 3 — PAID, LAST RESORT (email backfill only)
    - Hunter.io Domain Search + Email Verifier — env HUNTER_API_KEY; invoked ONLY when Tiers 1+2 produced no email for a row
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (read history + write enrichment fields)
    - WebFetch + WebSearch
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (default — research orchestration)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (deep mode + ambiguous product-fit reasoning)
    typical_cost_per_lead:
      light: $0.02-0.05
      standard: $0.05-0.15
      deep: $0.15-0.40

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    contact_id: string
    enrichment_id: string  # uuid
    enrichment_depth: light | standard | deep
    completed_at: ISO
    time_taken_seconds: number
    quality:
      data_points_verified: number
      data_points_unverified: number
      sources_cited: array of URLs
      confidence_overall: number (0-1)
    npi_verification:
      verified: boolean
      provider_name: string
      specialty: string
      status: active | deactivated | not-found | format-invalid
      flags: array
    clinic_dossier:
      # full output schema of clinic-research-agent, embedded
      clinic_type: object
      services_offered: array
      provider_count: object
      geographic_market: object
      matrix_product_fit: object
    signals:
      hire_signal:
        active: boolean
        roles_posted: array (e.g., ["Medical Director", "NP"])
        posted_days_ago: number
      funding_signal:
        active: boolean
        last_event: string  # e.g., "Series A 2024-Q3"
        amount: string
      adjacent_service_signal:
        new_services: array  # services added recently
        change_indicator: string  # "expanding offerings" / "stable" / "contracting"
      competitor_signal:
        competitor_customer_of: array  # competitors this clinic uses
        switching_signal: boolean  # any evidence they're shopping
      content_engagement:
        visits_to_matrix_sites: number  # last 90 days
        downloaded_resources: array
        engagement_score: number (0-100)
    preliminary_lead_scoring:
      lead_score: number (0-100)
      lead_tier: hot | warm | qualified | soft | cold
      top_drivers: array
    flags:
      - flag: enum (competitor / vip / fda-attention / multi-provider / sole-practitioner / pe-owned / chain-affiliated / regulatory-history)
        evidence: string
    handoff_recommendation:
      target_chain: enum (lead-to-booked-demo / inbound-call-to-handoff / expansion-opportunity / partnership-process / long-cycle-nurture / pause-for-review / pause-escalate-chris)
      reasoning: string
      priority: P0 | P1 | P2 | P3
  format: JSON
  quality_bar:
    - All required fields populated OR "unknown" with reason
    - Sources cited for fact claims
    - Confidence scores honest
    - No fabricated facts
    - PII / trade-secret discipline applied (run pii-redactor on logged output)

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream: nexus-routing-agent
  payload: full enrichment_id + dossier + signals + preliminary scoring + handoff_recommendation
  trigger_method:
    - GHL workflow tag advance: enrichment:done → nexus-routing-agent picks up
  fallback_routing:
    - if flag = "competitor" → pause + Chris
    - if flag = "fda-attention" → pause + compliance-officer review before routing
    - if all sources unreachable + can't enrich → re-queue with retry; if persistent → human review
    - if competitor-customer signal HIGH → handoff with "switching" angle to outreach-writer
  state_persistence:
    location:
      - GHL custom fields (top-level results)
      - GHL contact note (markdown summary visible to AMs)
      - _Logs/agents/nexus/enrichment/<contact_id>-<date>.json (full dossier)
    retention: 12 months
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/nexus-enrich/<step>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - enrichment_depth used
      - data_points_verified
      - confidence_overall
      - signals_detected (count per category)
      - cost
      - duration

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/nexus-enrichment-agent/:

  1. Standard depth, B2B provider clinic, full data → green dossier
  2. Light depth (volume processing) → minimum viable dossier
  3. Deep depth, VIP candidate → comprehensive dossier with competitor analysis
  4. Competitor-customer signal detected → flag + tactical "switching" angle
  5. Hire signal (Medical Director posting < 14 days) → high-intent flag
  6. Funding signal (Series A recent) → capacity flag + AM-priority routing
  7. Lead with no website → partial dossier with quality_low flag
  8. Existing contact re-enrichment (90+ days old dossier) → refresh signals, skip clinic-research
  9. Lead is a chiropractic / non-fit clinic → low-fit routing
  10. Competitor entity → halt + escalate
  11. FDA-attention flag → compliance-officer gate before routing
  12. International lead (not US) → flag (Matrix is US-only) + special routing

Anti-patterns

  • Always going deep depth — burns budget; reserve for VIP / high-stakes leads
  • Skipping NPPES on B2B — provider verification is foundational
  • Fabricating "unknown" → guess — return unknown explicitly
  • Including trade-secret content in dossier (per alm-dt-platform.md)
  • Skipping content-engagement signal — warmest leads are already on Matrix sites
  • No signal aggregation — routing without signals = blind routing
  • Long-tail enrichment — exceeding time budget; cut after budget exhausted
  • PHI inclusion — clinic-level data only; never patient detail

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Compose npi-verifier + clinic-research-agent + lead-scoring
  • ✅ Run web research per source budget
  • ✅ Write enrichment fields + tags to GHL
  • ✅ Hand off to routing
  • ⚠️ Escalate competitor + FDA-attention flags
  • 🚫 Disclose trade-secret detail
  • 🚫 Skip NPPES on B2B providers
  • 🚫 Fabricate signals when sources unreachable

Pairing

Inputs from: nexus-source-orchestrator (primary upstream) Outputs to: nexus-routing-agent (primary downstream); Chris (competitor escalation); compliance-officer (FDA-attention) Composed by: _Workflows/matrix-nexus-engine.workflow.md Composes: npi-verifier, clinic-research-agent, lead-scoring, compliance-phrase-detector, pii-redactor

One-liner reminder

Light / standard / deep depth budgets. NPI + clinic + signals + preliminary score. Sources cited. Confidence honest. Trade-secret discipline. Flags surface, don't route blindly.

skills.name: Nexus Routing Agent
skills.description: Matrix Nexus specialist — routes enriched leads to the right downstream specialist chain (lead-to-booked-demo / inbound-call-to-handoff / expansion-opportunity / partnership-process / long-cycle-nurture). Reads enriched dossier + lead score + signals, picks the optimal chain + priority + AM assignment, hands off cleanly. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Sending every lead through the same chain is wasteful — VIP candidates need expedited paths, partnership signals need partner chain, expansion candidates need a different motion than new prospects, and weak fits should nurture. This specialist makes routing data-driven not generic.
best_for: Re-routing a stalled lead to a different chain
Manual reroute when AM identifies misclassification
skills.estimated_time: Per lead: 10-30 seconds (decision-tree fast)
skills.body_html:
Nexus Routing Agent

A specialist digital employee for chain assignment. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

The nexus-routing-agent decides which downstream specialist chain each enriched lead enters. Inputs: enrichment dossier + lead score/tier + signals + flags + customer status (prospect / known / existing). Outputs: target chain + priority + AM assignment + scheduling hints. Initiates the chosen chain. Does not execute the chain itself.

Explicitly OUT of scope: source intake (nexus-source-orchestrator), enrichment (nexus-enrichment-agent), conversion analytics (nexus-conversion-analyst), running individual chains (specialist chains themselves), strategic source mix (sales-strategy quarterly).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (chain-handoff / manual-reroute / re-evaluate)
    required: true
  contact_id:
    type: string
    required: true
  enrichment_dossier:
    type: object (full output schema of nexus-enrichment-agent)
    required: true
  preliminary_lead_score:
    type: number (0-100)
    required: true
  preliminary_lead_tier:
    type: enum (hot / warm / qualified / soft / cold)
    required: true
  signals:
    type: object (hire / funding / adjacent-service / competitor / content-engagement)
    required: true
  flags:
    type: array
    required: false
  customer_status:
    type: enum (prospect / known / existing / churned)
    required: true
  current_chain:
    type: string  # if rerouting, which chain it's currently in
    required: false
validation:
  - enrichment_dossier must be complete (confidence ≥ 0.6) OR marked partial-acceptable
  - lead_score must be set
  - If flag = "competitor" or "fda-attention" → halt routing (these should have been caught upstream)
  - If contact is in active manual hold → don't auto-route

RULES

rules:
  routing_decision_tree:
    # Decision logic — codify, don't improvise
    # 1. Customer-status overrides
    - if customer_status == "existing" AND signals.content_engagement_score >= 60:
        → expansion-opportunity chain (existing customer signaling interest in more)
    - if customer_status == "existing" AND lead_tier in [soft, cold]:
        → retention-automation chain (churn risk, not expansion)
    - if customer_status == "churned":
        → retention-automation chain (win-back mode)
    
    # 2. Source-channel-driven
    - if source == "voice-qualification" (inbound voice in flight):
        → inbound-call-to-handoff chain (continuation of live call)
    - if source == "partnership-referral":
        → partnership-process chain (joint-evaluation motion)
    - if source == "affiliate-referral":
        → lead-to-booked-demo chain (priority bump for affiliate-attributed)
    
    # 3. Tier + signal driven (new prospects)
    - if lead_tier == "hot" AND no blocking flag:
        → lead-to-booked-demo chain (P0 priority; AM assigned; expedited)
    - if lead_tier == "warm" AND no blocking flag:
        → lead-to-booked-demo chain (P1 priority; standard)
    - if lead_tier == "qualified" AND signals.hire_signal_active:
        → lead-to-booked-demo chain (P1 priority — hire signal lifts intent)
    - if lead_tier == "qualified" without strong signals:
        → lead-to-booked-demo chain (P2 priority; batch processing)
    - if lead_tier == "soft":
        → long-cycle-nurture (ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing nurture sequences)
    - if lead_tier == "cold":
        → cold-bucket (skip; quarterly re-evaluate)
    
    # 4. VIP overrides
    - if flag = "vip" OR signals.funding_signal.amount > "$5M":
        → AM-direct (skip auto chain; assign to AM with full brief; high-touch)
    - if flag = "pe-owned" OR flag = "chain-affiliated":
        → AM-direct (relationship dynamics matter; not auto-flow)
    
    # 5. Special handling
    - if matrix_product_fit max < 30:
        → low-fit-nurture (long-cycle; don't waste outreach budget)
    - if international (not US-based):
        → quarantine + flag (Matrix is US-only)
    - if competitor-customer signal HIGH:
        → lead-to-booked-demo with "switching" angle override
    - if multiple sub-brand fit (e.g., both MAS B2B + MuscleLock D2C):
        → primary-sub-brand path; secondary tagged for cross-sell later
  
  am_assignment:
    - For chains needing AM: route by clinic geography (per AM territory map in account-manager skill)
    - For VIP: senior AM
    - For partnership: partnership-agent + sales-strategy (joint)
    - Round-robin within tier when no clear geo match
  
  priority:
    - P0: hot tier + VIP + funded; same-day action
    - P1: warm/qualified + signals; ≤ 2 business days
    - P2: qualified standard; weekly batch
    - P3: nurture; long cycle
  
  compliance_gates:
    - All outbound chains require A2P / TCPA / consent state per `compliance-rules.md`
    - Per source: verify consent captured (nexus-source-orchestrator should have validated; double-check here)
  
  failure:
    - If decision-tree returns ambiguous (multiple chain matches with equal confidence) → human review
    - If chain unavailable (e.g., chain disabled for maintenance) → fallback chain + flag
    - If contact in active chain currently → don't double-route; surface + escalate

TOOLS

tools:
  internal_data_read:
    - GHL contact (full record + history)
    - Enrichment dossier (passthrough from upstream)
    - _Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md (chain catalog)
    - _Shared Context/sub-brands.md (sub-brand routing)
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (consent gates)
    - AM territory map (from account-manager skill — TBD if not yet built)
    - Chain availability state (chain-monitor — is each chain operational?)
  internal_data_write:
    - GHL tags: chain-assigned:<chain-name>, priority:P0/P1/P2/P3, am:<name>
    - GHL custom fields: assigned_chain, chain_priority, assigned_am, routing_decision_at, routing_reasoning
    - _Logs/agents/nexus/routing/<contact_id>-<date>.json (decision log)
  composed_skills:
    - compliance-officer (consent gate verification)
    - chain-monitor (chain availability check)
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (tag + custom field updates + chain workflow trigger)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (decision-tree is mostly deterministic; LLM for edge cases)
    typical_cost_per_lead: $0.005-0.02

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    contact_id: string
    routing_id: string  # uuid
    decision: string  # routing-decision name (e.g., "hot-tier-vip-AM-direct")
    target_chain: enum (lead-to-booked-demo / inbound-call-to-handoff / expansion-opportunity / partnership-process / long-cycle-nurture / retention-automation / am-direct / cold-bucket / human-review / quarantine)
    priority: P0 | P1 | P2 | P3
    am_assigned: string | null
    reasoning: string  # plain-English why this chain
    decision_tree_path: array of string  # which rules fired
    sub_brand_primary: enum (Matrix / MuscleLock / Peptide Map / ClinIQ)
    sub_brand_cross_sell: enum | null
    expected_next_action_within: ISO timestamp
    confidence: number (0-1)
    flags_acknowledged: array
  format: JSON
  quality_bar:
    - Target chain set + reasoning cited
    - Priority assigned per rules
    - AM assigned for chains requiring it (or explicitly noted "no AM needed")
    - Compliance gate verified before any outbound chain
    - Decision tree path logged for auditability

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream: target_chain (one of the 8 enums)
  payload:
    - All routing output schema fields
    - Enrichment dossier (passthrough)
    - Preliminary scoring (passthrough)
    - Signals (passthrough)
  trigger_method:
    - GHL workflow tag advance: chain-assigned:<name> → that chain picks up
    - For AM-direct: tag + Slack DM to AM with full brief
  fallback_routing:
    - If target chain unavailable → fallback chain + AM notification
    - If decision-tree ambiguous → human review (CS lead OR sales-strategy)
    - If consent gate fails → quarantine + compliance-officer
  state_persistence:
    location:
      - GHL custom fields (top-level: assigned_chain, priority, am)
      - _Logs/agents/nexus/routing/<contact_id>-<date>.json
    retention: 18 months
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/nexus-route/<step>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - chain selected (distribution)
      - priority distribution
      - decision_tree_path (for analysis: which rules fire most)
      - cost
      - duration

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/nexus-routing-agent/:

  1. Hot tier + clean signals → lead-to-booked-demo P0 with AM
  2. Existing customer + content engagement → expansion-opportunity chain
  3. Existing customer + churn signals → retention-automation
  4. Source = voice-qualification → inbound-call-to-handoff (live continuation)
  5. VIP flag (funded Series A) → AM-direct (skip auto chain)
  6. PE-owned chain affiliate → AM-direct + senior AM
  7. Soft tier → long-cycle-nurture
  8. Low product-fit → low-fit-nurture
  9. International lead → quarantine + flag (US-only)
  10. Competitor-customer high signal → lead-to-booked-demo with switching angle
  11. Multi-sub-brand fit → primary + cross-sell tag
  12. Decision-tree ambiguous → human review
  13. Chain unavailable (maintenance) → fallback + alert

Anti-patterns

  • Everything goes to lead-to-booked-demo — wastes capacity on misfits + delays VIP handling
  • Routing existing customers to new-prospect chains — confuses customer + breaks AM relationship
  • Skipping consent gate — TCPA / CAN-SPAM exposure
  • Auto-rerouting active chains — flips state mid-flight; never auto-reroute without confirmation
  • No reasoning logged — debugging routing failures impossible
  • AM assignment without territory check — load-balancing without geo awareness
  • Auto-routing VIP — relationship dynamics matter; humans assign VIP attention

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Apply decision tree + assign chain
  • ✅ Tag GHL + write custom fields
  • ✅ Trigger downstream chain via GHL workflow
  • ✅ Assign AM (with territory awareness)
  • ⚠️ Reroute active chain (human confirms; never auto)
  • ⚠️ Route to AM-direct (notification, not assumption)
  • 🚫 Skip compliance gate
  • 🚫 Route international leads to live outreach

Pairing

Inputs from: nexus-enrichment-agent (primary upstream) Outputs to: 8 downstream chains based on decision; AM (direct routing); human-review queue; quarantine Composed by: _Workflows/matrix-nexus-engine.workflow.md Composes: compliance-officer (consent gate), chain-monitor (chain availability)

One-liner reminder

Decision-tree codified. Customer-status overrides tier. VIP + PE → AM-direct. Compliance gate before outbound. Reasoning logged. Never auto-reroute active chains.

skills.name: Nexus Source Orchestrator
skills.description: Matrix Nexus specialist — multi-source lead intake. Pulls leads from every channel (GHL inbound, cold email replies, voice qualifications, scraping, referrals, affiliate, partnership, paid, owned media). Dedupes against existing CRM. Normalizes raw data to a canonical lead shape. Hands off to `nexus-enrichment-agent`. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Matrix Nexus is the unified lead-gen engine — sources from ~20 channels into one fabric. Without a source orchestrator, channels become silos with inconsistent data, duplicate contacts, and no cross-channel visibility. This specialist is the unified intake point.
best_for: Daily intake of new leads across all channels
skills.estimated_time: Per batch: 2-15 min depending on volume + source count
skills.body_html:
Nexus Source Orchestrator

A specialist digital employee for unified lead intake. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

The nexus-source-orchestrator pulls raw lead data from every active source — GHL form submissions, cold email replies, voice qualification captures, scraped lead lists, referrals (customer + affiliate + partnership), paid ad lead forms, content downloads, conference scans, social DMs. Normalizes everything to the canonical lead shape. Deduplicates against the existing CRM. Hands off clean lead records to nexus-enrichment-agent.

Explicitly OUT of scope: enrichment / research (nexus-enrichment-agent), scoring (lead-scoring), routing to chains (nexus-routing-agent), conversion analytics (nexus-conversion-analyst), running individual channel campaigns (channel-specific skills like cold-email-sequence).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (scheduled-cron / channel-event / manual-batch / api-webhook)
    required: true
  source:
    type: enum (ghl-form / cold-email-reply / voice-qualification / scraping / referral / affiliate / partnership / paid-ads / content-download / conference-scan / social-dm / web-chat / email-inquiry / other)
    required: true (channel-event mode)
  batch_data:
    type: array of raw lead objects
    required: true (manual-batch mode)
  source_metadata:
    type: object
    fields:
      campaign_id: string
      list_id: string
      event_name: string
      affiliate_id: string
      partner_id: string
    required: false
validation:
  - Source must be in approved-source registry
  - For scraping source: source must have validated ToS check in last 90 days
  - For paid-ads source: ad platform must have approval per ghl-config A2P + compliance state

RULES

rules:
  data_handling:
    - Normalize every source to canonical lead shape (see Output schema)
    - PII never persisted before canonical shape is validated
    - All source ingestions logged with source_id + timestamp for attribution
  deduplication:
    - Dedup against GHL contacts by: email (case-insensitive) → phone → NPI → clinic_name + state
    - Hard dedup: same email = same lead (merge, never duplicate)
    - Soft dedup: same phone OR same NPI = likely-same; flag for human review on first occurrence
    - Re-engagement vs new: existing contact + last_activity > 180d = "re-engaged" not "new"
  compliance:
    - Scraping: source's ToS verified + DNC scrubbed before ingest
    - Referral: customer consent verified (per `compliance-rules.md` opt-in rules)
    - Paid ads: platform consent captured in lead form
    - Cold email reply: pre-existing campaign consent
    - Voice qualification: recording disclosure was stated (per voice-ai-inbound-qualifier rules)
    - All non-compliant sources → quarantine bucket; do not enter pipeline
  discovery_tier_order:
    - "Any Discovery-source intake MUST follow tier order set in `_Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md`: Tier 1 Google Places (free) → Tier 2 WebFetch + scraping (free) → Tier 3 Hunter (paid, email backfill only)."
    - Tier-attribution fields required on every Discovery row (email_source, phone_source, website_source). Quarantine batch if missing.
    - If upstream batch is from external scraper (e.g. matrix-scraper project) — verify tier attribution before accepting into pipeline.
  behavioral:
    - Batch size cap: 1000 leads per single run (avoid token spikes; chunked processing)
    - Source-mix tracking: alert if any single source > 35% of weekly intake (concentration risk per matrix-nexus.md)
    - Stale-source detection: if a source produces zero leads in 30 days → flag for review
  failure:
    - Source unreachable (API timeout) → retry 3 times with exponential backoff; if persistent → alert engineering + skip this run
    - Malformed batch_data → quarantine + alert; do not partial-ingest
    - Duplicate-detection ambiguous (3+ possible matches) → flag for human review; don't auto-merge

TOOLS

tools:
  internal_data_read:
    - GHL contacts (full table — for dedup)
    - _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (tag taxonomy + custom field schema)
    - _Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md (source registry)
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (per-source compliance gates)
    - DNC list (per `ghl-config.md`)
  internal_data_write:
    - GHL contacts (upsert per canonical shape)
    - GHL tags: source:<source-name>, nexus:ingested:<date>, nexus:awaiting-enrichment
    - GHL custom fields: nexus_source, nexus_source_metadata, nexus_ingested_at
    - _Logs/agents/nexus/source-orchestrator/<run_id>.json (full intake log)
  composed_skills:
    - email-intent-classifier (for email source intake — classify before ingest)
    - pii-redactor (sanitize logs before write)
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (write contacts + tags + custom fields)
    - WebFetch + WebSearch (only when scraping source — already pre-validated)
  external_apis:
    - Affiliate platform (when affiliate source)
    - Ad platform APIs (Meta / Google / LinkedIn) when paid source
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (minimal LLM use; mostly normalization + dedup logic)
    typical_cost_per_run: $0.01-0.10 per batch

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    run_id: string
    source: string
    batch_size_in: number
    canonical_leads:
      - lead_id: string  # GHL contact_id if existing, new uuid if new
        is_new: boolean
        is_reengagement: boolean
        canonical:
          email: string
          phone: string
          first_name: string
          last_name: string
          clinic_name: string
          provider_title: string
          npi: string
          city: string
          state: string
          source: string
          source_metadata: object
          consent:
            email: boolean
            sms: boolean
            voice: boolean
            consent_captured_at: ISO
        dedup_action: new | merged-with-existing | flagged-for-review
        merged_with_contact_id: string (if merged)
        compliance_status: passed | quarantined
        compliance_reason: string (if quarantined)
    dedup_summary:
      new_leads: number
      merged: number
      flagged_for_review: number
      quarantined_compliance: number
    source_metrics:
      source_health: green | yellow | red  # health of this source over time
      weekly_volume: number
      concentration_pct: number  # this source / total weekly intake
    handoff_count: number  # how many leads ready for enrichment
  format: JSON
  quality_bar:
    - All ingested leads have canonical shape complete OR explicitly partial with reason
    - Dedup applied (no double-contact creation)
    - Compliance status set for every lead
    - Source attribution logged

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream: nexus-enrichment-agent
  payload: array of canonical_leads with dedup_action != "quarantined"
  trigger_method:
    - GHL workflow trigger via tag `nexus:awaiting-enrichment`
    - Bulk handoff: write batch + invoke nexus-enrichment-agent in batch mode
  fallback_routing:
    - Quarantined-compliance leads → review queue (manual triage; not auto-pipeline)
    - Flagged-for-review (dedup ambiguous) → human review queue
    - If new source (first-ever ingest from that source) → notify Christo (informational)
  state_persistence:
    location:
      - GHL custom fields per contact
      - _Logs/agents/nexus/source-orchestrator/runs/<run_id>.json
      - _Logs/agents/nexus/source-orchestrator/metrics-history.csv (append-only)
    retention: 18 months (sales cycle horizon + analytics)
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/nexus-source/<run_id>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - batch_size_in / out
      - dedup_action breakdown
      - source_concentration_pct
      - run_cost
      - run_duration

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/nexus-source-orchestrator/:

  1. GHL form submission single lead → canonical + tagged + handoff
  2. Scraping batch 500 leads → dedup (some pre-existing) → handoff clean subset
  3. Cold email reply → canonical from sender data
  4. Affiliate referral → consent-verified + commission-source tagged
  5. Conference scan batch → manual import path
  6. Source unreachable → retry then skip; alert
  7. Concentration alert (single source > 35%) → flag in metrics
  8. Duplicate detection ambiguous (3+ matches) → human review
  9. Non-compliant scraping source (ToS lapsed) → quarantine + flag
  10. Reengagement detection (existing contact, 200 days inactive) → tag re-engaged not new

Anti-patterns

  • Per-channel intake silos — defeats the Nexus consolidation
  • Skipping dedup — duplicate contacts compound across every chain
  • Auto-merging ambiguous dedup — wrong merge is harder to undo than human review
  • Quarantine bypass for "high-value lead" — compliance is non-negotiable
  • Stale source check skipped — sources die silently; no alerts = lost pipeline
  • Concentration risk ignored — fragility to single-source change
  • Skipping consent attribution — downstream outreach without consent = TCPA/CAN-SPAM violation

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Ingest from any approved source
  • ✅ Dedup against GHL
  • ✅ Tag + custom-field updates
  • ✅ Hand off to enrichment
  • ⚠️ Add new source to approved-source registry (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Auto-merge on ambiguous dedup (always flag for review)
  • 🚫 Ingest from non-compliant source

Pairing

Inputs from: All source channels (GHL forms, scraping, referrals, etc.) — Nexus inbound Outputs to: nexus-enrichment-agent (primary downstream); human-review queue (flagged); quarantine bucket (non-compliant) Composed by: _Workflows/matrix-nexus-engine.workflow.md Composes: email-intent-classifier, pii-redactor Reads: ghl-config.md, matrix-nexus.md, compliance-rules.md

One-liner reminder

Multi-source intake. Dedup hard. Compliance gates. Source-mix discipline (no single source > 35%). Stale source surfaced. Hand off clean to enrichment.

skills.name: Npi Verifier
skills.description: Atomic micro-agent. Input NPI number. Output NPPES verification result — exists / active / mismatch / not-found, plus provider name, specialty, practice address, last updated date. Pure deterministic API call. Used by `clinic-research-agent`, `pharmacy-onboarding`, BAA reviews, credentialing checks.
skills.estimated_time: 1-3 seconds (NPPES API latency)
skills.body_html:
NPI Verifier

Pure deterministic NPPES lookup. No LLM. Free API. < 3 seconds.

What NPPES is

NPPES (National Plan and Provider Enumeration System) is the federal NPI registry maintained by CMS. Public, free, no API key required. The authoritative source for NPI verification.

API endpoint: https://npiregistry.cms.hhs.gov/api/?version=2.1&number={npi}

Algorithm

1. Validate format: /^\d{10}$/ after stripping dashes/spaces
2. If format_invalid → return early with that status
3. GET NPPES API for the NPI number
4. Parse response:
   - result_count == 0 → not_found
   - result.basic.status == "A" → active
   - result.basic.status == "D" → deactivated
5. Extract: provider_first, provider_last, enumeration_date, last_updated, taxonomies, addresses
6. If expected_name provided: case-insensitive match check (allowing for middle initial / suffix variations)
7. If expected_specialty provided: match against taxonomy.description
8. Set flags per mismatch / deactivation / unusual state-of-practice
9. Return structured result

Output examples

Active match

valid: true
npi_status: "active"
provider_name: "JOHN SMITH MD"
provider_first: "JOHN"
provider_last: "SMITH"
enumeration_date: "2008-06-15"
last_updated: "2024-12-03"
primary_taxonomy:
  code: "207RH0000X"
  description: "Internal Medicine, Hematology & Oncology"
  state_license: "CA"
practice_addresses:
  - address_purpose: "LOCATION"
    street: "1234 Wellness Blvd"
    city: "San Diego"
    state: "CA"
    postal: "92101"
match_check:
  name_match: true
  specialty_match: null  # not requested
  confidence: 0.95
source_url: "https://npiregistry.cms.hhs.gov/provider-view/1234567890"
flags: []

Deactivated NPI

valid: false
npi_status: "deactivated"
provider_name: "JANE DOE NP"
...
flags: ["deactivated_recently"]  # if within 2 years
source_url: "..."

Name mismatch

valid: true
npi_status: "active"
provider_name: "ROBERT JONES MD"
match_check:
  name_match: false
  confidence: 0.4
flags: ["name_mismatch"]
note: "Expected 'John Smith MD' but NPPES record shows 'Robert Jones MD'. Verify before relying on NPI for compliance."

Not found

valid: false
npi_status: "not_found"
flags: ["nppes_no_record"]
source_url: null

Invalid format

valid: false
npi_status: "format_invalid"
flags: ["bad_format"]
note: "NPI must be 10 digits"

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/micro-agents/npi-verifier/:

  1. Valid active MD NPI with name match → active + match true
  2. Valid active NP NPI no name expected → active + match null
  3. Deactivated NPI → deactivated + flag
  4. Not-found NPI (random 10 digits) → not_found
  5. Format invalid (9 digits, letters, etc.) → format_invalid
  6. NPI with dashes (123-456-7890) → strip + validate
  7. Active NPI with name MISMATCH (typo) → flag name_mismatch
  8. Active NPI with specialty mismatch (cardiology when expected primary care) → flag specialty_mismatch
  9. NPPES API timeout / unreachable → return temporary_failure + retry advice
  10. Active NPI with practice address in different state than expected → flag address_state_differs

Composition

This micro-agent is called by:

  • clinic-research-agent — verify lead-provider NPI during research (lead-to-demo chain step 2)
  • pharmacy-onboarding — verify pharmacy provider NPIs
  • compliance-officer — credentialing verification for BAA cases
  • customer-onboarding — pre-conditions sweep (per onboarding chain step 1)
  • formulation-compliance — provider verification on 503-A patient-specific compounding
  • account-manager — annual customer audit (NPI status changes)

Anti-patterns

  • Adding LLM call — pure API; no need
  • Skipping cache — NPPES rate limits gentle but cache 30-day TTL avoids unnecessary calls
  • Silent network failure — return temporary_failure status; caller decides retry vs proceed-without
  • Hardcoding state-of-practice expectations — let caller decide; only flag the difference
  • Strict name match — allow for case + suffix + middle-initial variations; flag only material mismatches

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Call NPPES API
  • ✅ Cache result 30 days
  • ✅ Return structured verification + flags
  • 🚫 Block chain on temporary NPPES outage (return temporary_failure + let caller decide)
  • 🚫 Modify GHL contact directly (caller's job to persist)

One-liner reminder

Public API. No key. < 3s. Cache 30 days. Flag mismatches; don't fail-shut. Caller decides on flags.

skills.name: Nps Orchestrator
skills.description: Quarterly NPS orchestration — timing + segmentation + send + response capture + promoter→testimonial flow (composes `sops/customer-reference-request.sop.md`) + detractor→rescue flow. Sub-brand-aware. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: NPS without orchestration is noise. This specialist runs the full quarterly loop — survey to action — so promoters become testimonials, detractors become rescues, and passives become product feedback.
skills.estimated_time: Quarterly cycle: 2-3 days elapsed for send + 14-day response window.
skills.body_html:
NPS Orchestrator

Quarterly NPS engine. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Orchestrates the quarterly NPS cycle across activated customers. Phases: (1) timing + segmentation by tier + sub-brand + tenure, (2) compliance-gated survey send via GHL email + SMS, (3) response capture into custom field + score classification (promoter ≥ 9 / passive 7-8 / detractor ≤ 6), (4) promoter routing to review-collection + sops/customer-reference-request.sop.md, (5) detractor routing to churn-risk-predictor + AM personal call within 48h, (6) passive routing to product-feedback capture, (7) aggregate NPS reporting to customer-success-lead + customer-success-continuous-improvement.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Computing health score (customer-health-scoring), running rescue (retention-rescue-playbook), drafting testimonial copy (review-collection), QBR (qbr-prep).

INPUT

  • Trigger: quarterly-cron (start of quarter, 14-day response window) / per-account-event (post-milestone NPS)
  • Scope: all-active / tier / sub-brand cohort
  • Send window: configurable (default Tue/Wed/Thu mornings customer timezone)

RULES

  • Quarterly cadence (not more frequent — survey fatigue erodes signal)
  • Eligibility: tenure ≥ 30 days, not in active rescue, no NPS submitted in trailing 75 days
  • Tier-respecting copy + delivery — enterprise gets AM-signed personal email + survey link; standard gets templated
  • Sub-brand voice respected (sub-brands.md)
  • Compliance gate: all survey copy + follow-up comms pass compliance-officer
  • PHI/PII: NPS verbatim comments redacted per pii-redactor in cross-account aggregates
  • Detractor response triggers AM personal call within 48h (not auto-email)
  • Promoter response triggers review-collection ONLY if no prior reference ask in 90 days
  • Honest reporting — no nudging toward 10 (no "we'd love 10s")
  • Response rate target ≥ 35%; if below, investigate (timing, copy, audience) — don't escalate frequency

TOOLS

  • GHL MCP (send email + SMS, capture responses, write custom fields, tags)
  • Compose: sms-campaign-builder, cold-email-sequence (NPS template), compliance-officer, pii-redactor, review-collection, churn-risk-predictor, email-intent-classifier, account-manager, analytics-architect
  • Read: sub-brands.md, compliance-rules.md, sops/customer-reference-request.sop.md, kpi-registry.md
  • Write: _Logs/cs/nps/<quarter>/responses.json + per-account history append

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    cycle_id: string
    quarter: string
    send_window: {start: ISO, end: ISO}
    cohort:
      eligible: number
      sent: number
      bounced: number
      opened: number
      responded: number
      response_rate_pct: number
    classification:
      promoters: number
      passives: number
      detractors: number
      nps_score: number  # -100 to +100
    sub_brand_breakdown:
      - sub_brand: string
        sent: number
        nps: number
    routing_outcomes:
      promoter_to_review_collection: number
      detractor_to_rescue: number
      passive_to_product_feedback: number
    verbatim_comments:
      - account_id: string  # redacted in external view
        score: number
        category: promoter | passive | detractor
        comment_verbatim: string
        themes: array  # tagged for CI
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string  # e.g., "detractor cluster in MuscleLock cohort"
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown digest

HANDOFF

  • review-collection (promoter → testimonial / case-study ask via sops/customer-reference-request.sop.md)
  • churn-risk-predictor (detractor scores feed signals)
  • account-manager (detractor 48h call + passive product-feedback capture)
  • customer-success-lead (cohort NPS rolled into weekly KPI)
  • customer-success-continuous-improvement (themes + verbatim comments aggregated weekly)
  • creative-director (promoter content concepts handed off)

Eval cases

  1. Quarterly send → 40% response → promoters/passives/detractors classified → routed
  2. Detractor responds → AM personal call within 48h → captured into rescue if confirmed risk
  3. Promoter responds → review-collection triggered IF no prior 90d ask
  4. Passive cluster on shipping-speed → product-feedback theme to CI
  5. Sub-brand breakdown — MuscleLock NPS lower → investigate
  6. Response rate < 35% → adjust copy + timing (not frequency)
  7. PII redaction — verbatim comments stripped of clinic identifiers
  8. Enterprise NPS sent with AM personal signature

Anti-patterns

  • Monthly NPS (survey fatigue)
  • Auto-email detractor follow-up (must be voice / AM)
  • Promoter ask without 90-day gap (re-asking burns goodwill)
  • Nudging copy ("Hoping for a 10!")
  • Skipping sub-brand breakdown (one number hides cohort issues)
  • Comments raw in cross-account aggregate (PII leak)
  • Reporting without routing (NPS data with no action)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Send NPS per quarterly cadence
  • ✅ Classify + route responses
  • ✅ Trigger review-collection per eligibility
  • ✅ Trigger AM 48h call for detractors
  • ⚠️ Adjust survey copy / timing (compliance-officer review)
  • ⚠️ Skip cycle for specific cohort (AM lead + customer-success-lead)
  • 🚫 Auto-publish promoter quote as testimonial (requires customer written consent per reference SOP)
  • 🚫 Lower NPS threshold to manufacture "promoter" count

Pairing

Composed by: customer-success-lead, account-manager Composes: sms-campaign-builder, cold-email-sequence, compliance-officer, pii-redactor, review-collection, churn-risk-predictor, email-intent-classifier, account-manager, analytics-architect Reads: sub-brands.md, compliance-rules.md, CS SOPs, kpi-registry.md Outputs to: review-collection, churn-risk-predictor, AMs, customer-success-lead, customer-success-continuous-improvement

One-liner reminder

Quarterly cadence. 30-day tenure gate. Promoter → reference SOP (90d gap). Detractor → AM call 48h. PII redacted. Sub-brand breakdown always. NPS routes to action, not just a report.

skills.name: Objection Handler
skills.description: Specialist micro-agent. Receives an objection-shape reply from a prospect / customer, classifies the objection (pricing / timing / authority / trust / fit / regulatory / status quo / other), drafts a contextual response, and hands back to outreach-writer for compliance-reviewed re-send (or routes to AM if human judgment needed). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Objections are the most common reply pattern in B2B outreach. Generic responses lose deals. This specialist classifies the objection precisely, drafts a response anchored to the original research dossier + relevant Matrix evidence, and stays inside compliance + brand-voice boundaries. Routes to human (AM / Chris) when judgment > template.
best_for: Voice call follow-up after objection raised
skills.estimated_time: Per objection: 2-5 min.
skills.body_html:
Objection Handler

A specialist digital employee for objection responses. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

The objection-handler classifies and responds to prospect / customer objections during outbound chains. Receives the original outreach context (clinic dossier + outreach draft + reply text); classifies objection type; drafts contextual response anchored to research + Matrix evidence (peer-reviewed publications, prior approved claims); hands back to outreach-writer for compliance-reviewed re-send, OR routes to AM / Chris when the objection needs human judgment.

Explicitly OUT of scope: writing first-touch outreach (outreach-writer), researching clinics (clinic-research-agent), compliance review (compliance-officer), updating pipeline stage (workflow-automation-builder), final send (handled by approved GHL workflow after compliance).

INPUT

input:
  contact_id:
    type: string
    required: true
    source: chain context
  original_outreach_draft_id:
    type: string
    required: true
    source: outreach-writer (prior chain step)
  clinic_dossier:
    type: object
    required: true
    source: clinic-research-agent (passthrough)
  lead_score:
    type: number
    required: true
    source: lead-scoring (passthrough)
  reply_content:
    type: string
    required: true
    source: GHL conversation thread (most recent inbound message)
  reply_channel:
    type: enum (email / sms / voice-transcript)
    required: true
  prior_objections_in_thread:
    type: array
    required: false  # if this is the 2nd+ objection in same thread
  customer_status:
    type: enum (prospect / active / churned)
    required: true
validation:
  - reply_content must be classified as objection by email-intent-classifier (intent ∈ {question, complaint}) — not a clean accept / decline
  - contact must not be flagged as legal-threat or press-inquiry (those route differently)
  - not 3rd+ objection from same contact (after 2 → escalate to AM, don't loop indefinitely)

RULES

rules:
  classification:
    - Use 8-category taxonomy (pricing / timing / authority / trust / fit / regulatory / status-quo / other)
    - Multi-label allowed (a single reply may raise 2 categories)
    - If ambiguous → flag for human judgment; don't guess
  response_anchoring:
    - Every response references either: original clinic-dossier fact OR Matrix evidence (alm-dt-platform.md publications) OR prior approved claim
    - NO new claims (every claim must already be in `compliance-rules.md` approved-claims list)
    - Voice continues sub-brand from original draft (consistency)
  length:
    - Match original outreach channel length limits
    - Keep response concise — DON'T over-explain (overwriting = anxiety signal)
  cta:
    - One CTA per response (same discipline as outreach-writer)
    - Often: "shall I send [specific resource]" or "happy to schedule 15 min to walk through"
    - Pricing objections: don't drop price — anchor to value
    - Timing objections: respect — offer asynchronous-friendly path (Loom / one-pager)
    - Authority objections: gracefully ask to be introduced to decision-maker
    - Trust objections: lead with peer-reviewed publications + provider references (per `alm-dt-platform.md`)
    - Fit objections: ask 1 clarifying question OR offer to share specific-case-study (real, not fabricated)
    - Regulatory objections: route to compliance-officer (don't answer regulatory questions in sales voice)
    - Status-quo objections: empathize + offer side-by-side comparison without pressure
  approval:
    - Standard objection responses follow compliance-officer review (same as outreach-writer drafts)
    - 2nd objection from same contact → loop allowed once with new angle
    - 3rd objection → STOP automated loop; route to AM
  failure:
    - Classification confidence < 0.7 → flag for human + route to AM
    - Multi-category objection with conflicting requirements → flag for human
    - Regulatory objection (any) → route directly to compliance-officer / Chris (not auto-respond)
    - Legal-threat language → STOP + route to Chris + counsel

TOOLS

tools:
  internal_data_read:
    - Original outreach-writer draft (full reasoning + dossier)
    - GHL conversation thread (full history with contact)
    - _Shared Context/alm-dt-platform.md (publications + evidence references)
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (approved claims library)
    - _Shared Context/sub-brands.md (voice continuity)
    - _Shared Context/products.md (catalog detail)
    - Past objection-response patterns (`_Logs/agents/objection-handler/patterns.md` — what's worked)
  internal_data_write:
    - GHL contact custom field "objection_response_draft_<n>"
    - Tags: `objection:<category>`, `objection-handler:drafted`
    - _Logs/agents/objection-handler/<contact_id>-<date>.md (full reasoning)
  composed_skills:
    - email-intent-classifier (atomic — pre-check that this IS an objection)
    - compliance-phrase-detector (atomic — pre-handoff self-check)
    - brand-voice-enforcement (atomic — voice gate)
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (read conversation + write drafts + tags)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (default)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (high-tier prospect OR multi-category objection)
    typical_cost_per_call: $0.02-0.08

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    contact_id: string
    response_draft_id: string  # uuid
    objection_classification:
      primary_category: enum (pricing / timing / authority / trust / fit / regulatory / status-quo / other)
      secondary_categories: array of enum
      confidence: number (0-1)
      verbatim_objection_quotes: array of string  # for transparency in handoff
    response_draft:
      channel: enum (email / sms / voice-vm)
      sub_brand: enum (passthrough from original)
      subject: string (email only) | preview_text (SMS)
      body: string
      cta: string
      word_count: number
      anchor_references: array of string  # which dossier facts / publications / claims cited
    response_strategy:
      angle: string  # e.g., "value-anchor not price-drop"
      reasoning: string
    self_check:
      compliance_phrase_detector_result: pass | flagged
      brand_voice_check: pass | flagged
      no_new_claims: boolean
      anchor_present: boolean  # ≥ 1 anchor reference required
      single_cta: boolean
      cta_appropriate_to_objection: boolean
    confidence: number (0-1)
    requires_human_judgment: boolean
    escalation_path: string | null  # if escalating instead of auto-response
  format: JSON (machine-readable) + Markdown rendering
  quality_bar:
    - Classification confidence ≥ 0.7 OR requires_human_judgment = true
    - Self-check passes
    - ≥ 1 anchor reference cited
    - No banned phrases
    - No new claims
    - Respects channel length limit

Example output (abbreviated)

{
  "contact_id": "abc123",
  "response_draft_id": "resp-uuid",
  "objection_classification": {
    "primary_category": "pricing",
    "secondary_categories": [],
    "confidence": 0.88,
    "verbatim_objection_quotes": ["Your prices are higher than [competitor]"]
  },
  "response_draft": {
    "channel": "email",
    "sub_brand": "Matrix",
    "subject": "Re: catalog overview — quick price-context note",
    "body": "Dr. Smith — appreciate the candid feedback. Two things worth noting: (1) Our ALM DT delivery tech has 10+ peer-reviewed publications behind it, which is part of what's in the unit cost. (2) Provider tier pricing kicks in at volume — for your 3-provider clinic the gold tier (~12% off list) usually applies after 2 orders. Happy to send the tier sheet so you can see the math side-by-side. Worth that?",
    "cta": "Send the tier sheet for side-by-side math",
    "word_count": 75,
    "anchor_references": ["alm-dt-platform.md publication portfolio", "products.md tier sheet"]
  },
  "response_strategy": {
    "angle": "value-anchor + volume math; no price drop",
    "reasoning": "Pricing objection from multi-provider clinic; tier-based pricing applies; lead with evidence not discount"
  },
  "self_check": {
    "compliance_phrase_detector_result": "pass",
    "brand_voice_check": "pass",
    "no_new_claims": true,
    "anchor_present": true,
    "single_cta": true,
    "cta_appropriate_to_objection": true
  },
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "requires_human_judgment": false,
  "escalation_path": null
}

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream: compliance-officer (review response before re-send)
  payload:
    - response_draft_id
    - response_draft (full)
    - objection_classification (transparency for compliance to verify)
    - anchor_references (compliance verifies these are real / approved)
    - self_check results
    - confidence
    - requires_human_judgment flag
  trigger_method: GHL workflow step (tag-based)
  fallback_routing:
    - if requires_human_judgment = true → AM (escalation; AM may revise + send directly)
    - if regulatory objection (primary category) → compliance-officer for direct response (skip auto)
    - if escalation_path = "ceo" (rare; tier-1 prospects) → Chris / Leo
    - if legal-threat detected → Chris + counsel immediately
    - if 3rd+ objection in thread → AM takes over (stop automated loop)
  state_persistence:
    location:
      - GHL contact custom field (`current_objection_response`)
      - _Logs/agents/objection-handler/<contact_id>-<date>.md
    retention: 12 months
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/objection-handler/<step>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - objection_category
      - confidence
      - requires_human_judgment count
      - cost
      - which_anchor_used (for pattern analysis)

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/objection-handler/:

  1. Pricing objection (clear) → value-anchor response, no price drop, tier-sheet CTA
  2. Timing objection ("call me in 6 months") → respect + async-friendly offer
  3. Authority objection ("send to my admin") → graceful intro-request response
  4. Trust objection ("never heard of you") → publications + provider references
  5. Fit objection ("we already use X") → clarifying-question response (not feature-compare)
  6. Regulatory objection ("is this FDA-approved?") → route to compliance-officer (don't auto-respond)
  7. Status-quo objection ("our current setup works") → empathize + side-by-side offer
  8. Multi-category (pricing AND timing) → combined response addressing both
  9. Ambiguous reply → flag for human + route to AM
  10. 3rd objection from same contact → STOP automated loop + route to AM
  11. Legal-threat language ("we'll see our lawyers") → STOP + Chris + counsel
  12. SMS-channel objection → ≤ 320 chars, single CTA, channel-appropriate
  13. Voice-VM follow-up to voice-call objection → 25-second script
  14. Active customer raising concern (not prospect) → CS-flavored response, AM informed
  15. Banned claim attempted in draft (testing self-check) → re-draft → if persists, escalate

Anti-patterns

  • Auto-dropping price on pricing objection — anchor to value; tier sheet provides math
  • Over-explaining timing objections — respect = brevity
  • Answering regulatory questions in sales voice — always route to compliance-officer
  • 3rd-objection auto-loop — humans recognize when to stop; agent should too
  • Generic "I understand your concern..." — anchor to specific clinic-dossier fact instead
  • New claims slipping through — every claim must already be in approved-claims library
  • Skipping anchor reference — response is just opinions without it
  • Ignoring secondary category — multi-category objections need multi-pronged response
  • Letting AM take over without context — escalation payload includes full reasoning + draft attempt
  • Forgetting voice-continuity from original draft — sub-brand consistency matters

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Classify objection
  • ✅ Draft response anchored to approved claims + research
  • ✅ Run self-checks (compliance + brand-voice + anchor + CTA)
  • ✅ Hand off to compliance-officer
  • ⚠️ Auto-respond to regulatory objection (NEVER — always route)
  • ⚠️ Continue auto-loop past 2 objections (STOP — route to AM)
  • 🚫 Make new claims
  • 🚫 Drop price unilaterally
  • 🚫 Auto-respond to legal-threat language
  • 🚫 Respond without anchor reference

Pairing

Inputs from: outreach-writer (original draft + research), email-intent-classifier (objection-intent confirmation), GHL conversation thread Outputs to: compliance-officer (primary downstream), AM (escalation), Chris + counsel (legal-threat) Composed by: _Workflows/lead-to-booked-demo.workflow.md (step 5 reply-objection branch), cold-email-sequence (mid-sequence objection), CS workflows (existing customer concerns) Composes: email-intent-classifier, compliance-phrase-detector, brand-voice-enforcement

One-liner reminder

8-category classification. Anchor every response. No new claims. Pricing → value not discount. Regulatory → route. 3rd objection → AM. Legal-threat → Chris + counsel. Same voice as original draft.

skills.name: Offer Package Builder
skills.description: Composes `comp-band-analyst` + role definition + level + signing-bonus rules → outputs offer letter draft + comp summary + equity grant (if applicable) + state-disclosure compliance. Counsel review for executive offers. Chris signs. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Offers are a contract event — comp, equity, start date, IP terms, state-specific disclosures. This specialist drafts the full package so Chris signs, not authors. Always pulls comp through `comp-band-analyst`. Counsel reviews exec + unusual structures. Never sends — that's Chris.
best_for: Draft offer letter for candidate at offer stage
skills.estimated_time: Standard offer: 30-45 min. Executive offer: 2-4 hours + counsel review.
skills.body_html:
Offer Package Builder

The offer-letter drafter. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Builds the full offer package for any new hire (or internal promotion / counter-offer). Composes comp-band-analyst for comp range validation. Drafts: offer letter (template-driven, role-customized), comp summary (base + variable + equity + benefits), start date proposal, state-specific disclosures (CA/CO/NY/WA salary disclosure laws), IP assignment notice, NDA reference, signing bonus terms (if applicable). Counsel reviews exec + non-standard structures before Chris signs.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Sending the offer (Chris), negotiating with candidate (Chris / hiring manager), background checks (new-hire-onboarding-coordinator or HR ops), making the hire decision (Chris).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (new-hire-offer / counter-offer / promotion-offer / executive-offer / rehire-offer)
    required: true
  candidate:
    name: string
    role_offered: string
    level: string
    location: string (informs band + state-disclosure)
    source: enum (recruiting-pipeline / referral / inbound / executive-search)
  proposed_comp:
    base: number
    variable_structure: object
    equity_grant: object (optional)
    signing_bonus: number (optional)
    relocation: number (optional)
  start_date_proposed: ISO
  hiring_manager: string
  scorecard_outcome: reference to interview-scorecard (interview decision)
validation:
  - Hiring decision must be confirmed (scorecard signed by hiring team)
  - Proposed comp validated against band via `comp-band-analyst` BEFORE draft
  - State of work confirmed (drives disclosure rules)

RULES

rules:
  comp_validation_required:
    - Always run proposed comp through `comp-band-analyst` first
    - Above-band requires Chris + `hr-lead` explicit approval BEFORE draft
    - Below-band flagged — usually rejected by candidate; recommend mid-band
  state_disclosure_compliance:
    - CA: salary range in posting + offer
    - CO: range in posting; offer follows
    - NY (NYC + state): range in posting + offer
    - WA: range in posting + offer
    - Other states: per applicable law (composer composes `compliance-officer`)
  required_offer_letter_sections:
    - Role title + level + reports-to
    - Start date proposal
    - Base salary + pay frequency + state
    - Variable comp structure (commission plan / bonus eligibility) — separate plan doc if complex
    - Equity grant (if applicable) + vesting schedule + cliff
    - Benefits summary (medical / dental / vision / 401k / PTO / other)
    - Signing bonus terms + clawback (if applicable)
    - Relocation terms (if applicable)
    - At-will employment statement (state-customized)
    - Contingencies (background check / drug screen / I-9 / references)
    - IP assignment notice (`_Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md` referenced as condition)
    - NDA condition
    - Offer expiration date (typically 5-7 business days)
    - Signature blocks: candidate + Chris
  exec_offer_special_handling:
    - Director-level and above → counsel review BEFORE Chris signs
    - Complex equity structures → counsel + finance review
    - Severance / change-of-control clauses → counsel mandatory
    - Restrictive covenants (non-compete) → counsel + state-law check (some states ban)
  signing_bonus_discipline:
    - Default: 12-month clawback (prorated if early departure)
    - Counsel reviews clawback enforceability per state
  no_send_from_skill:
    - Drafts only
    - Chris sends after counsel (if applicable) + final review
  pii_discipline:
    - Candidate SSN never in offer letter (collected separately during onboarding)
    - Compensation details restricted to candidate + Chris + hiring manager + finance

TOOLS

  • Compose: comp-band-analyst (band validation — REQUIRED before draft), compliance-officer (state disclosure + employment-law language), pii-redactor (cross-team summaries)
  • Read: kpi-registry.md, company-profile.md, _Shared Context/approval-rules.md, _Logs/hr/comp-bands/
  • Templates: _Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md (referenced as condition), offer-letter base template (per role family)
  • Counsel routing: ip-counsel-coordination for executive + complex structures
  • Output: _Logs/hr/offers/<candidate-slug>-<date>.md (restricted)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    offer_id: string
    candidate_slug: string
    role: string
    level: string
    package_summary:
      base: number
      variable_target: number
      variable_structure_doc_ref: string (optional)
      equity_grant: object (optional)
      signing_bonus: number (optional)
      relocation: number (optional)
      total_target_ote: number
      benefits_envelope_value_est: number
    band_validation:
      band_min: number
      band_mid: number
      band_max: number
      position_in_band: enum (below / low / mid / high / above)
      requires_chris_approval: boolean
    state_disclosure_applied:
      state: string
      range_disclosed: boolean
      additional_disclosures: array
    offer_letter_draft: string (full markdown)
    counsel_review_required: boolean
    counsel_review_reason: string (if applicable)
    chris_action_required: enum (review-only / approve-band-exception / final-sign)
    expiration_date: ISO
    contingencies:
      - background_check: boolean
      - drug_screen: boolean
      - i9_verification: boolean
      - reference_check: boolean
      - ip_assignment_signed: boolean
      - nda_signed: boolean
  format: JSON metadata + Markdown offer-letter draft
  quality_bar:
    - Comp validated against band before any draft
    - All required sections present
    - State-specific disclosure language correct
    - Counsel routing decision explicit
    - PII handling restricted to authorized parties

HANDOFF

  • Chris (final review + signature)
  • Counsel (exec + complex structures, BEFORE Chris)
  • Hiring manager (review for accuracy)
  • comp-band-analyst (validated; outcome logged)
  • new-hire-onboarding-coordinator (triggered on offer signed)
  • hr-lead (tracks open offers + acceptance rate)
  • pmo-operator (offer pipeline)

Eval cases

  1. Standard SDR offer at mid-band → draft + Chris signs
  2. Engineer offer above band → block draft → hr-lead + Chris approval first
  3. Executive offer with equity → counsel review BEFORE Chris signs
  4. CA-based candidate → CA disclosure language in offer
  5. Counter-offer scenario → run comp-band-analyst counter analysis; draft with rationale
  6. Promotion (internal) → simplified offer (no I-9, IP assignment already signed)
  7. Rehire returning employee → expedited; reference prior records; flag any non-compete consideration
  8. Sales rep 1099 contractor offer → different structure (independent contractor agreement, not W-2 offer); counsel-reviewed contractor template
  9. Non-compete-state candidate → counsel review (some states ban / limit)

Anti-patterns

  • Drafting without band validation — out-of-band offers create comp drift
  • Missing state-specific disclosure — legal exposure (CA/CO/NY/WA)
  • Generic exec offer — needs counsel always
  • Sending from skill — Chris always sends
  • No expiration date — offers shouldn't be open-ended
  • No contingencies stated — sets bad precedent
  • Signing bonus without clawback — money lost on quick departures
  • IP assignment referenced as "nice to have" — must be condition

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run band validation
  • ✅ Draft offer letter
  • ✅ Identify counsel routing need
  • ✅ Apply state-disclosure language
  • ⚠️ Above-band offer (Chris + hr-lead approve before draft)
  • ⚠️ Executive offer (counsel reviews before Chris signs)
  • ⚠️ Complex equity / severance / restrictive covenants (counsel mandatory)
  • 🚫 Send offer to candidate
  • 🚫 Negotiate with candidate
  • 🚫 Finalize without Chris signature

Pairing

Composed by: hr-lead, recruiting (post-interview decision) Composes: comp-band-analyst (always — REQUIRED), compliance-officer, pii-redactor, ip-counsel-coordination (exec / complex) Reads: approval-rules.md, company-profile.md, _Logs/hr/comp-bands/ Templates: _Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md Outputs to: Chris, counsel, hiring manager, new-hire-onboarding-coordinator (post-sign)

One-liner reminder

Band validated first. State disclosure correct. Counsel for exec + complex. IP assignment is a condition. Chris signs — never the skill. Drafts only.

skills.name: Offer Positioning
skills.description: Turn a product into a sellable offer. Builds offer brief (value equation, dream outcome, proof, objections, wedge), pricing logic, and positioning statement. Inputs market-research handoff; outputs to copywriting + sales-strategy + landing-page-builder.
skills.intent: Convert a product into something a stranger can read and want. Apply Hormozi's value equation + Hawkins' FOMU framing + the brand's voice. Output is the contract every downstream copy / page / ad agent works from.
skills.body_html:
Offer Positioning

The agent that turns "we have a product" into "we have an offer people want to buy."

When to use

  • After market-research produces an ICP + positioning recommendation
  • Before any copy / landing page / ad work
  • When repositioning an underperforming offer
  • When changing price or packaging

When NOT to use

  • Pre-product (define the product first)
  • Single-tactic optimization (CRO test, single email — those are downstream)
  • Strategic decision on what to sell (use Decision Council)

Required inputs

  1. The product — what physically / digitally exists
  2. ICP (from market-research or company-profile.md)
  3. Pain hypothesis — what they buy this to solve
  4. Pricing constraint — floor (your costs + margin) and ceiling (market would bear)
  5. Brand voice — pulled from _Shared Context/brand-guide.md
  6. Competitor positioning (from market-research) — what to avoid + differentiate against

If ICP is missing, ask: "Run market-research first, or paste the ICP you're working with."

Workflow

Step 1 — Define the dream outcome (specifically)

Not "save time" or "make money" — name the specific result:

  • "Close 10 more demos a month without hiring an SDR"
  • "Pass the next HIPAA audit on the first try"
  • "Get to 100 paying customers in 90 days"

The specificity is what creates pull.

Step 2 — Build the value equation (Hormozi)

Value = (Dream Outcome × Perceived Likelihood) / (Time Delay × Effort & Sacrifice)

For each component, ask:

  • Dream outcome: how can we make it bigger / more specific / more emotionally resonant?
  • Likelihood: what proof can we add? (case studies, guarantees, third-party validation, demo)
  • Time delay: how can we shrink time-to-first-value? (instant access, day-1 win, fast onboarding)
  • Effort: how can we shrink effort? (done-for-you, white-glove, automation)

Step 3 — Stack the value

List every distinct piece of value in the offer:

  • Core product / service
  • Bonuses (templates, scripts, guides)
  • Onboarding support
  • Done-for-you setup
  • Community / network
  • Exclusive content / events
  • Guarantees

For each, attach a dollar value (what it would cost to acquire separately). Sum it. Compare to price. The ratio is the perceived deal.

Step 4 — Apply the FOMU wedge (Hawkins)

What can ONLY we deliver — or what do we deliver in a way no one else can match? One paragraph.

Examples:

  • "Only platform that combines X + Y in one workflow"
  • "Only built specifically for "
  • "Only with <unique proof / track record / methodology>"

Step 5 — Anticipate objections + write rebuttals

For each likely objection, write a 2-3 sentence rebuttal:

  • "Too expensive" → reframe as cost-of-inaction or ROI
  • "We have an in-house solution" → quantify what they're missing
  • "Bad timing" → show the cost of waiting (Hawkins FOMU)
  • "Need to talk to my team" → equip them to sell it internally (one-pager)
  • "Will it work for us specifically" → case study for their segment

Step 6 — Pricing logic

Choose pricing strategy from the matrix:

  • Outcome-based: pay-per-result (high trust required, but max alignment)
  • Tiered: good / better / best (anchor with "best", default to "better")
  • Per-seat / per-user: simple, scales with customer
  • Per-task / per-call: aligns with consumption
  • Flat fee: simple, predictable
  • Freemium: only if free tier has zero CS burden

State the chosen strategy + the prices + the math behind them (cost + margin + market benchmark from market-research).

Step 7 — Write the positioning statement

One sentence, this format:

"For [ICP] who [pain trigger], [offer name] is the [category] that [unique benefit]. Unlike [main competitor / status quo], we [the wedge]."

Iterate until it's:

  • Specific (not generic)
  • Memorable (could be repeated)
  • Differentiated (the wedge is concrete, not "better")

Step 8 — Package it as the Offer Brief

This is the deliverable downstream agents work from.

Output structure

# Offer Brief: <Offer Name>

## Quick reference
**One-line positioning**: <positioning statement>
**Price**: $X
**ICP**: <one-liner>
**Wedge**: <one-line FOMU statement>

## ICP (from market-research)
- Industry: <>
- Size: <>
- Buyer: <>
- User: <>
- Pain trigger: <>

## The dream outcome
<Specific, vivid, 1-2 sentences. What life looks like after this offer works.>

## Value equation analysis

| Component | Description | Lever |
|---|---|---|
| Dream outcome | <what they get> | <how we made it bigger> |
| Likelihood | <proof element> | <case studies, guarantees, demos> |
| Time delay | <X days to first value> | <how we shrunk it> |
| Effort | <what they have to do> | <how we shrunk it> |

## Value stack

| Item | Description | Standalone value |
|---|---|---|
| <core product/service> | <description> | $X |
| <bonus 1> | <description> | $Y |
| <bonus 2> | <description> | $Z |
| <onboarding> | <description> | $W |
| ... | | |
| **Total stack value** | | **$Total** |
| **Price** | | **$P** |
| **Perceived deal** | | <Total / P>x |

## Wedge (FOMU)
<One paragraph: what only we deliver, or deliver meaningfully better.>

## What we are NOT
<One paragraph: positioning by negation. What we explicitly don't compete on, so we own our chosen ground.>

## Pricing

**Strategy**: <outcome / tiered / per-seat / per-task / flat / freemium>

| Tier | Price | What's included | Best for |
|---|---|---|---|
| <Tier 1> | $X | <list> | <ICP segment> |
| <Tier 2> | $Y | <list> | <ICP segment> |
| <Tier 3> | $Z | <list> | <ICP segment> |

**Math**: cost-to-deliver = $C; margin target = M%; competitor benchmark = $B; chosen price = $P because <reasoning>.

**Payment terms**: <monthly / annual / one-time / hybrid>
**Guarantee**: <if any — terms>
**Refund policy**: <terms>

## Objections + rebuttals

| Objection | Rebuttal (2-3 sentences) |
|---|---|
| <obj> | <rebuttal> |
| <obj> | <rebuttal> |
| <obj> | <rebuttal> |
| <obj> | <rebuttal> |
| <obj> | <rebuttal> |

## Positioning statement (one sentence)
**For** <ICP> **who** <pain trigger>, **<offer name>** is the <category> that <unique benefit>. Unlike <competitor / status quo>, we <wedge>.

## Hook variations (for downstream copy agents to pick from)
1. <hook 1 — curiosity angle>
2. <hook 2 — proof angle>
3. <hook 3 — pain angle>
4. <hook 4 — outcome angle>
5. <hook 5 — counterintuitive angle>

## Proof inventory (what we can cite)
- **Case studies**: <list with link>
- **Testimonials**: <list with link>
- **Stats / data**: <with sources>
- **Awards / certifications**: <list>
- **Customer logos** (with permission): <list>

## Handoff (use _Templates/handoff-template.md)
**To**: Copywriter Agent (primary), Landing Page Builder, SDR Outreach, Paid Ads Strategist
**Approval required**: yes — Chris reviews price + wedge + positioning statement before downstream agents fire

## Risks surfaced
- <risk: e.g., wedge isn't proven yet — needs case study>
- <risk>

## Open questions
- <question>
- <question>

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-offer-positioning-<slug>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Generic dream outcome ("save time" — too vague)
  • No FOMU wedge — head-to-head competing without a unique angle
  • Value stack with $999,999 fake numbers — credible standalone values only
  • Pricing without math — every tier needs cost + margin + benchmark reasoning
  • Objections written generically — they should be the actual phrases customers say
  • Skipping the "what we are NOT" — positioning by addition only is weaker than positioning by subtraction too
  • Position before knowing ICP — always run market-research first

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ All synthesis + writing
  • ⚠️ Pricing change recommendations on existing offers — Chris approves before applied anywhere
  • ⚠️ Wedge changes — Chris approves before downstream copy work

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • market-research handoff
  • _Shared Context/company-profile.md
  • _Shared Context/offers.md (current offers — for positioning consistency)
  • _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (voice + claims discipline)

Outputs to:

  • copywriting (primary)
  • Landing Page Builder (planned)
  • SDR Outreach / cold-email-sequence
  • Paid Ads Strategist (planned)

Updates:

  • _Shared Context/offers.md (after Chris approves — register the new / updated offer)

One-liner reminder

Specific dream outcome. Stack with credible values. FOMU wedge. Price with math. Positioning in one sentence. Objections with verbatim rebuttals.

skills.name: Order Lifecycle Manager
skills.description: Own the order state machine — pending → paid → fulfilled → delivered → complete, plus refund / return / cancel branches. Handles state transitions, customer comms per state, edge cases (partial refund, partial return, replacement orders), and audit log. Net new skill.
skills.intent: Be the single owner of "what state is this order in and what fires when it changes." Every state transition is logged, every customer comm tied to a state, every edge case (partial refund, RMA, replacement) has a documented flow. The cart → backend may write order rows; only this skill decides state transitions.
best_for: Wiring order workflows on a fresh store
Adding return / RMA flow to existing store
skills.estimated_time: Full lifecycle wiring: 6-12 hours. Single new state: 1-2 hours.
skills.body_html:
Order Lifecycle Manager

The state-machine owner for every order. Every state. Every transition. Every comm.

When to use

  • New store needs order workflows
  • Adding return / RMA / replacement flow
  • Refund policy needs codifying
  • Debugging "why didn't the customer get notified?"

When NOT to use

  • Order creation logic → use ecommerce-backend
  • Shipping state → use fulfillment-shipping (it updates this skill's state)
  • Inventory adjustments → use inventory-manager
  • Customer payment dispute → use compliance-officer + account-manager

State machine

                    [cart abandoned]
                          ↓
                    [no state — never created]

[order created]
   ↓
pending ────────────→ expired (15 min timeout; release inventory)
   ↓ (payment confirmed)
paid
   ↓ (label created)
fulfilled
   ↓ (tracking webhook)
shipped
   ↓ (tracking webhook)
out_for_delivery
   ↓
delivered
   ↓ (return window passes OR no return filed)
complete

[refund branches]
paid / fulfilled / shipped / delivered → partial_refunded (still in main state)
paid / fulfilled / shipped / delivered → refunded (terminal)

[cancel branch]
pending → cancelled (no charge captured)
paid → refund flow (capture already happened)

[return / RMA branch]
delivered → return_requested → return_in_transit → return_received → refunded (or replacement)
                                                                     ↓
                                                          replacement order = new order, linked

[exception]
shipped → lost / damaged → CS resolution → refund OR replacement OR no-action

Required inputs

  1. Return window — typical 30 days from delivery; longer for B2B?
  2. Restocking fee? — flat or percentage; none for damage/our-error
  3. Refund method — original payment only / store credit option / mixed
  4. Replacement allowed? — yes for damage/loss; sometimes for defect
  5. Regulated SKU rules — some can't be returned at all (controlled substances, temperature-sensitive)

State transition rules

Every transition writes to order_events with: order_id, from_state, to_state, event_kind, actor (customer/system/admin), payload jsonb, created_at.

Invalid transitions throw + log. Only specific actors can trigger certain transitions:

  • Customer: cancel (only from pending), return_requested
  • System (webhook): payment confirmation, label gen, tracking updates
  • Admin: refund issuance, replacement creation, manual state override (rare + logged)

Workflow surfaces

Refund flow (admin-initiated)

1. CS / admin opens order in admin UI
