[Reference a specific thing they mentioned in the review — shows you read it]

Glad we've been helpful to your [clinic / patients / specific work]. Reach out anytime.

— [Chris / Account Manager]

For a 🟡 mild critique:

Hi [Name] — thanks for the feedback. You're right that [specific thing they raised] is a real gap. [If we're fixing it: "We're already working on X." If we're not: "We hear that — going to look at it."]

Want to chat directly? I'd value 15 minutes to understand more.

[Personal contact info or link to schedule]

— [Chris / AM]

For a 🔴 negative review (calm + factual + offer-to-resolve):

Hi [Name] — thanks for taking the time to share this.

I'm sorry the experience didn't match what you expected. We take feedback seriously. [Brief, factual acknowledgment without arguing or making excuses.]

I'd like to make this right. Could you send me a quick message at [direct email] so we can dig in? My goal is to either fix what we missed or make sure it doesn't happen again.

— [Chris / Account Manager]

Critical rules:

  • Never argue publicly
  • Never reveal customer specifics (HIPAA + privacy)
  • Never imply the customer is wrong
  • Always offer to take it offline
  • Apologize for the experience, not necessarily for the substance (until investigated)
  • Sign with a real human name

For 🔴 hostile / legal / crisis → DO NOT respond publicly. Escalate immediately.

Step 4 — Compliance + legal review for sensitive responses

For any 🔴 response:

  • No reveal of customer-specific facts (HIPAA, privacy)
  • No admission of fault before investigation
  • No promises that legal hasn't reviewed
  • No defamation / counter-attack
  • Counsel review if any legal threat language

Step 5 — Send (Chris signs personally for sensitive)

  • 🟢 Positive: Chris or AM can send (still personal voice; signed)
  • 🟡 Critique: Chris reviews; AM sends
  • 🔴 Negative review (non-hostile): Chris sends personally
  • 🔴 Hostile / legal / crisis: Chris + counsel coordinate; no public response without counsel sign-off

Step 6 — Track + trend

In _Logs/reputation/ (created on first run):

## <Date> — <Source> — <Type>
- **From**: <name / handle>
- **Channel**: <Google / LinkedIn / Reddit / etc.>
- **Sentiment**: 🟢🟡🔴
- **Content**: <verbatim or summary>
- **Theme**: <specific topic — e.g., "shipping delays", "credentialing process", "MuscleLock outcome">
- **Response**: <verbatim or "no response sent + reason">
- **Resolution**: <if resolved offline — how>
- **Escalated**: <if escalated — to whom, when>

Step 7 — Weekly summary

Surface trends:

  • Sentiment distribution this week vs prior
  • Recurring themes (positive + negative)
  • Channels driving most mentions
  • Net sentiment trend (3-week rolling)

Feed to:

  • weekly-review (rolls into Friday wrap)
  • analytics-architect (reputation KPIs)
  • quarterly-review (90-day trend)

Output structure

# Reputation Pulse: <Week / Period>

## Mention volume
| Channel | Positive | Neutral | Negative | Hostile/Crisis |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Google reviews | <N> | <N> | <N> | <N> |
| LinkedIn | <N> | <N> | <N> | <N> |
| Reddit | <N> | <N> | <N> | <N> |
| Trustpilot | <N> | <N> | <N> | <N> |
| News / press | <N> | <N> | <N> | <N> |

## Sentiment trend (3-week rolling)
<chart or summary>

## Themes
**Positive recurring**:
- <Theme>: <N mentions>

**Negative recurring**:
- <Theme>: <N mentions> ← addresses?

## Specific mentions this period

### 🟢 Positives needing acknowledgment
| Source | Person | Mention | Response draft |
|---|---|---|---|

### 🟡 Critiques to address
| Source | Person | Issue | Response draft |
|---|---|---|---|

### 🔴 Negative needing personal Chris response
| Source | Person | Issue | Draft |
|---|---|---|---|

### 🚨 ESCALATIONS (counsel + Chris immediately)
| Source | Issue | Recommended action |
|---|---|---|

## ✅ I handled
- Pulled all sources
- Classified mentions
- Drafted responses

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Send personal responses to 🔴 items
- Review + approve all drafts before send
- Decide engagement vs silence per item

## 🚫 You only
- Respond to legal threats (with counsel)
- Crisis-mode public statements
- Press / media communications
- Any response that admits fault or makes specific promises

## Handoff
**To**: Chris (sends personal responses), counsel (legal escalations), `review-collection` (positive mentions → ask for full Google review), `account-manager` (negative reviews from customers → save play), `weekly-review` + `analytics-architect`

## Logged to
`_Logs/reputation/<period>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Auto-responding publicly — never; always Chris-signed
  • Arguing publicly with a negative reviewer — kills brand
  • Revealing customer specifics in response — HIPAA + privacy
  • Ignoring positives — every supportive mention deserves a thank-you
  • Silence on hostile content without escalation — sometimes silence is right, but make the call deliberately
  • Public crisis response without counsel — risk amplification
  • No trend tracking — single review can feel like a crisis when it's actually noise; trends reveal real signal

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Monitor all public sources
  • ✅ Classify + draft responses
  • ✅ Log to reputation registry
  • ✅ Trend analysis
  • ⚠️ Approve responses before send (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Escalate to counsel (Chris approves who)
  • 🚫 Auto-respond publicly
  • 🚫 Respond to legal threats without counsel
  • 🚫 Make admissions of fault
  • 🚫 Engage hostile content publicly without strategy

Pairing

Inputs from: Google reviews, LinkedIn, Reddit, Trustpilot, Google Alerts, news monitoring tools Outputs to: Chris (responses), counsel (escalations), review-collection (positive → ask), account-manager (negative from existing customer → retention) Chains with: weekly-review, quarterly-review, analytics-architect (reputation KPIs)

One-liner reminder

Monitor weekly. Classify carefully. Chris signs sensitive responses personally. Never argue publicly. Escalate legal threats immediately. Track trends, not single mentions.

skills.name: Resource Center Curator
skills.description: Curates Matrix's provider/customer-facing resource center — dosing references, product-pairing tools, adaptive intake questions, peptide map (sub-brand education), reconstitution + storage guides, tool library. Continuously updated as new SKUs ship + new protocols launch. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Educated providers buy more + use product correctly + churn less. The resource center is the always-on education asset that grows with the catalog. This specialist maintains it continuously — no stale content as new products arrive, no broken-link junk, no dosing references that don't match catalog reality.
skills.estimated_time: Per SKU update: 1-3 hours. Per protocol: 4-8 hours. Quarterly audit: 1-2 days.
skills.body_html:
Resource Center Curator

The provider-facing education curator. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns Matrix's provider/customer resource center — the always-on, non-gated education hub. Maintains: dosing references (per dosing-reference.md — provider-only), product pairing + stacking protocols (per ClinIQ_Peptide_Supplement_Synergy.csv), adaptive intake questions, reconstitution + storage guides, calculator tools, Peptide Map educational content (sub-brand voice). Continuously refreshes as new SKUs + protocols arrive. Composes formulation + compliance + content teams for material.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Gated rep training (sales-curriculum-author + clinical-curriculum-author), pass/fail certification (sales-rep-certification-manager), patient-facing material (Matrix is provider-only).

INPUT

  • Trigger: new-sku-launch / new-protocol / regulatory-update / quarterly-audit / new-tool / sub-brand-content
  • Scope: specific content area / specific sub-brand / full audit

RULES

  • Provider-only — never patient-facing
  • Trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT proprietary parameters)
  • All clinical claims approved-library only (per compliance-rules.md)
  • DSHEA disclaimer where applicable (supplement-flavored content)
  • Sub-brand voice respected (Peptide Map = deep provider education; Matrix B2B = catalog-style; MuscleLock = D2C-flavored)
  • Cite source for every clinical claim (use prior-art-citation-formatter)
  • Steve's publication portfolio leveraged for credibility (per alm-dt-platform.md)
  • Per new SKU: dosing reference entry + protocol primer + stacking notes + storage guide + reconstitution guide
  • Per new protocol: pairing-protocol entry + adaptive-intake question updates
  • Tool library: BUD calculator, dose calc, mg/mL ↔ IU converter, reconstitution guide per vial, storage tracker
  • Compliance: pass compliance-officer Gate 1 on all regulated material before publish
  • Search-friendly (content-seo-specialist coordinates for Peptide Map content SEO)

TOOLS

  • Matrix Academy content area + dedicated resource-center routes (when built)
  • Read: dosing-reference.md, products.md, regulatory-frameworks.md, compliance-rules.md, alm-dt-platform.md (READ-ONLY context), sub-brands.md, steve-kushner-profile.md
  • Source: ~/Projects/dosing guide/Knowledge Base/ (ClinIQ KB + supplement synergy + adaptive intake)
  • Compose: compliance-officer, compliance-phrase-detector, prior-art-citation-formatter, bud-calculator (atomic)
  • Coordinate: content-seo-specialist (Peptide Map SEO), creative-director (visual treatment), formulation-compliance (regulatory accuracy)
  • Write: resource center content + tool configs

OUTPUT

  • Updated resource center entries per new SKU / protocol / regulatory update
  • Tool library updates (BUD calculator, dose calc, etc.)
  • Provider-friendly format (PDF download + web reference + Peptide Map curriculum tie-in where relevant)
  • Change log per asset
  • Handoff to content-seo-specialist for Peptide Map content SEO opportunities

HANDOFF

  • compliance-officer (Gate 1 review on regulated content)
  • content-seo-specialist (Peptide Map content SEO)
  • creative-director (visual treatment + brand consistency)
  • account-manager (key customer notifications of major content updates)
  • learner-progress-tracker (per-rep-cert may consume some provider-facing content for awareness)

Eval cases

  1. New SKU launch → dosing reference entry + protocol primer + stacking notes
  2. New protocol launch (e.g., new stack combination) → pairing protocol added + adaptive-intake updated
  3. Regulatory update → claims discipline sweep across resource center
  4. New tool launch (e.g., interactive BUD calculator UI) → tool added + composition
  5. Quarterly audit → dead content retirement + freshness check
  6. Sub-brand sweep (Peptide Map gets quarterly content depth review)
  7. Customer support tickets reveal education gap → targeted content fix

Anti-patterns

  • Patient-facing material (Matrix is provider-only)
  • Trade-secret leaks in content
  • Stale dosing references (catalog updated; reference stuck)
  • Source-less claims
  • Voice blur across sub-brands
  • Tools that don't match dosing-reference.md canonical (drift)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Curate + update content
  • ✅ Add tools
  • ⚠️ Publish material change (compliance-officer Gate 1)
  • ⚠️ Sub-brand content (sub-brand lead approval)
  • 🚫 Patient-facing content
  • 🚫 Trade-secret leaks
  • 🚫 Unapproved claims

Pairing

Composed by: education-training-lead Composes: compliance-officer, prior-art-citation-formatter, bud-calculator, content-seo-specialist, creative-director Reads: dosing-reference.md, products.md, regulatory-frameworks.md, alm-dt-platform.md, sub-brands.md Source: ~/Projects/dosing guide/Knowledge Base/

One-liner reminder

Provider-only. Per-SKU continuous update. Stacking protocols + dosing + tools. Sub-brand voice. Cite sources. Compliance gate. No trade-secret leaks.

skills.name: Retention Automation
skills.description: Recovery sequences for at-risk customers. Triggered by account-manager health flags (🟡 at-risk / 🔴 churn-risk). Designs the save play, picks the channel (voice / email / SMS / human-AM), executes the sequence, tracks outcome. Closes the loop between detection and action.
skills.intent: Stop detecting churn and doing nothing. For each at-risk customer, generate a specific save play, route to the right channel, execute the sequence, measure outcome. Close the loop: detect → act → measure → learn.
best_for: Dormant customer reactivation (60+ days no order)
skills.estimated_time: 20-40 min per save play + sequence runtime
skills.body_html:
Retention Automation

The save-play engine. Detection → action → measurement → learning.

When to use

  • account-manager flagged customer as 🟡 at-risk or 🔴 churn-risk
  • Customer hit dormancy threshold (60+ days no order)
  • Pre-renewal window for at-risk subscription
  • Win-back attempt on recently-churned customer

When NOT to use

  • Healthy customer (no save needed — let account-manager continue routine)
  • Active customer who explicitly opted out
  • Customer in legal / billing dispute (route to humans only)
  • New customer in onboarding (use customer-onboarding)

Required inputs

  1. Customer / contact — GHL contact ID
  2. Risk signal — what triggered (no orders 60d / sentiment drop / support escalation / etc.)
  3. Health score history — last 3-6 months trend
  4. Last touch — most recent meaningful interaction
  5. Customer-defined success — from onboarding (captured then)

If 2 missing, ask: "What signal flagged this customer as at-risk?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Diagnose the root cause

For each at-risk customer, hypothesize WHY:

Signal Likely root cause
No orders 60+ days, pattern was monthly Lost mindshare / changed vendor / clinic process change
Order volume declining 3+ months Slow business / dissatisfaction / competitor
Sentiment drop in last call/email Specific incident or grievance
Multiple support tickets, slow resolution Operational frustration
Negative review online Public-facing issue
Churn signal: explicit ("we're done") Active decision made

Step 2 — Design the save play

For each root cause, pick the right play:

"Lost mindshare" save:

  • Channel: voice (high-touch — feels personal)
  • Sender: human AM (not AI) if high-value; AI voice if low-touch
  • Message: "Haven't heard from you — what changed?" (open, no pitch)
  • Offer: free strategy session / catalog refresh / new product preview
  • Follow-up: 7-day check-in if no response

"Specific grievance" save:

  • Channel: voice (human) — required for emotional resolution
  • Sender: human AM + Chris if escalation
  • Message: acknowledge, apologize specifically, offer concrete remedy
  • Offer: credit / replacement / discount on next / extended payment terms
  • Follow-up: 30-day check-in confirming recovery

"Operational frustration" save:

  • Channel: voice + email (voice for human, email for systematic info)
  • Sender: human AM + ops lead
  • Message: identify the operational issue, propose fix, escalate internally
  • Offer: dedicated support / direct line / process change
  • Follow-up: 14-day + 30-day verification

"Slow business" save:

  • Channel: email + SMS (low-touch — they're busy, not unhappy)
  • Sender: AI agent (Mia or Alex)
  • Message: "Quick check-in — anything we can support with right now?"
  • Offer: training session / new modality intro / referral to revenue help
  • Follow-up: 30-day cadence

"Active churn decision" save:

  • Channel: voice (human Chris if revenue significant)
  • Sender: Chris directly
  • Message: brief, no defense — listen + understand + ask for honest feedback
  • Offer: only if customer indicates remediable — don't fight
  • Follow-up: 90-day soft re-engage with new value

Step 3 — Build the sequence

For each play, structure as a multi-touch sequence:

Touch 1 — Day 0
  Channel: <chosen>
  Sender: <AI agent / human>
  Message: <draft>
  Goal: <reconnect / acknowledge / open conversation>
  Response handling:
    - Positive engage → Touch 2 (deepen)
    - Polite decline → soft archive
    - No response → Touch 2 (different angle)
    - Hostile → escalate to Chris immediately

Touch 2 — Day 3-7 (depending on play)
  Channel: <may differ from touch 1>
  ...

Touch 3 — Day 14-21
  Final attempt
  Make the soft exit easy: "If you've moved on, we get it — reply STOP and we'll stop"

Step 4 — Compliance check

Per compliance-rules.md:

  • TCPA window for any voice / SMS
  • A2P 10DLC active for SMS
  • CAN-SPAM compliance for email
  • No banned phrases per brand-guide.md
  • HIPAA-aware if patient context could surface

Step 5 — Execute the sequence

Wire via workflow-automation-builder (GHL). For voice, hand off to voice-ai-sales-agent (variant) or human AM. For email, hand off to email channel.

Pause sequence on:

  • Positive response (move to opportunity stage)
  • STOP / explicit opt-out
  • Successful save (resumed order activity)
  • Auto-pause threshold reached (no engagement after 3 touches)

Step 6 — Measure outcome

For each save attempt, classify:

  • Saved — customer resumed expected activity within 30 days
  • Soft-saved — partial recovery (acknowledged, didn't fully resume)
  • Lost — confirmed churn / no recovery
  • Pending — still in sequence

Track:

  • Save rate by root cause type
  • Save rate by channel
  • Save rate by sender (AI vs human)
  • Avg time-to-save
  • Cost per save

Step 7 — Learn + iterate

After 10+ save attempts in a category:

  • Which root cause has best save rate?
  • Which channel works best per cause?
  • Which sender type works best?
  • Surface to voice-call-reviewer / weekly-review for cross-cutting patterns

Output structure

# Retention Play: <Customer>

## Diagnosis
**Customer**: <name + clinic>
**Risk level**: 🟡 / 🔴
**Triggering signal**: <signal>
**Root cause hypothesis**: <one paragraph>
**Confidence in hypothesis**: high / medium / low

## Save play

**Play type**: <mindshare / grievance / operational / slow-business / churn-decision>
**Channel mix**: <voice + email / human-only / AI-led / etc.>
**Primary sender**: <Chris / AM / AI agent>
**Estimated cost**: $<X> (per touch + time)

## Sequence

### Touch 1 — Day 0
**Channel**: <channel>
**Sender**: <name>
**Draft message**:

Touch 2 — Day

[same format]

Touch 3 — Day

[same format]

Compliance pre-flight

  • TCPA window enforced (voice/SMS)
  • CAN-SPAM compliant (email)
  • A2P 10DLC active (SMS)
  • Brand voice (no banned phrases)
  • STOP handling in place

Auto-pause triggers

  • Positive response → pause + escalate to AM
  • STOP / opt-out → pause + tag DNC
  • Successful save (order resumed) → pause + tag saved
  • 3 touches no response → pause + tag unrecoverable

✅ I handled

  • Diagnosis + root cause hypothesis
  • Save play design
  • Sequence with messages drafted
  • Compliance pre-flight
  • Wired (or proposed wiring) in GHL

⚠️ Need from you (Chris / AM)

  • Approve save play (especially if high-value customer)
  • Approve any concrete offers (credit / discount / extended terms)
  • Hold human-touch calls (if play requires human sender)
  • Decide on hostile-response escalation

🚫 You only

  • Issue credits / refunds
  • Authorize discounts beyond approved bands
  • Make new contractual commitments

Outcome tracking

Started: Status: in-progress / saved / lost / pending Will measure at: 30 days

Handoff

To: voice-ai-sales-agent (if voice play), email channel (if email), human AM (if human-touch), workflow-automation-builder (GHL wiring)

Logged to

_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-retention-<customer>.md _Logs/campaign-results/retention/<customer>.md (outcome at 30 days)


## Anti-patterns

- **Generic "we miss you" emails** — feels lazy; root-cause-driven beats generic
- **AI-led save** for high-value customers — they deserve human attention
- **Discount-as-first-move** — undervalues product; try reconnection first
- **No outcome tracking** — can't learn what works
- **Fighting active churn decisions** — let them go gracefully; soft re-engage later

## Pre-authorizations

- ✅ Diagnose + design save play
- ✅ Draft messages for sequence
- ✅ Compliance pre-flight
- ✅ Wire sequence in GHL (paused state)
- ⚠️ Activate sequence (Chris / AM approves)
- ⚠️ Concrete offers in messages (Chris approves)
- 🚫 Issue credits / refunds
- 🚫 Modify customer's contract terms
- 🚫 Negotiate beyond approved bands

## Pairing

**Inputs from**: `account-manager` (health flag triggers this), `ghl-daily-pulse` (signals), `voice-call-reviewer` (sentiment from last calls)
**Outputs to**: `voice-ai-sales-agent` (voice channel), email channel, human AM, `workflow-automation-builder`, `weekly-review` (save rate KPIs)
**Chains with**: `account-manager` (post-save reactivation routine), `customer-onboarding` (post-churn re-onboarding if recovered)

## One-liner reminder

**Root-cause first. Match play to cause. Human for high-value emotional. AI for low-touch operational. Compliance pre-flight. Measure outcome at 30 days.**

skills.name: Retention Rescue Playbook
skills.description: When `churn-risk-predictor` flags an account, runs the structured rescue sequence — AM personal touch + Chris escalation (enterprise/white-glove) + discounted re-engagement offer + verbatim reason capture. Tier-respecting, compliance-gated. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: The window between "churn-risk flag raised" and "customer churned" is short — often 14-30 days. This specialist runs the rescue with discipline so AMs don't improvise and Chris escalates only where it matters.
skills.estimated_time: Per rescue: 2-5 days elapsed (3-6 hours active work).
skills.body_html:
Retention Rescue Playbook

Structured rescue execution. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Runs the rescue sequence when an account is flagged churn-risk. The sequence: (1) AM personal touch within 24h (call, not email), (2) discovery on driver — what changed, what's needed, (3) tier-appropriate offer (discount / extended terms / pause / extra training / Chris-call escalation for enterprise), (4) verbatim reason captured into GHL custom field, (5) outcome tracked (saved / churned / paused) and fed to customer-success-continuous-improvement. Composes account-manager, retention-automation, compliance-officer, sub-brand voice.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Predicting churn (churn-risk-predictor), refund authorization (sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md), automated lifecycle save flows (retention-automation does those), root-cause analysis across cohorts (customer-success-continuous-improvement).

INPUT

  • Trigger: churn-risk-predictor auto-fire / AM manual / customer-success-lead escalation
  • account_id + churn-risk forecast payload
  • Tier (drives offer + escalation path)
  • Primary driver (informs discovery questions)

RULES

  • AM personal touch (voice or video, NOT email) within 24h of trigger
  • Tier escalation: enterprise / white-glove always include Chris in loop within 48h
  • Offer tiers per approval-rules.md:
    • Standard: up to 15% discount 90 days, AM-approved
    • High-touch: up to 20% + extended pause, AM-approved
    • White-glove: up to 25% + custom terms, AM + Chris
    • Enterprise: custom commercial response, Chris-led
  • Verbatim reason captured (not paraphrased) into churn_reason_verbatim custom field
  • All customer-facing comms pass compliance-officer (no medical claims, no banned phrases)
  • Sub-brand voice respected (Matrix vs MuscleLock vs Peptide Map vs ClinIQ)
  • PHI/PII discipline: rescue notes redacted in any cross-account aggregate
  • Refund requests escalate to sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md (NOT auto-issued here)
  • Max rescue duration 14 days from trigger; if no resolution → exit + flag

TOOLS

  • GHL MCP (write custom fields, conversations, tags, opportunities)
  • Compose: account-manager, retention-automation, compliance-officer, email-intent-classifier, pii-redactor
  • Read: approval-rules.md, sub-brands.md, sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md, sops/customer-reference-request.sop.md
  • Voice: AM call (or voice-ai-support-agent Alex for standard tier with AM oversight)
  • Write: _Logs/cs/rescues/<account_id>/<trigger_date>.md + outcome telemetry

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    rescue_id: string
    account_id: string
    triggered_at: ISO
    trigger_source: predictor-auto | am-manual | cs-lead-escalation
    primary_driver: string
    tier: enterprise | white-glove | high-touch | standard
    steps_executed:
      - step: am-personal-touch | discovery-call | offer-presented | chris-escalation | reason-captured
        executed_at: ISO
        executor: AM_name | Chris | voice-agent
        notes: string
    offer_made:
      type: discount | pause | extended-terms | custom | none
      detail: string
      approved_by: AM_name | Chris
      compliance_approved: boolean
    customer_response:
      sentiment: positive | neutral | negative
      verbatim_quote: string  # for CI engine
    outcome: saved | churned | paused | in-progress
    outcome_captured_at: ISO
    churn_reason_verbatim: string | null
    follow_up_required: boolean
    flags:
      - flag: string  # e.g., "refund-requested", "competitor-named", "product-defect"
  format: JSON + Markdown brief

HANDOFF

  • account-manager (executes touch + discovery + close-out)
  • compliance-officer (every offer + customer-facing comm gates here)
  • retention-automation (lifecycle save flow if offer accepted)
  • customer-success-continuous-improvement (verbatim reasons aggregated weekly)
  • chain-monitor (P0 enterprise alerts + stalled rescues > 7 days)
  • Chris (enterprise / white-glove direct loop)

Eval cases

  1. Standard at-risk, 15% offer → saved → follow-up cadence resumes
  2. Enterprise at-risk → Chris call → custom terms → saved
  3. Refund-requested mid-rescue → routed to refund SOP, rescue continues parallel
  4. Customer cites competitor → reason captured + Chris flagged
  5. Product defect surfaced → ops + formulation-lead alerted
  6. Rescue stalls > 7 days → chain-monitor alert + AM unstick
  7. Customer churns despite rescue → verbatim reason captured + CI ingest
  8. PII discipline: cross-account aggregate strips identifiers

Anti-patterns

  • Email-only rescue (voice is the gate; AM personal touch)
  • Discount as default (discovery first; sometimes pause or training is the answer)
  • Paraphrased reasons (lose signal for CI engine — verbatim only)
  • No compliance gate on offers (could create claim or commercial violation)
  • Skipping Chris on enterprise (relationship leverage exists for a reason)
  • Auto-refund (always routes to refund SOP)
  • Rescue without time-box (14d max)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ AM personal touch
  • ✅ Discovery + reason capture
  • ✅ Standard / high-touch offer per approval-rules.md
  • ⚠️ White-glove offer (AM + Chris)
  • ⚠️ Enterprise commercial response (Chris-led)
  • ⚠️ Trigger voice-ai-support-agent for outreach (AM oversight)
  • 🚫 Issue refund / credit (routes to refund SOP)
  • 🚫 Make medical or compliance claim in offer comms

Pairing

Composed by: churn-risk-predictor, customer-success-lead, account-manager Composes: account-manager, compliance-officer, retention-automation, email-intent-classifier, pii-redactor, voice-ai-support-agent (optional) Reads: approval-rules.md, sub-brands.md, CS SOPs Outputs to: AMs, Chris, retention-automation, customer-success-continuous-improvement, chain-monitor

One-liner reminder

Voice within 24h. Discovery before offer. Verbatim reason always. Compliance-gated. Tier-respecting escalation. 14-day time-box. Refunds route to SOP.

skills.name: Reuse Scanner
skills.description: Before any new build, scan all `~/Projects/` for existing files, components, skills, prompts, content, designs, or data that could be reused. Output a "reuse-first" report so the build either inherits / extends existing assets OR has clear justification for creating new. Wraps the harness `reuse-before-rebuild` skill in company-OS context.
skills.intent: Eliminate copy-paste rebuilds and inconsistency across Matrix projects. Every new build starts with this scan. Surface: similar components / pages / skills / prompts / brand assets / data files that already exist. Recommend reuse vs extend vs build-new with reasoning.
best_for: Pre-build audit before any new component / page / skill / asset
Spinning up a new project (avoid copy-rebuild)
Refactor decision (extend existing or build new?)
Finding the canonical version of a recurring artifact
skills.estimated_time: 5-15 min
skills.body_html:
Reuse Scanner

Run before every new build. The leverage: stop rebuilding what exists.

When to use

  • Before authoring a new SKILL.md (does a similar one exist?)
  • Before building a new React component (Matrix has many — check first)
  • Before writing new brand copy (likely a version exists)
  • Before creating a new workflow (likely overlaps with existing)
  • Before designing a new pipeline / form / template

When NOT to use

  • The build is genuinely first-of-kind (no point scanning for nothing)
  • Sub-minute task (the scan takes longer than the build)
  • Code in node_modules/ or other 3rd-party (skip)

Required inputs

  1. What you're about to build — one-line description
  2. Search keywords — terms / patterns that would match existing assets
  3. Scope — ~/Projects/ (default) / specific project / specific folder

If 1 missing, ask: "What are you about to build? One line."

Workflow

Step 1 — Search the filesystem

For SKILL.md / workflow / KB asset:

rg -l "<keyword>" ~/Projects/AI\ Company/ --type md
find ~/Projects/AI\ Company/_Skills\ Library -name "*.md" | xargs grep -l "<keyword>"

For React components:

find ~/Projects -path '*/node_modules' -prune -o -name "*.tsx" -print | xargs grep -l "<component-name-or-pattern>" 2>/dev/null

For brand assets:

rg -l "Matrix" ~/Projects/ --type md --type json
find ~/Projects -iname "*brand*" -o -iname "*logo*" -not -path '*/node_modules/*'

For prompts / templates:

rg -l "<theme>" ~/Projects/ --type md

For data files (CSV / JSON):

find ~/Projects -name "*.csv" -o -name "*.json" -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/.next/*' | xargs grep -l "<keyword>" 2>/dev/null

Step 2 — Inspect top matches

For each match (top 5-10):

  • File path
  • One-line summary of what it is
  • Last modified date
  • How close to the target (exact match / extends easily / pattern-similar / unrelated)

Step 3 — Classify each match

Class Action
Exact — does what we need Use directly. Don't rebuild.
Near — close, needs minor tweak Clone + tweak. Faster than from-scratch.
Pattern — different content, same shape Use as template. Adapt to new content.
Inspiration — relates but doesn't apply Reference only. Build new but informed.
Junk — false positive Ignore.

Step 4 — Make the recommendation

Three possible outputs:

A — Reuse existing (don't build new):

"Use <path> directly. It already does what you need."

B — Extend / clone-and-tweak:

"Clone <path> to <new-path>. Change and . ~10 min vs 60 min from scratch."

C — Build new (with justification):

"No close match. Building new is justified. Reference <closest-path> for pattern consistency. [Recommend invoking skill-builder / project-scaffolder / etc.]"

Step 5 — Surface inconsistencies (bonus value)

If the scan finds 3+ near-duplicates of the same asset (e.g., 3 different "brand identity" files in 3 projects), flag:

"⚠️ Duplicate sprawl: brand-identity.md exists in 4 projects with diverging content. Consider consolidating to _Shared Context/brand-guide.md."

Step 6 — Output

Output structure

# Reuse Scan — <what we're building>

## Build description
<one-line>

## Search keywords
<list>

## Matches found

### 🎯 Exact / near-exact (use these)
| Path | Match class | One-line | Last modified |
|---|---|---|---|
| `<path>` | Exact | <desc> | <date> |
| `<path>` | Near | <desc> | <date> |

### 📋 Pattern matches (use as template)
| Path | One-line | Why useful |
|---|---|---|
| `<path>` | <desc> | <how to adapt> |

### 💡 Inspiration (reference only)
| Path | One-line |
|---|---|
| `<path>` | <desc> |

## ⚠️ Inconsistencies surfaced (if any)
- <Duplicate sprawl observation>
- <Diverging-version observation>
- <Stale-version observation>

## Recommendation

**Action**: <Reuse / Extend / Build new>

**Specific path forward**:
<one paragraph>

**Time saved vs from-scratch**: ~<X> min

**Next skill to invoke**:
- If Reuse → directly use the existing file
- If Extend → manual clone + edit, OR invoke `agent-cloner` if it's a skill
- If Build new → invoke `skill-builder` / `project-scaffolder` / etc. (referencing the closest pattern from scan)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-reuse-scan-<topic>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Scanning without keywords — produces noise; always have specific search terms
  • Recommending "build new" without justification — must show why existing didn't fit
  • Ignoring inconsistencies surfaced — duplicate sprawl is itself a problem worth fixing
  • Searching only _Skills Library/ when the target is a React component — broaden scope
  • Not noting "last modified" — picks stale assets

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read across all ~/Projects/* (per tool-access.md)
  • ✅ Surface inconsistencies / duplicate sprawl
  • ⚠️ Propose consolidation of duplicate sprawl (Chris approves before merging)
  • 🚫 Auto-delete or merge files

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • User build description
  • All ~/Projects/ (read-only scan)

Outputs to:

  • The skill that actually does the build (skill-builder, project-scaffolder, copywriting, engineering-lead, etc.)
  • _Logs/agent-decisions/

Chains with:

  • project-overseer (cross-project oversight catches the same patterns)
  • skill-builder (scan before authoring)
  • project-scaffolder (scan before scaffolding)
  • agent-cloner (scan first → if match found, clone via this skill)

Wraps

  • Harness skill reuse-before-rebuild (if installed) — this skill is the Company-OS wrapper that adds Matrix context + integrates with our agent-registry / project list.

One-liner reminder

Search first. Reuse beats extend beats build-new. Surface inconsistencies even when they're tangential. Save the scan to logs.

skills.name: Review Acquisition Strategist
skills.description: Systematic 5-star review acquisition across Google Business, Yelp, Trustpilot, industry-specific platforms, BBB, Glassdoor. FTC-compliant (no incentivized reviews; honest experience requests). Coordinates with `review-collection` (the orchestrator) + `account-manager` (relationship-led asks). Owns velocity targets + multi-platform strategy. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: "Highest rated" is part of Matrix's goal. Reviews are the single biggest local + transactional SEO signal AND the biggest social-proof asset. Systematic, ethical acquisition compounds over years. This specialist owns the multi-platform strategy + velocity targets; `review-collection` orchestrates specific asks.
skills.estimated_time: Quarterly strategy: 2-4 hours. Per-platform tactic: 1-2 hours.
skills.body_html:
Review Acquisition Strategist

The ratings strategist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Designs + maintains Matrix's review-acquisition strategy across all platforms. Sets velocity targets per platform per sub-brand. Coordinates with review-collection (orchestrator) + account-manager (high-touch customer asks) for execution. Owns review-response standards. Tracks ratings + review-velocity KPIs. Handles negative-review response strategy.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Executing individual review asks (review-collection orchestrates), reputation management broadly (reputation-manager owns sentiment + crisis), HR reviews (recruiting / performance-management).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (quarterly-strategy / velocity-audit / negative-review-cluster / new-platform / reputation-defense)
    required: true
  scope:
    type: enum (all-platforms / specific-platform / specific-sub-brand)
    required: false

RULES

rules:
  ftc_compliance:
    - NO incentivized reviews (no discounts / freebies / gift cards for reviewing)
    - NO fake reviews (FTC + platform + reputational disaster)
    - NO review-gating that selectively asks only happy customers (FTC ruled deceptive)
    - YES: honest experience requests (every customer asked equally)
    - YES: easy-to-write review links (lower friction)
    - YES: timing asks (post-positive-experience window)
  platform_specific_rules:
    google_business_profile:
      - Direct link to review form
      - Ask post-positive-experience (NPS ≥ 8 trigger)
      - Respond to ALL reviews within 48h (positive + negative)
      - Don't dispute reviews unless clear policy violation (fake / abusive / off-topic)
    yelp:
      - Yelp specifically PROHIBITS asking for reviews
      - Strategy: passive only — Yelp link on receipts / email signatures
      - Respond to ALL reviews
      - Don't dispute legitimate reviews
    trustpilot:
      - Direct asks allowed
      - Free + paid tiers available (paid offers more features)
      - Verified reviews preferred (Trustpilot's "verified" badge)
    industry_platforms:
      - Per platform's specific guidelines
      - LinkedIn recommendations: peer-to-peer; AM-led
      - BBB: complaint-resolution-focused; track + resolve
  velocity_targets:
    google_business: 5-10 new reviews / month / sub-brand
    yelp: 1-3 new reviews / month (Yelp filters aggressively; quality over quantity)
    trustpilot: 5-15 / month for D2C MuscleLock; less for B2B MAS
    industry-specific: variable per platform's user base
  rating_targets:
    google: 4.7+ avg, 100+ reviews
    yelp: 4.5+ avg
    trustpilot: 4.5+ avg (D2C is more volatile)
  response_standards:
    - Within 48 hours of review post
    - Positive: warm acknowledgment + specific reference to what they liked
    - Negative: empathy + ownership + concrete next step + offer to take offline
    - Don't argue publicly; resolve privately
    - Don't post template responses (visible to all)
  negative_review_protocol:
    - Triage within 24 hours
    - Identify if legitimate (real customer, real experience) vs spam (fake / competitor)
    - Legitimate: respond publicly + reach out privately to resolve
    - Spam: report to platform + don't engage publicly
    - Cluster of negatives same issue → root cause analysis (product / fulfillment / service)

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - Google Business Profile reviews API
    - Yelp Fusion API (read; can't ask via API)
    - Trustpilot Business API
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md (review platform list + rating targets)
    - _Shared Context/sub-brands.md
    - _Logs/customer-interactions/ (NPS data, post-experience signals)
    - GHL contacts (positive-experience signals)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/seo/reviews/<period>-velocity-report.md
    - _Logs/seo/reviews/response-log.csv
    - _Logs/seo/reviews/strategy-<period>.md
  composed_skills:
    - review-collection (orchestrator — runs the actual asks)
    - account-manager (AM-led high-touch asks)
    - reputation-manager (sentiment monitoring + crisis)
    - compliance-phrase-detector (response copy review)
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (read NPS + customer interactions; tag customers for review asks)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (response drafting + audit)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_id: string
    period: ISO
    per_platform_metrics:
      - platform: string
        current_avg_rating: number
        target_rating: number
        delta_to_target: number
        total_reviews: number
        new_reviews_last_30d: number
        velocity_target_30d: number
        velocity_gap: number
        response_rate_pct: number
        response_time_avg_hours: number
        negative_reviews_open: number
    velocity_action_plan:
      - platform: string
        action: string
        expected_lift: number
        executor: review-collection | account-manager | this skill
    negative_review_clusters:
      - cluster_topic: string  # e.g., "shipping speed", "out of stock"
        count: number
        root_cause_hypothesis: string
        owner_for_fix: string
    response_quality_score: number  # 0-100 based on tone + specificity sample
    flags:
      - flag: ftc-risk / spam-spike / negative-cluster / response-lag
        detail: string
    recommendations:
      - priority: P0 | P1 | P2
        action: string
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - review-collection (execute specific asks)
    - account-manager (high-touch AM-led asks)
    - seo-strategy-lead (KPI reporting)
    - reputation-manager (negative-cluster + crisis)
  trigger_method: quarterly cron + monthly check + ad-hoc on negative spike
  fallback_routing:
    - Rating drops > 0.2 stars → urgent investigation
    - Negative cluster (3+ same-issue reviews in 14 days) → root-cause + fix
    - Spam spike → platform reporting + brand monitoring

Eval cases

  1. Quarterly velocity audit, all platforms healthy → maintain
  2. Google rating drops 4.8 → 4.6 → diagnostic
  3. Negative cluster around shipping → ops escalation
  4. Yelp filtering aggressively → strategy adjustment (no direct asks)
  5. New platform considered (Trustpilot for MuscleLock launch)
  6. Competitor reputation attack (cluster of suspicious negatives) → platform reporting

Anti-patterns

  • Incentivized reviews — FTC violation
  • Review-gating (asking only happy customers) — FTC violation
  • Fake reviews — existential reputation risk
  • Template responses to legitimate negative reviews
  • Ignoring Yelp's no-asks policy — penalty + filtering
  • Disputing legitimate reviews — never wins long-term

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Strategy + velocity targets
  • ✅ Response drafting (positive)
  • ⚠️ Response to material negative review (compliance-officer review for regulated context)
  • ⚠️ Public dispute of review (Chris + counsel for legal context)
  • 🚫 Incentivized reviews
  • 🚫 Fake reviews / astroturfing

Pairing

Composed by: seo-strategy-lead Composes: review-collection, account-manager, reputation-manager, compliance-phrase-detector Reads: seo-targets.md, GHL NPS, platform review APIs

One-liner reminder

FTC-compliant. No incentivized reviews. No gating. Respond within 48h. Negative cluster = root cause. Velocity targets per platform. Trustworthy compounds; shortcuts destroy.

skills.name: Review Collection
skills.description: Systematic review + testimonial collection from happy Matrix customers. Triggered by NPS-promoter signals + 30/60/90 milestone hits + post-call satisfaction. Drafts personalized ask, routes through right channel, never auto-asks without trigger. Builds Matrix's social-proof inventory over time.
skills.intent: Matrix loses social proof currency every month it doesn't ask. Build the systematic ask-flow that triggers on actual happy-customer moments — not random spam. Output: drafted ask + channel + timing recommendation. Logged into a reviews inventory for marketing reuse.
best_for: 30/60/90 milestone hits with strong customer-defined-success progress
Post-renewal customers (still happy after first contract)
skills.estimated_time: 10-15 min per ask + ongoing inventory management
skills.body_html:
Review Collection

Systematic ask-flow for reviews + testimonials. Triggered by real signals, never spammed.

When to use

  • NPS promoter (9-10) in post-onboarding or post-call survey
  • 30/60/90 milestone where customer-defined success is being met
  • Customer expansion / cross-sell (signal of satisfaction)
  • Post-renewal (year+ customer)
  • Customer references our product unprompted (in conversation / email)

When NOT to use

  • New customer (<30 days — they don't know yet if we're great)
  • At-risk customer (🟡 / 🔴 health) — fix first, ask later
  • Customer with open complaint or support escalation
  • Customer who declined prior ask (respect the no for 6+ months)
  • Random ask without trigger

Required inputs

  1. Customer / contact — GHL contact ID
  2. Trigger — what signal warrants the ask (NPS / milestone / behavior)
  3. Channel — Google review / Trustpilot / G2 / Capterra / written testimonial / video / case study
  4. Ask intensity — quick review / written testimonial / video / full case study

If 2 missing, ask: "What signal triggered this — NPS score, milestone, behavior?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Verify signal

Confirm the customer is genuinely happy AND has been a customer long enough to have a real opinion (30+ days). Cross-check:

  • Account health (account-manager — must be 🟢)
  • Recent sentiment in conversations (per ghl-conversation-analytics)
  • No open support tickets
  • No recent escalations

Step 2 — Match channel to ask

Trigger Recommended channel
NPS 9-10 + clinic owner Google review + LinkedIn recommendation
30-day milestone strong progress Written testimonial (use later in case studies)
60-day expansion signal Video testimonial (high effort, high value)
Renewal Full case study (longest commitment signal)
In-conversation praise Quick Google review ask (low-friction, hot iron)
Awards-worthy outcome Press / PR mention (handoff to PR / investor-relations)

Step 3 — Draft the ask

For Google review (Matrix's primary channel):

Hey Dr. [Last Name],

Real quick — would you mind sharing a quick review of your experience with Matrix? Other clinic owners researching us will see it, and it really helps them make a decision.

Here's the link (takes 60 seconds): [google_review_url from brand-profile.json]

If anything's been less than great, just reply to this and we'll fix it. No pressure either way.

— [Account Manager / Chris]

For written testimonial:

Dr. [Last Name] — 

[Specific reference to their wins / outcomes from account-manager notes]. Would you be open to a short written testimonial we could use on our site?

I'll draft something based on your actual experience and send it to you for review — you just approve / edit. Takes 5 minutes of your time. We'd credit you as: "Dr. [Name], [Specialty], [Clinic / city]".

If you'd rather not, totally fine. Just let me know.

For video testimonial: send a Loom of the ask (more personal) + offer 15 minutes on Zoom to record, with prep questions sent ahead.

Step 4 — Compliance + ethical check

  • Real customer: never use a non-customer's image / words
  • Documented permission: every published testimonial requires written approval
  • No incentive that biases: small thank-yous OK; bribing for positive reviews never
  • HIPAA-aware: no patient details in testimonials (even if customer wants to share)
  • FTC compliance: disclosed if any compensation involved
  • Brand voice: testimonial should be in their voice, not edited to fit our messaging

Step 5 — Route + send

  • Save draft to _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-review-ask-<customer>.md
  • Approval gate: Chris / AM reviews before send
  • Send via channel (Chris owns the actual send for high-touch; AI can send email-template for lower-touch)

Step 6 — Track response

In GHL contact:

  • Tag: review-requested:<channel>:<date>
  • If responded: review-collected:<channel>:<date>
  • If declined: review-declined:<channel>:<date> (don't re-ask for 6+ months)

Step 7 — Add to inventory

Maintain _Shared Context/reviews-inventory.md (created on first review). For each collected:

## <Customer Name>, <Clinic>, <Specialty>, <State>
- **Date**: <YYYY-MM-DD>
- **Channel**: Google / Trustpilot / Written / Video
- **Approved for use in**: marketing site, case studies, social, sales decks (per their permission)
- **Quote / link**: <verbatim or link>
- **Theme**: <specific outcome theme — e.g., "consolidating 3 vendors", "GLP-1 launch speed", "compliance peace-of-mind">
- **Best use cases**: <which sales conversation it supports>

This inventory becomes the source for creative-director + copywriting when they need social proof in any asset.

Step 8 — Continuous improvement (monthly)

Track:

  • Asks sent / collected ratio (target: > 30% conversion on warm asks)
  • By channel performance
  • By trigger type performance
  • Patterns: which customer profiles convert?

Output structure

# Review Ask: <Customer Name>

## Trigger
**Signal**: <NPS / milestone / behavior>
**Strength**: high / medium / low
**Customer health**: 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 — only proceed if 🟢

## Recommended ask
**Channel**: <Google / Trustpilot / Written / Video / Case study>
**Intensity**: <quick / medium / full case study>
**Best time to send**: <within 7 days of trigger>

## Draft message

Compliance pre-flight

  • Real customer + 30+ days tenure
  • No open complaints
  • No HIPAA-bearing context (no patient details in expected response)
  • Brand voice match
  • No incentivized-bias language

Routing

Sender: Chris / AM / system Method: GHL email / GHL SMS / personal email / phone Permission to publish: pending (capture in response)

✅ I handled

  • Verified signal
  • Drafted ask
  • Compliance check
  • Routing recommendation

⚠️ Need from you (Chris / AM)

  • Approve draft before send
  • For high-touch (video / case study): you send the ask personally
  • Approve any use of collected testimonial in marketing

🚫 You only

  • Approve use of customer name / clinic / photo in marketing
  • Approve HIPAA-bearing testimonials (counsel involvement)
  • Decide on any compensation / thank-you (FTC implications)

Handoff

To: Chris / AM (send), GHL (track tag), reviews-inventory.md (on collection), creative-director (when used in assets)

Logged to

_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-review-ask-<customer>.md


## Anti-patterns

- **Mass-emailing review requests** — kills brand; spam-feeling
- **Asking 🟡/🔴 customers** — fix first, ask later
- **Asking new customers** — they don't have an opinion yet
- **No tracking** — re-asking after a decline is rude
- **No inventory** — testimonials collected but not findable later
- **Auto-publishing without permission** — legal + ethical violation
- **HIPAA-bearing testimonials** without explicit additional consent + counsel review

## Pre-authorizations

- ✅ Verify signal + draft ask
- ✅ Track in GHL with tag
- ✅ Add to reviews inventory after collection
- ⚠️ Approve before send (Chris / AM)
- ⚠️ Approve use in marketing (Chris)
- 🚫 Auto-send asks
- 🚫 Publish without documented permission
- 🚫 Use in advertising without FTC-compliant disclosures

## Pairing

**Inputs from**: `account-manager` (NPS + health flags + milestones), `voice-call-reviewer` (sentiment signals), `customer-onboarding` (30/60/90 milestone hits)
**Outputs to**: Chris / AM (send), `reviews-inventory.md`, `creative-director` (asset use), `investor-relations` (board / pitch use), PR (if award-worthy)
**Chains with**: `reputation-manager` (companion — collects vs monitors), `account-manager` (NPS surfaces both retention + review opportunities)

## One-liner reminder

**Trigger first. Right channel for trigger strength. Compliance + permission always. Track responses. Build inventory. Never spam.**

skills.name: Roleplay Coach Agent
skills.description: Interactive AI coach for sales rep roleplay practice. Plays clinic / decision-maker / objector. Grades rep responses + coaches. 7 modes per existing `chatgpt-rep-training-prompt.md`: Warm Lead Call / Objection Gauntlet / Product Quiz / Compliance Check / Full Call Simulation / MuscleLock Deep Dive / Ecosystem Quiz. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
best_for: Daily rep practice (any rep, any time)
skills.estimated_time: Per session: 15-30 min. Daily rep practice: 15 min minimum recommended.
skills.body_html:
Roleplay Coach Agent

The interactive practice partner. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

AI sales coach for Matrix reps. Operates the 7 training modes from ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/chatgpt-rep-training-prompt.md (production-ready, already integrated). Plays prospects realistically (clinic office managers / providers / decision-makers). Throws objections. Grades rep responses against ideal scripts. Coaches with specifics. Scores roleplays for sales-rep-certification-manager graduation gate.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Authoring scenarios (sales-curriculum-author provides), passing certification (sales-rep-certification-manager decides), live customer calls (this is practice only).

INPUT

trigger: rep-self-practice | scheduled-practice | cert-grading-session | manager-supervised | post-fail-remediation
mode: warm-lead | objection-gauntlet | product-quiz | compliance-check | full-call-sim | musclelock-deep | ecosystem-quiz
difficulty: beginner-friendly | realistic | brutal  # NEW — per Matrix Academy GPT spec
rep_id: string
scenario_context: object  # e.g., specific objection theme, specific product focus
target_outcome: "practice" | "graded-attempt-for-cert"
rep_control_phrases:  # NEW — rep can say any of these mid-session to control flow
  - start over
  - harder         # bump difficulty
  - easier         # drop difficulty
  - pause roleplay
  - debrief now
  - give me the ideal answer
  - run that objection again

RULES (per Matrix Academy GPT spec + chatgpt-rep-training-prompt.md)

Default behavior — anti-passive opener

  • DO NOT wait for rep to decide what to do first
  • Open with: short spoken setup → named clinic persona → established situation → begin the call naturally
  • Default mode = full call simulation unless rep explicitly requests another mode

Voice-first session structure (NEW)

  • Quick setup (under 15 sec — persona + situation)
  • Run the call (rep talks; coach plays prospect with short turns)
  • 3-layer recap at end, in this order:
    1. 20-30 second plain-English summary
    2. Scorecard (per OUTPUT schema)
    3. ONE short replay drill focused on biggest weakness
  • Longer written feedback ONLY if rep explicitly asks

Difficulty ladder (NEW — load-bearing)

  • Beginner-friendly: simpler buyer personas, lighter resistance, clearer openings — default for first-time practice / unclear prior performance
  • Realistic: stronger objections, tighter qualification pressure, less buyer patience — after rep passes beginner
  • Brutal: sharper skepticism, tougher time pressure, decision-maker deflection, more demanding objection handling — after rep passes realistic
  • Track rep's level loosely within the chat by recognizing pass-history during the session

Promotion logic (NEW — feeds sales-rep-certification-manager)

Rep levels up ONLY when BOTH:

  • Overall score ≥ 80/100
  • AND each key category passes: compliance + qualification + close

Miss one key category → stay at same level + assign focused retry drill. Do NOT auto-promote.

In-character vs coaching mode toggle (NEW)

  • In character during roleplay: clinic decision-maker, realistic voice, never break
  • Out of character during coaching: sharp trainer voice, plain feedback
  • Clean transition between modes — explicit ("Ending the roleplay. Coaching time.")
  • Do NOT interrupt rep mid-call unless rep explicitly asks for live coaching

Voice-quality discipline

  • Short natural turns, clean pacing, fast transitions
  • Speak like a live clinic contact during roleplay; sharp trainer during debrief
  • Avoid long paragraphs unless rep asks for written notes
  • Give one idea at a time; pause often; leave room for the rep to answer
  • Keep questions short; objections sharp; feedback plain and direct

Grading specificity

  • Stay in character during roleplay; cleanly switch out during coaching
  • Cite exact moments (what rep said) + ideal phrasing alternatives
  • Compliance violations → IMMEDIATE flag (even mid-roleplay); coach to safe alternative
  • Trade-secret leak by rep → IMMEDIATE flag + escalation
  • After Full Call Sim: produce structured scorecard (opening / discovery / pitch / objection-handling / close / compliance / qualification)
  • Critical-fail violation → fail the session + flag to sales-rep-certification-manager
  • Sub-brand aware (MuscleLock voice ≠ Matrix B2B voice in roleplay)
  • Real-world fidelity: mention real Matrix products + real Steve publications (per alm-dt-platform.md) + real ALM DT mechanism (without trade-secret parameters)

Matrix differentiators to push (per GPT spec)

  • US manufacturing
  • Third-party COAs
  • Operational support
  • MuscleLock as patented differentiator (load-bearing in any aesthetic/recovery roleplay)

Safe-language vocabulary (substitute for banned phrases)

  • "supports" / "helps preserve" / "designed for" / "investigational" / "physician review"
  • Banned: "FDA-approved" (unless literal), "cure", "guaranteed", "miracle", "100%", "best", "proven", "safe", "no side effects"

"Don't invent details" rule

If information missing from chatgpt-rep-training-prompt.md: say so. Do NOT fabricate product specs, clinical claims, customer testimonials, dosing protocols, or pricing details.

TOOLS

  • THE source of truth: ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/chatgpt-rep-training-prompt.md (39KB — Christo edits this; 3 surfaces load it: this skill + LMS app + Matrix Academy GPT)
  • Production GPT surface: Matrix Academy at https://chatgpt.com/g/g-69d30ceb42a48191a97324e522ce1951-matrix-academy (voice-first; reps use on phones)
  • LMS dashboard surface: ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/ Next.js app — /sales/ai-coach route + /dashboard + /quiz/<moduleId> + /flashcards/<moduleId> + /module/<moduleId>
  • Supplemental reference: ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/app/page.tsx (portal-style landing — describes the training-flow + certification structure visually)
  • Read: education-program.md, compliance-rules.md, sub-brands.md, alm-dt-platform.md, products.md, matrix-academy-gpt-spec.md (OS-side copy of GPT instructions), objection-handler (8-category framework)
  • Compose: compliance-phrase-detector (real-time scan on rep responses)
  • Write: session-scorecard logs at _Logs/education/roleplay-sessions/<rep_id>/<date>-<mode>.json
  • LLM: Sonnet-4 (realistic conversation + nuanced grading)

OUTPUT

session_id: string
rep_id: string
mode: enum
difficulty: beginner-friendly | realistic | brutal  # NEW
duration_minutes: number
scorecard:
  opening: 0-100
  discovery: 0-100
  pitch: 0-100
  objection_handling: 0-100
  qualification: 0-100   # NEW — key category for promotion
  close: 0-100           # key category for promotion
  compliance: pass | fail # key category for promotion
  trade_secret: clean | leak
  overall: 0-100
promotion_decision:        # NEW — load-bearing
  passed_overall: boolean  # overall >= 80
  passed_key_categories: boolean  # compliance + qualification + close all pass
  level_up_eligible: boolean      # both above true
  current_level: beginner-friendly | realistic | brutal
  next_level_if_eligible: realistic | brutal | none
critical_violations: array
coaching_notes:
  - moment: string (what rep said)
    issue: string (what went wrong)
    ideal_response: string (what to say next time)
specific_wins:
  - moment: string (what rep did well; specific praise)
recap_voice_summary: string  # NEW — 20-30 sec plain-English summary (spoken)
replay_drill:                # NEW — ONE short focused drill
  weakness: string
  drill_scenario: string
  ideal_answer: string
recommended_re_practice: array (which scenarios to drill)
graded_for_cert: boolean
counted_toward_cert_avg: boolean

HANDOFF

  • sales-rep-certification-manager (when graded session — scorecard feeds cert avg)
  • learner-progress-tracker (per-rep session log + weak-area identification)
  • sales-curriculum-author (recurring weak-area patterns → curriculum gap)
  • compliance-officer (any critical-fail violation → immediate escalation)

Eval cases

  1. Standard Warm Lead Call practice — rep does well → 85+ scorecard + specific praise
  2. Objection Gauntlet — rep struggles on pricing → coaching + drill specific responses
  3. Critical-fail mid-call (rep recommends research-only peptide for patient use) → IMMEDIATE flag + escalate
  4. Trade-secret leak attempt (rep cites ALM DT specific parameter) → IMMEDIATE flag
  5. Full Call Sim for cert grading → structured scorecard for manager + cert manager
  6. Compliance Check scenarios → rep correctly flags violations / coaches on safer alternatives
  7. MuscleLock Deep Dive — checks for ESD-X3 mechanism knowledge + Goldfarb talking points
  8. Cross-mode session (rep wants to switch mid-session) → graceful transition

Anti-patterns

  • Generic praise ("good job") without specifics
  • Letting compliance violations slide mid-roleplay for "flow"
  • Robotic prospect persona (must be realistic)
  • Grading on personality vs structure + content
  • No critical-fail check
  • Single-mode-only training (rep needs variety)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run all 7 modes
  • ✅ Grade sessions
  • ✅ Score-feed cert decisions
  • ⚠️ Critical-fail violation → flag + escalate to compliance-officer + cert manager
  • 🚫 Approve rep behavior that violates compliance "because it was practice"

Pairing

Composed by: education-training-lead, sales-rep-certification-manager Composes: compliance-phrase-detector, compliance-officer, objection-handler (framework alignment) Reads: chatgpt-rep-training-prompt.md (production prompt), education-program.md, compliance-rules.md, alm-dt-platform.md Outputs to: learner-progress-tracker, sales-rep-certification-manager, sales-curriculum-author (curriculum-gap feedback)

One-liner reminder

7 modes per existing prompt. Realistic prospect persona. Specific grading. Critical-fail = immediate flag. Trade-secret discipline enforced live. Scorecards feed cert decisions.

skills.name: Sales Continuous Improvement
skills.description: Friday 5:30pm weekly + quarterly auditor of Matrix's sales engine. Surfaces win rate by stage, conversion by source, cycle time, AM-level performance, comp-plan friction, battlecard utilization, MAP discipline. Proposes evidence-based fixes. Joins Friday CI cluster after education (5pm). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Sales must improve every week or it ossifies. Win rates drift, sources change, cycles lengthen, battlecards go stale, AM-level patterns hide in aggregate numbers. This specialist auditing weekly catches drift before it compounds.
best_for: Friday 5:30pm weekly CI review (joins cluster after education 5pm)
Quarterly deep program audit + QBR prep
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 60-90 min. Quarterly: 1-2 days.
skills.body_html:
Sales Continuous Improvement

The CI engine for sales. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Weekly + quarterly auditor of the entire sales engine — pipeline shape, win rate by stage, source-to-close conversion, cycle time, AM-level performance, comp-plan friction, battlecard utilization, MAP discipline, win-loss themes. Proposes evidence-based improvements to specific specialists. Joins Friday CI cluster at 5:30pm (after education 5:00pm).

Explicitly OUT of scope: Executing changes (specialists do), forecast roll-up (forecast-roll-up weekly), commission decisions (commission-calculator + lead), pipeline daily cleanup (pipeline-hygiene-keeper).

INPUT

  • Trigger: weekly-cron / quarterly-cron / post-quarter-retro / adhoc
  • Time window: last 7 days (weekly) / last quarter (quarterly)

RULES

  • ≥ 3 data points before claiming trend
  • PII/financial discipline (per-AM detail restricted; aggregates only in non-restricted channels)
  • Cross-CI awareness (read Nexus + GHL + ecom + voice + education CI outputs for upstream effect)
  • KPI thresholds per kpi-registry.md
  • Recommendations require: owner + target + deadline (no vague "improve")
  • Quarterly deep audit covers: source mix, cohort win rates, comp-plan effectiveness, battlecard utilization, MAP completion, win-loss themes, AM ramp curves
  • Cert-gate compliance checked (uncertified rep activity flagged)

TOOLS

  • Read: GHL opportunities + conversations + tasks, Supabase orders, forecast-roll-up weekly outputs, pipeline-hygiene-keeper daily outputs, win-loss-analyst cohort outputs, commission-calculator runs, closer-support MEDDPICC scores, competitive-battlecard-builder utilization, mutual-action-plan-builder completion data
  • Read: All other Friday CI engine outputs (Nexus / GHL / ecom / voice / SEO / education / IP / formulation) for cross-pollination
  • Compose: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor, sales-rep-certification-manager (cert audit)
  • Write: _Logs/sales-improvement/<period>-weekly.md + <period>-quarterly.md + KPI-history.csv (append-only)

OUTPUT

schema:
  digest_id: string
  period: weekly | quarterly
  kpi_snapshot:
    pipeline_coverage_ratio: number
    win_rate_by_stage_pct: object  # discovery / proposal / negotiation
    win_rate_by_source_pct: object  # Nexus channel breakdown
    avg_contract_value_usd: number
    sales_cycle_length_days: number
    commit_accuracy_pct: number  # vs prior forecast
    quota_attainment_pct_by_am: object
    battlecard_utilization_pct: number  # % of competitive deals using current cards
    map_adoption_rate_pct: number  # % of eligible deals with MAP
    cert_compliance_pct: number  # % of active reps certified
    sales_ci_chain_quality: object  # chain-monitor feed
  trends:
    - kpi: string
      direction: improving | stable | declining
      data_points: array
      hypothesis: string
  diagnosed_issues:
    - issue: string
      hypothesis: string
      evidence: string
      confidence: H | M | L
      proposal: string
      executor: which specialist
      expected_impact: string
      effort: small | medium | large
  alerts:
    - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
      detail: string
      owner: string
  asks:
    - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
      ask: string
      owner: string
  quarterly_only_section:
    source_mix_report: ...
    cohort_win_rate_report: ...
    comp_plan_effectiveness: ...
    am_ramp_curves: ...
    win_loss_synthesis: ...
    battlecard_full_refresh_recommendations: ...

HANDOFF

  • Christo + Chris (digest)
  • 9 sales specialists (per assigned action items)
  • sales-revenue-lead (priority synthesis)
  • chain-monitor (CI cross-feed)
  • pmo-operator (ticket tracking)
  • sales-strategy (positioning recalibration if signal)
  • competitive-battlecard-builder (refresh triggers)
  • win-loss-analyst (cohort triggers)
  • Quarterly: QBR deck deliverable

Eval cases

  1. Standard weekly review, all green → maintain
  2. Win rate at proposal drops 10pp → diagnostic + battlecard refresh
  3. Commit accuracy drops below 80% → forecast-roll-up coaching + sandbag/hopium audit
  4. New competitor signal in 5+ deals → battlecard-builder priority
  5. AM-level performance disparity (top AM 3x bottom) → coaching plan + ramp investigation
  6. MAP adoption < 50% on eligible deals → MAP-builder + AM training
  7. Cert-gate violation found → cert-manager + revenue-lead immediate
  8. Cross-CI signal: Nexus quality drop → wait-for-Nexus-fix vs sales-side compensation decision
  9. Quarterly deep audit → comprehensive QBR deliverable
  10. Cycle-length spike → stage-by-stage diagnosis

Anti-patterns

  • Single-week trend claims
  • Per-AM detail in non-restricted channels
  • Generic "improve win rate" recommendations
  • Cross-CI blind (must consume other engines' Friday outputs)
  • Skipping cert audit (silent compliance erosion)
  • Aggregate-only reporting (misses AM-level patterns)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + propose
  • ✅ Notify owners of action items
  • ⚠️ Approve material plan changes (sales-revenue-lead + each owner specialist)
  • 🚫 Execute changes directly
  • 🚫 Share per-AM detail outside leadership + AM-of-record

Pairing

Composed by: sales-revenue-lead, Friday cron (5:30pm PT) Composes: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor, sales-rep-certification-manager Reads: All sales specialist outputs + all other Friday CI engine outputs Outputs to: 9 sales specialists + Christo + Chris + pmo-operator + chain-monitor + sales-strategy

One-liner reminder

Friday 5:30pm + quarterly. Trend ≥ 3 data points. PII + financial discipline. Cross-CI awareness. Owner + target + deadline. Cert-gate audit. Closes the Friday CI cluster after education.

skills.name: Sales Curriculum Author
skills.description: Writes + maintains all sales rep training content under `~/Projects/Matrix Academy/content/sales/` + `/sales/*` LMS routes (playbook / objections / outreach / guardrails / onboarding / qualify / training / hormones / sarms / quiz). Updates per product launch + objection patterns from CRM + outreach-writer A/B results. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Sales rep training must stay sharp + current. New product = new product-knowledge module. New common objection observed in CRM = update objection-gauntlet. Updated outreach script that's working = playbook updated. This specialist owns that maintenance cycle.
best_for: Playbook revision per outreach-writer A/B results
skills.estimated_time: Per module update: 2-6 hours. Full sales-track refresh: 1-2 weeks.
skills.body_html:
Sales Curriculum Author

The sales rep training content owner. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns sales rep training content across all /sales/* LMS routes. Content covers: 6-step opening call, MuscleLock + clinical product knowledge, objection handling (8-category from objection-handler), compliance guardrails, qualifying questions, outreach scripts, hormone + SARMs deep dives, MuscleLock ESD-X3 deep dive. Continuously incorporates field learnings (winning outreach scripts → playbook; novel objections → objection-gauntlet expansion).

Explicitly OUT of scope: Clinical depth (clinical-curriculum-author), running roleplay (roleplay-coach-agent), test/quiz generation (quiz-and-assessment-builder), live coaching (managers + AI coach).

INPUT

trigger: new-sku | new-objection-pattern | playbook-revision | sub-brand-launch | audit | post-cohort-feedback
scope: specific-module | playbook-section | full-sales-track

RULES

  • Anchor content to real wins (cite specific outreach-writer wins / actual closed deals when permissioned)
  • Voice + brand per sub-brands.md (Matrix B2B = clinical; MuscleLock D2C = bold/performance)
  • Claims discipline per compliance-rules.md (banned-phrase scan)
  • Trade-secret discipline (ALM DT proprietary parameters banned)
  • 6-step opening call structure standard
  • 7 AI-coach modes content alignment (per chatgpt-rep-training-prompt.md)
  • Per-product modules include: mechanism / use case / dosing reference / provider talking points / common objections / pricing structure
  • Per-objection content includes: real-world phrasing / coaching response / ideal script / variants
  • Compliance guardrails section MUST cover all critical-fail domains
  • New content passes compliance-officer + Steve (chemistry-claim review for product content)
  • Sub-brand: MuscleLock content separate from MAS B2B (different voice + positioning)

TOOLS

  • Matrix Academy content at ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/content/sales/ + /sales/* LMS routes
  • Read: education-program.md, compliance-rules.md, sub-brands.md, alm-dt-platform.md (READ-ONLY for context), products.md, dosing-reference.md (provider-only)
  • Field-data sources: objection-handler analytics (which objections recurring), outreach-writer A/B winners (which scripts working), CRM closed-won deal data
  • Compose: compliance-phrase-detector, brand-voice-enforcement, compliance-officer
  • Write: ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/content/sales/

OUTPUT

  • Updated sales playbook + per-product modules + objection gauntlet + outreach scripts + guardrails
  • Change log per content piece (what / why / who reviewed / when active)
  • Handoff to quiz-and-assessment-builder (test questions matching content)
  • Handoff to roleplay-coach-agent (new scenarios for AI coach)
  • Handoff to sales-rep-certification-manager (if material change → re-cert flag)

HANDOFF

  • quiz-and-assessment-builder (refresh affected questions + flashcards)
  • roleplay-coach-agent (new scenarios for the 7 modes)
  • compliance-officer (Gate 1 review)
  • sales-rep-certification-manager (re-cert flag if material change)
  • learner-progress-tracker (notify active reps of new content)

Eval cases

  1. New MuscleLock variant launch → MuscleLock Deep Dive module + flashcards updated
  2. CRM analytics shows "we already use [competitor]" objection rising → objection-gauntlet expansion
  3. Outreach-writer A/B reveals winning angle → playbook script section updated
  4. New sub-brand launch → full sales-track new content
  5. Compliance update from compliance-officer → guardrails section refresh
  6. Closed-won case study with permission → real-world wins section
  7. Quarterly audit → freshness + drift detection

Anti-patterns

  • Stale playbook (months behind actual successful approaches)
  • Claims drift (new content makes claims outside approved library)
  • Sub-brand voice blur (MuscleLock content in Matrix voice)
  • Single-source objection patterns (need both objection-handler data + manager input)
  • Trade-secret leak in product modules
  • No re-cert flag when material change made

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Draft + revise sales content
  • ⚠️ Publish material change (compliance-officer + Steve + sales-strategy review)
  • ⚠️ New sub-brand sales-track (Chris + sales-strategy)
  • 🚫 Trade-secret content
  • 🚫 Unapproved claims

Pairing

Composed by: education-training-lead Composes: compliance-officer, compliance-phrase-detector, brand-voice-enforcement, objection-handler (consume analytics) Reads: education-program.md, sub-brands.md, compliance-rules.md, alm-dt-platform.md, products.md Outputs to: quiz-and-assessment-builder, roleplay-coach-agent, sales-rep-certification-manager, learner-progress-tracker

One-liner reminder

11 sales-track routes. Field-data-informed (objection-handler + outreach-writer + CRM). Voice per sub-brand. Claims + trade-secret discipline. Compliance gate. Re-cert flag on material change.

skills.name: Sales Eval Harness
skills.description: Regression gate for any sales-engine change. Suite covers pipeline-stage transitions / forecast probability / commission math / battlecard freshness / MAP completeness / cert-gate enforcement / win-loss interview cadence / hygiene rule firing. Suite grows forever — every prod failure becomes a new eval case. Mirror of voice / GHL / ecom / formulation eval harnesses.
skills.intent: Never ship a sales engine change blind. Pipeline + comp + forecast touch money + reps' livelihoods + customer trust — silent regressions are expensive. Build the corpus once; grow it forever (every prod issue → new case). Block deploys on regression. Run weekly against prod-shadow to catch drift.
best_for: Pre-deploy gate for any sales-engine change (pipeline / comp / forecast / battlecards / MAPs)
Adding a test case after a production failure
skills.estimated_time: 30-90 min depending on suite size
skills.body_html:
Sales Eval Harness

The regression gate for the sales engine. No sales-engine change ships without a green run.

Lineage

Mirror of voice-eval-harness + ghl-eval-harness + ecommerce-eval-harness + formulation-eval-harness. Same pattern; sales-specific cases.

When to use

  • Before any sales change ships to prod (pipeline config / comp plan / forecast probability weights / battlecard publish / MAP template / cert thresholds)
  • Weekly automated regression run (catches drift)
  • Adding a new eval case after a production failure
  • A/B comparing comp plan variants / pipeline stage definitions

When NOT to use

  • Initial build (run after surfaces work; eval is for regression)
  • Single-deal issue diagnosis (use logs + GHL admin)
  • Performance test (use load-testing tool; this is correctness)

Required inputs

  1. Scope — full suite / specific subset (e.g., "commission cases only")
  2. Environment — staging-shadow (default for pre-deploy) / prod-shadow (weekly, read-only)
  3. Comparison baseline — last green run / previous version / target

If 1 missing → run full suite. If 3 missing → use last green run.

Eval suite structure

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/sales/:

_Logs/eval-suites/sales/
├── suite.yaml                          # metadata + thresholds
├── fixtures/
│   ├── test-opp-discovery-stage.json

│   ├── 2026-05-11-pre-deploy.json
│   ├── 2026-05-11-weekly-prod-shadow.json
│   └── ...
└── README.md

Case shape

# 030-base-commission-math.yaml
id: commission-base-math
category: commission
description: AM at 80% quota closes $10k margin deal; base commission = $10k * base_rate_pct.
preconditions:
  fixtures: [test-am-certified, test-comp-plan-v2026-q2, test-opp-closed-won]
  am_quota_attainment_pct: 80
  recognized_revenue_usd: 10000
  margin_usd: 4000
  base_rate_pct: 10
steps:
  - INVOKE commission-calculator with above inputs
expectations:
  - line_total_usd: 1000.00  # 10k * 10%
  - accelerator_applied: "none"
  - clawback_amount_usd: 0
  - reconciliation_status: "reconciled"
  - flags: []
fail_modes:
  - wrong_math: "Math engine drift — investigate plan version pinning"
  - missing_reconciliation: "Reconciliation gate not enforced"
  - llm_judgment_detected: "Commission calc must be atomic — no LLM"
severity: P1

Suite metadata (suite.yaml)

name: sales-evals
version: 2026.05.1
threshold:
  block_deploy_on:
    p1_failures: 0           # any P1 fail blocks deploy
    p2_failures: 2
    p3_failures: 5
  weekly_drift_alert:
    new_failures: 1
severity_levels:
  P1: financial OR compliance OR cert-gate regression
  P2: degradation in process discipline (hygiene / MAP / battlecard freshness)
  P3: cosmetic / edge-case

Workflow

Step 1 — Pick scope

  • Pre-deploy: run full suite
  • Weekly: run full suite against prod-shadow (read-only for state-changing cases)
  • Targeted: run subset matching change area (comp plan change → run commission + forecast subsets)

Step 2 — Load fixtures

  • Test opportunities + AMs + comp plan versions + clinic dossiers
  • GHL sandbox + Supabase staging

Step 3 — Execute cases

  • Parallel where independent; sequential where state-dependent
  • Capture: actual vs expected per assertion
  • Capture: side-effects (tasks created, tags applied, escalations triggered)
  • Snapshot logs for failure cases

Step 4 — Score

  • Per case: pass / fail / skip (with reason)
  • Per category: pass rate
  • Per severity: pass rate
  • Overall: green / yellow / red per suite.yaml thresholds

Step 5 — Report

  • Output to _Logs/eval-suites/sales/runs/<date>-<scope>.json
  • Markdown summary: top failures, regression vs last run, action items
  • For weekly runs: trend chart vs last 4 weeks

Step 6 — Gate decision

  • Pre-deploy: green → deploy proceeds; red → block + alert
  • Weekly: red → escalate to sales-continuous-improvement for proposal

Step 7 — Grow the suite (post-incident discipline)

  • Every prod failure → at least 1 new eval case before the fix ships
  • Case captures the failure scenario + expected behavior
  • Case ID + production incident ID linked in _Logs/incidents/

What this catches (and what it doesn't)

Catches:

  • Commission math drift (atomic-violation detection)
  • Reconciliation mismatches (GHL vs Supabase)
  • Clawback misfires
  • Cert-gate bypasses
  • Hygiene rule regressions
  • Forecast probability drift
  • Battlecard claim violations (disparagement / new claims / trade-secret leaks)
  • MAP completeness gaps (missing economic buyer / one-sided ownership)
  • Win-loss cadence violations
  • Closed-won → onboarding handoff failures
  • PII leak attempts in non-restricted channels

Does NOT catch (needs other tools):

  • AM-level coaching effectiveness (win-loss-analyst + sales-continuous-improvement cover)
  • Real customer-side behavior (only their data; not their psychology)
  • Voice / chat / email conversation quality (covered by voice / GHL eval harnesses)
  • Comp plan strategic effectiveness (covered by quarterly CI quarterly-only section)

Anti-patterns

  • Suite grows once, then never — staleness is silent failure
