  • Mocking the comp plan (use real plan version pinning)
  • No reconciliation cases (most expensive class of bug; always test)
  • No cert-gate cases (compliance + comp regression risk)
  • Skipping P1 failures "to ship now" — defeats the gate; bypass logged + Chris approval
  • Running against prod customer data (always prod-shadow read-only / sandbox)
  • Single eval run = green forever — flaky tests need flakiness budgets, not retries-until-green

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build + maintain suite
  • ✅ Run against staging-shadow
  • ✅ Run against prod-shadow (read-only)
  • ✅ Block deploys on red runs
  • ⚠️ Bypass gate for emergency hotfix (Chris approval + bypass logged)
  • 🚫 Run state-changing cases against prod customer data

Pairing

Inputs from: All 9 sales specialists + 8 upstream sales specialists (each has eval cases tied to its surface) Outputs to: sales-continuous-improvement (weekly synthesis), devops-deployment (gate signal), code-qa (failure context), sales-revenue-lead (gate decisions) Chains with: voice-eval-harness + ghl-eval-harness + ecommerce-eval-harness + formulation-eval-harness (pattern siblings; cross-pollinate techniques)

One-line reminder

Test corpus grows forever. P1 fails block deploys. Weekly drift on prod-shadow. Every incident → new case. State-changing tests never against real customers. Commission must be atomic — eval enforces.

skills.name: Sales Rep Certification Manager
skills.description: Owns the rep graduation gate — pass/fail decisions per rep + cohort management + activation on graduation + annual re-certification + per-product re-cert. Reps CANNOT work live leads until certified. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: No certification = no live work. This specialist owns the integrity of that gate. A rep slipping through uncertified is a compliance + reputation + revenue disaster. Strict pass/fail by criteria; activation only after manager + AI signoff.
skills.estimated_time: Per rep gate decision: 30-60 min. Cohort kickoff: 1-2 hours.
skills.body_html:
Sales Rep Certification Manager

The graduation gate. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Manages every rep's path from hired → certified → activated. Tracks completion of required components (onboarding module + product knowledge quiz + compliance check + roleplay scorecards + final call simulation + manager signoff). Issues pass/fail per the thresholds in education-program.md. On pass: activates rep (GHL tags, access grants, live-lead eligibility). On fail: routes back to specific weak-area training + sets re-attempt window.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Authoring curriculum (sales-curriculum-author), running roleplay (roleplay-coach-agent), tracking real-time progress UI (learner-progress-tracker — but consumes), HR / hiring (recruiting).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (new-rep-enrolled / completion-attempt / cohort-kickoff / annual-recert / product-mini-recert / quarterly-audit)
    required: true
  rep_id:
    type: string
    required: true (per-rep mode)
  cohort_id:
    type: string
    required: false
validation:
  - Rep exists in HR system + GHL contact record
  - All required components attempted before pass evaluation

RULES

rules:
  difficulty_ladder:  # NEW per Matrix Academy GPT spec
    - level_1: beginner-friendly (simpler personas, lighter resistance, clearer openings)
    - level_2: realistic (stronger objections, tighter qualification pressure, less patience)
    - level_3: brutal (sharper skepticism, time pressure, decision-maker deflection)
    - first_time_default: beginner-friendly
  promotion_logic:  # NEW per Matrix Academy GPT spec
    # Rep levels up ONLY when BOTH conditions met
    - condition_1: overall_score >= 80
    - condition_2_AND: compliance_pass AND qualification_pass AND close_pass
    - if_misses_one_key_category: STAY at same level + assign focused retry drill (do NOT auto-promote)
  graduation_requirements:
    # To graduate (full cert), rep must pass at REALISTIC level minimum
    # Some elite cert tracks require BRUTAL level (e.g., enterprise sales)
    - Sales onboarding module: 100% completion
    - Product knowledge quiz: ≥ 85%
    - Compliance check: 100% pass on graded scenarios (zero violations)
    - Roleplay scorecard average at REALISTIC difficulty (Warm Lead + Objection Gauntlet, 3 sessions): ≥ 80%
    - Full call simulation final at REALISTIC difficulty: ≥ 85% scorecard
    - Promotion logic satisfied (overall + 3 key categories)
    - Manager signoff required (always — no auto-activation)
  critical_fail_zero_tolerance:
    # Per `education-program.md` critical-fail domains
    - Research-only peptide recommendation
    - Cross-state prescribing without licensure
    - Missing monitoring plan on active GH therapy
    - Admin staff making clinical decisions
    - Automated refill without prescriber review
    - ANY violation of these = automatic fail regardless of other scores
  failure_handling:
    - Score < threshold on one component → re-take that component (max 3 attempts in 14-day window)
    - 3 attempts failed → mandatory 1:1 with manager + targeted re-training
    - Critical-fail violation → immediate fail + compliance-officer escalation + remedial training
  activation_on_pass:
    - GHL tag: `rep:certified:<date>`
    - GHL tag: `rep:active`
    - Custom field: certification_date, certification_track, certified_by
    - Remove tag: `rep:training-only`
    - Grant access: live-leads in lead-scoring, voice-agent handoffs, GHL CRM write
    - Notify: rep + manager + Christo
    - Log: `_Logs/education/sales-certifications/<rep_id>-<date>.md`
  annual_recert:
    - 30-day notice + scheduling
    - Compliance scenarios re-test (100% required)
    - Product knowledge quiz (≥ 80% — slightly lower than initial since active rep)
    - Failed annual → suspend live-lead access until retake passed
  per_product_recert:
    - On new SKU launch with material novelty: 10-question mini-quiz, ≥ 85% required
    - 14-day window from product launch announcement
  audit:
    - Quarterly: random sample of certified reps; spot-check competency
    - Annual: full cohort certification audit + threshold review with sales-strategy

TOOLS

  • LMS at ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/ (read learner progress, score data)
  • Supabase (Matrix Academy backend) — read cert data
  • GHL MCP (write rep tags + custom fields + notifications)
  • Read: education-program.md, compliance-rules.md, regulatory-frameworks.md
  • Compose: learner-progress-tracker (current state), compliance-officer (critical-fail escalation)
  • Notify: manager + rep + Christo via email/Slack

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    decision_id: string
    rep_id: string
    decision_type: graduation | annual-recert | product-recert | suspension
    decision: PASS | FAIL | CONDITIONAL | SUSPENDED
    component_scores:
      onboarding_completion: pct
      product_quiz: pct
      compliance_check: pass | fail
      roleplay_avg: pct
      full_call_sim: pct
      manager_signoff: yes | no | pending
    critical_fails_detected: array
    weak_areas_for_retraining: array  # if FAIL or CONDITIONAL
    activation_status:
      activated: boolean
      activation_date: ISO
      tags_applied: array
      access_granted: array
    re_attempt_window:
      eligible_after: ISO
      mandatory_remedial: array
    approver: { manager_name, ai_co_signed: true }
  format: JSON + Markdown signoff doc
  quality_bar:
    - All required components evaluated
    - Critical-fail check explicit
    - Manager signoff captured (timestamp + name)

HANDOFF

  • PASS → activation via GHL workflow → notify manager + rep + Christo
  • FAIL → learner-progress-tracker schedules re-attempt + remedial assignments
  • CRITICAL-FAIL → compliance-officer review + Chris notification + remediation track
  • Cohort-level → education-continuous-improvement (pass-rate trends)

Eval cases

  1. Standard graduation with all components > threshold → PASS + activate
  2. Critical-fail violation in compliance check → AUTO FAIL + escalation
  3. Roleplay avg 78% (just below 80%) → CONDITIONAL → 1 more attempt
  4. Failed product quiz 3 times → manager 1:1 + targeted re-training
  5. Annual re-cert failure → suspend live-lead access
  6. New SKU mini-recert 14-day window missed → re-cert mandatory + access pause
  7. Manager signoff withheld despite scores → CONDITIONAL until resolved
  8. Quarterly audit reveals certified-but-poor-performing rep → flag + retrain
  9. Re-hire (previously certified, left, returning) → expedited path; current-content sweep + delta quiz

Anti-patterns

  • Activating without manager signoff — even with perfect scores
  • Ignoring critical-fail domains because rep "almost got it"
  • More than 3 attempts in window — pattern of failure needs different intervention
  • Manual tag-only activation without LMS verification of completion
  • Skipping annual re-cert for "experienced" reps
  • Per-product re-cert skipped because rep is "good with everything"

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Evaluate cert decisions
  • ✅ Apply GHL activation tags on PASS + manager signoff
  • ✅ Schedule re-attempts
  • ⚠️ Activate against critical-fail (NEVER — escalate to compliance-officer + Chris)
  • ⚠️ Override threshold for special case (Chris + compliance-officer)
  • 🚫 Auto-activate without manager signoff
  • 🚫 Skip critical-fail check

Pairing

Composed by: education-training-lead Composes: learner-progress-tracker, compliance-officer, GHL MCP Reads: education-program.md, Matrix Academy LMS data Outputs to: GHL (activation), rep + manager + Christo (notification), HR (status sync)

One-liner reminder

Pass = thresholds met + critical-fail clean + manager signoff. No live work without all three. Re-cert annually + per-product. Critical-fail = automatic + compliance escalation.

skills.name: Sales Revenue Lead
skills.description: Department lead for Sales Revenue Operations. Distinct from `sales-strategy` (positioning + messaging). This lead is operational: pipeline + forecast + AM coverage + comp + revenue ops. Composes all sales specialists. Owns pipeline coverage ratio, win rate by stage, ACV, sales-cycle length, quota attainment. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
best_for: Monthly quota attainment review + comp run
Cross-team revenue projects (pricing changes, comp plan updates, RFP responses)
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 60-90 min. Monthly: 2-4 hours. Quarterly: 1-2 days.
skills.body_html:
Sales & Revenue Lead

The operational department orchestrator. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Operates Matrix's revenue engine end-to-end after the Nexus lead-gen flow hands off to pipeline. Owns: pipeline coverage ratio (target ≥ 3x quarterly quota), win rate by stage, average contract value (ACV), sales-cycle length, quota attainment per AM. Sequences specialist work, runs the weekly forecast call, signs off on commission runs, surfaces deals at risk, escalates pricing-discount approvals.

Explicitly OUT of scope: positioning + messaging (sales-strategy), individual outreach drafts (outreach-writer), objection responses (objection-handler), customer success after close (customer-success-lead), rep certification (sales-rep-certification-manager).

Composition (the team)

Specialist Owns
closer-support Late-stage deal support — MEDDPICC scoring, multi-thread strategy, mutual-action-plan drafting
competitive-battlecard-builder Per-competitor + per-deal battlecards; quarterly refresh
win-loss-analyst Post-deal interviews; quarterly pattern synthesis
pipeline-hygiene-keeper Daily GHL opportunity sweep — stale / missing-next-step / stuck-stage flags
forecast-roll-up Weekly forecast (commit / best-case / gap-to-quota) per AM + team
commission-calculator ⚛️ Deterministic monthly comp math + clawback flags
mutual-action-plan-builder Buyer/seller MAPs for late-stage complex deals
sales-continuous-improvement Friday 5:30pm weekly + quarterly deep audit
sales-eval-harness Regression gate; suite grows forever
Upstream specialists composed sales-strategy, clinic-research-agent, outreach-writer, objection-handler, proposal-builder, discovery-call-prep, cold-email-sequence, lead-magnet-generator

INPUT

  • Weekly cadence (Friday forecast)
  • Monthly cadence (close + comp run)
  • Quarterly cadence (QBR + plan refresh)
  • Nexus hand-off (new opportunities created)
  • AM escalation (pricing exception, late-stage support needed)

RULES

  • Sales reps must be certified (sales-rep-certification-manager) before being assigned opportunities
  • Every opportunity has next-step + amount + close-date OR pipeline-hygiene-keeper flags it
  • Forecast commits are AM-attested (AM signs, lead approves the roll-up)
  • Discount > 10% off list requires lead approval; > 20% requires Chris
  • PII discipline (contact + financial data per compliance-rules.md)
  • Commission math is deterministic (commission-calculator atomic) — never LLM-judged
  • Cross-CI awareness (consume Friday Nexus + SEO + GHL outputs for upstream signal)

TOOLS

  • All 9 sales specialists above + 8 upstream sales specialists
  • GHL MCP (opportunities + pipelines + tasks read/write)
  • Supabase MCP (orders → ACV + cycle-length analytics)
  • Read: _Shared Context/ghl-config.md, compliance-rules.md, approval-rules.md, kpi-registry.md, matrix-nexus.md, vendors.md
  • Compose: compliance-officer, chain-monitor, analytics-architect, pmo-operator

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_type: weekly-forecast | monthly-close | quarterly-qbr | adhoc | escalation
    kpi_snapshot:
      pipeline_coverage_ratio: number  # target ≥ 3x
      win_rate_by_stage_pct: object    # discovery / proposal / negotiation / close
      avg_contract_value_usd: number
      sales_cycle_length_days: number
      quota_attainment_pct_by_am: object
      forecast_commit_usd: number
      forecast_best_case_usd: number
      gap_to_quota_usd: number
    at_risk_deals:
      - opp_id: string
        am: string
        amount: number
        reason: string
        recommended_action: string
    priorities:
      - priority: string
        owner_specialist: string
        target: string
        deadline: ISO
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        owner: string
    asks:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

  • Christo + Chris (weekly forecast + monthly close + quarterly QBR)
  • AMs (per-deal at-risk lists + assigned actions)
  • customer-success-lead (closed-won handoff via new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md)
  • chain-monitor (chain-quality cross-feed)
  • pmo-operator (ticket tracking)
  • Finance (monthly comp run + revenue recognition)

Eval cases

  1. Weekly forecast, coverage 3.2x, all green → commit roll-up
  2. Coverage drops below 2x → diagnostic + Nexus escalation
  3. AM at 40% quota attainment mid-quarter → coaching plan + closer-support assignment
  4. Discount > 20% requested → escalate to Chris with margin math
  5. Win rate at proposal stage drops 10pp → battlecard refresh + win-loss deep-dive
  6. Closed-won → handoff to customer-success-lead within 24h
  7. Commission clawback flagged → finance + AM notification + approval gate
  8. Quarterly QBR → full kpi + win-loss synthesis + plan adjustments

Anti-patterns

  • Forecasting without AM attestation (lead inflates or deflates without owner sign-off)
  • Letting deals sit without next-step (pipeline-hygiene-keeper exists for this)
  • LLM-judging commission math (always atomic + deterministic)
  • Discounting without margin check (price-margin-check atomic must pass)
  • Closed-won not handed to CS (drops the customer in the gap between teams)
  • Skipping certified-rep gate (every assignment routes through cert status check)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run forecast + monthly close + quarterly QBR
  • ✅ Set quarterly priorities for sales team
  • ✅ Sign off on commission runs (after atomic calc + finance review)
  • ⚠️ Approve discount 10-20% (lead alone)
  • ⚠️ Approve closed-won transition to CS
  • 🚫 Approve discount > 20% (Chris only)
  • 🚫 Change comp plan (Chris + finance + counsel)
  • 🚫 Override certification gate

Pairing

Composed by: company-os-orchestrator, pmo-operator, daily-driver-orchestrator Composes: All 9 sales specialists + 8 upstream sales + compliance-officer + analytics-architect + chain-monitor Reads: ghl-config.md, kpi-registry.md, matrix-nexus.md, approval-rules.md, compliance-rules.md

One-liner reminder

Operate the engine. AM-attested forecast. Coverage ≥ 3x. Every deal has next-step. Cert gate enforced. Commission deterministic. Closed-won → CS within 24h.

skills.name: Sales Strategy
skills.description: The sales department lead. Builds the sales playbook for an offer — discovery questions, qualification criteria, objection library, close path, talk tracks. Orchestrates discovery-call-prep + cold-email-sequence + voice-ai-sales-agent + proposal-builder. For Matrix: clinic-provider B2B motion with 3% rule + FOMU framing.
best_for: Pre-launch sales preparation for a new offer
skills.body_html:
Sales Strategy

The sales lead. Builds the playbook. Downstream sales agents execute against it.

When to use

  • After offer-positioning produces an Offer Brief
  • Before any cold outbound on a new offer
  • Refactoring underperforming sales motion
  • New segment / vertical sales adaptation

When NOT to use

  • Already have a strong playbook (don't redo)
  • Single-tactic optimization (use the specific tactical skill)
  • Single deal close (use discovery-call-prep directly)

Required inputs

  1. Offer Brief — from offer-positioning
  2. ICP — from market-research
  3. Channel mix — voice / email / SMS / social / in-person (from conversation-channel-router)
  4. Sales motion type — self-serve / SDR-led / AE / strategic (Hawkins 4-quadrant)
  5. Volume target — meetings / deals per month
  6. Sales-cycle benchmark — expected days from first touch to close

If 1 missing, ask: "Run offer-positioning first, or paste the Offer Brief."

Workflow

Step 1 — Apply Hawkins 4-quadrant

Complexity × Stakeholder count → sales mode:

  • Low + Solo → self-serve
  • Low + Group → AE
  • High + Solo → AE
  • High + Group → strategic

Document the mode + the implications (lead routing, time per deal, qualification rigor).

Step 2 — Define qualification criteria

Apply the 3% rule (Hawkins). Define:

  • In-window signals: what indicates buying intent NOW (funding event, hire, content engagement, etc.)
  • ICP-match criteria: minimum required (NPI active, clinic stage, geography)
  • Disqualification criteria: explicit drop signals (already a customer, in DNC, not credentialed, etc.)

Step 3 — Build the discovery question tree

Per discovery-call-prep. Prescriptive narrowing (Hawkins: "one path, not a menu"). For each branch, define follow-ups.

Standard tree:

  • Situation question → drill on current state
  • Pain question → quantify the gap
  • Process question → how they evaluate
  • Budget framing → is it a "now" or "later"
  • Authority → who else decides

Step 4 — Build the objection library

For each likely objection, 2-3 sentence rebuttal:

  • "Already have a vendor" → switch-cost framing + wedge
  • "Too expensive" → ROI / value equation
  • "Need to think about it" → specific next step
  • "Bad timing" → cost-of-waiting (FOMU)
  • "Send me info" → email follow-up + book a follow-up call

Pull from _Knowledge Base/06 - Sales & Lead-Gen Agent Patterns.md + Matrix-specific objections from offers.md.

Step 5 — Build the close path

Document the sequence:

  1. First touch (channel + script)
  2. Qualification (when to advance / disqualify)
  3. Discovery (when to schedule, who attends)
  4. Proposal / pricing presentation
  5. Negotiation
  6. Close

For each step:

  • Triggering event
  • Owner (SDR / AE / voice agent / human closer)
  • Artifacts produced
  • SLA (max days before next step)
  • Failure path (when to disqualify and stop)

Step 6 — Build the talk tracks

For each step + channel:

  • Voice (cold open): from voice-script-author
  • Voice (discovery): question tree
  • Voice (objection): rebuttals
  • Voice (close): trial-close + final
  • Email (cold): from cold-email-sequence
  • Email (follow-up): per-stage templates
  • Proposal (deck / one-pager): handoff to proposal-builder (planned)

Step 7 — Volume + capacity math

  • Target meetings/month
  • Connect rate → meetings booked → discovery completed → proposals → closed-won
  • Required dials/sends per day per channel
  • Capacity per sales motion (voice AI: 100-300/day; human SDR: 50-80/day)
  • Cost-per-meeting estimate

Step 8 — KPIs + monitoring

  • Connect rate
  • Meeting-set rate
  • Discovery-to-proposal rate
  • Proposal-to-close rate
  • Avg deal size
  • Avg cycle days
  • Cost per acquisition (CAC)

Step 9 — Output the playbook

Output structure

# Sales Playbook: <Offer Name>

## Quick reference
- **Offer**: <name>
- **ICP**: <one-line>
- **Sales mode**: <self-serve / SDR / AE / strategic> (Hawkins 4-quadrant: <complexity × stakeholders>)
- **Wedge** (FOMU): <one-line>
- **Target**: <N meetings/month → N closes/month → $X MRR/ARR>

## Qualification

### Must-haves (disqualify if absent)
- <criterion>
- <criterion>

### In-window signals (3% rule)
- <signal>
- <signal>

### Auto-disqualify
- <signal>
- <signal>

## Discovery question tree
(Per discovery-call-prep)

## Objection library
| Objection | Rebuttal | Source |
|---|---|---|
| <obj> | <3-sentence rebuttal> | <KB / Offer Brief> |

## Close path
| # | Stage | Owner | SLA | Failure path |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | First touch | <SDR / voice / etc> | <days> | <when to drop> |
| 2 | Qualification | <owner> | <days> | <disqualify reasons> |
| 3 | Discovery scheduled | <owner> | <days> | <reschedule / drop> |
| ... | | | | |

## Channel mix + talk tracks

### Channel 1: <e.g., voice AI outbound>
- Open: <ref to voice-script-author output>
- Discovery: <ref>
- Objection handling: <ref>
- Close: <ref>

### Channel 2: <e.g., email follow-up>
(per channel)

## Volume math
- Target: <N> meetings/month
- Required dials: <N>/day for <N> agents
- Required sends: <N>/day
- Capacity: <feasible / hire / scale>

## KPIs
| Metric | Target | Current | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Connect rate | <%> | TBD | TBD |
| Meeting-set rate | <%> | TBD | TBD |
| Disc-to-proposal | <%> | TBD | TBD |
| Close rate | <%> | TBD | TBD |
| CAC | $<X> | TBD | TBD |

## ✅ I handled
- Playbook draft (qualification + objections + close path + channel talk tracks)

## ⚠️ Need from you
- Approve qualification criteria
- Approve close path SLAs
- Approve channel mix + budget
- Approve volume targets

## 🚫 You only
- Sign vendor contracts (Retell tier, etc.)
- Approve compensation structure for human SDRs / AEs

## Handoff
**To**: `voice-ai-sales-agent` (executes), `cold-email-sequence` (executes), `discovery-call-prep` (per-call prep), `proposal-builder` (planned, for proposal stage), `ghl-crm-architect` (pipeline + tags reflect this playbook)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-sales-playbook-<offer>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Playbook without disqualification criteria — wastes SDR / AE time
  • No volume math — can't measure / scale
  • One-size playbook across offers — each offer is its own playbook
  • Talk tracks without brand voice — every script through brand-guide.md
  • No KPI definitions — can't measure success
  • Multiple CTAs per stage — confuses prospects (Hawkins prescriptive narrowing)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Draft the playbook
  • ✅ Reference offers + market-research
  • ⚠️ Approve playbook before sales motion launches
  • ⚠️ Channel-mix budget (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Launch the sales motion (separate approval per workflow)

Pairing

Inputs from: offer-positioning, market-research, conversation-channel-router Outputs to: voice-ai-sales-agent, cold-email-sequence, discovery-call-prep, ghl-crm-architect, proposal-builder (planned) Chains with: pmo-operator (sales motion as a project), weekly-review (KPI tracking)

One-liner reminder

4-quadrant mode → qualification → discovery tree → objections → close path → channel talk tracks → volume math → KPIs. One offer = one playbook.

skills.name: Security Inspector
skills.description: The OS-level entry point for security audits. Routes to fortress-audit (general repo security) or hipaa-spoon-test-industrial-builder (PHI/ePHI healthcare) based on context. Enforces the audit-template + tiered-consent protocol. Required gate before any production deploy or customer-data launch.
skills.intent: Be the gate that prevents shipping unsafe builds. Detect whether the work involves PHI/ePHI (route to HIPAA Spoon Test) or general repo security (route to Fortress Audit). Run inspection. Output findings via audit-template. Block downstream agents / launches when Critical findings exist.
best_for: Pre-launch gate for any production deploy
Pre-customer-launch gate for any new build
Security review of a refactor / new feature
skills.estimated_time: 30-90 min for quick audit; 2-4 hours for full
skills.body_html:
Security Inspector

The OS-level security gate. Routes to the right deep inspector based on context. Required before any production deploy or customer-facing launch.

When to use

  • Before any production deploy
  • Before any customer-facing launch (new app, new feature, new integration)
  • Before connecting any third-party tool to production data
  • Quarterly health check on existing systems
  • After a major refactor
  • When the user says "is this safe?"

When NOT to use

  • Pre-production / staging dev iteration (use lighter tooling like linters / SAST in CI)
  • Inspection of a single file (read it directly)
  • Compliance audit unrelated to security (different audit skill)

Required inputs

  1. Target — repo path, live URL, or both
  2. Context — what's about to ship / what we're inspecting
  3. Stakes — customer-facing? regulated data? payment data?
  4. PHI/ePHI involvement? — yes / no / unknown
  5. Scope flag — quick (top-risk-only, ≤20 min) or full (default)

If 4 is unknown, ask: "Does this system create, receive, store, display, or transmit any patient identifiers, health, medical, or PHI data?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Triage: which inspector to fire

Decision tree:

PHI / ePHI involved?
├── YES → Route to hipaa-spoon-test-industrial-builder
│   (which is the v3 inspection-first HIPAA builder with the Spoon Test gate)
│
└── NO → Continue with Fortress Audit
    ├── --quick → Top-risk-only (auth, secrets, AI surface, data isolation)
    └── --full → All 10 categories with weighted rubric

Mixed-stack rule: if ANY component touches PHI, treat the whole stack as PHI-bearing and route to HIPAA.

Step 2 — Pre-flight

Apply the 5 operating principles (verbatim from Fortress Audit):

  1. Evidence over assumptions. Every finding cites file:line OR command+output OR observed response.
  2. Mark missing proof UNVERIFIED. No invented evidence.
  3. Never claim the app is unhackable. Banned: "secure", "hardened against", "cannot be". Use relative-risk language.
  4. Highest-risk paths first. Auth → secrets → AI prompts/tool perms → data isolation → admin → file upload → supply chain → CI/CD → infra → breach readiness.
  5. Respect safety rails on live probing. Tier-1 authorization before any live request. Tier-2 per-probe consent for active probes.

Load:

  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md for domain rules
  • _Shared Context/tool-access.md for what the system should have access to
  • Existing audit reports (if any) for diffing

Step 3 — Live probe consent (if applicable)

If the inspection involves a live URL:

  • Emit the Tier-1 Authorization prompt verbatim
  • Wait for valid reply: authorized-staging / authorized-production / dry-run-acknowledged
  • Any other reply → mark all live-dependent checks UNVERIFIED

For each active probe within the run → Tier-2 per-probe consent.

Step 4 — Hand off to deep inspector

For HIPAA path:

→ Routing to hipaa-spoon-test-industrial-builder skill (v3)
   Loads inspection scripts: scripts/hipaa_repo_inspection.sh
   Produces: HIPAA_SPOON_TEST_REPORT.md + 10 mandatory artifacts

For general path:

→ Routing to fortress-audit skill
   Runs multi-agent fan-out across 10 categories
   Produces: weighted 0-100 score + audit report

This skill doesn't re-implement the deep inspectors. It chooses the right one and validates the output against _Templates/audit-template.md.

Step 5 — Validate the deep inspector's output

Check:

  • Audit-template.md format used
  • Findings have evidence (file:line, command+output, OR observed response)
  • Critical findings have remediation + owner + due date
  • No absolute claims in language
  • UNVERIFIED markers where evidence couldn't be gathered
  • Consent log present (if live probes used)
  • Comparison to prior audit (if exists)

If validation fails → loop back to the deep inspector with the gap list.

Step 6 — Apply the launch-gate decision

Based on findings:

Result Decision
0 Critical, 0 High 🟢 OK to launch
0 Critical, >0 High 🟡 OK to launch IF each High has owner + remediation + due date documented
>0 Critical 🔴 BLOCK launch. No exceptions. Remediate and re-inspect.
HIPAA: Spoon Test fail 🔴 BLOCK launch. No exceptions. Run remediation + re-test.
>1 UNVERIFIED in core categories 🟡 BLOCK production launch. OK for staging. Get the evidence then re-decide.

Communicate the gate decision clearly. Don't soften it.

Step 7 — Output the audit summary + gate status

Output structure

# Security Inspection: <Target>

## Routing decision
- **Inspector used**: <fortress-audit | hipaa-spoon-test-industrial-builder>
- **Reason**: <PHI involvement / general audit / etc.>
- **Scope**: <quick | full>

## Pre-flight
- [x] Operating principles loaded
- [x] `compliance-rules.md` loaded
- [x] Tool-access map loaded
- [x] Prior audit diffed (if exists)
- [x] Tier-1 consent obtained (if live probes — log: <status>)

## Audit summary (from deep inspector)
- **Weighted score**: <X> / 100 (if fortress)
- **Tier**: <Strong / Adequate / At-Risk / Critical>
- **Top blocker**: <one-line>

### Critical findings: <N>
### High findings: <N>
### Medium findings: <N>
### Low findings: <N>
### Unverified: <N>

(See full report at `_Logs/security-audits/<date>-<target>.md`.)

## 🚪 Launch gate decision

**Status**: 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 <one-line>

**Reasoning**: <one paragraph>

**If 🔴**:
- BLOCKING items: <numbered list>
- Required next: remediate, then re-run security-inspector
- No downstream agents may produce production-targeting work until this clears

**If 🟡**:
- High findings with owners + due dates: <list>
- Conditions for launch: <list>

**If 🟢**:
- All clear
- Schedule next inspection: <date / trigger>

## Comparison to prior audit (if any)
- Previous date / score: <date> / <score>
- Resolved since: <list>
- New since: <list>
- Trend: <improving / stable / regressing>

## ✅ I handled
- Routed to correct inspector
- Validated output against audit template
- Logged to `_Logs/security-audits/`
- Computed gate decision

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve any exceptions (mark findings as ACCEPTED with reason + date)
- Sign off on launch if 🟡 gate (with each High remediated)
- For HIPAA Spoon Test fail: review the Spoon Test report before reset

## 🚫 You only
- If 🔴 gate: decide whether to delay launch or escalate to counsel
- If HIPAA: sign-off from counsel + clinician before any production launch
- Any legal disclosure decisions (notifying customers, regulators)

## Handoff (use _Templates/handoff-template.md)
**To** (if 🟢 or 🟡-cleared):
- DevOps Deployment Agent (planned)
- Workflow Automation Agent (if CRM go-live)
- Sales / Marketing (if customer-facing launch)

**To** (if 🔴):
- Engineering Lead / specific dev agents — for remediation
- Counsel (if regulated)

**Approval required**: yes — Chris approves any gate transition

## Risks surfaced
- <risk: e.g., dependency X has CVE — needs upgrade before launch>

## Logged to
`_Logs/security-audits/<date>-<target>-security-inspector.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Softening 🔴 gates to make a launch possible → that's how breaches happen
  • Routing PHI work to fortress-audit (the general inspector) — always HIPAA path if PHI present
  • Skipping Tier-1 consent on live probes → respect the safety rail
  • Inventing evidence to clear a finding → never. Mark UNVERIFIED.
  • Validating own output as the deep inspector → this skill is the gate, not the auditor. Always validate via audit-template.
  • No comparison to prior audit — every audit is a snapshot; trend matters

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read all code / configs / infra (read-only inspection)
  • ✅ Run inspection scripts
  • ✅ Compare to prior audits
  • ✅ Generate audit reports
  • ⚠️ Live probes (require Tier-1 + per-probe Tier-2 consent)
  • ⚠️ Mark findings ACCEPTED (Chris approves with reason)
  • 🚫 Modify any code, config, or infra
  • 🚫 Disable security controls for testing
  • 🚫 Override a 🔴 gate

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • The thing being inspected (repo / URL / config)
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md
  • _Shared Context/tool-access.md
  • Prior audits in _Logs/security-audits/

Outputs to:

  • DevOps / Engineering Lead (for remediation)
  • Compliance QA
  • Counsel (for HIPAA / regulated paths)

Wraps:

  • _Skills Library/fortress-audit-extracted/fortress-audit/SKILL.md (general repo audit)
  • _Skills Library/hipaa-spoon-test-industrial-builder-skill/SKILL.md (PHI/ePHI audit)

One-liner reminder

Triage PHI vs general. Route to correct deep inspector. Validate output via audit-template. Apply gate decision. Don't soften 🔴.

skills.name: Seo Strategy Lead
skills.description: Department lead for SEO. Orchestrates the 8-specialist SEO team (local / technical / content / voice / AI search / business-listings / reviews / competitor-monitor) toward Matrix's visibility goal — first page Google for every commercially-relevant query, highest rated business across every directory + AI search engine. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Matrix wants maximum visibility: top 3 Google rankings for commercial queries, top of voice-search results, cited by ChatGPT/Perplexity/Gemini, listed everywhere it's possible to be listed, highest rated everywhere ratings exist. This skill orchestrates the SEO team toward that compound goal — strategic prioritization, cross-specialist coordination, weekly progress tracking.
best_for: SEO crisis (ranking drop / penalty / algorithm shift)
skills.estimated_time: Strategy session: 2-4 hours. Quarterly review: 1 day. Per-priority orchestration: 1-2 hours.
skills.body_html:
SEO Strategy Lead

The orchestrator for Matrix's visibility goal. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

The seo-strategy-lead is the SEO department's quarterback. Sets quarterly priorities per seo-targets.md, dispatches work to the 8 SEO specialists, tracks progress against KPIs (Tier 1 query positions / GBP rating / Yelp rating / Core Web Vitals / business listings / AI citation rate / featured snippets / organic growth), and reports up to Christo + Chris weekly. Does NOT execute SEO tasks directly (specialists do); does NOT make pricing / brand decisions; does NOT touch product copy outside SEO advisory.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Page-by-page implementation (specialists own), product / pricing decisions (their own departments), brand voice changes (brand-voice-enforcement), creative direction (creative-director).

Composition (the SEO team)

Specialist Owns
local-seo-specialist Google Business Profile + local citations + NAP + local-pack ranking
technical-seo-specialist Core Web Vitals + schema markup + indexing + crawl + sitemaps
content-seo-specialist Keyword research + on-page + content-gap + topical authority
voice-search-optimizer Featured snippets + question-pattern content + voice-search optimization
ai-search-optimizer ChatGPT / Perplexity / Gemini / Claude citation visibility
business-listing-manager 30+ free directory registrations + NAP consistency + listing updates
review-acquisition-strategist 5-star reviews across Google / Yelp / Trustpilot / industry sites
competitor-serp-monitor Competitor rank tracking + steal-rank opportunities

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (quarterly-strategy / weekly-review / new-site / ranking-drop / competitor-response / launch-prep / ad-hoc)
    required: true
  scope:
    type: enum (all-matrix / sub-brand-specific / specific-site / specific-query-cluster)
    required: false
    default: all-matrix
  sub_brand:
    type: enum (Matrix / MuscleLock / Peptide Map / ClinIQ / all)
    required: false
  priority_override:
    type: array of priorities to focus on
    required: false
validation:
  - For new-site trigger: site URL provided + content brief available
  - For ranking-drop: which queries dropped + when
  - For competitor-response: competitor name + which queries they ranked for that we want

RULES

rules:
  prioritization:
    - Always anchor work to `seo-targets.md` Tier 1 queries first (P0 commercial intent)
    - Local SEO foundation MUST be in place before national content campaigns
    - Schema + Core Web Vitals are prerequisites; address before content scaling
    - Voice + AI search are amplifiers, not foundations — sequence after Tier 1 commercial intent
  cadence:
    - Weekly review: 30 min status + alerts surfacing
    - Monthly SERP audit (`competitor-serp-monitor`)
    - Quarterly strategy review: full priority refresh + budget allocation
    - Annual: full SEO audit + targets re-baseline
  delegation:
    - Specialists own their domain; lead orchestrates
    - Cross-specialist tasks: lead defines the spec; specialists execute their part
    - When two specialists would conflict (e.g., content vs technical): lead arbitrates with reasoning
  measurement:
    - Every initiative has a baseline + target + measurement window
    - No vague "improve SEO" — always specific (e.g., "Tier 1 query X from page 3 to page 1 in 90 days")
    - Trends > snapshots (≥ 4 weeks data minimum before claiming progress)
  compliance:
    - All on-page content passes `compliance-officer` Gate 1 (claims discipline)
    - Regulated product copy follows `regulatory-frameworks.md` rules
    - No black-hat tactics (cloaking / hidden text / link schemes / fake reviews)
    - Review acquisition follows FTC guidelines (no incentivized reviews; honest experience requests)
  failure:
    - Algorithm update detected → specialists triage; lead synthesizes impact + plan
    - Manual penalty → counsel-loop + SEO lead presents reconsideration request strategy
    - Ranking drop > 5 positions on Tier 1 query → P0 alert + diagnostic chain

TOOLS

tools:
  composed_skills:
    - All 8 SEO specialists (delegate by domain)
    - compliance-officer (Gate 1 review on content claims)
    - analytics-architect (KPI computation)
    - chain-monitor (SEO-related alert surfacing)
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md (targets registry)
    - _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (voice for content)
    - _Shared Context/sub-brands.md (per-sub-brand strategy)
    - _Shared Context/products.md (commerce surface for product SEO)
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (claims discipline)
    - _Logs/seo/* (SEO run history)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/seo/strategy/<YYYY-Q>-quarterly.md (quarterly plans)
    - _Logs/seo/weekly/<YYYY-WW>.md (weekly status)
    - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md (update target list as ranks shift)
  external_apis:
    - Google Search Console (rank + impressions + clicks data)
    - Bing Webmaster Tools
    - Google Analytics 4 / GA4 (organic sessions)
    - PageSpeed Insights / CrUX (Core Web Vitals data)
    - Schema.org Validator
    - SERP-tracking tool when wired (Ahrefs / SEMrush / similar)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch + WebSearch (manual SERP checks + competitor research)
    - gohighlevel (for reviews + customer reach)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (synthesis)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (strategic recommendations + algorithm impact analysis)
    typical_cost_per_run: $0.05-0.50 weekly; $1-3 quarterly

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_id: string
    review_type: weekly | monthly | quarterly | ad-hoc
    period: { from: ISO, to: ISO }
    kpi_snapshot:
      tier_1_top3_pct: number  # % of P0 queries in top 3
      tier_1_top10_pct: number  # % of P0 queries on page 1
      gbp_rating: number
      gbp_review_count: number
      yelp_rating: number
      listings_live_count: number
      listings_nap_consistent_pct: number
      core_web_vitals_pass_pct: number
      ai_citation_rate: number  # % of test prompts where Matrix is cited
      featured_snippets_captured: number
      organic_sessions_4w: number
      organic_sessions_yoy_pct: number
    movement_alerts:
      - alert_type: rank-drop | rank-gain | listing-drop | review-spike | review-drop | new-competitor | algorithm-impact
        severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        query_or_listing: string
        suggested_action: string
        owner: string  # which specialist takes it
    priorities_this_period:
      - priority: string
        owner_specialist: string
        target_outcome: string
        deadline: ISO
        progress: not-started | in-progress | done | blocked
    asks:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown digest
  quality_bar:
    - KPIs sourced from real tools (not estimated)
    - Alerts have specific suggested action + owner
    - Priorities have measurable target + deadline

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - 8 SEO specialists (per priority + alert assignment)
    - chain-monitor (SEO-related alerts feed daily digest)
    - ghl-continuous-improvement Friday (SEO impact on inbound lead-gen)
  payload:
    - Per-specialist task brief
    - Per-alert action item with owner + deadline
  trigger_method:
    - Weekly cron at Friday 4:45pm (after Nexus 4:30 — final CI cluster slot if scheduled)
    - Quarterly review: first Friday of new quarter
    - Ad-hoc via direct invocation
  fallback_routing:
    - P0 ranking drops → emergency triage + Chris notified
    - Manual penalty → counsel-loop + SEO lead pause routine work + crisis response
    - Algorithm update with negative impact → diagnostic + recovery plan
  state_persistence:
    location:
      - _Logs/seo/weekly/<YYYY-WW>.md (status)
      - _Logs/seo/strategy/<YYYY-Q>-quarterly.md (plan)
      - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md (target updates)
    retention: indefinite
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/seo/lead/<run_id>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - tier_1_top3_pct change
      - listings_live_count change
      - reviews_added_count
      - core_web_vitals_pct change
      - cost

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/seo-strategy-lead/:

  1. Quarterly review with all KPIs green → maintain + propose stretch goals
  2. Tier 1 query dropped from #2 → #8 → P0 alert + diagnostic chain to technical-seo + content-seo
  3. New competitor crowding SERP → competitor-response trigger
  4. Algorithm update with mixed impact → specialists triage + lead synthesizes recovery plan
  5. New sub-brand launch (e.g., ClinIQ) → foundation playbook applied
  6. Manual penalty (theoretical) → pause + counsel + reconsideration strategy
  7. AI search citation rate dropping → ai-search-optimizer triggered + content audit
  8. Listing-disappearance from major directory → business-listing-manager + recovery
  9. Review velocity below target → review-acquisition-strategist intervention
  10. Voice-search query landscape shifts (new query patterns) → voice-search-optimizer + content updates

Anti-patterns

  • Doing the work instead of orchestrating — specialists own execution
  • Ignoring local SEO foundation in pursuit of national content authority
  • Vague targets ("rank better") — must be specific
  • Single-quarter quick-fix attempts on long-term plays (content authority needs 12-24 months)
  • Black-hat tactics — bans + manual penalties are existential
  • Fake reviews / incentivized reviews — FTC + platform penalties
  • No measurement window — declaring success too early or too late
  • Algorithm-update overreaction — diagnose carefully before pivoting
  • Specialist-conflict left unresolved — lead must arbitrate

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Orchestrate specialist team
  • ✅ Set quarterly priorities + delegate
  • ✅ Update seo-targets.md per quarterly review
  • ✅ Generate weekly + quarterly reports
  • ⚠️ Budget allocation requests (Chris + cfo-finance)
  • ⚠️ New site / sub-brand SEO foundation kickoff (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Crisis response on penalty / algorithm impact (Chris)
  • 🚫 Black-hat tactics
  • 🚫 Incentivized review schemes

Pairing

Inputs from: Matrix's web properties, Search Console / Bing Webmaster / GA4, all 8 SEO specialists Outputs to: Christo + Chris (weekly + quarterly), chain-monitor (alerts), ghl-continuous-improvement (organic-lead-gen tie-in) Composes: All 8 SEO specialists + compliance-officer + analytics-architect Chains with: nexus-conversion-analyst (organic = a Nexus source), content-director (content strategy alignment), creative-director (brand consistency in SEO assets)

One-liner reminder

Tier 1 queries first. Local foundation before national content. Specific + measurable targets. Specialists execute; lead orchestrates. No black hat. Compliance gate on content. Trends > snapshots.

skills.name: Should I Build An Agent
skills.description: The Phase 0 gate before any agent build. Runs the decision hierarchy from _Knowledge Base/01 to determine if the task is an agent, a workflow, RAG, single LLM call, or "just write the code". Outputs a clear go/no-go with reasoning.
triggers: do I need an agent for
skills.body_html:
Should I Build an Agent?

The gate. Run this BEFORE any agent build. Most projects shouldn't be agents.

This skill operationalizes _Knowledge Base/01 - Should You Build an Agent.md. Read that file for full context.

Purpose

Save hours/weeks by classifying the task at the lowest level of the decision hierarchy that solves it.

When to use

  • User wants to build something "with AI"
  • New agent / chatbot / automation idea on the table
  • Before committing engineering time

When NOT to use

  • The user explicitly said "this is a workflow" / "I just need a script" — respect that
  • The user is debugging an existing agent (use other skills)

Required inputs

  1. Problem statement — one sentence describing what the user wants to solve
  2. Worst-case if it goes wrong — what's the blast radius if the agent does the wrong thing 100x in a row?
  3. Available data / signals — what does the agent get as input?
  4. Reversibility — is the agent's action recoverable?

If 1 is too vague, ask: "What's the one sentence problem this needs to solve?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Walk the decision hierarchy (from file 01)

For each level, ask: "Could this solve the problem?" Stop at the first YES.

Level What it is Test
0 Just write the code Rules deterministic? Inputs typed? No NLU needed?
1 Single LLM call One prompt → one response? No tools? No memory?
2 Single LLM call + RAG Needs grounded knowledge but still one-shot?
3 Workflow with LLMs at branch points Control flow knowable + want determinism?
4 Single-agent tool-use loop Branching dynamic + tools required?
5 Multi-agent Specialist domains + single-agent already tried?

Stopping rule: at the first level that demonstrably works, STOP. Don't jump more than one level above the simplest solution.

Step 2 — Apply the 5-question gate

If you reached Level 4 or 5, validate with file 01's gate:

  1. Does the task require multiple steps with branching decisions?
  2. Does the task require calling tools / acting in the world?
  3. Is the input space too large to script?
  4. Will autonomous actions have reversible, bounded blast radius?
  5. Do you have eval data to know when it's working?

4+ YES → proceed. 3 or fewer → drop a level.

Step 3 — Check the 8 AI problem classes (Bratsis)

If the problem doesn't map to one of these, AI/agent isn't the answer:

  1. Classification, 2. Extraction, 3. Generation, 4. Summarization,
  2. Question answering, 6. Recommendation, 7. Prediction, 8. Conversation

Step 4 — Blast radius check

What's the worst case if the agent does the wrong thing 100x in a row?

  • Recoverable → can be automated
  • Irreversible → use escalate-pattern (agent prepares, human approves)
  • Catastrophic → don't automate; build a workflow with explicit gates

Step 5 — Output a verdict

One of:

  • "Don't build it" + which lower-level approach to use
  • "Build a workflow" + LLM call sites identified
  • "Build a single agent" + tools + decision boundary + eval set
  • "Build multi-agent" + only after single-agent insufficiency proven

Output structure

# Should I Build an Agent? — <one-line problem>

## Decision: <verdict>

## Reasoning

**Hierarchy level reached**: <0-5> — <name>
**Why not lower**: <1 line>
**Why not higher**: <1 line>

## 5-question gate (if Level 4+)

| Q | Y/N | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Branching decisions? | <Y/N> | <note> |
| Tool use required? | <Y/N> | <note> |
| Input space too large? | <Y/N> | <note> |
| Reversible blast radius? | <Y/N> | <note> |
| Eval data exists? | <Y/N> | <note> |

## AI problem class
<which of the 8 classes — or "none, this isn't an AI task">

## Blast radius
<one paragraph: worst case + recoverability>

## Recommended next step

<concrete next action>:
- If "build a workflow" → list LLM call sites
- If "single agent" → invoke `agent-build-plan` skill
- If "don't build" → name the simpler approach
- If "build but escalate" → spec the escalation point

Anti-patterns

  • Jumping to Level 4-5 without testing Levels 0-3 first
  • Skipping the blast-radius check for any agent that takes real-world action
  • Building before the eval harness exists (per cross-cutting principle 2)
  • Calling it an "agent" because that's the trend — be honest about what level you actually need

Common framings that mean "this is a workflow, not an agent"

  • "We want it to call ChatGPT for some of the steps"
  • "It needs to follow this exact process every time"
  • "We can list all the if/then cases"
  • "The user always asks one of 5 things"

Common framings that mean "this is genuinely an agent"

  • "We don't know in advance what tools it will need"
  • "The user describes the goal; the agent figures out the steps"
  • "It needs to react to what it finds in the data"
  • "It runs until it succeeds or hits a limit"

Chain to next skill

If verdict is "build" → agent-build-plan (full spec). If verdict is "workflow" → name the workflow's branch points, no agent skill needed. If verdict is "don't build" → done.

skills.name: Skill Builder
skills.description: Generate a new SKILL.md file from a one-line description. Auto-applies the conventions in _Knowledge Base/16. Asks ONE clarifying question if scope is unclear. Outputs a publication-ready SKILL.md with frontmatter, body anatomy, anti-patterns, pre-auths, pairings. The META-skill that makes every other skill author faster.
skills.intent: Be the canonical generator for new skills. Input: a description + use-case. Output: a complete, conventions-compliant SKILL.md ready to drop into _Skills Library/. Forces the 6-section body anatomy + locked frontmatter. Auto-validates against the file 16 quality bar.
best_for: Converting a one-off process into a reusable skill
skills.body_html:
Skill Builder (META)

The skill that builds skills. Read _Knowledge Base/16 - Skill Authoring Conventions.md first if you haven't.

When to use

  • A repeatable pattern keeps appearing in your work (3+ times) — extract as a skill
  • Authoring the next department's skills (per agent-registry roadmap)
  • Refactoring an existing skill that doesn't match conventions
  • Cloning a successful skill for a new use case (use agent-cloner instead for that)

When NOT to use

  • One-off task (no need to formalize)
  • The "skill" is actually a thematic knowledge file (use a markdown file in _Knowledge Base/)
  • Tweaking an existing skill (just edit it)

Required inputs

  1. One-line description of what the skill does
  2. Trigger phrases the user might say (2-5 verbatim phrases)
  3. Required inputs the skill needs to operate (with one-question fallback if missing)
  4. Output shape (artifact, decision, document, deployment)
  5. Theme from the locked taxonomy: pm-artifacts / discovery / strategy / growth / delivery / comms / safety / audit / content / productivity / tutor / meta / voice-ai / conversation-design / market-intel / offer-strategy / sales / sales-ops / crm-automation / operations / engineering

If 1-2 missing, ask ONE question: "What does this skill do, and what would the user type to invoke it?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Decide: SKILL vs thematic knowledge file

Per file 16:

  • SKILL.md if it produces a specific artifact and is user/orchestrator-invoked
  • Thematic file if it informs planning across many builds

Step 2 — Pick frontmatter shape

  • Lean (Shape A) — user-invoked via triggers (typical for Cowork-style, single-purpose)
  • Rich (Shape B) — catalog-discovered or orchestrated (typical for Company-OS-Skills)

Default for new Company-OS skills: Shape B with full schema (intent / type / theme / best_for / scenarios / estimated_time).

Step 3 — Name the skill

  • kebab-case
  • <verb>-<noun> or <domain>-<role> pattern
  • 2-4 words max
  • Check agent-registry.md for conflicts

Step 4 — Generate the SKILL.md

Apply the 6-section body anatomy verbatim:

---
name: <kebab-case-name>
description: <one-sentence outcome + triggers + use-when>
intent: >-
  <2-3 sentences: what this solves, what tension it resolves, what shape output takes>
type: <workflow | reference | advisor | generator | gate | orchestrator>
theme: <theme from taxonomy>
triggers: [...]
best_for: [...]
scenarios: [...]
estimated_time: "<range>"
---

# <Skill Name>

<One-paragraph framing.>

## When to use
- ...

## When NOT to use
- ...

## Required inputs
1. ...
(Include the one-question fallback if any input is commonly missing.)

## Workflow
### Step 1 — ...
### Step 2 — ...

## Output structure
```markdown
<verbatim output template the agent fills>

Anti-patterns

  • ...

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ ...
  • ⚠️ ...
  • 🚫 ...

Pairing

Inputs from: ... Outputs to: ... Chains with: ...

One-liner reminder

...


### Step 5 — Run the quality bar (file 16's checklist)

Before locking:
- [ ] Frontmatter complete (name, description with triggers, intent for Shape B)
- [ ] Purpose in 2-3 sentences
- [ ] When to use + When NOT to use both present
- [ ] Inputs explicit with required/optional
- [ ] Core rules / Workflow are constraints, not aspirations
- [ ] Workflow 3-7 numbered steps
- [ ] Output template concrete (markdown skeleton or schema)
- [ ] Anti-patterns section present
- [ ] One-question fallback if a required input might be missing
- [ ] Triggers include real user phrasing
- [ ] Theme assigned
- [ ] Cross-referenced from at least one thematic KB file (if applicable)
- [ ] Pre-authorizations split into ✅/⚠️/🚫

### Step 6 — Save + register

1. Save to `_Skills Library/<correct-folder>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`
2. Update `_Shared Context/agent-registry.md` — add row to the right department
3. Update `_Skills Library/README.md` catalog
4. Update orchestrator routing tables if the new skill has a unique trigger pattern
5. Log creation to `_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-new-skill-<name>.md`

## Output structure

```markdown
# Skill Created: <skill-name>

## Spec
**Location**: `_Skills Library/<folder>/<skill-name>/SKILL.md`
**Frontmatter shape**: <Lean / Rich>
**Type**: <type>
**Theme**: <theme>

## SKILL.md content
<paste the full SKILL.md>

## Quality bar
- [x] All 12 checks passed (or list any deferred)

## Registry updates
- ✅ Added to `agent-registry.md` under <department>
- ✅ Added to `_Skills Library/README.md` catalog
- ✅ Updated routing in <orchestrator> with trigger phrases

## Test plan
- Test 1: <invoke with [trigger]> → expect <output shape>
- Test 2: <edge case> → expect <handling>

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-new-skill-<name>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Generating skills without checking agent-registry — produces duplicates
  • Vague descriptions — leads to vague output. Description must contain triggers + use-when.
  • Skipping When-NOT-to-use — produces over-firing skills
  • No output template — produces inconsistent outputs across runs
  • Triggers that are formal task names ("email composition") — must be real user phrasing ("draft an email")
  • No _Logs/ log — breaks audit trail

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Generate SKILL.md content
  • ✅ Save to _Skills Library/
  • ✅ Update agent-registry.md (additive)
  • ✅ Update _Skills Library/README.md (additive)
  • ⚠️ Update orchestrator routing (potentially-breaking — Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Delete or overwrite existing skills (use agent-cloner for variants)

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • User description
  • _Knowledge Base/16 - Skill Authoring Conventions.md (the meta source)
  • _Shared Context/agent-registry.md (conflict check)

Outputs to:

  • New SKILL.md file
  • agent-registry.md (updated)
  • _Skills Library/README.md (updated)
  • Orchestrators (routing additions, with approval)

Chains with:

  • workflow-builder (if the new skill is a multi-step orchestrator, also build a workflow)
  • agent-cloner (if cloning rather than building from scratch)

One-liner reminder

Description + triggers → conventions-compliant SKILL.md. Quality bar before save. Register in 3 places. Never duplicate.

skills.name: Sku Name Validator
skills.description: Atomic micro-agent. Input SKU string. Output pass/fail against Matrix's naming convention regex + duplicate check + retired-name check. Pure deterministic — no LLM call. Composed by `product-catalog-builder`, `product-naming-gate`, `formulation-batch-record`.
skills.intent: Catch malformed SKUs before they hit the catalog. SKU naming is a foundation — drift here cascades into reporting, search, fulfillment, and customer-facing displays. Pure regex + DB lookup, no LLM cost.
skills.body_html:
SKU Name Validator

Pure deterministic. No LLM. No cost. < 100ms.

Matrix SKU convention

<BRAND>-<CATEGORY>-<PRODUCT>-<VARIANT>-<PACKAGING>[-V<N>]

Rules

Rule Detail
All caps [A-Z0-9-]+ only
Separators Dashes (-) only — no spaces, slashes, dots, underscores
Max length 32 chars total
5 segments required Brand / Category / Product / Variant / Packaging
6th segment optional Version suffix -V<N> for reformulations (V2, V3, etc.)
No reused SKUs Once retired, never reused; new variant gets new SKU
Brand prefix valid Must match Matrix brand registry
Category code valid Must match Matrix category taxonomy
Each segment ≥ 2 chars No single-letter segments
Each segment ≤ 8 chars Long codes break labels

Brand prefixes (canonical list)

Code Brand
MAS Matrix Advanced Solutions
ML MuscleLock
PM Peptide Map
CIQ ClinIQ
MUS (reserved for future muscle-focused sub-brand)

Category codes (canonical list — per _Shared Context/products.md)

Code Category
PEP Peptide injectable
HRM Hormone therapy
GLP GLP-1
TOP Topical serum
TDS Transdermal patch / serum
TRO Troche (sublingual/buccal)
PEL Pressed pellet (subcutaneous implant)
CAP Capsule
SUP Supplement (DSHEA)
COS Cosmetic
ANC Ancillary / support supplies

Valid examples

  • MAS-PEP-SEMA-5MG-VIAL ✅
  • MAS-HRM-TEST-200MG-MULTIDOSE ✅
  • ML-SUP-CREATINE-300G-POWDER ✅
  • MAS-TDS-POS720-1ML-DROPPER ✅
  • MAS-PEL-TESTO-100MG-IMPLANT-V2 ✅ (reformulation)
  • MAS-TRO-HRT-50MG-LOZENGE ✅

Invalid examples + reason

  • mas-pep-sema-5mg-vial ❌ (lowercase)
  • MAS PEP SEMA 5MG VIAL ❌ (spaces)
  • MAS_PEP_SEMA_5MG_VIAL ❌ (underscores)
  • MAS-PEP-SEMAGLUTIDE-5MG-VIAL ❌ (>32 chars)
  • MAS-X-SEMA-5MG-VIAL ❌ (single-char category)
  • MAS-PEP-SEMA-5MG ❌ (missing packaging segment)
  • MAS-NEW-SEMA-5MG-VIAL ❌ (invalid category code)
  • MS-PEP-SEMA-5MG-VIAL ❌ (invalid brand prefix)

Deterministic algorithm

1. Strip whitespace
2. Check: matches /^[A-Z0-9-]+$/
3. Check: length ≤ 32
4. Split on '-'
5. Check: 5 segments OR 6 segments (V-suffix)
6. If 6 segments: 6th must match /^V[0-9]+$/
7. Each segment 2-8 chars
8. Segment 1 (brand): in brand-prefix list
9. Segment 2 (category): in category-code list
10. Lookup in active catalog: duplicate exists?
11. Lookup in retired-catalog: previously used?
12. If all pass → valid: true

Per-rule fail produces specific finding in output.

Output examples

Valid SKU

valid: true
reasons: []
suggested_correction: null
duplicate_of: null
retired_at: null

Invalid — lowercase

valid: false
reasons:
  - rule: "All caps required"
    detail: "Input contains lowercase: 'mas-pep-sema-5mg-vial'"
suggested_correction: "MAS-PEP-SEMA-5MG-VIAL"
duplicate_of: null
retired_at: null

Invalid — missing segment

valid: false
reasons:
  - rule: "5 segments required (brand/category/product/variant/packaging)"
    detail: "Only 4 segments found"
suggested_correction: null  # ambiguous which segment to add
duplicate_of: null
retired_at: null

Invalid — duplicate

valid: false
reasons:
  - rule: "SKU already exists in active catalog"
    detail: "MAS-PEP-SEMA-5MG-VIAL is already an active SKU"
suggested_correction: "Use a variant suffix or different variant code"
duplicate_of: "MAS-PEP-SEMA-5MG-VIAL"
retired_at: null

Invalid — retired

valid: false
reasons:
  - rule: "SKU previously used + retired — Matrix never reuses SKUs"
    detail: "Retired 2024-03-15 due to supplier change"
suggested_correction: "Append -V2 for reformulation: MAS-PEP-SEMA-5MG-VIAL-V2"
duplicate_of: null
retired_at: "2024-03-15"

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/micro-agents/sku-name-validator/:

  1. Valid 5-segment ✅
  2. Valid 6-segment with V2 ✅
  3. Lowercase ❌
  4. Spaces in SKU ❌
  5. Underscore separators ❌

  6. 32 chars ❌

  7. < 5 segments ❌

  8. 6 segments ❌

  9. V-suffix malformed (V0, Vx) ❌
  10. Invalid brand prefix ❌
  11. Invalid category code ❌
  12. Single-letter segment ❌
  13. Duplicate of active SKU ❌
  14. Reused retired SKU ❌
  15. Edge case: trailing whitespace (should be stripped + pass) ✅
  16. Edge case: cold-chain SKU with proper packaging code ✅

Composition

This micro-agent is called by:

  • product-catalog-builder — every SKU creation
  • product-naming-gate — Gate 1 of naming-gate (prerequisite to other 3 gates)
  • formulation-batch-record — verify SKU on master batch record
  • inventory-manager — verify SKU on inventory rows
  • ecommerce-backend — input validation on cart-add
  • product-pricing-engine — verify SKU before pricing

Anti-patterns

  • Adding LLM call — defeats the purpose; pure deterministic
  • Output prose instead of structured — orchestrators rely on schema
  • Silently passing invalid SKUs — block early
  • Not checking retired — Matrix never reuses SKUs; reused SKU breaks reporting
  • Hardcoding brand / category lists in this skill — load from products.md registry to stay current
  • Auto-correcting and proceeding — return suggestion, let orchestrator decide

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Validate any SKU passed in
  • ✅ Return structured pass/fail
  • ✅ Check duplicates against active + retired catalog
  • 🚫 Auto-create the SKU (orchestrator's job)
  • 🚫 Reuse retired SKU (never)

Pairing

Composed by: product-catalog-builder, product-naming-gate, formulation-batch-record, inventory-manager, ecommerce-backend, product-pricing-engine Inputs from: Active SKU catalog (DB), retired SKU registry, brand + category lookup tables in products.md Outputs to: Calling orchestrator (which decides next action)

One-liner reminder

Pure deterministic. No LLM. No cost. < 100ms. Structured output. Brand + category from registry. Never reuse retired SKUs. 16 eval cases.

skills.name: Slide Deck Builder
skills.description: Generate visual slide decks with rich components
skills.body_html:
Slide Deck Builder

Generate beautiful, self-contained HTML slide decks with rich visual components. No PowerPoint, no Google Slides, no dependencies. Just open the HTML file in a browser and present.

How It Works

When you say "make a slide deck about [topic]" or "create slides for [presentation]", this skill builds a complete HTML file with fullscreen slides, keyboard navigation, and polished visual components.

Slide Structure

Every deck follows this proven flow:

Slide Purpose Content
1. Title Set the stage Big title, subtitle, your name or brand
2. Setup Frame the problem or context Why this matters, what the audience will learn
3-8. Core Content Deliver the meat One idea per slide, each with a visual component
9. Evidence Prove your point Stats, quotes, case studies, examples
10. Takeaway Land the message Summary, call to action, next steps

Adjust the number of core content slides based on topic complexity. Simple topics: 5-7 total slides. Complex topics: 10-15 slides. Never exceed 15 slides.

Visual Components

Every single slide MUST have a visual component. Text-only slides are not allowed. Choose from:

Card Grid

2-4 cards in a row, each with an icon/emoji, title, and one-line description. Use for: features, benefits, categories.

Comparison Panel

Two columns side by side with a vs divider. Use for: old vs new, tool A vs tool B, before vs after.

Stat Callout

One huge number or percentage with a short label below. Use for: impressive data points, growth numbers, costs.

Step Flow

Horizontal or vertical numbered steps with arrows between them. Use for: processes, tutorials, how-it-works.

Quote Block

Large quotation marks, italic text, attribution below. Use for: testimonials, expert quotes, key statements.

Icon + Label List

Vertical list with emoji or icons on the left, labels on the right. Use for: feature lists, agendas, checklists.

Code Block

Monospace text on a dark card with syntax-style coloring. Use for: code examples, command line, config files.

Timeline

Horizontal line with dots and labels above and below. Use for: history, roadmap, project phases.

Image + Caption

Centered image placeholder with a caption below. Use for: screenshots, diagrams, product photos.

Metric Dashboard

3-4 stat boxes in a row, each with a label and value. Use for: KPIs, performance data, comparisons.

HTML Output

Generate a single self-contained HTML file. No external fonts, CDN links, or images. Everything inline.

Layout: Fullscreen slides (100vw x 100vh), content centered, max-width 900px, 60px padding.

Colors: Background #0a0a0a, text #ffffff, secondary #a0a0a0, cards #1a1a1a, borders #2a2a2a, plus one accent color per deck.

Typography: Headings bold sans-serif 48-64px, body 24-28px, stat numbers 72-96px in accent color, code monospace 18px.

Navigation: Arrow keys to move slides, counter in bottom-right, smooth transitions, optional click-to-advance.

Content Rules

  1. One idea per slide. If you have two points, make two slides
  2. Max 30 words per slide. Slides are visual aids, not documents
  3. Every slide has a visual component. No exceptions. See the list above
  4. Consistent accent color. Pick one accent and use it for all highlights, buttons, and emphasis
  5. No bullet point dumps. If you need bullets, use an Icon + Label List component instead
  6. Big text beats small text. When in doubt, make it bigger
  7. Whitespace is your friend. Crowded slides lose the audience

Accent Color Selection

Pick an accent color based on the topic:

Topic Area Suggested Accent Hex
AI / Tech Electric blue #3B82F6
Business Warm amber #F59E0B
Design Coral pink #F43F5E
Finance Emerald green #10B981
Health Soft teal #14B8A6
Education Royal purple #8B5CF6
Marketing Vibrant orange #F97316
General Clean white accent on dark #E5E5E5

Example Usage

  • "Make a slide deck about how AI agents work"
  • "Create slides for my team meeting on Q1 results"
  • "Build a presentation explaining our new product"
  • "Generate slides for a 5-minute talk on prompt engineering"

Tips for Best Results

  • Tell the skill how many slides you want and it will adjust density
  • Mention your audience so the content level is calibrated
  • Share your brand color and it becomes the accent
  • Ask for speaker notes if you want talking points alongside each slide
  • The HTML file works offline -- no internet needed to present

skills.name: Sms Campaign Builder
skills.description: Build SMS sequences for outbound, nurture, transactional, and reactivation. Companion to cold-email-sequence but tuned for SMS constraints (160 char, TCPA compliance, A2P 10DLC, STOP/HELP). For Matrix: SMS via GoHighLevel, registered short/long codes, compliance-first.
skills.estimated_time: 30-60 min per sequence
skills.body_html:
SMS Campaign Builder

The SMS channel companion. Same disciplines as cold-email-sequence but tighter constraints.

When to use

  • Outbound SMS sequence to consented contacts
  • Reactivation of dormant customers
  • Transactional / reminder sequences (order shipped, appointment reminder)
  • Multi-touch nurture (email → SMS combo)

When NOT to use

  • Cold SMS to non-consented contacts (TCPA exposure — illegal)
  • One-off transactional message (just send it via GHL conversation directly)
  • Email-only campaign (use cold-email-sequence)
  • Voice campaign (use voice-ai-sales-agent)

Required inputs

  1. Use case — nurture / reminder / reactivation / transactional
  2. Audience — verified-consent contact list (must have opted in)
  3. Goal — book a call / drive to web / catalog request / order action
  4. Brand voice (from brand-guide.md)
  5. Sender — Matrix-registered short/long code

If 2 not verified-consent, STOP and refuse. SMS without opt-in is illegal.

SMS-specific constraints (vs email)

Constraint Email SMS
Length Unlimited 160 char (1 segment) / 1600 max
Opt-in CAN-SPAM (post-hoc unsubscribe OK) TCPA (prior express consent required)
Sender ID Email address Short/long code (Matrix-registered via A2P 10DLC)
Reply rate 1-3% cold 30-50% from warm
Time window Anytime 8am-9pm recipient local (TCPA)
STOP / HELP Unsubscribe link Keywords required
Throughput limit Most don't have Carriers throttle ~1 msg/sec/number
Links Friendly Branded short links only (no IP-leakers)
Emojis Sometimes Sparingly (some carriers strip)

Matrix config reference

Always load _Shared Context/ghl-config.md first. It has the 5 registered A2P 10DLC campaigns:

  1. MAS Order Status — transactional (Mixed)
  2. MAS Appointment Reminders — low-volume Mixed
  3. MAS Affiliate Program — Marketing
  4. MAS Cart Recovery — Marketing (1 msg/clinic/30days cap)
  5. MAS Account Notifications — Mixed (low volume)

Plus universal opt-in/out keywords (START / STOP / HELP), SMS custom value IDs (e.g., 0TKZbUzRWjlGlcszIOX2 = sms credentialing_approved), TCPA window per Matrix's CT timezone.

⚠️ A2P status is REGISTRATION PENDING. Brand + campaigns not yet Verified — SMS workflows silently fail until approved (~5 business days from submit). Block live SMS until status flips to Verified.

Workflow

Step 1 — Verify consent

Before any sequence runs:

  • All contacts have sms_consent: true in GHL custom field
  • Consent timestamp documented per contact (consent_date)
  • Source of consent documented (form, checkbox, double-opt-in confirmation)
  • No contacts in DNC

Without verified consent, this skill refuses to build / send.

Step 2 — Apply A2P 10DLC compliance

For US business SMS:

  • Matrix's brand is registered with The Campaign Registry
  • Campaign use case is registered (Lead Gen / Customer Care / Polling / etc.)
  • Throughput limit set per campaign rating
  • Sender code matches the registered campaign

If A2P registration is not active for this use case, STOP.

Step 3 — Design the sequence

Standard cadence templates:

Lead nurture (3-touch, post-opt-in):

  • T+0min: Welcome + value (confirms opt-in, sets expectations)
  • T+24h: Soft value (catalog link, helpful resource)
  • T+72h: Soft ask (book a strategy call)

Appointment reminder (2-touch):

  • T-24h: "Hey Dr. X — reminder of your strategy call tomorrow at 2pm CT. Reply C to confirm or R to reschedule."
  • T-1h: "Heads up — your call is in an hour. Link: [short URL]"

Reactivation (2-touch, gentle):

  • Day 1: "Hey — haven't seen you in a bit. Want a quick refresher on what's new in our catalog?"
  • Day 4: "Last note — if you're not interested in updates anymore, reply STOP. Otherwise we'll send the highlight reel next week."

Order status (transactional, 1-3 touches per order):

  • Order placed: "Got your order #X. Confirmation: [link]. ETA [date]."
  • Shipped: "Order #X shipped. Tracking: [link]"
  • Delivered: "Order #X delivered. Anything off, just reply."

Step 4 — Write each message

Per brand-guide.md + SMS constraints:

  • 1 segment (≤ 160 char) if at all possible
  • Sender identity ("— Matrix" at end if needed)
  • Branded short link (matrix.link/X) not raw URL
  • No exclamation marks (per Matrix brand)
  • Contractions OK (more natural)
  • One clear next step per message
  • STOP / HELP keywords mentioned in first message of a sequence

Step 5 — Set throttle + window

  • TCPA window: 8am-9pm recipient local time (use contact's timezone field)
  • Throughput: per A2P 10DLC rating (typically 1-10 msg/sec)
  • Daily cap per sequence (avoid carrier flagging — typically < 1000/day per long code)
  • Pause on quiet hours: never override TCPA window

Step 6 — Compliance check (via compliance-qa)

Run every message through:

  • Within 160 char (1 segment) — flag if not
  • STOP/HELP keywords mentioned (in first message of sequence)
  • Sender ID present
  • No banned phrases (per brand-guide.md)
  • No medical claims
  • No financial commitments
  • Link is branded (Matrix short link, not raw)
  • Within A2P 10DLC use case scope

Step 7 — Wire in GHL

Via workflow-automation-builder:

  • Sequence triggers on the right event (form submit / tag added / stage moved)
  • Wait steps respect TCPA window
  • Throttle respects A2P cap
  • Replies route appropriately (yes → book call, no → end, STOP → unsubscribe)
  • Unsubscribe handling automatic

Step 8 — Eval before launch

Test against:

  • 5 test contacts (you / team)
  • Verify: message delivers, STOP works, HELP works, no unintended chars (curly quotes break SMS)
  • Verify timing: T+0 fires, T+24h fires at right local time
  • Verify reply routing

Step 9 — Batch approval before each campaign

Per outbound-voice-sales.workflow.md pattern — every campaign requires Chris approval:

  • List size + verified consent status
  • Campaign window + daily cap
  • Throttle settings
  • Auto-pause triggers

Output structure

# SMS Campaign: <name>

## Spec
**Use case**: nurture / reminder / reactivation / transactional
**Audience**: <verified-consent list, count: N>
**Goal**: <book call / drive web / etc.>
**Sender code**: <Matrix-registered short/long code>
**A2P campaign**: <registered use case>

## Sequence

### Touch 1 — T+<offset>
**Message** (<X> char):

Touch 2 — T+

(same format)

Touch 3 — T+

(same format)

Compliance checklist

  • All contacts have sms_consent: true
  • A2P 10DLC active for use case
  • TCPA window enforced (8am-9pm recipient local)
  • STOP/HELP in first message
  • Sender ID in each message
  • All messages ≤ 160 char
  • No banned phrases / medical claims
  • Branded short links only

Throttle + caps

  • Throughput: <N msg/sec>
  • Daily cap: <N msg/day>
  • Quiet hours: enforced per recipient timezone

Reply routing

Keyword / pattern Action
STOP / UNSUBSCRIBE unsubscribe + tag do-not-contact
HELP auto-reply with help text
YES / Y / C confirm action (book call / etc.)
NO / N tag + end sequence
Other free text route to human review queue

Pre-flight test results

  • 5 test contacts received messages
  • STOP unsubscribes correctly
  • Timing respected TCPA window
  • No character encoding issues

✅ I'll handle

  • Build sequence + messages
  • Wire in GHL (via workflow-automation-builder)
  • Pre-flight test
  • Daily monitoring

⚠️ Need from you (Chris)

  • Approve final messages
  • Approve list (verified consent confirmed)
  • Approve campaign launch (per batch)
  • Authorize first 50 sends in monitoring

🚫 You only

  • A2P 10DLC registration / re-registration
  • Any contract / pricing in SMS (transfer to email/voice)
  • Hostile reply handling

Risks surfaced

  • <risk: e.g., if consent timestamps > 12 months old, refresh opt-in before re-engaging>

Logged to

_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-sms-campaign-<name>.md


## Anti-patterns

- **Sending without verified consent** — illegal
- **Multi-segment messages** when 1 would do — costs more + worse UX
- **Raw URLs** in SMS — branded links only
- **Sending outside TCPA window** — automatic violation
- **No STOP keyword** — required by carrier policies
- **Sales pitch in transactional messages** — TCPA exposure (mixed-use)
- **Generic "Hi [first name]"** — feels automated; reference signal/context

## Pre-authorizations

- ✅ Build sequence + messages
- ✅ Wire in GHL (additive — paused state)
- ✅ Run pre-flight tests on internal numbers
- ⚠️ Activate sequence (per campaign approval)
- ⚠️ Add new sequence to existing live customers (Chris approves)
- 🚫 Send to any contact without `sms_consent: true`
- 🚫 Send outside TCPA window
- 🚫 Override STOP / unsubscribe

## Pairing

**Inputs from**: `cold-email-sequence` (cross-channel sequence design), `_Shared Context/brand-guide.md`, `_Shared Context/compliance-rules.md`, GHL consent records
**Outputs to**: `workflow-automation-builder` (GHL deploy), `compliance-qa` (gate), `weekly-review` (KPI tracking)
**Chains with**: `call-handoff-orchestrator` (SMS as a channel target), `account-manager` (SMS for routine touches)

## One-liner reminder

**Verified consent. A2P active. TCPA window. STOP/HELP required. ≤ 160 char. Branded links. Batch approval per campaign. Never override unsubscribe.**

skills.name: Sop Builder
skills.description: Codify repeated processes into Standard Operating Procedures. Each SOP has: trigger, who owns it, step-by-step actions, expected outputs, failure-mode handling, time estimate. Companion to workflow-builder (which codifies multi-agent workflows). SOPs codify human-led or hybrid processes.
best_for: Process repeated 3+ times that needs documentation
Onboarding handoff (recurring process for new hires)
skills.estimated_time: 30-60 min per SOP
skills.body_html:
SOP Builder

The process documenter. SOPs codify human-led / hybrid processes. Companion to workflow-builder (multi-agent workflows).

When to use

  • A process has been done 3+ times and will recur
  • Onboarding new hire needs documented procedures to follow
  • Compliance audit requires documented procedures
  • Pre-handoff to a new owner (process needs to travel)

When NOT to use

  • One-off task (don't formalize the unique)
  • Multi-agent workflow (use workflow-builder — different format)
  • Code-side procedure (use repo CONTRIBUTING.md or runbook)
  • Trivial process < 10 minutes single-step

Required inputs

  1. Process name — clear noun phrase
  2. Trigger — what initiates this process
  3. Owner — who does it (or which agent)
  4. Frequency — daily / weekly / monthly / quarterly / event-triggered
  5. Stakes — what's the cost of doing it wrong

If 2 missing, ask: "What triggers this process — event, schedule, request?"

Standard SOP structure

# SOP: <Process Name>

## Quick reference
- **Trigger**: <event / schedule / request>
- **Owner**: <name / role / agent>
- **Frequency**: <daily / weekly / monthly / event>
- **Avg time**: <minutes>
- **Stakes**: <low / medium / high>

## When to use
- <Condition 1>
- <Condition 2>

## When NOT to use
- <Exception>

## Prerequisites
- [ ] <Required access / tool / approval>
- [ ] <Required info / input>

## Step-by-step

### Step 1: <action>
- **What**: <one-line>
