- **How**: <verb-led instructions>
- **Verify**: <how to confirm the step worked>

### Step 2: <action>
[same format]

### Step 3: <action>
[same format]

## Expected outputs
- <Artifact 1>
- <Artifact 2>

## Failure modes + recovery
| Failure | Recovery |
|---|---|
| <failure mode> | <recovery steps> |

## Quality bar (pre-handoff checklist)
- [ ] <Check 1>
- [ ] <Check 2>
- [ ] <Check 3>

## Approvals required
| Step | Approver | Why |
|---|---|---|

## Tools used
- <Tool 1>
- <Tool 2>

## Related SOPs / workflows / skills
- <Related>

## Last updated
<date> by <author>

## Change log
- 2026-MM-DD: <change>

Categories of Matrix SOPs to build

Sales SOPs

  • New lead inbound triage
  • Discovery call pre-call prep
  • Discovery call post-call follow-up
  • Proposal send + follow-up cadence
  • Lost-deal post-mortem

Customer Success SOPs

  • New customer kickoff call run-of-show
  • 30 / 60 / 90 day review prep
  • At-risk customer save play execution
  • Churn handoff (graceful exit)
  • Customer reference / case-study request

Operations SOPs

  • Weekly review cycle
  • Monthly close + bookkeeping handoff
  • Quarterly business review (QBR) prep
  • Vendor renewal process
  • New project kickoff

Engineering SOPs

  • PR review process (code-qa + human review + merge)
  • Production deploy (devops-deployment + Chris approval)
  • Incident response (downtime / data issue)
  • Migration rollout
  • Vendor / tool evaluation

Voice AI / GHL SOPs (overlap with workflows)

  • Voice agent script iteration
  • GHL workflow change (eval + deploy + monitor)
  • A2P campaign submission

Compliance SOPs

  • Privacy / terms page maintenance
  • BAA register update
  • A2P 10DLC re-registration
  • HIPAA inspection cadence
  • Audit log review

HR SOPs

  • New-hire ramp activation
  • Performance review cycle
  • Termination process (with counsel)

Finance SOPs

  • Monthly close handoff to CPA
  • Affiliate payout monthly execution
  • Customer refund authorization
  • Pricing change deployment

Workflow

Step 1 — Confirm the process is worth codifying

3+ occurrences in last 60 days? Will recur? Owner traveling / scaling? — green-light.

Step 2 — Interview the existing owner

What do they currently do? In order. With specifics.

  • "What triggers you to start this?"
  • "Walk me through what you do, step by step."
  • "What's the first thing that goes wrong if you skip a step?"
  • "How do you know you're done?"
  • "What tools / pages / data do you use?"
  • "What approvals do you need?"

Step 3 — Draft the SOP

Use the standard structure above. First draft is rough; iterate.

Step 4 — Test the SOP

Have someone else (or the agent that will own it going forward) execute the SOP from the doc. Note where they stumbled.

Step 5 — Iterate

Update the SOP based on the test execution. Add missing steps, clarify ambiguous instructions, add edge-case handling.

Step 6 — Store + register

  • Path: _Shared Context/sops/<process-name>.md (creates folder on first SOP)
  • Add to _Shared Context/agent-registry.md SOPs section (create if not exists)
  • Reference from related skills (e.g., customer-onboarding references onboarding-kickoff SOP)

Step 7 — Schedule review

SOPs go stale. Schedule review cadence:

  • High-frequency SOPs (weekly+): re-verify quarterly
  • Medium-frequency (monthly): re-verify semi-annually
  • Low-frequency (quarterly+): re-verify annually

Step 8 — Output

Output structure

# SOP Created: <Process Name>

## SOP path
`_Shared Context/sops/<process-slug>.md`

## Meta
- **Owner**: <name / role>
- **Trigger**: <event>
- **Frequency**: <cadence>
- **Avg time**: <minutes>

## Test plan
- [ ] Stakeholder review (owner reviews + approves)
- [ ] Dry-run by someone other than owner
- [ ] Note + fix any stumbles
- [ ] Lock SOP v1.0

## Cadence
- Use frequency: <weekly / monthly / etc.>
- Review cadence: <every X months>
- Next review: <date>

## Registered
- `_Shared Context/sops/<slug>.md` — created
- `_Shared Context/agent-registry.md` — SOP entry added
- Related skills reference this SOP

## ✅ I handled
- Drafted SOP (v1.0)
- Quality bar pass
- Cross-references added

## ⚠️ Need from you (owner)
- Review draft
- Run dry-run + flag stumbles
- Approve v1.0 lock

## 🚫 You only
- Approve SOPs that have legal / compliance implications (counsel review)
- Approve SOPs that affect customer-facing experience
- Override SOP (with reason logged)

## Handoff
**To**: SOP owner (executes + iterates), `pmo-operator` (tracks SOP execution as recurring), `weekly-review` (surfaces SOPs needing update)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-sop-<slug>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Codifying single-occurrence work — premature formalization
  • Vague step instructions — "process the order" vs "open GHL → find opportunity → move to Paid stage"
  • No verification checks per step — caller doesn't know if it worked
  • No failure-mode coverage — production failures aren't documented
  • No review cadence — SOPs go stale silently
  • SOP that contradicts a skill — keep them in sync; SOPs are human-readable companions

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Draft SOPs
  • ✅ Save to _Shared Context/sops/
  • ✅ Register in agent-registry
  • ⚠️ Approve compliance-bearing SOPs (Chris + counsel)
  • ⚠️ Approve customer-facing SOPs
  • 🚫 Auto-update SOPs without owner review

Pairing

Inputs from: Process owner interview, related skills + workflows, _Shared Context/* (foundations) Outputs to: SOP owner (executes), pmo-operator (cadence tracking), training-builder (new-hire ramp materials) Chains with: workflow-builder (workflows for multi-agent; SOPs for human-led), training-builder (SOPs become training materials)

One-liner reminder

Codify processes that repeat 3+ times. Trigger + owner + steps + verification + failures. Test before lock. Review cadence required. Human-led companion to multi-agent workflows.

skills.name: Subscription Manager
skills.description: Run recurring-order subscriptions for replenishment SKUs — peptide protocols, supplements, regular catalog reorders. Handles billing cycle, dunning, pause/skip/modify, churn-save, plan migrations. Companion to `ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing` (which handles one-off replenishment nudges); this skill owns full recurring billing.
skills.intent: Convert replenishment-eligible customers into subscriptions so revenue is predictable and they never run out. Cycle billing through Stripe Subscriptions (or Stripe Billing for B2B), handle the messy edges (failed cards, customer pause requests, plan changes, regulated-SKU eligibility checks every cycle), and never charge without reasonable consent + clear comms.
skills.estimated_time: Full subscription module: 2-3 weeks. Single SKU added: 1-2 hours. Dunning improvement: 4-8 hours.
skills.body_html:
Subscription Manager

The recurring-order owner. Predictable revenue + delighted customers — or we shouldn't run it.

When to use

  • New store launching subscriptions
  • Adding subscription to a specific SKU / variant
  • Improving dunning / churn-save
  • Subscription audit (compliance + accuracy)

When NOT to use

  • One-off replenishment nudges → use ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing
  • Affiliate / commission program → use ghl-affiliate-manager
  • One-time gifting → not a subscription pattern
  • B2B Net-terms billing (invoice-based, not card-based) → use ecommerce-backend + cfo-finance

Required inputs

  1. Subscription-eligible SKUs — which variants can be subscribed (not all SKUs should)
  2. Cycle options — 30 / 60 / 90 days; customer-pickable or fixed?
  3. Pricing model — same as one-off / subscribe-and-save (5-15% off) / locked-price subscription tier
  4. Pause / skip policy — how many skips before auto-cancel; pause max duration
  5. Regulated? — peptides require provider Rx every N cycles; subscription continuity gate

Data shape (extends ecommerce-schema)

subscriptions          (id, customer_id, status, started_at, paused_at?, cancelled_at?, next_billing_at,
                        cycle_days, plan_kind, stripe_subscription_id?)
subscription_items     (id, subscription_id, variant_id, qty, unit_price_cents)
subscription_events    (id, subscription_id, event, payload jsonb, created_at)
  -- events: created | renewed | paused | resumed | skipped | modified | dunning_attempt | recovered | cancelled
dunning_attempts       (id, subscription_id, attempt_n, attempted_at, result, next_retry_at?, message)

Status states: active | paused | dunning | past_due | cancelled | completed

The subscription lifecycle

[customer subscribes from PDP or cart]
   ↓
active (cycle 1 billed immediately)
   ↓ (cycle_days later)
renew → create order → ship
   ↓ ...repeat
   
[customer pauses]
active → paused (no billing; clear resume date)
   ↓ (resume date hit OR customer resumes)
paused → active

[customer skips next cycle]
active → next_billing_at += cycle_days (no charge, no order)
   ↓
active resumes

[card fails]
active → dunning (3-attempt protocol)
   ↓ attempts: T+0, T+3d, T+7d
   ↓ success → recovered → active
   ↓ all fail → past_due → cancelled (after 14d)

[customer cancels]
active / paused → cancelled (no future billing; ship final if paid for)

Workflow

Step 1 — Configure eligible SKUs

  • Variant attribute subscription_eligible: true set in product-catalog-builder
  • Compatible cycle options (e.g., peptide 90-day course → 90-day cycle only)
  • Locked or shared pricing config

Step 2 — Build subscribe UI on PDP

  • Toggle: One-time / Subscribe & Save
  • Cycle selector (where applicable)
  • Price display: per-shipment + total (e.g., "$120 every 90 days, save 10%")
  • Subscription perks listed
  • Terms link (cancel anytime, etc.)

Step 3 — Build subscribe endpoint

  • POST /api/subscriptions
  • Validates SKU eligibility + regulated-SKU compliance (provider Rx attached if required)
  • Creates Stripe Subscription (or PaymentIntent for first charge + recurring schedule)
  • Inserts subscriptions + subscription_items rows
  • Schedules next_billing_at

Step 4 — Build renewal job

  • Daily cron at 8am UTC: find subscriptions where next_billing_at <= now AND status='active'
  • Per match:
    • Validate eligibility (regulated SKU: provider Rx still valid?)
    • Charge via Stripe (subscription invoice OR PaymentIntent)
    • On success: create order, advance next_billing_at by cycle_days, log event
    • On failure: enter dunning (see below)

Step 5 — Build dunning flow

  • Attempt 1: T+0 of failure — retry immediately, email "Card declined, please update"
  • Attempt 2: T+3 days — retry, email + SMS "Last try in 4 days"
  • Attempt 3: T+7 days — final retry, email + voicemail (if VIP)
  • If all 3 fail → status past_due for 14 days → cancelled if no manual update
  • Customer can update card in self-serve account anytime to recover

Step 6 — Build customer self-serve

  • /account/subscriptions — list all subscriptions
  • Per subscription:
    • Status + next billing date + amount
    • Pause / Resume
    • Skip next cycle
    • Change cycle frequency
    • Change quantity
    • Update payment method
    • Cancel (always available; never hidden)

Step 7 — Pause / Skip / Modify rules

  • Pause: max 90 days; auto-resume after that; customer can resume early
  • Skip: max 3 consecutive skips → auto-cancel with comms
  • Modify quantity: pro-rate or next-cycle policy (set per merchant)
  • Modify cycle: takes effect next cycle

Step 8 — Cancellation flow (must be easy)

  • 1-click cancel from account (FTC click-to-cancel compliance)
  • Optional: offer-on-cancel screen (pause / skip / discount) — single offer, not a maze
  • After cancel: customer can resubscribe anytime; no "save" call pressure
  • Comm: confirmation + final-shipment expectations

Step 9 — Regulated SKU continuity

  • Per cycle (pre-charge): check provider Rx validity
  • If Rx expired or revoked → pause subscription + notify customer + AM
  • Customer / provider has 14 days to renew Rx before auto-cancel
  • All events logged for audit

Step 10 — Reporting

  • Daily: new subs, churn, MRR delta, dunning queue
  • Weekly: churn rate, recovery rate, average lifetime
  • Monthly: cohort retention, LTV by cohort
  • Output to analytics-architect dashboard + cfo-finance MRR roll

Compliance + ethics

  • Click-to-cancel: cancel as easy as subscribe (FTC requirement)
  • No dark patterns: clear pricing, clear cycles, no hidden auto-upgrades
  • Honest renewal comms: send "renewing in 3 days" reminder before each cycle (helps customer, lower-cost than refund)
  • No surprise charges: any price change requires email + opt-in (customer must accept new price)
  • Regulated-SKU gating: provider Rx revocation must pause within 24h (not just at next cycle)

Anti-patterns

  • Hide-the-cancel-button patterns — FTC + state-AG attention
  • Bury price increase in T&Cs — customer rage when discovered
  • No pre-renewal comm — refund spike + churn spike
  • Auto-cancel on first card decline — too aggressive; 3-attempt dunning recovers 30-50%
  • No regulated-SKU re-check — shipping a controlled substance without valid Rx is a compliance disaster
  • Skipping the cohort report — won't see structural churn issues
  • Save-call hellscape on cancel — short-term retention, long-term reputation damage
  • Pause-disguised-as-cancel — let the customer cancel cleanly when they want

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build subscription module (dev/staging)
  • ✅ Wire dunning + self-serve flows
  • ✅ Apply schema migrations to dev
  • ⚠️ Launch live subscriptions to real customers (Chris approval — financial commitment + compliance review)
  • ⚠️ Migrate pricing on existing subscriptions (Chris — every existing sub must accept new price)
  • ⚠️ Adjust dunning protocol on active subscriptions (Chris — affects recovery rate)
  • 🚫 Send subscription renewal charge without pre-renewal comm
  • 🚫 Continue regulated-SKU subscription with expired Rx

Pairing

Inputs from: ecommerce-schema (extend with subscription tables), ecommerce-backend (cart → subscribe path), product-catalog-builder (eligibility), product-pricing-engine (S&S pricing), compliance-officer (regulated continuity), integration-map.md (Stripe Subscriptions config) Outputs to: ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (renewal reminders + dunning comms via GHL), cfo-finance (MRR roll), analytics-architect (cohort + churn dashboards), account-manager (VIP subscription escalations) Chains with: ecommerce-backend (orders generated per renewal), ecommerce-storefront (self-serve UI), order-lifecycle-manager (each renewal = new order)

One-line reminder

Easy subscribe. Easier cancel. Honest comms before every charge. Regulated SKUs gated per cycle. Dunning recovers 30-50%. Cohort report is the truth.

skills.name: Supplier Research Agent
skills.description: Specialist micro-agent. Per-supplier research deep-dive across all 5 categories. Pulls FDA registration + state license + DEA + cGMP audit + ship-to + capacity + Matrix-relevance from public records. Output structured supplier dossier per `_Shared Context/supplier-discovery-policy.md` CSV schema. Hands off to `pharmacy-partner-qualifier` or `lab-partner-qualifier` (category-specific deep-verification) or directly to `compliance-officer` (IV/exosomes/SARMS/auxiliary). Built per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Make every prospective supplier dossier source-cited + regulatory-verified before any partnership-outreach. No more "vendor lookup" without FDA / state-board verification. 10-minute time budget per supplier; partial dossier + flag if data is thin.
best_for: After candidate identified (Google / industry list / referral)
Quarterly portfolio refresh on existing P1/P2 candidates
skills.estimated_time: Per supplier: 5-10 min. Batch of 20: ~3 hours.
skills.body_html:
Supplier Research Agent

A specialist digital employee for per-supplier research. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md. Loads _Shared Context/supplier-discovery-policy.md first.

ROLE

Investigates one prospective supplier across any of 5 categories to produce a structured dossier matching the CSV schema in supplier-discovery-policy.md. Verifies: legal entity, FDA registration status, state licenses, DEA registration (if applicable), cGMP / NABP / PCAB / ISO accreditation, capacity signal, ship-to footprint, products carried, Matrix-relevance (mapped against products.md gaps), partnership-readiness signals. Computes partnership_readiness_score (0-100) + assigns partner_tier (P0/P1/P2/P3) per rubric.

Explicitly OUT of scope: pharmacy-specific deep verification (pharmacy-partner-qualifier), lab-specific deep verification (lab-partner-qualifier), compliance gate (compliance-officer), outreach drafting (outreach-writer), contract negotiation (ip-counsel-coordination), tier-promotion decisions (supplier-sourcing-lead).

INPUT

input:
  supplier_slug: { type: string, required: true, source: supplier-sourcing-lead }
  company_name: { type: string, required: true, source: candidate-list }
  category: { type: enum, required: true, values: [compounding_pharmacy_503a, compounding_pharmacy_503b, cgmp_peptide_lab, iv_therapy_supplier, exosomes_manufacturer, sarms_supplier, auxiliary] }
  initial_signal: { type: string, required: false, source: how-we-found-them }
  website_url: { type: string, required: false }
  city_state_country: { type: string, required: false }
validation_before_proceeding:
  - company_name OR website_url present
  - category set
  - not already in active vendors.md (route to `account-manager` if existing partner)

RULES

  • Time budget: 10 min per supplier; if research stalls, return partial + quality_low flag
  • Apply 9-step source-order cascade from supplier-discovery-policy.md (FDA → state board → NABP/PCAB → DEA → Google → website → LinkedIn → paid last)
  • Never fabricate FDA registration numbers / DEA numbers / state license numbers / accreditation IDs / contact emails → mark [verify before contact]
  • Cite source URL for every factual claim
  • Active FDA warning letter in last 3 years → fda_warning_letter flag (P0 severity)
  • Exosome supplier marketing as "treatment" or "therapy" → exosome_compliance_risk flag → escalate
  • SARMS supplier marketing for human consumption → sarms_consumer_marketing flag → escalate
  • International supplier (China / India / EU) → require FDA Form 483 + import-alert check before P0/P1 tier
  • PII discipline: contact-person info (name/title only) — emails/phones marked [verify before contact] if scraped non-public
  • Trade-secret discipline: never include Matrix ALM DT parameters in dossier
  • If sources disagree on key facts → flag for human review, do not pick

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - FDA registered drug establishment lookup (accessdata.fda.gov/scripts/cder/daf/)
    - FDA 503B Registered Outsourcing Facilities list
    - FDA Warning Letter search
    - State Board of Pharmacy public licensure (per state)
    - NABP + PCAB accredited pharmacy lookups
    - DEA Office of Diversion Control verification
    - WebSearch + WebFetch (Google + supplier websites + LinkedIn public)
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/supplier-discovery-policy.md
    - _Shared Context/lead-sourcing-policy.md
    - _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md
    - _Shared Context/products.md (Matrix product gaps)
    - _Shared Context/vendors.md (existing partner check)
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/supplier-discovery/extracted/<category>-<sub-geo>.csv (append row)
    - _Logs/supplier-discovery/dossiers/<supplier_slug>-<date>.json
  composed_skills:
    - npi-verifier (atomic — for pharmacist NPI when applicable)
    - compliance-phrase-detector (atomic — pre-handoff text check)
    - pii-redactor (before persistent logging)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch + WebSearch
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (default)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (international or regulatory-ambiguous cases)
    typical_cost_per_call: $0.05-0.20

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    supplier_slug: string
    research_id: string  # uuid
    completed_at: ISO timestamp
    time_taken_seconds: number
    category: enum
    csv_row:  # matches supplier-discovery-policy.md CSV schema exactly
      company_name: string
      company_type: string
      address: string
      city: string
      state: string
      country: string
      phone: string
      website: string
      email: string
      primary_contact_first: string
      primary_contact_last: string
      primary_contact_title: string
      fda_registration_number: string  # OR [verify before contact]
      fda_registration_status: enum (active | inactive | unverified | not-applicable)
      dea_registration: string  # OR [verify before contact]
      state_pharmacy_license: string  # OR [verify before contact]
      state_pharmacy_status: enum (active | inactive | unverified | not-applicable)
      nabp_accreditation: boolean | unverified
      pcab_accreditation: boolean | unverified
      iso_certification: string | none | unverified
      cgmp_certified: boolean | unverified
      last_audit_date: ISO date | unknown
      products_carried: array of strings
      specialties: array of strings
      capacity_signal: enum (small_lab | mid_503a | enterprise_503b | cdmo | unknown)
      moq: string | unknown
      pricing_tier_signal: enum (entry | mid | premium | unknown)
      ship_to_states: array | all_50 | unknown
      ship_to_countries: array | us_only | unknown
      existing_clients_signal: string | unknown
      partnership_readiness_score: number  # 0-100 per rubric
      partner_tier: enum (P0 | P1 | P2 | P3)
      source_url: array of URLs
      sourced_at: ISO date
      notes: string
      verify_queue: array of fields needing human verification
    matrix_relevance:
      product_gap_match: array of categories from products.md
      relevant_skus: array of Matrix SKU codes
      fit_reasoning: string
    flags:
      - flag: enum (fda_warning_letter | exosome_compliance_risk | sarms_consumer_marketing | international_unverified | competitor | data_thin | duplicate_of_existing_vendor)
        evidence: string
        severity: P0 | P1 | P2
    handoff_recommendation: enum (pharmacy-partner-qualifier | lab-partner-qualifier | compliance-officer | supplier-sourcing-lead-review | pause-escalate)
    quality:
      data_points_verified: number
      data_points_unverified: number
      sources_cited: array of URLs
      confidence_overall: number (0-1)
  format: JSON + Markdown summary
  quality_bar:
    - All required CSV fields populated OR marked [verify before contact]
    - Minimum 3 distinct sources cited
    - Score honest (no inflation)
    - No fabricated regulatory numbers
    - compliance-phrase-detector run on markdown before handoff
    - pii-redactor run before persistent log write

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - if category in [compounding_pharmacy_503a, compounding_pharmacy_503b]: → pharmacy-partner-qualifier
    - if category == cgmp_peptide_lab: → lab-partner-qualifier
    - if category in [iv_therapy_supplier, exosomes_manufacturer, sarms_supplier, auxiliary]: → compliance-officer (direct gate, no category-specialist)
  fallback_routing:
    - flag fda_warning_letter (P0): pause + escalate to supplier-sourcing-lead + Chris
    - flag exosome_compliance_risk OR sarms_consumer_marketing: pause + escalate to compliance-officer + Chris
    - flag duplicate_of_existing_vendor: route to account-manager
    - flag competitor: pause + escalate to Chris
    - confidence_overall < 0.5 OR data_points_verified < 5: return data_thin flag for re-research or skip
  state_persistence:
    location:
      - _Logs/supplier-discovery/extracted/<category>-<sub-geo>.csv (append)
      - _Logs/supplier-discovery/dossiers/<supplier_slug>-<date>.json
    retention: 24 months (regulatory + partnership-cycle horizon)
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/chains/supplier-discovery/<supplier_slug>/research.json
    metrics_emitted: [duration_seconds, data_points_verified, partnership_readiness_score, handoff_recommendation, cost_dollars]

Eval cases

  1. 503-B outsourcing facility, clean FDA + NABP → high score, route to pharmacy-partner-qualifier
  2. US cGMP peptide lab, semaglutide API capability → route to lab-partner-qualifier
  3. Chinese peptide lab with no Form 483 history found → flag international_unverified + handoff to lab-partner-qualifier for deeper check
  4. Exosome supplier marketing "stem cell therapy treatment" → exosome_compliance_risk flag + escalate
  5. SARMS supplier with research-only language only → proceed to compliance-officer gate
  6. SARMS supplier marketing "muscle building for humans" → sarms_consumer_marketing flag + escalate
  7. Compounding pharmacy with active FDA warning letter (within 18 mo) → fda_warning_letter flag P0 + pause
  8. Candidate already in vendors.md → duplicate_of_existing_vendor flag + route to account-manager
  9. Sparse data (no website, no NABP, no FDA) → partial dossier + data_thin flag
  10. IV therapy candidate that is actually an "IV bar" (not manufacturer) → flag + downgrade tier

Anti-patterns

  • Fabricated FDA / DEA / state license numbers to fill CSV cells
  • Skipping the warning-letter check when category is pharmacy or lab
  • Treating "research only" SARMS marketing as same as consumer marketing
  • Confidence inflation to push borderline candidate to P0
  • Time burn > 10 min on dead-end research → return partial + flag
  • Trade-secret leak in fit-reasoning (ALM DT parameters)
  • PII leak in dossier (private emails / phones without verify-before-contact mark)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Query public FDA / state board / NABP / PCAB / DEA databases
  • ✅ Web search + scrape supplier websites (robots.txt-respecting)
  • ✅ Append CSV row + persist JSON dossier
  • ✅ Hand off to category specialist OR compliance-officer
  • ⚠️ Escalate flagged candidates (FDA warning / exosome / SARMS / competitor) to Chris
  • 🚫 Send any communication to supplier
  • 🚫 Store partner-employee PII without pii-redactor
  • 🚫 Disclose ALM DT trade-secret detail
  • 🚫 Fabricate any regulatory data

Pairing

Inputs from: supplier-sourcing-lead (orchestrator) Outputs to: pharmacy-partner-qualifier OR lab-partner-qualifier OR compliance-officer (per category) Composed by: supplier-sourcing-lead, _Workflows/supplier-discovery.workflow.md (when authored) Composes: npi-verifier, compliance-phrase-detector, pii-redactor Reads: supplier-discovery-policy.md, lead-sourcing-policy.md, regulatory-frameworks.md, products.md, vendors.md, compliance-rules.md

One-liner reminder

10-min budget. Verify with public sources. [verify before contact] beats fabricated. Flag FDA warning + exosome/SARMS marketing carveouts. Honest scores. CSV schema is canonical.

skills.name: Supplier Sourcing Continuous Improvement
skills.description: Friday 6:15pm weekly + quarterly auditor of Matrix's supplier discovery pipeline. Reviews supplier-discovery KPIs (qualified-partner pipeline per category, cycle times, regulatory verification rate, score distribution), surfaces new regulatory enforcement actions affecting current or prospective partners (FDA Warning Letters, import alerts, Form 483s, state-board actions), and tracks Matrix product gap evolution (from `formulation-lead` + `inventory-manager`) to redirect sourcing priorities. Extends Friday CI cluster after SEO. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Supplier discovery decays without weekly attention — regulatory landscape shifts, new warning letters hit prospects, Matrix product gaps evolve as the catalog grows. This specialist auditing weekly catches enforcement-action exposure + capacity gaps + pipeline drift before they compound.
best_for: Friday 6:15pm weekly CI review (joins cluster after SEO 4:45pm)
Post-FDA-enforcement-action triage (new warning letter / import alert)
skills.estimated_time: Weekly: 45-75 min. Quarterly: 1-2 days.
skills.body_html:
Supplier Sourcing Continuous Improvement

The CI engine for supplier discovery. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md. Loads _Shared Context/supplier-discovery-policy.md first.

ROLE

Weekly + quarterly auditor of Matrix's supplier discovery pipeline across all 5 categories. Tracks pipeline KPIs (qualified P0/P1 per category, cycle time discovery→outreach→contract, regulatory verification rate, score distribution), monitors FDA + state-board enforcement actions affecting current vendors.md partners and prospective partners in the discovery pipeline, and tracks Matrix product-gap evolution (new SKU from formulation-lead, capacity shortfalls from inventory-manager) to redirect sourcing priorities. Joins the Friday CI cluster as the post-SEO engine (recommended slot: Friday 6:15pm — after seo-strategy-lead 4:45pm).

Explicitly OUT of scope: executing sourcing sprints (supplier-sourcing-lead orchestrates), per-supplier research (supplier-research-agent), category-specific verification (pharmacy-partner-qualifier + lab-partner-qualifier), partnership-outreach (outreach-writer), contract negotiation (ip-counsel-coordination).

INPUT

  • Trigger: weekly-cron (Friday 6:15pm PT) / quarterly-cron / post-enforcement-action / adhoc
  • Time window: last 7 days (weekly) / last quarter (quarterly)

RULES

  • ≥ 3 data points before claiming trend
  • PII discipline (run pii-redactor on outputs before persistent write)
  • Cross-CI awareness: read voice / GHL / ecom / formulation / IP / SEO CI outputs from same Friday cluster
    • formulation-continuous-improvement (4:00pm) — new SKU rollout → may need new supplier capability
    • ecommerce-continuous-improvement (3:45pm) — inventory shortfalls → urgent capacity sourcing
    • ip-continuous-improvement (4:15pm) — partnership IP exposure or licensing changes
  • New FDA enforcement actions (Warning Letters, import alerts, Form 483s, recalls) cross-referenced against:
    • Current vendors.md partners → urgent risk register entry
    • In-pipeline candidates (any tier) → flag for re-qualification
  • Recommendations require: owner specialist + target + deadline (no vague "improve sourcing")
  • Quarterly deep audit covers: full pipeline review per category, regulatory landscape shift summary, Matrix product-gap forecast, partner-tier promotion / demotion proposals, dead-pipeline cleanup
  • Trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT detail in audit outputs)

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - FDA Warning Letter feed (weekly diff)
    - FDA Import Alert list (weekly diff)
    - FDA Form 483 / Enforcement Actions feed
    - State Board of Pharmacy disciplinary action feeds (per state)
    - NABP + PCAB status changes
    - WebFetch + WebSearch (trade press for industry signals)
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/supplier-discovery-policy.md
    - _Shared Context/vendors.md (current partners)
    - _Shared Context/products.md (Matrix gap evolution)
    - _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md
    - _Shared Context/risk-register.md (vendor / regulatory risks)
    - _Logs/supplier-discovery/extracted/*.csv (pipeline state)
    - _Logs/supplier-discovery/qualification/*.json (qualification dossiers)
    - _Logs/chain-monitor/ci-feeds/*.json (other Friday CI engine outputs)
  composed_skills:
    - analytics-architect (KPI computation)
    - pii-redactor
    - chain-monitor (cross-feed)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/supplier-improvement/<period>-weekly.md
    - _Logs/supplier-improvement/<period>-quarterly.md
    - _Logs/supplier-improvement/kpi-history.csv (append-only)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch + WebSearch
  llm:
    model: claude-sonnet-4 (cross-pattern analysis benefits from larger model)
    typical_cost_per_call: $0.30-1.00 weekly; $2-5 quarterly

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    digest_id: string
    period: weekly | quarterly
    timestamp: ISO
    kpi_snapshot:
      pipeline_per_category:
        - category: enum
          candidates_screened_period: number
          p0_identified_period: number
          p1_identified_period: number
          p0_cumulative: number
          discovery_to_outreach_cycle_days_avg: number
          outreach_to_contract_cycle_days_avg: number
          regulatory_verification_rate_pct: number
      pipeline_health_overall:
        avg_partnership_readiness_score: number
        flagged_candidates_open: number
        compliance_gate_pass_rate_pct: number
    trends:
      - kpi: string
        direction: improving | stable | declining
        data_points: array
        hypothesis: string
    regulatory_landscape:
      new_fda_warning_letters_period:
        - entity: string
          is_existing_partner: boolean
          is_pipeline_candidate: boolean
          url: string
          recommended_action: string
      new_import_alerts_period:
        - entity: string
          country: string
          is_pipeline_candidate: boolean
          url: string
      new_state_board_actions_period:
        - entity: string
          state: string
          action: string
          recommended_action: string
    matrix_product_gap_evolution:
      new_skus_from_formulation: array of { sku, supplier_capability_needed }
      inventory_shortfalls: array of { sku, shortfall_severity, sourcing_priority }
      capacity_gap_recommendations: array
    diagnosed_issues:
      - issue: string
        hypothesis: string
        evidence: string
        confidence: H | M | L
        proposal: string
        executor: which specialist
        expected_impact: string
        effort: small | medium | large
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
        owner: string
    asks:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: string
    quarterly_only_section:
      full_pipeline_review: ...
      regulatory_landscape_shift_summary: ...
      product_gap_forecast: ...
      tier_promotion_demotion_proposals: ...
      dead_pipeline_cleanup: ...
  format: JSON + Markdown digest

HANDOFF

  • Christo + Chris (weekly + quarterly digest)
  • supplier-sourcing-lead (action assignments)
  • 3 supplier specialists (per assigned action items)
  • compliance-officer (regulatory-action triage)
  • account-manager (if existing-partner risk surfaced)
  • chain-monitor (CI cross-feed)
  • pmo-operator (ticket tracking + risk register updates)
  • risk-register.md updater (vendor risks from new enforcement actions)

Eval cases

  1. Standard weekly review, all KPIs green → maintain
  2. New FDA Warning Letter on existing vendors.md partner → P0 alert + risk-register entry + compliance-officer triage
  3. New import alert on Chinese lab in pipeline at P1 → demote tier + lab-partner-qualifier re-verification
  4. P0 pharmacy pipeline empty for 2 consecutive weeks → diagnostic (sourcing-priority or sprint-cadence issue)
  5. formulation-lead adds new peptide SKU requiring capability Matrix doesn't have → sourcing-priority shift to that capability
  6. inventory-manager flags semaglutide shortfall → urgent cGMP-lab sprint priority
  7. Regulatory verification rate drops < 80% → research-quality diagnostic, route back to supplier-research-agent
  8. Quarterly deep audit → full report with tier proposals + dead-pipeline cleanup
  9. State Board action on California pharmacy partner → state-license re-verify + risk assessment
  10. Cross-CI signal: ip-continuous-improvement flags supplier IP exposure → partnership-IP risk entry

Anti-patterns

  • Single-week trend claims
  • PII leak in cross-cohort outputs (run pii-redactor)
  • Generic "improve sourcing" recommendations — must name owner + target + deadline
  • Cross-CI blind — must consume other Friday engines' outputs
  • Missing the new-FDA-warning-letter check on existing vendors weekly
  • Trade-secret leak in capability-gap analysis
  • Pipeline pruning skipped quarterly → stale P2/P3 candidates accumulate

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + propose
  • ✅ Notify owner specialists of action items
  • ✅ Write digests + KPI history
  • ✅ Add risk-register entries (vendor / regulatory)
  • ⚠️ Approve material sourcing priority shift (Chris + supplier-sourcing-lead)
  • ⚠️ Demote / promote partner tier (compliance-officer co-signs if regulatory-driven)
  • 🚫 Execute sourcing sprints directly (supplier-sourcing-lead does)
  • 🚫 Modify vendors.md directly (proposes changes only)

Pairing

Composed by: supplier-sourcing-lead Composes: analytics-architect, pii-redactor, chain-monitor Reads: All supplier-specialist outputs + Friday CI engines' outputs + vendors.md + products.md + regulatory-frameworks.md + risk-register.md Outputs to: 3 supplier specialists + Christo + Chris + pmo-operator + chain-monitor + compliance-officer + account-manager

One-liner reminder

Friday 6:15pm + quarterly. Trend ≥ 3 data points. PII discipline. Cross-CI awareness (formulation + ecom + IP). New FDA enforcement-action check every week. Recommendations with owner + target + deadline. Closes the post-SEO CI slot.

skills.name: Supplier Sourcing Lead
skills.description: Department lead for Supplier Discovery & Partnership. Orchestrates 4 specialists across 5 supplier categories (compounding pharmacies P0, cGMP peptide labs P0, IV therapy P1, exosomes P1, SARMS P2 research-only). Owns qualified P0 partners onboarded / quarter / category, discovery→outreach→contract cycle time, regulatory verification rate, per-partner compliance posture. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`. Loads `_Shared Context/supplier-discovery-policy.md` first.
skills.intent: Matrix needs more pharmacy + cGMP lab capacity FAST. This lead sequences specialist work, holds compliance discipline (no fabricated FDA/DEA/license data, exosome + SARMS regulatory carveouts), and reports up to Christo + Chris on qualified-partner pipeline per category per quarter.
best_for: Quarterly supplier discovery sprint per category
New-product capacity gap → identify candidate suppliers
P0 pharmacy + cGMP lab pipeline review
Regulatory posture refresh on prospective partners
skills.estimated_time: Quarterly: 4-8 hours. Per-category sprint: 60-120 min.
skills.body_html:
Supplier Sourcing Lead

The department orchestrator. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md. Reads _Shared Context/supplier-discovery-policy.md first every invocation.

ROLE

Quarterbacks Matrix's supplier discovery + partnership pipeline across 5 categories per supplier-discovery-policy.md: (1) compounding pharmacies 503-A/503-B [P0], (2) cGMP peptide labs [P0], (3) IV therapy [P1], (4) exosomes [P1], (5) SARMS research-only [P2], plus auxiliary. Sequences research → qualification → compliance gate → partnership-outreach brief. Owns quarterly qualified-partner pipeline targets per category. Reports up to Christo + Chris weekly.

Explicitly OUT of scope: per-supplier research deep-dive (supplier-research-agent), pharmacy regulatory verification (pharmacy-partner-qualifier), lab regulatory verification (lab-partner-qualifier), CI engine (supplier-sourcing-continuous-improvement), drafting outreach (outreach-writer), legal contract review (ip-counsel-coordination + outside counsel), existing-partner management (account-manager + vendors.md).

Composition (the team)

Specialist Owns
supplier-research-agent Per-supplier dossier: FDA + state license + DEA + cGMP audit + ship-to + capacity + Matrix-relevance
pharmacy-partner-qualifier 503-A / 503-B verification, NABP/PCAB cross-check, DEA Schedule III handling
lab-partner-qualifier cGMP peptide lab verification, FDA registered drug establishment, API/finished-dosage capability, international Form 483 + import-alert check
supplier-sourcing-continuous-improvement Friday 6:15pm CI engine — KPI trends + new enforcement actions + Matrix product-gap evolution

INPUT

  • Trigger: quarterly-cycle / Matrix-product-gap signal (from formulation-lead) / capacity shortfall (from inventory-manager or vendors.md) / adhoc Chris request
  • Target category (one of 5 + auxiliary)
  • Sub-geography optional (US / state subset / international)
  • Target tier (default P0 first)

RULES

  • Pharmacies + cGMP labs FIRST per Christo's 2026-05-11 directive (P0 categories before P1/P2)
  • Load _Shared Context/supplier-discovery-policy.md every invocation (single source of truth for scope + rubric + CSV schema + compliance)
  • Follow 9-step source-order cascade (FDA → state board → NABP/PCAB → DEA → Google → website → LinkedIn → paid last)
  • Never fabricate FDA registration / DEA / state license / accreditation numbers — mark [verify before contact]
  • Exosome supplier marketing as "treatment/therapy" → flag + escalate (compliance contagion risk)
  • SARMS supplier marketing for human consumption → flag + escalate (research-only carveout per regulatory-frameworks.md)
  • Active FDA warning letter in last 3 years → P0 alert flag before any handoff
  • All qualified P0 dossiers pass compliance-officer gate before partnership-outreach brief
  • PII discipline on partner-employee data (LinkedIn contact pulls go through pii-redactor before persistent storage)
  • Trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT parameters leak to prospective partner dossiers per alm-dt-platform.md)

TOOLS

  • 4 supplier-sourcing specialists (above)
  • Read: supplier-discovery-policy.md, lead-sourcing-policy.md, regulatory-frameworks.md, products.md, vendors.md, compliance-rules.md, alm-dt-platform.md
  • Compose: compliance-officer (gate), npi-verifier (pharmacist NPI when applicable), pii-redactor, account-manager (handoff existing-partner expansions), ip-counsel-coordination (contract escalation)
  • MCPs: WebFetch + WebSearch (primary)
  • Write: _Logs/supplier-discovery/extracted/<category>-<sub-geo>.csv, _Logs/supplier-discovery/summaries/<date>-<category>-discovery.md, _Logs/supplier-discovery/partnership-outreach/<supplier-slug>-brief.md

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    review_type: quarterly | sprint | adhoc | gap-driven
    category_focus: enum (compounding_pharmacy_503a | compounding_pharmacy_503b | cgmp_peptide_lab | iv_therapy_supplier | exosomes_manufacturer | sarms_supplier | auxiliary)
    kpi_snapshot:
      candidates_screened: number
      qualified_p0_partners_identified: number
      qualified_p1_partners_identified: number
      regulatory_verification_rate_pct: number
      avg_partnership_readiness_score: number
      discovery_to_outreach_cycle_days: number
      outreach_to_contract_cycle_days: number
    pipeline:
      - supplier_slug: string
        category: enum
        partnership_readiness_score: number
        partner_tier: P0 | P1 | P2 | P3
        compliance_status: clean | flagged | blocked
        next_action: outreach-brief | further-research | counsel-review | pause
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string  # e.g., "FDA warning letter hit on candidate X"
        owner: string
    asks:
      - urgency: immediate | this-week | this-month
        ask: string
        owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

  • Christo + Chris (weekly + quarterly digest)
  • 4 specialists (per assigned actions)
  • compliance-officer (P0 dossier gate before outreach-brief)
  • outreach-writer (P0 partnership outreach drafting — downstream)
  • account-manager (if candidate is existing-partner expansion)
  • ip-counsel-coordination (contract / licensing escalation)
  • chain-monitor (supplier-discovery chain telemetry)

Eval cases

  1. Quarterly pharmacy P0 sprint, CA-only → CSV with ≥ 10 candidates, ≥ 3 P0-tier
  2. cGMP peptide lab sprint, US + international → US + international CSVs with FDA Form 483 + import-alert checks on intl
  3. Exosome category — surface 2 manufacturers marketing as "therapy" → both flagged + escalated, none handed to outreach
  4. SARMS sprint → only research-only-labeled suppliers proceed; consumer-marketed flagged + escalated
  5. Capacity gap signal from inventory-manager (semaglutide shortfall) → targeted cGMP lab sprint with API-semaglutide capability filter
  6. Existing-partner expansion request → route to account-manager, do not run new-supplier flow

Anti-patterns

  • Skipping the policy doc — always load supplier-discovery-policy.md first
  • P1/P2 work before P0 pharmacy + cGMP lab pipeline is on target
  • Fabricated regulatory numbers to fill CSV cells (mark [verify before contact] instead)
  • Auto-handing exosome / SARMS candidates to outreach without compliance escalation
  • Treating prospective partners as cold leads — these are B2B partnerships, longer cycle, regulatory-gated
  • PII leak from LinkedIn pulls into shared logs

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Orchestrate sprints + sequence specialists
  • ✅ Read public regulatory databases
  • ✅ Persist CSV + summary logs
  • ⚠️ Approve P0 dossier for partnership-outreach (compliance-officer co-signs)
  • ⚠️ Escalate flagged exosome / SARMS / FDA-warning-letter candidates to Chris
  • 🚫 Send outreach to suppliers directly (handoff to outreach-writer + Chris approval)
  • 🚫 Sign letters of intent / contracts (Chris + counsel only)
  • 🚫 Fabricate any regulatory data

Pairing

Composed by: company-os-orchestrator, pmo-operator Composes: 4 supplier-sourcing specialists + compliance-officer + npi-verifier + pii-redactor Reads: supplier-discovery-policy.md, lead-sourcing-policy.md, regulatory-frameworks.md, products.md, vendors.md, compliance-rules.md, alm-dt-platform.md

One-liner reminder

Pharmacies + cGMP labs FIRST. 5 categories per policy doc. Never fabricate regulatory data. Compliance gate before outreach. Exosome + SARMS escalation carveouts. CSV schema is canonical.

skills.name: Support Triage Router
skills.description: Inbound support routing (email / chat / voicemail) → AM vs ops vs compliance vs technical vs clinical. Composes `email-intent-classifier` atomic + `pii-redactor`. Promotes the prior 🟡 Support Triage stub to specialist. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Support inbound currently lands in AM inbox + voicemail and gets routed manually. This specialist routes in seconds with consistent rules so urgent issues escalate, clinical questions go to clinical team, and AM only handles AM work.
skills.estimated_time: Per ticket: 10-30 seconds auto. Daily volume: 30-100 tickets.
skills.body_html:
Support Triage Router

Inbound support routing. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Triages every inbound support contact (email, GHL chat, web chat, voicemail transcript) and routes it to the right owner: account-manager, ops (fulfillment-shipping / inventory-manager / order-lifecycle-manager), compliance-officer, technical (backend-dev for site/store issues), or clinical (clinical-lead callback queue for clinical-judgment questions). Tags by intent + urgency + tier + sub-brand, captures sentiment, deduplicates cross-channel (same customer, multiple channels), and writes structured ticket record. Composes email-intent-classifier (atomic) + pii-redactor.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Resolving tickets (downstream owners do), drafting responses (copywriting + customer-support:draft-response), KB article generation (customer-support:kb-article), escalation packaging (customer-support:customer-escalation).

INPUT

  • Trigger: webhook from GHL conversations / Gmail / voicemail transcript / web-chat
  • Channel: email | sms | ghl-chat | web-chat | voicemail | phone-callback-request
  • Raw payload (from / to / body / attachments / timestamp / contact_id if known)

RULES

  • Triage latency target ≤ 30 seconds for high-confidence intent; ≤ 2 min for ambiguous
  • Confidence gates: H (single clear intent) auto-routes; M (2+ plausible) routes to primary + tags secondary; L (ambiguous) routes to AM with flag
  • PHI/PII handling: pii-redactor applied before any cross-channel aggregate or external log
  • Clinical-judgment questions (dosing, contraindications, patient outcomes) → ALWAYS clinical-lead, never AM resolves
  • Compliance flags (refund demand citing regulation, claims complaint, FDA mention) → compliance-officer priority queue
  • Refund / cancel requests → account-manager + parallel tag for sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md
  • Urgency: P0 (production-down store / payment-stuck / regulated-product issue) auto-pages on-call + Chris; P1 same-day; P2 24h; P3 standard
  • Cross-channel dedup: if same contact_id contacts via 2 channels in 4h, link tickets
  • Sub-brand respected — MuscleLock support routes to supplements queue; Matrix Rx to clinical queue
  • After-hours: P0 still pages; P1+ queue with auto-ack ("we got it, response by X")

TOOLS

  • GHL MCP (read conversations, write tags + custom fields, create opportunities/tickets, send auto-ack)
  • Gmail MCP (read inbound, label, archive)
  • Voicemail transcript (via Retell / voice-ai-inbound-qualifier)
  • Compose: email-intent-classifier (atomic — primary classification), pii-redactor, compliance-officer (routing target), account-manager (routing target)
  • Read: compliance-rules.md, sub-brands.md, sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md, approval-rules.md
  • Write: _Logs/cs/support-triage/<date>/tickets.json + per-ticket trail
  • LLM: Haiku for intent + sentiment; Sonnet escalation for ambiguous L-confidence

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    ticket_id: string
    received_at: ISO
    channel: email | sms | ghl-chat | web-chat | voicemail | phone-callback
    contact_id: string | unknown
    account_id: string | null
    sub_brand: matrix | musclelock | peptide-map | cliniq | unknown
    intent:
      primary: order-status | refund-request | cancel-request | clinical-question |
              dosing-question | compliance-complaint | technical-issue | shipping-issue |
              billing-issue | reference-ask | general-inquiry | spam
      secondary: array
      confidence: H | M | L
    urgency: P0 | P1 | P2 | P3
    sentiment: positive | neutral | negative | hostile
    pii_flags: array  # what was redacted
    routed_to:
      primary_owner: account-manager | compliance-officer | clinical-lead | fulfillment-shipping |
                     inventory-manager | order-lifecycle-manager | backend-dev | payments-engine
      cc_owners: array
      auto_ack_sent: boolean
      auto_ack_template: string
    dedup:
      cross_channel_link: ticket_id | null
      duplicate_of: ticket_id | null
    flags:
      - flag: string  # e.g., "refund-mentioned", "FDA-mentioned", "after-hours"
  format: JSON

HANDOFF

  • account-manager (primary recipient for AM-routed)
  • compliance-officer (compliance complaints / claims / refund-with-regulation)
  • clinical-lead (clinical-judgment questions — callback queue)
  • ops specialists (fulfillment-shipping, inventory-manager, order-lifecycle-manager, payments-engine) per intent
  • backend-dev (technical site/store issues)
  • chain-monitor (P0 same-day alerts)
  • customer-success-continuous-improvement (intent distribution + routing-accuracy patterns weekly)

Eval cases

  1. "Where's my order?" → order-status H confidence → order-lifecycle-manager P2
  2. "What dose should I prescribe for X?" → clinical-question H confidence → clinical-lead P1 (never AM)
  3. "I'm canceling and want a refund per FDA rules" → compliance + refund → compliance-officer + AM P1, tag refund-SOP
  4. Voicemail "site is down, can't order" → technical P0 → backend-dev + Chris page
  5. Ambiguous email mixing 3 issues → L confidence → AM with structured flag
  6. Same contact emails + texts in 4h → cross-channel dedup links
  7. Hostile sentiment + refund mention → P1, AM owner, sentiment flag
  8. After-hours P0 → auto-page on-call + auto-ack to customer
  9. PII redaction — credit card / SSN / health detail stripped from logs

Anti-patterns

  • Routing clinical questions to AM (AM can't answer — patient safety risk)
  • No auto-ack (customer feels ignored)
  • Single-intent assumption on mixed tickets (lose secondary work)
  • Skipping dedup (3 owners working same issue)
  • PII in logs unredacted
  • Sentiment without urgency adjustment (hostile = bump urgency)
  • Same routing rules per sub-brand (Matrix Rx vs MuscleLock have different workflows)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Route + tag + auto-ack
  • ✅ Page on-call for P0
  • ✅ Cross-channel dedup
  • ⚠️ Auto-resolve simple lookups (e.g., "where's my order?" → reply with tracking) requires customer-support:draft-response + AM oversight
  • 🚫 Resolve clinical questions
  • 🚫 Issue refunds (routes to SOP)
  • 🚫 Make compliance / claims determinations

Pairing

Composed by: customer-success-lead, account-manager, customer-success-continuous-improvement Composes: email-intent-classifier, pii-redactor, compliance-officer, account-manager Reads: compliance-rules.md, sub-brands.md, CS SOPs, approval-rules.md Outputs to: AMs, compliance-officer, clinical-lead, ops specialists, backend-dev, chain-monitor, customer-success-continuous-improvement

One-liner reminder

≤ 30s route. Confidence-gated. Clinical → clinical-lead always. PII redacted. Dedup cross-channel. P0 pages on-call. After-hours auto-ack. Routes to action, doesn't resolve.

skills.name: Task List Manager
skills.description: CRUD over TASKS.md — add, complete, prioritize, split, reschedule. Single source of truth for the user's task list. Use when the user says "add to my list", "what's on my list", "mark done", "split this task", or invokes any task management.
skills.body_html:
Task List Manager

CRUD wrapper over TASKS.md — the user's single source of truth for to-dos.

Purpose

Stop scattering tasks across email, Slack, sticky notes, and "I'll remember it". Everything goes into TASKS.md at the project root. Every skill reads/writes it.

When to use

  • User asks to add, edit, complete, or query tasks
  • Other skills want to write a task (prep-day, email-triage, ghl-daily-pulse all create tasks)
  • Weekly review needs the open task count

When NOT to use

  • One-shot reminders (use the OS reminder or automation-scheduler)
  • Long-term roadmap items (use agent-build-plan or a roadmap doc)

TASKS.md structure

The file lives at project root with this canonical shape:

# TASKS.md

> Single source of truth. Updated by humans and the assistant.

## Today
- [ ] [P0] Task description — added 2026-05-11 — context/why
- [ ] [P1] Task — added 2026-05-10 — context

## This week
- [ ] [P0] Task — due 2026-05-15
- [ ] [P1] Task — due 2026-05-17

## Backlog
- [ ] [P2] Task — added 2026-05-09
- [ ] [P3] Task — someday/maybe

## Waiting on
- [ ] Reply from Alice on contract — sent 2026-05-09
- [ ] Vendor X to confirm — emailed 2026-05-10

## Done (recent 7 days)
- [x] Task — completed 2026-05-10
- [x] Task — completed 2026-05-09

Sections (in order): Today | This week | Backlog | Waiting on | Done (recent 7 days).

Priority tags: [P0] = today must-do, [P1] = today should-do, [P2] = this week, [P3] = someday.

Workflow operations

ADD

operation: add
section: today | week | backlog | waiting
priority: P0-P3
description: <text>
context: <optional why / link>

→ Inserts as new line in the right section, with today's date.

COMPLETE

operation: complete
match: <task ID or unique substring>

→ Moves to "Done (recent 7 days)" with today's date.

EDIT

operation: edit
match: <task ID or substring>
field: priority | description | section | context
new_value: <value>

MOVE

operation: move
match: <task>
to_section: <today | week | backlog | waiting>

SPLIT

operation: split
match: <task>
into:
  - <subtask 1>
  - <subtask 2>

→ Replaces original with N subtasks under the same section/priority.

QUERY

operation: query
filter: section / priority / age / contains

→ Returns matching tasks without modification.

REPRIORITIZE

operation: reprioritize
context: <current load / new priorities>

→ Analyzes the full list, proposes a re-ordering (today/week/backlog reshuffle), asks for approval before applying.

PRUNE

operation: prune
filter: done > 7 days ago | waiting > 14 days ago | backlog > 30 days ago

→ Surfaces stale items + proposes archiving (or killing).

Workflow

  1. Parse user intent — what operation, what task
  2. Read TASKS.md — get current state
  3. Apply operation — but for destructive ops (delete, prune, mass reprioritize) → ⚠️ propose first, await approval
  4. Write TASKS.md — preserve formatting
  5. Output the change with confirmation

Output structure

# Tasks Updated — <Day, Month DD HH:MM>

## Operation: <add | complete | edit | move | split | reprioritize | prune | query>

### Changes
- ✅ <change description>
- ✅ <change description>

[For destructive ops, before applying:]
### ⚠️ Proposed changes (awaiting approval)
- Will move: <task A> → done
- Will prune: <task B>, <task C> (idle > 30 days)

Reply "approve" to apply, or "skip <id>" to keep specific items.

### Current snapshot
- **Today**: <N tasks> (<X P0>, <Y P1>)
- **This week**: <N tasks>
- **Backlog**: <N>
- **Waiting**: <N> (oldest: <days>)

### Recommendation
<one-line — e.g., "your Today list has 8 items. Suggest moving 3 to This week.">

Anti-patterns

  • Auto-pruning without approval — destructive ops always ⚠️ first
  • Tasks without priority tags — every task gets P0-P3
  • Tasks without context — add a one-line "why" so future-Chris knows
  • Letting "Today" balloon past 5-7 items — flag and propose reprioritization
  • Done items piling up forever — keep last 7 days only; older → archive file

Pre-authorizations

  • Auto-add tasks from skill outputs (prep-day, email-triage, ghl-daily-pulse all add tasks)
  • Auto-mark "Waiting on" items as overdue after 14 days (flag, don't delete)
  • Auto-archive done items > 7 days into TASKS-archive.md
  • NEVER auto-delete tasks — always confirm

Integration with productivity:task-management

The harness has a productivity:task-management skill that maintains shared TASKS.md across teammates. This skill is compatible — same format. When that skill is available, defer to it for cross-team tasks. This skill is the single-user version for the AI Company project specifically.

Pairing

  • Read by prep-day (which tasks today)
  • Read by weekly-review (week's completed + open)
  • Written to by email-triage (Action emails → tasks)
  • Written to by ghl-daily-pulse (hot opportunities → tasks)
  • Manual CRUD by user any time

Where TASKS.md lives

Default: /Users/christomac/Projects/AI Company/TASKS.md. Per-project override: each project root has its own TASKS.md. project-overseer aggregates across all projects when asked.

One-liner reminder

Single file, 5 sections, priority-tagged, every change explicit. Destructive ops always need approval.

skills.name: Tax Calc
skills.description: Configure and operate sales-tax calculation at checkout — nexus detection, rate lookup, exemption handling, marketplace facilitator rules, remittance prep. Stripe Tax is default; TaxJar / Avalara backup options. Owns the calc; CPA owns the remittance.
skills.intent: Get sales tax right at checkout so we don't owe back-taxes + penalties later. Stripe Tax handles 80% of the work for SaaS-shape merchants; e-commerce with regulated SKUs needs more care (some categories taxable in one state, exempt in another). This skill is the operational layer; CPA + `compliance-officer` set policy.
best_for: New store wiring tax at checkout
skills.estimated_time: Stripe Tax setup: 2-4 hours. State nexus addition: 1-2 hours. Exemption flow: 4-8 hours.
skills.body_html:
Tax Calc

The checkout-time sales-tax owner. Right rate. Right state. Exemptions honored. Audit-ready.

When to use

  • New store needs tax wired
  • Adding a state nexus
  • Building exemption-certificate flow for B2B
  • Audit before monthly close
  • Tax authority inquiry (CPA-led, this skill assists)

When NOT to use

  • Income tax / corporate tax → CPA
  • International VAT (separate skill; not yet built — currently US-only)
  • Tax strategy / structuring → CPA + counsel
  • Refund / dispute on taxes paid → CPA-led

Required inputs

  1. States with nexus — current physical / economic nexus list (per CPA)
  2. Product tax categories — per SKU: standard / supplement (taxability varies) / Rx (varies) / clothing / SaaS
  3. Customer types — taxable / exempt (non-profit, reseller, wholesale)
  4. Marketplace facilitator — is the store the seller-of-record, or do we sell via 3rd-party that remits?
  5. Tax-exempt cert collection — manual upload, third-party validation, none

Architecture

Cart at checkout
   ↓
tax-calc.calculate(ship_to, items, customer_kind)
   ↓
Stripe Tax API call (preferred default)
   ↓ ← returns: tax_amount, tax_rate, jurisdiction breakdown
Display tax line at checkout
   ↓
Customer pays (tax included in total)
   ↓
Stripe records tax for remittance reporting
   ↓
Monthly: tax report exported → CPA per `monthly-close-handoff.sop.md`
   ↓
CPA files state returns + remits

Provider comparison

Provider Pros Cons Best for
Stripe Tax Native to checkout; automatic nexus monitoring; calc + reporting in one Cost (0.5% of taxable revenue); limited customization Default — choose unless reason not to
TaxJar (Stripe) Reporting + filing service add-on Acquired by Stripe; redundant if already using Stripe Tax Stripe-shop wanting full filing
Avalara AvaTax Enterprise-grade; multi-currency; custom taxability rules Expensive; complex integration High-volume / international / complex catalog
Manual Cheapest Error-prone; doesn't scale; audit risk Pre-revenue testing only

Matrix default: Stripe Tax. Reconsider if international ships or if Avalara categories needed for a regulated SKU edge case.

Nexus

Physical nexus: office, warehouse, employee, inventory storage (any of these in a state = nexus) Economic nexus: revenue or transaction count thresholds; ~$100K revenue OR 200 transactions in most states (varies)

Matrix nexus checklist (per CPA):

  • Home state (where incorporated)
  • Warehouse states (current single warehouse — confirm location)
  • Any economic threshold crossed in last 12 months

Stripe Tax monitors economic nexus automatically; reports when crossing imminent. CPA confirms + we register.

Product taxability

Tax categories vary by state. Key Matrix-relevant categories:

Category Stripe code Federal/State variance
Compounded pharmaceutical (503-A) txcd_99999999 (general) — varies Usually taxable; some states exempt Rx
Supplements txcd_31130000 Often exempt as food; varies
Apparel (MuscleLock) txcd_99999999 (general) Some states exempt; varies by price
Shipping charges txcd_92010001 Some states tax shipping; varies
Educational content (Peptide Map) txcd_60000000 (SaaS-ish) SaaS-state-specific

Assign category per variant in product-catalog-builder. Stripe Tax applies state-specific rules.

Exemption flow (B2B)

1. B2B customer signs up
2. Account page → "Tax-exempt status"
3. Upload state resale certificate or 501(c)(3) determination letter
4. Compliance reviews + validates (manual today; can wire third-party validation later)
5. On approval: customer.tax_exempt flag set per state
6. At checkout: tax_calc returns 0 tax for that customer in that state
7. Exemption logged + cert on file (audit-ready)

Cert renewal:

  • Re-upload required every 3 years (varies by state)
  • Auto-reminder 30 days before expiration
  • On expiration: tax-exempt flag removed; customer warned + offered to renew

Workflow

Step 1 — Confirm tax posture

  • Read CPA's current nexus list
  • Confirm provider choice (Stripe Tax default)
  • Get product category mapping decisions

Step 2 — Configure Stripe Tax

  • Enable Stripe Tax in dashboard
  • Configure tax registrations per state with nexus
  • Map product categories per SKU (sync from catalog)
  • Test with test addresses across 5 states

Step 3 — Build calc endpoint

  • POST /api/tax/calculate
  • Input: ship_to address + cart items + customer_id (for exemption check)
  • Output: tax_total + per-line breakdown + jurisdiction
  • Call Stripe Tax Calculation API
  • Cache for cart duration (don't recompute on every render)

Step 4 — Build checkout display

  • Tax line item visible before final pay click
  • Hover/tooltip: jurisdiction breakdown
  • Updates if customer changes ship address
  • Loading state while calculating

Step 5 — Build exemption UI

  • Account page: upload cert
  • Per-state exemption status
  • Renewal reminders

Step 6 — Build reporting export

  • Monthly: tax collected per state per product category
  • Export format: state-specific (varies — Stripe handles most)
  • Hand off to CPA per monthly-close-handoff.sop.md

Step 7 — Monitor nexus

  • Weekly: review Stripe Tax nexus dashboard
  • New state crossing 80% of threshold → alert + register before reaching
  • Document registrations in _Logs/tax/registrations.md

Step 8 — Audit-ready archive

  • Every cart calc → archive (Stripe stores)
  • Every exemption cert → archive (Supabase Storage)
  • Every monthly export → archive (_Logs/tax/<YYYY-MM>/)

Failure modes

Failure Recovery
Stripe Tax API down Fail-closed: don't allow checkout (don't undercharge) OR fall back to cached rate with stale flag (CPA reviews)
Wrong tax category assigned to SKU Audit; if customers overcharged, refund difference; if undercharged, absorb + fix going forward (don't bill back)
Nexus crossed without registering Stop selling in state OR register retroactively + back-file; CPA-led
Exemption cert fraud Honored at face value; CPA + counsel if pattern emerges
Customer disputes tax Refer to invoice + jurisdiction breakdown; CPA reviews edge cases

Anti-patterns

  • Calculating tax on the client — guaranteed manipulation
  • Hiding tax until final step — biggest cart-abandonment driver
  • Manual tax tables — outdated within 6 months; audit risk
  • Skipping product category mapping — Stripe defaults to "general" — overtaxes exempt items
  • Ignoring nexus alerts — back-taxes + penalties when discovered
  • Exemption "trust by name" — always require cert
  • No archive of calc receipts — audit defense weak
  • Bundling tax into "total" without breakdown — customer trust erodes

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Configure Stripe Tax in dev/staging
  • ✅ Test calc with sample addresses
  • ✅ Build UI for tax display + exemptions
  • ⚠️ Enable Stripe Tax in live mode (Chris — billing decision)
  • ⚠️ Register in new state (Chris + CPA — legal commitment)
  • ⚠️ Approve exemption cert (compliance / Chris if unclear)
  • 🚫 Process checkout with tax fail-open silently
  • 🚫 Modify historical tax records (always append corrections)

Pairing

Inputs from: ecommerce-backend (cart calc trigger), product-catalog-builder (tax category per SKU), ecommerce-schema (customer.tax_exempt flag), compliance-officer (regulated-SKU taxability questions) Outputs to: cfo-finance (monthly tax accrual), bookkeeping (tax in monthly close), analytics-architect (effective tax rate trend), CPA (remittance handoff) Chains with: monthly-close-handoff.sop.md (monthly export), production-deploy.sop.md (changes to tax config gate through deploy SOP)

One-line reminder

Stripe Tax by default. Right category per SKU. Cert before exemption. Fail-closed on outages. Archive every calc. CPA owns filing — we own calc.

skills.name: Technical Seo Specialist
skills.description: SEO specialist for technical foundations — Core Web Vitals, schema markup, indexing, crawl, sitemaps, robots, canonical, mobile-friendliness, HTTPS, structured data validation. Owns the "Google can find + crawl + understand Matrix" layer. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Technical SEO is the boring layer that gates everything else. Slow site = bad CWV = ranking penalty. Missing schema = no rich results. Broken canonical = duplicate-content penalties. This specialist owns the discipline that lets content + local + AI search amplify properly.
best_for: Initial technical audit on new site
Indexing / crawl errors in Search Console
skills.estimated_time: Initial audit: 4-8 hours. Per fix: 30 min-2 hours.
skills.body_html:
Technical SEO Specialist

The foundation. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Owns Matrix's technical SEO foundation across all web properties (matrix-store, matrix-advanced-solutions, ai-command-center, MuscleLock site, Peptide Map, ClinIQ when live). Audits + maintains: Core Web Vitals, schema.org markup, indexing status, crawl health, sitemap submission, robots.txt, canonical tags, mobile-friendliness, HTTPS, structured-data validation, HTTP status hygiene, redirect cleanliness.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Content / on-page copy (content-seo-specialist), local SEO (local-seo-specialist), engineering implementation (frontend-dev + backend-dev execute the fixes; this skill specifies them).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (initial-audit / quarterly-audit / cwv-failing / schema-error / indexing-issue / new-site-launch / specific-fix)
    required: true
  site_url:
    type: string
    required: true
  scope:
    type: enum (full-site / specific-section / specific-page / specific-issue)
    required: false
    default: full-site

RULES

rules:
  core_web_vitals:
    - LCP < 2.5s p75 mobile (target; 2.5-4 needs improvement; > 4 poor)
    - INP < 200ms p75 (target; 200-500 needs work; > 500 poor)
    - CLS < 0.1 (target; 0.1-0.25 needs work; > 0.25 poor)
    - Measure both lab (Lighthouse) and field (CrUX / real-user)
    - Don't optimize lab at expense of field
  schema_discipline:
    - Every page type has appropriate schema per `seo-targets.md` matrix
    - Validate via Schema.org validator + Google Rich Results Test
    - Never schema content not visible on the page (Google manual-action risk)
    - Update schema in tandem with content changes
  indexing:
    - Submit sitemap to Search Console + Bing Webmaster
    - Monitor coverage report weekly
    - Address coverage errors within 7 days
    - Robots.txt allows crawling intended pages (no accidental blocks)
    - Canonical tags resolve to single preferred URL
  https_security:
    - HTTPS everywhere (no mixed content)
    - Valid SSL with auto-renewal
    - HSTS enabled
    - Security headers (CSP / X-Frame-Options / X-Content-Type-Options)
  redirects:
    - 301 for permanent moves; 302 only for genuine temporary
    - No redirect chains (max 1 hop)
    - No broken redirects (4xx after redirect)
  prohibited:
    - Cloaking (different content to crawlers vs users) — manual penalty
    - Keyword-stuffed metadata
    - Hidden text / hidden links
    - Doorway pages

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - Google Search Console API (indexing + CWV + coverage)
    - Bing Webmaster Tools
    - PageSpeed Insights API (Lighthouse + CrUX)
    - Schema.org validator
    - Google Rich Results Test
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md (schema markup standards table)
    - Site source code via filesystem (for self-audit)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/seo/technical/<period>-audit.md
    - _Logs/seo/technical/issue-tracker.csv
  composed_skills:
    - code-qa (for engineering implementation of fixes)
    - frontend-dev (specifies fix; eng executes)
    - backend-dev (server-side fixes — headers / caching / TTFB)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch (manual page checks)
    - Vercel MCP (deployment + runtime logs for performance)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4 (audit synthesis)
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (complex algorithm-update diagnostics)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    audit_id: string
    site: string
    scope: string
    cwv:
      lcp_p75: number
      inp_p75: number
      cls_p75: number
      ttfb_p75: number
      pass_rate: number  # % of URLs passing all 3 core metrics
      worst_offenders: array of {url, lcp, inp, cls}
    indexing:
      indexed_urls: number
      submitted_urls: number
      coverage_errors: number
      errors_breakdown: array
    schema:
      pages_with_schema: number
      schema_types_used: array
      validation_errors: array
    crawl_health:
      4xx_count: number
      5xx_count: number
      redirect_chains: number
      orphan_pages: number
    mobile_friendly:
      pass_rate: number
      issues: array
    https_security:
      mixed_content_urls: array
      hsts_enabled: boolean
      ssl_valid: boolean
    issues:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        type: cwv | indexing | schema | crawl | mobile | security
        url: string
        detail: string
        suggested_fix: string
        executor: frontend-dev | backend-dev | devops-deployment
        estimated_effort: small | medium | large
  format: JSON + Markdown
  quality_bar:
    - Real-user data (CrUX) included, not just lab
    - Fixes assigned to executor
    - Severity correctly graded

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - seo-strategy-lead (prioritization)
    - frontend-dev / backend-dev / devops-deployment (execution)
    - code-qa (review fix PRs)
  payload: audit + per-issue spec
  trigger_method: quarterly cron OR seo-strategy-lead trigger
  fallback_routing:
    - P0 (site down / not crawlable / mass deindex) → immediate engineering + Chris
    - CWV failing on commerce flow → eng-priority queue
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/seo/technical/

Eval cases

  1. New site audit (matrix-store) → comprehensive baseline
  2. CWV LCP > 4s → identify image / JS / CSS culprits
  3. Schema validation errors on PDP → fix spec
  4. Coverage error spike in Search Console → diagnosis
  5. Mobile-friendly fail on key page → fix
  6. Mixed content warning → SSL / asset audit
  7. Redirect chain 4+ hops → cleanup
  8. Sitemap not auto-updating on new SKUs → fix

Anti-patterns

  • Lab-only optimization ignoring real-user data
  • Schema for content not on page — manual-action risk
  • Skipping mobile in 2026
  • Letting redirect chains grow
  • Ignoring Search Console coverage errors

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + diagnose
  • ✅ Specify fixes (engineering executes)
  • ⚠️ Approve deindexing pages (Chris)
  • 🚫 Cloaking / hidden text / black-hat

Pairing

Composed by: seo-strategy-lead Composes: frontend-dev, backend-dev, devops-deployment, code-qa Reads: Search Console, PageSpeed, Schema validator, source code

One-liner reminder

CWV thresholds non-negotiable. Schema valid + reflects page. Real-user > lab data. No cloaking. Fixes routed to engineering. Quarterly audit.

skills.name: Trademark Clearance
skills.description: Search and clear a proposed name (brand / product / sub-brand) before commit. USPTO TESS + common-law + phonetic + similar-mark + class-specific evaluation. Scores each candidate; recommends path forward. Mandatory before any name goes public.
skills.intent: Discovering a trademark conflict AFTER marketing spend is the most expensive mistake. This skill runs the search + analysis upfront and recommends names that will actually clear. Counsel does final clearance opinion; this skill produces the pre-counsel work product.
skills.estimated_time: Per name: 30-60 min. Per batch of 5-10 candidates: 2-4 hours.
skills.body_html:
Trademark Clearance

The pre-commit name search. Find the conflict before you spend money on marketing.

When to use

  • Before committing to a new brand / product / sub-brand name
  • Before filing a trademark application (do clearance first)
  • Defensive — checking if someone else uses a mark too close to ours
  • Variation check (we want "MatrixGLP1" — is it clear given our existing marks?)

When NOT to use

  • After name is already used publicly extensively (counsel for any rebrand decision)
  • Counsel-only situations (litigation, opposition, complex foreign filings)
  • Generic / descriptive terms with no inherent distinctiveness (cannot trademark)

Required inputs

  1. Candidate name(s) — single OR list of 3-10 candidates (more is better; many will fail)
  2. Intended use class(es) — pharmaceutical / supplement / cosmetic / software / educational / clinical service
  3. Geography — US only / North America / international
  4. Channel of use — D2C / B2B / wholesale / educational
  5. Existing related marks — what other Matrix marks live in the same class (helps spot family-of-marks issues)

Trademark classes (Nice classification — relevant to Matrix)

Class Description Matrix relevance
005 Pharmaceuticals + veterinary Most catalog SKUs
003 Cosmetics + non-medicated toiletries Some topicals
029 Foods + supplements (foods) Supplement edibles
030 Supplements (other) MuscleLock category
032 Non-alcoholic beverages Performance drinks
035 Advertising / business services Sub-brand business operations
041 Education + training Peptide Map educational content
042 Scientific / technological / SaaS ClinIQ if SaaS-flavored
044 Medical services Clinic-services brand if applicable

A mark must be filed in every class it's used. Cross-class conflicts assessed.

Search layers (in order)

Layer 1 — USPTO TESS (federal registrations + applications)

  • USPTO Trademark Electronic Search System
  • Search: exact + phonetic + word-element-based
  • Filter by class
  • Flag: active registrations, pending applications, cancelled (recently — might be re-filed)

Layer 2 — Common-law (unregistered use)

  • Google + business search
  • Industry-specific directories
  • Social media handles (@handle on major platforms)
  • Domain availability (often signals existing common-law use)
  • Conferences + trade publications

Layer 3 — Phonetic + similar marks

  • Sound-alike (e.g., "Matrix" vs "Matricks", "Symatrix")
  • Look-alike (typography / visual)
  • Translation similarity (if international)
  • Acronym conflict (M.A.S. vs MAS Inc.)
  • Plural / hyphenation variants

Layer 4 — Foreign (if international planned)

  • Madrid Protocol search via WIPO
  • EU TMview
  • Major-market national offices (UK, Canada, Mexico, Australia)

Layer 5 — Class-adjacent risk

  • Even if cleared in class 005, check 029 / 030 (food / supplement) and 003 (cosmetic) for likely-confusion analysis. Trademark protection is "in connection with goods or services" — a "DerMatrix" mark for cosmetics could block a "DerMatrix" topical in pharma class if consumers might confuse.

Risk scoring per candidate

For each candidate:
  Search Layer 1 (TESS) — direct conflicts: count
  Search Layer 2 (common law) — direct conflicts: count
  Search Layer 3 (phonetic / similar) — meaningful similarities: count
  Search Layer 4 (foreign) — if applicable
  Search Layer 5 (adjacent class) — risk evaluation

  Combine into risk score:
  - 🟢 CLEAR — no direct conflicts; no meaningful phonetic; no adjacent-class risk
  - 🟡 RISKY — minor phonetic overlap OR adjacent-class concern; counsel-judgment needed
  - 🔴 BLOCKED — direct conflict in target class; abandon
  - ⚫ CANCELLED-RISK — was registered, recently cancelled, might re-file; counsel watches

The "good name" filter (post-clearance)

Even if cleared, ask:

  • Distinctive vs descriptive? Strongest marks are arbitrary / fanciful / suggestive. Descriptive marks ("Peptide Therapy") get weak protection.
  • Pronounceable? International + voice-search friendly.
  • Spellable? Customers Googling find us.
  • Domain available?
  • Social handles available?
  • Negative associations? (slang, foreign-language meaning)
  • Brand-voice fit per sub-brands.md + brand-guide.md?
  • Trademark-able strength:
    • Fanciful (invented word, e.g., "Kodak") = strongest
    • Arbitrary (real word, unrelated to product, e.g., "Apple" for computers) = strong
    • Suggestive (hints at product, requires imagination, e.g., "Coppertone") = moderate
    • Descriptive (describes product directly, e.g., "Cold Cream") = weak; requires acquired distinctiveness
    • Generic = no trademark possible

Workflow

Step 1 — Intake candidates

Capture all candidates + class + geography + channel.

Step 2 — Run Layer 1 (USPTO TESS)

For each candidate:

  • Exact search
  • Wildcard / truncated search
  • Phonetic equivalents
  • Capture: hit list with status (registered / pending / cancelled / abandoned)

Step 3 — Run Layer 2 (common-law)

  • Google search across major channels
  • Social handle availability
  • Domain availability (.com first; others if relevant)
  • Industry-directory checks
