Step 4 — Run Layer 3 (phonetic + similar)

  • Phonetic search tools
  • Visual similarity
  • Meaningful variants

Step 5 — Run Layer 4 (foreign if applicable)

  • WIPO + national offices

Step 6 — Run Layer 5 (adjacent class risk)

  • Per Nice class table

Step 7 — Score each candidate

  • 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / ⚫ per scoring rubric
  • Notes per candidate

Step 8 — "Good name" filter on 🟢 + 🟡

  • Distinctive vs descriptive?
  • Pronounceable / spellable?
  • Domain + handles available?
  • Brand voice fit?

Step 9 — Recommendation

# Trademark Clearance — <brand / product>

## Search scope
- Candidates: <list>
- Class(es): <list>
- Geography: <scope>
- Date: <today>

## Results per candidate
| Candidate | Layer 1 (TESS) | Layer 2 (common law) | Layer 3 (similar) | Layer 4 (foreign) | Layer 5 (adjacent) | Score |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## Detailed analysis (per 🟢 / 🟡)
### <Candidate name>
- Strengths
- Risks
- Good-name filter result
- Recommendation: proceed / proceed-with-counsel-opinion / pivot

## Recommendation
- Top choice: <name> — reasoning
- Backup: <name>
- Avoid: <name(s)> — reasons

## Next step
- File via `ip-counsel-coordination` if confirmed
- Or run additional candidates if all 🔴 / ⚫

Step 10 — Hand off

  • If proceeding: → ip-counsel-coordination for filing
  • If pivoting: → naming exercise again with fresh candidates

Defensive search (other direction)

When someone might be using a mark close to ours:

  • Search for our mark + related variants
  • Identify any new conflicting filings
  • Flag for counsel: opposition (USPTO) or enforcement (cease-and-desist) decision

Quarterly: run this automatically as part of ip-lead portfolio review.

Anti-patterns

  • Single-candidate clearance — when the candidate fails, you start over from zero
  • Skipping common-law — TESS-clear can still infringe an active common-law user
  • Ignoring phonetic similarity — "likelihood of confusion" considers sound
  • Skipping adjacent-class check — confusion analysis crosses classes
  • Descriptive mark approval — weak protection + costly enforcement
  • Filing without "good name" sanity — name clears but flops in market
  • Foreign filing without geographic scope clarity — wasted money on countries we won't enter
  • Counsel-led for routine searches — counsel time is for opinion + filing, not search-button-clicking

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run all 5 search layers
  • ✅ Score + rank candidates
  • ✅ Run "good name" filter
  • ✅ Defensive searches (quarterly + ad-hoc)
  • ⚠️ Final clearance opinion (counsel — always)
  • ⚠️ Commit to file (Chris)
  • 🚫 Public use before clearance + filing decision

Pairing

Inputs from: ip-lead (intake), creative-director + brand-voice-enforcement (name ideation), sub-brands.md + brand-guide.md (voice fit) Outputs to: ip-counsel-coordination (filing brief), product-naming-gate (clearance result + good-name score), ip-portfolio.md (registry update), creative-director (defensive scan results) Chains with: ip-lead (orchestrator), external counsel (final opinion)

One-line reminder

5 search layers. Rank multiple candidates. 🔴 is final. 🟡 needs counsel. "Good name" filter on 🟢. Defensive quarterly. Counsel writes opinion; we prep the work.

skills.name: Training Builder
skills.description: Build onboarding + training programs for new Matrix hires. Per-role training plans, week-by-week curriculum, skill-check milestones, materials curation, 30/60/90-day reviews. Companion to recruiting + interview-scorecard for the full HR cycle. Never auto-promotes or terminates — proposes assessments.
skills.intent: Reduce time-to-productivity for new hires. Generate a 90-day onboarding curriculum per role with weekly milestones, materials, and competency checks. Track progress. Surface gaps. Hand to manager for 30/60/90 reviews.
best_for: Authoring training plan for newly hired role
Updating training based on hire-success patterns
skills.estimated_time: 60-120 min initial plan per role
skills.body_html:
Training Builder

The new-hire onboarding + ramp curriculum builder. Companion to recruiting + interview-scorecard.

When to use

  • New hire starts → build their 30/60/90 ramp plan
  • Existing role's training feels ad-hoc / inconsistent
  • Multiple hires in the same role → standardize curriculum
  • Cross-training existing employee into a new function

When NOT to use

  • Existing-employee performance management (use performance-management planned)
  • Customer training (use customer-onboarding)
  • External training material (e.g., conference) — different motion

Required inputs

  1. Role + level — what the new hire is doing (e.g., "Senior SDR" / "Account Manager")
  2. Hire start date
  3. Manager — who owns the new hire's ramp
  4. Competencies — from interview-scorecard output for this role

If 4 missing, ask: "What were the 4-6 competencies in the interview scorecard for this role?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Load context

  • _Shared Context/company-profile.md (company overview)
  • _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (voice rules — every customer-facing hire memorizes this)
  • _Shared Context/products.md + offers.md (what we sell)
  • Role-specific: relevant SKILL.md files (e.g., for SDR → cold-email-sequence, discovery-call-prep, sales-strategy)
  • The role's interview-scorecard output (competencies to develop)

Step 2 — Pick the curriculum shape

Standard Matrix curriculum (90 days):

Week 1 — Foundations

  • Company overview (mission, ICP, offers, compliance posture)
  • Brand voice training (read brand-guide.md + exemplars)
  • Tools tour (GHL, Slack, internal docs)
  • Shadow Chris + AM for 2-3 customer calls
  • Read _Shared Context/ files + relevant _Knowledge Base/ files

Week 2 — Role-specific basics

  • Per role: study + practice the load-bearing skills
  • Mock exercises (role-play, sample tasks)
  • Submit first-week output for review

Week 3-4 — Live work with safety net

  • Take real tasks with manager review on each
  • 1:1 daily debriefs
  • Week 4 self-assessment

Week 5-8 — Independent work, weekly review

  • Take ownership of tasks; manager reviews weekly
  • Quality bar: > 80% of work passes review without major edit
  • Week 8 mid-point review

Week 9-12 — Full autonomy with metric ownership

  • Own KPIs for the role
  • 30/60/90 day review at week 12
  • Decision: promote / stay / coach / part ways

Step 3 — Per-role curriculum (examples)

Senior SDR:

  • Week 1: foundations (company / brand / tools)
  • Week 2: study 06 - Sales & Lead-Gen Agent Patterns.md, cold-email-sequence, discovery-call-prep, sales-strategy
  • Week 3: write 20 cold emails (reviewed by Chris before send)
  • Week 4: book 5 discovery calls (Chris attends as observer)
  • Week 5-8: run own pipeline, weekly 1:1 review, target 10 meetings/week
  • Week 9-12: full ownership of quota (target: 40 meetings/month + 5 closes)
  • 90-day decision criteria: close rate ≥ X%, brand voice consistent, CRM hygiene clean

Account Manager:

  • Week 1: foundations
  • Week 2: study account-manager skill + customer-onboarding + retention-automation
  • Week 3-4: shadow Chris on QBR calls, study 5 customer accounts deeply
  • Week 5-8: take 5 accounts as primary owner with weekly Chris review
  • Week 9-12: take 15 accounts as primary owner, run own QBR cadence
  • 90-day decision criteria: customer NPS ≥ 4.5, churn rate ≤ baseline, expansion identified

Engineer (Frontend / Backend):

  • Week 1: foundations + project tour (matrix-store, matrix-advanced-solutions)
  • Week 2: study engineering-lead, frontend-dev / backend-dev, project CLAUDE.md
  • Week 3: 2-3 small tickets (with code-qa review on every PR)
  • Week 4-8: medium tickets, autonomous PRs (code-qa gate stays)
  • Week 9-12: own a feature area (own architecture decisions + execution)
  • 90-day decision criteria: ship velocity at baseline, code-qa green-rate ≥ 90%, autonomy on architecture

Designer:

  • Week 1: foundations + creative-director study
  • Week 2: study brand-guide.md, brand-identity.md, sub-brand specs, both palettes
  • Week 3: small briefs (single asset turnaround per creative-director output)
  • Week 4-8: medium briefs (multi-asset campaigns)
  • Week 9-12: own a campaign visual system end-to-end
  • 90-day decision criteria: brand consistency 100%, on-time delivery ≥ 90%, creative quality (subjective + customer-feedback)

Step 4 — Materials curation

For each week, list specific things to read + do:

  • Required reading (KB files, source summaries, project docs)
  • Skill files relevant to role
  • Tools to set up (accounts, access, permissions)
  • People to meet (with intro context)

Step 5 — Milestones + assessments

Per role + week:

  • What "good" looks like at this milestone
  • What "concerning" looks like
  • What manager should check at the milestone meeting

Define the 30 / 60 / 90 day decision criteria upfront.

Step 6 — Surface the gaps

After hire completes ramp (or doesn't):

  • What did the curriculum miss?
  • Where did they need help that wasn't documented?
  • What signal would have predicted earlier?

Feed back into the curriculum for next hire.

Step 7 — Output

Output structure

# Training Plan: <Hire Name> — <Role>

## Setup
**Hire**: <name>
**Role**: <role>
**Manager**: <name>
**Start date**: <date>
**30-day review date**: <+30 from start>
**60-day review**: <+60>
**90-day review**: <+90>

## Competencies to develop (from scorecard)
1. <competency>
2. <competency>
...

## Week-by-week curriculum

### Week 1: Foundations (<dates>)
**Required reading**:
- `_Shared Context/company-profile.md`
- `_Shared Context/brand-guide.md`
- ...

**Activities**:
- [ ] <activity with owner>
- [ ] <activity>

**Manager touchpoints**: end-of-day check-in × 5

### Week 2: <focus>
[same format]

### Week 3-4: <focus>
[same format]

(... repeat through Week 12)

## Decision criteria
- **30 days**: <criterion>
- **60 days**: <criterion>
- **90 days**: <criterion + decision>

## Materials checklist
- [ ] Slack access provisioned
- [ ] GHL access (role-scoped)
- [ ] GitHub access (if engineer)
- [ ] Read access to relevant project repos
- [ ] Calendar shared with manager
- [ ] Internal docs in handbook readable

## Progress tracking
| Week | Status | Notes | Manager review |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | <not started / in progress / complete> | | |
| 2 | | | |
| ... | | | |

## ✅ I handled
- Curriculum draft + week-by-week plan
- Materials curation
- Milestone + assessment criteria
- Access checklist

## ⚠️ Need from you (manager)
- Run daily / weekly check-ins per cadence
- Document each milestone review
- Submit 30 / 60 / 90 day decision

## 🚫 You only (Chris / hiring manager)
- Make ramp-pace adjustments (faster / slower)
- Make 30 / 60 / 90 day go / no-go decisions
- Approve any extension / PIP / termination

## Handoff
**To**: hiring manager (executes), HR onboarding (admin), `performance-management` (planned — post-ramp)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-training-<hire-name>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Generic curriculum for all roles — each role needs role-specific
  • No 30/60/90 decision criteria upfront — decisions become political
  • No materials checklist — new hire waits for access
  • No manager touchpoint cadence — silent ramps fail
  • Re-using same curriculum without updates — fails new hires when role evolves

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Generate curriculum
  • ✅ Curate materials
  • ✅ Define decision criteria
  • ✅ Set up tracking
  • ⚠️ Approve curriculum before hire starts (manager + Chris)
  • 🚫 Make hiring / firing decisions
  • 🚫 Adjust comp / role mid-ramp

Pairing

Inputs from: recruiting (hire context), interview-scorecard (competencies), _Shared Context/* (foundations), role-relevant skill files Outputs to: hiring manager, HR, pmo-operator (ramp tracked as project) Chains with: customer-onboarding (parallel pattern but for customers)

One-liner reminder

Per-role curriculum. 90 days. Manager touchpoint cadence. 30/60/90 decision criteria upfront. Materials checklist. Feed gaps back to next curriculum.

skills.name: Vendor Management
skills.description: Manage vendor relationships — labs, compounding pharmacy, payment processors, hosting, SaaS, fulfillment. Tracks: contracts, renewal dates, performance, BAA status, alternative options. Surfaces risks (single-vendor dependencies, expiring contracts, cost creep). For Matrix: critical because the whole business depends on labs + compounding partner.
skills.estimated_time: 30-60 min quarterly review; 15 min per new vendor
skills.body_html:
Vendor Management

The supply-side risk manager. Matrix runs on vendors — labs + compounding pharmacy + tech stack.

When to use

  • Quarterly vendor health review
  • New vendor onboarding (vetting + contract setup)
  • Pre-renewal decision (renew / renegotiate / replace)
  • After a vendor incident (delivery issue, compliance flag, outage)
  • Annual cost / margin audit

When NOT to use

  • One-off vendor question (just look it up directly)
  • Vendor selection from scratch (use engineering-lead if it's a tech vendor; otherwise sourcing research)
  • Litigation / dispute (legal counsel handles)

Required inputs

  1. Vendor list — maintained in _Shared Context/vendors.md (created by this skill on first run)
  2. Period — quarterly / annual review
  3. Specific vendor (if mid-cycle review)

Vendor registry structure

Maintained at _Shared Context/vendors.md:

# Vendors

## <Vendor Name>
- **Category**: lab / compounding pharmacy / payments / hosting / SaaS / fulfillment / legal / accounting / other
- **Contact**: <primary contact name + email + phone>
- **Started**: <date>
- **Status**: active / paused / terminated
- **Contract**:
  - Renewal date: <date>
  - Term: monthly / annual / multi-year
  - Auto-renew: yes / no
  - Notice period: <days>
- **Compliance**:
  - BAA signed: <date / N/A>
  - SOC 2: <yes / no>
  - HIPAA-aware: <yes / no>
- **Annual spend**: $<X>
- **Performance score**: 1-5 (last review)
- **Replaceability**:
  - Backup option identified: yes / no
  - Switching cost: low / medium / high
  - Migration time: <days>
- **Dependencies**:
  - Matrix workflow depending: <list>
  - Other vendors connecting: <list>
- **Recent issues**: <list>
- **Last reviewed**: <date>

Standard Matrix vendor categories

Pharmacy / labs (mission-critical):

  • Compounding pharmacy partner (503-A fulfillment)
  • 503-B outsourcing facility
  • Lab partners: Nugen, RAM, Florida, Kaduceus

Payments:

  • Stripe (transaction processing)
  • GoHighLevel (CRM + payments)

Hosting / SaaS:

  • Vercel (hosting)
  • Supabase (DB + auth)
  • Clerk (auth, if used)
  • Retell AI (voice)
  • ElevenLabs / Tavus (voice + video, if used)
  • Sentry (error tracking)

Fulfillment / logistics:

  • Shippo (shipping labels)
  • Carriers (UPS / FedEx / USPS)

Professional services:

  • Legal counsel
  • CPA / accounting
  • Insurance broker

Marketing tools:

  • Ad platforms (Meta / Google)
  • Email infrastructure
  • Analytics tools

Workflow

Step 1 — Inventory active vendors

If vendors.md doesn't exist, create it. Pull from:

  • Bookkeeping records (vendors with payments)
  • Tech stack documentation
  • Active integrations (_Shared Context/tool-access.md)
  • Stripe + bank statements for monthly vendors

Step 2 — Score each vendor

For each, rate 1-5 on:

  • Reliability: outages / errors / failures
  • Cost vs value: are we getting value for the spend?
  • Compliance: BAA / SOC 2 / state requirements met
  • Support: response time when we need help
  • Replaceability: how hard to switch
  • Strategic fit: aligns with Matrix's direction

Overall: 🟢 (4-5 avg, no critical issues) / 🟡 (3-4 with some flags) / 🔴 (< 3 or critical compliance gap)

Step 3 — Risk surfacing

For each vendor, flag:

  • Single-point-of-failure: Matrix has no backup, switching cost is high
  • Expiring contract: renewal in < 60 days
  • Compliance gap: BAA missing / SOC 2 expired / state cert lapsed
  • Cost creep: spend growing > 20% YoY without proportional value
  • Performance decline: scores trending down 2+ quarters
  • Vendor concentration: too much business with one vendor

Step 4 — Surface decisions

For each flagged risk:

  • Risk:
  • Action: renew / renegotiate / find-backup / replace / accept-and-document
  • Owner: Chris / counsel / etc.
  • Timeline: when this must be decided
  • Cost of inaction: what happens if we don't act

Step 5 — Backup options

For mission-critical vendors with no backup:

  • Research 2-3 alternatives
  • Document each: capability fit, pricing, switching cost, migration time
  • Even if we don't switch, having the option in pocket reduces vendor leverage

For Matrix specifically:

  • Compounding pharmacy: critical to have at least 1 evaluated backup
  • Lab partners: already have 4 (Nugen / RAM / Florida / Kaduceus) — good
  • Payments: Stripe → backup = direct ACH or LeadConnector
  • Hosting: Vercel → backup = Cloudflare Pages / AWS / Fly.io
  • Voice AI: Retell → backup = Vapi / Synthflow

Step 6 — Quarterly review output

Output structure

# Vendor Review: Q<N> <YYYY>

## Active vendors
**Total**: <N> across <M> categories
**Annual spend**: $<X>
**Average score**: <Y>/5

## Health
- 🟢 Healthy: <N>
- 🟡 At-risk: <N>
- 🔴 Critical: <N>

## Vendor table

| Vendor | Category | Status | Score | Renewal | Annual $ | Risk flag |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| <name> | <cat> | 🟢/🟡/🔴 | <X>/5 | <date> | $<Y> | <flag or "none"> |

## 🔴 Critical risks
| Risk | Vendor | Action | Owner | Deadline |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <risk> | <vendor> | <action> | <name> | <date> |

## 🟡 Watch list
| Watch | Vendor | Action | Owner | Window |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## Single-point-of-failure dependencies
| Dependency | Vendor | Backup option | Switching cost |
|---|---|---|---|

## Expiring contracts (next 90 days)
| Vendor | Renewal date | Auto-renew | Action | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|---|

## Cost analysis
| Category | Q<N> spend | Prior Q | YoY trend |
|---|---|---|---|
| Pharmacy / labs | $<X> | $<Y> | <delta> |
| Tech / SaaS | $<X> | $<Y> | <delta> |
| Marketing | $<X> | $<Y> | <delta> |
| ... | | | |

## ✅ I handled
- Pulled vendor list
- Scored each
- Surfaced risks
- Documented backups
- Generated quarterly report

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Decide on each 🔴 risk action
- Approve renewals coming due
- Authorize backup-option evaluations (RFPs)
- Approve any vendor termination

## 🚫 You only
- Sign any vendor contract / renewal
- Approve cost increases > 10%
- Terminate critical vendors (legal + transition plan needed)
- Negotiate enterprise terms

## Handoff
**To**: Chris (decisions), `bookkeeping` (cost tracking flows here), Counsel (for any contract negotiation), `cfo-finance` (cost trend integration)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-vendor-review-Q<N>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Stale registry — every vendor change updates vendors.md
  • No backup for mission-critical — single-point-of-failure unaddressed is a 🔴
  • Renewing on auto without review — surprise cost creep
  • Ignoring small vendors — they add up + can have compliance issues
  • No quarterly cadence — risks accumulate silently
  • Counting affiliate payouts as vendor cost — that's COGS-equivalent, handled in bookkeeping

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read all vendor records / Stripe / bookkeeping
  • ✅ Score + flag risks
  • ✅ Update _Shared Context/vendors.md (additive + status changes)
  • ✅ Research backup options (web research)
  • ⚠️ Initiate backup-option RFP (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Sign / renew / terminate contracts
  • 🚫 Approve cost increases
  • 🚫 Negotiate on behalf

Pairing

Inputs from: bookkeeping (vendor payments), _Shared Context/tool-access.md (tech vendors), Stripe / bank Outputs to: Chris + counsel (decisions), bookkeeping (vendor changes flow back), cfo-finance (cost trends), pmo-operator (vendor review as quarterly project) Chains with: weekly-review (risk register integration), security-inspector (compliance vendors specifically)

One-liner reminder

Quarterly cadence. Score 1-5. Surface risks. Backup options for mission-critical. Update registry on every change. Chris signs contracts.

skills.name: Vendor Payment Orchestrator
skills.description: Bi-weekly vendor payment runs. Pulls approved AP bills, validates against `vendors.md` terms, batches per payment method (ACH / check / card), executes, captures confirmations. Approval gate for batches above threshold. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Vendors paid on terms = vendors who keep working with us. This specialist runs the bi-weekly payment cycle from approved-batch to confirmation, with hard approval gates over thresholds, and a full audit trail.
skills.estimated_time: Standard batch: 30-60 min. Large / year-end: 2-4 hours.
skills.body_html:
Vendor Payment Orchestrator

The bi-weekly AP execution specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Runs the bi-weekly vendor payment cycle (default: 1st + 15th of month, aligned with affiliate payout 15th per sops/affiliate-payout-monthly.sop.md). Pulls approved bills from ar-ap-automation, validates against _Shared Context/vendors.md terms (correct vendor, contract rate, not duplicate), batches by payment method (ACH / wire / check / card), routes for approval per approval-rules.md, executes, captures confirmations. Does NOT set vendor terms, negotiate contracts, or approve individual line items over threshold (Chris does).

Explicitly OUT of scope: Vendor selection / negotiation (vendor-management planned), affiliate payouts (ghl-affiliate-manager + sops/affiliate-payout-monthly.sop.md), payroll (HR + provider), customer refunds (refund SOP), tax payments (CPA + tax-calc).

INPUT

input:
  trigger: cron-1st-and-15th | adhoc-critical-vendor | year-end-catchup
  approved_bills: from ar-ap-automation
  batch_target_method: ACH | wire | check | card | mixed
  cash_position_check: from cash-flow-monitor (must have funds before batch)

RULES

  • Pre-batch cash check — composes cash-flow-monitor; if operating cash < (batch total + 7-day safety buffer), pause + escalate.
  • Validate every bill against vendors.md:
    • Known vendor (not on hold)
    • Amount within contract rate ± 10% (flag outliers)
    • Not duplicate within 30 days
    • Terms match (Net 15 / Net 30 / etc.)
  • Approval tiers per approval-rules.md:
    • Batch total < $25,000: controller-lead approves
    • Batch total $25,000 - $50,000: Chris approves
    • Batch total > $50,000: Chris + cfo-finance review
    • Any single bill > $10,000: Chris approves individually
    • Any single bill > $50,000: Chris + counsel
  • Batch by method — separate ACH file, separate check run, separate card payments.
  • Idempotency — each bill has unique payment_id; never double-pay; check before execute.
  • Confirmation capture — every payment has confirmation number / check number / ACH trace logged.
  • Notification — vendor receives remittance advice (email + AP portal where applicable).
  • Failed payment recovery — retry once with corrected info; escalate to Chris if 2 failures.
  • Cold-chain / critical vendors (per vendors.md) can be expedited with Chris approval (don't risk supply disruption).
  • PII — vendor bank routing masked in logs.

TOOLS

  • Bank ACH portal (or Stripe Treasury when available)
  • Check-writing tool / vendor (if checks)
  • Corporate card management (Brex / Ramp / etc.)
  • cash-flow-monitor (pre-batch cash check)
  • ar-ap-automation (approved bill source)
  • bookkeeping (post-execute payment recording)
  • Read: _Shared Context/vendors.md, approval-rules.md, integration-map.md, sops/affiliate-payout-monthly.sop.md (timing alignment)
  • Compose: compliance-officer (only for vendor-comm with sensitive language)
  • Write: _Logs/finance/ap-runs/<YYYY-MM-DD>/batch.csv + confirmations.csv + summary.md

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    run_id: string
    run_date: ISO
    batch_summary:
      total_amount: number
      total_bill_count: number
      by_method:
        ach: {count, amount}
        wire: {count, amount}
        check: {count, amount}
        card: {count, amount}
      pre_batch_cash_position: number
      post_batch_cash_position: number
      cash_safety_buffer_days: number
    approval:
      tier_required: per approval-rules.md
      approver: string
      timestamp: ISO
    execution_results:
      - bill_id: string
        vendor: string
        amount: number
        method: ACH | wire | check | card
        status: success | failed | pending
        confirmation_id: string
        executed_at: ISO
    validation_flags:
      - bill_id: string
        flag: rate-outlier | possible-duplicate | terms-mismatch | hold-vendor | unknown-vendor
        action: paused | escalated | overridden-by-chris
    notifications_sent:
      - vendor: string
        method: email | portal | other
        status: delivered | bounced
    alerts:
      - severity: P0 | P1 | P2
        detail: string
  format: CSV + Markdown summary

HANDOFF

  • finance-controller-lead (run summary)
  • Chris (approvals per tier; failed-payment escalations)
  • bookkeeping (record payments to sub-ledger)
  • cash-flow-monitor (post-batch cash update)
  • ar-ap-automation (mark bills as paid)
  • cfo-finance (large-batch awareness)

Eval cases

  1. Standard bi-weekly batch under $25k → controller-approves → executes
  2. Single bill > $10k → escalates to Chris before batch executes
  3. Duplicate-bill flag from ar-ap-automation → bill held, not in batch
  4. Pre-batch cash < batch total + buffer → P0 pause, escalate to Chris + cfo-finance
  5. ACH file rejected by bank → 1 retry → if failed again, Chris escalation
  6. Critical cold-chain vendor expedite → Chris explicit approval logged → executed off-cycle
  7. Year-end catchup batch > $50k → Chris + cfo-finance review path

Anti-patterns

  • Auto-paying over approval threshold
  • Skipping cash check before batch
  • No idempotency — risk of double-payment
  • Missing confirmation capture — audit gap
  • Mixing affiliate payouts into vendor batch — different SOP, different ledger
  • Paying held vendors without explicit unblock
  • No vendor notification — remittance advice is standard practice

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull approved bills + build batch proposal
  • ✅ Run validation against vendors.md
  • ✅ Pre-batch cash check
  • ✅ Send vendor remittance notifications (standard template)
  • ⚠️ Execute batch < $25k (controller-lead approval logged)
  • ⚠️ Execute batch $25k-$50k (Chris approves)
  • ⚠️ Execute single bill > $10k (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Execute batch > $50k without Chris + cfo-finance
  • 🚫 Execute single bill > $50k without Chris + counsel
  • 🚫 Pay held / disputed vendor
  • 🚫 Modify vendor record (vendor-management owns)

Pairing

Composed by: finance-controller-lead, monthly-close-orchestrator (catchup) Composes: cash-flow-monitor, ar-ap-automation, bookkeeping Reads: vendors.md, approval-rules.md, integration-map.md, sops/affiliate-payout-monthly.sop.md Hands off to: bookkeeping, Chris, cfo-finance

One-liner reminder

Bi-weekly. Validate against vendors.md. Pre-batch cash check. Approval tiers strict. Idempotent. Confirmations captured. Cold-chain expedites with Chris OK.

skills.name: Visual Page Builder
skills.description: Generate beautiful self-contained HTML pages that explain any concept visually
skills.body_html:
Visual Page Builder

Generate a polished, self-contained HTML page that explains any concept using visual components. No frameworks, no dependencies, no build step. One file that looks like a real product page.

Page Types

Type Best for Key components
Architecture Overview Systems, tech stacks, infrastructure Flow diagram, connection lines, component cards
Comparison Table Tool vs tool, plan vs plan, before vs after Side-by-side grids, checkmarks, highlight winner
Process Flow Tutorials, onboarding, how-to guides Numbered steps, arrows, status indicators
Timeline Roadmaps, project history, changelogs Vertical timeline, date markers, milestone cards
Dashboard Metrics, KPIs, performance summaries Stat cards, bar charts, progress rings
Concept Explainer Abstract ideas, frameworks, mental models Analogy visuals, layered diagrams, callout boxes
Project Recap End-of-project summaries, case studies Before/after, stats, testimonial cards, gallery

Ask the user which type fits their content, or auto-detect from their description.

Workflow

Step 1: Gather Content

Ask the user for:

  • Title of the page
  • 3-6 sections they want to cover (or let you suggest based on the topic)
  • Key data points (numbers, stats, comparisons)
  • Relationships between concepts (what connects to what)
  • Hierarchy (what is most important, what is supporting detail)

If the user provides a document, transcript, or notes, extract these elements automatically and confirm.

Step 2: Plan the Layout

Map each section to a visual component (see Visual Components below). Present the plan:

Section 1: "What is X" -> Concept card with icon + 3 key points
Section 2: "How it works" -> 4-step process flow with arrows
Section 3: "Performance" -> Stat cards (3 across) + comparison grid
Section 4: "Get started" -> Callout box with CTA

Get user approval before building.

Step 3: Build the HTML

Generate a single self-contained HTML file.

Design System:

Token Value
Background #0f0f10
Surface #1a1a1d
Surface border #2a2a2d
Text primary #ffffff
Text secondary #a0a0a0
Accent #D97757 (or user-chosen)
Accent muted rgba(217, 119, 87, 0.15)
Font Inter via Google Fonts, fallback system-ui, sans-serif
Max width 1000px
Card radius 12px
Section spacing 80px vertical

Visual Components Available:

Component When to use Structure
Stat cards Key numbers Horizontal row, large number + label + optional trend arrow
Section cards Feature lists, grouped info Grid of cards with icon/emoji, title, description
Comparison grid Side-by-side analysis 2-3 columns, rows with checkmarks or values
Flow steps Sequential processes Numbered circles connected by lines, title + description
Status badges Tags, categories, states Colored pill badges (green/yellow/red/blue)
Code blocks Technical content, configs Dark bg, monospace font, syntax-highlighted if applicable
Callout boxes Important notes, warnings, tips Accent left border, icon, highlighted background
Progress bars Completion, capacity, scores Horizontal bar with fill percentage and label
Tables Structured data, specs Striped rows, sticky header, responsive scroll
Timelines Chronological events Vertical line with dots, alternating left/right cards
Quote blocks Testimonials, key statements Large quotation mark, italic text, attribution
Icon grids Feature overviews, tool lists 2-3 column grid with emoji + title + one-line desc

Step 4: Save and Preview

Save the file as [topic-slug]-visual.html in the current working directory.

Open in the default browser:

open [topic-slug]-visual.html

Step 5: Iterate

Ask: "How does it look? I can adjust colors, swap components, add sections, or change any text."

Common tweaks:

  • Change accent color
  • Add or remove sections
  • Swap a component type (e.g., table to comparison grid)
  • Adjust text content
  • Add a logo or header image (base64 inline)

Rules

  • Every section needs a visual component. No section should be just a wall of text. If there is no obvious visual, use a callout box or icon grid.
  • Max 50 words before a visual. Introductory text for a section must be short. Let the visual do the heavy lifting.
  • All CSS and JS inline. Zero external dependencies. The file must work when double-clicked from the desktop with no server.
  • Zero external fetches required. Google Fonts is the one allowed exception (with system font fallback). Everything else is inline.
  • Responsive. Must look good on mobile (stack grids to single column, scale stat cards, scrollable tables).
  • No JavaScript required for content. JS is only for optional enhancements (smooth scroll, fade-in animations). The page must be fully readable with JS disabled.
  • Consistent spacing. Use the design system tokens. Do not eyeball padding or margins.
  • Dark theme only. Do not offer a light mode. The dark palette is part of the brand.
  • Accessible. Sufficient color contrast (WCAG AA minimum), semantic HTML, alt text on images.
  • Keep the total file under 500KB. If inlining images, compress them first.
  • The page title should appear in the browser tab (title tag).

skills.name: Voice Ai Agent Builder
skills.description: Design, configure, and ship a production voice AI agent on Retell / Vapi / Synthflow. Composes provider-specific skills from the harness (retellai-*, synthflow-agent-builder, agents). Outputs a full agent spec, prompt pack, function definitions, webhook config, evaluation plan, and deployment checklist.
skills.intent: Be the OS-level entry point for building any voice AI agent. Triage the use case to the right provider, design the conversation flow, write the agent prompt + functions, wire webhooks to GHL, ship to staging, evaluate, then promote to production with the prod checklist gate.
skills.estimated_time: 60 min spec + 60-180 min build depending on complexity
skills.body_html:
Voice AI Agent Builder

The orchestrator for building any voice AI agent. Picks the right platform, composes provider-specific harness skills, and ships through the prod checklist gate.

When to use

  • New voice agent build (outbound, inbound, or hybrid)
  • Migrating from one voice provider to another
  • Rebuilding an underperforming voice agent
  • After should-i-build-an-agent confirms voice is the right modality

When NOT to use

  • Single-agent prompt tweak (just edit the prompt)
  • Text/chat agent (use conversation-flow-designer then channel-specific skills)
  • Live human-in-the-loop call assist (use discovery-call-prep)

Required inputs

  1. Use case — outbound cold qualification / inbound front desk / appointment booking / post-purchase / support / discovery / etc.
  2. ICP / who's on the other end — provider / staff / patient (for Matrix: clinic owner, NP/PA, front desk, MA)
  3. Goal of the call — book a meeting / qualify + route / collect info / answer Q&A / handoff to human
  4. Brand voice — load _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (for Matrix: clinical, peer-level, no exclamation, no hype)
  5. Functions needed — what the agent can DO mid-call (book a calendar, check order status, lookup contact, transfer to human, send SMS, send email, log to CRM)
  6. Compliance constraints — TCPA (outbound), call recording consent, HIPAA-flag if any patient data, A2P 10DLC for SMS handoffs

If 1 or 3 is missing, ask: "What's the use case and what counts as a successful call outcome?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Triage to platform

Decision matrix:

Platform Strengths Best for Harness skills available
Retell AI Lowest latency, fast LLM swap, strong function-calling, deep dev tooling Most production voice agents — outbound + inbound retellai-* (29 skills in harness)
Vapi Strong web SDK, easy embed, multi-LLM Web voice / in-app voice (use general patterns)
Synthflow Visual builder, fast non-dev setup Simple call flows, no-code teams synthflow-agent-builder
ElevenLabs Agents Best-in-class voice quality, easy clone Brand-voice-sensitive el-agents, agents
Tavus Video-avatar conversations Homepage / video chat tavus-homepage-agent
Deepgram + own stack Lowest cost, full control Cost-sensitive high-volume deepgram-voice-optimizer

Default for Matrix: Retell AI (production maturity + harness skill depth). Fall back to Synthflow if non-dev team will operate it.

Step 2 — Design the conversation flow

Invoke conversation-flow-designer with the use case. It produces:

  • State machine of conversation states
  • Decision branches at each state
  • Required information capture per state
  • Handoff conditions (to human / to another agent / to SMS / to email)

For outbound, also: gate conditions (when do we hang up early — voicemail, no-match, gatekeeper).

Step 3 — Write the agent prompt + scripts

Invoke voice-script-author to produce:

  • System prompt for the LLM behind the voice (Retell uses GPT-4 / Claude / etc. with the system prompt)
  • State-specific scripts (opening line, transition lines, ask-for-info phrasing, closing line)
  • Reframes / objection responses in spoken voice (different from email)
  • Voicemail script for outbound
  • Holding language for function-call waits ("let me look that up real quick — one moment")
  • Escalation script when the agent needs to hand off to a human

Step 4 — Define functions (what the agent can do)

For each function the agent calls mid-conversation:

  • Name + description (what the LLM sees)
  • Input schema (what it needs to pass)
  • Output schema (what comes back)
  • Backend implementation (webhook to GHL / API / Supabase)
  • Failure mode (what the agent says if the function fails)

Common Matrix functions:

  • lookup_contact_by_phone (caller ID → GHL contact)
  • verify_npi (real-time NPPES check via NPI MCP if available)
  • check_credentialing_status (provider's onboarding stage)
  • check_order_status (recent orders for caller)
  • book_strategy_call (Calendar create with calendar_id)
  • send_followup_sms (templated SMS post-call)
  • send_followup_email (templated email with catalog link)
  • transfer_to_human (warm transfer with summary)
  • log_call_outcome (write back to GHL + add tags)
  • tag_contact (add tag: interest:peptides, stage:qualified, do-not-contact)

Apply harness skill retellai-sdk-patterns for the SDK contract.

Step 5 — Wire the webhooks

Voice agent → webhook → GHL / Supabase / your backend.

Default Matrix webhook map:

  • Call started → log to _Logs/agent-decisions/ + GHL note
  • Call ended → update GHL contact: outcome tag, summary, next-step task
  • Booked meeting → calendar event + confirmation email/SMS sequence
  • Transferred to human → SMS the receiving human with context

Apply harness skills: retellai-webhooks-events, retellai-deploy-integration.

Step 6 — Compliance gates

Before the agent goes live:

For outbound (TCPA + state laws):

  • Consent documented or call type qualifies (B2B prospecting often does — verify with counsel)
  • Time-of-day window enforced (no calls outside 8am-9pm local recipient time)
  • State-specific consent: 12-party states require both-party consent for recording
  • DNC list scrubbed before dialing
  • Caller ID legitimate (no spoofing)

For inbound:

  • Call recording disclosure at the start ("This call may be recorded for quality and training")
  • Consent capture if any health/financial data discussed
  • HIPAA flag: if patient data could come up, route to HIPAA-aware handler

For all:

  • No medical claims beyond approved scope (Matrix: provider-only, peer-level, no diagnosis)
  • Required disclosures recorded (provider-only access, credentialing required, etc.)
  • Transfer-to-human option always available ("Want me to connect you with a human? Just say 'human' or 'agent'.")

Run security-inspector with compliance focus before launch.

Step 7 — Evaluate before launch

Apply Retell harness skill retellai-observability + retellai-policy-guardrails.

Eval set:

  • 20+ test conversations covering: happy path, voicemail, objections, edge cases, function failure
  • Metrics: meeting-set rate, hand-off rate, voicemail rate, avg call length, dropoff points, customer-feedback themes
  • Refusal accuracy: tests where the agent SHOULD hand off to human (medical question, complaint, billing dispute)

Step 8 — Ship through prod checklist

Apply retellai-prod-checklist + the OS audit-template. Block launch on:

  • 🔴 Critical compliance findings
  • Missing fallback (function fails → agent loops)
  • No kill switch
  • No monitoring dashboard

Step 9 — Output the build spec

Output structure

# Voice AI Agent: <Agent Name>

## Use case
- **Type**: outbound cold / inbound front-desk / discovery / support / etc.
- **Audience**: <who's on the other end>
- **Success outcome**: <what defines a successful call>

## Platform
**Chosen**: <Retell / Vapi / Synthflow / ElevenLabs / Tavus / custom>
**Reasoning**: <one-line why this platform>
**Harness skills to invoke**: <list of retellai-* etc.>

## Conversation flow
(from `conversation-flow-designer` — paste or link)

## Scripts
(from `voice-script-author` — paste or link)

## System prompt

Functions

Function What it does Inputs Output Backend

Webhook map

Event Endpoint Action
call.started log + GHL note
call.ended update contact, tag, summary
function.called route to specific MCP

Voice / persona

Setting Value
Voice ID (TTS) <Retell voice / ElevenLabs voice / etc.>
Speech rate <normal / slow / fast>
Filler tolerance <strict / natural>
Interruption handling <allow / wait>
LLM
LLM temperature <0.3-0.7 typical>

Compliance gates

  • TCPA window enforced
  • State-specific consent rules applied
  • DNC scrub configured
  • Recording disclosure scripted
  • HIPAA route (if applicable)
  • Transfer-to-human always available
  • Approved disclosures present

Evaluation

Eval set: <N test conversations, path to file> KPIs to track:

  • Meeting-set rate (target: <%>)
  • Hand-off rate (target: <%>)
  • Voicemail rate (target: <%>)
  • Avg call length (target: )
  • Customer feedback score (target: <X/5>)

Deployment

Staging URL: <retell agent ID / vapi assistant ID> Prod URL: Kill switch: <how to halt in 30 sec> Monitoring dashboard: Owner (on-call):

3-lane delegation

✅ I'll handle

  • Build the prompt + functions + state machine
  • Wire webhooks to GHL / Supabase
  • Deploy to staging
  • Run eval set
  • Produce launch checklist

⚠️ Need from you (Chris)

  • Approve the system prompt (final)
  • Approve compliance gates (especially TCPA + recording disclosure)
  • Approve the voice (ID + style)
  • Authorize production deploy
  • Approve first 50 live calls in monitoring (then auto-clear)

🚫 You only

  • Sign off on any contract with the voice provider (Retell / Vapi pricing)
  • Sign off on counsel-required compliance (state law specifics)
  • Any HIPAA-bearing voice path (counsel + clinician approval)

Handoff (use _Templates/handoff-template.md)

To:

  • security-inspector (compliance audit before launch)
  • ghl-crm-architect (if new pipeline / tags needed for call outcomes)
  • pmo-operator (register project + assign deadlines)

Risks surfaced

  • <risk: e.g., TCPA exposure if outbound to mobile-only numbers without prior consent>

Logged to

_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-voice-ai-build-<agent-name>.md


## Anti-patterns

- **Picking the platform before the use case** — use case dictates platform
- **Voice agent with no transfer-to-human** — every voice agent needs an "escape hatch"
- **No voicemail script** for outbound — half of calls hit voicemail
- **Writing the script in email voice** — invoke `voice-script-author`, written voice ≠ spoken voice
- **Functions without failure modes** — function fails → agent loops or hangs up
- **Launching without an eval set** — production-shipping with no test conversations is malpractice
- **Skipping `retellai-prod-checklist`** when on Retell

## Pre-authorizations

- ✅ Build prompt, design flow, write scripts, define functions (drafting)
- ✅ Deploy to staging
- ✅ Run evals
- ⚠️ Approve production deploy
- ⚠️ Approve compliance gates
- 🚫 Approve outbound campaign launch — always batch approval per campaign
- 🚫 Override a 🔴 from `security-inspector`

## Pairing

**Invokes**:
- `conversation-flow-designer` (flow)
- `voice-script-author` (scripts)
- `retellai-*` skills from harness (provider-specific)
- `synthflow-agent-builder` (if Synthflow chosen)
- `el-agents` / `agents` (if ElevenLabs)

**Outputs to**:
- `security-inspector` (compliance gate)
- `ghl-crm-architect` (CRM-side wiring)
- `pmo-operator` (project tracking)

**Chains with**:
- For outbound: `voice-ai-sales-agent` (uses this builder to ship)
- For inbound: `voice-ai-inbound-qualifier` (uses this builder)
- For evaluation: `retellai-observability` + `retellai-debug-bundle`

## One-liner reminder

**Use case → platform → flow → scripts → functions → webhooks → compliance → eval → prod gate. Never skip the eval. Never ship without a kill switch.**

skills.name: Voice Ai Inbound Qualifier
skills.description: Specific voice agent use case — answer inbound calls, qualify, route. Handles catalog requests, credentialing questions, demo requests, status check-ins, complaints. Books strategy calls. Transfers complex / sensitive cases to humans. For Matrix: replace front desk during/after hours, qualify inbound interest, book strategy calls.
skills.estimated_time: 2-4 hours build; ongoing weekly review of escalation rate
skills.body_html:
Voice AI Inbound Qualifier

The inbound side. Answers when Matrix's number rings. Qualifies. Routes. Books. Transfers to humans when needed.

When to use

  • Inbound number answering (any hours)
  • After-hours coverage (Mon-Fri 5pm-9am + weekends)
  • Overflow from human staff
  • Specific routing IVR replacement

When NOT to use

  • Outbound (use voice-ai-sales-agent)
  • Voice support for technical product questions (use a dedicated voice-support agent — different workflow)
  • HIPAA-bearing inbound (use HIPAA-compliant variant — flag in workflow)

Required inputs

  1. Phone number — the inbound number the agent answers
  2. Hours of operation — 24/7 or business-hours only or after-hours only
  3. Routing tree — what types of calls go where (catalog request → catalog send + book call, complaint → human transfer, etc.)
  4. Calendar IDs — for booking strategy calls
  5. Knowledge base — answers to FAQ (credentialing process, payment terms, lead times, return policy)
  6. Transfer numbers — where humans pick up (sales, support, accounting, exec)

If 1 or 3 is missing, ask: "What's the inbound number, and what are the 3-5 most common reasons people call?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Design the routing tree

For Matrix, typical inbound reasons + routes:

Caller intent Path Outcome
Catalog request Capture email + NPI Send catalog + book strategy call
Credentialing question Walk through NPPES process Send credentialing packet + book follow-up
Demo / strategy call Book directly Calendar event + confirmation
Order status / ETA Lookup in GHL → answer If complex → transfer to support
Billing question Transfer Human (accounting)
Complaint Transfer + escalate Human (account manager) + tag escalation
Affiliate question Send affiliate info Email packet
Hostile / threatening Transfer immediately Human + log
Medical question (about products) Disclaim + offer human callback Human (clinical lead)
"Speak to Chris / Leo / [exec]" Verify legitimacy → callback offer Voicemail or callback
Wrong number Polite end End

Step 2 — Design the flow (invoke conversation-flow-designer)

Use case: inbound qualification + routing

Flow shape:

  • GREETING → recording disclosure + open-ended ask
  • INTENT_DETECTION → categorize the call within 1-2 exchanges
  • ROUTE → branch per routing tree
  • HANDLE → execute the path (book / send / lookup / transfer)
  • CLOSE → confirm + next steps

Step 3 — Write scripts (invoke voice-script-author)

Per Matrix brand voice:

  • Persona: "Mia, Matrix Advanced Solutions front desk" (could be same persona as outbound for brand consistency, or a different name for inbound — pick one and stick with it)
  • Tone: calm, clinical, peer-level (NEVER customer-service-bot-cheery)

Critical scripts:

Greeting + disclosure:

"Thanks for calling Matrix Advanced Solutions. This call may be recorded for quality and training. How can I help today?"

Intent detection (open-ended; categorize after 1-2 user turns):

"Got it. Just to make sure I route you right — are you looking to request a catalog, ask about credentialing, book a strategy call, or something else?"

Catalog request path:

"Perfect. I'll send the catalog right now. What's the best email to send it to?" [Confirm email] "Got it. While I have you — would you like to grab 15 minutes on the calendar with our team to walk through what's most relevant for your clinic?"

Credentialing question path:

"Sure — credentialing for us means NPI verification through NPPES and a quick provider intake form. Most providers get cleared within 24-48 hours. I'll send the intake packet to your email. What's the best email?"

Strategy call booking:

"Got it. Looking at the calendar — I've got Tuesday at 2pm or Thursday at 10am open this week. Which works better?"

Transfer to human:

"Let me connect you with [team]. Hold one moment." [Transfer with context summary via webhook]

Medical question disclaimer:

"I'm not able to give clinical advice on specific patient cases — that's something our clinical team would handle. Want me to grab someone for you, or have them call you back?"

After-hours:

"Thanks for calling Matrix Advanced Solutions. We're closed right now — our hours are Monday through Friday, 9 to 5 Central. I can take a message, send you our catalog, or book a strategy call for the next business day. Which would you like?"

Hostile / aggressive caller:

"Let me connect you with a human right away." [Immediate transfer + flag the call]

"Are you a bot?"

"Yeah, I'm an AI voice assistant for Matrix. I can handle most things, but if you'd rather talk to a human, just say 'human' and I'll connect you."

Step 4 — Define the function set

Function Purpose Backend
lookup_caller_by_phone Caller ID → GHL contact GHL MCP
lookup_order_status Order ID or contact → recent orders GHL payments_*
send_catalog_email Trigger email with catalog link GHL email automation
send_credentialing_packet Email + SMS with intake form GHL templates
book_strategy_call Calendar event GHL calendars_create-event
transfer_to_human Warm transfer Twilio / Retell transfer
log_call Outcome + summary GHL contact note
tag_contact Tags per intent GHL tags
check_business_hours Is it currently open? local logic

Step 5 — After-hours handling

After 5pm CT or weekends → agent should NOT auto-book real-time. Instead:

  • Confirm message receipt
  • Offer email follow-up
  • Promise callback first business morning
  • Tag contact for AM callback queue

Step 6 — Compliance

  • Recording disclosure in greeting always
  • Transfer-to-human option always offered ("Want me to grab a human?")
  • No medical claims beyond approved language
  • No financial / billing commitments without human approval
  • HIPAA flag: if patient details surface, immediately route to HIPAA-trained handler (not this agent)

Run security-inspector before launch.

Step 7 — Eval

Test conversations:

  • Catalog request (happy path)
  • Credentialing question
  • Strategy call booking (happy path)
  • Order status question (in DB)
  • Order status question (not in DB → email follow-up)
  • Billing dispute → transfer
  • Complaint → transfer
  • After-hours catalog request → message taken
  • "Speak to Leo" → callback offer
  • Wrong number
  • "Are you a bot?"
  • Mis-hearing (twice → SMS fallback)
  • Medical question (must NOT answer → transfer or callback)

Step 8 — Ship through prod checklist

Apply retellai-prod-checklist.

Step 9 — Monitor + weekly tuning

Key metrics:

  • Handle rate — % of calls fully resolved by agent (target: 70%+)
  • Transfer rate — % escalated to humans (target: < 25%)
  • Booking rate — % of qualified inbound that books a call (target: 40%+)
  • Avg call length — for handled calls (target: 2-4 min)
  • Caller satisfaction — via post-call SMS survey (target: 4/5+)

Review weekly in weekly-review. Iterate scripts based on failure patterns.

Output structure

# Voice AI Inbound Qualifier: <Number / Department>

## Spec
**Number**: <inbound number>
**Hours**: <24/7 / business / after-hours>
**Persona**: Mia, Matrix Advanced Solutions front desk
**Top 5 call intents**: <list>

## Routing tree
(See routing table)

## Flow
(See `conversation-flow-designer` output)

## Scripts
(See `voice-script-author` output)

## Function set
(See voice-ai-agent-builder spec)

## Compliance
- [x] Recording disclosure
- [x] Transfer-to-human always available
- [x] No medical claims
- [x] No financial commitments
- [x] HIPAA route documented

## After-hours protocol
- No real-time booking after 5pm CT or weekends
- Message capture + AM callback queue
- Catalog send OK 24/7

## Evaluation
- 13 test conversations passed
- Handle rate (eval): <%>
- Transfer rate (eval): <%>

## ✅ I'll handle
- Build agent + scripts + functions
- Deploy to staging
- Eval pass
- Monitoring dashboard
- Daily / weekly outcome reporting

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve final scripts
- Authorize production cutover (current number traffic flips to agent)
- Approve transfer-target numbers (who picks up sales / support / accounting)
- Approve calendar IDs

## 🚫 You only
- Decide on Retell prod tier
- Hostile / litigious call escalations
- Any HIPAA-flagged call

## Risks surfaced
- <risk: e.g., if handle rate stays < 60% after week 2, need flow refactor>

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-voice-ai-inbound-<dept>.md`

Matrix-specific patterns

  • Always recording disclosure in greeting (default to most-restrictive state law)
  • Catalog send is the universal soft-close — even "wrong number" callers leave with catalog if they want
  • Provider-only language in any credentialing conversation
  • "Are you a bot?" answered honestly + transfer option offered
  • Compounding pharmacy logistics questions (lead time, shipping) — agent can handle from KB
  • Pricing questions — agent can give ranges + offer catalog send; doesn't quote specific Rx prices without context

Anti-patterns

  • No recording disclosure in greeting → state-law exposure
  • Auto-routing all calls to humans → defeats the purpose; tune the intent detector
  • No after-hours protocol → calls drop silently
  • Trying to answer medical questions → never; always disclaim + transfer
  • Agent forgets to offer transfer → frustrates callers, hurts brand
  • No post-call survey → can't measure CSAT
  • One-size persona for both outbound + inbound → diverging personas cause confusion when they overlap

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build + staging deploy
  • ✅ Eval set
  • ✅ Daily monitoring reports
  • ⚠️ Production cutover (traffic flip)
  • ⚠️ Calendar booking on Chris's calendar (per pre-auth in approval-rules.md)
  • 🚫 Any commitment of money / refund / pricing change

Pairing

Invokes:

  • voice-ai-agent-builder
  • conversation-flow-designer
  • voice-script-author
  • retellai-* skills
  • security-inspector

Outputs to:

  • ghl-crm-architect (CRM-side flow)
  • discovery-call-prep (auto-fire for each booked call)
  • email-triage (for transferred complaints requiring follow-up)
  • pmo-operator (project tracking)

Chains with:

  • Daily review via prep-day + ghl-daily-pulse
  • Weekly tuning via weekly-review

One-liner reminder

Recording disclosure first. Transfer-to-human always available. Catalog send is universal soft-close. Medical Qs → disclaim + transfer. After-hours = capture + AM callback. Never auto-quote pricing.

skills.name: Voice Ai Onboarding Agent
skills.description: Voice AI agent for new-customer kickoff calls. Walks new clinics through credentialing status, catalog access, account-manager intro, dosing-guide reference, first-order flow. Uses Sam persona (patient, thorough). Chains with customer-onboarding skill for the broader 30-day journey.
skills.intent: Hold the kickoff voice call after a deal closes. Patient pacing, thorough walk-through, confirmation of understanding at each step. End with: customer knows AM, has catalog access, knows where dosing guide lives, has clear next step (first order). Hand off to customer-onboarding for the 30-day journey.
skills.estimated_time: 2-3 hours build (clone from voice-ai-inbound-qualifier)
skills.body_html:
Voice AI Onboarding Agent

The kickoff voice. Patient. Thorough. Sam persona.

When to use

  • New customer kickoff call (within 7 days of deal close)
  • Re-onboarding a returning / reactivated customer
  • Multi-call onboarding for white-glove tier
  • Conducting the "30-day milestone" call autonomously

When NOT to use

  • Sales calls (use voice-ai-sales-agent)
  • Existing-customer status questions (use voice-ai-support-agent)
  • Cold prospects (different motion)
  • Anything sensitive / clinical (transfer to human)

Required inputs

  1. Customer record — GHL contact ID with onboarding stage info
  2. Closed deal — opportunity ID + offer + tier (per customer-onboarding)
  3. Call type — initial kickoff / 14-day / 30-day milestone
  4. Scheduled time — calendar event for the call
  5. Account manager — name (introduced by the agent during the call)

If 1 missing, ask: "Which customer (GHL contact ID) is being onboarded?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Clone from voice-ai-inbound-qualifier

Use agent-cloner:

  • Source: voice-ai-inbound-qualifier
  • New name: voice-ai-onboarding-agent
  • Differences:
    • Audience: just-closed customers (verified)
    • Persona: Sam (patient, thorough, slightly slower pace)
    • Goal: kickoff completion, NOT qualification
    • Pace: slow — give customer time to absorb
    • Functions: lookup-heavy (account status, AM contact, KB references)
    • Tone: warm welcome (still no exclamations / consumer tone)
    • Length: longer OK (15-30 min for kickoff is fine)
    • Skip TCPA cold restrictions (this is scheduled with consent)

Step 2 — Design the kickoff flow

1. OPEN (warm greeting)
   → "Hey Dr. [last name] — Sam from Matrix Advanced Solutions. Thanks for hopping on. Got 20 minutes to walk through your account?"

2. CONFIRM CONTEXT
   → Pull from GHL: offer purchased, tier, status
   → "Quick recap — you're set up with [offer], onboarding tier is [tier]. Sound right?"

3. WALK-THROUGH (modular per tier)
   → STANDARD tier:
     - Credentialing status (NPI verified Y/N)
     - Catalog access (logged in yet?)
     - Account manager intro ([AM name], email, what they handle)
     - Dosing guide pointer (matrix-dosing-guide URL or PDF)
     - First-order flow walk-through
   → WHITE-GLOVE tier (add):
     - Education resources (Peptide Map, Matrix Academy)
     - Custom protocol consultation offering
     - Direct AM cell number for urgent

4. CUSTOMER-DEFINED SUCCESS
   → "Last question — what would a 'successful' onboarding look like in 30 days for your clinic?"
   → Capture verbatim, save to GHL contact custom field `onboarding_success_definition`

5. CONFIRM NEXT STEPS
   → "Quick recap: [list 3-5 specific things they should do this week]"
   → "Any blockers I should know about?"

6. CLOSE
   → "Welcome aboard. [AM name] will follow up in 14 days. If anything comes up before then, hit [AM email] or call us back at [number]. Talk soon."

7. POST-CALL
   → Log full summary + customer success def to GHL contact
   → Tag onboarding milestone "kickoff_complete"
   → Trigger 14-day check-in calendar event for AM

Step 3 — Scripts (voice-script-author, Sam persona)

Sam-voice patterns:

  • Patient pacing: short sentences, frequent pauses, never rushed
  • Confirm understanding mid-flow: "Does that make sense so far?"
  • Allow time for absorption: built-in 2-3 sec pauses after dense info
  • Warm + welcoming (not effusive — still no exclamations)
  • Specific next steps at each transition
  • No upsell — kickoff is about activation, not expansion

Example transitions:

"Okay so first, let's check credentialing. [function call] Got it — your NPI's verified, you're cleared for catalog access. Have you had a chance to log in yet?"

"Next — your account manager is [name]. Their email is [email]. They handle [scope]. They'll do the 14-day check-in. Want me to text you their contact info now?"

Step 4 — Function set

Function Purpose Backend
lookup_customer_full All onboarding-relevant fields GHL contact + custom fields
check_credentialing NPI status, BAA status Contact custom fields
check_catalog_access Logged in? Last login date? Store DB or GHL tag
get_account_manager AM name + contact Contact account_manager field
get_first_order Has customer placed first order? GHL payments_list-transactions
send_followup_sms AM contact info, dosing guide link, login link GHL SMS
send_followup_email Onboarding packet, KB links GHL email
log_kickoff_complete Onboarding milestone tag GHL contact tags + note
schedule_14day_checkin Calendar event for AM GHL calendars + AM calendar
save_success_definition Customer-defined success GHL contact custom field

Step 5 — Eval cases (via voice-eval-harness)

  • Happy path standard tier
  • Happy path white-glove tier
  • Customer hasn't logged in to catalog yet (walks them through)
  • Customer has clinical question mid-call (disclaim + transfer + schedule callback)
  • Customer asks about a product not in their tier (no upsell — note + AM follows up)
  • Customer wants to add a stakeholder mid-call (schedule second kickoff with both)
  • Customer expresses concern about pricing (no negotiate — AM follows up)
  • Customer is hostile (rare for paid customers but possible — transfer + log)

Step 6 — Compliance (HIPAA-aware)

  • Recording disclosure: "This call may be recorded — okay?"
  • Patient identifiers: never logged unless minimum-necessary for the conversation
  • Clinical specifics: agent never gives advice; transfers to clinical team
  • Customer-success-def: stored in GHL custom field with PII flag

Output structure

# Voice AI Onboarding Agent: <Customer / Project>

## Spec
**Use case**: post-deal-close kickoff call (or 14-day, or 30-day)
**Persona**: Sam (patient, thorough)
**Pace**: slower than sales / support
**Length**: 15-30 min (kickoff); 5-15 min (check-ins)

## Flow + Scripts
(See `conversation-flow-designer` + `voice-script-author` outputs)

## Function set
(See full set above)

## Compliance
- [x] Recording disclosure
- [x] HIPAA-aware (minimum-necessary)
- [x] No clinical advice
- [x] No upsell

## Customer-defined success captured
Field in GHL: `onboarding_success_definition`

## Evaluation
- 8+ eval cases passed
- Kickoff-completion rate target: 95%+
- Customer satisfaction target: 4.5/5+

## KPIs in production
- Kickoff completion rate
- Customer satisfaction (post-call SMS)
- First-order rate within 14 days
- 30-day Active Customer conversion rate

## ✅ I'll handle
- Build via clone from voice-ai-inbound-qualifier
- Wire functions to GHL + customer record
- Deploy to staging + eval

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve scripts (Sam persona pacing review)
- Approve compliance posture (HIPAA review with counsel if needed)
- Authorize production
- Approve calendar booking on AM calendars

## 🚫 You only
- HIPAA-bearing customer escalations
- Any pricing / contract changes mid-call

## Risks surfaced
- <risk: e.g., customer-defined success captures might surface clinical questions — clear transfer path required>

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-voice-ai-onboarding-<customer>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Sales pitch during onboarding — kickoff is about activation, not expansion
  • Rushing the walk-through — slow pacing IS the value here
  • Skipping customer-success-def — kills the 30-day review's effectiveness
  • No confirmation of understanding — customers nod through and then forget
  • Generic kickoff for all tiers — standard vs white-glove need different scripts
  • AM intro buried — should be ~1/3 of the call, not a 10-second mention

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build + staging
  • ✅ Eval cases
  • ✅ Production deploy after Chris approval
  • ✅ Calendar booking on AM calendars (pre-authorized in approval-rules.md for scheduled onboarding)
  • ⚠️ Add new persona variants
  • 🚫 Modify the customer's pricing / offer / contract
  • 🚫 Make commitments on behalf of AM

Pairing

Inputs from: customer-onboarding (the broader 30-day flow), voice-persona-library (Sam), voice-ai-agent-builder Outputs to: customer-onboarding (kickoff_complete milestone), ghl-crm-architect (CRM tagging), Account Manager Agent (planned), voice-call-reviewer (weekly review) Chains with: voice-eval-harness (gate), voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md

One-liner reminder

Sam persona. Patient pacing. Walk-through, not pitch. Customer-defined success captured. AM intro emphasized. Tag kickoff_complete + trigger 14-day check-in.

skills.name: Voice Ai Sales Agent
skills.description: Specific voice agent use case — outbound cold qualification for B2B sales. Calls a target list, navigates gatekeepers, identifies decision-makers, qualifies against ICP, books strategy calls, leaves voicemail, sends follow-up SMS/email. Composes voice-ai-agent-builder + conversation-flow-designer + voice-script-author end-to-end. For Matrix: cold provider offices.
best_for: Outbound cold prospecting to a verified list
skills.estimated_time: 2-4 hours build; ongoing per-campaign approval gates
skills.body_html:
Voice AI Sales Agent — Outbound

The specific outbound use case. Wraps the meta voice agent builder with sales-specific patterns and Matrix-grade compliance.

When to use

  • Outbound cold campaign to a verified list (NPI-verified for Matrix)
  • Re-engagement of dormant leads (last touch > 90 days)
  • Conference / event lead follow-up at scale
  • Trial / catalog request followers who haven't booked

When NOT to use

  • Inbound calls (use voice-ai-inbound-qualifier)
  • Live human SDR work (no agent needed)
  • Existing customers (use voice-ai-support / customer-success voice)
  • Mass cold-call to unverified consumer list (TCPA exposure)

Required inputs

  1. Target list — verified phone numbers + names + clinic / org names + (ideally) intent signals
  2. Campaign objective — book strategy call / qualify + tag / book demo / catalog send
  3. Offer to lead with — usually one specific offer (per _Shared Context/offers.md)
  4. Daily call volume — feasible per provider rate limits + TCPA windows
  5. Caller ID — what number shows up on recipient's phone (must be legitimate, owned by Matrix)
  6. State restrictions — list of states with single-party / two-party consent requirements
  7. Existing GHL pipeline — where qualified contacts go after success

If 1 is missing, ask: "Where's the target list — GHL filter, CSV path, or Apollo export?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Verify the list

Before any agent build, validate the list:

  • All phone numbers verified (no scrubbed / disconnect / wrong-format)
  • NPI-verified (for Matrix — required)
  • DNC list scrubbed (national + state DNC)
  • No prior unsubscribes (check GHL do-not-contact tag)
  • No active customer (don't cold-call existing customers)
  • Intent signal documented per contact (recent funding, hire, post, ad engagement, content view, etc.)

Without intent signals → output rejected. Outbound to unsegmented list = TCPA exposure + brand damage (Hawkins' 97% rule).

Step 2 — Design the flow (invoke conversation-flow-designer)

Use case: outbound cold qualification

Specific Matrix flow:

  • OPEN → permission ask + signal-driven first line
  • IDENTIFY (if gatekeeper) → request the decision-maker or callback time
  • DISCOVER → 2-3 qualifying questions (pain, current vendor, volume)
  • QUALIFY → ICP match / mismatch → branch
  • PROPOSE (if match) → catalog + strategy call
  • HANDLE (objections) → reframe
  • CLOSE (success) → book + confirmation + SMS/email
  • CLOSE (soft) → email catalog only, no call
  • CLOSE (hard no) → tag DNC, end

Step 3 — Write scripts (invoke voice-script-author)

Per the Matrix brand voice:

  • Persona: "Mia, Matrix Advanced Solutions provider success specialist"
  • Tone: calm, clinical, peer-level
  • Compliance: provider-only language present; recording disclosure if state requires

Critical scripts to produce:

  • Opener with signal reference: not generic. References the intent signal.

    "Hey Dr. Patel — Mia from Matrix Advanced Solutions. Saw your clinic just added a weight-loss vertical last month. Quick context: we work with clinics in your spot to consolidate 503-A compounding, 503-B catalog, and peptides into one credentialed line. Got a couple minutes?"

  • Gatekeeper pivot:

    "Got it. When's a good time to reach Dr. Patel directly, or is there a better number?"

  • Voicemail: 20-25 sec script (see voice-script-author template)

  • Catalog-only soft close:

    "All good — I'll send the catalog over right now. What's the best email?"

  • Hard no with DNC:

    "Totally understand. I'll make sure we don't reach out again. Thanks for your time."

Step 4 — Define the function set

Functions this agent needs (per voice-ai-agent-builder Step 4):

Function Purpose Backend
lookup_contact_by_phone Caller ID → GHL contact + last interaction GHL MCP
verify_npi_active Confirm provider NPI is still active NPI/NPPES check
book_strategy_call Create calendar event GHL calendars_create-event
send_followup_sms Send templated SMS post-call GHL conversations_send-a-new-message (SMS)
send_followup_email Send catalog email GHL email send
tag_contact Add tags (stage:qualified, interest:peptides, do-not-contact, etc.) GHL contacts_add-tags
log_call_outcome Write call summary + outcome to contact note GHL contacts_update-contact
transfer_to_human Warm transfer with summary Twilio / Retell transfer

Step 5 — Compliance gates (TCPA + state laws)

Hard requirements before launch:

  • TCPA window: 8am–9pm recipient local time. Enforce via Retell timezone config + GHL contact timezone field.
  • State two-party consent (CA, FL, IL, MD, MA, MT, NH, NV, PA, WA, etc.): include recording disclosure in opener.
  • DNC scrub: at list-load time, scrub against national + state DNC lists.
  • Caller ID legitimate: Matrix-owned number, not spoofed.
  • B2B vs. residential: B2B is generally TCPA-exempt but cell-phone-on-business-list still risks DNC violation. Verify list source.
  • No prerecorded messages on cell phones without prior express written consent.
  • Hang-up gracefully if voicemail is the company's, not provider's.

Run security-inspector → routes to general path (not HIPAA unless patient data could surface).

Step 6 — Daily call volume math

Per provider (Retell agent) daily capacity:

  • 100-300 dials/day per agent (Retell can run parallel)
  • ~30-40% connect rate (rest = voicemail / no-answer)
  • ~20-30% reach decision-maker (rest = gatekeeper / wrong number)
  • ~5-15% book a meeting on connected calls (depends on signal quality + ICP fit)

Realistic for Matrix: 100 dials → 30 connects → 8-12 decision-maker conversations → 2-4 meetings booked.

To hit 20 meetings/month: ~5 days of dialing/month per agent × 100/day. Capacity: 1 Retell agent + monitoring.

State the math in the launch plan.

Step 7 — Eval before launch

Test conversations (run via Retell staging):

  • Happy path: receptive provider → meeting booked
  • Gatekeeper: blocked, request callback
  • Voicemail
  • "We have a vendor" objection
  • "Send me info" soft close
  • "Are you a bot?" (must answer honestly)
  • Recording-disclosure state (12-party state)
  • Mis-hearing (twice → SMS fallback)
  • Silence drop
  • Transfer to human request

Minimum pass criteria:

  • 95%+ correct outcome classification
  • 100% compliance phrase delivery (recording disclosure in required states)
  • 0 medical claims beyond brand-approved language
  • 100% transfer-to-human availability

Step 8 — Ship through prod checklist

Apply retellai-prod-checklist + security-inspector gate.

Step 9 — Batch approval before each live campaign

Critical: even after agent is shipped, each new campaign requires Chris approval:

  • List size
  • List source + verification status
  • Campaign window (days + hours)
  • Daily call cap
  • Auto-stop trigger (if connect-rate < threshold or DNC complaints > threshold)

Step 10 — Monitor + iterate

After launch, daily monitoring via retellai-observability:

  • Call outcomes distribution
  • Meeting-set rate trend
  • Hand-off rate
  • Customer feedback themes
  • Cost per call + cost per meeting booked

Weekly: review with weekly-review skill → propose script iterations.

Output structure

# Voice AI Sales Agent: Outbound Cold — <Campaign Name>

## Spec
**Use case**: outbound cold qualification
**Objective**: book strategy call with credentialed clinic decision-makers
**Offer led with**: <offer name from `offers.md`>
**Target list size**: <N contacts>
**List source**: <where verified>
**Daily call cap**: <N dials/day>

## Platform
**Provider**: Retell AI
**Agent ID** (staging): <id>
**Agent ID** (prod, after approval): <id>
**LLM**: <model>
**TTS voice**: <Retell voice>
**Persona**: Mia, Matrix Advanced Solutions provider success specialist

## Flow
(See `conversation-flow-designer` output: `_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-flow-<campaign>.md`)

## Scripts
(See `voice-script-author` output: `_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-scripts-<campaign>.md`)

## Function set
(See full function definitions in voice-ai-agent-builder spec)

## Compliance gates
- [ ] TCPA window: 8am-9pm recipient local
- [ ] DNC scrub: complete (date)
- [ ] State two-party consent: <list of states + how handled>
- [ ] Caller ID verified: <number>
- [ ] B2B-eligibility: <verified>
- [ ] Voicemail script: present

## Daily volume math
- List: <N> verified contacts
- Connect rate (assumed): 30%
- Decision-maker reach (assumed): 25% of connects
- Meeting-set rate (assumed): 10% of DM conversations
- Daily dials: <N>
- Daily meetings booked (target): <N>
- Days to exhaust list: <N>

## Evaluation
- Eval set: 12 test conversations (see <link>)
- Pass criteria: <criteria>
- Status: ✅ passed / ⚠️ <issues>

## ✅ I'll handle
- Build the agent (flow + scripts + functions)
- Deploy to Retell staging
- Run eval set
- Compliance checklist
- Set up monitoring dashboard
- Daily monitoring + outcome logging to GHL

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve final scripts before staging deploy
- Approve list source + verification
- Approve compliance posture (state law specifics)
- Authorize production deploy
- Batch-approve each campaign launch (list + cap + window)
- Monitor first 50 live calls + clear or pause

## 🚫 You only
- Sign Retell pricing tier
- Counsel sign-off on state-specific compliance
- Any escalation from a live call where caller is hostile / litigious

## Handoff
**To** (post-call success):
- `ghl-crm-architect` (ensure pipeline + tags exist for the call outcomes)
- `discovery-call-prep` (auto-fire for each booked meeting)
- `cold-email-sequence` (for catalog-only soft-close path)

**To** (failures):
- `security-inspector` if compliance issue
- Human escalation if hostile call

## Risks surfaced
- <risk: e.g., 18 of 200 contacts in 2-party-consent states — disclosure script must fire reliably>
- <risk>

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-voice-ai-sales-<campaign>.md`
`_Logs/campaign-results/<campaign>-<date>.md` (post-launch results)

Matrix-specific patterns

  • Open with NPI signal: "Saw your NPI shows you're credentialed in [specialty]" — peer-level acknowledgment.
  • Lead with consolidation pain: "Most clinics in your spot juggle 3-4 vendors..."
  • Always mention credentialing reality — providers respect this; positions us upstream.
  • Catalog-as-magnet soft close — even soft-no's get value (catalog email).
  • Never claim medical outcomes — provider audience knows the literature. We sell supply + systems, not outcomes.

Anti-patterns

  • Cold-calling unverified lists — TCPA exposure
  • Generic openers ("Hi, this is Mia from Matrix Advanced Solutions, calling to see if you might be interested in...") — kills connect → conversation rate
  • Multiple CTAs in one call — "book OR email OR get a catalog" confuses
  • Auto-launching campaigns without batch approval — never
  • No voicemail script — half the calls
  • No transfer-to-human path — providers occasionally want a person
  • Skipping the eval set — production-deploying a voice agent without testing is malpractice
  • Persisting calls after voicemail twice — TCPA exposure + brand damage

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build agent + run staging
  • ✅ Run eval set
  • ✅ Send daily monitoring reports
  • ⚠️ Production deploy
  • ⚠️ Each campaign launch (batch)
  • 🚫 Auto-launch campaign without approval

Pairing

Invokes:

  • voice-ai-agent-builder (the meta builder)
  • conversation-flow-designer
  • voice-script-author
  • retellai-* skills (production patterns)
  • security-inspector (compliance gate)

Outputs to:

  • ghl-crm-architect (CRM-side wiring)
  • discovery-call-prep (for each booked call)
  • cold-email-sequence (for soft-close paths)
  • pmo-operator (project + sprint tracking)

Chains with:

  • outbound-voice-sales.workflow.md (the end-to-end flow file)

One-liner reminder

Verified list + intent signal. Permission ask. One offer. One next step. Voicemail script ready. Recording disclosure in 2-party states. Batch-approve every campaign. Never auto-launch.

skills.name: Voice Ai Support Agent
skills.description: Voice AI agent for existing-customer support — order status, catalog questions, credentialing follow-ups, simple FAQ. Cloned pattern from voice-ai-inbound-qualifier but tuned for customer ops (faster pace, more functional, less qualification). Uses Alex persona. Transfers to humans for anything sensitive.
skills.intent: Handle the high-volume, low-complexity customer ops calls autonomously (order status, "is my catalog request processed", "what's the lead time on X", "can I get a tracking number"). Reduce front-desk load. Transfer complex / sensitive / clinical questions to humans.
skills.estimated_time: 2-3 hours build (faster than from scratch — clones inbound-qualifier pattern)
skills.body_html:
Voice AI Support Agent

The customer-side voice agent. Existing customers, functional questions, fast resolution.

When to use

  • Inbound calls from existing customers (verified by phone → GHL contact lookup)
  • Outbound proactive callbacks (order shipped, catalog ready, credentialing approved)
  • Routing existing-customer calls away from sales agents

When NOT to use

  • Cold prospect calls (use voice-ai-sales-agent)
  • New-customer onboarding kickoff (use voice-ai-onboarding-agent)
  • Clinical questions (immediate transfer-to-human)
  • Billing disputes (transfer to human accounting)

Required inputs

  1. Inbound number — which line this agent answers
  2. CRM linkage — must have GHL contact lookup wired
  3. Order DB linkage — must have order-status function via GHL payments_* or store API
  4. Knowledge base — for FAQ answers (see voice-knowledge-base skill)
  5. Transfer destinations — human escalation numbers

If 2-3 missing, ask: "Is the GHL contact + order lookup wired? Support without those is impossible."

Workflow

Step 1 — Clone from voice-ai-inbound-qualifier

Use agent-cloner with:

  • Source: voice-ai-inbound-qualifier
  • New name: voice-ai-support-agent
  • Differences:
    • Audience: existing customers (verified) instead of cold inbound
    • Persona: Alex (faster pace, functional) not Mia
    • Goal: resolve issue + log, NOT qualify + book
    • Functions: more lookup-heavy (order status, catalog status, credentialing status)
    • Pace: faster (customers want answers, not rapport)
    • No sales pitch (never propose new offers in support — separate skill)

Step 2 — Design the flow

Per conversation-flow-designer for support use case:

1. GREETING + recording disclosure
   → "Thanks for calling Matrix support. This call may be recorded. How can I help?"

2. CALLER VERIFICATION
   → Lookup contact by caller ID
   → If known: "Hey [first name], I see you with [clinic name]. How can I help?"
   → If unknown: "Could I get your name + clinic name?"

3. INTENT DETECTION
   → Route per:
     - "checking on an order" → ORDER_STATUS
     - "catalog / credentialing question" → STATUS_LOOKUP
     - "have a question about [product]" → FAQ
     - "billing / payment issue" → TRANSFER (human)
     - "clinical / medical question" → DISCLAIM + TRANSFER (human clinical)
     - "want to talk to my account manager" → TRANSFER to AM
