     - "complaint" → TRANSFER (human + flag)

4. RESOLUTION
   → Per intent path

5. CONFIRMATION + close
   → "Anything else?"
   → Log call summary to GHL contact note

Step 3 — Write scripts (voice-script-author, Alex persona)

Specific Alex-voice scripts for support:

Greeting:

"Matrix support — this is Alex. This call may be recorded. How can I help?"

Verified customer:

"Hey [first name], I see you with [clinic]. What's going on today?"

Order status (after lookup):

"Got it — order [number] from [date]. It's [status]. [If shipped:] Tracking shows [carrier] [number], expected [date]. [If processing:] Looks like it's in production at the pharmacy; ETA is [date]."

Order issue (item missing, wrong, damaged):

"Sorry to hear — let me log this and route it to support. You'll get a follow-up email within an hour."

Credentialing status:

"Pulling that up... I see [status]. [If approved:] You should have catalog access — try logging in at matrix-order-form.vercel.app. [If pending:] Still in review, typically 24-48 hours — I'll send you a text confirmation when it clears."

Clinical question — disclaim + transfer:

"Clinical question — that's our clinical lead's call. Let me get them on the line or have them call you back. Which works better?"

Account manager request:

"Let me connect you with [AM name]. One moment."

Function-call wait:

"Pulling that up — one second."

Function-call fail:

"My system's a little slow — let me text you the info instead. What's the best number?"

Step 4 — Define the function set

Function Purpose Backend
lookup_contact_by_phone Caller ID → GHL contact GHL MCP
lookup_order_by_id Order ID → status + ETA + tracking GHL payments_get-order-by-id
lookup_recent_orders Contact → last N orders GHL payments_list-transactions
check_credentialing_status Contact → onboarding stage GHL contact custom fields
check_catalog_access Contact → can they log into store GHL contact tag check
lookup_product_lead_time Product → current ETA from products.md or product DB
send_followup_email Templated email with details GHL email
send_followup_sms Templated SMS with link GHL SMS
log_call_outcome Note + tag on contact GHL contact note
transfer_to_human Warm transfer with summary Twilio / Retell transfer
transfer_to_account_manager Specific AM for this contact GHL contact account_manager field

Step 5 — Compliance (lighter than sales)

  • Recording disclosure in greeting ✓
  • Transfer-to-human always available ✓
  • No clinical advice (immediate disclaim + transfer) ✓
  • No payment commitments (transfer to accounting) ✓
  • No upselling / sales pitch mid-support call ✓
  • HIPAA-aware: if patient identifiers come up, log only minimum-necessary

Step 6 — Eval set (via voice-eval-harness)

Specific support cases:

  • Order status (recent + old)
  • Order issue (missing item, wrong dosing, damaged)
  • Credentialing status (approved, pending, denied)
  • Product lead-time question
  • Account manager request
  • Clinical question (must disclaim + transfer)
  • Billing question (must transfer)
  • "Are you a bot?"
  • Mis-heard
  • Hostile caller (transfer immediately)

Step 7 — Ship through prod checklist

Per voice-ai-agent-builder Step 8.

Output structure

# Voice AI Support Agent: <Number / Department>

## Spec
**Number**: <support inbound>
**Hours**: <24/7 / business / after-hours>
**Persona**: Alex (functional, fast-paced)
**Goal**: resolve in 60-180 sec OR transfer

## Flow + Scripts
(See `conversation-flow-designer` + `voice-script-author` outputs)

## Function set
(See full set above)

## Compliance
- [x] Recording disclosure
- [x] Transfer-to-human always
- [x] No clinical advice
- [x] No upsell

## Evaluation
- 12 test cases passed via `voice-eval-harness`
- Resolution rate (eval): <%>
- Transfer rate (eval): <%>

## KPIs in production
- Resolution rate (target 75%+)
- Transfer rate (target < 20%)
- Avg call length (target < 180 sec)
- Customer satisfaction (post-call SMS: target 4.2/5+)

## ✅ I'll handle
- Build via clone from voice-ai-inbound-qualifier
- Wire functions to GHL + product DB
- Deploy to staging + eval

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve final scripts (Alex persona)
- Approve compliance gates
- Authorize production cutover
- Approve calendar transfer destinations (AM numbers)

## 🚫 You only
- Decide tier upgrade (high-volume CS warrants more capacity)
- Hostile / litigious call escalation
- Any HIPAA-flagged call

## Risks surfaced
- <risk: e.g., if function failure rate > 5%, agent becomes useless — needs robust fallbacks>

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-voice-ai-support-<dept>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Upselling mid-support call — banned
  • Treating support as qualification — different motion entirely
  • Long rapport-building — customers want answers fast
  • Trying to answer clinical questions — immediate disclaim + transfer
  • No order-DB function — support without lookup is useless
  • Same persona as sales — sales = rapport-first; support = answer-first

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build + staging deploy
  • ✅ Eval set
  • ✅ Daily monitoring + outcome logging
  • ⚠️ Production cutover
  • ⚠️ Calendar booking for AM (per approval-rules.md)
  • 🚫 Issue credits / refunds
  • 🚫 Modify customer orders / pricing

Pairing

Inputs from: voice-ai-agent-builder, conversation-flow-designer, voice-script-author, voice-persona-library (Alex), voice-knowledge-base Outputs to: ghl-crm-architect (CRM wiring), pmo-operator (project tracking), voice-call-reviewer (weekly review) Chains with: voice-eval-harness (gate), voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md

One-liner reminder

Existing customers. Alex persona. Lookup-heavy. Resolve fast or transfer. Never upsell. Never give clinical advice. Recording disclosure first.

skills.name: Voice Call Reviewer
skills.description: Analyzes real call transcripts + recordings from production voice agents. Surfaces patterns (what's working, what's failing), classifies dropoffs by stage, proposes specific script + prompt + flow improvements. The continuous-improvement engine for every voice AI agent. Runs weekly minimum, or after any 50-call batch.
skills.intent: Stop shipping voice agents and hoping. Read real call data, surface patterns, propose evidence-based improvements. Output goes to voice-script-author / conversation-flow-designer / voice-ai-agent-builder for the next iteration. Closes the production-to-improvement loop.
best_for: Weekly review of production voice agents
skills.estimated_time: 30-90 min depending on call volume
skills.body_html:
Voice Call Reviewer

The improvement engine. Real data → specific script / flow / prompt changes.

When to use

  • Weekly review (auto-scheduled via automation-scheduler)
  • After any batch of 50+ calls
  • After a script iteration to compare before/after
  • When a KPI dropped > 10% week-over-week

When NOT to use

  • Initial pre-launch evaluation (use voice-eval-harness for synthetic tests)
  • Single-call coaching (read the transcript directly)
  • Code-side debugging (use Retell retellai-debug-bundle)

Required inputs

  1. Agent ID — which Retell/Vapi/Synthflow agent
  2. Date range — last 7 days / 30 days / specific window
  3. Sample size target — full / random 50 / failed-only / specific outcome
  4. Comparison baseline — vs prior period / vs eval set / vs target

If 1 missing, ask: "Which agent — sales / inbound / support / onboarding?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Pull the data

  • Call recordings + transcripts via Retell observability API (or equivalent)
  • Outcome tags from GHL contact notes (outcome:booked, outcome:catalog-only, etc.)
  • Duration per call
  • Function call success/failure logs
  • Transfer events
  • Caller feedback (post-call SMS survey if collected)

Step 2 — Classify each call by outcome

Outcome Definition
success-booked Goal achieved (meeting booked, sale, action taken)
success-soft Partial win (catalog sent, email follow-up scheduled)
lost-objection Caller engaged but rejected — note the objection
lost-disqualified Caller doesn't fit ICP — disqualified mid-call
voicemail Hit voicemail; voicemail script left
gatekeeper Blocked by receptionist / front desk
technical Agent / system failure
transferred Escalated to human
abandoned Caller hung up mid-call without clear reason
wrong-number Wrong contact

Step 3 — Identify dropoff stage

For lost / abandoned calls, identify the stage where conversation went off-rails:

  • OPEN — caller wasn't engaged from line 1
  • DISCOVER — couldn't extract key info
  • PROPOSE — value pitch missed
  • HANDLE — objection wasn't answered well
  • CLOSE — failed to lock the next step

For each stage, count occurrences. Surface the worst-performing stage.

Step 4 — Pattern detection (qualitative)

For each outcome class, sample 5-10 transcripts and extract:

  • Verbatim quotes of what worked (in success calls)
  • Verbatim quotes of what failed (in lost calls)
  • Objections actually heard (vs anticipated)
  • Questions the agent struggled to answer
  • Function call failures (and what the agent said when they failed)
  • Caller emotional signals (frustration, confusion, interest)

Step 5 — Quantify the metrics

Metric This period Prior Delta Target
Connect rate <%> <%> <%>
Meeting-set rate <%> <%> <%>
Avg call length (success)
Avg call length (lost)
Transfer rate <%> <%> <%>
Voicemail rate <%> <%> <%>
Function failure rate <%> <%> <%>
Cost per call $ $ $
Cost per success $ $ $

Step 6 — Generate improvement proposals

For each pattern identified, propose specific changes. Use a Recommendations table:

Issue Evidence Proposal Owner skill Priority
23% drop at PROPOSE for clinic owners 12 transcripts show generic value pitch Rewrite PROPOSE state with clinic-owner-specific wedge from offers.md voice-script-author High
Voicemail rate 65% (target 45%) TCPA window vs call times mismatch Tighten dialing window to 9-11am + 2-4pm recipient local voice-ai-agent-builder High
11 "are you a bot" with awkward responses Current handler too long Shorten "are you a bot" response to 12 words voice-script-author Medium

Step 7 — Surface coaching moments (for humans)

If human reviewers / closers are involved:

  • "Calls 3, 7, 12 — listen to these for [pattern]"
  • "Caller mentioned [signal] — worth following up via SMS"
  • "Booked meeting 5 was unusual — caller had [context], approach worked because [reason]"

Step 8 — Output the review

Output structure

# Call Review: <Agent Name> — <Date Range>

## Summary
- **Calls analyzed**: <N>
- **Period**: <range>
- **Sample method**: <full / random / outcome-stratified>

## Outcomes

| success-soft | <N> | <%> | <%> | <pp> |
| lost-objection | <N> | <%> | <%> | <pp> |
| ... | | | | |

## Stage dropoff

| OPEN | <N> | <%> | <observation> |
| DISCOVER | <N> | <%> | <observation> |

| CLOSE | <N> | <%> | <observation> |

## Worst-performing stage: <stage>

## Patterns from transcripts

- "<quote>"
- "<quote>"
- "<quote>"

### What failed (verbatim quotes from lost calls)
- "<quote>"
- "<quote>"
- "<quote>"

### Objections actually heard (vs anticipated)

|---|---|---|---|

### Questions agent struggled with

## Metrics

|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| (full table from Step 5) |

## 🛠 Improvement proposals

| # | Issue | Evidence | Proposal | Owner skill | Priority | ETA |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | <issue> | <evidence count + quote refs> | <specific change> | voice-script-author | High | 2 days |
| 2 | <issue> | <evidence> | <change> | conversation-flow-designer | Med | 3 days |
| 3 | <issue> | <evidence> | <change> | voice-ai-agent-builder | High | 1 day |

## Coaching moments (for humans, if applicable)
- <specific call IDs to review>

## ✅ I handled
- Pulled + classified + analyzed all <N> calls
- Surfaced patterns + proposals
- Generated specific change requests

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve which proposals to ship this iteration
- Approve any auto-pause decisions (if metrics dropped sharply)
- Review the "what worked" quotes — surface anything to add to brand voice exemplars

## 🚫 You only
- Decide to kill the agent if metrics are catastrophic
- Approve major scope / persona changes
- Decision on caller-specific follow-ups (sensitive context)

## Handoff
**To** (per proposal owners):
- `voice-script-author` (for script changes)
- `conversation-flow-designer` (for flow changes)
- `voice-ai-agent-builder` (for platform changes)
- `voice-eval-harness` (add the new patterns as eval cases)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-call-review-<agent>.md`
`_Logs/campaign-results/<agent>/<date>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Vibes-based review — must cite specific transcript evidence
  • Recommending changes without expected impact — note "should improve X by Y%"
  • Same proposal every week — if 3 reviews surface the same issue, escalate to flow/persona refactor, not script tweak
  • Skipping the "what worked" section — losing the winning patterns is the bigger loss
  • No regression check — every proposal goes through voice-eval-harness before ship

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull recordings + transcripts (read-only)
  • ✅ Classify + analyze
  • ✅ Generate proposals
  • ⚠️ Approve which proposals to ship (Chris)
  • 🚫 Auto-edit production agent scripts
  • 🚫 Auto-pause production agent (alert + recommend; Chris pauses)

Pairing

Inputs from: Retell observability API / equivalent, GHL contact notes, post-call survey data Outputs to: voice-script-author, conversation-flow-designer, voice-ai-agent-builder, voice-eval-harness Chains with: voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md, weekly-review, pmo-operator

Scheduled automation

Recommended cron via automation-scheduler:

  • Weekly Friday 3pm: voice-call-reviewer for every active production voice agent → output to _Logs/campaign-results/

One-liner reminder

Real transcript evidence. Classify outcomes. Find the dropoff stage. Propose specific changes with owner skills. Never auto-edit production. Never skip "what worked".

skills.name: Voice Eval Harness
skills.description: Build + run + maintain the eval suite for voice AI agents. Grows continuously — every failure pattern from voice-call-reviewer becomes a new eval case. Catches regressions before any script change goes live. Required gate before every voice agent production change.
skills.intent: Never ship a voice agent change without an eval pass. Build the test corpus once, grow it forever (each production failure → new eval case). Run on every prompt/script/flow change. Block prod deploy if any regression.
best_for: Pre-deploy gate for voice agent changes
Weekly regression run on active agents
Adding a new test case after a production failure
skills.estimated_time: 20-60 min depending on suite size
skills.body_html:
Voice Eval Harness

The regression gate. Suite grows forever. No production change ships without a clean pass.

When to use

  • Before any voice agent script / prompt / flow change goes to production
  • Weekly automated run on every production agent (catches drift)
  • Adding a new eval case (after voice-call-reviewer surfaces a new failure pattern)
  • A/B comparison between two script versions

When NOT to use

  • Initial script authoring (use voice-script-author + manual review first; eval comes after)
  • Code-side debugging (use retellai-debug-bundle)
  • One-time spot check (just listen to 3 sample calls)

Required inputs

  1. Agent under test — which Retell agent ID (could be staging vs prod)
  2. Eval suite — the set of test conversations to run
  3. Pass criteria — overall thresholds per metric
  4. Comparison baseline — vs last run / vs target

If 2 missing, ask: "Which eval suite — sales / inbound / support / onboarding — or fresh build?"

Eval suite structure

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/<agent-name>/ with this shape:

_Logs/eval-suites/voice-ai-sales/
├── suite.yaml                    # metadata + thresholds
├── cases/
│   ├── 001-happy-path-cold.yaml
│   ├── 002-gatekeeper-pivot.yaml
│   ├── 003-voicemail.yaml
│   ├── 004-objection-vendor.yaml
│   ├── 005-objection-price.yaml
│   ├── 006-objection-timing.yaml
│   ├── 007-are-you-a-bot.yaml
│   ├── 008-misheard-recovery.yaml
│   ├── 009-silence-handling.yaml
│   ├── 010-function-call-fail.yaml
│   ├── 011-transfer-request.yaml
│   ├── 012-hostile-caller.yaml
│   ├── 013-medical-question.yaml   (must NOT answer)
│   ├── 014-recording-disclosure-state.yaml
│   ├── 015-after-hours-callback.yaml
│   └── (grows over time from voice-call-reviewer findings)
└── runs/
    └── YYYY-MM-DD-HHmm.md          # one per run

Case file shape (each .yaml)

id: 001-happy-path-cold
description: Provider answers, receptive, agrees to book
caller_persona: clinic-owner-receptive
initial_state: outbound-dialing
expected_outcome: success-booked
pass_criteria:
  - meeting_booked: true
  - call_length: 90-180 sec
  - recording_disclosure_delivered: true (if state requires)
  - transfer_offered_if_requested: n/a
  - no_banned_phrases: true
test_script: |
  [Tester input lines, simulated]
  Line 1 (caller): "Hello?"
  Line 2 (agent): [expected to deliver opener]
  Line 3 (caller): "Yes, this is Dr. Patel."
  ...
soft_assertions:
  - opener_references_signal: true
  - permission_ask_present: true
  - one_clear_next_step_in_close: true

Workflow

Step 1 — Verify the suite exists

ls "_Logs/eval-suites/<agent>/cases/"

If empty, bootstrap from _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/voice-ai-agent-builder/SKILL.md Step 7's recommended eval set (12-15 baseline cases).

Step 2 — Run each case

For each case file:

  1. Spin up the agent in eval mode (Retell sandbox or staging environment)
  2. Simulate the tester script
  3. Capture the agent's actual responses
  4. Check against pass_criteria and soft_assertions
  5. Mark PASS / FAIL / SOFT (passes hard but fails soft)

Step 3 — Aggregate the run

Metric Pass Fail Soft fail
Total cases
Hard pass rate <%>
Soft pass rate <%>
Critical (e.g., compliance) pass rate <%>

Step 4 — Compare to baseline (regression check)

Diff against the most recent successful run:

  • NEW failures: cases that previously passed and now fail → regression flag 🔴
  • NEW passes: cases that previously failed and now pass → improvement ✅
  • Persistent failures: cases that have failed multiple runs → escalate priority

Step 5 — Apply the gate decision

Result Decision
100% hard pass + 100% critical pass 🟢 GREEN — OK to deploy
100% hard pass + some soft fails 🟡 YELLOW — deploy with note + open ticket
Any hard fail in non-critical case 🟡 YELLOW with explicit list — Chris decides
Any hard fail in CRITICAL case (compliance, refusal, transfer-to-human) 🔴 RED — BLOCK deploy
Any NEW regression vs baseline 🔴 RED — BLOCK; fix or document acceptance

Step 6 — Output the run report

Output structure

# Voice Eval Run: <Agent Name>

## Run metadata
- **Agent**: <agent ID + version / git SHA>
- **Suite**: `_Logs/eval-suites/<agent>/`
- **Cases**: <N>
- **Started**: <timestamp>
- **Duration**: <minutes>
- **Run by**: <user / cron>

## Aggregate result
- **Hard pass**: <N>/<M> (<%>)
- **Soft pass**: <N>/<M> (<%>)
- **Critical pass**: <N>/<M> (<%>)

## Gate decision
**Status**: 🟢 GREEN / 🟡 YELLOW / 🔴 RED

**Reasoning**: <one paragraph>

## Per-case results

### 🟢 Passes (<N>)
- ✅ 001 happy-path-cold
- ✅ 002 gatekeeper-pivot
- ...

### 🟡 Soft fails (<N>)
| Case | Soft criterion failed | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| <id> | <criterion> | <low/med> |

### 🔴 Hard fails (<N>)
| Case | Critical? | What failed | Evidence (transcript excerpt) |
|---|---|---|---|
| <id> | yes/no | <criterion> | <quote> |

## Regression check (vs <prior date>)
- **New failures**: <list> ← 🔴 critical
- **New passes**: <list> ← improvement
- **Persistent failures (3+ runs)**: <list> ← escalate

## Recommendations
| # | Failure | Proposed fix | Owner skill |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | <case> | <change> | voice-script-author |

## ✅ I handled
- Ran the suite
- Aggregated + diffed
- Applied gate decision
- Logged run

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- If 🟡 YELLOW: decide deploy + ticket vs fix-first
- Approve any "acceptance" of a soft-fail case (mark as acceptable in case YAML)

## 🚫 You only
- Override 🔴 RED gate
- Decide to delete an eval case (the suite only grows; deletion is a Chris-only decision)

## Handoff
**If 🟢**: → `devops-deployment` for production push
**If 🟡**: → `voice-script-author` / `voice-ai-agent-builder` to address soft fails + open ticket
**If 🔴**: → fix-first loop with originating skill
**Always**: log to `_Logs/eval-suites/<agent>/runs/<timestamp>.md`

Maintenance: how the suite grows

Every Friday's voice-call-reviewer finds new failure patterns. For each pattern:

  1. New eval case added to suite (one YAML file per failure pattern)
  2. Run the suite once → confirm the new case fails on current agent
  3. Apply fix → re-run → confirm new case now passes
  4. Lock the case in the suite (never delete unless explicit Chris approval)

The suite only grows. Never shrinks without approval.

Anti-patterns

  • Deploying without a clean eval pass — never
  • Adding cases without auto-failing them first (regression-test-driven development)
  • Removing eval cases — only with explicit Chris approval + reason
  • Eval set that's all happy-path — must include compliance, edge cases, hostile callers, refusals
  • No critical-case gate — compliance failures must hard-block
  • Manual-only runs — schedule weekly via automation-scheduler for regression detection

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run the suite against staging
  • ✅ Diff against prior runs
  • ✅ Log results
  • ✅ Add new cases (additive)
  • ⚠️ Apply gate decision (informs deploy; doesn't auto-deploy)
  • 🚫 Delete eval cases
  • 🚫 Run against production agents that handle real callers without isolation

Pairing

Inputs from: voice-call-reviewer (failure patterns → new cases), voice-ai-agent-builder (agent under test), _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (critical checks) Outputs to: devops-deployment (deploy gate), voice-script-author / voice-ai-agent-builder (fix loop) Chains with: voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md, security-inspector (compliance overlap)

Scheduled automation

Via automation-scheduler:

  • Weekly Monday 6am: full eval run on every production voice agent
  • On-demand: before any agent deploy

One-liner reminder

Suite grows forever. Compliance cases are critical (🔴 blocks). Diff every run vs baseline. New regressions = 🔴. Production change requires green run.

skills.name: Voice Knowledge Base
skills.description: A RAG layer optimized for voice agents. Indexes FAQ, dosing guide, policy docs, product details — and serves SHORT, voice-friendly answers (not long paragraphs). Voice agents query it mid-call via a function. Different from general RAG because voice answers must be ≤ 2 sentences + read-aloud-ready.
skills.intent: Build the knowledge layer voice agents call mid-conversation for accurate info. Curate from Matrix's existing docs (dosing-guide, products, offers, FAQ). Index for voice retrieval (short answers, no markdown, spell-out numbers / URLs). Continuous improvement: each "I don't know" surfaced by voice-call-reviewer becomes a new KB entry.
best_for: Standing up a voice agent's lookup layer
Adding new FAQ entries (from voice-call-reviewer findings)
Auditing voice KB for accuracy / freshness
skills.estimated_time: 60-120 min initial build; 5-10 min per new entry
skills.body_html:
Voice Knowledge Base

The RAG layer for voice agents. Short, spoken-friendly, mid-call queryable.

When to use

  • Building a new voice agent that needs lookups beyond simple function calls
  • Adding entries surfaced by voice-call-reviewer (gaps in coverage)
  • Quarterly accuracy / freshness audit
  • Bootstrapping a voice agent for a new department

When NOT to use

  • General company KB (use _Knowledge Base/ directly — different format, longer answers)
  • Real-time data (use a function call to live system — order status, inventory)
  • Decision-making (KB serves info; agent decides)

What's different from general RAG

General RAG Voice KB
Answer can be paragraphs Answer must be ≤ 2 sentences
Markdown OK Plain text only
URLs verbatim Spelled or simplified ("matrix dot com slash login")
Numbers as digits Spelled when < 20 ("twelve hours")
Long context OK Maximum ~30 words returned to agent
Search precision priority Search precision + brevity priority

Workflow

Step 1 — Curate source docs

Pull from Matrix's existing content:

  • _Shared Context/offers.md (what we sell)
  • _Shared Context/products.md (catalog overview + compliance flags)
  • ~/Projects/dosing guide/matrix-dosing-guide/ (dosing reference)
  • ~/Projects/matrix-store/docs/ghl/ (GHL setup docs)
  • Matrix Academy education content
  • Internal FAQ docs

Step 2 — Build the KB entries

Each KB entry has this shape (stored as JSON or YAML in a Supabase table):

id: kb-001
question_aliases:
  - "what's the lead time on Semaglutide"
  - "how long does Semaglutide take to ship"
  - "Semaglutide ETA"
answer_text: "Semaglutide ships within 2 to 3 business days after order confirmation. Tracking goes to your email."
answer_long: "(optional longer version for follow-up if asked)"
source: "products.md + matrix-store fulfillment SLAs"
last_verified: "2026-05-10"
tags:
  - "lead-time"
  - "semaglutide"
  - "fulfillment"
voice_safe: true
compliance_flag: "no-clinical"

Critical fields:

  • question_aliases: 3-5 ways someone might ask
  • answer_text: the spoken-friendly version (≤ 2 sentences, ~30 words)
  • voice_safe: confirmed safe to read aloud to a caller (no PHI, no clinical advice, no commitments)
  • compliance_flag: any compliance constraint on this answer

Step 3 — Categories to cover

Initial Matrix voice KB (~50-100 entries):

Catalog / products:

  • Lead times per product class (503-A vs 503-B)
  • Active vs backordered status
  • Available variants (concentrations, doses)
  • Bundle contents
  • IV drips coming soon
  • SARMs preorder-intent only

Credentialing:

  • NPPES verification process
  • Typical timing (24-48 hours)
  • What docs are required
  • BAA — when needed
  • State licensing relevance

Ordering:

  • How to place a first order (login flow)
  • Payment terms (Immediate / EOD / EOW / Net 30)
  • Volume discount thresholds
  • Affiliate commission tiers

Fulfillment + shipping:

  • Carrier(s) used
  • Avg shipping time
  • Temperature-controlled shipping availability
  • Order tracking

Account:

  • How to reach account manager
  • How to update payment method
  • How to add team members
  • How to access dosing guide
  • How to access Matrix Academy

Compliance / professional-use:

  • Provider-only access (always)
  • Patient consultation not provided by MAS
  • Clinical questions → clinical team
  • Medical claims policy
  • Records / audit availability

Brand sub-products:

  • MuscleLock — what it is
  • Peptide Map — what it is
  • Matrix Academy — what it is

Step 4 — Voice-format each answer

Run every entry through the voice-format rules:

  • Replace ! with .
  • Spell numbers < 20
  • Spell URLs / emails or simplify (matrix dot com)
  • Cut adverbs
  • Use contractions
  • Max 30 words
  • Read each one aloud (or have agent read it) — if it sounds awkward, rewrite

Step 5 — Wire as a function

Voice agent calls lookup_kb(question) mid-call. The function:

  1. Embeds the question
  2. Vector-searches KB for top-3 matches
  3. Re-ranks by voice_safe + recency
  4. Returns the best match's answer_text (≤ 30 words)
  5. If no match above similarity threshold → return "I don't have that — want me to text it after?" + log the gap

Step 6 — Log gaps for continuous improvement

Every "I don't have that" response gets logged with the actual user question. voice-call-reviewer surfaces these weekly → new KB entries proposed.

Step 7 — Output

Output structure

# Voice KB: <Department / Agent>

## Coverage
- Total entries: <N>
- Categories: <list>
- Last full audit: <date>
- Recent additions: <N> in last 7 days

## Quality
- Voice-safe verified: <%>
- Avg answer length: <words>
- Avg similarity for top-match: <score>
- "I don't have that" rate: <%>

## Gaps surfaced this period (from voice-call-reviewer)
| Gap | Frequency | Proposed entry |
|---|---|---|
| <missed question> | <N> times | <draft answer> |

## ✅ I handled
- Curated from source docs
- Voice-formatted all entries
- Wired as `lookup_kb` function
- Logged gaps from production

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve new entries before they go live (especially regulatory / clinical edge cases)
- Audit accuracy quarterly

## 🚫 You only
- Approve entries involving clinical claims
- Approve entries with regulatory language

## Handoff
**To**: `voice-ai-*` agents (all consume this KB), `voice-call-reviewer` (gap surfacing → new entries)

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-voice-kb-update.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Long answers — agent reads them and caller tunes out
  • Markdown in answer text — TTS reads **bold** as "asterisk asterisk bold asterisk asterisk"
  • Numbers as digits for small numbers — sounds robotic
  • Clinical claims in KB — never. Always disclaim + transfer.
  • Stale entries — quarterly audit minimum
  • No voice_safe review — entries from general KB can have PHI / clinical content

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Curate + format entries
  • ✅ Wire as function
  • ✅ Log gaps
  • ⚠️ Add new entries (Chris approves regulatory / clinical edge cases)
  • 🚫 Add entries that imply medical advice
  • 🚫 Add entries with patient identifiers

Pairing

Inputs from: _Shared Context/offers.md, _Shared Context/products.md, Matrix existing docs, voice-call-reviewer (gaps) Outputs to: All voice-ai-* agents (consumed via lookup_kb function) Chains with: voice-eval-harness (eval cases test KB accuracy), kb-add-source (for general KB additions; this skill is voice-specific)

One-liner reminder

Short. Voice-formatted. voice_safe checked. Log gaps. New entries from production gaps. Never clinical advice.

skills.name: Voice Persona Library
skills.description: Reusable named voice personas (Mia, Alex, Sam, etc.) with TTS voice IDs, tone profiles, brand-voice mappings, and use-case fit. Single source of truth so every voice skill picks from a consistent set — no "should this agent be Mia or a new persona?" debate per build.
skills.intent: Codify the agent voices used across all voice AI builds. Each persona has a specific role (sales / inbound / support / onboarding / closer-assist) + voice ID + tone profile. Cloning a voice skill inherits the persona automatically.
best_for: Picking persona for a new voice agent build
Adding a new persona with proper documentation
skills.estimated_time: 5 min lookup; 15-20 min to add a new persona
skills.body_html:
Voice Persona Library

Single source of truth for voice agent personas. Every voice skill picks from this library.

When to use

  • New voice agent build (pick from existing personas)
  • Onboarding a new department's voice work (which persona fits)
  • Adding a new persona (rare — only when an existing one truly doesn't fit)
  • Audit: are all production agents using approved personas?

When NOT to use

  • One-time TTS for a marketing video (use TTS skills directly)
  • Personal note dictation (not a persona thing)

The persona library (initial set)

Mia — Provider Success Specialist

  • Role: General-purpose Matrix voice. Sales (outbound + inbound), discovery, catalog requests.
  • TTS voice: Retell female voice ID <retell-voice-id> (or ElevenLabs <voice-id>) — calm, clinical, mid-pitch
  • Speech rate: Normal (no rush)
  • Tone profile:
    • Authoritative + clinical
    • Peer-level (talks to providers as colleagues)
    • Warm but never effusive
    • Direct (no hedging)
    • No exclamations, no consumer tone
  • Default use cases: voice-ai-sales-agent (outbound), voice-ai-inbound-qualifier (inbound)
  • Brand voice: 100% match to _Shared Context/brand-guide.md
  • Sample opener: "Hey Dr. [LastName] — Mia from Matrix Advanced Solutions. Quick context..."

Alex — Technical / Operational Specialist

  • Role: Detail-oriented operational voice. Order status, function-heavy calls, technical Q&A.
  • TTS voice: Retell male voice ID <retell-voice-id> — even pitch, slightly faster pace
  • Speech rate: Normal-fast (operational tasks)
  • Tone profile:
    • Precise + functional
    • Faster cadence (operational urgency)
    • Direct facts, less rapport-building
    • Same compliance discipline as Mia
  • Default use cases: voice-ai-support-agent (order status, lookups), function-heavy IVR flows
  • Sample opener: "Matrix Advanced Solutions — this is Alex. How can I help?"

Sam — Onboarding / Account Specialist

  • Role: Patient, walking-through voice. Onboarding kickoffs, training, account setup walk-throughs.
  • TTS voice: Retell voice ID <retell-voice-id> — warm, slightly slower than Mia
  • Speech rate: Slightly slow (walking the user through steps)
  • Tone profile:
    • Patient + thorough
    • Pauses naturally for caller to absorb info
    • Confirms understanding mid-flow ("does that make sense so far?")
    • Empathetic when caller is overwhelmed
  • Default use cases: voice-ai-onboarding-agent (post-deal-close kickoff calls), training calls
  • Sample opener: "Hey Dr. [LastName] — Sam from Matrix. Excited to walk you through your account setup..." (replace "Excited" — per brand voice; use "Glad to help" or simply move past it)

Reception — Brief, Inbound, IVR-replacement

  • Role: Minimal-friction inbound greeting and routing
  • TTS voice: Retell voice ID <retell-voice-id> — neutral, professional
  • Speech rate: Normal
  • Tone profile:
    • Brief — gets out of the way
    • Clear options
    • No personality (this is IVR-replacement)
  • Default use cases: After-hours inbound, high-volume inbound routing
  • Sample greeting: "Thanks for calling Matrix Advanced Solutions. This call may be recorded. How can I help today?"

Persona-to-skill mapping (defaults)

Skill Default persona Override allowed?
voice-ai-sales-agent (outbound) Mia Yes (per campaign)
voice-ai-inbound-qualifier Mia Yes (Reception for after-hours)
voice-ai-support-agent Alex Yes (Mia for high-touch)
voice-ai-onboarding-agent Sam No (onboarding requires patient pacing)
voice-ai-closer-assist (planned) Live human + Alex on hold music + function calls n/a

Workflow (when invoked)

Step 1 — Identify use case

What voice work is being built? Match to the use-case grid above.

Step 2 — Pick the persona

Default per the mapping. Override only with reason.

Step 3 — Pull persona spec

Return the full persona block for the chosen one. Downstream skills (voice-script-author, voice-ai-agent-builder) consume this.

Step 4 — Validate against brand voice

Every persona must comply with _Shared Context/brand-guide.md. If a use case seems to require deviating (e.g., "we need a more energetic persona for X"), STOP and escalate to Chris — brand voice deviations need explicit approval.

Adding a new persona

Only when an existing persona truly doesn't fit:

  1. Document the new persona with all fields above (role, voice ID, rate, tone profile, use cases, sample lines)
  2. Get Chris approval (personas are part of brand identity)
  3. Add to this file under the persona list
  4. Update persona-to-skill mapping
  5. Add to _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-new-persona-<name>.md

Never add a persona without approval. Never use a persona that's not in this library.

Output structure (when querying)

# Persona: <name>

## Spec
- **Role**: <role>
- **TTS voice**: <Retell ID / ElevenLabs ID>
- **Speech rate**: <setting>
- **LLM model**: <model + temperature>
- **Tone profile**:
  - <trait>
  - <trait>

## Default use cases
- <skill / context>

## Brand voice fit
- ✅ matches `_Shared Context/brand-guide.md` rules

## Sample lines
- Opener: "<verbatim>"
- Mid-call: "<verbatim>"
- Close: "<verbatim>"

## Used in production
- <agent ID(s)>

## Last verified
<date> by <name>

Anti-patterns

  • One persona for everything — sales and onboarding and support have different cadences
  • Random persona per build — defeats consistency
  • Adding a persona without approval — fragments the voice
  • Persona that violates brand voice (e.g., adds emoji, exclamations, consumer tone) — never
  • Using non-library voice IDs in production agents

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Read the library (lookups)
  • ✅ Recommend persona for a new build
  • ⚠️ Add a new persona (Chris approves with reason)
  • ⚠️ Update persona-to-skill mapping (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Use a persona / voice ID not in this library

Pairing

Inputs from: _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (voice rules) Outputs to: voice-script-author, voice-ai-agent-builder, all voice-ai-* skills Chains with: voice-call-reviewer (which personas perform best per use case)

Last updated

2026-05-10 by Chris (initial library — Mia, Alex, Sam, Reception)

skills.name: Voice Script Author
skills.description: Write voice scripts in spoken voice (not written voice). Inputs a conversation flow + brand voice; outputs the verbatim language for each state, including opening lines, transitions, ask-for-info phrasing, objection responses, voicemail script, closing lines, and holding language for function-call waits. Voice ≠ copy.
skills.intent: Produce voice script language that sounds natural when spoken aloud. Account for cadence, breath, contraction usage, filler tolerance, interruption recovery. Honor brand voice but adapt to spoken constraints. Output is the exact text a voice agent (Retell/Vapi/Synthflow/ElevenLabs) reads.
best_for: Pairing with conversation-flow-designer to populate a voice agent
Generating filler / hold language for function waits
skills.body_html:
Voice Script Author

Written voice ≠ spoken voice. This skill writes for the ear, not the eye.

When to use

  • After conversation-flow-designer produces a flow → populate the states
  • Refactoring voice scripts that feel robotic
  • Voicemail scripts for outbound
  • Hold / filler scripts for mid-call function-call waits

When NOT to use

  • Email / page / ad copy (use copywriting)
  • Long-form documentation (different format entirely)
  • SMS templates (similar but tighter — use a dedicated SMS template skill or write them per state)

Required inputs

  1. Conversation flow — from conversation-flow-designer (state machine + branches)
  2. Brand voice — load _Shared Context/brand-guide.md
  3. TTS voice profile — which voice will speak this (Retell voice ID, ElevenLabs voice, etc.) — affects cadence / persona
  4. Compliance disclosures required — recording disclosure, provider-only language, etc.
  5. Agent persona — name + 1-sentence personality (e.g., "Mia — a Matrix Advanced Solutions provider success specialist, calm and clinical")

If 1 is missing, ask: "Run conversation-flow-designer first, or paste the flow."

What spoken voice does differently

Written voice Spoken voice
Sentences can be long Short sentences. Frequent stops.
Punctuation lightly used Periods drive cadence + breath
Hyphens / dashes for emphasis Replace with periods or word emphasis
Numbers as digits ("15%") Words ("fifteen percent") or readable ("3 hundred")
URLs / emails written out Spelled or simplified ("dot com" not ".com")
Tables / lists Inline enumeration ("Three things: first... second... third...")
Headers + paragraphs None — pure flowing speech
No filler Light filler natural ("So...", "Right.", "Okay.") — but agent doesn't say "um"
Same word twice OK Avoid repeats; use synonym or restructure
Click-driven CTA Spoken next-step ("I'll text you the link" not "click here")

Brand voice → spoken voice translation rules

For Matrix specifically (from brand-guide.md):

Brand voice rule Spoken adaptation
"No exclamation marks" TTS reads ! as raised pitch — replace ! with . always
"Clinical, peer-level" Use clinical terms freely (provider audience); don't over-define
"Outcomes-first" Open with the outcome before the mechanism
"Authoritative" Short declarative sentences. No hedging ("I might be able to...").
"Direct" Cut adverbs. Cut "just" and "really".
"Mechanism-aware" When asked how, give 1-sentence mechanism — don't lecture
"No consumer tone" No "boost", "amazing", "fire up", "biohack"

Workflow

Step 1 — Read the flow

Load the conversation-flow-designer output. For each state, identify what the script must accomplish.

Step 2 — Define the agent persona

In one sentence: who's speaking, with what energy.

Examples:

  • "Mia, Matrix provider success specialist — calm, clinical, peer-level, never rushed"
  • "Sam, scheduling assistant — friendly, efficient, focused on booking"
  • "Alex, technical voice agent — direct, precise, function-call oriented"

The persona drives word choice + cadence.

Step 3 — Write each state's script

For each state from the flow, write the verbatim language for the agent. Always include:

OPEN state:

  • Opening line (5-15 words for outbound; longer is fine for inbound)
  • Permission ask ("got a minute?" / "is this still a good time?")
  • Recovery if no answer (after 3 sec silence: "still there?")

Discovery questions — one at a time. Layered:

"What's been the biggest pain in your [clinical area / operations / supply chain]?"
[wait, listen, restate]
"Got it. And how long has that been an issue?"
[wait, listen, restate]

Restate / mirror moves — the load-bearing pattern:

"Sounds like [restate user's situation in one sentence]."
"So if I'm hearing you right, [restate]."
"Let me make sure I've got this — [restate]."

Transitions between states — natural connectives:

  • "That's helpful — here's where I think we can help..."
  • "Okay so based on what you said, I'd suggest..."
  • "Got it. So a couple options..."

Function-call hold (when the agent calls an API mid-call):

"Give me one moment — I'm pulling up that info."
"Let me check on that real quick."
"One second — looking that up now."

Voicemail (outbound):

  • Identify (name + company)
  • Reason for call (one sentence)
  • Specific next step (call back / I'll send a text / I'll email)
  • Close ("Talk soon")
  • Total: 15-25 seconds

Closing — always specific:

  • "I'll send a text right now with the calendar link. You'll get it in about 10 seconds."
  • "We're booked for next Tuesday at 2pm. You'll get a confirmation email in a minute."
  • "Thanks for your time. If anything changes, just text us at [number]."

Step 4 — Add edge-case language

Write language for every edge case from the flow:

  • "Are you a bot?" — honest answer:

    "Yeah, I'm an AI voice assistant for Matrix Advanced Solutions. I can help with most things, but if you'd rather talk to a human, just say 'human' and I'll connect you."

  • Misheard (max 2 attempts):

    "Sorry, I didn't catch that — could you say it again?" [If 2nd fail]: "I'm having trouble hearing — let me text you instead. What's the best number?"

  • Silence > 10 sec:

    "Still there? Take your time." [If still silence after 5 more sec]: "Sounds like we got disconnected — I'll send a text follow-up."

  • Interruption / talk-over:

    [Stop speaking immediately, listen, restate what was caught] "Sorry, go ahead — you were saying [restate]?"

  • Function call fail:

    "Hmm, my system's a little slow on that. Want me to email you the details instead?"

Step 5 — Apply brand voice pass

Run the output through brand-guide rules:

  • No exclamation marks (replace any auto-generated with periods)
  • No banned phrases ("great question", "absolutely", "of course", "amazing")
  • No consumer tone
  • Compliance phrases present where context demands
  • Peer-level (for Matrix: clinical without over-explaining)

Step 6 — Spoken-language pass

  • All sentences under 20 words (most under 12)
  • Numbers readable as speech
  • No URLs / emails verbatim (refer to "I'll text it" or spell with care)
  • Contractions used naturally ("you'll" not "you will" except for emphasis)
  • Cadence varied — mix short and shorter sentences
  • No "I'm sorry, did I lose you?" loops — one check then move

Step 7 — Output the script pack

Output structure

# Voice Script: <Agent Name> — <Use Case>

## Quick reference
- **Agent persona**: <one-line persona>
- **TTS voice**: <Retell voice ID / ElevenLabs voice name>
- **LLM**: <model>
- **LLM temperature**: <0.3-0.7>
- **Speech rate**: <normal / slightly slow / fast>
- **Brand voice**: <one-line summary>

## State-by-state scripts

### OPEN (outbound) / GREETING (inbound)

"Hey, this is Mia from Matrix Advanced Solutions. I'm calling for Dr. [LastName] — is this her?"

[Wait for response. Routes per flow:]

If yes → Permission ask: "Quick context — we work with clinics like yours on advanced treatment supply. Got a couple minutes?"

If no (gatekeeper) → Identify + reschedule: "Got it. When's a good time to reach Dr. [LastName] directly?"

If voicemail → see VOICEMAIL state.


### DISCOVER (capture pain + qualification)

Q1 — Opening pain question: "What's been the biggest pain in your supply or treatment offering right now?"

[Listen. Restate.] "Sounds like [restated pain]."

Q2 — Volume / scale: "And roughly how many patients are you seeing for [related modality]?"

Q3 — Current state: "How are you handling that today? Working with one vendor or piecing it together?"


### PROPOSE

Anchor with the wedge: "Here's where we usually help clinics in your spot. Instead of juggling three or four vendors, you'd have one credentialed-access line for 503-A patient-specific compounding, 503-B catalog, peptides, and hormones — plus a dedicated account person on your team."

Soft ask: "Want me to send over the catalog and grab 15 minutes on the calendar to walk through it?"


### HANDLE — common objections

**"Send me the info first."**

"Yep, I'll send the catalog right after this call. Want to grab a 15-minute slot for [Day] or [Day] this week so we can walk through it together once you've had a look?"


**"We already have a vendor."**

"Right, most clinics we work with did. Usually it's not about replacing — it's about adding one line that gives them more flexibility on patient-specific compounding without changing what's working. Worth a 15-minute look?"


**"What's the pricing?"**

"Pricing varies by product class — 503-A patient-specific is per-Rx, 503-B catalog is per-unit, both have volume tiers. Easiest to send you the full catalog with pricing transparent — okay if I email that today?"


**"I'm not the decision-maker."**

"Got it — who's the right person on that side? I'll connect there, and if you want, I can email you the catalog too so you have it for context."


### CLOSE — booked

"Perfect — I've got you for [Day] at [Time]. You'll get a confirmation email in a minute. I'll also text you the calendar link right now in case you need to reschedule. Anything else before I let you go?"

[If no:] "Sounds good. Talk to you on [Day]."


### CLOSE — email follow-up only

"All good — I'll send the catalog over right now. What's the best email?"

[Confirm spelling if unusual.]

"Got it. You'll have it in about thirty seconds. If anything jumps out, just reply to that email and I'll get back to you."


### CLOSE — hard no

"Totally understand. Thanks for your time. If anything changes, you can reach us at hello@matrixadvancedsolutions.com or just call us back at this number."


### VOICEMAIL (outbound)

"Hey Dr. [LastName] — this is Mia from Matrix Advanced Solutions. We help credentialed clinics like yours consolidate compounded peptide and GLP supply into one credentialed-access line. Wanted to share our catalog and see if a fifteen-minute call this week makes sense.

I'll send you a text with my contact and the catalog link. Or you can call us back at this number. Talk soon."


(20-25 seconds at normal speech rate.)

### EDGE-CASE LANGUAGE

**"Are you a bot?"**

"Yeah, I'm an AI voice assistant for Matrix. I can handle most of this myself, but if you'd rather talk to a human, just say 'human' and I'll connect you."


**Mis-hear (1st time)**

"Sorry, I didn't catch that — could you say it again?"


**Mis-hear (2nd time)**

"I'm having trouble hearing — let me text you instead. What's the best number?"


**Silence 10+ sec**

"Still there? Take your time."


**Silence 15+ sec**

"Sounds like the connection dropped — I'll send a text to follow up. Talk soon."


**Function-call wait**

"Give me one moment — pulling that up." [function call] "Got it." "Let me check on that real quick." [function call] "Okay, here's what I see..."


**Transfer to human**

"Sure — let me connect you. One moment." [Pass full transcript + key facts to human via webhook]


### Compliance disclosures (where required)

**Outbound recording disclosure** (in 12-party states):

"Hey Dr. [LastName] — this is Mia from Matrix Advanced Solutions. Quick heads up, this call may be recorded for quality and training. Got a couple minutes?"


**Inbound recording disclosure** (default):

"Thanks for calling Matrix Advanced Solutions. This call may be recorded for quality and training. How can I help?"


**Provider-only access language** (when caller asks about availability):

"Our catalog is provider-only access — you'd need to be NPI-verified before ordering. I can walk you through credentialing on the call if you'd like."


## Voice / cadence / TTS notes for engineer

- Replace ALL `!` with `.` in TTS input (else TTS over-emphasizes)
- Insert `,` pauses for natural breath in long sentences
- Use SSML if Retell supports it for specific pause control
- Numbers: write out under 20 ("fifteen"), digits over 20 ("$2,500")
- Phone numbers / orders / SKUs: write with hyphens or spaces ("eight three one — two nine eight — eight nine three three")

## Handoff
**To**: `voice-ai-agent-builder` (implements scripts in the platform)
**Approval required**: yes — Chris approves final scripts before deploy

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-voice-script-<agent>-<use-case>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Writing in email voice — long sentences, formal punctuation, no contractions = robotic TTS
  • Exclamation marks — they survive into TTS as pitch spikes
  • Pricing / specifics in voice when complex — refer to text follow-up
  • Linear scripts with no branches — voice agents always need branches per flow
  • "Hi how are you today!" openers — banned per brand voice; replace with situational opener
  • No edge-case language — production calls hit edge cases hourly
  • No voicemail script for outbound — half of calls
  • Scripts that don't read aloud — always read your draft OUT LOUD before locking. If it sounds robotic, rewrite.

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Write any voice script
  • ✅ Save to _Logs/
  • ⚠️ Approve scripts before production deploy (Chris)
  • 🚫 Deploy to production without approval

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • conversation-flow-designer (state machine + branches)
  • _Shared Context/brand-guide.md (voice rules)
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (required disclosures)
  • _Shared Context/agent-registry.md (agent persona library)

Outputs to:

  • voice-ai-agent-builder (deploys the scripts)
  • TTS voice config (TTS engineer uses the voice/cadence notes)

One-liner reminder

Short sentences. No exclamations. Spell phone/email. Branches at every state. Voicemail script always. Read aloud before locking.

skills.name: Voice Search Optimizer
skills.description: SEO specialist for voice search visibility — Siri, Alexa, Google Assistant. Featured-snippet capture, conversational-query content, FAQ schema, position-zero targeting. Optimizes content so when a provider says "Hey Google, find a peptide pharmacy near me" — Matrix is the answer. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Voice search returns ONE answer (or 3 max). Position zero / featured snippet wins everything. Conversational queries use natural language patterns (questions; "near me"; "best for X"). This specialist tunes content + schema so Matrix captures voice answers.
skills.estimated_time: Per query target: 1-3 hours. Audit: 4-8 hours.
skills.body_html:
Voice Search Optimizer

The position-zero specialist. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Tunes Matrix content + schema for voice search dominance. Voice assistants (Siri, Alexa, Google Assistant) typically return ONE answer pulled from the featured snippet (position 0) or top organic result. This specialist identifies voice-shaped queries Matrix should win, structures content to capture snippets, deploys FAQ + HowTo + Speakable schema, and tracks position-zero captures over time.

Explicitly OUT of scope: Local map-pack voice queries (local-seo-specialist for "near me"), general keyword strategy (content-seo-specialist), AI-search optimization (ai-search-optimizer), technical schema implementation (technical-seo-specialist — but specifies which schema to use).

INPUT

input:
  trigger:
    type: enum (snippet-targeting / faq-build / voice-audit / new-query / refresh)
    required: true
  target_query:
    type: string
    required: true (snippet-targeting mode)
  scope:
    type: enum (full-site / specific-cluster / specific-page)
    required: false

RULES

rules:
  voice_query_patterns:
    - Question-shaped (Who / What / When / Where / Why / How / Can / Is / Does)
    - Conversational phrasing ("Hey Google, find me a..." or "What's the best...")
    - Often longer than typed queries (5-10 words vs 2-4)
    - High intent: "near me" / "open now" / "best [thing] for [purpose]"
    - Comparative ("X vs Y") / shortlist ("top 5...")
  content_structure_for_snippets:
    - Direct answer in first 40-60 words after the question (the snippet excerpt)
    - Question as H2 / H3 (matches voice queries that start with the question)
    - Concise + factual + cite-able
    - Use lists for "how to" + comparison snippets
    - Use tables for "comparison" snippets
    - Use definitions box for "what is" snippets
    - Define key terms; provide concrete examples
  schema_for_voice:
    - FAQPage schema for Q&A blocks
    - HowTo schema for instruction content
    - Speakable schema (for voice-first content where appropriate)
    - Article / NewsArticle schema for editorial
    - LocalBusiness for "near me" queries
  ranking_signals_for_voice:
    - Page already ranking page 1 (typically top 3) for the query
    - Featured snippet earned already OR clear path to it
    - Page load fast (CWV pass)
    - Mobile-friendly mandatory
    - Authority signals (E-E-A-T)
    - Concise direct answers
  prohibited:
    - Schema markup not matching visible content (manual-action risk)
    - Buying snippets via PBN — guaranteed penalty
    - Cloaking voice-assistant user agents

TOOLS

tools:
  external_apis:
    - Google Search Console (snippet capture data)
    - PeopleAlsoAsk scraper (via WebSearch) — discover related question patterns
    - Schema.org validator
  internal_data_read:
    - _Shared Context/seo-targets.md (Tier 4 voice patterns)
    - Current site content (filesystem + WebFetch)
    - _Logs/seo/content/keyword-map.csv (which queries Matrix targets)
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/seo/voice/<period>-audit.md
    - _Logs/seo/voice/snippet-captures.csv (which snippets earned + lost)
    - _Logs/seo/voice/briefs/<query>.md (voice-shaped briefs handed to copywriters)
  composed_skills:
    - content-seo-specialist (keyword/intent alignment)
    - technical-seo-specialist (schema implementation request)
    - copywriting (brief execution)
    - compliance-phrase-detector (claims discipline on FAQ answers)
  mcps:
    - WebFetch + WebSearch (PAA + snippet manual check)
  llm:
    model: claude-haiku-4
    upgrade_to: claude-sonnet-4 (snippet-optimization re-writes)

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    audit_id: string
    snippet_targets:
      - query: string
        current_snippet_holder: string
        our_current_position: number
        ranking_probability: high | medium | low
        recommended_action: brief | restructure | wait | abandon
        brief_path: string  # if brief generated
    faq_recommendations:
      - page_url: string
        proposed_questions: array
        schema_to_deploy: FAQPage | HowTo | Speakable
    schema_gaps:
      - page_url: string
        missing_schema: string
        priority: P0 | P1 | P2
    voice_query_landscape:
      - query_cluster: string
        related_queries: array
        people_also_ask: array
        snippet_competitor: string
    captured_snippets:
      - query: string
        captured_at: ISO
        url: string
    lost_snippets:
      - query: string
        lost_at: ISO
        replaced_by: string
        recovery_plan: string
  format: JSON + Markdown

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - content-seo-specialist (briefs aligned with overall content strategy)
    - copywriting (brief execution)
    - technical-seo-specialist (schema deploy)
  trigger_method: monthly cron + ad-hoc
  fallback_routing:
    - Featured snippet lost on Tier 1 query → urgent recovery
    - Voice-search query landscape shifts (e.g., new "best X" pattern emerges) → strategy update

Eval cases

  1. Featured snippet captured for "what is BPC-157" → maintain
  2. Snippet lost on Tier 1 query → recovery plan
  3. FAQ page audit → missing schema deploy
  4. New conversational query pattern detected → content opportunity
  5. PAA discovery → 10 new question targets

Anti-patterns

  • Schema for content not visible — manual action
  • Trying for snippet without page-1 rank first — wasted effort
  • Stuffing FAQ schema with non-FAQ content — quality guideline violation
  • Ignoring mobile performance — voice search is mobile-heavy

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Audit + recommendations
  • ✅ Generate snippet-targeting briefs
  • ✅ Specify schema additions
  • 🚫 Black-hat snippet schemes

Pairing

Composed by: seo-strategy-lead Composes: content-seo-specialist, copywriting, technical-seo-specialist, compliance-phrase-detector Reads: Search Console, PAA, seo-targets.md

One-liner reminder

Position zero wins voice. Question as H2; answer in 40-60 words. FAQ + HowTo + Speakable schema where applicable. Need page-1 rank before chasing snippet. Mobile + CWV mandatory.

skills.name: Web Chat Agent Builder
skills.description: Build a web chat agent (in-site chat widget, support chat, lead qualifier) using the same conversation-flow-designer + voice-script-author pattern adapted for text. Different cadence than voice — chat can be longer, scrollback exists, multitasking is the norm. Outputs a deployable chat config (GHL chat widget, Crisp, Intercom, or custom).
skills.intent: Cover the text-channel companion to voice-ai-agent-builder. Same flow design, same persona library, same KB — different output format (chat-friendly text instead of spoken). Adapts brand voice to text constraints (still no exclamations, still peer-level, but can use formatting like lists / links).
best_for: Lead qualification chat for new visitor
skills.estimated_time: 2-4 hours build
skills.body_html:
Web Chat Agent Builder

The text-channel companion. Same patterns, different output format.

When to use

  • Building an in-site chat widget for a Matrix site
  • Sister chat agent to a deployed voice agent (same use case, different channel)
  • After-hours coverage (when voice is unavailable)
  • Lead qualification on the website

When NOT to use

  • Voice agent (use voice-ai-agent-builder)
  • SMS conversation (use sms-conversation-agent — planned)
  • One-shot copy / FAQ static page (just write the page)

Required inputs

  1. Use case — lead-qualifier / support / FAQ / onboarding chat
  2. Platform — GHL Conversational AI / Crisp / Intercom / Drift / custom
  3. Site — which Matrix site embeds it
  4. Hours of operation — 24/7 / business / after-hours-only
  5. Handoff destinations — human (when), voice (callback), email (catalog send), etc.

If 1-2 missing, ask: "Use case + chat platform?"

What's different from voice (per modality)

Voice Chat
Real-time, turn-taking Async-ish, but often near-real-time
Short sentences (TTS cadence) Longer OK — user can re-read
No formatting Markdown / lists / links / images OK
User can't backtrack Scrollback exists
One thing at a time User multitasks while chatting
Disconnect = end User idle ≠ end (could come back)
No "type" indicator "Agent is typing" UX expected
Verbal nuance Emoji / tone via word choice
Phone-app context Browser context (more friction to switch)

Chat agents can:

  • Send links, buttons, quick-reply suggestions
  • Show product cards / cards / files
  • Use lists for options
  • Persist conversation history visibly

Chat agents must:

  • Handle idle / abandoned chats (close after N min, send email follow-up)
  • Identify themselves as AI when asked
  • Always offer human transfer

Workflow

Step 1 — Reuse: scan for existing chat patterns

Always start with reuse-scanner. Matrix may have existing chat configs in GHL.

Step 2 — Design the conversation flow

Use conversation-flow-designer with modality=chat:

  • Same 5-stage arc (OPEN → DISCOVER → PROPOSE → HANDLE → CLOSE)
  • Branches per stage
  • Edge cases include: idle, return-after-hours, "are you a bot", file upload (if supported)

Step 3 — Adapt the brand voice to chat

Per _Shared Context/brand-guide.md:

  • Still no exclamations
  • Still no consumer tone
  • Still peer-level for providers
  • Add: quick-reply buttons where helpful
  • Add: short messages over paragraph dumps (chat fatigue is real)
  • Add: links instead of long text ("Here's the catalog: [link]")

Step 4 — Pick the persona

From voice-persona-library. Most chat → Mia (same as voice sales/inbound for consistency). Support chat → Alex.

Step 5 — Define the function set

Function Purpose
lookup_contact_by_email_or_chat_id Identify returning visitor
book_strategy_call Calendar via GHL
send_followup_email Catalog send
send_sms Mobile follow-up
transfer_to_human Live human takeover (with full chat history)
lookup_kb (voice KB also works for chat) FAQ answers
check_credentialing Status lookup
tag_contact Add tags for flow analytics

Step 6 — UX patterns

  • Welcome message auto-fires when user opens chat (one short message, not a paragraph)
  • Quick-reply chips for common intents: "Request catalog" / "Check order status" / "Talk to human"
  • Typing indicator when agent is processing
  • Idle handler: after 90 sec of no response, send "Still here? Take your time."
  • Close handler: if user leaves the chat, save state + offer to resume via email
  • Hand-off UX: when transferring to human, show "Connecting you with [name]..." while routing

Step 7 — Eval cases (specific to chat)

  • Happy path: visitor → catalog request → email captured → booked call
  • Returning visitor: recognized from prior chat → resume context
  • Multitasking: visitor opens chat, types something, closes tab, comes back 10 min later
  • "Are you a bot?" — honest answer
  • File upload attempt (if not supported): graceful message + workaround
  • Hostile / spammy visitor (rare on chat but possible)
  • Mobile vs desktop (test both)
  • After-hours visitor
  • Visitor who only wants links (provide + close gracefully)

Step 8 — Wire to platform

For Matrix default (GHL Conversational AI):

  • Use harness skill ghl-convai if available
  • Map functions to GHL automations
  • Embed widget on the site
  • Set hours / availability
  • Configure transfer rules

Step 9 — Ship through prod checklist

Same gates as voice:

  • compliance-qa on all message templates
  • security-inspector on the embed code (XSS, data flow)
  • Eval pass via expanded voice-eval-harness (chat cases added)
  • Chris approval before live

Output structure

# Web Chat Agent: <Site / Use Case>

## Spec
**Use case**: <lead-qualifier / support / FAQ>
**Platform**: <GHL ConvAI / Crisp / etc.>
**Site**: <URL>
**Hours**: <coverage>
**Persona**: <Mia / Alex / Sam>

## Flow + scripts
(See `conversation-flow-designer` + adapted scripts)

## UX patterns implemented
- [x] Welcome auto-fire
- [x] Quick-reply chips
- [x] Typing indicator
- [x] Idle handler (90 sec)
- [x] Close handler (resume via email)
- [x] Human handoff UX

## Function set
(See full set above)

## Compliance
- [x] AI identity disclosed on "are you a bot?"
- [x] No medical / clinical advice
- [x] Privacy policy linked
- [x] Consent for marketing opt-in

## Eval
- 10+ chat-specific cases passed
- Resolution rate target: 65%+ (chat often hands off more than voice)
- Avg session length: < 5 min for routine, < 15 min for complex

## KPIs in production
- Chat open rate (% visitors who open)
- Engagement rate (% who reply to first message)
- Resolution rate (no human transfer)
- Lead capture rate (email / contact info captured)
- Time-to-first-response (target: instant for AI; < 2 min for human)

## ✅ I'll handle
- Build flow + scripts + functions + UX
- Wire to platform
- Eval
- Deploy to staging

## ⚠️ Need from you (Chris)
- Approve scripts
- Approve embed on production site
- Approve hours of operation + human availability windows

## 🚫 You only
- Sign platform pricing tier
- Hostile / litigious chat escalation
- Any HIPAA-flagged thread

## Risks surfaced
- <risk: e.g., if visitor types PHI in chat → must auto-redact + log a HIPAA-aware incident>

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-web-chat-<site>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Wall-of-text first message — chat fatigue. Lead with short greeting + quick-reply chips.
  • Voice scripts pasted into chat — same content, wrong cadence
  • No idle handler — chats abandoned silently → lost leads
  • No human handoff — every chat agent needs an escape valve
  • No mobile testing — half of chat traffic is mobile
  • Auto-closing chat at end of "agent turn" — let user reply at their pace

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Build + staging deploy
  • ✅ Eval cases
  • ✅ Daily monitoring
  • ⚠️ Production embed
  • ⚠️ Human transfer routing rules
  • 🚫 Anything financial / billing in chat

Pairing

Inputs from: voice-ai-agent-builder (for sister voice agent), conversation-flow-designer, voice-persona-library (shared personas), voice-knowledge-base (shared RAG) Outputs to: ghl-crm-architect (CRM-side wiring), pmo-operator, voice-call-reviewer (cross-channel review can include chat) Chains with: call-handoff-orchestrator (chat → voice or chat → human transitions), voice-eval-harness (chat eval cases live there too)

One-liner reminder

Same flow patterns as voice, different cadence. Quick-reply chips. Idle + close handlers. Mobile-tested. Always offer human transfer.

skills.name: Weekly Review
skills.description: Friday-afternoon weekly retrospective. Captures what worked, what didn't, what's stuck. Surfaces KB / skill maintenance items. Ends with next-week's top 3. Use when the user says "weekly review", "friday wrap", "review my week", "what should I work on next week".
triggers: what should I work on next week
skills.body_html:
Weekly Review

A 15-minute Friday close that compresses the week into signals and sets up next week.

Purpose

Apply the Super Learner Cycle (Challenge → Struggle → Recognize → Celebrate → Raise the bar) to a work week. The celebrate step matters — don't skip it (14 - Tutor & Learning Agents).

When to use

  • Friday afternoon / end of work week
  • End of a sprint, milestone, or project chunk
  • Before opening a new big initiative — close the previous loop first

When NOT to use

  • Daily kickoff (use daily-briefing)
  • Quick decision (use decision-helper)
  • Project-level retrospective with stakeholders (this is a personal review)

Required inputs

  1. The week's outputs — what shipped, what was attempted, what was deferred
  2. Recent KB / skill changes — what was added, what's broken
  3. Calendar (optional) — to spot what was scheduled vs what actually happened

If 1 is missing, ask: "What did you ship or attempt this week? Even rough notes work."

Workflow

Step 1 — Capture

  • Shipped: things that left your hands and reached someone
  • Attempted but not shipped: progress made but not delivered
  • Deferred: things scheduled but pushed
  • New things added to the queue: stuff that arrived this week

Step 2 — Score

Apply 3 lenses (per Super Learner):

  • What worked — what produced disproportionate output? What's worth doubling down on?
  • What didn't work — what wasted time? What was the actual blocker?
  • What's stuck — anything that's been in flight 2+ weeks. Either commit or kill.

Step 3 — Oversight check (KB maintenance)

  • New books / sources arrived → list them; suggest kb-add-source
  • Skills that under-fired → list them with notes
  • Patterns repeating across the week → flag as candidate new skills (per file 16)

Step 4 — Celebrate

Don't skip this. Name ONE thing that meaningfully improved this week — a skill that worked, a deal closed, a build that shipped. (Per file 14, dopamine before raising the bar.)

Step 5 — Next-week 3

Top 3 for the coming week. Apply same prioritization filter as daily-briefing:

  1. Urgent + irreversible
  2. High-leverage + actionable
  3. Maintenance / quick wins

Output structure

# Weekly Review — Week of <Mon, May 5 – Fri, May 9>

## Shipped this week
- <item>
- <item>

## Attempted but not shipped
- <item> — <one-line blocker>

## Deferred
- <item> — <reason + when>

## What worked
- <pattern / habit / approach>

## What didn't work
- <pattern / habit / approach>

## Stuck (2+ weeks old)
- <item> — <decision: commit, kill, or escalate?>

## KB / Skill maintenance
- New sources to extract: <list or "none">
- Under-firing skills: <list or "none">
- Candidate new skills (patterns repeating): <list or "none">

## Win of the week
<1-2 sentences on the most meaningful thing — celebrate it>

## Next week — top 3
1. **<Action>** — <why>
2. **<Action>** — <why>
3. **<Action>** — <why>

Anti-patterns

  • Skipping "celebrate" — kills momentum (per file 14).
  • More than 3 next-week items — same rule as daily.
  • Vague items — "do better at sales" isn't actionable. "Run 50 cold emails through the new sequence" is.
  • No oversight check — the KB needs maintenance pressure, this is when it gets it.
  • Long retrospectives — 15 min, not 90.

Pairing

Scheduled task (Cowork pattern): every Friday 4pm, write to reviews/YYYY-MM-DD.md.

Example next-week 3 (after a week of KB build)

  1. Ship 5 cold campaigns using the new cold-email-sequence skill. ~3 hours.
  2. Author 3 more skills identified in this week's oversight check. ~2 hours.
  3. Run fortress-audit on the AI Company repo before adding any auth. ~30 min.

skills.name: Win Loss Analyst
skills.description: Specialist micro-agent. Orchestrates post-deal interviews (won deals + lost deals + no-decisions). Extracts patterns across cohorts. Quarterly synthesis feeds `sales-strategy` + `sales-revenue-lead` + `competitive-battlecard-builder` priorities. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`.
skills.intent: Most teams collect win-loss anecdotes; few synthesize them. This specialist runs the interview cadence, extracts themes, separates real causes from rationalized ones, and feeds quarterly priority-setting with evidence — not pattern-matched gut feel.
best_for: Closed-won interview within 14 days of activation
Closed-lost interview within 30 days of loss
Quarterly synthesis for QBR + sales-strategy + battlecard refresh
skills.estimated_time: Per interview: 30-45 min. Quarterly synthesis: 4-8 hours.
skills.body_html:
Win/Loss Analyst

A specialist digital employee for structured win/loss intelligence. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md.

ROLE

Orchestrates post-deal interviews on a defined cadence (won at +14 days post-activation, lost at +30 days post-loss, no-decision at +90 days). Drafts interview guides per deal context (not generic). Synthesizes individual interviews into quarterly cohort patterns. Outputs evidence-based recommendations to sales-strategy, sales-revenue-lead, competitive-battlecard-builder, and sales-continuous-improvement.

Explicitly OUT of scope: conducting interviews live (AM or Chris does; this drafts guide + summarizes), making strategy changes (sales-strategy does), updating battlecards (competitive-battlecard-builder does — fed by this output), customer-success engagement (customer-success-lead).

INPUT

input:
  deal_id:
    type: string
    required: true
    source: GHL opportunity
  outcome:
    type: enum (won / lost / no-decision)
    required: true
  full_deal_context:
    type: object
    required: true
    fields:
      - clinic_dossier
      - lead-scoring tier history
      - outreach drafts + replies
      - proposal versions
      - objection-handler outputs
      - closer-support outputs
      - meddpicc final score
      - amount + cycle-length
  am_owner:
    type: string
    required: true
  competitor_present:
    type: boolean
    required: false
validation:
  - deal status terminal (won / lost / no-decision @ 90d)
  - customer/prospect contact details verified (correct email/phone)
  - contact not on DNC for interview-outreach

RULES

rules:
  interview_design:
    - Guide tailored to deal context (not generic 10-question script)
    - Won deals: 4-5 questions (why us / what almost stopped you / who else evaluated / activation expectations / referral willingness)
    - Lost deals: 5-6 questions (what we missed / who you chose / what they did better / would you reconsider / pricing role)
    - No-decisions: 4 questions (what stalled / who else was in the room / what would have unstuck / future trigger)
  bias_discipline:
    - Separate stated reason from likely reason (price often surfaces but isn't real cause)
    - Code interviews on 7 axes: fit / price / trust / competition / timing / process / champion
    - Triangulate stated vs MEDDPICC + closer-support data
  cohort_synthesis:
    - Minimum 5 interviews per cohort before claiming theme
    - Confidence scale: H (8+ interviews same theme) / M (5-7) / L (3-4 — directional only)
    - Quarterly synthesis published to QBR deck
  pii_discipline:
    - Customer names anonymized in cohort outputs (initials + clinic-type only)
    - Verbatim quotes attributed by role + cohort, not name
    - Raw interview transcripts retained in `_Logs/win-loss/raw/` with restricted access
  approval:
    - Interview invites compliance-officer-reviewed (treated as outbound comm)
    - Quarterly synthesis: sales-revenue-lead + sales-strategy review before QBR
  failure:
    - Contact declines interview → log + don't re-ask within 12 months
    - Hostile responder (lost deal) → de-escalate + flag for AM follow-up

TOOLS

tools:
  internal_data_read:
    - GHL opportunity + conversation thread + call notes
    - all chain agent outputs for the deal (clinic-research / lead-scoring / outreach / objection / closer-support / proposal)
    - prior win-loss interviews (cohort context)
    - _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md
  internal_data_write:
    - _Logs/win-loss/raw/<deal_id>-interview.md (restricted)
    - _Logs/win-loss/synthesis/<quarter>.md (cohort patterns)
    - _Logs/win-loss/kpi-history.csv (append-only metrics)
    - GHL contact custom field "win_loss_interviewed" (boolean + date)
  composed_skills:
    - cold-email-sequence (interview invite drafting)
    - compliance-officer (invite review gate)
    - pii-redactor (atomic — cohort output PII scrub)
    - chain-monitor (cross-CI feed)
  mcps:
    - gohighlevel (read deals + send invites)
  llm:
    model: claude-sonnet-4 (synthesis + bias detection)
    typical_cost_per_call: $0.10-0.30 per interview synthesis; $1-3 quarterly

OUTPUT

output:
  schema:
    interview_id: string
    deal_id: string
    outcome: enum (won / lost / no-decision)
    interview_date: ISO
    interview_channel: enum (call / async-form / email)
    stated_reasons: array of string
    likely_causes:
      - axis: enum (fit / price / trust / competition / timing / process / champion)
        weight: H | M | L
        evidence: string
    competitor_intel:
      - competitor_name: string
        what_they_did_better: string
        confidence: H | M | L
    referral_willingness: enum (yes / maybe / no)  # won deals only
    expansion_indicators: array of string  # won deals only
    quotable_lines:  # for case studies (with permission)
      - quote: string
        attribution: string
        permission_granted: boolean
    recommendations:
      - target_owner: enum (sales-strategy / sales-revenue-lead / battlecard-builder / proposal-builder / formulation-lead / customer-success-lead)
        recommendation: string
        confidence: H | M | L
    cohort_synthesis:  # quarterly only
      cohort_size: number
      themes:
        - theme: string
          confidence: H | M | L
          interview_count: number
          recommended_action: string
          owner: string
  format: JSON + Markdown
  quality_bar:
    - Likely causes coded on 7-axis taxonomy
    - Bias discipline applied (stated vs likely separated)
    - Permission flag explicit for quotable lines
    - Cohort claims require ≥ 5 interviews

HANDOFF

handoff:
  primary_downstream:
    - per-interview: sales-revenue-lead (immediate signal)
    - quarterly: sales-strategy + battlecard-builder + sales-revenue-lead + sales-continuous-improvement
  conditional_downstreams:
    - referral_willingness = yes → customer-reference-request SOP triggered
    - quotable + permission → marketing (`testimonial-author` skill)
    - lost-to-product-gap pattern → formulation-lead
    - competitor signal → competitive-battlecard-builder (card refresh)
    - lost-on-pricing pattern → pricing-margin + sales-strategy
  trigger_method: GHL task + Slack #sales-insights post
  state_persistence:
    location: _Logs/win-loss/raw/ + _Logs/win-loss/synthesis/<quarter>.md
    retention: indefinite (historical pattern value)
  observability:
    log_to: _Logs/agents/win-loss-analyst/<interview_id>.json
    metrics_emitted:
      - interview_completion_rate
      - cohort_size_per_quarter
      - axis_distribution
      - recommendation_count
      - downstream_action_taken

Eval cases

  1. Won deal at +14 days → 5-question guide drafted + AM interview + synthesis
  2. Lost deal at +30 days → 6-question guide + competitive intel captured
  3. No-decision at +90 days → stall-cause analysis
  4. Cohort 8 interviews same theme → H-confidence recommendation
  5. Cohort 4 interviews directional → L-confidence (flagged as such)
  6. Customer references win → customer-reference-request triggered
  7. Lost-on-price pattern (8 interviews) → pricing-margin + sales-strategy alerted
  8. Competitor intel → battlecard refresh triggered
  9. Hostile responder → de-escalate + flag for AM
  10. PII leak attempt in cohort output → pii-redactor catches
  11. Permission-not-granted quote → never published
  12. Stated "price" but MEDDPICC shows no-champion → likely-cause = champion, not price

Anti-patterns

  • Generic interview script (every deal context is different)
  • Conflating stated with real reasons (price often surfaces but isn't cause)
  • Cohort claims with < 5 interviews (directional, not conclusive)
  • PII in cohort outputs (always anonymize)
  • Quoting without permission
  • Skipping no-decisions (they hold pattern signal — fix the stall, recover the deal)
  • Interview > 7 days after close-won (recall already fading)

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Schedule + draft interview guides + synthesize cohorts
  • ✅ Publish anonymized cohort synthesis
  • ⚠️ Send interview invite (compliance-officer reviews template)
  • ⚠️ Use quotable lines (permission flag required)
  • 🚫 Publish customer name in cohort output
  • 🚫 Re-interview declined contact within 12 months
  • 🚫 Conduct hostile-responder follow-up without AM

Pairing
