Inputs from: GHL opportunity + all chain agent outputs + prior win-loss cohorts Outputs to: sales-strategy, sales-revenue-lead, competitive-battlecard-builder, proposal-builder, formulation-lead, customer-success-lead, sales-continuous-improvement, marketing (testimonials), pmo-operator Composed by: sales-revenue-lead, _Workflows/pipeline-to-closed-won.workflow.md (post-close branch), QBR cycle Composes: cold-email-sequence, compliance-officer, pii-redactor, chain-monitor

One-liner reminder

7-axis coding. Stated ≠ likely. Cohort ≥ 5 for theme claims. PII scrubbed. Permission for quotes. Won @ 14d / Lost @ 30d / No-decision @ 90d.

skills.name: Workflow Automation Builder
skills.description: Take a `ghl-crm-architect` Build Map (or similar automation design) and deploy it as live workflows in GoHighLevel (or n8n / Zapier / Make). Wraps the gohighlevel MCP + harness skills (ghl-workflow-library, n8n-workflow-patterns). Fills the critical gap — currently architects design but nothing deploys.
skills.intent: Be the executor. Reads a Build Map (CRM pipelines, stages, fields, tags, automations) and turns it into live config in GHL or another automation tool. Logs every mutation. Always dry-runs first. Never auto-applies destructive changes.
best_for: After `ghl-crm-architect` produces a Build Map — deploy it
skills.estimated_time: 30-120 min depending on complexity
skills.body_html:
Workflow Automation Builder

The executor. Designs go in, live config comes out.

When to use

  • After ghl-crm-architect produces an approved Build Map
  • Standing up a new sub-account's automations end-to-end
  • Migrating workflows between accounts
  • Adding new offer-specific automations to an existing GHL account

When NOT to use

  • Designing the Build Map (use ghl-crm-architect first)
  • Single-record CRM update (just use the MCP directly)
  • One-off Zapier (overkill to formalize)

Required setup

  • mcp__gohighlevel__* MCP connected (Matrix location: JhHwwzVOQOZO2IFLSJAD)
  • Approved Build Map from ghl-crm-architect (or n8n / Zapier design)
  • Production-vs-staging decision made

Required inputs

  1. Build Map — approved spec from ghl-crm-architect
  2. Target environment — staging / production
  3. Tool target — GHL / n8n / Zapier / Make / other
  4. Migration mode (if refactor) — additive only / refactor / full replace
  5. Approval status — must be ✅ approved by Chris (verify before any mutation)

If 5 isn't documented, STOP and request approval before proceeding.

Matrix config reference

Always load _Shared Context/ghl-config.md first. It documents the 7 production Matrix workflows already spec'd:

  1. MAS · Credentialing Approved
  2. MAS · Order Placed
  3. MAS · Payment Received
  4. MAS · Shipped
  5. MAS · Delivered + Reorder Prompt
  6. MAS · Abandoned Cart Recovery
  7. MAS · Appointment Confirmation + Reminders

Plus email/SMS template IDs (e.g., 69ef510d12412f4bbb73d8b5 = MAS · credentialing_approved), tag taxonomy, pipeline stage IDs. Use these as building blocks; don't reinvent.

Note: GHL API doesn't support workflow POST — workflows must be created in GHL UI from blueprints. This skill verifies + deploys field-level + tag-level + custom-value changes.

Workflow

Step 1 — Verify approval

Check _Logs/agent-decisions/ for the corresponding ghl-crm-architect output with Chris's approval. If not approved → stop + escalate.

Step 2 — Dry-run

Apply the Build Map in simulation mode first:

  • Verify every referenced custom field exists or will be created
  • Verify pipeline/stage IDs map correctly
  • Verify tag prefixes don't collide with existing
  • Verify calendar IDs are valid
  • Verify webhook URLs respond (200 OK on test ping)

Output a dry-run report. If any error → halt, surface, do not proceed.

Step 3 — Execute additive operations first

Apply in this safe order:

  1. Create custom fields (additive — never overwrites)
  2. Create pipelines + stages (additive — never deletes existing)
  3. Create tags (additive — case-insensitive dedup)
  4. Create calendars (additive)
  5. Create automations (additive — paused state initially)
  6. Wire webhooks (additive)

After each step, log the change to _Logs/agent-decisions/.

Step 4 — Test automations in paused state

Run each new automation against a test contact (created for this purpose):

  • Verify trigger fires
  • Verify each action executes
  • Verify wait steps work
  • Verify exit conditions
  • Verify failure path
  • Verify webhook calls return success

If any automation fails → fix the design, not the deploy. Loop back to ghl-crm-architect.

Step 5 — Refactor operations (with approval)

If the Build Map includes refactors of existing structures:

  • ⚠️ Stop here and surface the exact diff (before / after)
  • Wait for explicit Chris approval per change
  • Apply one at a time, log each
  • Re-test after each

For irreversible operations (delete custom field, merge contacts) → 🚫 escalate to Chris + counsel if patient-bearing.

Step 6 — Activate automations

After all tested + approved:

  • Switch automations from paused to active
  • Set monitoring expectations (what KPIs to check)
  • Document rollback path (how to pause / disable / restore)

Step 7 — Migration tasks (if refactor)

Per the Build Map's migration plan:

  1. Tag-based migration of contacts to new structures
  2. Validate sample of 20 contacts post-migration
  3. Cut over conversations + automations from old to new
  4. 14-day stability window before decommissioning old structures

Step 8 — Update the agent-registry + automations.md

  • Add deployed automations to agent-registry.md (under CRM / Automation)
  • Create automations.md at the project root (if doesn't exist) — registry of every active automation per automation-scheduler pattern

Step 9 — Output

Output structure

# Workflow Automation Deployed

## Source
**Build Map**: `_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-ghl-crm-architect-<account>.md`
**Approval**: ✅ approved by Chris on <date>

## Environment
**Tool**: GoHighLevel
**Location ID**: <id>
**Stage**: <staging / production>

## Operations executed

### ✅ Additive (auto-applied)
| Operation | Type | Count | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Create custom fields | additive | <N> | ✅ done |
| Create pipelines | additive | <N> | ✅ done |
| Create stages | additive | <N> | ✅ done |
| Create tags | additive | <N> | ✅ done |
| Create calendars | additive | <N> | ✅ done |
| Create automations (paused) | additive | <N> | ✅ done |
| Wire webhooks | additive | <N> | ✅ done |

### ⚠️ Refactor (per-change approvals)
| Change | Before | After | Approved | Applied |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| <change> | <state> | <state> | <date by Chris> | <date> |

## Tests executed
| Automation | Test contact | Result |
|---|---|---|
| <name> | <test-contact-id> | ✅ pass / ❌ fail (with details) |

## Activation
- Automations switched from paused → active: <list>
- Monitoring KPIs: <list>
- Rollback path: <how to disable>

## Migration (if applicable)
- Contacts migrated: <N> of <M>
- Validation sample: <result>
- Old structures decommission date: <14 days from now>

## Risks surfaced
- <risk>

## Registered in
- `agent-registry.md` (CRM / Automation section)
- Project root `automations.md`

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-workflow-deploy-<account>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Deploying without approved Build Map — design first, then deploy
  • Skipping the dry-run — production mutations without simulation = breakage
  • Auto-applying refactor / delete operations — always per-change approval
  • Activating automations without testing them — they fire on real contacts immediately
  • No rollback path — if something breaks, you need a kill switch
  • No migration validation when refactoring an existing account — leaves orphan contacts

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run dry-run simulation
  • ✅ Create new custom fields / pipelines / stages / tags / calendars (additive)
  • ✅ Create automations in paused state
  • ✅ Wire webhooks (additive)
  • ✅ Test automations against test contacts
  • ⚠️ Refactor existing structures (per-change approval from Chris)
  • ⚠️ Activate automations (move from paused → active)
  • ⚠️ Run contact migrations
  • 🚫 Delete custom fields / pipelines / stages / contacts without explicit Chris approval
  • 🚫 Modify payment / billing config
  • 🚫 Bulk-update contacts in destructive ways

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • ghl-crm-architect (Build Map)
  • _Shared Context/tool-access.md (which MCPs available)
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (TCPA / A2P / HIPAA)
  • _Shared Context/approval-rules.md

Outputs to:

  • Live GHL config
  • agent-registry.md (CRM/Automation entries)
  • automations.md at project root (registry of live automations)
  • _Logs/agent-decisions/

Chains with:

  • ghl-crm-architect (upstream — designs the map)
  • security-inspector (verifies compliance before activation)
  • pmo-operator (tracks the deploy as a project)
  • weekly-review (KPIs surface in weekly metrics)

Wraps

  • gohighlevel MCP (the actual API surface)
  • Harness skills ghl-workflow-library, ghl-snippet-library, ghl-pipeline-builder (component-level patterns)

One-liner reminder

Verify approval. Dry-run. Additive operations first (auto). Refactors per-approval. Test before activate. Log every mutation.

skills.name: Workflow Builder
skills.description: Generate a new `.workflow.md` file in `_Workflows/`. Input is a multi-agent process; output is an executable workflow spec with agent chain, approval gates, inputs/outputs, failure modes. Forces the standard workflow format so company-os-orchestrator can load any new workflow without changes.
skills.intent: Be the canonical generator for multi-agent workflow files. Repeatable patterns that span 3+ agents get codified here so they survive the first run and become repeatable. Auto-detects approval gates, links to shared context, registers handoffs.
best_for: Codifying a multi-agent process that ran once and should run again
Designing a new department's standard work
Replacing ad-hoc agent chains with named workflows
skills.body_html:
Workflow Builder

The skill that turns ad-hoc multi-agent runs into named, repeatable workflow files.

When to use

  • A multi-agent task ran successfully and will run again — codify it
  • Spinning up a new department needs a standard work pattern
  • The orchestrator keeps re-deriving the same agent chain — name it
  • Per the company OS principle: "if a chain runs more than once, write it as a workflow"

When NOT to use

  • Single-agent task (just invoke the skill)
  • One-time exploration (don't formalize the unique)
  • Existing workflow already covers the case (extend it instead)

Required inputs

  1. Use case — what triggers this workflow (e.g., "outbound voice campaign", "new offer launch")
  2. Agent chain — ordered list of agents involved, OR a description from which to derive it
  3. Approval gates — where humans must approve (between which steps)
  4. Inputs — what the workflow needs to start
  5. Outputs — what artifacts the workflow produces

If 2 is unclear, ask: "What's the start-to-finish sequence — which agent does what, in what order?"

Workflow

Step 1 — Decide if it qualifies as a workflow

Check:

  • 3+ agents involved? (If not → it's a skill, not a workflow)
  • Will run more than once? (If not → don't formalize)
  • Has a defined start trigger and end state? (If not → not ready)
  • Crosses departments OR has approval gates? (If yes → workflow has high value)

Step 2 — Map the agent chain

Use the standard workflow file structure:

Step 1: <agent name>
   → <input>
   → <output>
   → Approval gate (if applicable)
   ↓
Step 2: <agent name>
   → <input from previous step>
   → <output>
   ↓
...

For each step, identify:

  • Agent: which skill from agent-registry.md
  • Inputs: from previous step or external
  • Outputs: artifact name + path
  • Approval gate: yes/no + what's being approved
  • Auto-skip condition: when this step can be skipped (e.g., "skip if ICP already defined")
  • Failure path: what happens if this step fails

Step 3 — Identify approval gates

Per _Shared Context/approval-rules.md. Typical gates:

  • Pre-launch (before any commitment)
  • Pre-production deploy (after eval, before live)
  • Post-soft-launch (before scale-up)
  • Post-mortem (before declaring success / kill)

Step 4 — Document failure modes + recovery

For each likely failure point:

  • Failure: what could go wrong
  • Detection: how we know it failed
  • Recovery: specific remediation steps
  • Auto-pause trigger: any auto-halt conditions

Step 5 — Specify required pre-authorizations

Per _Shared Context/tool-access.md + approval-rules.md. List ✅ autonomous / ⚠️ approval / 🚫 never per action type.

Step 6 — Generate the workflow file

Apply the standard workflow file template:

# Workflow: <Name>

## When this workflow fires
- <Trigger phrases>
- <Manual invocations>

## Agent chain
[step-by-step diagram]

## Standard inputs
- ...

## Standard outputs
- <artifact 1 path>
- ...

## Approval gates (N)
1. After Step X — <what's being approved>
2. After Step Y — <what>

## Estimated time
- Setup: <range>
- Per-run: <range>

## Auto-pause triggers
- <condition>
- ...

## Failure modes + recovery
| Failure | Recovery |
|---|---|

## Pre-authorizations
- ✅ ...
- ⚠️ ...
- 🚫 ...

## Roll-up to executives
After completion, this workflow's results surface in:
- <where>

## Last updated
<date> by <name>

Step 7 — Save + register

  1. Save to _Workflows/<name>.workflow.md
  2. Update company-os-orchestrator/SKILL.md routing table to include the new workflow
  3. Update agent-registry.md if any agents in the chain are new
  4. Log creation to _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-new-workflow-<name>.md

Output structure

# Workflow Created: <name>

## File
**Path**: `_Workflows/<name>.workflow.md`
**Trigger phrases**: <list>
**Agent chain**: <N> agents
**Approval gates**: <N>

## Workflow content
<paste the full workflow file>

## Registry / routing updates
- ✅ Added to `company-os-orchestrator` routing table
- ✅ Cross-referenced in `agent-registry.md` if new agents

## Test plan
- Dry-run: simulate first 2 steps without committing
- First real run: do under monitoring
- After 3 successful runs: declare "stable"

## Logged to
`_Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-new-workflow-<name>.md`

Anti-patterns

  • Codifying ad-hoc work that only happens once — premature formalization
  • Workflows without approval gates — when stakes are real, gates are non-optional
  • Workflows without failure recovery — production workflows hit failures; specify the path
  • Vague agent assignments ("then somebody writes the copy") — name the specific skill
  • No auto-pause triggers for high-volume / high-stakes workflows
  • Skipping the dry-run before first real production execution

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Generate workflow file
  • ✅ Save to _Workflows/
  • ✅ Update agent-registry.md (additive)
  • ⚠️ Update orchestrator routing table (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Run the workflow live without dry-run

Pairing

Inputs from:

  • User description of process
  • agent-registry.md (to validate referenced agents exist)
  • _Templates/handoff-template.md (handoff format between steps)
  • _Templates/project-brief.md (workflows tied to a project use this)

Outputs to:

  • New workflow file in _Workflows/
  • company-os-orchestrator (added to routing)
  • agent-registry.md

Chains with:

  • skill-builder (if any agents in the chain don't exist yet)
  • pmo-operator (workflows execute through PMO tracking)

One-liner reminder

Process → workflow file with agent chain + gates + failure modes. Dry-run first. Register in orchestrator routing.

skills.name: Workflow Visualizer
skills.description: Map any system or workflow as a beautiful interactive HTML diagram
skills.body_html:
Workflow Visualizer

Turn any system description into a beautiful, interactive HTML diagram. Click nodes, hover for details, and see exactly how data flows through your workflow. One self-contained HTML file, no dependencies.

How It Works

When you say "visualize this workflow" or "map out this system", this skill parses your description, identifies every component, and builds an interactive diagram you can open in any browser.

Step 1: Parse the System

Extract these components from the user's description:

Component What to Look For Example
Triggers What starts the workflow "When a new email arrives", "Every morning at 9am"
Inputs Data that enters the system "Email body", "CSV file", "API response"
Processing Steps Actions that transform data "Extract key info", "Summarize text", "Filter results"
Tools / Services External tools or APIs used "Claude", "Zapier", "Google Sheets", "Slack"
Decision Points Conditional branches "If priority is high", "When amount > $100"
Outputs Final results or deliverables "Send Slack message", "Update spreadsheet", "Generate report"
Loops Recurring or repeating steps "Repeat for each item", "Run daily"
Data Stores Where data lives between steps "Database", "JSON file", "Spreadsheet"

If the user's description is vague, ask clarifying questions before building.

Step 2: Choose a Layout

Select the layout that best fits the workflow shape:

Layout Structure Best For
Left-to-Right Flow Horizontal chain of nodes Linear processes, pipelines, simple workflows
Top-Down Waterfall Vertical cascade Sequential steps, decision trees, funnels
Hub and Spoke Central node with radiating connections One tool that connects to many, API integrations
Swimlane Horizontal lanes per tool/person Multi-team processes, handoffs between systems
Circular Loop back to start Recurring workflows, feedback loops, monitoring

Step 3: Build the Interactive HTML

Generate a single self-contained HTML file with these features:

Node Types and Colors

Node Type Background Border Icon Style
Trigger #1e3a5f #3B82F6 (blue) Lightning bolt, clock, webhook
Processing #1a3d2e #10B981 (green) Gear, wand, filter
Tool / Service #3d2e1a #F59E0B (amber) Tool logo or wrench
Output #3d1a1a #EF4444 (red) Check, send, export
Data Store #1a1a2e #6366F1 (indigo) Database, folder, file
Decision #2e1a3d #A855F7 (purple) Question mark, fork

Node Content

Each node displays:

  • Icon (emoji or SVG) at the top
  • Label (2-4 words, bold)
  • Subtitle (one short line describing what happens)

Connections and Interactivity

Lines: Solid with arrowheads, color matches source node border, optional data label, animated dash on highlight.

Hover: Node scales 1.05x, border glows, tooltip with step description.

Click: Highlights node and direct connections, dims unconnected nodes, click background to reset.

Responsive:

  • Diagram scales to fit the viewport
  • Works on desktop and tablet screens
  • Nodes wrap or scroll on very small screens

Style Defaults

Page bg #0f0f0f, container #161616, header text #ffffff, subtitle #888888, node text #ffffff, connections #444444 (colored when highlighted). System sans-serif font, 12px border radius, 20px node padding, 160px min node width.

Include a header (workflow name + description), the diagram container, and a color-coded legend (Blue=Triggers, Green=Processing, Amber=Tools, Red=Outputs, Indigo=Data Stores, Purple=Decisions). All styles and JS inline, no external dependencies.

Rules

  1. Every node must be a specific action, not a vague label. "Process data" is bad. "Extract invoice total from PDF" is good
  2. Connections must show what data flows between nodes. Not just that they connect, but WHAT passes through
  3. Decision points need labeled branches. "Yes" and "No", or the specific conditions
  4. No floating nodes. Every node must connect to at least one other node
  5. Dark background always. Light mode diagrams wash out and look less professional
  6. Max 20 nodes per diagram. If the workflow is bigger, split into sub-diagrams
  7. Self-contained HTML. No external CSS, JS, or image dependencies. Must work offline

Example Usage

  • "Visualize this workflow: new lead from website form, added to CRM, sales gets Slack notification"
  • "Map out this system: Claude writes drafts, Grammarly checks, Google Docs for review"
  • "Diagram my workflow: check email, triage urgent vs non-urgent, route to Slack or Notion"

Tips for Best Results

  • Describe your workflow step by step in plain language
  • Mention every tool or service involved by name
  • Include any conditions or branches ("if X then Y, otherwise Z")
  • The more detail you give, the more accurate the diagram will be

workflows.name: Workflow: A2P 10DLC Approval Recovery (Specialist Chain)
workflows.description: End-to-end chain from TCR rejection (or stuck-pending state) → approved brand + campaigns + post-approval validation. Composed from new A2P specialists + existing compliance/deploy infrastructure. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md` specialist-chain pattern + `_Shared Context/sops/a2p-resubmission.sop.md`.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: A2P 10DLC Approval Recovery (Specialist Chain)

End-to-end chain from TCR rejection (or stuck-pending state) → approved brand + campaigns + post-approval validation. Composed from new A2P specialists + existing compliance/deploy infrastructure. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md specialist-chain pattern + _Shared Context/sops/a2p-resubmission.sop.md.

Chain overview

[cron] a2p-status-watcher  (daily 10am PT Mon-Fri)
   │
   ├─→ stable: silent log → exit
   ├─→ approved transition: notify Chris + queue SOP Step 9 validation → exit
   ├─→ stuck > 14 days: escalate Twilio human-review (per SOP Step 8) → exit
   ▼
[1] a2p-registration-inspector  (fires on rejection / suspension)
   ↓ (output: diagnostic + root cause + fix_required + confidence)
[2] a2p-resubmission-builder  (only if confidence ≥ M and not escalated)
   ↓ (output: brand profile JSON + campaigns + samples + opt-in docs)
[3] compliance-officer  (Gate 1 — pre-approval compliance review)
   ↓ (output: signoff OR revise-list)
[4] ⚠️ Chris approval gate  (review samples + opt-in + sign approval.md)
   ↓ (output: signed approval)
[5] workflow-automation-builder  (push to GHL → trigger TCR submission)
   ↓ (output: submission confirmation + submission_id + timestamp)
[6] a2p-status-watcher (watch mode T+1, T+3, T+5, T+10 business days)
   ↓
   ├─→ approved → SOP Step 9 post-approval validation → chain ends
   └─→ rejected → loop to [1] (count attempts; if 2nd → escalate per SOP Step 8)

When this workflow fires

  • TCR / carrier rejection email landed (manual trigger or status-watcher auto-detect)
  • Brand or campaign stuck pending > 14 business days
  • Approved campaign suddenly suspended (post-approval audit)
  • First-time A2P submission after /privacy + /terms shipped (pre-flight via inspector check)

Pre-flight checks

  • /privacy page reachable at https://matrix-order-form.vercel.app/privacy (200, not 404) — known Matrix blocker per ghl-config.md
  • /terms page reachable at https://matrix-order-form.vercel.app/terms (200, not 404)
  • EIN of record confirmed against IRS records (Chris to verify if [verify with Chris] flag present)
  • DUNS number on file OR explicit decision to skip (small business)
  • State pharmacy board license summary accessible
  • Matrix legal entity: Matrix Advanced Solutions LLC per company-profile.md (verify exact spelling matches IRS)
  • No active resubmission chain already running (idempotency on submission_id)
  • HELP/STOP wiring verified end-to-end via test send (post-approval only)

Hard block: if /privacy or /terms 404 → chain halts at pre-flight + routes to _Shared Context/sops/production-deploy.sop.md to ship pages BEFORE inspector runs.

Specialist 1: a2p-registration-inspector

Role

Diagnose rejection root cause. Map to category. Cite source-of-truth for every fix. Healthcare scrutiny applied.

Input

  • trigger (rejection-email | status-watcher-alert | manual)
  • rejection_payload (verbatim text + reason code if available)
  • location_id = JhHwwzVOQOZO2IFLSJAD

Rules

  • Per a2p-registration-inspector SKILL
  • Capture rejection verbatim — no paraphrase
  • Cross-check brand fields against company-profile.md
  • Cross-check samples against compliance-phrase-detector
  • Confidence honest (H / M / L)

Tools

  • GHL MCP (read location + custom fields)
  • WebFetch (TCR reason-code docs)
  • compliance-phrase-detector

Output

  • diagnostic schema (root_cause_category + specific_fields_failing + fix_required + confidence + escalation_needed)

Handoff

  • If escalation_needed = true → Chris + counsel; chain pauses
  • If root_cause = privacy_terms_404 → production-deploy.sop.md; chain pauses
  • If confidence ≥ M → a2p-resubmission-builder
  • If confidence = L → Chris manual review → resume

Approval gate

  • None at this step (diagnostic is non-mutating)

Observability

  • _Logs/ghl/a2p/<YYYY-MM-DD>-diagnostic.{md,json}

Specialist 2: a2p-resubmission-builder

Role

Author corrected brand profile + use-case-split campaigns + 5-7 sample messages per campaign + opt-in flow docs + HELP/STOP specs.

Input

  • diagnostic from [1]
  • scope (brand-only / campaign-only / both)
  • privacy_terms_live = true (validated at pre-flight)

Rules

  • Per a2p-resubmission-builder SKILL
  • Brand fields match company-profile.md exactly
  • Split "Mixed" use cases (transactional / customer-care / marketing separately)
  • Every sample message passes compliance-phrase-detector
  • Opt-in audited not described (screenshots + capture language)
  • Industry sub-category = Pharmaceuticals — Compounding
  • Attach: state pharmacy board summary, 503-A/B disclosure, provider-only policy

Tools

  • GHL MCP (read brand state pre-write)
  • compliance-phrase-detector (every sample)
  • compliance-officer (Gate 1 — composed at end of this skill)

Output

  • package manifest + brand-profile.json + campaign-.json + opt-in-flow.md + Chris approval ask

Handoff

  • On compliance-officer Gate 1 pass → Chris approval gate ([4])
  • On Gate 1 fail → loop within this skill (revise samples) until pass

Approval gate

  • compliance-officer Gate 1 (internal to this skill)

Observability

  • _Logs/ghl/a2p/<package_id>/ (full package versioned)

Specialist 3: compliance-officer (Gate 1)

Role

Final cross-cutting compliance review of the package before it touches Chris. Apply claims discipline + TCPA + 503-A/B + provider-only language gates.

Input

  • Full package from [2]

Rules

  • Per compliance-officer SKILL
  • No banned phrases (cross-check compliance-rules.md)
  • No medical claims requiring FDA approval
  • Opt-in language matches TCPA discipline
  • Provider-only positioning preserved (never D2C)
  • Healthcare extras attached + accurate

Output

gate_1_status: pass | revise | block
issues: array of strings
required_changes: array of strings
sign_off_at: ISO | null

Handoff

  • Pass → Chris approval gate ([4])
  • Revise → loop to [2] with required_changes list
  • Block (regulatory exposure detected) → Chris + counsel; chain pauses

Observability

  • _Logs/compliance-reviews/a2p-<package_id>.md

Approval gate [4]: Chris

Role

Final human gate before TCR submission.

Input

  • Full package + compliance-officer Gate 1 signoff
  • Chris reviews: brand fields, sample messages per campaign, opt-in flow, extra-docs attachments

Asks of Chris

  • ✅ Confirm EIN matches IRS records exactly
  • ✅ Confirm DUNS (or decision to skip)
  • ✅ Approve sample message tone + provider-only framing
  • ✅ Approve opt-in flow documentation
  • ✅ Sign approval at _Logs/ghl/a2p/<package_id>/approval.md

Output

  • signed approval (timestamped)
  • OR revision notes

Handoff

  • Signed → workflow-automation-builder ([5])
  • Revisions → loop to [2]

Observability

  • _Logs/ghl/a2p/<package_id>/approval.md

Specialist 5: workflow-automation-builder

Role

Push approved brand + campaign config to GHL A2P registration surface. Capture TCR submission_id + timestamp.

Input

  • Approved package + Chris signoff

Rules

  • Per workflow-automation-builder SKILL
  • Dry-run first (preview brand JSON + campaign JSON)
  • Then write to GHL
  • Capture submission confirmation + ID
  • Never destructive (additive only on first submit; updates on resubmit explicit)

Tools

  • GHL MCP (write brand + campaigns)

Output

submission:
  submission_id: string
  submitted_at: ISO
  brand_state_after: pending
  campaigns_state_after:
    - name: string
      state: pending
      campaign_id: string

Handoff

  • Always → a2p-status-watcher watch mode (T+1, T+3, T+5, T+10 business days)

Approval gate

  • Already cleared at [4]

Observability

  • _Logs/ghl/a2p/<package_id>/submission-receipt.json

Specialist 6: a2p-status-watcher (watch mode)

Role

Heightened-cadence polling after submission. T+1, T+3, T+5, T+10 business days. Same skill, different schedule.

Input

  • submission_id from [5]

Rules

  • Per a2p-status-watcher SKILL
  • On approval → SOP Step 9 (test send + ramp plan) → chain ends ✅
  • On rejection → fire [1] again; increment attempt counter
  • 2nd rejection on same package → escalate per SOP Step 8 (Twilio human-review)

  • 14 days pending → escalate

Output

  • terminal state (approved / rejected-loop / escalated)

Handoff

  • Approved → SOP Step 9 validation (Chris sends test SMS, verifies HELP/STOP, ramp plan) → chain ends
  • Rejected 1st time → loop to [1]
  • Rejected 2nd time → escalate (Twilio human-review ticket + Chris + counsel decision on alternate channels)

Chain-wide observability

State + tags

  • GHL contact-org tag: a2p-chain:<status>:<step-N>
  • Status enum: running / paused-preflight / paused-escalation / awaiting-chris-approval / submitted-watching / approved-validating / completed-approved / escalated-twilio / failed
  • Submission attempts counter: a2p_attempt_count

Logs

  • _Logs/ghl/a2p/<package_id>/ — package + diagnostic + approval + submission receipt + state changes
  • _Logs/ghl/a2p/status-history.csv — append-only audit trail
  • _Logs/ghl/a2p/status-snapshot-latest.json — current state

Metrics

  • Time from rejection → diagnostic complete (target < 4 hours)
  • Time from diagnostic → Chris-approved package (target < 48 hours)
  • Time from submission → TCR decision (typical 3-7 business days)
  • First-attempt-approval rate (target ≥ 80% post-this-system)
  • Escalation rate to Twilio human-review (target < 10%)
  • Cost per recovery cycle (target < $2.00 — heavy use of atomic compliance-phrase-detector)

Surfaced in:

  • chain-monitor daily 7am digest
  • ghl-continuous-improvement weekly Friday 3:30pm review
  • Chris immediate ping on adverse transitions

Eval coverage

Stored at _Logs/eval-suites/a2p-approval-recovery/:

  1. First-time submission, clean → approved within 5 business days
  2. Mixed use-case rejection → split into 3 campaigns → approved
  3. Sample-message banned phrase ("FDA-approved") → revised → approved
  4. Opt-in not documented → screenshots added → approved
  5. EIN mismatch with IRS → Chris corrects → resubmitted → approved
  6. /privacy still 404 → chain blocks at pre-flight → routes to deploy SOP
  7. 2nd rejection on same package → escalate to Twilio human-review
  8. Brand suspended after approval → fires watcher alert → diagnostic → corrective resubmission
  9. Healthcare extra-docs request from TCR → Chris attaches state board summary → approved
  10. compliance-officer Gate 1 blocks (regulatory exposure) → chain pauses → counsel consult

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Pre-flight blocked (privacy/terms 404) Route to production-deploy.sop.md to ship pages; pause chain
Inspector confidence = L Chris manual review before resubmission-builder fires
Inspector escalation_needed = true Chris + counsel; chain paused indefinitely until decision
compliance-officer Gate 1 revise Builder iterates; max 3 cycles before Chris ask
compliance-officer Gate 1 block Chris + counsel; potential channel-strategy change
Chris rejects sample tone Builder revises; same cycle
GHL MCP unavailable during submission Retry with backoff; if 3 fails → Chris ping + manual GHL UI fallback
TCR 2nd rejection on same package Twilio human-review ticket per SOP Step 8
TCR 14-day pending no decision Twilio support ticket per SOP Step 8
Brand approved → suspended post-launch Watcher fires inspector; treat as fresh rejection
Chain stalled > 7 days at any step Auto-escalate to Chris via chain-monitor

Approval gates summary

Gate Step Who Why
Inspector escalation After [1] Chris + counsel Industry-class or regulatory question
compliance-officer Gate 1 After [2] compliance-officer Compliance review before Chris
Chris final approval [4] Chris Sign-off before TCR submission
Twilio human-review escalation After 2nd rejection Chris + counsel Strategic decision (pattern of rejection)

Cost + latency targets

  • Total chain cost (single cycle): < $2.00 (samples + opt-in docs are LLM-heavy; rest is atomic)
  • Time from rejection → diagnostic: < 4 hours
  • Time from diagnostic → Chris-approved package: < 48 hours
  • Time from submission → approval: 3-7 business days (TCR-side; out of our control)
  • End-to-end (rejection → approved + validated): target < 14 calendar days

Related

  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/a2p-registration-inspector/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/a2p-resubmission-builder/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/a2p-status-watcher/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/compliance-officer/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/workflow-automation-builder/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/chain-monitor/SKILL.md
  • _Shared Context/sops/a2p-resubmission.sop.md (the operational SOP this workflow operationalizes)
  • _Shared Context/sops/production-deploy.sop.md (if privacy/terms ship needed)
  • _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (A2P prerequisite blockers + campaign list)
  • _Shared Context/company-profile.md (legal entity SOT)
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (banned phrases, TCPA)
  • _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md (TCPA + A2P 10DLC pathway)
  • _Templates/micro-agent.template.md

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris — initial composition; operationalizes a2p-resubmission.sop.md end-to-end

workflows.name: Workflow: AI Command Center Build (Front-End for AI Company OS)
workflows.description: Spec for the AI Command Center web app at `~/Projects/ai-command-center/`. Built using the AI Company OS skills: `engineering-lead` orchestrates, `frontend-dev` builds React/UI, `backend-dev` builds API routes, `integration-agent` wires GHL/Anthropic/Retell, `code-qa` reviews, `devops-deployment` ships. This workflow codifies the pattern so future iterations of the front-end reuse the same chain.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: AI Command Center Build (Front-End for AI Company OS)

Spec for the AI Command Center web app at ~/Projects/ai-command-center/. Built using the AI Company OS skills: engineering-lead orchestrates, frontend-dev builds React/UI, backend-dev builds API routes, integration-agent wires GHL/Anthropic/Retell, code-qa reviews, devops-deployment ships. This workflow codifies the pattern so future iterations of the front-end reuse the same chain.

Purpose

A single front-end where Chris (and Leo) can:

  1. Browse + chat with all 160+ AI Company OS specialists — agents respond per their SKILL.md spec
  2. See production-ready output from every department — drafts queue, awaiting-approval, scheduled sends, blocked
  3. Test voice + conversation AI agents — Mack / Mia / Sam / Alex
  4. Watch chains run — lead-to-demo / refund / onboarding / inbound-call / expansion live status
  5. Approve / decline / send from one console

When this workflow fires

  • New surface needed (new page / new dashboard / new tester)
  • Adding live data wiring (replacing placeholder with GHL MCP / Anthropic / Retell)
  • Visual / UX refresh
  • Bug fix or refactor in the command-center repo

Stack (per command-center/CLAUDE.md)

  • Next.js 16 (App Router)
  • React 19
  • Tailwind v4
  • Zustand stores (UI / system / audio)
  • Three.js + React Three Fiber + Drei (for sci-fi 3D scenes)
  • Framer Motion
  • MCP SDK (already wired to ghl-operator-v3-project)
  • Anthropic API for specialist invocation

Agent chain

Step 1: reuse-scanner
   → Check existing pages / components / API routes in ai-command-center
   → If close match → propose extending vs new
   ↓
Step 2: engineering-lead
   → Architecture decision (page vs component vs api route vs lib helper)
   → Identify which existing files change + what's new
   → Define new endpoint(s) + their input/output schemas if API work
   → Approval gate: Chris approves architecture
   ↓
Step 3: frontend-dev
   → Build / edit React components per the architecture
   → Follow command-center conventions (Tailwind v4, cyan accent, motion, "use client" only when interactive)
   → Reuse existing SiteShell + NavHeader layout
   ↓
Step 4: backend-dev (when API work involved)
   → Build / edit API routes under src/app/api/
   → For AI Company integration: read from manifest + invoke Anthropic with SKILL.md
   → For GHL data: use existing MCP client at src/lib/mcp/client.ts
   → For new external integrations: use `integration-agent` per integration-map.md
   ↓
Step 5: integration-agent (when external API wiring)
   → Anthropic / GHL / Retell / Stripe per integration-map.md
   → Idempotency + signature verification + dead-letter where applicable
   → Env vars documented in command-center CLAUDE.md
   ↓
Step 6: code-qa
   → Review per pr-review-process.sop.md
   → TypeScript strict; no `any`; no console.log; structured error handling
   → Security: no secrets client-side; RLS respected
   → Accessibility: keyboard navigation + focus rings + WCAG AA
   → Approval gate: human reviewer approves PR
   ↓
Step 7: compliance-officer (when customer-facing surface OR PHI-touching)
   → Spoon Test on any agent surface that might handle PHI
   → Banned-phrase scan on any customer-visible copy
   → Block if 🔴 findings
   ↓
Step 8: devops-deployment
   → Per production-deploy.sop.md
   → Staging deploy → smoke test → prod deploy
   → 60-min post-deploy monitoring
   ↓
Step 9: chain-monitor (post-launch observability)
   → Feeds command-center metrics into daily 7am digest
   → Surface stuck / errored / slow surfaces

Canonical pages + APIs

Surface Route Reads from Status
Home / Static + system stores ✅ existing (sci-fi shell)
Dashboard /dashboard Live systems grid (placeholder data) ✅ existing
Specialists /specialists /api/ai-company/skills + manifest ✅ 2026-05-11
Production /production /api/ai-company/production-output ✅ placeholder; wire GHL
Campaigns /campaigns GHL campaigns via MCP ✅ existing
Compliance /compliance compliance-officer logs + decisions ✅ existing
Agents Test /agents Simulated chat (legacy) ✅ existing; supersede with /specialists
Terminal /terminal MCP tool exec ✅ existing
Voice Test /voice-test Retell SDK 🟡 planned
Chain Monitor /chain-monitor _Logs/agents/chains/ 🟡 planned
Eval Harness Status /evals All 4 eval harness suites 🟡 planned
Friday CI Review /ci-review 5 CI engine outputs 🟡 planned

API routes

Route Method Purpose
/api/mcp POST/GET/DELETE GHL MCP client (existing)
/api/ai-company/skills GET Manifest + filters (✅ NEW)
/api/ai-company/specialist POST Invoke specialist with messages (✅ NEW; needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
/api/ai-company/production-output GET Department queue (✅ placeholder; wire GHL)
/api/retell/voice-session POST Start Retell call session (🟡 planned)
/api/chains/status GET Live chain state from _Logs/agents/chains/ (🟡 planned)
/api/evals/last-run GET Latest run per eval harness (🟡 planned)

Key conventions

Reading AI Company OS

// src/lib/ai-company/loadSkills.ts already provides:
import { loadSkillsManifest, findSkill, loadSkillContent } from "@/lib/ai-company/loadSkills";

const manifest = await loadSkillsManifest();         // 160 skills
const skill = await findSkill("clinic-research-agent");
const md = await loadSkillContent("clinic-research-agent");  // full SKILL.md

Specialist invocation pattern

// /api/ai-company/specialist already handles:
// 1. Load skill from manifest
// 2. Read full SKILL.md as system prompt
// 3. Call Anthropic API with messages
// 4. Return structured response

// Frontend:
const res = await fetch("/api/ai-company/specialist", {
  method: "POST",
  body: JSON.stringify({ skill: "outreach-writer", messages: [...] })
});

Live data conventions

  • All API routes use export const dynamic = "force-dynamic" for SSR freshness
  • Manifest is cached server-side after first load (in-memory; restart to refresh)
  • For production-output: GHL MCP read calls cached 5 min
  • All routes return { ok: boolean, ...payload, error?: string } shape

Visual

  • Use existing --cyan / --cyan-dim / --ghost / --green / --border-dim CSS vars from globals.css
  • Animate panel-in transitions with framer-motion initial / animate / exit
  • Status colors: pending-review = amber, approved = cyan, blocked = red, scheduled = violet, in-flight = emerald
  • Flavor badges: specialist = cyan, atomic = violet, orchestrator = amber, meta = emerald

Coding standards

  • TypeScript strict; no any
  • No console.log in shipped code
  • Server Components default; "use client" only when state/interactivity required
  • Error states + loading states + empty states for every async surface
  • Accessibility: keyboard nav + focus rings + WCAG AA

Approval gates

Gate When Who
Architecture Step 2 Chris
Live API key activation (Anthropic / Retell / Stripe live) Integration time Chris
Compliance signoff If PHI / customer-facing compliance-officer
Prod deploy Per production-deploy.sop.md Chris
New page in primary nav After build complete Chris

Failure recovery

Failure Recovery
Manifest stale (skills added in AI Company but not rebuilt) Run bash scripts/build-ai-company-manifest.sh; restart Next.js dev
Anthropic API errors /api/ai-company/specialist returns 503 with skillPreview (dry-run mode) so UI still works
GHL MCP unavailable /api/ai-company/production-output returns placeholder data + note
Three.js / heavy components fail SSR Move under "use client" + check next/dynamic
Tailwind v4 build issue Verify postcss.config.mjs uses @tailwindcss/postcss plugin

Outputs

Per build cycle:

  • New / modified files committed
  • Updated CLAUDE.md if architecture shifted
  • Updated this workflow if pattern evolved
  • Manifest rebuilt if AI Company skills changed

Per launch:

  • Vercel deploy preview reviewed
  • Smoke test passes
  • 60-min post-deploy monitoring
  • Logged to _Logs/devops/command-center/<deploy-date>.md

Cost + latency targets

  • Per Anthropic API call (specialist invocation): $0.01-0.20 depending on model
  • Per page load: < 1 sec to interactive (manifest cached)

workflows.name: Workflow: Customer Health to Renewal (Specialist Chain)
workflows.description: End-to-end chain that runs the activated-customer lifecycle from daily health refresh → branch (healthy / at-risk / critical) → rescue OR expansion OR QBR cadence → renewal. workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Customer Health to Renewal (Specialist Chain)

End-to-end chain that runs the activated-customer lifecycle from daily health refresh → branch (healthy / at-risk / critical) → rescue OR expansion OR QBR cadence → renewal. Composed primarily from CS department specialists. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md specialist-chain pattern + _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/customer-success-lead/SKILL.md.

Chain overview

Activated customer (post-onboarding) — daily refresh tick
   │
   ▼
[1] customer-health-scoring (daily 0-100 + tier)
   ↓ (output: score + signals + tier_status)
   │
   ├── if healthy + tenure ≥ 90d ──▶ [2A] expansion-playbook-runner
   │                                       ↓ (output: brief + Nexus chain triggered)
   │                                   Nexus `expansion-opportunity` chain → AM
   │
   ├── if at-risk ──▶ [2B] churn-risk-predictor
   │                       ↓ (output: risk_30d + drivers + confidence)
   │                       │
   │                       └── if risk_30d ≥ 0.6 ──▶ [3] retention-rescue-playbook
   │                                                     ↓ (output: rescue outcome + verbatim reason)
   │                                                 AM personal touch + tier-appropriate offer
   │                                                 ↓
   │                                                 saved | churned | paused
   │
   └── if critical ──▶ [2C] retention-rescue-playbook (skip predictor, fire directly)
                            ↓ AM 24h + Chris loop if enterprise / white-glove
                            ↓
                            saved | churned | paused

PARALLEL (quarterly cadence per tier):
[Q1] qbr-prep → AM + Chris review T-3 → QBR T-0 (white-glove + enterprise)
[Q2] nps-orchestrator → promoter to review-collection / detractor to rescue / passive to product-feedback

Renewal moment (annual or per-tier-cadence):
[R] account-manager + qbr-prep retrospective → renewal outcome (renewed / expanded / churned)

When this workflow fires

  • Daily 6am PT cron — health-scoring refresh + downstream branch routing
  • Quarterly cron — QBR prep cycle (28d before quarter end)
  • Quarterly cron — NPS orchestrator cycle (start of quarter)
  • Manual — AM-initiated for specific account (mid-cycle review)
  • Event-triggered — onboarding 30-day milestone → enter chain; payment failure → re-evaluate; NPS detractor → enter rescue branch

Pre-flight checks

  • Account is in active-customer state (onboarding chain completed)
  • AM assigned + tier identified
  • No active onboarding chain running (idempotency vs new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md)
  • PII redaction enabled on cross-account aggregates
  • Friday CI cluster awareness — chain feeds customer-success-continuous-improvement weekly

Specialist 1: customer-health-scoring (daily refresh)

Role

Compute 0-100 health score + tier (healthy / at-risk / critical) + signal breakdown for every activated account.

Input

  • Trigger: daily-6am-cron
  • Scope: all activated accounts
  • Window: trailing 90 days

Rules

  • Deterministic weighted score (per signal weights in kpi-registry.md)
  • Cache 24h; invalidate on payment failure / NPS / critical ticket
  • Tenure-normalize accounts < 30 days (use onboarding baseline)

Tools

  • GHL MCP, Stripe, Supabase, pii-redactor, analytics-architect

Output

per_account_score:
  score: number  # 0-100
  status_tier: healthy | at-risk | critical
  volatility_flag: boolean
  signals: object
  recommended_next_action: string

Handoff

  • healthy + tenure ≥ 90d → expansion-playbook-runner (branch 2A)
  • at-risk (40-69) → churn-risk-predictor (branch 2B)
  • critical (< 40) → retention-rescue-playbook direct (branch 2C — skip predictor)
  • volatility ≥ 15 in 7d → chain-monitor alert + AM attention

Observability

  • _Logs/cs/health-scoring/<date>/scores.json
  • Per-account history append

Specialist 2A: expansion-playbook-runner (healthy branch)

Role

Surface expansion-ready accounts + produce AM brief + trigger Nexus expansion chain.

Input

  • Health-score payload (healthy + tenure ≥ 90d)
  • Eligibility filters (no open rescue / open compliance / payment current / AM green-light)

Rules

  • Product-fit signal required (usage / explicit-ask / cohort-lookalike)
  • Max 1 expansion outreach per quarter
  • Compliance gate on all customer-facing drafts
  • Sub-brand voice respected

Tools

  • GHL, Supabase, customer-health-scoring, compliance-officer, discovery-call-prep, clinic-research-agent, pii-redactor

Output

eligible_accounts:
  - account_id: string
    recommended_expansion: {type, products, rationale, estimated_acv_lift}
    am_assigned: string
    nexus_chain_triggered: boolean

Handoff

  • Nexus expansion-opportunity.workflow.md chain → 8-agent downstream
  • account-manager (AM owns conversation)
  • customer-success-lead (KPI feed)

Approval gate

  • Enterprise expansion → Chris loop required

Specialist 2B: churn-risk-predictor (at-risk branch)

Role

Forecast 30/60/90-day churn probability + drivers + confidence.

Input

  • Health-score payload (at-risk tier)
  • Trailing 90d signals (order, support, NPS, payment, AM notes)

Rules

  • ≥ 3 corroborating signals before risk_30d ≥ 0.6
  • Confidence band always
  • Payment health override: 2+ failed charges → minimum risk 0.5

Tools

  • GHL, Stripe, Supabase, customer-health-scoring, pii-redactor, email-intent-classifier

Output

forecast:
  risk_30d: number
  risk_60d: number
  risk_90d: number
  confidence: H | M | L
  primary_drivers: array
  rescue_recommended: boolean
  rescue_priority: P0 | P1 | P2 | none

Handoff

  • risk_30d ≥ 0.6 + confidence ≥ M → retention-rescue-playbook auto-trigger
  • risk_30d ≥ 0.6 + L confidence → AM confirms before rescue
  • Else → log + monitor; feed customer-success-continuous-improvement

Approval gate

  • Enterprise risk → P0 same-day to Chris

Specialist 2C / 3: retention-rescue-playbook (rescue branch)

Role

Run the rescue sequence: AM 24h personal touch → discovery → tier-appropriate offer → verbatim reason capture → outcome tracked.

Input

  • Churn-risk forecast OR critical health-score direct
  • Tier + primary driver

Rules

  • Voice within 24h (not email)
  • Tier offer ceilings per approval-rules.md
  • Verbatim reason captured (not paraphrased)
  • All offers compliance-gated
  • 14-day max rescue duration

Tools

  • GHL, account-manager, compliance-officer, retention-automation, email-intent-classifier, pii-redactor, voice-ai-support-agent (optional)

Output

rescue_outcome:
  outcome: saved | churned | paused | in-progress
  offer_made: {type, detail, approved_by, compliance_approved}
  churn_reason_verbatim: string | null
  follow_up_required: boolean
  flags: array

Handoff

  • retention-automation (lifecycle save flow if saved)
  • customer-success-continuous-improvement (verbatim reasons aggregated)
  • chain-monitor (P0 enterprise + stalled > 7d)
  • Chris (enterprise / white-glove loop)

Approval gates

  • Standard / high-touch offer → AM
  • White-glove offer → AM + Chris
  • Enterprise commercial response → Chris-led
  • Refund requested → routes to sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md

Parallel Track Q1: qbr-prep (quarterly)

Role

Generate QBR brief T-7 days for every white-glove + enterprise account.

Input

  • Quarterly cron (28d before quarter end)
  • account_id (white-glove + enterprise)
  • Customer-defined success from onboarding logs

Rules

  • T-7 minimum delivery
  • Outcomes scorecard ties to customer-defined success
  • Open issues surfaced honestly
  • Roadmap public-OK only (ip-lead clearance for sensitive)
  • Compliance-gated exec summary

Tools

  • GHL, Supabase, Stripe, customer-health-scoring, expansion-playbook-runner, compliance-officer, pii-redactor, analytics-architect, slides

Output

qbr_brief:
  exec_summary: string
  scorecard: array
  quarter_in_numbers: object
  outcomes: array
  open_issues: array
  roadmap_preview: array
  expansion_options: array

Handoff

  • AM + Chris review T-3 → QBR T-0
  • Post-QBR action items → pmo-operator

Parallel Track Q2: nps-orchestrator (quarterly)

Role

Send NPS, classify, route promoter / passive / detractor.

Input

  • Quarterly cron (start of quarter)
  • Eligibility (tenure ≥ 30d, no active rescue, no NPS in trailing 75d)

Rules

  • Quarterly cadence (no more frequent)
  • Sub-brand voice + tier-respecting copy
  • Compliance-gated send
  • 14-day response window

Tools

  • GHL, sms-campaign-builder, cold-email-sequence, compliance-officer, pii-redactor, review-collection, churn-risk-predictor, email-intent-classifier, account-manager

Output

nps_cycle:
  nps_score: number
  response_rate_pct: number
  classification: {promoters, passives, detractors}
  routing_outcomes: object
  verbatim_comments: array  # PII-redacted

Handoff

  • Promoter → review-collection (via sops/customer-reference-request.sop.md, 90d gap rule)
  • Detractor → AM 48h call + churn-risk-predictor signal feed
  • Passive → product-feedback theme capture
  • All → customer-success-continuous-improvement

Renewal moment (annual / per-tier-cadence)

Role

Final renewal decision point. account-manager + qbr-prep retrospective drives outcome.

Outcomes

  • Renewed (same terms) → chain continues
  • Renewed + expanded → expansion-playbook-runner confirmed + new ACV captured
  • Renewed + downgraded → flag + investigate via customer-success-continuous-improvement
  • Churned → verbatim reason capture + cohort learnings + exit

Chain-wide observability

State + tags

  • GHL contact tag: chain:cs-lifecycle:<status>:<branch>
  • Status enum: healthy / at-risk / critical / rescue-active / rescue-saved / rescue-churned / expansion-active / qbr-prep / qbr-completed / nps-cycle / renewed / churned
  • Custom fields: current_health_score, last_score_refresh, risk_30d, last_rescue_id, last_qbr_id, last_nps_score, last_nps_date, churn_reason_verbatim

Logs

  • _Logs/cs/health-scoring/<date>/scores.json — daily refresh
  • _Logs/cs/churn-forecast/<date>/forecast.json — daily forecast
  • _Logs/cs/rescues/<account_id>/<trigger_date>.md — per rescue
  • _Logs/cs/expansion-briefs/<account_id>/<date>.md — per expansion brief
  • _Logs/cs/qbr-briefs/<account_id>/<quarter>.md — per QBR
  • _Logs/cs/nps/<quarter>/responses.json — per cycle
  • _Logs/agents/chains/cs-lifecycle/<chain_id>/<step>/<account_id>.json — chain trail

Metrics

  • NRR (target ≥ 110%)
  • GRR (target ≥ 90%)
  • Quarterly churn (target ≤ 5%)
  • NPS (target ≥ 50)
  • CSAT (target ≥ 4.5/5)
  • TTFV (time-to-first-value, target ≤ 14 days)
  • Rescue win rate (target ≥ 50% standard, ≥ 70% high-touch+)
  • Predictor precision + recall
  • Expansion conversion rate (brief → expansion)
  • QBR completion rate (target 100% white-glove + enterprise)
  • Customer-defined-success delivery rate (target ≥ 80%)

Surfaced in:

  • chain-monitor daily 7am PT digest
  • customer-success-continuous-improvement Friday 5:15pm
  • customer-success-lead weekly KPI snapshot

Approval gates summary

Gate Step Who
Rescue offer standard / high-touch 3 AM
Rescue offer white-glove 3 AM + Chris
Enterprise commercial response 3 Chris-led
Refund request mid-rescue 3 sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md SOP route
Enterprise expansion outreach 2A Chris loop
Cross-sub-brand expansion 2A AM + creative-director
QBR exec summary publish Q1 compliance-officer
QBR roadmap sensitive item Q1 ip-lead
NPS copy / timing change Q2 compliance-officer
Predictor recalibration Ongoing Chris + customer-success-lead

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Health-score refresh stalled chain-monitor alert + manual re-run; downstream branches use last cached score with stale-flag
Predictor low-confidence cluster Hold auto-trigger; queue for AM confirmation; CI engine investigates next Friday
Rescue 7-day stall chain-monitor alert + AM unstick; if 14d max hit → exit + log
QBR brief not delivered T-7 AM escalation + same-day generation; T-3 minimum
NPS response rate < 35% Diagnose copy / timing / audience; don't escalate frequency
Expansion chain triggered duplicate Idempotency check on 90d window; skip re-trigger
Triage misroute → wrong owner CI engine logs; next-week rule update via support-triage-router
Predictor false-negative (churn no rescue) P0 to customer-success-continuous-improvement + Chris; model review
PII leak in cross-account log Immediate quarantine + pii-redactor re-run + incident-response per SOP

Eval cases

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/chains/customer-health-to-renewal/:

  1. Healthy enterprise, 18mo tenure → expansion brief → AM call → expanded → NRR up
  2. At-risk standard, predictor risk_30d 0.65 H confidence → rescue → 15% offer → saved
  3. Critical white-glove → rescue + Chris loop → custom commercial → saved
  4. Critical → rescue → customer cites competitor → reason captured + Chris flagged
  5. Healthy account → NPS quarterly → promoter (10) → review-collection triggered (no prior 90d ask)
  6. NPS detractor (5) → AM 48h call → discovery → rescue branch entered
  7. Enterprise QBR cycle → brief T-7 → review T-3 → present T-0 → 2 expansion options closed
  8. Predictor false-positive cluster (5 rescues, 0 churns) → CI engine recalibration
  9. Cross-channel triage — customer emails + calls in 4h → dedup links → AM owns
  10. Refund requested mid-rescue → routes to refund SOP, rescue continues parallel
  11. PII discipline test — cross-account NPS aggregate output → identifiers redacted
  12. Sub-brand expansion (Matrix Rx → MuscleLock) → creative-director voice alignment + compliance refresh
  13. Renewal moment — annual review → renewed + expanded → expansion captured
  14. Churn despite rescue → verbatim reason captured + cohort learnings to CI

Cost + latency targets

  • Daily health-scoring refresh: < 20 min, < $0.50
  • Daily churn-risk forecast: < 25 min, < $1.00
  • Per rescue: 2-5 days elapsed, < $5 (mostly AM time, not API)
  • Per expansion brief: < $1
  • Per QBR brief: < $3 (heavier composition)
  • Per NPS cycle (quarterly): < $10 (send + classify + route)
  • Per triage ticket: < $0.05 (Haiku-dominant)

Cross-workflow integration

  • Upstream: new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md ends → this workflow begins (post-30d milestone)
  • Downstream: Nexus expansion-opportunity.workflow.md triggered from branch 2A
  • Parallel: GHL workflows (lifecycle save automations from retention-automation)
  • Friday CI cluster: customer-success-continuous-improvement 5:15pm closes the week — reads Voice / GHL / Ecom / Formulation / IP / Nexus / SEO / Education engine outputs

Related

  • _Templates/micro-agent.template.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/customer-success-lead/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/customer-health-scoring/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/churn-risk-predictor/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/retention-rescue-playbook/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/expansion-playbook-runner/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/qbr-prep/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/nps-orchestrator/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/support-triage-router/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/customer-success-continuous-improvement/SKILL.md
