workflows.name: Workflow: Customer Journey (discovery → close → onboard → active → expand → renew)
workflows.description: The end-to-end customer lifecycle workflow. Composes sales + onboarding + CS + retention agents into one continuous flow. Every active Matrix customer flows through this.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Customer Journey (discovery → close → onboard → active → expand → renew)

The end-to-end customer lifecycle workflow. Composes sales + onboarding + CS + retention agents into one continuous flow. Every active Matrix customer flows through this.

When this workflow fires

  • New qualified lead enters pipeline (auto-tagged after qualification)
  • Activated implicitly: every customer is on this journey from acquisition forward
  • Manual invocation: "review customer journey for X" or "where's customer Y in the funnel"

The journey

PHASE 1: Discovery + Qualification
  → market-research (if new ICP segment)
  → cold-email-sequence OR voice-ai-sales-agent OR inbound voice qualifier
  → conversation-channel-router (chooses channel)
  → discovery-call-prep (per discovery call)
  → sales-strategy (playbook governs the motion)
  Trigger: contact created in GHL → tag `stage:contacted`
  Exit: meeting booked → tag `stage:discovery-completed`

PHASE 2: Proposal + Close
  → proposal-builder (after discovery call)
  → discovery-call-prep (close call prep if needed)
  → voice-ai-closer-assist (planned)
  Trigger: proposal sent → tag `stage:proposal-sent`
  Exit: deal closed-won → tag `stage:closed-won`
  Alternative exit: closed-lost → log reason, retention-automation if recoverable

PHASE 3: Onboarding
  → customer-onboarding (broader 30-day plan)
  → voice-ai-onboarding-agent (kickoff call)
  → ghl-crm-architect (ensures CRM stages + tags exist)
  Trigger: closed-won → kickoff call scheduled
  Exit: 30-day milestone hit → tag `status:active`

PHASE 4: Active Customer Operations
  → account-manager (post-onboarding owner)
  → voice-ai-support-agent (existing-customer ops)
  → ghl-daily-pulse (catches anomalies)
  → email-triage / SMS for routine touches
  Trigger: status:active
  Continuous — until expansion / renewal / churn

PHASE 5: Expansion
  → account-manager (surfaces expansion signal)
  → sales-strategy (expansion motion: usually softer than new-customer sales)
  → offer-positioning (new offer briefing if cross-sell)
  → proposal-builder (expansion proposal)
  → voice-ai-sales-agent (variant for expansion — Sam persona — patient)
  Trigger: expansion signal (ordering pattern, hire signal, customer ask)
  Exit: expansion closed → revenue grows on this account

PHASE 6: Renewal
  → account-manager (renewal prep)
  → cfo-finance (renewal terms / pricing)
  → proposal-builder (renewal proposal if non-auto)
  → human-AM or Chris (renewal call for high-value)
  Trigger: renewal window (60 days before)
  Exit: renewed → continue active

PHASE 7: At-Risk Detection + Save (parallel to active phases)
  → ghl-daily-pulse detects signal
  → account-manager classifies risk
  → retention-automation builds save play
  → voice-ai-sales-agent OR human-AM executes save
  Trigger: account-manager flag 🟡 or 🔴
  Outcome: saved / soft-saved / lost

PHASE 8: Churn (if save fails)
  → human exit call (Chris if high-value)
  → cfo-finance logs the churn financially
  → analytics-architect updates churn KPI
  → retention-automation soft re-engage after 90 days

Standard inputs

  • Customer / contact ID (GHL)
  • Current stage (auto-detected from GHL tags)
  • Trigger event (which lifecycle event fired this workflow run)

Standard outputs

  • Customer progresses through GHL stages with full audit trail
  • Every phase logs handoff to _Logs/agent-decisions/
  • Account health tracked in _Logs/account-health/<customer>.md
  • KPIs roll up to analytics-architect dashboard

Approval gates (only at critical money points)

  • Phase 2 close: Chris approves proposal before send (per proposal-builder)
  • Phase 5 expansion offer: Chris approves new offer if non-standard pricing
  • Phase 7 save play with concrete offer: Chris approves any credit / discount
  • Phase 8 churn confirmation: human exit conversation for high-value

All other transitions auto-route per approval-rules.md pre-auths.

Estimated time per customer

  • Phase 1: 1-30 days (varies wildly by lead temperature)
  • Phase 2: 1-21 days (proposal + close)
  • Phase 3: 30 days (onboarding milestone)
  • Phase 4: continuous until expansion / renewal / churn
  • Phase 5-6: triggered events
  • Phase 7-8: triggered events

KPIs tracked per phase

Phase Primary KPI Source
1 Connect rate, meeting-set rate, qualification rate sales-strategy, voice-call-reviewer
2 Proposal sent, win rate, sales cycle proposal-builder, sales-strategy
3 Onboarding completion, kickoff call held, first-order rate customer-onboarding
4 Order cadence, revenue, engagement, NPS account-manager, ghl-daily-pulse
5 Expansion ARR growth, expansion rate account-manager, cfo-finance
6 Renewal rate, GRR (gross retention) account-manager
7 Save rate (by cause, by channel) retention-automation
8 Churn rate (gross + net), churn reason analytics-architect

Failure modes + recovery

Phase Failure Recovery
1 Lead disqualified mid-discovery Tag + remove from pipeline; analyze for ICP refinement
1 Cold campaign auto-paused (per outbound-voice-sales.workflow.md triggers) Chris reviews + restarts
2 Proposal sent, no response 14 days Auto-nudge → 21 days → soft close → retention re-engage 90 days later
3 Onboarding fails to complete by Day 30 Investigate root cause (credentialing? engagement? AM availability?); extend or escalate
4 Customer goes silent account-manager triggers retention-automation
5 Expansion offer rejected Log reason; don't pressure; continue active relationship
6 Renewal at risk retention-automation kicks in 60 days before
7 Save fails Phase 8 graceful exit
8 Customer churns Log reason; soft re-engage 90 days later

Auto-pause / safety triggers

If any:

  • Negative review online → human-AM + Chris immediately
  • Legal threat → counsel + Chris
  • HIPAA / compliance complaint → security-inspector + counsel
  • Sentiment drop in last 2+ touches → account-manager 🔴 flag

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ All routine phase transitions
  • ✅ All routine touches per cadence
  • ✅ All logging / GHL tagging
  • ⚠️ Send any external communication (per channel-specific approval rules)
  • ⚠️ Concrete offers / discounts / credits (Chris approves)
  • 🚫 Issue refunds / charge cards
  • 🚫 Sign contracts
  • 🚫 Make commitments on behalf

Roll-up to executives

Every customer's journey state visible in:

  • pmo-operator master customer board
  • weekly-review (movements + at-risk customers)
  • analytics-architect (phase-level KPIs)
  • cfo-finance (revenue tied to phase)
  • account-manager (per-customer health)

How to use

This workflow runs continuously — it's the canonical customer-journey shape. Specific events trigger specific agents per phase. Chris can ask "where's in their journey?" and the response should reference this workflow's phases.

When designing new sales / CS / retention skills, slot them into the right phase here.

Last updated

2026-05-10 by Chris (initial seed; refines as customers flow through)

workflows.name: Workflow: Customer Refund Request (Specialist Chain)
workflows.description: End-to-end chain for handling any customer refund request. Composed entirely from existing specialist agents — no new skills required to execute. Built per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md` specialist-chain pattern + `customer-refund-authorization.sop.md` policy.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Customer Refund Request (Specialist Chain)

End-to-end chain for handling any customer refund request. Composed entirely from existing specialist agents — no new skills required to execute. Built per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md specialist-chain pattern + customer-refund-authorization.sop.md policy.

Chain overview

Customer email / call / form submission
   │
   ▼
[1] email-intent-classifier (or voice-ai-support-agent if inbound voice)
   ↓ (output: intent=refund + urgency + flags)
[2] account-manager (case-builder mode)
   ↓ (output: case-file with order detail + LTV + history)
[3] customer-refund-authorization.sop.md (approval routing per tier)
   ↓ (output: approved/declined/escalated + amount + method)
[4] payments-engine (Stripe refund execution)
   ↓ (output: refund_id + amount + ETA to customer's card)
[5] order-lifecycle-manager (state advance: refunded / partial_refunded)
   ↓ (output: order state updated + restock decision)
[6] inventory-manager (restock OR write-off per disposition)
   ↓ (output: inventory movement logged)
[7] ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (refund-confirmation comm)
   ↓ (output: email + SMS confirmation delivered)
[8] account-manager (post-refund care follow-up)
   ↓ (output: care follow-up scheduled if warranted)
→ Chain complete; logged

When this workflow fires

  • Customer email contains refund intent (per email-intent-classifier)
  • Inbound voice call → voice-ai-support-agent flags refund request
  • AM identifies refund-warranted situation
  • CS dashboard manual escalation
  • Stripe dispute received (pre-chargeback proactive refund)

Pre-flight checks

  • Contact exists in GHL with order history
  • Order found in system + state allows refund (per order-lifecycle-manager)
  • Order not already refunded (idempotency check)
  • No active chain processing this refund_request_id
  • Customer hasn't requested same refund 3+ times (escalate to Chris if pattern)

Specialist 1: email-intent-classifier (or voice-ai-support-agent)

Role

Classify inbound message intent. For voice: capture transcript + classify in-call.

Input

  • Inbound message body / voice transcript
  • Sender / caller info

Rules

  • Per email-intent-classifier skill rules
  • Flag legal-threat OR press-inquiry → STOP chain, route to Chris

Output

intent: refund | complaint | question | order-status | other
confidence: number
detected_urgency: routine | time-sensitive | urgent
flags: array  # legal-threat / press / etc.
sender_status: prospect / active / churned
suggested_routing: cs / am / chris / compliance

Handoff

  • If intent = refund + no blocking flags → account-manager (case-builder)
  • If legal-threat flag → STOP + Chris + counsel
  • If confidence < 0.7 → human-review queue (CS lead)

Approval gate

  • None at this step (classification only)

Specialist 2: account-manager (case-builder mode)

Role

Build the refund case file. Pull order detail + customer LTV + prior interaction history. Frame the refund situation for authorization decision.

Input

  • Classification output (passthrough)
  • contact_id

Rules

  • Pull ALL relevant context (per account-manager SKILL.md)
  • Categorize refund per customer-refund-authorization.sop.md taxonomy (product issue / service issue / buyer remorse / subscription / regulated)
  • Document reason in customer's own words (verbatim quote)
  • Compute customer LTV + status
  • Surface pattern flags (repeat refunder? VIP?)

Tools

  • GHL MCP (read contact + orders + conversations)
  • Stripe (read payment + dispute history)
  • Supabase / orders DB

Output

case_id: string
contact_id: string
order_id: string
customer_summary:
  ltv: number
  status: active / churned / vip / new
  prior_refund_count: number
  prior_refund_pattern: string  # if applicable
refund_request:
  customer_words: string  # verbatim
  category: product / service / buyer-remorse / subscription / regulated / other
  estimated_amount: number  # what customer asked for
  recommended_method: original / store-credit / replacement / partial
  case_assessment: string  # AM's framing
authorization_tier_required: tier-1 / tier-2 / tier-3 / tier-4 / compliance / counsel

Handoff

  • All cases → customer-refund-authorization.sop.md (approval routing)

Approval gate

  • AM signs off on case-file accuracy

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/02-am-casebuilder/<contact_id>.json

Specialist 3: customer-refund-authorization.sop.md (approval routing)

Role

Apply tier-based approval matrix (per _Shared Context/sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md).

Input

  • Case file (from AM)

Rules

  • Tier-1 (≤ $100): CS lead OR AM approves
  • Tier-2 ($101-$500): CS lead + 1 (AM or Chris)
  • Tier-3 ($501-$2500): Chris OR finance-lead
  • Tier-4 (> $2500): Chris + compliance review
  • Compliance angle (regulated SKU complaint, alleged harm): Chris + compliance-officer
  • PR / litigation risk: Chris + counsel

Output

authorization_decision: approved / approved-modified / declined / escalated
approver: string
approval_timestamp: ISO
approved_amount: number
approved_method: original / store-credit / replacement / partial
reasoning: string
required_communications: array (customer comm template references)

Handoff

  • If approved → customer-comm BEFORE action (Step 3a — comm sent), then payments-engine
  • If declined → customer-comm with decline reasoning + path-forward, chain ends
  • If escalated to counsel → pause + counsel-review queue

Approval gate

  • Per tier (this IS the approval gate)

Specialist 3a: Customer comm before action

Role

Tell customer what we're doing + when, BEFORE processing.

Input

  • Authorization decision + amount + method + ETA

Rules

  • Sent in channel customer reached out on
  • Concrete: "$45.99 refund to Visa ending 4242, 5-10 business days"
  • Acknowledges what went wrong (per case_assessment)
  • No promises beyond approved amount

Tools

  • GHL conversation thread (send via GHL MCP — approved template if standard tier)

Output

  • Comm sent confirmation + delivery status

Handoff

  • After comm delivered → payments-engine (refund execution)

Specialist 4: payments-engine (refund execution)

Role

Execute Stripe refund with idempotency + audit trail.

Input

  • order_id
  • amount_cents
  • reason
  • approver
  • idempotency_key (computed: order_<id>_refund_<amount>_<reason_hash>)

Rules

  • Per payments-engine SKILL.md
  • Idempotency key always required
  • Stripe webhook signature verified on confirmation
  • Persist to refunds table + order_events log

Output

refund_id: string (Stripe refund id)
amount_cents: number
status: succeeded / pending / failed
estimated_arrival: ISO timestamp (5-10 business days typical)
stripe_event_id: string

Handoff

  • On success → order-lifecycle-manager (state update)
  • On failure → AM + Chris immediate; pause chain; retry path per payments-engine

Approval gate

  • None (already approved in step 3)

Specialist 5: order-lifecycle-manager

Role

Update order state to refunded or partial_refunded. Log to order_events.

Input

  • order_id
  • refund_id
  • amount

Rules

  • Per order-lifecycle-manager SKILL.md state machine
  • State transition logged with actor (chain-id + step)
  • Decide restock disposition: yes (no shipment yet) / yes (good condition return) / no (write-off — damage / regulated / hygiene)

Output

order_state: refunded / partial_refunded
restock_disposition: restock / write-off
restock_reasoning: string
audit_log_entry_id: string

Handoff

  • If restock → inventory-manager (restock movement)
  • If write-off → inventory-manager (write-off movement) + flag for ops

Approval gate

  • None

Specialist 6: inventory-manager

Role

Apply inventory movement per disposition.

Input

  • variant_id
  • delta (positive = restock; zero with kind=write_off)
  • kind: refund | return | write_off
  • ref: order_id + refund_id

Rules

  • Per inventory-manager SKILL.md
  • If variant was out_of_stock → flip back active on restock
  • Negative inventory loud (alert)

Output

inventory_movement_id: string
new_on_hand: number
variant_status_change: boolean  # if status flipped

Handoff

  • Always → ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (confirmation comm)

Approval gate

  • Write-off > $X (per approval-rules.md) → notify Chris

Specialist 7: ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (refund confirmation)

Role

Send refund-confirmation email + SMS (if opted in).

Input

  • contact_id
  • refund_id + amount + ETA
  • order_id

Rules

  • Per ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing SKILL.md
  • Template: "Refund of $X processed to your [card]. 5-10 business days to appear."
  • Brief; no upsell at this moment

Output

comm_sent: boolean
channels_used: array (email / sms)
delivery_confirmed: boolean

Handoff

  • Always → account-manager (post-refund care)

Approval gate

  • None (standard template)

Specialist 8: account-manager (post-refund care)

Role

Don't just refund and disappear. Determine if care follow-up warranted.

Input

  • Case file (full chain context)
  • Customer LTV + status

Rules

  • Our-error refunds → offer replacement OR modest goodwill credit
  • Repeat-issue customers → AM personal follow-up
  • Cold-chain fail / clinical complaint → compliance-officer follow-up
  • High-LTV (> $500 refund value) → personal note from Chris

Output

care_action: replacement-offer / goodwill-credit / am-call / chris-note / none
scheduled_for: ISO timestamp

Handoff

  • If pattern (SKU has 3+ refunds in 30 days) → flag for product-catalog-builder quality review
  • If customer > 3 refunds → AM relationship review (legit vs serial-returner)
  • Chain end → log to _Logs/refunds/<order_id>.md (per refund SOP)

Approval gate

  • Care action > $100 (goodwill / replacement value) → Chris

Chain-wide observability

State + tags

  • GHL contact tag: chain:refund:<status>:<step-N>
  • Custom fields: refund_chain_id, last_refund_amount, refund_count
  • Stripe metadata: chain_id linked

Logs

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/<step-N>-<agent>/<contact_id>.json
  • _Logs/refunds/<order_id>.md (per refund SOP — final case file)

Metrics

  • Total refund volume + count per period
  • Avg time-to-refund (request → Stripe processed)
  • Per-step latency
  • Per-step pass rate
  • Repeat-refund-customer rate
  • Per-SKU refund rate (pattern detection)

Surfaced in:

  • chain-monitor daily digest
  • ecommerce-continuous-improvement (weekly Friday)
  • cfo-finance (monthly close per monthly-close-handoff.sop.md)

Eval coverage

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/chains/customer-refund-request/:

  1. Tier-1 product-issue refund → fully automated end-to-end
  2. Tier-3 buyer-remorse refund → Chris approval gate triggered
  3. Tier-4 compliance-angle refund → compliance-officer + Chris approval
  4. Refund declined → customer-comm decline + path-forward
  5. Stripe refund fails → retry + escalation
  6. Repeat refunder (3rd refund) → AM relationship-review flag
  7. Cold-chain-fail refund → write-off restock + compliance follow-up
  8. Subscription refund → integration with subscription-manager
  9. Legal-threat detected at step 1 → STOP chain + Chris + counsel
  10. Pattern (4 refunds same SKU 14 days) → SKU quality flag to product-catalog-builder

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Step 1 classification confidence < 0.7 Route to CS-lead human review
Step 2 case file incomplete AM completes manually; chain resumes
Step 3 authorization escalated to counsel Pause chain; counsel responds; resume
Step 4 Stripe refund fails Retry once; if fails → AM + Chris immediate; chain pauses
Step 5 state already advanced (duplicate webhook) Idempotency check passes; no double-update
Step 6 inventory write-off > $X without approval Pause; Chris approves; resume
Step 7 comm delivery fails Retry email; SMS fallback; if both fail → AM calls customer
Step 8 care action declined by customer Log; no further action; relationship monitored

Cost + latency targets

  • Total chain cost: < $0.30 (most steps are existing-skill compositions)
  • End-to-end latency: tier-1 < 4 hours; tier-3+ < 24 hours (approval-dependent)
  • Customer-comm-sent SLA: within 30 min of authorization decision

Related

  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/micro-agent-team-builder/SKILL.md (meta-pattern)
  • _Templates/micro-agent.template.md
  • _Shared Context/sops/customer-refund-authorization.sop.md (policy)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/account-manager/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/payments-engine/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/order-lifecycle-manager/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/inventory-manager/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/email-intent-classifier/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/chain-monitor/SKILL.md

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris — composed entirely from existing specialist agents per Christo's specialist-chain pattern

workflows.name: Workflow: E-commerce Build (end-to-end)
workflows.description: Standard chain for building any e-commerce store / storefront. Composes the 13 e-commerce specialists. Orchestrated by `ecommerce-lead`. Codifies the phase plan as an executable workflow with verification gates per phase.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: E-commerce Build (end-to-end)

Standard chain for building any e-commerce store / storefront. Composes the 13 e-commerce specialists. Orchestrated by ecommerce-lead. Codifies the phase plan as an executable workflow with verification gates per phase.

When this workflow fires

  • "Build a store for [sub-brand]"
  • "Spin up the [new store / portal]"
  • "Re-platform [existing store]"
  • Manual invocation by Chris after a brand / product decision

Pre-flight checks (before Step 1)

  • Sub-brand decided (per sub-brands.md)
  • Catalog scope known (10 / 100 / 1K / 10K SKUs)
  • B2B / B2C / hybrid decided
  • Regulated SKUs identified (drives compliance gates)
  • Budget + timeline approved by Chris
  • Initial PRD or design brief exists

Agent chain

Phase 0 — Foundations (week 1)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 1: reuse-scanner                                     │
│   → Check matrix-store, existing sub-brand sites, ecom    │
│     patterns for ≥ 80% match                              │
│   → If match → propose reuse vs new build (cost decision) │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 2: ecommerce-lead (orchestrator entry)               │
│   → Confirm scope (one-question fallback)                 │
│   → Generate phase plan + gantt                           │
│   → Approval gate: Chris approves phase plan + timeline   │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 3: engineering-lead                                  │
│   → Stack decision (canonical: Next.js + Supabase +       │
│     Stripe + Shippo + Clerk)                              │
│   → Compliance gates per regulated-SKU detection          │
│   → Ticket breakdown                                      │
│   → Approval gate: Chris approves architecture            │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 4: ecommerce-scaffolder                              │
│   → Bootstrap repo (GitHub + Vercel + Supabase dev)       │
│   → Wire env vars + CI                                    │
│   → Verify `pnpm dev` runs cleanly                        │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 5: ecommerce-schema                                  │
│   → Apply canonical 20-table migration to dev             │
│   → Generate TS types                                     │
│   → Seed sample data                                      │
│   → Approval gate: Chris approves schema for prod         │
│     (especially if multi-warehouse, multi-currency)       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 1 — Catalog (week 1-2, parallel)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 6: product-catalog-builder                           │
│   → Build taxonomy (brands → categories → variants)       │
│   → SKU convention validation                             │
│   → Bulk import or hand-add first batch                   │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 7 (parallel a): product-pricing-engine               │
│   → Set list prices (margin floor enforced)               │
│   → Set B2B tiers if applicable                           │
│   → Stripe Products + Prices created                      │
│   → Approval gate: Chris approves pricing                 │
│                                                           │
│ Step 7 (parallel b): product-copy                         │
│   → Title + bullets + description + specs + meta per SKU  │
│   → Sub-brand voice applied                               │
│   → Regulated-SKU copy through compliance-officer         │
│                                                           │
│ Step 7 (parallel c): product-imagery                      │
│   → Hero + variant + scale + packaging shots per SKU      │
│   → AI gen or photographer brief                          │
│   → Optimized + CDN-uploaded                              │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 2 — Plumbing (week 2-3)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 8: ecommerce-backend                                 │
│   → Cart, checkout, order, webhook endpoints              │
│   → Idempotent + deduped + state-logged                   │
│   → Stripe + Shippo webhook receivers                     │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 9: integration-agent                                 │
│   → Stripe live config (test mode first)                  │
│   → Shippo carrier accounts                               │
│   → Tax calc wired (Stripe Tax default)                   │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 10: fulfillment-shipping                             │
│   → Rate-shop endpoint                                    │
│   → Label-gen endpoint                                    │
│   → Tracking webhook receiver                             │
│   → Cold-chain rules (if applicable)                      │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 11: code-qa                                          │
│   → Security review (RLS, idempotency, signatures)        │
│   → Correctness review (state machine, edge cases)        │
│   → Block 🔴 findings                                     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 3 — Surface (week 3-4)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 12: ecommerce-storefront                             │
│   → Home / Catalog / PDP / Cart / Checkout / Account UI   │
│   → Brand theme from sub-brands.md                        │
│   → Mobile-first, accessibility AA                        │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 13: creative-director                                │
│   → Brand pulse review                                    │
│   → Visual hierarchy check                                │
│   → Sub-brand voice consistency                           │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 14: compliance-officer                               │
│   → Claims discipline review                              │
│   → Disclosure presence                                   │
│   → Regulated-SKU gate verification                       │
│   → Block if 🔴 findings                                  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 4 — Ops (week 4)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 15: order-lifecycle-manager                          │
│   → Wire all state transitions + comms                    │
│   → Refund / return / cancel flows                        │
│   → Test end-to-end                                       │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 16: inventory-manager                                │
│   → Reserve / commit / restock movement automation        │
│   → Reorder-point alerts                                  │
│   → Notify-me-on-restock                                  │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 17: devops-deployment                                │
│   → Staging deploy                                        │
│   → Smoke test pass                                       │
│   → Prod deploy via `production-deploy.sop.md`            │
│   → Approval gate: Chris approves prod                    │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 5 — Growth (week 4-5)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 18: ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing                    │
│   → Build 6 sequences in GHL (abandoned cart, browse,     │
│     post-purchase, replenishment, win-back, VIP)          │
│   → A/B framework wired                                   │
│   → Soft launch 10% → ramp                                │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 19: analytics-architect                              │
│   → KPI dashboard (conversion / AOV / LTV / repeat rate)  │
│   → Funnel instrumentation                                │
│   → Eval baselines locked                                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 6 — Launch gate (the 16-box checklist from ecommerce-lead)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 20: ecommerce-lead (launch verification)             │
│   → Run 16-box checklist                                  │
│   → Every box must be ✅ before flipping live             │
│   → Approval gate: Chris flips the live switch            │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 7 — Post-launch (week 5-6)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 21: account-manager                                  │
│   → Customer success motion ready for first orders        │
│   → Kickoff calls scheduled per kickoff-call SOP          │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 22: ghl-continuous-improvement                       │
│   → Weekly review of new store performance                │
│   → Pattern detection: drop-offs, errors, complaints      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Approval gates

Gate Phase Who Approving
Phase plan 0 Chris Scope + timeline + budget
Architecture 0 Chris Stack + compliance gates
Schema for prod 0 Chris Especially destructive migrations
Pricing 1 Chris Margin floor + B2B tiers
Code (per PR) 2-3 Chris Merge approval
Prod deploy 4 Chris Per production-deploy.sop.md
Launch live 6 Chris Flip the switch

Failure recovery

Phase fails Recovery
Schema drift after Phase 0 Roll back migration; re-design with engineering-lead + database-schema
Catalog QA fails (Phase 1) Loop on product-catalog-builder until QA pass; don't proceed
Backend security 🔴 (Phase 2) Block; loop on ecommerce-backend + code-qa
Compliance 🔴 (Phase 3) Block; loop on product-copy + compliance-officer
Launch-gate checklist incomplete (Phase 6) Block; close gap; re-run gate; never bypass
Post-launch revenue regression Roll back per production-deploy.sop.md; root-cause + fix forward

Outputs

Per phase, an artifact lands in:

  • _Logs/ecommerce-builds/<store>/<phase>/<date>.md

Final launch artifact:

  • _Logs/ecommerce-builds/<store>/launch-<date>.md — phase summaries + launch-gate checklist + approvals

Related

  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ecommerce-lead/SKILL.md (orchestrator)
  • _Workflows/software-build.workflow.md (general software pattern this builds on)
  • _Shared Context/sops/production-deploy.sop.md
  • _Shared Context/sops/kickoff-call-run-of-show.sop.md (post-launch customer)
  • _Shared Context/integration-map.md (webhook + idempotency canon)
  • _Shared Context/sub-brands.md

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris (initial workflow)

workflows.name: Workflow: E-commerce Continuous Improvement
workflows.description: Weekly Friday + quarterly deep audit cadence for the e-commerce surface. Mirrors `ghl-continuous-improvement.workflow.md` + `voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md` patterns. Orchestrated by `ecommerce-continuous-improvement` skill.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: E-commerce Continuous Improvement

Weekly Friday + quarterly deep audit cadence for the e-commerce surface. Mirrors ghl-continuous-improvement.workflow.md + voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md patterns. Orchestrated by ecommerce-continuous-improvement skill.

When this workflow fires

  • Weekly: Friday 3:45pm PT (scheduled — slot after GHL CI at 3:30)
  • Quarterly: First Friday of new quarter (deep audit, all-day)
  • Ad-hoc: After major launch, pricing change, or KPI alarm

Pre-flight

  • Prior week / quarter complete (data settled)
  • analytics-architect KPI dashboard refreshed
  • ecommerce-eval-harness weekly run complete (Monday 7am)
  • reputation-manager sentiment pulse current
  • No active production incident (postpone if one is open)

Weekly agent chain

Step 1: analytics-architect
   → Pull KPIs for last 7 days vs prior 7d, prior 4-week avg
   → Per surface: storefront / checkout / pricing / catalog / inventory /
     fulfillment / order-lifecycle / subscription / lifecycle-marketing / tax
   → Flag drift > 1 stddev
   ↓
Step 2: ecommerce-eval-harness (read last run)
   → Status: green / yellow / red
   → New failures vs baseline
   → Drift candidates per surface
   ↓
Step 3: reputation-manager (read last 7d)
   → Review velocity + sentiment
   → Complaint clusters
   → Product-specific feedback patterns
   ↓
Step 4: ecommerce-continuous-improvement
   → Synthesize Steps 1-3
   → Diagnose top 3-5 issues (hypothesis + evidence + confidence)
   → Propose changes per issue (skill + change brief + expected impact)
   → Order by impact ÷ effort
   ↓
Step 5: Approval gate — Chris
   → Reviews proposals
   → Approves / declines / amends per item
   ↓
Step 6: Hand off approved proposals (parallel)
   → ecommerce-storefront (UX changes)
   → product-pricing-engine (pricing changes)
   → product-catalog-builder (catalog hygiene)
   → product-copy (copy refresh)
   → product-imagery (imagery gap fixes)
   → fulfillment-shipping (carrier mix / cold-chain tuning)
   → order-lifecycle-manager (refund / return flow improvements)
   → inventory-manager (reorder-point tuning)
   → subscription-manager (dunning / pause flow tuning)
   → ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (sequence iteration)
   → ecommerce-backend (any endpoint fix needed)
   ↓
Step 7: ecommerce-eval-harness (per change — pre-deploy)
   → Block deploys with regressions
   → Add new eval cases for fixes
   ↓
Step 8: devops-deployment (per change — staging → prod)
   → Per `production-deploy.sop.md`
   → Per `pricing-change-deployment.sop.md` for price changes
   ↓
Step 9: Measure (T+1w, T+4w)
   → Per change: actual impact vs expected
   → Update impact-history.csv
   → Update playbook if change underperformed
   ↓
Step 10: pmo-operator
   → Track tickets through completion
   → Surface stalled tickets in next weekly

Quarterly deep audit chain

Adds to weekly:

Step Q1: product-catalog-builder
   → Full catalog hygiene sweep
   → Dead SKU list (90+ days no orders)
   → Variants missing image / description / price
   → SEO health audit
   → Output: retire / promote / refresh decisions

Step Q2: product-pricing-engine
   → Margin audit per variant vs floor
   → Stripe sync drift check
   → Orphan promo cleanup
   → Tier mix analysis

Step Q3: subscription-manager
   → Cohort retention analysis
   → Dunning recovery rate trend
   → Click-to-cancel compliance verification (still 1-click? — FTC)
   → Regulated-SKU continuity audit

Step Q4: ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing
   → Workflow sprawl cleanup
   → Sequence performance vs baseline
   → A/B winner consolidation
   → Compliance recheck (CAN-SPAM, TCPA, A2P)

Step Q5: ecommerce-eval-harness
   → Suite refresh (retire flaky, add coverage gaps)
   → Severity recalibration
   → Threshold review

Step Q6: tax-calc
   → Nexus proximity review (states approaching threshold)
   → Exemption certs expiring < 90 days
   → CPA confirmation that all states current

Step Q7: ecommerce-continuous-improvement (synthesis)
   → Quarterly report → Chris
   → Strategic recommendations for next quarter
   → Integrate into `quarterly-review.workflow.md`

Approval gates

Gate Where Who
Proposal approval Step 5 Chris (weekly + quarterly)
Pricing changes Step 6 Chris (per pricing-change-deployment.sop.md)
Regulated SKU changes Step 6 compliance-officer
Prod deploy Step 8 Chris (per production-deploy.sop.md)
Quarterly strategic recommendations Step Q7 Chris + sub-brand stakeholders

Failure recovery

Phase fails Recovery
Data incomplete (Step 1) Postpone weekly 1-2 days; alert engineering for pipeline fix
Eval-harness red (Step 2) Block all proposals affected; treat as incident if customer-impacting
Proposal rejected by Chris Document why; refine; revisit next week
Change measured worse than expected (Step 9) Roll back; root-cause; update playbook
Quarterly audit reveals critical drift Escalate to immediate action item; not deferred

Outputs

Per weekly:

  • _Logs/ecommerce-improvement/<YYYY-WW>-weekly.md
  • Approved tickets to skills

Per quarterly:

  • _Logs/ecommerce-improvement/<YYYY-Q>-deep-audit.md
  • Strategic recommendations document
  • Feeds into _Workflows/quarterly-review.workflow.md

KPI references

Definitions per _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md:

  • Conversion rate (visitor → paid)
  • AOV (average order value)
  • Refund rate (% of orders)
  • Subscription MRR + churn
  • Cohort retention (m1 / m3 / m12)
  • LTV by cohort
  • Inventory turn
  • Stockout rate
  • Carrier on-time delivery

workflows.name: Workflow: Expansion Opportunity (Specialist Chain)
workflows.description: Existing-customer growth chain. Quarterly portfolio review surfaces expansion candidates → research what they're using vs full catalog → AM-led outreach with specific recommendation → close as expansion order. Composed entirely from existing specialists. 5th executable chain.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Expansion Opportunity (Specialist Chain)

Existing-customer growth chain. Quarterly portfolio review surfaces expansion candidates → research what they're using vs full catalog → AM-led outreach with specific recommendation → close as expansion order. Composed entirely from existing specialists. 5th executable chain.

Chain overview

Quarterly customer cohort review
   │
   ▼
[1] account-manager (cohort sweep — flag expansion-eligible customers)
   ↓ (output: ranked list with expansion-candidate scores)
[2] lead-scoring (existing-customer intent computation)
   ↓ (output: expansion-readiness score per candidate)
[3] clinic-research-agent (refresh research for cohort)
   ↓ (output: updated dossier — what they CURRENTLY use vs MIGHT use)
[4] outreach-writer (expansion-specific message — AM voice, not cold)
   ↓ (output: tailored expansion recommendation message)
[5] compliance-officer (review)
   ↓ (output: approved / amend / blocked)
[6] AM personal outreach (human-led; AI prepares; AM sends OR calls)
   ↓ (customer response captured)
[7] objection-handler (if pushback) OR ghl-calendar-builder (if interested)
   ↓ (output: meeting booked OR objection-response in chain)
[8] discovery-call-prep (expansion call brief)
   ↓ (delivered to AM)
→ Chain ends; outcome (expanded / declined / further-nurture) logged

When this workflow fires

  • Quarterly: account-manager cohort review identifies expansion candidates
  • Trigger-based: customer signals expansion intent (browsed new category / asked about new SKU / mentioned new service offering in conversation)
  • Post-success-event: customer hit a milestone (NPS=10 at 30-day; reorder pattern strong; provider added more providers to clinic)
  • Manual: AM identifies during regular contact

Pre-flight checks

  • Customer is active-healthy per account-manager health rating
  • No active support ticket / refund / complaint
  • Customer NOT on do-not-expand list (some customers explicitly prefer single-category)
  • Account is current on payment (no Net 30 overdue)
  • Last expansion-attempt > 90 days ago (cooldown)

Specialist 1: account-manager (cohort sweep)

Role

Quarterly review of active customer base. Identify customers ready for expansion based on engagement signals + current-category penetration + provider feedback patterns.

Input

  • Customer cohort (all active customers in cohort definition)
  • Per-customer: order history, support history, NPS data, engagement signals, current categories purchased

Rules

  • Per account-manager SKILL.md cohort review pattern
  • Expansion-eligible signals:
    • Reorder rate > target on current category
    • Customer growing (more providers added; more locations; PE acquired)
    • Adjacent-category signals (browsed but didn't buy; asked about; mentioned in convo)
    • Provider feedback positive
    • NPS recent ≥ 8
  • Disqualifiers:
    • Recent refund (within 60 days)
    • Open complaint / support ticket
    • Net 30 overdue
    • Customer-stated category limit
    • Recent expansion-attempt (< 90 days)

Output

cohort_id: string  # e.g., "Q2-2026-expansion-cohort"
review_date: ISO
candidates:
  - contact_id: string
    customer_name: string
    expansion_candidate_score: number (0-100)
    score_drivers: array of {signal, weight}
    current_categories_purchased: array
    candidate_categories_for_expansion: array of {category, evidence_basis}
    suggested_priority: hot | warm | cool
  - ...
total_candidates_identified: number
expected_pursue_count: number  # top 20-30% target

Handoff

  • Top-tier candidates (suggested_priority = hot) → lead-scoring for expansion-readiness scoring
  • Mid-tier (warm) → batched; pursued if AM capacity
  • Cool tier → don't pursue this cycle; monitor

Approval gate

  • Chris reviews cohort list before chain proceeds at scale (top 20 expansion candidates per quarter — high-touch impact)

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/01-cohort-sweep/<cohort_id>.json

Specialist 2: lead-scoring (existing-customer expansion-readiness)

Role

Score expansion-readiness specifically (different framing than new-prospect lead-scoring).

Input

  • Cohort sweep candidate
  • Full customer history

Rules

  • Per lead-scoring retrofitted spec, in existing-customer mode
  • Higher weight on intent signals (engagement) vs fit (already a customer)
  • Recency factor on existing-category orders
  • Flag if customer is moving toward churn (negative-direction expansion-readiness = retention concern instead)

Output

  • Per retrofitted lead-scoring schema
  • Special flag: expansion-ready | retention-risk | stable-no-expansion-signal

Handoff

  • expansion-ready + score ≥ 60 → clinic-research-agent (refresh)
  • retention-risk → retention-automation (different chain — save plan)
  • stable-no-signal → continue monitoring; don't auto-expand-pitch

Approval gate

  • None

Specialist 3: clinic-research-agent (refresh)

Role

Refresh research dossier. What's changed at the clinic since last research? New services? New providers? Growth signals? Competitor activity?

Input

  • Existing customer dossier (last research date typically months-old)
  • Recent customer signals (orders / conversations / feedback)

Rules

  • Per clinic-research-agent SKILL.md (refresh mode)
  • Compare against last dossier — surface CHANGES
  • Time budget: 5 min standard
  • Focus on candidate_categories (from cohort sweep) — what evidence supports / refutes?

Output

  • Same schema as clinic-research-agent + changes_since_last: object with deltas

Handoff

  • → outreach-writer (expansion-specific message)

Approval gate

  • Competitor flag (new entrant in clinic's space) → AM notified; expansion message adapted to differentiate

Specialist 4: outreach-writer (expansion message)

Role

Draft expansion-recommendation message. AM-voice, not cold. Anchored to specific clinic facts + Matrix's growth path.

Input

  • Refreshed clinic dossier
  • Cohort sweep evidence
  • Candidate categories + reasoning

Rules

  • Per outreach-writer SKILL.md, in expansion mode (different than cold-outreach mode):
    • Acknowledges existing relationship + recent wins (per dossier)
    • Specific category recommendation (NOT "want to see our full catalog?")
    • Anchored to clinic-specific evidence (you added 2 providers / your reorder velocity suggests / you asked about X)
    • One CTA — typical: "shall I send the [category] one-pager + tier pricing for your volume?" OR "worth 15 min with [AM name] to walk through how clinics like yours add [category]?"
    • AM voice, not generic Matrix voice
    • Channel: usually email; SMS allowed if existing-customer relationship strong
  • All standard outreach-writer rules: no fabricated claims, no trade-secret leaks, single CTA, length-appropriate

Output

  • Per outreach-writer schema, with mode: "expansion" flag

Handoff

  • → compliance-officer (review)

Specialist 5: compliance-officer (review)

Role

Same as in lead-to-demo chain. Confirm message claims + voice + disclosures.

Output

  • 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / ⚠️ decision

Handoff

  • 🟢 → AM personal outreach (step 6)
  • 🟡 → loop back to outreach-writer
  • 🔴 → STOP chain
  • ⚠️ → counsel

Specialist 6: AM personal outreach (human-led)

Role

AM personally sends OR calls. Approved draft is the starting point; AM may adapt to relationship-specific context.

Input

  • Approved draft
  • Full chain context (cohort sweep + research + scoring)

Rules

  • AM owns this step (NOT auto-sent)
  • AM has ≤ 5 business days to act on approved draft (else chain pauses + reminder)
  • AM logs outcome (sent / called / scheduled-meeting / customer-replied / declined)
  • Each customer touched gets AM-personal touch (no batch-mass-blast)

Output

am_action_taken: email-sent | call-made | scheduled | no-action-yet
action_date: ISO
am_notes: string
initial_response: positive | objection | neutral | declined | no-response-yet

Handoff

  • Positive → ghl-calendar-builder (book expansion call)
  • Objection → objection-handler
  • Neutral / no-response (7d) → AM follow-up (one touch); if still no response → nurture
  • Declined → tag + thank-you + return to cohort monitoring next quarter

Approval gate

  • AM's own approval (decides whether to send approved draft as-is, adapt, or call instead)

Specialist 7: ghl-calendar-builder OR objection-handler

Role

Standard booking OR objection-response flow.

Output

  • Per respective SKILL.md schemas

Handoff

  • Booked → discovery-call-prep (expansion-call-specific brief)
  • Objection resolved + interest → loop to step 6 with re-engaged customer

Specialist 8: discovery-call-prep (expansion call brief)

Role

Brief for AM on expansion call. Different than new-prospect brief — leverages relationship.

Input

  • Full chain context
  • Booked appointment details

Rules

  • Per discovery-call-prep SKILL.md, in expansion mode
  • Leads with relationship context ("3 reorders this quarter, customer-defined success: ___")
  • Recommends specific opening for AM ("Since last we talked, you added providers — want to start with how clinics like that scaled [category]?")
  • Anti-pitch: AM consults, not pitches. Goal is to surface need, not just upsell

Output

  • 1-page brief delivered to AM Slack DM + email 24 hours before call
  • Pre-meeting calendar attached

Handoff

  • AM takes the call → outcome captured via meeting-magic post-call → chain ends

Chain-wide observability

State + tags

  • GHL contact tag: chain:expansion:<status>:<step-N>
  • Chain status: cohort-sweep / expansion-ready / researching / drafting / compliance-review / am-action / booked / completed-expanded / completed-declined / paused
  • Custom fields: last_expansion_attempt_at, expansion_outcomes_history, current_categories, next_expansion_candidate_categories

Logs

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/<step>-<agent>/<contact_id>.json
  • _Logs/expansion/<cohort_id>/results.csv (cohort-level outcomes)

Metrics

  • Cohort expansion-candidate rate (% of active customers identified per quarter)
  • Pursue rate (% of identified that actually get touched)
  • Conversion rate (touched → booked → expanded)
  • Avg time from cohort sweep to first expansion order
  • AM personal-touch rate (humans driving, not auto-batch)
  • Per-category expansion success rates

Surfaced in:

  • chain-monitor daily digest
  • account-manager cohort dashboards
  • ghl-continuous-improvement Friday review
  • cfo-finance (expansion revenue tracking)
  • quarterly-review.workflow.md (strategic level)

Eval coverage

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/chains/expansion-opportunity/:

  1. Hot-tier expansion candidate → research refresh → message → AM call → booked → expanded order placed
  2. Mid-tier candidate, AM uses message-as-template → email sent → customer replies positive → booked
  3. Pricing objection from existing customer → objection-handler → tier-pricing math → re-engaged
  4. Customer-stated category limit → AM identifies; chain marks contact do-not-expand:<category>; no future auto-attempts on that category
  5. Retention-risk flag (negative direction) → routed to retention-automation instead
  6. Competitor flag during refresh research → AM strategy adapted; chain continues with differentiation angle
  7. AM doesn't act within 5 days → chain paused + reminder; if still no action after 10 days → re-queue
  8. Compliance block on new claim attempt → chain halts; outreach-writer re-drafts with approved claims only
  9. Customer expanded successfully → tag + record success pattern for cohort-sweep model improvement
  10. Multiple expansions same customer (later quarter) → cooldown enforced + chain handles gracefully

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Cohort sweep returns 0 candidates Review scoring model; expansion-ready signals may have shifted (likely a ghl-continuous-improvement proposal)
AM bandwidth insufficient for all candidates Top 20 only; remaining held for next quarter; capacity report to Christo
Customer declines AND signals retention risk Pivot to retention chain; not just nurture
Compliance blocks expansion message persistently Pattern analysis — compliance-officer + outreach-writer iterate together
Customer replies negatively (annoyed at expansion ask) Apology + cooldown 180 days; log learning
Stale dossier (last research > 12 months) Mandatory full refresh, not skim

Approval gates summary

Gate Step Who
Quarterly cohort list (top 20) 1 Chris
Per-customer expansion attempt 6 AM (judgment + relationship)
New claim attempt in expansion message 4 compliance-officer (always required)
Material discount in expansion message 4 Chris (margin discipline)
White-glove customer expansion All Chris + AM lead

Cost + latency targets

  • Per-candidate chain cost: < $0.40
  • Quarterly cohort (20 candidates): < $10 total agent cost + AM time
  • Time from cohort sweep to AM-action-taken: < 5 business days
  • Time from AM-action to expanded order (if expanded): 7-30 days typical
  • Expansion conversion rate target: 25%+ of pursued candidates

Related

  • _Templates/micro-agent.template.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/account-manager/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/lead-scoring/SKILL.md (retrofitted)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/clinic-research-agent/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/outreach-writer/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/compliance-officer/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/objection-handler/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ghl-calendar-builder/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/KB-Skills/discovery-call-prep/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/retention-automation/SKILL.md (fork for retention-risk cases)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/chain-monitor/SKILL.md

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris — 5th executable specialist chain

workflows.name: Workflow: Formulation Continuous Improvement
workflows.description: Weekly Friday + quarterly deep audit cadence for the formulation department. Mirrors voice / GHL / e-commerce CI patterns. Orchestrated by `formulation-continuous-improvement` skill.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Formulation Continuous Improvement

Weekly Friday + quarterly deep audit cadence for the formulation department. Mirrors voice / GHL / e-commerce CI patterns. Orchestrated by formulation-continuous-improvement skill.

When this workflow fires

  • Weekly: Friday 4:00pm PT (after voice 3pm + GHL 3:30 + e-com 3:45)
  • Quarterly: First Friday of new quarter (deep audit, all-day)
  • Ad-hoc: After major launch / supplier change / adverse-event cluster / FDA inspection / OOS event

Pre-flight

  • Prior week / quarter complete (data settled)
  • formulation-eval-harness weekly run complete (catalog regression status known)
  • Stability dashboard refreshed (lab partners' latest data ingested)
  • Adverse-event log current
  • Customer-complaint log current (cross-ref ghl-continuous-improvement)
  • No active production incident

Weekly agent chain

Step 1: analytics-architect
   → Pull formulation KPIs (sales / margin / refund rate per SKU)
   → Inventory turn, stockout rate per SKU
   → Compare last 7d vs prior 7d, prior 4-week avg
   ↓
Step 2: formulation-eval-harness (read last run)
   → Status: green / yellow / red
   → New failures vs baseline
   ↓
Step 3: compliance-officer
   → Last 7d compliance findings
   → Adverse-event triage status
   → State / DEA / FDA landscape changes
   ↓
Step 4: vendor-management
   → Supplier reliability data
   → Pharmacy partner quality
   → Single-source risk per R-V-01
   ↓
Step 5: ip-lead
   → Patent filing status
   → Trademark office actions
   → Disclosure-status conflicts
   ↓
Step 6: formulation-continuous-improvement (synthesis)
   → Combine Steps 1-5
   → Diagnose top 3-5 issues (hypothesis + evidence + confidence)
   → Propose changes per issue
   → Order by impact ÷ effort
   ↓
Step 7: Approval gate
   → Steve approves chemistry-relevant items
   → Chris approves business-relevant items
   → Compliance-officer approves regulatory items
   ↓
Step 8: Hand off approved proposals (parallel)
   → formulation-designer (formula tweaks)
   → formulation-compliance (regulatory reclassification / labeling updates)
   → formulation-stability (protocol changes / BUD extensions)
   → formulation-batch-record (MBR change control)
   → product-catalog-builder (catalog hygiene)
   → product-copy (copy refresh)
   → vendor-management (supplier diversification)
   → ip-counsel-coordination (IP filings)
   ↓
Step 9: formulation-eval-harness (per change — pre-deploy)
   → Block deploys with regressions
   → Add new eval cases for fixes
   ↓
Step 10: Measure (T+1w, T+4w)
   → Actual impact vs expected per change
   → Update impact-history.csv
   ↓
Step 11: pmo-operator
   → Track tickets through completion
   → Surface stalled tickets next weekly

Quarterly deep audit chain

Adds to weekly:

Step Q1: product-catalog-builder
   → Full catalog retirement review
   → Dead SKU list (90+ days no orders)
   → Variants missing assets
   → SEO health audit on PDPs
   → Output: retire / refresh / discontinue decisions

Step Q2: formulation-stability
   → Annual stability program review
   → BUD extensions feasible per accumulated data
   → Cohort stability data analysis
   → Output: BUD extension proposals + new stability protocol candidates

Step Q3: formulation-compliance
   → Quarterly regulatory landscape review (DEA / FDA / state)
   → USP monograph updates affecting actives
   → MoCRA implementation progress
   → Adverse event reporting compliance audit

Step Q4: formulation-batch-record
   → MBR audit (all active SKUs)
   → Manufacturing partner performance review
   → Change-control hygiene review

Step Q5: ip-lead
   → Full IP portfolio audit (per ip-clearance.workflow.md)
   → Patent maintenance fees coming due
   → Trademark Section 8 / renewal calendar
   → Defensive search for competitor filings

Step Q6: vendor-management
   → Supplier diversification review (single-source risks)
   → Cost trend per ingredient
   → Quality complaints per supplier
   → New-supplier qualification status

Step Q7: formulation-continuous-improvement (synthesis)
   → Quarterly report → Steve + Chris
   → Strategic recommendations for next quarter
   → Integrate into quarterly-review.workflow.md

Approval gates

Gate Where Who
Proposal approval Step 7 Steve (chem) + Chris (business) + compliance-officer (regulatory)
Reformulation of active SKU Step 8 Steve + Chris
Supplier change Step 8 Chris + Steve (chem compat) + compliance-officer (regulatory)
Catalog retirement Step Q1 Chris (customer-comm impact)
BUD extension Step Q2 Steve + compliance-officer
New patent filing Step Q5 Chris + counsel

Failure recovery

Phase fails Recovery
Data incomplete (Step 1) Postpone weekly 1-2 days; alert eng for pipeline fix
Eval-harness red (Step 2) Block all proposals affected; treat as incident if regulatory-impacting
Proposal rejected Document why; refine; revisit next week
Change measured worse than expected (Step 10) Roll back; root-cause; update playbook
Quarterly audit reveals critical drift Immediate action item, not deferred

Outputs

Per weekly:

  • _Logs/formulation-improvement/<YYYY-WW>-weekly.md
  • Approved tickets to formulation specialist skills

Per quarterly:

  • _Logs/formulation-improvement/<YYYY-Q>-deep-audit.md
  • Feeds _Workflows/quarterly-review.workflow.md

Related

  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/formulation-continuous-improvement/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/formulation-eval-harness/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/formulation-lead/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/compliance-officer/SKILL.md
  • _Workflows/ghl-continuous-improvement.workflow.md (sibling)
  • _Workflows/voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md (sibling)
  • _Workflows/ecommerce-continuous-improvement.workflow.md (sibling)
  • _Workflows/quarterly-review.workflow.md (consumes deep audit)

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris (initial workflow — completes CI pattern across 4 priority departments)

workflows.name: Workflow: Formulation Development (end-to-end)
workflows.description: Standard chain for any new formulation Steve takes through to launch. Orchestrated by `formulation-lead`. 6 phases + launch gate. Calendar time: 12-24 weeks typical (stability is the rate-limiter).
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Formulation Development (end-to-end)

Standard chain for any new formulation Steve takes through to launch. Orchestrated by formulation-lead. 6 phases + launch gate. Calendar time: 12-24 weeks typical (stability is the rate-limiter).

When this workflow fires

  • Steve has a candidate that passed formulation-market-fit
  • Customer / market signal triggers a "we should make X"
  • Reformulation of existing SKU (supplier change, cost pressure, stability extension)
  • Sub-brand launch needing hero SKU

Pre-flight

  • Candidate description + use case + target form decided
  • Compliance class hypothesis exists (503-A / 503-B / supplement / cosmetic)
  • Budget available for chemistry + lab + counsel
  • Steve has bandwidth in queue
  • No outstanding compliance issues with existing similar products

Agent chain

Phase 0 — Intake (1-3 days)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 1: formulation-market-fit                            │
│   → 4-dimension scoring (demand × margin × moat × fit)    │
│   → Decision: pursue / conditional / park / decline       │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 2: patent-strategy (parallel)                        │
│   → Prior art landscape                                   │
│   → Patentability + freedom-to-operate                    │
│   → Recommendation: file / defensive publish / pass       │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 3: Steve + Chris intake decision                     │
│   → Go / no-go                                            │
│   → Approval gate: Chris + Steve                          │
│   → If patentable: provisional file BEFORE any public     │
│     disclosure (via ip-counsel-coordination)              │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 1 — Design (1-4 weeks)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 4: formulation-designer                              │
│   → Form-specific spec template filled                    │
│   → Ingredient sourcing check (via vendor-management)     │
│   → Compatibility check                                   │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 5: Steve bench work                                  │
│   → Pilot batch preparation                               │
│   → Initial QC                                            │
│   → Document at _Logs/formulation/<id>/bench-notes.md     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 2 — Compliance (2-6 weeks, parallel with Phase 3)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 6: formulation-compliance                            │
│   → Final regulatory class                                │
│   → USP monograph alignment                               │
│   → cGMP level                                            │
│   → DEA scheduling                                        │
│   → State restrictions                                    │
│   → Labeling spec                                         │
│   → Adverse event reporting setup                         │
│   → Approval gate: compliance-officer signoff             │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 3 — Stability (6-24 weeks calendar)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 7: formulation-stability                             │
│   → Protocol design (real-time + accelerated)             │
│   → Sample timepoints + assay panel                       │
│   → Acceptance criteria                                   │
│   → BUD determination plan                                │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 8: Lab partner executes stability                    │
│   → Pull samples per protocol                             │
│   → Run assays at each timepoint                          │
│   → Capture data + trend analysis                         │
│   → For 503-B: minimum 3 batches required                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 4 — Documentation (2-3 weeks)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 9: formulation-batch-record                          │
│   → Master Batch Record (MBR)                             │
│   → COA template                                          │
│   → SDS                                                   │
│   → Label artwork spec                                    │
│   → Release-test SOP                                      │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 10: Validation batches                               │
│   → 3 pilot batches per MBR                               │
│   → All 3 must pass release testing                       │
│   → MBR frozen → production-ready                         │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 5 — Pre-launch artifacts (parallel, 2-4 weeks)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 11: product-naming-gate                              │
│   → 4 gates: trademark + compliance + brand + good-name   │
│   → Approval gate: name cleared + filed                   │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 12: product-catalog-builder                          │
│   → SKU created (status: draft)                           │
│   → Attributes per form set                               │
│   → Cold-chain flag set if applicable                     │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 13: product-copy                                     │
│   → Provider-facing description                           │
│   → Through compliance-officer for claims                 │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 14: product-imagery                                  │
│   → Hero + variant + packaging                            │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 15: product-pricing-engine                           │
│   → Margin floor enforced                                 │
│   → B2B tier prices                                       │
│   → Approval gate: Chris approves pricing                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 6 — Launch gate (final review)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 16: formulation-lead launch checklist                │
│   → All 17 launch-gate boxes ✅                           │
│   → Approval gate: Steve + Chris + compliance-officer     │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 17: SKU status → active                              │
│   → Goes live in catalog                                  │
│   → ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (NEW PRODUCT sequence)  │
│   → account-manager notifies key providers                │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Phase 7 — Post-launch (ongoing)
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Step 18: Ongoing stability                                │
│   → Continue protocol through label-claim shelf life      │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 19: Customer feedback monitoring                     │
│   → reputation-manager + account-manager                  │
│   → Adverse event reporting per setup                     │
│   ↓                                                       │
│ Step 20: Quarterly performance review                     │
│   → Sales / margin / refund rate                          │
│   → Stability ongoing data                                │
│   → IP portfolio status (patent prosecution)              │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Critical gates (cannot bypass)

Phase Gate Approver Why
0 Intake go/no-go Steve + Chris Resource allocation
0 Patent decision before disclosure Chris + counsel Destroys novelty if missed
2 Compliance signoff compliance-officer Regulatory exposure
3 BUD determination Steve + compliance-officer Label claim must match data
4 MBR frozen Steve + quality + compliance Manufacturing law
5 Naming clearance product-naming-gate (4 gates) Trademark + claim risk
5 Pricing Chris Margin discipline
6 Launch Steve + Chris + compliance Final triad approval

Failure recovery

Phase fails Recovery
Market fit declines (Phase 0) Park; revisit quarterly
Patent blocked (Phase 0) Design around OR license OR accept generic-status; sometimes still pursue if margin OK
Compatibility fails (Phase 1) Steve re-formulates; back to Step 4
Compliance fails (Phase 2) Class re-evaluation; counsel; may pivot to different form / class
Stability OOS (Phase 3) Investigation per USP <1010>; may force reformulation OR shorter BUD
Validation batch fails (Phase 4) Root-cause; MBR revision; re-run validation
Naming gate fails (Phase 5) Pivot to alternate name; potentially fast since name doesn't affect chemistry
Launch gate incomplete (Phase 6) Block; no exceptions; close gaps
Adverse event post-launch Per incident-response.sop.md; possible field action per stability OOS pattern

Outputs (artifact set per formulation)

Stored in _Logs/formulation/<id>/:

  • intake.md — initial intake form
  • market-fit.md — Phase 0 scoring
  • patent-strategy.md — Phase 0 IP brief
  • spec.yaml + spec.md — Phase 1 design
  • bench-notes.md — Phase 1 Steve's notes
  • compliance-report.md — Phase 2
  • stability/protocol.md — Phase 3
  • stability/results/<timepoint>.csv — Phase 3 ongoing
  • mbr/mbr-v<X>.md — Phase 4
  • mbr/coa-template.md — Phase 4
  • mbr/sds.md — Phase 4
  • mbr/label-artwork.pdf — Phase 4
  • mbr/validation-batches/ — Phase 4
  • launch-checklist.md — Phase 6
  • post-launch/ — ongoing data

Calendar-time expectations

  • 503-A patient-specific (peptide vial example): 12-20 weeks (compliance + stability fast; documentation lighter)
  • 503-B catalog (multi-batch validation): 24-40 weeks (3-batch validation + accelerated + 6-month real-time)
  • Supplement: 16-24 weeks
  • Cosmetic: 12-20 weeks
  • Drug: outside scope without partner; typically 2+ years

Related

  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/formulation-lead/SKILL.md (orchestrator)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ip-lead/SKILL.md (Phase 0 + Phase 5 IP gate)
  • _Workflows/ecommerce-build.workflow.md (downstream catalog work)
  • _Shared Context/sops/new-formulation-intake.sop.md
  • _Shared Context/sops/product-name-clearance.sop.md
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris (initial workflow)

workflows.name: Workflow: GHL Continuous Improvement
workflows.description: Weekly cycle to keep all GHL surfaces (pipelines, workflows, conversations, forms, contact data) getting better. Mirror of `voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md` for the rest of GHL.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: GHL Continuous Improvement

Weekly cycle to keep all GHL surfaces (pipelines, workflows, conversations, forms, contact data) getting better. Mirror of voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md for the rest of GHL.

When this workflow fires

  • Scheduled: every Friday 3:30pm via automation-scheduler (after voice-call-reviewer)
  • On-demand: anomaly investigation, post-campaign review
  • Triggered: when ghl-continuous-improvement flags 🔴 (sharp metric drop or compliance issue)

Agent chain

Step 1: ghl-continuous-improvement
   → Pull all GHL surfaces (pipelines, workflows, conversations, forms, contacts)
   → Classify patterns
   → Generate improvement proposals
   ↓
Step 2: Specialist deep-dives (PARALLEL)
   ├── 2a. ghl-conversation-analytics → cross-channel conversation patterns
   ├── 2b. ghl-pipeline-optimizer → stage-by-stage health
   └── 2c. ghl-form-builder (analytics mode) → form conversion patterns
   ↓
Step 3: Consolidate proposals
   → Merge specialist findings into a single priority list
   → Sort by leverage (impact × ease)
   ↓
Step 4: Approval gate (Chris)
   → Review proposals; approve top 3-5 to ship this cycle
   → Defer / kill others
   ↓
Step 5: For each approved proposal
   ├── 5a. Workflow changes → workflow-automation-builder
   ├── 5b. Structural changes (pipelines / fields / tags) → ghl-crm-architect
   ├── 5c. Form changes → ghl-form-builder
   ├── 5d. Funnel / page changes → ghl-funnel-builder
   ├── 5e. SMS sequence changes → sms-campaign-builder
   └── 5f. Conversation patterns → voice-script-author (if voice) / copywriting (if text)
   ↓
Step 6: ghl-eval-harness
   → Run regression suite against changes
   → Block if 🔴
   ↓
Step 7: Approval gate (Chris)
   → Review eval results
   → Approve production deploy
   ↓
Step 8: workflow-automation-builder
   → Deploy approved changes to GHL production
   → Document rollback per change
   ↓
Step 9: Monitor first week post-change
   → Real-time KPI watch
   → Auto-pause triggers active
   ↓
Step 10: Log to campaign-results
   → Capture what changed + measured impact
   → Update iteration history
   ↓
Step 11: Quarterly: ghl-workflow-auditor
   → Deep audit + cleanup proposal (every March/June/Sept/Dec)
   ↓
Step 12: Cycle complete; next Friday repeats

Standard inputs

  • Location ID (Matrix primary)
  • Period (default: last 7 days)
  • Comparison baseline (default: prior period)

Standard outputs

  • Weekly review report: _Logs/campaign-results/ghl/<location>/<date>-review.md
  • Conversation analytics report: _Logs/campaign-results/ghl-conversations/<date>.md
  • Pipeline optimizer report: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-pipeline-opt-<pipeline>.md
  • Approved proposals shipped: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-ghl-iteration-v<N>.md
  • Eval run report: _Logs/eval-suites/ghl/<workflow>/runs/<date>.md
  • First-week observation: _Logs/campaign-results/ghl/<date>-post-ship.md

Approval gates (2 per weekly cycle + quarterly audit)

  1. After Step 3 — Chris approves which proposals ship (top 3-5)
  2. After Step 6 — Chris reviews eval results before production deploy
  3. Quarterly Step 11 — Chris approves workflow cleanup plan

Estimated time

  • Step 1-3 (review): 60-90 min
  • Steps 5-6 (implementation + eval): 1-3 hours per proposal
  • Steps 7-8 (gate + deploy): 30 min
  • Step 9 (post-ship monitoring): 1-3 days

Total wall-clock per cycle: 1 week (Friday review → next Friday post-ship complete).

Auto-pause triggers (Step 9)

If any KPI drops > 15% post-change:

  • Pipeline velocity
  • Workflow execution rate
  • Conversation response time
  • Form conversion rate
  • Compliance flag (TCPA / consent / STOP not honored)

→ Auto-pause + alert Chris. Rollback to prior version.

Per-location iteration log

In _Logs/iterations/ghl-<location>.md:

| Cycle | Date | Changes shipped | KPI before | KPI after | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 2026-05-09 | Auto-nudge added to "Discovery Scheduled" stage | 14d avg-in-stage | 9d | Win |
| 2 | 2026-05-16 | Catalog form: NPI moved to post-submit | 33% conversion | 51% | Win |
| 3 | 2026-05-23 | Welcome SMS: shorter copy + branded link | 18% reply rate | 24% | Win |

Trend visible across cycles. After 4-6 cycles, GHL KPIs measurably better than launch.

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run reviews (read-only)
  • ✅ Generate proposals
  • ✅ Add eval cases (additive)
  • ✅ Run eval suite on staging
  • ⚠️ Approve proposals to ship (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Production deploy of changes (Chris)
  • 🚫 Auto-deploy without eval pass
  • 🚫 Remove eval cases or delete workflows

Roll-up to executives

After each cycle, GHL health surfaces in:

  • pmo-operator (current state + KPI trend)
  • weekly-review (cross-system summary including voice + GHL)
  • analytics-architect dashboard (CRM KPIs)
  • Monthly board / investor section (efficiency + retention metrics)

Quarterly deep audit

Every quarter, Step 11 (ghl-workflow-auditor) runs:

  • Dead workflow cleanup
  • Redundancy consolidation
  • Loop / security risk fixes
  • Tag bloat cleanup
  • Custom field hygiene

This is the "spring cleaning" that prevents sprawl over months.

Last updated

2026-05-10 by Chris (initial cycle definition)

workflows.name: Workflow: Inbound Call → Handoff (Specialist Chain)
workflows.description: Voice-side chain. Inbound phone call → AI qualifier (Mack/Reception persona) → live clinic research → either book on calendar OR warm-handoff to AM with brief delivered before AM picks up. Composed primarily from existing specialist agents. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md` specialist-chain pattern.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Inbound Call → Handoff (Specialist Chain)

Voice-side chain. Inbound phone call → AI qualifier (Mack/Reception persona) → live clinic research → either book on calendar OR warm-handoff to AM with brief delivered before AM picks up. Composed primarily from existing specialist agents. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md specialist-chain pattern.

Chain overview

Inbound phone call (number rings into Matrix line)
   │
   ▼
[1] voice-ai-inbound-qualifier (Mack / Reception persona)
   ↓ (output: intent + qualified/disqualified + capture data)
[2] lead-scoring (live, fast — Haiku tier)
   ↓ (output: lead_score + tier)
[3] clinic-research-agent (LIVE — speed-optimized — during call)
   ↓ (output: brief dossier — 60-90 sec budget)
[4] DECISION: book-now vs warm-handoff vs callback
   ├── book-now → ghl-calendar-builder
   └── warm-handoff → call-handoff-orchestrator
                       ↓
[5] (parallel) discovery-call-prep (rapid brief for AM)
   ↓ (output: 1-page brief delivered to AM Slack DM + email)
[6] AM picks up handed-off call OR books own time
   ↓
[7] meeting-magic (post-call action extraction)
   ↓ (output: action items + next-step tickets)
→ Chain ends; contact tagged + routed to standard customer-onboarding chain if closed

When this workflow fires

  • Inbound call to Matrix main line during business hours
  • Inbound call after hours (per ghl-config.md voice configuration — Mack agent id 698be65e8eeb7470511829d9)
  • Voicemail left during business hours (transcribed → enters chain at step 1)
  • Returning-caller callback (existing contact calls back; chain skips re-research)

Pre-flight checks

  • Caller phone number captured (caller ID active OR caller provides)
  • Phone number scrubbed against DNC list (if outbound chain candidate later)
  • No active chain processing this contact (idempotency by caller phone)
  • Matrix voice line operational (Retell agent healthy per voice-eval-harness)

Specialist 1: voice-ai-inbound-qualifier (Mack)

Role

Answer the call. Qualify the caller — intent (catalog inquiry / existing-customer support / vendor pitch / wrong number / press / other), identify (clinic name, provider name, NPI if known, contact details). Capture transcript live.

Input

  • Inbound call (real-time)
  • Caller phone number
  • Time of day (drives tone — friendly during business / professional after hours)

Rules

  • Per voice-ai-inbound-qualifier SKILL.md (Mack / Reception persona)
  • Recording disclosure stated immediately
  • TCPA-compliant (no auto-spam outbound)
  • If existing-customer voice → recognize via phone-number lookup; warmer tone; faster path
  • If clinical question → disclaim "I can route you to clinical team — not something I can advise on" (compliance-officer rule)
  • If legal-threat or press → STOP qualification + transfer to AM / Chris immediately
  • Max 4 min before route decision (don't trap caller in qualification loop)

Tools

  • Retell AI (voice infrastructure)
  • GHL MCP (real-time contact lookup by phone)
  • pii-redactor (sanitize transcript before logging)

Output

call_id: string
caller_phone: string
caller_status: existing-customer | known-prospect | new-prospect | unknown
intent_classification: enum (catalog-inquiry / support / vendor-pitch / wrong-number / press / legal-threat / other)
captured:
  clinic_name: string
  provider_name: string
  provider_title: string
  email: string
  npi: string (if provided)
qualification_decision: qualified | unqualified | route-to-cs | route-to-am | route-to-chris | callback-only
transcript_path: string  # logged + redacted
flags: array  # legal-threat / press / vip-customer / etc.

Handoff

  • If qualified + new-prospect → lead-scoring (live, fast)
  • If existing-customer + support intent → voice-ai-support-agent (different chain — order-status / refund / etc.)
  • If route-to-cs → CS rep callback queue
  • If route-to-am → call-handoff-orchestrator (immediate)
  • If route-to-chris → Chris's queue; Sam answers expectation-setting
  • If legal-threat → STOP + immediately notify Chris + counsel

Approval gate

  • Live transfer to a human (AM / Chris): requires human availability check first; otherwise offer scheduled callback

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/01-voice-qualifier/<call_id>.json
  • Transcript at _Logs/voice/<date>/<call_id>.transcript.md (PII-redacted)

Specialist 2: lead-scoring (live, fast)

Role

Score the caller in real time. Speed-optimized vs batch scoring.

Input

  • voice-ai-inbound-qualifier output
  • GHL contact record (if existing)

Rules

  • Per lead-scoring SKILL.md retrofitted spec
  • Time budget: 3 seconds (Haiku tier; minimal reasoning)
  • If unknown caller: score based on captured data only (limited)
