  • Skip recency-decay computation (live caller = current intent)

Output

  • Per retrofitted lead-scoring schema (tier + drivers + flags)

Handoff

  • If qualified+ → clinic-research-agent (LIVE mode)
  • If soft/cold → polite call-end + nurture sequence enrollment

Approval gate

  • None

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/02-lead-scoring-live/<call_id>.json

Specialist 3: clinic-research-agent (LIVE mode)

Role

Compress normal 5-min research into 60-90 sec while caller waits OR runs in parallel while voice agent continues capturing data.

Input

  • voice-ai-inbound-qualifier captured data
  • lead-scoring output

Rules

  • Time budget: 90 seconds MAX
  • Parallel-fetch all sources (NPPES + Google + WebFetch concurrent)
  • Skip deep cross-validation; return partial dossier with confidence
  • If website blocks scraping: skip; rely on NPPES + sales-intelligence cache
  • All standard clinic-research-agent rules apply (no fabricated facts; trade-secret discipline)
  • If competitor flag detected → notify voice agent IMMEDIATELY for course correction in conversation

Tools

  • Same as clinic-research-agent standard mode, but parallel-fetched
  • npi-verifier (atomic, fast)

Output

  • Same schema as standard clinic-research-agent, with live_mode: true and lower confidence_overall typical

Handoff

  • Decision logic flows to step 4 (book-now vs warm-handoff vs callback)

Approval gate

  • Competitor flag → voice agent notified mid-call; AM informed; chain may pause

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/03-clinic-research-live/<call_id>.json

Specialist 4: DECISION — book-now vs warm-handoff vs callback

Role

Decide call's next move based on caller signals + research dossier + AM availability.

Decision logic

IF caller wants "to book a call with someone" + AM available:
    → warm-handoff (Step 5a)
ELIF caller wants "to schedule for later":
    → book-now (Step 5b)
ELIF caller is researching / wants info:
    → email-catalog + scheduled callback (Step 5c)
ELIF caller is VIP / tier-1 partner / press / Chris-only:
    → transfer to Chris OR Chris's voicemail with brief
ELIF AM not available + caller wants live person:
    → scheduled callback within 1 business day + brief delivered

Approval gate

  • Live transfer requires confirmed AM availability (calendar check + AM-active-on-channel signal)

Specialist 5a: call-handoff-orchestrator (warm-handoff)

Role

Bridge the live call to the assigned AM. Deliver brief to AM BEFORE they pick up.

Input

  • Decision output
  • Call_id (active)
  • Assigned AM

Rules

  • Per call-handoff-orchestrator SKILL.md
  • Brief delivered to AM's Slack DM + dashboard at moment of transfer initiation
  • 5-10 sec voice handoff confirmation ("I have Dr. Smith from Coastal Wellness — they're a 3-provider HRT + GLP-1 clinic — I'm transferring now")
  • AM has 30 sec to accept; otherwise route to AM voicemail with brief

Tools

  • Retell + voice transfer
  • Slack (brief delivery)
  • discovery-call-prep (rapid brief generation in parallel)

Output

handoff_status: succeeded | am-declined | am-voicemail | callback-scheduled
am_assigned: string
am_brief_delivered_at: ISO timestamp
am_brief_path: string
call_continued_with_human: boolean

Handoff

  • Succeeded → live call continues with AM; chain pauses (continues after call via meeting-magic)
  • AM declined → schedule callback + comfort caller
  • AM voicemail → caller leaves message OR opts for callback

Approval gate

  • None (real-time decision)

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/05a-handoff/<call_id>.json

Specialist 5b: ghl-calendar-builder (book-now)

Role

Book the call directly while caller is on the line.

Input

  • Decision output
  • Caller's preferred time / window

Rules

  • Per ghl-calendar-builder SKILL.md
  • Honor ghl-config.md discovery calendar (wsJVS5rjdUmRE06PFH4O)
  • Offer max 3 time options
  • Confirm in caller's timezone
  • Send confirmation immediately (SMS + email if both available)
  • Set T-24h and T-1h SMS reminders

Output

appointment_status: booked | proposed | declined
appointment_time: ISO
appointment_link: string
reminders_scheduled: array

Handoff

  • Booked → discovery-call-prep (brief for whoever's taking the call)
  • Declined → polite call-end + nurture-sequence enrollment

Approval gate

  • VIP / tier-1: AM confirms time

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/05b-booking/<call_id>.json

Specialist 5c: Email catalog + scheduled callback

Role

For caller who wants info first, schedule a callback + send catalog via approved template.

Rules

  • Send catalog only if caller is verified provider (NPI-verified ideally)
  • Per compliance-officer Gate 1 (catalog copy already compliance-approved)
  • Schedule callback within 1 business day

Output

catalog_sent: boolean
callback_scheduled: boolean
callback_time: ISO

Handoff

  • Callback at scheduled time → AM (with brief from discovery-call-prep)

Specialist 6: AM-side call (human; outside automation but tracked)

The AM picks up handed-off call OR books own time. This step is human; chain just tracks state.

State transitions tracked:

  • am-accepted-handoff → call duration logged
  • am-call-ended → transcript captured (if AM recorded; consent required)
  • am-call-outcome → next-step recorded by AM (move-to-opp / schedule-next / decline / etc.)

Specialist 7: meeting-magic (post-call action extraction)

Role

After AM call concludes, extract action items + decisions from recorded transcript.

Input

  • AM call transcript (if recorded with consent)
  • Caller / contact context (passthrough from full chain)

Rules

  • Per meeting-magic SKILL.md
  • 2-prompt extraction (summary + action table)
  • Resolve TBD owners via roster

Output

  • Action table delivered to AM + relevant departments
  • Tickets created in PMO if applicable

Handoff

  • If outcome = closed-won → _Workflows/new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md (next chain)
  • If outcome = scheduled-follow-up → calendar reminder + nurture
  • If outcome = declined → polite drop + nurture-sequence (long cycle)

Chain-wide observability

State + tags

  • GHL contact tag: chain:inbound-call:<status>:<step-N>
  • Chain status: qualifying / researching-live / handoff / booking / am-with-caller / post-call-processing / completed
  • Custom fields: last_inbound_call_at, last_inbound_outcome, live_research_dossier_id

Logs

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/<step>-<agent>/<call_id>.json
  • _Logs/voice/<date>/<call_id>.transcript.md (PII-redacted via pii-redactor)

Metrics

  • Call answer rate (Mack handles vs drops)
  • Qualification rate per period
  • Live-research success rate (within 90s)
  • Handoff success rate (AM accepts in time)
  • Average call duration
  • Conversion rate (call → booked / handed-off / scheduled-callback / dropped)
  • Customer satisfaction (post-call SMS survey)
  • Compliance issues per period (clinical question / claims / legal-threat)

Surfaced in:

  • chain-monitor daily digest
  • voice-call-reviewer (Friday 3pm CI)
  • ghl-continuous-improvement (Friday 3:30pm CI)

Eval coverage

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/chains/inbound-call-to-handoff/:

  1. New-prospect catalog inquiry + AM available → warm-handoff success
  2. Existing-customer support intent → route to support chain
  3. Vendor pitch → polite decline; tag; archive
  4. Wrong number → polite end; no contact record created
  5. Clinical question → disclaimer + offer clinical-team callback
  6. Press inquiry → transfer to Chris (or voicemail if not avail)
  7. Legal-threat language → STOP + Chris + counsel notified
  8. Competitor caller (research flag mid-call) → AM notified live; conversation pivots
  9. AM unavailable + caller wants live → callback scheduled with brief delivered
  10. VIP existing-customer call → priority routing; Chris loop-in if material
  11. Caller wants info before talking → catalog email + callback scheduled
  12. After-hours call → voicemail + transcribed; chain enters async at step 1 morning

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Voice agent (Mack) fails mid-call Transfer to AM voicemail; log; alert engineering
Live research times out (> 90s) Continue handoff with partial dossier + flag
AM declines handoff Schedule callback + comfort caller
Caller hangs up mid-chain Log + tag inbound-incomplete; AM follow-up next business day
Transcript capture fails AM proceeds without transcript; meeting-magic skipped; manual notes
pii-redactor flags high-density PHI Quarantine transcript; compliance-officer review
Recording disclosure not stated (Mack bug) STOP recording; alert engineering; transcript NOT used

Approval gates summary

Gate Step Who
Live transfer to AM 4 AM availability check
Transfer to Chris 4 Chris availability (else voicemail with brief)
VIP booking 5b AM confirms time
Catalog send to unverified caller 5c NPI verification first; if not provider, generic-info-only
Legal-threat detected Any STOP + Chris + counsel
Adverse-event mention Any STOP + clinical-lead + compliance-officer

Cost + latency targets

  • Total chain cost: < $0.50 (Mack call cost + live research + handoff coord)
  • Mack call duration: < 4 min before route decision
  • Live research turnaround: < 90 seconds
  • AM handoff acceptance: < 30 sec
  • Customer-facing total: feels seamless (caller doesn't perceive "automation")

Related

  • _Templates/micro-agent.template.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/voice-ai-inbound-qualifier/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/lead-scoring/SKILL.md (retrofitted)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/clinic-research-agent/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/call-handoff-orchestrator/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ghl-calendar-builder/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/KB-Skills/discovery-call-prep/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/meeting-magic/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/pii-redactor/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/npi-verifier/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/chain-monitor/SKILL.md
  • _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (voice configuration)

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris — 4th executable specialist chain

workflows.name: Workflow: IP Clearance (end-to-end)
workflows.description: Standard chain for the IP department. Covers patent strategy + trademark clearance + product naming + counsel coordination. Orchestrated by `ip-lead`. Plugs into `formulation-development.workflow.md` (Phase 0 + Phase 5) and `ecommerce-build.workflow.md` (catalog naming).
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: IP Clearance (end-to-end)

Standard chain for the IP department. Covers patent strategy + trademark clearance + product naming + counsel coordination. Orchestrated by ip-lead. Plugs into formulation-development.workflow.md (Phase 0 + Phase 5) and ecommerce-build.workflow.md (catalog naming).

When this workflow fires

  • Steve identifies novel formulation / process / use → patent track
  • New product / brand / sub-brand name proposed → trademark + naming track
  • Quarterly portfolio review → maintenance + competitive scan
  • Competitor IP signal → defensive analysis
  • Pre-launch IP gate for any formulation

Pre-flight

  • Intake type clear: patent / trademark / copyright / opposition / enforcement
  • Counsel relationship active (per vendors.md)
  • Budget approved per matter
  • No public disclosure of patent candidate yet (or clock started)

Agent chain — Patent track

Step 1: ip-lead (intake)
   → Capture invention + inventor + disclosure status + commercial value
   ↓
Step 2: patent-strategy
   → Prior-art search (6 source categories)
   → Novelty + non-obviousness assessment
   → Claim type recommendation (composition / process / use)
   → Freedom-to-operate
   → Commercial valuation
   → Decision: file / defensive publish / pass
   ↓
Step 3: Approval gate — Chris
   → Patent filing budget
   → Strategy approval
   → If high-risk / novel mechanism: counsel call before filing
   ↓
Step 4: ip-counsel-coordination
   → Strategic brief assembled
   → Counsel queued for PPA drafting
   → Filing target locked
   ↓
Step 5: Counsel drafts + files
   → PPA (cheap; 12-month priority)
   → OR non-provisional if confidence high
   ↓
Step 6: Post-filing controls
   → Disclosure now allowable
   → NDA still required for technical detail
   → 12-month clock to convert (if PPA)
   ↓
Step 7: 12-month decision point (if PPA)
   → Commercial signal evaluation
   → Convert to non-provisional / let lapse / PCT international
   → Approval gate: Chris
   ↓
Step 8: Prosecution (ongoing, 1-3 years to grant)
   → ip-counsel-coordination tracks office actions
   → Per office action: response strategy + cost approval
   ↓
Step 9: Grant + maintenance
   → 4, 8, 12-year maintenance fees (utility patent)
   → ip-portfolio.md updated

Agent chain — Trademark / naming track

Step 1: ip-lead (intake)
   → Capture candidate name(s), use class, geography, channel
   ↓
Step 2: trademark-clearance
   → 5 search layers (TESS + common law + phonetic + foreign + adjacent)
   → Score per candidate (🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / ⚫)
   → Good-name filter on survivors
   ↓
Step 3: product-naming-gate
   → 4 gates: trademark + compliance + brand + good-name
   → Rank candidates
   → Recommendation
   ↓
Step 4: Approval gate — Chris (+ sub-brand lead if applicable)
   → Name committed
   ↓
Step 5: ip-counsel-coordination
   → File trademark application (ITU or actual use)
   → Defensive domain + handle registrations (parallel)
   ↓
Step 6: Prosecution (typically 8-14 months to registration in US)
   → ip-counsel-coordination tracks office actions
   → Specimens of use submitted at appropriate time (ITU)
   ↓
Step 7: Registration
   → ip-portfolio.md updated
   → ® symbol becomes available
   → 5-year Section 8 + 10-year renewal calendar set

Agent chain — Quarterly portfolio review

Step 1: ip-lead initiates quarterly cycle
   ↓
Step 2: Inventory current IP (trademark / patent / copyright / trade secret / domains)
   ↓
Step 3: Health check per asset
   → Maintenance fees coming due
   → Actual-use verification (trademarks)
   → Competitive coverage (patents)
   → Renewal decisions
   ↓
Step 4: trademark-clearance defensive scan
   → Anyone using too-similar marks?
   → Flag for opposition / enforcement
   ↓
Step 5: patent-strategy competitive scan
   → New competitor filings
   → Potential blockers
   → Opportunities for design-around / licensing
   ↓
Step 6: Recommendations to Chris
   → File / abandon / enforce / defend / license
   ↓
Step 7: Counsel quarterly call
   → ip-counsel-coordination schedules
   → Recommendations executed
   ↓
Step 8: ip-portfolio.md refreshed

Cross-workflow plug-in points

Plugs into formulation-development.workflow.md

  • Phase 0: patent-strategy runs in parallel with formulation-market-fit
  • Phase 5: product-naming-gate mandatory before catalog SKU goes active

Plugs into ecommerce-build.workflow.md

  • Phase 1 (Catalog): product-naming-gate for any new SKU
  • Brand assets: trademark-clearance for any new taglines / brand expressions

Plugs into marketing-campaign.workflow.md

  • Campaign taglines + slogans run through trademark-clearance if used long-term

Critical gates

Track Gate Approver Why
Patent Pre-disclosure filing Chris Lose international rights otherwise
Patent 12-month convert PPA Chris Cost vs commercial commitment
Patent Office action response Chris + counsel Budget per response
Trademark Name commit Chris (+ sub-brand) Brand identity
Trademark Specimen of use (ITU) Chris Trademark registration
All Abandonment Chris Irreversible
Enforcement Cease-and-desist Chris + counsel Reputation + cost

Failure recovery

Failure Recovery
Trademark blocked (Gate 1) Pivot name; back to Step 2 with new candidates
Compliance fail in naming gate Replace candidate with compliance-friendly alternative
Patent prior-art blocker Design around OR license OR abandon strategy
FTO blocker on patent strategy Design around OR license OR don't pursue
Public disclosure happened pre-filing US 1-year grace; international may be lost; counsel decides
Office action rejection final Appeal (rare) OR abandon
Maintenance fee missed Grace period (6 months for patents); pay with surcharge; if past grace, irreversible
Counsel invoice over-scope Push back via ip-counsel-coordination

Outputs

Per matter:

  • _Logs/ip/inventions/<id>/ — patent matters
  • _Logs/ip/names/<name>/ — trademark matters
  • _Logs/ip/matters/<matter-id>.md — counsel-tracked matters

Registry:

  • _Shared Context/ip-portfolio.md — authoritative

Quarterly report:

  • _Logs/ip/quarterly/<YYYY-Q>.md

Calendar-time expectations

  • PPA filing: 1-2 weeks from decision (drafting time)
  • Non-provisional drafting: 4-8 weeks
  • US trademark to registration: 8-14 months (no objections); longer with office actions
  • PCT international: 30 months from priority to national phase decisions
  • Patent grant: 1-3 years from non-provisional filing typical
  • Trademark renewal: 6 months prep before deadline

Related

  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ip-lead/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/patent-strategy/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/trademark-clearance/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/product-naming-gate/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ip-counsel-coordination/SKILL.md
  • _Workflows/formulation-development.workflow.md (Phase 0 + Phase 5 plug-in)
  • _Workflows/ecommerce-build.workflow.md (catalog naming plug-in)
  • _Shared Context/sops/product-name-clearance.sop.md
  • _Shared Context/sops/new-formulation-intake.sop.md

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris (initial workflow)

workflows.name: Workflow: Label Design & Production (end-to-end)
workflows.description: Umbrella workflow for the Label Design Department in Product. From new SKU → Adobe-Illustrator-pro-level label → compliance-approved → printed + applied. Composes the 6-skill Label team + cross-cutting departments. Plugs into `formulation-development.workflow.md` Phase 5.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Label Design & Production (end-to-end)

Umbrella workflow for the Label Design Department in Product. From new SKU → Adobe-Illustrator-pro-level label → compliance-approved → printed + applied. Composes the 6-skill Label team + cross-cutting departments. Plugs into formulation-development.workflow.md Phase 5.

Chain overview

New SKU triggers label production
       │
       ▼
[1] label-design-lead — orchestrates
       │
       ▼
[2] label-spec-builder — validate / extend per-form spec
       │
       ▼
[3] label-template-designer — Adobe Illustrator master template
       │  (sub-brand variant if applicable)
       ▼
[4] label-variable-data-engine — CSV → all formats (AI/EPS/PDF/PNG/SVG/Avery)
       │
       ▼
[5] label-compliance-reviewer — regulatory + IP + brand gate
       │  🟢 → proceed; 🟡 → loop back; 🔴 → block; ⚠️ → counsel
       ▼
[6] creative-director — final brand review (sub-brand consistency)
       │
       ▼
[7] print-vendor-coordination — vendor bundle + production handoff
       │  proof review cycle
       ▼
[8] Production run at vendor → labels delivered
       │
       ▼
[9] post-production QC by print-vendor-coordination
       │
       ▼
[10] inventory-manager — labels enter inventory; tag as available
       │
       ▼
→ Labels applied at fulfillment (per `fulfillment-shipping` for shipped products
  OR per pharmacy partner for compounded products in `formulation-batch-record`)

When this workflow fires

  • New SKU approved at end of formulation-development.workflow.md Phase 4 → label generation starts
  • New packaging form added (spec-builder runs first)
  • Sub-brand visual refresh requires template-redesign across all forms
  • Regulatory class change requires label-field update across affected SKUs
  • Print partner change requires per-vendor re-spec
  • Quarterly label portfolio audit
  • Bulk label batch (e.g., quarterly reprint inventory)

Pre-flight

  • SKU exists in product-catalog-builder master
  • Packaging form has spec in label-specs.md
  • Sub-brand context locked
  • Regulatory class declared
  • Print partner identified
  • No active blocker (compliance issue / vendor outage)

Per-step detail

Step 1: label-design-lead (orchestrator)

  • Reads request (new SKU / batch / refresh / etc.)
  • Sequences specialist work
  • Tracks chain state + handoffs

Step 2: label-spec-builder (when new form / new variant)

  • Skip if spec exists in label-specs.md
  • Otherwise: physical measurement + vendor dieline + sub-brand variant + regulatory minimum fields
  • Updates label-specs.md
  • Gate: Chris signoff on new spec

Step 3: label-template-designer

  • Builds master .ai + matching .svg per spec + sub-brand
  • Token system stable (compatible with variable-data engine)
  • Sub-brand visual treatment per sub-brands.md
  • Trade-secret discipline (no ALM DT parameters)
  • Gate: creative-director review on visual

Step 4: label-variable-data-engine

  • Reads master .svg + SKU CSV
  • Substitutes {{TOKENS}} per row
  • Exports all formats (AI / EPS / PDF print / PDF preview / PNG transparent / PNG white / SVG / Avery)
  • Wraps ~/Projects/labels generator/generate_labels.py Python pipeline
  • Validation: PDF/X-1a compliance, CMYK, 300 DPI, ink coverage < 300%

Step 5: label-compliance-reviewer (gate)

  • Required regulatory fields per class (503-A / 503-B / supplement / cosmetic)
  • Trademark symbols correct
  • Trade-secret discipline
  • compliance-phrase-detector banned-phrase scan
  • brand-voice-enforcement text check
  • Decision: 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / ⚠️
  • Gate: 🟢 required to proceed; else loop

Step 6: creative-director

  • Final brand review
  • Sub-brand consistency at scale
  • Aesthetic quality (Adobe Illustrator pro level enforced)
  • Gate: brand-OK signoff

Step 7: print-vendor-coordination — handoff

  • Bundle formats per vendor's prepress spec
  • Submit production order with delivery date
  • Receive proof; review within 24h
  • 🟢 → authorize production
  • 🟡 → redlines + revision
  • 🔴 → reject + diagnose
  • Gate: proof approved before production

Step 8: Production at vendor

  • Vendor runs production
  • Tracking + ETA visibility
  • Communication via vendor portal / email

Step 9: Post-production QC

  • print-vendor-coordination random-sample inspection
  • Adhesive test
  • Color match (delta E < 2.0 target)
  • Dimension verification
  • Defect rate documented
  • Gate: accept / reject / partial-accept

Step 10: inventory-manager

  • Labels enter inventory
  • Tag as available for application
  • Track via lot per lot-number-generator

Final: Application

  • For shipped products: applied at fulfillment per fulfillment-shipping
  • For compounded products: applied at pharmacy partner per formulation-batch-record MBR

Approval gates

Gate Step Approver
New spec 2 Chris (per approval-rules.md if material cost)
Template visual 3-6 creative-director + sub-brand lead
Compliance review 5 label-compliance-reviewer (🟢 required) + compliance-officer (escalation)
Trade-secret leak detected 5 Chris (immediate)
Proof approval 7 print-vendor-coordination (per spec)
Production cost > $X 7 Chris (per approval-rules.md)
QC reject 9 vendor-management + Chris if vendor relationship affected

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Spec doesn't exist for new form label-spec-builder runs first (Step 2 mandatory)
Template token mismatch with CSV Fix template OR CSV; never partial generate
Compliance 🔴 (required field missing) Fix at TEMPLATE (not per-label); regenerate batch
Trade-secret leak detected Block; Chris notified; template + CSV both audited
Print partner rejects file Diagnose vs vendor spec; fix; resubmit; track for vendor performance
Proof fails 3x Escalate to vendor-management for relationship review
QC defect rate > target Root cause + vendor corrective action; consider alternate vendor
Vendor delayed delivery Notify formulation-lead + inventory-manager for stockout risk
Sub-brand visual drift detected Quarterly template audit fix + reissue if material

Outputs

Per-SKU:

  • Master .ai template + matching .svg
  • Per-label .ai / .eps / .pdf-print / .pdf-preview / .png-transparent / .png-white / .svg / .avery files
  • Compliance review log
  • Proof + production records
  • Lot-applied tracking

Per-batch:

  • Zip archive of all formats
  • Compliance summary
  • Print partner handoff package
  • Proof approval log
  • Production QC report

Filesystem canonical paths:

  • Masters: ~/Projects/labels generator/templates/illustrator/<form>/<sub-brand>.ai
  • Per-SKU deliverables: ~/Projects/labels generator/output/<sub-brand>/<form>/<sku>/
  • Compliance logs: _Logs/labels/<sku>/<date>/compliance.md
  • Proof records: _Logs/print-vendors/<vendor>/<run-id>/

KPI targets

  • Adobe Illustrator pro-design quality: 100% of new labels
  • Compliance 🟢 rate first-pass: > 85% (else template needs work)
  • PDF/X-1a validation pass: 100%
  • Proof approval first-pass: > 90% (else vendor spec or our template issue)
  • Production defect rate per vendor: < 1%
  • Time from new SKU approval → label printed in inventory: < 21 days standard

Surfaced in:

  • chain-monitor daily digest (any label-chain alerts)
  • formulation-continuous-improvement Friday (label production part of Phase 5 monitoring)

Cost + cadence targets

  • Per single new label (standard): $200-500 (template work if new form; mostly print partner cost)
  • Per batch of 20 SKUs (existing template): $50-200 in agent costs + print
  • Quarterly portfolio audit: 1-2 days specialist time

Related

  • _Shared Context/label-specs.md
  • _Shared Context/sub-brands.md
  • _Shared Context/regulatory-frameworks.md
  • _Shared Context/ip-portfolio.md (trademark usage)
  • _Shared Context/alm-dt-platform.md (trade-secret discipline)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/label-design-lead/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/label-spec-builder/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/label-template-designer/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/label-variable-data-engine/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/label-compliance-reviewer/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/print-vendor-coordination/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/formulation-batch-record/SKILL.md (MBR references labels)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/product-catalog-builder/SKILL.md (SKU master)
  • _Workflows/formulation-development.workflow.md (Phase 5 plug-in)
  • Source artifacts: ~/Projects/labels generator/ + ~/Projects/Peptide Label/ + ~/Projects/Sarms Labels/

Last updated

2026-05-11 — initial label production workflow; 6 specialists + lead built same day

workflows.name: Workflow: Lead → Booked Demo (Specialist Chain)
workflows.description: Christo's canonical example workflow. End-to-end chain from GHL inbound lead to demo booked + AM brief delivered. Built per the **Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff** specialist-chain pattern (see `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md` + `micro-agent-team-builder`).
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Lead → Booked Demo (Specialist Chain)

Christo's canonical example workflow. End-to-end chain from GHL inbound lead to demo booked + AM brief delivered. Built per the Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff specialist-chain pattern (see _Templates/micro-agent.template.md + micro-agent-team-builder).

Each agent in the chain is a specialist digital employee. Output from each step is structured data; next agent picks it up as input. No free-form handoffs. Approval gates marked. Observability wired throughout.

Chain overview

GHL Inbound Lead (form / inbound voice / cold-add)
   │
   ▼
[1] lead-scoring          ← qualifies; tier + driver
   │
   ▼
[2] clinic-research-agent  ← clinic type, services, providers, product fit
   │
   ▼
[3] outreach-writer        ← drafts message tuned to dossier + tier + channel
   │
   ▼
[4] compliance-officer     ← review claims / disclosures / brand voice
   │
   ▼  (approved)
[5] workflow-automation-builder  ← CRM stage advance + tags + comm trigger
   │
   ▼  (message sent; reply or non-reply)
[6] ghl-calendar-builder + sms-campaign-builder  ← book / nurture
   │
   ▼  (demo booked)
[7] discovery-call-prep   ← sales-prep brief delivered to AM / Chris

When this workflow fires

  • New GHL form submission (catalog request / consultation request / partner inquiry)
  • Inbound voice call qualifies via voice-ai-inbound-qualifier
  • Cold-add to GHL (vetted source — never raw scraped)
  • Re-engagement of dormant prospect (existing contact with new signal)
  • Sales team manually triggers chain for a specific contact

Pre-flight checks

  • Contact exists in GHL with at least: email OR phone + clinic_name OR provider_name
  • Contact is not in DNC list
  • No active chain currently processing this contact (idempotency — don't double-process)
  • No outreach in last 21 days (cooldown)
  • Contact tagged chain:lead-to-demo:eligible (gate prevents accidental triggering)

Specialist 1: lead-scoring

Role

Compute fit + intent score (0-100) for the contact based on _Shared Context/ghl-config.md + lead-scoring skill rubric. Assign tier (hot / warm / qualified / soft / cold) + capture top score drivers.

Input

  • GHL contact record (email / firstname / lastname / clinic_name / npi / title / etc.)
  • Recent engagement signals (form fills, page views, opens, calls)
  • Existing custom-field data

Rules

  • Per lead-scoring SKILL.md rubric
  • Score updated on every meaningful signal change
  • Don't process if do-not-score tag present

Tools

  • GHL MCP (read)
  • lead-scoring skill (orchestrator)

Output

contact_id: string
lead_score: number (0-100)
lead_tier: enum (hot / warm / qualified / soft / cold)
fit_score: number
intent_score: number
top_drivers: array of string
flags: array (e.g., "vip", "regulatory-attention")
scored_at: ISO timestamp

Handoff

  • If lead_tier ∈ {hot, warm, qualified} AND no flags blocking → clinic-research-agent
  • If lead_tier ∈ {soft, cold} → low-touch nurture sequence (skip rest of chain)
  • If flag = "vip" → escalate to AM with research expedited
  • If flag = "regulatory-attention" → pause + route to compliance-officer before research

Approval gate

  • None for routine scoring
  • Hot tier crossings: notify AM + Christo (FYI; doesn't block chain)

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/01-lead-scoring/<contact_id>.json
  • Metrics: score, tier, drivers, processing time

Specialist 2: clinic-research-agent

Role

(Per clinic-research-agent SKILL.md.) Research clinic type, services, providers, geo market, product fit.

Input

  • lead-scoring output (full payload)
  • GHL contact record (read)

Rules

  • 5-min time budget per clinic
  • Verify with 3+ sources
  • "Unknown" beats fabricated
  • Flag competitors / FDA-attention → escalate

Tools

  • NPPES, WebSearch, WebFetch
  • compliance-phrase-detector (pre-handoff text check)
  • GHL MCP (read + write custom fields)
  • Anthropic API (Haiku default)

Output

Per clinic-research-agent SKILL.md output schema:

  • clinic_type / services / provider_count / lead_provider (NPI verified) / geographic_market / matrix_product_fit / sub_brand_alignment / flags / handoff_recommendation

Handoff

  • If handoff_recommendation == "outreach-writer" → next step
  • If low-fit-nurture → ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (nurture branch)
  • If pause-for-review → human review queue
  • If route-to-am-for-vip → assign to AM with research dossier
  • If pause-escalate-to-chris → tag + Slack ping

Approval gate

  • None for standard research
  • Competitor / FDA-attention flag: Chris approval before chain continues

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/02-clinic-research/<contact_id>.json
  • Metrics: data_points_verified, confidence, time_taken, cost

Specialist 3: outreach-writer

Role

(Per outreach-writer SKILL.md.) Draft outreach message tuned to clinic dossier + tier + channel.

Input

  • clinic-research-agent output (full payload)
  • lead-scoring tier (passthrough)
  • Campaign context (optional)
  • Channel override (optional)

Rules

  • Sub-brand voice from dossier.sub_brand_alignment.primary
  • Word-count limits per channel (email 60-120 / SMS ≤ 320 / VM 25-30s)
  • One CTA per message
  • 21-day cooldown enforced
  • No banned phrases / no trade-secret leaks
  • At least 1 personalization fact from dossier

Tools

  • compliance-phrase-detector, brand-voice-enforcement, conversation-channel-router
  • GHL MCP (write to drafts custom field — NOT conversation thread)
  • Read: sub-brands.md, brand-guide.md, compliance-rules.md, alm-dt-platform.md, products.md
  • Anthropic API (Haiku for SMS/VM; Sonnet for nuanced emails)

Output

  • draft_id / channel / sub_brand / timing_recommendation / message_draft / self_check / confidence / requires_human_judgment

Handoff

  • Always → compliance-officer (no draft ships without compliance review)
  • If requires_human_judgment = true → human compliance review (longer SLA)
  • If self-check fails twice → escalate to human review

Approval gate

  • Compliance review is mandatory next step
  • New claim attempts always require human

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/03-outreach-writer/<contact_id>.json
  • Metrics: word_count, confidence, channel, requires_human_judgment count, cost

Specialist 4: compliance-officer

Role

(Per compliance-officer SKILL.md — Gate 1 "Claims Discipline" applied to the draft.) Final compliance review before send.

Input

  • outreach-writer draft + self-check results
  • clinic-research-agent dossier (for fact verification)
  • lead-scoring tier (passthrough)

Rules

  • Apply 6 standing gates (Gate 1 primary for outreach review)
  • 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / ⚠️ decision per draft
  • 🟡 → required edits returned; back to outreach-writer for revision
  • 🔴 → block + reason; chain stops
  • ⚠️ → counsel required → pause

Tools

  • compliance-phrase-detector (deterministic pass)
  • regulatory-frameworks.md (framework reference)
  • compliance-rules.md (operational rules)
  • Anthropic API (judgment layer)

Output

review_id: string
draft_id: string (passthrough)
decision: 🟢 approved | 🟡 edit-required | 🔴 blocked | ⚠️ counsel-required
required_edits: array of {location, issue, suggested_fix}
approval_signature: {name, timestamp}
escalation_path: string (if applicable)

Handoff

  • If 🟢 approved → workflow-automation-builder (CRM update + comm trigger)
  • If 🟡 edit-required → loop back to outreach-writer with edits
  • If 🔴 blocked → chain stops; log reason; tag contact chain:blocked-compliance
  • If ⚠️ counsel → pause chain; route to ip-counsel-coordination (or external counsel)

Approval gate

  • Standard reviews: this skill signs off
  • Novel claims / new mechanism: counsel required before approval
  • Public-statement-flagged content: Chris approval

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/04-compliance-officer/<contact_id>.json
  • _Logs/compliance/outreach-reviews/<date>.md (per compliance-officer archive pattern)

Specialist 5: workflow-automation-builder (CRM updater)

Role

Move contact through pipeline stage; update tags; trigger send via GHL workflow.

Input

  • Approved draft (from compliance-officer 🟢)
  • contact_id

Rules

  • Idempotent (same draft_id processed twice = no double-send)
  • Pipeline stage advance per ghl-config.md MAS Order Lifecycle pipeline
  • Tags follow taxonomy per ghl-config.md
  • Send via approved GHL workflow (not direct API call)

Tools

  • GHL MCP (write — tags + custom fields + workflow triggers)
  • workflow-automation-builder skill (orchestrator)

Output

pipeline_stage_advanced: enum (new → researching → outreached / nurturing / blocked)
tags_applied: array
send_workflow_triggered: boolean
send_workflow_id: string
sent_at: ISO timestamp (if synchronous send)

Handoff

  • After send fires → wait for reply OR non-reply window
  • On reply (positive) → ghl-calendar-builder (booking branch)
  • On reply (objection) → outreach-writer (objection-handling branch) → loop to compliance-officer → re-send
  • On reply (decline) → tag outreach:declined; route to long-cycle nurture
  • On non-reply T+7 days → outreach-writer (follow-up draft) → loop
  • After 3 non-reply touches → drop to nurture

Approval gate

  • None (automated execution of approved draft via approved workflow)

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/05-workflow-automation/<contact_id>.json
  • Metrics: send_success, reply_within_window

Specialist 6: ghl-calendar-builder + sms-campaign-builder (booking)

Role

On positive reply, propose times + book the discovery / catalog-overview call.

Input

  • contact_id
  • Reply content
  • Contact's timezone
  • Calendar availability (Chris / AM / both)

Rules

  • Confirm time in contact's timezone
  • ≤ 3 time options offered (reduce decision fatigue)
  • 24-hour reminder (SMS if opted in; email always)
  • 1-hour reminder (SMS if opted in)
  • Honor calendar config per ghl-config.md discovery calendar (wsJVS5rjdUmRE06PFH4O)

Tools

  • GHL MCP (calendar + send messages)
  • ghl-calendar-builder skill
  • sms-campaign-builder skill (for reminders)

Output

appointment_status: booked | proposed_pending_confirmation | declined
appointment_time: ISO timestamp (if booked)
appointment_link: string (Zoom / call-in)
reminders_scheduled: array of {time, channel}

Handoff

  • If booked → discovery-call-prep (sales-prep brief for AM / Chris)
  • If declined → tag outreach:not-now; route to long-cycle nurture
  • If pending → wait + nudge after 48h

Approval gate

  • For VIP contacts: AM confirms time before booking
  • For tier-1 partner CEOs: Chris confirms

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/06-booking/<contact_id>.json

Specialist 7: discovery-call-prep

Role

Generate the sales-prep brief for whoever's taking the call (Chris / AM / Leo).

Input

  • contact_id
  • Appointment details
  • All prior chain output (lead-scoring + clinic-research + outreach history + compliance approvals + reply content)

Rules

  • Brief delivered ≥ 24 hours before call
  • Concise (1-page summary + 1-page deep)
  • Cite specific dossier facts for the call-taker to reference
  • Surface ANY flags from the chain (vip / regulatory / etc.)

Tools

  • discovery-call-prep skill (orchestrator — per existing skill)
  • GHL MCP (read all prior data)

Output

  • Call-brief markdown delivered to AM / Chris inbox + Slack DM
  • Pre-meeting calendar attached as agenda

Handoff

  • To the human call-taker (Chris / AM / Leo per assignment)
  • After call → call notes captured via meeting-magic → action items extracted → next-step automation triggered

Approval gate

  • None — brief is informational

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/07-sales-prep/<contact_id>.json
  • Chain complete; close chain state

Chain-wide observability + state

State persistence

Each chain run has a unique chain_id. State persists at:

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/index.md — chain manifest + status
  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/<step-N>-<agent>/<contact_id>.json — per-step output
  • GHL contact tags reflect current chain state (chain:lead-to-demo:step-N:status)

Chain status enum

  • running (active step in progress)
  • paused (awaiting human input)
  • escalated (Chris / counsel involvement)
  • completed-booked (success — demo on calendar)
  • completed-nurturing (low-fit; routed to long-cycle)
  • completed-declined (contact said no)
  • failed (technical / unrecoverable error)

Metrics aggregated per chain

  • Total duration (lead-in to demo-booked)
  • Cost per chain ($)
  • Per-step latency + cost
  • Conversion rate per step (% advancing to next)
  • Human-review interventions per chain

Surfaced in:

  • ghl-continuous-improvement (weekly CI)
  • analytics-architect dashboard
  • PMO board (for chains-in-flight visibility)

Eval coverage

Per-agent eval suites

Each specialist has its own eval cases per its SKILL.md (_Logs/eval-suites/<agent>/).

Chain integration evals

End-to-end scenarios stored in _Logs/eval-suites/chains/lead-to-booked-demo/:

  1. Happy path — hot tier hormone clinic → research → outreach → compliance pass → CRM update → reply yes → booked
  2. Soft tier — routed to nurture (chain ends at step 1)
  3. Competitor flag during research → escalated to Chris
  4. Compliance edit-required → loops back to outreach-writer → re-draft → compliance pass → continues
  5. Reply objection ("too expensive") → outreach-writer objection-handling → compliance → re-send
  6. Three non-replies → routed to nurture
  7. VIP customer → expedited research + AM-assigned booking
  8. Cooldown violation attempt → blocked at pre-flight
  9. DNC list contact → blocked at pre-flight
  10. Counsel-required claim → paused at compliance step

Run cadence

  • Per chain run: automatic per-step eval
  • Weekly: full integration eval suite vs production
  • Monthly: chain-quality report to Chris

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Step 1 (lead-scoring) crashes Retry once; if fails again → flag contact for manual review
Step 2 research times out Return partial dossier + low-confidence flag; chain continues with caveat
Step 3 outreach can't self-check pass Re-draft once; if still fails → human review
Step 4 compliance blocked Chain stops; reason logged; contact tagged
Step 5 GHL workflow trigger fails Retry once; if fails → alert engineering + tag for manual send
Step 5 contact replies during processing Pause automated chain; route reply to AM
Step 6 calendar unavailable for proposed times Generate 3 new proposed times + retry
Step 7 brief delivery fails Retry email; if fails → SMS notify call-taker + manual generation
Chain detected stuck > 7 days at any step Auto-escalate to AM for manual unstick

Approval gates summary

Gate Step Who What
Hot tier crossing 1 AM + Christo notified (informational) None blocking
Competitor / FDA-attention flag 2 Chris Approves continue OR chain stops
New claim attempt 3 Compliance-officer Human review required
Novel-mechanism claim 4 Counsel Approves before send
Public-statement-flagged content 4 Chris Approves
VIP contact booking 6 AM Confirms time before booking
Tier-1 partner 6 Chris Confirms time

Cost + latency targets

Metric Target
Cost per chain run < $1.00 (most chains $0.30-0.60)
Total chain latency (lead-in to outreach-sent) < 5 min async
Total chain duration (lead-in to demo-booked) 24-72 hours typical
Human-review intervention rate < 15% of chains
Auto-approval rate at compliance step > 80%

If actuals drift from targets > 2 weeks running → ghl-continuous-improvement proposes adjustment.

Related

  • _Templates/micro-agent.template.md (template every specialist uses)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/micro-agent-team-builder/SKILL.md (meta-pattern)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/lead-scoring/SKILL.md (step 1)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/clinic-research-agent/SKILL.md (step 2)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/outreach-writer/SKILL.md (step 3)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/compliance-officer/SKILL.md (step 4)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/workflow-automation-builder/SKILL.md (step 5)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ghl-calendar-builder/SKILL.md (step 6)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/sms-campaign-builder/SKILL.md (step 6 — reminders)
  • _Skills Library/KB-Skills/discovery-call-prep/SKILL.md (step 7)
  • _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (pipeline + workflow IDs)
  • _Shared Context/alm-dt-platform.md (credibility references for outreach)

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris — initial chain spec per Christo's definition (Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff)

workflows.name: Workflow: Marketing Campaign (end-to-end)
workflows.description: Standard chain for running a marketing campaign — from theme decision through publish through measurement. Composes content / sales / GHL / voice / analytics agents into one continuous flow.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Marketing Campaign (end-to-end)

Standard chain for running a marketing campaign — from theme decision through publish through measurement. Composes content / sales / GHL / voice / analytics agents into one continuous flow.

When this workflow fires

  • "Run a campaign on [topic] for [audience]"
  • "Launch the [offer] campaign"
  • "Spin up the marketing push for [event / theme]"
  • Manual invocation by Chris after a strategic decision

Agent chain

Phase 1: Strategy + Foundation
  Step 1: market-research (if new ICP segment or audience)
     → ICP + pain + competitive position
     → Skip if existing-segment campaign
     ↓
  Step 2: offer-positioning (if new offer or repositioning)
     → Offer Brief
     → Skip if existing offer
     ↓
  Step 3: sales-strategy (if new sales motion needed)
     → Sales playbook
     → Skip if motion already exists
     ↓
  Step 4: conversation-channel-router
     → Pick the right channels per audience
     → Voice / email / SMS / social / paid mix
     ↓
  Step 5: content-director
     → Build the campaign-specific calendar
     → Tie themes to offer + audience + sales motion
     → Plan repurposing across channels
     → Approval gate: Chris approves themes + calendar

Phase 2: Asset Production
  Step 6: creative-director
     → Visual system brief (palette, typography, imagery)
     → Hand off to executors
     ↓
  Step 7: Per-channel asset creation (PARALLEL)
     ├── 7a. copywriting (long-form, landing pages, ads, emails)
     ├── 7b. cold-email-sequence (outbound email)
     ├── 7c. sms-campaign-builder (SMS — if A2P live)
     ├── 7d. branded-carousel-generator (LinkedIn/IG carousels)
     ├── 7e. ghl-funnel-builder (landing pages — GHL native if quick)
     ├── 7f. frontend-dev (landing pages — Next.js if flagship)
     ├── 7g. voice-script-author (if voice campaign — outbound list, scripts)
     └── 7h. slide-deck-builder / explainer-graphic (visuals)
     ↓
  Step 8: compliance-qa
     → Every asset reviewed for banned phrases, claims, disclaimers
     → Block 🔴 findings
     → Approval gate: Chris approves any 🟡 with mitigations

Phase 3: Distribution Setup
  Step 9: ghl-crm-architect
     → Confirm pipeline + tags for campaign outcomes exist
     → Add campaign-specific tags (`campaign:<slug>`)
     ↓
  Step 10: ghl-form-builder (if forms involved)
     → Build / verify catalog request, registration, NPS forms
     → Wire to right pipeline + workflow + tags
     ↓
  Step 11: workflow-automation-builder
     → Wire campaign-specific workflows in GHL
     → Welcome sequence, nurture, abandon-cart, post-conversion
     → Run via ghl-eval-harness for regression check
     ↓
  Step 12: If voice campaign: voice-ai-sales-agent + outbound-voice-sales.workflow.md
     → Build / configure the voice agent
     → Compliance gates (TCPA, recording disclosure)
     → Eval-harness pass

Phase 4: Launch
  Step 13: security-inspector (if any new code shipped — landing pages, API routes)
     → Pre-launch security gate
     ↓
  Step 14: Approval gate (Chris)
     → Review all assets + workflows + voice agent
     → Authorize launch
     ↓
  Step 15: devops-deployment (if code involved)
     → Push to production
     ↓
  Step 16: Activate
     → Schedule paid ads (Meta / Google)
     → Schedule organic content via social-media-manager (planned)
     → Activate GHL workflows
     → Activate voice campaign (if any)
     → Send first outbound batch

Phase 5: Monitoring + Iteration
  Step 17: Daily monitoring (first 7 days)
     → ghl-daily-pulse surfaces campaign-specific metrics
     → voice-call-reviewer (if voice involved)
     → analytics-architect updates campaign dashboard
     → Auto-pause if KPIs drop sharply OR compliance flag fires
     ↓
  Step 18: Weekly review (during campaign)
     → ghl-continuous-improvement includes campaign performance
     → voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md (if voice)
     → Iteration proposals → ghl-eval-harness / voice-eval-harness
     → Ship approved iterations
     ↓
  Step 19: Campaign close (after campaign window)
     → Final report → _Logs/campaign-results/<campaign>/<date>-final.md
     → Lead-scoring pass on contacts touched
     → account-manager picks up any closed-won customers
     → Lessons fed into next campaign's market-research / content-director input

Standard inputs

  • Campaign name + slug — kebab-case identifier
  • Audience — ICP segment(s)
  • Offer / objective — what we're pitching + success outcome (meetings booked / orders placed / revenue)
  • Channel mix — selected via conversation-channel-router
  • Budget — $ amount + time
  • Campaign window — start date, end date
  • KPI targets — specific numeric goals
  • Compliance flags — HIPAA / A2P / TCPA / state-specific

Standard outputs

  • Campaign brief: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-campaign-brief-<slug>.md
  • Content calendar: _Logs/campaign-results/<slug>/calendar.md
  • Per-asset outputs: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-{copy,carousel,form,landing,script}-<slug>.md
  • Daily reports: _Logs/campaign-results/<slug>/<date>.md
  • Final report: _Logs/campaign-results/<slug>/final-<date>.md

Approval gates (5)

  1. After Step 5 — Content themes + calendar approved
  2. After Step 8 — Compliance pass on all assets approved
  3. After Step 14 — Pre-launch comprehensive review approved
  4. After Step 16 — Daily KPI auto-pause may trigger Chris approval before resume
  5. After Step 19 — Final report + lessons-learned reviewed

Estimated time

  • Phase 1 (strategy): 3-5 days (gated by approvals)
  • Phase 2 (assets): 5-15 days depending on scope
  • Phase 3 (distribution setup): 2-5 days
  • Phase 4 (launch): 1-2 days
  • Phase 5 (monitoring): full campaign window + 7 days observation

Total wall-clock for medium campaign: 3-6 weeks.

Auto-pause triggers (Step 17)

If during first 7 days post-launch:

  • Compliance flag fires (claims, TCPA, A2P)
  • Cost per acquisition > 2× target
  • Conversion rate < 50% of target
  • Negative sentiment uptick > 15% on campaign-tagged conversations
  • Auto-pause + alert Chris

Per-channel performance KPIs

Channel Primary KPI Secondary
Cold email Reply rate, meeting-set rate Open rate, unsubscribe rate
SMS Reply rate, opt-out rate Click rate (if links)
Voice (outbound) Meeting-set rate, connect rate Voicemail rate, transfer rate
Voice (inbound) Resolution rate, booking rate Avg call length, NPS
Paid ads (Meta) CTR, CPL (cost per lead), CPM Frequency, audience overlap
Paid ads (Google) CTR, CPL, conversion rate Quality score, impression share
LinkedIn organic Engagement rate, profile views, DMs Connection-acceptance rate
Web chat Conversation-completion rate Lead-capture rate
Landing page Conversion rate Bounce rate, time-on-page

Failure modes + recovery

Phase Failure Recovery
1 ICP unclear / contested Decision Council to settle; pause until clear
2 Asset doesn't pass compliance-qa Loop back to authoring skill; fix; re-review
3 GHL workflow fails eval Loop back to workflow-automation-builder; fix
3 Voice agent fails eval Loop back to voice-script-author / agent-builder
4 Security gate 🔴 Remediate; don't proceed
5 KPI dropped > 15% Day 1 Auto-pause; investigate; re-launch only after fix
5 Compliance violation Stop; investigate; counsel review if material

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Strategy + asset drafts (Phase 1-2)
  • ✅ Compliance-qa passes
  • ✅ Distribution setup (Phase 3 wiring, additive)
  • ✅ Eval-harness regression checks
  • ✅ Pre-flight tests
  • ✅ Daily monitoring + auto-pause triggers
  • ⚠️ Theme approval (Step 5)
  • ⚠️ Final pre-launch approval (Step 14)
  • ⚠️ Auto-pause resume (Step 17)
  • 🚫 Skip compliance-qa
  • 🚫 Skip eval-harness
  • 🚫 Override 🔴 security gate

Roll-up to executives

Every campaign shows in:

  • pmo-operator master board
  • analytics-architect campaign dashboard
  • weekly-review (during campaign + post-campaign)
  • cfo-finance (campaign cost vs revenue generated)
  • Monthly board section (campaigns shipped + ROAS / CAC)

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris (initial workflow definition)

workflows.name: Workflow: Matrix Nexus Engine (Lead Generation
workflows.description: Matrix's named, unified lead-generation engine. Source → Enrich → Route → Convert → Measure. Composed of 4 Nexus specialist agents + 5 downstream specialist chains + cross-cutting observability. Documented in `_Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md`. UI surface: `/nexus` page in ai-command-center.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Matrix Nexus Engine (Lead Generation — End-to-End)

Matrix's named, unified lead-generation engine. Source → Enrich → Route → Convert → Measure. Composed of 4 Nexus specialist agents + 5 downstream specialist chains + cross-cutting observability. Documented in _Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md. UI surface: /nexus page in ai-command-center.

Engine overview

                    ┌────────────────────────────────────┐
                    │     MATRIX NEXUS                   │
                    └────────────────────────────────────┘
                                    │
   ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
   ║                                                            ║
   ║  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   ║
   ║  │  INTAKE LAYER                                       │   ║
   ║  │                                                     │   ║
   ║  │  20+ sources:                                       │   ║
   ║  │  • Inbound (GHL forms, voice qualify, email, chat) │   ║
   ║  │  • Outbound (cold email, voice AI, LinkedIn)       │   ║
   ║  │  • Discovery (scraping, hire signal, funding sig)  │   ║
   ║  │  • Referrals (customer, affiliate, partnership)    │   ║
   ║  │  • Paid (Meta, Google, LinkedIn ads)               │   ║
   ║  │  • Owned media (Peptide Map, dosing guide)         │   ║
   ║  │                                                     │   ║
   ║  │  [1] nexus-source-orchestrator                      │   ║
   ║  │      → dedup, normalize, compliance gate            │   ║
   ║  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   ║
   ║                          ↓                                 ║
   ║  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   ║
   ║  │  ENRICHMENT LAYER                                   │   ║
   ║  │                                                     │   ║
   ║  │  [2] nexus-enrichment-agent                         │   ║
   ║  │      composes:                                      │   ║
   ║  │      - npi-verifier (atomic)                        │   ║
   ║  │      - clinic-research-agent                        │   ║
   ║  │      - lead-scoring (preliminary)                   │   ║
   ║  │      adds: hire / funding / adjacent-service /      │   ║
   ║  │           competitor / content-engagement signals   │   ║
   ║  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   ║
   ║                          ↓                                 ║
   ║  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   ║
   ║  │  ROUTING LAYER                                      │   ║
   ║  │                                                     │   ║
   ║  │  [3] nexus-routing-agent                            │   ║
   ║  │      → decision tree (customer-status, tier,        │   ║
   ║  │        source-channel, signals, flags)              │   ║
   ║  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   ║
   ║                          ↓                                 ║
   ╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
   ║                  DOWNSTREAM CHAINS                         ║
   ║                                                            ║
   ║   • lead-to-booked-demo (P0/P1/P2 by tier)                ║
   ║   • inbound-call-to-handoff (voice continuation)          ║
   ║   • expansion-opportunity (existing customer growth)      ║
   ║   • partnership-process (partner-flavored intake)         ║
   ║   • long-cycle-nurture (lifecycle marketing)              ║
   ║   • retention-automation (churn-risk save plan)           ║
   ║   • am-direct (VIP / PE / chain-affiliate skip-auto)      ║
   ║   • cold-bucket (quarterly re-evaluate)                   ║
   ║                                                            ║
   ╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
   ║                                                            ║
   ║  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   ║
   ║  │  MEASUREMENT LAYER                                  │   ║
   ║  │                                                     │   ║
   ║  │  [4] nexus-conversion-analyst                       │   ║
   ║  │      → per-source ROI, signal quality, trends      │   ║
   ║  │      → weekly Friday 4:30pm digest + quarterly     │   ║
   ║  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   ║
   ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

When this workflow fires

  • Continuous: source-orchestrator pulls / receives leads constantly (per source cadence)
  • Per-lead chain: each lead goes through Source → Enrich → Route within minutes-to-hours
  • Weekly Friday 4:30pm: conversion analyst weekly digest (joins CI cluster after IP at 4:15pm)
  • Quarterly: deep audit + source-mix re-allocation
  • Ad-hoc: manual invocation for specific lead OR campaign post-mortem

Pre-flight (engine readiness)

  • All 4 Nexus specialist agents healthy (eval-harness recent)
  • All downstream chains green (chain-monitor not flagging any)
  • Source registry current in _Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md
  • Compliance gates active (compliance-officer operational)
  • AM territory map current
  • Budget per paid source approved (per cfo-finance)

Per-lead chain (Source → Enrich → Route → Convert)

Step 1: nexus-source-orchestrator

  • Trigger: source-specific (GHL form / voice call / scrape batch / referral / etc.)
  • Action: dedup + normalize + compliance-gate + canonical-shape
  • Output: array of canonical leads with consent + source attribution
  • Handoff: nexus-enrichment-agent
  • Approval: none for routine; new source = Chris

Step 2: nexus-enrichment-agent

  • Trigger: tag nexus:awaiting-enrichment from upstream
  • Action: compose npi-verifier + clinic-research-agent + lead-scoring + signal aggregation
  • Discovery tier order (STRICT — enforced by enrichment-agent Rules):
    • Tier 1 (FREE, primary): Google Places API — name / address / phone / website / hours / rating. Always first.
    • Tier 2 (FREE, fallback): WebFetch /contact /about /team /privacy + WebSearch for emails, news, hire / funding signals.
    • Tier 3 (PAID, last resort): Hunter.io Domain Search + Email Verifier — ONLY when Tiers 1+2 produced no email. Skip entirely if email already populated.
    • Every dossier records *_source per data point. Free-quota guardrails: Google Places ≤ $180/mo, Hunter ≤ 200 credits / batch.
  • Output: full enrichment dossier + preliminary score + signals + flags + handoff_recommendation
  • Handoff: nexus-routing-agent
  • Approval: competitor / FDA-attention flag → Chris before continuing
  • Depth budget: light 2 min / standard 5 min / deep 10 min per lead

Step 3: nexus-routing-agent

  • Trigger: tag enrichment:done
  • Action: decision-tree → target chain + priority + AM
  • Output: chain assignment + priority + AM + reasoning
  • Handoff: one of 8 downstream targets
  • Approval: VIP / AM-direct routing surfaces to AM (notification)
  • Compliance: consent gate verified before any outbound chain

Step 4+: Downstream chain execution

Per assigned chain — see respective workflow file:

  • _Workflows/lead-to-booked-demo.workflow.md
  • _Workflows/inbound-call-to-handoff.workflow.md
  • _Workflows/expansion-opportunity.workflow.md
  • Partnership-process (orchestrated by partnership-agent)
  • Long-cycle-nurture (ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing nurture sequences)
  • Retention-automation (retention-automation skill)
  • AM-direct (human-led; no further auto-flow)
  • Cold-bucket (quarterly re-evaluate)

Weekly measurement cycle

Friday 4:30pm cron — nexus-conversion-analyst

  • Joins CI cluster (after IP CI at 4:15pm)
  • Pulls full week of Nexus + downstream chain data
  • Per-source: volume, ROI, signal quality, conversion per stage, trend
  • Per-chain: entries, completions, bottleneck step, cost
  • Alerts: concentration / stagnation / death / cost-overrun / compliance
  • Recommendations: scale / maintain / improve / cut / test
  • Output: digest to Christo + Chris + sales-strategy

Daily 7am — chain-monitor (already scheduled)

  • Includes Nexus-specific section in digest
  • Stalled Nexus leads (> 24h between steps without progress)
  • Source-level error rates

Quarterly — first Friday of new quarter

  • Full source lifecycle decisions (scale / sunset / test candidates)
  • Budget re-allocation per cfo-finance
  • Source-mix concentration review

Approval gates (engine-wide)

Gate Step Approver
New source onboarding Pre-Step 1 Chris + compliance-officer
Scraping source ToS revalidation Pre-Step 1 (quarterly) compliance-officer
Competitor flag during enrichment Step 2 Chris
FDA-attention flag Step 2 compliance-officer + Chris
VIP / PE / chain-affiliate routing Step 3 AM + Chris
Source-mix shift (budget reallocation) Step 4 measurement Chris + cfo-finance
Sunset a source Quarterly Chris
New candidate source test Quarterly Chris (90-day commit)

Outputs

Per-lead:

  • GHL contact updated with full dossier
  • Assigned to chain with priority + AM
  • Audit trail logged

Per-week:

  • Conversion-analyst digest at _Logs/nexus-improvement/<YYYY-WW>-weekly.md
  • Updated source-roi-history.csv (append-only)

Per-quarter:

  • Deep audit at _Logs/nexus-improvement/<YYYY-Q>-deep-audit.md
  • Source lifecycle decisions actioned

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Source produces malformed data Quarantine batch; alert engineering; manual review
Enrichment source unreachable Continue with partial dossier + quality flag
Compliance gate fails (consent missing) Quarantine + compliance-officer; do not enter pipeline
Routing decision tree ambiguous Human review queue
Downstream chain unavailable Fallback chain + AM notification
Concentration alert (single source > 35%) Surface in weekly + propose source-mix shift
Source death (zero leads 30 days) Sunset evaluation in quarterly review
Cost-overrun on paid source Pause source + cfo-finance review

Cost + latency targets

  • Per-lead end-to-end Source→Routed: < 15 min (standard depth)
  • Per-source weekly intake processing: < 60 min total
  • Conversion-analyst weekly digest: < 90 min
  • Per-lead cost (Nexus layer only, excluding downstream chains): $0.10-0.25
  • Whole-Nexus weekly cost (excluding paid-source ad spend): < $50

Sub-brand-aware operation

Per _Shared Context/sub-brands.md:

Sub-brand Primary Nexus paths
Matrix Advanced Solutions (B2B) Inbound + outbound + voice + referrals + partnerships
MuscleLock (D2C) Paid + social + email lifecycle
Peptide Map (provider education) Content-led inbound + organic + paid education
ClinIQ Outbound + partnerships + conferences + referrals

nexus-routing-agent selects sub-brand context per dossier; downstream chains inherit.

Related

  • _Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md — engine definition
  • _Shared Context/sub-brands.md
  • _Shared Context/ghl-config.md
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md
  • _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/nexus-source-orchestrator/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/nexus-enrichment-agent/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/nexus-routing-agent/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/nexus-conversion-analyst/SKILL.md
  • _Workflows/lead-to-booked-demo.workflow.md (downstream)
  • _Workflows/inbound-call-to-handoff.workflow.md (downstream)
  • _Workflows/expansion-opportunity.workflow.md (downstream)
  • ai-command-center: /nexus page (UI surface)

Last updated

2026-05-11 — initial Matrix Nexus engine spec

workflows.name: Workflow: Monthly Close & CPA Handoff
workflows.description: Executable specialist chain wrapping `_Shared Context/sops/monthly-close-handoff.sop.md`. Drives the 5th-of-month close from trigger to archived close + CPA-approved books + locked `cfo-finance` dashboard.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Monthly Close & CPA Handoff

Executable specialist chain wrapping _Shared Context/sops/monthly-close-handoff.sop.md. Drives the 5th-of-month close from trigger to archived close + CPA-approved books + locked cfo-finance dashboard.

When this fires

  • Cron: 5th business day of each month, 7:00am PT (for prior month)
  • Manual triggers: Pre-quarterly consolidation, pre-investor / board, pre-audit hardening
  • Owner: finance-controller-lead orchestrates; monthly-close-orchestrator drives
  • Avg duration: 4-8 hours coordinated across 2-3 business days; ~90 min Chris time

Chain

[cron trigger 5th business day]
    ↓
[monthly-close-orchestrator]
    ↓ (loads sops/monthly-close-handoff.sop.md, prerequisites check)
[bookkeeping]
    ↓ (Step 1 — generates packet: transactions.csv, p-and-l.csv, balance-sheet.csv, reconciliation.csv, summary.md)
[payments-engine]
    ↓ (Stripe data pull — revenue, refunds, fees, payouts, balance for prior month)
[tax-calc]
    ↓ (Step 5 — sales tax nexus check + collected/remitted reconciliation)
[ar-ap-automation]
    ↓ (AR aging snapshot + AP aging snapshot for packet)
[gross-margin-tracker]
    ↓ (monthly margin view: per-SKU + per-channel + reconciliation to P&L)
[ghl-affiliate-manager]
    ↓ (Step 4 — affiliate commission accrual confirmed for next-month 15th payout alignment)
⚠️ [Chris review — Step 2 SOP] (45-90 min eyeball; spot-check unusual; verify reconciliation)
    ↓
[monthly-close-orchestrator: package CPA handoff packet] (Step 6)
    ↓ (artifacts: P&L draft, Balance sheet draft, Cash flow draft, Reconciliation, Uncategorized, Anomalies, AR aging, AP aging, Affiliate accrual, Sales tax accrual)
⚠️ [Chris approves CPA email — Step 7 SOP first send; subsequent months auto-OK if template unchanged]
    ↓
[CPA receives packet — Step 8 external] (5-10 business day turnaround)
    ↓ (CPA-approved P&L + balance sheet returned)
[monthly-close-orchestrator: apply CPA corrections] (re-runs bookkeeping with corrections if any)
    ↓
⚠️ [Chris approves final numbers — Step 9 SOP]
    ↓
[cfo-finance: lock to dashboard] (Step 9 SOP — trend lines updated; KPI snapshot finalized)
    ↓
[monthly-close-orchestrator: archive] (Step 10 — `_Logs/bookkeeping/<YYYY-MM>/` tagged closed)
    ↓
[finance-continuous-improvement: late-close ticket if applicable] (only if past 6th business day)
    ↓
[chain-monitor: daily digest reflects close-complete]

Observability

  • Every step emits to _Logs/finance/close/<YYYY-MM>/step-<N>-<verifier>.json with timestamp
  • monthly-close-orchestrator maintains canonical state field across steps (in-progress → pending-cpa → cpa-approved → locked → archived)
  • chain-monitor 7am daily digest surfaces close progress + any stalled step
  • Variance > $100 unexplained = blocker logged + step marked blocked
  • Late close (past 6th business day) = finance-continuous-improvement ticket + Chris alert

Approval gates

Gate Approver What's being approved SOP step
⚠️ Pre-CPA review Chris Drafted close packet before CPA send Step 2
⚠️ CPA email send Chris (first send / template change only) CPA comm with subject + targets Step 7
⚠️ Apply CPA corrections Chris Diff between draft and CPA-corrected Post Step 8
⚠️ Lock dashboard Chris Final numbers in cfo-finance dashboard Step 9
🚫 Tax filings CPA only n/a (external) Step 8
🚫 Material write-offs Chris + CPA + counsel n/a exception

Failure handling

Per SOP Failure modes + recovery table:

Failure Recovery in chain
Reconciliation variance > $100 unexplained monthly-close-orchestrator blocks at Step 3; ar-ap-automation + bookkeeping investigate; if blocked > 24h, escalate to Chris
CPA flags additional issues Re-runs bookkeeping with corrections; new packet shipped; re-enters chain at packaging step
Late close (past 5th business day) Continues but finance-continuous-improvement opens process-improvement ticket
Affiliate commission calc error Resolve before payout (15th); re-runs ghl-affiliate-manager
Sales tax remittance missed P0 to Chris + CPA immediately; late-penalty mitigation path
Stripe-vs-bookkeeping variance payments-engine + bookkeeping reconcile; blocks until tied

Eval cases (chain-level, owned by finance-eval-harness)

  1. Happy path — prereqs green, no variances, packet generated, CPA acknowledges, dashboard locks → archived in 3 business days
  2. Variance block — $250 unexplained at Step 3 → chain pauses, investigation triggered, resumes after explanation logged
  3. Affiliate accrual mismatch — projection from ghl-affiliate-manager ≠ accrual entry → blocks until reconciled (must be clean before 15th payout)
  4. New nexus state crossed — tax-calc flags new state → CPA-flag in packet + Chris notification
  5. CPA returns 5 corrections — chain re-enters at bookkeeping apply step → re-packets → re-locks
  6. Late close (past 6th) — chain completes but CI ticket auto-opens
  7. Pre-quarter consolidation — 3 closes run back-to-back; each completes Step 9 before next starts

KPIs tracked (feed kpi-registry.md + finance-continuous-improvement)

  • Close on-time-rate (target: 100% by 5th business day)
  • Days-to-CPA-approval (target: ≤ 7 business days from packet send)
  • Reconciliation variance count > $100 (target: ≤ 1 per close; declining)
  • Affiliate accrual accuracy vs payout actual (target: variance < $50)
  • Chris time per close (target: declining over time as automation matures)

Related artifacts

  • SOP: _Shared Context/sops/monthly-close-handoff.sop.md (canonical procedure)
  • SOP: _Shared Context/sops/affiliate-payout-monthly.sop.md (downstream 15th; depends on accrual confirmed here)
  • Skills:
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/monthly-close-orchestrator/SKILL.md (chain driver)
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/finance-controller-lead/SKILL.md (orchestrator)
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/bookkeeping/SKILL.md
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/cfo-finance/SKILL.md
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/payments-engine/SKILL.md
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/tax-calc/SKILL.md
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ar-ap-automation/SKILL.md
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/gross-margin-tracker/SKILL.md
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ghl-affiliate-manager/SKILL.md
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/finance-continuous-improvement/SKILL.md
    • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/finance-eval-harness/SKILL.md
  • Logs: _Logs/finance/close/<YYYY-MM>/

Downstream consumers

  • quarterly-review.workflow.md (consumes 3 closed months for quarterly view)
  • affiliate-payout-monthly.sop.md (must run after close clean — 15th payout depends on it)
  • cfo-finance (board / investor section pulls from locked dashboard)
  • pmo-operator (risk register update on any P0/P1 from close)

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris (initial workflow; wraps SOP into executable chain)

workflows.name: Workflow: New Customer Onboarding (Specialist Chain)
workflows.description: End-to-end chain from closed-won deal → activated paying customer (first order placed + 30-day milestone hit). Composed primarily from existing specialist agents. Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md` specialist-chain pattern + `kickoff-call-run-of-show.sop.md` + `customer-onboarding` skill.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: New Customer Onboarding (Specialist Chain)

End-to-end chain from closed-won deal → activated paying customer (first order placed + 30-day milestone hit). Composed primarily from existing specialist agents. Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md specialist-chain pattern + kickoff-call-run-of-show.sop.md + customer-onboarding skill.

Chain overview

Closed-won opportunity in GHL
   │
   ▼
[1] customer-onboarding (pre-conditions sweep)
   ↓ (output: credentialing status + BAA + payment-method + readiness flags)
[2] kickoff-call-run-of-show.sop.md (calendar + comm scheduling)
   ↓ (output: kickoff scheduled + reminders queued)
[3] voice-ai-onboarding-agent (Sam) OR Chris hosts (per tier)
   ↓ (output: kickoff completed + customer-defined success captured + 14-day check-in scheduled)
[4] account-manager (AM intro + relationship init)
   ↓ (output: AM-customer relationship live + contact info delivered)
[5] First-order-monitor (sub-routine — watch for first order)
   ↓ (output: first-order placed within 14 days OR escalation)
[6] account-manager (14-day check-in)
   ↓ (output: check-in completed + engagement signals captured)
[7] account-manager (30-day milestone review)
   ↓ (output: milestone hit OR retention-automation triggered)
→ Chain ends; customer transitions to ongoing account-manager cadence

When this workflow fires

  • GHL opportunity moves to closed-won stage
  • Manual trigger for re-onboarding (returning / reactivated customer)
  • White-glove tier customer (Chris hosts personally; chain still runs)
  • Enterprise tier (Chris + specialist hosts; chain still runs)

Pre-flight checks

  • Opportunity is closed-won (not negotiation or earlier)
  • Contact has minimum data: email + phone + organization name + primary contact name
  • customer_type identified — clinic (provider practice) / provider (solo practitioner / med-spa) / pharmacy (503-A/503-B partner pharmacy) / institution (hospital, ASC, research) — drives credentialing path + doc set + tier defaults
  • No active onboarding chain already running for this contact (idempotency)
  • Tier identified (standard / high-touch / white-glove / enterprise)
  • AM assigned (per account-manager skill routing rules)

Note: this is the Matrix master intake — the single canonical chain for ALL customer/partner onboarding. Clinic / provider / pharmacy / institution customers all flow through here; credentialing branches by customer_type (e.g., pharmacy needs NCPDP + DEA + state board; clinic needs NPI + state license + BAA). Education department does NOT duplicate any of this.

Specialist 1: customer-onboarding (pre-conditions sweep)

Role

Verify customer is ready for kickoff. Check credentialing, BAA status, payment method, NPPES verification.

Input

  • contact_id
  • opportunity_id
  • customer_type (clinic / provider / pharmacy / institution)
  • tier (standard / high-touch / white-glove / enterprise)

Rules

  • All pre-conditions must pass OR chain pauses
  • Credentialing branches by customer_type:
    • clinic / provider: NPI (NPPES) + state medical/DO license + DEA (if controlled) + BAA (if PHI surface)
    • pharmacy: NCPDP + state board of pharmacy license + DEA Form 224 (if dispensing controlled) + 503-A/B status + EMPOWER SSOA (if Empower-network) + BAA
    • institution: TIN + accreditation (Joint Commission / similar) + procurement agreement + BAA
  • BAA on file IF customer will access PHI-touching surface
  • Payment method on file (Stripe customer_id valid)
  • Sales notes pulled (discovery context for kickoff prep)
  • Regulatory specs sourced from regulatory-frameworks.md + ~/Projects/pharmacy onboarding/vendors/ (NPPES, DEA, NCPDP, EMPOWER SSOA integration docs)

Tools

  • GHL MCP (read contact + opportunity + custom fields)
  • NPPES API (verify if not recent)
  • Stripe (verify payment method)
  • Read: compliance-rules.md, regulatory-frameworks.md

Output

preconditions_status: ready | partial | blocked
checks:
  - check: string  # e.g., "NPI verified"
    status: pass | fail | n/a
    detail: string
blocked_reason: string | null  # if blocked
ready_for_kickoff_at: ISO timestamp | null  # when ready
sales_context_for_kickoff:
  pain_points_mentioned: array
  use_cases_discussed: array
  pricing_discussed: object
  decision_criteria: array
  champion: string  # who advocated internally

Handoff

  • If ready → kickoff-call-run-of-show.sop.md (calendar)
  • If partial → tag + AM resolves blocker (e.g., chase BAA signature) → resume chain
  • If blocked → AM + Christo notified; chain pauses

Approval gate

  • White-glove / enterprise tier → Chris confirms readiness before scheduling

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/01-preconditions/<contact_id>.json

Specialist 2: kickoff-call-run-of-show.sop.md (scheduling)

Role

Schedule kickoff call. Send invite. Queue T-24h and T-1h SMS reminders. Prep brief for host.

Input

  • contact_id + ready-state (passthrough)
  • Tier (drives duration: 30 / 60 / 90 min)
  • Host assignment:
    • Standard tier → voice-ai-onboarding-agent (Sam persona)
    • High-touch tier → AM
    • White-glove tier → Chris
    • Enterprise tier → Chris + specialist (rotate per dept)

Rules

  • Per kickoff-call-run-of-show.sop.md
  • Customer's timezone respected
  • Calendar invite sent + acceptance tracked
  • Reminders queued
  • Pre-call brief generated for host (using discovery-call-prep style)

Tools

  • GHL calendar MCP
  • ghl-calendar-builder skill
  • sms-campaign-builder (reminders)
  • discovery-call-prep (brief generation — adapted for kickoff)

Output

kickoff_scheduled:
  appointment_id: string
  appointment_time: ISO
  duration_min: number
  host_assigned: string
  channel: voice / zoom / phone
  customer_acceptance: confirmed | pending | reschedule-needed
reminders_queued:
  - time: ISO
    channel: SMS / email
    template_id: string
pre_call_brief_path: string  # _Logs/kickoff-briefs/<contact_id>.md

Handoff

  • On confirmed time → wait for kickoff time → voice-ai-onboarding-agent (or human host)
  • On no-show → reschedule via auto-flow (per kickoff SOP failure mode)
  • If 2nd no-show → escalate to AM call

Approval gate

  • White-glove / enterprise: Chris confirms time slot
  • Other tiers: auto-scheduled

Specialist 3: voice-ai-onboarding-agent (Sam) OR human host

Role

Host the kickoff call. 10-step run-of-show per kickoff-call-run-of-show.sop.md. Capture customer-defined success verbatim.

Input

  • Pre-call brief
  • contact_id
  • All prior chain context

Rules

  • Per voice-ai-onboarding-agent SKILL.md (Sam persona)
  • For human-hosted (white-glove / enterprise): per kickoff-call-run-of-show.sop.md
  • Customer-defined success MUST be captured verbatim
  • Clinical questions → disclaim + route to clinical team (per SOP)
  • No clinical advice / no pricing changes mid-call

Tools

  • Voice MCP (Retell — Sam persona)
  • OR Zoom / phone (human-hosted)
  • GHL conversations (real-time note capture)
  • compliance-officer on-call (for any compliance question mid-call)

Output

kickoff_completed:
  actual_duration_min: number
  host_actual: string
  call_recording_path: string
  transcript_path: string
customer_defined_success:
  patient_outcome_wanted: string  # verbatim
  clinic_outcome_wanted: string   # verbatim
  30_day_success_definition: string  # verbatim
catalog_access_confirmed: boolean
am_contact_delivered: boolean
14_day_checkin_scheduled: boolean
14_day_checkin_id: string
follow_up_items:
  - item: string
    owner: customer | AM | host
    due: ISO
flags:
  - flag: string  # e.g., "clinical-question-raised", "pricing-concern", "stakeholder-add"

Handoff

  • All completions → account-manager (AM intro + post-call follow-up)
  • If clinical question flagged → clinical-lead callback queue
  • If pricing concern flagged → Chris follow-up (don't negotiate mid-kickoff)

Approval gate

  • None (call is the gate itself; customer participated)

Specialist 4: account-manager (AM intro)

Role

Send AM intro comm + post-call recap. Solidify relationship. Confirm 14-day check-in.

Input

  • Kickoff completion record
  • AM assignment

Rules

  • Per account-manager SKILL.md
  • Recap email within 4 hours of kickoff (auto-fires for Sam-hosted; manual for Chris-hosted)
