  • Includes: AM contact, catalog login link, dosing-guide link, 14-day check-in confirmation
  • SMS: brief follow-up acknowledging the call
  • White-glove: include AM direct cell

Tools

  • GHL MCP (send via approved template)
  • cold-email-sequence (post-kickoff template MAS — kickoff recap)
  • sms-campaign-builder

Output

post_kickoff_comms_sent: boolean
am_introduction_delivered: boolean
relationship_state: active
14_day_checkin_confirmed: boolean

Handoff

  • Always → First-order-monitor sub-routine (watch for first order)

Approval gate

  • None for standard / high-touch templates
  • White-glove custom note → Chris approval

Specialist 5: First-order-monitor (sub-routine)

Role

Watch for first order from this customer. Trigger interventions if no order within thresholds.

Input

  • contact_id
  • kickoff_completed_at

Rules

  • Monitor active orders in ecommerce-backend for contact
  • Threshold 1: 7 days post-kickoff without order → AM nudge (gentle)
  • Threshold 2: 14 days → AM call (per 14-day check-in already scheduled)
  • Threshold 3: 30 days → flag for retention-automation (activation failure)

Tools

  • GHL MCP (monitor orders)
  • Stripe (verify payment captured)
  • account-manager skill (for nudges)
  • retention-automation (if 30-day threshold)

Output

first_order_status: placed-day-N | not-yet | flagged-30d
first_order_id: string | null
first_order_amount: number | null
first_order_categories: array  # which Matrix product categories
intervention_triggered: none | 7d-nudge | 14d-call-emphasis | 30d-retention-flag

Handoff

  • If placed within 14 days → continue to 14-day check-in step (already scheduled)
  • If 30-day flag → retention-automation (activation-failure save plan) + AM personal call

Approval gate

  • None (sub-routine monitoring only)

Specialist 6: account-manager (14-day check-in)

Role

Conduct the 14-day check-in. Per account-manager skill standard cadence.

Input

  • Customer-defined success (from kickoff)
  • First-order details (if placed)
  • All prior chain context

Rules

  • Compare current state vs customer-defined success
  • Surface any blockers
  • Confirm catalog use, COA access, support pathways understood
  • Schedule 30-day milestone review

Output

checkin_completed: boolean
checkin_summary: string
on_track_vs_customer_defined_success: yes / partial / no
blockers_identified: array
30_day_review_scheduled: boolean
30_day_review_id: string
engagement_signals:
  catalog_logins: number
  orders_placed: number
  support_contacts: number
  nps_taken: boolean / score

Handoff

  • On-track → 30-day milestone review (already scheduled)
  • Partial / blockers → AM resolves + may invoke specific support skills
  • NPS ≥ 9 + signals strong → review-collection skill flags for testimonial ask (after milestone)

Approval gate

  • None

Specialist 7: account-manager (30-day milestone review)

Role

Final chain step. Confirm customer is activated + healthy. Hand off to ongoing account-manager cadence.

Input

  • 14-day check-in record
  • Full chain context

Rules

  • Per account-manager milestone review standard
  • Customer status determined: activated-healthy / activated-needs-attention / not-activated
  • Transition to ongoing AM cadence (no longer in onboarding chain)
  • Surface any pattern for ghl-continuous-improvement

Output

milestone_30d_outcome: activated-healthy | activated-needs-attention | not-activated | churned
customer_state_transition: onboarding → active-customer
ongoing_cadence_initialized: boolean
expansion_opportunities_identified: array
churn_risk_signals: array
chain_end: true

Handoff

  • On activated-healthy → chain ends; ongoing AM cadence per account-manager SKILL
  • On activated-needs-attention → AM accelerated cadence
  • On not-activated → retention-automation save plan
  • On churned → exit + log learnings

Approval gate

  • None (chain end)

Chain-wide observability

State + tags

  • GHL contact tag: chain:onboarding:<status>:<step-N>
  • Status enum: running / paused-preconditions / paused-rescheduling / kickoff-completed / first-order-watch / 14d-checkin / 30d-milestone / completed-activated / completed-failed-activation / escalated
  • Custom fields: onboarding_started_at, onboarding_completed_at, customer_defined_success, first_order_at, 30d_outcome

Logs

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/<step-N>-<agent>/<contact_id>.json
  • _Logs/customer-onboarding/<contact_id>/ — full customer onboarding history

Metrics

  • Time from closed-won to kickoff scheduled
  • Time from kickoff to first order
  • 14-day check-in completion rate
  • 30-day activation rate
  • Customer-defined success captured rate (target 100%)
  • NPS at 30-day milestone

Surfaced in:

  • chain-monitor daily digest
  • ghl-continuous-improvement weekly Friday
  • account-manager cohort health dashboard

Eval coverage

Stored in _Logs/eval-suites/chains/new-customer-onboarding/:

  1. Standard tier happy path → Sam kickoff → first order day 5 → activated-healthy at 30 days
  2. Pre-conditions blocked (BAA missing) → AM resolves → chain resumes
  3. White-glove tier → Chris hosts → all comms manual
  4. No-show kickoff → reschedule → second attempt
  5. 2nd no-show → escalate to AM call
  6. Clinical question mid-kickoff → flagged + routed to clinical-lead
  7. Pricing concern raised mid-kickoff → flagged + Chris follow-up
  8. No first order by day 30 → retention-automation triggered
  9. NPS = 10 + strong engagement → review-collection flag
  10. Customer churn during onboarding → exit + learnings logged

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Pre-conditions blocked AM works to resolve; chain pauses; resume when ready
Kickoff scheduling conflicts Try 3 alternative times; if all rejected → AM call to align
Sam voice agent fails mid-call Human AM joins; recording continues; brief regenerated
14-day check-in customer non-responsive 2 follow-up attempts; if silent → flag retention-risk
First order never placed by 30 days Trigger retention-automation activation-failure path
Customer requests cancellation mid-chain Honor immediately; route to retention-automation save plan or clean exit
Chain detected stalled > 7 days at any step Auto-escalate to AM for manual unstick (via chain-monitor)

Approval gates summary

Gate Step Who
White-glove / enterprise readiness 1 Chris
White-glove / enterprise kickoff time 2 Chris
Custom white-glove follow-up note 4 Chris
Retention save plan triggered 5 (if 30-day no-order) AM lead + Chris if material
Customer-cancel mid-chain Any step AM + Chris

Cost + latency targets

  • Total chain cost: < $0.50 (most steps are existing-skill compositions + voice call cost)
  • Time from closed-won → kickoff scheduled: < 48 hours
  • Time from closed-won → first order placed: < 14 days (target 80%)
  • Time from closed-won → 30-day activation: 30 days exact

Related

  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/micro-agent-team-builder/SKILL.md (meta-pattern)
  • _Templates/micro-agent.template.md
  • _Shared Context/sops/kickoff-call-run-of-show.sop.md (host SOP)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/customer-onboarding/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/account-manager/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/voice-ai-onboarding-agent/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/retention-automation/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/review-collection/SKILL.md (NPS-triggered)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/chain-monitor/SKILL.md

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris — composed entirely from existing specialist agents

workflows.name: Workflow: New Hire → Productive (end-to-end)
workflows.description: The tight chain from offer-signed → productive milestone for any Matrix employee. Orchestrated by `hr-lead`. Branches at Day 2 by role: sales reps go to Education's `sales-rep-certification-manager`; everyone else goes to `training-builder`.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: New Hire → Productive (end-to-end)

The tight chain from offer-signed → productive milestone for any Matrix employee. Orchestrated by hr-lead. Branches at Day 2 by role: sales reps go to Education's sales-rep-certification-manager; everyone else goes to training-builder.

Distinction from new-hire-ramp.workflow.md

  • new-hire-ramp.workflow.md covers the broader hire pipeline (open req → applicants → interviews → offer). It includes recruiting + interview design.
  • This workflow (new-hire-to-productive) covers post-offer-acceptance through productive milestone. It is the operational chain that hr-lead runs after the offer is signed. It is composed by new-hire-onboarding-coordinator.

When this workflow fires

  • Offer signed by candidate + Chris (countersigned)
  • Re-hire (returning employee — expedited path)
  • Role change (internal promotion or lateral)
  • Cohort kickoff (batch of new hires starting same day)

Pre-flight

  • Offer fully signed (candidate + Chris)
  • Counsel reviewed (if exec / complex structure)
  • Start date confirmed in writing
  • Manager identified + briefed
  • For sales reps: cohort capacity confirmed with sales-rep-certification-manager
  • For non-sales: training-builder has role-specific path ready

Agent chain

Phase 0: Offer Accepted (T-7 to T-0)
  Step 1: offer-package-builder → status: SIGNED
     → Triggers chain
     → Hand off to new-hire-onboarding-coordinator
     ↓
  Step 2: new-hire-onboarding-coordinator (pre-start)
     → Prep paperwork pack (I-9, W-4, NDA, IP assignment via `_Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md`)
     → Coordinate counsel review of IP assignment if exec / sensitive role
     → Approval gate: Chris approves IP assignment send
     → Send paperwork to candidate
     → Coordinate IT for system provisioning prep
     → Notify manager: prep Day 1 schedule
     ↓
Phase 1: Day 1 (T-0)
  Step 3: new-hire-onboarding-coordinator
     → Verify paperwork complete (I-9 Section 1 minimum; NDA + IP signed)
     → Trigger provisioning:
       - GHL seat (sales rep → `rep:training-only` tag ONLY; NEVER `rep:active`)
       - Google Workspace account
       - Slack invite
       - 1Password vault grant
       - Role-specific tools
     → Compliance: I-9 Section 2 deadline tracked (3 business days)
     → Welcome + Day 1 schedule with manager
     → Run `compliance-officer` on welcome comm
     ↓
Phase 2: Day 2 — Role Branch
  ┌─────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────┐
  │ IF role_track = sales-rep   │ ELSE (all other roles)      │
  ├─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┤
  │ Step 4a: new-hire-onboarding│ Step 4b: new-hire-onboarding│
  │   -coordinator              │   -coordinator              │
  │   → Confirm cohort with     │   → Hand off to             │
  │     sales-rep-certification │     training-builder        │
  │     -manager                │   → Role-specific 30/60/90  │
  │   → Education dept OWNS     │     curriculum begins       │
  │     from this point         │   → Manager owns reviews    │
  │   → HR coordinates timing,  │   → HR retains              │
  │     never makes cert calls  │     coordination role       │
  │                             │                             │
  │ Step 5a: sales-rep-         │ Step 5b: training-builder   │
  │   certification-manager     │   → Role-specific           │
  │   → Cohort enrollment       │     curriculum:             │
  │   → Onboarding module       │     - Engineer: stack       │
  │   → Product knowledge       │       ramp + first PR       │
  │   → Compliance check        │     - Designer: brand       │
  │     (zero critical-fail)    │       + first asset         │
  │   → Roleplay scorecards     │     - AM: account           │
  │     (avg ≥ 80%)             │       shadow + first call   │
  │   → Full call sim (≥ 85%)   │     - Ops/Support/Mkt:      │
  │   → Manager signoff         │       role-specific         │
  │                             │                             │
  │ Step 6a: APPROVAL GATE      │ Step 6a: APPROVAL GATE      │
  │   Cert PASS/FAIL decision   │   30-day milestone:         │
  │   = Education's call        │   manager + HR check        │
  │   (sales-rep-cert-mgr)      │                             │
  │   → If PASS:                │                             │
  │     - Education sets         │                             │
  │       `rep:active` tag      │                             │
  │     - Live-lead access on   │                             │
  │     - HR re-enters for      │                             │
  │       performance cadence   │                             │
  │   → If FAIL:                │                             │
  │     - Remedial track        │                             │
  │     - HR coordinates retake │                             │
  └─────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┘
       ↓ (merge — both branches converge)
Phase 3: Day 30/60/90 Milestones
  Step 7: new-hire-onboarding-coordinator (Day 30 check)
     → Paperwork: all complete (I-9 Section 2 verified)
     → Provisioning: all access working
     → Cohort or training: on track
     → Manager 1:1 status: green / yellow / red
     → If yellow/red → coaching plan triggered via `performance-management`
     ↓
  Step 8: new-hire-onboarding-coordinator (Day 60 check)
     → Mid-ramp review: hitting role-specific milestones
     → Re-align if drift; escalate to `hr-lead` if concern
     ↓
  Step 9: new-hire-onboarding-coordinator (Day 90 check)
     → APPROVAL GATE: Productive milestone
     → Manager + `hr-lead` co-sign:
       PRODUCTIVE | EXTENDED-RAMP | AT-RISK
     → If PRODUCTIVE:
       - Hand off to `performance-management` (quarterly cadence)
       - First quarterly review scheduled at Q-end
       - Trigger `employee-engagement-pulse` cohort tracking
     → If EXTENDED-RAMP:
       - 30-day extension with specific plan via `training-builder`
       - Re-check at Day 120
     → If AT-RISK:
       - Coaching plan + `performance-management` PIP eval
       - Counsel involvement if PIP path
     ↓
Phase 4: Handoff Complete
  Step 10: new-hire-onboarding-coordinator
     → Log to `_Logs/personnel/<slug>/onboarding/complete.md`
     → Final handoff to `performance-management` (steady-state)
     → Notify `hr-lead`, manager, employee
     → Onboarding closed; performance cadence begins

Approval gates (explicit)

Gate Decision Owner Lane
Offer signed Final offer countersigned Chris 🚫 (only Chris signs)
IP assignment send Counsel-reviewed template send Chris ⚠️
System access grant Paperwork complete check new-hire-onboarding-coordinator (auto) ✅
Sales rep cert PASS/FAIL Graduation gate decision sales-rep-certification-manager (Education) ⚠️ (Education owns; HR coordinates only)
rep:active tag set Live-lead access enabled sales-rep-certification-manager (Education) 🚫 (HR cannot set this tag)
Day 90 productive milestone Productive / extended / at-risk Manager + hr-lead co-sign ⚠️
PIP path At-risk → PIP Chris + counsel 🚫 (counsel mandatory)

Observability

  • State location: _Logs/personnel/<slug>/onboarding/
  • PII discipline: pii-redactor on all cross-team distribution
  • Chain telemetry: chain-monitor daily 7am digest tracks open onboarding chains
  • Specialist outputs: per-skill output schemas (see each SKILL.md OUTPUT block)
  • KPI feed: hire-to-productive cycle time → hr-continuous-improvement Friday 6pm

Eval cases

  1. Standard sales-rep hire → paperwork + provisioning Day 1 → Education cert track Day 2-21 → PASS + activate → Day 30 first solo calls → Day 90 productive → handoff to performance-management
  2. Engineer hire → paperwork Day 1 → training-builder engineering ramp → Day 30 first PR shipped → Day 60 owning a feature → Day 90 productive
  3. Designer hire → paperwork + Figma access Day 1 → training-builder design ramp → Day 90 productive with 3 shipped assets
  4. Sales rep cert FAIL → remedial track + retake within 14 days → second attempt PASS → productive timeline extended
  5. Engineer Day 30 yellow → coaching plan via performance-management → Day 60 back on track
  6. Re-hire returning sales rep → expedited paperwork + Education's expedited cert path → activated within 7 days
  7. Role change (existing AM → Director) → no I-9, IP already signed; provisioning refresh + new training-builder path
  8. Remote hire new state → compliance-officer runs state-specific employment-law check at provisioning
  9. IP assignment delayed by counsel → block system access; escalate to hr-lead; do NOT release access
  10. Day 90 at-risk → manager + hr-lead + Chris + counsel evaluate PIP path
  11. Sales rep critical-fail in compliance check → AUTO FAIL + compliance-officer escalation → remedial training; HR coordinates timing
  12. Cohort kickoff (3 reps same day) → batch paperwork + cohort enrollment in Education → cohort-level CI feedback

Failure handling

Failure Response
I-9 Section 2 missed Day 3 P0 compliance alert → immediate remediation + compliance-officer
IP assignment refused by employee Block access + escalate to hr-lead + counsel
System provisioning failure Manager + IT escalation; offline manual access until resolved
Sales rep cert fail × 3 Manager 1:1 + targeted remedial; if 4th fail → terminate hire per education-program.md
Day 90 at-risk → PIP Counsel mandatory; performance-review-builder PIP packet
Engagement red on new-hire cohort employee-engagement-pulse → investigate onboarding + manager + training
Manager unavailable at Day 30 check Reschedule within 5 business days; flag to hr-lead

Composed by

  • hr-lead (orchestrator)
  • Triggered by offer-package-builder on offer-signed event

Composes

  • new-hire-onboarding-coordinator (main driver across phases)
  • compliance-officer (employment-law gates)
  • pii-redactor (PII handling)
  • sales-rep-certification-manager (Education dept — sales rep branch)
  • training-builder (non-sales branch)
  • performance-management (Day 90+ handoff)
  • employee-engagement-pulse (cohort tracking)
  • pmo-operator (chain tracking)
  • chain-monitor (telemetry)

Reads

  • _Templates/ip-assignment-agreement.md
  • _Templates/handoff-template.md
  • _Shared Context/approval-rules.md
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md
  • _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (seat provisioning)
  • _Shared Context/education-program.md (cert gate context)

Cross-references

  • Upstream: new-hire-ramp.workflow.md (broader hire pipeline)
  • Sibling: sales-rep-onboarding-certification.workflow.md (Education-side cert detail)
  • Downstream: quarterly-review.workflow.md (performance review cadence picks up Day 90+)
  • NOT this workflow: new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md (customers — different scope entirely)

One-liner reminder

Offer-signed → paperwork + provisioning Day 1 → Day 2 role-branch (sales rep to Education cert / other to training-builder) → 30/60/90 milestones → Day 90 productive → handoff to performance-management. HR coordinates; Education certifies; manager + Chris decide.

workflows.name: Workflow: New Hire Ramp (end-to-end)
workflows.description: Standard chain from job-post-open through 90-day-review for any new hire. Composes recruiting → interview → onboarding → training → performance-management.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: New Hire Ramp (end-to-end)

Standard chain from job-post-open through 90-day-review for any new hire. Composes recruiting → interview → onboarding → training → performance-management.

When this workflow fires

  • "Hire a [role]"
  • "Open the [role] position"
  • "Spin up recruiting for [role]"
  • Triggered by cfo-finance headcount-plan approval

Agent chain

Phase 1: Pre-recruit
  Step 1: Decision to hire
     → Chris approves headcount slot (per cfo-finance forecast)
     → Approval gate: Chris approves the role + comp band
     ↓
  Step 2: interview-scorecard
     → Design scorecard for the role (competencies + questions + work-sample + rubric)
     → Define 30 / 60 / 90 decision criteria upfront
     → Approval gate: hiring team approves scorecard

Phase 2: Recruit
  Step 3: recruiting
     → Author job post (compliance + brand voice)
     → Sourcing strategy
     → Approval gate: Chris approves job post
     ↓
  Step 4: Publish job post
     → LinkedIn, careers page, referral push
     → Sourcing channels per role
     ↓
  Step 5: Applicant screening
     → recruiting skill screens applicants against scorecard
     → Rank candidates
     → Surface top 10 to hiring team

Phase 3: Interview
  Step 6: Phone screen (human-led)
     → 30-min initial screen
     → Brand-alignment + basic fit
     → Approval gate: advance to deeper interview
     ↓
  Step 7: Hiring manager interview (human-led)
     → 60 min, scorecard-driven
     → Top 2 competencies covered
     ↓
  Step 8: Peer interview (human-led)
     → 45 min
     → Different competency + culture
     ↓
  Step 9: Work sample (async, ≤ 4 hours)
     → Per scorecard
     ↓
  Step 10: Founder interview (Chris, human-led)
     → 30-45 min final
     → Ownership / autonomy / Matrix fit
     ↓
  Step 11: Reference checks (HR-led)
     → 2-3 references
     → Confirm signal patterns
     ↓
  Step 12: Calibration debrief
     → All interviewers share scores BEFORE discussing
     → Decide: offer / decline
     → Approval gate: Chris approves offer

Phase 4: Offer + Sign
  Step 13: Offer letter (Chris + counsel review)
     → Draft per comp band approved in Step 1
     → Approval gate: counsel reviews terms
     ↓
  Step 14: Send offer (Chris signs)
     → Candidate has window to decide
     ↓
  Step 15: Offer accepted → start date set
     → OR offer declined → loop back to Step 5

Phase 5: Pre-start (week before)
  Step 16: training-builder
     → Generate 90-day ramp curriculum (per role)
     → Materials curation
     → Approval gate: hiring manager approves curriculum
     ↓
  Step 17: Access provisioning
     → Slack invite + onboarding channels
     → GHL role-scoped access
     → GitHub access (if engineer)
     → Calendar shared with manager
     → Required-reading list sent

Phase 6: Days 1-30 (Foundations + role basics)
  Step 18: Day 1 — Welcome + foundations
     → Company overview meeting (Chris)
     → Tool tour
     → First-week reading list
     → Daily 1:1 with hiring manager
     ↓
  Step 19: Week 1 — Foundations
     → Read all `_Shared Context/` files
     → Shadow customer calls (3-5)
     → End-of-week check-in
     ↓
  Step 20: Week 2 — Role-specific basics
     → Per training-builder curriculum
     → Mock exercises
     → First-week output submission
     ↓
  Step 21: Week 3-4 — Live work with safety net
     → Real tasks, manager review on each
     → Daily debriefs
     ↓
  Step 22: 30-day milestone review
     → Manager + Chris meeting
     → Score against scorecard competencies (1-5 per)
     → Approval gate: continue / coach / part ways
     → Output: 30-day review note in `_Logs/personnel/<hire>/`

Phase 7: Days 31-60 (Independent work)
  Step 23: Weeks 5-8 — Independent work, weekly review
     → Manager weekly 1:1
     → Quality bar: > 80% pass first review
     ↓
  Step 24: 60-day milestone review
     → Score scorecard
     → Approval gate: continue / coach / part ways

Phase 8: Days 61-90 (Full autonomy)
  Step 25: Weeks 9-12 — Full autonomy + metric ownership
     → Own role KPIs
     → Weekly + monthly check-ins
     ↓
  Step 26: 90-day final review
     → Comprehensive scorecard scoring
     → Compare to 90-day decision criteria
     → Approval gate: promote / stay / coach / part ways
     → Output: 90-day decision note

Phase 9: Post-ramp transition to performance-management
  Step 27: performance-management takeover (planned)
     → Quarterly performance reviews ongoing
     → Goals set per role
     → Ramp lessons fed back to training-builder for next hire

Standard inputs

  • Role title + level
  • Reports to (hiring manager)
  • Comp band (approved by Chris + cfo-finance)
  • Start date target

Standard outputs

  • Job post: published + logged
  • Scorecard: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-scorecard-<role>.md
  • Candidate pipeline log: per recruiting
  • Offer letter: per legal
  • Ramp curriculum: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-training-<hire>.md
  • 30 / 60 / 90 review notes: _Logs/personnel/<hire>/30day.md etc.

Approval gates (8)

  1. Step 1 — Hire decision + comp band approved
  2. Step 2 — Scorecard approved
  3. Step 3 — Job post approved
  4. Step 6 — Phone screen advance
  5. Step 12 — Hire / no-hire calibration decision
  6. Step 13 — Offer terms + counsel review
  7. Step 16 — Ramp curriculum approved
  8. Steps 22, 24, 26 — 30/60/90 milestone decisions

Estimated time

  • Phase 1 (pre-recruit): 1 week
  • Phase 2 (recruit): 2-6 weeks (varies by role + market)
  • Phase 3 (interview): 2-4 weeks
  • Phase 4 (offer + sign): 1-2 weeks
  • Phase 5 (pre-start): 1 week
  • Phases 6-8 (ramp): 90 days
  • Total time-to-productivity: 4-6 months from open → fully ramped

Auto-flags (process health)

  • Time-to-fill > 60 days → review job post quality + sourcing
  • 30-day review score < 3 in 2+ competencies → coaching plan
  • 60-day score not improving → consider parting ways
  • 90-day decision: continue rate < 80% → recruiting / scorecard process needs adjustment

Failure modes + recovery

Phase Failure Recovery
2 Few applicants Broaden sourcing + referral push + recruiting fee
3 All applicants 🟡 fit Adjust scorecard OR continue searching OR adjust comp
4 Top candidate declines Backup candidate; loop to Step 5 if no backup
6 Hire ramps poorly 30-day intervention + targeted coaching
8 90-day decline Part ways (with counsel involvement) + feed lessons back to next hire's training

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Author job post + scorecard
  • ✅ Screen applicants
  • ✅ Build training curriculum
  • ✅ Provision access (in role-scoped tools)
  • ⚠️ All 8 approval gates
  • 🚫 Make hire decisions
  • 🚫 Sign offer
  • 🚫 Make termination decisions
  • 🚫 Discriminate on protected class (absolute block; flag immediately)

Roll-up

Hire status surfaces in:

  • pmo-operator master board
  • weekly-review (during ramp)
  • cfo-finance (headcount cost tracked)
  • Quarterly review (hire success rate, time-to-fill)
  • agent-registry.md (when hire takes on agent-related work)

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris (initial workflow)

workflows.name: Workflow: New Product → Curriculum + Resource Center Update
workflows.description: Triggers automatically when a new SKU launches (per `formulation-development.workflow.md` Phase 5 / 6). Ensures Matrix Academy + Resource Center stay current with every product release. Per `_Shared Context/education-program.md`.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: New Product → Curriculum + Resource Center Update

Triggers automatically when a new SKU launches (per formulation-development.workflow.md Phase 5 / 6). Ensures Matrix Academy + Resource Center stay current with every product release. Per _Shared Context/education-program.md.

Chain overview

[1] formulation-lead Phase 6 (SKU launching) → triggers curriculum update
       ↓
[2] education-training-lead receives notification + scope
       ↓
[3] clinical-curriculum-author: identify affected clinical modules + update
       ↓
[4] sales-curriculum-author: per-product module content + new objection patterns
       ↓
[5] quiz-and-assessment-builder: refresh affected questions + new product mini-quiz (for re-cert)
       ↓
[6] resource-center-curator: dosing reference entry + protocol primer + stacking notes + tool updates
       ↓
[7] roleplay-coach-agent: new scenario library entries (product-specific roleplay)
       ↓
[8] compliance-officer: Gate 1 on all new content
       ↓
[9] learner-progress-tracker: identify reps needing re-cert mini-quiz (per material-change rule)
       ↓
[10] sales-rep-certification-manager: triggers per-product re-cert window (14 days)
       ↓
[11] All active reps + providers notified of new content / available resources
       ↓
[12] education-continuous-improvement: tracks adoption + first-pass quiz rates on new content

When this workflow fires

  • formulation-development.workflow.md Phase 6 (launch gate) signals new SKU active
  • Existing SKU reformulation (material change in chemistry / dose / mechanism)
  • Sub-brand launch (e.g., new MuscleLock variant requires sales module + resource center entry)
  • Major protocol introduction (even without new SKU)

Pre-flight

  • New SKU has full data in product-catalog-builder master
  • Compliance class confirmed (formulation-compliance)
  • Label artwork complete (per label-design-and-production.workflow.md)
  • Steve / formulation-lead sign-off on chemistry content for education team
  • Stability + BUD data available

Per-step detail

Step 1: SKU launches (trigger)

  • formulation-lead Phase 6 launch gate complete
  • Tag in product-catalog-builder: status:active
  • Triggers this workflow

Step 2: education-training-lead scope

  • Identify which modules affected (clinical + sales)
  • Assess material change scope (minor SKU variant vs new product category)
  • Estimate effort + deadline (target: new content live within 14 days of SKU launch)
  • Assign work to specialists

Step 3: clinical-curriculum-author updates

  • Affected modules per the new product's clinical implications:
    • Module 02 (intake): contraindications + screening
    • Module 03 (labs): lab gating per new product
    • Module 05 (dosing): dose tables + ranges + storage
    • Module 06 (peptide classes): if new class/category
    • Module 07 (evidence): cite per prior-art-citation-formatter
    • Module 08 (protocols): protocol primer if novel protocol
    • Module 09 (longitudinal): monitoring plan if applicable
  • Per affected module: revise lesson + update flashcards' source

Step 4: sales-curriculum-author updates

  • Per-product knowledge module (mechanism / use case / provider talking points / pricing structure)
  • New objection patterns from objection-handler analytics (pre-launch CRM signals)
  • Sub-brand voice alignment

Step 5: quiz-and-assessment-builder

  • Refresh affected questions per content change
  • Generate 10-question mini-quiz for per-product re-cert
  • Tag questions with new SKU reference
  • Item-analysis baseline established

Step 6: resource-center-curator

  • Dosing reference entry per dosing-reference.md discipline
  • Protocol primer
  • Stacking notes per ClinIQ_Peptide_Supplement_Synergy.csv pattern
  • Storage / handling guide
  • Reconstitution guide (vial products)
  • BUD reference per bud-calculator micro-agent
  • Tool updates (calculator UI if new dose range)

Step 7: roleplay-coach-agent scenarios

  • New product-specific scenarios across all 7 modes
  • Provider-objection scenarios (likely common objections per new SKU)
  • Compliance-check scenarios per new claims library entries
  • MuscleLock-style deep-dive prompt if D2C-applicable

Step 8: compliance-officer Gate 1

  • All new content reviewed
  • Claim discipline confirmed
  • DSHEA disclaimer where applicable
  • Trade-secret discipline confirmed
  • Sub-brand voice respected
  • 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 / ⚠️ decision per content piece
  • 🔴 → block + loop back; 🟡 → required edits; 🟢 → publish

Step 9: learner-progress-tracker re-cert identification

  • Identify all currently-active reps
  • Identify all certified providers (where applicable)
  • Flag for per-product re-cert mini-quiz
  • Schedule re-cert window (14 days)

Step 10: sales-rep-certification-manager

  • Per-product re-cert window opens
  • 10-question mini-quiz required
  • ≥ 85% to maintain access
  • Failed → access pause until retake passed

Step 11: Active rep + provider notifications

  • Reps: LMS notification + email
  • Providers: resource center notification + AM follow-up for key accounts
  • New content discoverable via LMS dashboard + resource center landing

Step 12: education-continuous-improvement tracking

  • Mini-quiz first-attempt pass rates monitored
  • Content adoption metrics (LMS engagement on new content)
  • Resource center page-view + tool-usage growth
  • Customer support tickets traced to new product (gap?) → loop to resource-center-curator

Approval gates

Gate Step Approver
Scope assignment 2 education-training-lead
Material accuracy (chemistry) 3 Steve + formulation-compliance
Sales claims accuracy 4 compliance-officer + sales-strategy
Compliance Gate 1 8 compliance-officer
Per-product re-cert window 10 sales-rep-certification-manager
Resource center publish 6 compliance-officer Gate 1

Failure recovery

Failure Recovery
Compliance 🔴 on new content Block publish; loop to author for fix; re-review
Material chemistry error caught by Steve Halt; correct; re-review
Mini-quiz pass rate < 70% (too hard) quiz-and-assessment-builder revisits question difficulty
Mini-quiz pass rate > 95% (too easy) Same — recalibrate
Rep misses 14-day re-cert window Access pause; manager + cert manager intervention
Resource center content shows error post-launch Hot-fix + audit

Outputs

Per new SKU:

  • Updated clinical modules + sales modules
  • Refreshed question bank entries
  • Resource center entries (dosing + protocol + stacking + storage + tool)
  • New roleplay scenarios
  • Compliance signoff doc
  • Re-cert mini-quiz live + assigned to active learners

Per active rep:

  • Per-product mini-quiz completion record
  • Re-cert status updated

Per cohort:

  • Adoption metrics
  • First-attempt pass rate on new content

KPI targets

  • Time from SKU launch → curriculum + resource center updated: < 14 days
  • Compliance 🟢 first-pass on new content: ≥ 90% (else authors need patterns)
  • Mini-quiz first-attempt pass rate: 70-95% (calibrated)
  • Per-product re-cert completion within window: ≥ 95%

Surfaced in:

  • education-continuous-improvement Friday 5pm
  • chain-monitor daily digest
  • formulation-continuous-improvement (cross-pollinates with product-launch metrics)

Related

  • _Shared Context/education-program.md
  • _Workflows/formulation-development.workflow.md (upstream Phase 6 trigger)
  • _Workflows/sales-rep-onboarding-certification.workflow.md (re-cert ties in)
  • _Workflows/label-design-and-production.workflow.md (parallel artwork chain)
  • 10 education specialists in _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/
  • Matrix Academy LMS at ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/

Last updated

2026-05-11 — initial new-product curriculum + resource center update chain

workflows.name: Workflow: NorCal Outbound Multi-Channel Cadence (Specialist Chain)
workflows.description: **Q2 2026 NorCal lead-gen push — the canonical multi-channel outbound cadence.** Ties voice + email + SMS together into one chain for the three priority verticals (med spa / weight-loss / HRT) across three geo tiers (Bay Area / Sacramento / Wine Country). Downstream of Matrix Nexus (`_Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md`); upstream of `pipeline-to-closed-won.workflow.md`.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: NorCal Outbound Multi-Channel Cadence (Specialist Chain)

Q2 2026 NorCal lead-gen push — the canonical multi-channel outbound cadence. Ties voice + email + SMS together into one chain for the three priority verticals (med spa / weight-loss / HRT) across three geo tiers (Bay Area / Sacramento / Wine Country). Downstream of Matrix Nexus (_Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md); upstream of pipeline-to-closed-won.workflow.md.

Built per the Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff specialist-chain pattern (see _Templates/micro-agent.template.md + micro-agent-team-builder). Mirrors _Workflows/lead-to-booked-demo.workflow.md structure. Voice-first by default per CLAUDE.md Matrix channel defaults; signal-based override to email-first only on explicit signal.

This workflow is the orchestration spec. Per-vertical email body copy, voice scripts, SMS templates, lead magnets, battlecards, objection libraries are produced and maintained by parallel content-owning specialists; this chain composes them.

Chain overview

Prospect imported to GHL (from nexus-source-orchestrator / prospect-list-builder)
   │                tagged `nexus:norcal:<vertical>` + `geo:tier-<1|2|3>` + `chain:norcal-outbound:eligible`
   ▼
[1] clinic-research-agent         ← per-prospect dossier (clinic type, providers, signals, ICP score)
   │
   ▼
[2] conversation-channel-router   ← decide voice-first (default) OR email-first (signal override)
   │
   ├──── voice-first path (default) ────────────────────────────────────┐
   │                                                                    │
   ▼ Day 1                                                              ▼ Day 1 (email-first override)
[3a] voice-ai-sales-agent (Mia)  attempt 1                            [3b] Email 1 (cold intro)
       ├─ connected + interested  → book → call-handoff-orchestrator → discovery-call-prep
       ├─ connected + objection   → live objection-handler → re-route OR callback
       ├─ voicemail left          → tag + fire Email 1 same-day
       └─ gatekeeper / no-answer  → tag + continue cadence
   │
   ▼ Day 1 (if VM left)
[4] Email 1 — cold intro (vertical-specific wedge)
   │
   ▼ Day 3
[5] voice-ai-sales-agent (Mia)  attempt 2
   │
   ▼ Day 5
[6] Email 2 — pattern interrupt
   │
   ▼ Day 8
[7] voice-ai-sales-agent (Mia)  attempt 3
   │
   ▼ Day 12
[8] Email 3 — dosing-guide / lead-magnet bait
   │
   ▼ Day 15
[9] Email 4 — testimonial / proof
   │
   ▼ Day 19
[10] Email 5 — objection preempt
   │
   ▼ Day 22
[11] Email 6 — breakup
   │
   ▼ Day 30+
[12] Reactivation OR archive  ← decision: re-engage with new angle OR move to long-cycle nurture
   │
   ▼ (throughout — parallel branch on any inbound reply)
[R] email-intent-classifier   ← classify inbound reply → route
       ├─ booking-intent          → discovery-call-prep + ghl-calendar-builder
       ├─ objection               → objection-handler (followup) → compliance-officer
       ├─ unsubscribe / DNC       → workflow-automation-builder honor + suppression-list write
       ├─ wrong-person            → workflow-automation-builder update contact + retarget
       ├─ AM-personal-touch       → assign to AM (human takeover; pause automation)
       └─ noise                   → archive + continue cadence
   │
   ▼ (on book — any path)
[B] discovery-call-prep       ← AM-ready brief delivered ≥ 24h pre-call
   │
   ▼ (AM runs discovery; closed-won path)
[X] HANDOFF → pipeline-to-closed-won.workflow.md

Cadence calendar at a glance (per _Shared Context/icp-norcal-q2-2026.md cadence template):

Day Touch Channel Specialist
1 Voice attempt 1 + VM voice voice-ai-sales-agent (Mia)
1 (if VM) Email 1 — cold intro email cold-email-sequence
3 Voice attempt 2 voice voice-ai-sales-agent
5 Email 2 — pattern interrupt email cold-email-sequence
8 Voice attempt 3 voice voice-ai-sales-agent
12 Email 3 — dosing-guide bait email cold-email-sequence
15 Email 4 — testimonial / proof email cold-email-sequence
19 Email 5 — objection preempt email cold-email-sequence
22 Email 6 — breakup email cold-email-sequence
30+ Reactivation OR archive varies ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (nurture)

SMS is NOT in cold cadence. SMS only fires post-opt-in for booking confirmations + reminders (24h + 1h pre-call) and only when A2P 10DLC + TCPA prior-express-written-consent both confirmed. See SMS gate in Specialist B + Approval gates summary.

When this workflow fires

  • Primary: New prospect imported to GHL by nexus-source-orchestrator with tags nexus:norcal:<vertical> + geo:tier-<1|2|3> + chain:norcal-outbound:eligible
  • Manual: AM manually triggers chain for a specific NorCal contact (cert-verified)
  • Re-engagement: Prospect previously archived (≥ 90 days cold) with new signal — re-enters at step 4 (Email 1) with different vertical angle
  • Vertical-specific batch: New cohort assembled (e.g., 100 new Bay Area HRT clinics) — batch-fires through chain with rate-limit
  • Conference follow-up: Post-conference (A4M, AMMG, IFM) — same chain with conference-specific opener variant

Pre-flight checks

Before chain fires, all must pass (chain blocks at pre-flight if any fail):

  • Contact exists in GHL with: email AND phone (both required for multi-channel) + clinic_name + state = "CA"
  • nexus:norcal:<vertical> tag present (med-spa / weight-loss / hrt)
  • geo:tier-<1|2|3> tag present (geographic priority assigned)
  • Lead score ≥ 40 (per lead-scoring rubric; below = soft/cold → routed to nurture instead)
  • Contact NOT on DNC list (per compliance-rules.md)
  • Contact NOT on suppression-list (per prior unsubscribe / wrong-person)
  • No active chain processing this contact (idempotency — don't double-process)
  • No outreach in last 21 days (cooldown enforced)
  • NPI verified (if provider lead — via npi-verifier atomic; soft-fail if clinic-only lead)
  • California state pharmacy / medical board licensure not flagged
  • Tag chain:norcal-outbound:eligible present (explicit gate prevents accidental fire)
  • Compliance flags clear (no chain:blocked-compliance from prior chain)
  • Vertical-specific content readiness verified — voice scripts at _Logs/outreach/voice-scripts/<vertical>/ AND email templates at _Logs/outreach/email-templates/<vertical>/ AND objection library at _Logs/outreach/objections/<vertical>/ are populated and compliance-approved within last 30 days

Specialist 1: clinic-research-agent

Role

(Per _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/clinic-research-agent/SKILL.md.) Per-prospect personalization research: clinic type confirmed, practitioner count, current pharmacy signals (if discoverable), competing-vendor signals, ICP fit score for the assigned vertical, sub-brand alignment.

Input

  • contact_id + assigned vertical tag (med-spa / weight-loss / hrt)
  • GHL contact record (clinic_name, address, phone, website, NPI if present)
  • Lead score from lead-scoring (passthrough)

Rules

  • 5-min time budget per clinic
  • Verify with 3+ sources (NPPES + Google Maps + clinic website minimum)
  • "Unknown" beats fabricated
  • Flag competitor pharmacy signal → embed in dossier for outreach personalization
  • Flag FDA-attention or warning-letter signal → escalate; chain pauses
  • California-specific: verify CA Board of Pharmacy + CA Medical Board status if surfaceable
  • Vertical-specific personalization facts required (med-spa: services offered; WL: GLP-1 program signal; HRT: men's vs women's vs both)

Tools

  • NPPES (free), WebSearch, WebFetch
  • npi-verifier atomic (composed)
  • compliance-phrase-detector atomic (pre-handoff text check on dossier text)
  • GHL MCP (read + write enrichment fields)
  • Anthropic API (Haiku default for speed)

Output

Per clinic-research-agent SKILL.md output schema:

contact_id: string
vertical: enum (med-spa | weight-loss | hrt)
clinic_type: string
services: array
provider_count: number
lead_provider: {name, npi, verified_bool}
geo_tier: enum (1 | 2 | 3)
matrix_product_fit: array of {product, fit_score, why}
sub_brand_alignment: {primary, secondary}
personalization_facts: array (≥ 3, vertical-relevant)
current_vendor_signal: string | null
flags: array ("competitor", "fda-attention", "warning-letter", "pe-backed", "expansion-signal")
handoff_recommendation: enum (continue | pause-for-review | route-to-am-vip | escalate-to-chris)
confidence: number (0-1)
researched_at: ISO timestamp

Handoff

  • If handoff_recommendation == continue → conversation-channel-router
  • If pause-for-review → human review queue + chain status = paused
  • If route-to-am-vip → assign to AM with research dossier + chain status = escalated
  • If escalate-to-chris → Slack ping + chain status = escalated
  • If FDA-attention / warning-letter → chain stops + Chris notified

Approval gate

  • Standard research: none
  • FDA-attention / warning-letter flag: Chris approval before chain continues

Observability

  • _Logs/outreach/chains/<contact_id>/01-clinic-research/dossier.json
  • Metrics: data_points_verified, confidence, time_taken, cost, vertical, geo_tier

Specialist 2: conversation-channel-router

Role

(Per _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/conversation-channel-router/SKILL.md.) Decide first-touch channel: voice-first (Matrix default) OR email-first (signal-based override). Per CLAUDE.md voice-first defaults for clinic-owner audience.

Input

  • clinic-research-agent dossier (full payload)
  • Contact phone + email + DNC status
  • Time-of-day + day-of-week (avoid voice attempts outside business hours per geo tier timezone)

Rules

Default: voice-first (Matrix doctrine per CLAUDE.md — clinic owners answer phones, ignore inboxes).

Override to email-first ONLY when ANY of these signals present:

  • Contact has tag prefers-email (explicit opt-out of voice from prior interaction)
  • Phone validation flagged invalid / disconnected by atomic phone-validator (when wired)
  • Contact role = Office Manager AND no direct-line phone available (only main reception)
  • DNC voice-only (still email-eligible per CAN-SPAM)
  • Geo tier-3 timezone outside business hours at fire-time (queue voice for next business day; send email same-day)
  • Prior chain history shows 3+ unanswered voice attempts without VM landed (efficiency override)

Never override to email-first based on: "felt safer", "seems busy" — voice attempts at scale ARE the strategy.

Tools

  • conversation-channel-router skill (orchestrator)
  • GHL MCP (read tags, history)
  • phone-validator atomic (when available)

Output

contact_id: string
channel_decision: enum (voice-first | email-first)
override_signal: string | null  # why email-first, if applicable
fire_time: ISO timestamp  # when to fire step 3
voice_window: {timezone, start_hour, end_hour}  # geo-tier-aware
fallback_plan: string  # what happens if voice fails

Handoff

  • If voice-first → voice-ai-sales-agent (Mia) — Specialist 3a
  • If email-first → cold-email-sequence Email 1 path — Specialist 3b → resume voice attempt 2 on Day 3

Approval gate

  • None for standard routing
  • New override-signal type proposed: Chris approves

Observability

  • _Logs/outreach/chains/<contact_id>/02-channel-router/decision.json
  • Aggregate: % voice-first vs % email-first per vertical / per geo tier (Friday CI reviews)

Specialist 3a: voice-ai-sales-agent (Mia) — Day 1 outbound attempt 1

Role

(Per _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/voice-ai-sales-agent/SKILL.md.) Outbound cold qualification call. Mia persona per voice-persona-library. Vertical-specific opener + value-prop scripted to dossier facts. Goal: qualified-conversation → discovery-call booking, OR VM left + email triggered.

Input

  • clinic-research-agent dossier (passthrough)
  • Channel-router decision (passthrough)
  • Voice script path: _Logs/outreach/voice-scripts/<vertical>/01-cold-intro-mia.md
  • Vertical-specific objection library: _Logs/outreach/objections/<vertical>/
  • Battlecard for current_vendor_signal if competitor flagged: _Logs/outreach/battlecards/<competitor>/

Rules

  • Mia persona only (per voice-persona-library — single source of truth)
  • 25-30 second voicemail max if no answer
  • Open with personalization fact from dossier (verified, not fabricated)
  • Sub-brand voice = Matrix Advanced Solutions (B2B primary per ICP brief)
  • NEVER use banned phrases: "FDA-approved" (unless literal), "miracle", "cure", "guaranteed weight loss", "safe", "no side effects", "proven", "best", "100%" — pre-checked via compliance-phrase-detector atomic on every script variant
  • One CTA per call: "15-minute discovery call with our specialist"
  • TCPA-discipline: voice cold calls ARE permitted under TCPA without prior-express-consent provided business-to-business + during business hours + not on DNC; document call attempt + outcome
  • California time-of-day: 9am-5pm PT only; never outside business hours
  • 3-attempt cap total across whole cadence (Days 1, 3, 8 voice attempts)
  • Compliance-officer pre-reviewed every script variant; no on-the-fly script generation in production

Tools

  • Voice platform (Retell / Vapi / Synthflow per voice-ai-agent-builder)
  • voice-script-author (script authoring layer — content owned by parallel agents)
  • conversation-flow-designer (flow logic)
  • objection-handler skill (live in-call objection routing)
  • call-handoff-orchestrator (warm transfer to AM if hot)
  • GHL MCP (write call outcome + tags + tasks)
  • compliance-phrase-detector atomic (script pre-check)

Output

call_id: string
contact_id: string
attempt_number: 1
outcome: enum (connected-interested | connected-objection | connected-decline | voicemail | gatekeeper | no-answer | wrong-number | dnc-request)
duration_seconds: number
transcript_path: string
objections_raised: array (if any; per objection-handler taxonomy)
booking_status: enum (booked | callback-scheduled | not-booked)
appointment_id: string | null
handoff_triggered: boolean
tags_applied: array
notes: string
called_at: ISO timestamp

Handoff

  • If connected-interested + booking: → call-handoff-orchestrator → discovery-call-prep (book + warm transfer if AM available NOW)
  • If connected-objection (live): live objection-handler resolves → re-attempts close OR schedules callback (≤ 2 attempts in-call)
  • If connected-decline (hard no): tag outreach:declined-day-1; chain → long-cycle nurture (ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing nurture mode)
  • If voicemail: tag voice:vm-day-1; fire Specialist 4 (Email 1) same-day
  • If gatekeeper / no-answer: tag accordingly; continue cadence (Email 1 fires anyway per design)
  • If wrong-number: tag data:wrong-number; trigger contact-update flow (workflow-automation-builder); pause chain pending data fix
  • If dnc-request: tag dnc:voice; suppression-list write; chain → email-only OR full archive per request specificity; compliance-officer notified

Approval gate

  • Script variant changes: compliance-officer approves before deploy
  • New persona introduction: Chris approves (Mia is the only approved cold-outbound voice persona for Matrix)
  • Live in-call escalation to AM warm transfer: AM availability check via call-handoff-orchestrator (no approval needed at runtime)

Observability

  • _Logs/outreach/chains/<contact_id>/03a-voice-attempt-1/<call_id>.json
  • Metrics: connect_rate, vm_rate, booking_rate, decline_rate, avg_duration, cost_per_call
  • Per-vertical aggregation feeds voice-call-reviewer Friday 3pm CI

SLA: fire within 4 business hours of pre-flight pass

Specialist 4: Email 1 — cold intro (Day 1, if VM left OR email-first override)

Role

First email send. Vertical-specific cold intro. Loads pre-built compliance-approved template + personalization-fill from clinic dossier. Goal: open + reply OR open + click lead-magnet OR open-no-reply (re-targetable).

Input

  • clinic-research-agent dossier

Rules

  • Template pre-approved by compliance-officer within last 30 days
  • All banned phrases pre-scrubbed (compliance-phrase-detector atomic)
  • 60-120 word body
  • Single clear CTA: "Reply YES for our 2026 catalog" OR "15-min discovery call link" (vertical-specific)
  • CAN-SPAM compliant: physical address footer + unsubscribe link + truthful subject line (per compliance-rules.md + regulatory-frameworks.md)
  • Subject line ≤ 50 chars; no spam-trigger phrases ("free", "act now", "guaranteed")
  • Sender: AM-of-record OR rep-of-record (NOT a generic info@ — real person)
  • Personalization fact required (≥ 1 from dossier — verified, not fabricated)
  • 21-day cooldown enforced (won't fire if prior outreach in window)

subject_line: string
sender: {name, email}
sent_at: ISO timestamp
delivery_status: enum (sent | bounced | deferred)
tags_applied: array

Handoff

Approval gate

Specialist 12: Reactivation OR archive (Day 30+)

Role

Decision step. Based on full chain engagement signals (opens, clicks, magnet downloads, voice partial-conversations), decide:

  • Archive: move to long-cycle nurture via ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (quarterly educational drip)

  • Archive (default) for cold-throughout chains
  • Suppress if any hard-decline or DNC signal observed
  • 90-day minimum cooldown before reactivation OR re-entry

  • ecommerce-lifecycle-marketing (nurture path)

Reply branch [R]: email-intent-classifier + downstream routing

Role

Fires throughout cadence on any inbound reply (email OR SMS reply post-opt-in). Classifies reply intent per email-intent-classifier atomic; routes to appropriate downstream specialist or human-takeover.

Input

  • Inbound reply content (email body / SMS body)
  • contact_id + chain context

Rules

  • Classify into ONE of: booking-intent | objection | unsubscribe-DNC | wrong-person | AM-personal-touch | noise
  • Confidence threshold: 0.75; below → route to human review (don't guess)
  • Pause cadence on classify (don't fire next scheduled email while reply pending response)

Tools

  • email-intent-classifier atomic
  • Anthropic API (Haiku for speed)

Output

reply_id: string
contact_id: string
intent: enum
confidence: number
recommended_route: string
classified_at: ISO timestamp

Handoff routes

Intent Route Specialist
booking-intent Book discovery ghl-calendar-builder → discovery-call-prep
objection Compliance-reviewed objection response objection-handler → compliance-officer → reply
unsubscribe-DNC Honor + suppression-list write workflow-automation-builder (suppression + tag)
wrong-person Update contact + retarget if known correct contact workflow-automation-builder (contact update)
AM-personal-touch Pause automation; assign to AM AM (human takeover)
noise (auto-reply, out-of-office, etc.) Archive + continue cadence none

Approval gate

  • Unsubscribe / DNC: auto-honored, compliance-officer notified (informational)
  • AM-personal-touch route: AM availability check; if AM unavailable → escalate to backup AM
  • Low-confidence (< 0.75): human review before route

Observability

  • _Logs/outreach/chains/<contact_id>/reply-classify/<reply_id>.json
  • Reply-rate per template / per vertical surfaced in Friday CI

Booking branch [B]: discovery-call-prep

Role

(Per _Skills Library/KB-Skills/discovery-call-prep/SKILL.md.) When prospect books (via reply, voice connect, or magnet-download AM-takeover), generate AM-ready brief for the discovery call.

Input

  • Full chain history + dossier

  • Channel of book (voice / email / referral)

Rules

  • Brief delivered ≥ 24 hours before call
  • 1-page summary + 1-page deep
  • Cite specific dossier facts for AM to reference
  • Surface ANY flags from chain (vip / regulatory / competitor / objection-raised-already)
  • Pre-meeting agenda included

Tools

  • discovery-call-prep skill (orchestrator)
  • GHL MCP (read all prior data + write attachment to opp)
  • ghl-calendar-builder (booking infrastructure)
  • sms-campaign-builder (booking confirmations + reminders — A2P 10DLC + TCPA gated, see below)

SMS reminders gate

  • 24h pre-call SMS reminder + 1h pre-call SMS reminder → ONLY fire if BOTH:
    • GHL A2P 10DLC brand + campaign registration = active (per _Shared Context/ghl-config.md)
    • Prospect provided prior express written consent (booking form opt-in checkbox checked, captured in GHL custom field sms_consent_at)
  • If either condition fails → email reminders only; SMS suppressed; compliance-officer notified to track gap

Output

  • Call-brief markdown to AM inbox + Slack DM + GHL opportunity attachment
  • Pre-meeting agenda
  • Reminders scheduled (email always; SMS if gate clears)

Handoff

  • AM runs discovery call (human step)
  • Post-call → meeting-magic extracts action items
  • Post-call → handoff to _Workflows/pipeline-to-closed-won.workflow.md (THIS WORKFLOW ENDS HERE)

Approval gate

  • VIP contacts: AM confirms time before booking
  • Tier-1 partner-equivalent prospects: Chris confirms
  • New SMS reminder copy: compliance-officer approves per A2P 10DLC

Observability

  • _Logs/outreach/chains/<contact_id>/booking/<appointment_id>.json
  • Chain status → completed-booked upon AM brief delivery

Chain-wide observability + state

State persistence

Each chain run has a unique chain_id. State persists at:

  • _Logs/outreach/chains/<contact_id>/index.md — chain manifest + status + per-step links
  • _Logs/outreach/chains/<contact_id>/<step-N>-<agent>/<event_id>.json — per-step output
  • GHL contact tags reflect current chain state (chain:norcal-outbound:step-N:<status>)

Chain status enum

  • running — active step in progress (between touches OR fired step in process)
  • paused-reply — inbound reply received; awaiting classify + route
  • paused-am — AM-personal-touch route active; human takeover
  • escalated — Chris / compliance-officer involvement required
  • completed-booked — discovery call booked; handoff to pipeline-to-closed-won
  • completed-declined — prospect hard-declined
  • completed-suppressed — DNC / unsubscribe / hard-decline
  • completed-archived — full cadence with no engagement; routed to nurture
  • failed — technical / unrecoverable error

Metrics aggregated per chain

  • Total duration (Day 1 to terminal status)
  • Cost per chain ($ — LLM + voice-platform + email-platform)
  • Per-step latency + cost
  • Per-step outcome distribution (connect / VM / no-answer / reply / unsubscribe / book)
  • Conversion rate per step (% advancing to next desired state)
  • Per-vertical / per-geo-tier aggregates

Chain-monitor coverage

Per chain-monitor skill (live, scheduled 7am PT daily digest):

  • Chains running / paused / completed in last 24h
  • Stalled chains > 7 days at same step → alert
  • Failed chains → alert + Chris ping
  • Per-vertical funnel snapshot daily

Friday CI coverage

  • ghl-continuous-improvement (Fri 3:30pm) — includes this chain's GHL workflow performance
  • voice-call-reviewer (Fri 3pm) — every Mia call in chain reviewed weekly
  • nexus-conversion-analyst (Fri 4:30pm) — per-source attribution back to Nexus
  • Aggregate reports surfaced at /nexus page in ai-command-center

Eval coverage

Per-agent eval suites

Each specialist has its own eval cases per its SKILL.md. Aggregated in:

  • voice-eval-harness (voice steps)
  • ghl-eval-harness (email + SMS steps)

Chain integration evals

End-to-end scenarios stored in _Logs/eval-suites/chains/norcal-outbound-multichannel/:

  1. Happy path — voice connect → book on Day 1: Bay Area med-spa, Mia connects, owner interested, books discovery, AM brief delivered ≥ 24h, handoff to pipeline-to-closed-won
  2. Happy path — email-only book: Sacramento HRT clinic, voice attempts all VM/no-answer, Email 3 (lead magnet) clicked → magnet download → reply YES → book → AM brief → handoff
  3. Gatekeeper-throughout: Marin med-spa, all 3 voice attempts hit gatekeeper, 6 emails sent, no reply → Day 30 archive decision → routed to nurture
  4. Live objection on Day 1 voice: Wine Country WL clinic, Mia connects, owner says "we have a pharmacy", live objection-handler resolves with fulfillment-speed angle, books callback Day 3 → re-attempts → books discovery
  5. No-response → archive: Tier-3 Chico HRT clinic, all 9 touches no engagement, Day 30 → archive (not reactivate; no engagement signal)
  6. Mid-cadence opt-out: SF med-spa, Email 2 → reply STOP → suppression-list write + chain status = completed-suppressed; compliance-officer notified
  7. Wrong-person: Email 1 reply "I'm not the right contact, try Dr. Smith" → email-intent-classifier routes to wrong-person → workflow-automation-builder updates contact + retargets correct contact in new chain
  8. AM-personal-touch trigger: Magnet download on Day 12, AM picks up, automation pauses, AM books discovery directly → handoff to pipeline-to-closed-won
  9. DNC voice request mid-call: Day 3 voice attempt, prospect says "don't call me again", Mia honors, tag dnc:voice, chain continues email-only OR fully suppresses per request specificity
  10. Cooldown violation attempt: Chain triggered for contact with outreach in last 21 days → pre-flight blocks → no fire; chain status = failed:cooldown-violation
  11. Compliance-template-stale: Template last reviewed > 30 days ago → pre-flight blocks; alerts compliance-officer to refresh
  12. California pharmacy board flag: clinic-research-agent surfaces warning-letter signal → chain pauses → Chris notified → manual decide

Run cadence

  • Per chain run: automatic per-step eval logged
  • Weekly: full integration eval suite runs vs production
  • Monthly: chain-quality report to Chris

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Step 1 (research) timeout Return partial dossier + low-confidence flag; chain continues with caveat
Step 1 NPI verification fails for provider lead Soft-fail (clinic-only context); flag for AM judgment; chain continues
Step 2 (channel-router) decision ambiguous Default to voice-first (Matrix doctrine); log for review
Step 3a (Mia call) platform fails Retry once after 30 min; if fails again → tag voice:platform-fail + fire Email 1 as fallback
Step 3a Mia mis-classifies outcome Human reviewer can re-classify within 24h; chain re-routes if needed
GHL workflow timeout firing email Retry once; if fails → alert engineering + tag for manual send
Inbound reply during voice call queued Pause cadence; route reply via classifier; voice attempt deferred
Reply classifier confidence < 0.75 Human review queue; pause cadence until classified
Opt-out received mid-cadence Immediate suppression + chain ends + compliance log
Wrong-person update points to existing contact in DB Merge logic via workflow-automation-builder; chain re-fires for correct contact
Dead-number / disconnected number Tag + skip voice attempts; email-only remainder
SMS reminder fires without A2P + consent both confirmed Block + alert compliance-officer; email-only reminders used
Chain detected stuck > 7 days at any step Auto-escalate to AM for manual unstick
Compliance-officer flags new banned-phrase variant post-deploy Hot-pull all in-flight emails; pause cadence until template revised

Approval gates summary

Gate Step Who What
FDA-attention / warning-letter 1 Chris Approves continue OR chain stops
Vertical-specific copy changes 4-11 (any email step) Chris Approves per-vertical template variant
Per-template compliance 4-11 compliance-officer Approves template before deploy (refreshed quarterly)
Testimonial inclusion 9 (Email 4) compliance-officer + documented permission Approves testimonial source
Voice script variant 3a / 5 / 7 compliance-officer Approves Mia script variants before deploy
New voice persona 3a / 5 / 7 Chris Only Mia approved; new persona requires Chris
New override-signal in channel-router 2 Chris Approves new email-first override criteria
A2P 10DLC gate B (booking reminders) compliance-officer + GHL config SMS step ONLY activates after A2P brand + campaign active per ghl-config.md
TCPA prior-express-written-consent B (booking reminders) Per-contact opt-in SMS only after explicit opt-in captured (cold prospects NEVER receive marketing SMS)
AM-personal-touch route R AM availability Real-time check; backup AM if primary unavailable
Reactivation decision 12 lead-scoring + engagement signal Auto-decision unless engagement ambiguous → AM judgment
VIP contact booking B AM Confirms time before booking
Tier-1 prospect B Chris Confirms time

Cost + cadence targets

Per-prospect cost estimate

Component Cost (est) Notes
clinic-research-agent (1×) $0.05-0.15 Haiku + WebSearch + NPPES (free)
conversation-channel-router (1×) $0.001-0.005 Atomic-level decision
Voice attempts (up to 3×) $0.30-0.90 total Retell/Vapi at ~$0.10-0.30/min × 1-3 min calls × 3 attempts
Email sends (up to 6×) $0.001-0.006 GHL workflow trigger cost (LLM cost = $0 — templates pre-filled)
Personalization fill per email $0.005-0.02 × 6 = $0.03-0.12 Haiku for template-fill
Reply classification (if fired) $0.005-0.02 per reply Haiku
Discovery call brief (if booked) $0.10-0.30 Sonnet for nuanced synthesis
Compliance pre-check (atomic) $0 Deterministic, no LLM
Total per prospect (full cadence, no book) $0.50-1.20 Most prospects $0.60-0.90
Total per prospect (book + brief) $0.70-1.50 Adds discovery brief

Per-vertical cohorts of 500 (Bay Area Tier 1 target per ICP brief) → cohort cost $250-750 per vertical for full cadence pass.

Per-rep capacity

Resource Capacity
Mia (voice AI) — simultaneous calls Platform-dependent; assume 5-10 concurrent at scale
Mia per day — outbound dials 200-400 per day per concurrent thread × concurrent threads
Email — per day Unlimited at GHL workflow level; deliverability limited per-sender (warm 50-200/day)
AM (human takeover) — discovery calls per week 15-25 per AM (per sales-strategy capacity model)
Compliance-officer template reviews 2-5 per week capacity (refresh quarterly OR ad-hoc)

Scale targets (Q2 2026)

Metric Target
Prospects per vertical (Tier 1 Bay Area) 500 (per ICP brief)
Total prospects Q2 (3 verticals × Tier 1) 1,500
Cadence completion rate (full 9 touches OR booked OR opted-out) ≥ 85% within 30 days
Booked discoveries per 100 prospects (combined voice + email) ≥ 10 (per ICP KPI targets)
Total Q2 booked discoveries (1,500 × 10%) ≥ 150
Total Q2 SQLs (discoveries × 60% SQL rate) ≥ 90
Total Q2 closed-won (SQLs × 30% close rate) ≥ 27

KPIs

Per _Shared Context/icp-norcal-q2-2026.md + _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md + Matrix Nexus:

Metric Target Source
Voice connect rate (Mia outbound) ≥ 18% voice-call-reviewer aggregates from chain
Voice → booked discovery ≥ 8% of dials Chain conversion
Email open rate ≥ 35% cold-email-sequence + GHL workflow
Email reply rate ≥ 6% email-intent-classifier events
Email → booked discovery ≥ 2% of sends Chain conversion
Discovery → SQL ≥ 60% pipeline-to-closed-won downstream
SQL → closed-won ≥ 30% pipeline-to-closed-won downstream
Cycle time SQL → closed ≤ 21 days pipeline-to-closed-won downstream
Cost per booked discovery < $50 fully-loaded Chain cost ÷ books
% chains with at least 1 engagement signal (open/click/voice-partial/reply) ≥ 55% Chain aggregate
% chains hitting compliance violation < 1% (target zero) compliance-officer aggregate
% chains escalated (FDA / counsel / DNC complaint) < 2% Chain status aggregate

Cohort tracking per _Shared Context/icp-norcal-q2-2026.md — separate cohort per vertical (med-spa / WL / HRT) × geo tier (Tier 1 / Tier 2 / Tier 3) = 9 cohorts tracked independently.

If actuals drift from targets > 2 weeks running → ghl-continuous-improvement + nexus-conversion-analyst propose adjustments at Friday CI.

Related

  • _Templates/micro-agent.template.md (template every specialist uses)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/micro-agent-team-builder/SKILL.md (meta-pattern)
  • _Shared Context/icp-norcal-q2-2026.md (THE ICP brief — read first for any NorCal outreach work)
  • _Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md (upstream engine — this chain is a Nexus downstream)
  • _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (GHL pipeline + workflow IDs + A2P registration status)
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md (claims discipline + banned phrases + TCPA + CAN-SPAM)
  • _Shared Context/sub-brands.md (Matrix Advanced Solutions + Peptide Map voice routing)
  • _Shared Context/kpi-registry.md (KPI definitions)
  • _Workflows/lead-to-booked-demo.workflow.md (canonical sibling chain — mirror reference)
  • _Workflows/pipeline-to-closed-won.workflow.md (downstream — Specialist B handoff)
  • _Workflows/new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md (post-close downstream)
  • _Workflows/expansion-opportunity.workflow.md (related — existing-customer Nexus chain)
  • _Workflows/inbound-call-to-handoff.workflow.md (related — Nexus voice channel)
  • _Workflows/matrix-nexus-engine.workflow.md (umbrella)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/clinic-research-agent/SKILL.md (step 1)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/conversation-channel-router/SKILL.md (step 2)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/voice-ai-sales-agent/SKILL.md (Mia outbound — steps 3a / 5 / 7)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/call-handoff-orchestrator/SKILL.md (warm transfer)
  • _Skills Library/KB-Skills/cold-email-sequence/SKILL.md (steps 3b / 4 / 6 / 8 / 9 / 10 / 11)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/objection-handler/SKILL.md (live + email objection branches)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/compliance-officer/SKILL.md (template + testimonial + script gates)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/email-intent-classifier/SKILL.md (reply branch)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/sms-campaign-builder/SKILL.md (booking SMS — A2P + TCPA gated)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/workflow-automation-builder/SKILL.md (CRM updates + suppression)
  • _Skills Library/KB-Skills/discovery-call-prep/SKILL.md (booking branch — AM brief)
  • _Logs/outreach/voice-scripts/<vertical>/ (Mia script library — owned by parallel content agents)
  • _Logs/outreach/email-templates/<vertical>/ (email library — owned by parallel content agents)
  • _Logs/outreach/lead-magnets/<vertical>/ (lead magnets — owned by lead-magnet-generator)
  • _Logs/outreach/battlecards/<competitor>/ (competitor battlecards — owned by competitive-battlecard-builder)
  • _Logs/outreach/objections/<vertical>/ (vertical objection libraries — owned by objection-handler)

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris — initial chain spec for NorCal Q2 2026 multi-channel outbound push (voice + email, SMS post-opt-in only)

workflows.name: Workflow: Outbound Voice Sales Campaign
workflows.description: End-to-end flow for running an outbound voice sales campaign against a verified target list. Loaded by `company-os-orchestrator` for campaign launches.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Outbound Voice Sales Campaign

End-to-end flow for running an outbound voice sales campaign against a verified target list. Loaded by company-os-orchestrator for campaign launches.

When this workflow fires

  • "Run an outbound voice campaign for [audience]"
  • "Spin up a cold-call campaign for the new offer"
  • "Re-engage dormant leads via voice"
  • Manual invocation by Chris

Two-stage workflow

Stage A: One-time setup (only first time per use case)

Already covered by voice-agent-build.workflow.md. Runs once. Output is a production-deployed voice agent.

Stage B: Per-campaign execution (this workflow)

Each campaign reuses the production voice agent but goes through this approval flow.

Agent chain (per-campaign)

Step 1: market-research (if new ICP segment)
   → ICP refinement for this campaign's audience
   → Skip if already-known segment
   ↓
Step 2: List acquisition + verification
   → Source list (Apollo, Crunchbase, in-house, NPI database)
   → Verify each contact:
     • Phone number format + ownership
     • NPI active (Matrix-specific — NPPES check)
     • Not in DNC (national + state)
     • Not currently in active customer pipeline
     • Not previously opted out
   → Intent signal documented per contact (recent funding, hire, post, etc.)
   ↓
Step 3: conversation-channel-router validation
   → Confirm voice is the right channel for this list
   → If not → switch to email-only campaign
   ↓
Step 4: cold-email-sequence (parallel — for voicemail/no-answer fallback)
   → 3-touch email sequence drafted
   → Will trigger after voicemail or no-answer
   → Provides the "catalog soft-close" backup path
   ↓
Step 5: ghl-crm-architect (additive — confirm CRM is ready)
   → Stage exists for "Voice-Touched" lead status
   → Tags exist for outcome (`outcome:booked`, `outcome:catalog-only`, `outcome:hard-no`)
   → Custom fields for call disposition
   ↓
Step 6: Campaign brief
   → Use `_Templates/project-brief.md`
   → Define: list size, daily cap, hours, target meetings, kill-switch criteria
   → Document: caller ID number, agent ID, monitoring contact
   ↓
Step 7: Approval gate 1 (Chris) — Pre-launch
   → Reviews list + brief + compliance posture
   → Approves to proceed (or sends back with edits)
   ↓
Step 8: Pre-flight test
   → 5-10 test calls to internal numbers / friendly testers
   → Verify functions, scripts, recording disclosure, transfer
   → Check eval pass
   ↓
Step 9: Approval gate 2 (Chris) — Go-live
   → Reviews test outcomes
   → Authorizes live calls
   ↓
Step 10: Day 1 — soft launch (10-20% of daily cap)
   → Limited volume
   → Live monitoring of every call outcome
   → Chris reviews end-of-day
   ↓
Step 11: Approval gate 3 (Chris) — Scale up
   → Reviews Day 1 results
   → Approves full daily cap
   ↓
Step 12: Full campaign runs
   → Daily volume to target
   → `ghl-daily-pulse` surfaces outcomes each morning
   → `prep-day` highlights booked meetings for the day
   ↓
Step 13: Daily monitoring (autonomous)
   → Outcomes logged
   → Voicemail rate / connect rate / meeting-set rate tracked
   → Auto-pause if anomaly:
     • DNC complaints > 0 in any 24h period
     • Voicemail rate > 80% (signals deliverability issue)
     • Meeting-set rate < target floor for 3 consecutive days
   ↓
Step 14: For each meeting booked
   → discovery-call-prep auto-fires for Chris (or designated closer)
   → Calendar event confirmed + SMS reminders
   → Pre-call prep doc in inbox 24h before
   ↓
Step 15: For each voicemail / soft-close
   → cold-email-sequence triggers automatically
   → Catalog send + 3-touch email cadence
   ↓
Step 16: Weekly review
   → weekly-review picks up campaign metrics
   → Iteration proposals if KPIs lag
   → Decision: continue / pause / kill / expand
   ↓
Step 17: Campaign end
   → Final report → _Logs/campaign-results/<campaign>-<date>.md
   → Post-mortem: what worked, what didn't
   → Lessons fed into agent-registry for next campaign

Standard inputs

  • Target list source — Apollo / Crunchbase / in-house / NPI db
  • List size — total contacts in scope
  • Audience description — ICP + intent signal type
  • Offer / objective — what we're pitching + the success outcome
  • Daily cap — calls per day (typically 50-300 depending on agent capacity)
  • Campaign window — start date, end date, daily hours (within TCPA)
  • Kill-switch criteria — when to auto-pause
  • Caller ID — Matrix-owned legitimate number
  • Transfer destination — who picks up if recipient asks for human

Standard outputs

  • Campaign brief: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-campaign-brief-<campaign>.md
  • Verified list: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-verified-list-<campaign>.csv (with sources + intent signals per contact)
  • Daily monitoring report: _Logs/campaign-results/<campaign>/<date>.md (one per active day)
  • Final report: _Logs/campaign-results/<campaign>-final-<date>.md

Approval gates (3 in this workflow)

  1. After Step 7 — Pre-launch (list + brief approved)
  2. After Step 9 — Go-live (test outcomes reviewed)
  3. After Step 11 — Scale-up (Day 1 results approved)

After scale-up, daily monitoring runs autonomously with the auto-pause guardrails.

Estimated time

  • Per-campaign setup (Steps 1-9): 1-2 days (gated by Chris approvals)
  • Soft launch (Step 10): 1 day
  • Full campaign (Steps 12-15): 5-20 days depending on list size + cap
  • Wrap (Steps 16-17): 1-2 days

Auto-pause triggers (built into Step 13)

The agent auto-pauses (waits for Chris) if ANY of these:

  • 1+ DNC complaint received in 24h
  • Recipient explicitly threatens legal / asks for compliance officer
  • Meeting-set rate < 50% of target floor for 3 consecutive days
  • Voicemail rate > 80% in any 24h (signals deliverability issue)
  • Connect rate < 5% (list quality issue)
  • Any "Critical" finding from real-time security-inspector monitoring
  • Calls outside TCPA window detected (timezone bug)

Compliance checks per-campaign

Check Frequency Outcome if fail
DNC scrub Once per list Cannot launch
TCPA window enforcement Continuous (every call) Specific call cancelled
State 2-party recording disclosure Continuous (state-detected) Disclosure inserted automatically
A2P 10DLC active (for SMS) At Step 5 Block SMS auto-fire if not active
HIPAA flag check Continuous (if patient data surfaces) Route to HIPAA-aware handler + log

Failure modes + recovery

Failure Recovery
List verification finds < 50% verified Reject list; source better data
Pre-flight tests fail eval Pause; iterate scripts; re-test
Day 1 has 1+ DNC complaint Auto-pause; investigate; resume only with new list
Meeting-set rate too low Pause; script iteration via voice-script-author
Discovery prep doc not generated for booked call Manual prep; log to weekly-review
Voice agent technical failure Apply retellai-incident-runbook

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ List verification + DNC scrub
  • ✅ Email follow-up drafting (cold-email-sequence)
  • ✅ CRM stage / tag setup (additive)
  • ✅ Pre-flight testing
  • ✅ Daily monitoring reports
  • ⚠️ Approve pre-launch
  • ⚠️ Approve go-live
  • ⚠️ Approve scale-up
  • ⚠️ Re-approve after any auto-pause
  • 🚫 Override auto-pause without resolving root cause
  • 🚫 Skip the approval gates

Roll-up to executives

After Step 16 weekly review:

  • Campaign status to master project board
  • KPIs vs target
  • Cumulative pipeline value generated
  • Cost per meeting booked
  • Recommend continuing / iterating / killing / expanding

Last updated

2026-05-10 by Chris (initial seed)

workflows.name: Workflow: Pipeline → Closed-Won (Specialist Chain)
workflows.description: Operational sales chain. From GHL opportunity created (downstream of Nexus or inbound) through discovery → proposal → objection handling → late-stage close → handoff to customer success. Built per the **Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff** specialist-chain pattern (see `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md`).
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Pipeline → Closed-Won (Specialist Chain)

Operational sales chain. From GHL opportunity created (downstream of Nexus or inbound) through discovery → proposal → objection handling → late-stage close → handoff to customer success. Built per the Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff specialist-chain pattern (see _Templates/micro-agent.template.md).

Each agent in the chain is a specialist digital employee. Output from each step is structured data; next agent picks it up as input. No free-form handoffs. Approval gates marked. Observability wired throughout.

Chain overview

GHL Opportunity Created (from Nexus or inbound)
   │
   ▼
[1] clinic-research-agent       ← clinic type / services / providers / product fit (if not cached)
   │
   ▼
[2] discovery-call-prep         ← sales-prep brief delivered to AM
   │
   ▼  (AM runs discovery call)
[3] proposal-builder            ← compliance-reviewed proposal draft
   │
   ▼  (proposal delivered; reply path)
[4a] objection-handler          ← if objection raised
[4b] (verbal-yes path)          ← skip to closer-support
   │
   ▼
[5] closer-support              ← MEDDPICC + multi-thread + next-best-action (late stage trigger)
   │
   ▼  (if complex)
[6] mutual-action-plan-builder  ← buyer/seller MAP for $25k+ complex deals
   │
   ▼
[7] sales-revenue-lead          ← discount approval (if needed) + commit signoff
   │
   ▼  (closed-won)
[8] HANDOFF to customer-success-lead via new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md
   │
   ▼
[9] win-loss-analyst            ← interview cadence (14d post-activation)

When this workflow fires

  • New GHL opportunity created with lead_tier ∈ {hot, warm, qualified} (from Nexus lead-to-booked-demo chain or other origination)
  • Inbound deal manually created by AM with rep cert verified
  • Re-engagement opp (existing customer expansion → may bypass discovery)
  • Cooled-deal revival (was no-decision; new signal; back to step 3 typically)

Pre-flight checks

  • Opportunity exists in GHL MAS Order Lifecycle pipeline with amount populated
  • AM owner certified (sales-rep-certification-manager status = active)
  • Contact not in DNC list
  • Pipeline-hygiene-keeper has run within 24h (clean state precondition)
  • Compliance flags clear (no chain:blocked-compliance tag from upstream)
  • Contact dossier cached OR clinic-research-agent re-fire scheduled

Specialist 1: clinic-research-agent (cached-or-refresh)

Role

(Per clinic-research-agent SKILL.md.) Confirm clinic dossier currency; refresh if > 90 days stale. Most opps arrive from Nexus chain with current dossier — usually a passthrough.

Input

  • contact_id + opportunity_id
