  • Prior dossier (if cached)

Rules

  • Skip if dossier < 90 days old AND no dossier:stale tag
  • Time budget: 5 min if refreshing

Output

Passthrough or refreshed dossier per clinic-research-agent schema.

Handoff

  • → discovery-call-prep

Approval gate

  • None (research only)

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/01-clinic-research-cached/<opp_id>.json

SLA: < 1 hour async

Specialist 2: discovery-call-prep

Role

(Per discovery-call-prep SKILL.md.) Generate sales-prep brief for AM ≥ 24h before discovery call.

Input

  • Full dossier + lead score + outreach history + opportunity context

Rules

  • Brief delivered ≥ 24h before call
  • 1-page summary + 1-page deep
  • Surface flags from upstream chain (vip / regulatory / competitor / etc.)

Output

  • Call-brief markdown to AM inbox + Slack DM + GHL opportunity attachment
  • Pre-meeting agenda

Handoff

  • AM runs discovery call (human step)
  • Post-call: meeting-magic extracts action items → triggers next step

Approval gate

  • None — brief is informational

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/02-discovery-call-prep/<opp_id>.json

SLA: brief delivered ≥ 24h pre-call

Specialist 3: proposal-builder

Role

(Per proposal-builder SKILL.md.) Generate compliance-reviewed proposal post-discovery.

Input

  • Discovery call notes (via meeting-magic)
  • Updated dossier (any new info from discovery)
  • Pricing requested (catalog + tier)

Rules

  • Approved claims only (per compliance-rules.md)
  • Margin floor respected (price-margin-check atomic gate)
  • Tier pricing per ghl-config.md
  • Sub-brand voice per dossier
  • No trade-secret leaks
  • Discount > 10% requires sales-revenue-lead approval; > 20% requires Chris

Output

  • proposal_id / version / amount / line items / claims / signoff section
  • Compliance review status

Handoff

  • → compliance-officer (gate)
  • After compliance approval → AM delivers + GHL opportunity advances to "proposal" stage

Approval gate

  • Compliance-officer review mandatory
  • Discount thresholds escalate per rules

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/03-proposal-builder/<opp_id>.json

SLA: proposal delivered within 48h of discovery call

Specialist 4a: objection-handler (objection branch)

Role

(Per objection-handler SKILL.md.) Classify + respond to objections raised in reply.

Input

  • Reply content + proposal context + dossier

Rules

  • 8-category classification
  • Anchor every response to approved claim or publication
  • 2 attempts max; 3rd → AM takeover
  • Regulatory objection → compliance-officer
  • Legal-threat → Chris + counsel

Output

  • Classified objection + drafted response + compliance gate result

Handoff

  • → compliance-officer → AM sends response
  • After resolution → back to chain (proposal stage or → closer-support if late-stage)

Approval gate

  • Compliance gate per objection-handler skill

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/04a-objection-handler/<opp_id>.json

SLA: response drafted within 4 business hours

Specialist 4b: Stage advance to verbal-yes (no objection path)

Role

Pipeline advance — GHL workflow moves opp to "negotiation" or "verbal-yes" stage.

Input

  • Proposal accepted signal (verbal or written)

Output

  • pipeline_stage advanced + tags applied

Handoff

  • → closer-support (always for negotiation+ stages)

Specialist 5: closer-support (always fires for negotiation+ stages)

Role

(Per closer-support SKILL.md.) MEDDPICC scoring + multi-thread strategy + next-best-action.

Input

  • Full chain context + proposal + reply history + opportunity stage

Rules

  • MEDDPICC scored with reasoning per dimension
  • Multi-thread on $25k+ deals
  • Specific next-action with channel + owner + deadline
  • Stuck > 21d → escalate to sales-revenue-lead
  • Discount > 10% → routes to sales-revenue-lead

Output

  • deal_health + meddpicc_scores + flags + next_best_actions + handoff_recommendation

Handoff

  • → AM (coaching brief + GHL tasks written)
  • If complex multi-stakeholder + amount > $50k → mutual-action-plan-builder
  • If specific objection → objection-handler
  • If proposal revision needed → proposal-builder
  • If competitor flag → competitive-battlecard-builder (refresh) + embed in coaching
  • If deal-health < 12 → sales-revenue-lead (escalation)

Approval gate

  • Discount > 10% routes to sales-revenue-lead

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/05-closer-support/<opp_id>.json

SLA: coaching brief within 24h of stage entry

Specialist 6: mutual-action-plan-builder (conditional — complex deals)

Role

(Per mutual-action-plan-builder SKILL.md.) Buyer/seller MAP for $25k+ complex deals.

Input

  • closer-support output (MEDDPICC + stakeholders + flags)
  • Proposal + dossier
  • Buyer stakeholder list

Rules

  • Both sides own each milestone
  • Decision gates explicit (legal / procurement / clinical / executive)
  • Economic buyer named
  • Buyer-facing version compliance-reviewed
  • 14-day refresh cadence
  • PHI/DPA → counsel

Output

  • Buyer-facing MAP (PDF + shared doc) + internal version

Handoff

  • → AM delivers MAP to buyer
  • Sign-off achieved → forecast-roll-up (commit category) + sales-revenue-lead signoff
  • PHI/DPA → ip-counsel-coordination

Approval gate

  • Compliance-officer reviews buyer-facing version
  • Major price/scope mid-plan → sales-revenue-lead + Chris per discount tiers

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/06-mutual-action-plan/<opp_id>.json

SLA: initial MAP within 5 business days of stage entry

Specialist 7: sales-revenue-lead (commit signoff + discount approval)

Role

(Per sales-revenue-lead SKILL.md.) Approve commit category + discount (if > 10%) + verify cert-gate compliance.

Input

  • Full chain context + closer-support output + MAP (if present) + proposal final version

Rules

  • AM-attestation required for commit
  • Discount > 10% lead approval; > 20% Chris approval
  • Margin floor verified via price-margin-check
  • Cert-gate: AM must be certified

Output

  • approval_decision (commit / best-case / blocked / counter-proposal-needed)
  • Discount approval (if applicable)
  • forecast_category assigned

Handoff

  • If approved + verbal/written yes → contract execution (counsel for non-standard)
  • If counter-proposal needed → back to proposal-builder
  • If blocked → AM coaching path

Approval gate

  • This is the gate

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/07-sales-revenue-lead/<opp_id>.json

SLA: within 1 business day

Specialist 8: HANDOFF to customer-success-lead (closed-won)

Role

Closed-won transition. Triggers new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md.

Input

  • Full deal context + contract terms + proposal + MAP (if present) + clinic dossier
  • AM-of-record + AM-handoff notes

Rules

  • Handoff within 24h of closed-won
  • All deal context transferred (not just contract)
  • Customer-success-lead acknowledges receipt
  • commission-calculator runs preview for AM line item
  • pipeline-hygiene-keeper marks opp closed-won + reason

Output

  • onboarding chain initiated
  • AM-of-record retained as relationship-of-record
  • expected_first_order_date

Handoff

  • → customer-success-lead (primary)
  • → commission-calculator (per-deal preview; full run on monthly cycle)
  • → forecast-roll-up (closed-won category update)
  • → win-loss-analyst (queued for +14d interview)
  • → new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md (the entire onboarding chain)

Approval gate

  • None (closed-won is a hard signal; CS receives or escalates)

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/08-handoff-to-cs/<opp_id>.json

SLA: 24h handoff

Specialist 9: win-loss-analyst (post-deal interview cadence)

Role

(Per win-loss-analyst SKILL.md.) Schedule + run won-deal interview at +14 days post-activation.

Input

  • Full deal chain context
  • Activation confirmation (customer-success-lead signal)

Rules

  • Won @ +14d / Lost @ +30d / No-decision @ +90d
  • AM interviews live; analyst drafts guide + synthesizes
  • Permission required for quotable lines
  • PII anonymized in cohort outputs

Output

  • Interview synthesis (per deal) + quarterly cohort feed

Handoff

  • → sales-strategy (positioning signal)
  • → competitive-battlecard-builder (refresh triggers)
  • → sales-revenue-lead (priority synthesis)
  • → marketing (testimonial-author) if permission granted + quotable
  • → formulation-lead if product-gap signal

Approval gate

  • Compliance-officer reviews invite template (treated as outbound comm)

Observability

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/09-win-loss/<opp_id>.json

Chain-wide observability + state

State persistence

Each chain run has unique chain_id. State persists at:

  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/index.md — manifest + status
  • _Logs/agents/chains/<chain_id>/<step-N>-<agent>/<opp_id>.json — per-step output
  • GHL opportunity tags reflect current chain state (chain:pipeline-to-close:step-N:status)

Chain status enum

  • running (active step in progress)
  • paused (awaiting human input — discovery call, customer reply, etc.)
  • escalated (sales-revenue-lead / Chris / counsel involvement)
  • completed-won (success — handoff to CS done)
  • completed-lost (lost — win-loss-analyst queued)
  • completed-no-decision (90d stall — win-loss queued + nurture)
  • failed (technical / unrecoverable error)

Metrics aggregated per chain

  • Total duration (opp-created to closed-won/lost)
  • Cost per chain ($)
  • Per-step latency + cost
  • Conversion rate per step (% advancing to next)
  • Human-review interventions per chain

Surfaced in:

  • sales-continuous-improvement (Friday 5:30pm)
  • forecast-roll-up (Friday weekly)
  • analytics-architect dashboard
  • pmo-operator PMO board

Per-stage SLAs

Stage SLA
clinic-research refresh (if needed) < 1 hour
discovery-call-prep brief ≥ 24h pre-call
proposal-builder draft within 48h of discovery
objection-handler response within 4 business hours
closer-support coaching brief within 24h of stage entry
mutual-action-plan initial within 5 business days
sales-revenue-lead signoff within 1 business day
customer-success handoff within 24h of closed-won
win-loss interview won @ +14d / lost @ +30d / no-decision @ +90d

Eval coverage

Per-agent eval suites

Each specialist has its own eval cases per its SKILL.md (_Logs/eval-suites/<agent>/). Aggregated in sales-eval-harness.

Chain integration evals

End-to-end scenarios stored in _Logs/eval-suites/chains/pipeline-to-closed-won/:

  1. Happy path — qualified opp → discovery → proposal compliance pass → verbal yes → closer-support strong → closed-won → CS handoff @ 24h
  2. Objection path — proposal → pricing objection → objection-handler → value-anchor response → resumed
  3. Complex multi-stakeholder $75k → MAP built → sign-off → closed-won
  4. Stuck at proposal 21+ days → closer-support escalates → sales-revenue-lead intervenes
  5. Discount 15% requested → closer-support + price-margin-check → sales-revenue-lead approves
  6. Discount 25% requested → sales-revenue-lead escalates to Chris
  7. Competitor flagged mid-deal → battlecard refresh triggered → counter-strategy in coaching
  8. Closed-lost → win-loss interview @ +30d → cohort signal
  9. No-decision at 90d → win-loss interview → revival decision
  10. Uncertified rep attempts to advance opp → pre-flight blocks + cert-manager routing
  11. PHI/DPA requirement surfaces in MAP → counsel routing
  12. Trade-secret leak attempted in proposal → compliance-officer blocks
  13. Closed-won handoff fails to CS → escalation
  14. Hygiene-keeper precondition fails → chain blocked at pre-flight

Run cadence

  • Per chain run: automatic per-step eval
  • Weekly: full integration eval suite vs prod-shadow
  • Monthly: chain-quality report to Chris

Failure recovery matrix

Failure Recovery
Step 1 research timeout Use cached dossier + low-confidence flag; chain continues
Step 2 brief delivery fails Retry email; SMS notify AM; manual brief generation fallback
Step 3 proposal compliance blocked Loop back; if 2x blocked → human compliance review
Step 4a objection 3rd attempt AM takes over; chain stops automated loop
Step 5 closer-support deal-health < 12 Escalate to sales-revenue-lead
Step 6 buyer rejects MAP framing Route back to closer-support for soft re-approach
Step 7 discount approval denied Counter-proposal path → back to proposal-builder
Step 8 CS handoff acknowledgment missing > 24h Escalate to customer-success-lead + sales-revenue-lead
Step 9 customer declines interview Log + don't re-ask within 12 months
Chain stuck > 14 days at any step Auto-escalate to sales-revenue-lead

Approval gates summary

Gate Step Who What
Cert verification Pre-flight sales-rep-certification-manager Blocks uncertified rep activity
Hygiene preconditions Pre-flight pipeline-hygiene-keeper Blocks dirty-data chain
Proposal compliance 3 compliance-officer Approves outbound proposal
Objection compliance 4a compliance-officer Approves objection response
Discount > 10% 5 / 7 sales-revenue-lead Approves
Discount > 20% 7 Chris Approves
MAP buyer-facing 6 compliance-officer Approves before sharing
PHI / DPA 6 ip-counsel-coordination Counsel reviews
Commit signoff 7 sales-revenue-lead Signs commit category
Win-loss interview invite 9 compliance-officer Reviews template
Closed-won handoff 8 customer-success-lead Acknowledges receipt

Cost + latency targets

Metric Target
Cost per chain run < $2.50 (most chains $1.00-1.80)
Total chain duration (opp → closed-won) 30-90 days typical
Total chain duration (opp → closed-lost) 14-45 days typical
Human-review intervention rate < 25% of chains
Compliance auto-approval rate at proposal > 75%
CS handoff SLA (closed-won → onboarding fire) ≤ 24h
Win rate at proposal stage ≥ 35%
Win rate at negotiation stage ≥ 55%
Win rate at verbal-yes stage ≥ 85%

If actuals drift from targets > 2 weeks running → sales-continuous-improvement proposes adjustment.

Related

  • _Templates/micro-agent.template.md (template every specialist uses)
  • _Workflows/lead-to-booked-demo.workflow.md (upstream — Nexus chain feeds into step 1 here)
  • _Workflows/new-customer-onboarding.workflow.md (downstream — step 8 handoff)
  • _Workflows/customer-refund-request.workflow.md (related — post-close, may surface refund signal for win-loss-analyst no-decision cohort)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/sales-revenue-lead/SKILL.md (lead orchestrator)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/clinic-research-agent/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/KB-Skills/discovery-call-prep/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/proposal-builder/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/objection-handler/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/closer-support/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/mutual-action-plan-builder/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/competitive-battlecard-builder/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/pipeline-hygiene-keeper/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/forecast-roll-up/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/commission-calculator/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/win-loss-analyst/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/sales-continuous-improvement/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/sales-eval-harness/SKILL.md
  • _Shared Context/ghl-config.md (pipeline + workflow IDs)
  • _Shared Context/compliance-rules.md
  • _Shared Context/approval-rules.md
  • _Shared Context/matrix-nexus.md (upstream engine)

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris — initial chain spec per Christo's definition (Role / Input / Rules / Tools / Output / Handoff)

workflows.name: Workflow: Product Launch (end-to-end)
workflows.description: Standard chain for launching a new product / offer / sub-brand. Composes strategy → product → engineering → marketing → sales → ops into one continuous flow. Used for: new product line (IV drips), new bundle, new sub-brand expansion.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Product Launch (end-to-end)

Standard chain for launching a new product / offer / sub-brand. Composes strategy → product → engineering → marketing → sales → ops into one continuous flow. Used for: new product line (IV drips), new bundle, new sub-brand expansion.

When this workflow fires

  • "Launch [product name]"
  • "Ship the [new offer]"
  • "Bring [product] to market"
  • Manual invocation after Chris commits to a product decision

Agent chain

Phase 1: Strategy + Validation
  Step 1: market-research
     → ICP, demand signals, competitor positioning
     → Approval gate: Chris approves direction
     ↓
  Step 2: should-i-build-an-agent (if AI agent component)
     → Verify if any agent functionality needed
     ↓
  Step 3: product-manager-agent
     → PRD (problem, users, solution, success metrics, out-of-scope)
     → Approval gate: Chris approves PRD
     ↓
  Step 4: offer-positioning
     → Offer Brief (positioning, pricing, FOMU wedge)
     → Approval gate: Chris approves offer
     ↓
  Step 5: pricing-margin
     → Cost-to-deliver + competitor benchmark + margin math
     → Approval gate: Chris approves pricing
     ↓
  Step 6: vendor-management (if new vendor needed)
     → Verify supplier capacity; check backup options
     → Approval gate: Chris approves vendor

Phase 2: Build
  Step 7: engineering-lead (if software involved)
     → Architecture + tickets
     ↓
  Step 8: software-build.workflow.md (if applicable)
     → Full engineering chain runs
     ↓
  Step 9: database-schema (if data model changes)
     → Migrations
     ↓
  Step 10: ghl-crm-architect
     → New product / offer added to CRM (products, custom fields, pipeline tags if needed)
     → Update `_Shared Context/products.md` + `offers.md`
     ↓
  Step 11: integration-agent
     → Wire any new integrations (Stripe SKU, Shippo handling, Retell if voice involved)

Phase 3: Brand + Content
  Step 12: creative-director
     → Visual system brief (palette, typography, imagery — likely cinematic for premium)
     ↓
  Step 13: copywriting
     → Long-form: positioning page, FAQ, sales-enablement one-pager
     → Cold email sequence (per `cold-email-sequence`)
     → Social posts
     ↓
  Step 14: branded-carousel-generator + slide-deck-builder + explainer-graphic (PARALLEL)
     → Marketing visuals
     ↓
  Step 15: content-director
     → Launch content calendar (4-8 week ramp)
     ↓
  Step 16: compliance-qa
     → Every asset reviewed (banned phrases, claims, disclaimers, FDA-language)
     → Block 🔴

Phase 4: Distribution + Sales
  Step 17: ghl-form-builder
     → Pre-launch interest form (catalog request variant for this product)
     ↓
  Step 18: ghl-funnel-builder (or frontend-dev if Next.js flagship)
     → Landing page
     ↓
  Step 19: sales-strategy
     → Adapt sales playbook for the new offer
     → Update discovery question tree, objection library, FOMU wedge
     ↓
  Step 20: workflow-automation-builder
     → Wire launch-specific GHL workflows
     → Run ghl-eval-harness
     ↓
  Step 21: marketing-campaign.workflow.md (composes most of phase 3 + 4)
     → Full campaign launch
     ↓
  Step 22: voice-ai-sales-agent (if voice campaign)
     → Adapt Mack persona / scripts for the new offer
     → Run voice-eval-harness

Phase 5: Compliance Gate + Launch
  Step 23: security-inspector + compliance-qa (final pass)
     → Pre-launch gate
     → 🔴 blocks
     → Approval gate: Chris authorizes launch
     ↓
  Step 24: devops-deployment (if code involved)
     → Production push
     ↓
  Step 25: Activate
     → Forms live
     → Landing page live
     → GHL workflows active
     → Voice agent active (if any)
     → First outbound batch sent
     → Inventory available

Phase 6: Monitoring + Iteration (first 60 days)
  Step 26: Daily monitoring
     → ghl-daily-pulse highlights launch metrics
     → voice-call-reviewer (if voice)
     → analytics-architect dashboard updated
     → Auto-pause if metrics drop > 15%
     ↓
  Step 27: Weekly review
     → ghl-continuous-improvement.workflow.md cycles include launch metrics
     → Iteration proposals via voice-continuous-improvement.workflow.md (if voice)
     ↓
  Step 28: Day-60 launch retrospective
     → product-manager-agent leads
     → Pull all data: orders, customers, NPS, complaints, refunds
     → Compare to PRD success metrics
     → Decision: continue / iterate / kill / scale
     ↓
  Step 29: Customer-journey continues
     → New customers from launch flow into customer-journey.workflow.md
     → account-manager picks up active customers

Standard inputs

  • Product / offer name
  • Target audience (specific ICP segment within Matrix's clinic ICP)
  • Launch date (target)
  • Budget (build + launch marketing)
  • Success metrics (per PRD)
  • Compliance flags (HIPAA / FDA / state-specific)

Standard outputs

  • PRD: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-prd-<product>.md
  • Offer Brief: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-offer-brief-<product>.md
  • Architecture brief (if software): _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-eng-plan-<product>.md
  • Marketing assets: per asset's logged location
  • Launch report: _Logs/campaign-results/launch-<product>/<launch-date>-report.md
  • 60-day retrospective: _Logs/campaign-results/launch-<product>/60-day-retro.md

Approval gates (8)

  1. Step 1 — Market direction approved
  2. Step 3 — PRD approved
  3. Step 4 — Offer positioning approved
  4. Step 5 — Pricing approved
  5. Step 6 — Vendor approved (if new vendor)
  6. Step 16 — Compliance pass on all assets approved
  7. Step 23 — Final pre-launch security + compliance gate approved
  8. Step 28 — Day-60 decision (continue / iterate / kill / scale)

Estimated time

  • Phase 1 (strategy): 2-4 weeks
  • Phase 2 (build): 4-12 weeks depending on scope
  • Phase 3 (brand + content): 2-4 weeks (parallel with build)
  • Phase 4 (distribution): 1-3 weeks (parallel)
  • Phase 5 (launch): 1-2 weeks
  • Phase 6 (monitoring): 60 days post-launch
  • Total wall-clock: 12-24 weeks for medium launch (e.g., IV drips, new bundle)

Auto-pause / rollback triggers

If post-launch:

  • Orders drop > 50% below target in week 1
  • Compliance complaint (FDA / state / customer)
  • Critical bug in software components
  • Vendor / supply issue (compounding pharmacy backorder)
  • Refund rate > 5% in first 30 days
  • NPS < 40 in first 30 days

→ Pause active campaigns; investigate; loop back to relevant phase.

Failure modes + recovery

Phase Failure Recovery
1 Market signal weak Defer; revisit in 90 days OR pivot
2 Build delays Re-scope; ship MVP; defer V2
3 Compliance blocks assets Loop back; fix; re-review
4 Launch ramp slow Adjust outreach mix; tighten ICP filter
5 Security gate 🔴 Remediate before launch
6 KPIs miss target at Day 60 Iteration plan OR kill decision

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ All strategy / planning / design (drafts)
  • ✅ Asset production
  • ✅ Distribution setup (additive)
  • ✅ Eval-harness runs
  • ⚠️ All 8 approval gates
  • ⚠️ Vendor onboarding
  • ⚠️ Production deploys
  • 🚫 Skip compliance gate
  • 🚫 Skip security gate

Roll-up to executives

Every product launch shows in:

  • pmo-operator master board (current phase, milestone status)
  • weekly-review (until 60-day retro)
  • analytics-architect launch-specific dashboard
  • cfo-finance launch P&L (cost vs revenue generated)
  • Monthly + quarterly investor sections (launches shipped, performance)

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris (initial workflow)

workflows.name: Workflow: Quarterly Review
workflows.description: Standard 90-day rhythm — board / investor / strategic review. Composes finance + analytics + vendor + customer + product into one consolidated pull.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Quarterly Review

Standard 90-day rhythm — board / investor / strategic review. Composes finance + analytics + vendor + customer + product into one consolidated pull.

When this workflow fires

  • Scheduled: 1st of March / June / September / December at 9am CT via automation-scheduler
  • Pre-board meeting: 2 weeks before any board call
  • Pre-investor update: per IR cadence

Agent chain

Phase 1: Data pull (PARALLEL)
  Step 1a: cfo-finance
     → Quarterly P&L, balance sheet, cash flow
     → Forecast next 90 days
     → Pull from bookkeeping monthly closes
     ↓
  Step 1b: bookkeeping (verify final close for prior 3 months)
     → Confirm CPA-approved monthly packets exist
     ↓
  Step 1c: analytics-architect
     → Full quarterly KPI dashboard
     → Trend analysis (vs prior quarter, vs prior year)
     → Flag movers + lagging indicators
     ↓
  Step 1d: ghl-continuous-improvement (full quarter)
     → GHL pipeline health, conversation analytics, workflow performance
     → Roll up monthly weekly reviews
     ↓
  Step 1e: voice-call-reviewer (quarterly aggregate)
     → Voice AI agent performance across quarter
     → Iteration history + KPI trends
     ↓
  Step 1f: account-manager (portfolio aggregate)
     → Customer health distribution (🟢🟡🔴 mix over quarter)
     → Churn count + reasons
     → Expansion / renewal summary
     → NPS trend
     ↓
  Step 1g: vendor-management
     → Quarterly vendor review (per `vendor-management` workflow)
     → Renewals coming up next 90 days
     → Risk surfacing
     ↓
  Step 1h: project-overseer
     → All projects status (active / paused / stale / dormant)
     → Velocity by quarter

Phase 2: Synthesis
  Step 2: pmo-operator
     → Consolidate findings into single executive view
     → Score: 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 per department
     → Identify the "top 3" + "bottom 3" stories
     ↓
  Step 3: Strategic decision queue
     → Surface decisions needed in next quarter
     → Each with: impact, urgency, owner, deadline
     ↓
  Step 4: investor-relations (if board / investor cadence)
     → Refresh deck v2 with quarter's numbers
     → Draft monthly investor update with quarterly context
     → Update data room

Phase 3: Strategic Planning
  Step 5: product-manager-agent
     → Review roadmap status (Now / Next / Later)
     → Re-prioritize based on quarter's signal
     → Identify backlog candidates from customer feedback / sales data
     ↓
  Step 6: sales-strategy
     → Sales motion performance review
     → Identify highest-ROI segments
     → Adjust playbook for next quarter
     ↓
  Step 7: content-director
     → Next-quarter content plan tied to sales priorities
     → Themes for 90 days

Phase 4: Decisions + Approval
  Step 8: Compile the Quarterly Report
     → Single document with all sections
     ↓
  Step 9: Approval gate (Chris)
     → Review report
     → Decide: continue / iterate / pivot per department
     → Approve strategic decisions queue
     → Lock next-quarter priorities

Phase 5: Communication
  Step 10: Internal team brief
     → Distribute report to team (with appropriate scoping)
     ↓
  Step 11: Investor / board (if applicable)
     → Send report (per investor-relations approval)
     ↓
  Step 12: Customer comms (if material changes)
     → Roadmap-facing customer update

Standard inputs

  • Quarter (Q1 / Q2 / Q3 / Q4 + year)
  • Audience — internal / board / investor / customer
  • Period — last 90 days

Standard outputs

  • Quarterly Report: _Logs/quarterly/<YYYY>-Q<N>/report.md
  • Financial section: cfo-finance + bookkeeping output (pre-CPA approved)
  • KPI dashboard: analytics-architect output
  • Pipeline review: ghl-continuous-improvement quarterly roll-up
  • Voice AI review: voice-call-reviewer quarterly aggregate
  • Customer health: account-manager portfolio view
  • Vendor scorecard: vendor-management output
  • Project board: project-overseer status
  • Investor materials (if applicable): investor-relations refresh

Approval gates (1)

  • Step 9 — Chris reviews + approves strategic priorities for next quarter

Estimated time

  • Phase 1 (data pull, parallel): 1-2 days
  • Phase 2 (synthesis): 1-2 days
  • Phase 3 (planning): 2-3 days
  • Phase 4 (compile + approve): 1-2 days
  • Phase 5 (communication): 1-2 days
  • Total: 5-9 business days (week of quarter-end through following week)

Quarterly Report structure

# Q<N> <YYYY> Review — Matrix Advanced Solutions

## Headline
<one sentence: the quarter's main story>

## Numbers
- Revenue Q<N>: $<X> (vs Q<N-1>: $<Y>, delta: <%>)
- MRR / ARR at quarter-end: $<X>
- New customers acquired: <N>
- Active customers: <N> (vs Q<N-1>: <M>)
- Churn: <N> ($<Z> ARR impact)
- Cash position: $<X>
- Runway: <N> months
- Top 3 KPIs vs target: <list>

## Big wins
- <Concrete win 1>
- <Concrete win 2>
- <Concrete win 3>

## Department snapshots
### Voice AI ⭐⭐
<KPI table + iteration history + key learnings>

### GoHighLevel ⭐⭐
<KPI table + iteration history>

### Engineering
<Tickets shipped, deploys, incidents>

### Sales
<Pipeline value, win rate, CAC, top deals>

### Customer Success
<Health distribution, NPS, save rate, expansion>

### Finance
<Margins, AR aging, burn trend>

### (per department)

## Customer feedback themes
<Top 3 themes from voice-call-reviewer + account-manager + complaints>

## Strategic decisions made this quarter
- <Decision> — <outcome>

## Strategic decisions needed next quarter
- <Decision> — <impact / urgency / deadline>

## Roadmap status
- Shipped: <list>
- In progress: <list>
- Deferred: <list>
- New backlog candidates: <list>

## Risks (top 5)
| Risk | Severity | Mitigation | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|

## Vendor scorecard
<Top scoring, lowest scoring, renewals next 90 days, action items>

## Next quarter — top 3 priorities
1. <Priority>
2. <Priority>
3. <Priority>

## Asks (investors / advisors / board)
- <Specific intro / decision / advice>

## Forward-looking statements (disclaimed)
<For investor versions: "Forward-looking statements...">

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Pull all data
  • ✅ Synthesize
  • ✅ Generate quarterly report (drafts)
  • ✅ Refresh investor deck data layer
  • ⚠️ Approve report final (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Approve next-quarter priorities (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Distribute internally (Chris signs)
  • ⚠️ Send investor / board (Chris signs)

Failure modes + recovery

Step Failure Recovery
1b Monthly close not finalized Pause until bookkeeping + CPA close prior months
2 Departments score 🔴 broadly Compile but flag — Chris decides whether to delay external communication
4 Investor section data feels off Cross-verify with bookkeeping + Stripe direct
9 Chris not available for approval Defer external send; internal team brief OK to ship

Roll-up

The quarterly report becomes the input for:

  • Annual review (year-end)
  • Investor / board distribution
  • Strategic planning for next quarter
  • Customer-facing roadmap update (if material)

Last updated

2026-05-11 by Chris (initial workflow)

workflows.name: Workflow: Sales Rep Onboarding & Certification
workflows.description: End-to-end chain from new hire → certified + activated rep. Per `_Shared Context/education-program.md` + `~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/`. The gate that prevents uncertified reps from making customer-facing calls.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Sales Rep Onboarding & Certification

End-to-end chain from new hire → certified + activated rep. Per _Shared Context/education-program.md + ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/. The gate that prevents uncertified reps from making customer-facing calls.

Chain overview

[1] HR: New rep hired
       ↓
[2] education-training-lead: cohort assignment
       ↓
[3] learner-progress-tracker: enroll learner; assign track
       ↓
[4] LMS: rep completes onboarding module
       ↓ (per education-program.md graduation requirements)
[5] LMS: product knowledge quiz (≥ 85%)
       ↓
[6] LMS: compliance check (100% pass; zero critical-fail)
       ↓
[7] roleplay-coach-agent: 3 graded roleplays (Warm Lead + Objection Gauntlet); avg ≥ 80%
       ↓
[8] roleplay-coach-agent: full call simulation final (≥ 85%)
       ↓
[9] Manager signoff
       ↓
[10] sales-rep-certification-manager: pass/fail decision
       ↓ pass
[11] GHL activation: tags + access + notifications
       ↓
[12] Rep activated → eligible for live-lead routing + lead-to-booked-demo chain

When this workflow fires

  • New hire signed (HR creates record → triggers chain)
  • 1099 contractor sales rep signed
  • Re-hire (previously certified, returning) → expedited path
  • Cohort kickoff (batch of new hires at once)

Pre-flight

  • HR record + GHL contact created for rep
  • Rep assigned to certification track (sales-rep is default; specialized tracks for clinical-flavored roles)
  • Manager assigned
  • Welcome email sent
  • LMS access provisioned

Per-step detail

Step 1: HR creates new rep record

  • Triggered by HR / recruiting flow
  • New rep record in HRIS + GHL contact

Step 2: education-training-lead cohort assignment

  • Assign to current cohort OR start new cohort (batch every 2-4 weeks typical)
  • Assign manager
  • Set target cert date (14 days from start standard)

Step 3: learner-progress-tracker enrollment

  • Create learner record in LMS Supabase
  • Assign sales-rep track (with appropriate role-tier modules)
  • Send welcome email + LMS login
  • Pre-deadline nudge schedule (7d / 3d / 1d before cert date)

Step 4: Onboarding module completion

  • 100% completion required
  • LMS tracks per-lesson + per-section
  • Module sections per sales-curriculum-author

Step 5: Product knowledge quiz

  • ≥ 85% required
  • Drawn from question bank (per quiz-and-assessment-builder; 3x bank size for anti-cheat)
  • Up to 3 attempts in 14-day window
  • After 3 fails → mandatory 1:1 with manager + targeted re-training

Step 6: Compliance check

  • 100% required (zero violations on graded scenarios)
  • Critical-fail domains (per education-program.md) explicitly tested
  • Critical-fail violation → escalate to compliance-officer + Chris

Step 7: Roleplay sessions (graded)

  • 3 sessions minimum: 2x Warm Lead + 1x Objection Gauntlet (OR mix)
  • AI coach (roleplay-coach-agent) plays prospect
  • Each scored 0-100 across opening / discovery / pitch / objection-handling / close / compliance
  • Average ≥ 80% across 3 sessions

Step 8: Full call simulation final

  • Complete 6-step opening call with 2-3 objections
  • Compliance check live
  • Trade-secret discipline live
  • Final scorecard ≥ 85%

Step 9: Manager signoff

  • Manager reviews: LMS completion, quiz scores, roleplay scorecards, compliance pass
  • Final signoff in LMS
  • Signoff timestamp + name captured

Step 10: sales-rep-certification-manager decision

  • All thresholds met + manager signoff + no critical-fails → PASS
  • Else: CONDITIONAL (specific remediation) or FAIL (re-attempt window)
  • Decision logged

Step 11: Activation

  • GHL tags: rep:certified:<date> + rep:active
  • Remove tag: rep:training-only
  • Grant access: live-lead routing entitlement, GHL CRM write, voice agent handoffs
  • Notify: rep + manager + Christo
  • Log: _Logs/education/sales-certifications/<rep_id>-<date>.md

Step 12: Live work begins

  • Rep eligible for live leads
  • First lead routed via live-lead pipeline
  • 30-day post-cert close monitoring (cert manager reviews first-30 outcomes for false-positive cert risk)

Approval gates

Gate Step Approver
Track assignment 2 education-training-lead
Critical-fail violation 6, 7, 8 compliance-officer + Chris
Manager signoff 9 Manager (required; no auto-override)
Activation 11 sales-rep-certification-manager
Re-attempt > 3 same component 5, 7 Manager + cert manager (mandatory 1:1)

Failure recovery

Failure Recovery
Quiz fail 3x Manager 1:1 + targeted re-training + re-attempt scheduled
Compliance critical-fail Escalate to compliance-officer + Chris + remedial training; possibly disqualify
Roleplay scorecard < 80% Re-practice + re-grade up to 2 more attempts
Manager signoff withheld despite scores Manager + cert manager discussion; CONDITIONAL pending resolution
30+ days no progress Stale-learner nudge + manager intervention
Cert deadline missed Extension OR de-cohort (assign next cohort)
First 30 days post-cert reveals competency gap Suspension + remedial training + re-cert path

Outputs

Per rep:

  • Cert decision doc at _Logs/education/sales-certifications/<rep_id>-<date>.md
  • GHL contact tags updated
  • LMS state updated
  • Manager + Christo notifications

Per cohort:

  • Cohort completion report
  • Pass rate + time-to-cert metrics
  • Patterns for education-continuous-improvement

KPI targets

  • Time from hire → certified: ≤ 14 days standard
  • First-attempt pass rate: ≥ 70%
  • Critical-fail rate: < 5%
  • Manager signoff completion: 100% within 24h of last component pass

Related

  • _Shared Context/education-program.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/education-training-lead/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/sales-rep-certification-manager/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/learner-progress-tracker/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/roleplay-coach-agent/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/sales-curriculum-author/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/quiz-and-assessment-builder/SKILL.md
  • ~/Projects/Matrix Academy/lms/ (canonical LMS surface)

Last updated

2026-05-11 — initial sales rep onboarding & cert workflow

workflows.name: Workflow: SEO Rank & Visibility (umbrella)
workflows.description: The umbrella workflow for Matrix's SEO team — 8 specialists coordinated by `seo-strategy-lead` toward maximum visibility (top 3 Google for Tier 1 queries + highest rated on every directory + cited by AI search engines). Per `_Templates/micro-agent.template.md` chain pattern.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: SEO Rank & Visibility (umbrella)

The umbrella workflow for Matrix's SEO team — 8 specialists coordinated by seo-strategy-lead toward maximum visibility (top 3 Google for Tier 1 queries + highest rated on every directory + cited by AI search engines). Per _Templates/micro-agent.template.md chain pattern.

Engine overview

                ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
                │     SEO RANK & VISIBILITY ENGINE          │
                └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                  │
   ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
   ║  STRATEGY                                                  ║
   ║  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  ║
   ║  │  seo-strategy-lead                                   │  ║
   ║  │  → quarterly priorities + weekly orchestration       │  ║
   ║  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  ║
   ║                          ↓                                 ║
   ╠════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
   ║  FOUNDATION (Stage 1 — must be in place first)             ║
   ║  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  ║
   ║  │  local-seo-specialist   ← GBP + local pack           │  ║
   ║  │  technical-seo-specialist ← CWV + schema + indexing  │  ║
   ║  │  business-listing-manager ← 30+ directories          │  ║
   ║  │  review-acquisition-strategist ← 4.7+ stars          │  ║
   ║  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  ║
   ╠════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
   ║  AUTHORITY (Stage 2 — content + content authority)         ║
   ║  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  ║
   ║  │  content-seo-specialist  ← keyword + on-page         │  ║
   ║  │     ↓                                                │  ║
   ║  │  copywriting + product-copy + content-director       │  ║
   ║  │     (execute briefs from content-seo)                │  ║
   ║  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  ║
   ╠════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
   ║  AMPLIFICATION (Stage 3 — voice + AI search)               ║
   ║  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  ║
   ║  │  voice-search-optimizer ← featured snippets + voice  │  ║
   ║  │  ai-search-optimizer ← ChatGPT/Perplexity/Gemini    │  ║
   ║  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  ║
   ╠════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
   ║  COMPETITION (continuous)                                  ║
   ║  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  ║
   ║  │  competitor-serp-monitor ← monthly SOV tracking      │  ║
   ║  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  ║
   ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
                                  ↓
                  Tier 1 query top 3 ranks
                  + 4.7+ star avg everywhere
                  + 30+ directory presence
                  + AI search citation rate growing
                  + Featured snippets captured
                  + Organic-sessions YoY growth > 30%

When this workflow fires

  • Quarterly: full strategy review + priority reset (per seo-strategy-lead)
  • Weekly: weekly status sync (Friday 4:45pm if added to CI cluster — see scheduling section)
  • Monthly: SERP audit (competitor-serp-monitor)
  • On-demand: new sub-brand launch, ranking drop, algorithm update, crisis

Pre-flight (engine readiness)

  • seo-targets.md current (last updated < 90 days)
  • All 8 specialists healthy + eval-suite green
  • Search Console + Bing Webmaster Tools configured for all Matrix properties
  • PageSpeed Insights + CrUX field data available
  • Canonical NAP defined in company-profile.md
  • Competitor set current in seo-targets.md

Stage 1: Foundation (4-8 weeks for new site; ongoing thereafter)

Step 1.1: technical-seo-specialist baseline audit

  • Core Web Vitals current state per property
  • Schema markup audit
  • Indexing + crawl health
  • Mobile-friendliness pass
  • HTTPS / security headers
  • Handoff: issues → frontend-dev + backend-dev + devops-deployment per pr-review-process.sop.md

Step 1.2: local-seo-specialist Google Business Profile

  • Claim + verify GBP for Matrix HQ
  • Categories + attributes + services list
  • Photos + posts cadence
  • Q&A pre-populated
  • NAP canonical established → business-listing-manager

Step 1.3: business-listing-manager 30+ directory sweep

  • Tier 1: Google + Apple + Bing + Yelp
  • Tier 2-3: industry (NPPES, state pharmacy boards, FDA)
  • Tier 4: LinkedIn / Facebook / Instagram / YouTube / X
  • Tier 5: ChatGPT Business / Perplexity / Wikidata
  • Tier 6: local-specific
  • NAP consistent everywhere
  • Handoff: status → seo-strategy-lead quarterly + ongoing

Step 1.4: review-acquisition-strategist velocity + standards

  • Per-platform velocity targets
  • Response standards (within 48h)
  • Negative-review protocol
  • Handoff: ask execution → review-collection + account-manager

Stage 2: Authority (3-12 months — sustained content effort)

Step 2.1: content-seo-specialist keyword + content strategy

  • Keyword research per sub-brand
  • Search-intent mapping
  • Content-gap analysis
  • Topic-cluster design (pillar + supporting)
  • On-page recommendations
  • Handoff: briefs → copywriting + product-copy + content-director

Step 2.2: Content production

  • copywriting produces long-form per brief
  • product-copy optimizes PDPs per spec
  • content-director aligns to campaigns
  • compliance-officer Gate 1 review on regulated copy
  • Published with proper schema (per technical-seo-specialist)

Step 2.3: Internal linking + cluster building

  • Pillar pages link to all supporting
  • Supporting link back + to peers
  • Cross-sub-brand linking where strategically beneficial

Stage 3: Amplification (alongside Stage 2)

Step 3.1: voice-search-optimizer snippet targeting

  • Identify Tier 2 queries with snippet opportunity
  • Restructure content for snippet capture (Q as H2, 40-60 word answer)
  • Deploy FAQ + HowTo + Speakable schema
  • Track snippet capture rates

Step 3.2: ai-search-optimizer citation foundation

  • Wikipedia eligibility + entry (when press density met)
  • Wikidata entry
  • High-DR press placements via partnership-agent
  • Original-research publication (Steve's portfolio = gold)
  • Podcast + conference exposure
  • Per-AI-engine visibility audit quarterly

Continuous: Competition + iteration

Monthly: competitor-serp-monitor

  • 50+ queries tracked
  • SOV per competitor
  • Steal-rank opportunities surfaced
  • Competitor content patterns analyzed

Weekly: seo-strategy-lead orchestration

  • Status check on priorities
  • Alert triage (rank drops / listing issues / negative reviews)
  • KPI tracking

Quarterly: Strategy reset

  • seo-targets.md refreshed
  • Competitor set updated
  • Priorities re-baselined
  • Budget allocation

Critical gates + approvals

Gate Step Approver
Initial GBP claim / verification 1.2 Chris (business representation)
Compliance on regulated content 2.2 compliance-officer
Wikipedia article filing 3.2 Chris + counsel (eligibility-dependent)
Negative review response (material) continuous compliance-officer if regulatory context; Chris if PR-risk
Steal-rank content brief vs competitor continuous seo-strategy-lead
New directory + listing approval 1.3 compliance-officer (claims-discipline)

Failure recovery

Failure Recovery
Algorithm update mass-impact All specialists triage; lead synthesizes; report to Chris within 7 days
GBP suspended Recovery request via Google + audit causes
Mass NAP drift discovered business-listing-manager urgent cleanup
Tier 1 query rank drop > 5 positions Diagnostic chain: technical + content + competitor
Negative review cluster (3+ same issue) Root-cause + product / process fix + reputation response
Wikipedia article rejected / nominated for deletion Pause + counsel + community engagement
AI search citation rate declining ai-search-optimizer deep audit + authority-build refresh

KPI targets

Per _Shared Context/seo-targets.md:

  • Tier 1 query top 3 ranks: > 80% within 12 months
  • GBP rating: > 4.7 + 100+ reviews
  • Yelp rating: > 4.5
  • Listings live + NAP-consistent: 30+
  • Core Web Vitals pass: > 90% pages
  • AI search citation rate: quarterly growth
  • Featured snippets captured: 25%+ of Tier 2 targets
  • Organic sessions YoY: > 30% growth

Surfaced in:

  • /seo page in ai-command-center (when built)
  • chain-monitor daily digest (any SEO-related alerts)
  • Friday CI cluster (Nexus 4:30pm + potentially seo-strategy-lead at 4:45pm)
  • Quarterly review

Scheduling considerations

This workflow's per-skill cadences:

  • Daily: chain-monitor includes SEO alerts in 7am digest
  • Weekly Friday 4:45pm (proposed addition to CI cluster): seo-strategy-lead weekly review
  • Monthly: competitor-serp-monitor audit
  • Quarterly: seo-strategy-lead strategic review; business-listing-manager audit; ai-search-optimizer visibility audit

To schedule the weekly seo-strategy-lead via mcp__scheduled-tasks__create_scheduled_task — closes out the Friday CI cluster as the SEO summary.

Outputs

Per-stage:

  • Audit reports per specialist at _Logs/seo/<specialist>/<period>-audit.md
  • Briefs (content) at _Logs/seo/content/briefs/<topic>.md
  • Keyword map at _Logs/seo/content/keyword-map.csv
  • NAP canonical at _Logs/seo/local/nap-canonical.md
  • Directory tracker at _Logs/seo/listings/directory-tracker.csv

Per-period:

  • Weekly status at _Logs/seo/weekly/<YYYY-WW>.md
  • Monthly competitor at _Logs/seo/serp/<period>-monthly.md
  • Quarterly strategy at _Logs/seo/strategy/<YYYY-Q>-quarterly.md

Cost + cadence targets

  • Per-specialist monthly cost: $5-20 LLM (audits + briefs)
  • External tools: Search Console + Bing Webmaster (free); SEMrush/Ahrefs (paid; eval value quarterly)
  • Initial foundation phase: 4-8 weeks specialist-time
  • Quarterly review cycle: 1-2 days specialist-time

Related

  • _Shared Context/seo-targets.md
  • _Shared Context/sub-brands.md
  • _Shared Context/brand-guide.md
  • _Shared Context/company-profile.md (canonical NAP)
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/seo-strategy-lead/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/local-seo-specialist/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/technical-seo-specialist/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/content-seo-specialist/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/voice-search-optimizer/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/ai-search-optimizer/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/business-listing-manager/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/review-acquisition-strategist/SKILL.md
  • _Skills Library/Company-OS-Skills/competitor-serp-monitor/SKILL.md

Last updated

2026-05-11 — initial SEO umbrella workflow; 8 specialists + 1 lead + 1 targets doc + 1 workflow built same day

workflows.name: Workflow: Software Build (end-to-end)
workflows.description: Standard chain for building any Matrix software feature / product. Composes engineering agents from product spec to production deploy.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Software Build (end-to-end)

Standard chain for building any Matrix software feature / product. Composes engineering agents from product spec to production deploy.

When this workflow fires

  • "Build the [feature] for [Matrix product]"
  • "Ship the [PRD-name]"
  • "Spec + build [scope]"
  • Manual invocation by Chris after a product decision

Agent chain

Step 1: reuse-scanner
   → Check existing Matrix projects for similar components / patterns
   → If close match → propose reuse vs build-new
   ↓
Step 2: product-manager-agent
   → Write PRD (problem, users, solution, metrics, out-of-scope)
   → Approval gate: Chris approves PRD
   ↓
Step 3: engineering-lead
   → Architecture brief + stack decision + ticket breakdown
   → Compliance gates identified (HIPAA / PCI / etc.)
   → Approval gate: Chris approves architecture
   ↓
Step 4: project-scaffolder (if new project)
   → Folder + CLAUDE.md + TASKS.md + README + initial commit
   → Skip if existing project
   ↓
Step 5: database-schema (if data involved)
   → Schema design + RLS policies + migrations
   → Apply to staging
   → Approval gate: Chris approves migrations (especially destructive)
   ↓
Step 6: backend-dev + frontend-dev + integration-agent (PARALLEL — different tickets)
   → backend-dev: API routes, server logic, webhooks
   → frontend-dev: components, pages, UI flows
   → integration-agent: external API / webhook glue
   ↓
Step 7: code-qa
   → Review each PR before merge
   → Block 🔴 findings
   → Approval gate: Chris merges (per `approval-rules.md`)
   ↓
Step 8: compliance-qa (if any user-facing copy)
   → Audit copy/claims in UI
   → Banned phrases, required disclaimers
   ↓
Step 9: security-inspector
   → Pre-launch security audit
   → Routes to HIPAA Spoon Test if PHI involved
   → Approval gate: 🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴 — 🔴 blocks
   ↓
Step 10: voice-eval-harness (if voice agent involved)
   → If the build includes a voice agent → run full eval before deploy
   ↓
Step 11: devops-deployment
   → Pre-flight checks (lint / typecheck / tests / build / eval)
   → Deploy to staging
   → Approval gate: Chris authorizes production
   → Deploy to production
   → Wire monitoring + rollback path
   ↓
Step 12: pmo-operator
   → Register build outcome, mark milestones complete
   → Update agent-registry if new persistent agents shipped
   ↓
Step 13: analytics-architect
   → Wire success metrics defined in PRD (Step 2)
   → Create dashboard view
   ↓
Step 14: Post-launch monitoring (first 7 days)
   → Watch error rate, latency, business metric
   → Auto-rollback triggers if KPIs drop > 15%
   ↓
Step 15: Retrospective (Friday after first-week launch)
   → Per `weekly-review` — what worked, what didn't
   → Feed lessons into agent-registry for next build

Standard inputs

  • Product / project: which Matrix product
  • Business goal: outcome served
  • Customer feedback / signal: what triggered this
  • Constraints: time, budget, compliance, dependencies
  • Stakeholders: Chris + relevant team

Standard outputs

  • PRD: ~/Projects/<product>/PRDs/<feature>.md
  • Architecture brief: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-eng-plan-<feature>.md
  • Tickets: in PMO board / GitHub Issues / Linear
  • Code: shipped to production
  • Audits: _Logs/security-audits/ + _Logs/compliance-reviews/
  • Monitoring: dashboards live
  • Retrospective: in weekly-review

Approval gates (5)

  1. After Step 2 — PRD approved
  2. After Step 3 — Architecture approved
  3. After Step 5 — DB migrations approved (if any destructive)
  4. After Step 9 — Security/compliance cleared (🟢 or 🟡 with mitigations)
  5. After Step 11 staging — Production deploy authorized

Estimated time

  • Steps 1-4 (planning): 2-4 hours
  • Step 5 (schema, if any): 1-3 hours
  • Step 6 (build, parallel): 1-3 weeks depending on scope
  • Steps 7-10 (review + gates): 2-5 days (mostly gate-wait time)
  • Step 11 (deploy): 30-60 min
  • Steps 12-13 (registration + metrics wiring): 1-2 hours
  • Step 14-15 (monitor + retro): 1 week

Total wall-clock for medium build: 3-5 weeks.

Auto-pause / rollback triggers (Step 14)

If during first 7 days post-launch:

  • Error rate > baseline + 50%
  • Critical path latency > p99 budget
  • Business metric (defined in PRD) drops > 15% vs target
  • Any compliance flag fires
  • User-reported critical bug

→ Pause + rollback (per devops-deployment rollback path)

Failure modes + recovery

Step Failure Recovery
1 reuse-scanner says rebuild what exists Stop; use existing
2 PRD vague / scope unclear Loop with PM agent for sharper PRD
3 Stack decision contested Decision Council to settle
5 Migration would break production Refactor approach; staged migration plan
6 Ticket scope exceeds estimate PM + Eng-lead re-scope; defer non-essential
7 Code QA blocks Loop back to dev agents; fix; re-review
9 Security gate 🔴 Remediate; do not proceed; re-inspect
11 Deploy fails Apply retellai-incident-runbook pattern
14 Post-launch metric drops Rollback; investigate; re-iterate

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run reuse-scanner, PRD draft, architecture draft
  • ✅ Scaffold new projects
  • ✅ Apply staging migrations
  • ✅ Build code (parallel)
  • ✅ Run code-qa, compliance-qa, security-inspector audits
  • ✅ Deploy to staging
  • ✅ Run evals
  • ⚠️ Approve PRD
  • ⚠️ Approve architecture / stack
  • ⚠️ Approve production migrations
  • ⚠️ Approve production deploy
  • 🚫 Auto-deploy without gate clearance
  • 🚫 Override 🔴 from security or compliance gates

Roll-up to executives

Every build shows in:

  • pmo-operator master board (status, milestones, owners)
  • weekly-review (shipped + in-flight)
  • analytics-architect dashboard (success metrics post-launch)
  • agent-registry.md (if new persistent agents)

Last updated

2026-05-10 by Chris (initial seed; refines per first 3-5 builds run)

workflows.name: Workflow: Voice Agent Build (end-to-end)
workflows.description: Standard chain for building, testing, and shipping a voice AI agent. Loaded by `company-os-orchestrator` when a voice agent request comes in.
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Voice Agent Build (end-to-end)

Standard chain for building, testing, and shipping a voice AI agent. Loaded by company-os-orchestrator when a voice agent request comes in.

When this workflow fires

  • "build a voice agent"
  • "deploy a voice agent for [use case]"
  • "ship a Retell agent"
  • Manual invocation by Chris

Agent chain

Step 1: should-i-build-an-agent
   → Verify voice agent is right tool (not workflow / live human / async)
   → Approval gate: Chris approves "go"
   ↓
Step 2: conversation-channel-router
   → Confirm voice is the right channel for the use case
   → If voice not right → recommend alternative + STOP
   ↓
Step 3: market-research (if cold use case + no ICP yet)
   → ICP + pain + competitive position
   → Skip if ICP already defined for the use case
   ↓
Step 4: offer-positioning (if pitching an offer)
   → Offer Brief
   → Skip if not a sales call
   ↓
Step 5: conversation-flow-designer
   → State machine + branches + edge cases
   → Output: flow doc
   ↓
Step 6: voice-script-author
   → State-by-state scripts + edge-case language + voicemail
   → Output: full script pack
   ↓
Step 7: voice-ai-agent-builder
   → Platform selection (Retell / Vapi / Synthflow / etc.)
   → System prompt assembled
   → Functions defined
   → Webhooks wired
   → Deploy to STAGING
   ↓
Step 8: ghl-crm-architect (additive)
   → Pipeline + stages for call outcomes
   → Tags for intent / outcome
   → Custom fields if needed
   → Auto-tasks for hot leads
   ↓
Step 9: security-inspector
   → Compliance audit (TCPA + state laws + HIPAA if applicable)
   → Recording disclosure verified
   → Transfer-to-human always available
   → Gate: 🔴 BLOCK / 🟡 conditional / 🟢 clear
   ↓
Step 10: Eval set run
   → 12+ test conversations via Retell staging
   → Pass criteria check
   → Iterate scripts/prompts if failures
   ↓
Step 11: Approval gate (Chris)
   → Review eval results
   → Review compliance posture
   → Approve production deploy
   ↓
Step 12: Production deploy
   → Retell agent ID flipped to prod
   → Phone number routed
   → Monitoring dashboard wired
   → Kill switch documented
   ↓
Step 13: First-50-calls observation
   → Daily monitoring outputs
   → Chris reviews + approves clear-to-scale
   ↓
Step 14: pmo-operator registers project
   → Adds to master project board
   → Sets owner + weekly check-in cadence
   ↓
Step 15: weekly-review picks up
   → Ongoing tuning based on KPIs
   → Script iteration approvals

Standard inputs to the workflow

  • Use case: outbound cold / inbound front-desk / discovery-call assist / support / etc.
  • Goal of the call: book strategy call / qualify + tag / send catalog / answer FAQ / etc.
  • Offer (if applicable): from _Shared Context/offers.md
  • List (if outbound): verified contacts with intent signals
  • Phone number (if inbound): the inbound number to route
  • Hours: 24/7 / business / after-hours
  • Compliance flags: HIPAA? state two-party consent? A2P?

Standard outputs

  • Spec file: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-voice-agent-spec-<use-case>.md
  • Flow file: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-flow-<use-case>.md
  • Scripts file: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-scripts-<use-case>.md
  • Audit file: _Logs/security-audits/<date>-voice-<use-case>.md
  • Eval file: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-eval-<use-case>.md
  • Project entry: in agent-registry.md + pmo-operator master board

Approval gates (3)

  1. After Step 1 (should-i-build-an-agent) — Chris approves voice is right answer
  2. After Step 11 (eval pass + compliance pass) — Chris approves production deploy
  3. After Step 13 (first 50 calls) — Chris approves clear-to-scale

Estimated time

  • Build (Steps 1-10): 4-8 hours
  • Approval gate cycles: 2-5 business days (depending on Chris's availability)
  • First-50-calls: 1-3 days at usual volume
  • Total time to scale: 1-2 weeks

Required pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Retell account access
  • ✅ GHL location ID configured (JhHwwzVOQOZO2IFLSJAD for Matrix)
  • ✅ Phone number provisioned + verified
  • ⚠️ TCPA / state consent registration (one-time per state)
  • ⚠️ A2P 10DLC (if SMS follow-ups)
  • ⚠️ HIPAA-aware infrastructure (if patient data could surface)

Failure modes + recovery

Failure Recovery
Step 1: agent not right Re-route to email / chat / live human; STOP voice build
Step 3-4: no ICP / offer clarity Pause; complete those first; resume
Step 5-6: flow / scripts won't pass brand-voice QA Iterate before moving to platform
Step 7: platform tooling broken Check retellai-debug-bundle + harness troubleshooting skills
Step 9: 🔴 compliance Remediate findings; do NOT proceed; re-inspect
Step 10: eval fails Iterate flow + scripts; re-eval; if persists → reconsider use case
Step 12: prod deploy fails Apply retellai-incident-runbook
Step 13: first 50 calls show issues Pause; refactor; re-test

Roll-up to executives

After Step 14, the project shows on the master board with:

  • Status (active / paused / production)
  • Owner (Chris by default)
  • Last activity (most recent monitoring report)
  • Open KPI gaps (vs targets)
  • Next milestone (next iteration or expansion)

Last updated

2026-05-10 by Chris (initial seed)

workflows.name: Workflow: Voice AI Continuous Improvement
workflows.description: Weekly cycle to keep production voice agents getting better, not drifting. Composes `voice-call-reviewer` + `voice-eval-harness` + the originating skills (`voice-script-author`, `voice-ai-agent-builder`).
workflows.body_html:
Workflow: Voice AI Continuous Improvement

Weekly cycle to keep production voice agents getting better, not drifting. Composes voice-call-reviewer + voice-eval-harness + the originating skills (voice-script-author, voice-ai-agent-builder).

When this workflow fires

  • Scheduled: every Friday 3pm via automation-scheduler for every active production voice agent
  • On-demand: after any major spike / drop in metrics
  • Triggered: when voice-call-reviewer flags 🔴 (sharp metric drop or compliance issue)

Agent chain

Step 1: voice-call-reviewer
   → Pull last 7 days of call data per agent
   → Classify outcomes
   → Identify stage dropoffs
   → Surface patterns from transcripts
   → Generate improvement proposals
   ↓
Step 2: Review meeting (Chris + optional)
   → Read the review report
   → Approve which proposals ship this cycle
   → Defer others to backlog
   ↓
Step 3: For each approved proposal
   ├── 3a. Script changes → voice-script-author
   ├── 3b. Flow changes → conversation-flow-designer
   ├── 3c. Agent / platform changes → voice-ai-agent-builder
   ├── 3d. KB gaps → voice-knowledge-base (add entries)
   └── 3e. Persona adjustments → voice-persona-library (rare; escalate)
   ↓
Step 4: voice-eval-harness
   → Add new test cases for the patterns being fixed
   → Run full suite against the proposed change in staging
   → Diff vs baseline — flag regressions
   ↓
Step 5: Approval gate (Chris)
   → Review eval results
   → If 🟢 → approve ship
   → If 🟡 → discuss soft fails; ship or defer
   → If 🔴 → fix-first loop back to Step 3
   ↓
Step 6: devops-deployment
   → Ship the changes to production (per Retell agent version)
   → Document rollback
   ↓
Step 7: Monitor first 50 calls
   → Real-time KPI watch
   → Auto-pause triggers active
   ↓
Step 8: Log to campaign-results
   → Capture what changed + measured impact
   → Update agent's "iteration history" in agent-registry
   ↓
Step 9: Cycle complete; next Friday repeats

Standard inputs

  • Agent ID(s) to review (one or many)
  • Date range (default: last 7 days)
  • Comparison baseline (default: prior period)
  • KPI targets (from agent's project brief or persona-library entry)

Standard outputs

  • Weekly review report: _Logs/campaign-results/<agent>/<date>-review.md
  • Approved proposals shipped: _Logs/agent-decisions/<date>-iteration-<agent>-v<N>.md
  • Eval run report: _Logs/eval-suites/<agent>/runs/<date>.md
  • Production deploy log: per devops-deployment
  • First-50-calls observation: _Logs/campaign-results/<agent>/<date>-post-ship.md

Approval gates (2 per cycle)

  1. After Step 1 — Chris reviews and approves which proposals ship this cycle (not all proposals every week; pick top 3-5)
  2. After Step 5 — Chris reviews eval results before production deploy

Estimated time

  • Step 1 (review): 30-60 min (depends on call volume)
  • Steps 3-4 (implementation + eval): 1-2 hours per proposal
  • Steps 5-6 (gate + deploy): 30 min
  • Step 7 (post-ship monitoring): 1-3 days

Total wall-clock per agent per cycle: 1 week (Friday review → next Friday post-ship observation complete).

Auto-pause triggers (Step 7)

If during the first 50 post-ship calls, ANY:

  • KPI dropped > 15% vs prior period
  • New compliance flag fires (recording disclosure missed, transfer-to-human failed, etc.)
  • Function-call failure rate > 10%
  • Customer complaints (post-call survey or direct)

→ Auto-pause the agent + alert Chris. Rollback to prior version. Re-investigate.

Failure modes + recovery

Failure Recovery
voice-call-reviewer produces no actionable findings Skip this cycle; flag for re-review in 2 weeks
Proposed change fails eval Loop back to script/flow author with the failing eval case
Eval suite has too many soft fails Investigate suite quality; some may be outdated cases — Chris approves removal
Production deploy fails Apply retellai-incident-runbook; rollback via devops-deployment
First 50 calls show drop Auto-pause; rollback; investigate; re-cycle

Per-agent iteration log

In agent-registry.md (or a dedicated _Logs/iterations/<agent>.md):

| Cycle | Date | Changes shipped | KPI before | KPI after | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 2026-05-09 | Opener rewrite for clinic-owner ICP | 8% meeting-set | 12% | Win — pattern: lead with specific signal |
| 2 | 2026-05-16 | Voicemail script shorter | 65% voicemail rate | 58% | Win — but still above target |
| 3 | 2026-05-23 | Added objection rebuttal for "we have a vendor" | 22% objection-lost | 16% | Win |

Trend visible across cycles. After 4-6 cycles, the agent's KPIs should be measurably better than launch.

Pre-authorizations

  • ✅ Run voice-call-reviewer (read-only)
  • ✅ Generate proposals
  • ✅ Add eval cases (additive)
  • ✅ Run eval suite against staging
  • ⚠️ Approve which proposals ship per cycle (Chris)
  • ⚠️ Production deploy of changes (Chris)
  • 🚫 Auto-deploy without eval pass
  • 🚫 Remove eval cases without approval

Roll-up to executives

After each cycle, the agent's iteration entry surfaces in:

  • pmo-operator master board (current agent version + KPI trend)
  • weekly-review (cross-agent summary)
  • Monthly board / investor section (KPI improvements quarter-over-quarter)

Last updated

2026-05-10 by Chris (initial cycle definition — refines as we run iterations)
